# RPCE Practice Questions (generated, RONR 12th-ed.-grounded)

6241 questions across 2012 concepts. Types are varied so the same fact resurfaces in different shapes (cloze with hints, applied multiple choice, select-all, order-of-precedence ranking and ordering, and motion-characteristic questions). Every question is answerable from what it shows; the RONR (12th ed.) section is cited only in the answer.

Counts by type: Amendability (18), Applied multiple-choice (2768), Cloze recall (3036), Debatability (18), Precedence (ordering) (77), Precedence (ranking) (154), Precedence (select all) (74), Precedence (which is higher) (60), Second required (18), Vote required (18)

> Reproduce with `python pylib/tools/rpce_generate_questions.py`.

## Domain 1: Motions in General and Main Motions

**1.** `[K0049 · Vote required]` What vote does a main motion require to be adopted?
   A. Majority vote
   B. Two-thirds vote
   C. No vote (chair rules / demand)
   D. Unanimous consent

   *Answer:* Majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:25 — “In the case of a series of resolutions relating to a single subject, if members desire one or more of the resolutions to be considered separately, the motion for Division of a Question (27) must be made and adopted by a majority vote.”

**2.** `[K0050 · Debatability]` Is a main motion debatable?
   A. Debatable
   B. Not debatable

   *Answer:* Debatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:7 — “(Compare the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, 15, and the privileged motion to Recess, 20.) Referring to the Table of Rules Relating to Motions on pages t6–t33, most of the motions listed as “main”—with the exception of No.”

**3.** `[K0051 · Second required]` Does a main motion require a second?
   A. Requires a second
   B. No second required

   *Answer:* Requires a second. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:7 — “Most of the incidental main motions closely correspond to secondary (subsidiary, privileged, or incidental) motions described by the same or similar names—as in the last two of the four examples above.”

**4.** `[K0052 · Amendability]` Can a main motion be amended?
   A. Amendable
   B. Not amendable

   *Answer:* Amendable. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:10 — “also, possible attempts to amend a motion to reaffirm would come into conflict with the rules for the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35);”

**5.** `[C0157 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is a motion whose introduction brings business before the assembly.

   *Answer:* main motion — “A main motion is a motion whose introduction brings business before the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:1 — “A main motion is a motion whose introduction brings business before the assembly.”

**6.** `[C0157 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A main motion is a motion whose _____ brings business before the assembly.
   A. undebatable
   B. Organization
   C. subsequently
   D. introduction

   *Answer:* D) introduction. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:1 — “A main motion is a motion whose introduction brings business before the assembly.”

**7.** `[C0158 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is usual to distinguish between _____ and incidental main motions—which differ principally in the nature of their subject matter.

   *Answer:* original main motions — “It is usual to distinguish between original main motions and incidental main motions—which differ principally in the nature of their subject matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:2 — “It is usual to distinguish between original main motions and incidental main motions—which differ principally in the nature of their subject matter.”

**8.** `[C0158 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is usual to distinguish between original main motions and _____—which differ principally in the nature of their subject matter.
   A. Effect of the Objection
   B. Articles of Association
   C. parliamentary authority
   D. incidental main motions

   *Answer:* D) incidental main motions. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:2 — “It is usual to distinguish between original main motions and incidental main motions—which differ principally in the nature of their subject matter.”

**9.** `[C0158 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that incidental main motions form a category completely separate from _____—the fourth general class of motions.

   *Answer:* incidental motions — “It should be noted that incidental main motions form a category completely separate from incidental motions—the fourth general class of motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:2 — “It should be noted that incidental main motions form a category completely separate from incidental motions—the fourth general class of motions.”

**10.** `[C0158 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ in the rules governing the use of main motions in these two subclasses is only slight.

   *Answer:* difference — “The difference in the rules governing the use of main motions in these two subclasses is only slight.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:2 — “The difference in the rules governing the use of main motions in these two subclasses is only slight.”

**11.** `[C0158 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that incidental main motions form a category completely separate from _____—the fourth general class of motions.
   A. incidental motions
   B. Subsidiary motions
   C. executive director
   D. present and voting

   *Answer:* A) incidental motions. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:2 — “It should be noted that incidental main motions form a category completely separate from incidental motions—the fourth general class of motions.”

**12.** `[C0159 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ assist the assembly in treating or disposing of a main motion (and sometimes other motions).

   *Answer:* Subsidiary motions — “Subsidiary motions assist the assembly in treating or disposing of a main motion (and sometimes other motions).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:3 — “Subsidiary motions assist the assembly in treating or disposing of a main motion (and sometimes other motions).”

**13.** `[C0159 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ motions assist the assembly in treating or disposing of a main motion (and sometimes other motions).
   A. committees
   B. landscaped
   C. Permission
   D. Subsidiary

   *Answer:* D) Subsidiary. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:3 — “Subsidiary motions assist the assembly in treating or disposing of a main motion (and sometimes other motions).”

**14.** `[C0160 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of the subsidiary motions only, their distinguishing characteristics as a class are explained _____ this description of the individual motions, since these characteristics will be more easily understood if the material is read in that order.

   *Answer:* after — “In the case of the subsidiary motions only, their distinguishing characteristics as a class are explained after this description of the individual motions, since these characteristics will be more easily understood if the material is read in that order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:4 — “In the case of the subsidiary motions only, their distinguishing characteristics as a class are explained after this description of the individual motions, since these characteristics will be more easily understood if the material is read in that order.”

**15.** `[C0160 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of the subsidiary motions only, their distinguishing _____ as a class are explained after this description of the individual motions, since these characteristics will be more easily understood if the material is read in that order.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. characteristics
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* C) characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:4 — “In the case of the subsidiary motions only, their distinguishing characteristics as a class are explained after this description of the individual motions, since these characteristics will be more easily understood if the material is read in that order.”

**16.** `[C0160 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The subsidiary motions are listed below in reverse order of rank—which is the _____ order in which they would be moved if all of them became pending at one time.

   *Answer:* chronological — “The subsidiary motions are listed below in reverse order of rank—which is the chronological order in which they would be moved if all of them became pending at one time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:4 — “The subsidiary motions are listed below in reverse order of rank—which is the chronological order in which they would be moved if all of them became pending at one time.”

   C. chronological
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) chronological. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:4 — “The subsidiary motions are listed below in reverse order of rank—which is the chronological order in which they would be moved if all of them became pending at one time.”

**20.** `[C0162 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Subsidiary motions as a class are distinguished by having _____ five of the following characteristics: (1) They are always applied to another motion while it is pending, to aid in treating or disposing of it;

   *Answer:* all — “Subsidiary motions as a class are distinguished by having all five of the following characteristics: (1) They are always applied to another motion while it is pending, to aid in treating or disposing of it;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:6 — “Subsidiary motions as a class are distinguished by having all five of the following characteristics: (1) They are always applied to another motion while it is pending, to aid in treating or disposing of it;”

**21.** `[C0162 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Subsidiary motions as a class are distinguished by having all _____ of the following characteristics: (1) They are always applied to another motion while it is pending, to aid in treating or disposing of it;
   A. floor
   B. Refer
   C. five
   D. Forum

   *Answer:* C) five. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:6 — “Subsidiary motions as a class are distinguished by having all five of the following characteristics: (1) They are always applied to another motion while it is pending, to aid in treating or disposing of it;”

**22.** `[C0162 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: the adoption of one of them always _____ something to this other motion—that is, changes its status in some way—without adopting or expressly rejecting it.
   A. exact
   B. does
   C. clerk
   D. Amend

   *Answer:* B) does. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:6 — “the adoption of one of them always does something to this other motion—that is, changes its status in some way—without adopting or expressly rejecting it.”

**23.** `[C0162 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: the adoption of one of them always does _____ this other motion—that is, changes its status in some way—without adopting or expressly rejecting it.

   *Answer:* something to — “the adoption of one of them always does something to this other motion—that is, changes its status in some way—without adopting or expressly rejecting it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:6 — “the adoption of one of them always does something to this other motion—that is, changes its status in some way—without adopting or expressly rejecting it.”

   A. class

   C. after
   D. any

   *Answer:* D) any. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:6 — “(2) They can be applied to any main motion.”

**25.** `[C0163 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Cases Where One Subsidiary Motion Can Be Applied to _____.

   *Answer:* Another — “Cases Where One Subsidiary Motion Can Be Applied to Another.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:7 — “Cases Where One Subsidiary Motion Can Be Applied to Another.”

**26.** `[C0163 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ is applicable to many other motions in addition to the main motion.
   A. Amend
   B. limit
   C. Annul
   D. clerk

   *Answer:* A) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:7 — “The subsidiary motion to Amend is applicable to many other motions in addition to the main motion.”

**27.** `[C0163 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: All of the subsidiary motions can be amended except _____ Indefinitely, the Previous Question, and Lay on the Table (which, by the nature of what they propose, do not lend themselves to amendment).

   *Answer:* Postpone — “All of the subsidiary motions can be amended except Postpone Indefinitely, the Previous Question, and Lay on the Table (which, by the nature of what they propose, do not lend themselves to amendment).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:7 — “All of the subsidiary motions can be amended except Postpone Indefinitely, the Previous Question, and Lay on the Table (which, by the nature of what they propose, do not lend themselves to amendment).”

**28.** `[C0163 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: All of the subsidiary motions can be amended except Postpone _____, the Previous Question, and Lay on the Table (which, by the nature of what they propose, do not lend themselves to amendment).
   A. Information
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Convention
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* B) Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:7 — “All of the subsidiary motions can be amended except Postpone Indefinitely, the Previous Question, and Lay on the Table (which, by the nature of what they propose, do not lend themselves to amendment).”

   *Answer:* Limit or — “In such a case, motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, for the Previous Question, and to Lay on the Table are in order;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:7 — “In such a case, motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, for the Previous Question, and to Lay on the Table are in order;”

**30.** `[C0164 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The four lowest-ranking subsidiary motions can be debated (except that _____ is undebatable when it is applied to an undebatable motion).

   *Answer:* Amend — “The four lowest-ranking subsidiary motions can be debated (except that Amend is undebatable when it is applied to an undebatable motion).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:8 — “The four lowest-ranking subsidiary motions can be debated (except that Amend is undebatable when it is applied to an undebatable motion).”

**31.** `[C0164 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: From these rules, it follows that the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied to any of the four subsidiary motions of lower rank (but not to the two that rank above it), while the _____ can be applied to any of the five subsidiary motions that rank below it (but not to the one subsidiary motion that ranks above it).
   A. Consider Seriatim
   B. Call of the House
   C. Previous Question
   D. committee reports

   *Answer:* C) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:8 — “From these rules, it follows that the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied to any of the four subsidiary motions of lower rank (but not to the two that rank above it), while the Previous Question can be applied to any of the five subsidiary motions that rank below it (but not to the one subsidiary motion that ranks above it).”

**32.** `[C0164 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debate can also be limited or extended on any debatable motion that is _____ pending (or on a specified series of pending motions including the immediately pending question, 15);

   *Answer:* immediately — “Debate can also be limited or extended on any debatable motion that is immediately pending (or on a specified series of pending motions including the immediately pending question, 15);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:8 — “Debate can also be limited or extended on any debatable motion that is immediately pending (or on a specified series of pending motions including the immediately pending question, 15);”

**33.** `[C0164 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and, _____, debate and amendment can be closed on a motion or series of motions that are debatable and amendable, or amendment can be closed on motions that can be amended but not debated (16).
   A. similarly
   B. auditors’
   C. reception
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* A) similarly. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:8 — “and, similarly, debate and amendment can be closed on a motion or series of motions that are debatable and amendable, or amendment can be closed on motions that can be amended but not debated (16).”

See RONR (12th ed.) §6:8 — “The four lowest-ranking subsidiary motions can be debated (except that Amend is undebatable when it is applied to an undebatable motion).”

   B. deliberative
   C. consideration

**56.** `[C0172 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Usually, but not always, an incidental motion is legitimately incidental to another _____ motion only while the other motion is immediately pending.

   B. Reports
   C. Adjourn
   D. pending

   *Answer:* D) pending. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:18 — “Usually, but not always, an incidental motion is legitimately incidental to another pending motion only while the other motion is immediately pending.”

   B. legitimately

   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) legitimately. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:18 — “Usually, but not always, an incidental motion is legitimately incidental to another pending motion only while the other motion is immediately pending.”

**59.** `[C0172 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each incidental motion has its own rules that determine the conditions under which it is incidental—that is, the motions or situations to which it can be applied, and the _____ or stage of consideration at which this can be done.

   B. Announcements
   C. circumstances

**60.** `[C0174 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is legitimate only against an _____ main motion, and the objection is no longer in order after consideration of the question has begun—even though an original main motion is immediately pending (26).

   *Answer:* original — “but an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is legitimate only against an original main motion, and the objection is no longer in order after consideration of the question has begun—even though an original main motion is immediately pending (26).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:20 — “but an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is legitimate only against an original main motion, and the objection is no longer in order after consideration of the question has begun—even though an original main motion is immediately pending (26).”

**61.** `[C0174 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but an Objection to the _____ of a Question is legitimate only against an original main motion, and the objection is no longer in order after consideration of the question has begun—even though an original main motion is immediately pending (26).

   *Answer:* Consideration — “but an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is legitimate only against an original main motion, and the objection is no longer in order after consideration of the question has begun—even though an original main motion is immediately pending (26).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:20 — “but an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is legitimate only against an original main motion, and the objection is no longer in order after consideration of the question has begun—even though an original main motion is immediately pending (26).”

**62.** `[C0176 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While a series consisting of a main motion and a number of subsidiary or privileged motions is being considered, it is possible for some of the incidental motions—such as a _____ Order, an Appeal, or a Division of the Assembly—to arise more than once, in connection with different motions in the series.

   *Answer:* Point of — “While a series consisting of a main motion and a number of subsidiary or privileged motions is being considered, it is possible for some of the incidental motions—such as a Point of Order, an Appeal, or a Division of the Assembly—to arise more than once, in connection with different motions in the series.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:22 — “While a series consisting of a main motion and a number of subsidiary or privileged motions is being considered, it is possible for some of the incidental motions—such as a Point of Order, an Appeal, or a Division of the Assembly—to arise more than once, in connection with different motions in the series.”

**63.** `[C0176 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While a series consisting of a main motion and a number of subsidiary or privileged motions is being considered, it is possible for some of the incidental motions—such as a Point of _____, an Appeal, or a Division of the Assembly—to arise more than once, in connection with different motions in the series.
   A. Point
   B. Order
   C. floor
   D. amend

   *Answer:* B) Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:22 — “While a series consisting of a main motion and a number of subsidiary or privileged motions is being considered, it is possible for some of the incidental motions—such as a Point of Order, an Appeal, or a Division of the Assembly—to arise more than once, in connection with different motions in the series.”

**64.** `[C0177 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Incidental Main _____ Corresponding to Incidental Motions.

   *Answer:* Motions — “Incidental Main Motions Corresponding to Incidental Motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:23 — “Incidental Main Motions Corresponding to Incidental Motions.”

**65.** `[C0177 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, a _____ (2) can be suspended for the duration of a session (8);
   A. Filling Blanks
   B. Main Motions
   C. standing rule
   D. majority vote

   *Answer:* C) standing rule. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:23 — “For example, a standing rule (2) can be suspended for the duration of a session (8);”

**66.** `[C0177 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Incidental Main Motions _____ to Incidental Motions.

   *Answer:* Corresponding — “Incidental Main Motions Corresponding to Incidental Motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:23 — “Incidental Main Motions Corresponding to Incidental Motions.”

**67.** `[C0177 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of some of the incidental motions may occur as incidental main motions.
   A. requirement
   B. Organization
   C. Particular
   D. Counterparts

   *Answer:* D) Counterparts. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:23 — “Counterparts of some of the incidental motions may occur as incidental main motions.”

**68.** `[C0177 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, a standing rule (2) can be _____ for the duration of a session (8);

   *Answer:* suspended — “For example, a standing rule (2) can be suspended for the duration of a session (8);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:23 — “For example, a standing rule (2) can be suspended for the duration of a session (8);”

**69.** `[C0178 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Four motions are grouped in this book as motions that bring a question again before the _____ since, either by their adoption or by their introduction, they allow the assembly to consider again the merits of a question that has previously been disposed of in some way.
   A. Interest
   B. assembly
   C. Meetings
   D. majority

   *Answer:* B) assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:25 — “Four motions are grouped in this book as motions that bring a question again before the assembly since, either by their adoption or by their introduction, they allow the assembly to consider again the merits of a question that has previously been disposed of in some way.”

**70.** `[C0178 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Four motions are grouped in this book as motions that bring a question again before the assembly since, either by their adoption or by their _____, they allow the assembly to consider again the merits of a question that has previously been disposed of in some way.

   *Answer:* introduction — “Four motions are grouped in this book as motions that bring a question again before the assembly since, either by their adoption or by their introduction, they allow the assembly to consider again the merits of a question that has previously been disposed of in some way.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:25 — “Four motions are grouped in this book as motions that bring a question again before the assembly since, either by their adoption or by their introduction, they allow the assembly to consider again the merits of a question that has previously been disposed of in some way.”

**71.** `[C0178 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: All of the motions that bring a question again before the assembly are usually made—and, like main motions, three of them can only be made—while no business is _____.
   A. Regular
   B. censure
   C. Charges
   D. pending

   *Answer:* D) pending. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:25 — “All of the motions that bring a question again before the assembly are usually made—and, like main motions, three of them can only be made—while no business is pending.”

**73.** `[C0178 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: All of the motions that bring a question again before the assembly are usually made—and, like main motions, three of them can only be made—while no business is _____.

   *Answer:* pending — “All of the motions that bring a question again before the assembly are usually made—and, like main motions, three of them can only be made—while no business is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §6:25 — “All of the motions that bring a question again before the assembly are usually made—and, like main motions, three of them can only be made—while no business is pending.”

   A. Particular

**79.** `[C0180 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motions that bring a question again before the assembly have a number of _____ among themselves, and they may be variously subgrouped depending on the point of view, as follows:

   A. Parliamentary
   B. Relationships

**85.** `[C0236 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As explained in 3–6, a main motion is a motion whose _____ brings business before the assembly;

   B. introduction
   C. Incidental
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* B) introduction. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:1 — “As explained in 3–6, a main motion is a motion whose introduction brings business before the assembly;”

**86.** `[C0236 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: such a motion can be made only while no other motion is _____.

   *Answer:* pending — “such a motion can be made only while no other motion is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:1 — “such a motion can be made only while no other motion is pending.”

**87.** `[C0236 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: such a motion can be made only while no other motion is _____.
   A. Charges
   B. pending
   C. applied
   D. adopted

**88.** `[C0237 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The division of main motions into _____ and incidental main motions was mentioned in 6 and is discussed below.

   *Answer:* original — “The division of main motions into original and incidental main motions was mentioned in 6 and is discussed below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:2 — “The division of main motions into original and incidental main motions was mentioned in 6 and is discussed below.”

**89.** `[C0237 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The division of main motions into original and _____ main motions was mentioned in 6 and is discussed below.

   B. substitute
   C. incidental
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:2 — “The division of main motions into original and incidental main motions was mentioned in 6 and is discussed below.”

**90.** `[C0238 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ is a main motion that introduces a substantive question as a new subject.

   *Answer:* original main motion — “An original main motion is a main motion that introduces a substantive question as a new subject.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:3 — “An original main motion is a main motion that introduces a substantive question as a new subject.”

**91.** `[C0238 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or “… to adopt the following resolution: ‘_____, That the Northridge Improvement Association oppose a municipal tax increase at this time.’” It may be more suitable for an original main motion to be made orally, or to be submitted in writing, depending on its length, complexity, or importance.
   A. adoption
   B. decision
   C. Resolved
   D. dilatory

   *Answer:* C) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:3 — “or “… to adopt the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That the Northridge Improvement Association oppose a municipal tax increase at this time.’” It may be more suitable for an original main motion to be made orally, or to be submitted in writing, depending on its length, complexity, or importance.”

**92.** `[C0238 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An original main motion is a main motion that introduces a _____ question as a new subject.
   A. constitution
   B. substantive
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) substantive. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:3 — “An original main motion is a main motion that introduces a substantive question as a new subject.”

**93.** `[C0238 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This is the motion most often used, and is the basic device by which a matter is presented to the assembly for possible action, as “… that the Club contribute $50 to the centennial _____”;

   *Answer:* celebration — “This is the motion most often used, and is the basic device by which a matter is presented to the assembly for possible action, as “… that the Club contribute $50 to the centennial celebration”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:3 — “This is the motion most often used, and is the basic device by which a matter is presented to the assembly for possible action, as “… that the Club contribute $50 to the centennial celebration”;”

**94.** `[C0238 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or “… that the Society go on record as _____ the Popular Run route for the proposed new beltway”;
   A. Question
   B. favoring
   C. division
   D. meetings

   *Answer:* B) favoring. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:3 — “or “… that the Society go on record as favoring the Popular Run route for the proposed new beltway”;”

**95.** `[C0239 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ is a main motion that is incidental to or relates to the business of the assembly, or its past or future action.

   *Answer:* incidental main motion — “An incidental main motion is a main motion that is incidental to or relates to the business of the assembly, or its past or future action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:4 — “An incidental main motion is a main motion that is incidental to or relates to the business of the assembly, or its past or future action.”

**96.** `[C0239 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ main motion is a main motion that is incidental to or relates to the business of the assembly, or its past or future action.
   A. incidental
   B. Permission
   C. Unfinished
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* A) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:4 — “An incidental main motion is a main motion that is incidental to or relates to the business of the assembly, or its past or future action.”

**97.** `[C0239 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a motion is distinguished by the following _____:

   *Answer:* characteristics — “Such a motion is distinguished by the following characteristics:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:4 — “Such a motion is distinguished by the following characteristics:”

**98.** `[C0239 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a motion is distinguished by the following _____:
   A. presentation
   B. Indefinitely
   C. President-Elect
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* D) characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:4 — “Such a motion is distinguished by the following characteristics:”

**99.** `[C0240 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An incidental main motion involving a subject already entered into might be a motion to _____ recommendations which a committee has prepared upon instructions (not relating to a referred motion), or a motion to ratify action taken at a meeting when no quorum was present.

   *Answer:* adopt — “An incidental main motion involving a subject already entered into might be a motion to adopt recommendations which a committee has prepared upon instructions (not relating to a referred motion), or a motion to ratify action taken at a meeting when no quorum was present.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:5 — “An incidental main motion involving a subject already entered into might be a motion to adopt recommendations which a committee has prepared upon instructions (not relating to a referred motion), or a motion to ratify action taken at a meeting when no quorum was present.”

**100.** `[C0240 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An incidental main _____ involving a subject already entered into might be a motion to adopt recommendations which a committee has prepared upon instructions (not relating to a referred motion), or a motion to ratify action taken at a meeting when no quorum was present.
   A. beyond
   B. motion
   C. common
   D. adopts

   *Answer:* B) motion. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:5 — “An incidental main motion involving a subject already entered into might be a motion to adopt recommendations which a committee has prepared upon instructions (not relating to a referred motion), or a motion to ratify action taken at a meeting when no quorum was present.”

**101.** `[C0240 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An example of an incidental main motion relating to procedure without reference to an item of business would be a motion to take a _____, made when no business is pending, or a motion to place a special limit on the length of speeches throughout a meeting.
   A. Appeal
   B. Debate
   C. Repeal
   D. recess

   *Answer:* D) recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:5 — “An example of an incidental main motion relating to procedure without reference to an item of business would be a motion to take a recess, made when no business is pending, or a motion to place a special limit on the length of speeches throughout a meeting.”

**102.** `[C0240 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An example of an incidental main motion relating to procedure without reference to an item of business would be a motion to take a recess, made when no business is pending, or a motion to place a special _____ on the length of speeches throughout a meeting.

   *Answer:* limit — “An example of an incidental main motion relating to procedure without reference to an item of business would be a motion to take a recess, made when no business is pending, or a motion to place a special limit on the length of speeches throughout a meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:5 — “An example of an incidental main motion relating to procedure without reference to an item of business would be a motion to take a recess, made when no business is pending, or a motion to place a special limit on the length of speeches throughout a meeting.”

**103.** `[C0241 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ main motion is usually made orally.

   *Answer:* incidental — “An incidental main motion is usually made orally.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:6 — “An incidental main motion is usually made orally.”

**104.** `[C0241 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chief difference in the rules governing original and incidental main motions is that an Objection to the _____ of a Question (26) can be applied only to original main and not to incidental main motions.
   A. subsequently
   B. recommendation
   C. Consideration
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* C) Consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:6 — “The chief difference in the rules governing original and incidental main motions is that an Objection to the Consideration of a Question (26) can be applied only to original main and not to incidental main motions.”

**105.** `[C0241 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reason is that, in the case of an incidental main motion dealing with a subject previously entered into, the _____ has already begun and it is too late to object;

   *Answer:* involvement — “The reason is that, in the case of an incidental main motion dealing with a subject previously entered into, the involvement has already begun and it is too late to object;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:6 — “The reason is that, in the case of an incidental main motion dealing with a subject previously entered into, the involvement has already begun and it is too late to object;”

**106.** `[C0241 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____, incidental main motions are not referred to a resolutions committee (59:67ff.).
   A. Indefinitely
   B. conventions
   C. immediately
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:6 — “In conventions, incidental main motions are not referred to a resolutions committee (59:67ff.).”

**107.** `[C0241 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ main motion is usually made orally.
   A. variations
   B. persuasive
   C. incidental
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:6 — “An incidental main motion is usually made orally.”

**108.** `[C0242 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (Compare the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, 15, and the privileged motion to _____, 20.) Referring to the Table of Rules Relating to Motions on pages t6–t33, most of the motions listed as “main”—with the exception of No.
