**109.** `[C0242 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motions to _____ and to ratify are briefly discussed at the end of this chapter.
   A. Table
   B. Forum
   C. clerk
   D. adopt

   *Answer:* D) adopt. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:7 — “The motions to adopt and to ratify are briefly discussed at the end of this chapter.”

**110.** `[C0242 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motions to adopt and to _____ are briefly discussed at the end of this chapter.

   *Answer:* ratify — “The motions to adopt and to ratify are briefly discussed at the end of this chapter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:7 — “The motions to adopt and to ratify are briefly discussed at the end of this chapter.”

**111.** `[C0242 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Most of the _____ main motions closely correspond to secondary (subsidiary, privileged, or incidental) motions described by the same or similar names—as in the last two of the four examples above.
   A. resolution
   B. introduced
   C. privileged
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* D) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:7 — “Most of the incidental main motions closely correspond to secondary (subsidiary, privileged, or incidental) motions described by the same or similar names—as in the last two of the four examples above.”

**112.** `[C0242 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Most of the incidental main motions closely correspond to secondary (_____, privileged, or incidental) motions described by the same or similar names—as in the last two of the four examples above.

   *Answer:* subsidiary — “Most of the incidental main motions closely correspond to secondary (subsidiary, privileged, or incidental) motions described by the same or similar names—as in the last two of the four examples above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:7 — “Most of the incidental main motions closely correspond to secondary (subsidiary, privileged, or incidental) motions described by the same or similar names—as in the last two of the four examples above.”

**113.** `[C0243 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a main motion is adopted, it becomes the _____ recorded statement of an action taken by the assembly.

   *Answer:* officially — “If a main motion is adopted, it becomes the officially recorded statement of an action taken by the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:9 — “If a main motion is adopted, it becomes the officially recorded statement of an action taken by the assembly.”

**114.** `[C0243 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion should therefore be worded in a concise, _____, and complete form appropriate to such a purpose.
   A. constitution
   B. Indefinitely
   C. authorizes
   D. unambiguous

   *Answer:* D) unambiguous. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:9 — “A motion should therefore be worded in a concise, unambiguous, and complete form appropriate to such a purpose.”

**115.** `[C0243 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It cannot employ _____ that is not allowed in debate (43).

   *Answer:* language — “It cannot employ language that is not allowed in debate (43).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:9 — “It cannot employ language that is not allowed in debate (43).”

**116.** `[C0243 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member making a motion embodying something that has just been said by the chair or another member in informal _____ during a meeting should avoid statements such as “I so move,” and should himself recite the complete motion that he offers.
   A. resolutions
   B. immediately
   C. consultation
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) consultation. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:9 — “A member making a motion embodying something that has just been said by the chair or another member in informal consultation during a meeting should avoid statements such as “I so move,” and should himself recite the complete motion that he offers.”

**117.** `[C0243 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a main motion is adopted, it becomes the _____ recorded statement of an action taken by the assembly.
   A. Definitely
   B. Unfinished
   C. officially
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* C) officially. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:9 — “If a main motion is adopted, it becomes the officially recorded statement of an action taken by the assembly.”

**118.** `[C0244 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: also, possible attempts to _____ a motion to reaffirm would come into conflict with the rules for the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35);

   *Answer:* amend — “also, possible attempts to amend a motion to reaffirm would come into conflict with the rules for the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:10 — “also, possible attempts to amend a motion to reaffirm would come into conflict with the rules for the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35);”

**119.** `[C0244 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: also, possible attempts to amend a motion to reaffirm would come into conflict with the rules for the motion to Amend _____ (35);
   A. Something Previously Adopted
   B. Descriptive Characteristics
   C. Objection or With Permission
   D. Resignation from Membership

   *Answer:* A) Something Previously Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:10 — “also, possible attempts to amend a motion to reaffirm would come into conflict with the rules for the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35);”

**120.** `[C0244 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to “reaffirm” a position _____ taken by adopting a motion or resolution are not in order.

   *Answer:* previously — “Motions to “reaffirm” a position previously taken by adopting a motion or resolution are not in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:10 — “Motions to “reaffirm” a position previously taken by adopting a motion or resolution are not in order.”

**121.** `[C0244 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a motion serves no useful purpose because the _____ motion is still in effect;
   A. adoption
   B. original
   C. Question
   D. assuming

   *Answer:* B) original. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:10 — “Such a motion serves no useful purpose because the original motion is still in effect;”

**122.** `[C0244 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and if such a motion to reaffirm failed, it would create an _____ situation.

   *Answer:* ambiguous — “and if such a motion to reaffirm failed, it would create an ambiguous situation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:10 — “and if such a motion to reaffirm failed, it would create an ambiguous situation.”

**123.** `[C0245 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion whose only effect is to propose that the assembly _____ from doing something should not be offered if the same result can be accomplished by offering no motion at all.

   *Answer:* refrain — “A motion whose only effect is to propose that the assembly refrain from doing something should not be offered if the same result can be accomplished by offering no motion at all.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:11 — “A motion whose only effect is to propose that the assembly refrain from doing something should not be offered if the same result can be accomplished by offering no motion at all.”

**124.** `[C0245 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion whose only effect is to propose that the assembly refrain from doing something should not be offered if the same result can be _____ by offering no motion at all.
   A. Definitely
   B. accomplished
   C. interrupted
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) accomplished. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:11 — “A motion whose only effect is to propose that the assembly refrain from doing something should not be offered if the same result can be accomplished by offering no motion at all.”

**125.** `[C0245 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is incorrect, for example, to move “that no response be made” to a request for a _____ to a fund, or “that our delegates be given no instructions,” unless some purpose would be served by adoption of such a motion.

   *Answer:* contribution — “It is incorrect, for example, to move “that no response be made” to a request for a contribution to a fund, or “that our delegates be given no instructions,” unless some purpose would be served by adoption of such a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:11 — “It is incorrect, for example, to move “that no response be made” to a request for a contribution to a fund, or “that our delegates be given no instructions,” unless some purpose would be served by adoption of such a motion.”

**126.** `[C0245 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This could be the case, for example, if the membership of an _____ wishes to make certain that a subordinate body, such as its executive board, will not take such action at a later date, or if the motion expresses an opinion or reason as to why no action should be taken.
   A. deliberative
   B. organization
   C. Procedures
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:11 — “This could be the case, for example, if the membership of an organization wishes to make certain that a subordinate body, such as its executive board, will not take such action at a later date, or if the motion expresses an opinion or reason as to why no action should be taken.”

**127.** `[C0245 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is incorrect, for example, to move “that no response be made” to a request for a _____ to a fund, or “that our delegates be given no instructions,” unless some purpose would be served by adoption of such a motion.
   A. constitution
   B. consideration
   C. contribution
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* C) contribution. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:11 — “It is incorrect, for example, to move “that no response be made” to a request for a contribution to a fund, or “that our delegates be given no instructions,” unless some purpose would be served by adoption of such a motion.”

**128.** `[C0246 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is _____ to avoid a motion containing a negative statement even in cases where the effect of the motion is to propose that something be done, since members may become confused as to the effect of voting for or against such a motion.

   *Answer:* preferable — “It is preferable to avoid a motion containing a negative statement even in cases where the effect of the motion is to propose that something be done, since members may become confused as to the effect of voting for or against such a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:12 — “It is preferable to avoid a motion containing a negative statement even in cases where the effect of the motion is to propose that something be done, since members may become confused as to the effect of voting for or against such a motion.”

**129.** `[C0246 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rather than moving, for example, that the _____ go on record as “not in favor of the proposed public bond issue,” it should be moved that the association “oppose” or “declare its opposition to” the bond issue.
   A. immediately
   B. association
   C. Organization
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) association. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:12 — “Rather than moving, for example, that the association go on record as “not in favor of the proposed public bond issue,” it should be moved that the association “oppose” or “declare its opposition to” the bond issue.”

**130.** `[C0246 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In this _____, it should be noted that voting down a motion or resolution that would express a particular opinion is not the same as adopting a motion expressing the opposite opinion, since—if the motion is voted down—neither opinion has been expressed.

   *Answer:* connection — “In this connection, it should be noted that voting down a motion or resolution that would express a particular opinion is not the same as adopting a motion expressing the opposite opinion, since—if the motion is voted down—neither opinion has been expressed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:12 — “In this connection, it should be noted that voting down a motion or resolution that would express a particular opinion is not the same as adopting a motion expressing the opposite opinion, since—if the motion is voted down—neither opinion has been expressed.”

**131.** `[C0246 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member may be in complete agreement with the views contained in such a resolution yet feel that his _____ should not speak out on the matter, and he might therefore vote against the resolution.
   A. organization
   B. Reconsider
   C. adjournment
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:12 — “A member may be in complete agreement with the views contained in such a resolution yet feel that his organization should not speak out on the matter, and he might therefore vote against the resolution.”

**132.** `[C0246 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is _____ to avoid a motion containing a negative statement even in cases where the effect of the motion is to propose that something be done, since members may become confused as to the effect of voting for or against such a motion.
   A. Unfinished
   B. preferable
   C. landscaped
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* B) preferable. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:12 — “It is preferable to avoid a motion containing a negative statement even in cases where the effect of the motion is to propose that something be done, since members may become confused as to the effect of voting for or against such a motion.”

**133.** `[C0247 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As previously stated, a main motion—particularly an original main motion—is frequently offered as a _____, either because of its importance or because of its length or complexity.

   *Answer:* resolution — “As previously stated, a main motion—particularly an original main motion—is frequently offered as a resolution, either because of its importance or because of its length or complexity.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:13 — “As previously stated, a main motion—particularly an original main motion—is frequently offered as a resolution, either because of its importance or because of its length or complexity.”

**134.** `[C0247 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: for example, “That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest open to high school students of the city, to be conducted according to the following specifications:.…” If put in the form of a resolution, the preceding example would be written, “_____, That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest.…” In a resolution, the name of the adopting organization can also be made a part of the enacting words, as in “Resolved by the International Benevolent Association in convention assembled, That.…”
   A. meetings
   B. Resolved
   C. adoption
   D. dilatory

   *Answer:* B) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:13 — “for example, “That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest open to high school students of the city, to be conducted according to the following specifications:.…” If put in the form of a resolution, the preceding example would be written, “Resolved, That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest.…” In a resolution, the name of the adopting organization can also be made a part of the enacting words, as in “Resolved by the International Benevolent Association in convention assembled, That.…””

**135.** `[C0247 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: for example, “That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest open to high school students of the city, to be conducted according to the following specifications:.…” If put in the form of a resolution, the preceding example would be written, “Resolved, That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest.…” In a resolution, the name of the adopting organization can also be made a part of the enacting words, as in “_____ International Benevolent Association in convention assembled, That.…”

   *Answer:* Resolved by the — “for example, “That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest open to high school students of the city, to be conducted according to the following specifications:.…” If put in the form of a resolution, the preceding example would be written, “Resolved, That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest.…” In a resolution, the name of the adopting organization can also be made a part of the enacting words, as in “Resolved by the International Benevolent Association in convention assembled, That.…””. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:13 — “for example, “That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest open to high school students of the city, to be conducted according to the following specifications:.…” If put in the form of a resolution, the preceding example would be written, “Resolved, That the Merchants’ Association sponsor an essay contest.…” In a resolution, the name of the adopting organization can also be made a part of the enacting words, as in “Resolved by the International Benevolent Association in convention assembled, That.…””

**136.** `[C0247 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As previously stated, a main motion—_____ an original main motion—is frequently offered as a resolution, either because of its importance or because of its length or complexity.
   A. consideration
   B. particularly
   C. specification
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) particularly. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:13 — “As previously stated, a main motion—particularly an original main motion—is frequently offered as a resolution, either because of its importance or because of its length or complexity.”

**137.** `[C0249 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the resolution is offered in a mass meeting (or in any meeting where there is no established organization whose act the adopted resolution would become), it may begin, “_____, That it is the sense of this meeting that…” A resolution can consist of more than one resolving clause, as in the following example:

   *Answer:* Resolved — “If the resolution is offered in a mass meeting (or in any meeting where there is no established organization whose act the adopted resolution would become), it may begin, “Resolved, That it is the sense of this meeting that…” A resolution can consist of more than one resolving clause, as in the following example:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:15 — “If the resolution is offered in a mass meeting (or in any meeting where there is no established organization whose act the adopted resolution would become), it may begin, “Resolved, That it is the sense of this meeting that…” A resolution can consist of more than one resolving clause, as in the following example:”

**138.** `[C0249 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the resolution is offered in a mass meeting (or in any meeting where there is no established _____ whose act the adopted resolution would become), it may begin, “Resolved, That it is the sense of this meeting that…” A resolution can consist of more than one resolving clause, as in the following example:
   A. organization
   B. Announcements
   C. undebatable
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:15 — “If the resolution is offered in a mass meeting (or in any meeting where there is no established organization whose act the adopted resolution would become), it may begin, “Resolved, That it is the sense of this meeting that…” A resolution can consist of more than one resolving clause, as in the following example:”

**139.** `[C0250 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In general, the _____ should be limited to cases where it provides little-known information without which the point or the merits of a resolution are likely to be poorly understood, where unusual importance is attached to making certain reasons for an action a matter of record, or the like.

   *Answer:* use of a preamble — “In general, the use of a preamble should be limited to cases where it provides little-known information without which the point or the merits of a resolution are likely to be poorly understood, where unusual importance is attached to making certain reasons for an action a matter of record, or the like.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:16 — “In general, the use of a preamble should be limited to cases where it provides little-known information without which the point or the merits of a resolution are likely to be poorly understood, where unusual importance is attached to making certain reasons for an action a matter of record, or the like.”

**140.** `[C0250 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When special circumstances make it desirable to include a brief statement of background, the motion should be cast in the form of a resolution, with the background or reasons incorporated in a _____ that is placed before the resolving clauses.
   A. Previous
   B. majority
   C. Question
   D. preamble

   *Answer:* D) preamble. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:16 — “When special circumstances make it desirable to include a brief statement of background, the motion should be cast in the form of a resolution, with the background or reasons incorporated in a preamble that is placed before the resolving clauses.”

**141.** `[C0250 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is usually _____ to attempt to include reasons for a motion’s adoption within the motion itself.

   *Answer:* inadvisable — “It is usually inadvisable to attempt to include reasons for a motion’s adoption within the motion itself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:16 — “It is usually inadvisable to attempt to include reasons for a motion’s adoption within the motion itself.”

**142.** `[C0250 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To do so may _____ the motion and may weigh against its adoption—since some members who approve of the action it proposes may dislike voting for it if it states reasons with which they disagree.
   A. encumber
   B. dilatory
   C. Previous
   D. majority

   *Answer:* A) encumber. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:16 — “To do so may encumber the motion and may weigh against its adoption—since some members who approve of the action it proposes may dislike voting for it if it states reasons with which they disagree.”

**143.** `[C0250 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When special _____ make it desirable to include a brief statement of background, the motion should be cast in the form of a resolution, with the background or reasons incorporated in a preamble that is placed before the resolving clauses.

   *Answer:* circumstances — “When special circumstances make it desirable to include a brief statement of background, the motion should be cast in the form of a resolution, with the background or reasons incorporated in a preamble that is placed before the resolving clauses.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:16 — “When special circumstances make it desirable to include a brief statement of background, the motion should be cast in the form of a resolution, with the background or reasons incorporated in a preamble that is placed before the resolving clauses.”

**144.** `[C0251 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To avoid _____ from the force of the resolution itself, a preamble generally should contain no more clauses than are strictly necessary.

   *Answer:* detracting — “To avoid detracting from the force of the resolution itself, a preamble generally should contain no more clauses than are strictly necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:18 — “To avoid detracting from the force of the resolution itself, a preamble generally should contain no more clauses than are strictly necessary.”

**145.** `[C0251 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To avoid detracting from the force of the _____ itself, a preamble generally should contain no more clauses than are strictly necessary.
   A. landscaped
   B. authorizes
   C. Procedures
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:18 — “To avoid detracting from the force of the resolution itself, a preamble generally should contain no more clauses than are strictly necessary.”

**146.** `[C0252 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the _____ (16) is ordered on the resolution before the preamble has been considered for amendment, however, the Previous Question does not apply to the preamble, which becomes open to debate and amendment unless the Previous Question is then separately ordered on it also.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “When the Previous Question (16) is ordered on the resolution before the preamble has been considered for amendment, however, the Previous Question does not apply to the preamble, which becomes open to debate and amendment unless the Previous Question is then separately ordered on it also.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:19 — “When the Previous Question (16) is ordered on the resolution before the preamble has been considered for amendment, however, the Previous Question does not apply to the preamble, which becomes open to debate and amendment unless the Previous Question is then separately ordered on it also.”

**147.** `[C0252 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the _____ Question (16) is ordered on the resolution before the preamble has been considered for amendment, however, the Previous Question does not apply to the preamble, which becomes open to debate and amendment unless the Previous Question is then separately ordered on it also.
   A. Division
   B. Previous
   C. Meetings
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* B) Previous. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:19 — “When the Previous Question (16) is ordered on the resolution before the preamble has been considered for amendment, however, the Previous Question does not apply to the preamble, which becomes open to debate and amendment unless the Previous Question is then separately ordered on it also.”

**148.** `[C0252 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the _____ of a resolution having a preamble, the preamble is always amended last, since changes in the resolving clauses may require changes in the preamble.
   A. specification
   B. Parliamentary
   C. deliberative
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:19 — “In the consideration of a resolution having a preamble, the preamble is always amended last, since changes in the resolving clauses may require changes in the preamble.”

**149.** `[C0252 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the consideration of a _____ having a preamble, the preamble is always amended last, since changes in the resolving clauses may require changes in the preamble.

   *Answer:* resolution — “In the consideration of a resolution having a preamble, the preamble is always amended last, since changes in the resolving clauses may require changes in the preamble.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:19 — “In the consideration of a resolution having a preamble, the preamble is always amended last, since changes in the resolving clauses may require changes in the preamble.”

**150.** `[C0253 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Details of Form and Variations in _____.

   *Answer:* Resolutions — “Details of Form and Variations in Resolutions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:20 — “Details of Form and Variations in Resolutions.”

**151.** `[C0253 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Details of Form and Variations in _____.
   A. requirement
   B. Permission
   C. Reconsider
   D. Resolutions

   *Answer:* D) Resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:20 — “Details of Form and Variations in Resolutions.”

**152.** `[C0254 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is a preamble, each clause, written as a separate _____, begins with the word “Whereas” followed by a comma, and the next word should begin with a capital letter.

   *Answer:* paragraph — “If there is a preamble, each clause, written as a separate paragraph, begins with the word “Whereas” followed by a comma, and the next word should begin with a capital letter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:21 — “If there is a preamble, each clause, written as a separate paragraph, begins with the word “Whereas” followed by a comma, and the next word should begin with a capital letter.”

**153.** `[C0254 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The preamble, _____ of how many paragraphs it has, should never contain a period.
   A. Procedures
   B. substitute
   C. authorizes
   D. regardless

   *Answer:* D) regardless. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:21 — “The preamble, regardless of how many paragraphs it has, should never contain a period.”

**154.** `[C0254 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The preamble, regardless of how many _____ it has, should never contain a period.

   *Answer:* paragraphs — “The preamble, regardless of how many paragraphs it has, should never contain a period.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:21 — “The preamble, regardless of how many paragraphs it has, should never contain a period.”

**155.** `[C0254 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The last paragraph of the preamble should close with a semicolon, after which a _____ expression such as “therefore” or “therefore, be it” or “now, therefore, be it” is sometimes added.
   A. Substitute
   B. connecting
   C. Incidental
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) connecting. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:21 — “The last paragraph of the preamble should close with a semicolon, after which a connecting expression such as “therefore” or “therefore, be it” or “now, therefore, be it” is sometimes added.”

**156.** `[C0254 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When one of these phrases is included, no _____ should follow it, and it should always be placed at the end of the preamble paragraph, never at the beginning of the resolving paragraph, thus:

   *Answer:* punctuation — “When one of these phrases is included, no punctuation should follow it, and it should always be placed at the end of the preamble paragraph, never at the beginning of the resolving paragraph, thus:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:21 — “When one of these phrases is included, no punctuation should follow it, and it should always be placed at the end of the preamble paragraph, never at the beginning of the resolving paragraph, thus:”

**157.** `[C0255 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The word “_____” is underlined or printed in italics, and is followed by a comma and the word “That”—which begins with a capital “T.” If there is more than one resolving clause, each of them should be a separate paragraph.

   *Answer:* Resolved — “The word “Resolved” is underlined or printed in italics, and is followed by a comma and the word “That”—which begins with a capital “T.” If there is more than one resolving clause, each of them should be a separate paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:22 — “The word “Resolved” is underlined or printed in italics, and is followed by a comma and the word “That”—which begins with a capital “T.” If there is more than one resolving clause, each of them should be a separate paragraph.”

**158.** `[C0255 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The word “Resolved” is _____ or printed in italics, and is followed by a comma and the word “That”—which begins with a capital “T.” If there is more than one resolving clause, each of them should be a separate paragraph.
   A. Unfinished
   B. underlined
   C. privileged
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) underlined. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:22 — “The word “Resolved” is underlined or printed in italics, and is followed by a comma and the word “That”—which begins with a capital “T.” If there is more than one resolving clause, each of them should be a separate paragraph.”

**159.** `[C0255 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless the paragraphs are numbered as in the _____ form described below, each paragraph begins with the words “Resolved, That,” just as the first resolving clause.

   *Answer:* alternative — “Unless the paragraphs are numbered as in the alternative form described below, each paragraph begins with the words “Resolved, That,” just as the first resolving clause.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:22 — “Unless the paragraphs are numbered as in the alternative form described below, each paragraph begins with the words “Resolved, That,” just as the first resolving clause.”

**160.** `[C0255 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A resolving paragraph should not contain a period within its structure, though _____ of this rule is becoming less strict.

   *Answer:* observance — “A resolving paragraph should not contain a period within its structure, though observance of this rule is becoming less strict.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:22 — “A resolving paragraph should not contain a period within its structure, though observance of this rule is becoming less strict.”

**161.** `[C0255 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the paragraphs are numbered as in the _____ form described below, each paragraph begins with the words “Resolved, That,” just as the first resolving clause.
   A. Unfinished
   B. alternative
   C. Information
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) alternative. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:22 — “Unless the paragraphs are numbered as in the alternative form described below, each paragraph begins with the words “Resolved, That,” just as the first resolving clause.”

**162.** `[C0256 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In organizations with employees, the assembly or the board can give instructions to an employee in the form of an _____, which is written just as a resolution except that the word “Ordered” is used in place of the word “Resolved.” An example would be: “Ordered, That the steward obtain impoundment of all unauthorized vehicles found parked on the club premises.”

   *Answer:* order — “In organizations with employees, the assembly or the board can give instructions to an employee in the form of an order, which is written just as a resolution except that the word “Ordered” is used in place of the word “Resolved.” An example would be: “Ordered, That the steward obtain impoundment of all unauthorized vehicles found parked on the club premises.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:24 — “In organizations with employees, the assembly or the board can give instructions to an employee in the form of an order, which is written just as a resolution except that the word “Ordered” is used in place of the word “Resolved.” An example would be: “Ordered, That the steward obtain impoundment of all unauthorized vehicles found parked on the club premises.””

**163.** `[C0256 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In organizations with employees, the assembly or the board can give instructions to an employee in the form of an order, which is written just as a resolution except that the word “_____” is used in place of the word “Resolved.” An example would be: “Ordered, That the steward obtain impoundment of all unauthorized vehicles found parked on the club premises.”
   A. Regular
   B. Ordered
   C. censure
   D. Rescind

   *Answer:* B) Ordered. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:24 — “In organizations with employees, the assembly or the board can give instructions to an employee in the form of an order, which is written just as a resolution except that the word “Ordered” is used in place of the word “Resolved.” An example would be: “Ordered, That the steward obtain impoundment of all unauthorized vehicles found parked on the club premises.””

**164.** `[C0257 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of a series of resolutions relating to a single subject, if members desire one or more of the resolutions to be considered separately, the motion for _____ (27) must be made and adopted by a majority vote.

   *Answer:* Division of a Question — “In the case of a series of resolutions relating to a single subject, if members desire one or more of the resolutions to be considered separately, the motion for Division of a Question (27) must be made and adopted by a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:25 — “In the case of a series of resolutions relating to a single subject, if members desire one or more of the resolutions to be considered separately, the motion for Division of a Question (27) must be made and adopted by a majority vote.”

**165.** `[C0257 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of a series of resolutions relating to a single subject, if members desire one or more of the resolutions to be considered separately, the motion for Division of a Question (27) must be made and adopted by a _____ vote.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:25 — “In the case of a series of resolutions relating to a single subject, if members desire one or more of the resolutions to be considered separately, the motion for Division of a Question (27) must be made and adopted by a majority vote.”

**166.** `[C0257 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Series of _____ Offered by a Single Main Motion.

   *Answer:* Resolutions — “Series of Resolutions Offered by a Single Main Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:25 — “Series of Resolutions Offered by a Single Main Motion.”

**167.** `[C0257 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a single composite proposal for taking a number of actions in reference to a particular subject has too many elements to be _____ written into one resolution (even of several clauses), it can be set forth in a series of separate resolutions that can be numbered and offered by means of a single main motion, thus: “Mr.
   A. consideration
   B. Permission
   C. Convention
   D. conveniently

   *Answer:* D) conveniently. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:25 — “If a single composite proposal for taking a number of actions in reference to a particular subject has too many elements to be conveniently written into one resolution (even of several clauses), it can be set forth in a series of separate resolutions that can be numbered and offered by means of a single main motion, thus: “Mr.”

**168.** `[C0257 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes a series of _____ resolutions relating to completely different subjects is offered by a single main motion in the same way.

   *Answer:* independent — “Sometimes a series of independent resolutions relating to completely different subjects is offered by a single main motion in the same way.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:25 — “Sometimes a series of independent resolutions relating to completely different subjects is offered by a single main motion in the same way.”

**169.** `[C0258 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Below are stated a number of _____ or conditions that cause a particular main motion to be out of order, and— where applicable—the alternative courses that are open for accomplishing the desired result:

   *Answer:* characteristics — “Below are stated a number of characteristics or conditions that cause a particular main motion to be out of order, and— where applicable—the alternative courses that are open for accomplishing the desired result:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:26 — “Below are stated a number of characteristics or conditions that cause a particular main motion to be out of order, and— where applicable—the alternative courses that are open for accomplishing the desired result:”

**170.** `[C0258 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Below are stated a number of _____ or conditions that cause a particular main motion to be out of order, and— where applicable—the alternative courses that are open for accomplishing the desired result:
   A. recommendation
   B. characteristics
   C. Announcements
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:26 — “Below are stated a number of characteristics or conditions that cause a particular main motion to be out of order, and— where applicable—the alternative courses that are open for accomplishing the desired result:”

**171.** `[C0259 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rules relating to the motions to _____ (25), Take from the Table (34), Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), Discharge a Committee (36), and Reconsider (37), and the rules relating to the renewal of motions (38), should be read in connection with the application of these methods.

   *Answer:* Suspend the Rules — “The rules relating to the motions to Suspend the Rules (25), Take from the Table (34), Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), Discharge a Committee (36), and Reconsider (37), and the rules relating to the renewal of motions (38), should be read in connection with the application of these methods.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:27 — “The rules relating to the motions to Suspend the Rules (25), Take from the Table (34), Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), Discharge a Committee (36), and Reconsider (37), and the rules relating to the renewal of motions (38), should be read in connection with the application of these methods.”

**172.** `[C0259 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rules relating to the motions to Suspend the Rules (25), _____ (34), Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), Discharge a Committee (36), and Reconsider (37), and the rules relating to the renewal of motions (38), should be read in connection with the application of these methods.
   A. Committee Procedure
   B. Take from the Table
   C. Before Its Adoption
   D. ARTICLE V: MEETINGS

   *Answer:* B) Take from the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:27 — “The rules relating to the motions to Suspend the Rules (25), Take from the Table (34), Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), Discharge a Committee (36), and Reconsider (37), and the rules relating to the renewal of motions (38), should be read in connection with the application of these methods.”

**173.** `[C0260 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The basic procedure by which main motions are _____ and considered is described in detail in 4.

   *Answer:* introduced — “The basic procedure by which main motions are introduced and considered is described in detail in 4.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:28 — “The basic procedure by which main motions are introduced and considered is described in detail in 4.”

**174.** `[C0260 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ points to be noted in connection with the handling of main motions are as follows:
   A. convention
   B. Incidental
   C. Additional
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* C) Additional. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:28 — “Additional points to be noted in connection with the handling of main motions are as follows:”

**175.** `[C0260 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The basic procedure by which main motions are _____ and considered is described in detail in 4.
   A. settlement
   B. introduced
   C. Unfinished
   D. variations

   *Answer:* B) introduced. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:28 — “The basic procedure by which main motions are introduced and considered is described in detail in 4.”

**176.** `[C0260 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ points to be noted in connection with the handling of main motions are as follows:

   *Answer:* Additional — “Additional points to be noted in connection with the handling of main motions are as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:28 — “Additional points to be noted in connection with the handling of main motions are as follows:”

**177.** `[C0261 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ by Which the Proposal Contained in a Main Motion Can Be Improved Upon Before Action Is Taken.

   *Answer:* Procedures — “Procedures by Which the Proposal Contained in a Main Motion Can Be Improved Upon Before Action Is Taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:29 — “Procedures by Which the Proposal Contained in a Main Motion Can Be Improved Upon Before Action Is Taken.”

**178.** `[C0261 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion should be as well thought out as possible before it is _____.
   A. Substitute
   B. persuasive
   C. introduced
   D. variations

   *Answer:* C) introduced. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:29 — “A motion should be as well thought out as possible before it is introduced.”

**179.** `[C0261 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At the same time, it will _____ happen that the assembly—although desiring to take some kind of action on the subject that a main motion has brought to its attention—wishes to make some change in the proposal before voting on its adoption.

   *Answer:* frequently — “At the same time, it will frequently happen that the assembly—although desiring to take some kind of action on the subject that a main motion has brought to its attention—wishes to make some change in the proposal before voting on its adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:29 — “At the same time, it will frequently happen that the assembly—although desiring to take some kind of action on the subject that a main motion has brought to its attention—wishes to make some change in the proposal before voting on its adoption.”

**180.** `[C0261 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ by Which the Proposal Contained in a Main Motion Can Be Improved Upon Before Action Is Taken.
   A. incidental
   B. assemblies
   C. Procedures
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) Procedures. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:29 — “Procedures by Which the Proposal Contained in a Main Motion Can Be Improved Upon Before Action Is Taken.”

**181.** `[C0261 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion should be as well thought out as possible before it is _____.

   *Answer:* introduced — “A motion should be as well thought out as possible before it is introduced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:29 — “A motion should be as well thought out as possible before it is introduced.”

   B. Definitely
   C. accomplished
   D. constitution

**186.** `[C0263 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Introduction of Subsidiary or Incidental Motions as a Part of—or Privileged Motions as an Interruption of—the _____ of a Main Motion.

See RONR (12th ed.) §10:31 — “Introduction of Subsidiary or Incidental Motions as a Part of—or Privileged Motions as an Interruption of—the Consideration of a Main Motion.”

**187.** `[C0263 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member may speak in debate on the main motion and conclude by offering a _____ motion.
   A. auditors’
   B. extending
   C. secondary
   D. reception

   *Answer:* C) secondary. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:31 — “A member may speak in debate on the main motion and conclude by offering a secondary motion.”

**188.** `[C0263 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Introduction of Subsidiary or Incidental Motions as a Part of—or Privileged Motions as an Interruption of—the _____ of a Main Motion.
   A. resolutions
   B. Consideration
   C. Information
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* B) Consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:31 — “Introduction of Subsidiary or Incidental Motions as a Part of—or Privileged Motions as an Interruption of—the Consideration of a Main Motion.”

**189.** `[C0263 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member may speak in debate on the main motion and conclude by offering a _____ motion.

   *Answer:* secondary — “A member may speak in debate on the main motion and conclude by offering a secondary motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:31 — “A member may speak in debate on the main motion and conclude by offering a secondary motion.”

**190.** `[C0264 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such motions are usually made during the period while the main motion is open to debate, although certain _____ motions can also arise before or after this stage in its handling.

See RONR (12th ed.) §10:32 — “Such motions are usually made during the period while the main motion is open to debate, although certain incidental motions can also arise before or after this stage in its handling.”

**191.** `[C0264 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The precise times and circumstances when each of these motions is in order are determined by the first three of the motion’s standard descriptive _____.
   A. Preparation
   B. characteristics
   C. interrupted
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:32 — “The precise times and circumstances when each of these motions is in order are determined by the first three of the motion’s standard descriptive characteristics.”

**192.** `[C0264 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such motions are usually made during the period while the main motion is open to debate, although certain _____ motions can also arise before or after this stage in its handling.
   A. settlement
   B. convention
   C. assemblies
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* D) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:32 — “Such motions are usually made during the period while the main motion is open to debate, although certain incidental motions can also arise before or after this stage in its handling.”

**194.** `[C0265 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: that is (as far as applicable in the existing parliamentary situation), its consideration is resumed at the point where the _____ motion interrupted it.

   *Answer:* higher-ranking — “that is (as far as applicable in the existing parliamentary situation), its consideration is resumed at the point where the higher-ranking motion interrupted it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:33 — “that is (as far as applicable in the existing parliamentary situation), its consideration is resumed at the point where the higher-ranking motion interrupted it.”

**195.** `[C0265 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This motion is again open to debate and _____ if it is a debatable and amendable motion.
   A. Privilege
   B. reception
   C. amendment
   D. Secretary

   *Answer:* C) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:33 — “This motion is again open to debate and amendment if it is a debatable and amendable motion.”

**196.** `[C0265 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: that is (as far as applicable in the existing parliamentary situation), its _____ is resumed at the point where the higher-ranking motion interrupted it.

   *Answer:* consideration — “that is (as far as applicable in the existing parliamentary situation), its consideration is resumed at the point where the higher-ranking motion interrupted it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:33 — “that is (as far as applicable in the existing parliamentary situation), its consideration is resumed at the point where the higher-ranking motion interrupted it.”

**197.** `[C0265 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This motion is again open to debate and _____ if it is a debatable and amendable motion.

   *Answer:* amendment — “This motion is again open to debate and amendment if it is a debatable and amendable motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:33 — “This motion is again open to debate and amendment if it is a debatable and amendable motion.”

**198.** `[C0266 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This principle may be _____ by a somewhat more complicated example than may ordinarily occur in practice.

   *Answer:* illustrated — “This principle may be illustrated by a somewhat more complicated example than may ordinarily occur in practice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:34 — “This principle may be illustrated by a somewhat more complicated example than may ordinarily occur in practice.”

**199.** `[C0266 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Referring to the _____ of subsidiary, privileged, and incidental motions in 6, assume that the following series of motions is pending and that the motions have been moved in the order shown:
   A. Definitely
   B. immediately
   C. explanation
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) explanation. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:34 — “Referring to the explanation of subsidiary, privileged, and incidental motions in 6, assume that the following series of motions is pending and that the motions have been moved in the order shown:”

**200.** `[C0266 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This principle may be _____ by a somewhat more complicated example than may ordinarily occur in practice.
   A. Particular
   B. Organization
   C. illustrated
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) illustrated. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:34 — “This principle may be illustrated by a somewhat more complicated example than may ordinarily occur in practice.”

**201.** `[C0266 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Referring to the _____ of subsidiary, privileged, and incidental motions in 6, assume that the following series of motions is pending and that the motions have been moved in the order shown:

   *Answer:* explanation — “Referring to the explanation of subsidiary, privileged, and incidental motions in 6, assume that the following series of motions is pending and that the motions have been moved in the order shown:”. **202.** `[C0267 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A subsidiary or incidental motion _____ to a main question if it is related to the main question in such a way that—once introduced—it must be decided before the main question can be decided.

   *Answer:* adhere — “A subsidiary or incidental motion adhere to a main question if it is related to the main question in such a way that—once introduced—it must be decided before the main question can be decided.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:35 — “A subsidiary or incidental motion adhere to a main question if it is related to the main question in such a way that—once introduced—it must be decided before the main question can be decided.”

**203.** `[C0267 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ or incidental motion adhere to a main question if it is related to the main question in such a way that—once introduced—it must be decided before the main question can be decided.
   A. assemblies
   B. Convention
   C. subsidiary
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) subsidiary. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:35 — “A subsidiary or incidental motion adhere to a main question if it is related to the main question in such a way that—once introduced—it must be decided before the main question can be decided.”

**204.** `[C0267 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Adhering motions thus remain connected with the main question if that question is _____ or temporarily disposed of, and remain to be decided first if and when the main question is taken up again.

   *Answer:* interrupted — “Adhering motions thus remain connected with the main question if that question is interrupted or temporarily disposed of, and remain to be decided first if and when the main question is taken up again.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:35 — “Adhering motions thus remain connected with the main question if that question is interrupted or temporarily disposed of, and remain to be decided first if and when the main question is taken up again.”

**205.** `[C0267 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motion (8) in the _____ does not adhere to the main question.
   A. advance
   B. Adopted
   C. abstain
   D. example

   *Answer:* D) example. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:35 — “Motion (8) in the example does not adhere to the main question.”

**206.** `[C0267 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Adhering motions thus remain connected with the main question if that question is _____ or temporarily disposed of, and remain to be decided first if and when the main question is taken up again.
   A. Substitute
   B. convention
   C. interrupted
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) interrupted. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:35 — “Adhering motions thus remain connected with the main question if that question is interrupted or temporarily disposed of, and remain to be decided first if and when the main question is taken up again.”

**207.** `[C0268 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Certain questions may come before the assembly with the status of main motions after having interrupted other business, however, if they are brought up by means of either of the two lowest-ranking _____ motions.

   *Answer:* privileged — “Certain questions may come before the assembly with the status of main motions after having interrupted other business, however, if they are brought up by means of either of the two lowest-ranking privileged motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:36 — “Certain questions may come before the assembly with the status of main motions after having interrupted other business, however, if they are brought up by means of either of the two lowest-ranking privileged motions.”

**208.** `[C0268 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To Call for the Orders of the Day (18) and to _____ of Privilege (19) each have privileged rank and can therefore interrupt pending business.
   A. Consent Calendar
   B. Raise a Question
   C. Which to Adjourn
   D. Minimum Officers

   *Answer:* B) Raise a Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:36 — “To Call for the Orders of the Day (18) and to Raise a Question of Privilege (19) each have privileged rank and can therefore interrupt pending business.”

**209.** `[C0268 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Main Motions Brought Up by Means of a Call for the Orders of the Day or by Raising a Question _____.

   *Answer:* of Privilege — “Main Motions Brought Up by Means of a Call for the Orders of the Day or by Raising a Question of Privilege.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:36 — “Main Motions Brought Up by Means of a Call for the Orders of the Day or by Raising a Question of Privilege.”

**210.** `[C0268 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As a result of the application of one of these devices to interrupt a pending question, an _____ of the day (that is, another question previously set as due to come up automatically at the time) may be taken up, or an urgent motion relating to the privileges of the assembly or of a member may be admitted as a question of privilege to be entertained immediately.
   A. order
   B. Refer
   C. Limit
   D. Annul

   *Answer:* A) order. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:36 — “As a result of the application of one of these devices to interrupt a pending question, an order of the day (that is, another question previously set as due to come up automatically at the time) may be taken up, or an urgent motion relating to the privileges of the assembly or of a member may be admitted as a question of privilege to be entertained immediately.”

**211.** `[C0268 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Main Motions Brought Up by Means of a Call for the Orders _____ or by Raising a Question of Privilege.

   *Answer:* of the Day — “Main Motions Brought Up by Means of a Call for the Orders of the Day or by Raising a Question of Privilege.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:36 — “Main Motions Brought Up by Means of a Call for the Orders of the Day or by Raising a Question of Privilege.”

**212.** `[C0269 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Below for the main motion, and in each of the succeeding sections covering the different motions of parliamentary law (11–37), is a subsection headed _____ giving a script showing the words to be spoken by a member making the motion and by the chair in stating and putting the question on it.

   *Answer:* Form and Example — “Below for the main motion, and in each of the succeeding sections covering the different motions of parliamentary law (11–37), is a subsection headed Form and Example giving a script showing the words to be spoken by a member making the motion and by the chair in stating and putting the question on it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:37 — “Below for the main motion, and in each of the succeeding sections covering the different motions of parliamentary law (11–37), is a subsection headed Form and Example giving a script showing the words to be spoken by a member making the motion and by the chair in stating and putting the question on it.”

**213.** `[C0269 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Below for the main motion, and in each of the succeeding sections covering the different motions of _____ law (11–37), is a subsection headed Form and Example giving a script showing the words to be spoken by a member making the motion and by the chair in stating and putting the question on it.
   A. resolutions
   B. subsequently
   C. characteristics
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:37 — “Below for the main motion, and in each of the succeeding sections covering the different motions of parliamentary law (11–37), is a subsection headed Form and Example giving a script showing the words to be spoken by a member making the motion and by the chair in stating and putting the question on it.”

   A. subsections

   *Answer:* A) subsections. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:37 — “In these subsections and throughout this book as applicable, the following methods of notation are used:”

**216.** `[C0271 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Member A is _____ rising to claim the floor.

   *Answer:* already — “Member A is already rising to claim the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:40 — “Member A is already rising to claim the floor.”

**217.** `[C0271 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Member A is _____ rising to claim the floor.
   A. Reports
   B. advance
   C. already
   D. Another

   *Answer:* C) already. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:40 — “Member A is already rising to claim the floor.”

**218.** `[C0272 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Member A explains the reasons why the _____ should be made, followed by others who also speak in debate after having obtained the floor.

   *Answer:* contribution — “Member A explains the reasons why the contribution should be made, followed by others who also speak in debate after having obtained the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:41 — “Member A explains the reasons why the contribution should be made, followed by others who also speak in debate after having obtained the floor.”

**219.** `[C0272 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When debate _____ to have ended, the chair makes sure that no one else wishes to speak, as by asking, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any further debate?” and then puts the question—that is, puts the motion to a vote—as follows:
   A. appears
   B. Inquiry
   C. Pending
   D. abstain

   *Answer:* A) appears. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:41 — “When debate appears to have ended, the chair makes sure that no one else wishes to speak, as by asking, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any further debate?” and then puts the question—that is, puts the motion to a vote—as follows:”

**220.** `[C0272 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Member A explains the reasons why the _____ should be made, followed by others who also speak in debate after having obtained the floor.
   A. contribution
   B. Parliamentary
   C. Organization
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* A) contribution. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:41 — “Member A explains the reasons why the contribution should be made, followed by others who also speak in debate after having obtained the floor.”

**221.** `[C0272 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When debate _____ to have ended, the chair makes sure that no one else wishes to speak, as by asking, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any further debate?” and then puts the question—that is, puts the motion to a vote—as follows:

   *Answer:* appears — “When debate appears to have ended, the chair makes sure that no one else wishes to speak, as by asking, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any further debate?” and then puts the question—that is, puts the motion to a vote—as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §10:41 — “When debate appears to have ended, the chair makes sure that no one else wishes to speak, as by asking, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any further debate?” and then puts the question—that is, puts the motion to a vote—as follows:”

**223.** `[C0274 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For examples of the use of subsidiary and incidental motions in the handling of main motions and the _____ of privileged motions while a main motion is pending, see 11–33.)
   A. Definitely
   B. Permission
   C. application
   D. Particular

   A. Preparation
   B. Substitute

   D. illustrated
