   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Commit or Refer
   D. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   E. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**155.** `[K0179 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. Postpone to a Certain Time
   D. Commit or Refer
   E. Recess

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**156.** `[K0180 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Recess → Lay on the Table → Postpone to a Certain Time → Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**157.** `[K0181 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Amend
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**158.** `[K0182 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Postpone to a Certain Time
   B. Amend
   C. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:96 — “As soon as a member has made a suggestion (or, if the member had obtained the floor, as soon as the member has yielded the floor), the chair repeats the member’s suggestion and calls for further suggestions.”

**159.** `[K0183 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Adjourn → Postpone to a Certain Time → Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**160.** `[K0184 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. Commit or Refer
   D. Adjourn
   E. Recess

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**161.** `[K0185 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Commit or Refer
   C. Adjourn
   D. Recess
   E. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**162.** `[K0186 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Adjourn → Recess → Raise a Question of Privilege → Lay on the Table → Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**163.** `[K0187 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Commit or Refer
   B. Previous Question
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   D. Recess
   E. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**164.** `[K0188 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Commit or Refer
   C. Recess
   D. Raise a Question of Privilege
   E. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**165.** `[K0189 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Recess → Raise a Question of Privilege → Previous Question → Limit or Extend Limits of Debate → Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**166.** `[K0190 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Commit or Refer
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Postpone Indefinitely
   E. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**167.** `[K0191 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Commit or Refer
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. Postpone Indefinitely
   D. Adjourn
   E. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “Postpone Indefinitely is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.”

**168.** `[K0192 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Adjourn → Limit or Extend Limits of Debate → Commit or Refer → Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**169.** `[K0193 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. Postpone Indefinitely
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “Postpone Indefinitely is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.”

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Raise a Question of Privilege → Call for the Orders of the Day → Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**172.** `[K0196 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**173.** `[K0197 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “Postpone Indefinitely is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.”

**174.** `[K0198 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate → Postpone to a Certain Time → Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**175.** `[K0199 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Raise a Question of Privilege
   B. Recess
   C. Previous Question
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

   B. Previous Question
   C. Recess
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**177.** `[K0201 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Recess → Raise a Question of Privilege → Call for the Orders of the Day → Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**179.** `[K0203 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer → Amend → Postpone Indefinitely.    A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. Amend

   *Answer:* Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:96 — “As soon as a member has made a suggestion (or, if the member had obtained the floor, as soon as the member has yielded the floor), the chair repeats the member’s suggestion and calls for further suggestions.”

**183.** `[K0207 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Limit or Extend Limits of Debate → Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**184.** `[K0208 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Raise a Question of Privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

**185.** `[K0209 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**186.** `[K0210 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Raise a Question of Privilege → Call for the Orders of the Day → Lay on the Table → Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

   B. Amend
   C. Commit or Refer

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “Postpone Indefinitely is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.”

**222.** `[K0246 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer → Amend → Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**223.** `[K0247 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Raise a Question of Privilege
   B. Call for the Orders of the Day
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Recess

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**224.** `[K0248 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Recess
   C. Raise a Question of Privilege
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**225.** `[K0249 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Recess → Raise a Question of Privilege → Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**226.** `[K0250 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Adjourn
   B. Amend
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**227.** `[K0251 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Amend
   B. Adjourn
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:96 — “As soon as a member has made a suggestion (or, if the member had obtained the floor, as soon as the member has yielded the floor), the chair repeats the member’s suggestion and calls for further suggestions.”

**228.** `[K0252 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Adjourn → Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**229.** `[K0253 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Postpone to a Certain Time
   B. Recess
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**230.** `[K0254 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Recess
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**231.** `[K0255 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Recess → Limit or Extend Limits of Debate → Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**232.** `[K0256 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Recess
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**233.** `[K0257 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Recess
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “Postpone Indefinitely is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.”

**234.** `[K0258 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Recess → Lay on the Table → Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**235.** `[K0259 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Lay on the Table
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**236.** `[K0260 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Lay on the Table
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**237.** `[K0261 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Lay on the Table → Limit or Extend Limits of Debate → Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**238.** `[K0262 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Previous Question
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Adjourn
   E. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**239.** `[K0263 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Adjourn
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Raise a Question of Privilege
   E. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**240.** `[K0264 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Adjourn → Raise a Question of Privilege → Lay on the Table → Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**241.** `[K0265 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Previous Question
   C. Raise a Question of Privilege
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time
   E. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**242.** `[K0266 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. Adjourn
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time
   E. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**243.** `[K0267 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Adjourn → Raise a Question of Privilege → Previous Question → Limit or Extend Limits of Debate → Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**244.** `[K0268 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the highest precedence?
   A. Raise a Question of Privilege
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**245.** `[K0269 · Precedence (ranking)]` Which of these motions has the lowest precedence?
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   C. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**246.** `[K0270 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Raise a Question of Privilege → Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

   D. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**249.** `[K0273 · Precedence (ordering)]` Put these motions in order of precedence (top = higher, bottom = lower).

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn → Lay on the Table → Limit or Extend Limits of Debate → Commit or Refer.    C. Lay on the Table
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time

   A. Postpone to a Certain Time
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**286.** `[K0310 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Amend or Postpone to a Certain Time?
   A. Amend
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**287.** `[K0311 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn or Raise a Question of Privilege?
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**288.** `[K0312 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Commit or Refer or Limit or Extend Limits of Debate?
   A. Commit or Refer
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**289.** `[K0313 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Recess or Limit or Extend Limits of Debate?
   A. Recess
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**290.** `[K0314 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Lay on the Table or Previous Question?
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**291.** `[K0315 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn or Raise a Question of Privilege?
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**292.** `[K0316 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Postpone to a Certain Time or Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn?
   A. Postpone to a Certain Time
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**293.** `[K0317 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Lay on the Table or Previous Question?
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**294.** `[K0318 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Amend or Postpone to a Certain Time?
   A. Amend
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**295.** `[K0319 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Previous Question or Raise a Question of Privilege?
   A. Previous Question
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Raise a Question of Privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

**296.** `[K0320 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Adjourn or Postpone to a Certain Time?
   A. Adjourn
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**297.** `[K0321 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Postpone Indefinitely or Postpone to a Certain Time?
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**298.** `[K0322 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Adjourn or Previous Question?
   A. Adjourn
   B. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**299.** `[K0323 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Call for the Orders of the Day or Postpone to a Certain Time?
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**300.** `[K0324 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Call for the Orders of the Day or Lay on the Table?
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

   B. Postpone to a Certain Time

**304.** `[K0328 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Previous Question or Adjourn?
   A. Previous Question
   B. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**305.** `[K0329 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Recess or Raise a Question of Privilege?
   A. Recess

   *Answer:* Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

   A. Amend
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**329.** `[K0353 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Postpone Indefinitely or Commit or Refer?
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Commit or Refer

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**330.** `[K0354 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Previous Question or Amend?
   A. Previous Question

**332.** `[K0356 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Raise a Question of Privilege or Recess?

**338.** `[K0362 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Call for the Orders of the Day or Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn?

**339.** `[K0363 · Precedence (which is higher)]` Which motion takes precedence — Recess or Postpone to a Certain Time?
   A. Recess
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**340.** `[K0364 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Raise a Question of Privilege.
   A. Adjourn
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Commit or Refer
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely, Commit or Refer, Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

**341.** `[K0365 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.
   A. Commit or Refer
   B. Call for the Orders of the Day
   C. Postpone Indefinitely
   D. Amend

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**342.** `[K0366 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion for the Previous Question.
   A. Amend
   B. Commit or Refer
   C. Recess
   D. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Amend, Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**343.** `[K0367 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate.
   A. Recess
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Commit or Refer
   D. Adjourn
   E. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely, Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**344.** `[K0368 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Raise a Question of Privilege
   B. Commit or Refer
   C. Previous Question
   D. Adjourn
   E. Amend

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer, Previous Question, Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**345.** `[K0369 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Recess
   C. Amend
   D. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

   A. Postpone to a Certain Time
   B. Lay on the Table
   C. Previous Question
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table, Previous Question, Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**347.** `[K0371 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Lay on the Table.

   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Call for the Orders of the Day
   D. Commit or Refer
   E. Amend

   A. Previous Question
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   C. Call for the Orders of the Day
   D. Adjourn
   E. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

**349.** `[K0373 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Lay on the Table.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Previous Question
   C. Amend
   D. Recess

   *Answer:* Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**350.** `[K0374 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Commit or Refer.
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Postpone Indefinitely
   D. Previous Question
   E. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**351.** `[K0375 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Adjourn.
   A. Postpone to a Certain Time
   B. Commit or Refer
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Amend
   E. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**352.** `[K0376 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Commit or Refer.
   A. Amend
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time, Lay on the Table, Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**353.** `[K0377 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Adjourn
   B. Amend
   C. Commit or Refer
   D. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Amend, Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**354.** `[K0378 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate.
   A. Recess
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. Raise a Question of Privilege
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time
   E. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**355.** `[K0379 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion for the Previous Question.
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. Recess
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   E. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table, Raise a Question of Privilege, Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**356.** `[K0380 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate.
   A. Commit or Refer
   B. Adjourn
   C. Postpone to a Certain Time
   D. Amend

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer, Postpone to a Certain Time, Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**357.** `[K0381 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Previous Question
   C. Adjourn
   D. Commit or Refer

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**358.** `[K0382 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Adjourn.
   A. Commit or Refer
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day
   E. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer, Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, Call for the Orders of the Day, Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**359.** `[K0383 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Raise a Question of Privilege.
   A. Recess
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   D. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

**360.** `[K0384 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Previous Question
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   C. Commit or Refer
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time
   E. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Previous Question, Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, Commit or Refer, Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**361.** `[K0385 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Recess.
   A. Previous Question
   B. Amend
   C. Adjourn
   D. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**362.** `[K0386 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Raise a Question of Privilege.
   A. Adjourn
   B. Amend
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   D. Recess

   *Answer:* Amend, Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

**363.** `[K0387 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Amend.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Previous Question
   C. Call for the Orders of the Day
   D. Commit or Refer

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:96 — “As soon as a member has made a suggestion (or, if the member had obtained the floor, as soon as the member has yielded the floor), the chair repeats the member’s suggestion and calls for further suggestions.”

**364.** `[K0388 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Commit or Refer
   C. Previous Question
   D. Amend
   E. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**365.** `[K0389 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Raise a Question of Privilege.
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. Lay on the Table
   C. Amend
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   E. Recess

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day, Lay on the Table, Amend, Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

**366.** `[K0390 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   C. Postpone to a Certain Time
   D. Previous Question
   E. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, Postpone to a Certain Time, Previous Question, Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**367.** `[K0391 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Previous Question
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   E. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Previous Question, Lay on the Table, Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**368.** `[K0392 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Commit or Refer.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Call for the Orders of the Day
   C. Adjourn
   D. Previous Question
   E. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**369.** `[K0393 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Commit or Refer.
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Previous Question
   D. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**370.** `[K0394 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. Adjourn
   D. Lay on the Table
   E. Recess

   *Answer:* Raise a Question of Privilege, Adjourn, Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**371.** `[K0395 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Adjourn
   B. Commit or Refer
   C. Previous Question
   D. Recess
   E. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Adjourn, Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**372.** `[K0396 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate.
   A. Amend
   B. Lay on the Table
   C. Previous Question
   D. Recess
   E. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Amend, Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**373.** `[K0397 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Lay on the Table.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Amend
   C. Previous Question
   D. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**374.** `[K0398 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate.
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Amend
   C. Call for the Orders of the Day
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time
   E. Commit or Refer

   *Answer:* Amend, Postpone to a Certain Time, Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**375.** `[K0399 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion for the Previous Question.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. Call for the Orders of the Day
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely, Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**376.** `[K0400 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate.
   A. Amend
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. Recess
   D. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Amend, Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**377.** `[K0401 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion for the Previous Question.
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Raise a Question of Privilege
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time
   E. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, Postpone Indefinitely, Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**378.** `[K0402 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Lay on the Table.
   A. Recess
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Raise a Question of Privilege
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**379.** `[K0403 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Recess.
   A. Postpone to a Certain Time
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time, Lay on the Table, Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**380.** `[K0404 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Recess.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Amend
   C. Previous Question
   D. Postpone to a Certain Time
   E. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**381.** `[K0405 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Lay on the Table.
   A. Postpone to a Certain Time
   B. Recess
   C. Raise a Question of Privilege
   D. Amend
   E. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time, Amend, Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**382.** `[K0406 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Lay on the Table.
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Amend
   D. Previous Question
   E. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**383.** `[K0407 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Commit or Refer
   B. Postpone to a Certain Time
   C. Postpone Indefinitely
   D. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer, Postpone to a Certain Time, Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**384.** `[K0408 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Adjourn.
   A. Commit or Refer
   B. Amend
   C. Previous Question
   D. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer, Amend, Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**385.** `[K0409 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Lay on the Table.
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. Previous Question
   C. Postpone to a Certain Time
   D. Amend
   E. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day, Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**386.** `[K0410 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Previous Question
   C. Recess
   D. Adjourn
   E. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**387.** `[K0411 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.
   A. Adjourn
   B. Previous Question
   C. Recess
   D. Commit or Refer

   *Answer:* Adjourn, Previous Question, Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**388.** `[K0412 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Recess
   C. Adjourn
   D. Lay on the Table
   E. Commit or Refer

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely, Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**389.** `[K0413 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate.
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Adjourn
   D. Amend

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table, Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “This motion’s effect upon other pending and subsequent motions is closely related to its position in the order of precedence of motions (5) and further depends on the nature of its specific provisions, as explained below.”

**390.** `[K0414 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Recess
   D. Commit or Refer
   E. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, Recess, Raise a Question of Privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**391.** `[K0415 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Lay on the Table.
   A. Amend
   B. Recess
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   D. Previous Question
   E. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* Amend, Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**402.** `[K0426 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Raise a Question of Privilege.

   C. Recess
   D. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, Adjourn, Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

**403.** `[K0427 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Recess.
   A. Raise a Question of Privilege
   B. Previous Question
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   D. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   E. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Raise a Question of Privilege, Previous Question, Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**404.** `[K0428 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Raise a Question of Privilege.
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Recess
   D. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely, Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The “raising” of a question of privilege is governed by rules appropriate to the device’s high rank in the order of precedence of motions.”

**405.** `[K0429 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion for the Previous Question.
   A. Amend
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Adjourn
   D. Recess
   E. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Adjourn, Recess, Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**406.** `[K0430 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.
   A. Recess
   B. Call for the Orders of the Day
   C. Previous Question
   D. Amend

   *Answer:* Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**407.** `[K0431 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Lay on the Table.
   A. Postpone to a Certain Time
   B. Call for the Orders of the Day
   C. Commit or Refer
   D. Adjourn

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time, Commit or Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**408.** `[K0432 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Call for the Orders of the Day.
   A. Commit or Refer
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. Amend
   D. Previous Question
   E. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Commit or Refer, Amend, Previous Question, Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**409.** `[K0433 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Adjourn.
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Amend
   C. Raise a Question of Privilege
   D. Postpone Indefinitely
   E. Lay on the Table

   *Answer:* Amend, Raise a Question of Privilege, Postpone Indefinitely, Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**410.** `[K0434 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Recess.
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. Amend
   C. Commit or Refer
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**411.** `[K0435 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.
   A. Adjourn
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Amend
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely, Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**412.** `[K0436 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank lower than (yield to) the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Call for the Orders of the Day
   C. Previous Question
   D. Recess
   E. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**413.** `[K0437 · Precedence (select all)]` Select ALL of these motions that rank higher than (take precedence over) the motion to Recess.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Adjourn
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. Raise a Question of Privilege
   E. Previous Question

   *Answer:* Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**414.** `[C0288 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “Postpone Indefinitely is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “Postpone Indefinitely is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.”

**415.** `[C0288 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Postpone _____ is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.
   A. Procedures
   B. resolutions
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “Postpone Indefinitely is a motion that the assembly decline to take a position on the main question.”

**416.** `[C0288 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Its _____ kills the main motion (for the duration of the session) and avoids a direct vote on the question.

   *Answer:* adoption — “Its adoption kills the main motion (for the duration of the session) and avoids a direct vote on the question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “Its adoption kills the main motion (for the duration of the session) and avoids a direct vote on the question.”

**417.** `[C0288 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is useful in disposing of a badly chosen main motion that cannot be either adopted or expressly rejected without possibly undesirable _____.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Permission
   C. immediately
   D. consequences

   *Answer:* D) consequences. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “It is useful in disposing of a badly chosen main motion that cannot be either adopted or expressly rejected without possibly undesirable consequences.”

**418.** `[C0288 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Its _____ kills the main motion (for the duration of the session) and avoids a direct vote on the question.
   A. assembly
   B. Interest
   C. Nominees
   D. adoption

   *Answer:* D) adoption. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:1 — “Its adoption kills the main motion (for the duration of the session) and avoids a direct vote on the question.”

**419.** `[C0289 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Consequently, the adoption of the motion to _____ Indefinitely is in effect an indirect rejection of the main motion.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “Consequently, the adoption of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely is in effect an indirect rejection of the main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:3 — “Consequently, the adoption of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely is in effect an indirect rejection of the main motion.”

**420.** `[C0289 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The effect of postponing a question _____ is to suppress it throughout the current session.
   A. Information
   B. presentation
   C. indefinitely
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:3 — “The effect of postponing a question indefinitely is to suppress it throughout the current session.”

**421.** `[C0289 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a convention or conference consisting of several meetings, the _____ continues throughout the entire series of meetings, and in ordinary societies, throughout the weekly, monthly, or other meeting, as the case may be.

   *Answer:* suppression — “In a convention or conference consisting of several meetings, the suppression continues throughout the entire series of meetings, and in ordinary societies, throughout the weekly, monthly, or other meeting, as the case may be.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:3 — “In a convention or conference consisting of several meetings, the suppression continues throughout the entire series of meetings, and in ordinary societies, throughout the weekly, monthly, or other meeting, as the case may be.”

**422.** `[C0289 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, the adoption of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely is in effect an indirect rejection of the main motion.
   A. Convention
   B. constitution
   C. Consequently
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) Consequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:3 — “Consequently, the adoption of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely is in effect an indirect rejection of the main motion.”

**423.** `[C0289 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The effect of postponing a question _____ is to suppress it throughout the current session.

   *Answer:* indefinitely — “The effect of postponing a question indefinitely is to suppress it throughout the current session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:3 — “The effect of postponing a question indefinitely is to suppress it throughout the current session.”

**424.** `[C0290 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Effect of Referral (of the Main Motion) on a Pending Motion _____.

   *Answer:* to Postpone Indefinitely — “Effect of Referral (of the Main Motion) on a Pending Motion to Postpone Indefinitely.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:4 — “Effect of Referral (of the Main Motion) on a Pending Motion to Postpone Indefinitely.”

**425.** `[C0290 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Effect of Referral (of the Main Motion) on a Pending Motion to _____.
   A. Conforming Amendments
   B. Debate on Nominations
   C. Types of Deliberative
   D. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* D) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:4 — “Effect of Referral (of the Main Motion) on a Pending Motion to Postpone Indefinitely.”

**426.** `[C0290 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a main motion is referred to a committee while Postpone Indefinitely is pending, the latter motion is ignored and does not go to the committee, since the adoption of the motion to _____ indicates that the assembly is not in favor of postponing indefinitely.

   *Answer:* Commit — “If a main motion is referred to a committee while Postpone Indefinitely is pending, the latter motion is ignored and does not go to the committee, since the adoption of the motion to Commit indicates that the assembly is not in favor of postponing indefinitely.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:4 — “If a main motion is referred to a committee while Postpone Indefinitely is pending, the latter motion is ignored and does not go to the committee, since the adoption of the motion to Commit indicates that the assembly is not in favor of postponing indefinitely.”

**427.** `[C0290 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a main motion is referred to a committee while Postpone Indefinitely is pending, the latter motion is ignored and does not go to the committee, since the adoption of the motion to _____ indicates that the assembly is not in favor of postponing indefinitely.
   A. Appeal
   B. common
   C. Commit
   D. action

   *Answer:* C) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:4 — “If a main motion is referred to a committee while Postpone Indefinitely is pending, the latter motion is ignored and does not go to the committee, since the adoption of the motion to Commit indicates that the assembly is not in favor of postponing indefinitely.”

**428.** `[C0291 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to _____ is sometimes employed by strategists to test their strength on a motion they oppose.
   A. Interruption of Votes
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. prevent consideration
   D. Committees of a Board

   *Answer:* B) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:5 — “The motion to Postpone Indefinitely is sometimes employed by strategists to test their strength on a motion they oppose.”

**429.** `[C0291 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to Postpone _____ is sometimes employed by strategists to test their strength on a motion they oppose.

   *Answer:* Indefinitely — “The motion to Postpone Indefinitely is sometimes employed by strategists to test their strength on a motion they oppose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:5 — “The motion to Postpone Indefinitely is sometimes employed by strategists to test their strength on a motion they oppose.”

**430.** `[C0291 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Making this motion enables members who have exhausted their right of debate on the main question to speak further because, as explained under Standard _____ 5, the motion to Postpone Indefinitely, though technically a new question, necessarily involves debate of the main question.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. recommendation
   C. deliberative
   D. Characteristic

   *Answer:* D) Characteristic. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:5 — “Making this motion enables members who have exhausted their right of debate on the main question to speak further because, as explained under Standard Characteristic 5, the motion to Postpone Indefinitely, though technically a new question, necessarily involves debate of the main question.”

**431.** `[C0291 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Its effect, therefore, is to give the _____ of the pending measure a chance to kill it without risking its adoption, as they would be doing if the vote were taken on the main motion itself.

   *Answer:* opponents — “Its effect, therefore, is to give the opponents of the pending measure a chance to kill it without risking its adoption, as they would be doing if the vote were taken on the main motion itself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:5 — “Its effect, therefore, is to give the opponents of the pending measure a chance to kill it without risking its adoption, as they would be doing if the vote were taken on the main motion itself.”

**432.** `[C0291 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If opponents of the main question carry the indefinite _____, the main question is suppressed for the session;
   A. postponement
   B. constitution
   C. consideration
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) postponement. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:5 — “If opponents of the main question carry the indefinite postponement, the main question is suppressed for the session;”

**433.** `[C0292 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume that the following resolution is pending in a meeting of a local unit of a state professional society: “_____, That the Ferndale Unit endorse the State President of the Society, James Thornton, for the office of United States Senator.” Debate creates a delicate situation.

   *Answer:* Resolved — “Assume that the following resolution is pending in a meeting of a local unit of a state professional society: “Resolved, That the Ferndale Unit endorse the State President of the Society, James Thornton, for the office of United States Senator.” Debate creates a delicate situation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:6 — “Assume that the following resolution is pending in a meeting of a local unit of a state professional society: “Resolved, That the Ferndale Unit endorse the State President of the Society, James Thornton, for the office of United States Senator.” Debate creates a delicate situation.”

**434.** `[C0292 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that the following resolution is pending in a meeting of a local unit of a state _____ society: “Resolved, That the Ferndale Unit endorse the State President of the Society, James Thornton, for the office of United States Senator.” Debate creates a delicate situation.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. professional
   C. Indefinitely
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) professional. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:6 — “Assume that the following resolution is pending in a meeting of a local unit of a state professional society: “Resolved, That the Ferndale Unit endorse the State President of the Society, James Thornton, for the office of United States Senator.” Debate creates a delicate situation.”

**435.** `[C0292 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Members are loyal to their state president, but in questions of public office they wish to _____ the nominee of their choice.

   *Answer:* support — “Members are loyal to their state president, but in questions of public office they wish to support the nominee of their choice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:6 — “Members are loyal to their state president, but in questions of public office they wish to support the nominee of their choice.”

**436.** `[C0292 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Yet, for them to vote no on the _____ might appear to be a repudiation of their state president.
   A. endorsement
   B. Reconsider
   C. Information
   D. Preparation
