   *Answer:* A) endorsement. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:6 — “Yet, for them to vote no on the endorsement might appear to be a repudiation of their state president.”

**437.** `[C0292 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, a vote on this question either in the affirmative or in the negative might tend to create an unfortunate division within the local unit.

   *Answer:* Furthermore — “Furthermore, a vote on this question either in the affirmative or in the negative might tend to create an unfortunate division within the local unit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:6 — “Furthermore, a vote on this question either in the affirmative or in the negative might tend to create an unfortunate division within the local unit.”

**438.** `[C0293 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debate on the subsidiary motion to _____ will likely involve also the advisability of the resolution itself.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “Debate on the subsidiary motion to Postpone Indefinitely will likely involve also the advisability of the resolution itself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:7 — “Debate on the subsidiary motion to Postpone Indefinitely will likely involve also the advisability of the resolution itself.”

**439.** `[C0293 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Debate on the subsidiary motion to Postpone _____ will likely involve also the advisability of the resolution itself.
   A. Incidental
   B. presentation
   C. Permission
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* D) Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:7 — “Debate on the subsidiary motion to Postpone Indefinitely will likely involve also the advisability of the resolution itself.”

**440.** `[C0294 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to _____ is lost, the chair announces the result and immediately states the question on the main motion.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “If the motion to Postpone Indefinitely is lost, the chair announces the result and immediately states the question on the main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:8 — “If the motion to Postpone Indefinitely is lost, the chair announces the result and immediately states the question on the main motion.”

**441.** `[C0294 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to Postpone _____ is lost, the chair announces the result and immediately states the question on the main motion.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Permission
   C. undebatable
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* A) Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §11:8 — “If the motion to Postpone Indefinitely is lost, the chair announces the result and immediately states the question on the main motion.”

**442.** `[C0295 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ is a motion to modify the wording—and within certain limits the meaning—of a pending motion before the pending motion itself is acted upon.

   *Answer:* Amend — “The subsidiary motion to Amend is a motion to modify the wording—and within certain limits the meaning—of a pending motion before the pending motion itself is acted upon.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Amend is a motion to modify the wording—and within certain limits the meaning—of a pending motion before the pending motion itself is acted upon.”

**443.** `[C0295 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ motion to Amend is a motion to modify the wording—and within certain limits the meaning—of a pending motion before the pending motion itself is acted upon.
   A. subsidiary
   B. committees
   C. landscaped
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* A) subsidiary. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Amend is a motion to modify the wording—and within certain limits the meaning—of a pending motion before the pending motion itself is acted upon.”

**444.** `[C0296 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that the motion then used is not the subsidiary motion to _____, but a main motion having particular characteristics.

   *Answer:* Amend — “It should be noted that the motion then used is not the subsidiary motion to Amend, but a main motion having particular characteristics.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:2 — “It should be noted that the motion then used is not the subsidiary motion to Amend, but a main motion having particular characteristics.”

**445.** `[C0296 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For the motion to _____ Previously Adopted, see 35.)
   A. as thus amended
   B. Making a Motion
   C. Amend Something
   D. Article I: Name

   *Answer:* C) Amend Something. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:2 — “(For the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted, see 35.)”

**446.** `[C0296 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (For the motion to Amend Something _____, see 35.)

   *Answer:* Previously Adopted — “(For the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted, see 35.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:2 — “(For the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted, see 35.)”

**447.** `[C0296 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Less _____, it may become desirable to apply a similar process to something already adopted—as bylaws, a program, or a resolution.
   A. resolution
   B. frequently
   C. Particular
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) frequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:2 — “Less frequently, it may become desirable to apply a similar process to something already adopted—as bylaws, a program, or a resolution.”

**448.** `[C0296 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that the motion then used is not the subsidiary motion to Amend, but a main motion having particular _____.

   *Answer:* characteristics — “It should be noted that the motion then used is not the subsidiary motion to Amend, but a main motion having particular characteristics.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:2 — “It should be noted that the motion then used is not the subsidiary motion to Amend, but a main motion having particular characteristics.”

**449.** `[C0297 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ is probably the most widely used of the subsidiary motions, although the full procedure for its most effective application is not generally well understood by the meetinggoing public at large.

   *Answer:* Amend — “Amend is probably the most widely used of the subsidiary motions, although the full procedure for its most effective application is not generally well understood by the meetinggoing public at large.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:3 — “Amend is probably the most widely used of the subsidiary motions, although the full procedure for its most effective application is not generally well understood by the meetinggoing public at large.”

**450.** `[C0297 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To understand how the process works, it is important to realize that, when an assembly “takes action,” all it ever actually does itself is to _____—directing that a certain action be carried out, or expressing a certain view or aspiration.
   A. adopt a statement
   B. executive session
   C. Agenda or Program
   D. Robert’s Rules of

   *Answer:* A) adopt a statement. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:3 — “To understand how the process works, it is important to realize that, when an assembly “takes action,” all it ever actually does itself is to adopt a statement—directing that a certain action be carried out, or expressing a certain view or aspiration.”

**451.** `[C0297 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Amend is probably the most widely used of the subsidiary motions, although the full procedure for its most effective application is not generally well understood by the _____ public at large.

   *Answer:* meetinggoing — “Amend is probably the most widely used of the subsidiary motions, although the full procedure for its most effective application is not generally well understood by the meetinggoing public at large.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:3 — “Amend is probably the most widely used of the subsidiary motions, although the full procedure for its most effective application is not generally well understood by the meetinggoing public at large.”

**452.** `[C0297 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The ordinary member’s becoming at home with the formal amendment process is the keystone of the power of the general membership to keep details of the direction of an _____ under its control to the extent it wishes to do so.
   A. organization
   B. Definitely
   C. presentation
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:3 — “The ordinary member’s becoming at home with the formal amendment process is the keystone of the power of the general membership to keep details of the direction of an organization under its control to the extent it wishes to do so.”

**453.** `[C0297 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To _____ how the process works, it is important to realize that, when an assembly “takes action,” all it ever actually does itself is to adopt a statement—directing that a certain action be carried out, or expressing a certain view or aspiration.

   *Answer:* understand — “To understand how the process works, it is important to realize that, when an assembly “takes action,” all it ever actually does itself is to adopt a statement—directing that a certain action be carried out, or expressing a certain view or aspiration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:3 — “To understand how the process works, it is important to realize that, when an assembly “takes action,” all it ever actually does itself is to adopt a statement—directing that a certain action be carried out, or expressing a certain view or aspiration.”

**454.** `[C0298 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Adoption of a subsidiary motion to _____ does not adopt the motion thereby amended;

   *Answer:* Amend — “Adoption of a subsidiary motion to Amend does not adopt the motion thereby amended;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:4 — “Adoption of a subsidiary motion to Amend does not adopt the motion thereby amended;”

**455.** `[C0298 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Adoption of a subsidiary motion to Amend does _____ adopt the motion thereby amended;
   A. not
   B. after
   C. Order
   D. CHAIR

   *Answer:* A) not. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:4 — “Adoption of a subsidiary motion to Amend does not adopt the motion thereby amended;”

**456.** `[C0298 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: that motion remains pending in its _____ form.
   A. Standing
   B. chairman
   C. Resolved
   D. modified

   *Answer:* D) modified. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:4 — “that motion remains pending in its modified form.”

**457.** `[C0298 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of a motion to Amend leaves the pending motion worded as it was before the amendment was voted on.

   *Answer:* Rejection — “Rejection of a motion to Amend leaves the pending motion worded as it was before the amendment was voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:4 — “Rejection of a motion to Amend leaves the pending motion worded as it was before the amendment was voted on.”

**458.** `[C0298 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: that motion remains pending in its _____ form.

   *Answer:* modified — “that motion remains pending in its modified form.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:4 — “that motion remains pending in its modified form.”

**459.** `[C0299 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Neither the member who offers an amendment nor the maker of the main motion _____ or “makes an amendment”;

   *Answer:* amends — “Neither the member who offers an amendment nor the maker of the main motion amends or “makes an amendment”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:5 — “Neither the member who offers an amendment nor the maker of the main motion amends or “makes an amendment”;”

**460.** `[C0299 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Neither the member who offers an _____ nor the maker of the main motion amends or “makes an amendment”;
   A. secondary
   B. Privilege
   C. Objection
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* D) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:5 — “Neither the member who offers an amendment nor the maker of the main motion amends or “makes an amendment”;”

**461.** `[C0299 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member’s vote on an amendment does not obligate him to vote in a _____ way on the motion to which the amendment applies;

   *Answer:* particular — “A member’s vote on an amendment does not obligate him to vote in a particular way on the motion to which the amendment applies;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:5 — “A member’s vote on an amendment does not obligate him to vote in a particular way on the motion to which the amendment applies;”

**462.** `[C0299 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: he is free to vote as he _____ on the main motion, whether it is amended or not.
   A. pleases
   B. Charges
   C. Another
   D. approve

   *Answer:* A) pleases. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:5 — “he is free to vote as he pleases on the main motion, whether it is amended or not.”

**463.** `[C0299 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member’s vote on an amendment does not obligate him to vote in a _____ way on the motion to which the amendment applies;
   A. particular
   B. convention
   C. landscaped
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* A) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:5 — “A member’s vote on an amendment does not obligate him to vote in a particular way on the motion to which the amendment applies;”

**464.** `[C0300 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An amendment must always be _____—that is, closely related to or having bearing on the subject of the motion to be amended.

   *Answer:* germane — “An amendment must always be germane—that is, closely related to or having bearing on the subject of the motion to be amended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:6 — “An amendment must always be germane—that is, closely related to or having bearing on the subject of the motion to be amended.”

**465.** `[C0300 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ must always be germane—that is, closely related to or having bearing on the subject of the motion to be amended.
   A. amendment
   B. rejecting
   C. Privilege
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* A) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:6 — “An amendment must always be germane—that is, closely related to or having bearing on the subject of the motion to be amended.”

**466.** `[C0301 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: There are three basic processes of amendment, the third of which is an _____ combination of the first two.
   A. undebatable
   B. Information
   C. Permission
   D. indivisible

   *Answer:* D) indivisible. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:8 — “There are three basic processes of amendment, the third of which is an indivisible combination of the first two.”

**467.** `[C0301 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: There are three basic _____ of amendment, the third of which is an indivisible combination of the first two.

   *Answer:* processes — “There are three basic processes of amendment, the third of which is an indivisible combination of the first two.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:8 — “There are three basic processes of amendment, the third of which is an indivisible combination of the first two.”

**468.** `[C0301 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: so that each _____ has two forms, as follows:
   A. Divided
   B. Motions
   C. Program
   D. process

   *Answer:* D) process. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:8 — “so that each process has two forms, as follows:”

**469.** `[C0301 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: so that each _____ has two forms, as follows:

   *Answer:* process — “so that each process has two forms, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:8 — “so that each process has two forms, as follows:”

**470.** `[C0302 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Forms 1(a), 2(a), and 3(a), relating to _____, can be applied to change the wording within a single sentence, or occasionally within two or more consecutive sentences that make up a part of a single paragraph.

   *Answer:* words — “Forms 1(a), 2(a), and 3(a), relating to words, can be applied to change the wording within a single sentence, or occasionally within two or more consecutive sentences that make up a part of a single paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:9 — “Forms 1(a), 2(a), and 3(a), relating to words, can be applied to change the wording within a single sentence, or occasionally within two or more consecutive sentences that make up a part of a single paragraph.”

**471.** `[C0302 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Forms 1(a), 2(a), and 3(a), relating to words, can be applied to change the wording within a single sentence, or occasionally within two or more consecutive sentences that make up a _____ of a single paragraph.
   A. event
   B. part
   C. floor
   D. hours

   *Answer:* B) part. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:9 — “Forms 1(a), 2(a), and 3(a), relating to words, can be applied to change the wording within a single sentence, or occasionally within two or more consecutive sentences that make up a part of a single paragraph.”

**472.** `[C0304 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As noted in Standard Characteristics 2 and 6, above, a subsidiary motion to Amend can, in general, be amended, so that two _____—primary and secondary—are possible.

   *Answer:* degrees of amendment — “As noted in Standard Characteristics 2 and 6, above, a subsidiary motion to Amend can, in general, be amended, so that two degrees of amendment—primary and secondary—are possible.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:11 — “As noted in Standard Characteristics 2 and 6, above, a subsidiary motion to Amend can, in general, be amended, so that two degrees of amendment—primary and secondary—are possible.”

**473.** `[C0304 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As noted in Standard Characteristics 2 and 6, above, a subsidiary motion to _____ can, in general, be amended, so that two degrees of amendment—primary and secondary—are possible.
   A. Amend
   B. after
   C. makes
   D. CHAIR

   *Answer:* A) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:11 — “As noted in Standard Characteristics 2 and 6, above, a subsidiary motion to Amend can, in general, be amended, so that two degrees of amendment—primary and secondary—are possible.”

**474.** `[C0304 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A primary amendment applies directly to the pending _____, main motion, or other motion (except Amend) to be amended.
   A. resolution
   B. substitute
   C. Convention
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:11 — “A primary amendment applies directly to the pending resolution, main motion, or other motion (except Amend) to be amended.”

**475.** `[C0304 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A primary amendment applies directly to the pending _____, main motion, or other motion (except Amend) to be amended.

   *Answer:* resolution — “A primary amendment applies directly to the pending resolution, main motion, or other motion (except Amend) to be amended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:11 — “A primary amendment applies directly to the pending resolution, main motion, or other motion (except Amend) to be amended.”

**476.** `[C0305 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The terms _____ of the first degree and amendment of the second degree, or amendment to the main question and amendment to the amendment, are correct expressions, but the terms primary amendment and secondary amendment are preferred.

   *Answer:* amendment — “The terms amendment of the first degree and amendment of the second degree, or amendment to the main question and amendment to the amendment, are correct expressions, but the terms primary amendment and secondary amendment are preferred.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:12 — “The terms amendment of the first degree and amendment of the second degree, or amendment to the main question and amendment to the amendment, are correct expressions, but the terms primary amendment and secondary amendment are preferred.”

**477.** `[C0305 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The terms amendment of the first degree and amendment _____, or amendment to the main question and amendment to the amendment, are correct expressions, but the terms primary amendment and secondary amendment are preferred.
   A. Planning the Program
   B. One Person, One Vote
   C. of the second degree
   D. motions that bring a

   *Answer:* C) of the second degree. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:12 — “The terms amendment of the first degree and amendment of the second degree, or amendment to the main question and amendment to the amendment, are correct expressions, but the terms primary amendment and secondary amendment are preferred.”

**478.** `[C0306 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except as described under the next two headings, no more than one primary amendment and one secondary amendment are permitted to be pending at a time, but any number of each can be _____ in succession—so long as they do not again raise questions already decided during the same session.
   A. incidental
   B. Substitute
   C. considered
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:13 — “Except as described under the next two headings, no more than one primary amendment and one secondary amendment are permitted to be pending at a time, but any number of each can be considered in succession—so long as they do not again raise questions already decided during the same session.”

**479.** `[C0306 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except as described under the next two headings, no more than one primary amendment and one secondary amendment are permitted to be pending at a time, but any number of each can be _____ in succession—so long as they do not again raise questions already decided during the same session.

   *Answer:* considered — “Except as described under the next two headings, no more than one primary amendment and one secondary amendment are permitted to be pending at a time, but any number of each can be considered in succession—so long as they do not again raise questions already decided during the same session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:13 — “Except as described under the next two headings, no more than one primary amendment and one secondary amendment are permitted to be pending at a time, but any number of each can be considered in succession—so long as they do not again raise questions already decided during the same session.”

**480.** `[C0307 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A series of amendments to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one subsidiary motion to _____.

   *Answer:* Amend — “A series of amendments to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one subsidiary motion to Amend.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:14 — “A series of amendments to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one subsidiary motion to Amend.”

**481.** `[C0307 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Series of _____ Offered by a Single Motion.
   A. Definitely
   B. subsidiary
   C. Permission
   D. Amendments

   *Answer:* D) Amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:14 — “Series of Amendments Offered by a Single Motion.”

**482.** `[C0307 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless these amendments meet the standard for conforming amendments described _____ below, any member may demand a separate vote on one or more of them.

   *Answer:* immediately — “Unless these amendments meet the standard for conforming amendments described immediately below, any member may demand a separate vote on one or more of them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:14 — “Unless these amendments meet the standard for conforming amendments described immediately below, any member may demand a separate vote on one or more of them.”

**483.** `[C0307 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A series of _____s to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one subsidiary motion to Amend.
   A. amendment
   B. Privilege
   C. secretary
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* A) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:14 — “A series of amendments to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one subsidiary motion to Amend.”

**484.** `[C0307 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Series of _____ Offered by a Single Motion.

   *Answer:* Amendments — “Series of Amendments Offered by a Single Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:14 — “Series of Amendments Offered by a Single Motion.”

**485.** `[C0308 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes several changes throughout a motion or resolution are needed in order to achieve one end, in which case these separate amendments are proposed and adopted by a single subsidiary motion to _____.
   A. Amend
   B. Table
   C. after
   D. legal

   *Answer:* A) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:15 — “Sometimes several changes throughout a motion or resolution are needed in order to achieve one end, in which case these separate amendments are proposed and adopted by a single subsidiary motion to Amend.”

**486.** `[C0308 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes several changes _____ a motion or resolution are needed in order to achieve one end, in which case these separate amendments are proposed and adopted by a single subsidiary motion to Amend.

   *Answer:* throughout — “Sometimes several changes throughout a motion or resolution are needed in order to achieve one end, in which case these separate amendments are proposed and adopted by a single subsidiary motion to Amend.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:15 — “Sometimes several changes throughout a motion or resolution are needed in order to achieve one end, in which case these separate amendments are proposed and adopted by a single subsidiary motion to Amend.”

**487.** `[C0308 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, suppose a lengthy resolution is pending relating to the creation of a new standing committee, called the “Ways and Means Committee,” to study and make _____ concerning the society’s financial circumstances and requirements, and this committee is referred to by name in a number of places scattered throughout the resolution.
   A. characteristics
   B. resolutions
   C. recommendations
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* C) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:15 — “For example, suppose a lengthy resolution is pending relating to the creation of a new standing committee, called the “Ways and Means Committee,” to study and make recommendations concerning the society’s financial circumstances and requirements, and this committee is referred to by name in a number of places scattered throughout the resolution.”

**488.** `[C0308 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, suppose a lengthy resolution is pending relating to the creation of a new standing committee, called the “Ways and Means Committee,” to study and make _____ concerning the society’s financial circumstances and requirements, and this committee is referred to by name in a number of places scattered throughout the resolution.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “For example, suppose a lengthy resolution is pending relating to the creation of a new standing committee, called the “Ways and Means Committee,” to study and make recommendations concerning the society’s financial circumstances and requirements, and this committee is referred to by name in a number of places scattered throughout the resolution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:15 — “For example, suppose a lengthy resolution is pending relating to the creation of a new standing committee, called the “Ways and Means Committee,” to study and make recommendations concerning the society’s financial circumstances and requirements, and this committee is referred to by name in a number of places scattered throughout the resolution.”

**489.** `[C0309 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As already stated, an amendment must be _____ to be in order.

   *Answer:* germane — “As already stated, an amendment must be germane to be in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:16 — “As already stated, an amendment must be germane to be in order.”

**490.** `[C0309 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To be germane, an amendment must _____ involve the same question that is raised by the motion to which it is applied.
   A. and Example
   B. of managers
   C. Mass Meeting
   D. in some way

   *Answer:* D) in some way. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:16 — “To be germane, an amendment must in some way involve the same question that is raised by the motion to which it is applied.”

**491.** `[C0309 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To be germane, an amendment must in some way _____ the same question that is raised by the motion to which it is applied.

   *Answer:* involve — “To be germane, an amendment must in some way involve the same question that is raised by the motion to which it is applied.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:16 — “To be germane, an amendment must in some way involve the same question that is raised by the motion to which it is applied.”

**492.** `[C0309 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a secondary amendment proposes a change in the primary amendment, it must be germane _____ primary amendment—not just to the motion the primary amendment would change.
   A. the one
   B. Raise a
   C. Form and
   D. to that

   *Answer:* D) to that. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:16 — “When a secondary amendment proposes a change in the primary amendment, it must be germane to that primary amendment—not just to the motion the primary amendment would change.”

**493.** `[C0309 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a secondary amendment proposes a change in the _____, it must be germane to that primary amendment—not just to the motion the primary amendment would change.

   *Answer:* primary amendment — “When a secondary amendment proposes a change in the primary amendment, it must be germane to that primary amendment—not just to the motion the primary amendment would change.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:16 — “When a secondary amendment proposes a change in the primary amendment, it must be germane to that primary amendment—not just to the motion the primary amendment would change.”

**494.** `[C0310 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Aside from these principles, there is no single, _____ test for determining when a proposed amendment is germane and when it is not.

   *Answer:* all-inclusive — “Aside from these principles, there is no single, all-inclusive test for determining when a proposed amendment is germane and when it is not.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:17 — “Aside from these principles, there is no single, all-inclusive test for determining when a proposed amendment is germane and when it is not.”

**495.** `[C0310 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A method by which the germaneness of an amendment can often be verified, however, grows out of the following general rules of _____ law:
   A. representatives
   B. Announcements
   C. parliamentary
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:17 — “A method by which the germaneness of an amendment can often be verified, however, grows out of the following general rules of parliamentary law:”

**496.** `[C0310 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Aside from these principles, there is no single, _____ test for determining when a proposed amendment is germane and when it is not.
   A. specification
   B. presentation
   C. all-inclusive
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* C) all-inclusive. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:17 — “Aside from these principles, there is no single, all-inclusive test for determining when a proposed amendment is germane and when it is not.”

**497.** `[C0310 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A method by which the germaneness of an amendment can often be verified, however, grows out of the following general rules of _____ law:

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “A method by which the germaneness of an amendment can often be verified, however, grows out of the following general rules of parliamentary law:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:17 — “A method by which the germaneness of an amendment can often be verified, however, grows out of the following general rules of parliamentary law:”

**498.** `[C0311 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By these rules, if a proposed amendment is related to the main motion in such a way that, after the adoption, rejection, or temporary disposal of the present main motion, the essential idea of the amendment could _____ be introduced as an independent resolution during the same session, the amendment is germane and must be admitted, since there will not, or may not, be any opportunity to present it later.

   *Answer:* not — “By these rules, if a proposed amendment is related to the main motion in such a way that, after the adoption, rejection, or temporary disposal of the present main motion, the essential idea of the amendment could not be introduced as an independent resolution during the same session, the amendment is germane and must be admitted, since there will not, or may not, be any opportunity to present it later.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:18 — “By these rules, if a proposed amendment is related to the main motion in such a way that, after the adoption, rejection, or temporary disposal of the present main motion, the essential idea of the amendment could not be introduced as an independent resolution during the same session, the amendment is germane and must be admitted, since there will not, or may not, be any opportunity to present it later.”

**499.** `[C0311 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By these rules, if a proposed amendment is related to the main motion in such a way that, after the adoption, rejection, or temporary disposal of the present main motion, the essential idea of the amendment could not be introduced as an _____ resolution during the same session, the amendment is germane and must be admitted, since there will not, or may not, be any opportunity to present it later.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Definitely
   C. Convention
   D. independent

   *Answer:* D) independent. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:18 — “By these rules, if a proposed amendment is related to the main motion in such a way that, after the adoption, rejection, or temporary disposal of the present main motion, the essential idea of the amendment could not be introduced as an independent resolution during the same session, the amendment is germane and must be admitted, since there will not, or may not, be any opportunity to present it later.”

**500.** `[C0311 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This test cannot be reliably used to determine that an amendment is not in order, since it is sometimes possible for an amendment to be germane even if, regardless of action on the present main motion, the idea embodied in the amendment could be introduced _____ later in the same session.

   *Answer:* independently — “This test cannot be reliably used to determine that an amendment is not in order, since it is sometimes possible for an amendment to be germane even if, regardless of action on the present main motion, the idea embodied in the amendment could be introduced independently later in the same session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:18 — “This test cannot be reliably used to determine that an amendment is not in order, since it is sometimes possible for an amendment to be germane even if, regardless of action on the present main motion, the idea embodied in the amendment could be introduced independently later in the same session.”

**501.** `[C0311 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This test cannot be reliably used to determine that an amendment is not in order, since it is sometimes possible for an amendment to be germane even if, regardless of action on the present main motion, the idea embodied in the amendment could be introduced _____ later in the same session.
   A. characteristics
   B. presentation
   C. independently
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) independently. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:18 — “This test cannot be reliably used to determine that an amendment is not in order, since it is sometimes possible for an amendment to be germane even if, regardless of action on the present main motion, the idea embodied in the amendment could be introduced independently later in the same session.”

**502.** `[C0312 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of a germane _____, assume that a motion is pending “that the Society authorize the purchase of a new desk for the Secretary.” It would be germane and in order to amend by inserting after “desk” the words “and matching chair,” since both relate to providing the secretary with the necessary furniture.

   *Answer:* amendment — “As an example of a germane amendment, assume that a motion is pending “that the Society authorize the purchase of a new desk for the Secretary.” It would be germane and in order to amend by inserting after “desk” the words “and matching chair,” since both relate to providing the secretary with the necessary furniture.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:19 — “As an example of a germane amendment, assume that a motion is pending “that the Society authorize the purchase of a new desk for the Secretary.” It would be germane and in order to amend by inserting after “desk” the words “and matching chair,” since both relate to providing the secretary with the necessary furniture.”

**503.** `[C0312 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, an amendment to add to the motion the words “and the payment of the President’s expenses to the State _____” is not germane.
   A. privileged
   B. committees
   C. Particular
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* D) Convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:19 — “On the other hand, an amendment to add to the motion the words “and the payment of the President’s expenses to the State Convention” is not germane.”

**504.** `[C0312 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As an example of a germane _____, assume that a motion is pending “that the Society authorize the purchase of a new desk for the Secretary.” It would be germane and in order to amend by inserting after “desk” the words “and matching chair,” since both relate to providing the secretary with the necessary furniture.
   A. amendment
   B. delegates
   C. Reporting
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* A) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:19 — “As an example of a germane amendment, assume that a motion is pending “that the Society authorize the purchase of a new desk for the Secretary.” It would be germane and in order to amend by inserting after “desk” the words “and matching chair,” since both relate to providing the secretary with the necessary furniture.”

**505.** `[C0312 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, an amendment to add to the motion the words “and the payment of the President’s expenses to the State _____” is not germane.

   *Answer:* Convention — “On the other hand, an amendment to add to the motion the words “and the payment of the President’s expenses to the State Convention” is not germane.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:19 — “On the other hand, an amendment to add to the motion the words “and the payment of the President’s expenses to the State Convention” is not germane.”

**506.** `[C0313 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Or assume that the following is the pending motion: “that the City Council commend Officer George for his action in…” An amendment to strike out “commend” and insert “censure,” although antagonistic to the original intent, is germane and in _____ because both ideas deal with the council’s opinion of the officer’s action.

   *Answer:* order — “Or assume that the following is the pending motion: “that the City Council commend Officer George for his action in…” An amendment to strike out “commend” and insert “censure,” although antagonistic to the original intent, is germane and in order because both ideas deal with the council’s opinion of the officer’s action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:20 — “Or assume that the following is the pending motion: “that the City Council commend Officer George for his action in…” An amendment to strike out “commend” and insert “censure,” although antagonistic to the original intent, is germane and in order because both ideas deal with the council’s opinion of the officer’s action.”

**507.** `[C0313 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Or assume that the following is the pending motion: “that the City Council _____ Officer George for his action in…” An amendment to strike out “commend” and insert “censure,” although antagonistic to the original intent, is germane and in order because both ideas deal with the council’s opinion of the officer’s action.
   A. commend
   B. approve
   C. Session
   D. applied

   *Answer:* A) commend. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:20 — “Or assume that the following is the pending motion: “that the City Council commend Officer George for his action in…” An amendment to strike out “commend” and insert “censure,” although antagonistic to the original intent, is germane and in order because both ideas deal with the council’s opinion of the officer’s action.”

**508.** `[C0313 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that censure is different from _____ (see Improper Amendments, below).
   A. Request for
   B. not commend
   C. Objection to
   D. was yielded

   *Answer:* B) not commend. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:20 — “It should be noted that censure is different from not commend (see Improper Amendments, below).”

**509.** `[C0313 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that censure is different from not commend (see _____, below).

   *Answer:* Improper Amendments — “It should be noted that censure is different from not commend (see Improper Amendments, below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:20 — “It should be noted that censure is different from not commend (see Improper Amendments, below).”

**510.** `[C0314 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Those of the opinion that the amendment is germane, say _____.

   *Answer:* aye — “Those of the opinion that the amendment is germane, say aye.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:21 — “Those of the opinion that the amendment is germane, say aye.”

**511.** `[C0314 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: There are borderline cases where a presiding officer will find it difficult to judge the _____ of an amendment.
   A. authorizes
   B. germaneness
   C. Preparation
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) germaneness. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:21 — “There are borderline cases where a presiding officer will find it difficult to judge the germaneness of an amendment.”

**512.** `[C0314 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: There are borderline cases where a presiding officer will find it difficult to judge the germaneness of an _____.

   *Answer:* amendment — “There are borderline cases where a presiding officer will find it difficult to judge the germaneness of an amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:21 — “There are borderline cases where a presiding officer will find it difficult to judge the germaneness of an amendment.”

**513.** `[C0314 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: [Debate, if any, provided that debate is in order.] The question is on whether the amendment is germane to the _____ [or “to the primary amendment”].
   A. convention
   B. resolution
   C. Particular
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:21 — “[Debate, if any, provided that debate is in order.] The question is on whether the amendment is germane to the resolution [or “to the primary amendment”].”

**514.** `[C0314 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Those of the opinion that the amendment is germane, say _____.
   A. after
   B. hours
   C. Limit
   D. aye

   *Answer:* D) aye. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:21 — “Those of the opinion that the amendment is germane, say aye.”

**515.** `[C0315 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a _____ has a preamble (one or more clauses beginning “Whereas”), the preamble is not opened to amendment until after amendment of the resolving clauses has been completed.

   *Answer:* resolution — “When a resolution has a preamble (one or more clauses beginning “Whereas”), the preamble is not opened to amendment until after amendment of the resolving clauses has been completed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:23 — “When a resolution has a preamble (one or more clauses beginning “Whereas”), the preamble is not opened to amendment until after amendment of the resolving clauses has been completed.”

**516.** `[C0315 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a _____ has a preamble (one or more clauses beginning “Whereas”), the preamble is not opened to amendment until after amendment of the resolving clauses has been completed.
   A. Unfinished
   B. resolution
   C. Permission
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:23 — “When a resolution has a preamble (one or more clauses beginning “Whereas”), the preamble is not opened to amendment until after amendment of the resolving clauses has been completed.”

**517.** `[C0316 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Because _____ can be moved in different forms, rules pertaining to each form of the motion are given separately below, with examples.

   *Answer:* Amend — “Because Amend can be moved in different forms, rules pertaining to each form of the motion are given separately below, with examples.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:24 — “Because Amend can be moved in different forms, rules pertaining to each form of the motion are given separately below, with examples.”

**518.** `[C0316 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Because Amend can be moved in different _____s, rules pertaining to each form of the motion are given separately below, with examples.
   A. clerk
   B. form
   C. floor
   D. Table

   *Answer:* B) form. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:24 — “Because Amend can be moved in different forms, rules pertaining to each form of the motion are given separately below, with examples.”

**519.** `[C0316 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules for the _____ Forms of Amendment.
   A. execution
   B. auditors’
   C. Signature
   D. Different

   *Answer:* D) Different. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:24 — “Rules for the Different Forms of Amendment.”

**520.** `[C0316 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules for the _____ Forms of Amendment.

   *Answer:* Different — “Rules for the Different Forms of Amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:24 — “Rules for the Different Forms of Amendment.”

**521.** `[C0317 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that many of the rules governing the different forms of amendment are particular applications of the following principle: After the assembly has voted that certain words (or a certain paragraph) shall, or shall not, form part of a pending resolution, it is not in order, during the same session at which that vote was taken, to make another motion to _____ that raises the same question of content and effect.

   *Answer:* Amend — “It should be noted that many of the rules governing the different forms of amendment are particular applications of the following principle: After the assembly has voted that certain words (or a certain paragraph) shall, or shall not, form part of a pending resolution, it is not in order, during the same session at which that vote was taken, to make another motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:25 — “It should be noted that many of the rules governing the different forms of amendment are particular applications of the following principle: After the assembly has voted that certain words (or a certain paragraph) shall, or shall not, form part of a pending resolution, it is not in order, during the same session at which that vote was taken, to make another motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect.”

**522.** `[C0317 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that many of the rules governing the different forms of amendment are particular _____ of the following principle: After the assembly has voted that certain words (or a certain paragraph) shall, or shall not, form part of a pending resolution, it is not in order, during the same session at which that vote was taken, to make another motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect.
   A. applications
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Definitely
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* A) applications. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:25 — “It should be noted that many of the rules governing the different forms of amendment are particular applications of the following principle: After the assembly has voted that certain words (or a certain paragraph) shall, or shall not, form part of a pending resolution, it is not in order, during the same session at which that vote was taken, to make another motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect.”

**523.** `[C0317 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Common sense should guide the presiding officer in _____ the rules, both to give freedom for improvement of the main motion finally to be voted on, and at the same time to protect the assembly from motions for amendment that present questions it has already decided.

   *Answer:* interpreting — “Common sense should guide the presiding officer in interpreting the rules, both to give freedom for improvement of the main motion finally to be voted on, and at the same time to protect the assembly from motions for amendment that present questions it has already decided.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:25 — “Common sense should guide the presiding officer in interpreting the rules, both to give freedom for improvement of the main motion finally to be voted on, and at the same time to protect the assembly from motions for amendment that present questions it has already decided.”

**524.** `[C0317 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Common sense should guide the presiding officer in _____ the rules, both to give freedom for improvement of the main motion finally to be voted on, and at the same time to protect the assembly from motions for amendment that present questions it has already decided.
   A. interpreting
   B. immediately
   C. Organization
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) interpreting. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:25 — “Common sense should guide the presiding officer in interpreting the rules, both to give freedom for improvement of the main motion finally to be voted on, and at the same time to protect the assembly from motions for amendment that present questions it has already decided.”

**525.** `[C0318 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to insert words must specify the exact place of _____ by naming the word before or after which, or the words between which, the insertion is to be made—whichever will better locate and point out the effect of the change.

   *Answer:* insertion — “A motion to insert words must specify the exact place of insertion by naming the word before or after which, or the words between which, the insertion is to be made—whichever will better locate and point out the effect of the change.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:26 — “A motion to insert words must specify the exact place of insertion by naming the word before or after which, or the words between which, the insertion is to be made—whichever will better locate and point out the effect of the change.”

**526.** `[C0318 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For long or printed copies, the line number and paragraph number should be _____.
   A. introduced
   B. Substitute
   C. persuasive
   D. designated

   *Answer:* D) designated. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:26 — “For long or printed copies, the line number and paragraph number should be designated.”

**527.** `[C0318 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to insert words must specify the exact place of _____ by naming the word before or after which, or the words between which, the insertion is to be made—whichever will better locate and point out the effect of the change.
   A. Reporting
   B. debatable
   C. insertion
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* C) insertion. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:26 — “A motion to insert words must specify the exact place of insertion by naming the word before or after which, or the words between which, the insertion is to be made—whichever will better locate and point out the effect of the change.”

**528.** `[C0318 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For long or printed copies, the line number and paragraph number should be _____.

   *Answer:* designated — “For long or printed copies, the line number and paragraph number should be designated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:26 — “For long or printed copies, the line number and paragraph number should be designated.”

**529.** `[C0319 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the consideration of a motion to _____ certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.

   *Answer:* insert — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:27 — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”

**530.** `[C0319 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to _____ certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.
   A. Amend
   B. hours
   C. add
   D. exact

   *Answer:* C) add. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:27 — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”

**531.** `[C0319 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Otherwise there may be no _____ to perfect the inserted or added words, for reasons explained in the next paragraph.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “Otherwise there may be no opportunity to perfect the inserted or added words, for reasons explained in the next paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:27 — “Otherwise there may be no opportunity to perfect the inserted or added words, for reasons explained in the next paragraph.”

**532.** `[C0319 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Otherwise there may be no _____ to perfect the inserted or added words, for reasons explained in the next paragraph.
   A. constitution
   B. Preparation
   C. authorizes
   D. opportunity

   *Answer:* D) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:27 — “Otherwise there may be no opportunity to perfect the inserted or added words, for reasons explained in the next paragraph.”

**533.** `[C0320 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After words have been inserted or added, they cannot be changed or struck out during the same session, except through a _____ of the vote (see 37), or through an amendment presenting a new question in the form of a motion:

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “After words have been inserted or added, they cannot be changed or struck out during the same session, except through a reconsideration of the vote (see 37), or through an amendment presenting a new question in the form of a motion:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:28 — “After words have been inserted or added, they cannot be changed or struck out during the same session, except through a reconsideration of the vote (see 37), or through an amendment presenting a new question in the form of a motion:”

**534.** `[C0320 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After words have been inserted or added, they cannot be changed or struck out during the same session, except through a _____ of the vote (see 37), or through an amendment presenting a new question in the form of a motion:
   A. interrupted
   B. reconsideration
   C. requirement
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:28 — “After words have been inserted or added, they cannot be changed or struck out during the same session, except through a reconsideration of the vote (see 37), or through an amendment presenting a new question in the form of a motion:”

**535.** `[C0321 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to insert certain words in a particular place is voted down, it is still in order—provided that it will present an _____ new question—to make a motion:

   *Answer:* essentially — “If a motion to insert certain words in a particular place is voted down, it is still in order—provided that it will present an essentially new question—to make a motion:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:29 — “If a motion to insert certain words in a particular place is voted down, it is still in order—provided that it will present an essentially new question—to make a motion:”

**536.** `[C0321 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to insert certain words in a particular place is voted down, it is still in order—provided that it will present an _____ new question—to make a motion:
   A. essentially
   B. Definitely
   C. Information
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) essentially. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:29 — “If a motion to insert certain words in a particular place is voted down, it is still in order—provided that it will present an essentially new question—to make a motion:”

**537.** `[C0322 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to insert, or to add, words can have applied to it secondary _____ in any of the three forms relating to words—inserting or adding;

   *Answer:* amendments — “A motion to insert, or to add, words can have applied to it secondary amendments in any of the three forms relating to words—inserting or adding;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:30 — “A motion to insert, or to add, words can have applied to it secondary amendments in any of the three forms relating to words—inserting or adding;”

**538.** `[C0322 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to insert, or to add, words can have applied to it secondary _____ in any of the three forms relating to words—inserting or adding;
   A. amendments
   B. Permission
   C. Convention
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:30 — “A motion to insert, or to add, words can have applied to it secondary amendments in any of the three forms relating to words—inserting or adding;”

**539.** `[C0323 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rules for the insertion or addition of a paragraph are _____ the same as those given above, except that after a paragraph has been inserted, words can still be added to it (that is, placed at the end of the paragraph only) provided that they do not conflict with or modify anything in the paragraph as already inserted.

   *Answer:* essentially — “The rules for the insertion or addition of a paragraph are essentially the same as those given above, except that after a paragraph has been inserted, words can still be added to it (that is, placed at the end of the paragraph only) provided that they do not conflict with or modify anything in the paragraph as already inserted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:31 — “The rules for the insertion or addition of a paragraph are essentially the same as those given above, except that after a paragraph has been inserted, words can still be added to it (that is, placed at the end of the paragraph only) provided that they do not conflict with or modify anything in the paragraph as already inserted.”

**540.** `[C0323 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a paragraph is to be inserted or added, any necessary _____ should first be done by secondary amendments.
   A. introduced
   B. assemblies
   C. sustaining
   D. perfecting

   *Answer:* D) perfecting. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:31 — “When a paragraph is to be inserted or added, any necessary perfecting should first be done by secondary amendments.”

**541.** `[C0323 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After its insertion or addition, the paragraph cannot be struck out except in _____ with other paragraphs that make the question materially different.

   *Answer:* connection — “After its insertion or addition, the paragraph cannot be struck out except in connection with other paragraphs that make the question materially different.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:31 — “After its insertion or addition, the paragraph cannot be struck out except in connection with other paragraphs that make the question materially different.”

**542.** `[C0323 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rules for the insertion or addition of a _____ are essentially the same as those given above, except that after a paragraph has been inserted, words can still be added to it (that is, placed at the end of the paragraph only) provided that they do not conflict with or modify anything in the paragraph as already inserted.
   A. delegates
   B. Secretary
   C. postponed
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* D) paragraph. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:31 — “The rules for the insertion or addition of a paragraph are essentially the same as those given above, except that after a paragraph has been inserted, words can still be added to it (that is, placed at the end of the paragraph only) provided that they do not conflict with or modify anything in the paragraph as already inserted.”

**543.** `[C0323 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a paragraph is to be inserted or added, any necessary _____ should first be done by secondary amendments.

   *Answer:* perfecting — “When a paragraph is to be inserted or added, any necessary perfecting should first be done by secondary amendments.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:31 — “When a paragraph is to be inserted or added, any necessary perfecting should first be done by secondary amendments.”

**545.** `[C0324 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Typical forms in which a motion to insert, or to add, may be made are: “I move to amend the _____ by inserting the word ‘waterfront’ before the word ‘property’”;
   A. convention
   B. privileged
   C. sustaining
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:32 — “Typical forms in which a motion to insert, or to add, may be made are: “I move to amend the resolution by inserting the word ‘waterfront’ before the word ‘property’”;”

**546.** `[C0324 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: “I move to insert ‘plus _____’ after ‘$100’”;

   *Answer:* expenses — ““I move to insert ‘plus expenses’ after ‘$100’”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:32 — ““I move to insert ‘plus expenses’ after ‘$100’”;”

**547.** `[C0324 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: “I move to insert ‘_____’ between ‘all’ and ‘employees’”;
   A. permanent
   B. Signature
   C. Committee
   D. delegates

   *Answer:* A) permanent. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:32 — ““I move to insert ‘permanent’ between ‘all’ and ‘employees’”;”

**548.** `[C0324 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: “I move to insert after Paragraph 3 the _____ paragraph:…”;

   *Answer:* following — ““I move to insert after Paragraph 3 the following paragraph:…”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:32 — ““I move to insert after Paragraph 3 the following paragraph:…”;”

**549.** `[C0325 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In stating the question on each motion in any series involving amendments, and again when putting the motion to a vote, the chair should take care that the members understand which motion is under immediate _____, as well as the effect of adopting or rejecting it.

   *Answer:* consideration — “In stating the question on each motion in any series involving amendments, and again when putting the motion to a vote, the chair should take care that the members understand which motion is under immediate consideration, as well as the effect of adopting or rejecting it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:33 — “In stating the question on each motion in any series involving amendments, and again when putting the motion to a vote, the chair should take care that the members understand which motion is under immediate consideration, as well as the effect of adopting or rejecting it.”

**550.** `[C0325 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In stating the question on each motion in any series involving amendments, and again when putting the motion to a vote, the chair should take care that the members understand which motion is under immediate _____, as well as the effect of adopting or rejecting it.
   A. resolutions
   B. consideration
   C. Organization
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:33 — “In stating the question on each motion in any series involving amendments, and again when putting the motion to a vote, the chair should take care that the members understand which motion is under immediate consideration, as well as the effect of adopting or rejecting it.”

   *Answer:* amendment — “Therefore, when stating the question on an amendment, the chair may find it advisable to employ three steps:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:34 — “Therefore, when stating the question on an amendment, the chair may find it advisable to employ three steps:”

   A. auditors’

   D. amendment

**555.** `[C0328 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Similarly, when putting to a vote the question on an amendment, if debate or _____ of other motions has intervened since the amendment was stated, the chair may find it advisable to employ a comparable set of three steps:

   *Answer:* consideration — “Similarly, when putting to a vote the question on an amendment, if debate or consideration of other motions has intervened since the amendment was stated, the chair may find it advisable to employ a comparable set of three steps:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:36 — “Similarly, when putting to a vote the question on an amendment, if debate or consideration of other motions has intervened since the amendment was stated, the chair may find it advisable to employ a comparable set of three steps:”

**556.** `[C0328 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Similarly, when putting to a vote the question on an amendment, if debate or _____ of other motions has intervened since the amendment was stated, the chair may find it advisable to employ a comparable set of three steps:
   A. adjournment
   B. specification
   C. constitution
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:36 — “Similarly, when putting to a vote the question on an amendment, if debate or consideration of other motions has intervened since the amendment was stated, the chair may find it advisable to employ a comparable set of three steps:”

**557.** `[C0329 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After taking the vote, the chair announces the result of the vote on the amendment, and then states the question that _____ becomes immediately pending:

   *Answer:* consequently — “After taking the vote, the chair announces the result of the vote on the amendment, and then states the question that consequently becomes immediately pending:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:37 — “After taking the vote, the chair announces the result of the vote on the amendment, and then states the question that consequently becomes immediately pending:”

**558.** `[C0329 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After taking the vote, the chair announces the result of the vote on the amendment, and then states the question that _____ becomes immediately pending:
   A. undebatable
   B. consequently
   C. resolutions
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) consequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:37 — “After taking the vote, the chair announces the result of the vote on the amendment, and then states the question that consequently becomes immediately pending:”

**559.** `[C0330 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At this point, _____ of whether the amendment is adopted or rejected, the resolution is again open to debate, and it is in order to offer a different amendment.

   *Answer:* regardless — “At this point, regardless of whether the amendment is adopted or rejected, the resolution is again open to debate, and it is in order to offer a different amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:39 — “At this point, regardless of whether the amendment is adopted or rejected, the resolution is again open to debate, and it is in order to offer a different amendment.”

**560.** `[C0330 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At this point, _____ of whether the amendment is adopted or rejected, the resolution is again open to debate, and it is in order to offer a different amendment.
   A. persuasive
   B. Permission
   C. regardless
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) regardless. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:39 — “At this point, regardless of whether the amendment is adopted or rejected, the resolution is again open to debate, and it is in order to offer a different amendment.”

**561.** `[C0331 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, in circumstances of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ _____ steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);

   *Answer:* four — “On the other hand, in circumstances of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ four steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:40 — “On the other hand, in circumstances of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ four steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);”

**562.** `[C0331 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some cases—depending on the length of the resolution and the _____, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.
   A. execution
   B. amendment
   C. Privilege
   D. Reporting

   *Answer:* B) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:40 — “In some cases—depending on the length of the resolution and the amendment, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.”

**563.** `[C0331 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some cases—depending on the length of the _____ and the amendment, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.

   *Answer:* resolution — “In some cases—depending on the length of the resolution and the amendment, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:40 — “In some cases—depending on the length of the resolution and the amendment, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.”

**564.** `[C0331 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, in _____ of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ four steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);
   A. circumstances
   B. recommendation
   C. interrupted
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* A) circumstances. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:40 — “On the other hand, in circumstances of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ four steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);”

**565.** `[C0332 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and (2) (immediately pending) a primary amendment, “to add the words, ‘and convert it into a parking lot.’” (Details as to probable costs of proposed phases of the project are assumed to be known.) MEMBER X (obtaining the floor): I move to insert in the pending amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” (Second.) _____: It is moved and seconded to amend by inserting in the primary amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” If the word is inserted, the primary amendment will be, “to add to the resolution for the purchase of the property, the words ‘and convert it into a landscaped parking lot.’” The question is on inserting the word “landscaped.”

   *Answer:* CHAIR — “and (2) (immediately pending) a primary amendment, “to add the words, ‘and convert it into a parking lot.’” (Details as to probable costs of proposed phases of the project are assumed to be known.) MEMBER X (obtaining the floor): I move to insert in the pending amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” (Second.) CHAIR: It is moved and seconded to amend by inserting in the primary amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” If the word is inserted, the primary amendment will be, “to add to the resolution for the purchase of the property, the words ‘and convert it into a landscaped parking lot.’” The question is on inserting the word “landscaped.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:41 — “and (2) (immediately pending) a primary amendment, “to add the words, ‘and convert it into a parking lot.’” (Details as to probable costs of proposed phases of the project are assumed to be known.) MEMBER X (obtaining the floor): I move to insert in the pending amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” (Second.) CHAIR: It is moved and seconded to amend by inserting in the primary amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” If the word is inserted, the primary amendment will be, “to add to the resolution for the purchase of the property, the words ‘and convert it into a landscaped parking lot.’” The question is on inserting the word “landscaped.””

**566.** `[C0332 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As an example of _____, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;
   A. Other limitations or extensions
   B. Platforms or Policy Statements
   C. Electronic Meetings in Committees
   D. secondary amendment by inserting

   *Answer:* D) secondary amendment by inserting. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:41 — “As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;”

**567.** `[C0332 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “_____, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;

   *Answer:* Resolved — “As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:41 — “As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;”

**568.** `[C0332 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and (2) (immediately pending) a primary amendment, “to add the words, ‘and convert it into a parking lot.’” (Details as to probable costs of proposed phases of the project are assumed to be known.) MEMBER X (obtaining the floor): I move to insert in the pending amendment the word “_____” before “parking lot.” (Second.) CHAIR: It is moved and seconded to amend by inserting in the primary amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” If the word is inserted, the primary amendment will be, “to add to the resolution for the purchase of the property, the words ‘and convert it into a landscaped parking lot.’” The question is on inserting the word “landscaped.”
   A. authorizes
   B. sustaining
   C. Substitute
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* D) landscaped. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:41 — “and (2) (immediately pending) a primary amendment, “to add the words, ‘and convert it into a parking lot.’” (Details as to probable costs of proposed phases of the project are assumed to be known.) MEMBER X (obtaining the floor): I move to insert in the pending amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” (Second.) CHAIR: It is moved and seconded to amend by inserting in the primary amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” If the word is inserted, the primary amendment will be, “to add to the resolution for the purchase of the property, the words ‘and convert it into a landscaped parking lot.’” The question is on inserting the word “landscaped.””

**569.** `[C0333 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that in the preceding example the chair gives the _____ amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.

   *Answer:* primary — “It should be noted that in the preceding example the chair gives the primary amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:42 — “It should be noted that in the preceding example the chair gives the primary amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.”

**570.** `[C0333 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that in the _____ example the chair gives the primary amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.
   A. exhausted
   B. preceding
   C. extending
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* B) preceding. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:42 — “It should be noted that in the preceding example the chair gives the primary amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.”

**571.** `[C0334 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debate, if any, must be confined to the issue: If the _____ to a parking lot is to be included in the project at this time, should the lot be landscaped? When this debate has ended, the chair puts the secondary amendment to a vote, announces the result, and states the question before the assembly, as follows:

   *Answer:* conversion — “Debate, if any, must be confined to the issue: If the conversion to a parking lot is to be included in the project at this time, should the lot be landscaped? When this debate has ended, the chair puts the secondary amendment to a vote, announces the result, and states the question before the assembly, as follows:”.
