See RONR (12th ed.) §12:43 — “Debate, if any, must be confined to the issue: If the conversion to a parking lot is to be included in the project at this time, should the lot be landscaped? When this debate has ended, the chair puts the secondary amendment to a vote, announces the result, and states the question before the assembly, as follows:”

**572.** `[C0334 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Debate, if any, must be confined to the issue: If the _____ to a parking lot is to be included in the project at this time, should the lot be landscaped? When this debate has ended, the chair puts the secondary amendment to a vote, announces the result, and states the question before the assembly, as follows:
   A. landscaped
   B. Incidental
   C. conversion
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* C) conversion. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:43 — “Debate, if any, must be confined to the issue: If the conversion to a parking lot is to be included in the project at this time, should the lot be landscaped? When this debate has ended, the chair puts the secondary amendment to a vote, announces the result, and states the question before the assembly, as follows:”

**573.** `[C0335 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, assume that while a main motion is pending, “That the _____ Committee be directed to secure estimates for the necessary building repairs,” a member moves “to amend by inserting the words ‘at least three’ before ‘estimates.’” If the chair senses that there is general approval, he may say, “If there is no objection, the words ‘at least three’ will be inserted.

   *Answer:* Properties — “For example, assume that while a main motion is pending, “That the Properties Committee be directed to secure estimates for the necessary building repairs,” a member moves “to amend by inserting the words ‘at least three’ before ‘estimates.’” If the chair senses that there is general approval, he may say, “If there is no objection, the words ‘at least three’ will be inserted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:45 — “For example, assume that while a main motion is pending, “That the Properties Committee be directed to secure estimates for the necessary building repairs,” a member moves “to amend by inserting the words ‘at least three’ before ‘estimates.’” If the chair senses that there is general approval, he may say, “If there is no objection, the words ‘at least three’ will be inserted.”

**574.** `[C0335 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, assume that while a main motion is pending, “That the Properties Committee be directed to secure _____ for the necessary building repairs,” a member moves “to amend by inserting the words ‘at least three’ before ‘estimates.’” If the chair senses that there is general approval, he may say, “If there is no objection, the words ‘at least three’ will be inserted.
   A. committee
   B. estimates
   C. expressly
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* B) estimates. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:45 — “For example, assume that while a main motion is pending, “That the Properties Committee be directed to secure estimates for the necessary building repairs,” a member moves “to amend by inserting the words ‘at least three’ before ‘estimates.’” If the chair senses that there is general approval, he may say, “If there is no objection, the words ‘at least three’ will be inserted.”

**575.** `[C0336 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to strike out words must specify their location when it is not _____ clear.

   *Answer:* otherwise — “A motion to strike out words must specify their location when it is not otherwise clear.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:46 — “A motion to strike out words must specify their location when it is not otherwise clear.”

**576.** `[C0336 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to strike out words must specify their location when it is not _____ clear.
   A. reception
   B. exhausted
   C. otherwise
   D. extending

   *Answer:* C) otherwise. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:46 — “A motion to strike out words must specify their location when it is not otherwise clear.”

**577.** `[C0337 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion to strike out certain words is made, it can be applied only to _____ words;

   *Answer:* consecutive — “When a motion to strike out certain words is made, it can be applied only to consecutive words;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:47 — “When a motion to strike out certain words is made, it can be applied only to consecutive words;”

**578.** `[C0337 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but the words to be struck out may become separated as a result of secondary _____, in which case the primary amendment is voted on in a form in which it could not have been moved directly.
   A. authorizes
   B. sustaining
   C. recognized
   D. amendments

   *Answer:* D) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:47 — “but the words to be struck out may become separated as a result of secondary amendments, in which case the primary amendment is voted on in a form in which it could not have been moved directly.”

**579.** `[C0337 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To strike out separated words, the best method is to make a motion to strike out the entire clause or sentence _____ the separated words and insert a new clause or sentence as desired.

   *Answer:* containing — “To strike out separated words, the best method is to make a motion to strike out the entire clause or sentence containing the separated words and insert a new clause or sentence as desired.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:47 — “To strike out separated words, the best method is to make a motion to strike out the entire clause or sentence containing the separated words and insert a new clause or sentence as desired.”

**580.** `[C0337 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion to strike out certain words is made, it can be applied only to _____ words;
   A. Definitely
   B. Particular
   C. consecutive
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) consecutive. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:47 — “When a motion to strike out certain words is made, it can be applied only to consecutive words;”

**581.** `[C0337 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but the words to be struck out may become separated as a result of secondary _____, in which case the primary amendment is voted on in a form in which it could not have been moved directly.

   *Answer:* amendments — “but the words to be struck out may become separated as a result of secondary amendments, in which case the primary amendment is voted on in a form in which it could not have been moved directly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:47 — “but the words to be struck out may become separated as a result of secondary amendments, in which case the primary amendment is voted on in a form in which it could not have been moved directly.”

**582.** `[C0338 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to strike out certain words is adopted, the same words cannot be inserted again during the same session unless the place or the wording is so changed as to make a new _____.

   *Answer:* proposition — “If a motion to strike out certain words is adopted, the same words cannot be inserted again during the same session unless the place or the wording is so changed as to make a new proposition.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:48 — “If a motion to strike out certain words is adopted, the same words cannot be inserted again during the same session unless the place or the wording is so changed as to make a new proposition.”

**583.** `[C0338 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to strike out certain words fails, it is still in order—subject to the _____ that the words involved must be consecutive—to move:
   A. requirement
   B. Reconsider
   C. assemblies
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) requirement. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:48 — “If a motion to strike out certain words fails, it is still in order—subject to the requirement that the words involved must be consecutive—to move:”

**584.** `[C0338 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to strike out certain words is adopted, the same words cannot be inserted again during the same session unless the place or the wording is so changed as to make a new _____.
   A. immediately
   B. proposition
   C. Information
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) proposition. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:48 — “If a motion to strike out certain words is adopted, the same words cannot be inserted again during the same session unless the place or the wording is so changed as to make a new proposition.”

**585.** `[C0338 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to strike out certain words fails, it is still in order—subject to the _____ that the words involved must be consecutive—to move:

   *Answer:* requirement — “If a motion to strike out certain words fails, it is still in order—subject to the requirement that the words involved must be consecutive—to move:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:48 — “If a motion to strike out certain words fails, it is still in order—subject to the requirement that the words involved must be consecutive—to move:”

**586.** `[C0339 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words _____.

   *Answer:* from the primary amendment — “It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:49 — “It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.”

**587.** `[C0339 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is _____ to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.
   A. important
   B. Signature
   C. exhausted
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* A) important. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:49 — “It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.”

**588.** `[C0339 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The effect of such a secondary amendment is that words struck out of the primary amendment will remain in the main motion _____ of whether the primary amendment is adopted or rejected.

   *Answer:* regardless — “The effect of such a secondary amendment is that words struck out of the primary amendment will remain in the main motion regardless of whether the primary amendment is adopted or rejected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:49 — “The effect of such a secondary amendment is that words struck out of the primary amendment will remain in the main motion regardless of whether the primary amendment is adopted or rejected.”

**589.** `[C0339 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, assume that the following are pending: (1) a main motion directing the secretary to write to _____ Altman, Brock, Crowley, Davidson, and Edwards;
   A. Substitute
   B. Congressmen
   C. convention
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) Congressmen. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:49 — “For example, assume that the following are pending: (1) a main motion directing the secretary to write to Congressmen Altman, Brock, Crowley, Davidson, and Edwards;”

**590.** `[C0339 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The effect of such a _____ amendment is that words struck out of the primary amendment will remain in the main motion regardless of whether the primary amendment is adopted or rejected.
   A. committee
   B. Precedent
   C. postponed
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* D) secondary. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:49 — “The effect of such a secondary amendment is that words struck out of the primary amendment will remain in the main motion regardless of whether the primary amendment is adopted or rejected.”

**591.** `[C0340 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion is made to strike out a sentence that might desirably be retained with some changes, the form of secondary amendment allowed for motions to strike out certain words may not be readily _____.

   *Answer:* applicable — “When a motion is made to strike out a sentence that might desirably be retained with some changes, the form of secondary amendment allowed for motions to strike out certain words may not be readily applicable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:50 — “When a motion is made to strike out a sentence that might desirably be retained with some changes, the form of secondary amendment allowed for motions to strike out certain words may not be readily applicable.”

**592.** `[C0340 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion is made to strike out a sentence that might desirably be retained with some changes, the form of secondary _____ allowed for motions to strike out certain words may not be readily applicable.
   A. delegates
   B. debatable
   C. amendment
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* C) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:50 — “When a motion is made to strike out a sentence that might desirably be retained with some changes, the form of secondary amendment allowed for motions to strike out certain words may not be readily applicable.”

**593.** `[C0341 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: There is an essential difference between the motion to strike out a paragraph and the motion to strike out certain words, which lies in the rules and effect of _____ amendment.

   *Answer:* secondary — “There is an essential difference between the motion to strike out a paragraph and the motion to strike out certain words, which lies in the rules and effect of secondary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “There is an essential difference between the motion to strike out a paragraph and the motion to strike out certain words, which lies in the rules and effect of secondary amendment.”

**594.** `[C0341 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the primary amendment to strike out is voted down, the paragraph then remains in the resolution with any changes that were _____.
   A. Procedure of Consideration
   B. Basis of This Classification
   C. made by secondary amendment
   D. Agenda Provided in Advance

   *Answer:* C) made by secondary amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “If the primary amendment to strike out is voted down, the paragraph then remains in the resolution with any changes that were made by secondary amendment.”

**595.** `[C0341 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: There is an essential _____ between the motion to strike out a paragraph and the motion to strike out certain words, which lies in the rules and effect of secondary amendment.

   *Answer:* difference — “There is an essential difference between the motion to strike out a paragraph and the motion to strike out certain words, which lies in the rules and effect of secondary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “There is an essential difference between the motion to strike out a paragraph and the motion to strike out certain words, which lies in the rules and effect of secondary amendment.”

**596.** `[C0341 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is moved to strike out an entire paragraph, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to _____ by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (inserting or adding;
   A. improvement
   B. Indefinitely
   C. adjournment
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) improvement. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “When it is moved to strike out an entire paragraph, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to improvement by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (inserting or adding;”

**597.** `[C0341 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is moved to strike out an entire paragraph, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to improvement by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (_____ or adding;

   *Answer:* inserting — “When it is moved to strike out an entire paragraph, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to improvement by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (inserting or adding;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “When it is moved to strike out an entire paragraph, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to improvement by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (inserting or adding;”

**598.** `[C0342 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a paragraph has been struck out, it cannot be inserted again unless the wording (or, possibly, the place) is changed in a way that presents an _____ new question.

   *Answer:* essentially — “After a paragraph has been struck out, it cannot be inserted again unless the wording (or, possibly, the place) is changed in a way that presents an essentially new question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:52 — “After a paragraph has been struck out, it cannot be inserted again unless the wording (or, possibly, the place) is changed in a way that presents an essentially new question.”

**599.** `[C0342 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a motion to strike out a _____ paragraph has been voted down, any amendment presenting a materially new question involving the same paragraph, or any part of it, is still in order.
   A. particular
   B. Definitely
   C. Incidental
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:52 — “After a motion to strike out a particular paragraph has been voted down, any amendment presenting a materially new question involving the same paragraph, or any part of it, is still in order.”

**600.** `[C0342 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a paragraph has been struck out, it cannot be inserted again unless the wording (or, possibly, the place) is changed in a way that presents an _____ new question.
   A. constitution
   B. essentially
   C. immediately
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) essentially. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:52 — “After a paragraph has been struck out, it cannot be inserted again unless the wording (or, possibly, the place) is changed in a way that presents an essentially new question.”

**601.** `[C0342 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a motion to strike out a _____ paragraph has been voted down, any amendment presenting a materially new question involving the same paragraph, or any part of it, is still in order.

   *Answer:* particular — “After a motion to strike out a particular paragraph has been voted down, any amendment presenting a materially new question involving the same paragraph, or any part of it, is still in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:52 — “After a motion to strike out a particular paragraph has been voted down, any amendment presenting a materially new question involving the same paragraph, or any part of it, is still in order.”

**602.** `[C0343 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to strike out may be made in such forms as “I move to strike out the word ‘_____’ before ‘pavement’ in Line 5”;

   *Answer:* concrete — “The motion to strike out may be made in such forms as “I move to strike out the word ‘concrete’ before ‘pavement’ in Line 5”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:53 — “The motion to strike out may be made in such forms as “I move to strike out the word ‘concrete’ before ‘pavement’ in Line 5”;”

**603.** `[C0343 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to strike out may be made in such forms as “I move to strike out the word ‘_____’ before ‘pavement’ in Line 5”;
   A. chairman
   B. concrete
   C. Assembly
   D. Interest

   *Answer:* B) concrete. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:53 — “The motion to strike out may be made in such forms as “I move to strike out the word ‘concrete’ before ‘pavement’ in Line 5”;”

**604.** `[C0344 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume that the _____ main motion is pending: “That the Bowling League establish a division open to the juniors and seniors of Southwood High School.”

   *Answer:* following — “Assume that the following main motion is pending: “That the Bowling League establish a division open to the juniors and seniors of Southwood High School.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:54 — “Assume that the following main motion is pending: “That the Bowling League establish a division open to the juniors and seniors of Southwood High School.””

**605.** `[C0344 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that the _____ main motion is pending: “That the Bowling League establish a division open to the juniors and seniors of Southwood High School.”
   A. following
   B. committee
   C. Secretary
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* A) following. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:54 — “Assume that the following main motion is pending: “That the Bowling League establish a division open to the juniors and seniors of Southwood High School.””

**606.** `[C0345 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: From this point, the procedure is similar to that already _____ for inserting or adding.

   *Answer:* illustrated — “From this point, the procedure is similar to that already illustrated for inserting or adding.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:55 — “From this point, the procedure is similar to that already illustrated for inserting or adding.”

**607.** `[C0345 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: From this point, the procedure is similar to that already _____ for inserting or adding.
   A. undebatable
   B. Reconsider
   C. Convention
   D. illustrated

   *Answer:* D) illustrated. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:55 — “From this point, the procedure is similar to that already illustrated for inserting or adding.”

**608.** `[C0346 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The two parts of this motion cannot be separated, either by secondary amendment or by a _____.

   *Answer:* Division of a Question — “The two parts of this motion cannot be separated, either by secondary amendment or by a Division of a Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:56 — “The two parts of this motion cannot be separated, either by secondary amendment or by a Division of a Question.”

**609.** `[C0346 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To Strike Out and Insert (_____ to words).
   A. applying
   B. limiting
   C. majority
   D. Approved

   *Answer:* A) applying. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:56 — “To Strike Out and Insert (applying to words).”

**610.** `[C0346 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to strike out and insert is especially applicable in situations where it may be impossible to secure the desired result without making the act of “striking out” _____ from that of “inserting”—as may happen if some members are unwilling to vote for the one unless assured of the other.

   *Answer:* inseparable — “The motion to strike out and insert is especially applicable in situations where it may be impossible to secure the desired result without making the act of “striking out” inseparable from that of “inserting”—as may happen if some members are unwilling to vote for the one unless assured of the other.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:56 — “The motion to strike out and insert is especially applicable in situations where it may be impossible to secure the desired result without making the act of “striking out” inseparable from that of “inserting”—as may happen if some members are unwilling to vote for the one unless assured of the other.”

**611.** `[C0346 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The two parts of this motion cannot be _____, either by secondary amendment or by a Division of a Question.
   A. Precedent
   B. amendment
   C. extending
   D. separated

   *Answer:* D) separated. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:56 — “The two parts of this motion cannot be separated, either by secondary amendment or by a Division of a Question.”

**612.** `[C0346 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To Strike Out and Insert (_____ to words).

   *Answer:* applying — “To Strike Out and Insert (applying to words).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:56 — “To Strike Out and Insert (applying to words).”

**613.** `[C0348 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The two parts of the motion to strike out and insert must not represent two independent questions, unless the mover receives _____ consent to make such a combined motion;

   *Answer:* unanimous — “The two parts of the motion to strike out and insert must not represent two independent questions, unless the mover receives unanimous consent to make such a combined motion;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:59 — “The two parts of the motion to strike out and insert must not represent two independent questions, unless the mover receives unanimous consent to make such a combined motion;”

**614.** `[C0348 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The two parts of the motion to strike out and insert must not represent two _____ questions, unless the mover receives unanimous consent to make such a combined motion;
   A. authorizes
   B. independent
   C. Permission
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) independent. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:59 — “The two parts of the motion to strike out and insert must not represent two independent questions, unless the mover receives unanimous consent to make such a combined motion;”

**615.** `[C0348 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: rather, the two parts must be _____ to each other.

   *Answer:* germane — “rather, the two parts must be germane to each other.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:59 — “rather, the two parts must be germane to each other.”

**616.** `[C0348 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, in motions of the first type _____ in the previous paragraph—to strike words out of and insert words into the same place—the words to be inserted must in some way be related to, or address the same issue as, the words to be struck out;
   A. amendment
   B. Precedent
   C. mentioned
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* C) mentioned. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:59 — “Thus, in motions of the first type mentioned in the previous paragraph—to strike words out of and insert words into the same place—the words to be inserted must in some way be related to, or address the same issue as, the words to be struck out;”

**617.** `[C0348 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and in motions of the second type—to strike words out of one place and insert words into a different place—the words to be inserted must not be _____ different from the words to be struck out.

   *Answer:* materially — “and in motions of the second type—to strike words out of one place and insert words into a different place—the words to be inserted must not be materially different from the words to be struck out.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:59 — “and in motions of the second type—to strike words out of one place and insert words into a different place—the words to be inserted must not be materially different from the words to be struck out.”

**618.** `[C0349 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The first type of the motion to strike out and insert—the kind that proposes to insert a _____ wording in the same place—is perhaps more common.

   *Answer:* different — “The first type of the motion to strike out and insert—the kind that proposes to insert a different wording in the same place—is perhaps more common.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:60 — “The first type of the motion to strike out and insert—the kind that proposes to insert a different wording in the same place—is perhaps more common.”

**619.** `[C0349 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The first type of the motion to strike out and insert—the kind that proposes to insert a _____ wording in the same place—is perhaps more common.
   A. president
   B. Committee
   C. different
   D. committee

   *Answer:* C) different. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:60 — “The first type of the motion to strike out and insert—the kind that proposes to insert a different wording in the same place—is perhaps more common.”

**620.** `[C0350 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When such a primary _____ is offered, the chair states the question on it and lets debate of its merits begin in the usual way.

   *Answer:* amendment — “When such a primary amendment is offered, the chair states the question on it and lets debate of its merits begin in the usual way.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:61 — “When such a primary amendment is offered, the chair states the question on it and lets debate of its merits begin in the usual way.”

**621.** `[C0350 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For purposes of _____ amendment, however, this type of motion is treated as if resolved into its two elements, with secondary amendment of each element following the rules that would apply to two separate motions for primary amendment—one to strike out (amendable only by striking out) and another to insert (amendable in any of three forms relating to words).
   A. execution
   B. exhausted
   C. Crestwood
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* D) secondary. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:61 — “For purposes of secondary amendment, however, this type of motion is treated as if resolved into its two elements, with secondary amendment of each element following the rules that would apply to two separate motions for primary amendment—one to strike out (amendable only by striking out) and another to insert (amendable in any of three forms relating to words).”

**622.** `[C0350 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to strike out and insert involves enough words that several secondary amendments— _____ to the words to be struck out—might be possible, it is often best to take any amendments to the words to be struck out first, because members who wish to perfect the words to be inserted by secondary amendment may need to know exactly what language those words will replace, to be able to perfect them effectively.

   *Answer:* particularly — “If a motion to strike out and insert involves enough words that several secondary amendments— particularly to the words to be struck out—might be possible, it is often best to take any amendments to the words to be struck out first, because members who wish to perfect the words to be inserted by secondary amendment may need to know exactly what language those words will replace, to be able to perfect them effectively.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:61 — “If a motion to strike out and insert involves enough words that several secondary amendments— particularly to the words to be struck out—might be possible, it is often best to take any amendments to the words to be struck out first, because members who wish to perfect the words to be inserted by secondary amendment may need to know exactly what language those words will replace, to be able to perfect them effectively.”

**623.** `[C0350 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to strike out and insert involves enough words that several secondary _____— particularly to the words to be struck out—might be possible, it is often best to take any amendments to the words to be struck out first, because members who wish to perfect the words to be inserted by secondary amendment may need to know exactly what language those words will replace, to be able to perfect them effectively.
   A. resolution
   B. Procedures
   C. committees
   D. amendments

   *Answer:* D) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:61 — “If a motion to strike out and insert involves enough words that several secondary amendments— particularly to the words to be struck out—might be possible, it is often best to take any amendments to the words to be struck out first, because members who wish to perfect the words to be inserted by secondary amendment may need to know exactly what language those words will replace, to be able to perfect them effectively.”

**624.** `[C0350 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But the chair should make a judgment depending on the _____ as to whether the assembly wishes to follow this procedure or will find it helpful.

   *Answer:* conditions — “But the chair should make a judgment depending on the conditions as to whether the assembly wishes to follow this procedure or will find it helpful.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:61 — “But the chair should make a judgment depending on the conditions as to whether the assembly wishes to follow this procedure or will find it helpful.”

**625.** `[C0351 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While a primary amendment to strike out and insert certain words is pending, if an _____ secondary amendment to the words to be struck out is introduced and no amendments to the words to be inserted have been proposed, the chair simply proceeds to entertain the secondary amendment, letting debate on the primary amendment resume after the secondary amendment has been dealt with.

   *Answer:* admissible — “While a primary amendment to strike out and insert certain words is pending, if an admissible secondary amendment to the words to be struck out is introduced and no amendments to the words to be inserted have been proposed, the chair simply proceeds to entertain the secondary amendment, letting debate on the primary amendment resume after the secondary amendment has been dealt with.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:62 — “While a primary amendment to strike out and insert certain words is pending, if an admissible secondary amendment to the words to be struck out is introduced and no amendments to the words to be inserted have been proposed, the chair simply proceeds to entertain the secondary amendment, letting debate on the primary amendment resume after the secondary amendment has been dealt with.”

**626.** `[C0351 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While a primary amendment to strike out and insert certain words is pending, if an admissible secondary amendment to the words to be struck out is introduced and no _____ to the words to be inserted have been proposed, the chair simply proceeds to entertain the secondary amendment, letting debate on the primary amendment resume after the secondary amendment has been dealt with.
   A. Convention
   B. Particular
   C. resolution
   D. amendments

   *Answer:* D) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:62 — “While a primary amendment to strike out and insert certain words is pending, if an admissible secondary amendment to the words to be struck out is introduced and no amendments to the words to be inserted have been proposed, the chair simply proceeds to entertain the secondary amendment, letting debate on the primary amendment resume after the secondary amendment has been dealt with.”

**627.** `[C0351 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Accordingly, before _____ the amendment just offered, the chair will ask: Are there any amendments [or “any further amendments”] to the words to be struck out?” When it appears that no one else wishes to propose an amendment to the words to be struck out, the chair then says: “The primary amendment as amended is open to debate, and secondary amendments to the words to be inserted are now in order.” After reasonable opportunity to offer any amendments of the latter type has been given, secondary amendments to either element are in order.

   *Answer:* entertaining — “Accordingly, before entertaining the amendment just offered, the chair will ask: Are there any amendments [or “any further amendments”] to the words to be struck out?” When it appears that no one else wishes to propose an amendment to the words to be struck out, the chair then says: “The primary amendment as amended is open to debate, and secondary amendments to the words to be inserted are now in order.” After reasonable opportunity to offer any amendments of the latter type has been given, secondary amendments to either element are in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:62 — “Accordingly, before entertaining the amendment just offered, the chair will ask: Are there any amendments [or “any further amendments”] to the words to be struck out?” When it appears that no one else wishes to propose an amendment to the words to be struck out, the chair then says: “The primary amendment as amended is open to debate, and secondary amendments to the words to be inserted are now in order.” After reasonable opportunity to offer any amendments of the latter type has been given, secondary amendments to either element are in order.”

**628.** `[C0351 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When these and any further debate have _____, the vote is taken on the motion to strike out and insert as it stands after secondary amendment.
   A. concluded
   B. Committee
   C. Precedent
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* A) concluded. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:62 — “When these and any further debate have concluded, the vote is taken on the motion to strike out and insert as it stands after secondary amendment.”

**629.** `[C0351 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Accordingly, before _____ the amendment just offered, the chair will ask: Are there any amendments [or “any further amendments”] to the words to be struck out?” When it appears that no one else wishes to propose an amendment to the words to be struck out, the chair then says: “The primary amendment as amended is open to debate, and secondary amendments to the words to be inserted are now in order.” After reasonable opportunity to offer any amendments of the latter type has been given, secondary amendments to either element are in order.
   A. entertaining
   B. specification
   C. adjournment
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) entertaining. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:62 — “Accordingly, before entertaining the amendment just offered, the chair will ask: Are there any amendments [or “any further amendments”] to the words to be struck out?” When it appears that no one else wishes to propose an amendment to the words to be struck out, the chair then says: “The primary amendment as amended is open to debate, and secondary amendments to the words to be inserted are now in order.” After reasonable opportunity to offer any amendments of the latter type has been given, secondary amendments to either element are in order.”

**630.** `[C0352 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for _____, and for Strike Out).

   *Answer:* Insert, or Add — “After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:63 — “After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).”

**631.** `[C0352 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a motion to _____ and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).
   A. Roll Call
   B. strike out
   C. Disposed Of
   D. the Record

   *Answer:* B) strike out. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:63 — “After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).”

**632.** `[C0353 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a primary amendment to strike out a passage of any length or _____ and insert a new version is pending and some members would prefer to insert something different or keep something closer to the original, it may be difficult or impossible to reach the desired end by secondary amendment.

   *Answer:* complexity — “When a primary amendment to strike out a passage of any length or complexity and insert a new version is pending and some members would prefer to insert something different or keep something closer to the original, it may be difficult or impossible to reach the desired end by secondary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:65 — “When a primary amendment to strike out a passage of any length or complexity and insert a new version is pending and some members would prefer to insert something different or keep something closer to the original, it may be difficult or impossible to reach the desired end by secondary amendment.”

**633.** `[C0353 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of this kind can occur especially when the amendment relates to one or more complete sentences that do not constitute an entire paragraph.
   A. Situations
   B. Permission
   C. recognized
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* A) Situations. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:65 — “Situations of this kind can occur especially when the amendment relates to one or more complete sentences that do not constitute an entire paragraph.”

**634.** `[C0353 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a primary _____ to strike out a passage of any length or complexity and insert a new version is pending and some members would prefer to insert something different or keep something closer to the original, it may be difficult or impossible to reach the desired end by secondary amendment.

   *Answer:* amendment — “When a primary amendment to strike out a passage of any length or complexity and insert a new version is pending and some members would prefer to insert something different or keep something closer to the original, it may be difficult or impossible to reach the desired end by secondary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:65 — “When a primary amendment to strike out a passage of any length or complexity and insert a new version is pending and some members would prefer to insert something different or keep something closer to the original, it may be difficult or impossible to reach the desired end by secondary amendment.”

**635.** `[C0353 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: He should present his case as strongly as possible, because if the first proposed version is inserted, it cannot be changed afterward during the same session except in _____ with a different question.
   A. Definitely
   B. Unfinished
   C. connection
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) connection. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:65 — “He should present his case as strongly as possible, because if the first proposed version is inserted, it cannot be changed afterward during the same session except in connection with a different question.”

**636.** `[C0353 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of this kind can occur especially when the amendment relates to one or more complete sentences that do not constitute an entire paragraph.

   *Answer:* Situations — “Situations of this kind can occur especially when the amendment relates to one or more complete sentences that do not constitute an entire paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:65 — “Situations of this kind can occur especially when the amendment relates to one or more complete sentences that do not constitute an entire paragraph.”

**637.** `[C0354 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to _____, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.

   *Answer:* strike out — “As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.”

**638.** `[C0354 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to _____ can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.
   A. Conforming Amendments
   B. original main motions
   C. strike out and insert
   D. or Motion Was Pending

   *Answer:* C) strike out and insert. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.”

**639.** `[C0354 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If several changes in a paragraph are desired, it is usually better to rewrite the paragraph and offer the new version as a _____, as explained below.

   *Answer:* substitute — “If several changes in a paragraph are desired, it is usually better to rewrite the paragraph and offer the new version as a substitute, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “If several changes in a paragraph are desired, it is usually better to rewrite the paragraph and offer the new version as a substitute, as explained below.”

**640.** `[C0354 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To do this, a member can move to strike out of the _____ a passage long enough to include all of the words to be removed or changed, and insert the revised passage.

   *Answer:* resolution — “To do this, a member can move to strike out of the resolution a passage long enough to include all of the words to be removed or changed, and insert the revised passage.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “To do this, a member can move to strike out of the resolution a passage long enough to include all of the words to be removed or changed, and insert the revised passage.”

**641.** `[C0354 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If several changes in a paragraph are desired, it is usually better to rewrite the paragraph and offer the new version as a _____, as explained below.
   A. variations
   B. substitute
   C. introduced
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* B) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “If several changes in a paragraph are desired, it is usually better to rewrite the paragraph and offer the new version as a substitute, as explained below.”

**642.** `[C0355 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “_____, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.”

   *Answer:* Resolved — “As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:67 — “As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.””

**643.** `[C0355 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To Strike Out and Insert (_____ to words).
   A. applying
   B. Standard
   C. dilatory
   D. Assembly

   *Answer:* A) applying. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:67 — “To Strike Out and Insert (applying to words).”

**644.** `[C0355 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Citizens’ _____ endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.”

   *Answer:* Association — “As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:67 — “As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.””

**645.** `[C0355 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To Strike Out and Insert (_____ to words).

   *Answer:* applying — “To Strike Out and Insert (applying to words).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:67 — “To Strike Out and Insert (applying to words).”

**646.** `[C0356 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for _____.

   *Answer:* Insert or Add — “After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for Insert or Add.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:68 — “After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for Insert or Add.”

**647.** `[C0356 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Debate on the amendment is limited to the relative _____ of the two sites.
   A. Definitely
   B. advantages
   C. Substitute
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* B) advantages. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:68 — “Debate on the amendment is limited to the relative advantages of the two sites.”

**648.** `[C0356 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already _____ for Insert or Add.

   *Answer:* illustrated — “After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for Insert or Add.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:68 — “After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for Insert or Add.”

**649.** `[C0356 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Debate on the resolution can go into the question of whether the _____ should express approval of any site.
   A. association
   B. requirement
   C. immediately
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) association. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:68 — “Debate on the resolution can go into the question of whether the association should express approval of any site.”

**650.** `[C0356 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debate on the amendment is limited to the relative _____ of the two sites.

   *Answer:* advantages — “Debate on the amendment is limited to the relative advantages of the two sites.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:68 — “Debate on the amendment is limited to the relative advantages of the two sites.”

**651.** `[C0357 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to _____ by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.
   A. floor
   B. Other
   C. board
   D. Amend

   *Answer:* D) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:69 — “A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.”

**652.** `[C0357 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to _____, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.

   *Answer:* substitute — “A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:69 — “A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.”

**653.** `[C0357 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For the replacement of sentences _____ a paragraph, see below.) A substitute offered for a main motion or resolution, or for a paragraph within a resolution, is a primary amendment and can therefore be moved only when no other amendment is pending.
   A. within
   B. Bylaws
   C. Appeal
   D. Taking

   *Answer:* A) within. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:69 — “(For the replacement of sentences within a paragraph, see below.) A substitute offered for a main motion or resolution, or for a paragraph within a resolution, is a primary amendment and can therefore be moved only when no other amendment is pending.”

**654.** `[C0357 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For the _____ of sentences within a paragraph, see below.) A substitute offered for a main motion or resolution, or for a paragraph within a resolution, is a primary amendment and can therefore be moved only when no other amendment is pending.
   A. Information
   B. replacement
   C. Definitely
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) replacement. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:69 — “(For the replacement of sentences within a paragraph, see below.) A substitute offered for a main motion or resolution, or for a paragraph within a resolution, is a primary amendment and can therefore be moved only when no other amendment is pending.”

**655.** `[C0358 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and such debate may go fully into the merits of both the original text and the _____, since this is necessary to determine the desirability of the primary amendment.

   *Answer:* substitute — “and such debate may go fully into the merits of both the original text and the substitute, since this is necessary to determine the desirability of the primary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:70 — “and such debate may go fully into the merits of both the original text and the substitute, since this is necessary to determine the desirability of the primary amendment.”

**656.** `[C0358 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In contrast to the rules for striking out and inserting _____, however, when a motion to substitute is under consideration the paragraph to be struck out as well as the paragraph to be inserted can be perfected by secondary amendment in any of the three basic forms (inserting or adding;

   *Answer:* words — “In contrast to the rules for striking out and inserting words, however, when a motion to substitute is under consideration the paragraph to be struck out as well as the paragraph to be inserted can be perfected by secondary amendment in any of the three basic forms (inserting or adding;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:70 — “In contrast to the rules for striking out and inserting words, however, when a motion to substitute is under consideration the paragraph to be struck out as well as the paragraph to be inserted can be perfected by secondary amendment in any of the three basic forms (inserting or adding;”

**657.** `[C0358 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or striking out and _____ing), since this is the procedure when either of the separate motions to insert or to strike out is applied to the paragraph.
   A. insert
   B. adhere
   C. Appeal
   D. before

   *Answer:* A) insert. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:70 — “or striking out and inserting), since this is the procedure when either of the separate motions to insert or to strike out is applied to the paragraph.”

**658.** `[C0358 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or striking out and inserting), since this is the procedure when either of the separate motions to insert or to _____ is applied to the paragraph.

   *Answer:* strike out — “or striking out and inserting), since this is the procedure when either of the separate motions to insert or to strike out is applied to the paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:70 — “or striking out and inserting), since this is the procedure when either of the separate motions to insert or to strike out is applied to the paragraph.”

**659.** `[C0358 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is open to debate at all times while it is pending with no _____ amendment pending;
   A. postponed
   B. delegates
   C. secondary
   D. Secretary

   *Answer:* C) secondary. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:70 — “It is open to debate at all times while it is pending with no secondary amendment pending;”

**660.** `[C0359 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As in the case of a motion to strike out and insert words, the chair has the option of accepting only _____ to the paragraph to be struck out first, and then only amendments to the proposed substitute, thereafter accepting either type of secondary amendment.

   *Answer:* amendments — “As in the case of a motion to strike out and insert words, the chair has the option of accepting only amendments to the paragraph to be struck out first, and then only amendments to the proposed substitute, thereafter accepting either type of secondary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:71 — “As in the case of a motion to strike out and insert words, the chair has the option of accepting only amendments to the paragraph to be struck out first, and then only amendments to the proposed substitute, thereafter accepting either type of secondary amendment.”

**661.** `[C0359 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Following this procedure is likely to be more often indicated in the case of a motion to substitute, _____ if a substantive issue hangs on such a motion, as in the example given below.
   A. deliberative
   B. immediately
   C. particularly
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) particularly. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:71 — “Following this procedure is likely to be more often indicated in the case of a motion to substitute, particularly if a substantive issue hangs on such a motion, as in the example given below.”

**662.** `[C0359 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After all secondary amendments have been disposed of and after any further debate on the motion to substitute, the vote is taken on whether to make this _____.

   *Answer:* substitution — “After all secondary amendments have been disposed of and after any further debate on the motion to substitute, the vote is taken on whether to make this substitution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:71 — “After all secondary amendments have been disposed of and after any further debate on the motion to substitute, the vote is taken on whether to make this substitution.”

**663.** `[C0359 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As in the case of a motion to strike out and insert words, the chair has the option of accepting only _____ to the paragraph to be struck out first, and then only amendments to the proposed substitute, thereafter accepting either type of secondary amendment.
   A. privileged
   B. Particular
   C. amendments
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:71 — “As in the case of a motion to strike out and insert words, the chair has the option of accepting only amendments to the paragraph to be struck out first, and then only amendments to the proposed substitute, thereafter accepting either type of secondary amendment.”

**664.** `[C0359 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Following this procedure is likely to be more often indicated in the case of a motion to substitute, _____ if a substantive issue hangs on such a motion, as in the example given below.

   *Answer:* particularly — “Following this procedure is likely to be more often indicated in the case of a motion to substitute, particularly if a substantive issue hangs on such a motion, as in the example given below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:71 — “Following this procedure is likely to be more often indicated in the case of a motion to substitute, particularly if a substantive issue hangs on such a motion, as in the example given below.”

**665.** `[C0360 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For replacing an unbroken _____ of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.

   *Answer:* part — “For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:72 — “For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.”

**666.** `[C0360 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to _____), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.
   A. strike out and insert
   B. precedence of motions
   C. Misuses of the Motion
   D. Question on the Table

   *Answer:* A) strike out and insert. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:72 — “For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.”

**667.** `[C0360 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Either of these motions, as applied to the same case, will present _____ the same question when it is made, but the effect of each of them is different as to permissible secondary amendment.
   A. deliberative
   B. Indefinitely
   C. substantially
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* C) substantially. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:72 — “Either of these motions, as applied to the same case, will present substantially the same question when it is made, but the effect of each of them is different as to permissible secondary amendment.”

**668.** `[C0360 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Either of these motions, as applied to the same case, will present substantially the same question when it is made, but the effect of each of them is different as to permissible _____ amendment.

   *Answer:* secondary — “Either of these motions, as applied to the same case, will present substantially the same question when it is made, but the effect of each of them is different as to permissible secondary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:72 — “Either of these motions, as applied to the same case, will present substantially the same question when it is made, but the effect of each of them is different as to permissible secondary amendment.”

**669.** `[C0361 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed _____—as they stand at the time as amended.

   *Answer:* substitute — “In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:73 — “In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.”

**670.** `[C0361 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even if the entire resolution or main motion is replaced, adopting the motion to substitute only _____ the resolution, which remains pending as amended.
   A. Recess
   B. before
   C. caucus
   D. amends

   *Answer:* D) amends. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:73 — “Even if the entire resolution or main motion is replaced, adopting the motion to substitute only amends the resolution, which remains pending as amended.”

**671.** `[C0361 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In taking the vote on whether to make the _____, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.

   *Answer:* substitution — “In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:73 — “In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.”

**672.** `[C0361 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even if the entire _____ or main motion is replaced, adopting the motion to substitute only amends the resolution, which remains pending as amended.
   A. Definitely
   B. resolution
   C. persuasive
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* B) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:73 — “Even if the entire resolution or main motion is replaced, adopting the motion to substitute only amends the resolution, which remains pending as amended.”

**673.** `[C0362 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been substituted for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by _____ something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.

   *Answer:* adding — “After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been substituted for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by adding something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:74 — “After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been substituted for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by adding something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.”

**674.** `[C0362 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been _____ for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by adding something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.
   A. Information
   B. substituted
   C. Procedures
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) substituted. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:74 — “After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been substituted for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by adding something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.”

**675.** `[C0363 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to _____ is lost, the assembly has decided only that the paragraph proposed as a substitute shall not replace the one specified.

   *Answer:* substitute — “If a motion to substitute is lost, the assembly has decided only that the paragraph proposed as a substitute shall not replace the one specified.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:75 — “If a motion to substitute is lost, the assembly has decided only that the paragraph proposed as a substitute shall not replace the one specified.”

**676.** `[C0363 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to substitute is lost, the assembly has decided only that the _____ proposed as a substitute shall not replace the one specified.
   A. rejecting
   B. debatable
   C. paragraph
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* C) paragraph. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:75 — “If a motion to substitute is lost, the assembly has decided only that the paragraph proposed as a substitute shall not replace the one specified.”

**677.** `[C0363 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, the paragraph that was retained in the _____ can be further amended, or struck out (if it is not the entire resolution);

   *Answer:* resolution — “On the other hand, the paragraph that was retained in the resolution can be further amended, or struck out (if it is not the entire resolution);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:75 — “On the other hand, the paragraph that was retained in the resolution can be further amended, or struck out (if it is not the entire resolution);”

**678.** `[C0363 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, the paragraph that was retained in the _____ can be further amended, or struck out (if it is not the entire resolution);
   A. resolution
   B. Substitute
   C. Procedures
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:75 — “On the other hand, the paragraph that was retained in the resolution can be further amended, or struck out (if it is not the entire resolution);”

**679.** `[C0364 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is being considered _____ seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.

   *Answer:* by paragraph or — “When a question is being considered by paragraph or seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:76 — “When a question is being considered by paragraph or seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.”

**680.** `[C0364 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is being considered by paragraph or _____ (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.
   A. adoption
   B. division
   C. seriatim
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* C) seriatim. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:76 — “When a question is being considered by paragraph or seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.”

**681.** `[C0364 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But it is not in order to move a substitute for the entire document until all of the paragraphs or sections have been _____ considered and the point is reached when the chair announces that the entire paper is open to amendment.
   A. Organization
   B. Convention
   C. individually
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) individually. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:76 — “But it is not in order to move a substitute for the entire document until all of the paragraphs or sections have been individually considered and the point is reached when the chair announces that the entire paper is open to amendment.”

**682.** `[C0364 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But it is not in order to move a substitute for the entire document until all of the paragraphs or sections have been _____ considered and the point is reached when the chair announces that the entire paper is open to amendment.

   *Answer:* individually — “But it is not in order to move a substitute for the entire document until all of the paragraphs or sections have been individually considered and the point is reached when the chair announces that the entire paper is open to amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:76 — “But it is not in order to move a substitute for the entire document until all of the paragraphs or sections have been individually considered and the point is reached when the chair announces that the entire paper is open to amendment.”

**683.** `[C0365 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a resolution is referred to a committee while a primary amendment—or a primary and a secondary amendment—are pending, the committee can report by _____ a substitute for the resolution, even though the substitute cannot become pending until the other amendments have been voted on in their normal order.

   *Answer:* recommending — “If a resolution is referred to a committee while a primary amendment—or a primary and a secondary amendment—are pending, the committee can report by recommending a substitute for the resolution, even though the substitute cannot become pending until the other amendments have been voted on in their normal order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:77 — “If a resolution is referred to a committee while a primary amendment—or a primary and a secondary amendment—are pending, the committee can report by recommending a substitute for the resolution, even though the substitute cannot become pending until the other amendments have been voted on in their normal order.”

**684.** `[C0365 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a _____ is referred to a committee while a primary amendment—or a primary and a secondary amendment—are pending, the committee can report by recommending a substitute for the resolution, even though the substitute cannot become pending until the other amendments have been voted on in their normal order.
   A. committees
   B. resolution
   C. Permission
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* B) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:77 — “If a resolution is referred to a committee while a primary amendment—or a primary and a secondary amendment—are pending, the committee can report by recommending a substitute for the resolution, even though the substitute cannot become pending until the other amendments have been voted on in their normal order.”

**685.** `[C0365 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As soon as this primary amendment is disposed of, the chair states the question on the substitute _____ by the committee, and proceeds as he would with any other motion to substitute.

   *Answer:* recommended — “As soon as this primary amendment is disposed of, the chair states the question on the substitute recommended by the committee, and proceeds as he would with any other motion to substitute.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:77 — “As soon as this primary amendment is disposed of, the chair states the question on the substitute recommended by the committee, and proceeds as he would with any other motion to substitute.”

**686.** `[C0365 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As soon as this primary amendment is disposed of, the chair states the question on the substitute _____ by the committee, and proceeds as he would with any other motion to substitute.
   A. recommended
   B. constitution
   C. Substitute
   D. committees

   *Answer:* A) recommended. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:77 — “As soon as this primary amendment is disposed of, the chair states the question on the substitute recommended by the committee, and proceeds as he would with any other motion to substitute.”

**687.** `[C0366 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a similar way, if a resolution is referred to a committee while a substitute and a secondary amendment (either to the original or to the substitute) are pending, the committee can report in favor of either version, with any desired _____ as to secondary amendment;

   *Answer:* recommendation — “In a similar way, if a resolution is referred to a committee while a substitute and a secondary amendment (either to the original or to the substitute) are pending, the committee can report in favor of either version, with any desired recommendation as to secondary amendment;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:78 — “In a similar way, if a resolution is referred to a committee while a substitute and a secondary amendment (either to the original or to the substitute) are pending, the committee can report in favor of either version, with any desired recommendation as to secondary amendment;”

**688.** `[C0366 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a similar way, if a resolution is referred to a committee while a _____ and a secondary amendment (either to the original or to the substitute) are pending, the committee can report in favor of either version, with any desired recommendation as to secondary amendment;
   A. settlement
   B. resolution
   C. substitute
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:78 — “In a similar way, if a resolution is referred to a committee while a substitute and a secondary amendment (either to the original or to the substitute) are pending, the committee can report in favor of either version, with any desired recommendation as to secondary amendment;”

**689.** `[C0366 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In all such cases, the chair starts with the _____ situation as it was when the resolution was committed;
