   *Answer:* parliamentary — “In all such cases, the chair starts with the parliamentary situation as it was when the resolution was committed;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:78 — “In all such cases, the chair starts with the parliamentary situation as it was when the resolution was committed;”

**690.** `[C0366 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: he then proceeds as with any motion to substitute, and states the question on any new amendment (_____ by the committee) as soon as it is in order for that amendment to be pending under the usual rules.
   A. adjournment
   B. Organization
   C. Particular
   D. recommended

   *Answer:* D) recommended. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:78 — “he then proceeds as with any motion to substitute, and states the question on any new amendment (recommended by the committee) as soon as it is in order for that amendment to be pending under the usual rules.”

**691.** `[C0366 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In all such cases, the chair starts with the _____ situation as it was when the resolution was committed;
   A. deliberative
   B. presentation
   C. parliamentary
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:78 — “In all such cases, the chair starts with the parliamentary situation as it was when the resolution was committed;”

**692.** `[C0367 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.

   *Answer:* substitute — “The motion to substitute often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:79 — “The motion to substitute often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.”

**693.** `[C0367 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it appropriate, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the _____ (16) on them.
   A. Order of Business
   B. extend the limits
   C. Quorum of Members
   D. Previous Question

   *Answer:* D) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:79 — “If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it appropriate, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the Previous Question (16) on them.”

**694.** `[C0367 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to substitute often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for _____ a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.

   *Answer:* introducing — “The motion to substitute often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:79 — “The motion to substitute often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.”

**695.** `[C0367 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While changes by separate amendments are in progress, a member who feels that he has a better solution by _____ can indicate its features briefly and announce his intention of offering the substitute as soon as no other amendment is pending.
   A. Definitely
   B. Parliamentary
   C. substitution
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) substitution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:79 — “While changes by separate amendments are in progress, a member who feels that he has a better solution by substitution can indicate its features briefly and announce his intention of offering the substitute as soon as no other amendment is pending.”

**696.** `[C0367 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it _____, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the Previous Question (16) on them.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it appropriate, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the Previous Question (16) on them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:79 — “If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it appropriate, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the Previous Question (16) on them.”

**697.** `[C0368 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An amendment in the form of a _____ can also be used to defeat or work against the purpose of the measure originally introduced.

   *Answer:* substitute — “An amendment in the form of a substitute can also be used to defeat or work against the purpose of the measure originally introduced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:80 — “An amendment in the form of a substitute can also be used to defeat or work against the purpose of the measure originally introduced.”

**698.** `[C0368 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a stratagem can be utilized with a view either to converting the measure into a weakened form before its final adoption, or to _____ a version that is likely to be rejected in the final vote.
   A. constitution
   B. deliberative
   C. substituting
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) substituting. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:80 — “Such a stratagem can be utilized with a view either to converting the measure into a weakened form before its final adoption, or to substituting a version that is likely to be rejected in the final vote.”

**699.** `[C0368 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An amendment in the form of a _____ can also be used to defeat or work against the purpose of the measure originally introduced.
   A. substitute
   B. assemblies
   C. introduced
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:80 — “An amendment in the form of a substitute can also be used to defeat or work against the purpose of the measure originally introduced.”

**700.** `[C0368 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a stratagem can be utilized with a view either to converting the measure into a weakened form before its final adoption, or to _____ a version that is likely to be rejected in the final vote.

   *Answer:* substituting — “Such a stratagem can be utilized with a view either to converting the measure into a weakened form before its final adoption, or to substituting a version that is likely to be rejected in the final vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:80 — “Such a stratagem can be utilized with a view either to converting the measure into a weakened form before its final adoption, or to substituting a version that is likely to be rejected in the final vote.”

**701.** `[C0369 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to _____ automatically operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.

   *Answer:* substitute — “Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to substitute automatically operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:81 — “Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to substitute automatically operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.”

**702.** `[C0369 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to substitute _____ operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.
   A. automatically
   B. consideration
   C. adjournment
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* A) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:81 — “Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to substitute automatically operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.”

**703.** `[C0369 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Furthermore, by the requirement that internal amendment of the substitute be done before the vote on the motion to make the _____, the members are protected from having to decide whether to reject the original version without knowing what may finally replace it.
   A. immediately
   B. deliberative
   C. subsequently
   D. substitution

   *Answer:* D) substitution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:81 — “Furthermore, by the requirement that internal amendment of the substitute be done before the vote on the motion to make the substitution, the members are protected from having to decide whether to reject the original version without knowing what may finally replace it.”

**704.** `[C0369 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Furthermore, by the requirement that internal amendment of the substitute be done before the vote on the motion to make the _____, the members are protected from having to decide whether to reject the original version without knowing what may finally replace it.

   *Answer:* substitution — “Furthermore, by the requirement that internal amendment of the substitute be done before the vote on the motion to make the substitution, the members are protected from having to decide whether to reject the original version without knowing what may finally replace it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:81 — “Furthermore, by the requirement that internal amendment of the substitute be done before the vote on the motion to make the substitution, the members are protected from having to decide whether to reject the original version without knowing what may finally replace it.”

**705.** `[C0370 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “_____, That the Parish Federation undertake the construction and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.”

   *Answer:* Resolved — “As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Parish Federation undertake the construction and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:82 — “As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Parish Federation undertake the construction and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.””

**706.** `[C0370 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Parish Federation undertake the _____ and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.”
   A. Procedures
   B. Preparation
   C. construction
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) construction. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:82 — “As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Parish Federation undertake the construction and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.””

**707.** `[C0371 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debate points strongly to a need for further _____, but many members are determined to secure immediate authorization.

   *Answer:* investigation — “Debate points strongly to a need for further investigation, but many members are determined to secure immediate authorization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:83 — “Debate points strongly to a need for further investigation, but many members are determined to secure immediate authorization.”

**708.** `[C0371 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The meeting seems evenly divided, and the outcome is _____.
   A. Organization
   B. requirement
   C. unpredictable
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) unpredictable. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:83 — “The meeting seems evenly divided, and the outcome is unpredictable.”

**709.** `[C0371 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Debate points strongly to a need for further _____, but many members are determined to secure immediate authorization.
   A. Information
   B. Organization
   C. investigation
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) investigation. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:83 — “Debate points strongly to a need for further investigation, but many members are determined to secure immediate authorization.”

**710.** `[C0371 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The meeting seems evenly divided, and the outcome is _____.

   *Answer:* unpredictable — “The meeting seems evenly divided, and the outcome is unpredictable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:83 — “The meeting seems evenly divided, and the outcome is unpredictable.”

**711.** `[C0372 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution _____ objectionable to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.

   *Answer:* less — “Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution less objectionable to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:84 — “Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution less objectionable to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.”

**712.** `[C0372 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair states the question on this _____, making its effect clear, and, after debate, puts it to vote.
   A. execution
   B. Objection
   C. amendment
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* C) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:84 — “The chair states the question on this amendment, making its effect clear, and, after debate, puts it to vote.”

**713.** `[C0372 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For purposes of the example, assume that the _____ of the original resolution differ on the amendment, some voting for it, others opposing it.

   *Answer:* proponents — “For purposes of the example, assume that the proponents of the original resolution differ on the amendment, some voting for it, others opposing it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:84 — “For purposes of the example, assume that the proponents of the original resolution differ on the amendment, some voting for it, others opposing it.”

**714.** `[C0372 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution less _____ to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.
   A. Information
   B. objectionable
   C. characteristics
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) objectionable. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:84 — “Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution less objectionable to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.”

**715.** `[C0372 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair _____ the result as follows:

   *Answer:* announces — “The chair announces the result as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:84 — “The chair announces the result as follows:”

**716.** `[C0373 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Brief debate shows that the _____ survey could not be properly carried out in twenty days.

   *Answer:* proposed — “Brief debate shows that the proposed survey could not be properly carried out in twenty days.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:85 — “Brief debate shows that the proposed survey could not be properly carried out in twenty days.”

**717.** `[C0373 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that this _____ is voted down.
   A. president
   B. exhausted
   C. amendment
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* C) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:85 — “Assume that this amendment is voted down.”

**718.** `[C0373 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Brief debate shows that the _____ survey could not be properly carried out in twenty days.
   A. decision
   B. assembly
   C. proposed
   D. assuming

   *Answer:* C) proposed. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:85 — “Brief debate shows that the proposed survey could not be properly carried out in twenty days.”

**719.** `[C0373 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume that this _____ is voted down.

   *Answer:* amendment — “Assume that this amendment is voted down.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:85 — “Assume that this amendment is voted down.”

**720.** `[C0374 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair states the question on this _____ and (after brief debate) puts it to vote.

   *Answer:* amendment — “The chair states the question on this amendment and (after brief debate) puts it to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:86 — “The chair states the question on this amendment and (after brief debate) puts it to vote.”

**721.** `[C0374 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ the result, the chair continues:
   A. authorizes
   B. substitute
   C. announcing
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) announcing. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:86 — “In announcing the result, the chair continues:”

**722.** `[C0374 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair states the question on this _____ and (after brief debate) puts it to vote.
   A. Precedent
   B. amendment
   C. auditors’
   D. extending

   *Answer:* B) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:86 — “The chair states the question on this amendment and (after brief debate) puts it to vote.”

**723.** `[C0374 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ the result, the chair continues:

   *Answer:* announcing — “In announcing the result, the chair continues:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:86 — “In announcing the result, the chair continues:”

**724.** `[C0375 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When all debate and secondary amendment has concluded, the chair puts to vote the motion to _____.

   *Answer:* substitute — “When all debate and secondary amendment has concluded, the chair puts to vote the motion to substitute.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:88 — “When all debate and secondary amendment has concluded, the chair puts to vote the motion to substitute.”

**725.** `[C0375 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Both _____ are read, usually by the chair—the pending resolution first, then the resolution proposed as a substitute.
   A. resolutions
   B. Incidental
   C. constitution
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:88 — “Both resolutions are read, usually by the chair—the pending resolution first, then the resolution proposed as a substitute.”

**726.** `[C0375 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When all debate and secondary amendment has concluded, the chair puts to vote the motion to _____.
   A. committees
   B. substitute
   C. authorizes
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* B) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:88 — “When all debate and secondary amendment has concluded, the chair puts to vote the motion to substitute.”

**727.** `[C0375 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Both _____ are read, usually by the chair—the pending resolution first, then the resolution proposed as a substitute.

   *Answer:* resolutions — “Both resolutions are read, usually by the chair—the pending resolution first, then the resolution proposed as a substitute.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:88 — “Both resolutions are read, usually by the chair—the pending resolution first, then the resolution proposed as a substitute.”

**728.** `[C0376 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair announces the result of the vote and states the question on whichever _____ is left pending, as follows:

   *Answer:* resolution — “The chair announces the result of the vote and states the question on whichever resolution is left pending, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:89 — “The chair announces the result of the vote and states the question on whichever resolution is left pending, as follows:”

**729.** `[C0376 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair announces the result of the vote and states the question on whichever _____ is left pending, as follows:
   A. resolution
   B. Incidental
   C. incidental
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:89 — “The chair announces the result of the vote and states the question on whichever resolution is left pending, as follows:”

**730.** `[C0377 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by _____ nonmodifying matter.

   *Answer:* adding — “If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by adding nonmodifying matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:90 — “If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by adding nonmodifying matter.”

**731.** `[C0377 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of which resolution is now pending, there may be further debate.
   A. Definitely
   B. incidental
   C. resolution
   D. Regardless

   *Answer:* D) Regardless. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:90 — “Regardless of which resolution is now pending, there may be further debate.”

**732.** `[C0377 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by adding _____ matter.

   *Answer:* nonmodifying — “If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by adding nonmodifying matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:90 — “If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by adding nonmodifying matter.”

**733.** `[C0377 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but in _____ whether an amendment now offered presents a new question and can therefore be admitted during the same session, account must be taken of any motions to amend that were voted on before the motion to substitute was introduced, or while it was pending.

   *Answer:* determining — “but in determining whether an amendment now offered presents a new question and can therefore be admitted during the same session, account must be taken of any motions to amend that were voted on before the motion to substitute was introduced, or while it was pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:90 — “but in determining whether an amendment now offered presents a new question and can therefore be admitted during the same session, account must be taken of any motions to amend that were voted on before the motion to substitute was introduced, or while it was pending.”

**734.** `[C0377 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of which resolution is now pending, there may be further debate.

   *Answer:* Regardless — “Regardless of which resolution is now pending, there may be further debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:90 — “Regardless of which resolution is now pending, there may be further debate.”

**735.** `[C0378 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term “friendly amendment” is often used to describe an amendment offered by someone who is in sympathy with the purposes of the main motion, in the belief that the amendment will either improve the statement or effect of the main motion, presumably to the _____ of its maker, or will increase the chances of the main motion’s adoption.

   *Answer:* satisfaction — “The term “friendly amendment” is often used to describe an amendment offered by someone who is in sympathy with the purposes of the main motion, in the belief that the amendment will either improve the statement or effect of the main motion, presumably to the satisfaction of its maker, or will increase the chances of the main motion’s adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:91 — “The term “friendly amendment” is often used to describe an amendment offered by someone who is in sympathy with the purposes of the main motion, in the belief that the amendment will either improve the statement or effect of the main motion, presumably to the satisfaction of its maker, or will increase the chances of the main motion’s adoption.”

**736.** `[C0378 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term “friendly amendment” is often used to describe an amendment offered by someone who is in sympathy with the purposes of the main motion, in the belief that the amendment will either improve the statement or effect of the main motion, presumably to the _____ of its maker, or will increase the chances of the main motion’s adoption.
   A. satisfaction
   B. Organization
   C. specification
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* A) satisfaction. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:91 — “The term “friendly amendment” is often used to describe an amendment offered by someone who is in sympathy with the purposes of the main motion, in the belief that the amendment will either improve the statement or effect of the main motion, presumably to the satisfaction of its maker, or will increase the chances of the main motion’s adoption.”

**737.** `[C0379 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Filling blanks, although not a form of amendment in itself, is a closely related device by which an unlimited number of alternative choices for a particular _____ in a main motion or primary amendment can be pending at the same time.

   *Answer:* specification — “Filling blanks, although not a form of amendment in itself, is a closely related device by which an unlimited number of alternative choices for a particular specification in a main motion or primary amendment can be pending at the same time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:92 — “Filling blanks, although not a form of amendment in itself, is a closely related device by which an unlimited number of alternative choices for a particular specification in a main motion or primary amendment can be pending at the same time.”

**738.** `[C0379 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Filling blanks, although not a form of amendment in itself, is a closely related device by which an unlimited number of alternative choices for a particular _____ in a main motion or primary amendment can be pending at the same time.
   A. specification
   B. Announcements
   C. Preparation
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* A) specification. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:92 — “Filling blanks, although not a form of amendment in itself, is a closely related device by which an unlimited number of alternative choices for a particular specification in a main motion or primary amendment can be pending at the same time.”

**739.** `[C0380 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In amending by the ordinary method, a maximum of three _____ can be pending at once, and the last one moved must be voted on first.

   *Answer:* alternatives — “In amending by the ordinary method, a maximum of three alternatives can be pending at once, and the last one moved must be voted on first.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:93 — “In amending by the ordinary method, a maximum of three alternatives can be pending at once, and the last one moved must be voted on first.”

**740.** `[C0380 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: members have an _____ to weigh all choices before voting and to vote on them in a fair and logical order.
   A. Preparation
   B. opportunity
   C. adjournment
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* B) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:93 — “members have an opportunity to weigh all choices before voting and to vote on them in a fair and logical order.”

**741.** `[C0380 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Among cases adapted to such treatment are main motions or primary _____ containing names of persons or places, dates, numbers, or amounts.

   *Answer:* amendments — “Among cases adapted to such treatment are main motions or primary amendments containing names of persons or places, dates, numbers, or amounts.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:93 — “Among cases adapted to such treatment are main motions or primary amendments containing names of persons or places, dates, numbers, or amounts.”

**742.** `[C0380 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In amending by the ordinary method, a maximum of three _____ can be pending at once, and the last one moved must be voted on first.
   A. deliberative
   B. Information
   C. alternatives
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) alternatives. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:93 — “In amending by the ordinary method, a maximum of three alternatives can be pending at once, and the last one moved must be voted on first.”

**743.** `[C0380 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: members have an _____ to weigh all choices before voting and to vote on them in a fair and logical order.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “members have an opportunity to weigh all choices before voting and to vote on them in a fair and logical order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:93 — “members have an opportunity to weigh all choices before voting and to vote on them in a fair and logical order.”

**744.** `[C0381 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The vote that fills a blank does _____ decide the question that contained the blank.

   *Answer:* not — “The vote that fills a blank does not decide the question that contained the blank.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:94 — “The vote that fills a blank does not decide the question that contained the blank.”

**745.** `[C0381 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When debate on the suggestions has ended (or, if the pending motion is undebatable, when no more suggestions are offered), the assembly decides, by _____ vote, which suggestion(s) shall fill the blank.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:94 — “When debate on the suggestions has ended (or, if the pending motion is undebatable, when no more suggestions are offered), the assembly decides, by majority vote, which suggestion(s) shall fill the blank.”

**746.** `[C0381 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The basic procedure for filling blanks is as follows: After a blank in a pending motion has been created by any of the methods described below, members suggest _____ to fill the blank, and these are stated by the chair.

   *Answer:* alternatives — “The basic procedure for filling blanks is as follows: After a blank in a pending motion has been created by any of the methods described below, members suggest alternatives to fill the blank, and these are stated by the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:94 — “The basic procedure for filling blanks is as follows: After a blank in a pending motion has been created by any of the methods described below, members suggest alternatives to fill the blank, and these are stated by the chair.”

**747.** `[C0381 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When debate on the _____ has ended (or, if the pending motion is undebatable, when no more suggestions are offered), the assembly decides, by majority vote, which suggestion(s) shall fill the blank.
   A. Information
   B. adjournment
   C. suggestions
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) suggestions. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:94 — “When debate on the suggestions has ended (or, if the pending motion is undebatable, when no more suggestions are offered), the assembly decides, by majority vote, which suggestion(s) shall fill the blank.”

**748.** `[C0381 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The vote that fills a blank does not decide the question that _____ the blank.

   *Answer:* contained — “The vote that fills a blank does not decide the question that contained the blank.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:94 — “The vote that fills a blank does not decide the question that contained the blank.”

**749.** `[C0382 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a blank exists or has been created, the chair calls for _____, by asking for them in a manner appropriate to the particular question, such as by asking, “How many members shall the committee consist of?”;

   *Answer:* suggestions — “When a blank exists or has been created, the chair calls for suggestions, by asking for them in a manner appropriate to the particular question, such as by asking, “How many members shall the committee consist of?”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:96 — “When a blank exists or has been created, the chair calls for suggestions, by asking for them in a manner appropriate to the particular question, such as by asking, “How many members shall the committee consist of?”;”

**750.** `[C0382 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, “In the pending resolution relating to the renovation of the _____, the amounts $350,000 and $300,000 have been proposed to fill the blank specifying the maximum cost.
   A. headquarters
   B. consideration
   C. Parliamentary
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) headquarters. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:96 — “or, “In the pending resolution relating to the renovation of the headquarters, the amounts $350,000 and $300,000 have been proposed to fill the blank specifying the maximum cost.”

**751.** `[C0382 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member need not be _____ by the chair to make a suggestion unless he or she wishes to speak in debate on it at the same time (see below).

   *Answer:* recognized — “A member need not be recognized by the chair to make a suggestion unless he or she wishes to speak in debate on it at the same time (see below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:96 — “A member need not be recognized by the chair to make a suggestion unless he or she wishes to speak in debate on it at the same time (see below).”

**752.** `[C0382 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a blank exists or has been created, the chair calls for _____, by asking for them in a manner appropriate to the particular question, such as by asking, “How many members shall the committee consist of?”;
   A. Reconsider
   B. Unfinished
   C. suggestions
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) suggestions. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:96 — “When a blank exists or has been created, the chair calls for suggestions, by asking for them in a manner appropriate to the particular question, such as by asking, “How many members shall the committee consist of?”;”

**753.** `[C0382 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, “In the pending resolution relating to the renovation of the _____, the amounts $350,000 and $300,000 have been proposed to fill the blank specifying the maximum cost.

   *Answer:* headquarters — “or, “In the pending resolution relating to the renovation of the headquarters, the amounts $350,000 and $300,000 have been proposed to fill the blank specifying the maximum cost.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:96 — “or, “In the pending resolution relating to the renovation of the headquarters, the amounts $350,000 and $300,000 have been proposed to fill the blank specifying the maximum cost.”

**754.** `[C0383 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No member can suggest more than one proposal for filling a blank—or, when the blank can be filled with multiple _____, such as in a motion to appoint members of a committee, more than the number allowed for in the blank.

   *Answer:* suggestions — “No member can suggest more than one proposal for filling a blank—or, when the blank can be filled with multiple suggestions, such as in a motion to appoint members of a committee, more than the number allowed for in the blank.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:97 — “No member can suggest more than one proposal for filling a blank—or, when the blank can be filled with multiple suggestions, such as in a motion to appoint members of a committee, more than the number allowed for in the blank.”

**755.** `[C0383 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the latter case, a member cannot make more than one suggestion at a time, if any objection is made, but must wait until after all other members have had the same _____ to make their suggestions.
   A. opportunity
   B. Procedures
   C. requirement
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:97 — “In the latter case, a member cannot make more than one suggestion at a time, if any objection is made, but must wait until after all other members have had the same opportunity to make their suggestions.”

**756.** `[C0383 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No member can suggest more than one proposal for filling a blank—or, when the blank can be filled with multiple _____, such as in a motion to appoint members of a committee, more than the number allowed for in the blank.
   A. Information
   B. suggestions
   C. resolutions
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) suggestions. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:97 — “No member can suggest more than one proposal for filling a blank—or, when the blank can be filled with multiple suggestions, such as in a motion to appoint members of a committee, more than the number allowed for in the blank.”

**757.** `[C0383 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the latter case, a member cannot make more than one suggestion at a time, if any objection is made, but must wait until after all other members have had the same _____ to make their suggestions.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “In the latter case, a member cannot make more than one suggestion at a time, if any objection is made, but must wait until after all other members have had the same opportunity to make their suggestions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:97 — “In the latter case, a member cannot make more than one suggestion at a time, if any objection is made, but must wait until after all other members have had the same opportunity to make their suggestions.”

**758.** `[C0384 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ to fill a blank in a debatable motion are debatable.
   A. exhausted
   B. debatable
   C. Proposals
   D. execution

   *Answer:* C) Proposals. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:98 — “Proposals to fill a blank in a debatable motion are debatable.”

**759.** `[C0384 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When there is no response to the chair’s call for further _____, the chair asks, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any debate?” To speak in debate, a member must first be recognized by the chair.

   *Answer:* suggestions — “When there is no response to the chair’s call for further suggestions, the chair asks, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any debate?” To speak in debate, a member must first be recognized by the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:98 — “When there is no response to the chair’s call for further suggestions, the chair asks, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any debate?” To speak in debate, a member must first be recognized by the chair.”

**760.** `[C0384 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The member may then speak in favor of or against a _____ made previously or make a new suggestion and speak in favor of it.
   A. resolution
   B. settlement
   C. suggestion
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* C) suggestion. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:98 — “The member may then speak in favor of or against a suggestion made previously or make a new suggestion and speak in favor of it.”

**761.** `[C0384 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ to fill a blank in a debatable motion are debatable.

   *Answer:* Proposals — “Proposals to fill a blank in a debatable motion are debatable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:98 — “Proposals to fill a blank in a debatable motion are debatable.”

**762.** `[C0384 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When there is no response to the chair’s call for further _____, the chair asks, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any debate?” To speak in debate, a member must first be recognized by the chair.
   A. constitution
   B. suggestions
   C. Unfinished
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) suggestions. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:98 — “When there is no response to the chair’s call for further suggestions, the chair asks, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any debate?” To speak in debate, a member must first be recognized by the chair.”

**763.** `[C0385 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a consequence of the rules stated in the preceding paragraphs, debate on the comparative merits of the different suggestions may take place both _____ with the making of suggestions and after all suggestions have been made.

   *Answer:* concurrently — “As a consequence of the rules stated in the preceding paragraphs, debate on the comparative merits of the different suggestions may take place both concurrently with the making of suggestions and after all suggestions have been made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:99 — “As a consequence of the rules stated in the preceding paragraphs, debate on the comparative merits of the different suggestions may take place both concurrently with the making of suggestions and after all suggestions have been made.”

**764.** `[C0385 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As a consequence of the rules stated in the preceding paragraphs, debate on the comparative merits of the different _____ may take place both concurrently with the making of suggestions and after all suggestions have been made.
   A. Permission
   B. constitution
   C. Incidental
   D. suggestions

   *Answer:* D) suggestions. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:99 — “As a consequence of the rules stated in the preceding paragraphs, debate on the comparative merits of the different suggestions may take place both concurrently with the making of suggestions and after all suggestions have been made.”

**765.** `[C0386 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the _____ can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:101 — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”

**766.** `[C0386 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the _____ Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.
   A. majority
   B. Previous
   C. dilatory
   D. assembly

   *Answer:* B) Previous. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:101 — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”

**767.** `[C0386 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, if a member obtains the floor and moves the Previous Question before a reasonable _____ to make suggestions has been given, the chair must call for suggestions before stating the motion for the Previous Question.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “However, if a member obtains the floor and moves the Previous Question before a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions has been given, the chair must call for suggestions before stating the motion for the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:101 — “However, if a member obtains the floor and moves the Previous Question before a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions has been given, the chair must call for suggestions before stating the motion for the Previous Question.”

**768.** `[C0386 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, if a member obtains the floor and moves the Previous Question before a reasonable _____ to make suggestions has been given, the chair must call for suggestions before stating the motion for the Previous Question.
   A. immediately
   B. Substitute
   C. opportunity
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:101 — “However, if a member obtains the floor and moves the Previous Question before a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions has been given, the chair must call for suggestions before stating the motion for the Previous Question.”

**769.** `[C0387 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (The order in which suggestions are voted on in particular cases is discussed below.) As soon as one of the suggestions (or the proper number of suggestions that will fill the blank) receives a _____ vote, the chair declares the blank thereby filled, and no vote is taken on any remaining suggestions.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* A) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:102 — “(The order in which suggestions are voted on in particular cases is discussed below.) As soon as one of the suggestions (or the proper number of suggestions that will fill the blank) receives a majority vote, the chair declares the blank thereby filled, and no vote is taken on any remaining suggestions.”

**770.** `[C0387 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ for filling a blank can be voted on by any of the regular methods (4:35ff., 45:11ff.), in which case each of the proposals is voted on, in turn, as a separate question.

   *Answer:* suggestions — “The suggestions for filling a blank can be voted on by any of the regular methods (4:35ff., 45:11ff.), in which case each of the proposals is voted on, in turn, as a separate question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:102 — “The suggestions for filling a blank can be voted on by any of the regular methods (4:35ff., 45:11ff.), in which case each of the proposals is voted on, in turn, as a separate question.”

**771.** `[C0387 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (The order in which suggestions are voted on in particular cases is discussed below.) As soon as one of the suggestions (or the proper number of suggestions that will fill the blank) receives a _____ vote, the chair declares the blank thereby filled, and no vote is taken on any remaining suggestions.

   *Answer:* majority — “(The order in which suggestions are voted on in particular cases is discussed below.) As soon as one of the suggestions (or the proper number of suggestions that will fill the blank) receives a majority vote, the chair declares the blank thereby filled, and no vote is taken on any remaining suggestions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:102 — “(The order in which suggestions are voted on in particular cases is discussed below.) As soon as one of the suggestions (or the proper number of suggestions that will fill the blank) receives a majority vote, the chair declares the blank thereby filled, and no vote is taken on any remaining suggestions.”

**772.** `[C0387 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ for filling a blank can be voted on by any of the regular methods (4:35ff., 45:11ff.), in which case each of the proposals is voted on, in turn, as a separate question.
   A. Incidental
   B. requirement
   C. convention
   D. suggestions

   *Answer:* D) suggestions. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:102 — “The suggestions for filling a blank can be voted on by any of the regular methods (4:35ff., 45:11ff.), in which case each of the proposals is voted on, in turn, as a separate question.”

**773.** `[C0388 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: 45:18ff.), in which case each member marks or announces his or her preference for the choice (or choices) that shall fill the blank—which need not be confined to the suggestions previously made—and the blank is filled by the choice receiving a _____ of the votes cast (or the proper number of choices receiving a majority that receive the largest number of votes;

   *Answer:* majority — “45:18ff.), in which case each member marks or announces his or her preference for the choice (or choices) that shall fill the blank—which need not be confined to the suggestions previously made—and the blank is filled by the choice receiving a majority of the votes cast (or the proper number of choices receiving a majority that receive the largest number of votes;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:103 — “45:18ff.), in which case each member marks or announces his or her preference for the choice (or choices) that shall fill the blank—which need not be confined to the suggestions previously made—and the blank is filled by the choice receiving a majority of the votes cast (or the proper number of choices receiving a majority that receive the largest number of votes;”

**774.** `[C0388 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, the assembly may direct that voting be by ballot or roll call (see 30;
   A. consideration
   B. Alternatively
   C. President-Elect
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* B) Alternatively. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:103 — “Alternatively, the assembly may direct that voting be by ballot or roll call (see 30;”

**775.** `[C0388 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: 45:18ff.), in which case each member marks or announces his or her preference for the choice (or choices) that shall fill the blank—which need not be confined to the _____ previously made—and the blank is filled by the choice receiving a majority of the votes cast (or the proper number of choices receiving a majority that receive the largest number of votes;

   *Answer:* suggestions — “45:18ff.), in which case each member marks or announces his or her preference for the choice (or choices) that shall fill the blank—which need not be confined to the suggestions previously made—and the blank is filled by the choice receiving a majority of the votes cast (or the proper number of choices receiving a majority that receive the largest number of votes;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:103 — “45:18ff.), in which case each member marks or announces his or her preference for the choice (or choices) that shall fill the blank—which need not be confined to the suggestions previously made—and the blank is filled by the choice receiving a majority of the votes cast (or the proper number of choices receiving a majority that receive the largest number of votes;”

**776.** `[C0388 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Voting by ballot or roll call is seldom used except in the case of names, however, unless there is keen _____—for example, among several cities seeking a convention.
   A. Reconsider
   B. competition
   C. authorizes
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) competition. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:103 — “Voting by ballot or roll call is seldom used except in the case of names, however, unless there is keen competition—for example, among several cities seeking a convention.”

**777.** `[C0388 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When names are being voted on, the ballot has an _____ in more truly revealing the will of the voting body;

   *Answer:* advantage — “When names are being voted on, the ballot has an advantage in more truly revealing the will of the voting body;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:103 — “When names are being voted on, the ballot has an advantage in more truly revealing the will of the voting body;”

**778.** `[C0389 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that the vote that fills a blank does not decide the main question or other pending question that _____ the blank.

   *Answer:* contained — “It should be noted that the vote that fills a blank does not decide the main question or other pending question that contained the blank.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:104 — “It should be noted that the vote that fills a blank does not decide the main question or other pending question that contained the blank.”

**779.** `[C0389 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the blank is filled, the chair must _____ state the question on the adoption of the completed motion.
   A. immediately
   B. interrupted
   C. Particular
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:104 — “When the blank is filled, the chair must immediately state the question on the adoption of the completed motion.”

**780.** `[C0389 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that the vote that fills a blank does not decide the main question or other pending question that _____ the blank.
   A. contained
   B. amendment
   C. Reporting
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) contained. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:104 — “It should be noted that the vote that fills a blank does not decide the main question or other pending question that contained the blank.”

**781.** `[C0389 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the blank is filled, the chair must _____ state the question on the adoption of the completed motion.

   *Answer:* immediately — “When the blank is filled, the chair must immediately state the question on the adoption of the completed motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:104 — “When the blank is filled, the chair must immediately state the question on the adoption of the completed motion.”

**782.** `[C0390 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the _____ Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;

   *Answer:* Previous — “However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:105 — “However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;”

**783.** `[C0390 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous _____, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;
   A. division
   B. Question
   C. original
   D. Interest

   *Answer:* B) Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:105 — “However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;”

**784.** `[C0390 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, blanks in a pending motion should be filled before the motion itself is voted on.

   *Answer:* Normally — “Normally, blanks in a pending motion should be filled before the motion itself is voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:105 — “Normally, blanks in a pending motion should be filled before the motion itself is voted on.”

**785.** `[C0390 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This may happen, for example, when two thirds of those voting are _____ that the motion will be rejected in any case.

   *Answer:* confident — “This may happen, for example, when two thirds of those voting are confident that the motion will be rejected in any case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:105 — “This may happen, for example, when two thirds of those voting are confident that the motion will be rejected in any case.”

**786.** `[C0390 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But if the motion is actually adopted with an unfilled blank, the assembly _____ proceeds to fill it, and no new subject (except a privileged one) may be introduced before the motion is thereby completed.
   A. immediately
   B. Indefinitely
   C. adjournment
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:105 — “But if the motion is actually adopted with an unfilled blank, the assembly immediately proceeds to fill it, and no new subject (except a privileged one) may be introduced before the motion is thereby completed.”

**787.** `[C0391 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no more suggestions are offered than the number required to fill the blank, then unless the voting is to be by ballot or roll call, those suggestions are deemed inserted by _____ consent, and no vote is taken on inserting them.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “If no more suggestions are offered than the number required to fill the blank, then unless the voting is to be by ballot or roll call, those suggestions are deemed inserted by unanimous consent, and no vote is taken on inserting them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:106 — “If no more suggestions are offered than the number required to fill the blank, then unless the voting is to be by ballot or roll call, those suggestions are deemed inserted by unanimous consent, and no vote is taken on inserting them.”

**788.** `[C0391 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If no more _____ are offered than the number required to fill the blank, then unless the voting is to be by ballot or roll call, those suggestions are deemed inserted by unanimous consent, and no vote is taken on inserting them.
   A. Procedures
   B. immediately
   C. suggestions
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) suggestions. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:106 — “If no more suggestions are offered than the number required to fill the blank, then unless the voting is to be by ballot or roll call, those suggestions are deemed inserted by unanimous consent, and no vote is taken on inserting them.”

**789.** `[C0391 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This procedure is _____ common when filling a blank with names (see below).

   *Answer:* especially — “This procedure is especially common when filling a blank with names (see below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:106 — “This procedure is especially common when filling a blank with names (see below).”

**790.** `[C0391 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This procedure is _____ common when filling a blank with names (see below).
   A. especially
   B. convention
   C. recognized
   D. subsidiary

   *Answer:* A) especially. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:106 — “This procedure is especially common when filling a blank with names (see below).”

**791.** `[C0392 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The following _____ apply to the process of filling a blank with one or more names:

   *Answer:* principles — “The following principles apply to the process of filling a blank with one or more names:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:107 — “The following principles apply to the process of filling a blank with one or more names:”

**792.** `[C0392 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The following _____ apply to the process of filling a blank with one or more names:
   A. Reconsider
   B. incidental
   C. principles
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) principles. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:107 — “The following principles apply to the process of filling a blank with one or more names:”

**793.** `[C0393 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that _____ least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.

   *Answer:* the one — “In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that the one least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:108 — “In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that the one least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.”

**794.** `[C0393 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: New supporters may then be gained with each succeeding vote until a _____ in favor of one entry is reached.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* A) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:108 — “New supporters may then be gained with each succeeding vote until a majority in favor of one entry is reached.”

**795.** `[C0393 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes the _____ nature of the blank determines the order in which proposals for filling it should be put to vote.

   *Answer:* particular — “Sometimes the particular nature of the blank determines the order in which proposals for filling it should be put to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:108 — “Sometimes the particular nature of the blank determines the order in which proposals for filling it should be put to vote.”

**796.** `[C0393 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Typical _____ of this kind are blanks to be filled with amounts of money.
   A. instances
   B. committee
   C. execution
   D. rejecting

   *Answer:* A) instances. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:108 — “Typical instances of this kind are blanks to be filled with amounts of money.”

**797.** `[C0393 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that the one least likely to be _____ will be voted on first, and so on.

   *Answer:* acceptable — “In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that the one least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:108 — “In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that the one least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.”

**798.** `[C0394 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The character of this measure—to _____ money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.

   *Answer:* spend — “The character of this measure—to spend money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:109 — “The character of this measure—to spend money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.”

**799.** `[C0394 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The question is now on the resolution: ‘_____, That… at a cost not to exceed $300,000.’” Note that if the smallest sum had been voted on first, it might have been adopted, with the result that those who preferred the added advantages possible through a larger expenditure would have been cut off from considering larger sums.
   A. adoption
   B. Resolved
   C. decision
   D. assuming

   *Answer:* B) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:109 — “The question is now on the resolution: ‘Resolved, That… at a cost not to exceed $300,000.’” Note that if the smallest sum had been voted on first, it might have been adopted, with the result that those who preferred the added advantages possible through a larger expenditure would have been cut off from considering larger sums.”

**800.** `[C0394 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of the procedure for filling a blank with an amount of money, assume that a resolution to renovate the _____ “at a cost not to exceed $ ” is pending, and that it is proposed to fill the blank with the following amounts: $350,000, $250,000, $400,000, and $300,000.

   *Answer:* headquarters — “As an example of the procedure for filling a blank with an amount of money, assume that a resolution to renovate the headquarters “at a cost not to exceed $ ” is pending, and that it is proposed to fill the blank with the following amounts: $350,000, $250,000, $400,000, and $300,000.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:109 — “As an example of the procedure for filling a blank with an amount of money, assume that a resolution to renovate the headquarters “at a cost not to exceed $ ” is pending, and that it is proposed to fill the blank with the following amounts: $350,000, $250,000, $400,000, and $300,000.”

**801.** `[C0394 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of this measure—to spend money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.
   A. Privilege
   B. character
   C. Precedent
   D. Reporting

   *Answer:* B) character. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:109 — “The character of this measure—to spend money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.”

**802.** `[C0394 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If $400,000 is _____, the vote is taken next on $350,000;

   *Answer:* rejected — “If $400,000 is rejected, the vote is taken next on $350,000;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:109 — “If $400,000 is rejected, the vote is taken next on $350,000;”

**803.** `[C0395 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to _____ a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.

   *Answer:* accept — “On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:110 — “On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.”

**804.** `[C0395 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in _____—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.
   A. committees
   B. settlement
   C. authorizes
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) settlement. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:110 — “On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.”

**805.** `[C0395 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, those who are willing to sell for the smallest amount, and some additional members, will be willing to sell for the next larger sum, and so on, until the smallest sum for which the _____ is willing to sell is reached.

   *Answer:* majority — “Thus, those who are willing to sell for the smallest amount, and some additional members, will be willing to sell for the next larger sum, and so on, until the smallest sum for which the majority is willing to sell is reached.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:110 — “Thus, those who are willing to sell for the smallest amount, and some additional members, will be willing to sell for the next larger sum, and so on, until the smallest sum for which the majority is willing to sell is reached.”

**806.** `[C0395 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, those who are willing to sell for the smallest amount, and some _____ members, will be willing to sell for the next larger sum, and so on, until the smallest sum for which the majority is willing to sell is reached.

   *Answer:* additional — “Thus, those who are willing to sell for the smallest amount, and some additional members, will be willing to sell for the next larger sum, and so on, until the smallest sum for which the majority is willing to sell is reached.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:110 — “Thus, those who are willing to sell for the smallest amount, and some additional members, will be willing to sell for the next larger sum, and so on, until the smallest sum for which the majority is willing to sell is reached.”

**807.** `[C0396 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ a Blank with Places, Dates, or Numbers.

   *Answer:* Filling — “Filling a Blank with Places, Dates, or Numbers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:111 — “Filling a Blank with Places, Dates, or Numbers.”

**808.** `[C0396 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a blank is to be filled with a place, date, or number, a choice of methods for _____ and voting on the proposals can be made as follows:
   A. debatable
   B. Reporting
   C. arranging
   D. rejecting

   *Answer:* C) arranging. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:111 — “When a blank is to be filled with a place, date, or number, a choice of methods for arranging and voting on the proposals can be made as follows:”

**809.** `[C0396 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ a Blank with Places, Dates, or Numbers.
   A. Filling
   B. Reports
   C. Inquiry
   D. Ordered

   *Answer:* A) Filling. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:111 — “Filling a Blank with Places, Dates, or Numbers.”

**810.** `[C0396 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a blank is to be filled with a place, date, or number, a choice of methods for _____ and voting on the proposals can be made as follows:

   *Answer:* arranging — “When a blank is to be filled with a place, date, or number, a choice of methods for arranging and voting on the proposals can be made as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:111 — “When a blank is to be filled with a place, date, or number, a choice of methods for arranging and voting on the proposals can be made as follows:”

**811.** `[C0397 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an amount has been struck out in order to create a blank, that amount is voted on in its proper place in the logical _____ among the other amounts.

   *Answer:* sequence — “If an amount has been struck out in order to create a blank, that amount is voted on in its proper place in the logical sequence among the other amounts.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:112 — “If an amount has been struck out in order to create a blank, that amount is voted on in its proper place in the logical sequence among the other amounts.”

**812.** `[C0397 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an amount has been struck out in order to create a blank, that amount is voted on in its proper place in the logical _____ among the other amounts.
   A. Approved
   B. Business
   C. sequence
   D. Question

   *Answer:* C) sequence. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:112 — “If an amount has been struck out in order to create a blank, that amount is voted on in its proper place in the logical sequence among the other amounts.”

**813.** `[C0399 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.

   *Answer:* Commit — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**814.** `[C0399 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The subsidiary motion to Commit or _____ is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.
   A. Refer
   B. limit
   C. Annul
   D. Amend

   *Answer:* A) Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**815.** `[C0400 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.

   *Answer:* Commit — “The motion to Commit also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:2 — “The motion to Commit also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.”

**816.** `[C0400 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to Commit also has three _____ whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.
   A. sustaining
   B. assemblies
   C. variations
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) variations. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:2 — “The motion to Commit also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.”

**817.** `[C0402 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____ is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that previously considered it or to a different one.

   *Answer:* recommit — “The term recommit is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that previously considered it or to a different one.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:4 — “The term recommit is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that previously considered it or to a different one.”

**818.** `[C0402 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term recommit is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that _____ considered it or to a different one.
   A. previously
   B. landscaped
   C. privileged
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* A) previously. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:4 — “The term recommit is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that previously considered it or to a different one.”

**819.** `[C0403 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: All of the rules in this section, except when stated to the contrary, apply equally to _____ (a), (b), and (c) above, and to a motion to recommit.

   *Answer:* variations — “All of the rules in this section, except when stated to the contrary, apply equally to variations (a), (b), and (c) above, and to a motion to recommit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:5 — “All of the rules in this section, except when stated to the contrary, apply equally to variations (a), (b), and (c) above, and to a motion to recommit.”

**820.** `[C0403 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: All of the rules in this section, except when stated to the contrary, apply equally to _____ (a), (b), and (c) above, and to a motion to recommit.
   A. Substitute
   B. convention
   C. variations
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) variations. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:5 — “All of the rules in this section, except when stated to the contrary, apply equally to variations (a), (b), and (c) above, and to a motion to recommit.”

**821.** `[C0404 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to _____, but is a main motion.

   *Answer:* Commit — “When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Commit, but is a main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:6 — “When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Commit, but is a main motion.”

**822.** `[C0404 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the _____ motion to Commit, but is a main motion.
   A. privileged
   B. Unfinished
   C. subsidiary
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) subsidiary. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:6 — “When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Commit, but is a main motion.”

**823.** `[C0404 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is an _____ main motion if the assignment or referral is pursuant to a subject on which the assembly has already taken some action;

   *Answer:* incidental — “It is an incidental main motion if the assignment or referral is pursuant to a subject on which the assembly has already taken some action;”.
