See RONR (12th ed.) §13:6 — “It is an incidental main motion if the assignment or referral is pursuant to a subject on which the assembly has already taken some action;”

**824.** `[C0404 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but it is an _____ main motion if the matter to be assigned or referred relates to a new subject.
   A. original
   B. Postpone
   C. majority
   D. business

   *Answer:* A) original. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:6 — “but it is an original main motion if the matter to be assigned or referred relates to a new subject.”

**825.** `[C0404 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is an _____ main motion if the assignment or referral is pursuant to a subject on which the assembly has already taken some action;
   A. variations
   B. Particular
   C. incidental
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:6 — “It is an incidental main motion if the assignment or referral is pursuant to a subject on which the assembly has already taken some action;”

**826.** `[C0405 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ usually should include all necessary details:

   *Answer:* Commit — “The motion to Commit usually should include all necessary details:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:8 — “The motion to Commit usually should include all necessary details:”

**827.** `[C0405 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to Commit usually should include all _____ details:
   A. necessary
   B. paragraph
   C. debatable
   D. committee

   *Answer:* A) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:8 — “The motion to Commit usually should include all necessary details:”

**828.** `[C0406 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair has a duty to rule out of order, as dilatory, any motion to _____ that is obviously absurd or unreasonable—such as one that (because of the time involved or any other reason) would have the effect of defeating the purpose of the main question.
   A. amends
   B. Commit
   C. ballot
   D. common

   *Answer:* B) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:9 — “The chair has a duty to rule out of order, as dilatory, any motion to Commit that is obviously absurd or unreasonable—such as one that (because of the time involved or any other reason) would have the effect of defeating the purpose of the main question.”

**829.** `[C0406 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair has a duty to rule out of order, as dilatory, any motion to Commit that is obviously absurd or _____—such as one that (because of the time involved or any other reason) would have the effect of defeating the purpose of the main question.

   *Answer:* unreasonable — “The chair has a duty to rule out of order, as dilatory, any motion to Commit that is obviously absurd or unreasonable—such as one that (because of the time involved or any other reason) would have the effect of defeating the purpose of the main question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:9 — “The chair has a duty to rule out of order, as dilatory, any motion to Commit that is obviously absurd or unreasonable—such as one that (because of the time involved or any other reason) would have the effect of defeating the purpose of the main question.”

**830.** `[C0407 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion to _____ merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.

   *Answer:* Commit — “When a motion to Commit merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:10 — “When a motion to Commit merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.”

**831.** `[C0407 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Procedures When the Motion Is Incomplete.
   A. undebatable
   B. Permission
   C. Unfinished
   D. Alternative

   *Answer:* D) Alternative. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:10 — “Alternative Procedures When the Motion Is Incomplete.”

**832.** `[C0407 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion to Commit merely lacks _____ details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.

   *Answer:* essential — “When a motion to Commit merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:10 — “When a motion to Commit merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.”

**833.** `[C0407 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ Procedures When the Motion Is Incomplete.

   *Answer:* Alternative — “Alternative Procedures When the Motion Is Incomplete.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:10 — “Alternative Procedures When the Motion Is Incomplete.”

**834.** `[C0408 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the event that any of the above _____ results in the adoption of an incomplete motion “to refer the question to a committee,” the details must be completed as described in the following paragraphs.

   *Answer:* procedures — “In the event that any of the above procedures results in the adoption of an incomplete motion “to refer the question to a committee,” the details must be completed as described in the following paragraphs.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:11 — “In the event that any of the above procedures results in the adoption of an incomplete motion “to refer the question to a committee,” the details must be completed as described in the following paragraphs.”

**835.** `[C0408 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, no new subject (except a _____ one) can be introduced until the assembly has decided all of these related questions.
   A. authorizes
   B. committees
   C. privileged
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:11 — “In such a case, no new subject (except a privileged one) can be introduced until the assembly has decided all of these related questions.”

**836.** `[C0408 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In completing the details, the member who made the motion to commit has no preference in recognition, since he or she could have included any desired _____ in that motion.

   *Answer:* specifications — “In completing the details, the member who made the motion to commit has no preference in recognition, since he or she could have included any desired specifications in that motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:11 — “In completing the details, the member who made the motion to commit has no preference in recognition, since he or she could have included any desired specifications in that motion.”

**837.** `[C0408 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the event that any of the above _____ results in the adoption of an incomplete motion “to refer the question to a committee,” the details must be completed as described in the following paragraphs.
   A. Particular
   B. procedures
   C. recognized
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* B) procedures. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:11 — “In the event that any of the above procedures results in the adoption of an incomplete motion “to refer the question to a committee,” the details must be completed as described in the following paragraphs.”

**838.** `[C0408 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, no new subject (except a _____ one) can be introduced until the assembly has decided all of these related questions.

   *Answer:* privileged — “In such a case, no new subject (except a privileged one) can be introduced until the assembly has decided all of these related questions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:11 — “In such a case, no new subject (except a privileged one) can be introduced until the assembly has decided all of these related questions.”

**839.** `[C0409 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Completing an Incomplete Motion to _____.

   *Answer:* Commit — “Completing an Incomplete Motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:12 — “Completing an Incomplete Motion to Commit.”

**840.** `[C0409 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ an Incomplete Motion to Commit.

   *Answer:* Completing — “Completing an Incomplete Motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:12 — “Completing an Incomplete Motion to Commit.”

**841.** `[C0409 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (4) standing _____, in the order in which they are proposed;
   A. Permission
   B. privileged
   C. Substitute
   D. committees

   *Answer:* D) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:12 — “(4) standing committees, in the order in which they are proposed;”

**842.** `[C0409 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A proposal to recommit to the same standing or special committee that _____ considered the question is voted on before other proposals for standing or special committees are voted on.

   *Answer:* previously — “A proposal to recommit to the same standing or special committee that previously considered the question is voted on before other proposals for standing or special committees are voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:12 — “A proposal to recommit to the same standing or special committee that previously considered the question is voted on before other proposals for standing or special committees are voted on.”

**843.** `[C0409 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (4) standing _____, in the order in which they are proposed;

   *Answer:* committees — “(4) standing committees, in the order in which they are proposed;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:12 — “(4) standing committees, in the order in which they are proposed;”

**844.** `[C0410 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The first of these methods of selection that receives a majority vote is then inserted into the motion to _____ and the remainder are ignored.

   *Answer:* Commit — “The first of these methods of selection that receives a majority vote is then inserted into the motion to Commit and the remainder are ignored.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:13 — “The first of these methods of selection that receives a majority vote is then inserted into the motion to Commit and the remainder are ignored.”

**845.** `[C0410 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The first of these methods of selection that receives a _____ vote is then inserted into the motion to Commit and the remainder are ignored.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* D) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:13 — “The first of these methods of selection that receives a majority vote is then inserted into the motion to Commit and the remainder are ignored.”

**846.** `[C0410 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is decided that the committee is to be a special one, the chair then asks—unless the rules provide the method—“How shall the committee be appointed?” Again, if only one suggestion is made, it is inserted by _____ consent, but if different methods are suggested or moved, they are voted on in the following order: (1) election by ballot;

   *Answer:* unanimous — “If it is decided that the committee is to be a special one, the chair then asks—unless the rules provide the method—“How shall the committee be appointed?” Again, if only one suggestion is made, it is inserted by unanimous consent, but if different methods are suggested or moved, they are voted on in the following order: (1) election by ballot;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:13 — “If it is decided that the committee is to be a special one, the chair then asks—unless the rules provide the method—“How shall the committee be appointed?” Again, if only one suggestion is made, it is inserted by unanimous consent, but if different methods are suggested or moved, they are voted on in the following order: (1) election by ballot;”

**847.** `[C0410 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is decided that the committee is to be a special one, the chair then asks—unless the rules provide the method—“How shall the committee be appointed?” Again, if only one _____ is made, it is inserted by unanimous consent, but if different methods are suggested or moved, they are voted on in the following order: (1) election by ballot;
   A. convention
   B. suggestion
   C. Definitely
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* B) suggestion. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:13 — “If it is decided that the committee is to be a special one, the chair then asks—unless the rules provide the method—“How shall the committee be appointed?” Again, if only one suggestion is made, it is inserted by unanimous consent, but if different methods are suggested or moved, they are voted on in the following order: (1) election by ballot;”

**848.** `[C0410 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (2) _____ from the floor (“open nominations”) with viva-voce election;

   *Answer:* nominations — “(2) nominations from the floor (“open nominations”) with viva-voce election;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:13 — “(2) nominations from the floor (“open nominations”) with viva-voce election;”

**849.** `[C0411 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to _____ lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.

   *Answer:* Commit — “If the motion to Commit lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “If the motion to Commit lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.”

**850.** `[C0411 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As soon as it is completed, if it is a _____ motion to Commit, the chair states the question on it, thus opening it to additional debate during which any member can move—or the chair himself can suggest—that it be amended by adding instructions.
   A. Divided
   B. adopted
   C. applied
   D. pending

   *Answer:* D) pending. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “As soon as it is completed, if it is a pending motion to Commit, the chair states the question on it, thus opening it to additional debate during which any member can move—or the chair himself can suggest—that it be amended by adding instructions.”

**851.** `[C0411 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By a _____ vote, instructions can also be added to a motion to Commit that is being completed after its adoption.

   *Answer:* majority — “By a majority vote, instructions can also be added to a motion to Commit that is being completed after its adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “By a majority vote, instructions can also be added to a motion to Commit that is being completed after its adoption.”

**852.** `[C0411 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to Commit lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain _____ of the motion.
   A. recognized
   B. Substitute
   C. completion
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* C) completion. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “If the motion to Commit lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.”

**853.** `[C0411 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As soon as it is completed, if it is a pending motion to Commit, the chair states the question on it, thus opening it to additional debate during which any member can move—or the chair himself can suggest—that it be amended by adding _____.

   *Answer:* instructions — “As soon as it is completed, if it is a pending motion to Commit, the chair states the question on it, thus opening it to additional debate during which any member can move—or the chair himself can suggest—that it be amended by adding instructions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “As soon as it is completed, if it is a pending motion to Commit, the chair states the question on it, thus opening it to additional debate during which any member can move—or the chair himself can suggest—that it be amended by adding instructions.”

**854.** `[C0412 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The committee cannot act before such an announcement of its membership is made, unless otherwise authorized by the assembly, which may be done in the motion establishing the special committee or by a later incidental main motion (which can be adopted by a _____ vote).

   *Answer:* majority — “The committee cannot act before such an announcement of its membership is made, unless otherwise authorized by the assembly, which may be done in the motion establishing the special committee or by a later incidental main motion (which can be adopted by a majority vote).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:15 — “The committee cannot act before such an announcement of its membership is made, unless otherwise authorized by the assembly, which may be done in the motion establishing the special committee or by a later incidental main motion (which can be adopted by a majority vote).”

**855.** `[C0412 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ or special committee may include, or even have as its chairman, one or more persons who are not members of the assembly or the society;
   A. standing
   B. Previous
   C. Standard
   D. Business

   *Answer:* A) standing. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:15 — “A standing or special committee may include, or even have as its chairman, one or more persons who are not members of the assembly or the society;”

**856.** `[C0412 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion to refer to a special committee has been adopted, no business except _____ matters can intervene until selection of the committee members is completed—except that if the chair is to appoint the committee, he can, if he wishes and time permits, state that he will announce the names of its members later.

   *Answer:* privileged — “When a motion to refer to a special committee has been adopted, no business except privileged matters can intervene until selection of the committee members is completed—except that if the chair is to appoint the committee, he can, if he wishes and time permits, state that he will announce the names of its members later.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:15 — “When a motion to refer to a special committee has been adopted, no business except privileged matters can intervene until selection of the committee members is completed—except that if the chair is to appoint the committee, he can, if he wishes and time permits, state that he will announce the names of its members later.”

**857.** `[C0412 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, however, the committee must be left with _____ time to accomplish its purpose after the names of its members have been announced for the record and any non–society members have been approved in a meeting of the assembly as necessary under the rule stated at the beginning of this paragraph.
   A. recognized
   B. reasonable
   C. Procedures
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) reasonable. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:15 — “In such a case, however, the committee must be left with reasonable time to accomplish its purpose after the names of its members have been announced for the record and any non–society members have been approved in a meeting of the assembly as necessary under the rule stated at the beginning of this paragraph.”

**858.** `[C0412 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The committee cannot act before such an _____ of its membership is made, unless otherwise authorized by the assembly, which may be done in the motion establishing the special committee or by a later incidental main motion (which can be adopted by a majority vote).

   *Answer:* announcement — “The committee cannot act before such an announcement of its membership is made, unless otherwise authorized by the assembly, which may be done in the motion establishing the special committee or by a later incidental main motion (which can be adopted by a majority vote).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:15 — “The committee cannot act before such an announcement of its membership is made, unless otherwise authorized by the assembly, which may be done in the motion establishing the special committee or by a later incidental main motion (which can be adopted by a majority vote).”

**859.** `[C0413 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to _____, it is usual to do so when such a person is interested and qualified.

   *Answer:* Commit — “Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to Commit, it is usual to do so when such a person is interested and qualified.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:16 — “Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to Commit, it is usual to do so when such a person is interested and qualified.”

**860.** `[C0413 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to Commit, it is usual to do so when such a person is _____ and qualified.
   A. interested
   B. landscaped
   C. Substitute
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* A) interested. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:16 — “Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to Commit, it is usual to do so when such a person is interested and qualified.”

**861.** `[C0414 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair appoints or _____ the committee, he has the duty to select its chairman—which he does by naming that person to the committee first—and the committee cannot elect another.
   A. nominates
   B. rejecting
   C. secondary
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* A) nominates. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:17 — “If the chair appoints or nominates the committee, he has the duty to select its chairman—which he does by naming that person to the committee first—and the committee cannot elect another.”

**862.** `[C0414 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair should not state the name of any committee member until he has decided his _____ for chairman.

   *Answer:* preference — “The chair should not state the name of any committee member until he has decided his preference for chairman.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:17 — “The chair should not state the name of any committee member until he has decided his preference for chairman.”

**863.** `[C0414 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair should _____ mention as chairman the first committee member he names, but if he neglects to state this fact, the designation nevertheless is automatic unless the first-named member immediately declines the chairmanship (which the member can do, and remain on the committee).
   A. Permission
   B. specifically
   C. Incidental
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) specifically. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:17 — “The chair should specifically mention as chairman the first committee member he names, but if he neglects to state this fact, the designation nevertheless is automatic unless the first-named member immediately declines the chairmanship (which the member can do, and remain on the committee).”

**864.** `[C0414 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ member declines to serve as chairman, the chair then names his next choice for this position.

   *Answer:* firstnamed — “If the firstnamed member declines to serve as chairman, the chair then names his next choice for this position.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:17 — “If the firstnamed member declines to serve as chairman, the chair then names his next choice for this position.”

**865.** `[C0414 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee’s task is heavy and will require some time to complete, it often is advisable to appoint a _____.
   A. Preparation
   B. immediately
   C. interrupted
   D. vice-chairman

   *Answer:* D) vice-chairman. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:17 — “If the committee’s task is heavy and will require some time to complete, it often is advisable to appoint a vice-chairman.”

**866.** `[C0415 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee is named by a power other than the chair (such as the assembly or the executive board), the body that elects the committee members has the power, at the time the _____ are made, to designate any one of them as chairman.

   *Answer:* appointments — “If the committee is named by a power other than the chair (such as the assembly or the executive board), the body that elects the committee members has the power, at the time the appointments are made, to designate any one of them as chairman.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:18 — “If the committee is named by a power other than the chair (such as the assembly or the executive board), the body that elects the committee members has the power, at the time the appointments are made, to designate any one of them as chairman.”

**867.** `[C0415 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a chairman is not _____ when the committee is appointed, the committee has the right to elect its own chairman.
   A. designated
   B. subsidiary
   C. Procedures
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) designated. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:18 — “If a chairman is not designated when the committee is appointed, the committee has the right to elect its own chairman.”

**868.** `[C0415 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the latter case, the _____ member has the duty of calling the committee together and of acting as temporary chairman until the committee elects a chairman.

   *Answer:* first-named — “In the latter case, the first-named member has the duty of calling the committee together and of acting as temporary chairman until the committee elects a chairman.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:18 — “In the latter case, the first-named member has the duty of calling the committee together and of acting as temporary chairman until the committee elects a chairman.”

**869.** `[C0415 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since such a committee may confirm its first-named member in the _____, it is important that this person be qualified and dependable.
   A. interrupted
   B. Convention
   C. constitution
   D. chairmanship

   *Answer:* D) chairmanship. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:18 — “Since such a committee may confirm its first-named member in the chairmanship, it is important that this person be qualified and dependable.”

**870.** `[C0415 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee is named by a power other than the chair (such as the assembly or the executive board), the body that elects the committee members has the power, at the time the _____ are made, to designate any one of them as chairman.
   A. Incidental
   B. appointments
   C. Organization
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) appointments. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:18 — “If the committee is named by a power other than the chair (such as the assembly or the executive board), the body that elects the committee members has the power, at the time the appointments are made, to designate any one of them as chairman.”

**871.** `[C0416 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to _____ is pending when the main motion is referred to a committee, the motion for indefinite postponement is dropped from further consideration.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “If a motion to Postpone Indefinitely is pending when the main motion is referred to a committee, the motion for indefinite postponement is dropped from further consideration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:19 — “If a motion to Postpone Indefinitely is pending when the main motion is referred to a committee, the motion for indefinite postponement is dropped from further consideration.”

**872.** `[C0416 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for _____ a Question or Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.
   A. Discharge a
   B. Division of
   C. from office
   D. Strike Out

   *Answer:* B) Division of. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:19 — “On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for Division of a Question or Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.”

**873.** `[C0416 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for Division of _____ or Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.

   *Answer:* a Question — “On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for Division of a Question or Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:19 — “On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for Division of a Question or Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.”

**874.** `[C0416 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (No question can be referred to a committee while an _____ appeal or point of order is pending.) If, at the time a main motion is committed, a motion to reconsider an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion is pending or has been made but not yet taken up, the motion to Reconsider is thereafter ignored.
   A. resolutions
   B. Substitute
   C. assemblies
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* D) undebatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:19 — “(No question can be referred to a committee while an undebatable appeal or point of order is pending.) If, at the time a main motion is committed, a motion to reconsider an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion is pending or has been made but not yet taken up, the motion to Reconsider is thereafter ignored.”

**875.** `[C0416 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (No question can be referred to a committee while an undebatable appeal or point of order is pending.) If, at the time a main motion is committed, a motion to _____ an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion is pending or has been made but not yet taken up, the motion to Reconsider is thereafter ignored.

   *Answer:* reconsider — “(No question can be referred to a committee while an undebatable appeal or point of order is pending.) If, at the time a main motion is committed, a motion to reconsider an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion is pending or has been made but not yet taken up, the motion to Reconsider is thereafter ignored.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:19 — “(No question can be referred to a committee while an undebatable appeal or point of order is pending.) If, at the time a main motion is committed, a motion to reconsider an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion is pending or has been made but not yet taken up, the motion to Reconsider is thereafter ignored.”

**876.** `[C0417 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since the purpose of referring a motion to a committee is generally to allow for a more thorough consideration of the matter, greater freedom of action is obtained once the committee commences its deliberations, as follows: The usual prohibition against introducing a motion to _____ that raises the same question of content and effect as one already decided by the assembly at the same session (cf.

   *Answer:* Amend — “Since the purpose of referring a motion to a committee is generally to allow for a more thorough consideration of the matter, greater freedom of action is obtained once the committee commences its deliberations, as follows: The usual prohibition against introducing a motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect as one already decided by the assembly at the same session (cf.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:20 — “Since the purpose of referring a motion to a committee is generally to allow for a more thorough consideration of the matter, greater freedom of action is obtained once the committee commences its deliberations, as follows: The usual prohibition against introducing a motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect as one already decided by the assembly at the same session (cf.”

**877.** `[C0417 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since the purpose of referring a motion to a committee is generally to allow for a more thorough _____ of the matter, greater freedom of action is obtained once the committee commences its deliberations, as follows: The usual prohibition against introducing a motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect as one already decided by the assembly at the same session (cf.
   A. consideration
   B. subsequently
   C. Organization
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:20 — “Since the purpose of referring a motion to a committee is generally to allow for a more thorough consideration of the matter, greater freedom of action is obtained once the committee commences its deliberations, as follows: The usual prohibition against introducing a motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect as one already decided by the assembly at the same session (cf.”

**878.** `[C0417 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Therefore, a committee to which a resolution or other motion has been referred is free to consider, and recommend for adoption, its own amendments to the referred motion, or a _____ for definite or indefinite postponement (11, 14), without regard to whether or not the assembly, by either adopting or rejecting the same or related amendments or motions prior to the referral, had effectively voted against making such changes or taking such action.

   *Answer:* recommendation — “Therefore, a committee to which a resolution or other motion has been referred is free to consider, and recommend for adoption, its own amendments to the referred motion, or a recommendation for definite or indefinite postponement (11, 14), without regard to whether or not the assembly, by either adopting or rejecting the same or related amendments or motions prior to the referral, had effectively voted against making such changes or taking such action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:20 — “Therefore, a committee to which a resolution or other motion has been referred is free to consider, and recommend for adoption, its own amendments to the referred motion, or a recommendation for definite or indefinite postponement (11, 14), without regard to whether or not the assembly, by either adopting or rejecting the same or related amendments or motions prior to the referral, had effectively voted against making such changes or taking such action.”

**879.** `[C0417 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Therefore, a committee to which a resolution or other motion has been referred is free to consider, and recommend for adoption, its own amendments to the referred motion, or a recommendation for definite or indefinite _____ (11, 14), without regard to whether or not the assembly, by either adopting or rejecting the same or related amendments or motions prior to the referral, had effectively voted against making such changes or taking such action.
   A. Procedures
   B. interrupted
   C. adjournment
   D. postponement

   *Answer:* D) postponement. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:20 — “Therefore, a committee to which a resolution or other motion has been referred is free to consider, and recommend for adoption, its own amendments to the referred motion, or a recommendation for definite or indefinite postponement (11, 14), without regard to whether or not the assembly, by either adopting or rejecting the same or related amendments or motions prior to the referral, had effectively voted against making such changes or taking such action.”

**880.** `[C0418 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is referred to a committee, any orders limiting or extending the limits of debate (15) or for the _____ (16) are thereby exhausted, so that when the question is brought back from the committee, debate in the assembly takes place according to the regular rules, even if at the same session in which the motion to Commit was adopted.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “When a question is referred to a committee, any orders limiting or extending the limits of debate (15) or for the Previous Question (16) are thereby exhausted, so that when the question is brought back from the committee, debate in the assembly takes place according to the regular rules, even if at the same session in which the motion to Commit was adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:21 — “When a question is referred to a committee, any orders limiting or extending the limits of debate (15) or for the Previous Question (16) are thereby exhausted, so that when the question is brought back from the committee, debate in the assembly takes place according to the regular rules, even if at the same session in which the motion to Commit was adopted.”

**881.** `[C0418 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is referred to a committee, any orders limiting or extending the limits of debate (15) or for the Previous Question (16) are thereby exhausted, so that when the question is brought back from the committee, debate in the assembly takes place according to the regular rules, even if at the same session in which the motion to _____ was adopted.
   A. Commit
   B. accept
   C. amends
   D. Taking

   *Answer:* A) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:21 — “When a question is referred to a committee, any orders limiting or extending the limits of debate (15) or for the Previous Question (16) are thereby exhausted, so that when the question is brought back from the committee, debate in the assembly takes place according to the regular rules, even if at the same session in which the motion to Commit was adopted.”

**882.** `[C0418 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Effect on _____ Debate and Methods of Voting.

   *Answer:* Subsequent — “Effect on Subsequent Debate and Methods of Voting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:21 — “Effect on Subsequent Debate and Methods of Voting.”

**883.** `[C0418 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If _____ of a referred question is resumed in the assembly on the same day on which it was referred, any members who have exhausted their right to debate (that is, those who have already spoken twice on the question that day, unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise) cannot speak on it again that day without permission of the assembly (by a two-thirds vote or unanimous consent;
   A. consideration
   B. resolutions
   C. President-Elect
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:21 — “If consideration of a referred question is resumed in the assembly on the same day on which it was referred, any members who have exhausted their right to debate (that is, those who have already spoken twice on the question that day, unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise) cannot speak on it again that day without permission of the assembly (by a two-thirds vote or unanimous consent;”

**884.** `[C0418 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Effect on _____ Debate and Methods of Voting.
   A. sustaining
   B. authorizes
   C. Subsequent
   D. committees

   *Answer:* C) Subsequent. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:21 — “Effect on Subsequent Debate and Methods of Voting.”

**885.** `[C0420 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The power to appoint a committee _____ the power to fill any vacancy that may arise in it.

   *Answer:* includes — “The power to appoint a committee includes the power to fill any vacancy that may arise in it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:23 — “The power to appoint a committee includes the power to fill any vacancy that may arise in it.”

**886.** `[C0420 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Committee members are presumed to serve until their _____ are appointed.
   A. resolution
   B. subsidiary
   C. successors
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) successors. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:23 — “Committee members are presumed to serve until their successors are appointed.”

**887.** `[C0420 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The power to appoint a committee _____ the power to fill any vacancy that may arise in it.
   A. chairman
   B. includes
   C. meetings
   D. adoption

   *Answer:* B) includes. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:23 — “The power to appoint a committee includes the power to fill any vacancy that may arise in it.”

**888.** `[C0420 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Committee members are presumed to serve until their _____ are appointed.

   *Answer:* successors — “Committee members are presumed to serve until their successors are appointed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:23 — “Committee members are presumed to serve until their successors are appointed.”

**889.** `[C0421 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For the _____ when a committee submits its report on a referred question, see 51;

   *Answer:* procedure — “For the procedure when a committee submits its report on a referred question, see 51;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:24 — “For the procedure when a committee submits its report on a referred question, see 51;”

**890.** `[C0421 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For the _____ when a committee submits its report on a referred question, see 51;
   A. amendment
   B. procedure
   C. Objection
   D. president

   *Answer:* B) procedure. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:24 — “For the procedure when a committee submits its report on a referred question, see 51;”

**891.** `[C0422 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ or Refer may be made in many forms.

   *Answer:* Commit — “The motion to Commit or Refer may be made in many forms.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:25 — “The motion to Commit or Refer may be made in many forms.”

**892.** `[C0422 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to Commit or _____ may be made in many forms.
   A. Refer
   B. Order
   C. Again
   D. class

   *Answer:* A) Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:25 — “The motion to Commit or Refer may be made in many forms.”

**893.** `[C0422 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Brownley, _____ to be elected by ballot from open nominations, and the committee to be instructed to report at the April meeting”;

   *Answer:* six members — “Brownley, six members to be elected by ballot from open nominations, and the committee to be instructed to report at the April meeting”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:25 — “Brownley, six members to be elected by ballot from open nominations, and the committee to be instructed to report at the April meeting”;”

**894.** `[C0422 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ are typical: “I move to refer the motion to a committee”;
   A. Precedent
   B. reception
   C. following
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* C) following. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:25 — “The following are typical: “I move to refer the motion to a committee”;”

**895.** `[C0422 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: “I move that the motion be _____ to the Social Committee”;

   *Answer:* referred — ““I move that the motion be referred to the Social Committee”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:25 — ““I move that the motion be referred to the Social Committee”;”

**896.** `[C0423 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume that a _____ is pending which, after debate, apparently requires careful amendment before the assembly will be willing to act on it.

   *Answer:* resolution — “Assume that a resolution is pending which, after debate, apparently requires careful amendment before the assembly will be willing to act on it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:26 — “Assume that a resolution is pending which, after debate, apparently requires careful amendment before the assembly will be willing to act on it.”

**897.** `[C0423 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, the assembly is _____ for time.
   A. pressed
   B. applied
   C. adopted
   D. Program

   *Answer:* A) pressed. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:26 — “However, the assembly is pressed for time.”

**898.** `[C0423 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that a _____ is pending which, after debate, apparently requires careful amendment before the assembly will be willing to act on it.
   A. resolution
   B. assemblies
   C. introduced
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:26 — “Assume that a resolution is pending which, after debate, apparently requires careful amendment before the assembly will be willing to act on it.”

**899.** `[C0423 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, the assembly is _____ for time.

   *Answer:* pressed — “However, the assembly is pressed for time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:26 — “However, the assembly is pressed for time.”

**900.** `[C0424 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or _____, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.

   *Answer:* Postpone Definitely — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**901.** `[C0424 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.
   A. Postpone
   B. assembly
   C. Division
   D. meetings

   *Answer:* A) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**902.** `[C0424 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This motion should not be confused with _____, which, as explained earlier (11), does not actually postpone the pending question, but kills it.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “This motion should not be confused with Postpone Indefinitely, which, as explained earlier (11), does not actually postpone the pending question, but kills it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “This motion should not be confused with Postpone Indefinitely, which, as explained earlier (11), does not actually postpone the pending question, but kills it.”

**903.** `[C0424 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (The _____ “to defer” should be avoided, since it is often subject to vague usage.) This motion can be moved regardless of how much debate there has been on the motion it proposes to postpone.

   *Answer:* expression — “(The expression “to defer” should be avoided, since it is often subject to vague usage.) This motion can be moved regardless of how much debate there has been on the motion it proposes to postpone.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “(The expression “to defer” should be avoided, since it is often subject to vague usage.) This motion can be moved regardless of how much debate there has been on the motion it proposes to postpone.”

**904.** `[C0424 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A question may be postponed either so that it may be _____ at a more convenient time, or because debate has shown reasons for holding off a decision until later.
   A. assemblies
   B. recognized
   C. considered
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “A question may be postponed either so that it may be considered at a more convenient time, or because debate has shown reasons for holding off a decision until later.”

**905.** `[C0425 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a _____ order, which cannot interrupt pending business.

   *Answer:* general — “In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:2 — “In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business.”

**906.** `[C0425 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general _____, which cannot interrupt pending business.
   A. Forum
   B. order
   C. class
   D. Limit

   *Answer:* B) order. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:2 — “In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business.”

**907.** `[C0425 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To give the question being postponed a _____ type of priority that will enable it to interrupt the consideration of other business at the specified time, the motion to Postpone may, by a two-thirds vote, be adopted in a form that makes the question a special order.
   A. special
   B. Inquiry
   C. advance
   D. Charges

   *Answer:* A) special. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:2 — “To give the question being postponed a special type of priority that will enable it to interrupt the consideration of other business at the specified time, the motion to Postpone may, by a two-thirds vote, be adopted in a form that makes the question a special order.”

**908.** `[C0425 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To give the question being postponed a special type of priority that will enable it to interrupt the consideration of other business at the specified time, the motion to Postpone may, by a _____ vote, be adopted in a form that makes the question a special order.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* B) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:2 — “To give the question being postponed a special type of priority that will enable it to interrupt the consideration of other business at the specified time, the motion to Postpone may, by a two-thirds vote, be adopted in a form that makes the question a special order.”

**909.** `[C0426 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion proposes to _____ a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Postpone, but is an incidental main motion (10).

   *Answer:* postpone — “When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Postpone, but is an incidental main motion (10).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:3 — “When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Postpone, but is an incidental main motion (10).”

**910.** `[C0426 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to _____ Something Previously Adopted (35).
   A. Table
   B. Amend
   C. Rules
   D. Board

   *Answer:* B) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:3 — “If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**911.** `[C0426 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to Amend _____ (35).

   *Answer:* Something Previously Adopted — “If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:3 — “If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**912.** `[C0426 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the _____ motion to Postpone, but is an incidental main motion (10).

   *Answer:* subsidiary — “When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Postpone, but is an incidental main motion (10).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:3 — “When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Postpone, but is an incidental main motion (10).”

**913.** `[C0427 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Limits on Postponement and Their Relation to Meeting and _____.

   *Answer:* Session — “Limits on Postponement and Their Relation to Meeting and Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:5 — “Limits on Postponement and Their Relation to Meeting and Session.”

**914.** `[C0427 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Limits on Postponement and Their Relation to _____ and Session.
   A. approve
   B. Another
   C. Meeting
   D. Regular

   *Answer:* C) Meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:5 — “Limits on Postponement and Their Relation to Meeting and Session.”

**915.** `[C0427 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules limiting the time to which a question can be _____ are related to the terms meeting and session (8), as follows:
   A. secondary
   B. postponed
   C. amendment
   D. reception

   *Answer:* B) postponed. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:5 — “Rules limiting the time to which a question can be postponed are related to the terms meeting and session (8), as follows:”

**916.** `[C0427 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules limiting the time to which a question can be _____ are related to the terms meeting and session (8), as follows:

   *Answer:* postponed — “Rules limiting the time to which a question can be postponed are related to the terms meeting and session (8), as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:5 — “Rules limiting the time to which a question can be postponed are related to the terms meeting and session (8), as follows:”

**917.** `[C0428 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases where no more than a _____ time interval will elapse between sessions, a question can be postponed until, but not beyond, the next regular business session.

   *Answer:* quarterly — “In cases where no more than a quarterly time interval will elapse between sessions, a question can be postponed until, but not beyond, the next regular business session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:6 — “In cases where no more than a quarterly time interval will elapse between sessions, a question can be postponed until, but not beyond, the next regular business session.”

**918.** `[C0428 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where no more than a quarterly time interval will elapse between sessions, a question can be _____ until, but not beyond, the next regular business session.
   A. postponed
   B. Precedent
   C. auditors’
   D. rejecting

   *Answer:* A) postponed. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:6 — “In cases where no more than a quarterly time interval will elapse between sessions, a question can be postponed until, but not beyond, the next regular business session.”

**919.** `[C0429 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an _____, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;

   *Answer:* adjourned meeting — “If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an adjourned meeting, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an adjourned meeting, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;”

**920.** `[C0429 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to _____ Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.
   A. at the
   B. the same
   C. of Order
   D. Fix the

   *Answer:* D) Fix the. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.”

**921.** `[C0429 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to _____ a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an adjourned meeting, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;

   *Answer:* postpone — “If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an adjourned meeting, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an adjourned meeting, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;”

**922.** `[C0429 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: then the question can be _____ to that meeting.

   *Answer:* postponed — “then the question can be postponed to that meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “then the question can be postponed to that meeting.”

**923.** `[C0429 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed _____ will be to the adjourned meeting.
   A. Information
   B. Incidental
   C. Particular
   D. postponement

   *Answer:* D) postponement. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.”

**924.** `[C0430 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the time to which a question has been postponed arrives and the question is taken up, it can be postponed again if the _____ delay will not interfere with the proper handling of the postponed motion.

   *Answer:* additional — “When the time to which a question has been postponed arrives and the question is taken up, it can be postponed again if the additional delay will not interfere with the proper handling of the postponed motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:8 — “When the time to which a question has been postponed arrives and the question is taken up, it can be postponed again if the additional delay will not interfere with the proper handling of the postponed motion.”

**925.** `[C0430 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the time to which a question has been postponed arrives and the question is taken up, it can be postponed again if the _____ delay will not interfere with the proper handling of the postponed motion.
   A. additional
   B. Permission
   C. subsidiary
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* A) additional. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:8 — “When the time to which a question has been postponed arrives and the question is taken up, it can be postponed again if the additional delay will not interfere with the proper handling of the postponed motion.”

**926.** `[C0431 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to _____—that is, if it would kill the measure.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:9 — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”

**927.** `[C0431 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Neither the motion to _____ to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.
   A. meetings
   B. Postpone
   C. Standing
   D. Question

   *Answer:* B) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:9 — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”

**928.** `[C0431 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, a motion to postpone until tomorrow a pending question of accepting an _____ to a banquet tonight cannot be recognized as a motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.

   *Answer:* invitation — “For example, a motion to postpone until tomorrow a pending question of accepting an invitation to a banquet tonight cannot be recognized as a motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:9 — “For example, a motion to postpone until tomorrow a pending question of accepting an invitation to a banquet tonight cannot be recognized as a motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.”

**929.** `[C0431 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, a motion to postpone until tomorrow a pending question of accepting an _____ to a banquet tonight cannot be recognized as a motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.
   A. subsidiary
   B. sustaining
   C. invitation
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* C) invitation. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:9 — “For example, a motion to postpone until tomorrow a pending question of accepting an invitation to a banquet tonight cannot be recognized as a motion to Postpone to a Certain Time.”

**930.** `[C0432 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As already noted, the subsidiary motion to _____ can be applied only to a question that is actually pending;

   *Answer:* Postpone — “As already noted, the subsidiary motion to Postpone can be applied only to a question that is actually pending;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:10 — “As already noted, the subsidiary motion to Postpone can be applied only to a question that is actually pending;”

**931.** `[C0432 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rule Against _____ of a Class of Subjects.
   A. deliberative
   B. Postponement
   C. Permission
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) Postponement. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:10 — “Rule Against Postponement of a Class of Subjects.”

**932.** `[C0432 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As already noted, the _____ motion to Postpone can be applied only to a question that is actually pending;

   *Answer:* subsidiary — “As already noted, the subsidiary motion to Postpone can be applied only to a question that is actually pending;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:10 — “As already noted, the subsidiary motion to Postpone can be applied only to a question that is actually pending;”

**933.** `[C0432 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but an individual item of business that is not pending can, when _____, be postponed by means of a main motion.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. appropriate
   C. constitution
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) appropriate. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:10 — “but an individual item of business that is not pending can, when appropriate, be postponed by means of a main motion.”

**934.** `[C0432 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rule Against _____ of a Class of Subjects.

   *Answer:* Postponement — “Rule Against Postponement of a Class of Subjects.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:10 — “Rule Against Postponement of a Class of Subjects.”

**935.** `[C0433 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is not in order, either through a subsidiary motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see _____, 41);

   *Answer:* Order of Business — “It is not in order, either through a subsidiary motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see Order of Business, 41);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:11 — “It is not in order, either through a subsidiary motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see Order of Business, 41);”

**936.** `[C0433 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is not in order, either through a _____ motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see Order of Business, 41);
   A. subsidiary
   B. settlement
   C. recognized
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* A) subsidiary. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:11 — “It is not in order, either through a subsidiary motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see Order of Business, 41);”

**937.** `[C0433 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but each report can be postponed _____ as it is announced or called for.

   *Answer:* separately — “but each report can be postponed separately as it is announced or called for.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:11 — “but each report can be postponed separately as it is announced or called for.”

**938.** `[C0433 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but each report can be postponed _____ as it is announced or called for.
   A. substitute
   B. separately
   C. persuasive
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) separately. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:11 — “but each report can be postponed separately as it is announced or called for.”

**939.** `[C0434 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Postponement of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a _____.

   *Answer:* Particular Session — “Postponement of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:12 — “Postponement of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.”

**940.** `[C0434 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the matter has actually been taken up during the specified session as required, it also may be postponed beyond that session in accordance with the regular rules for the motion to _____.
   A. Standing
   B. Meetings
   C. Assembly
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* D) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:12 — “If the matter has actually been taken up during the specified session as required, it also may be postponed beyond that session in accordance with the regular rules for the motion to Postpone.”

**941.** `[C0434 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.

   *Answer:* Postponement — “Postponement of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:12 — “Postponement of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.”

**942.** `[C0434 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A matter that the bylaws require to be attended to at a _____ session, such as the election of officers, cannot, in advance and through a main motion, be postponed to another session.
   A. delegates
   B. committee
   C. Privilege
   D. specified

   *Answer:* D) specified. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:12 — “A matter that the bylaws require to be attended to at a specified session, such as the election of officers, cannot, in advance and through a main motion, be postponed to another session.”

**943.** `[C0434 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It can be taken up at any time when it is in order during the specified session (that is, either as originally convened or at any _____ of it);

   *Answer:* adjournment — “It can be taken up at any time when it is in order during the specified session (that is, either as originally convened or at any adjournment of it);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:12 — “It can be taken up at any time when it is in order during the specified session (that is, either as originally convened or at any adjournment of it);”

**944.** `[C0435 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A postponed question becomes an _____ for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.

   *Answer:* order of the day — “A postponed question becomes an order of the day for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “A postponed question becomes an order of the day for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.”

**945.** `[C0435 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It cannot be taken up _____ the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote on the motion to Postpone, or by suspending the rules by a two-thirds vote.
   A. before
   B. charge
   C. caucus
   D. beyond

   *Answer:* A) before. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “It cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote on the motion to Postpone, or by suspending the rules by a two-thirds vote.”

**946.** `[C0435 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote on the motion to _____, or by suspending the rules by a two-thirds vote.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “It cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote on the motion to Postpone, or by suspending the rules by a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “It cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote on the motion to Postpone, or by suspending the rules by a two-thirds vote.”

**947.** `[C0435 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Time at Which a _____ Question Is Taken Up Again.

   *Answer:* Postponed — “Time at Which a Postponed Question Is Taken Up Again.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “Time at Which a Postponed Question Is Taken Up Again.”

**948.** `[C0435 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A postponed question becomes an _____ for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.
   A. order of the day
   B. in good standing
   C. Limits of Debate
   D. Dilatory Motions

   *Answer:* A) order of the day. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “A postponed question becomes an order of the day for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.”

**949.** `[C0436 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Orders of the day consist of _____ and special orders.

   *Answer:* general orders — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”

**950.** `[C0436 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Orders of the day consist of general orders and _____ orders.
   A. Suspend
   B. special
   C. Meeting
   D. adopted

   *Answer:* B) special. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”

**951.** `[C0436 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to _____ does not make the postponed question a special order, it becomes a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business even if the time for which it is set has arrived or passed.
   A. majority
   B. Postpone
   C. dilatory
   D. division

   *Answer:* B) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “If the motion to Postpone does not make the postponed question a special order, it becomes a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business even if the time for which it is set has arrived or passed.”

**952.** `[C0436 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By a _____ vote, however, a question can be postponed and made a special order, giving it priority over general orders as well as the ability to interrupt pending business.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* A) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “By a two-thirds vote, however, a question can be postponed and made a special order, giving it priority over general orders as well as the ability to interrupt pending business.”

**953.** `[C0436 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to _____ does not make the postponed question a special order, it becomes a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business even if the time for which it is set has arrived or passed.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “If the motion to Postpone does not make the postponed question a special order, it becomes a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business even if the time for which it is set has arrived or passed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “If the motion to Postpone does not make the postponed question a special order, it becomes a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business even if the time for which it is set has arrived or passed.”

**954.** `[C0438 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The full rules _____ the priority of orders of the day, and their relation to each other and to the order of business, are given in 41.

   *Answer:* regarding — “The full rules regarding the priority of orders of the day, and their relation to each other and to the order of business, are given in 41.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:16 — “The full rules regarding the priority of orders of the day, and their relation to each other and to the order of business, are given in 41.”

**955.** `[C0438 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The full rules _____ the priority of orders of the day, and their relation to each other and to the order of business, are given in 41.
   A. reception
   B. regarding
   C. president
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* B) regarding. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:16 — “The full rules regarding the priority of orders of the day, and their relation to each other and to the order of business, are given in 41.”

**956.** `[C0439 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the appointed time has been reached for a postponed question to be taken up, and as soon as no other business of a higher priority _____, the chair states the question as pending.

   *Answer:* interferes — “When the appointed time has been reached for a postponed question to be taken up, and as soon as no other business of a higher priority interferes, the chair states the question as pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:17 — “When the appointed time has been reached for a postponed question to be taken up, and as soon as no other business of a higher priority interferes, the chair states the question as pending.”

**957.** `[C0439 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the appointed time has been reached for a _____ question to be taken up, and as soon as no other business of a higher priority interferes, the chair states the question as pending.
   A. reception
   B. postponed
   C. amendment
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* B) postponed. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:17 — “When the appointed time has been reached for a postponed question to be taken up, and as soon as no other business of a higher priority interferes, the chair states the question as pending.”

**958.** `[C0440 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to _____, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”

**959.** `[C0440 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, _____, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.
   A. Rules
   B. Forum
   C. Amend
   D. class

   *Answer:* C) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”

**960.** `[C0440 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (No question can be postponed while an _____ appeal or point of order is pending.)

   *Answer:* undebatable — “(No question can be postponed while an undebatable appeal or point of order is pending.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “(No question can be postponed while an undebatable appeal or point of order is pending.)”

**961.** `[C0440 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Effect on Motions Adhering to a _____ Question.
   A. committee
   B. paragraph
   C. secretary
   D. Postponed

   *Answer:* D) Postponed. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “Effect on Motions Adhering to a Postponed Question.”

**962.** `[C0440 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (No question can be postponed while an _____ appeal or point of order is pending.)
   A. Procedures
   B. undebatable
   C. Reconsider
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) undebatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “(No question can be postponed while an undebatable appeal or point of order is pending.)”

**963.** `[C0441 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But when consideration is resumed at the _____ session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.
   A. legal
   B. Order
   C. same
   D. Annul

   *Answer:* C) same. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:19 — “But when consideration is resumed at the same session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.”
