**964.** `[C0441 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except for the effect of an unexhausted order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up _____ from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;

   *Answer:* on a different day — “Except for the effect of an unexhausted order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up on a different day from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:19 — “Except for the effect of an unexhausted order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up on a different day from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;”

**965.** `[C0441 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Effect on _____ Debate and Methods of Voting.
   A. Subsequent
   B. Definitely
   C. privileged
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) Subsequent. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:19 — “Effect on Subsequent Debate and Methods of Voting.”

**966.** `[C0441 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But when _____ is resumed at the same session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.

   *Answer:* consideration — “But when consideration is resumed at the same session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:19 — “But when consideration is resumed at the same session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.”

**967.** `[C0441 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except for the effect of an _____ order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up on a different day from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;
   A. assemblies
   B. Preparation
   C. unexhausted
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) unexhausted. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:19 — “Except for the effect of an unexhausted order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up on a different day from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;”

**968.** `[C0442 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume that a _____ resolution is pending at a convention and that many of the delegates who are most interested and best informed on the subject will not be able to be present until tomorrow.

   *Answer:* controversial — “Assume that a controversial resolution is pending at a convention and that many of the delegates who are most interested and best informed on the subject will not be able to be present until tomorrow.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:21 — “Assume that a controversial resolution is pending at a convention and that many of the delegates who are most interested and best informed on the subject will not be able to be present until tomorrow.”

**969.** `[C0442 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that a _____ resolution is pending at a convention and that many of the delegates who are most interested and best informed on the subject will not be able to be present until tomorrow.
   A. characteristics
   B. adjournment
   C. deliberative
   D. controversial

   *Answer:* D) controversial. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:21 — “Assume that a controversial resolution is pending at a convention and that many of the delegates who are most interested and best informed on the subject will not be able to be present until tomorrow.”

**970.** `[C0443 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the _____ motion to postpone.

   *Answer:* unamended — “If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the unamended motion to postpone.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:22 — “If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the unamended motion to postpone.”

**971.** `[C0443 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But if the resolution _____ been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:
   A. class
   B. event
   C. Again
   D. has

   *Answer:* D) has. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:22 — “But if the resolution has been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:”

**972.** `[C0443 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the unamended motion to postpone.

   *Answer:* amendment — “If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the unamended motion to postpone.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:22 — “If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the unamended motion to postpone.”

**973.** `[C0443 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to postpone is not adopted, he again states the question on the _____.
   A. sustaining
   B. Reconsider
   C. Unfinished
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:22 — “If the motion to postpone is not adopted, he again states the question on the resolution.”

**974.** `[C0443 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But if the resolution _____ been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:

   *Answer:* has — “But if the resolution has been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:22 — “But if the resolution has been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:”

**975.** `[C0444 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (The other motion serving such a purpose is the _____, 16.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “(The other motion serving such a purpose is the Previous Question, 16.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:1 — “(The other motion serving such a purpose is the Previous Question, 16.”

**976.** `[C0444 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.
   A. Permission
   B. subsidiary
   C. variations
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* B) subsidiary. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.”

**977.** `[C0444 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (The other motion serving such a purpose is the _____ Question, 16.

   *Answer:* Previous — “(The other motion serving such a purpose is the Previous Question, 16.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:1 — “(The other motion serving such a purpose is the Previous Question, 16.”

**978.** `[C0444 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Neither of these motions is allowed in _____;
   A. committees
   B. privileged
   C. authorizes
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* A) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:1 — “Neither of these motions is allowed in committees;”

**979.** `[C0444 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.

   *Answer:* subsidiary — “The subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.”

**980.** `[C0445 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ or Extend Limits of Debate can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;

   *Answer:* Limit — “The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:2 — “The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;”

**981.** `[C0445 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It cannot impose an immediate closing of debate, which requires a different motion—the _____.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “It cannot impose an immediate closing of debate, which requires a different motion—the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:2 — “It cannot impose an immediate closing of debate, which requires a different motion—the Previous Question.”

**982.** `[C0445 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches _____, without including specific provision for closing debate;
   A. permitted
   B. Reporting
   C. president
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) permitted. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:2 — “The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;”

**983.** `[C0445 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (2) _____ that, at a certain later hour or after debate for a specified length of time, debate shall be closed.

   *Answer:* requiring — “or (2) requiring that, at a certain later hour or after debate for a specified length of time, debate shall be closed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:2 — “or (2) requiring that, at a certain later hour or after debate for a specified length of time, debate shall be closed.”

**984.** `[C0445 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It cannot impose an _____ closing of debate, which requires a different motion—the Previous Question.
   A. immediate
   B. Objection
   C. execution
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) immediate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:2 — “It cannot impose an immediate closing of debate, which requires a different motion—the Previous Question.”

**985.** `[C0446 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an assembly adopts a motion to _____ Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:3 — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”

**986.** `[C0446 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend _____, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.
   A. Isolated Changes
   B. Limits of Debate
   C. Which to Adjourn
   D. during a meeting

   *Answer:* B) Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:3 — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”

**987.** `[C0447 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to _____ Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend — “If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:4 — “If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).”

**988.** `[C0447 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion proposing to change the regular _____ (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).
   A. Voting by Ballot
   B. chairman pro tem
   C. limits of debate
   D. Form and Example

   *Answer:* C) limits of debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:4 — “If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).”

**989.** `[C0448 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Effect on Pending and _____ Motions.

   *Answer:* Subsequent — “Effect on Pending and Subsequent Motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “Effect on Pending and Subsequent Motions.”

**990.** `[C0448 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any relevant _____ can be included in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).
   A. landscaped
   B. authorizes
   C. Convention
   D. provisions

   *Answer:* D) provisions. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “Any relevant provisions can be included in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).”

**991.** `[C0448 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Effect on Pending and _____ Motions.
   A. Procedures
   B. authorizes
   C. Subsequent
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* C) Subsequent. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “Effect on Pending and Subsequent Motions.”

**992.** `[C0448 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any relevant _____ can be included in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).

   *Answer:* provisions — “Any relevant provisions can be included in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “Any relevant provisions can be included in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).”

**993.** `[C0449 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, at the time the motion to _____ Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.

   *Answer:* Limit or — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:7 — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”

**994.** `[C0449 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, at the time the motion to Limit or _____ is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.
   A. Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Subsequent Instructions
   C. Recommending amendments
   D. constitution and bylaws

   *Answer:* A) Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:7 — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”

**995.** `[C0450 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order _____ debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.

   *Answer:* limiting — “Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order limiting debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:8 — “Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order limiting debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.”

**996.** `[C0450 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order limiting debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to _____, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.
   A. introduced
   B. Reconsider
   C. privileged
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* B) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:8 — “Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order limiting debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.”

**997.** `[C0450 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, an order _____ of debate—or one that both limits and extends the limits of debate, such as by limiting the length of each speech to two minutes while allowing each member to speak three times the same day on the question—applies only to the debate of the pending motion(s) on which it is ordered.
   A. extending limits
   B. Isolated Changes
   C. Legislative Body
   D. Raise a Question

   *Answer:* A) extending limits. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:8 — “On the other hand, an order extending limits of debate—or one that both limits and extends the limits of debate, such as by limiting the length of each speech to two minutes while allowing each member to speak three times the same day on the question—applies only to the debate of the pending motion(s) on which it is ordered.”

**998.** `[C0450 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other h_____, an order extending limits of debate—or one that both limits and extends the limits of debate, such as by limiting the length of each speech to two minutes while allowing each member to speak three times the same day on the question—applies only to the debate of the pending motion(s) on which it is ordered.

   *Answer:* and — “On the other hand, an order extending limits of debate—or one that both limits and extends the limits of debate, such as by limiting the length of each speech to two minutes while allowing each member to speak three times the same day on the question—applies only to the debate of the pending motion(s) on which it is ordered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:8 — “On the other hand, an order extending limits of debate—or one that both limits and extends the limits of debate, such as by limiting the length of each speech to two minutes while allowing each member to speak three times the same day on the question—applies only to the debate of the pending motion(s) on which it is ordered.”

**999.** `[C0452 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for _____ debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in Form and Example (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.

   *Answer:* closing — “If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in Form and Example (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:10 — “If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in Form and Example (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.”

**1000.** `[C0452 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in _____ (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.
   A. extending limits
   B. Consent Calendar
   C. Isolated Changes
   D. Form and Example

   *Answer:* D) Form and Example. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:10 — “If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in Form and Example (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.”

**1001.** `[C0452 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ (or extensions) that do not close debate.
   A. Limitations
   B. Incidental
   C. immediately
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) Limitations. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:10 — “Limitations (or extensions) that do not close debate.”

**1002.** `[C0452 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ (or extensions) that do not close debate.

   *Answer:* Limitations — “Limitations (or extensions) that do not close debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:10 — “Limitations (or extensions) that do not close debate.”

**1003.** `[C0453 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in _____ Example (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.

   *Answer:* Form and — “After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in Form and Example (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:11 — “After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in Form and Example (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.”

**1004.** `[C0453 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in Form and _____ (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.
   A. Pending
   B. abstain
   C. Meeting
   D. Example

   *Answer:* D) Example. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:11 — “After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in Form and Example (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.”

**1005.** `[C0453 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly decides, while the order is in effect, that it wishes to commit or postpone the main motion, then it may reconsider (37) and reverse the vote establishing the order or, by a _____ vote, it may adopt a new limitation or extension of debate that has no such restriction.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* C) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:11 — “If the assembly decides, while the order is in effect, that it wishes to commit or postpone the main motion, then it may reconsider (37) and reverse the vote establishing the order or, by a two-thirds vote, it may adopt a new limitation or extension of debate that has no such restriction.”

**1006.** `[C0453 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly decides, while the order is in effect, that it wishes to commit or postpone the main motion, then it may reconsider (37) and reverse the vote establishing the order or, by a _____ vote, it may adopt a new limitation or extension of debate that has no such restriction.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “If the assembly decides, while the order is in effect, that it wishes to commit or postpone the main motion, then it may reconsider (37) and reverse the vote establishing the order or, by a two-thirds vote, it may adopt a new limitation or extension of debate that has no such restriction.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:11 — “If the assembly decides, while the order is in effect, that it wishes to commit or postpone the main motion, then it may reconsider (37) and reverse the vote establishing the order or, by a two-thirds vote, it may adopt a new limitation or extension of debate that has no such restriction.”

**1007.** `[C0454 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than _____ Table (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;

   *Answer:* Lay on the — “When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than Lay on the Table (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:12 — “When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than Lay on the Table (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;”

**1008.** `[C0454 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than Lay on the _____ (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;
   A. Other
   B. Again
   C. Table
   D. Forum

   *Answer:* C) Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:12 — “When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than Lay on the Table (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;”

**1009.** `[C0454 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: see below) can be offered, and all pending questions are voted on _____.

   *Answer:* immediately — “see below) can be offered, and all pending questions are voted on immediately.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:12 — “see below) can be offered, and all pending questions are voted on immediately.”

**1010.** `[C0454 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In making an order that provides a time for closing debate on the main motion, sometimes it is specified that the question shall be put at that time, although the effect is the same whether or not this is _____ provided.
   A. recognized
   B. explicitly
   C. convention
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* B) explicitly. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:12 — “In making an order that provides a time for closing debate on the main motion, sometimes it is specified that the question shall be put at that time, although the effect is the same whether or not this is explicitly provided.”

**1011.** `[C0454 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: see below) can be offered, and all pending questions are voted on _____.
   A. immediately
   B. deliberative
   C. undebatable
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:12 — “see below) can be offered, and all pending questions are voted on immediately.”

**1012.** `[C0455 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to _____ or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.”

   *Answer:* Commit — “If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:13 — “If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.””

**1013.** `[C0455 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or _____ from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.”
   A. Postpone
   B. Standard
   C. Interest
   D. chairman

   *Answer:* A) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:13 — “If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.””

**1014.** `[C0455 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ that close debate on the main motion without limiting the making of subsidiary motions.

   *Answer:* Limitations — “Limitations that close debate on the main motion without limiting the making of subsidiary motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:13 — “Limitations that close debate on the main motion without limiting the making of subsidiary motions.”

**1015.** `[C0455 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ that close debate on the main motion without limiting the making of subsidiary motions.
   A. Organization
   B. Limitations
   C. convention
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) Limitations. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:13 — “Limitations that close debate on the main motion without limiting the making of subsidiary motions.”

**1016.** `[C0456 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is desired to order a limitation or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in _____ (e) below.
   A. Limits of Debate
   B. Form and Example
   C. Which to Adjourn
   D. entitled to vote

   *Answer:* B) Form and Example. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:14 — “If it is desired to order a limitation or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in Form and Example (e) below.”

**1017.** `[C0456 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to order a _____ or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in Form and Example (e) below.

   *Answer:* limitation — “If it is desired to order a limitation or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in Form and Example (e) below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:14 — “If it is desired to order a limitation or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in Form and Example (e) below.”

**1018.** `[C0457 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of the form of an order to limit or extend the limits of debate, the main question and any adhering motions can be laid on the table while the order is in effect.

   *Answer:* Regardless — “Regardless of the form of an order to limit or extend the limits of debate, the main question and any adhering motions can be laid on the table while the order is in effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:15 — “Regardless of the form of an order to limit or extend the limits of debate, the main question and any adhering motions can be laid on the table while the order is in effect.”

**1019.** `[C0457 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of the form of an order to limit or extend the limits of debate, the main question and any adhering motions can be laid on the table while the order is in effect.
   A. Regardless
   B. assemblies
   C. Unfinished
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* A) Regardless. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:15 — “Regardless of the form of an order to limit or extend the limits of debate, the main question and any adhering motions can be laid on the table while the order is in effect.”

**1020.** `[C0458 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that there can be a _____ difference in effect between (a) an order that closes debate at a particular hour and (b) one that limits debate to a specified length of time.

   *Answer:* significant — “It should be noted that there can be a significant difference in effect between (a) an order that closes debate at a particular hour and (b) one that limits debate to a specified length of time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:16 — “It should be noted that there can be a significant difference in effect between (a) an order that closes debate at a particular hour and (b) one that limits debate to a specified length of time.”

**1021.** `[C0458 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the first case, if _____ of the question to which the order applies is interrupted, such as by the question’s being laid on the table, and is resumed after the specified hour has passed (but during the same session, so that the order has not been exhausted;
   A. consideration
   B. Information
   C. Parliamentary
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:16 — “In the first case, if consideration of the question to which the order applies is interrupted, such as by the question’s being laid on the table, and is resumed after the specified hour has passed (but during the same session, so that the order has not been exhausted;”

**1022.** `[C0458 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: see below), no further debate is _____.

   *Answer:* allowed — “see below), no further debate is allowed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:16 — “see below), no further debate is allowed.”

**1023.** `[C0458 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the second case, however, any time consumed while the question is lying on the table, or during other _____ unrelated to the question to which the order applies (such as consideration of orders of the day (18, 41) or unrelated questions of privilege (19)), does not diminish the amount of time allowed for debate on the question.
   A. specification
   B. interruptions
   C. recommendation
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* B) interruptions. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:16 — “In the second case, however, any time consumed while the question is lying on the table, or during other interruptions unrelated to the question to which the order applies (such as consideration of orders of the day (18, 41) or unrelated questions of privilege (19)), does not diminish the amount of time allowed for debate on the question.”

**1024.** `[C0458 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that there can be a _____ difference in effect between (a) an order that closes debate at a particular hour and (b) one that limits debate to a specified length of time.
   A. constitution
   B. significant
   C. undebatable
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) significant. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:16 — “It should be noted that there can be a significant difference in effect between (a) an order that closes debate at a particular hour and (b) one that limits debate to a specified length of time.”

**1025.** `[C0459 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (_____, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:17 — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”

**1026.** `[C0459 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, _____, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.
   A. floor
   B. Amend
   C. Again
   D. makes

   *Answer:* B) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:17 — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”

**1027.** `[C0459 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The reason is that the _____ vote necessary for the adoption of any motion to modify the limits of debate also fulfills the requirement for suspending the rules (25).
   A. A majority vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* C) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:17 — “The reason is that the two-thirds vote necessary for the adoption of any motion to modify the limits of debate also fulfills the requirement for suspending the rules (25).”

**1028.** `[C0459 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reason is that the _____ vote necessary for the adoption of any motion to modify the limits of debate also fulfills the requirement for suspending the rules (25).

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “The reason is that the two-thirds vote necessary for the adoption of any motion to modify the limits of debate also fulfills the requirement for suspending the rules (25).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:17 — “The reason is that the two-thirds vote necessary for the adoption of any motion to modify the limits of debate also fulfills the requirement for suspending the rules (25).”

**1029.** `[C0460 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An order limiting or extending limits of debate is _____ (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;

   *Answer:* exhausted — “An order limiting or extending limits of debate is exhausted (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:18 — “An order limiting or extending limits of debate is exhausted (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;”

**1030.** `[C0460 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ for Exhaustion of Its Effect.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Conditions
   C. Procedures
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* B) Conditions. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:18 — “Conditions for Exhaustion of Its Effect.”

**1031.** `[C0460 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An order limiting or _____ limits of debate is exhausted (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;

   *Answer:* extending — “An order limiting or extending limits of debate is exhausted (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:18 — “An order limiting or extending limits of debate is exhausted (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;”

**1032.** `[C0460 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (2) when those questions affected by the order and not yet voted on have been either referred to a committee or postponed _____;
   A. undebatable
   B. Convention
   C. indefinitely
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:18 — “(2) when those questions affected by the order and not yet voted on have been either referred to a committee or postponed indefinitely;”

**1033.** `[C0460 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (3) at the _____ of the session in which the order was adopted—whichever occurs first.

   *Answer:* conclusion — “or (3) at the conclusion of the session in which the order was adopted—whichever occurs first.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:18 — “or (3) at the conclusion of the session in which the order was adopted—whichever occurs first.”

**1034.** `[C0461 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In announcing the result, the chair states the _____ situation as it then exists:
   A. recommendation
   B. Organization
   C. Indefinitely
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:21 — “In announcing the result, the chair states the parliamentary situation as it then exists:”

**1035.** `[C0461 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In announcing the result, the chair states the _____ situation as it then exists:

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “In announcing the result, the chair states the parliamentary situation as it then exists:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:21 — “In announcing the result, the chair states the parliamentary situation as it then exists:”

**1036.** `[C0462 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**1037.** `[C0462 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an _____ vote on one or more pending questions;
   A. committee
   B. Secretary
   C. debatable
   D. immediate

   *Answer:* D) immediate. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**1038.** `[C0462 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: its adoption does this with certain _____.

   *Answer:* exceptions — “its adoption does this with certain exceptions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “its adoption does this with certain exceptions.”

**1039.** `[C0462 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: its adoption does this with certain _____.
   A. exceptions
   B. Particular
   C. introduced
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* A) exceptions. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “its adoption does this with certain exceptions.”

**1040.** `[C0463 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion for the _____ has nothing to do with the last question previously considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “The motion for the Previous Question has nothing to do with the last question previously considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:3 — “The motion for the Previous Question has nothing to do with the last question previously considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.”

**1041.** `[C0463 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion for the Previous Question has nothing to do with the last question _____ considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.
   A. recognized
   B. substitute
   C. previously
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* C) previously. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:3 — “The motion for the Previous Question has nothing to do with the last question previously considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.”

**1042.** `[C0464 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Equal Application of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the _____,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the Previous Question made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.

   *Answer:* previous question — “Equal Application of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the previous question,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the Previous Question made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:6 — “Equal Application of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the previous question,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the Previous Question made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.”

**1043.** `[C0464 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Equal _____ of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the previous question,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the Previous Question made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.
   A. resolutions
   B. Application
   C. Reconsider
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) Application. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:6 — “Equal Application of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the previous question,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the Previous Question made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.”

**1044.** `[C0464 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Care should be taken that failure to _____ this fact does not lead to violation of members’ rights of debate.

   *Answer:* understand — “Care should be taken that failure to understand this fact does not lead to violation of members’ rights of debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:6 — “Care should be taken that failure to understand this fact does not lead to violation of members’ rights of debate.”

**1045.** `[C0464 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Care should be taken that failure to _____ this fact does not lead to violation of members’ rights of debate.
   A. substitute
   B. persuasive
   C. privileged
   D. understand

   *Answer:* D) understand. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:6 — “Care should be taken that failure to understand this fact does not lead to violation of members’ rights of debate.”

**1046.** `[C0465 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the _____ Question or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.

   *Answer:* Previous — “Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous Question or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:7 — “Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous Question or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.”

**1047.** `[C0465 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous _____ or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.
   A. assembly
   B. Question
   C. Assembly
   D. Standing

   *Answer:* B) Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:7 — “Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous Question or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.”

**1048.** `[C0465 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on _____ the Previous Question.

   *Answer:* whether to order — “or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on whether to order the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:7 — “or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on whether to order the Previous Question.”

**1049.** `[C0465 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on _____ the Previous Question.
   A. extending limits
   B. entitled to vote
   C. whether to order
   D. Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* C) whether to order. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:7 — “or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on whether to order the Previous Question.”

**1050.** `[C0466 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Exemption of Undebated Preamble from the Previous _____.

   *Answer:* Question Unless Separately Ordered — “Exemption of Undebated Preamble from the Previous Question Unless Separately Ordered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:8 — “Exemption of Undebated Preamble from the Previous Question Unless Separately Ordered.”

**1051.** `[C0466 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Exemption of Undebated Preamble from the _____ Unless Separately Ordered.
   A. Adjourned Meeting
   B. Order of Business
   C. Consider Seriatim
   D. Previous Question

   *Answer:* D) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:8 — “Exemption of Undebated Preamble from the Previous Question Unless Separately Ordered.”

**1052.** `[C0466 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the chair has declared the preamble open to debate and amendment in such a case, the entire _____ can be brought to an immediate vote, if desired, by then ordering the Previous Question on the preamble.
   A. privileged
   B. Permission
   C. convention
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:8 — “After the chair has declared the preamble open to debate and amendment in such a case, the entire resolution can be brought to an immediate vote, if desired, by then ordering the Previous Question on the preamble.”

**1053.** `[C0466 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the chair has declared the preamble open to debate and amendment in such a case, the entire _____ can be brought to an immediate vote, if desired, by then ordering the Previous Question on the preamble.

   *Answer:* resolution — “After the chair has declared the preamble open to debate and amendment in such a case, the entire resolution can be brought to an immediate vote, if desired, by then ordering the Previous Question on the preamble.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:8 — “After the chair has declared the preamble open to debate and amendment in such a case, the entire resolution can be brought to an immediate vote, if desired, by then ordering the Previous Question on the preamble.”

**1054.** `[C0467 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Voting on a Series of Motions Under the _____ Question;
   A. assuming
   B. Nominees
   C. Standard
   D. Previous

   *Answer:* D) Previous. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:9 — “Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous Question;”

**1055.** `[C0467 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous _____;

   *Answer:* Question — “Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous Question;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:9 — “Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous Question;”

**1056.** `[C0467 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the series includes motions to _____ Definitely, to Commit, or to Postpone Indefinitely and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.
   A. Previous
   B. Resolved
   C. original
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* D) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:9 — “If the series includes motions to Postpone Definitely, to Commit, or to Postpone Indefinitely and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.”

**1057.** `[C0467 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the series includes motions to Postpone _____, to Commit, or to Postpone Indefinitely and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.

   *Answer:* Definitely — “If the series includes motions to Postpone Definitely, to Commit, or to Postpone Indefinitely and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:9 — “If the series includes motions to Postpone Definitely, to Commit, or to Postpone Indefinitely and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.”

**1058.** `[C0468 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the _____ has been ordered) ceases to be the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:10 — “If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.”

**1059.** `[C0468 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) _____ the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.
   A. ceases to be
   B. one fifth of
   C. Main Motions
   D. or local law

   *Answer:* A) ceases to be. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:10 — “If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.”

**1060.** `[C0468 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of the execution of an order for the Previous Question may occur as follows:
   A. immediately
   B. Interruption
   C. Definitely
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) Interruption. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:10 — “Interruption of the execution of an order for the Previous Question may occur as follows:”

**1061.** `[C0468 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of the execution of an order for the Previous Question may occur as follows:

   *Answer:* Interruption — “Interruption of the execution of an order for the Previous Question may occur as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:10 — “Interruption of the execution of an order for the Previous Question may occur as follows:”

**1062.** `[C0469 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ Question is said to be exhausted (in reference to a particular order for it) when all of the motions on which it was ordered have been finally disposed of, or when any motions not yet finally disposed of are no longer affected by the order.

   *Answer:* Previous — “The Previous Question is said to be exhausted (in reference to a particular order for it) when all of the motions on which it was ordered have been finally disposed of, or when any motions not yet finally disposed of are no longer affected by the order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:11 — “The Previous Question is said to be exhausted (in reference to a particular order for it) when all of the motions on which it was ordered have been finally disposed of, or when any motions not yet finally disposed of are no longer affected by the order.”

**1063.** `[C0469 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Previous _____ is said to be exhausted (in reference to a particular order for it) when all of the motions on which it was ordered have been finally disposed of, or when any motions not yet finally disposed of are no longer affected by the order.
   A. Question
   B. dilatory
   C. limiting
   D. Nominees

   *Answer:* A) Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:11 — “The Previous Question is said to be exhausted (in reference to a particular order for it) when all of the motions on which it was ordered have been finally disposed of, or when any motions not yet finally disposed of are no longer affected by the order.”

**1064.** `[C0469 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ for exhaustion of the Previous Question are the same as for an order limiting or extending limits of debate— that is: (1) when all motions on which the Previous Question was ordered have been voted on;
   A. sustaining
   B. Substitute
   C. landscaped
   D. conditions

   *Answer:* D) conditions. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:11 — “The conditions for exhaustion of the Previous Question are the same as for an order limiting or extending limits of debate— that is: (1) when all motions on which the Previous Question was ordered have been voted on;”

**1065.** `[C0469 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ for exhaustion of the Previous Question are the same as for an order limiting or extending limits of debate— that is: (1) when all motions on which the Previous Question was ordered have been voted on;

   *Answer:* conditions — “The conditions for exhaustion of the Previous Question are the same as for an order limiting or extending limits of debate— that is: (1) when all motions on which the Previous Question was ordered have been voted on;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:11 — “The conditions for exhaustion of the Previous Question are the same as for an order limiting or extending limits of debate— that is: (1) when all motions on which the Previous Question was ordered have been voted on;”

**1066.** `[C0470 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the execution of an order for the _____ is interrupted and if the motion or motions that were pending come up again later, the rules in the foregoing paragraph apply as follows:

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If the execution of an order for the Previous Question is interrupted and if the motion or motions that were pending come up again later, the rules in the foregoing paragraph apply as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:12 — “If the execution of an order for the Previous Question is interrupted and if the motion or motions that were pending come up again later, the rules in the foregoing paragraph apply as follows:”

**1067.** `[C0470 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the execution of an order for the Previous Question is _____ and if the motion or motions that were pending come up again later, the rules in the foregoing paragraph apply as follows:
   A. Indefinitely
   B. resolutions
   C. interrupted
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) interrupted. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:12 — “If the execution of an order for the Previous Question is interrupted and if the motion or motions that were pending come up again later, the rules in the foregoing paragraph apply as follows:”

**1068.** `[C0471 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Ordered the Previous _____.

   *Answer:* Question — “Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Ordered the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:13 — “Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Ordered the Previous Question.”

**1069.** `[C0471 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Ordered the _____.
   A. General Procedure
   B. Previous Question
   C. Order of Business
   D. adjourned meeting

   *Answer:* B) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:13 — “Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Ordered the Previous Question.”

**1070.** `[C0471 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As noted in Standard _____ 8, a vote that has ordered the Previous Question can be reconsidered before, but not after, any of the motions affected by the order have been voted on.

   *Answer:* Characteristic — “As noted in Standard Characteristic 8, a vote that has ordered the Previous Question can be reconsidered before, but not after, any of the motions affected by the order have been voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:13 — “As noted in Standard Characteristic 8, a vote that has ordered the Previous Question can be reconsidered before, but not after, any of the motions affected by the order have been voted on.”

**1071.** `[C0471 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, it will frequently happen that a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the Previous Question itself can be made only in the brief moment after the vote ordering the Previous Question is completed and before the first vote is taken under the order.
   A. Procedures
   B. Definitely
   C. Consequently
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) Consequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:13 — “Consequently, it will frequently happen that a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the Previous Question itself can be made only in the brief moment after the vote ordering the Previous Question is completed and before the first vote is taken under the order.”

**1072.** `[C0471 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As noted in Standard _____ 8, a vote that has ordered the Previous Question can be reconsidered before, but not after, any of the motions affected by the order have been voted on.
   A. Vice-President
   B. Characteristic
   C. adjournment
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) Characteristic. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:13 — “As noted in Standard Characteristic 8, a vote that has ordered the Previous Question can be reconsidered before, but not after, any of the motions affected by the order have been voted on.”

**1073.** `[C0472 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the execution of an order for the _____ was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to reconsider the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If the execution of an order for the Previous Question was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to reconsider the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:14 — “If the execution of an order for the Previous Question was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to reconsider the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.”

**1074.** `[C0472 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the execution of an order for the Previous Question was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to _____ the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.
   A. resolution
   B. sustaining
   C. reconsider
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* C) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:14 — “If the execution of an order for the Previous Question was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to reconsider the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.”

**1075.** `[C0473 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that if a motion or series of motions that is under an order for the _____ comes up after having been postponed, there can never be a reconsideration of the order.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “It should be noted that if a motion or series of motions that is under an order for the Previous Question comes up after having been postponed, there can never be a reconsideration of the order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:15 — “It should be noted that if a motion or series of motions that is under an order for the Previous Question comes up after having been postponed, there can never be a reconsideration of the order.”

**1076.** `[C0473 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that if a motion or series of motions that is under an order for the Previous Question comes up after having been _____, there can never be a reconsideration of the order.
   A. Precedent
   B. presiding
   C. postponed
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* C) postponed. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:15 — “It should be noted that if a motion or series of motions that is under an order for the Previous Question comes up after having been postponed, there can never be a reconsideration of the order.”

**1077.** `[C0473 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reason is that the motion to _____ can only have been made before the Previous Question was ordered, so that the order for the Previous Question will always have been partly executed by the vote that caused the postponement.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “The reason is that the motion to Postpone can only have been made before the Previous Question was ordered, so that the order for the Previous Question will always have been partly executed by the vote that caused the postponement.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:15 — “The reason is that the motion to Postpone can only have been made before the Previous Question was ordered, so that the order for the Previous Question will always have been partly executed by the vote that caused the postponement.”

**1078.** `[C0473 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reason is that the motion to Postpone can only have been made before the Previous Question was ordered, so that the order for the Previous Question will always have been partly executed by the vote that caused the _____.

   *Answer:* postponement — “The reason is that the motion to Postpone can only have been made before the Previous Question was ordered, so that the order for the Previous Question will always have been partly executed by the vote that caused the postponement.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:15 — “The reason is that the motion to Postpone can only have been made before the Previous Question was ordered, so that the order for the Previous Question will always have been partly executed by the vote that caused the postponement.”

**1079.** `[C0474 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In practice, if a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the _____ prevails, the vote that adopted the motion to Reconsider is also presumed to have carried out the reconsideration and to have reversed the vote that is reconsidered.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “In practice, if a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the Previous Question prevails, the vote that adopted the motion to Reconsider is also presumed to have carried out the reconsideration and to have reversed the vote that is reconsidered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:16 — “In practice, if a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the Previous Question prevails, the vote that adopted the motion to Reconsider is also presumed to have carried out the reconsideration and to have reversed the vote that is reconsidered.”

**1080.** `[C0474 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In practice, if a motion to _____ an affirmative vote on the Previous Question prevails, the vote that adopted the motion to Reconsider is also presumed to have carried out the reconsideration and to have reversed the vote that is reconsidered.
   A. resolution
   B. Substitute
   C. Procedures
   D. reconsider

   *Answer:* D) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:16 — “In practice, if a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the Previous Question prevails, the vote that adopted the motion to Reconsider is also presumed to have carried out the reconsideration and to have reversed the vote that is reconsidered.”

**1081.** `[C0475 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reconsideration of a Vote While the Previous Question Is _____.

   *Answer:* in Effect — “Reconsideration of a Vote While the Previous Question Is in Effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:17 — “Reconsideration of a Vote While the Previous Question Is in Effect.”

**1082.** `[C0475 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Reconsideration of a Vote While the _____ Is in Effect.
   A. Previous Question
   B. executive session
   C. Agenda or Program
   D. Methods of Voting

   *Answer:* A) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:17 — “Reconsideration of a Vote While the Previous Question Is in Effect.”

**1083.** `[C0475 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An order for the Previous Question does not prevent the making of a motion to _____, nor does it prevent the assembly from taking up a motion to Reconsider that was made either before or after the Previous Question was ordered.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “An order for the Previous Question does not prevent the making of a motion to Reconsider, nor does it prevent the assembly from taking up a motion to Reconsider that was made either before or after the Previous Question was ordered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:17 — “An order for the Previous Question does not prevent the making of a motion to Reconsider, nor does it prevent the assembly from taking up a motion to Reconsider that was made either before or after the Previous Question was ordered.”

**1084.** `[C0475 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to Reconsider becomes pending while the Previous Question is in effect, the motion to Reconsider is undebatable and the motion to be _____ cannot be debated or amended.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “If a motion to Reconsider becomes pending while the Previous Question is in effect, the motion to Reconsider is undebatable and the motion to be reconsidered cannot be debated or amended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:17 — “If a motion to Reconsider becomes pending while the Previous Question is in effect, the motion to Reconsider is undebatable and the motion to be reconsidered cannot be debated or amended.”

**1085.** `[C0475 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An order for the Previous Question does not prevent the making of a motion to _____, nor does it prevent the assembly from taking up a motion to Reconsider that was made either before or after the Previous Question was ordered.
   A. privileged
   B. Reconsider
   C. persuasive
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:17 — “An order for the Previous Question does not prevent the making of a motion to Reconsider, nor does it prevent the assembly from taking up a motion to Reconsider that was made either before or after the Previous Question was ordered.”

**1086.** `[C0476 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An appeal is undebatable if it is made after the _____ has been moved or ordered and before the order is exhausted.
   A. Previous Question
   B. board of trustees
   C. executive session
   D. Order of Business

   *Answer:* A) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:18 — “An appeal is undebatable if it is made after the Previous Question has been moved or ordered and before the order is exhausted.”

**1087.** `[C0476 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An appeal is _____ if it is made after the Previous Question has been moved or ordered and before the order is exhausted.

   *Answer:* undebatable — “An appeal is undebatable if it is made after the Previous Question has been moved or ordered and before the order is exhausted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:18 — “An appeal is undebatable if it is made after the Previous Question has been moved or ordered and before the order is exhausted.”

**1088.** `[C0477 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The general rules as to the effect of an unexhausted order for the _____ Question on subsequent motions that would normally be debatable or amendable are as follows:

   *Answer:* Previous — “The general rules as to the effect of an unexhausted order for the Previous Question on subsequent motions that would normally be debatable or amendable are as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:19 — “The general rules as to the effect of an unexhausted order for the Previous Question on subsequent motions that would normally be debatable or amendable are as follows:”

**1089.** `[C0477 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The general rules as to the effect of an unexhausted order for the Previous _____ on subsequent motions that would normally be debatable or amendable are as follows:
   A. Interest
   B. assuming
   C. Approved
   D. Question

   *Answer:* D) Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:19 — “The general rules as to the effect of an unexhausted order for the Previous Question on subsequent motions that would normally be debatable or amendable are as follows:”

**1090.** `[C0478 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The forms used in making this motion include: “I move the _____” (to apply only to the immediately pending question);

   *Answer:* previous question — “The forms used in making this motion include: “I move the previous question” (to apply only to the immediately pending question);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:20 — “The forms used in making this motion include: “I move the previous question” (to apply only to the immediately pending question);”

**1091.** `[C0478 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The forms used in making this motion include: “I move the previous question” (to apply only to the _____ pending question);
   A. Preparation
   B. immediately
   C. Convention
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:20 — “The forms used in making this motion include: “I move the previous question” (to apply only to the immediately pending question);”

**1092.** `[C0478 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: “I move [or “call for”] the previous question on the motion to commit and its _____”;

   *Answer:* amendment — ““I move [or “call for”] the previous question on the motion to commit and its amendment”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:20 — ““I move [or “call for”] the previous question on the motion to commit and its amendment”;”
