**1093.** `[C0478 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Calls of “Question!” by members from their seats are not motions for the Previous Question and are disorderly if another member is speaking or seeking _____.

   *Answer:* recognition — “Calls of “Question!” by members from their seats are not motions for the Previous Question and are disorderly if another member is speaking or seeking recognition.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:20 — “Calls of “Question!” by members from their seats are not motions for the Previous Question and are disorderly if another member is speaking or seeking recognition.”

**1094.** `[C0478 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: “I move [or “call for”] the previous question on the motion to commit and its _____”;
   A. amendment
   B. rejecting
   C. Precedent
   D. exhausted

   *Answer:* A) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:20 — ““I move [or “call for”] the previous question on the motion to commit and its amendment”;”

**1095.** `[C0479 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In stating the question on this undebatable, unamendable motion, the chair does not pause or ask, “Are you ready for the question?” but _____ the question for a rising vote on the motion for the Previous Question immediately, as shown below.

   *Answer:* puts — “In stating the question on this undebatable, unamendable motion, the chair does not pause or ask, “Are you ready for the question?” but puts the question for a rising vote on the motion for the Previous Question immediately, as shown below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:21 — “In stating the question on this undebatable, unamendable motion, the chair does not pause or ask, “Are you ready for the question?” but puts the question for a rising vote on the motion for the Previous Question immediately, as shown below.”

**1096.** `[C0479 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In stating the question on this undebatable, unamendable motion, the chair does not pause or ask, “Are you ready for the question?” but puts the question for a rising vote on the motion for the _____ immediately, as shown below.
   A. Methods of Voting
   B. General Revisions
   C. Previous Question
   D. cumulative voting

   *Answer:* C) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:21 — “In stating the question on this undebatable, unamendable motion, the chair does not pause or ask, “Are you ready for the question?” but puts the question for a rising vote on the motion for the Previous Question immediately, as shown below.”

**1097.** `[C0479 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Similarly, in announcing an _____ result, he at once states the question on the motion that is then immediately pending.
   A. Convention
   B. undebatable
   C. requirement
   D. affirmative

   *Answer:* D) affirmative. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:21 — “Similarly, in announcing an affirmative result, he at once states the question on the motion that is then immediately pending.”

**1098.** `[C0479 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Similarly, in announcing an _____ result, he at once states the question on the motion that is then immediately pending.

   *Answer:* affirmative — “Similarly, in announcing an affirmative result, he at once states the question on the motion that is then immediately pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:21 — “Similarly, in announcing an affirmative result, he at once states the question on the motion that is then immediately pending.”

**1099.** `[C0480 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a first example, assume that a series of several _____ and amendable motions is pending.

   *Answer:* debatable — “As a first example, assume that a series of several debatable and amendable motions is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:22 — “As a first example, assume that a series of several debatable and amendable motions is pending.”

**1100.** `[C0480 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As a first example, assume that a series of several _____ and amendable motions is pending.

**1101.** `[C0481 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly is not familiar with the use of the _____ Question, the chair should at this point explain it.

**1103.** `[C0481 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In any case, the chair then _____ to take the vote:

   B. Nominees

**1104.** `[C0481 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any case, the chair then _____ to take the vote:

   *Answer:* proceeds — “In any case, the chair then proceeds to take the vote:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:23 — “In any case, the chair then proceeds to take the vote:”

**1105.** `[C0482 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume two thirds vote in favor of the _____.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “Assume two thirds vote in favor of the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:24 — “Assume two thirds vote in favor of the Previous Question.”

**1106.** `[C0482 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume two thirds vote in favor of the _____ Question.
   A. Standard
   B. Previous
   C. Standing
   D. Nominees

**1107.** `[C0483 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If less than two thirds vote in the affirmative, the chair announces the result of the vote on the motion for the _____ as follows:

**1108.** `[C0483 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If less than two thirds vote in the _____, the chair announces the result of the vote on the motion for the Previous Question as follows:

**1111.** `[C0485 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume that a resolution, an amendment to the resolution, and a motion to _____ are pending (in which case the motion to Commit is the immediately pending question).

See RONR (12th ed.) §16:27 — “Assume that a resolution, an amendment to the resolution, and a motion to Commit are pending (in which case the motion to Commit is the immediately pending question).”

**1112.** `[C0485 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that a resolution, an amendment to the resolution, and a motion to Commit are pending (in which case the motion to Commit is the _____ pending question).
   A. immediately
   B. Reconsider
   C. authorizes
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:27 — “Assume that a resolution, an amendment to the resolution, and a motion to Commit are pending (in which case the motion to Commit is the immediately pending question).”

**1113.** `[C0486 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member wishes to make a higher-ranking motion or to move a reconsideration while a motion for the _____ Question is pending or after the Previous Question has been ordered, he seeks limited recognition by rising and interrupting the chair just as in the example above.

**1114.** `[C0486 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a member wishes to make a higher-ranking motion or to move a reconsideration while a motion for the Previous _____ is pending or after the Previous Question has been ordered, he seeks limited recognition by rising and interrupting the chair just as in the example above.
   A. Approved
   B. meetings
   C. chairman
   D. Question

   *Answer:* D) Question. **1115.** `[C0487 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ enables the assembly to lay the pending question aside temporarily when something else of immediate urgency has arisen or when something else needs to be addressed before consideration of the pending question is resumed, in such a way that:

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table — “The motion to Lay on the Table enables the assembly to lay the pending question aside temporarily when something else of immediate urgency has arisen or when something else needs to be addressed before consideration of the pending question is resumed, in such a way that:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:1 — “The motion to Lay on the Table enables the assembly to lay the pending question aside temporarily when something else of immediate urgency has arisen or when something else needs to be addressed before consideration of the pending question is resumed, in such a way that:”

**1116.** `[C0487 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to Lay on the Table enables the assembly to lay the pending question aside temporarily when something else of immediate urgency has arisen or when something else needs to be addressed before _____ of the pending question is resumed, in such a way that:
   A. deliberative
   B. president-elect
   C. consideration
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:1 — “The motion to Lay on the Table enables the assembly to lay the pending question aside temporarily when something else of immediate urgency has arisen or when something else needs to be addressed before consideration of the pending question is resumed, in such a way that:”

**1117.** `[C0488 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By adopting the motion to _____, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**1118.** `[C0488 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a _____ has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.

   *Answer:* majority — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**1119.** `[C0488 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such action violates the rights of the minority and _____ members if it is for any other purpose than the one stated in the first sentence of this section.

   *Answer:* individual — “Such action violates the rights of the minority and individual members if it is for any other purpose than the one stated in the first sentence of this section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “Such action violates the rights of the minority and individual members if it is for any other purpose than the one stated in the first sentence of this section.”

**1120.** `[C0488 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In ordinary _____, the motion to Lay on the Table is not in order if the evident intent is to kill or avoid dealing with a measure.
   A. resolution
   B. assemblies
   C. Incidental
   D. convention

   *Answer:* B) assemblies. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “In ordinary assemblies, the motion to Lay on the Table is not in order if the evident intent is to kill or avoid dealing with a measure.”

**1121.** `[C0488 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such action violates the rights of the minority and _____ members if it is for any other purpose than the one stated in the first sentence of this section.
   A. Particular
   B. committees
   C. individual
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* C) individual. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “Such action violates the rights of the minority and individual members if it is for any other purpose than the one stated in the first sentence of this section.”

**1122.** `[C0489 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Laying a Question on the _____ and Taking It from the Table.

   *Answer:* Table — “Laying a Question on the Table and Taking It from the Table.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:4 — “Laying a Question on the Table and Taking It from the Table.”

**1123.** `[C0489 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules affecting the motion to _____ are closely related to the motion to Take from the Table (34), as follows:
   A. Extend Limits of
   B. Consent Calendar
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. Isolated Changes

   *Answer:* C) Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:4 — “Rules affecting the motion to Lay on the Table are closely related to the motion to Take from the Table (34), as follows:”

**1124.** `[C0489 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules affecting the motion to Lay on the Table are closely related to the motion to _____ (34), as follows:

   *Answer:* Take from the Table — “Rules affecting the motion to Lay on the Table are closely related to the motion to Take from the Table (34), as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:4 — “Rules affecting the motion to Lay on the Table are closely related to the motion to Take from the Table (34), as follows:”

**1125.** `[C0489 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Laying a Question on the _____ and Taking It from the Table.
   A. Table
   B. Amend
   C. after
   D. Again

   *Answer:* A) Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:4 — “Laying a Question on the Table and Taking It from the Table.”

**1126.** `[C0490 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Adopting a motion to _____ places on the table— that is, in the care of the secretary—the pending question and everything adhering to it.

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table — “Adopting a motion to Lay on the Table places on the table— that is, in the care of the secretary—the pending question and everything adhering to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:5 — “Adopting a motion to Lay on the Table places on the table— that is, in the care of the secretary—the pending question and everything adhering to it.”

**1127.** `[C0490 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, if a resolution with a proposed amendment and a motion to _____ are pending and the resolution is laid on the table, all of these questions go to the table at the same time and, if taken from the table, all will return together.
   A. Appeal
   B. Debate
   C. Commit
   D. Taking

   *Answer:* C) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:5 — “Thus, if a resolution with a proposed amendment and a motion to Commit are pending and the resolution is laid on the table, all of these questions go to the table at the same time and, if taken from the table, all will return together.”

**1128.** `[C0490 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Adopting a motion to Lay on the Table places on the table— that is, in the care of the secretary—the pending question and _____ adhering to it.

   *Answer:* everything — “Adopting a motion to Lay on the Table places on the table— that is, in the care of the secretary—the pending question and everything adhering to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:5 — “Adopting a motion to Lay on the Table places on the table— that is, in the care of the secretary—the pending question and everything adhering to it.”

**1129.** `[C0490 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, if a _____ with a proposed amendment and a motion to Commit are pending and the resolution is laid on the table, all of these questions go to the table at the same time and, if taken from the table, all will return together.
   A. resolution
   B. recognized
   C. substitute
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:5 — “Thus, if a resolution with a proposed amendment and a motion to Commit are pending and the resolution is laid on the table, all of these questions go to the table at the same time and, if taken from the table, all will return together.”

**1130.** `[C0490 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (But a proposed amendment to anything _____ adopted—existing bylaws, for example—is a main motion and when laid on the table does not carry with it what it proposes to amend.)

   *Answer:* previously — “(But a proposed amendment to anything previously adopted—existing bylaws, for example—is a main motion and when laid on the table does not carry with it what it proposes to amend.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:5 — “(But a proposed amendment to anything previously adopted—existing bylaws, for example—is a main motion and when laid on the table does not carry with it what it proposes to amend.)”

**1131.** `[C0491 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a question has been laid on the table, it can be taken from the table by a _____ vote as soon as the interrupting business is disposed of—or at some later time before the limits for taking it from the table have expired (see below)— whenever no question is pending, provided that business of the same class as the question on the table, or unfinished business, general orders, or new business, is in order.

   *Answer:* majority — “After a question has been laid on the table, it can be taken from the table by a majority vote as soon as the interrupting business is disposed of—or at some later time before the limits for taking it from the table have expired (see below)— whenever no question is pending, provided that business of the same class as the question on the table, or unfinished business, general orders, or new business, is in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:6 — “After a question has been laid on the table, it can be taken from the table by a majority vote as soon as the interrupting business is disposed of—or at some later time before the limits for taking it from the table have expired (see below)— whenever no question is pending, provided that business of the same class as the question on the table, or unfinished business, general orders, or new business, is in order.”

**1132.** `[C0491 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a question has been laid on the table, it can be taken from the table by a majority vote as soon as the _____ business is disposed of—or at some later time before the limits for taking it from the table have expired (see below)— whenever no question is pending, provided that business of the same class as the question on the table, or unfinished business, general orders, or new business, is in order.
   A. interrupting
   B. Parliamentary
   C. Information
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) interrupting. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:6 — “After a question has been laid on the table, it can be taken from the table by a majority vote as soon as the interrupting business is disposed of—or at some later time before the limits for taking it from the table have expired (see below)— whenever no question is pending, provided that business of the same class as the question on the table, or unfinished business, general orders, or new business, is in order.”

**1133.** `[C0493 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be _____ later as a new question.

   *Answer:* reintroduced — “If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be reintroduced later as a new question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:8 — “If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be reintroduced later as a new question.”

**1134.** `[C0493 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be _____ later as a new question.
   A. reintroduced
   B. undebatable
   C. requirement
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) reintroduced. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:8 — “If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be reintroduced later as a new question.”

**1135.** `[C0494 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To consider another motion on the same subject, it is necessary first to take the question from the table and then to move the new proposal as a substitute, or to make whatever other motion is _____ to the case.
   A. appropriate
   B. Convention
   C. Preparation
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) appropriate. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:9 — “To consider another motion on the same subject, it is necessary first to take the question from the table and then to move the new proposal as a substitute, or to make whatever other motion is appropriate to the case.”

**1136.** `[C0494 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To consider another motion on the same subject, it is necessary first to take the question from the table and then to move the new proposal as a substitute, or to make whatever other motion is _____ to the case.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “To consider another motion on the same subject, it is necessary first to take the question from the table and then to move the new proposal as a substitute, or to make whatever other motion is appropriate to the case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:9 — “To consider another motion on the same subject, it is necessary first to take the question from the table and then to move the new proposal as a substitute, or to make whatever other motion is appropriate to the case.”

   C. Suspend the Rules

See RONR (12th ed.) §17:10 — “Taking up the desired business may require an additional motion after the question has been laid on the table (see Suspend the Rules, 25;”

**1140.** `[C0495 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Steps That May Be Required When Laying a Question on the Table.
   A. Incidental

   C. Procedures

**1141.** `[C0495 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Laying a question on the table with the idea of _____ to something else does not suspend any rules or set aside an order of business that may interfere with doing the thing desired at the time.

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table — “Renewal of the Motion to Lay on the Table;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:11 — “Renewal of the Motion to Lay on the Table;”

**1143.** `[C0496 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to _____ (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.
   A. Speech
   B. charge
   C. amends
   D. Recess

   *Answer:* D) Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:11 — “(This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.”

**1144.** `[C0496 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to _____ (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “(This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:11 — “(This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.”

**1145.** `[C0496 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of the Motion to Lay on the Table;

   *Answer:* Renewal — “Renewal of the Motion to Lay on the Table;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:11 — “Renewal of the Motion to Lay on the Table;”

**1146.** `[C0496 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to Lay on the Table that has been voted down can be renewed, or a question that has been taken from the table can be laid on the table again, subject to the following condition in either case: A motion made the same day to lay the same question on the table is in order only after material progress in business or debate has been made, or when an _____ urgent matter requires immediate attention.
   A. landscaped
   B. convention
   C. settlement
   D. unforeseen

   *Answer:* D) unforeseen. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:11 — “A motion to Lay on the Table that has been voted down can be renewed, or a question that has been taken from the table can be laid on the table again, subject to the following condition in either case: A motion made the same day to lay the same question on the table is in order only after material progress in business or debate has been made, or when an unforeseen urgent matter requires immediate attention.”

**1147.** `[C0497 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Laying the Pending Questions on the Table After Debate _____.

   *Answer:* Has Been Closed — “Laying the Pending Questions on the Table After Debate Has Been Closed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:12 — “Laying the Pending Questions on the Table After Debate Has Been Closed.”

**1148.** `[C0497 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If debate has been closed by ordering the _____ or by the expiration of the time to which debate was limited, then up until the moment of taking the last vote under the order, the questions still before the assembly can be laid on the table.
   A. Effect on Appeals
   B. Agenda or Program
   C. Previous Question
   D. adjourned meeting

   *Answer:* C) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:12 — “If debate has been closed by ordering the Previous Question or by the expiration of the time to which debate was limited, then up until the moment of taking the last vote under the order, the questions still before the assembly can be laid on the table.”

**1149.** `[C0497 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Laying the _____ Questions on the Table After Debate Has Been Closed.

   *Answer:* Pending — “Laying the Pending Questions on the Table After Debate Has Been Closed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:12 — “Laying the Pending Questions on the Table After Debate Has Been Closed.”

**1150.** `[C0497 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If debate has been closed by ordering the Previous Question or by the _____ of the time to which debate was limited, then up until the moment of taking the last vote under the order, the questions still before the assembly can be laid on the table.
   A. privileged
   B. persuasive
   C. expiration
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) expiration. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:12 — “If debate has been closed by ordering the Previous Question or by the expiration of the time to which debate was limited, then up until the moment of taking the last vote under the order, the questions still before the assembly can be laid on the table.”

**1151.** `[C0497 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, while a _____ and an amendment are pending, if the Previous Question is ordered on both motions, it is in order to lay the resolution on the table, carrying with it the adhering amendment.

   *Answer:* resolution — “Thus, while a resolution and an amendment are pending, if the Previous Question is ordered on both motions, it is in order to lay the resolution on the table, carrying with it the adhering amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:12 — “Thus, while a resolution and an amendment are pending, if the Previous Question is ordered on both motions, it is in order to lay the resolution on the table, carrying with it the adhering amendment.”

**1152.** `[C0498 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As stated at the beginning of this section, the motion to _____ is subject to a number of incorrect uses that must be avoided.
   A. extending limits
   B. Lay on the Table
   C. Verifying a Vote
   D. Types of Reports

   *Answer:* B) Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:13 — “As stated at the beginning of this section, the motion to Lay on the Table is subject to a number of incorrect uses that must be avoided.”

**1153.** `[C0498 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As stated at the _____ of this section, the motion to Lay on the Table is subject to a number of incorrect uses that must be avoided.

   *Answer:* beginning — “As stated at the beginning of this section, the motion to Lay on the Table is subject to a number of incorrect uses that must be avoided.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:13 — “As stated at the beginning of this section, the motion to Lay on the Table is subject to a number of incorrect uses that must be avoided.”

**1154.** `[C0499 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, if members who command a _____ wish to bring up a measure out of its order but lack the two thirds required to suspend the rules to do so, they may lay each intervening matter on the table in succession, until the desired matter is reached.

   *Answer:* majority — “However, if members who command a majority wish to bring up a measure out of its order but lack the two thirds required to suspend the rules to do so, they may lay each intervening matter on the table in succession, until the desired matter is reached.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:14 — “However, if members who command a majority wish to bring up a measure out of its order but lack the two thirds required to suspend the rules to do so, they may lay each intervening matter on the table in succession, until the desired matter is reached.”

**1155.** `[C0499 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is not in order to move to lay a pending question on the table if there is _____ no other matter requiring immediate attention.
   A. evidently
   B. Crestwood
   C. amendment
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* A) evidently. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:14 — “It is not in order to move to lay a pending question on the table if there is evidently no other matter requiring immediate attention.”

**1156.** `[C0499 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, if members who command a majority wish to bring up a measure out of its order but lack the two thirds required to suspend the rules to do so, they may lay each _____ matter on the table in succession, until the desired matter is reached.

   *Answer:* intervening — “However, if members who command a majority wish to bring up a measure out of its order but lack the two thirds required to suspend the rules to do so, they may lay each intervening matter on the table in succession, until the desired matter is reached.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:14 — “However, if members who command a majority wish to bring up a measure out of its order but lack the two thirds required to suspend the rules to do so, they may lay each intervening matter on the table in succession, until the desired matter is reached.”

**1157.** `[C0499 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This is proper because their evident object is not to suppress without debate the items laid on the table, but instead to advance _____ of something they consider more urgent.
   A. consideration
   B. immediately
   C. Preparation
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:14 — “This is proper because their evident object is not to suppress without debate the items laid on the table, but instead to advance consideration of something they consider more urgent.”

**1158.** `[C0499 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is not in order to move to lay a pending question on the table if there is _____ no other matter requiring immediate attention.

   *Answer:* evidently — “It is not in order to move to lay a pending question on the table if there is evidently no other matter requiring immediate attention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:14 — “It is not in order to move to lay a pending question on the table if there is evidently no other matter requiring immediate attention.”

**1159.** `[C0500 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ is often incorrectly used and wrongly admitted as in order with the intention of either killing an embarrassing question without a direct vote, or of suppressing a question without debate.

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table — “The motion to Lay on the Table is often incorrectly used and wrongly admitted as in order with the intention of either killing an embarrassing question without a direct vote, or of suppressing a question without debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:15 — “The motion to Lay on the Table is often incorrectly used and wrongly admitted as in order with the intention of either killing an embarrassing question without a direct vote, or of suppressing a question without debate.”

**1160.** `[C0500 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: the second is in violation of the fundamental principle of parliamentary law that only a _____ vote can rightfully suppress a main question without allowing free debate.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* D) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:15 — “the second is in violation of the fundamental principle of parliamentary law that only a two-thirds vote can rightfully suppress a main question without allowing free debate.”

**1161.** `[C0500 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to Lay on the Table is often incorrectly used and wrongly admitted as in order with the intention of either killing an _____ question without a direct vote, or of suppressing a question without debate.

   *Answer:* embarrassing — “The motion to Lay on the Table is often incorrectly used and wrongly admitted as in order with the intention of either killing an embarrassing question without a direct vote, or of suppressing a question without debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:15 — “The motion to Lay on the Table is often incorrectly used and wrongly admitted as in order with the intention of either killing an embarrassing question without a direct vote, or of suppressing a question without debate.”

**1162.** `[C0500 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The first of these two uses is unsafe if there is any _____ on the issue;
   A. Motions
   B. Program
   C. contest
   D. adjourn

   *Answer:* C) contest. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:15 — “The first of these two uses is unsafe if there is any contest on the issue;”

**1163.** `[C0500 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: the second is in violation of the fundamental principle of _____ law that only a two-thirds vote can rightfully suppress a main question without allowing free debate.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “the second is in violation of the fundamental principle of parliamentary law that only a two-thirds vote can rightfully suppress a main question without allowing free debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:15 — “the second is in violation of the fundamental principle of parliamentary law that only a two-thirds vote can rightfully suppress a main question without allowing free debate.”

**1164.** `[C0501 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ were to lay a question on the table, erroneously supposing that it thereby becomes dead, some of those who voted with the majority might leave before the time of final adjournment and the minority might all stay.

   *Answer:* majority — “If the majority were to lay a question on the table, erroneously supposing that it thereby becomes dead, some of those who voted with the majority might leave before the time of final adjournment and the minority might all stay.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:16 — “If the majority were to lay a question on the table, erroneously supposing that it thereby becomes dead, some of those who voted with the majority might leave before the time of final adjournment and the minority might all stay.”

**1165.** `[C0501 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the majority were to lay a question on the table, _____ supposing that it thereby becomes dead, some of those who voted with the majority might leave before the time of final adjournment and the minority might all stay.
   A. Unfinished
   B. erroneously
   C. interrupted
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) erroneously. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:16 — “If the majority were to lay a question on the table, erroneously supposing that it thereby becomes dead, some of those who voted with the majority might leave before the time of final adjournment and the minority might all stay.”

**1166.** `[C0501 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The real minority might thus become a temporary majority, take the question from the table, and act upon it in the absence of many _____ parties.

   *Answer:* interested — “The real minority might thus become a temporary majority, take the question from the table, and act upon it in the absence of many interested parties.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:16 — “The real minority might thus become a temporary majority, take the question from the table, and act upon it in the absence of many interested parties.”

**1167.** `[C0501 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The real minority might thus become a temporary majority, take the question from the table, and act upon it in the absence of many _____ parties.
   A. resolution
   B. Permission
   C. assemblies
   D. interested

   *Answer:* D) interested. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:16 — “The real minority might thus become a temporary majority, take the question from the table, and act upon it in the absence of many interested parties.”

**1168.** `[C0502 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the situations that give rise to improper use of the motion to _____, the correct procedures are as follows:

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table — “In the situations that give rise to improper use of the motion to Lay on the Table, the correct procedures are as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:17 — “In the situations that give rise to improper use of the motion to Lay on the Table, the correct procedures are as follows:”

**1169.** `[C0502 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the _____ that give rise to improper use of the motion to Lay on the Table, the correct procedures are as follows:
   A. authorizes
   B. recognized
   C. Incidental
   D. situations

   *Answer:* D) situations. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:17 — “In the situations that give rise to improper use of the motion to Lay on the Table, the correct procedures are as follows:”

**1170.** `[C0503 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to dispose of a question without a direct vote, the suitable method is to use the motion to _____ Indefinitely.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “If it is desired to dispose of a question without a direct vote, the suitable method is to use the motion to Postpone Indefinitely.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:18 — “If it is desired to dispose of a question without a direct vote, the suitable method is to use the motion to Postpone Indefinitely.”

**1171.** `[C0503 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is desired to dispose of a question without a direct vote, the suitable method is to use the motion to Postpone _____.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Parliamentary
   C. Organization
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:18 — “If it is desired to dispose of a question without a direct vote, the suitable method is to use the motion to Postpone Indefinitely.”

**1172.** `[C0503 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is desired to do this without further debate, the motion to Postpone Indefinitely can be followed immediately by a motion for the _____.
   A. Previous Question
   B. Call of the House
   C. board of trustees
   D. Robert’s Rules of

   *Answer:* A) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:18 — “If it is desired to do this without further debate, the motion to Postpone Indefinitely can be followed immediately by a motion for the Previous Question.”

**1173.** `[C0503 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to do this without further debate, the motion to Postpone Indefinitely can be followed immediately by a motion for the _____.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If it is desired to do this without further debate, the motion to Postpone Indefinitely can be followed immediately by a motion for the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:18 — “If it is desired to do this without further debate, the motion to Postpone Indefinitely can be followed immediately by a motion for the Previous Question.”

**1174.** `[C0504 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For cases where _____ (14) is the proper motion in lieu of an incorrectly used motion to Lay on the Table, see “Form and Example,” below.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “For cases where Postpone (14) is the proper motion in lieu of an incorrectly used motion to Lay on the Table, see “Form and Example,” below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:19 — “For cases where Postpone (14) is the proper motion in lieu of an incorrectly used motion to Lay on the Table, see “Form and Example,” below.”

**1175.** `[C0504 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For cases where Postpone (14) is the proper motion in lieu of an incorrectly used motion to _____ on the Table, see “Form and Example,” below.
   A. Lay
   B. Rules
   C. Other
   D. Order

   *Answer:* A) Lay. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:19 — “For cases where Postpone (14) is the proper motion in lieu of an incorrectly used motion to Lay on the Table, see “Form and Example,” below.”

**1176.** `[C0504 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is believed that any discussion of a particular original main motion might do harm, the proper course is to raise Objection to the _____ of the Question (26) before its consideration has begun.
   A. Consideration
   B. adjournment
   C. President-Elect
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) Consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:19 — “If it is believed that any discussion of a particular original main motion might do harm, the proper course is to raise Objection to the Consideration of the Question (26) before its consideration has begun.”

**1177.** `[C0504 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is believed that any discussion of a particular original main motion might do harm, the proper course is to raise Objection to the _____ of the Question (26) before its consideration has begun.

   *Answer:* Consideration — “If it is believed that any discussion of a particular original main motion might do harm, the proper course is to raise Objection to the Consideration of the Question (26) before its consideration has begun.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:19 — “If it is believed that any discussion of a particular original main motion might do harm, the proper course is to raise Objection to the Consideration of the Question (26) before its consideration has begun.”

**1178.** `[C0505 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or “I move that the _____ be laid on the table.” (It is preferable to avoid moving “to table” a motion, or “that the motion be tabled.”)

   *Answer:* resolution — “or “I move that the resolution be laid on the table.” (It is preferable to avoid moving “to table” a motion, or “that the motion be tabled.”)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:20 — “or “I move that the resolution be laid on the table.” (It is preferable to avoid moving “to table” a motion, or “that the motion be tabled.”)”

**1179.** `[C0505 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or “I move that the _____ be laid on the table.” (It is preferable to avoid moving “to table” a motion, or “that the motion be tabled.”)
   A. privileged
   B. resolution
   C. convention
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:20 — “or “I move that the resolution be laid on the table.” (It is preferable to avoid moving “to table” a motion, or “that the motion be tabled.”)”

**1180.** `[C0506 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In moving it, a member can mention its intended purpose or name a time at which he plans to move that the question be taken from the table, but he cannot move to lay a question “on the table until after the completion of…,” or, “on the table until 2 P.M.” Rather than always ruling that such a motion is not in order, however, the chair should properly treat it as a motion “to _____ the question until…”;

   *Answer:* postpone — “In moving it, a member can mention its intended purpose or name a time at which he plans to move that the question be taken from the table, but he cannot move to lay a question “on the table until after the completion of…,” or, “on the table until 2 P.M.” Rather than always ruling that such a motion is not in order, however, the chair should properly treat it as a motion “to postpone the question until…”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:21 — “In moving it, a member can mention its intended purpose or name a time at which he plans to move that the question be taken from the table, but he cannot move to lay a question “on the table until after the completion of…,” or, “on the table until 2 P.M.” Rather than always ruling that such a motion is not in order, however, the chair should properly treat it as a motion “to postpone the question until…”;”

**1181.** `[C0506 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This motion, as explained earlier, is _____ and cannot be qualified in any way.
   A. undebatable
   B. Particular
   C. Organization
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) undebatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:21 — “This motion, as explained earlier, is undebatable and cannot be qualified in any way.”

**1182.** `[C0506 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In moving it, a member can mention its intended purpose or name a time at which he plans to move that the question be taken from the table, but he cannot move to lay a question “on the table until after the _____ of…,” or, “on the table until 2 P.M.” Rather than always ruling that such a motion is not in order, however, the chair should properly treat it as a motion “to postpone the question until…”;

   *Answer:* completion — “In moving it, a member can mention its intended purpose or name a time at which he plans to move that the question be taken from the table, but he cannot move to lay a question “on the table until after the completion of…,” or, “on the table until 2 P.M.” Rather than always ruling that such a motion is not in order, however, the chair should properly treat it as a motion “to postpone the question until…”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:21 — “In moving it, a member can mention its intended purpose or name a time at which he plans to move that the question be taken from the table, but he cannot move to lay a question “on the table until after the completion of…,” or, “on the table until 2 P.M.” Rather than always ruling that such a motion is not in order, however, the chair should properly treat it as a motion “to postpone the question until…”;”

**1183.** `[C0506 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: that is, he should state the motion as _____ in that form unless the motion to Postpone is not in order at the time.
   A. Standard
   B. Previous
   C. admitted
   D. chairman

   *Answer:* C) admitted. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:21 — “that is, he should state the motion as admitted in that form unless the motion to Postpone is not in order at the time.”

**1184.** `[C0506 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This motion, as explained earlier, is _____ and cannot be qualified in any way.

   *Answer:* undebatable — “This motion, as explained earlier, is undebatable and cannot be qualified in any way.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:21 — “This motion, as explained earlier, is undebatable and cannot be qualified in any way.”

**1185.** `[C0507 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since the motion to _____ can be neither debated nor amended, the chair puts it to a vote immediately after stating the question on it, as follows:

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table — “Since the motion to Lay on the Table can be neither debated nor amended, the chair puts it to a vote immediately after stating the question on it, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:22 — “Since the motion to Lay on the Table can be neither debated nor amended, the chair puts it to a vote immediately after stating the question on it, as follows:”

**1186.** `[C0507 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since the motion to Lay on the Table can be neither debated nor amended, the chair puts it to a vote _____ after stating the question on it, as follows:

   C. Organization
