   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:22 — “Since the motion to Lay on the Table can be neither debated nor amended, the chair puts it to a vote immediately after stating the question on it, as follows:”

**1187.** `[C0508 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For certain limited purposes not involving debate or amendment—such as to make a privileged motion or a motion to _____ (37)—a member can claim the floor while the motion to Lay on the Table is pending.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “For certain limited purposes not involving debate or amendment—such as to make a privileged motion or a motion to Reconsider (37)—a member can claim the floor while the motion to Lay on the Table is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:23 — “For certain limited purposes not involving debate or amendment—such as to make a privileged motion or a motion to Reconsider (37)—a member can claim the floor while the motion to Lay on the Table is pending.”

**1188.** `[C0508 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For certain limited purposes not involving debate or amendment—such as to make a privileged motion or a motion to Reconsider (37)—a member can claim the floor while the motion to _____ is pending.
   A. Form and Example
   B. essential to the
   C. Lay on the Table
   D. in good standing

   *Answer:* C) Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:23 — “For certain limited purposes not involving debate or amendment—such as to make a privileged motion or a motion to Reconsider (37)—a member can claim the floor while the motion to Lay on the Table is pending.”

**1189.** `[C0508 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: President!”— immediately _____ the chair has said, “It is moved and seconded to lay the pending question(s) on the table,” and before the vote is taken.

   *Answer:* after — “President!”— immediately after the chair has said, “It is moved and seconded to lay the pending question(s) on the table,” and before the vote is taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:23 — “President!”— immediately after the chair has said, “It is moved and seconded to lay the pending question(s) on the table,” and before the vote is taken.”

**1190.** `[C0508 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: President!”— immediately after the chair has said, “It is moved and seconded to lay the pending question(s) on the table,” and _____ the vote is taken.
   A. amends
   B. before
   C. agenda
   D. Motion

   *Answer:* B) before. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:23 — “President!”— immediately after the chair has said, “It is moved and seconded to lay the pending question(s) on the table,” and before the vote is taken.”

**1191.** `[C0508 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To do so, the member rises and _____ the chair by calling out “Mr.
   A. interrupts
   B. assemblies
   C. authorizes
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) interrupts. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:23 — “To do so, the member rises and interrupts the chair by calling out “Mr.”

**1192.** `[C0509 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a question has been laid on the table, if further action by the assembly is needed to reach the desired business, the chair _____ says, for example, “Is there a motion to suspend the rules that interfere with hearing the speaker at this time?” (Or, “The chair will entertain a motion to…”)

   *Answer:* immediately — “After a question has been laid on the table, if further action by the assembly is needed to reach the desired business, the chair immediately says, for example, “Is there a motion to suspend the rules that interfere with hearing the speaker at this time?” (Or, “The chair will entertain a motion to…”)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:24 — “After a question has been laid on the table, if further action by the assembly is needed to reach the desired business, the chair immediately says, for example, “Is there a motion to suspend the rules that interfere with hearing the speaker at this time?” (Or, “The chair will entertain a motion to…”)”

**1193.** `[C0509 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a question has been laid on the table, if further action by the assembly is needed to reach the desired business, the chair _____ says, for example, “Is there a motion to suspend the rules that interfere with hearing the speaker at this time?” (Or, “The chair will entertain a motion to…”)
   A. convention
   B. immediately
   C. Procedures
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:24 — “After a question has been laid on the table, if further action by the assembly is needed to reach the desired business, the chair immediately says, for example, “Is there a motion to suspend the rules that interfere with hearing the speaker at this time?” (Or, “The chair will entertain a motion to…”)”

**1194.** `[C0510 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A Call for the Orders of the Day is a _____ motion by which a member can require the assembly to conform to its agenda, program, or order of business, or to take up a general or special order that is due to come up at the time (14, 41), unless two thirds of those voting wish to do otherwise.

   *Answer:* privileged — “A Call for the Orders of the Day is a privileged motion by which a member can require the assembly to conform to its agenda, program, or order of business, or to take up a general or special order that is due to come up at the time (14, 41), unless two thirds of those voting wish to do otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:1 — “A Call for the Orders of the Day is a privileged motion by which a member can require the assembly to conform to its agenda, program, or order of business, or to take up a general or special order that is due to come up at the time (14, 41), unless two thirds of those voting wish to do otherwise.”

**1195.** `[C0510 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A Call for the Orders of the Day is a _____ motion by which a member can require the assembly to conform to its agenda, program, or order of business, or to take up a general or special order that is due to come up at the time (14, 41), unless two thirds of those voting wish to do otherwise.
   A. Unfinished
   B. Procedures
   C. authorizes
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* D) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:1 — “A Call for the Orders of the Day is a privileged motion by which a member can require the assembly to conform to its agenda, program, or order of business, or to take up a general or special order that is due to come up at the time (14, 41), unless two thirds of those voting wish to do otherwise.”

**1196.** `[C0511 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Taking up business in the prescribed order is of _____ importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.

   *Answer:* substantial — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**1197.** `[C0511 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For business to receive proper _____, officers, committee members, and the delegates who are principally involved in major questions must be able to know the approximate times at which subjects will come up.
   A. resolutions
   B. Organization
   C. consideration
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “For business to receive proper consideration, officers, committee members, and the delegates who are principally involved in major questions must be able to know the approximate times at which subjects will come up.”

**1198.** `[C0511 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Taking up business in the prescribed order is of _____ importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.
   A. substantial
   B. Unfinished
   C. Information
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) substantial. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “Taking up business in the prescribed order is of substantial importance, especially in conventions—which must follow a closely regulated schedule with much of the underlying work taking place off the convention floor in conferences and committees.”

**1199.** `[C0511 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For business to receive proper _____, officers, committee members, and the delegates who are principally involved in major questions must be able to know the approximate times at which subjects will come up.

   *Answer:* consideration — “For business to receive proper consideration, officers, committee members, and the delegates who are principally involved in major questions must be able to know the approximate times at which subjects will come up.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:2 — “For business to receive proper consideration, officers, committee members, and the delegates who are principally involved in major questions must be able to know the approximate times at which subjects will come up.”

**1200.** `[C0512 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the presiding officer _____ performs his duty of announcing the business to come before the assembly in its proper order, there will be no occasion for calling for the orders of the day.

   *Answer:* consistently — “If the presiding officer consistently performs his duty of announcing the business to come before the assembly in its proper order, there will be no occasion for calling for the orders of the day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:3 — “If the presiding officer consistently performs his duty of announcing the business to come before the assembly in its proper order, there will be no occasion for calling for the orders of the day.”

**1201.** `[C0512 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the presiding officer consistently performs his duty of _____ the business to come before the assembly in its proper order, there will be no occasion for calling for the orders of the day.
   A. announcing
   B. settlement
   C. Procedures
   D. committees

   *Answer:* A) announcing. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:3 — “If the presiding officer consistently performs his duty of announcing the business to come before the assembly in its proper order, there will be no occasion for calling for the orders of the day.”

**1202.** `[C0512 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The call must be simply “for the orders of the day” and not for a _____ one, as this motion is only a demand that the proper schedule of business—whatever it is—be followed.

   *Answer:* specified — “The call must be simply “for the orders of the day” and not for a specified one, as this motion is only a demand that the proper schedule of business—whatever it is—be followed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:3 — “The call must be simply “for the orders of the day” and not for a specified one, as this motion is only a demand that the proper schedule of business—whatever it is—be followed.”

**1203.** `[C0512 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In other words, while the member may remind the chair of what is scheduled, he cannot by this call obtain _____ of an order of the day that does not have first priority for consideration at that time.
   A. consideration
   B. Announcements
   C. deliberative
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:3 — “In other words, while the member may remind the chair of what is scheduled, he cannot by this call obtain consideration of an order of the day that does not have first priority for consideration at that time.”

**1204.** `[C0512 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The call must be simply “for the orders of the day” and not for a _____ one, as this motion is only a demand that the proper schedule of business—whatever it is—be followed.
   A. reception
   B. Reporting
   C. committee
   D. specified

   *Answer:* D) specified. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:3 — “The call must be simply “for the orders of the day” and not for a specified one, as this motion is only a demand that the proper schedule of business—whatever it is—be followed.”

**1205.** `[C0513 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Times When a Call for the Orders of _____ Is in Order.

   *Answer:* the Day — “Times When a Call for the Orders of the Day Is in Order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:5 — “Times When a Call for the Orders of the Day Is in Order.”

**1206.** `[C0513 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ conditions under which a Call for the Orders of the Day is in order are as follows:
   A. sustaining
   B. Procedures
   C. Convention
   D. particular

   *Answer:* D) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:5 — “The particular conditions under which a Call for the Orders of the Day is in order are as follows:”

**1207.** `[C0513 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Times When a Call for the Orders of _____ Is in Order.
   A. the Day
   B. sine die
   C. Point of
   D. at the

   *Answer:* A) the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:5 — “Times When a Call for the Orders of the Day Is in Order.”

**1208.** `[C0513 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ conditions under which a Call for the Orders of the Day is in order are as follows:

   *Answer:* particular — “The particular conditions under which a Call for the Orders of the Day is in order are as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:5 — “The particular conditions under which a Call for the Orders of the Day is in order are as follows:”

**1209.** `[C0514 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In contrast to the _____ Call for the Orders of the Day, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.

   *Answer:* privileged — “In contrast to the privileged Call for the Orders of the Day, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:7 — “In contrast to the privileged Call for the Orders of the Day, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.”

**1210.** `[C0514 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The orders of the day as a whole cannot be laid on the table or postponed, but an _____ order of the day when actually pending can be so disposed of.
   A. landscaped
   B. Procedures
   C. individual
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) individual. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:7 — “The orders of the day as a whole cannot be laid on the table or postponed, but an individual order of the day when actually pending can be so disposed of.”

**1211.** `[C0514 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As soon as the orders of the day that have interrupted business that was pending are completed, the interrupted business is taken up again at the point at which it was _____.

   *Answer:* discontinued — “As soon as the orders of the day that have interrupted business that was pending are completed, the interrupted business is taken up again at the point at which it was discontinued.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:7 — “As soon as the orders of the day that have interrupted business that was pending are completed, the interrupted business is taken up again at the point at which it was discontinued.”

**1212.** `[C0514 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In contrast to the _____ Call for the Orders of the Day, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.
   A. subsidiary
   B. landscaped
   C. privileged
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:7 — “In contrast to the privileged Call for the Orders of the Day, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.”

**1213.** `[C0514 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The orders of the day as a whole cannot be laid on the table or postponed, but an _____ order of the day when actually pending can be so disposed of.

   *Answer:* individual — “The orders of the day as a whole cannot be laid on the table or postponed, but an individual order of the day when actually pending can be so disposed of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:7 — “The orders of the day as a whole cannot be laid on the table or postponed, but an individual order of the day when actually pending can be so disposed of.”

**1214.** `[C0515 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases, the assembly by a _____ vote can set aside the orders of the day, as follows:

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “In such cases, the assembly by a two-thirds vote can set aside the orders of the day, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:8 — “In such cases, the assembly by a two-thirds vote can set aside the orders of the day, as follows:”

**1215.** `[C0515 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the orders of the day are called for, the chair can, and ordinarily should, immediately either interrupt or conclude _____ of the pending question (in accordance with the rules in 41) and proceed to take up the business prescribed for the present time.
   A. representatives
   B. subsequently
   C. consideration
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:8 — “When the orders of the day are called for, the chair can, and ordinarily should, immediately either interrupt or conclude consideration of the pending question (in accordance with the rules in 41) and proceed to take up the business prescribed for the present time.”

**1216.** `[C0515 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such cases, the assembly by a _____ vote can set aside the orders of the day, as follows:
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* A) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:8 — “In such cases, the assembly by a two-thirds vote can set aside the orders of the day, as follows:”

**1217.** `[C0515 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the orders of the day are called for, the chair can, and ordinarily should, immediately either interrupt or conclude _____ of the pending question (in accordance with the rules in 41) and proceed to take up the business prescribed for the present time.

   *Answer:* consideration — “When the orders of the day are called for, the chair can, and ordinarily should, immediately either interrupt or conclude consideration of the pending question (in accordance with the rules in 41) and proceed to take up the business prescribed for the present time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:8 — “When the orders of the day are called for, the chair can, and ordinarily should, immediately either interrupt or conclude consideration of the pending question (in accordance with the rules in 41) and proceed to take up the business prescribed for the present time.”

**1218.** `[C0516 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The form of the motion is as follows: To call for the orders of the day, a member rises and, addressing the chair without waiting for _____, says, “Mr.

   *Answer:* recognition — “The form of the motion is as follows: To call for the orders of the day, a member rises and, addressing the chair without waiting for recognition, says, “Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:9 — “The form of the motion is as follows: To call for the orders of the day, a member rises and, addressing the chair without waiting for recognition, says, “Mr.”

**1219.** `[C0516 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: President, I call for the orders of the day,” or “Madam President, I demand the regular order.” The member can, if _____, remind the chair of the matter set for that time.
   A. secondary
   B. debatable
   C. necessary
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* C) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:9 — “President, I call for the orders of the day,” or “Madam President, I demand the regular order.” The member can, if necessary, remind the chair of the matter set for that time.”

**1220.** `[C0516 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The form of the motion is as follows: To call for the orders of the day, a member rises and, addressing the chair without waiting for _____, says, “Mr.
   A. convention
   B. recognition
   C. Incidental
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:9 — “The form of the motion is as follows: To call for the orders of the day, a member rises and, addressing the chair without waiting for recognition, says, “Mr.”

**1221.** `[C0516 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: President, I call for the orders of the day,” or “Madam President, I demand the regular order.” The member can, if _____, remind the chair of the matter set for that time.

   *Answer:* necessary — “President, I call for the orders of the day,” or “Madam President, I demand the regular order.” The member can, if necessary, remind the chair of the matter set for that time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:9 — “President, I call for the orders of the day,” or “Madam President, I demand the regular order.” The member can, if necessary, remind the chair of the matter set for that time.”

**1222.** `[C0517 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: That time has now arrived, but a member is _____ on a pending question.

   *Answer:* speaking — “That time has now arrived, but a member is speaking on a pending question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:10 — “That time has now arrived, but a member is speaking on a pending question.”

**1223.** `[C0517 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: That time has now arrived, but a member is _____ on a pending question.
   A. speaking
   B. decision
   C. dilatory
   D. assuming

   *Answer:* A) speaking. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:10 — “That time has now arrived, but a member is speaking on a pending question.”

**1224.** `[C0518 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After _____ of the resolution is completed, the former business is resumed where it was left off:

   *Answer:* consideration — “After consideration of the resolution is completed, the former business is resumed where it was left off:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:11 — “After consideration of the resolution is completed, the former business is resumed where it was left off:”

**1225.** `[C0518 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After _____ of the resolution is completed, the former business is resumed where it was left off:
   A. Preparation
   B. Organization
   C. consideration
   D. Information

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:11 — “After consideration of the resolution is completed, the former business is resumed where it was left off:”

**1226.** `[C0519 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate _____ because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.

   *Answer:* consideration — “To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:1 — “To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.”

**1227.** `[C0519 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate _____ because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.
   A. requirement
   B. consideration
   C. Organization
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:1 — “To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.”

**1228.** `[C0520 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: the chair must then rule (subject to appeal, 24) whether the request or motion is in fact a question of _____ and, if so, whether it is urgent enough to interrupt the pending business.

   *Answer:* privilege — “the chair must then rule (subject to appeal, 24) whether the request or motion is in fact a question of privilege and, if so, whether it is urgent enough to interrupt the pending business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:2 — “the chair must then rule (subject to appeal, 24) whether the request or motion is in fact a question of privilege and, if so, whether it is urgent enough to interrupt the pending business.”

**1229.** `[C0520 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: the chair must then rule (subject to appeal, 24) whether the request or motion is in fact a question of _____ and, if so, whether it is urgent enough to interrupt the pending business.
   A. rejecting
   B. president
   C. postponed
   D. privilege

   *Answer:* D) privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:2 — “the chair must then rule (subject to appeal, 24) whether the request or motion is in fact a question of privilege and, if so, whether it is urgent enough to interrupt the pending business.”

**1230.** `[C0521 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a _____ (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it interrupted, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.

   *Answer:* request — “When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a request (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it interrupted, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a request (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it interrupted, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.”

**1231.** `[C0521 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is important to understand the _____ between the device Raise a Question of Privilege and the question of privilege itself.
   A. interrupted
   B. distinction
   C. authorizes
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) distinction. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “It is important to understand the distinction between the device Raise a Question of Privilege and the question of privilege itself.”

**1232.** `[C0521 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The point to be decided in connection with the former is whether a certain question shall be admitted for _____ with the status and priority of the latter.

   *Answer:* consideration — “The point to be decided in connection with the former is whether a certain question shall be admitted for consideration with the status and priority of the latter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “The point to be decided in connection with the former is whether a certain question shall be admitted for consideration with the status and priority of the latter.”

**1233.** `[C0521 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a request (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it _____, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.
   A. adjournment
   B. Organization
   C. constitution
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* D) interrupted. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a request (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it interrupted, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.”

**1234.** `[C0521 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is important to understand the distinction between the device Raise a Question of _____ and the question of privilege itself.
   A. debatable
   B. Privilege
   C. amendment
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* B) Privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “It is important to understand the distinction between the device Raise a Question of Privilege and the question of privilege itself.”

**1235.** `[C0522 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Questions of privilege or motions growing out of them should not be confused with “_____ motions” (or “privileged questions”).

   *Answer:* privileged — “Questions of privilege or motions growing out of them should not be confused with “privileged motions” (or “privileged questions”).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:4 — “Questions of privilege or motions growing out of them should not be confused with “privileged motions” (or “privileged questions”).”

**1236.** `[C0522 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Questions of privilege or motions growing out of them should not be confused with “_____ motions” (or “privileged questions”).
   A. subsidiary
   B. sustaining
   C. privileged
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* C) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:4 — “Questions of privilege or motions growing out of them should not be confused with “privileged motions” (or “privileged questions”).”

**1237.** `[C0523 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The eight characteristics below apply only to the device of _____ a question of privilege;

   *Answer:* raising — “The eight characteristics below apply only to the device of raising a question of privilege;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:5 — “The eight characteristics below apply only to the device of raising a question of privilege;”

**1238.** `[C0523 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The eight _____ below apply only to the device of raising a question of privilege;
   A. characteristics
   B. Announcements
   C. president-elect
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:5 — “The eight characteristics below apply only to the device of raising a question of privilege;”

**1239.** `[C0524 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Questions of privilege are of two types: (1) those relating to the _____ of the assembly as a whole;

   *Answer:* privileges — “Questions of privilege are of two types: (1) those relating to the privileges of the assembly as a whole;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:7 — “Questions of privilege are of two types: (1) those relating to the privileges of the assembly as a whole;”

**1240.** `[C0524 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Questions of _____ are of two types: (1) those relating to the privileges of the assembly as a whole;
   A. privilege
   B. Precedent
   C. secondary
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* A) privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:7 — “Questions of privilege are of two types: (1) those relating to the privileges of the assembly as a whole;”

**1241.** `[C0524 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Questions of the privileges of the assembly may relate to its _____ or existence;

   *Answer:* organization — “Questions of the privileges of the assembly may relate to its organization or existence;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:7 — “Questions of the privileges of the assembly may relate to its organization or existence;”

**1242.** `[C0524 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: to the comfort of its members with respect to heating, _____, lighting, and noise or other disturbance;
   A. resolutions
   B. constitution
   C. interrupted
   D. ventilation

   *Answer:* D) ventilation. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:7 — “to the comfort of its members with respect to heating, ventilation, lighting, and noise or other disturbance;”

**1243.** `[C0524 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: to the conduct of its officers and _____, or of visitors;

   *Answer:* employees — “to the conduct of its officers and employees, or of visitors;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:7 — “to the conduct of its officers and employees, or of visitors;”

**1244.** `[C0525 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Steps in Raising and _____ of a Question of Privilege.

   *Answer:* Disposing — “Steps in Raising and Disposing of a Question of Privilege.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:8 — “Steps in Raising and Disposing of a Question of Privilege.”

**1245.** `[C0525 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In raising a question of privilege, a member rises, addresses the chair without waiting for _____, and says, “I rise to a question of privilege affecting the assembly,” or “… to a question of personal privilege.”
   A. recognition
   B. Definitely
   C. Preparation
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:8 — “In raising a question of privilege, a member rises, addresses the chair without waiting for recognition, and says, “I rise to a question of privilege affecting the assembly,” or “… to a question of personal privilege.””

**1246.** `[C0525 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Steps in Raising and _____ of a Question of Privilege.
   A. delegates
   B. Disposing
   C. expressly
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* B) Disposing. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:8 — “Steps in Raising and Disposing of a Question of Privilege.”

**1247.** `[C0525 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In raising a question of privilege, a member rises, addresses the chair without waiting for _____, and says, “I rise to a question of privilege affecting the assembly,” or “… to a question of personal privilege.”

   *Answer:* recognition — “In raising a question of privilege, a member rises, addresses the chair without waiting for recognition, and says, “I rise to a question of privilege affecting the assembly,” or “… to a question of personal privilege.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:8 — “In raising a question of privilege, a member rises, addresses the chair without waiting for recognition, and says, “I rise to a question of privilege affecting the assembly,” or “… to a question of personal privilege.””

**1248.** `[C0526 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair, even if he has assigned the floor to another person, directs the member to state his question of _____.

   *Answer:* privilege — “The chair, even if he has assigned the floor to another person, directs the member to state his question of privilege.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:9 — “The chair, even if he has assigned the floor to another person, directs the member to state his question of privilege.”

**1249.** `[C0526 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ on the case, the member then either (a) describes the situation briefly and asks that it be remedied, or (b) if he believes that the matter will require formal action by the assembly, makes a motion covering his question of privilege, and another member seconds it.
   A. Depending
   B. amendment
   C. reception
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* A) Depending. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:9 — “Depending on the case, the member then either (a) describes the situation briefly and asks that it be remedied, or (b) if he believes that the matter will require formal action by the assembly, makes a motion covering his question of privilege, and another member seconds it.”

**1250.** `[C0526 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair at his _____ can ask a member to put into the form of a motion a question of privilege that the member has stated as a request.

   *Answer:* discretion — “The chair at his discretion can ask a member to put into the form of a motion a question of privilege that the member has stated as a request.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:9 — “The chair at his discretion can ask a member to put into the form of a motion a question of privilege that the member has stated as a request.”

**1251.** `[C0526 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the point is simple enough to be promptly adjusted (as in the first example, below) or unless it is in the form of a motion and is not seconded, the chair rules whether the question is a question of privilege, and, if so, whether it is of sufficient urgency to warrant interruption of the existing _____ situation.
   A. interrupted
   B. Preparation
   C. parliamentary
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:9 — “Unless the point is simple enough to be promptly adjusted (as in the first example, below) or unless it is in the form of a motion and is not seconded, the chair rules whether the question is a question of privilege, and, if so, whether it is of sufficient urgency to warrant interruption of the existing parliamentary situation.”

**1252.** `[C0526 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: From this ruling an _____ appeal can be taken.

   *Answer:* undebatable — “From this ruling an undebatable appeal can be taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:9 — “From this ruling an undebatable appeal can be taken.”

**1253.** `[C0527 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion made as a question of privilege is seconded, and if the chair admits it as such and decides that it should be _____ immediately, he states the question on it and proceeds as with any other main motion.

   *Answer:* entertained — “If the motion made as a question of privilege is seconded, and if the chair admits it as such and decides that it should be entertained immediately, he states the question on it and proceeds as with any other main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:10 — “If the motion made as a question of privilege is seconded, and if the chair admits it as such and decides that it should be entertained immediately, he states the question on it and proceeds as with any other main motion.”

**1254.** `[C0527 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the question of privilege has been disposed of, business is resumed at exactly the point at which it was _____.
   A. Information
   B. committees
   C. interrupted
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) interrupted. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:10 — “When the question of privilege has been disposed of, business is resumed at exactly the point at which it was interrupted.”

**1255.** `[C0527 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion made as a question of _____ is seconded, and if the chair admits it as such and decides that it should be entertained immediately, he states the question on it and proceeds as with any other main motion.

   *Answer:* privilege — “If the motion made as a question of privilege is seconded, and if the chair admits it as such and decides that it should be entertained immediately, he states the question on it and proceeds as with any other main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:10 — “If the motion made as a question of privilege is seconded, and if the chair admits it as such and decides that it should be entertained immediately, he states the question on it and proceeds as with any other main motion.”

**1256.** `[C0527 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the question of privilege has been disposed of, business is resumed at exactly the point at which it was _____.

   *Answer:* interrupted — “When the question of privilege has been disposed of, business is resumed at exactly the point at which it was interrupted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:10 — “When the question of privilege has been disposed of, business is resumed at exactly the point at which it was interrupted.”

**1257.** `[C0528 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The forms used in raising a question of privilege include: “I rise to a question of privilege affecting [or “relating to”] the assembly” (or “to a question of the _____ of the assembly”), and “I rise to a question of personal privilege.” The preceding forms should always be adhered to in cases where it is necessary to interrupt a person speaking.

   *Answer:* privileges — “The forms used in raising a question of privilege include: “I rise to a question of privilege affecting [or “relating to”] the assembly” (or “to a question of the privileges of the assembly”), and “I rise to a question of personal privilege.” The preceding forms should always be adhered to in cases where it is necessary to interrupt a person speaking.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:11 — “The forms used in raising a question of privilege include: “I rise to a question of privilege affecting [or “relating to”] the assembly” (or “to a question of the privileges of the assembly”), and “I rise to a question of personal privilege.” The preceding forms should always be adhered to in cases where it is necessary to interrupt a person speaking.”

**1258.** `[C0528 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question of the privileges of the assembly is raised in a small meeting without _____ a speaker, a variation such as “A question of privilege, Mr.
   A. interrupting
   B. presentation
   C. Parliamentary
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) interrupting. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:11 — “When a question of the privileges of the assembly is raised in a small meeting without interrupting a speaker, a variation such as “A question of privilege, Mr.”

**1259.** `[C0528 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The forms used in raising a question of privilege include: “I rise to a question of privilege affecting [or “relating to”] the assembly” (or “to a question of the _____ of the assembly”), and “I rise to a question of personal privilege.” The preceding forms should always be adhered to in cases where it is necessary to interrupt a person speaking.
   A. persuasive
   B. privileges
   C. Particular
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) privileges. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:11 — “The forms used in raising a question of privilege include: “I rise to a question of privilege affecting [or “relating to”] the assembly” (or “to a question of the privileges of the assembly”), and “I rise to a question of personal privilege.” The preceding forms should always be adhered to in cases where it is necessary to interrupt a person speaking.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §19:12 — “The following is an example of a question relating to the privileges of the assembly that can be stated as an informal request and that can be routinely adjusted by the chair:”

See RONR (12th ed.) §19:13 — “Assume that, while an important speech is in progress at a meeting in a large hall with upper windows, workmen begin to operate jackhammers in an alley beside the building.”

   A. privileged
   B. interrupts

   D. Reconsider

**1265.** `[C0530 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that, while an important speech is in progress at a meeting in a large hall with upper windows, workmen begin to operate _____ in an alley beside the building.
   A. interrupted

   C. undebatable
   D. jackhammers

**1267.** `[C0531 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The next example _____ a question of the privileges of the assembly requiring a formal motion which interrupts pending business.

See RONR (12th ed.) §19:14 — “The next example illustrates a question of the privileges of the assembly requiring a formal motion which interrupts pending business.”

**1268.** `[C0531 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an ordinary society these occasions are rare, but in a convention or large assembly a situation of unforeseen _____ may cause such a motion to become appropriate.
   A. presentation
   B. Information
   C. complications
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* C) complications. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:14 — “In an ordinary society these occasions are rare, but in a convention or large assembly a situation of unforeseen complications may cause such a motion to become appropriate.”

**1269.** `[C0531 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The next example _____ a question of the privileges of the assembly requiring a formal motion which interrupts pending business.
   A. Particular
   B. Unfinished
   C. illustrates
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) illustrates. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:14 — “The next example illustrates a question of the privileges of the assembly requiring a formal motion which interrupts pending business.”

**1270.** `[C0531 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an ordinary society these occasions are rare, but in a convention or large assembly a situation of unforeseen _____ may cause such a motion to become appropriate.

   *Answer:* complications — “In an ordinary society these occasions are rare, but in a convention or large assembly a situation of unforeseen complications may cause such a motion to become appropriate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:14 — “In an ordinary society these occasions are rare, but in a convention or large assembly a situation of unforeseen complications may cause such a motion to become appropriate.”

**1271.** `[C0532 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume that, to hear a prominent speaker, an _____ has opened one of its meetings to the public.

   *Answer:* association — “Assume that, to hear a prominent speaker, an association has opened one of its meetings to the public.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:15 — “Assume that, to hear a prominent speaker, an association has opened one of its meetings to the public.”

**1272.** `[C0532 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Because of the speaker’s _____ at a later hour, his address was given first, preceding the business meeting—which was expected to be brief and routine.
   A. Convention
   B. Information
   C. assemblies
   D. commitments

   *Answer:* D) commitments. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:15 — “Because of the speaker’s commitments at a later hour, his address was given first, preceding the business meeting—which was expected to be brief and routine.”

**1273.** `[C0532 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But Member X has surprised this meeting by _____ a resolution dealing with a delicate matter of obvious importance that may call for prompt action by the association.

   *Answer:* introducing — “But Member X has surprised this meeting by introducing a resolution dealing with a delicate matter of obvious importance that may call for prompt action by the association.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:15 — “But Member X has surprised this meeting by introducing a resolution dealing with a delicate matter of obvious importance that may call for prompt action by the association.”

**1274.** `[C0532 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that, to hear a prominent speaker, an _____ has opened one of its meetings to the public.
   A. Permission
   B. Definitely
   C. association
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* C) association. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:15 — “Assume that, to hear a prominent speaker, an association has opened one of its meetings to the public.”

**1275.** `[C0532 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Because of the speaker’s _____ at a later hour, his address was given first, preceding the business meeting—which was expected to be brief and routine.

   *Answer:* commitments — “Because of the speaker’s commitments at a later hour, his address was given first, preceding the business meeting—which was expected to be brief and routine.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:15 — “Because of the speaker’s commitments at a later hour, his address was given first, preceding the business meeting—which was expected to be brief and routine.”

**1276.** `[C0533 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Member Y, sensing that _____ of this question should be kept within the organization, interrupts Member X’s speech on the pending resolution by rising “to a question of privilege relating to the assembly.” As directed by the president, he states the question of privilege:

   *Answer:* consideration — “Member Y, sensing that consideration of this question should be kept within the organization, interrupts Member X’s speech on the pending resolution by rising “to a question of privilege relating to the assembly.” As directed by the president, he states the question of privilege:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:16 — “Member Y, sensing that consideration of this question should be kept within the organization, interrupts Member X’s speech on the pending resolution by rising “to a question of privilege relating to the assembly.” As directed by the president, he states the question of privilege:”

**1277.** `[C0533 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Member Y, sensing that _____ of this question should be kept within the organization, interrupts Member X’s speech on the pending resolution by rising “to a question of privilege relating to the assembly.” As directed by the president, he states the question of privilege:
   A. Organization
   B. consideration
   C. representatives
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:16 — “Member Y, sensing that consideration of this question should be kept within the organization, interrupts Member X’s speech on the pending resolution by rising “to a question of privilege relating to the assembly.” As directed by the president, he states the question of privilege:”

**1278.** `[C0534 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion is adopted, the president expresses _____ to guests.

   *Answer:* appreciation — “If the motion is adopted, the president expresses appreciation to guests.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:17 — “If the motion is adopted, the president expresses appreciation to guests.”

**1279.** `[C0534 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As soon as they have left, he states the resolution that was _____ by the question of privilege, and recognizes Member X, who had the floor.
   A. undebatable
   B. Convention
   C. interrupted
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) interrupted. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:17 — “As soon as they have left, he states the resolution that was interrupted by the question of privilege, and recognizes Member X, who had the floor.”

**1280.** `[C0534 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion is adopted, the president expresses _____ to guests.
   A. Incidental
   B. Information
   C. subsequently
   D. appreciation

   *Answer:* D) appreciation. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:17 — “If the motion is adopted, the president expresses appreciation to guests.”

**1281.** `[C0534 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As soon as they have left, he states the resolution that was _____ by the question of privilege, and recognizes Member X, who had the floor.

   *Answer:* interrupted — “As soon as they have left, he states the resolution that was interrupted by the question of privilege, and recognizes Member X, who had the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:17 — “As soon as they have left, he states the resolution that was interrupted by the question of privilege, and recognizes Member X, who had the floor.”

**1282.** `[C0535 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.

   *Answer:* recess — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**1283.** `[C0535 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A recess is a short _____ in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.
   A. Procedures
   B. Indefinitely
   C. intermission
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) intermission. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**1284.** `[C0535 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A recess may be taken, for example, to count ballots, to secure information, or to allow for informal _____.

   *Answer:* consultation — “A recess may be taken, for example, to count ballots, to secure information, or to allow for informal consultation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess may be taken, for example, to count ballots, to secure information, or to allow for informal consultation.”

**1285.** `[C0535 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A recess may be taken, for example, to count ballots, to secure information, or to allow for informal _____.
   A. Incidental
   B. subsequently
   C. consultation
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* C) consultation. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess may be taken, for example, to count ballots, to secure information, or to allow for informal consultation.”

**1286.** `[C0536 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The privileged motion to _____ (or to Take a Recess) is a motion that a recess begin immediately, made while another question is pending.

   *Answer:* Recess — “The privileged motion to Recess (or to Take a Recess) is a motion that a recess begin immediately, made while another question is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:2 — “The privileged motion to Recess (or to Take a Recess) is a motion that a recess begin immediately, made while another question is pending.”

**1287.** `[C0536 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The privileged motion to Recess (or to _____) is a motion that a recess begin immediately, made while another question is pending.
   A. dispense with
   B. of a Question
   C. Take a Recess
   D. general order

   *Answer:* C) Take a Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:2 — “The privileged motion to Recess (or to Take a Recess) is a motion that a recess begin immediately, made while another question is pending.”

**1288.** `[C0537 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to recess that is made _____ pending (whether the recess is to begin immediately or at a future time) is a main motion, and the eight characteristics given below do not apply to it.

   *Answer:* when no question is — “A motion to recess that is made when no question is pending (whether the recess is to begin immediately or at a future time) is a main motion, and the eight characteristics given below do not apply to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:3 — “A motion to recess that is made when no question is pending (whether the recess is to begin immediately or at a future time) is a main motion, and the eight characteristics given below do not apply to it.”

**1289.** `[C0537 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to recess that is made when no question is _____ (whether the recess is to begin immediately or at a future time) is a main motion, and the eight characteristics given below do not apply to it.
   A. carried
   B. pending
   C. adopted
   D. applied

   *Answer:* B) pending. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:3 — “A motion to recess that is made when no question is pending (whether the recess is to begin immediately or at a future time) is a main motion, and the eight characteristics given below do not apply to it.”

**1290.** `[C0537 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, a motion to recess is privileged only when another question is pending;

   *Answer:* Consequently — “Consequently, a motion to recess is privileged only when another question is pending;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:3 — “Consequently, a motion to recess is privileged only when another question is pending;”

**1291.** `[C0537 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, a motion to recess is privileged only when another question is pending;
   A. Parliamentary
   B. Consequently
   C. immediately
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) Consequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:3 — “Consequently, a motion to recess is privileged only when another question is pending;”

**1292.** `[C0538 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Declaring a Recess When It Has Been Provided For in the _____.

   *Answer:* Agenda or Program — “Declaring a Recess When It Has Been Provided For in the Agenda or Program.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:6 — “Declaring a Recess When It Has Been Provided For in the Agenda or Program.”

**1293.** `[C0538 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ a Recess When It Has Been Provided For in the Agenda or Program.
   A. Declaring
   B. Secretary
   C. Crestwood
   D. rejecting

   *Answer:* A) Declaring. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:6 — “Declaring a Recess When It Has Been Provided For in the Agenda or Program.”

**1294.** `[C0538 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a recess is provided for in the adopted agenda or program of a _____ or other meeting, the chair, without further action by the assembly, announces the fact and simply declares the assembly in recess when the specified time arrives.

   *Answer:* convention — “If a recess is provided for in the adopted agenda or program of a convention or other meeting, the chair, without further action by the assembly, announces the fact and simply declares the assembly in recess when the specified time arrives.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:6 — “If a recess is provided for in the adopted agenda or program of a convention or other meeting, the chair, without further action by the assembly, announces the fact and simply declares the assembly in recess when the specified time arrives.”

**1295.** `[C0538 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair does not announce the recess at the _____ time, a member can call for the orders of the day (18), thereby demanding that the recess be declared.
   A. amendment
   B. scheduled
   C. reception
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* B) scheduled. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:6 — “If the chair does not announce the recess at the scheduled time, a member can call for the orders of the day (18), thereby demanding that the recess be declared.”

**1296.** `[C0538 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a recess is provided for in the adopted agenda or program of a _____ or other meeting, the chair, without further action by the assembly, announces the fact and simply declares the assembly in recess when the specified time arrives.
   A. landscaped
   B. Procedures
   C. convention
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* C) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:6 — “If a recess is provided for in the adopted agenda or program of a convention or other meeting, the chair, without further action by the assembly, announces the fact and simply declares the assembly in recess when the specified time arrives.”

**1297.** `[C0539 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ the Time for Taking a Scheduled Recess.

   *Answer:* Postponing — “Postponing the Time for Taking a Scheduled Recess.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:7 — “Postponing the Time for Taking a Scheduled Recess.”

**1298.** `[C0539 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Postponing the Time for Taking a _____ Recess.
   A. Scheduled
   B. auditors’
   C. Precedent
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* A) Scheduled. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:7 — “Postponing the Time for Taking a Scheduled Recess.”

**1299.** `[C0541 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At the end of the _____ time, the chair gains the attention of the assembly and begins:

   *Answer:* specified — “At the end of the specified time, the chair gains the attention of the assembly and begins:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:10 — “At the end of the specified time, the chair gains the attention of the assembly and begins:”

**1300.** `[C0541 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At the end of the _____ time, the chair gains the attention of the assembly and begins:
   A. Secretary
   B. specified
   C. committee
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* B) specified. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:10 — “At the end of the specified time, the chair gains the attention of the assembly and begins:”

**1301.** `[C0542 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To _____ means to close the meeting (8).

   *Answer:* adjourn — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**1302.** `[C0542 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to adjourn may be a _____ or a main motion depending on a number of conditions.
   A. landscaped
   B. privileged
   C. resolution
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “A motion to adjourn may be a privileged or a main motion depending on a number of conditions.”

**1303.** `[C0542 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The adoption of any motion to adjourn closes the meeting _____ unless the motion specifies a later time for adjourning (but if it does specify such a time it is not a privileged motion).

   *Answer:* immediately — “The adoption of any motion to adjourn closes the meeting immediately unless the motion specifies a later time for adjourning (but if it does specify such a time it is not a privileged motion).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “The adoption of any motion to adjourn closes the meeting immediately unless the motion specifies a later time for adjourning (but if it does specify such a time it is not a privileged motion).”

**1304.** `[C0542 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To _____ means to close the meeting (8).
   A. Charges
   B. adopted
   C. Rescind
   D. adjourn

   *Answer:* D) adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**1305.** `[C0542 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to adjourn may be a _____ or a main motion depending on a number of conditions.

   *Answer:* privileged — “A motion to adjourn may be a privileged or a main motion depending on a number of conditions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “A motion to adjourn may be a privileged or a main motion depending on a number of conditions.”

**1306.** `[C0543 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.

   *Answer:* privileged — “The privileged motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “The privileged motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.”

**1307.** `[C0543 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The privileged motion to _____ (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.
   A. Adjourn
   B. Rescind
   C. Session
   D. abstain

   *Answer:* A) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “The privileged motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.”

**1308.** `[C0543 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: And for the same reason, such a motion has the unique characteristic that, _____ made while no question is pending, it is not debatable or amendable and it remains subject to all of the rules governing the privileged motion to Adjourn (except those that relate to making the motion while business is pending;

   *Answer:* even if it is — “And for the same reason, such a motion has the unique characteristic that, even if it is made while no question is pending, it is not debatable or amendable and it remains subject to all of the rules governing the privileged motion to Adjourn (except those that relate to making the motion while business is pending;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “And for the same reason, such a motion has the unique characteristic that, even if it is made while no question is pending, it is not debatable or amendable and it remains subject to all of the rules governing the privileged motion to Adjourn (except those that relate to making the motion while business is pending;”

**1309.** `[C0543 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: see _____ Descriptive Characteristics).
   A. Meetings
   B. Approved
   C. Postpone
   D. Standard

   *Answer:* D) Standard. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “see Standard Descriptive Characteristics).”

**1310.** `[C0543 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: see Standard _____).

   *Answer:* Descriptive Characteristics — “see Standard Descriptive Characteristics).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “see Standard Descriptive Characteristics).”

**1311.** `[C0544 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In ordinary societies having bylaws that provide for several regular meetings during the year and having no fixed hour for _____ment, a motion “to adjourn,” when unqualified, is always a privileged motion.

   *Answer:* adjourn — “In ordinary societies having bylaws that provide for several regular meetings during the year and having no fixed hour for adjournment, a motion “to adjourn,” when unqualified, is always a privileged motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:4 — “In ordinary societies having bylaws that provide for several regular meetings during the year and having no fixed hour for adjournment, a motion “to adjourn,” when unqualified, is always a privileged motion.”

**1312.** `[C0544 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In ordinary societies having bylaws that provide for several regular meetings during the year and having no fixed hour for _____, a motion “to adjourn,” when unqualified, is always a privileged motion.
   A. adjournment
   B. immediately
   C. Convention
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:4 — “In ordinary societies having bylaws that provide for several regular meetings during the year and having no fixed hour for adjournment, a motion “to adjourn,” when unqualified, is always a privileged motion.”

**1313.** `[C0544 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In meetings of these _____, such a motion to Adjourn is in order regardless of whether business is pending;

   *Answer:* organizations — “In meetings of these organizations, such a motion to Adjourn is in order regardless of whether business is pending;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:4 — “In meetings of these organizations, such a motion to Adjourn is in order regardless of whether business is pending;”

**1314.** `[C0544 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and even when business is not pending, this motion is _____ and is subject to the rules given below.
   A. Particular
   B. Indefinitely
   C. constitution
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* D) undebatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:4 — “and even when business is not pending, this motion is undebatable and is subject to the rules given below.”

**1315.** `[C0544 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In meetings of these _____, such a motion to Adjourn is in order regardless of whether business is pending;
   A. recommendation
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. organizations
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* C) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:4 — “In meetings of these organizations, such a motion to Adjourn is in order regardless of whether business is pending;”

**1316.** `[C0545 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Effect of Adjournment on Pending Business or on an _____.

   *Answer:* Uncompleted Order of Business — “Effect of Adjournment on Pending Business or on an Uncompleted Order of Business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:7 — “Effect of Adjournment on Pending Business or on an Uncompleted Order of Business.”

**1317.** `[C0545 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Effect of _____ on Pending Business or on an Uncompleted Order of Business.
   A. convention
   B. Preparation
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Adjournment

   *Answer:* D) Adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:7 — “Effect of Adjournment on Pending Business or on an Uncompleted Order of Business.”

**1318.** `[C0546 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Adjournment of Bodies Without Regularly Scheduled _____.

   *Answer:* Meetings — “Adjournment of Bodies Without Regularly Scheduled Meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:8 — “Adjournment of Bodies Without Regularly Scheduled Meetings.”

**1319.** `[C0546 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of Bodies Without Regularly Scheduled Meetings.

   *Answer:* Adjournment — “Adjournment of Bodies Without Regularly Scheduled Meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:8 — “Adjournment of Bodies Without Regularly Scheduled Meetings.”

**1320.** `[C0546 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the bylaws of an _____ provide for the calling of a special convention after the regular convention session has been held, this assembly should meet as a distinct session with a body of delegates and alternates that must be chosen anew under provisions established in the bylaws.
   A. undebatable
   B. constitution
   C. adjournment
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:8 — “If the bylaws of an organization provide for the calling of a special convention after the regular convention session has been held, this assembly should meet as a distinct session with a body of delegates and alternates that must be chosen anew under provisions established in the bylaws.”

**1321.** `[C0546 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the bylaws of an _____ provide for the calling of a special convention after the regular convention session has been held, this assembly should meet as a distinct session with a body of delegates and alternates that must be chosen anew under provisions established in the bylaws.

   *Answer:* organization — “If the bylaws of an organization provide for the calling of a special convention after the regular convention session has been held, this assembly should meet as a distinct session with a body of delegates and alternates that must be chosen anew under provisions established in the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:8 — “If the bylaws of an organization provide for the calling of a special convention after the regular convention session has been held, this assembly should meet as a distinct session with a body of delegates and alternates that must be chosen anew under provisions established in the bylaws.”

**1322.** `[C0547 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn _____ (or without day).

   *Answer:* sine die — “When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn sine die (or without day).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:9 — “When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn sine die (or without day).”

**1323.** `[C0547 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, since there usually is no fixed hour for adjournment, the unqualified motion to adjourn is usually privileged in boards or committees.
   A. Particular
   B. Information
   C. subsequently
   D. Consequently

   *Answer:* D) Consequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:9 — “Consequently, since there usually is no fixed hour for adjournment, the unqualified motion to adjourn is usually privileged in boards or committees.”

**1324.** `[C0547 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is _____ to the main motion to adjourn sine die (or without day).

   *Answer:* equivalent — “When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn sine die (or without day).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:9 — “When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn sine die (or without day).”

**1325.** `[C0547 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, since there usually is no fixed hour for adjournment, the unqualified motion to adjourn is usually privileged in boards or committees.

   *Answer:* Consequently — “Consequently, since there usually is no fixed hour for adjournment, the unqualified motion to adjourn is usually privileged in boards or committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:9 — “Consequently, since there usually is no fixed hour for adjournment, the unqualified motion to adjourn is usually privileged in boards or committees.”

**1326.** `[C0548 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Parliamentary Steps That Are in Order While the Privileged Motion to _____ Is Pending, or After the Assembly Has Voted to Adjourn.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “Parliamentary Steps That Are in Order While the Privileged Motion to Adjourn Is Pending, or After the Assembly Has Voted to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:10 — “Parliamentary Steps That Are in Order While the Privileged Motion to Adjourn Is Pending, or After the Assembly Has Voted to Adjourn.”

**1327.** `[C0548 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Steps That Are in Order While the Privileged Motion to Adjourn Is Pending, or After the Assembly Has Voted to Adjourn.
   A. adjournment
   B. Indefinitely
   C. resolutions
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) Parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:10 — “Parliamentary Steps That Are in Order While the Privileged Motion to Adjourn Is Pending, or After the Assembly Has Voted to Adjourn.”

**1328.** `[C0549 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If any matters of the types listed above arise after it has been moved to _____, the chair should state the facts briefly, or a member who rises and addresses the chair for the purpose may do so—or make the necessary motion or give the desired notice—before the vote is taken on the motion to Adjourn.

   *Answer:* adjourn — “If any matters of the types listed above arise after it has been moved to adjourn, the chair should state the facts briefly, or a member who rises and addresses the chair for the purpose may do so—or make the necessary motion or give the desired notice—before the vote is taken on the motion to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:11 — “If any matters of the types listed above arise after it has been moved to adjourn, the chair should state the facts briefly, or a member who rises and addresses the chair for the purpose may do so—or make the necessary motion or give the desired notice—before the vote is taken on the motion to Adjourn.”

**1329.** `[C0549 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any of the above steps that are desired should be taken care of earlier, if _____;
   A. possible
   B. Business
   C. Previous
   D. Approved

   *Answer:* A) possible. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:11 — “Any of the above steps that are desired should be taken care of earlier, if possible;”

**1330.** `[C0549 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but there may sometimes be no such opportunity, _____ in a convention or a session of several meetings that is following an adopted agenda or program (41), or in cases where a meeting of an ordinary society adjourns before completing its regular order of business.

   *Answer:* particularly — “but there may sometimes be no such opportunity, particularly in a convention or a session of several meetings that is following an adopted agenda or program (41), or in cases where a meeting of an ordinary society adjourns before completing its regular order of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:11 — “but there may sometimes be no such opportunity, particularly in a convention or a session of several meetings that is following an adopted agenda or program (41), or in cases where a meeting of an ordinary society adjourns before completing its regular order of business.”

**1331.** `[C0549 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If any matters of the types listed above arise after it has been moved to adjourn, the chair should state the facts briefly, or a member who rises and _____ the chair for the purpose may do so—or make the necessary motion or give the desired notice—before the vote is taken on the motion to Adjourn.
   A. reception
   B. addresses
   C. amendment
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* B) addresses.
