See RONR (12th ed.) §21:11 — “If any matters of the types listed above arise after it has been moved to adjourn, the chair should state the facts briefly, or a member who rises and addresses the chair for the purpose may do so—or make the necessary motion or give the desired notice—before the vote is taken on the motion to Adjourn.”

**1332.** `[C0549 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If something requires action before _____, the member who moved to adjourn can be requested to withdraw his motion.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “If something requires action before adjournment, the member who moved to adjourn can be requested to withdraw his motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:11 — “If something requires action before adjournment, the member who moved to adjourn can be requested to withdraw his motion.”

**1333.** `[C0550 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Regardless of the type of motion by which it is voted to adjourn, the meeting is not closed until the chair has declared that the meeting “is adjourned” (or “stands adjourned”), and members should not leave their seats until this _____ has been made.

   *Answer:* declaration — “Regardless of the type of motion by which it is voted to adjourn, the meeting is not closed until the chair has declared that the meeting “is adjourned” (or “stands adjourned”), and members should not leave their seats until this declaration has been made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:12 — “Regardless of the type of motion by which it is voted to adjourn, the meeting is not closed until the chair has declared that the meeting “is adjourned” (or “stands adjourned”), and members should not leave their seats until this declaration has been made.”

**1334.** `[C0550 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In announcing an _____ vote on a motion to adjourn, the chair should usually pause before declaring the meeting adjourned, saying: “The ayes seem to have it.
   A. convention
   B. Reconsider
   C. affirmative
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) affirmative. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:12 — “In announcing an affirmative vote on a motion to adjourn, the chair should usually pause before declaring the meeting adjourned, saying: “The ayes seem to have it.”

**1335.** `[C0550 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Regardless of the type of motion by which it is voted to adjourn, the meeting is not closed until the chair has declared that the meeting “is _____” (or “stands adjourned”), and members should not leave their seats until this declaration has been made.

   *Answer:* adjourned — “Regardless of the type of motion by which it is voted to adjourn, the meeting is not closed until the chair has declared that the meeting “is adjourned” (or “stands adjourned”), and members should not leave their seats until this declaration has been made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:12 — “Regardless of the type of motion by which it is voted to adjourn, the meeting is not closed until the chair has declared that the meeting “is adjourned” (or “stands adjourned”), and members should not leave their seats until this declaration has been made.”

**1336.** `[C0550 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair learns, immediately after declaring the assembly adjourned, that a member seeking the floor for one of these purposes had risen and addressed the chair before the adjournment was declared, then, since the adjournment was improper and this breach was promptly noted, the chair must call the meeting back to order—but only long enough for the purpose for which the member _____ sought the floor.
   A. Convention
   B. deliberative
   C. requirement
   D. legitimately

   *Answer:* D) legitimately. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:12 — “If the chair learns, immediately after declaring the assembly adjourned, that a member seeking the floor for one of these purposes had risen and addressed the chair before the adjournment was declared, then, since the adjournment was improper and this breach was promptly noted, the chair must call the meeting back to order—but only long enough for the purpose for which the member legitimately sought the floor.”

**1337.** `[C0550 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In announcing an _____ vote on a motion to adjourn, the chair should usually pause before declaring the meeting adjourned, saying: “The ayes seem to have it.

   *Answer:* affirmative — “In announcing an affirmative vote on a motion to adjourn, the chair should usually pause before declaring the meeting adjourned, saying: “The ayes seem to have it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:12 — “In announcing an affirmative vote on a motion to adjourn, the chair should usually pause before declaring the meeting adjourned, saying: “The ayes seem to have it.”

**1338.** `[C0551 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Legitimate Renewal of the Privileged Motion and Its _____.

   *Answer:* Abuses — “Legitimate Renewal of the Privileged Motion and Its Abuses.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “Legitimate Renewal of the Privileged Motion and Its Abuses.”

**1339.** `[C0551 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since a motion to _____ may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.
   A. censure
   B. Ordered
   C. Adjourn
   D. Another

   *Answer:* C) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “Since a motion to Adjourn may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.”

**1340.** `[C0551 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see _____, 39).

   *Answer:* Dilatory Motions — “If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see Dilatory Motions, 39).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see Dilatory Motions, 39).”

**1341.** `[C0551 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since a motion to Adjourn may be voted down because a _____ wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “Since a motion to Adjourn may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.”

**1342.** `[C0551 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by _____ consent (see Dilatory Motions, 39).

   *Answer:* unanimous — “If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see Dilatory Motions, 39).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see Dilatory Motions, 39).”

**1343.** `[C0552 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Cases Where the Assembly Can Adjourn Without a _____.

   *Answer:* Motion — “Cases Where the Assembly Can Adjourn Without a Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:14 — “Cases Where the Assembly Can Adjourn Without a Motion.”

**1344.** `[C0552 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Cases Where the Assembly Can _____ Without a Motion.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “Cases Where the Assembly Can Adjourn Without a Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:14 — “Cases Where the Assembly Can Adjourn Without a Motion.”

**1345.** `[C0552 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an hour for _____ a meeting within a convention or other session of more than one meeting has been scheduled—either in an agenda or program or by the adoption of a motion setting a time—no motion to adjourn is necessary when that hour arrives.
   A. Substitute
   B. Reconsider
   C. Procedures
   D. adjourning

   *Answer:* D) adjourning. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:14 — “If an hour for adjourning a meeting within a convention or other session of more than one meeting has been scheduled—either in an agenda or program or by the adoption of a motion setting a time—no motion to adjourn is necessary when that hour arrives.”

**1346.** `[C0552 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rules stated above regarding _____ steps that are in order after it has been voted to adjourn are applicable in this case also.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The rules stated above regarding parliamentary steps that are in order after it has been voted to adjourn are applicable in this case also.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:14 — “The rules stated above regarding parliamentary steps that are in order after it has been voted to adjourn are applicable in this case also.”

**1347.** `[C0552 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an hour for _____ a meeting within a convention or other session of more than one meeting has been scheduled—either in an agenda or program or by the adoption of a motion setting a time—no motion to adjourn is necessary when that hour arrives.

   *Answer:* adjourning — “If an hour for adjourning a meeting within a convention or other session of more than one meeting has been scheduled—either in an agenda or program or by the adoption of a motion setting a time—no motion to adjourn is necessary when that hour arrives.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:14 — “If an hour for adjourning a meeting within a convention or other session of more than one meeting has been scheduled—either in an agenda or program or by the adoption of a motion setting a time—no motion to adjourn is necessary when that hour arrives.”

**1348.** `[C0553 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it appears that there is no further business in a meeting of an ordinary local society that normally goes through a complete order of business (41) at each regular meeting (9), the chair, instead of waiting or calling for a motion to adjourn, can ask, “Is there any further business?” If there is no response, the chair can then say, “Since there is no further business, the meeting is _____.”

   *Answer:* adjourned — “When it appears that there is no further business in a meeting of an ordinary local society that normally goes through a complete order of business (41) at each regular meeting (9), the chair, instead of waiting or calling for a motion to adjourn, can ask, “Is there any further business?” If there is no response, the chair can then say, “Since there is no further business, the meeting is adjourned.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:15 — “When it appears that there is no further business in a meeting of an ordinary local society that normally goes through a complete order of business (41) at each regular meeting (9), the chair, instead of waiting or calling for a motion to adjourn, can ask, “Is there any further business?” If there is no response, the chair can then say, “Since there is no further business, the meeting is adjourned.””

**1349.** `[C0553 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it appears that there is no further business in a meeting of an ordinary local society that normally goes through a complete order of business (41) at each regular meeting (9), the chair, instead of waiting or calling for a motion to adjourn, can ask, “Is there any further business?” If there is no response, the chair can then say, “Since there is no further business, the meeting is _____.”
   A. paragraph
   B. Objection
   C. exhausted
   D. adjourned

   *Answer:* D) adjourned. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:15 — “When it appears that there is no further business in a meeting of an ordinary local society that normally goes through a complete order of business (41) at each regular meeting (9), the chair, instead of waiting or calling for a motion to adjourn, can ask, “Is there any further business?” If there is no response, the chair can then say, “Since there is no further business, the meeting is adjourned.””

**1350.** `[C0554 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a _____ motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.

   *Answer:* main — “The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a main motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:16 — “The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a main motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.”

**1351.** `[C0554 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: on April 10,” or “I move that the convention adjourn _____ [or “adjourn without day”].”
   A. Point of
   B. roll call
   C. a Session
   D. sine die

   *Answer:* D) sine die. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:16 — “on April 10,” or “I move that the convention adjourn sine die [or “adjourn without day”].””

**1352.** `[C0554 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The following forms may be used for either a _____ or a main motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.

   *Answer:* privileged — “The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a main motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:16 — “The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a main motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.”

**1353.** `[C0554 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: on April 10,” or “I move that the _____ adjourn sine die [or “adjourn without day”].”
   A. landscaped
   B. Particular
   C. Procedures
   D. convention

   *Answer:* D) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:16 — “on April 10,” or “I move that the convention adjourn sine die [or “adjourn without day”].””

**1354.** `[C0555 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume that while a _____ is pending in a regular monthly meeting of a local society, a member obtains the floor and moves to adjourn, and the motion is seconded.

   *Answer:* resolution — “Assume that while a resolution is pending in a regular monthly meeting of a local society, a member obtains the floor and moves to adjourn, and the motion is seconded.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:17 — “Assume that while a resolution is pending in a regular monthly meeting of a local society, a member obtains the floor and moves to adjourn, and the motion is seconded.”

**1355.** `[C0555 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since this motion is privileged and therefore _____, the chair immediately puts the question.
   A. undebatable
   B. Permission
   C. Indefinitely
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* A) undebatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:17 — “Since this motion is privileged and therefore undebatable, the chair immediately puts the question.”

**1356.** `[C0555 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that while a _____ is pending in a regular monthly meeting of a local society, a member obtains the floor and moves to adjourn, and the motion is seconded.
   A. recognized
   B. Convention
   C. resolution
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:17 — “Assume that while a resolution is pending in a regular monthly meeting of a local society, a member obtains the floor and moves to adjourn, and the motion is seconded.”

**1357.** `[C0555 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since this motion is privileged and therefore _____, the chair immediately puts the question.

   *Answer:* undebatable — “Since this motion is privileged and therefore undebatable, the chair immediately puts the question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:17 — “Since this motion is privileged and therefore undebatable, the chair immediately puts the question.”

**1358.** `[C0556 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, immediately restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to _____ was made.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, immediately restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to Adjourn was made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:18 — “If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, immediately restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to Adjourn was made.”

**1359.** `[C0556 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, _____ restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to Adjourn was made.
   A. Substitute
   B. Permission
   C. immediately
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:18 — “If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, immediately restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to Adjourn was made.”

**1360.** `[C0557 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the pending _____ has been disposed of, or if there has been sufficient debate to show that the assembly now wishes to adjourn, a new motion to adjourn is in order.
   A. Convention
   B. resolution
   C. Reconsider
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* B) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:19 — “After the pending resolution has been disposed of, or if there has been sufficient debate to show that the assembly now wishes to adjourn, a new motion to adjourn is in order.”

**1361.** `[C0557 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the pending _____ has been disposed of, or if there has been sufficient debate to show that the assembly now wishes to adjourn, a new motion to adjourn is in order.

   *Answer:* resolution — “After the pending resolution has been disposed of, or if there has been sufficient debate to show that the assembly now wishes to adjourn, a new motion to adjourn is in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:19 — “After the pending resolution has been disposed of, or if there has been sufficient debate to show that the assembly now wishes to adjourn, a new motion to adjourn is in order.”

**1362.** `[C0558 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The object of the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an _____ meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.

   *Answer:* adjourned — “The object of the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an adjourned meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:1 — “The object of the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an adjourned meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.”

**1363.** `[C0558 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The object of the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an _____ meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.
   A. president
   B. extending
   C. execution
   D. adjourned

   *Answer:* D) adjourned. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:1 — “The object of the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an adjourned meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.”

**1364.** `[C0559 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to _____ (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.

   *Answer:* Suspend the Rules — “If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.”

**1365.** `[C0559 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to _____ Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.
   A. Amend Something
   B. Article I: Name
   C. Trial Procedure
   D. regular meeting

   *Answer:* A) Amend Something. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.”

**1366.** `[C0559 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting _____ for later within the same session.
   A. scheduled
   B. amendment
   C. Secretary
   D. exhausted

   *Answer:* A) scheduled. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**1367.** `[C0559 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting _____ for later within the same session.

   *Answer:* scheduled — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**1368.** `[C0560 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is _____ only when it is made while a question is pending.

   *Answer:* privileged — “A motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is privileged only when it is made while a question is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:3 — “A motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is privileged only when it is made while a question is pending.”

**1369.** `[C0560 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is _____ only when it is made while a question is pending.
   A. Reconsider
   B. privileged
   C. assemblies
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:3 — “A motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is privileged only when it is made while a question is pending.”

**1370.** `[C0561 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is made in any assembly when no question is pending, it is in order and is debatable and subject to all of the other rules _____ to main motions.

   *Answer:* applicable — “If a motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is made in any assembly when no question is pending, it is in order and is debatable and subject to all of the other rules applicable to main motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:4 — “If a motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is made in any assembly when no question is pending, it is in order and is debatable and subject to all of the other rules applicable to main motions.”

**1371.** `[C0561 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If _____, any desired motion to fix the time to which to adjourn should be made while no other question is pending.
   A. Business
   B. division
   C. feasible
   D. adoption

   *Answer:* C) feasible. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:4 — “If feasible, any desired motion to fix the time to which to adjourn should be made while no other question is pending.”

**1372.** `[C0561 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But situations may arise in which immediate _____ of the time for an adjourned meeting is important, yet there is no opportunity to make a main motion.

   *Answer:* establishment — “But situations may arise in which immediate establishment of the time for an adjourned meeting is important, yet there is no opportunity to make a main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:4 — “But situations may arise in which immediate establishment of the time for an adjourned meeting is important, yet there is no opportunity to make a main motion.”

**1373.** `[C0561 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn can then be used.
   A. landscaped
   B. committees
   C. Reconsider
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* D) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:4 — “The privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn can then be used.”

**1374.** `[C0561 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is made in any assembly when no question is pending, it is in order and is debatable and subject to all of the other rules _____ to main motions.
   A. sustaining
   B. applicable
   C. Substitute
   D. subsidiary

   *Answer:* B) applicable. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:4 — “If a motion to fix the time to which to adjourn is made in any assembly when no question is pending, it is in order and is debatable and subject to all of the other rules applicable to main motions.”

**1375.** `[C0562 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The following eight characteristics apply only to the _____ motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.

   *Answer:* privileged — “The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:5 — “The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**1376.** `[C0562 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The following eight _____ apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.
   A. president-elect
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. characteristics
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:5 — “The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**1377.** `[C0563 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an organized society, the adjourned meeting scheduled by adoption of this motion (_____ or main) must be set for a time before that of the next regular meeting.

   *Answer:* privileged — “In an organized society, the adjourned meeting scheduled by adoption of this motion (privileged or main) must be set for a time before that of the next regular meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:7 — “In an organized society, the adjourned meeting scheduled by adoption of this motion (privileged or main) must be set for a time before that of the next regular meeting.”

**1378.** `[C0563 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an organized society, the _____ meeting scheduled by adoption of this motion (privileged or main) must be set for a time before that of the next regular meeting.
   A. reception
   B. adjourned
   C. expressly
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* B) adjourned. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:7 — “In an organized society, the adjourned meeting scheduled by adoption of this motion (privileged or main) must be set for a time before that of the next regular meeting.”

**1379.** `[C0564 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an assembly holding regularly scheduled business meetings adjourns to meet “at the call of the chair,” an adjourned meeting called accordingly is a _____ of the same session;

   *Answer:* continuation — “If an assembly holding regularly scheduled business meetings adjourns to meet “at the call of the chair,” an adjourned meeting called accordingly is a continuation of the same session;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:8 — “If an assembly holding regularly scheduled business meetings adjourns to meet “at the call of the chair,” an adjourned meeting called accordingly is a continuation of the same session;”

**1380.** `[C0564 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but, if no such meeting is held before the next regular session, the adjournment of the previous session becomes final _____ as of the date the last meeting adjourned, and the chair’s authority to call an adjourned meeting expires.
   A. retrospectively
   B. Parliamentary
   C. Organization
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* A) retrospectively. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:8 — “but, if no such meeting is held before the next regular session, the adjournment of the previous session becomes final retrospectively as of the date the last meeting adjourned, and the chair’s authority to call an adjourned meeting expires.”

**1381.** `[C0564 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an assembly holding regularly scheduled business meetings adjourns to meet “at the call of the chair,” an adjourned meeting called accordingly is a _____ of the same session;
   A. continuation
   B. Procedures
   C. Incidental
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) continuation. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:8 — “If an assembly holding regularly scheduled business meetings adjourns to meet “at the call of the chair,” an adjourned meeting called accordingly is a continuation of the same session;”

**1382.** `[C0564 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but, if no such meeting is held before the next regular session, the adjournment of the previous session becomes final _____ as of the date the last meeting adjourned, and the chair’s authority to call an adjourned meeting expires.

   *Answer:* retrospectively — “but, if no such meeting is held before the next regular session, the adjournment of the previous session becomes final retrospectively as of the date the last meeting adjourned, and the chair’s authority to call an adjourned meeting expires.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:8 — “but, if no such meeting is held before the next regular session, the adjournment of the previous session becomes final retrospectively as of the date the last meeting adjourned, and the chair’s authority to call an adjourned meeting expires.”

**1383.** `[C0565 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whether introduced as a privileged or a main motion, the effect of this motion is to establish an _____—that is, another meeting that will be a continuation of the session at which the motion is adopted.
   A. adjourned meeting
   B. adopt a statement
   C. executive session
   D. Cumulative Voting

   *Answer:* A) adjourned meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:9 — “Whether introduced as a privileged or a main motion, the effect of this motion is to establish an adjourned meeting—that is, another meeting that will be a continuation of the session at which the motion is adopted.”

**1384.** `[C0565 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike a _____, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.

   *Answer:* special meeting — “Unlike a special meeting, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:9 — “Unlike a special meeting, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.”

**1385.** `[C0565 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whether introduced as a privileged or a main motion, the effect of this motion is to establish an adjourned meeting—that is, another meeting that will be a _____ of the session at which the motion is adopted.
   A. undebatable
   B. Permission
   C. interrupted
   D. continuation

   *Answer:* D) continuation. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:9 — “Whether introduced as a privileged or a main motion, the effect of this motion is to establish an adjourned meeting—that is, another meeting that will be a continuation of the session at which the motion is adopted.”

**1386.** `[C0565 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An adjourned meeting should not be confused with a special meeting, which is a separate session called, in ordinary societies, as _____ by the bylaws.
   A. Particular
   B. prescribed
   C. introduced
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* B) prescribed. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:9 — “An adjourned meeting should not be confused with a special meeting, which is a separate session called, in ordinary societies, as prescribed by the bylaws.”

**1387.** `[C0565 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unlike a _____, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.
   A. Making a Motion
   B. Trial Procedure
   C. special meeting
   D. Fix the Time to

   *Answer:* C) special meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:9 — “Unlike a special meeting, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.”

**1388.** `[C0566 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to _____, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:10 — “Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.”

**1389.** `[C0566 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to _____, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.
   A. Motions
   B. Divided
   C. Adjourn
   D. Meeting

   *Answer:* C) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:10 — “Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.”

**1390.** `[C0566 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At the _____ meeting, except for the reading of the minutes, business will be taken up from the point at which the previous meeting adjourned or at which questions were postponed.
   A. committee
   B. Signature
   C. adjourned
   D. execution

   *Answer:* C) adjourned. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:10 — “At the adjourned meeting, except for the reading of the minutes, business will be taken up from the point at which the previous meeting adjourned or at which questions were postponed.”

**1391.** `[C0566 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At the _____ meeting, except for the reading of the minutes, business will be taken up from the point at which the previous meeting adjourned or at which questions were postponed.

   *Answer:* adjourned — “At the adjourned meeting, except for the reading of the minutes, business will be taken up from the point at which the previous meeting adjourned or at which questions were postponed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:10 — “At the adjourned meeting, except for the reading of the minutes, business will be taken up from the point at which the previous meeting adjourned or at which questions were postponed.”

**1392.** `[C0567 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that the adoption of this motion does not adjourn the present meeting or set a time for its _____;

   *Answer:* adjournment — “It should be noted that the adoption of this motion does not adjourn the present meeting or set a time for its adjournment;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:11 — “It should be noted that the adoption of this motion does not adjourn the present meeting or set a time for its adjournment;”

**1393.** `[C0567 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: thus, it has no direct effect on when the present meeting shall adjourn, and is very _____ from a motion to fix the time at which to adjourn (which is always a main motion).
   A. Signature
   B. different
   C. expressly
   D. rejecting

   *Answer:* B) different. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:11 — “thus, it has no direct effect on when the present meeting shall adjourn, and is very different from a motion to fix the time at which to adjourn (which is always a main motion).”

**1394.** `[C0567 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that the adoption of this motion does not adjourn the present meeting or set a time for its _____;
   A. interrupted
   B. undebatable
   C. committees
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* D) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:11 — “It should be noted that the adoption of this motion does not adjourn the present meeting or set a time for its adjournment;”

**1395.** `[C0567 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: thus, it has no direct effect on when the present meeting shall adjourn, and is very _____ from a motion to fix the time at which to adjourn (which is always a main motion).

   *Answer:* different — “thus, it has no direct effect on when the present meeting shall adjourn, and is very different from a motion to fix the time at which to adjourn (which is always a main motion).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:11 — “thus, it has no direct effect on when the present meeting shall adjourn, and is very different from a motion to fix the time at which to adjourn (which is always a main motion).”

**1396.** `[C0568 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: on _____, April 2, at the Riggs Hotel”;

   *Answer:* Wednesday — “on Wednesday, April 2, at the Riggs Hotel”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:12 — “on Wednesday, April 2, at the Riggs Hotel”;”

**1397.** `[C0568 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or “I move that on _____, the meeting adjourn to meet at the call of the chair.”
   A. adjournment
   B. requirement
   C. Preparation
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* A) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:12 — “or “I move that on adjournment, the meeting adjourn to meet at the call of the chair.””

**1398.** `[C0568 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: on _____, April 2, at the Riggs Hotel”;
   A. Wednesday
   B. auditors’
   C. president
   D. Secretary

   *Answer:* A) Wednesday. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:12 — “on Wednesday, April 2, at the Riggs Hotel”;”

**1399.** `[C0568 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or “I move that on _____, the meeting adjourn to meet at the call of the chair.”

   *Answer:* adjournment — “or “I move that on adjournment, the meeting adjourn to meet at the call of the chair.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:12 — “or “I move that on adjournment, the meeting adjourn to meet at the call of the chair.””

**1400.** `[C0569 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In announcing an _____ result, the chair says, for instance, “The ayes have it.

   *Answer:* affirmative — “In announcing an affirmative result, the chair says, for instance, “The ayes have it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:13 — “In announcing an affirmative result, the chair says, for instance, “The ayes have it.”

**1401.** `[C0569 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the meeting _____ this evening, it will adjourn to meet at 2 P.M.
   A. majority
   B. Approved
   C. meetings
   D. adjourns

   *Answer:* D) adjourns. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:13 — “When the meeting adjourns this evening, it will adjourn to meet at 2 P.M.”

**1402.** `[C0569 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In announcing an _____ result, the chair says, for instance, “The ayes have it.
   A. assemblies
   B. affirmative
   C. Substitute
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* B) affirmative. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:13 — “In announcing an affirmative result, the chair says, for instance, “The ayes have it.”

**1403.** `[C0569 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the meeting _____ this evening, it will adjourn to meet at 2 P.M.

   *Answer:* adjourns — “When the meeting adjourns this evening, it will adjourn to meet at 2 P.M.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:13 — “When the meeting adjourns this evening, it will adjourn to meet at 2 P.M.”

**1404.** `[C0570 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a first example, assume that a number of members wish to set up an _____ meeting to deal with an involved pending question, so that the remaining order of business can be completed now.

   *Answer:* adjourned — “As a first example, assume that a number of members wish to set up an adjourned meeting to deal with an involved pending question, so that the remaining order of business can be completed now.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:14 — “As a first example, assume that a number of members wish to set up an adjourned meeting to deal with an involved pending question, so that the remaining order of business can be completed now.”

**1405.** `[C0570 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As a first example, assume that a number of members wish to set up an _____ meeting to deal with an involved pending question, so that the remaining order of business can be completed now.
   A. adjourned
   B. execution
   C. Reporting
   D. rejecting

   *Answer:* A) adjourned. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:14 — “As a first example, assume that a number of members wish to set up an adjourned meeting to deal with an involved pending question, so that the remaining order of business can be completed now.”

**1406.** `[C0571 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to _____.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:15 — “The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**1407.** `[C0571 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ as to time and place is possible, but no debate is in order.
   A. Objection
   B. secondary
   C. Amendment
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* C) Amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:15 — “Amendment as to time and place is possible, but no debate is in order.”

**1408.** `[C0571 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After announcing the result—whether adoption or rejection—she says that the question is on the resolution, which she rereads or indicates by _____ title.

   *Answer:* descriptive — “After announcing the result—whether adoption or rejection—she says that the question is on the resolution, which she rereads or indicates by descriptive title.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:15 — “After announcing the result—whether adoption or rejection—she says that the question is on the resolution, which she rereads or indicates by descriptive title.”

**1409.** `[C0571 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to _____.
   A. Another
   B. Rescind
   C. Adjourn
   D. censure

   *Answer:* C) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:15 — “The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**1410.** `[C0571 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ as to time and place is possible, but no debate is in order.

   *Answer:* Amendment — “Amendment as to time and place is possible, but no debate is in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:15 — “Amendment as to time and place is possible, but no debate is in order.”

**1411.** `[C0572 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to postpone is _____ in the usual manner.

   *Answer:* considered — “The motion to postpone is considered in the usual manner.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:16 — “The motion to postpone is considered in the usual manner.”

**1412.** `[C0572 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to postpone is _____ in the usual manner.
   A. Definitely
   B. recognized
   C. considered
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:16 — “The motion to postpone is considered in the usual manner.”

**1413.** `[C0573 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a second example, assume that the motion to _____ Time to Which to Adjourn is to be made with a view to immediate adjournment to a specified time, when this purpose cannot be reached by a main motion:

   *Answer:* Fix the — “As a second example, assume that the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is to be made with a view to immediate adjournment to a specified time, when this purpose cannot be reached by a main motion:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:17 — “As a second example, assume that the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is to be made with a view to immediate adjournment to a specified time, when this purpose cannot be reached by a main motion:”

**1414.** `[C0573 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As a second example, assume that the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is to be made with a view to immediate _____ to a specified time, when this purpose cannot be reached by a main motion:
   A. adjournment
   B. Substitute
   C. constitution
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* A) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:17 — “As a second example, assume that the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is to be made with a view to immediate adjournment to a specified time, when this purpose cannot be reached by a main motion:”

**1415.** `[C0574 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At the annual meeting of a society, the hour is _____ late.

   *Answer:* growing — “At the annual meeting of a society, the hour is growing late.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:18 — “At the annual meeting of a society, the hour is growing late.”

**1416.** `[C0574 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ bylaw amendment is pending, on which a strong minority is determined to continue debate.
   A. controversial
   B. resolutions
   C. Organization
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) controversial. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:18 — “A controversial bylaw amendment is pending, on which a strong minority is determined to continue debate.”

**1417.** `[C0574 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At the annual meeting of a society, the hour is _____ late.
   A. Adopted
   B. Suspend
   C. growing
   D. Reports

   *Answer:* C) growing. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:18 — “At the annual meeting of a society, the hour is growing late.”

**1418.** `[C0574 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ bylaw amendment is pending, on which a strong minority is determined to continue debate.

   *Answer:* controversial — “A controversial bylaw amendment is pending, on which a strong minority is determined to continue debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:18 — “A controversial bylaw amendment is pending, on which a strong minority is determined to continue debate.”

**1419.** `[C0575 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion is _____ as in the first example.

   *Answer:* treated — “The motion is treated as in the first example.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:19 — “The motion is treated as in the first example.”

**1420.** `[C0575 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is adopted, Member X, after the question has been restated on the pending bylaw _____, again rises and addresses the chair.
   A. exhausted
   B. amendment
   C. secretary
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* B) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:19 — “If it is adopted, Member X, after the question has been restated on the pending bylaw amendment, again rises and addresses the chair.”

**1421.** `[C0575 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion is _____ as in the first example.
   A. treated
   B. Pending
   C. applied
   D. advance

   *Answer:* A) treated. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:19 — “The motion is treated as in the first example.”

**1422.** `[C0575 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is adopted, Member X, after the question has been restated on the pending bylaw _____, again rises and addresses the chair.

   *Answer:* amendment — “If it is adopted, Member X, after the question has been restated on the pending bylaw amendment, again rises and addresses the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:19 — “If it is adopted, Member X, after the question has been restated on the pending bylaw amendment, again rises and addresses the chair.”

**1423.** `[C0576 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair states the question on the motion to _____ and immediately puts it to vote.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “The chair states the question on the motion to Adjourn and immediately puts it to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:20 — “The chair states the question on the motion to Adjourn and immediately puts it to vote.”

**1424.** `[C0576 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair states the question on the motion to Adjourn and _____ puts it to vote.
   A. adjournment
   B. Procedures
   C. immediately
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:20 — “The chair states the question on the motion to Adjourn and immediately puts it to vote.”

**1425.** `[C0576 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is adopted, the chair _____ the result, as follows:

   *Answer:* announces — “If it is adopted, the chair announces the result, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:20 — “If it is adopted, the chair announces the result, as follows:”

**1426.** `[C0576 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is adopted, the chair _____ the result, as follows:
   A. Reporting
   B. announces
   C. Committee
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* B) announces. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:20 — “If it is adopted, the chair announces the result, as follows:”

---

## Domain 3: Incidental Motions and Motions that Bring a Question Again Before the Assembly

**1.** `[K0053 · Vote required]` What vote does a Point of Order require to be adopted?
   A. Majority vote
   B. Two-thirds vote
   C. No vote (chair rules / demand)
   D. Unanimous consent

   *Answer:* No vote (chair rules / demand). See RONR (12th ed.) §23:1 — “When a member thinks that the rules of the assembly are being violated, he can make a Point of Order (or “raise a question of order,” as it is sometimes expressed), thereby calling upon the chair for a ruling and an enforcement of the regular rules.”

**2.** `[K0054 · Debatability]` Is a Point of Order debatable?
   A. Debatable
   B. Not debatable

   *Answer:* Not debatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:5 — “After debate on such a motion has begun—no matter how clear it is that the chair should not have stated the question on the motion—a point of order is too late.”

**3.** `[K0055 · Second required]` Does a Point of Order require a second?
   A. Requires a second
   B. No second required

   *Answer:* No second required. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:3 — “In any event, when the presiding officer has made a ruling, any two members can appeal (one making the appeal and the other seconding it), as described in 24.”

**4.** `[K0056 · Amendability]` Can a Point of Order be amended?
   A. Amendable
   B. Not amendable

   *Answer:* Not amendable. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:11 — “Alternatively, adoption, rescission, or amendment (35) of a bylaw provision, special rule of order, standing rule, or other motion may alter the rule or policy on which the unsatisfactory precedent was based.”

**5.** `[K0057 · Vote required]` What vote does an Appeal require to be adopted?
   A. Majority vote
   B. Two-thirds vote
   C. No vote (chair rules / demand)
   D. Unanimous consent

   *Answer:* Majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:1 — “But any two members have the right to Appeal from his decision on such a question.”

**6.** `[K0058 · Debatability]` Is an Appeal debatable?
   A. Debatable
   B. Not debatable

   *Answer:* Debatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:8 — “If any debate or business has intervened, it is too late to appeal.”

**7.** `[K0059 · Second required]` Does an Appeal require a second?
   A. Requires a second
   B. No second required

   *Answer:* Requires a second. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:1 — “By one member making (or “taking”) the appeal and another seconding it, the question is taken from the chair and vested in the assembly for final decision.”

**8.** `[K0060 · Amendability]` Can an Appeal be amended?
   A. Amendable
   B. Not amendable

   *Answer:* Not amendable. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:1 — “But any two members have the right to Appeal from his decision on such a question.”

**9.** `[K0061 · Vote required]` What vote does the motion to Suspend the Rules require to be adopted?
   A. Majority vote
   B. Two-thirds vote
   C. No vote (chair rules / demand)
   D. Unanimous consent

   *Answer:* Two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:14 — “Rules Whose Suspension Requires a Two-Thirds Vote.”

**10.** `[K0062 · Debatability]` Is the motion to Suspend the Rules debatable?
   A. Debatable
   B. Not debatable

   *Answer:* Not debatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:3 — “The object of this motion is to suspend one or more rules applicable to the assembly— such as rules contained in the parliamentary authority, special rules of order, or standing rules—that interfere with proposed action during a meeting.”

**11.** `[K0063 · Second required]` Does the motion to Suspend the Rules require a second?
   A. Requires a second
   B. No second required

   *Answer:* Requires a second. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:3 — “The object of this motion is to suspend one or more rules applicable to the assembly— such as rules contained in the parliamentary authority, special rules of order, or standing rules—that interfere with proposed action during a meeting.”

**12.** `[K0064 · Amendability]` Can the motion to Suspend the Rules be amended?
   A. Amendable
   B. Not amendable

   *Answer:* Not amendable. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:13 — “(Such a rule can, however, be rescinded or amended;”

**13.** `[K0065 · Vote required]` What vote does an Objection to the Consideration of a Question require to be adopted?
   A. Majority vote
   B. Two-thirds vote
   C. No vote (chair rules / demand)
   D. Unanimous consent

   *Answer:* Two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:6 — “consequently, if a majority have voted for reconsideration of the objection, this implies that there are less than two thirds who wish to prevent consideration of the main question.)”

**14.** `[K0066 · Debatability]` Is an Objection to the Consideration of a Question debatable?
   A. Debatable
   B. Not debatable

   *Answer:* Not debatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:1 — “The purpose of an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is to enable the assembly to avoid a particular original main motion altogether when it believes it would be strongly undesirable for the motion even to come before the assembly.”

**15.** `[K0067 · Second required]` Does an Objection to the Consideration of a Question require a second?
   A. Requires a second
   B. No second required

   *Answer:* No second required. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:1 — “The purpose of an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is to enable the assembly to avoid a particular original main motion altogether when it believes it would be strongly undesirable for the motion even to come before the assembly.”

**16.** `[K0068 · Amendability]` Can an Objection to the Consideration of a Question be amended?
   A. Amendable
   B. Not amendable

   *Answer:* Not amendable. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:1 — “The purpose of an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is to enable the assembly to avoid a particular original main motion altogether when it believes it would be strongly undesirable for the motion even to come before the assembly.”

**17.** `[K0069 · Vote required]` What vote does a Division of the Assembly require to be adopted?
   A. Majority vote
   B. Two-thirds vote
   C. No vote (chair rules / demand)
   D. Unanimous consent

   *Answer:* No vote (chair rules / demand). See RONR (12th ed.) §29:5 — “When a Division is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.”

**18.** `[K0070 · Debatability]` Is a Division of the Assembly debatable?
   A. Debatable
   B. Not debatable

   *Answer:* Not debatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “Before or after the vote is thus retaken, however, any member still has the right to demand a Division if he believes it will obtain a more conclusive result.”

**19.** `[K0071 · Second required]` Does a Division of the Assembly require a second?
   A. Requires a second
   B. No second required

   *Answer:* No second required. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “Before or after the vote is thus retaken, however, any member still has the right to demand a Division if he believes it will obtain a more conclusive result.”

**20.** `[K0072 · Amendability]` Can a Division of the Assembly be amended?
   A. Amendable
   B. Not amendable

   *Answer:* Not amendable. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “Before or after the vote is thus retaken, however, any member still has the right to demand a Division if he believes it will obtain a more conclusive result.”

**21.** `[C0577 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a member thinks that the rules of the assembly are being violated, he can make a _____ (or “raise a question of order,” as it is sometimes expressed), thereby calling upon the chair for a ruling and an enforcement of the regular rules.

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “When a member thinks that the rules of the assembly are being violated, he can make a Point of Order (or “raise a question of order,” as it is sometimes expressed), thereby calling upon the chair for a ruling and an enforcement of the regular rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:1 — “When a member thinks that the rules of the assembly are being violated, he can make a Point of Order (or “raise a question of order,” as it is sometimes expressed), thereby calling upon the chair for a ruling and an enforcement of the regular rules.”

**22.** `[C0577 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a member thinks that the rules of the assembly are being violated, he can make a Point of Order (or “raise a question of order,” as it is sometimes expressed), thereby calling upon the chair for a ruling and an _____ of the regular rules.
   A. adjournment
   B. Permission
   C. Incidental
   D. enforcement

   *Answer:* D) enforcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:1 — “When a member thinks that the rules of the assembly are being violated, he can make a Point of Order (or “raise a question of order,” as it is sometimes expressed), thereby calling upon the chair for a ruling and an enforcement of the regular rules.”

**23.** `[C0578 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if he fails to do so—through oversight or otherwise—any member can make the appropriate _____ Order.

   *Answer:* Point of — “but if he fails to do so—through oversight or otherwise—any member can make the appropriate Point of Order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:3 — “but if he fails to do so—through oversight or otherwise—any member can make the appropriate Point of Order.”

**24.** `[C0578 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but if he fails to do so—through oversight or otherwise—any member can make the appropriate Point of _____.
   A. event
   B. Other
   C. Order
   D. Limit

   *Answer:* C) Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:3 — “but if he fails to do so—through oversight or otherwise—any member can make the appropriate Point of Order.”

**25.** `[C0578 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any event, when the presiding officer has made a ruling, any _____ can appeal (one making the appeal and the other seconding it), as described in 24.

   *Answer:* two members — “In any event, when the presiding officer has made a ruling, any two members can appeal (one making the appeal and the other seconding it), as described in 24.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:3 — “In any event, when the presiding officer has made a ruling, any two members can appeal (one making the appeal and the other seconding it), as described in 24.”

**26.** `[C0578 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is the right of every member who notices a breach of the rules to insist on their _____.
   A. constitution
   B. enforcement
   C. Organization
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* B) enforcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:3 — “It is the right of every member who notices a breach of the rules to insist on their enforcement.”

**27.** `[C0578 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair notices a breach, he corrects the matter _____;

   *Answer:* immediately — “If the chair notices a breach, he corrects the matter immediately;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:3 — “If the chair notices a breach, he corrects the matter immediately;”

**28.** `[C0579 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In ordinary meetings it is undesirable to raise points of order on minor _____ of a purely technical character, if it is clear that no one’s rights are being infringed upon and no real harm is being done to the proper transaction of business.

   *Answer:* irregularities — “In ordinary meetings it is undesirable to raise points of order on minor irregularities of a purely technical character, if it is clear that no one’s rights are being infringed upon and no real harm is being done to the proper transaction of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:4 — “In ordinary meetings it is undesirable to raise points of order on minor irregularities of a purely technical character, if it is clear that no one’s rights are being infringed upon and no real harm is being done to the proper transaction of business.”

**29.** `[C0579 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In ordinary meetings it is undesirable to raise points of order on minor _____ of a purely technical character, if it is clear that no one’s rights are being infringed upon and no real harm is being done to the proper transaction of business.
   A. interrupted
   B. specification
   C. Preparation
   D. irregularities

   *Answer:* D) irregularities. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:4 — “In ordinary meetings it is undesirable to raise points of order on minor irregularities of a purely technical character, if it is clear that no one’s rights are being infringed upon and no real harm is being done to the proper transaction of business.”

**30.** `[C0580 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Timeliness _____ for a Point of Order.

   *Answer:* Requirement — “Timeliness Requirement for a Point of Order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:5 — “Timeliness Requirement for a Point of Order.”

**31.** `[C0580 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The general rule is that if a question of order is to be raised, it must be raised _____ at the time the breach occurs.
   A. Division
   B. assuming
   C. promptly
   D. meetings

   *Answer:* C) promptly. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:5 — “The general rule is that if a question of order is to be raised, it must be raised promptly at the time the breach occurs.”

**32.** `[C0580 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, if the chair is stating the question on a motion that has not been seconded, or on a motion that is not in order in the existing _____ situation, the time to raise these points of order is when the chair states the motion.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “For example, if the chair is stating the question on a motion that has not been seconded, or on a motion that is not in order in the existing parliamentary situation, the time to raise these points of order is when the chair states the motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:5 — “For example, if the chair is stating the question on a motion that has not been seconded, or on a motion that is not in order in the existing parliamentary situation, the time to raise these points of order is when the chair states the motion.”

**33.** `[C0580 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a member is unsure of his point or wishes to hear what the maker has to say on behalf of the motion before pressing a point of order, he may, with the chair’s _____, “reserve a point of order” against the motion;
   A. Incidental
   B. subsidiary
   C. sufferance
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* C) sufferance. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:5 — “If a member is unsure of his point or wishes to hear what the maker has to say on behalf of the motion before pressing a point of order, he may, with the chair’s sufferance, “reserve a point of order” against the motion;”

**34.** `[C0580 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but after the maker has spoken, he must insist upon his point of order or _____ it.

   *Answer:* withdraw — “but after the maker has spoken, he must insist upon his point of order or withdraw it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:5 — “but after the maker has spoken, he must insist upon his point of order or withdraw it.”

**35.** `[C0581 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The only exceptions to the _____ that a point of order must be made promptly at the time of the breach arise in connection with breaches that are of a continuing nature, whereby the action taken in violation of the rules is null and void.

   *Answer:* requirement — “The only exceptions to the requirement that a point of order must be made promptly at the time of the breach arise in connection with breaches that are of a continuing nature, whereby the action taken in violation of the rules is null and void.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:6 — “The only exceptions to the requirement that a point of order must be made promptly at the time of the breach arise in connection with breaches that are of a continuing nature, whereby the action taken in violation of the rules is null and void.”

**36.** `[C0581 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such cases, a point of order can be made at any time during the _____ of the breach—that is, at any time that the action has continuing force and effect—regardless of how much time has elapsed.
   A. continuance
   B. authorizes
   C. Definitely
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) continuance. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:6 — “In such cases, a point of order can be made at any time during the continuance of the breach—that is, at any time that the action has continuing force and effect—regardless of how much time has elapsed.”

**37.** `[C0581 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The only exceptions to the _____ that a point of order must be made promptly at the time of the breach arise in connection with breaches that are of a continuing nature, whereby the action taken in violation of the rules is null and void.
   A. resolutions
   B. assemblies
   C. requirement
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* C) requirement. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:6 — “The only exceptions to the requirement that a point of order must be made promptly at the time of the breach arise in connection with breaches that are of a continuing nature, whereby the action taken in violation of the rules is null and void.”

**38.** `[C0581 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases, a point of order can be made at any time during the _____ of the breach—that is, at any time that the action has continuing force and effect—regardless of how much time has elapsed.

   *Answer:* continuance — “In such cases, a point of order can be made at any time during the continuance of the breach—that is, at any time that the action has continuing force and effect—regardless of how much time has elapsed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:6 — “In such cases, a point of order can be made at any time during the continuance of the breach—that is, at any time that the action has continuing force and effect—regardless of how much time has elapsed.”

**39.** `[C0582 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the vote was such that the number of members excluded from participating would not have affected the outcome, a member may wish, in the appropriate circumstances, to move to _____ or Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), to move to Reconsider (37), or to renew a motion (38), arguing that comments in debate by the excluded members could have led to a different result;

   *Answer:* Rescind — “If the vote was such that the number of members excluded from participating would not have affected the outcome, a member may wish, in the appropriate circumstances, to move to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), to move to Reconsider (37), or to renew a motion (38), arguing that comments in debate by the excluded members could have led to a different result;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:7 — “If the vote was such that the number of members excluded from participating would not have affected the outcome, a member may wish, in the appropriate circumstances, to move to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), to move to Reconsider (37), or to renew a motion (38), arguing that comments in debate by the excluded members could have led to a different result;”

**40.** `[C0582 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the vote was such that the number of members excluded from participating would not have affected the outcome, a member may wish, in the appropriate circumstances, to move to Rescind or _____ Something Previously Adopted (35), to move to Reconsider (37), or to renew a motion (38), arguing that comments in debate by the excluded members could have led to a different result;
   A. Point
   B. Table
   C. hours
   D. Amend

   *Answer:* D) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:7 — “If the vote was such that the number of members excluded from participating would not have affected the outcome, a member may wish, in the appropriate circumstances, to move to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), to move to Reconsider (37), or to renew a motion (38), arguing that comments in debate by the excluded members could have led to a different result;”

**41.** `[C0582 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Remedy for _____ of the Right to Vote.

   *Answer:* Violation — “Remedy for Violation of the Right to Vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:7 — “Remedy for Violation of the Right to Vote.”

**42.** `[C0582 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If one or more members have been denied the right to vote, or the right to attend all or part of a regular or properly called meeting during which a vote was taken while a quorum was present, a point of order _____ the action taken in denying the basic rights of the individual members can be raised so long as the decision arrived at as a result of the vote has continuing force and effect.
   A. Definitely
   B. resolution
   C. concerning
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* C) concerning. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:7 — “If one or more members have been denied the right to vote, or the right to attend all or part of a regular or properly called meeting during which a vote was taken while a quorum was present, a point of order concerning the action taken in denying the basic rights of the individual members can be raised so long as the decision arrived at as a result of the vote has continuing force and effect.”

**43.** `[C0582 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Remedy for _____ of the Right to Vote.
   A. Signature
   B. expressly
   C. exhausted
   D. Violation

   *Answer:* D) Violation. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:7 — “Remedy for Violation of the Right to Vote.”

**44.** `[C0583 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the announced result of a vote included votes cast in violation of a fundamental principle of _____ law, such as votes cast by nonmembers or by absent members, or multiple votes improperly cast by a single member, a point of order can be raised so long as the decision arrived at as a result of the vote has continuing force and effect.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “If the announced result of a vote included votes cast in violation of a fundamental principle of parliamentary law, such as votes cast by nonmembers or by absent members, or multiple votes improperly cast by a single member, a point of order can be raised so long as the decision arrived at as a result of the vote has continuing force and effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:8 — “If the announced result of a vote included votes cast in violation of a fundamental principle of parliamentary law, such as votes cast by nonmembers or by absent members, or multiple votes improperly cast by a single member, a point of order can be raised so long as the decision arrived at as a result of the vote has continuing force and effect.”

**45.** `[C0583 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is any _____ that the vote(s) would have affected the outcome, the results of the vote must be declared invalid if the point of order is sustained.
   A. possibility
   B. Procedures
   C. committees
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) possibility. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:8 — “If there is any possibility that the vote(s) would have affected the outcome, the results of the vote must be declared invalid if the point of order is sustained.”

**46.** `[C0583 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the announced result of a vote included votes cast in violation of a fundamental principle of _____ law, such as votes cast by nonmembers or by absent members, or multiple votes improperly cast by a single member, a point of order can be raised so long as the decision arrived at as a result of the vote has continuing force and effect.
   A. parliamentary
   B. President-Elect
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:8 — “If the announced result of a vote included votes cast in violation of a fundamental principle of parliamentary law, such as votes cast by nonmembers or by absent members, or multiple votes improperly cast by a single member, a point of order can be raised so long as the decision arrived at as a result of the vote has continuing force and effect.”

**47.** `[C0583 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is any _____ that the vote(s) would have affected the outcome, the results of the vote must be declared invalid if the point of order is sustained.

   *Answer:* possibility — “If there is any possibility that the vote(s) would have affected the outcome, the results of the vote must be declared invalid if the point of order is sustained.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:8 — “If there is any possibility that the vote(s) would have affected the outcome, the results of the vote must be declared invalid if the point of order is sustained.”

**48.** `[C0584 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Remedy When Action Taken by an Executive Board Is Null _____.

   *Answer:* and Void — “Remedy When Action Taken by an Executive Board Is Null and Void.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:9 — “Remedy When Action Taken by an Executive Board Is Null and Void.”

**49.** `[C0584 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Alternatively, the society’s assembly can adopt an incidental main motion by _____ vote declaring that the board’s action is null and void;
   A. A majority vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* A) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:9 — “Alternatively, the society’s assembly can adopt an incidental main motion by majority vote declaring that the board’s action is null and void;”

**50.** `[C0584 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Remedy When Action Taken by an _____ Board Is Null and Void.

   *Answer:* Executive — “Remedy When Action Taken by an Executive Board Is Null and Void.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:9 — “Remedy When Action Taken by an Executive Board Is Null and Void.”

**51.** `[C0584 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the point of order is _____, the action must be declared null and void.
   A. Precedent
   B. sustained
   C. amendment
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* B) sustained. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:9 — “If the point of order is sustained, the action must be declared null and void.”

**52.** `[C0584 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, the society’s assembly can adopt an incidental main motion by majority vote declaring that the board’s action is null and void;

   *Answer:* Alternatively — “Alternatively, the society’s assembly can adopt an incidental main motion by majority vote declaring that the board’s action is null and void;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:9 — “Alternatively, the society’s assembly can adopt an incidental main motion by majority vote declaring that the board’s action is null and void;”

**53.** `[C0585 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The ruling and its rationale serve as a _____ for future reference by the chair and the assembly, unless overturned on appeal, the result of which is also recorded in the minutes and may create a contrary precedent.

   *Answer:* precedent — “The ruling and its rationale serve as a precedent for future reference by the chair and the assembly, unless overturned on appeal, the result of which is also recorded in the minutes and may create a contrary precedent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:10 — “The ruling and its rationale serve as a precedent for future reference by the chair and the assembly, unless overturned on appeal, the result of which is also recorded in the minutes and may create a contrary precedent.”

**54.** `[C0585 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When similar issues arise in the future, such precedents are _____ in resolving them—that is, they carry weight in the absence of overriding reasons for following a different course—but they are not binding on the chair or the assembly.
   A. persuasive
   B. Convention
   C. assemblies
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* A) persuasive. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:10 — “When similar issues arise in the future, such precedents are persuasive in resolving them—that is, they carry weight in the absence of overriding reasons for following a different course—but they are not binding on the chair or the assembly.”
