**55.** `[C0585 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The ruling and its rationale serve as a precedent for future reference by the chair and the assembly, unless _____ on appeal, the result of which is also recorded in the minutes and may create a contrary precedent.

   *Answer:* overturned — “The ruling and its rationale serve as a precedent for future reference by the chair and the assembly, unless overturned on appeal, the result of which is also recorded in the minutes and may create a contrary precedent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:10 — “The ruling and its rationale serve as a precedent for future reference by the chair and the assembly, unless overturned on appeal, the result of which is also recorded in the minutes and may create a contrary precedent.”

**56.** `[C0585 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When similar issues arise in the future, such _____ are persuasive in resolving them—that is, they carry weight in the absence of overriding reasons for following a different course—but they are not binding on the chair or the assembly.
   A. authorizes
   B. Unfinished
   C. precedents
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) precedents. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:10 — “When similar issues arise in the future, such precedents are persuasive in resolving them—that is, they carry weight in the absence of overriding reasons for following a different course—but they are not binding on the chair or the assembly.”

**57.** `[C0585 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The weight given to precedent increases with the number of times the same or similar rulings have been repeated and with the length of time during which the assembly has _____ adhered to them.

   *Answer:* consistently — “The weight given to precedent increases with the number of times the same or similar rulings have been repeated and with the length of time during which the assembly has consistently adhered to them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:10 — “The weight given to precedent increases with the number of times the same or similar rulings have been repeated and with the length of time during which the assembly has consistently adhered to them.”

**58.** `[C0586 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an assembly is or becomes _____ with a precedent, it may be overruled, in whole or in part, by a later ruling of the chair or a decision of the assembly in an appeal in a similar situation, which will then create a new precedent.

   *Answer:* dissatisfied — “If an assembly is or becomes dissatisfied with a precedent, it may be overruled, in whole or in part, by a later ruling of the chair or a decision of the assembly in an appeal in a similar situation, which will then create a new precedent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:11 — “If an assembly is or becomes dissatisfied with a precedent, it may be overruled, in whole or in part, by a later ruling of the chair or a decision of the assembly in an appeal in a similar situation, which will then create a new precedent.”

**59.** `[C0586 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Alternatively, adoption, rescission, or amendment (35) of a bylaw provision, special rule of order, standing rule, or other motion may alter the rule or policy on which the _____ precedent was based.
   A. presentation
   B. Responsibilities
   C. consideration
   D. unsatisfactory

   *Answer:* D) unsatisfactory. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:11 — “Alternatively, adoption, rescission, or amendment (35) of a bylaw provision, special rule of order, standing rule, or other motion may alter the rule or policy on which the unsatisfactory precedent was based.”

**60.** `[C0586 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an assembly is or becomes _____ with a precedent, it may be overruled, in whole or in part, by a later ruling of the chair or a decision of the assembly in an appeal in a similar situation, which will then create a new precedent.
   A. dissatisfied
   B. deliberative
   C. Definitely
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* A) dissatisfied. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:11 — “If an assembly is or becomes dissatisfied with a precedent, it may be overruled, in whole or in part, by a later ruling of the chair or a decision of the assembly in an appeal in a similar situation, which will then create a new precedent.”

**61.** `[C0586 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Alternatively, adoption, rescission, or amendment (35) of a bylaw provision, special rule of order, standing rule, or other motion may alter the rule or policy on which the _____ precedent was based.

   *Answer:* unsatisfactory — “Alternatively, adoption, rescission, or amendment (35) of a bylaw provision, special rule of order, standing rule, or other motion may alter the rule or policy on which the unsatisfactory precedent was based.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:11 — “Alternatively, adoption, rescission, or amendment (35) of a bylaw provision, special rule of order, standing rule, or other motion may alter the rule or policy on which the unsatisfactory precedent was based.”

**62.** `[C0587 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a member notices a breach of order that may do harm if allowed to pass, he rises and, without waiting for _____, immediately addresses the chair as follows:

   *Answer:* recognition — “When a member notices a breach of order that may do harm if allowed to pass, he rises and, without waiting for recognition, immediately addresses the chair as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:12 — “When a member notices a breach of order that may do harm if allowed to pass, he rises and, without waiting for recognition, immediately addresses the chair as follows:”

**63.** `[C0587 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a member notices a breach of order that may do harm if allowed to pass, he rises and, without waiting for _____, immediately addresses the chair as follows:
   A. Information
   B. recognition
   C. Substitute
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* B) recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:12 — “When a member notices a breach of order that may do harm if allowed to pass, he rises and, without waiting for recognition, immediately addresses the chair as follows:”

**64.** `[C0588 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the point relates to a _____ of the rules of debate, the form used may be:

   *Answer:* transgression — “If the point relates to a transgression of the rules of debate, the form used may be:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:13 — “If the point relates to a transgression of the rules of debate, the form used may be:”

**65.** `[C0588 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the point relates to a _____ of the rules of debate, the form used may be:
   A. president-elect
   B. subsequently
   C. Indefinitely
   D. transgression

   *Answer:* D) transgression. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:13 — “If the point relates to a transgression of the rules of debate, the form used may be:”

**66.** `[C0589 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then asks the member to state his point of order, or what words in the debate he _____ to.

   *Answer:* objects — “The chair then asks the member to state his point of order, or what words in the debate he objects to.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:14 — “The chair then asks the member to state his point of order, or what words in the debate he objects to.”

**67.** `[C0589 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then asks the member to state his point of order, or what words in the debate he _____ to.
   A. adjourn
   B. objects
   C. Motions
   D. Program

   *Answer:* B) objects. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:14 — “The chair then asks the member to state his point of order, or what words in the debate he objects to.”

**68.** `[C0590 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On _____ his statement, the member resumes his seat.

   *Answer:* completing — “On completing his statement, the member resumes his seat.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:15 — “On completing his statement, the member resumes his seat.”

**69.** `[C0590 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then rules whether “the point of order is well taken” or “is not well taken,” stating briefly his reasons, which are _____ in the minutes.
   A. Nominees
   B. adoption
   C. Previous
   D. recorded

   *Answer:* D) recorded. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:15 — “The chair then rules whether “the point of order is well taken” or “is not well taken,” stating briefly his reasons, which are recorded in the minutes.”

**70.** `[C0590 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair desires, he can review the _____ situation without leaving the chair, but standing, before giving his ruling.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “If the chair desires, he can review the parliamentary situation without leaving the chair, but standing, before giving his ruling.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:15 — “If the chair desires, he can review the parliamentary situation without leaving the chair, but standing, before giving his ruling.”

**71.** `[C0590 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On _____ his statement, the member resumes his seat.
   A. Substitute
   B. completing
   C. Particular
   D. subsidiary

   *Answer:* B) completing. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:15 — “On completing his statement, the member resumes his seat.”

**72.** `[C0590 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then rules whether “the point of order is well taken” or “is not well taken,” stating briefly his reasons, which are _____ in the minutes.

   *Answer:* recorded — “The chair then rules whether “the point of order is well taken” or “is not well taken,” stating briefly his reasons, which are recorded in the minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:15 — “The chair then rules whether “the point of order is well taken” or “is not well taken,” stating briefly his reasons, which are recorded in the minutes.”

**73.** `[C0591 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair’s decision requires any action and no appeal is made, he sees that the necessary action is taken before _____ with the pending business.

   *Answer:* proceeding — “If the chair’s decision requires any action and no appeal is made, he sees that the necessary action is taken before proceeding with the pending business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:16 — “If the chair’s decision requires any action and no appeal is made, he sees that the necessary action is taken before proceeding with the pending business.”

**74.** `[C0591 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, if the point of order relates to a breach of decorum in debate that is not serious, the chair can allow the member to _____ his speech.
   A. continue
   B. Standing
   C. Nominees
   D. adoption

   *Answer:* A) continue. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:16 — “Thus, if the point of order relates to a breach of decorum in debate that is not serious, the chair can allow the member to continue his speech.”

**75.** `[C0591 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair’s decision requires any action and no appeal is made, he sees that the necessary action is taken before _____ with the pending business.
   A. sustaining
   B. proceeding
   C. introduced
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* B) proceeding. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:16 — “If the chair’s decision requires any action and no appeal is made, he sees that the necessary action is taken before proceeding with the pending business.”

**76.** `[C0591 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, if the point of order relates to a breach of decorum in debate that is not serious, the chair can allow the member to _____ his speech.

   *Answer:* continue — “Thus, if the point of order relates to a breach of decorum in debate that is not serious, the chair can allow the member to continue his speech.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:16 — “Thus, if the point of order relates to a breach of decorum in debate that is not serious, the chair can allow the member to continue his speech.”

**77.** `[C0592 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before rendering his decision, the chair can consult the _____, if there is one.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “Before rendering his decision, the chair can consult the parliamentarian, if there is one.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:17 — “Before rendering his decision, the chair can consult the parliamentarian, if there is one.”

**78.** `[C0592 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair can also request the advice of _____ members, but no one has the right to express such opinions in the meeting unless requested to do so by the chair.
   A. assemblies
   B. Particular
   C. constitution
   D. experienced

   *Answer:* D) experienced. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:17 — “The chair can also request the advice of experienced members, but no one has the right to express such opinions in the meeting unless requested to do so by the chair.”

**79.** `[C0592 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before rendering his decision, the chair can consult the _____, if there is one.
   A. presentation
   B. parliamentarian
   C. specification
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* B) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:17 — “Before rendering his decision, the chair can consult the parliamentarian, if there is one.”

**80.** `[C0592 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair can also request the advice of _____ members, but no one has the right to express such opinions in the meeting unless requested to do so by the chair.

   *Answer:* experienced — “The chair can also request the advice of experienced members, but no one has the right to express such opinions in the meeting unless requested to do so by the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:17 — “The chair can also request the advice of experienced members, but no one has the right to express such opinions in the meeting unless requested to do so by the chair.”

**81.** `[C0593 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the chair is in doubt as to how to rule on an _____ point, he can submit it to the assembly for decision in some such manner as:

   *Answer:* important — “When the chair is in doubt as to how to rule on an important point, he can submit it to the assembly for decision in some such manner as:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:18 — “When the chair is in doubt as to how to rule on an important point, he can submit it to the assembly for decision in some such manner as:”

**82.** `[C0593 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the chair is in doubt as to how to rule on an _____ point, he can submit it to the assembly for decision in some such manner as:
   A. Reporting
   B. rejecting
   C. important
   D. delegates

   *Answer:* C) important. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:18 — “When the chair is in doubt as to how to rule on an important point, he can submit it to the assembly for decision in some such manner as:”

**83.** `[C0594 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since no appeal can be made from a decision of the assembly itself, this question is open to debate whenever an appeal would be—that is, the question submitted by the chair to the assembly for decision is debatable except when it relates to indecorum or _____ of the rules of speaking, or to the priority of business, or when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the point of order.

   *Answer:* transgression — “Since no appeal can be made from a decision of the assembly itself, this question is open to debate whenever an appeal would be—that is, the question submitted by the chair to the assembly for decision is debatable except when it relates to indecorum or transgression of the rules of speaking, or to the priority of business, or when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the point of order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:19 — “Since no appeal can be made from a decision of the assembly itself, this question is open to debate whenever an appeal would be—that is, the question submitted by the chair to the assembly for decision is debatable except when it relates to indecorum or transgression of the rules of speaking, or to the priority of business, or when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the point of order.”

**84.** `[C0594 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As in the case of debate on an appeal (24), when a point of order that is submitted to a vote is debatable, no member can speak more than once in the debate except the chair, who can speak in _____ to other members the first time, and who is also entitled to speak a second time at the close of debate.
   A. Incidental
   B. preference
   C. recognized
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* B) preference. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:19 — “As in the case of debate on an appeal (24), when a point of order that is submitted to a vote is debatable, no member can speak more than once in the debate except the chair, who can speak in preference to other members the first time, and who is also entitled to speak a second time at the close of debate.”

**85.** `[C0594 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since no appeal can be made from a decision of the assembly itself, this question is open to debate whenever an appeal would be—that is, the question submitted by the chair to the assembly for decision is debatable except when it relates to indecorum or _____ of the rules of speaking, or to the priority of business, or when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the point of order.
   A. President-Elect
   B. Information
   C. interrupted
   D. transgression

   *Answer:* D) transgression. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:19 — “Since no appeal can be made from a decision of the assembly itself, this question is open to debate whenever an appeal would be—that is, the question submitted by the chair to the assembly for decision is debatable except when it relates to indecorum or transgression of the rules of speaking, or to the priority of business, or when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the point of order.”

**86.** `[C0594 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As in the case of debate on an appeal (24), when a point of order that is submitted to a vote is debatable, no member can speak more than once in the debate except the chair, who can speak in _____ to other members the first time, and who is also entitled to speak a second time at the close of debate.

   *Answer:* preference — “As in the case of debate on an appeal (24), when a point of order that is submitted to a vote is debatable, no member can speak more than once in the debate except the chair, who can speak in preference to other members the first time, and who is also entitled to speak a second time at the close of debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:19 — “As in the case of debate on an appeal (24), when a point of order that is submitted to a vote is debatable, no member can speak more than once in the debate except the chair, who can speak in preference to other members the first time, and who is also entitled to speak a second time at the close of debate.”

**87.** `[C0595 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, if a point is made that the chair is admitting a motion which is not in order, the question should be put so that an affirmative result of the vote will mean that the motion is in order—as in the example above, or as follows: “… Those of the opinion that the motion is in order, say _____.… ;

   *Answer:* aye — “Thus, if a point is made that the chair is admitting a motion which is not in order, the question should be put so that an affirmative result of the vote will mean that the motion is in order—as in the example above, or as follows: “… Those of the opinion that the motion is in order, say aye.… ;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:21 — “Thus, if a point is made that the chair is admitting a motion which is not in order, the question should be put so that an affirmative result of the vote will mean that the motion is in order—as in the example above, or as follows: “… Those of the opinion that the motion is in order, say aye.… ;”

**88.** `[C0595 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a point of order is submitted to a vote of the assembly and the point relates to stopping something from being done, it is usually best to put the question so that an _____ vote will be in favor of allowing the proceedings to continue as if the point had not been raised.
   A. Substitute
   B. authorizes
   C. Convention
   D. affirmative

   *Answer:* D) affirmative. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:21 — “When a point of order is submitted to a vote of the assembly and the point relates to stopping something from being done, it is usually best to put the question so that an affirmative vote will be in favor of allowing the proceedings to continue as if the point had not been raised.”

**89.** `[C0595 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: etc.” When a member has been called to order because of indecorum in debate, the _____ form is: “… Those of the opinion that the member should be allowed to resume speaking, say aye.…” If the foregoing principle has no clear application to the case, the question can be put so that an affirmative result will uphold the point of order: “… Those of the opinion that the point is well taken, say aye.…”

   *Answer:* corresponding — “etc.” When a member has been called to order because of indecorum in debate, the corresponding form is: “… Those of the opinion that the member should be allowed to resume speaking, say aye.…” If the foregoing principle has no clear application to the case, the question can be put so that an affirmative result will uphold the point of order: “… Those of the opinion that the point is well taken, say aye.…””. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:21 — “etc.” When a member has been called to order because of indecorum in debate, the corresponding form is: “… Those of the opinion that the member should be allowed to resume speaking, say aye.…” If the foregoing principle has no clear application to the case, the question can be put so that an affirmative result will uphold the point of order: “… Those of the opinion that the point is well taken, say aye.…””

**90.** `[C0595 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, if a point is made that the chair is admitting a motion which is not in order, the question should be put so that an affirmative result of the vote will mean that the motion is in order—as in the example above, or as follows: “… Those of the opinion that the motion is in order, say _____.… ;
   A. board
   B. Refer
   C. aye
   D. clerk

   *Answer:* C) aye. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:21 — “Thus, if a point is made that the chair is admitting a motion which is not in order, the question should be put so that an affirmative result of the vote will mean that the motion is in order—as in the example above, or as follows: “… Those of the opinion that the motion is in order, say aye.… ;”

**91.** `[C0595 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a point of order is submitted to a vote of the assembly and the point relates to stopping something from being done, it is usually best to put the question so that an _____ vote will be in favor of allowing the proceedings to continue as if the point had not been raised.

   *Answer:* affirmative — “When a point of order is submitted to a vote of the assembly and the point relates to stopping something from being done, it is usually best to put the question so that an affirmative vote will be in favor of allowing the proceedings to continue as if the point had not been raised.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §23:21 — “When a point of order is submitted to a vote of the assembly and the point relates to stopping something from being done, it is usually best to put the question so that an affirmative vote will be in favor of allowing the proceedings to continue as if the point had not been raised.”

**92.** `[C0596 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But any two members have the right to _____ from his decision on such a question.

   *Answer:* Appeal — “But any two members have the right to Appeal from his decision on such a question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:1 — “But any two members have the right to Appeal from his decision on such a question.”

**93.** `[C0596 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But any _____ have the right to Appeal from his decision on such a question.
   A. more than a
   B. Mass Meeting
   C. two members
   D. of managers

   *Answer:* C) two members. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:1 — “But any two members have the right to Appeal from his decision on such a question.”

**94.** `[C0596 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By _____ making (or “taking”) the appeal and another seconding it, the question is taken from the chair and vested in the assembly for final decision.

   *Answer:* one member — “By one member making (or “taking”) the appeal and another seconding it, the question is taken from the chair and vested in the assembly for final decision.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:1 — “By one member making (or “taking”) the appeal and another seconding it, the question is taken from the chair and vested in the assembly for final decision.”

**95.** `[C0596 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By electing a presiding officer, the assembly delegates to him the authority and duty to make necessary rulings on questions of _____ law.
   A. characteristics
   B. parliamentary
   C. resolutions
   D. specification

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:1 — “By electing a presiding officer, the assembly delegates to him the authority and duty to make necessary rulings on questions of parliamentary law.”

**96.** `[C0596 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By one member making (or “taking”) the appeal and another _____ it, the question is taken from the chair and vested in the assembly for final decision.
   A. seconding
   B. reception
   C. execution
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) seconding. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:1 — “By one member making (or “taking”) the appeal and another seconding it, the question is taken from the chair and vested in the assembly for final decision.”

**97.** `[C0597 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Members have no right to _____ a ruling of the chair unless they appeal from his decision.

   *Answer:* criticize — “Members have no right to criticize a ruling of the chair unless they appeal from his decision.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:2 — “Members have no right to criticize a ruling of the chair unless they appeal from his decision.”

**98.** `[C0597 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Members have no right to _____ a ruling of the chair unless they appeal from his decision.
   A. Crestwood
   B. extending
   C. criticize
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* C) criticize. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:2 — “Members have no right to criticize a ruling of the chair unless they appeal from his decision.”

**99.** `[C0598 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a member disagrees with a ruling of the chair affecting any _____ question, he should not hesitate to appeal.
   A. substantial
   B. adjournment
   C. assemblies
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) substantial. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:4 — “If a member disagrees with a ruling of the chair affecting any substantial question, he should not hesitate to appeal.”

**100.** `[C0598 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The situation is no more delicate than _____ with another member in debate.

   *Answer:* disagreeing — “The situation is no more delicate than disagreeing with another member in debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:4 — “The situation is no more delicate than disagreeing with another member in debate.”

**101.** `[C0598 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of serious questions when _____ and opponents appear nearly equal, a presiding officer may welcome an appeal from his decision.
   A. Incidental
   B. Substitute
   C. proponents
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) proponents. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:4 — “In the case of serious questions when proponents and opponents appear nearly equal, a presiding officer may welcome an appeal from his decision.”

**102.** `[C0598 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By relieving the chair of _____ in a strongly contested situation and placing it on the assembly itself, better relationships are often preserved.

   *Answer:* responsibility — “By relieving the chair of responsibility in a strongly contested situation and placing it on the assembly itself, better relationships are often preserved.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:4 — “By relieving the chair of responsibility in a strongly contested situation and placing it on the assembly itself, better relationships are often preserved.”

**103.** `[C0598 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member disagrees with a ruling of the chair affecting any _____ question, he should not hesitate to appeal.

   *Answer:* substantial — “If a member disagrees with a ruling of the chair affecting any substantial question, he should not hesitate to appeal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:4 — “If a member disagrees with a ruling of the chair affecting any substantial question, he should not hesitate to appeal.”

**104.** `[C0599 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As explained in Standard Characteristic 2, an appeal is applicable only to a _____ by the chair.
   A. accept
   B. Speech
   C. amends
   D. ruling

   *Answer:* D) ruling. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:5 — “As explained in Standard Characteristic 2, an appeal is applicable only to a ruling by the chair.”

**105.** `[C0599 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As explained in Standard _____ 2, an appeal is applicable only to a ruling by the chair.

   *Answer:* Characteristic — “As explained in Standard Characteristic 2, an appeal is applicable only to a ruling by the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:5 — “As explained in Standard Characteristic 2, an appeal is applicable only to a ruling by the chair.”

**106.** `[C0600 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No appeal can be made from the chair’s response to a parliamentary inquiry or other query, since such a reply is an _____ rendered by the chair, not a ruling on a question that has actually arisen.

   *Answer:* opinion — “No appeal can be made from the chair’s response to a parliamentary inquiry or other query, since such a reply is an opinion rendered by the chair, not a ruling on a question that has actually arisen.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:6 — “No appeal can be made from the chair’s response to a parliamentary inquiry or other query, since such a reply is an opinion rendered by the chair, not a ruling on a question that has actually arisen.”

**107.** `[C0600 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But the point can be put at issue before the assembly by making the motion despite the chair’s opinion and, when he _____ that the motion is not in order, appealing from the chair’s decision.
   A. Forum
   B. Point
   C. event
   D. rules

   *Answer:* D) rules. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:6 — “But the point can be put at issue before the assembly by making the motion despite the chair’s opinion and, when he rules that the motion is not in order, appealing from the chair’s decision.”

**108.** `[C0600 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No appeal can be made from the chair’s response to a _____ inquiry or other query, since such a reply is an opinion rendered by the chair, not a ruling on a question that has actually arisen.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “No appeal can be made from the chair’s response to a parliamentary inquiry or other query, since such a reply is an opinion rendered by the chair, not a ruling on a question that has actually arisen.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:6 — “No appeal can be made from the chair’s response to a parliamentary inquiry or other query, since such a reply is an opinion rendered by the chair, not a ruling on a question that has actually arisen.”

**109.** `[C0600 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But the point can be put at issue before the assembly by making the motion despite the chair’s opinion and, when he rules that the motion is not in order, _____ from the chair’s decision.
   A. appealing
   B. president
   C. auditors’
   D. extending

   *Answer:* A) appealing. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:6 — “But the point can be put at issue before the assembly by making the motion despite the chair’s opinion and, when he rules that the motion is not in order, appealing from the chair’s decision.”

**110.** `[C0601 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member doubts the correctness of such an announced result, however, he should call for a _____ (see 29) or move that the vote be counted.

   *Answer:* Division — “If a member doubts the correctness of such an announced result, however, he should call for a Division (see 29) or move that the vote be counted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:7 — “If a member doubts the correctness of such an announced result, however, he should call for a Division (see 29) or move that the vote be counted.”

**111.** `[C0601 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair’s judgment as to the more numerous side in a vote, or whether there are two thirds in the _____, also is not a ruling and is not subject to appeal.
   A. constitution
   B. Reconsider
   C. undebatable
   D. affirmative

   *Answer:* D) affirmative. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:7 — “The chair’s judgment as to the more numerous side in a vote, or whether there are two thirds in the affirmative, also is not a ruling and is not subject to appeal.”

**112.** `[C0601 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member doubts the _____ of such an announced result, however, he should call for a Division (see 29) or move that the vote be counted.

   *Answer:* correctness — “If a member doubts the correctness of such an announced result, however, he should call for a Division (see 29) or move that the vote be counted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:7 — “If a member doubts the correctness of such an announced result, however, he should call for a Division (see 29) or move that the vote be counted.”

**113.** `[C0601 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair’s judgment as to the more numerous side in a vote, or whether there are two thirds in the _____, also is not a ruling and is not subject to appeal.

   *Answer:* affirmative — “The chair’s judgment as to the more numerous side in a vote, or whether there are two thirds in the affirmative, also is not a ruling and is not subject to appeal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:7 — “The chair’s judgment as to the more numerous side in a vote, or whether there are two thirds in the affirmative, also is not a ruling and is not subject to appeal.”

**114.** `[C0602 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If any debate or business has _____, it is too late to appeal.

   *Answer:* intervened — “If any debate or business has intervened, it is too late to appeal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:8 — “If any debate or business has intervened, it is too late to appeal.”

**115.** `[C0602 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If any debate or business has _____, it is too late to appeal.
   A. landscaped
   B. intervened
   C. settlement
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) intervened. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:8 — “If any debate or business has intervened, it is too late to appeal.”

**116.** `[C0603 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member desiring to appeal rises and, without waiting to be _____, addresses the chair as follows:

   *Answer:* recognized — “A member desiring to appeal rises and, without waiting to be recognized, addresses the chair as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:9 — “A member desiring to appeal rises and, without waiting to be recognized, addresses the chair as follows:”

**117.** `[C0603 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member desiring to appeal rises and, without waiting to be _____, addresses the chair as follows:
   A. Convention
   B. Unfinished
   C. substitute
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* D) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:9 — “A member desiring to appeal rises and, without waiting to be recognized, addresses the chair as follows:”

**118.** `[C0604 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair, after stating clearly the exact question at issue, and the reasons for his decision if he thinks an _____ necessary, states the question on the appeal as follows:

   *Answer:* explanation — “The chair, after stating clearly the exact question at issue, and the reasons for his decision if he thinks an explanation necessary, states the question on the appeal as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:10 — “The chair, after stating clearly the exact question at issue, and the reasons for his decision if he thinks an explanation necessary, states the question on the appeal as follows:”

**119.** `[C0604 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair, after stating clearly the exact question at issue, and the reasons for his decision if he thinks an _____ necessary, states the question on the appeal as follows:
   A. explanation
   B. assemblies
   C. Particular
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) explanation. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:10 — “The chair, after stating clearly the exact question at issue, and the reasons for his decision if he thinks an explanation necessary, states the question on the appeal as follows:”

**120.** `[C0605 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The question is not on “sustaining the chair,” because the _____, not the presiding officer, is in question.

   *Answer:* decision — “The question is not on “sustaining the chair,” because the decision, not the presiding officer, is in question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:11 — “The question is not on “sustaining the chair,” because the decision, not the presiding officer, is in question.”

**121.** `[C0605 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The question is not on “_____ the chair,” because the decision, not the presiding officer, is in question.
   A. Substitute
   B. introduced
   C. Particular
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* D) sustaining. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:11 — “The question is not on “sustaining the chair,” because the decision, not the presiding officer, is in question.”

**122.** `[C0606 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The vote is taken so that the _____ will be in favor of sustaining the chair’s decision, as follows:

   *Answer:* affirmative — “The vote is taken so that the affirmative will be in favor of sustaining the chair’s decision, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:12 — “The vote is taken so that the affirmative will be in favor of sustaining the chair’s decision, as follows:”

**123.** `[C0606 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The vote is taken so that the _____ will be in favor of sustaining the chair’s decision, as follows:
   A. Definitely
   B. affirmative
   C. committees
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) affirmative. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:12 — “The vote is taken so that the affirmative will be in favor of sustaining the chair’s decision, as follows:”

**124.** `[C0607 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the result of the vote is announced, business is resumed in _____ with the situation existing after the action on the appeal.

   *Answer:* accordance — “After the result of the vote is announced, business is resumed in accordance with the situation existing after the action on the appeal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:13 — “After the result of the vote is announced, business is resumed in accordance with the situation existing after the action on the appeal.”

**125.** `[C0607 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the result of the vote is announced, business is resumed in _____ with the situation existing after the action on the appeal.
   A. accordance
   B. Unfinished
   C. landscaped
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* A) accordance. See RONR (12th ed.) §24:13 — “After the result of the vote is announced, business is resumed in accordance with the situation existing after the action on the appeal.”

**126.** `[C0608 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an assembly wishes to do something during a meeting that it cannot do without violating one or more of its regular rules, it can adopt a motion to _____ interfering with the proposed action—provided that the proposal is not in conflict with the organization’s bylaws (or constitution), with local, state, or national law prescribing procedural rules applicable to the organization or assembly, or with a fundamental principle of parliamentary law.

   *Answer:* Suspend the Rules — “When an assembly wishes to do something during a meeting that it cannot do without violating one or more of its regular rules, it can adopt a motion to Suspend the Rules interfering with the proposed action—provided that the proposal is not in conflict with the organization’s bylaws (or constitution), with local, state, or national law prescribing procedural rules applicable to the organization or assembly, or with a fundamental principle of parliamentary law.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:1 — “When an assembly wishes to do something during a meeting that it cannot do without violating one or more of its regular rules, it can adopt a motion to Suspend the Rules interfering with the proposed action—provided that the proposal is not in conflict with the organization’s bylaws (or constitution), with local, state, or national law prescribing procedural rules applicable to the organization or assembly, or with a fundamental principle of parliamentary law.”

**127.** `[C0608 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When an assembly wishes to do something during a meeting that it cannot do without violating one or more of its regular rules, it can adopt a motion to Suspend the Rules interfering with the proposed action—provided that the proposal is not in conflict with the organization’s bylaws (or constitution), with local, state, or national law prescribing procedural rules applicable to the organization or assembly, or with a fundamental principle of _____ law.
   A. Organization
   B. requirement
   C. President-Elect
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:1 — “When an assembly wishes to do something during a meeting that it cannot do without violating one or more of its regular rules, it can adopt a motion to Suspend the Rules interfering with the proposed action—provided that the proposal is not in conflict with the organization’s bylaws (or constitution), with local, state, or national law prescribing procedural rules applicable to the organization or assembly, or with a fundamental principle of parliamentary law.”

**128.** `[C0609 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to “take up a question out of its proper order,” or to consider one before a time to which it has been postponed, is an application of the motion to _____ (see 14, 41).

   *Answer:* Suspend the Rules — “A motion to “take up a question out of its proper order,” or to consider one before a time to which it has been postponed, is an application of the motion to Suspend the Rules (see 14, 41).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:3 — “A motion to “take up a question out of its proper order,” or to consider one before a time to which it has been postponed, is an application of the motion to Suspend the Rules (see 14, 41).”

**129.** `[C0609 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The object of this motion is to suspend one or more rules applicable to the assembly— such as rules contained in the _____ authority, special rules of order, or standing rules—that interfere with proposed action during a meeting.
   A. Announcements
   B. interrupted
   C. parliamentary
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:3 — “The object of this motion is to suspend one or more rules applicable to the assembly— such as rules contained in the parliamentary authority, special rules of order, or standing rules—that interfere with proposed action during a meeting.”

**130.** `[C0609 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to “take up a question out of its proper order,” or to consider one before a time to which it has been postponed, is an _____ of the motion to Suspend the Rules (see 14, 41).

   *Answer:* application — “A motion to “take up a question out of its proper order,” or to consider one before a time to which it has been postponed, is an application of the motion to Suspend the Rules (see 14, 41).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:3 — “A motion to “take up a question out of its proper order,” or to consider one before a time to which it has been postponed, is an application of the motion to Suspend the Rules (see 14, 41).”

**131.** `[C0609 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The object of this motion is to suspend one or more rules applicable to the assembly— such as rules contained in the _____ authority, special rules of order, or standing rules—that interfere with proposed action during a meeting.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The object of this motion is to suspend one or more rules applicable to the assembly— such as rules contained in the parliamentary authority, special rules of order, or standing rules—that interfere with proposed action during a meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:3 — “The object of this motion is to suspend one or more rules applicable to the assembly— such as rules contained in the parliamentary authority, special rules of order, or standing rules—that interfere with proposed action during a meeting.”

**132.** `[C0610 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In making the incidental motion to _____, the particular rule or rules to be suspended are not mentioned;

   *Answer:* Suspend the Rules — “In making the incidental motion to Suspend the Rules, the particular rule or rules to be suspended are not mentioned;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:4 — “In making the incidental motion to Suspend the Rules, the particular rule or rules to be suspended are not mentioned;”

**133.** `[C0610 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In making the incidental motion to _____ Rules, the particular rule or rules to be suspended are not mentioned;
   A. Lay on the
   B. Discharge a
   C. Suspend the
   D. Request for

   *Answer:* C) Suspend the. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:4 — “In making the incidental motion to Suspend the Rules, the particular rule or rules to be suspended are not mentioned;”

**134.** `[C0610 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but the motion must state its specific purpose, and its adoption permits nothing else to be done under the _____.
   A. assemblies
   B. sustaining
   C. suspension
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) suspension. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:4 — “but the motion must state its specific purpose, and its adoption permits nothing else to be done under the suspension.”

**135.** `[C0610 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but the motion must state its specific purpose, and its adoption permits nothing else to be done under the _____.

   *Answer:* suspension — “but the motion must state its specific purpose, and its adoption permits nothing else to be done under the suspension.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:4 — “but the motion must state its specific purpose, and its adoption permits nothing else to be done under the suspension.”

**136.** `[C0611 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to _____ is adopted and its object is to allow consideration of business that could not otherwise have been considered at the time, the chair immediately recognizes the member who moved the suspension of the rules, to make the appropriate motion that will bring up the desired business.

   *Answer:* Suspend the Rules — “If a motion to Suspend the Rules is adopted and its object is to allow consideration of business that could not otherwise have been considered at the time, the chair immediately recognizes the member who moved the suspension of the rules, to make the appropriate motion that will bring up the desired business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:5 — “If a motion to Suspend the Rules is adopted and its object is to allow consideration of business that could not otherwise have been considered at the time, the chair immediately recognizes the member who moved the suspension of the rules, to make the appropriate motion that will bring up the desired business.”

**137.** `[C0611 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to Suspend the Rules is adopted and its object is to allow _____ of business that could not otherwise have been considered at the time, the chair immediately recognizes the member who moved the suspension of the rules, to make the appropriate motion that will bring up the desired business.
   A. consideration
   B. president-elect
   C. Indefinitely
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:5 — “If a motion to Suspend the Rules is adopted and its object is to allow consideration of business that could not otherwise have been considered at the time, the chair immediately recognizes the member who moved the suspension of the rules, to make the appropriate motion that will bring up the desired business.”

**138.** `[C0611 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Or, if no further motion is _____ (for example, if the two motions were combined as indicated above, or if the question is one that was postponed), the chair announces the business as pending.

   *Answer:* necessary — “Or, if no further motion is necessary (for example, if the two motions were combined as indicated above, or if the question is one that was postponed), the chair announces the business as pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:5 — “Or, if no further motion is necessary (for example, if the two motions were combined as indicated above, or if the question is one that was postponed), the chair announces the business as pending.”

**139.** `[C0611 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Or, if no further motion is _____ (for example, if the two motions were combined as indicated above, or if the question is one that was postponed), the chair announces the business as pending.
   A. president
   B. necessary
   C. extending
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* B) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:5 — “Or, if no further motion is necessary (for example, if the two motions were combined as indicated above, or if the question is one that was postponed), the chair announces the business as pending.”

**140.** `[C0612 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to suspend the rules is voted down, it cannot be renewed by moving to suspend the rules for the same purpose at the same meeting, unless _____ consent is given.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* A) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:6 — “If a motion to suspend the rules is voted down, it cannot be renewed by moving to suspend the rules for the same purpose at the same meeting, unless unanimous consent is given.”

**141.** `[C0612 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It can, however, be renewed for the same purpose after an _____, even if the next meeting is held the same day.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “It can, however, be renewed for the same purpose after an adjournment, even if the next meeting is held the same day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:6 — “It can, however, be renewed for the same purpose after an adjournment, even if the next meeting is held the same day.”

**142.** `[C0612 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any number of motions to suspend the rules for different purposes can be _____ at the same meeting.
   A. Unfinished
   B. entertained
   C. immediately
   D. convention

   *Answer:* B) entertained. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:6 — “Any number of motions to suspend the rules for different purposes can be entertained at the same meeting.”

**143.** `[C0612 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to suspend the rules is voted down, it cannot be renewed by moving to suspend the rules for the same purpose at the same meeting, unless _____ consent is given.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “If a motion to suspend the rules is voted down, it cannot be renewed by moving to suspend the rules for the same purpose at the same meeting, unless unanimous consent is given.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:6 — “If a motion to suspend the rules is voted down, it cannot be renewed by moving to suspend the rules for the same purpose at the same meeting, unless unanimous consent is given.”

**144.** `[C0612 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It can, however, be renewed for the same purpose after an _____, even if the next meeting is held the same day.
   A. convention
   B. Unfinished
   C. adjournment
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:6 — “It can, however, be renewed for the same purpose after an adjournment, even if the next meeting is held the same day.”

**145.** `[C0613 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Nothing in a corporate charter can be suspended unless the charter or _____ law so provides.
   A. applicable
   B. Definitely
   C. committees
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* A) applicable. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:7 — “Nothing in a corporate charter can be suspended unless the charter or applicable law so provides.”

**146.** `[C0613 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Nothing in a corporate charter can be suspended unless the charter or _____ law so provides.

   *Answer:* applicable — “Nothing in a corporate charter can be suspended unless the charter or applicable law so provides.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:7 — “Nothing in a corporate charter can be suspended unless the charter or applicable law so provides.”

**147.** `[C0614 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No applicable procedural rule prescribed by federal, state, _____ can be suspended unless the rule specifically provides for its own suspension.

   *Answer:* or local law — “No applicable procedural rule prescribed by federal, state, or local law can be suspended unless the rule specifically provides for its own suspension.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:8 — “No applicable procedural rule prescribed by federal, state, or local law can be suspended unless the rule specifically provides for its own suspension.”

**148.** `[C0614 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No applicable procedural rule prescribed by federal, state, or local law can be suspended unless the rule _____ provides for its own suspension.
   A. specifically
   B. Permission
   C. immediately
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) specifically. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:8 — “No applicable procedural rule prescribed by federal, state, or local law can be suspended unless the rule specifically provides for its own suspension.”

**149.** `[C0616 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules _____ cannot be suspended, even by unanimous consent or an actual unanimous vote, because the absentees do not consent to such suspension.

   *Answer:* protecting absentees — “Rules protecting absentees cannot be suspended, even by unanimous consent or an actual unanimous vote, because the absentees do not consent to such suspension.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:10 — “Rules protecting absentees cannot be suspended, even by unanimous consent or an actual unanimous vote, because the absentees do not consent to such suspension.”

**150.** `[C0616 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules protecting absentees cannot be suspended, even by _____ consent or an actual unanimous vote, because the absentees do not consent to such suspension.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* A) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:10 — “Rules protecting absentees cannot be suspended, even by unanimous consent or an actual unanimous vote, because the absentees do not consent to such suspension.”

**151.** `[C0616 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, the rules requiring the presence of a quorum, _____ business transacted at a special meeting to that mentioned in the call of the meeting, and requiring previous notice of a proposed amendment to the bylaws protect absentees, if there are any, and cannot be suspended when any member is absent.
   A. assemblies
   B. restricting
   C. Reconsider
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) restricting. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:10 — “For example, the rules requiring the presence of a quorum, restricting business transacted at a special meeting to that mentioned in the call of the meeting, and requiring previous notice of a proposed amendment to the bylaws protect absentees, if there are any, and cannot be suspended when any member is absent.”

**152.** `[C0616 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, the rules requiring the presence of a quorum, _____ business transacted at a special meeting to that mentioned in the call of the meeting, and requiring previous notice of a proposed amendment to the bylaws protect absentees, if there are any, and cannot be suspended when any member is absent.

   *Answer:* restricting — “For example, the rules requiring the presence of a quorum, restricting business transacted at a special meeting to that mentioned in the call of the meeting, and requiring previous notice of a proposed amendment to the bylaws protect absentees, if there are any, and cannot be suspended when any member is absent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:10 — “For example, the rules requiring the presence of a quorum, restricting business transacted at a special meeting to that mentioned in the call of the meeting, and requiring previous notice of a proposed amendment to the bylaws protect absentees, if there are any, and cannot be suspended when any member is absent.”

**153.** `[C0617 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, while generally applicable limits on debate and the making of motions may be imposed by motions such as the _____, the rules may not be suspended so as to deny any particular member the right to attend meetings, make motions or nominations, speak in debate, give previous notice, or vote.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “Thus, while generally applicable limits on debate and the making of motions may be imposed by motions such as the Previous Question, the rules may not be suspended so as to deny any particular member the right to attend meetings, make motions or nominations, speak in debate, give previous notice, or vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:11 — “Thus, while generally applicable limits on debate and the making of motions may be imposed by motions such as the Previous Question, the rules may not be suspended so as to deny any particular member the right to attend meetings, make motions or nominations, speak in debate, give previous notice, or vote.”

**154.** `[C0617 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules _____ a basic right of the individual member cannot be suspended.
   A. subsidiary
   B. protecting
   C. authorizes
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) protecting. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:11 — “Rules protecting a basic right of the individual member cannot be suspended.”

**155.** `[C0617 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, while generally applicable limits on debate and the making of motions may be imposed by motions such as the Previous Question, the rules may not be suspended so as to deny any particular member the right to attend meetings, make motions or _____, speak in debate, give previous notice, or vote.

   *Answer:* nominations — “Thus, while generally applicable limits on debate and the making of motions may be imposed by motions such as the Previous Question, the rules may not be suspended so as to deny any particular member the right to attend meetings, make motions or nominations, speak in debate, give previous notice, or vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:11 — “Thus, while generally applicable limits on debate and the making of motions may be imposed by motions such as the Previous Question, the rules may not be suspended so as to deny any particular member the right to attend meetings, make motions or nominations, speak in debate, give previous notice, or vote.”

**156.** `[C0617 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: These basic rights may be curtailed only through _____ proceedings.
   A. Convention
   B. disciplinary
   C. Parliamentary
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:11 — “These basic rights may be curtailed only through disciplinary proceedings.”

**157.** `[C0617 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules _____ a basic right of the individual member cannot be suspended.

   *Answer:* protecting — “Rules protecting a basic right of the individual member cannot be suspended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:11 — “Rules protecting a basic right of the individual member cannot be suspended.”

**158.** `[C0618 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At a regular meeting of an organization that has an established order of business, the assembly cannot, even unanimously, vote to _____ that order of business (in the sense of voting, in advance of the time when it adjourns, that the order of business shall not be gone through at all at that meeting).

   *Answer:* dispense with — “At a regular meeting of an organization that has an established order of business, the assembly cannot, even unanimously, vote to dispense with that order of business (in the sense of voting, in advance of the time when it adjourns, that the order of business shall not be gone through at all at that meeting).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:12 — “At a regular meeting of an organization that has an established order of business, the assembly cannot, even unanimously, vote to dispense with that order of business (in the sense of voting, in advance of the time when it adjourns, that the order of business shall not be gone through at all at that meeting).”

**159.** `[C0618 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At a regular meeting of an _____ that has an established order of business, the assembly cannot, even unanimously, vote to dispense with that order of business (in the sense of voting, in advance of the time when it adjourns, that the order of business shall not be gone through at all at that meeting).
   A. Convention
   B. interrupted
   C. organization
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:12 — “At a regular meeting of an organization that has an established order of business, the assembly cannot, even unanimously, vote to dispense with that order of business (in the sense of voting, in advance of the time when it adjourns, that the order of business shall not be gone through at all at that meeting).”

**160.** `[C0618 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, when the matter taken up out of its proper order has been _____ of, even if it has consumed as much time as the usual meeting, the chair must return to the regular order of business and call for the items in sequence, unless the assembly then votes to adjourn (see 21).

   *Answer:* disposed — “In such a case, when the matter taken up out of its proper order has been disposed of, even if it has consumed as much time as the usual meeting, the chair must return to the regular order of business and call for the items in sequence, unless the assembly then votes to adjourn (see 21).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:12 — “In such a case, when the matter taken up out of its proper order has been disposed of, even if it has consumed as much time as the usual meeting, the chair must return to the regular order of business and call for the items in sequence, unless the assembly then votes to adjourn (see 21).”

**161.** `[C0618 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, when the matter taken up out of its proper order has been _____ of, even if it has consumed as much time as the usual meeting, the chair must return to the regular order of business and call for the items in sequence, unless the assembly then votes to adjourn (see 21).
   A. division
   B. chairman
   C. Standing
   D. disposed

   *Answer:* D) disposed. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:12 — “In such a case, when the matter taken up out of its proper order has been disposed of, even if it has consumed as much time as the usual meeting, the chair must return to the regular order of business and call for the items in sequence, unless the assembly then votes to adjourn (see 21).”

**162.** `[C0619 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules that have their _____ outside of the session which is in progress cannot be suspended.

   *Answer:* application — “Rules that have their application outside of the session which is in progress cannot be suspended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:13 — “Rules that have their application outside of the session which is in progress cannot be suspended.”

**163.** `[C0619 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, a policy prohibiting total _____ to any one charitable organization in excess of $500.00 in any one calendar year is a rule which has its application outside of a meeting context, and thus cannot be suspended so as to permit the adoption of a motion to make a contribution in excess of the specified amount.
   A. presentation
   B. contributions
   C. recommendation
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* B) contributions. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:13 — “For example, a policy prohibiting total contributions to any one charitable organization in excess of $500.00 in any one calendar year is a rule which has its application outside of a meeting context, and thus cannot be suspended so as to permit the adoption of a motion to make a contribution in excess of the specified amount.”

**164.** `[C0619 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (Such a rule can, however, be _____ or amended;

   *Answer:* rescinded — “(Such a rule can, however, be rescinded or amended;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:13 — “(Such a rule can, however, be rescinded or amended;”

**165.** `[C0619 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: see 35.) Likewise, the rules cannot be suspended in order to permit _____ of a motion to a future session that will be held after the next regular business session or that will be held after more than a quarterly time interval has elapsed.
   A. consideration
   B. postponement
   C. deliberative
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* B) postponement. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:13 — “see 35.) Likewise, the rules cannot be suspended in order to permit postponement of a motion to a future session that will be held after the next regular business session or that will be held after more than a quarterly time interval has elapsed.”

**166.** `[C0619 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules that have their _____ outside of the session which is in progress cannot be suspended.
   A. Unfinished
   B. resolutions
   C. application
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) application. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:13 — “Rules that have their application outside of the session which is in progress cannot be suspended.”

**167.** `[C0620 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of a society, as contained in the manual established by the bylaws as the parliamentary authority, or as included in any special rules of order adopted by the organization (see 2), are rules of parliamentary procedure, the suspension of which requires a two-thirds vote.

   *Answer:* rules of order — “The rules of order of a society, as contained in the manual established by the bylaws as the parliamentary authority, or as included in any special rules of order adopted by the organization (see 2), are rules of parliamentary procedure, the suspension of which requires a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:14 — “The rules of order of a society, as contained in the manual established by the bylaws as the parliamentary authority, or as included in any special rules of order adopted by the organization (see 2), are rules of parliamentary procedure, the suspension of which requires a two-thirds vote.”

**168.** `[C0620 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules Whose Suspension Requires a _____ Vote.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* C) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:14 — “Rules Whose Suspension Requires a Two-Thirds Vote.”

**169.** `[C0620 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules Whose _____ Requires a Two-Thirds Vote.

   *Answer:* Suspension — “Rules Whose Suspension Requires a Two-Thirds Vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:14 — “Rules Whose Suspension Requires a Two-Thirds Vote.”

**170.** `[C0620 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rules of order of a society, as contained in the manual established by the bylaws as the _____ authority, or as included in any special rules of order adopted by the organization (see 2), are rules of parliamentary procedure, the suspension of which requires a two-thirds vote.
   A. parliamentary
   B. deliberative
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:14 — “The rules of order of a society, as contained in the manual established by the bylaws as the parliamentary authority, or as included in any special rules of order adopted by the organization (see 2), are rules of parliamentary procedure, the suspension of which requires a two-thirds vote.”

**171.** `[C0621 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Through an incidental main motion adopted by a majority vote, a _____ can be suspended for the duration of the current session.

   *Answer:* standing rule — “Through an incidental main motion adopted by a majority vote, a standing rule can be suspended for the duration of the current session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:15 — “Through an incidental main motion adopted by a majority vote, a standing rule can be suspended for the duration of the current session.”

**172.** `[C0621 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules That Can Be Suspended by a _____ Vote.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:15 — “Rules That Can Be Suspended by a Majority Vote.”

**173.** `[C0621 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules That Can Be _____ by a Majority Vote.

   *Answer:* Suspended — “Rules That Can Be Suspended by a Majority Vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:15 — “Rules That Can Be Suspended by a Majority Vote.”

**174.** `[C0621 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Through an _____ main motion adopted by a majority vote, a standing rule can be suspended for the duration of the current session.
   A. Definitely
   B. subsidiary
   C. authorizes
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* D) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:15 — “Through an incidental main motion adopted by a majority vote, a standing rule can be suspended for the duration of the current session.”

**175.** `[C0622 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Suspension of Rules by _____ Consent.

   *Answer:* Unanimous — “Suspension of Rules by Unanimous Consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:16 — “Suspension of Rules by Unanimous Consent.”

**176.** `[C0622 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of Rules by Unanimous Consent.
   A. recognized
   B. Suspension
   C. Convention
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) Suspension. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:16 — “Suspension of Rules by Unanimous Consent.”

**177.** `[C0622 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Frequently, when the matter is clearly not _____, time may be saved by asking unanimous consent rather than by making a formal motion to suspend the rules.

   *Answer:* controversial — “Frequently, when the matter is clearly not controversial, time may be saved by asking unanimous consent rather than by making a formal motion to suspend the rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:16 — “Frequently, when the matter is clearly not controversial, time may be saved by asking unanimous consent rather than by making a formal motion to suspend the rules.”

**178.** `[C0622 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member who has obtained the floor can say, for example, “Madam President, I ask unanimous consent to offer the courtesy _____ before we receive the report of the special committee.” The chair then asks if anyone objects and, if so, proceeds to take a vote on suspending the rules, just as if a formal motion had been made.
   A. Convention
   B. Unfinished
   C. resolutions
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:16 — “A member who has obtained the floor can say, for example, “Madam President, I ask unanimous consent to offer the courtesy resolutions before we receive the report of the special committee.” The chair then asks if anyone objects and, if so, proceeds to take a vote on suspending the rules, just as if a formal motion had been made.”

**179.** `[C0622 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Frequently, when the matter is clearly not _____, time may be saved by asking unanimous consent rather than by making a formal motion to suspend the rules.
   A. requirement
   B. adjournment
   C. controversial
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) controversial. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:16 — “Frequently, when the matter is clearly not controversial, time may be saved by asking unanimous consent rather than by making a formal motion to suspend the rules.”

**180.** `[C0623 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member moving to suspend the rules can briefly give sufficient information to enable the members to vote _____ on his undebatable motion.

   *Answer:* intelligently — “A member moving to suspend the rules can briefly give sufficient information to enable the members to vote intelligently on his undebatable motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:19 — “A member moving to suspend the rules can briefly give sufficient information to enable the members to vote intelligently on his undebatable motion.”

**181.** `[C0623 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member moving to suspend the rules can briefly give sufficient information to enable the members to vote _____ on his undebatable motion.
   A. presentation
   B. interrupted
   C. Vice-President
   D. intelligently

   *Answer:* D) intelligently. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:19 — “A member moving to suspend the rules can briefly give sufficient information to enable the members to vote intelligently on his undebatable motion.”

**182.** `[C0624 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If such a motion does not receive the required _____ vote, the main motion can be taken up only in the normal way.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “If such a motion does not receive the required two-thirds vote, the main motion can be taken up only in the normal way.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:20 — “If such a motion does not receive the required two-thirds vote, the main motion can be taken up only in the normal way.”

**183.** `[C0624 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If such a motion does not receive the required _____ vote, the main motion can be taken up only in the normal way.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* D) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §25:20 — “If such a motion does not receive the required two-thirds vote, the main motion can be taken up only in the normal way.”

**184.** `[C0625 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The purpose of an Objection to the Consideration of a _____ is to enable the assembly to avoid a particular original main motion altogether when it believes it would be strongly undesirable for the motion even to come before the assembly.

   *Answer:* Question — “The purpose of an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is to enable the assembly to avoid a particular original main motion altogether when it believes it would be strongly undesirable for the motion even to come before the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:1 — “The purpose of an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is to enable the assembly to avoid a particular original main motion altogether when it believes it would be strongly undesirable for the motion even to come before the assembly.”

**185.** `[C0625 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The purpose of an Objection to the _____ of a Question is to enable the assembly to avoid a particular original main motion altogether when it believes it would be strongly undesirable for the motion even to come before the assembly.
   A. resolutions
   B. immediately
   C. Consideration
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* C) Consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:1 — “The purpose of an Objection to the Consideration of a Question is to enable the assembly to avoid a particular original main motion altogether when it believes it would be strongly undesirable for the motion even to come before the assembly.”

**186.** `[C0626 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ Consideration of a Question is similar in some ways to a Point of Order.

   *Answer:* Objection to the — “An Objection to the Consideration of a Question is similar in some ways to a Point of Order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:3 — “An Objection to the Consideration of a Question is similar in some ways to a Point of Order.”

**187.** `[C0626 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An Objection to the _____ is similar in some ways to a Point of Order.
   A. Descriptive Characteristics
   B. Objection or With Permission
   C. Consideration of a Question
   D. Amend Something Previously

   *Answer:* C) Consideration of a Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:3 — “An Objection to the Consideration of a Question is similar in some ways to a Point of Order.”

**188.** `[C0627 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ from Objection in Other Context.
   A. sustaining
   B. Procedures
   C. Difference
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) Difference. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:4 — “Difference from Objection in Other Context.”

**189.** `[C0627 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ from Objection in Other Context.

   *Answer:* Difference — “Difference from Objection in Other Context.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:4 — “Difference from Objection in Other Context.”

**190.** `[C0628 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an objection to consideration is sustained, the main motion is dismissed for that session and cannot be renewed during the same session except by _____ consent or by reconsideration of the vote on the objection.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* B) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:5 — “If an objection to consideration is sustained, the main motion is dismissed for that session and cannot be renewed during the same session except by unanimous consent or by reconsideration of the vote on the objection.”

**191.** `[C0628 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an objection to consideration is sustained, the main motion is dismissed for that session and cannot be renewed during the same session except by unanimous consent or by _____ of the vote on the objection.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “If an objection to consideration is sustained, the main motion is dismissed for that session and cannot be renewed during the same session except by unanimous consent or by reconsideration of the vote on the objection.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:5 — “If an objection to consideration is sustained, the main motion is dismissed for that session and cannot be renewed during the same session except by unanimous consent or by reconsideration of the vote on the objection.”

**192.** `[C0628 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even if the objection is sustained, the same main motion can be _____ at any succeeding session.
