   *Answer:* introduced — “Even if the objection is sustained, the same main motion can be introduced at any succeeding session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:5 — “Even if the objection is sustained, the same main motion can be introduced at any succeeding session.”

**193.** `[C0628 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even if the objection is sustained, the same main motion can be _____ at any succeeding session.
   A. introduced
   B. Incidental
   C. incidental
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) introduced. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:5 — “Even if the objection is sustained, the same main motion can be introduced at any succeeding session.”

**194.** `[C0629 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Sustained the _____.

   *Answer:* Objection — “Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Sustained the Objection.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:6 — “Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Sustained the Objection.”

**195.** `[C0629 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As noted in Standard Characteristic 8, a vote _____ an objection to consideration can be reconsidered.
   A. Particular
   B. sustaining
   C. recognized
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* B) sustaining. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:6 — “As noted in Standard Characteristic 8, a vote sustaining an objection to consideration can be reconsidered.”

**196.** `[C0629 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ such a vote is undebatable and requires a majority vote for its adoption, and it can be taken up or called up only when no other motion is pending.

   *Answer:* reconsider — “The motion to reconsider such a vote is undebatable and requires a majority vote for its adoption, and it can be taken up or called up only when no other motion is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:6 — “The motion to reconsider such a vote is undebatable and requires a majority vote for its adoption, and it can be taken up or called up only when no other motion is pending.”

**197.** `[C0629 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to reconsider such a vote is undebatable and requires a _____ vote for its adoption, and it can be taken up or called up only when no other motion is pending.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* A) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:6 — “The motion to reconsider such a vote is undebatable and requires a majority vote for its adoption, and it can be taken up or called up only when no other motion is pending.”

**198.** `[C0629 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of a Vote That Has Sustained the Objection.

   *Answer:* Reconsideration — “Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Sustained the Objection.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:6 — “Reconsideration of a Vote That Has Sustained the Objection.”

**199.** `[C0630 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the objection is put to a vote in its correct form (see _____, below), members are asked to vote for or against consideration of the question objected to (not for or against sustaining the objection).

   *Answer:* Form and Example — “When the objection is put to a vote in its correct form (see Form and Example, below), members are asked to vote for or against consideration of the question objected to (not for or against sustaining the objection).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:7 — “When the objection is put to a vote in its correct form (see Form and Example, below), members are asked to vote for or against consideration of the question objected to (not for or against sustaining the objection).”

**200.** `[C0630 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the objection is put to a vote in its correct form (see Form and Example, below), members are asked to vote for or against _____ of the question objected to (not for or against sustaining the objection).
   A. recommendation
   B. Organization
   C. consideration
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:7 — “When the objection is put to a vote in its correct form (see Form and Example, below), members are asked to vote for or against consideration of the question objected to (not for or against sustaining the objection).”

**201.** `[C0630 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Therefore, those who wish to _____ of the question vote in the negative.

   *Answer:* prevent consideration — “Therefore, those who wish to prevent consideration of the question vote in the negative.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:7 — “Therefore, those who wish to prevent consideration of the question vote in the negative.”

**202.** `[C0630 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Therefore, those who wish to prevent consideration of the question _____.
   A. original main motion
   B. vote in the negative
   C. One Person, One Vote
   D. Planning the Program

   *Answer:* B) vote in the negative. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:7 — “Therefore, those who wish to prevent consideration of the question vote in the negative.”

**203.** `[C0630 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The objection is sustained if there are at least twice as many negative as _____ votes.

   *Answer:* affirmative — “The objection is sustained if there are at least twice as many negative as affirmative votes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:7 — “The objection is sustained if there are at least twice as many negative as affirmative votes.”

**204.** `[C0631 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member rises, even if another has been assigned the floor, and without waiting to be _____, addresses the chair as follows:

   *Answer:* recognized — “A member rises, even if another has been assigned the floor, and without waiting to be recognized, addresses the chair as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:8 — “A member rises, even if another has been assigned the floor, and without waiting to be recognized, addresses the chair as follows:”

**205.** `[C0631 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member rises, even if another has been assigned the floor, and without waiting to be _____, addresses the chair as follows:
   A. variations
   B. Definitely
   C. recognized
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:8 — “A member rises, even if another has been assigned the floor, and without waiting to be recognized, addresses the chair as follows:”

**206.** `[C0632 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In putting the objection to vote, the chair must be careful _____ to say, “Shall the objection be sustained?” This would reverse the effect of affirmative and negative votes and might cause confusion.

   *Answer:* not — “In putting the objection to vote, the chair must be careful not to say, “Shall the objection be sustained?” This would reverse the effect of affirmative and negative votes and might cause confusion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:9 — “In putting the objection to vote, the chair must be careful not to say, “Shall the objection be sustained?” This would reverse the effect of affirmative and negative votes and might cause confusion.”

**207.** `[C0632 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In putting the objection to vote, the chair must be careful not to say, “Shall the objection be sustained?” This would reverse the effect of _____ and negative votes and might cause confusion.
   A. Convention
   B. constitution
   C. Preparation
   D. affirmative

   *Answer:* D) affirmative. See RONR (12th ed.) §26:9 — “In putting the objection to vote, the chair must be careful not to say, “Shall the objection be sustained?” This would reverse the effect of affirmative and negative votes and might cause confusion.”

**208.** `[C0633 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion relating to a single subject contains several parts, each of which is capable of standing as a complete proposition if the others are removed, the parts can be separated to be considered and voted on as if they were distinct questions—by adoption of the motion for _____ Question (or “to divide the question”).

   *Answer:* Division of a — “When a motion relating to a single subject contains several parts, each of which is capable of standing as a complete proposition if the others are removed, the parts can be separated to be considered and voted on as if they were distinct questions—by adoption of the motion for Division of a Question (or “to divide the question”).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:1 — “When a motion relating to a single subject contains several parts, each of which is capable of standing as a complete proposition if the others are removed, the parts can be separated to be considered and voted on as if they were distinct questions—by adoption of the motion for Division of a Question (or “to divide the question”).”

**209.** `[C0633 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion relating to a single subject contains several parts, each of which is capable of standing as a complete proposition if the others are removed, the parts can be separated to be considered and voted on as if they were distinct questions—by adoption of the motion for Division of a _____ (or “to divide the question”).
   A. Nominees
   B. division
   C. Question
   D. business

   *Answer:* C) Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:1 — “When a motion relating to a single subject contains several parts, each of which is capable of standing as a complete proposition if the others are removed, the parts can be separated to be considered and voted on as if they were distinct questions—by adoption of the motion for Division of a Question (or “to divide the question”).”

**210.** `[C0634 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The eight characteristics below apply only to the incidental _____ for Division of a Question.

   *Answer:* motion — “The eight characteristics below apply only to the incidental motion for Division of a Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:2 — “The eight characteristics below apply only to the incidental motion for Division of a Question.”

**211.** `[C0634 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The eight characteristics below apply only to the incidental motion for _____ Question.
   A. Filling Blanks
   B. Division of a
   C. show of hands
   D. Disposition of

   *Answer:* B) Division of a. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:2 — “The eight characteristics below apply only to the incidental motion for Division of a Question.”

**212.** `[C0635 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Specification of the Manner in Which the Question Is to Be _____.

   *Answer:* Divided — “Specification of the Manner in Which the Question Is to Be Divided.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:4 — “Specification of the Manner in Which the Question Is to Be Divided.”

**213.** `[C0635 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Usually, however, little formality is involved in dividing a question, and it is arranged by _____ consent.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* D) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:4 — “Usually, however, little formality is involved in dividing a question, and it is arranged by unanimous consent.”

**214.** `[C0635 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of the Manner in Which the Question Is to Be Divided.

   *Answer:* Specification — “Specification of the Manner in Which the Question Is to Be Divided.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:4 — “Specification of the Manner in Which the Question Is to Be Divided.”

**215.** `[C0635 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to divide must _____ state the manner in which the question is to be divided.
   A. Divided
   B. Adjourn
   C. clearly
   D. adjourn

   *Answer:* C) clearly. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:4 — “The motion to divide must clearly state the manner in which the question is to be divided.”

**216.** `[C0635 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While the motion to divide is pending, another member can propose a _____ division by moving an amendment.

   *Answer:* different — “While the motion to divide is pending, another member can propose a different division by moving an amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:4 — “While the motion to divide is pending, another member can propose a different division by moving an amendment.”

**217.** `[C0636 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion cannot be divided unless each part _____ a proper question for the assembly to act upon if none of the other parts is adopted, and unless the effect of adopting all of the parts will be exactly the same—no more, no less—as adoption of the compound main question.

   *Answer:* presents — “A motion cannot be divided unless each part presents a proper question for the assembly to act upon if none of the other parts is adopted, and unless the effect of adopting all of the parts will be exactly the same—no more, no less—as adoption of the compound main question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:5 — “A motion cannot be divided unless each part presents a proper question for the assembly to act upon if none of the other parts is adopted, and unless the effect of adopting all of the parts will be exactly the same—no more, no less—as adoption of the compound main question.”

**218.** `[C0636 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, if it is moved to establish a committee and give it _____, this motion is indivisible because, should the part establishing the committee fail, the part giving the committee instructions would be absurd.
   A. instructions
   B. Procedures
   C. Convention
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:5 — “Thus, if it is moved to establish a committee and give it instructions, this motion is indivisible because, should the part establishing the committee fail, the part giving the committee instructions would be absurd.”

**219.** `[C0636 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Similarly, if a single motion proposes a series of _____ to something previously adopted (35)—existing bylaws, for example—and all of the individual amendments must be made, if any one of them is made, in order for the document being amended to be coherent, then the motion cannot be divided.

   *Answer:* amendments — “Similarly, if a single motion proposes a series of amendments to something previously adopted (35)—existing bylaws, for example—and all of the individual amendments must be made, if any one of them is made, in order for the document being amended to be coherent, then the motion cannot be divided.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:5 — “Similarly, if a single motion proposes a series of amendments to something previously adopted (35)—existing bylaws, for example—and all of the individual amendments must be made, if any one of them is made, in order for the document being amended to be coherent, then the motion cannot be divided.”

**220.** `[C0636 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion cannot be divided unless each part _____ a proper question for the assembly to act upon if none of the other parts is adopted, and unless the effect of adopting all of the parts will be exactly the same—no more, no less—as adoption of the compound main question.
   A. Previous
   B. Postpone
   C. presents
   D. limiting

   *Answer:* C) presents. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:5 — “A motion cannot be divided unless each part presents a proper question for the assembly to act upon if none of the other parts is adopted, and unless the effect of adopting all of the parts will be exactly the same—no more, no less—as adoption of the compound main question.”

**221.** `[C0636 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, if it is moved to establish a committee and give it _____, this motion is indivisible because, should the part establishing the committee fail, the part giving the committee instructions would be absurd.

   *Answer:* instructions — “Thus, if it is moved to establish a committee and give it instructions, this motion is indivisible because, should the part establishing the committee fail, the part giving the committee instructions would be absurd.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:5 — “Thus, if it is moved to establish a committee and give it instructions, this motion is indivisible because, should the part establishing the committee fail, the part giving the committee instructions would be absurd.”

**222.** `[C0637 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If possible, the division should be carried out by no more than a renumbering of phrases or clauses, prefacing each part with the formal word(s), “That,” “_____, That,” or “Ordered, That,” dropping conjunctions where necessary, or replacing pronouns with the nouns for which they stand, with or without the definite article “the,” as required.

   *Answer:* Resolved — “If possible, the division should be carried out by no more than a renumbering of phrases or clauses, prefacing each part with the formal word(s), “That,” “Resolved, That,” or “Ordered, That,” dropping conjunctions where necessary, or replacing pronouns with the nouns for which they stand, with or without the definite article “the,” as required.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:6 — “If possible, the division should be carried out by no more than a renumbering of phrases or clauses, prefacing each part with the formal word(s), “That,” “Resolved, That,” or “Ordered, That,” dropping conjunctions where necessary, or replacing pronouns with the nouns for which they stand, with or without the definite article “the,” as required.”

**223.** `[C0637 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If possible, the division should be carried out by no more than a renumbering of phrases or clauses, prefacing each part with the formal word(s), “That,” “Resolved, That,” or “_____, That,” dropping conjunctions where necessary, or replacing pronouns with the nouns for which they stand, with or without the definite article “the,” as required.
   A. carried
   B. Ordered
   C. advance
   D. adopted

   *Answer:* B) Ordered. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:6 — “If possible, the division should be carried out by no more than a renumbering of phrases or clauses, prefacing each part with the formal word(s), “That,” “Resolved, That,” or “Ordered, That,” dropping conjunctions where necessary, or replacing pronouns with the nouns for which they stand, with or without the definite article “the,” as required.”

**224.** `[C0637 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Another type of motion that cannot be divided is one whose parts are not easily _____.

   *Answer:* separated — “Another type of motion that cannot be divided is one whose parts are not easily separated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:6 — “Another type of motion that cannot be divided is one whose parts are not easily separated.”

**225.** `[C0637 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The division cannot require a rewriting of the resolution beyond an _____ mechanical separation of it into the required parts.
   A. interrupted
   B. assemblies
   C. Permission
   D. essentially

   *Answer:* D) essentially. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:6 — “The division cannot require a rewriting of the resolution beyond an essentially mechanical separation of it into the required parts.”

**226.** `[C0637 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The division cannot require a rewriting of the _____ beyond an essentially mechanical separation of it into the required parts.
   A. resolution
   B. recognized
   C. Definitely
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:6 — “The division cannot require a rewriting of the resolution beyond an essentially mechanical separation of it into the required parts.”

**227.** `[C0638 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, suppose that the following resolution is pending: “_____, That the Society congratulate its member Ernest Dunn on his novel Crestwood, and that three copies be purchased for the Society’s library.” Suppose also that a member wishes to divide the question so as to consider the purchase of the books separately.

   *Answer:* Resolved — “For example, suppose that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Society congratulate its member Ernest Dunn on his novel Crestwood, and that three copies be purchased for the Society’s library.” Suppose also that a member wishes to divide the question so as to consider the purchase of the books separately.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:7 — “For example, suppose that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Society congratulate its member Ernest Dunn on his novel Crestwood, and that three copies be purchased for the Society’s library.” Suppose also that a member wishes to divide the question so as to consider the purchase of the books separately.”

**228.** `[C0638 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, suppose that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Society congratulate its member Ernest Dunn on his novel _____, and that three copies be purchased for the Society’s library.” Suppose also that a member wishes to divide the question so as to consider the purchase of the books separately.
   A. Crestwood
   B. Signature
   C. Objection
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* A) Crestwood. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:7 — “For example, suppose that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Society congratulate its member Ernest Dunn on his novel Crestwood, and that three copies be purchased for the Society’s library.” Suppose also that a member wishes to divide the question so as to consider the purchase of the books separately.”

**229.** `[C0639 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As indicated in this _____, if separating the elements of action in a proposed resolution would require recasting the parts more than described above, the resolution cannot be divided.

   *Answer:* subsection — “As indicated in this subsection, if separating the elements of action in a proposed resolution would require recasting the parts more than described above, the resolution cannot be divided.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:8 — “As indicated in this subsection, if separating the elements of action in a proposed resolution would require recasting the parts more than described above, the resolution cannot be divided.”

**230.** `[C0639 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As indicated in this _____, if separating the elements of action in a proposed resolution would require recasting the parts more than described above, the resolution cannot be divided.
   A. subsection
   B. incidental
   C. Permission
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* A) subsection. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:8 — “As indicated in this subsection, if separating the elements of action in a proposed resolution would require recasting the parts more than described above, the resolution cannot be divided.”

**231.** `[C0640 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Striking Out Part of an Indivisible Motion or Series of _____.

   *Answer:* Motions — “Striking Out Part of an Indivisible Motion or Series of Motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:9 — “Striking Out Part of an Indivisible Motion or Series of Motions.”

**232.** `[C0640 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to _____ (12) the part to which he is opposed.
   A. strike out
   B. in some way
   C. Suspend the
   D. is pending

   *Answer:* A) strike out. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:9 — “When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to strike out (12) the part to which he is opposed.”

**233.** `[C0640 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Striking Out Part of an _____ Motion or Series of Motions.

   *Answer:* Indivisible — “Striking Out Part of an Indivisible Motion or Series of Motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:9 — “Striking Out Part of an Indivisible Motion or Series of Motions.”

**234.** `[C0640 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In like manner, when a series of resolutions is proposed as a substitute for another series, the substitute series is indivisible if the several resolutions are not completely parallel, but a motion can be made to strike out of the series any of the component resolutions before the vote is taken on whether to make the _____.
   A. Incidental
   B. resolutions
   C. Particular
   D. substitution

   *Answer:* D) substitution. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:9 — “In like manner, when a series of resolutions is proposed as a substitute for another series, the substitute series is indivisible if the several resolutions are not completely parallel, but a motion can be made to strike out of the series any of the component resolutions before the vote is taken on whether to make the substitution.”

**235.** `[C0640 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to _____ (12) the part to which he is opposed.

   *Answer:* strike out — “When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to strike out (12) the part to which he is opposed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:9 — “When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to strike out (12) the part to which he is opposed.”

**236.** `[C0641 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for _____ is not used.

   *Answer:* Division of a Question — “In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for Division of a Question is not used.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:10 — “In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for Division of a Question is not used.”

**237.** `[C0641 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes a series of _____ resolutions or main motions dealing with different subjects is offered in one motion.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. resolutions
   C. independent
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* C) independent. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:10 — “Sometimes a series of independent resolutions or main motions dealing with different subjects is offered in one motion.”

**238.** `[C0641 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate _____ and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for Division of a Question is not used.

   *Answer:* consideration — “In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for Division of a Question is not used.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:10 — “In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for Division of a Question is not used.”

**239.** `[C0641 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a demand (which should not be _____ with a demand for a division of the assembly—that is, for a rising vote) can be made even when another has the floor, as in, “Mr.
   A. Previous
   B. business
   C. confused
   D. Division

   *Answer:* C) confused. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:10 — “Such a demand (which should not be confused with a demand for a division of the assembly—that is, for a rising vote) can be made even when another has the floor, as in, “Mr.”

**240.** `[C0641 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: President, I call for a separate vote on _____ No.

   *Answer:* Resolution — “President, I call for a separate vote on Resolution No.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:10 — “President, I call for a separate vote on Resolution No.”

**241.** `[C0642 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Similarly, a series of _____ to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one motion.

   *Answer:* amendments — “Similarly, a series of amendments to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:11 — “Similarly, a series of amendments to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one motion.”

**242.** `[C0642 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Similarly, a series of _____s to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one motion.
   A. Crestwood
   B. exhausted
   C. amendment
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* C) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:11 — “Similarly, a series of amendments to a pending main motion (or to a lengthy primary amendment, such as a substitute) may be offered in one motion.”

**243.** `[C0644 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion for the division prevails, the chair states each of the separated _____ in full as it is considered.

   *Answer:* resolutions — “If the motion for the division prevails, the chair states each of the separated resolutions in full as it is considered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:15 — “If the motion for the division prevails, the chair states each of the separated resolutions in full as it is considered.”

**244.** `[C0644 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion for the division prevails, the chair states each of the separated _____ in full as it is considered.
   A. convention
   B. Indefinitely
   C. constitution
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* D) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:15 — “If the motion for the division prevails, the chair states each of the separated resolutions in full as it is considered.”

**245.** `[C0645 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to _____ (or to Consider Seriatim).

   *Answer:* Consider by Paragraph — “If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to Consider Seriatim).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to Consider Seriatim).”

**246.** `[C0645 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to _____).
   A. Call of the House
   B. Consider Seriatim
   C. adopt a statement
   D. Methods of Voting

   *Answer:* B) Consider Seriatim. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to Consider Seriatim).”

**247.** `[C0645 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Several distinct main motions on _____ cannot be considered seriatim if a single member objects.

   *Answer:* different subjects — “Several distinct main motions on different subjects cannot be considered seriatim if a single member objects.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “Several distinct main motions on different subjects cannot be considered seriatim if a single member objects.”

**248.** `[C0645 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A report or long motion consisting of a series of _____, paragraphs, articles, or sections that are not totally separate questions can be considered by opening the different parts to debate and amendment separately, without a division of the question.
   A. Convention
   B. Definitely
   C. resolutions
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “A report or long motion consisting of a series of resolutions, paragraphs, articles, or sections that are not totally separate questions can be considered by opening the different parts to debate and amendment separately, without a division of the question.”

**249.** `[C0645 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A report or long motion consisting of a series of resolutions, paragraphs, articles, or sections that are not totally separate questions can be _____ by opening the different parts to debate and amendment separately, without a division of the question.
   A. assemblies
   B. Permission
   C. considered
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* C) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “A report or long motion consisting of a series of resolutions, paragraphs, articles, or sections that are not totally separate questions can be considered by opening the different parts to debate and amendment separately, without a division of the question.”

**250.** `[C0646 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The effect of _____ a document by paragraph or seriatim is as follows: If a member exhausts his right to debate under the usual rules on one part, his right to debate begins over again as each succeeding part is opened to debate and amendment;

   *Answer:* considering — “The effect of considering a document by paragraph or seriatim is as follows: If a member exhausts his right to debate under the usual rules on one part, his right to debate begins over again as each succeeding part is opened to debate and amendment;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:3 — “The effect of considering a document by paragraph or seriatim is as follows: If a member exhausts his right to debate under the usual rules on one part, his right to debate begins over again as each succeeding part is opened to debate and amendment;”

**251.** `[C0646 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: yet no vote on adoption is taken until there has been _____ to perfect all the parts by amendment.
   A. Procedures
   B. adjournment
   C. opportunity
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:3 — “yet no vote on adoption is taken until there has been opportunity to perfect all the parts by amendment.”

**252.** `[C0646 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Keeping all subdivisions of the series open until one final vote avoids the possibility of _____ which would result—especially in the case of bylaws—if amendments to later paragraphs necessitated changes in others that had already been adopted.

   *Answer:* complications — “Keeping all subdivisions of the series open until one final vote avoids the possibility of complications which would result—especially in the case of bylaws—if amendments to later paragraphs necessitated changes in others that had already been adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:3 — “Keeping all subdivisions of the series open until one final vote avoids the possibility of complications which would result—especially in the case of bylaws—if amendments to later paragraphs necessitated changes in others that had already been adopted.”

**253.** `[C0646 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The effect of _____ a document by paragraph or seriatim is as follows: If a member exhausts his right to debate under the usual rules on one part, his right to debate begins over again as each succeeding part is opened to debate and amendment;
   A. constitution
   B. considering
   C. committees
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) considering. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:3 — “The effect of considering a document by paragraph or seriatim is as follows: If a member exhausts his right to debate under the usual rules on one part, his right to debate begins over again as each succeeding part is opened to debate and amendment;”

**254.** `[C0646 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: yet no vote on adoption is taken until there has been _____ to perfect all the parts by amendment.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “yet no vote on adoption is taken until there has been opportunity to perfect all the parts by amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:3 — “yet no vote on adoption is taken until there has been opportunity to perfect all the parts by amendment.”

**255.** `[C0647 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or the manner of consideration can be settled by _____ consent.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “or the manner of consideration can be settled by unanimous consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:4 — “or the manner of consideration can be settled by unanimous consent.”

**256.** `[C0647 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Cases in Which the Chair _____ Applies the Method.
   A. decision
   B. Meetings
   C. Normally
   D. meetings

   *Answer:* C) Normally. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:4 — “Cases in Which the Chair Normally Applies the Method.”

**257.** `[C0647 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In adopting a set of bylaws or the articles of a platform, _____ by paragraph is the normal and advisable procedure, followed as a matter of course unless the assembly votes to do otherwise.

   *Answer:* consideration — “In adopting a set of bylaws or the articles of a platform, consideration by paragraph is the normal and advisable procedure, followed as a matter of course unless the assembly votes to do otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:4 — “In adopting a set of bylaws or the articles of a platform, consideration by paragraph is the normal and advisable procedure, followed as a matter of course unless the assembly votes to do otherwise.”

**258.** `[C0647 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair, on his own initiative, can apply this method to any elaborate _____ susceptible to such treatment, unless he thinks the assembly wishes to act on the question as a whole;
   A. Particular
   B. proposition
   C. Convention
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) proposition. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:4 — “The chair, on his own initiative, can apply this method to any elaborate proposition susceptible to such treatment, unless he thinks the assembly wishes to act on the question as a whole;”

**259.** `[C0647 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Should the chair neglect this, a member can move “that the _____ be considered by paragraph” (or “seriatim”).

   *Answer:* resolution — “Should the chair neglect this, a member can move “that the resolution be considered by paragraph” (or “seriatim”).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:4 — “Should the chair neglect this, a member can move “that the resolution be considered by paragraph” (or “seriatim”).”

**260.** `[C0648 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive _____ identical to those for Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim.

   *Answer:* characteristics — “If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive characteristics identical to those for Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:5 — “If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive characteristics identical to those for Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim.”

**261.** `[C0648 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive _____ identical to those for Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim.
   A. characteristics
   B. specification
   C. Information
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:5 — “If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive characteristics identical to those for Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim.”

**262.** `[C0649 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Procedure for _____ by Paragraph.

   *Answer:* Consideration — “Procedure for Consideration by Paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:6 — “Procedure for Consideration by Paragraph.”

**263.** `[C0649 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The procedure in _____ by paragraph or seriatim is as follows: The member who moved the adoption of the document, the secretary, or the presiding officer (as the chair may decide) reads the first subdivision, and it is explained by its proponent.
   A. considering
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Reconsider
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* A) considering. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:6 — “The procedure in considering by paragraph or seriatim is as follows: The member who moved the adoption of the document, the secretary, or the presiding officer (as the chair may decide) reads the first subdivision, and it is explained by its proponent.”

**264.** `[C0649 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then asks, “Is there any debate or amendment to this paragraph [or “section,” etc.]?” When there is no further debate or amendment to the first paragraph, each _____ one is taken up.

   *Answer:* succeeding — “The chair then asks, “Is there any debate or amendment to this paragraph [or “section,” etc.]?” When there is no further debate or amendment to the first paragraph, each succeeding one is taken up.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:6 — “The chair then asks, “Is there any debate or amendment to this paragraph [or “section,” etc.]?” When there is no further debate or amendment to the first paragraph, each succeeding one is taken up.”

**265.** `[C0649 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ are voted on as they arise, but no paragraph as amended is acted upon (as to final adoption or rejection) at that time.
   A. Particular
   B. convention
   C. sustaining
   D. Amendments

   *Answer:* D) Amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:6 — “Amendments are voted on as they arise, but no paragraph as amended is acted upon (as to final adoption or rejection) at that time.”

**266.** `[C0649 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After all parts have been _____, the chair opens the entire document to amendment.

   *Answer:* considered — “After all parts have been considered, the chair opens the entire document to amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:6 — “After all parts have been considered, the chair opens the entire document to amendment.”

**267.** `[C0650 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ is ordered before the preamble has been considered, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If the Previous Question is ordered before the preamble has been considered, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:7 — “If the Previous Question is ordered before the preamble has been considered, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.”

**268.** `[C0650 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is a _____, it is treated in the same way before the final vote.
   A. meetings
   B. preamble
   C. Question
   D. decision

   *Answer:* B) preamble. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:7 — “If there is a preamble, it is treated in the same way before the final vote.”

**269.** `[C0650 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Then the entire _____ is acted upon in a single vote.

   *Answer:* document — “Then the entire document is acted upon in a single vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:7 — “Then the entire document is acted upon in a single vote.”

**270.** `[C0650 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the Previous Question is ordered before the preamble has been _____, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.
   A. considered
   B. settlement
   C. privileged
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:7 — “If the Previous Question is ordered before the preamble has been considered, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.”

**271.** `[C0650 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is a _____, it is treated in the same way before the final vote.

   *Answer:* preamble — “If there is a preamble, it is treated in the same way before the final vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:7 — “If there is a preamble, it is treated in the same way before the final vote.”

**272.** `[C0651 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Application of Subsidiary and Incidental Motions During _____.

   *Answer:* Consideration by Paragraph — “Application of Subsidiary and Incidental Motions During Consideration by Paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “Application of Subsidiary and Incidental Motions During Consideration by Paragraph.”

**273.** `[C0651 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to _____, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.
   A. Renewal of the Motion
   B. Postpone to a Certain
   C. Postpone Indefinitely
   D. deliberative assembly

   *Answer:* C) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”

**274.** `[C0651 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, _____, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.

   *Answer:* Commit — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”

**275.** `[C0651 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ and Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied to amendments or to the entire document but not to the individual paragraphs.
   A. General Procedure
   B. Optional Headings
   C. Only one question
   D. Previous Question

   *Answer:* D) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “The Previous Question and Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied to amendments or to the entire document but not to the individual paragraphs.”

**276.** `[C0651 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Application of Subsidiary and Incidental Motions During _____.
   A. Agenda Provided in Advance
   B. Descriptive Characteristics
   C. Procedure in Small Boards
   D. Consideration by Paragraph

   *Answer:* D) Consideration by Paragraph. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “Application of Subsidiary and Incidental Motions During Consideration by Paragraph.”

**277.** `[C0652 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it has been decided to consider _____ material seriatim, even if the material was divisible on the demand of a single member, it is too late to move or demand a division of the question.

   *Answer:* divisible — “If it has been decided to consider divisible material seriatim, even if the material was divisible on the demand of a single member, it is too late to move or demand a division of the question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:9 — “If it has been decided to consider divisible material seriatim, even if the material was divisible on the demand of a single member, it is too late to move or demand a division of the question.”

**278.** `[C0652 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it has been decided to consider _____ material seriatim, even if the material was divisible on the demand of a single member, it is too late to move or demand a division of the question.
   A. debatable
   B. rejecting
   C. committee
   D. divisible

   *Answer:* D) divisible. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:9 — “If it has been decided to consider divisible material seriatim, even if the material was divisible on the demand of a single member, it is too late to move or demand a division of the question.”

**279.** `[C0653 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair suggests _____ by paragraph and a member feels that the proposition could be acted upon as a whole, this form may be used:

   *Answer:* consideration — “If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that the proposition could be acted upon as a whole, this form may be used:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:11 — “If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that the proposition could be acted upon as a whole, this form may be used:”

**280.** `[C0653 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair suggests _____ by paragraph and a member feels that the proposition could be acted upon as a whole, this form may be used:
   A. Organization
   B. Preparation
   C. interrupted
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:11 — “If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that the proposition could be acted upon as a whole, this form may be used:”

**281.** `[C0654 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a representative number of the members present have voted— he can call for a _____, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.

   *Answer:* Division of the Assembly — “Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a representative number of the members present have voted— he can call for a Division of the Assembly, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:1 — “Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a representative number of the members present have voted— he can call for a Division of the Assembly, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.”

**282.** `[C0654 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a _____ number of the members present have voted— he can call for a Division of the Assembly, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.
   A. president-elect
   B. representative
   C. requirement
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* B) representative. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:1 — “Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a representative number of the members present have voted— he can call for a Division of the Assembly, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.”

**283.** `[C0655 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a _____ the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.

   *Answer:* Division of — “A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:2 — “A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.”

**284.** `[C0655 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of _____, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.
   A. mass meeting
   B. New Business
   C. at a meeting
   D. the Assembly

   *Answer:* D) the Assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:2 — “A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.”

**285.** `[C0656 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a _____, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.

   *Answer:* Division — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”

**286.** `[C0656 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On an _____ voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.
   A. inconclusive
   B. deliberative
   C. Definitely
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) inconclusive. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”

**287.** `[C0656 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before or after the vote is thus retaken, however, any member still has the right to demand a Division if he believes it will obtain a more _____ result.

   *Answer:* conclusive — “Before or after the vote is thus retaken, however, any member still has the right to demand a Division if he believes it will obtain a more conclusive result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “Before or after the vote is thus retaken, however, any member still has the right to demand a Division if he believes it will obtain a more conclusive result.”

**288.** `[C0656 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before or after the vote is thus retaken, however, any member still has the right to demand a Division if he believes it will obtain a more _____ result.
   A. persuasive
   B. Procedures
   C. privileged
   D. conclusive

   *Answer:* D) conclusive. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “Before or after the vote is thus retaken, however, any member still has the right to demand a Division if he believes it will obtain a more conclusive result.”

**289.** `[C0657 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a _____ is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.

   *Answer:* Division — “When a Division is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:5 — “When a Division is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.”

**290.** `[C0657 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a Division is demanded, the chair _____ takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.

   *Answer:* immediately — “When a Division is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:5 — “When a Division is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.”

**291.** `[C0658 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair has the _____ of obtaining a correct expression of the will of the assembly.

   *Answer:* responsibility — “The chair has the responsibility of obtaining a correct expression of the will of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:6 — “The chair has the responsibility of obtaining a correct expression of the will of the assembly.”

**292.** `[C0658 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If he is uncertain of the result of a vote or if he feels that the vote is _____, the chair can of his own accord take the vote again by a rising vote.
   A. characteristics
   B. unrepresentative
   C. president-elect
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* B) unrepresentative. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:6 — “If he is uncertain of the result of a vote or if he feels that the vote is unrepresentative, the chair can of his own accord take the vote again by a rising vote.”

**293.** `[C0658 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair has the _____ of obtaining a correct expression of the will of the assembly.
   A. requirement
   B. responsibility
   C. Announcements
   D. Responsibilities

   *Answer:* B) responsibility. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:6 — “The chair has the responsibility of obtaining a correct expression of the will of the assembly.”

**294.** `[C0658 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If he is uncertain of the result of a vote or if he feels that the vote is _____, the chair can of his own accord take the vote again by a rising vote.

   *Answer:* unrepresentative — “If he is uncertain of the result of a vote or if he feels that the vote is unrepresentative, the chair can of his own accord take the vote again by a rising vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:6 — “If he is uncertain of the result of a vote or if he feels that the vote is unrepresentative, the chair can of his own accord take the vote again by a rising vote.”

**295.** `[C0659 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a _____ is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.
   A. Nominees
   B. Division
   C. Standing
   D. original

   *Answer:* B) Division. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:7 — “When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.”

**296.** `[C0659 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the _____, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.

   *Answer:* majority — “When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:7 — “When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.”

**297.** `[C0660 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While, or _____ after, the chair announces the result of a vote, “The ayes [or “noes”] have it and…,” a member can call for a division from his seat, without obtaining the floor:

   *Answer:* immediately — “While, or immediately after, the chair announces the result of a vote, “The ayes [or “noes”] have it and…,” a member can call for a division from his seat, without obtaining the floor:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:8 — “While, or immediately after, the chair announces the result of a vote, “The ayes [or “noes”] have it and…,” a member can call for a division from his seat, without obtaining the floor:”

**298.** `[C0660 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While, or _____ after, the chair announces the result of a vote, “The ayes [or “noes”] have it and…,” a member can call for a division from his seat, without obtaining the floor:
   A. Particular
   B. immediately
   C. Preparation
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:8 — “While, or immediately after, the chair announces the result of a vote, “The ayes [or “noes”] have it and…,” a member can call for a division from his seat, without obtaining the floor:”

**299.** `[C0661 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The object of motions relating to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by _____ (rising);

   *Answer:* Division — “The object of motions relating to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by Division (rising);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:1 — “The object of motions relating to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by Division (rising);”

**300.** `[C0661 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The object of motions _____ to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by Division (rising);
   A. decision
   B. meetings
   C. relating
   D. Previous

   *Answer:* C) relating. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:1 — “The object of motions relating to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by Division (rising);”

**301.** `[C0661 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or to _____ direct how and when voting is to be conducted.

   *Answer:* otherwise — “or to otherwise direct how and when voting is to be conducted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:1 — “or to otherwise direct how and when voting is to be conducted.”

**302.** `[C0661 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or to _____ direct how and when voting is to be conducted.
   A. secondary
   B. rejecting
   C. otherwise
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* C) otherwise. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:1 — “or to otherwise direct how and when voting is to be conducted.”

**303.** `[C0662 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion in this category is an _____ motion—and subject to the rules given here—only when a motion or election is pending or the vote on it has just been taken or announced;

   *Answer:* incidental — “A motion in this category is an incidental motion—and subject to the rules given here—only when a motion or election is pending or the vote on it has just been taken or announced;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:2 — “A motion in this category is an incidental motion—and subject to the rules given here—only when a motion or election is pending or the vote on it has just been taken or announced;”

**304.** `[C0662 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion in this category is an _____ motion—and subject to the rules given here—only when a motion or election is pending or the vote on it has just been taken or announced;
   A. incidental
   B. committees
   C. variations
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* A) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:2 — “A motion in this category is an incidental motion—and subject to the rules given here—only when a motion or election is pending or the vote on it has just been taken or announced;”

**305.** `[C0663 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In practice, the method of taking a vote usually can be agreed upon _____.
   A. recognized
   B. introduced
   C. informally
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) informally. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:4 — “In practice, the method of taking a vote usually can be agreed upon informally.”

**306.** `[C0663 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But when different methods are suggested, they are usually treated not as _____ but as filling blanks, the vote normally being taken first on the one taking the most time.

   *Answer:* amendments — “But when different methods are suggested, they are usually treated not as amendments but as filling blanks, the vote normally being taken first on the one taking the most time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:4 — “But when different methods are suggested, they are usually treated not as amendments but as filling blanks, the vote normally being taken first on the one taking the most time.”

**307.** `[C0663 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In practice, the method of taking a vote usually can be agreed upon _____.

   *Answer:* informally — “In practice, the method of taking a vote usually can be agreed upon informally.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:4 — “In practice, the method of taking a vote usually can be agreed upon informally.”

**308.** `[C0663 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But when different methods are suggested, they are usually treated not as _____ but as filling blanks, the vote normally being taken first on the one taking the most time.
   A. Procedures
   B. amendments
   C. landscaped
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:4 — “But when different methods are suggested, they are usually treated not as amendments but as filling blanks, the vote normally being taken first on the one taking the most time.”

**309.** `[C0664 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member who believes that a secret vote will give a truer _____ of the assembly’s will on a pending motion can move that the vote on the motion be taken by ballot.
   A. assemblies
   B. Incidental
   C. Permission
   D. expression

   *Answer:* D) expression. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:5 — “A member who believes that a secret vote will give a truer expression of the assembly’s will on a pending motion can move that the vote on the motion be taken by ballot.”

**310.** `[C0664 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An order that the vote on a main motion be taken by ballot also applies to a vote on whether to postpone the main motion _____.

   *Answer:* indefinitely — “An order that the vote on a main motion be taken by ballot also applies to a vote on whether to postpone the main motion indefinitely.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:5 — “An order that the vote on a main motion be taken by ballot also applies to a vote on whether to postpone the main motion indefinitely.”

**311.** `[C0664 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member who believes that a secret vote will give a truer _____ of the assembly’s will on a pending motion can move that the vote on the motion be taken by ballot.

   *Answer:* expression — “A member who believes that a secret vote will give a truer expression of the assembly’s will on a pending motion can move that the vote on the motion be taken by ballot.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:5 — “A member who believes that a secret vote will give a truer expression of the assembly’s will on a pending motion can move that the vote on the motion be taken by ballot.”

**312.** `[C0664 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An order that the vote on a main motion be taken by ballot also applies to a vote on whether to postpone the main motion _____.
   A. adjournment
   B. Preparation
   C. Convention
   D. indefinitely

   *Answer:* D) indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:5 — “An order that the vote on a main motion be taken by ballot also applies to a vote on whether to postpone the main motion indefinitely.”

**313.** `[C0665 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method _____ than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.

   *Answer:* other — “After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:6 — “After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.”

**314.** `[C0665 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other _____, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.
   A. cumulative voting
   B. General Procedure
   C. Methods of Voting
   D. Suspend the Rules

   *Answer:* C) Methods of Voting. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:6 — “After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.”

**315.** `[C0666 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Exhaustion of an Order _____ the Method of Voting.

   *Answer:* Prescribing — “Exhaustion of an Order Prescribing the Method of Voting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:7 — “Exhaustion of an Order Prescribing the Method of Voting.”

**316.** `[C0666 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the method of voting on a motion is ordered by the assembly (and not _____ by the assembly’s rules), such an order is exhausted (1) when the question on which it was imposed has been finally disposed of, or (2) at the conclusion of the session in which the order has been adopted—whichever occurs first.
   A. resolution
   B. Particular
   C. Permission
   D. prescribed

   *Answer:* D) prescribed. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:7 — “If the method of voting on a motion is ordered by the assembly (and not prescribed by the assembly’s rules), such an order is exhausted (1) when the question on which it was imposed has been finally disposed of, or (2) at the conclusion of the session in which the order has been adopted—whichever occurs first.”

**317.** `[C0666 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, after such an order is exhausted, the motion to which it previously applied comes to a vote (for example, during _____ or at a subsequent session), the order is no longer in effect.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “If, after such an order is exhausted, the motion to which it previously applied comes to a vote (for example, during reconsideration or at a subsequent session), the order is no longer in effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:7 — “If, after such an order is exhausted, the motion to which it previously applied comes to a vote (for example, during reconsideration or at a subsequent session), the order is no longer in effect.”

**318.** `[C0666 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Exhaustion of an Order _____ the Method of Voting.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. constitution
   C. Unfinished
   D. Prescribing

   *Answer:* D) Prescribing. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:7 — “Exhaustion of an Order Prescribing the Method of Voting.”

**319.** `[C0666 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the method of voting on a motion is ordered by the assembly (and not _____ by the assembly’s rules), such an order is exhausted (1) when the question on which it was imposed has been finally disposed of, or (2) at the conclusion of the session in which the order has been adopted—whichever occurs first.

   *Answer:* prescribed — “If the method of voting on a motion is ordered by the assembly (and not prescribed by the assembly’s rules), such an order is exhausted (1) when the question on which it was imposed has been finally disposed of, or (2) at the conclusion of the session in which the order has been adopted—whichever occurs first.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:7 — “If the method of voting on a motion is ordered by the assembly (and not prescribed by the assembly’s rules), such an order is exhausted (1) when the question on which it was imposed has been finally disposed of, or (2) at the conclusion of the session in which the order has been adopted—whichever occurs first.”

**320.** `[C0667 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions relating to opening and closing the polls are _____ only with respect to ballot votes.

   *Answer:* applicable — “Motions relating to opening and closing the polls are applicable only with respect to ballot votes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:8 — “Motions relating to opening and closing the polls are applicable only with respect to ballot votes.”

**321.** `[C0667 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the vote is taken by ballot, as soon as the chair thinks that all have voted who wish to, he _____ if all have voted.
   A. inquires
   B. Question
   C. majority
   D. Assembly

   *Answer:* A) inquires. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:8 — “When the vote is taken by ballot, as soon as the chair thinks that all have voted who wish to, he inquires if all have voted.”

**322.** `[C0667 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is no _____, he declares the polls closed, and the tellers proceed to count the vote.

   *Answer:* response — “If there is no response, he declares the polls closed, and the tellers proceed to count the vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:8 — “If there is no response, he declares the polls closed, and the tellers proceed to count the vote.”

**323.** `[C0667 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions relating to opening and closing the polls are _____ only with respect to ballot votes.
   A. Permission
   B. applicable
   C. landscaped
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) applicable. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:8 — “Motions relating to opening and closing the polls are applicable only with respect to ballot votes.”

**324.** `[C0667 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the vote is taken by ballot, as soon as the chair thinks that all have voted who wish to, he _____ if all have voted.

   *Answer:* inquires — “When the vote is taken by ballot, as soon as the chair thinks that all have voted who wish to, he inquires if all have voted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:8 — “When the vote is taken by ballot, as soon as the chair thinks that all have voted who wish to, he inquires if all have voted.”

**325.** `[C0668 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Like motions relating to the close of debate or nominations, the motion to close the polls requires a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “Like motions relating to the close of debate or nominations, the motion to close the polls requires a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:9 — “Like motions relating to the close of debate or nominations, the motion to close the polls requires a two-thirds vote.”

**326.** `[C0668 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion is made to close the polls when the voting has closed naturally, the chair can treat the motion as a _____-consent request and declare the polls closed.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* A) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:9 — “If a motion is made to close the polls when the voting has closed naturally, the chair can treat the motion as a unanimous-consent request and declare the polls closed.”

**327.** `[C0668 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion is made to close the polls when the voting has closed naturally, the chair can treat the motion as a _____ request and declare the polls closed.

   *Answer:* unanimous-consent — “If a motion is made to close the polls when the voting has closed naturally, the chair can treat the motion as a unanimous-consent request and declare the polls closed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:9 — “If a motion is made to close the polls when the voting has closed naturally, the chair can treat the motion as a unanimous-consent request and declare the polls closed.”

**328.** `[C0668 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In any case, a formal motion to close the polls may not be _____ until all have presumably voted.
   A. sustaining
   B. introduced
   C. recognized
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:9 — “In any case, a formal motion to close the polls may not be recognized until all have presumably voted.”

**329.** `[C0668 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Like motions relating to the close of debate or _____, the motion to close the polls requires a two-thirds vote.

   *Answer:* nominations — “Like motions relating to the close of debate or nominations, the motion to close the polls requires a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:9 — “Like motions relating to the close of debate or nominations, the motion to close the polls requires a two-thirds vote.”

**330.** `[C0669 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If members enter afterward and it is desired to reopen the polls, this can be done by a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “If members enter afterward and it is desired to reopen the polls, this can be done by a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:10 — “If members enter afterward and it is desired to reopen the polls, this can be done by a majority vote.”

**331.** `[C0669 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If members enter _____ and it is desired to reopen the polls, this can be done by a majority vote.
   A. Secretary
   B. reception
   C. afterward
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* C) afterward. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:10 — “If members enter afterward and it is desired to reopen the polls, this can be done by a majority vote.”

**332.** `[C0670 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The time at which the polls shall be closed or reopened can be _____ in the motion, or added by amendment.

   *Answer:* specified — “The time at which the polls shall be closed or reopened can be specified in the motion, or added by amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:11 — “The time at which the polls shall be closed or reopened can be specified in the motion, or added by amendment.”
