**333.** `[C0670 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The time at which the polls shall be closed or reopened can be _____ in the motion, or added by amendment.
   A. delegates
   B. Privilege
   C. expressly
   D. specified

   *Answer:* D) specified. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:11 — “The time at which the polls shall be closed or reopened can be specified in the motion, or added by amendment.”

**334.** `[C0671 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While an election is pending, a member may wish to offer a motion to determine the method of making _____ (when it is not prescribed in the bylaws or rules of order).

   *Answer:* nominations — “While an election is pending, a member may wish to offer a motion to determine the method of making nominations (when it is not prescribed in the bylaws or rules of order).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:1 — “While an election is pending, a member may wish to offer a motion to determine the method of making nominations (when it is not prescribed in the bylaws or rules of order).”

**335.** `[C0671 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While an election is pending, a member may wish to offer a motion to determine the method of making _____ (when it is not prescribed in the bylaws or rules of order).
   A. constitution
   B. Convention
   C. nominations
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:1 — “While an election is pending, a member may wish to offer a motion to determine the method of making nominations (when it is not prescribed in the bylaws or rules of order).”

**336.** `[C0672 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to Prescribe Methods of _____.

   *Answer:* Nominating — “Motions to Prescribe Methods of Nominating.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:3 — “Motions to Prescribe Methods of Nominating.”

**337.** `[C0672 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If no method of making _____ is designated by the bylaws or rules and the assembly has adopted no order on the subject, anyone can make a motion prescribing the method of nomination for an office to be filled.
   A. Incidental
   B. Particular
   C. immediately
   D. nominations

   *Answer:* D) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:3 — “If no method of making nominations is designated by the bylaws or rules and the assembly has adopted no order on the subject, anyone can make a motion prescribing the method of nomination for an office to be filled.”

**338.** `[C0672 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that not all of these methods are _____ or desirable in average societies.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “It should be noted that not all of these methods are appropriate or desirable in average societies.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:3 — “It should be noted that not all of these methods are appropriate or desirable in average societies.”

**339.** `[C0672 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions to Prescribe Methods of _____.
   A. Convention
   B. Nominating
   C. privileged
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) Nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:3 — “Motions to Prescribe Methods of Nominating.”

**340.** `[C0672 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no method of making _____ is designated by the bylaws or rules and the assembly has adopted no order on the subject, anyone can make a motion prescribing the method of nomination for an office to be filled.

   *Answer:* nominations — “If no method of making nominations is designated by the bylaws or rules and the assembly has adopted no order on the subject, anyone can make a motion prescribing the method of nomination for an office to be filled.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:3 — “If no method of making nominations is designated by the bylaws or rules and the assembly has adopted no order on the subject, anyone can make a motion prescribing the method of nomination for an office to be filled.”

**341.** `[C0673 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the average society, a motion to close _____ is not a necessary part of the election procedure and it should not generally be moved.

   *Answer:* nominations — “In the average society, a motion to close nominations is not a necessary part of the election procedure and it should not generally be moved.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:4 — “In the average society, a motion to close nominations is not a necessary part of the election procedure and it should not generally be moved.”

**342.** `[C0673 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: as noted above, it is out of order if a member is rising, _____ the chair, or otherwise attempting to make a nomination, and it always requires a twothirds vote.
   A. Reconsider
   B. convention
   C. persuasive
   D. addressing

   *Answer:* D) addressing. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:4 — “as noted above, it is out of order if a member is rising, addressing the chair, or otherwise attempting to make a nomination, and it always requires a twothirds vote.”

**343.** `[C0673 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: as noted above, it is out of order if a member is rising, addressing the chair, or otherwise attempting to make a _____, and it always requires a twothirds vote.

   *Answer:* nomination — “as noted above, it is out of order if a member is rising, addressing the chair, or otherwise attempting to make a nomination, and it always requires a twothirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:4 — “as noted above, it is out of order if a member is rising, addressing the chair, or otherwise attempting to make a nomination, and it always requires a twothirds vote.”

**344.** `[C0673 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the average society, a motion to close _____ is not a necessary part of the election procedure and it should not generally be moved.
   A. Reconsider
   B. nominations
   C. Incidental
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:4 — “In the average society, a motion to close nominations is not a necessary part of the election procedure and it should not generally be moved.”

**345.** `[C0674 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A legitimate use of the motion to close _____ would be, for example, to end delay of an election by numbers of nominations obviously intended only to honor persons who have no chance of being elected.

   *Answer:* nominations — “A legitimate use of the motion to close nominations would be, for example, to end delay of an election by numbers of nominations obviously intended only to honor persons who have no chance of being elected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:5 — “A legitimate use of the motion to close nominations would be, for example, to end delay of an election by numbers of nominations obviously intended only to honor persons who have no chance of being elected.”

**346.** `[C0674 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A legitimate use of the motion to close _____ would be, for example, to end delay of an election by numbers of nominations obviously intended only to honor persons who have no chance of being elected.
   A. nominations
   B. Definitely
   C. interrupted
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:5 — “A legitimate use of the motion to close nominations would be, for example, to end delay of an election by numbers of nominations obviously intended only to honor persons who have no chance of being elected.”

**347.** `[C0675 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When for any reason it is desired to reopen nominations, this can be done by a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “When for any reason it is desired to reopen nominations, this can be done by a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:6 — “When for any reason it is desired to reopen nominations, this can be done by a majority vote.”

**348.** `[C0675 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When for any reason it is desired to reopen _____, this can be done by a majority vote.
   A. nominations
   B. Substitute
   C. constitution
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:6 — “When for any reason it is desired to reopen nominations, this can be done by a majority vote.”

**349.** `[C0676 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The time at which _____ shall be closed or reopened can be specified in the motion, or added by amendment.

   *Answer:* nominations — “The time at which nominations shall be closed or reopened can be specified in the motion, or added by amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:7 — “The time at which nominations shall be closed or reopened can be specified in the motion, or added by amendment.”

**350.** `[C0676 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The time at which _____ shall be closed or reopened can be specified in the motion, or added by amendment.
   A. Procedures
   B. nominations
   C. Permission
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §31:7 — “The time at which nominations shall be closed or reopened can be specified in the motion, or added by amendment.”

**351.** `[C0677 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or _____ that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.

   *Answer:* demand — “In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:1 — “In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.”

**352.** `[C0677 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s _____ to be so excused.
   A. request
   B. Session
   C. Program
   D. Regular

   *Answer:* A) request. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:1 — “In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.”

**353.** `[C0677 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The request can be granted by _____ consent, or a motion to grant it, which is debatable and amendable, can be offered.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “The request can be granted by unanimous consent, or a motion to grant it, which is debatable and amendable, can be offered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:1 — “The request can be granted by unanimous consent, or a motion to grant it, which is debatable and amendable, can be offered.”

**354.** `[C0677 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ the bylaws of a society may impose specific duties on members beyond the mere payment of dues.
   A. interrupted
   B. Occasionally
   C. Preparation
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) Occasionally. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:1 — “Occasionally the bylaws of a society may impose specific duties on members beyond the mere payment of dues.”

**355.** `[C0677 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Members may be obligated to attend a certain number of meetings, to prepare talks or papers, to serve on _____, or even to accept office if elected.

   *Answer:* committees — “Members may be obligated to attend a certain number of meetings, to prepare talks or papers, to serve on committees, or even to accept office if elected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:1 — “Members may be obligated to attend a certain number of meetings, to prepare talks or papers, to serve on committees, or even to accept office if elected.”

**356.** `[C0678 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a duty is not compulsory, a member can decline when he is first named to it or, if absent at that time, when he first learns of his election or _____.

   *Answer:* appointment — “If a duty is not compulsory, a member can decline when he is first named to it or, if absent at that time, when he first learns of his election or appointment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:3 — “If a duty is not compulsory, a member can decline when he is first named to it or, if absent at that time, when he first learns of his election or appointment.”

**357.** `[C0678 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At times other than during a meeting, such a notice of _____ can be addressed to the secretary or to the appointing power.
   A. Organization
   B. Definitely
   C. declination
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) declination. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:3 — “At times other than during a meeting, such a notice of declination can be addressed to the secretary or to the appointing power.”

**358.** `[C0678 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a duty is not _____, a member can decline when he is first named to it or, if absent at that time, when he first learns of his election or appointment.

   *Answer:* compulsory — “If a duty is not compulsory, a member can decline when he is first named to it or, if absent at that time, when he first learns of his election or appointment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:3 — “If a duty is not compulsory, a member can decline when he is first named to it or, if absent at that time, when he first learns of his election or appointment.”

**359.** `[C0678 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At times other than during a meeting, such a notice of _____ can be addressed to the secretary or to the appointing power.

   *Answer:* declination — “At times other than during a meeting, such a notice of declination can be addressed to the secretary or to the appointing power.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:3 — “At times other than during a meeting, such a notice of declination can be addressed to the secretary or to the appointing power.”

**360.** `[C0679 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member who remains silent when presumably aware that he has been named to a duty is regarded as accepting, and he thereby places himself under the same _____ as if he had expressly accepted.

   *Answer:* obligations — “A member who remains silent when presumably aware that he has been named to a duty is regarded as accepting, and he thereby places himself under the same obligations as if he had expressly accepted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:4 — “A member who remains silent when presumably aware that he has been named to a duty is regarded as accepting, and he thereby places himself under the same obligations as if he had expressly accepted.”

**361.** `[C0679 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member who remains silent when presumably aware that he has been named to a duty is regarded as accepting, and he thereby places himself under the same _____ as if he had expressly accepted.
   A. obligations
   B. assemblies
   C. Particular
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) obligations. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:4 — “A member who remains silent when presumably aware that he has been named to a duty is regarded as accepting, and he thereby places himself under the same obligations as if he had expressly accepted.”

**362.** `[C0680 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member who has accepted an office, committee assignment, or other duty finds that he is unable to perform it, he should submit his _____.

   *Answer:* resignation — “If a member who has accepted an office, committee assignment, or other duty finds that he is unable to perform it, he should submit his resignation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:5 — “If a member who has accepted an office, committee assignment, or other duty finds that he is unable to perform it, he should submit his resignation.”

**363.** `[C0680 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, it may be submitted during a meeting either orally or in writing.
   A. resolutions
   B. characteristics
   C. immediately
   D. alternatively

   *Answer:* D) alternatively. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:5 — “alternatively, it may be submitted during a meeting either orally or in writing.”

**364.** `[C0680 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a member who has accepted an office, committee assignment, or other duty finds that he is unable to perform it, he should submit his _____.
   A. constitution
   B. adjournment
   C. authorizes
   D. resignation

   *Answer:* D) resignation. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:5 — “If a member who has accepted an office, committee assignment, or other duty finds that he is unable to perform it, he should submit his resignation.”

**365.** `[C0680 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, it may be submitted during a meeting either orally or in writing.

   *Answer:* alternatively — “alternatively, it may be submitted during a meeting either orally or in writing.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:5 — “alternatively, it may be submitted during a meeting either orally or in writing.”

**366.** `[C0681 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The duties of a position must not be abandoned until a _____ has been accepted and becomes effective, or at least until there has been a reasonable opportunity for it to be accepted.

   *Answer:* resignation — “The duties of a position must not be abandoned until a resignation has been accepted and becomes effective, or at least until there has been a reasonable opportunity for it to be accepted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:6 — “The duties of a position must not be abandoned until a resignation has been accepted and becomes effective, or at least until there has been a reasonable opportunity for it to be accepted.”

**367.** `[C0681 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The duties of a position must not be abandoned until a _____ has been accepted and becomes effective, or at least until there has been a reasonable opportunity for it to be accepted.
   A. authorizes
   B. adjournment
   C. resignation
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) resignation. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:6 — “The duties of a position must not be abandoned until a resignation has been accepted and becomes effective, or at least until there has been a reasonable opportunity for it to be accepted.”

**368.** `[C0682 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A request to be excused from a duty _____ functioning of a society or assembly is a question of privilege affecting the organization of the assembly;

   *Answer:* essential to the — “A request to be excused from a duty essential to the functioning of a society or assembly is a question of privilege affecting the organization of the assembly;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:7 — “A request to be excused from a duty essential to the functioning of a society or assembly is a question of privilege affecting the organization of the assembly;”

**369.** `[C0682 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of a resignation _____, unless the bylaws provide otherwise, the assembly cannot proceed to fill the vacancy immediately since notice is a requirement.
   A. in some way
   B. from office
   C. Mass Meeting
   D. Proxy Voting

   *Answer:* B) from office. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:7 — “In the case of a resignation from office, unless the bylaws provide otherwise, the assembly cannot proceed to fill the vacancy immediately since notice is a requirement.”

**370.** `[C0682 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A request to be excused from a duty essential to the functioning of a society or assembly is a question of privilege affecting the _____ of the assembly;

   *Answer:* organization — “A request to be excused from a duty essential to the functioning of a society or assembly is a question of privilege affecting the organization of the assembly;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:7 — “A request to be excused from a duty essential to the functioning of a society or assembly is a question of privilege affecting the organization of the assembly;”

**371.** `[C0682 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and so also is the filling of a vacancy created by the acceptance of a _____.
   A. Particular
   B. Definitely
   C. requirement
   D. resignation

   *Answer:* D) resignation. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:7 — “and so also is the filling of a vacancy created by the acceptance of a resignation.”

**372.** `[C0682 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases, the assembly can proceed _____ to fill the vacancy, unless notice is required or other provision for filling vacancies is made in the bylaws.

   *Answer:* immediately — “In such cases, the assembly can proceed immediately to fill the vacancy, unless notice is required or other provision for filling vacancies is made in the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:7 — “In such cases, the assembly can proceed immediately to fill the vacancy, unless notice is required or other provision for filling vacancies is made in the bylaws.”

**373.** `[C0683 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member in good standing with his dues paid cannot be compelled to continue his membership so that additional _____ are incurred.
   A. Convention
   B. interrupted
   C. Procedures
   D. obligations

   *Answer:* D) obligations. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:8 — “A member in good standing with his dues paid cannot be compelled to continue his membership so that additional obligations are incurred.”

**374.** `[C0683 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: His _____ should be accepted immediately, and if it is not, he incurs no obligation after his resignation has been sent in, provided he does not avail himself of the privileges of membership.

   *Answer:* resignation — “His resignation should be accepted immediately, and if it is not, he incurs no obligation after his resignation has been sent in, provided he does not avail himself of the privileges of membership.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:8 — “His resignation should be accepted immediately, and if it is not, he incurs no obligation after his resignation has been sent in, provided he does not avail himself of the privileges of membership.”

**375.** `[C0683 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is different with members who have not paid their dues up to the date of sending in their _____.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. requirement
   C. resignations
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) resignations. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:8 — “It is different with members who have not paid their dues up to the date of sending in their resignations.”

**376.** `[C0683 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member in good standing with his dues paid cannot be compelled to continue his membership so that additional _____ are incurred.

   *Answer:* obligations — “A member in good standing with his dues paid cannot be compelled to continue his membership so that additional obligations are incurred.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:8 — “A member in good standing with his dues paid cannot be compelled to continue his membership so that additional obligations are incurred.”

**377.** `[C0683 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: His _____ should be accepted immediately, and if it is not, he incurs no obligation after his resignation has been sent in, provided he does not avail himself of the privileges of membership.
   A. Organization
   B. interrupted
   C. resignation
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) resignation. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:8 — “His resignation should be accepted immediately, and if it is not, he incurs no obligation after his resignation has been sent in, provided he does not avail himself of the privileges of membership.”

**378.** `[C0684 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any member can make the following types of inquiry or request: (a) _____ Inquiry;

   *Answer:* Parliamentary — “Any member can make the following types of inquiry or request: (a) Parliamentary Inquiry;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:1 — “Any member can make the following types of inquiry or request: (a) Parliamentary Inquiry;”

**379.** `[C0684 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any member can make the following types of _____ or request: (a) Parliamentary Inquiry;
   A. Ordered
   B. Regular
   C. Reports
   D. inquiry

   *Answer:* D) inquiry. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:1 — “Any member can make the following types of inquiry or request: (a) Parliamentary Inquiry;”

**380.** `[C0684 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (c) _____ Permission (or Leave) to Withdraw or Modify a Motion;

   *Answer:* For — “(c) For Permission (or Leave) to Withdraw or Modify a Motion;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:1 — “(c) For Permission (or Leave) to Withdraw or Modify a Motion;”

   A. For
   B. class
   C. Order
   D. Point

   *Answer:* A) For. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:1 — “(c) For Permission (or Leave) to Withdraw or Modify a Motion;”

See RONR (12th ed.) §33:2 — “With respect to the requests and inquiries growing out of the business of the assembly that are listed above, the following rules apply:”

   A. inquiries
   B. amendment
   C. Precedent
   D. execution

   *Answer:* A) inquiries. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:2 — “With respect to the requests and inquiries growing out of the business of the assembly that are listed above, the following rules apply:”

**384.** `[C0686 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is a question directed to the presiding officer to obtain information on a matter of parliamentary law or the rules of the organization bearing on the business at hand.

   *Answer:* Parliamentary Inquiry — “A Parliamentary Inquiry is a question directed to the presiding officer to obtain information on a matter of parliamentary law or the rules of the organization bearing on the business at hand.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:3 — “A Parliamentary Inquiry is a question directed to the presiding officer to obtain information on a matter of parliamentary law or the rules of the organization bearing on the business at hand.”

**385.** `[C0686 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ Inquiry is a question directed to the presiding officer to obtain information on a matter of parliamentary law or the rules of the organization bearing on the business at hand.
   A. subsequently
   B. adjournment
   C. Parliamentary
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) Parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:3 — “A Parliamentary Inquiry is a question directed to the presiding officer to obtain information on a matter of parliamentary law or the rules of the organization bearing on the business at hand.”

**386.** `[C0686 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair is not obliged to answer _____ questions.

   *Answer:* hypothetical — “The chair is not obliged to answer hypothetical questions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:3 — “The chair is not obliged to answer hypothetical questions.”

**387.** `[C0686 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair is not obliged to answer _____ questions.
   A. interrupted
   B. Announcements
   C. Information
   D. hypothetical

   *Answer:* D) hypothetical. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:3 — “The chair is not obliged to answer hypothetical questions.”

**388.** `[C0687 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In making an inquiry, the inquirer arises, and without _____ the floor, addresses the chair as follows:

   *Answer:* obtaining — “In making an inquiry, the inquirer arises, and without obtaining the floor, addresses the chair as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:4 — “In making an inquiry, the inquirer arises, and without obtaining the floor, addresses the chair as follows:”

**389.** `[C0687 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In making an inquiry, the inquirer arises, and without _____ the floor, addresses the chair as follows:
   A. secondary
   B. exhausted
   C. obtaining
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* C) obtaining. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:4 — “In making an inquiry, the inquirer arises, and without obtaining the floor, addresses the chair as follows:”

**390.** `[C0688 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair’s reply to a _____ inquiry is not subject to an appeal, since it is an opinion, not a ruling.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The chair’s reply to a parliamentary inquiry is not subject to an appeal, since it is an opinion, not a ruling.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:5 — “The chair’s reply to a parliamentary inquiry is not subject to an appeal, since it is an opinion, not a ruling.”

**391.** `[C0688 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member may act contrary to this opinion, however, and may appeal from a _____ adverse ruling by the chair.
   A. reception
   B. amendment
   C. delegates
   D. resulting

   *Answer:* D) resulting. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:5 — “A member may act contrary to this opinion, however, and may appeal from a resulting adverse ruling by the chair.”

**392.** `[C0688 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair’s reply to a _____ inquiry is not subject to an appeal, since it is an opinion, not a ruling.
   A. Information
   B. parliamentary
   C. representatives
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:5 — “The chair’s reply to a parliamentary inquiry is not subject to an appeal, since it is an opinion, not a ruling.”

**393.** `[C0688 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member may act contrary to this opinion, however, and may appeal from a _____ adverse ruling by the chair.

   *Answer:* resulting — “A member may act contrary to this opinion, however, and may appeal from a resulting adverse ruling by the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:5 — “A member may act contrary to this opinion, however, and may appeal from a resulting adverse ruling by the chair.”

**394.** `[C0689 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ (also called a Point of Information) is a request directed to the chair, or through the chair to another officer or member, for information relevant to the business at hand but not related to parliamentary procedure.

   *Answer:* Request for Information — “A Request for Information (also called a Point of Information) is a request directed to the chair, or through the chair to another officer or member, for information relevant to the business at hand but not related to parliamentary procedure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:6 — “A Request for Information (also called a Point of Information) is a request directed to the chair, or through the chair to another officer or member, for information relevant to the business at hand but not related to parliamentary procedure.”

**395.** `[C0689 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A Request for Information (also called a _____) is a request directed to the chair, or through the chair to another officer or member, for information relevant to the business at hand but not related to parliamentary procedure.
   A. Rising and Reporting
   B. Point of Information
   C. ARTICLE III: MEMBERS
   D. Degrees of Amendment

   *Answer:* B) Point of Information. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:6 — “A Request for Information (also called a Point of Information) is a request directed to the chair, or through the chair to another officer or member, for information relevant to the business at hand but not related to parliamentary procedure.”

   *Answer:* information — “If information is desired of a member who is speaking, the inquirer, upon rising, may use the following form instead:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:8 — “If information is desired of a member who is speaking, the inquirer, upon rising, may use the following form instead:”

   A. information
   B. Unfinished
   C. undebatable
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:8 — “If information is desired of a member who is speaking, the inquirer, upon rising, may use the following form instead:”

**398.** `[C0691 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the speaker consents to the _____, the time consumed will be taken out of his allowed time.

   *Answer:* interruption — “If the speaker consents to the interruption, the time consumed will be taken out of his allowed time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:9 — “If the speaker consents to the interruption, the time consumed will be taken out of his allowed time.”

**399.** `[C0691 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair therefore asks if the speaker is willing to be _____, and if he consents, directs the inquirer to proceed.
   A. interrupted
   B. adjournment
   C. Information
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) interrupted. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:9 — “The chair therefore asks if the speaker is willing to be interrupted, and if he consents, directs the inquirer to proceed.”

**400.** `[C0691 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the _____ officer generally remains silent during the ensuing exchange, the inquiry, the reply, and any resulting colloquy are made in the third person through the chair.

   *Answer:* presiding — “Although the presiding officer generally remains silent during the ensuing exchange, the inquiry, the reply, and any resulting colloquy are made in the third person through the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:9 — “Although the presiding officer generally remains silent during the ensuing exchange, the inquiry, the reply, and any resulting colloquy are made in the third person through the chair.”

**401.** `[C0691 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To protect decorum, members are not allowed to carry on _____ directly with one another.
   A. sustaining
   B. subsidiary
   C. assemblies
   D. discussion

   *Answer:* D) discussion. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:9 — “To protect decorum, members are not allowed to carry on discussion directly with one another.”

**402.** `[C0691 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the speaker consents to the _____, the time consumed will be taken out of his allowed time.
   A. interruption
   B. adjournment
   C. Preparation
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* A) interruption. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:9 — “If the speaker consents to the interruption, the time consumed will be taken out of his allowed time.”

**403.** `[C0692 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An inquiry of this kind may also be for the purpose of _____ a speaker of a point to be made in argument, or it may be intended to rebut his position;

   *Answer:* reminding — “An inquiry of this kind may also be for the purpose of reminding a speaker of a point to be made in argument, or it may be intended to rebut his position;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:10 — “An inquiry of this kind may also be for the purpose of reminding a speaker of a point to be made in argument, or it may be intended to rebut his position;”

   A. reception
   B. rejecting
   C. reminding
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* C) reminding. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:10 — “An inquiry of this kind may also be for the purpose of reminding a speaker of a point to be made in argument, or it may be intended to rebut his position;”

**405.** `[C0693 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Request for Permission (or Leave) to Withdraw or _____.

   *Answer:* Modify a Motion — “Request for Permission (or Leave) to Withdraw or Modify a Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:11 — “Request for Permission (or Leave) to Withdraw or Modify a Motion.”

   A. convention
   B. Permission
   C. Particular
   D. substitute

**407.** `[C0693 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Conditions for _____ or modifying a motion depend upon how soon the mover states his wish to withdraw or modify it.

See RONR (12th ed.) §33:11 — “Conditions for withdrawing or modifying a motion depend upon how soon the mover states his wish to withdraw or modify it.”

   A. authorizes

   C. requirement
   D. Permission

**409.** `[C0694 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, _____ between the making of a motion and the time when the chair places it before the assembly by stating it, the maker can withdraw it as follows:

See RONR (12th ed.) §33:12 — “Thus, in the brief interval between the making of a motion and the time when the chair places it before the assembly by stating it, the maker can withdraw it as follows:”

**410.** `[C0694 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before a motion has been stated by the chair, it is the _____ of its mover, who can withdraw it or modify it without asking the consent of anyone.
   A. property
   B. chairman
   C. Question
   D. decision

   *Answer:* A) property. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:12 — “Before a motion has been stated by the chair, it is the property of its mover, who can withdraw it or modify it without asking the consent of anyone.”

**411.** `[C0694 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, in the brief _____ between the making of a motion and the time when the chair places it before the assembly by stating it, the maker can withdraw it as follows:

   *Answer:* interval — “Thus, in the brief interval between the making of a motion and the time when the chair places it before the assembly by stating it, the maker can withdraw it as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:12 — “Thus, in the brief interval between the making of a motion and the time when the chair places it before the assembly by stating it, the maker can withdraw it as follows:”

**412.** `[C0694 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before a motion has been stated by the chair, it is the _____ of its mover, who can withdraw it or modify it without asking the consent of anyone.

   *Answer:* property — “Before a motion has been stated by the chair, it is the property of its mover, who can withdraw it or modify it without asking the consent of anyone.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:12 — “Before a motion has been stated by the chair, it is the property of its mover, who can withdraw it or modify it without asking the consent of anyone.”

**413.** `[C0695 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a motion has been stated by the chair, it belongs to the meeting as a whole, and the maker must request the assembly’s permission to withdraw or modify his own motion, according to the rules stated in Standard _____ 1–8, above.

   *Answer:* Characteristics — “After a motion has been stated by the chair, it belongs to the meeting as a whole, and the maker must request the assembly’s permission to withdraw or modify his own motion, according to the rules stated in Standard Characteristics 1–8, above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:13 — “After a motion has been stated by the chair, it belongs to the meeting as a whole, and the maker must request the assembly’s permission to withdraw or modify his own motion, according to the rules stated in Standard Characteristics 1–8, above.”

   A. procedure
   B. postponed
   C. reception
   D. execution

   B. Characteristics

**416.** `[C0695 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases the _____ is as follows.

   *Answer:* procedure — “In such cases the procedure is as follows.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:13 — “In such cases the procedure is as follows.”

**417.** `[C0696 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To _____ a motion that is before the assembly, the member who made it may use this form:

   *Answer:* withdraw — “To withdraw a motion that is before the assembly, the member who made it may use this form:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:14 — “To withdraw a motion that is before the assembly, the member who made it may use this form:”

**418.** `[C0696 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To _____ a motion that is before the assembly, the member who made it may use this form:

   *Answer:* D) withdraw. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:14 — “To withdraw a motion that is before the assembly, the member who made it may use this form:”

**419.** `[C0697 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair treats this first as a _____-consent request.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “The chair treats this first as a unanimous-consent request.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:15 — “The chair treats this first as a unanimous-consent request.”

**420.** `[C0697 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair treats this first as a _____ request.
   A. president-elect
   B. President-Elect
   C. Announcements
   D. unanimous-consent

   *Answer:* D) unanimous-consent. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:15 — “The chair treats this first as a unanimous-consent request.”

**421.** `[C0697 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: That is, if no one objects, the _____ is:

   *Answer:* announcement — “That is, if no one objects, the announcement is:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:15 — “That is, if no one objects, the announcement is:”

**422.** `[C0697 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: That is, if no one objects, the _____ is:
   A. Preparation
   B. subsequently
   C. announcement
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:15 — “That is, if no one objects, the announcement is:”

**423.** `[C0698 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A request for _____ to withdraw a motion, or a motion to grant such permission, can be made at any time before voting on the question has begun, even though the motion has been amended, and even though subsidiary or incidental motions may be pending.

   *Answer:* permission — “A request for permission to withdraw a motion, or a motion to grant such permission, can be made at any time before voting on the question has begun, even though the motion has been amended, and even though subsidiary or incidental motions may be pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:16 — “A request for permission to withdraw a motion, or a motion to grant such permission, can be made at any time before voting on the question has begun, even though the motion has been amended, and even though subsidiary or incidental motions may be pending.”

**424.** `[C0698 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any such motions that adhere to the main motion cease to be before the assembly and require no further _____ if the main motion is withdrawn.
   A. disposition
   B. assemblies
   C. Convention
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) disposition. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:16 — “Any such motions that adhere to the main motion cease to be before the assembly and require no further disposition if the main motion is withdrawn.”

**425.** `[C0698 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A request for _____ to withdraw a motion, or a motion to grant such permission, can be made at any time before voting on the question has begun, even though the motion has been amended, and even though subsidiary or incidental motions may be pending.
   A. permission
   B. Incidental
   C. incidental
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* A) permission. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:16 — “A request for permission to withdraw a motion, or a motion to grant such permission, can be made at any time before voting on the question has begun, even though the motion has been amended, and even though subsidiary or incidental motions may be pending.”

**426.** `[C0698 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any such motions that adhere to the main motion cease to be before the assembly and require no further _____ if the main motion is withdrawn.

   *Answer:* disposition — “Any such motions that adhere to the main motion cease to be before the assembly and require no further disposition if the main motion is withdrawn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:16 — “Any such motions that adhere to the main motion cease to be before the assembly and require no further disposition if the main motion is withdrawn.”

**427.** `[C0699 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a question has been divided, one or more of the parts can be _____ without affecting the other parts.

   *Answer:* withdrawn — “After a question has been divided, one or more of the parts can be withdrawn without affecting the other parts.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:17 — “After a question has been divided, one or more of the parts can be withdrawn without affecting the other parts.”

**428.** `[C0699 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a question has been divided, one or more of the parts can be _____ without affecting the other parts.
   A. Objection
   B. auditors’
   C. withdrawn
   D. Reporting

   *Answer:* C) withdrawn. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:17 — “After a question has been divided, one or more of the parts can be withdrawn without affecting the other parts.”

**429.** `[C0700 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a motion has been _____, the situation is as though it had never been made;

   *Answer:* withdrawn — “After a motion has been withdrawn, the situation is as though it had never been made;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:18 — “After a motion has been withdrawn, the situation is as though it had never been made;”

**430.** `[C0700 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a motion has been _____, the situation is as though it had never been made;
   A. withdrawn
   B. Objection
   C. debatable
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* A) withdrawn. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:18 — “After a motion has been withdrawn, the situation is as though it had never been made;”

**431.** `[C0701 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If any member objects, a member has no right to read from—or to have the secretary read from—any paper or book as a part of his speech without _____ of the assembly.
   A. Substitute
   B. permission
   C. substitute
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* B) permission. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:20 — “If any member objects, a member has no right to read from—or to have the secretary read from—any paper or book as a part of his speech without permission of the assembly.”

**432.** `[C0701 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This rule is a _____ against the use of reading as a means of prolonging debate and delaying business.

   *Answer:* protection — “This rule is a protection against the use of reading as a means of prolonging debate and delaying business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:20 — “This rule is a protection against the use of reading as a means of prolonging debate and delaying business.”

**433.** `[C0701 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is _____, however, to permit members to read short, pertinent, printed extracts in debate so long as they do not abuse the privilege.
   A. customary
   B. Crestwood
   C. Objection
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* A) customary. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:20 — “It is customary, however, to permit members to read short, pertinent, printed extracts in debate so long as they do not abuse the privilege.”

**434.** `[C0701 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member wishes to do so, he can, while speaking in debate, say, “If there is no objection, I would like to read… [_____ the nature and length of the paper].” The member can then begin to read unless another member objects.

   *Answer:* indicating — “If a member wishes to do so, he can, while speaking in debate, say, “If there is no objection, I would like to read… [indicating the nature and length of the paper].” The member can then begin to read unless another member objects.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:20 — “If a member wishes to do so, he can, while speaking in debate, say, “If there is no objection, I would like to read… [indicating the nature and length of the paper].” The member can then begin to read unless another member objects.”

**435.** `[C0701 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, at any time until the speaker has finished reading, another member can _____ him by an objection, which must be addressed to the chair.
   A. paragraph
   B. interrupt
   C. secondary
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* B) interrupt. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:20 — “In such a case, at any time until the speaker has finished reading, another member can interrupt him by an objection, which must be addressed to the chair.”

**436.** `[C0702 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is an objection, a _____ vote is required to order that it be read.

   *Answer:* majority — “If there is an objection, a majority vote is required to order that it be read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:21 — “If there is an objection, a majority vote is required to order that it be read.”

**437.** `[C0702 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ paragraph applies only to papers or documents that are not before the assembly for action.
   A. delegates
   B. rejecting
   C. foregoing
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* C) foregoing. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:21 — “The foregoing paragraph applies only to papers or documents that are not before the assembly for action.”

**438.** `[C0702 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and, if there is any debate or _____, that it be read again before members are asked to vote on it.

   *Answer:* amendment — “and, if there is any debate or amendment, that it be read again before members are asked to vote on it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:21 — “and, if there is any debate or amendment, that it be read again before members are asked to vote on it.”

**439.** `[C0702 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except as just stated, no member has the right to have anything read without _____ of the assembly.
   A. introduced
   B. Reconsider
   C. Convention
   D. permission

   *Answer:* D) permission. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:21 — “Except as just stated, no member has the right to have anything read without permission of the assembly.”

**440.** `[C0702 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But whenever any member requests that a document that is before the assembly be read—obviously for _____ and not for delay—and no one objects, the chair normally should direct that it be read.

   *Answer:* information — “But whenever any member requests that a document that is before the assembly be read—obviously for information and not for delay—and no one objects, the chair normally should direct that it be read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:21 — “But whenever any member requests that a document that is before the assembly be read—obviously for information and not for delay—and no one objects, the chair normally should direct that it be read.”

**441.** `[C0703 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Generally, such matters are settled by _____ consent or informally, but if there is an objection, a motion can be made to grant the request.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “Generally, such matters are settled by unanimous consent or informally, but if there is an objection, a motion can be made to grant the request.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:22 — “Generally, such matters are settled by unanimous consent or informally, but if there is an objection, a motion can be made to grant the request.”

**442.** `[C0703 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a member desires to make a request not covered by one of the four types explained above—as, for example, a request to address remarks or make a _____ while no motion is pending— he rises, addresses the chair, and, as soon as he catches the presiding officer’s attention, states his request.
   A. Preparation
   B. Particular
   C. presentation
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:22 — “When a member desires to make a request not covered by one of the four types explained above—as, for example, a request to address remarks or make a presentation while no motion is pending— he rises, addresses the chair, and, as soon as he catches the presiding officer’s attention, states his request.”

**443.** `[C0703 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although he does not have to wait for _____ and can make his request even though another member has been assigned the floor, he should never interrupt a member speaking unless sure that urgency justifies it.

   *Answer:* recognition — “Although he does not have to wait for recognition and can make his request even though another member has been assigned the floor, he should never interrupt a member speaking unless sure that urgency justifies it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:22 — “Although he does not have to wait for recognition and can make his request even though another member has been assigned the floor, he should never interrupt a member speaking unless sure that urgency justifies it.”

**444.** `[C0703 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Generally, such matters are settled by unanimous consent or _____, but if there is an objection, a motion can be made to grant the request.
   A. convention
   B. assemblies
   C. subsidiary
   D. informally

   *Answer:* D) informally. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:22 — “Generally, such matters are settled by unanimous consent or informally, but if there is an objection, a motion can be made to grant the request.”

**445.** `[C0703 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If _____ is required, it can be requested or given, but this must not extend into debate.

   *Answer:* explanation — “If explanation is required, it can be requested or given, but this must not extend into debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §33:22 — “If explanation is required, it can be requested or given, but this must not extend into debate.”

**446.** `[C0704 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The object of the motion to _____ is to make pending again before the assembly a motion or a series of adhering motions that previously has been laid on the table (see 17).

   *Answer:* Take from the Table — “The object of the motion to Take from the Table is to make pending again before the assembly a motion or a series of adhering motions that previously has been laid on the table (see 17).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:1 — “The object of the motion to Take from the Table is to make pending again before the assembly a motion or a series of adhering motions that previously has been laid on the table (see 17).”

**447.** `[C0704 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The object of the motion to Take from the Table is to make pending again before the assembly a motion or a series of adhering motions that _____ has been laid on the table (see 17).
   A. Incidental
   B. previously
   C. recognized
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) previously. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:1 — “The object of the motion to Take from the Table is to make pending again before the assembly a motion or a series of adhering motions that previously has been laid on the table (see 17).”

**448.** `[C0705 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be _____ later as a new question.

   *Answer:* reintroduced — “If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be reintroduced later as a new question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:3 — “If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be reintroduced later as a new question.”

**449.** `[C0705 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As long as a question remains on the table, any member can move to take it from the table at a regular meeting, including a meeting that is an _____ (9) of a regular meeting.
   A. adjournment
   B. deliberative
   C. Permission
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:3 — “As long as a question remains on the table, any member can move to take it from the table at a regular meeting, including a meeting that is an adjournment (9) of a regular meeting.”

**450.** `[C0705 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At a special meeting, however, a question can be taken from the table only if: (a) it has been laid on the table earlier at the same session, or (b) the call of the special meeting specifies either the _____ question that lies on the table or its subject matter.

   *Answer:* particular — “At a special meeting, however, a question can be taken from the table only if: (a) it has been laid on the table earlier at the same session, or (b) the call of the special meeting specifies either the particular question that lies on the table or its subject matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:3 — “At a special meeting, however, a question can be taken from the table only if: (a) it has been laid on the table earlier at the same session, or (b) the call of the special meeting specifies either the particular question that lies on the table or its subject matter.”

**451.** `[C0705 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be _____ later as a new question.
   A. interrupted
   B. reintroduced
   C. Organization
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) reintroduced. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:3 — “If not taken from the table within these time limits, the question dies, although it can be reintroduced later as a new question.”

**452.** `[C0705 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As long as a question remains on the table, any member can move to take it from the table at a regular meeting, including a meeting that is an _____ (9) of a regular meeting.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “As long as a question remains on the table, any member can move to take it from the table at a regular meeting, including a meeting that is an adjournment (9) of a regular meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:3 — “As long as a question remains on the table, any member can move to take it from the table at a regular meeting, including a meeting that is an adjournment (9) of a regular meeting.”

**453.** `[C0706 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Right of Way in _____ to a New Main Motion.

   *Answer:* Preference — “Right of Way in Preference to a New Main Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:4 — “Right of Way in Preference to a New Main Motion.”

**454.** `[C0706 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In ordinary assemblies a question is supposed to be laid on the table only temporarily, with the expectation that its _____ will be resumed after disposal of the interrupting matter or at a more convenient time.
   A. consideration
   B. deliberative
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:4 — “In ordinary assemblies a question is supposed to be laid on the table only temporarily, with the expectation that its consideration will be resumed after disposal of the interrupting matter or at a more convenient time.”

**455.** `[C0706 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, as soon as the business or interrupting matter has been disposed of, any member can seek recognition for the purpose of moving to take the question from the table;

   *Answer:* Consequently — “Consequently, as soon as the business or interrupting matter has been disposed of, any member can seek recognition for the purpose of moving to take the question from the table;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:4 — “Consequently, as soon as the business or interrupting matter has been disposed of, any member can seek recognition for the purpose of moving to take the question from the table;”

**456.** `[C0706 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, so long as it remains on the table, he can do so at any time under the classes of business listed in Standard _____ 1 above—except while another motion is pending or while a series of motions connected with one question is being introduced, as explained in the next paragraph.
   A. Characteristic
   B. requirement
   C. president-elect
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) Characteristic. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:4 — “or, so long as it remains on the table, he can do so at any time under the classes of business listed in Standard Characteristic 1 above—except while another motion is pending or while a series of motions connected with one question is being introduced, as explained in the next paragraph.”

**457.** `[C0706 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair _____ someone else as having risen and addressed the chair first, a member who rose at about the same time to move to take the question from the table should remain standing and say that he rises for this purpose, and the chair then assigns him the floor.

   *Answer:* recognizes — “If the chair recognizes someone else as having risen and addressed the chair first, a member who rose at about the same time to move to take the question from the table should remain standing and say that he rises for this purpose, and the chair then assigns him the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:4 — “If the chair recognizes someone else as having risen and addressed the chair first, a member who rose at about the same time to move to take the question from the table should remain standing and say that he rises for this purpose, and the chair then assigns him the floor.”

**458.** `[C0707 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even if no question is pending, a motion to _____ Table cannot interrupt a series of motions connected with bringing up a single item of business, but must wait until the complete series is disposed of.

   *Answer:* Take from the — “Even if no question is pending, a motion to Take from the Table cannot interrupt a series of motions connected with bringing up a single item of business, but must wait until the complete series is disposed of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:5 — “Even if no question is pending, a motion to Take from the Table cannot interrupt a series of motions connected with bringing up a single item of business, but must wait until the complete series is disposed of.”

**459.** `[C0707 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even if no question is pending, a motion to Take from the _____ cannot interrupt a series of motions connected with bringing up a single item of business, but must wait until the complete series is disposed of.
   A. Annul
   B. CHAIR
   C. Table
   D. board

   *Answer:* C) Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:5 — “Even if no question is pending, a motion to Take from the Table cannot interrupt a series of motions connected with bringing up a single item of business, but must wait until the complete series is disposed of.”

**460.** `[C0707 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For _____, such a series of motions is in process of being dealt with:
   A. example
   B. Adopted
   C. Another
   D. advance

   *Answer:* A) example. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:5 — “For example, such a series of motions is in process of being dealt with:”

**461.** `[C0707 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For _____, such a series of motions is in process of being dealt with:

   *Answer:* example — “For example, such a series of motions is in process of being dealt with:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:5 — “For example, such a series of motions is in process of being dealt with:”

**462.** `[C0708 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, if amendments and a motion to _____ were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.

   *Answer:* Commit — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”

**463.** `[C0708 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The same would be true if a motion to _____ to a Certain Time were adhering to a resolution—that is, the motion to Postpone would become immediately pending —except that if the resolution is not taken from the table until after the time of proposed postponement, the motion to Postpone is ignored.
   A. Meetings
   B. Nominees
   C. Postpone
   D. chairman

   *Answer:* C) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “The same would be true if a motion to Postpone to a Certain Time were adhering to a resolution—that is, the motion to Postpone would become immediately pending —except that if the resolution is not taken from the table until after the time of proposed postponement, the motion to Postpone is ignored.”

**464.** `[C0708 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An order for the _____ or a limitation or extension of debate is not exhausted, however, if the question to which such an order was applied is taken from the table at the same session, even on another day—as in a convention.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “An order for the Previous Question or a limitation or extension of debate is not exhausted, however, if the question to which such an order was applied is taken from the table at the same session, even on another day—as in a convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “An order for the Previous Question or a limitation or extension of debate is not exhausted, however, if the question to which such an order was applied is taken from the table at the same session, even on another day—as in a convention.”

**465.** `[C0708 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is taken from the table, it is before the assembly, with _____ adhering to it, so far as possible (and with the exceptions noted below), exactly as it was when laid on the table.
   A. everything
   B. subsidiary
   C. Incidental
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* A) everything. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “When a question is taken from the table, it is before the assembly, with everything adhering to it, so far as possible (and with the exceptions noted below), exactly as it was when laid on the table.”

**466.** `[C0708 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, if _____ and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.

   *Answer:* amendments — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”

**467.** `[C0709 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At the next session any such order is _____ and the regular rules of debate prevail.

   *Answer:* exhausted — “At the next session any such order is exhausted and the regular rules of debate prevail.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:7 — “At the next session any such order is exhausted and the regular rules of debate prevail.”

**468.** `[C0709 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At the next session any such order is _____ and the regular rules of debate prevail.
   A. Privilege
   B. extending
   C. secretary
   D. exhausted

   *Answer:* D) exhausted. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:7 — “At the next session any such order is exhausted and the regular rules of debate prevail.”

**469.** `[C0710 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The form used in making this motion is, for example, “I move to take from the table the _____ relating to… and its amendment.”

   *Answer:* resolution — “The form used in making this motion is, for example, “I move to take from the table the resolution relating to… and its amendment.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:8 — “The form used in making this motion is, for example, “I move to take from the table the resolution relating to… and its amendment.””

**470.** `[C0710 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The form used in making this motion is, for example, “I move to take from the table the _____ relating to… and its amendment.”
   A. Permission
   B. authorizes
   C. settlement
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:8 — “The form used in making this motion is, for example, “I move to take from the table the resolution relating to… and its amendment.””

**471.** `[C0711 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If Member A, who has risen to seek the floor for the purpose of making this motion, observes that the chair has _____ another member who rose at about the same time and who apparently intends to make a new main motion, the procedure would be as follows:

   *Answer:* recognized — “If Member A, who has risen to seek the floor for the purpose of making this motion, observes that the chair has recognized another member who rose at about the same time and who apparently intends to make a new main motion, the procedure would be as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:9 — “If Member A, who has risen to seek the floor for the purpose of making this motion, observes that the chair has recognized another member who rose at about the same time and who apparently intends to make a new main motion, the procedure would be as follows:”

**472.** `[C0711 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If Member A, who has risen to seek the floor for the purpose of making this motion, observes that the chair has _____ another member who rose at about the same time and who apparently intends to make a new main motion, the procedure would be as follows:
   A. recognized
   B. introduced
   C. Unfinished
   D. committees

   *Answer:* A) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:9 — “If Member A, who has risen to seek the floor for the purpose of making this motion, observes that the chair has recognized another member who rose at about the same time and who apparently intends to make a new main motion, the procedure would be as follows:”

**473.** `[C0712 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If Member A did not rise to claim the floor before the chair _____ another member who already has made a new motion, then before this question has been stated by the chair, Member A can quickly rise and address the chair, thus:

   *Answer:* recognized — “If Member A did not rise to claim the floor before the chair recognized another member who already has made a new motion, then before this question has been stated by the chair, Member A can quickly rise and address the chair, thus:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:10 — “If Member A did not rise to claim the floor before the chair recognized another member who already has made a new motion, then before this question has been stated by the chair, Member A can quickly rise and address the chair, thus:”

**474.** `[C0712 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If Member A did not rise to claim the floor before the chair _____ another member who already has made a new motion, then before this question has been stated by the chair, Member A can quickly rise and address the chair, thus:
   A. Particular
   B. subsidiary
   C. recognized
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:10 — “If Member A did not rise to claim the floor before the chair recognized another member who already has made a new motion, then before this question has been stated by the chair, Member A can quickly rise and address the chair, thus:”

**475.** `[C0713 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By means of the motions to _____ and to Amend Something Previously Adopted—which are two forms of one incidental main motion governed by identical rules—the assembly can change an action previously taken or ordered.

   *Answer:* Rescind — “By means of the motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted—which are two forms of one incidental main motion governed by identical rules—the assembly can change an action previously taken or ordered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:1 — “By means of the motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted—which are two forms of one incidental main motion governed by identical rules—the assembly can change an action previously taken or ordered.”

**476.** `[C0713 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By means of the motions to Rescind and to _____ Something Previously Adopted—which are two forms of one incidental main motion governed by identical rules—the assembly can change an action previously taken or ordered.
   A. Amend
   B. Other
   C. limit
   D. floor

   *Answer:* A) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:1 — “By means of the motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted—which are two forms of one incidental main motion governed by identical rules—the assembly can change an action previously taken or ordered.”

**477.** `[C0713 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rescind—also known as _____ or Annul—is the motion by which a previous action or order can be canceled or countermanded.
   A. Abuses
   B. agenda
   C. Repeal
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* C) Repeal. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:1 — “Rescind—also known as Repeal or Annul—is the motion by which a previous action or order can be canceled or countermanded.”

**478.** `[C0713 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rescind—also known as Repeal or _____—is the motion by which a previous action or order can be canceled or countermanded.

   *Answer:* Annul — “Rescind—also known as Repeal or Annul—is the motion by which a previous action or order can be canceled or countermanded.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:1 — “Rescind—also known as Repeal or Annul—is the motion by which a previous action or order can be canceled or countermanded.”

**479.** `[C0714 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Right of Any Member to Make the Motions, Without Time _____.

   *Answer:* Limit — “Right of Any Member to Make the Motions, Without Time Limit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:3 — “Right of Any Member to Make the Motions, Without Time Limit.”

**480.** `[C0714 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In contrast to the case of the motion to _____, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.
   A. Substitute
   B. incidental
   C. Reconsider
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:3 — “In contrast to the case of the motion to Reconsider, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.”

**481.** `[C0714 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When _____ notice has been given, it is usual to wait for the member who gave notice of these motions to move them;

   *Answer:* previous — “When previous notice has been given, it is usual to wait for the member who gave notice of these motions to move them;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:3 — “When previous notice has been given, it is usual to wait for the member who gave notice of these motions to move them;”

**482.** `[C0714 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Right of Any Member to Make the Motions, Without Time _____.
   A. amend
   B. Board
   C. event
   D. Limit

   *Answer:* D) Limit. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:3 — “Right of Any Member to Make the Motions, Without Time Limit.”

**483.** `[C0714 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In contrast to the case of the motion to _____, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “In contrast to the case of the motion to Reconsider, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:3 — “In contrast to the case of the motion to Reconsider, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.”

**484.** `[C0715 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a _____ for the adoption of a motion to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.

   *Answer:* requirement — “As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a requirement for the adoption of a motion to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:4 — “As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a requirement for the adoption of a motion to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.”

**485.** `[C0715 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a requirement for the adoption of a motion to _____ or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.
   A. Program
   B. Rescind
   C. Regular
   D. applied

   *Answer:* B) Rescind. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:4 — “As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a requirement for the adoption of a motion to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.”

**486.** `[C0715 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In either of the situations described above, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change going beyond the scope of the notice which was given, for the reason that adoption of such a motion will destroy the effect of the notice, and the motion is thus tantamount to a motion to _____.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “In either of the situations described above, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change going beyond the scope of the notice which was given, for the reason that adoption of such a motion will destroy the effect of the notice, and the motion is thus tantamount to a motion to Postpone Indefinitely.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:4 — “In either of the situations described above, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change going beyond the scope of the notice which was given, for the reason that adoption of such a motion will destroy the effect of the notice, and the motion is thus tantamount to a motion to Postpone Indefinitely.”

**487.** `[C0715 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It will also be the case, as a practical matter, whenever a _____ of the entire membership is not in attendance at the time the vote is taken on a motion to rescind or amend a provision of the constitution or bylaws, or a special rule of order.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:4 — “It will also be the case, as a practical matter, whenever a majority of the entire membership is not in attendance at the time the vote is taken on a motion to rescind or amend a provision of the constitution or bylaws, or a special rule of order.”

**488.** `[C0715 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In either of the situations described above, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change going beyond the scope of the notice which was given, for the reason that adoption of such a motion will destroy the effect of the notice, and the motion is thus tantamount to a motion to _____.
   A. Renewal of the Motion
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Question on the Table
   D. or Motion Was Pending

   *Answer:* B) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:4 — “In either of the situations described above, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change going beyond the scope of the notice which was given, for the reason that adoption of such a motion will destroy the effect of the notice, and the motion is thus tantamount to a motion to Postpone Indefinitely.”

**489.** `[C0716 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is possible to offer and adopt several amendments to previously adopted text by means of a single incidental main motion to _____ governed by the rules in this section and in 10.
