   *Answer:* Amend Something Previously Adopted — “It is possible to offer and adopt several amendments to previously adopted text by means of a single incidental main motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted governed by the rules in this section and in 10.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:5 — “It is possible to offer and adopt several amendments to previously adopted text by means of a single incidental main motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted governed by the rules in this section and in 10.”

**490.** `[C0716 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Series of _____ to Previously Adopted Text.
   A. Procedures
   B. incidental
   C. Incidental
   D. Amendments

   *Answer:* D) Amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:5 — “Series of Amendments to Previously Adopted Text.”

**491.** `[C0716 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is possible to offer and adopt several amendments to previously adopted text by means of a single incidental main motion to _____ governed by the rules in this section and in 10.
   A. Motions to implement recommendations
   B. Question Unless Separately Ordered
   C. Appointment of the parliamentarian
   D. Amend Something Previously Adopted

   *Answer:* D) Amend Something Previously Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:5 — “It is possible to offer and adopt several amendments to previously adopted text by means of a single incidental main motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted governed by the rules in this section and in 10.”

**492.** `[C0716 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Series of _____ to Previously Adopted Text.

   *Answer:* Amendments — “Series of Amendments to Previously Adopted Text.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:5 — “Series of Amendments to Previously Adopted Text.”

**493.** `[C0717 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motions to _____ and to Amend Something Previously Adopted are not in order under the following circumstances:

   *Answer:* Rescind — “The motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted are not in order under the following circumstances:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:6 — “The motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted are not in order under the following circumstances:”

**494.** `[C0717 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motions to Rescind and to _____ Adopted are not in order under the following circumstances:
   A. Ordering a roll-call vote
   B. Consideration by Paragraph
   C. Nominations by a Committee
   D. Amend Something Previously

   *Answer:* D) Amend Something Previously. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:6 — “The motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted are not in order under the following circumstances:”

**495.** `[C0717 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Actions That Cannot Be _____ or Amended.
   A. reception
   B. Rescinded
   C. Precedent
   D. exhausted

   *Answer:* B) Rescinded. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:6 — “Actions That Cannot Be Rescinded or Amended.”

**496.** `[C0717 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Actions That Cannot Be _____ or Amended.

   *Answer:* Rescinded — “Actions That Cannot Be Rescinded or Amended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:6 — “Actions That Cannot Be Rescinded or Amended.”

**497.** `[C0718 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When previous notice has been given, the motions to _____ or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:

   *Answer:* Rescind — “When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:7 — “When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:”

**498.** `[C0718 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to _____ may be made as follows:
   A. Carrying Over Unfinished Business
   B. secondary amendment by inserting
   C. Amend Something Previously Adopted
   D. Electronic roll-call vote installation

   *Answer:* C) Amend Something Previously Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:7 — “When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:”

**499.** `[C0719 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When no notice of the motion to _____ or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:

   *Answer:* Rescind — “When no notice of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:8 — “When no notice of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:”

**500.** `[C0719 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When no notice of the motion to Rescind or to _____ Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:
   A. limit
   B. legal
   C. Amend
   D. Refer

   *Answer:* C) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:8 — “When no notice of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:”

**501.** `[C0720 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a great many instances, the motion or _____ originally adopted is not referred to, and only the bylaw, rule, or policy to be rescinded or amended is mentioned.

   *Answer:* resolution — “In a great many instances, the motion or resolution originally adopted is not referred to, and only the bylaw, rule, or policy to be rescinded or amended is mentioned.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:9 — “In a great many instances, the motion or resolution originally adopted is not referred to, and only the bylaw, rule, or policy to be rescinded or amended is mentioned.”

**502.** `[C0720 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a great many instances, the motion or _____ originally adopted is not referred to, and only the bylaw, rule, or policy to be rescinded or amended is mentioned.
   A. resolution
   B. introduced
   C. Reconsider
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:9 — “In a great many instances, the motion or resolution originally adopted is not referred to, and only the bylaw, rule, or policy to be rescinded or amended is mentioned.”

**503.** `[C0721 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion was made “To amend the motion relating to… adopted at the May meeting… by _____…”:

   *Answer:* inserting — “If the motion was made “To amend the motion relating to… adopted at the May meeting… by inserting…”:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:11 — “If the motion was made “To amend the motion relating to… adopted at the May meeting… by inserting…”:”

**504.** `[C0721 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion was made “To amend the motion relating to… adopted at the May meeting… by _____…”:
   A. Reporting
   B. exhausted
   C. inserting
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* C) inserting. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:11 — “If the motion was made “To amend the motion relating to… adopted at the May meeting… by inserting…”:”

**505.** `[C0722 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion was made “To rescind the resolution that authorized additional _____ of the grounds”:

   *Answer:* landscaping — “If the motion was made “To rescind the resolution that authorized additional landscaping of the grounds”:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:12 — “If the motion was made “To rescind the resolution that authorized additional landscaping of the grounds”:”

**506.** `[C0722 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion was made “To rescind the resolution that authorized additional _____ of the grounds”:
   A. landscaping
   B. Definitely
   C. Unfinished
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) landscaping. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:12 — “If the motion was made “To rescind the resolution that authorized additional landscaping of the grounds”:”

**507.** `[C0723 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or _____).

   *Answer:* the Record — “On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:13 — “On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).”

**508.** `[C0723 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Adoption of this motion requires an affirmative vote of a _____ of the entire membership, and may be inadvisable unless the support is even greater.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* D) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:13 — “Adoption of this motion requires an affirmative vote of a majority of the entire membership, and may be inadvisable unless the support is even greater.”

**509.** `[C0723 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest _____, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).
   A. authorizes
   B. immediately
   C. Information
   D. disapproval

   *Answer:* D) disapproval. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:13 — “On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).”

**510.** `[C0723 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Adoption of this motion requires an _____ vote of a majority of the entire membership, and may be inadvisable unless the support is even greater.

   *Answer:* affirmative — “Adoption of this motion requires an affirmative vote of a majority of the entire membership, and may be inadvisable unless the support is even greater.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:13 — “Adoption of this motion requires an affirmative vote of a majority of the entire membership, and may be inadvisable unless the support is even greater.”

**511.** `[C0723 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even a unanimous vote at a meeting is _____ if that vote is not a majority of the entire membership.
   A. insufficient
   B. resolutions
   C. Permission
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) insufficient. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:13 — “Even a unanimous vote at a meeting is insufficient if that vote is not a majority of the entire membership.”

**512.** `[C0724 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By means of the motion to _____ from further consideration of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.

   *Answer:* Discharge a Committee — “By means of the motion to Discharge a Committee from further consideration of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:1 — “By means of the motion to Discharge a Committee from further consideration of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.”

**513.** `[C0724 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By means of the motion to Discharge a Committee from further _____ of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. consideration
   C. recommendation
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:1 — “By means of the motion to Discharge a Committee from further consideration of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.”

**514.** `[C0725 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: So long as a question is in the hands of a committee, the assembly cannot consider another motion involving _____ the same question.

   *Answer:* practically — “So long as a question is in the hands of a committee, the assembly cannot consider another motion involving practically the same question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:2 — “So long as a question is in the hands of a committee, the assembly cannot consider another motion involving practically the same question.”

**515.** `[C0725 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: So long as a question is in the hands of a committee, the assembly cannot consider another motion involving _____ the same question.
   A. practically
   B. immediately
   C. Substitute
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) practically. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:2 — “So long as a question is in the hands of a committee, the assembly cannot consider another motion involving practically the same question.”

**516.** `[C0727 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Action to discharge a committee from further _____ of a question or subject is generally advisable only when the committee has failed to report with appropriate promptness or when, for some urgent reason, the assembly desires to proceed on the matter without further aid from the committee, or wishes to drop the matter.
   A. recommendation
   B. undebatable
   C. Vice-President
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:5 — “Action to discharge a committee from further consideration of a question or subject is generally advisable only when the committee has failed to report with appropriate promptness or when, for some urgent reason, the assembly desires to proceed on the matter without further aid from the committee, or wishes to drop the matter.”

**517.** `[C0727 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Action to discharge a committee from further _____ of a question or subject is generally advisable only when the committee has failed to report with appropriate promptness or when, for some urgent reason, the assembly desires to proceed on the matter without further aid from the committee, or wishes to drop the matter.

   *Answer:* consideration — “Action to discharge a committee from further consideration of a question or subject is generally advisable only when the committee has failed to report with appropriate promptness or when, for some urgent reason, the assembly desires to proceed on the matter without further aid from the committee, or wishes to drop the matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:5 — “Action to discharge a committee from further consideration of a question or subject is generally advisable only when the committee has failed to report with appropriate promptness or when, for some urgent reason, the assembly desires to proceed on the matter without further aid from the committee, or wishes to drop the matter.”

**518.** `[C0728 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to _____ (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to Reconsider (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:6 — “If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to Reconsider (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.”

**519.** `[C0728 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion of referral may have been a subsidiary motion to _____ (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.
   A. Motion
   B. adopts
   C. Commit
   D. adhere

   *Answer:* C) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:6 — “The motion of referral may have been a subsidiary motion to Commit (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.”

**520.** `[C0728 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to Reconsider (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to Reconsider (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:6 — “If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to Reconsider (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.”

**521.** `[C0728 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion of referral may have been a _____ motion to Commit (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.

   *Answer:* subsidiary — “The motion of referral may have been a subsidiary motion to Commit (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:6 — “The motion of referral may have been a subsidiary motion to Commit (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.”

**522.** `[C0729 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to _____, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.

   *Answer:* Discharge a Committee — “A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:7 — “A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.”

**523.** `[C0729 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If no instruction as to time of reporting has been given previously, this motion requires only a _____ vote for adoption.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:7 — “If no instruction as to time of reporting has been given previously, this motion requires only a majority vote for adoption.”

**524.** `[C0729 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Instead of _____ the committee, the assembly can instruct it to report at a reasonable specified time.

   *Answer:* discharging — “Instead of discharging the committee, the assembly can instruct it to report at a reasonable specified time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:7 — “Instead of discharging the committee, the assembly can instruct it to report at a reasonable specified time.”

**525.** `[C0729 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to do this can be moved as a _____ (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Incidental
   C. substitute
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:7 — “A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.”

**526.** `[C0729 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no _____ as to time of reporting has been given previously, this motion requires only a majority vote for adoption.

   *Answer:* instruction — “If no instruction as to time of reporting has been given previously, this motion requires only a majority vote for adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:7 — “If no instruction as to time of reporting has been given previously, this motion requires only a majority vote for adoption.”

**527.** `[C0730 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No motion to _____ is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then automatically discharged from further consideration of the matter.

   *Answer:* Discharge a Committee — “No motion to Discharge a Committee is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then automatically discharged from further consideration of the matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:8 — “No motion to Discharge a Committee is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then automatically discharged from further consideration of the matter.”

**528.** `[C0730 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No motion to Discharge a Committee is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then _____ discharged from further consideration of the matter.
   A. president-elect
   B. automatically
   C. Information
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:8 — “No motion to Discharge a Committee is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then automatically discharged from further consideration of the matter.”

**529.** `[C0731 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a committee is discharged from _____ a matter, either by the adoption of a motion to discharge it or by the submission of its final report, the committee continues in existence if it is a standing committee, but ceases to exist if it is a special committee that was appointed to take up the matter.

   *Answer:* considering — “When a committee is discharged from considering a matter, either by the adoption of a motion to discharge it or by the submission of its final report, the committee continues in existence if it is a standing committee, but ceases to exist if it is a special committee that was appointed to take up the matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:9 — “When a committee is discharged from considering a matter, either by the adoption of a motion to discharge it or by the submission of its final report, the committee continues in existence if it is a standing committee, but ceases to exist if it is a special committee that was appointed to take up the matter.”

**530.** `[C0731 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee is _____ from considering a matter, either by the adoption of a motion to discharge it or by the submission of its final report, the committee continues in existence if it is a standing committee, but ceases to exist if it is a special committee that was appointed to take up the matter.
   A. settlement
   B. Definitely
   C. discharged
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* C) discharged. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:9 — “When a committee is discharged from considering a matter, either by the adoption of a motion to discharge it or by the submission of its final report, the committee continues in existence if it is a standing committee, but ceases to exist if it is a special committee that was appointed to take up the matter.”

**531.** `[C0732 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a committee is discharged from further consideration of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to _____, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).

   *Answer:* Commit — “When a committee is discharged from further consideration of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “When a committee is discharged from further consideration of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).”

**532.** `[C0732 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to _____ specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. Discharge a Committee
   C. Renewal of the Motion
   D. question of privilege

   *Answer:* B) Discharge a Committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “If a motion to Discharge a Committee specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.”

**533.** `[C0732 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to Discharge a Committee includes a provision making the question a special order, it requires a _____ vote, just as any other motion to make a special order.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “If the motion to Discharge a Committee includes a provision making the question a special order, it requires a two-thirds vote, just as any other motion to make a special order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “If the motion to Discharge a Committee includes a provision making the question a special order, it requires a two-thirds vote, just as any other motion to make a special order.”

**534.** `[C0732 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee is discharged from further _____ of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).
   A. consideration
   B. Parliamentary
   C. interrupted
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “When a committee is discharged from further consideration of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).”

**535.** `[C0732 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no later time was _____ in the motion, the question can then be postponed, if desired;

   *Answer:* specified — “If no later time was specified in the motion, the question can then be postponed, if desired;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “If no later time was specified in the motion, the question can then be postponed, if desired;”

**536.** `[C0733 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a _____ motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).

   *Answer:* main — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:11 — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**537.** `[C0733 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to _____ or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).
   A. Rescind
   B. applied
   C. Adopted
   D. advance

   *Answer:* A) Rescind. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:11 — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**538.** `[C0734 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The form used in making this motion, as applied to a question being considered by a _____, may be:

   *Answer:* standing committee — “The form used in making this motion, as applied to a question being considered by a standing committee, may be:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:12 — “The form used in making this motion, as applied to a question being considered by a standing committee, may be:”

**539.** `[C0734 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The form used in making this motion, as applied to a question being _____ by a standing committee, may be:
   A. resolution
   B. landscaped
   C. considered
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:12 — “The form used in making this motion, as applied to a question being considered by a standing committee, may be:”

**540.** `[C0735 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to take up the question at a later time, there may be added to either of the above forms, for example, the words, “and that the _____ be considered at 4 P.M.” (in which case it is a general order for that time), or, “and that it be made a special order for…”

   *Answer:* resolution — “If it is desired to take up the question at a later time, there may be added to either of the above forms, for example, the words, “and that the resolution be considered at 4 P.M.” (in which case it is a general order for that time), or, “and that it be made a special order for…””. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:14 — “If it is desired to take up the question at a later time, there may be added to either of the above forms, for example, the words, “and that the resolution be considered at 4 P.M.” (in which case it is a general order for that time), or, “and that it be made a special order for…””

**541.** `[C0735 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is desired to take up the question at a later time, there may be added to either of the above forms, for example, the words, “and that the _____ be considered at 4 P.M.” (in which case it is a general order for that time), or, “and that it be made a special order for…”
   A. Unfinished
   B. persuasive
   C. privileged
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:14 — “If it is desired to take up the question at a later time, there may be added to either of the above forms, for example, the words, “and that the resolution be considered at 4 P.M.” (in which case it is a general order for that time), or, “and that it be made a special order for…””

**542.** `[C0736 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for consideration at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to _____), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.

   *Answer:* Commit — “If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for consideration at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:15 — “If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for consideration at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.”

**543.** `[C0736 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for _____ at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.
   A. Preparation
   B. Announcements
   C. resolutions
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:15 — “If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for consideration at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.”

**544.** `[C0737 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____—a motion of American origin—enables a majority in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Reconsider—a motion of American origin—enables a majority in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:1 — “Reconsider—a motion of American origin—enables a majority in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.”

**545.** `[C0737 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Reconsider—a motion of American origin—enables a _____ in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* A) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:1 — “Reconsider—a motion of American origin—enables a majority in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.”

**546.** `[C0737 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The purpose of _____ a vote is to permit correction of hasty, ill-advised, or erroneous action, or to take into account added information or a changed situation that has developed since the taking of the vote.
   A. reconsidering
   B. constitution
   C. recommendation
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* A) reconsidering. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:1 — “The purpose of reconsidering a vote is to permit correction of hasty, ill-advised, or erroneous action, or to take into account added information or a changed situation that has developed since the taking of the vote.”

**547.** `[C0737 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The purpose of _____ a vote is to permit correction of hasty, ill-advised, or erroneous action, or to take into account added information or a changed situation that has developed since the taking of the vote.

   *Answer:* reconsidering — “The purpose of reconsidering a vote is to permit correction of hasty, ill-advised, or erroneous action, or to take into account added information or a changed situation that has developed since the taking of the vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:1 — “The purpose of reconsidering a vote is to permit correction of hasty, ill-advised, or erroneous action, or to take into account added information or a changed situation that has developed since the taking of the vote.”

**548.** `[C0739 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “The motion to Reconsider can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:3 — “The motion to Reconsider can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.”

**549.** `[C0739 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is neither a main motion nor a secondary motion, but in certain respects it assumes the character of the motion to which it is applied (that is, the motion to be _____), as will be seen below.
   A. constitution
   B. Preparation
   C. Incidental
   D. reconsidered

   *Answer:* D) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:3 — “It is neither a main motion nor a secondary motion, but in certain respects it assumes the character of the motion to which it is applied (that is, the motion to be reconsidered), as will be seen below.”

**550.** `[C0739 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to _____ can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.
   A. sustaining
   B. Reconsider
   C. assemblies
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* B) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:3 — “The motion to Reconsider can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.”

**551.** `[C0739 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is neither a main motion nor a secondary motion, but in certain respects it assumes the character of the motion to which it is applied (that is, the motion to be _____), as will be seen below.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “It is neither a main motion nor a secondary motion, but in certain respects it assumes the character of the motion to which it is applied (that is, the motion to be reconsidered), as will be seen below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:3 — “It is neither a main motion nor a secondary motion, but in certain respects it assumes the character of the motion to which it is applied (that is, the motion to be reconsidered), as will be seen below.”

**552.** `[C0740 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The full procedure for _____ a vote involves essentially three stages, which may be summarized as follows:

   *Answer:* reconsidering — “The full procedure for reconsidering a vote involves essentially three stages, which may be summarized as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:4 — “The full procedure for reconsidering a vote involves essentially three stages, which may be summarized as follows:”

**553.** `[C0740 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The full procedure for _____ a vote involves essentially three stages, which may be summarized as follows:
   A. Indefinitely
   B. reconsidering
   C. Vice-President
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) reconsidering. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:4 — “The full procedure for reconsidering a vote involves essentially three stages, which may be summarized as follows:”

**554.** `[C0741 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The general rule therefore is that _____ cannot be applied to an affirmative vote whose provisions have been partly carried out or to any vote that has caused something to be done that it is impossible to undo (see Standard Characteristic 2, below).

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “The general rule therefore is that Reconsider cannot be applied to an affirmative vote whose provisions have been partly carried out or to any vote that has caused something to be done that it is impossible to undo (see Standard Characteristic 2, below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:5 — “The general rule therefore is that Reconsider cannot be applied to an affirmative vote whose provisions have been partly carried out or to any vote that has caused something to be done that it is impossible to undo (see Standard Characteristic 2, below).”

**555.** `[C0741 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The general rule therefore is that Reconsider cannot be applied to an affirmative vote whose provisions have been partly carried out or to any vote that has caused something to be done that it is impossible to undo (see Standard _____ 2, below).
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. adjournment
   C. consideration
   D. Characteristic

   *Answer:* D) Characteristic. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:5 — “The general rule therefore is that Reconsider cannot be applied to an affirmative vote whose provisions have been partly carried out or to any vote that has caused something to be done that it is impossible to undo (see Standard Characteristic 2, below).”

**556.** `[C0742 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although at this stage the motion to be reconsidered is not yet pending, debate on the motion to _____ (if it is debatable;

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Although at this stage the motion to be reconsidered is not yet pending, debate on the motion to Reconsider (if it is debatable;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:6 — “Although at this stage the motion to be reconsidered is not yet pending, debate on the motion to Reconsider (if it is debatable;”

**557.** `[C0742 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although at this stage the motion to be _____ is not yet pending, debate on the motion to Reconsider (if it is debatable;
   A. Indefinitely
   B. reconsidered
   C. Permission
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* B) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:6 — “Although at this stage the motion to be reconsidered is not yet pending, debate on the motion to Reconsider (if it is debatable;”

**558.** `[C0743 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Third, if the motion to _____ is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:7 — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”

**559.** `[C0743 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been _____, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.
   A. requirement
   B. reconsidered
   C. Definitely
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:7 — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”

**560.** `[C0743 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The original vote is thus canceled, and, before any new vote on it is taken, the question is again open to debate, amendment, or any other action _____ in the case.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “The original vote is thus canceled, and, before any new vote on it is taken, the question is again open to debate, amendment, or any other action appropriate in the case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:7 — “The original vote is thus canceled, and, before any new vote on it is taken, the question is again open to debate, amendment, or any other action appropriate in the case.”

**561.** `[C0743 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, however, the motion to Reconsider is rejected, the vote which it proposed to reconsider, as well as any action held up because of the proposed _____, goes back into effect, just as though the motion to Reconsider had never been made.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. reconsideration
   C. Announcements
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* B) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:7 — “If, however, the motion to Reconsider is rejected, the vote which it proposed to reconsider, as well as any action held up because of the proposed reconsideration, goes back into effect, just as though the motion to Reconsider had never been made.”

**562.** `[C0743 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The original vote is thus canceled, and, before any new vote on it is taken, the question is again open to debate, amendment, or any other action _____ in the case.
   A. Information
   B. Particular
   C. appropriate
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) appropriate. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:7 — “The original vote is thus canceled, and, before any new vote on it is taken, the question is again open to debate, amendment, or any other action appropriate in the case.”

**563.** `[C0745 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By Whom and When a Motion to Reconsider Can Be _____.

   *Answer:* Made — “By Whom and When a Motion to Reconsider Can Be Made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:10 — “By Whom and When a Motion to Reconsider Can Be Made.”

**564.** `[C0745 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By Whom and When a Motion to _____ Can Be Made.
   A. recognized
   B. authorizes
   C. Reconsider
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:10 — “By Whom and When a Motion to Reconsider Can Be Made.”

**565.** `[C0746 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Suspending Effect of _____ a Motion to Reconsider.

   *Answer:* Making — “Suspending Effect of Making a Motion to Reconsider.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:11 — “Suspending Effect of Making a Motion to Reconsider.”

**566.** `[C0746 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Suspending Effect of Making a Motion to _____.
   A. Unfinished
   B. assemblies
   C. subsidiary
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* D) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:11 — “Suspending Effect of Making a Motion to Reconsider.”

**567.** `[C0746 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The effect of making a motion to Reconsider is the suspension of all action that depends on the result of the vote proposed to be _____.
   A. immediately
   B. reconsidered
   C. Procedures
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:11 — “The effect of making a motion to Reconsider is the suspension of all action that depends on the result of the vote proposed to be reconsidered.”

**568.** `[C0746 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The effect of making a motion to Reconsider is the suspension of all action that depends on the result of the vote proposed to be _____.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “The effect of making a motion to Reconsider is the suspension of all action that depends on the result of the vote proposed to be reconsidered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:11 — “The effect of making a motion to Reconsider is the suspension of all action that depends on the result of the vote proposed to be reconsidered.”

**569.** `[C0747 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to _____ is adopted, the original vote on the motion being reconsidered is thereby canceled altogether;

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “If the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the original vote on the motion being reconsidered is thereby canceled altogether;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:12 — “If the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the original vote on the motion being reconsidered is thereby canceled altogether;”

**570.** `[C0747 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the original vote on the motion being _____ is thereby canceled altogether;
   A. Incidental
   B. undebatable
   C. Organization
   D. reconsidered

   *Answer:* D) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:12 — “If the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the original vote on the motion being reconsidered is thereby canceled altogether;”

**571.** `[C0747 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if the suspending effect is terminated by a vote rejecting the motion to Reconsider or in any other way, the situation becomes the same as if there had been no such motion, and the vote which it was proposed to reconsider—and any other action held up because of the proposed _____— comes into full force, as if in effect, so far as applicable, from the time the vote was originally taken.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “but if the suspending effect is terminated by a vote rejecting the motion to Reconsider or in any other way, the situation becomes the same as if there had been no such motion, and the vote which it was proposed to reconsider—and any other action held up because of the proposed reconsideration— comes into full force, as if in effect, so far as applicable, from the time the vote was originally taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:12 — “but if the suspending effect is terminated by a vote rejecting the motion to Reconsider or in any other way, the situation becomes the same as if there had been no such motion, and the vote which it was proposed to reconsider—and any other action held up because of the proposed reconsideration— comes into full force, as if in effect, so far as applicable, from the time the vote was originally taken.”

**572.** `[C0747 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but if the suspending effect is terminated by a vote rejecting the motion to Reconsider or in any other way, the situation becomes the same as if there had been no such motion, and the vote which it was proposed to reconsider—and any other action held up because of the proposed _____— comes into full force, as if in effect, so far as applicable, from the time the vote was originally taken.
   A. deliberative
   B. Responsibilities
   C. reconsideration
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* C) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:12 — “but if the suspending effect is terminated by a vote rejecting the motion to Reconsider or in any other way, the situation becomes the same as if there had been no such motion, and the vote which it was proposed to reconsider—and any other action held up because of the proposed reconsideration— comes into full force, as if in effect, so far as applicable, from the time the vote was originally taken.”

**573.** `[C0748 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reconsideration of a Motion That Is No Longer in Order _____.

   *Answer:* Because of Intervening Action — “Reconsideration of a Motion That Is No Longer in Order Because of Intervening Action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:13 — “Reconsideration of a Motion That Is No Longer in Order Because of Intervening Action.”

**574.** `[C0748 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of a Motion That Is No Longer in Order Because of Intervening Action.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. presentation
   C. Reconsideration
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* C) Reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:13 — “Reconsideration of a Motion That Is No Longer in Order Because of Intervening Action.”

**575.** `[C0748 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that, as a consequence of the rule set forth in (b) of Standard _____ 2 above, action taken by an assembly may preclude the making of a motion to reconsider the vote on a previously rejected motion.

   *Answer:* Characteristic — “It should be noted that, as a consequence of the rule set forth in (b) of Standard Characteristic 2 above, action taken by an assembly may preclude the making of a motion to reconsider the vote on a previously rejected motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:13 — “It should be noted that, as a consequence of the rule set forth in (b) of Standard Characteristic 2 above, action taken by an assembly may preclude the making of a motion to reconsider the vote on a previously rejected motion.”

**576.** `[C0748 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For an example, assume that a motion to spend all of an available sum of money for library books is voted down, and _____ a motion to spend the same money for athletic equipment is made and adopted.
   A. thereafter
   B. substitute
   C. incidental
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) thereafter. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:13 — “For an example, assume that a motion to spend all of an available sum of money for library books is voted down, and thereafter a motion to spend the same money for athletic equipment is made and adopted.”

**577.** `[C0748 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, if it is desired to reconsider the vote on the rejected motion, the _____ adopted motion must first be rescinded (or amended in some fashion so that it no longer conflicts), or the vote on it must be reconsidered and reversed.

   *Answer:* subsequently — “In such a case, if it is desired to reconsider the vote on the rejected motion, the subsequently adopted motion must first be rescinded (or amended in some fashion so that it no longer conflicts), or the vote on it must be reconsidered and reversed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:13 — “In such a case, if it is desired to reconsider the vote on the rejected motion, the subsequently adopted motion must first be rescinded (or amended in some fashion so that it no longer conflicts), or the vote on it must be reconsidered and reversed.”

**578.** `[C0749 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Taking Up the Motion to _____ at the Time It Is Made.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Taking Up the Motion to Reconsider at the Time It Is Made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:14 — “Taking Up the Motion to Reconsider at the Time It Is Made.”

**579.** `[C0749 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to Reconsider is made at a time when it can be taken up—that is, when the motion proposed to be _____ would be in order initially—the chair immediately states the question on the motion to Reconsider as pending before the assembly.
   A. adjournment
   B. reconsidered
   C. Preparation
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:14 — “If a motion to Reconsider is made at a time when it can be taken up—that is, when the motion proposed to be reconsidered would be in order initially—the chair immediately states the question on the motion to Reconsider as pending before the assembly.”

**580.** `[C0749 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In proposing a _____ of the vote on a main motion, it is usually better to make the motion to Reconsider when no other business is pending and the motion can be taken up immediately—unless it appears that there may be no such opportunity or there is an important reason for doing otherwise.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “In proposing a reconsideration of the vote on a main motion, it is usually better to make the motion to Reconsider when no other business is pending and the motion can be taken up immediately—unless it appears that there may be no such opportunity or there is an important reason for doing otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:14 — “In proposing a reconsideration of the vote on a main motion, it is usually better to make the motion to Reconsider when no other business is pending and the motion can be taken up immediately—unless it appears that there may be no such opportunity or there is an important reason for doing otherwise.”

**581.** `[C0749 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Taking Up the Motion to _____ at the Time It Is Made.
   A. subsidiary
   B. Convention
   C. recognized
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* D) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:14 — “Taking Up the Motion to Reconsider at the Time It Is Made.”

**582.** `[C0749 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to Reconsider is made at a time when it can be taken up—that is, when the motion proposed to be _____ would be in order initially—the chair immediately states the question on the motion to Reconsider as pending before the assembly.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “If a motion to Reconsider is made at a time when it can be taken up—that is, when the motion proposed to be reconsidered would be in order initially—the chair immediately states the question on the motion to Reconsider as pending before the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:14 — “If a motion to Reconsider is made at a time when it can be taken up—that is, when the motion proposed to be reconsidered would be in order initially—the chair immediately states the question on the motion to Reconsider as pending before the assembly.”

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Calling Up the Motion to Reconsider at a Later Time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:15 — “Calling Up the Motion to Reconsider at a Later Time.”

**584.** `[C0750 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When this motion is called up, the chair immediately states the question on it as pending (see _____ and Example).
   A. Annul
   B. after
   C. amend
   D. Form

   *Answer:* D) Form. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:15 — “When this motion is called up, the chair immediately states the question on it as pending (see Form and Example).”

**585.** `[C0750 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When this motion is called up, the chair immediately states the question on it as pending (see Form _____).

   *Answer:* and Example — “When this motion is called up, the chair immediately states the question on it as pending (see Form and Example).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:15 — “When this motion is called up, the chair immediately states the question on it as pending (see Form and Example).”

   B. Reports
   C. Session
   D. obtains
