**587.** `[C0750 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: President, I call up the motion to reconsider the vote on the motion… [_____ it].” (The member is entitled to preference in recognition for this purpose;

   *Answer:* identifying — “President, I call up the motion to reconsider the vote on the motion… [identifying it].” (The member is entitled to preference in recognition for this purpose;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:15 — “President, I call up the motion to reconsider the vote on the motion… [identifying it].” (The member is entitled to preference in recognition for this purpose;”

**588.** `[C0751 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Privilege accorded the mover in regard to the time at _____.

   *Answer:* which reconsideration takes place — “Privilege accorded the mover in regard to the time at which reconsideration takes place.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:16 — “Privilege accorded the mover in regard to the time at which reconsideration takes place.”

**589.** `[C0751 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although any member can call up the motion to _____ as just described, usually no one but the mover of the reconsideration calls it up on the day the motion is made—at least in cases where the session is to last beyond that day and there is no need for immediate action.
   A. recognized
   B. settlement
   C. Reconsider
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:16 — “Although any member can call up the motion to Reconsider as just described, usually no one but the mover of the reconsideration calls it up on the day the motion is made—at least in cases where the session is to last beyond that day and there is no need for immediate action.”

**590.** `[C0751 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Privilege accorded the mover in regard to the time at which _____ takes place.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “Privilege accorded the mover in regard to the time at which reconsideration takes place.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:16 — “Privilege accorded the mover in regard to the time at which reconsideration takes place.”

**591.** `[C0751 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although any member can call up the motion to _____ as just described, usually no one but the mover of the reconsideration calls it up on the day the motion is made—at least in cases where the session is to last beyond that day and there is no need for immediate action.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Although any member can call up the motion to Reconsider as just described, usually no one but the mover of the reconsideration calls it up on the day the motion is made—at least in cases where the session is to last beyond that day and there is no need for immediate action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:16 — “Although any member can call up the motion to Reconsider as just described, usually no one but the mover of the reconsideration calls it up on the day the motion is made—at least in cases where the session is to last beyond that day and there is no need for immediate action.”

**592.** `[C0752 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Duty of the chair when failure to call up the motion may _____.

   *Answer:* do harm — “Duty of the chair when failure to call up the motion may do harm.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:17 — “Duty of the chair when failure to call up the motion may do harm.”

**593.** `[C0752 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where a failure to call up a motion to _____ may do harm, the chair has the duty to point out the situation to the assembly.
   A. recognized
   B. substitute
   C. Reconsider
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:17 — “In cases where a failure to call up a motion to Reconsider may do harm, the chair has the duty to point out the situation to the assembly.”

**594.** `[C0752 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Should the present meeting _____ without taking up the motion to Reconsider, the measure proposed to be reconsidered would be killed unless an adjourned meeting or special meeting were held to consider it.

   *Answer:* adjourn — “Should the present meeting adjourn without taking up the motion to Reconsider, the measure proposed to be reconsidered would be killed unless an adjourned meeting or special meeting were held to consider it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:17 — “Should the present meeting adjourn without taking up the motion to Reconsider, the measure proposed to be reconsidered would be killed unless an adjourned meeting or special meeting were held to consider it.”

**595.** `[C0752 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Duty of the chair when _____ to call up the motion may do harm.
   A. carried
   B. failure
   C. Adopted
   D. Pending

   *Answer:* B) failure. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:17 — “Duty of the chair when failure to call up the motion may do harm.”

**596.** `[C0752 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Should the present meeting adjourn without taking up the motion to Reconsider, the measure proposed to be _____ would be killed unless an adjourned meeting or special meeting were held to consider it.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “Should the present meeting adjourn without taking up the motion to Reconsider, the measure proposed to be reconsidered would be killed unless an adjourned meeting or special meeting were held to consider it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:17 — “Should the present meeting adjourn without taking up the motion to Reconsider, the measure proposed to be reconsidered would be killed unless an adjourned meeting or special meeting were held to consider it.”

**597.** `[C0753 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whenever the motion to _____ is taken up, it is debatable if the motion proposed to be reconsidered is debatable, and debate can go into the merits of the question proposed to be reconsidered, as noted in Standard Characteristic 5.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Whenever the motion to Reconsider is taken up, it is debatable if the motion proposed to be reconsidered is debatable, and debate can go into the merits of the question proposed to be reconsidered, as noted in Standard Characteristic 5.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:18 — “Whenever the motion to Reconsider is taken up, it is debatable if the motion proposed to be reconsidered is debatable, and debate can go into the merits of the question proposed to be reconsidered, as noted in Standard Characteristic 5.”

**598.** `[C0753 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whenever the motion to Reconsider is taken up, it is debatable if the motion proposed to be reconsidered is debatable, and debate can go into the merits of the question proposed to be reconsidered, as noted in Standard _____ 5.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. requirement
   C. Characteristic
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) Characteristic. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:18 — “Whenever the motion to Reconsider is taken up, it is debatable if the motion proposed to be reconsidered is debatable, and debate can go into the merits of the question proposed to be reconsidered, as noted in Standard Characteristic 5.”

**599.** `[C0753 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The right of each member to debate the motion to Reconsider is separate from the original _____ of the motion proposed to be reconsidered.

   *Answer:* consideration — “The right of each member to debate the motion to Reconsider is separate from the original consideration of the motion proposed to be reconsidered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:18 — “The right of each member to debate the motion to Reconsider is separate from the original consideration of the motion proposed to be reconsidered.”

**600.** `[C0753 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For rules affecting a member’s right to debate in the _____ if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, see below.)
   A. recommendation
   B. Indefinitely
   C. reconsideration
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* C) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:18 — “(For rules affecting a member’s right to debate in the reconsideration if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, see below.)”

**601.** `[C0753 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The right of each member to debate the motion to Reconsider is separate from the original _____ of the motion proposed to be reconsidered.
   A. Announcements
   B. immediately
   C. consideration
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:18 — “The right of each member to debate the motion to Reconsider is separate from the original consideration of the motion proposed to be reconsidered.”

**602.** `[C0754 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Effect of Adoption of the Motion to _____;

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Effect of Adoption of the Motion to Reconsider;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:19 — “Effect of Adoption of the Motion to Reconsider;”

**603.** `[C0754 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules Governing Debate on the _____.
   A. adjournment
   B. requirement
   C. Information
   D. Reconsideration

   *Answer:* D) Reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:19 — “Rules Governing Debate on the Reconsideration.”

**604.** `[C0754 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The effect of the adoption of the motion to Reconsider is immediately to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote is to be _____—in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “The effect of the adoption of the motion to Reconsider is immediately to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote is to be reconsidered—in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:19 — “The effect of the adoption of the motion to Reconsider is immediately to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote is to be reconsidered—in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”

**605.** `[C0754 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Effect of Adoption of the Motion to _____;
   A. persuasive
   B. Reconsider
   C. Unfinished
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* B) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:19 — “Effect of Adoption of the Motion to Reconsider;”

**606.** `[C0754 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules Governing Debate on the _____.

   *Answer:* Reconsideration — “Rules Governing Debate on the Reconsideration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:19 — “Rules Governing Debate on the Reconsideration.”

**607.** `[C0755 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member’s right to debate the reconsideration of a vote is independent of the extent to which he took part in debate on the motion to _____.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “A member’s right to debate the reconsideration of a vote is independent of the extent to which he took part in debate on the motion to Reconsider.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:21 — “A member’s right to debate the reconsideration of a vote is independent of the extent to which he took part in debate on the motion to Reconsider.”

**608.** `[C0755 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of a vote on the same day.
   A. Reconsideration
   B. representatives
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) Reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:21 — “Reconsideration of a vote on the same day.”

**609.** `[C0755 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (But such a member can pursue an equivalent purpose while the motion to Reconsider is pending, since the motion proposed to be _____ is also open to discussion in debate on the motion to Reconsider.)

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “(But such a member can pursue an equivalent purpose while the motion to Reconsider is pending, since the motion proposed to be reconsidered is also open to discussion in debate on the motion to Reconsider.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:21 — “(But such a member can pursue an equivalent purpose while the motion to Reconsider is pending, since the motion proposed to be reconsidered is also open to discussion in debate on the motion to Reconsider.)”

**610.** `[C0755 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member’s right to debate the reconsideration of a vote is independent of the extent to which he took part in debate on the motion to _____.
   A. settlement
   B. convention
   C. landscaped
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* D) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:21 — “A member’s right to debate the reconsideration of a vote is independent of the extent to which he took part in debate on the motion to Reconsider.”

**611.** `[C0755 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of a vote on the same day.

   *Answer:* Reconsideration — “Reconsideration of a vote on the same day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:21 — “Reconsideration of a vote on the same day.”

**612.** `[C0756 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of a vote on a later day.

   *Answer:* Reconsideration — “Reconsideration of a vote on a later day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:22 — “Reconsideration of a vote on a later day.”

**613.** `[C0756 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of a vote on a later day.
   A. deliberative
   B. Indefinitely
   C. constitution
   D. Reconsideration

   *Answer:* D) Reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:22 — “Reconsideration of a vote on a later day.”

**614.** `[C0757 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reconsideration under an order limiting or extending _____.

   *Answer:* limits of debate — “Reconsideration under an order limiting or extending limits of debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:23 — “Reconsideration under an order limiting or extending limits of debate.”

**615.** `[C0757 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Reconsideration under an order _____ or extending limits of debate.
   A. adoption
   B. limiting
   C. business
   D. assembly

   *Answer:* B) limiting. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:23 — “Reconsideration under an order limiting or extending limits of debate.”

**616.** `[C0757 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a vote on one of a series of motions is taken under an order limiting debate or for the _____ Question, and then is reconsidered before such an order is exhausted (as explained in the sections on those motions, 15 and 16), the same restrictions continue to apply to debate both on the motion to Reconsider and on the reconsideration.

   *Answer:* Previous — “If a vote on one of a series of motions is taken under an order limiting debate or for the Previous Question, and then is reconsidered before such an order is exhausted (as explained in the sections on those motions, 15 and 16), the same restrictions continue to apply to debate both on the motion to Reconsider and on the reconsideration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:23 — “If a vote on one of a series of motions is taken under an order limiting debate or for the Previous Question, and then is reconsidered before such an order is exhausted (as explained in the sections on those motions, 15 and 16), the same restrictions continue to apply to debate both on the motion to Reconsider and on the reconsideration.”

**617.** `[C0757 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a vote on one of a series of motions is taken under an order limiting debate or for the Previous _____, and then is reconsidered before such an order is exhausted (as explained in the sections on those motions, 15 and 16), the same restrictions continue to apply to debate both on the motion to Reconsider and on the reconsideration.
   A. Question
   B. Meetings
   C. assuming
   D. majority

   *Answer:* A) Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:23 — “If a vote on one of a series of motions is taken under an order limiting debate or for the Previous Question, and then is reconsidered before such an order is exhausted (as explained in the sections on those motions, 15 and 16), the same restrictions continue to apply to debate both on the motion to Reconsider and on the reconsideration.”

**618.** `[C0758 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reconsideration of Subsidiary, Privileged, and Incidental _____.

   *Answer:* Motions — “Reconsideration of Subsidiary, Privileged, and Incidental Motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:24 — “Reconsideration of Subsidiary, Privileged, and Incidental Motions.”

**619.** `[C0758 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of Subsidiary, Privileged, and Incidental Motions.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. characteristics
   C. Reconsideration
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* C) Reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:24 — “Reconsideration of Subsidiary, Privileged, and Incidental Motions.”

**620.** `[C0759 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To reconsider a subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved while the main question _____.

   *Answer:* is pending — “To reconsider a subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved while the main question is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:25 — “To reconsider a subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved while the main question is pending.”

**621.** `[C0759 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To _____ a subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved while the main question is pending.
   A. recognized
   B. privileged
   C. substitute
   D. reconsider

   *Answer:* D) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:25 — “To reconsider a subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved while the main question is pending.”

**622.** `[C0759 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a main motion is pending (with or without a series of adhering motions) and it is moved to reconsider the vote on a related subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion, the motion to Reconsider becomes (a) immediately pending or (b) pending at a lower position in the series, depending on whether the motion proposed to be _____ would then be in order if moved for the first time.
   A. subsequently
   B. interrupted
   C. deliberative
   D. reconsidered

   *Answer:* D) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:25 — “When a main motion is pending (with or without a series of adhering motions) and it is moved to reconsider the vote on a related subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion, the motion to Reconsider becomes (a) immediately pending or (b) pending at a lower position in the series, depending on whether the motion proposed to be reconsidered would then be in order if moved for the first time.”

**623.** `[C0759 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a main motion is pending (with or without a series of adhering motions) and it is moved to reconsider the vote on a related subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion, the motion to Reconsider becomes (a) immediately pending or (b) pending at a lower position in the series, depending on whether the motion proposed to be _____ would then be in order if moved for the first time.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “When a main motion is pending (with or without a series of adhering motions) and it is moved to reconsider the vote on a related subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion, the motion to Reconsider becomes (a) immediately pending or (b) pending at a lower position in the series, depending on whether the motion proposed to be reconsidered would then be in order if moved for the first time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:25 — “When a main motion is pending (with or without a series of adhering motions) and it is moved to reconsider the vote on a related subsidiary, privileged, or incidental motion, the motion to Reconsider becomes (a) immediately pending or (b) pending at a lower position in the series, depending on whether the motion proposed to be reconsidered would then be in order if moved for the first time.”

**624.** `[C0760 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: see also the chart on pages t3–t5), in which case the motion to _____ becomes the immediately pending question at once.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “see also the chart on pages t3–t5), in which case the motion to Reconsider becomes the immediately pending question at once.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:26 — “see also the chart on pages t3–t5), in which case the motion to Reconsider becomes the immediately pending question at once.”

**625.** `[C0760 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the main question is now pending alone, or if no other questions are pending except motions to _____ or to Amend (which rank below the motion to Commit), the chair at once states the question on the motion to Reconsider as immediately pending.
   A. Question on the Table
   B. Postpone Indefinitely
   C. Resolutions Committee
   D. Parliamentary Inquiry

   *Answer:* B) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:26 — “If the main question is now pending alone, or if no other questions are pending except motions to Postpone Indefinitely or to Amend (which rank below the motion to Commit), the chair at once states the question on the motion to Reconsider as immediately pending.”

**626.** `[C0760 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the main question is now pending alone, or if no other questions are pending except motions to Postpone Indefinitely or to _____ (which rank below the motion to Commit), the chair at once states the question on the motion to Reconsider as immediately pending.

   *Answer:* Amend — “If the main question is now pending alone, or if no other questions are pending except motions to Postpone Indefinitely or to Amend (which rank below the motion to Commit), the chair at once states the question on the motion to Reconsider as immediately pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:26 — “If the main question is now pending alone, or if no other questions are pending except motions to Postpone Indefinitely or to Amend (which rank below the motion to Commit), the chair at once states the question on the motion to Reconsider as immediately pending.”

**627.** `[C0760 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: see also the chart on pages t3–t5), in which case the motion to Reconsider becomes the _____ pending question at once.

   *Answer:* immediately — “see also the chart on pages t3–t5), in which case the motion to Reconsider becomes the immediately pending question at once.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:26 — “see also the chart on pages t3–t5), in which case the motion to Reconsider becomes the immediately pending question at once.”

**628.** `[C0761 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, suppose that while a main motion, an amendment, and a motion to lay the pending questions on the table are pending, it is moved to _____ a previous negative vote on referring the same main question and amendment to a committee.
   A. reconsider
   B. Definitely
   C. authorizes
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* A) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:27 — “For example, suppose that while a main motion, an amendment, and a motion to lay the pending questions on the table are pending, it is moved to reconsider a previous negative vote on referring the same main question and amendment to a committee.”

**629.** `[C0761 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The order of rank, from highest to lowest, of the four motions is: (1) _____, (2) Commit, (3) Amend, and (4) the main motion.

   *Answer:* Lay on the Table — “The order of rank, from highest to lowest, of the four motions is: (1) Lay on the Table, (2) Commit, (3) Amend, and (4) the main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:27 — “The order of rank, from highest to lowest, of the four motions is: (1) Lay on the Table, (2) Commit, (3) Amend, and (4) the main motion.”

**630.** `[C0761 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The order of rank, from highest to lowest, of the four motions is: (1) Lay on the Table, (2) _____, (3) Amend, and (4) the main motion.
   A. amends
   B. Bylaws
   C. Commit
   D. before

   *Answer:* C) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:27 — “The order of rank, from highest to lowest, of the four motions is: (1) Lay on the Table, (2) Commit, (3) Amend, and (4) the main motion.”

**631.** `[C0761 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, suppose that while a main motion, an amendment, and a motion to lay the pending questions on the table are pending, it is moved to _____ a previous negative vote on referring the same main question and amendment to a committee.

   *Answer:* reconsider — “For example, suppose that while a main motion, an amendment, and a motion to lay the pending questions on the table are pending, it is moved to reconsider a previous negative vote on referring the same main question and amendment to a committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:27 — “For example, suppose that while a main motion, an amendment, and a motion to lay the pending questions on the table are pending, it is moved to reconsider a previous negative vote on referring the same main question and amendment to a committee.”

**632.** `[C0762 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ of a primary amendment is moved while another amendment of the same degree is pending, the pending amendment is disposed of first.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “If the reconsideration of a primary amendment is moved while another amendment of the same degree is pending, the pending amendment is disposed of first.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:28 — “If the reconsideration of a primary amendment is moved while another amendment of the same degree is pending, the pending amendment is disposed of first.”

**633.** `[C0762 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Then the chair states the question on the motion to _____ the amendment previously acted upon.
   A. reconsider
   B. introduced
   C. subsidiary
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* A) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:28 — “Then the chair states the question on the motion to reconsider the amendment previously acted upon.”

**634.** `[C0762 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the _____ of a primary amendment is moved while another amendment of the same degree is pending, the pending amendment is disposed of first.
   A. immediately
   B. Indefinitely
   C. subsequently
   D. reconsideration

   *Answer:* D) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:28 — “If the reconsideration of a primary amendment is moved while another amendment of the same degree is pending, the pending amendment is disposed of first.”

**635.** `[C0762 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Then the chair states the question on the motion to _____ the amendment previously acted upon.

   *Answer:* reconsider — “Then the chair states the question on the motion to reconsider the amendment previously acted upon.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:28 — “Then the chair states the question on the motion to reconsider the amendment previously acted upon.”

**636.** `[C0763 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is moved to _____ a debatable subsidiary or incidental motion which relates to a pending main question or a series of pending questions (in which case the motion to Reconsider is debatable, as noted in Standard Characteristic 5), debate on the motion to Reconsider can go into the merits of the motion proposed to be reconsidered, but not into the merits of any other pending question.

   *Answer:* reconsider — “When it is moved to reconsider a debatable subsidiary or incidental motion which relates to a pending main question or a series of pending questions (in which case the motion to Reconsider is debatable, as noted in Standard Characteristic 5), debate on the motion to Reconsider can go into the merits of the motion proposed to be reconsidered, but not into the merits of any other pending question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:29 — “When it is moved to reconsider a debatable subsidiary or incidental motion which relates to a pending main question or a series of pending questions (in which case the motion to Reconsider is debatable, as noted in Standard Characteristic 5), debate on the motion to Reconsider can go into the merits of the motion proposed to be reconsidered, but not into the merits of any other pending question.”

**637.** `[C0763 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is moved to reconsider a debatable subsidiary or incidental motion which relates to a pending main question or a series of pending questions (in which case the motion to Reconsider is debatable, as noted in Standard Characteristic 5), debate on the motion to Reconsider can go into the merits of the motion _____, but not into the merits of any other pending question.
   A. Business and General Orders
   B. Certificate of Incorporation
   C. proposed to be reconsidered
   D. Customs Observed by Members

   *Answer:* C) proposed to be reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:29 — “When it is moved to reconsider a debatable subsidiary or incidental motion which relates to a pending main question or a series of pending questions (in which case the motion to Reconsider is debatable, as noted in Standard Characteristic 5), debate on the motion to Reconsider can go into the merits of the motion proposed to be reconsidered, but not into the merits of any other pending question.”

**638.** `[C0764 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To reconsider an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved after the main question _____.

   *Answer:* has been acted upon — “To reconsider an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved after the main question has been acted upon.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:30 — “To reconsider an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved after the main question has been acted upon.”

**639.** `[C0764 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To _____ an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved after the main question has been acted upon.
   A. Incidental
   B. reconsider
   C. Unfinished
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* B) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:30 — “To reconsider an adhering subsidiary or incidental motion: Reconsideration moved after the main question has been acted upon.”

**640.** `[C0764 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is desired to reconsider the vote on a subsidiary or incidental motion (an amendment, for example) after the main question to which it adhered has been finally disposed of (by adoption, rejection, or indefinite postponement), the vote on the main question, or on its indefinite postponement, must also be reconsidered (see also Standard _____ 2).
   A. Preparation
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. president-elect
   D. Characteristic

   *Answer:* D) Characteristic. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:30 — “If it is desired to reconsider the vote on a subsidiary or incidental motion (an amendment, for example) after the main question to which it adhered has been finally disposed of (by adoption, rejection, or indefinite postponement), the vote on the main question, or on its indefinite postponement, must also be reconsidered (see also Standard Characteristic 2).”

**641.** `[C0764 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to reconsider the vote on a subsidiary or incidental motion (an amendment, for example) after the main question to which it adhered has been finally disposed of (by adoption, rejection, or indefinite postponement), the vote on the main question, or on its indefinite postponement, must also be reconsidered (see also Standard _____ 2).

   *Answer:* Characteristic — “If it is desired to reconsider the vote on a subsidiary or incidental motion (an amendment, for example) after the main question to which it adhered has been finally disposed of (by adoption, rejection, or indefinite postponement), the vote on the main question, or on its indefinite postponement, must also be reconsidered (see also Standard Characteristic 2).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:30 — “If it is desired to reconsider the vote on a subsidiary or incidental motion (an amendment, for example) after the main question to which it adhered has been finally disposed of (by adoption, rejection, or indefinite postponement), the vote on the main question, or on its indefinite postponement, must also be reconsidered (see also Standard Characteristic 2).”

**642.** `[C0765 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to _____ the secondary amendment after the main question has been finally disposed of, the secondary amendment, the primary amendment, and the main question must all be reconsidered, and one motion to Reconsider should be made covering the votes of these three motions.

   *Answer:* reconsider — “If it is desired to reconsider the secondary amendment after the main question has been finally disposed of, the secondary amendment, the primary amendment, and the main question must all be reconsidered, and one motion to Reconsider should be made covering the votes of these three motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:31 — “If it is desired to reconsider the secondary amendment after the main question has been finally disposed of, the secondary amendment, the primary amendment, and the main question must all be reconsidered, and one motion to Reconsider should be made covering the votes of these three motions.”

**643.** `[C0765 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The same principle applies to the _____ of a secondary amendment after the related primary amendment has been voted on.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Preparation
   C. subsequently
   D. reconsideration

   *Answer:* D) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:31 — “The same principle applies to the reconsideration of a secondary amendment after the related primary amendment has been voted on.”

**644.** `[C0765 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If such a reconsideration is desired while the main question is still pending, the primary amendment must also be _____.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “If such a reconsideration is desired while the main question is still pending, the primary amendment must also be reconsidered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:31 — “If such a reconsideration is desired while the main question is still pending, the primary amendment must also be reconsidered.”

**645.** `[C0765 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is desired to _____ the secondary amendment after the main question has been finally disposed of, the secondary amendment, the primary amendment, and the main question must all be reconsidered, and one motion to Reconsider should be made covering the votes of these three motions.
   A. settlement
   B. committees
   C. reconsider
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:31 — “If it is desired to reconsider the secondary amendment after the main question has been finally disposed of, the secondary amendment, the primary amendment, and the main question must all be reconsidered, and one motion to Reconsider should be made covering the votes of these three motions.”

**646.** `[C0765 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The same principle applies to the _____ of a secondary amendment after the related primary amendment has been voted on.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “The same principle applies to the reconsideration of a secondary amendment after the related primary amendment has been voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:31 — “The same principle applies to the reconsideration of a secondary amendment after the related primary amendment has been voted on.”

**647.** `[C0766 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion to _____ covers the votes on two or more connected motions, not all of these questions can be discussed in debate on the motion to Reconsider, but only the one that will be voted on first if the motion to Reconsider is adopted.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “When a motion to Reconsider covers the votes on two or more connected motions, not all of these questions can be discussed in debate on the motion to Reconsider, but only the one that will be voted on first if the motion to Reconsider is adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:32 — “When a motion to Reconsider covers the votes on two or more connected motions, not all of these questions can be discussed in debate on the motion to Reconsider, but only the one that will be voted on first if the motion to Reconsider is adopted.”

**648.** `[C0766 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If this motion to Reconsider is adopted, the chair states the question on the secondary amendment and recognizes the mover of the _____ as entitled to the floor.
   A. Vice-President
   B. Information
   C. representatives
   D. reconsideration

   *Answer:* D) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:32 — “If this motion to Reconsider is adopted, the chair states the question on the secondary amendment and recognizes the mover of the reconsideration as entitled to the floor.”

**649.** `[C0766 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The question is now in exactly the same _____ as it was just before the original vote was taken on the secondary amendment.

   *Answer:* condition — “The question is now in exactly the same condition as it was just before the original vote was taken on the secondary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:32 — “The question is now in exactly the same condition as it was just before the original vote was taken on the secondary amendment.”

**650.** `[C0766 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion to _____ covers the votes on two or more connected motions, not all of these questions can be discussed in debate on the motion to Reconsider, but only the one that will be voted on first if the motion to Reconsider is adopted.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Particular
   C. persuasive
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* A) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:32 — “When a motion to Reconsider covers the votes on two or more connected motions, not all of these questions can be discussed in debate on the motion to Reconsider, but only the one that will be voted on first if the motion to Reconsider is adopted.”

**651.** `[C0766 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If this motion to Reconsider is adopted, the chair states the question on the secondary amendment and recognizes the mover of the _____ as entitled to the floor.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “If this motion to Reconsider is adopted, the chair states the question on the secondary amendment and recognizes the mover of the reconsideration as entitled to the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:32 — “If this motion to Reconsider is adopted, the chair states the question on the secondary amendment and recognizes the mover of the reconsideration as entitled to the floor.”

**652.** `[C0767 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a main motion is included in a series covered by a single motion to _____, as just described, the reconsideration is in order at the same times as if it had been moved to reconsider the main motion alone.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “If a main motion is included in a series covered by a single motion to Reconsider, as just described, the reconsideration is in order at the same times as if it had been moved to reconsider the main motion alone.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:33 — “If a main motion is included in a series covered by a single motion to Reconsider, as just described, the reconsideration is in order at the same times as if it had been moved to reconsider the main motion alone.”

**653.** `[C0767 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a main motion is included in a series covered by a single motion to Reconsider, as just described, the _____ is in order at the same times as if it had been moved to reconsider the main motion alone.
   A. requirement
   B. reconsideration
   C. representatives
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:33 — “If a main motion is included in a series covered by a single motion to Reconsider, as just described, the reconsideration is in order at the same times as if it had been moved to reconsider the main motion alone.”

**654.** `[C0768 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The same is true if the motion to _____ the adhering subsidiary or incidental motion was made before, but was not called up before, the main question was postponed or laid on the table.

   *Answer:* reconsider — “The same is true if the motion to reconsider the adhering subsidiary or incidental motion was made before, but was not called up before, the main question was postponed or laid on the table.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:34 — “The same is true if the motion to reconsider the adhering subsidiary or incidental motion was made before, but was not called up before, the main question was postponed or laid on the table.”

**655.** `[C0768 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The same is true if the motion to _____ the adhering subsidiary or incidental motion was made before, but was not called up before, the main question was postponed or laid on the table.
   A. privileged
   B. incidental
   C. reconsider
   D. variations

   *Answer:* C) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:34 — “The same is true if the motion to reconsider the adhering subsidiary or incidental motion was made before, but was not called up before, the main question was postponed or laid on the table.”

**656.** `[C0769 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ in Standing and Special Committees.

   *Answer:* Reconsideration — “Reconsideration in Standing and Special Committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:35 — “Reconsideration in Standing and Special Committees.”

**657.** `[C0769 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ in Standing and Special Committees.
   A. interrupted
   B. Reconsideration
   C. resolutions
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) Reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:35 — “Reconsideration in Standing and Special Committees.”

**658.** `[C0770 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the maker of the motion to _____ fails to state which side he voted on, the chair, before making any other response, directs the member to do so:

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “If the maker of the motion to Reconsider fails to state which side he voted on, the chair, before making any other response, directs the member to do so:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:37 — “If the maker of the motion to Reconsider fails to state which side he voted on, the chair, before making any other response, directs the member to do so:”

**659.** `[C0770 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the maker of the motion to _____ fails to state which side he voted on, the chair, before making any other response, directs the member to do so:
   A. Reconsider
   B. assemblies
   C. incidental
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* A) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:37 — “If the maker of the motion to Reconsider fails to state which side he voted on, the chair, before making any other response, directs the member to do so:”

**660.** `[C0771 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the member did not vote with the prevailing side, another member who did so can make the motion to _____, if he desires.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “If the member did not vote with the prevailing side, another member who did so can make the motion to Reconsider, if he desires.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:38 — “If the member did not vote with the prevailing side, another member who did so can make the motion to Reconsider, if he desires.”

**661.** `[C0771 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the resolution was adopted by _____ consent, the chair asks whether the member was present at the time.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* D) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:38 — “If the resolution was adopted by unanimous consent, the chair asks whether the member was present at the time.”

**662.** `[C0771 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ was adopted by unanimous consent, the chair asks whether the member was present at the time.

   *Answer:* resolution — “If the resolution was adopted by unanimous consent, the chair asks whether the member was present at the time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:38 — “If the resolution was adopted by unanimous consent, the chair asks whether the member was present at the time.”

**663.** `[C0771 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the member did not vote with the _____ side, another member who did so can make the motion to Reconsider, if he desires.
   A. sustaining
   B. substitute
   C. prevailing
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* C) prevailing. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:38 — “If the member did not vote with the prevailing side, another member who did so can make the motion to Reconsider, if he desires.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §37:43 — “If a reconsideration that could not be taken up when it was moved is one that later comes before the assembly automatically, then when that point is reached, the chair says, for example:”

**671.** `[C0775 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a _____ that could not be taken up when it was moved is one that later comes before the assembly automatically, then when that point is reached, the chair says, for example:
   A. specification
   B. adjournment
   C. Organization

**672.** `[C0776 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After debate on a motion to _____, assuming that this motion is adopted, the chair puts the question and states the result as follows:

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “After debate on a motion to Reconsider, assuming that this motion is adopted, the chair puts the question and states the result as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:44 — “After debate on a motion to Reconsider, assuming that this motion is adopted, the chair puts the question and states the result as follows:”

**673.** `[C0776 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After debate on a motion to _____, assuming that this motion is adopted, the chair puts the question and states the result as follows:
   A. subsidiary
   B. Reconsider
   C. Substitute
   D. Unfinished

**674.** `[C0777 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Note that if the result of the vote on the motion to _____ is negative, it is the only vote taken.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Note that if the result of the vote on the motion to Reconsider is negative, it is the only vote taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:45 — “Note that if the result of the vote on the motion to Reconsider is negative, it is the only vote taken.”

**675.** `[C0777 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, this is followed—after any debate—by the taking of the vote or votes that are _____ reconsidered.
   A. Convention
   B. presentation
   C. Announcements
   D. consequently

   *Answer:* D) consequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:45 — “But if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, this is followed—after any debate—by the taking of the vote or votes that are consequently reconsidered.”

**676.** `[C0777 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Note that if the result of the vote on the motion to _____ is negative, it is the only vote taken.
   A. subsidiary
   B. Substitute
   C. Reconsider
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:45 — “Note that if the result of the vote on the motion to Reconsider is negative, it is the only vote taken.”

**677.** `[C0777 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, this is followed—after any debate—by the taking of the vote or votes that are _____ reconsidered.

   *Answer:* consequently — “But if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, this is followed—after any debate—by the taking of the vote or votes that are consequently reconsidered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:45 — “But if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, this is followed—after any debate—by the taking of the vote or votes that are consequently reconsidered.”

**678.** `[C0778 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ and Enter on the Minutes is a special form of the motion to Reconsider that has a different object from the regular motion.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes is a special form of the motion to Reconsider that has a different object from the regular motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:46 — “Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes is a special form of the motion to Reconsider that has a different object from the regular motion.”

**679.** `[C0778 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Its purpose is to prevent a temporary _____ from taking advantage of an unrepresentative attendance at a meeting to vote an action that is opposed by a majority of a society’s or a convention’s membership.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:46 — “Its purpose is to prevent a temporary majority from taking advantage of an unrepresentative attendance at a meeting to vote an action that is opposed by a majority of a society’s or a convention’s membership.”

**680.** `[C0778 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Its purpose is to prevent a temporary majority from taking advantage of an _____ attendance at a meeting to vote an action that is opposed by a majority of a society’s or a convention’s membership.

   *Answer:* unrepresentative — “Its purpose is to prevent a temporary majority from taking advantage of an unrepresentative attendance at a meeting to vote an action that is opposed by a majority of a society’s or a convention’s membership.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:46 — “Its purpose is to prevent a temporary majority from taking advantage of an unrepresentative attendance at a meeting to vote an action that is opposed by a majority of a society’s or a convention’s membership.”

**681.** `[C0778 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The effect of this form of the motion arises from the fact that when it is moved—on the same day that the vote to be _____ was taken—it cannot be called up until another day, even if another meeting is held on the same day.
   A. reconsidered
   B. Preparation
   C. Organization
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:46 — “The effect of this form of the motion arises from the fact that when it is moved—on the same day that the vote to be reconsidered was taken—it cannot be called up until another day, even if another meeting is held on the same day.”

**682.** `[C0778 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, with a view to obtaining a more representative attendance, it ensures _____ of a question on a different day from the one on which the question was put to vote.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “Thus, with a view to obtaining a more representative attendance, it ensures reconsideration of a question on a different day from the one on which the question was put to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:46 — “Thus, with a view to obtaining a more representative attendance, it ensures reconsideration of a question on a different day from the one on which the question was put to vote.”

**683.** `[C0779 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ and Enter on the Minutes differs from the regular form of Reconsider in the following respects:

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes differs from the regular form of Reconsider in the following respects:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:47 — “Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes differs from the regular form of Reconsider in the following respects:”

**684.** `[C0779 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ from the Regular Form of the Motion.
   A. assemblies
   B. Permission
   C. Information
   D. Differences

**685.** `[C0779 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ and Enter on the Minutes differs from the regular form of Reconsider in the following respects:
   A. incidental
