   *Answer:* Differences — “Differences from the Regular Form of the Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:47 — “Differences from the Regular Form of the Motion.”

**687.** `[C0780 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a motion to _____ and Enter on the Minutes has been called up, its treatment is the same as that of the regular motion to Reconsider.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “After a motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes has been called up, its treatment is the same as that of the regular motion to Reconsider.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:48 — “After a motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes has been called up, its treatment is the same as that of the regular motion to Reconsider.”

**688.** `[C0780 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a motion to _____ and Enter on the Minutes has been called up, its treatment is the same as that of the regular motion to Reconsider.
   A. Substitute
   B. committees
   C. Reconsider
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:48 — “After a motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes has been called up, its treatment is the same as that of the regular motion to Reconsider.”

**689.** `[C0781 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member in opposition can prevent the vote on such action from becoming final by moving “to _____ the minutes the vote on…” To be in a position to do this, such a member—detecting the hopelessness of preventing an affirmative result on the vote—should vote in the affirmative himself.

   *Answer:* reconsider and enter on — “A member in opposition can prevent the vote on such action from becoming final by moving “to reconsider and enter on the minutes the vote on…” To be in a position to do this, such a member—detecting the hopelessness of preventing an affirmative result on the vote—should vote in the affirmative himself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:49 — “A member in opposition can prevent the vote on such action from becoming final by moving “to reconsider and enter on the minutes the vote on…” To be in a position to do this, such a member—detecting the hopelessness of preventing an affirmative result on the vote—should vote in the affirmative himself.”

**690.** `[C0781 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member in opposition can prevent the vote on such action from becoming final by moving “to reconsider and enter on _____ the vote on…” To be in a position to do this, such a member—detecting the hopelessness of preventing an affirmative result on the vote—should vote in the affirmative himself.
   A. Vote by Mail
   B. the minutes
   C. Mass Meeting
   D. Strike Out

   *Answer:* B) the minutes. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:49 — “A member in opposition can prevent the vote on such action from becoming final by moving “to reconsider and enter on the minutes the vote on…” To be in a position to do this, such a member—detecting the hopelessness of preventing an affirmative result on the vote—should vote in the affirmative himself.”

**691.** `[C0781 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To illustrate the use of this form of the motion, suppose that at a long meeting of a county historical society, many members have left, _____ leaving a quorum composed mainly of a small group determined to commit the society to certain action that a few of those present believe would be opposed by most of the membership.

   *Answer:* unknowingly — “To illustrate the use of this form of the motion, suppose that at a long meeting of a county historical society, many members have left, unknowingly leaving a quorum composed mainly of a small group determined to commit the society to certain action that a few of those present believe would be opposed by most of the membership.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:49 — “To illustrate the use of this form of the motion, suppose that at a long meeting of a county historical society, many members have left, unknowingly leaving a quorum composed mainly of a small group determined to commit the society to certain action that a few of those present believe would be opposed by most of the membership.”

**692.** `[C0781 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes is seconded, all action required by the vote proposed to be _____ is suspended, and there is time to notify absent members of the proposed action.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “If the motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes is seconded, all action required by the vote proposed to be reconsidered is suspended, and there is time to notify absent members of the proposed action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:49 — “If the motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes is seconded, all action required by the vote proposed to be reconsidered is suspended, and there is time to notify absent members of the proposed action.”

**693.** `[C0781 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To illustrate the use of this form of the motion, suppose that at a long meeting of a county historical society, many members have left, _____ leaving a quorum composed mainly of a small group determined to commit the society to certain action that a few of those present believe would be opposed by most of the membership.
   A. requirement
   B. unknowingly
   C. Organization
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* B) unknowingly. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:49 — “To illustrate the use of this form of the motion, suppose that at a long meeting of a county historical society, many members have left, unknowingly leaving a quorum composed mainly of a small group determined to commit the society to certain action that a few of those present believe would be opposed by most of the membership.”

**694.** `[C0782 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At this next meeting, provided that such notice has been given, the motion to _____ can then be adopted by a majority vote.

   *Answer:* Rescind — “At this next meeting, provided that such notice has been given, the motion to Rescind can then be adopted by a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:50 — “At this next meeting, provided that such notice has been given, the motion to Rescind can then be adopted by a majority vote.”

**695.** `[C0782 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At this next meeting, provided that such notice has been given, the motion to Rescind can then be adopted by a _____ vote.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:50 — “At this next meeting, provided that such notice has been given, the motion to Rescind can then be adopted by a majority vote.”

**696.** `[C0783 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes may occasionally be subject to attempted abuse, particularly in ordinary societies with single-meeting sessions, since it gives any _____ power to hold up action taken by a meeting.

   *Answer:* two members — “The motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes may occasionally be subject to attempted abuse, particularly in ordinary societies with single-meeting sessions, since it gives any two members power to hold up action taken by a meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:51 — “The motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes may occasionally be subject to attempted abuse, particularly in ordinary societies with single-meeting sessions, since it gives any two members power to hold up action taken by a meeting.”

**697.** `[C0783 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Against Abuses of the Motion.
   A. Substitute
   B. Protecting
   C. Permission
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* B) Protecting. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:51 — “Protecting Against Abuses of the Motion.”

**698.** `[C0783 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes may occasionally be subject to attempted abuse, particularly in ordinary societies with _____ sessions, since it gives any two members power to hold up action taken by a meeting.

   *Answer:* single-meeting — “The motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes may occasionally be subject to attempted abuse, particularly in ordinary societies with single-meeting sessions, since it gives any two members power to hold up action taken by a meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:51 — “The motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes may occasionally be subject to attempted abuse, particularly in ordinary societies with single-meeting sessions, since it gives any two members power to hold up action taken by a meeting.”

**699.** `[C0783 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the average _____ this motion should generally be reserved for extreme cases, and is in order only when final decision on the question could, if necessary, wait until the next regular meeting, or when an adjourned or special meeting to take it up is a practical possibility.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. organization
   C. constitution
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:51 — “In the average organization this motion should generally be reserved for extreme cases, and is in order only when final decision on the question could, if necessary, wait until the next regular meeting, or when an adjourned or special meeting to take it up is a practical possibility.”

**700.** `[C0783 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ Against Abuses of the Motion.

   *Answer:* Protecting — “Protecting Against Abuses of the Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:51 — “Protecting Against Abuses of the Motion.”

**701.** `[C0784 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an actual minority in a representative meeting makes improper use of this motion by moving to reconsider and enter on the minutes a vote which requires action before the next regular meeting, the remedy is to fix the time for an adjourned meeting (9, 22) on another suitable day when the _____ can be called up and disposed of.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “If an actual minority in a representative meeting makes improper use of this motion by moving to reconsider and enter on the minutes a vote which requires action before the next regular meeting, the remedy is to fix the time for an adjourned meeting (9, 22) on another suitable day when the reconsideration can be called up and disposed of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:52 — “If an actual minority in a representative meeting makes improper use of this motion by moving to reconsider and enter on the minutes a vote which requires action before the next regular meeting, the remedy is to fix the time for an adjourned meeting (9, 22) on another suitable day when the reconsideration can be called up and disposed of.”

**702.** `[C0784 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, the mere making of a motion to set an adjourned meeting would likely cause _____ of the motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes, since its object would be defeated.
   A. withdrawal
   B. resolution
   C. Particular
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* A) withdrawal. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:52 — “In such a case, the mere making of a motion to set an adjourned meeting would likely cause withdrawal of the motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes, since its object would be defeated.”

**703.** `[C0784 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an actual minority in a representative meeting makes improper use of this motion by moving to reconsider and enter on the minutes a vote which requires action before the next regular meeting, the remedy is to fix the time for an adjourned meeting (9, 22) on another suitable day when the _____ can be called up and disposed of.
   A. Preparation
   B. reconsideration
   C. deliberative
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:52 — “If an actual minority in a representative meeting makes improper use of this motion by moving to reconsider and enter on the minutes a vote which requires action before the next regular meeting, the remedy is to fix the time for an adjourned meeting (9, 22) on another suitable day when the reconsideration can be called up and disposed of.”

**704.** `[C0784 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, the mere making of a motion to set an adjourned meeting would likely cause _____ of the motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes, since its object would be defeated.

   *Answer:* withdrawal — “In such a case, the mere making of a motion to set an adjourned meeting would likely cause withdrawal of the motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes, since its object would be defeated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:52 — “In such a case, the mere making of a motion to set an adjourned meeting would likely cause withdrawal of the motion to Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes, since its object would be defeated.”

---

## Domain 4: Organization and Conduct of Meetings

**1.** `[C0001 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____—the kind of gathering to which parliamentary law is generally understood to apply—has the following distinguishing characteristics:

   *Answer:* deliberative assembly — “A deliberative assembly—the kind of gathering to which parliamentary law is generally understood to apply—has the following distinguishing characteristics:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:1 — “A deliberative assembly—the kind of gathering to which parliamentary law is generally understood to apply—has the following distinguishing characteristics:”

**2.** `[C0001 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A deliberative assembly—the kind of gathering to which parliamentary law is generally understood to apply—has the following distinguishing _____:
   A. characteristics
   B. presentation
   C. President-Elect
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:1 — “A deliberative assembly—the kind of gathering to which parliamentary law is generally understood to apply—has the following distinguishing characteristics:”

**3.** `[C0002 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rules in this book are principally applicable to meeting bodies possessing all of the foregoing _____.

   *Answer:* characteristics — “The rules in this book are principally applicable to meeting bodies possessing all of the foregoing characteristics.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:2 — “The rules in this book are principally applicable to meeting bodies possessing all of the foregoing characteristics.”

**4.** `[C0002 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Certain of these _____ rules or customs may sometimes also find application in other gatherings which, although resembling the deliberative assembly in varying degrees, do not have all of its attributes as listed above.
   A. parliamentary
   B. presentation
   C. specification
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:2 — “Certain of these parliamentary rules or customs may sometimes also find application in other gatherings which, although resembling the deliberative assembly in varying degrees, do not have all of its attributes as listed above.”

**5.** `[C0002 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rules in this book are principally applicable to meeting bodies possessing all of the foregoing _____.
   A. specification
   B. characteristics
   C. adjournment
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:2 — “The rules in this book are principally applicable to meeting bodies possessing all of the foregoing characteristics.”

**6.** `[C0002 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Certain of these _____ rules or customs may sometimes also find application in other gatherings which, although resembling the deliberative assembly in varying degrees, do not have all of its attributes as listed above.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Certain of these parliamentary rules or customs may sometimes also find application in other gatherings which, although resembling the deliberative assembly in varying degrees, do not have all of its attributes as listed above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:2 — “Certain of these parliamentary rules or customs may sometimes also find application in other gatherings which, although resembling the deliberative assembly in varying degrees, do not have all of its attributes as listed above.”

**7.** `[C0003 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The distinction should be noted between the _____ (that is, the body of people who assemble) and the meeting (which is the event of their being assembled to transact business).

   *Answer:* assembly — “The distinction should be noted between the assembly (that is, the body of people who assemble) and the meeting (which is the event of their being assembled to transact business).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:3 — “The distinction should be noted between the assembly (that is, the body of people who assemble) and the meeting (which is the event of their being assembled to transact business).”

**8.** `[C0003 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The distinction should be noted between the assembly (that is, the _____ who assemble) and the meeting (which is the event of their being assembled to transact business).
   A. the convention
   B. body of people
   C. The Framing of
   D. Special Orders

   *Answer:* B) body of people. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:3 — “The distinction should be noted between the assembly (that is, the body of people who assemble) and the meeting (which is the event of their being assembled to transact business).”

**9.** `[C0003 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term meeting is also distinguished from _____, according to definitions stated in 8.

   *Answer:* session — “The term meeting is also distinguished from session, according to definitions stated in 8.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:3 — “The term meeting is also distinguished from session, according to definitions stated in 8.”

**10.** `[C0003 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term meeting is also distinguished from _____, according to definitions stated in 8.
   A. Motions
   B. session
   C. carried
   D. Reports

   *Answer:* B) session. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:3 — “The term meeting is also distinguished from session, according to definitions stated in 8.”

**11.** `[C0004 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ of an assembly, in the parliamentary sense, as mentioned above, is a person entitled to full participation in its proceedings, that is, as explained in 3 and 4, the right to attend meetings, to make motions, to speak in debate, and to vote.

   *Answer:* member — “A member of an assembly, in the parliamentary sense, as mentioned above, is a person entitled to full participation in its proceedings, that is, as explained in 3 and 4, the right to attend meetings, to make motions, to speak in debate, and to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:4 — “A member of an assembly, in the parliamentary sense, as mentioned above, is a person entitled to full participation in its proceedings, that is, as explained in 3 and 4, the right to attend meetings, to make motions, to speak in debate, and to vote.”

**12.** `[C0004 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member of an assembly, in the parliamentary sense, as mentioned above, is a person entitled to full participation in its proceedings, that is, as explained in 3 and 4, the right to _____ meetings, to make motions, to speak in debate, and to vote.
   A. attend
   B. Repeal
   C. adding
   D. accept

   *Answer:* A) attend. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:4 — “A member of an assembly, in the parliamentary sense, as mentioned above, is a person entitled to full participation in its proceedings, that is, as explained in 3 and 4, the right to attend meetings, to make motions, to speak in debate, and to vote.”

**13.** `[C0005 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A deliberative assembly that has not adopted any rules is commonly understood to hold itself bound by the rules and customs of the _____—or common parliamentary law (as discussed in the Introduction)—to the extent that there is agreement in the meeting body as to what these rules and practices are.

   *Answer:* general parliamentary law — “A deliberative assembly that has not adopted any rules is commonly understood to hold itself bound by the rules and customs of the general parliamentary law—or common parliamentary law (as discussed in the Introduction)—to the extent that there is agreement in the meeting body as to what these rules and practices are.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:5 — “A deliberative assembly that has not adopted any rules is commonly understood to hold itself bound by the rules and customs of the general parliamentary law—or common parliamentary law (as discussed in the Introduction)—to the extent that there is agreement in the meeting body as to what these rules and practices are.”

**14.** `[C0005 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A deliberative assembly that has not adopted any rules is _____ly understood to hold itself bound by the rules and customs of the general parliamentary law—or common parliamentary law (as discussed in the Introduction)—to the extent that there is agreement in the meeting body as to what these rules and practices are.
   A. Appeal
   B. Commit
   C. common
   D. beyond

   *Answer:* C) common. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:5 — “A deliberative assembly that has not adopted any rules is commonly understood to hold itself bound by the rules and customs of the general parliamentary law—or common parliamentary law (as discussed in the Introduction)—to the extent that there is agreement in the meeting body as to what these rules and practices are.”

**15.** `[C0005 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Most assemblies operate subject to one or more classes of written rules, however, that the particular body—or, sometimes, a higher authority under which it is _____—has formally adopted.

   *Answer:* constituted — “Most assemblies operate subject to one or more classes of written rules, however, that the particular body—or, sometimes, a higher authority under which it is constituted—has formally adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:5 — “Most assemblies operate subject to one or more classes of written rules, however, that the particular body—or, sometimes, a higher authority under which it is constituted—has formally adopted.”

**16.** `[C0005 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Taken as a whole, such rules may relate to the _____ of the organization or society of which the assembly is the meeting body, they may interpret or supplement the general parliamentary law, or they may involve provisions not directly related to the transaction of business.
   A. recommendation
   B. Vice-President
   C. Information
   D. establishment

   *Answer:* D) establishment. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:5 — “Taken as a whole, such rules may relate to the establishment of the organization or society of which the assembly is the meeting body, they may interpret or supplement the general parliamentary law, or they may involve provisions not directly related to the transaction of business.”

**17.** `[C0005 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Most assemblies operate subject to one or more classes of written rules, however, that the particular body—or, sometimes, a higher authority under which it is _____—has formally adopted.
   A. Preparation
   B. interrupted
   C. constituted
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) constituted. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:5 — “Most assemblies operate subject to one or more classes of written rules, however, that the particular body—or, sometimes, a higher authority under which it is constituted—has formally adopted.”

**18.** `[C0006 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The basic principle of decision in a deliberative assembly is that, to become the act or choice of the body, a proposition must be adopted by a _____;

   *Answer:* majority vote — “The basic principle of decision in a deliberative assembly is that, to become the act or choice of the body, a proposition must be adopted by a majority vote;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:6 — “The basic principle of decision in a deliberative assembly is that, to become the act or choice of the body, a proposition must be adopted by a majority vote;”

**19.** `[C0006 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The basic principle of decision in a deliberative assembly is that, to become the act or choice of the body, a proposition must be adopted by a _____ vote;
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:6 — “The basic principle of decision in a deliberative assembly is that, to become the act or choice of the body, a proposition must be adopted by a majority vote;”

**20.** `[C0006 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of the foregoing principle that impose a requirement of more than a majority vote arise: (a) where required by law;
   A. Parliamentary
   B. Indefinitely
   C. deliberative
   D. Modifications

   *Answer:* D) Modifications. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:6 — “Modifications of the foregoing principle that impose a requirement of more than a majority vote arise: (a) where required by law;”

**21.** `[C0006 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (b) where provided by special rule of a particular _____ or assembly as dictated by its own conditions;

   *Answer:* organization — “(b) where provided by special rule of a particular organization or assembly as dictated by its own conditions;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:6 — “(b) where provided by special rule of a particular organization or assembly as dictated by its own conditions;”

**22.** `[C0006 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or (c) where required under the general _____ law in the case of certain steps or procedures that impinge on the normal rights of the minority, of absentees, or of some other group within the assembly’s membership.
   A. parliamentary
   B. Information
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:6 — “or (c) where required under the general parliamentary law in the case of certain steps or procedures that impinge on the normal rights of the minority, of absentees, or of some other group within the assembly’s membership.”

**23.** `[C0007 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a decision is to be based on more than a majority, the requirement most commonly specified is a _____— that is, the expressed approval of at least two thirds of those present and voting.

   *Answer:* two-thirds vote — “When a decision is to be based on more than a majority, the requirement most commonly specified is a two-thirds vote— that is, the expressed approval of at least two thirds of those present and voting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:7 — “When a decision is to be based on more than a majority, the requirement most commonly specified is a two-thirds vote— that is, the expressed approval of at least two thirds of those present and voting.”

**24.** `[C0007 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a decision is to be based on more than a _____, the requirement most commonly specified is a two-thirds vote— that is, the expressed approval of at least two thirds of those present and voting.

   *Answer:* majority — “When a decision is to be based on more than a majority, the requirement most commonly specified is a two-thirds vote— that is, the expressed approval of at least two thirds of those present and voting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:7 — “When a decision is to be based on more than a majority, the requirement most commonly specified is a two-thirds vote— that is, the expressed approval of at least two thirds of those present and voting.”

**25.** `[C0007 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Other bases for decision which find use in certain cases are defined in 44, such as a majority _____—that is, more than half of all the members.
   A. Additional Bylaw Articles
   B. of the entire membership
   C. Debatability of Motions
   D. Article VII: Committees

   *Answer:* B) of the entire membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:7 — “Other bases for decision which find use in certain cases are defined in 44, such as a majority of the entire membership—that is, more than half of all the members.”

**26.** `[C0007 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Other bases for decision which find use in certain cases are defined in 44, such as a majority _____—that is, more than half of all the members.

   *Answer:* of the entire membership — “Other bases for decision which find use in certain cases are defined in 44, such as a majority of the entire membership—that is, more than half of all the members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:7 — “Other bases for decision which find use in certain cases are defined in 44, such as a majority of the entire membership—that is, more than half of all the members.”

**27.** `[C0009 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Among the principal types that it is convenient to distinguish for the purposes of parliamentary law are: (1) the _____;

   *Answer:* mass meeting — “Among the principal types that it is convenient to distinguish for the purposes of parliamentary law are: (1) the mass meeting;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:9 — “Among the principal types that it is convenient to distinguish for the purposes of parliamentary law are: (1) the mass meeting;”

**28.** `[C0009 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ assembly may exist in many forms.

   *Answer:* deliberative — “The deliberative assembly may exist in many forms.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:9 — “The deliberative assembly may exist in many forms.”

**29.** `[C0009 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Among the principal types that it is convenient to distinguish for the purposes of _____ law are: (1) the mass meeting;
   A. representatives
   B. immediately
   C. parliamentary
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:9 — “Among the principal types that it is convenient to distinguish for the purposes of parliamentary law are: (1) the mass meeting;”

**30.** `[C0009 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ assembly may exist in many forms.
   A. deliberative
   B. presentation
   C. Organization
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:9 — “The deliberative assembly may exist in many forms.”

**31.** `[C0010 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ is the simplest form of assembly in principle, although not the one most frequently encountered.

   *Answer:* mass meeting — “The mass meeting is the simplest form of assembly in principle, although not the one most frequently encountered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:10 — “The mass meeting is the simplest form of assembly in principle, although not the one most frequently encountered.”

**32.** `[C0010 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The mass meeting is the simplest form of assembly in principle, although not the one most frequently _____.
   A. encountered
   B. Particular
   C. requirement
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) encountered. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:10 — “The mass meeting is the simplest form of assembly in principle, although not the one most frequently encountered.”

**33.** `[C0010 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A mass meeting is a meeting of an _____ group that is announced as open to everyone (or everyone within a specified sector of the population) interested in a particular problem or purpose defined by the meeting’s sponsors, and that is called with a view to appropriate action to be decided on and taken by the meeting body.

   *Answer:* unorganized — “A mass meeting is a meeting of an unorganized group that is announced as open to everyone (or everyone within a specified sector of the population) interested in a particular problem or purpose defined by the meeting’s sponsors, and that is called with a view to appropriate action to be decided on and taken by the meeting body.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:10 — “A mass meeting is a meeting of an unorganized group that is announced as open to everyone (or everyone within a specified sector of the population) interested in a particular problem or purpose defined by the meeting’s sponsors, and that is called with a view to appropriate action to be decided on and taken by the meeting body.”

**34.** `[C0010 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A series of _____ meetings making up a session may be held on such a basis.
   A. execution
   B. rejecting
   C. connected
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* C) connected. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:10 — “A series of connected meetings making up a session may be held on such a basis.”

**35.** `[C0010 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The class of persons invited might be, for example, _____ of a given political party, homeowners residing within a certain city, persons opposed to a tax increase, or any similar group.

   *Answer:* supporters — “The class of persons invited might be, for example, supporters of a given political party, homeowners residing within a certain city, persons opposed to a tax increase, or any similar group.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:10 — “The class of persons invited might be, for example, supporters of a given political party, homeowners residing within a certain city, persons opposed to a tax increase, or any similar group.”

**36.** `[C0011 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that a large _____ is not an essential feature of the mass meeting, although it may usually be desired.

   *Answer:* attendance — “It should be noted that a large attendance is not an essential feature of the mass meeting, although it may usually be desired.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:11 — “It should be noted that a large attendance is not an essential feature of the mass meeting, although it may usually be desired.”

**37.** `[C0011 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A series of meetings held for the purpose of _____ a society are in the nature of mass meetings until the society has been formed.
   A. Convention
   B. incidental
   C. organizing
   D. committees

   *Answer:* C) organizing. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:11 — “A series of meetings held for the purpose of organizing a society are in the nature of mass meetings until the society has been formed.”

**38.** `[C0011 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that a large _____ is not an essential feature of the mass meeting, although it may usually be desired.
   A. authorizes
   B. attendance
   C. Particular
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) attendance. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:11 — “It should be noted that a large attendance is not an essential feature of the mass meeting, although it may usually be desired.”

**39.** `[C0011 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A series of meetings held for the purpose of _____ a society are in the nature of mass meetings until the society has been formed.

   *Answer:* organizing — “A series of meetings held for the purpose of organizing a society are in the nature of mass meetings until the society has been formed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:11 — “A series of meetings held for the purpose of organizing a society are in the nature of mass meetings until the society has been formed.”

**40.** `[C0012 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As the highest authority within such a society or branch (subject only to the provisions of the _____ or other basic document establishing the organization), this body acts for the total membership in the transaction of its business.

   *Answer:* bylaws — “As the highest authority within such a society or branch (subject only to the provisions of the bylaws or other basic document establishing the organization), this body acts for the total membership in the transaction of its business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:13 — “As the highest authority within such a society or branch (subject only to the provisions of the bylaws or other basic document establishing the organization), this body acts for the total membership in the transaction of its business.”

**41.** `[C0012 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such an assembly’s membership is limited to persons who are recorded on the rolls of the society as voting members and who are _____.
   A. consent calendar
   B. during a meeting
   C. Minimum Officers
   D. in good standing

   *Answer:* D) in good standing. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:13 — “Such an assembly’s membership is limited to persons who are recorded on the rolls of the society as voting members and who are in good standing.”

**42.** `[C0012 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Local Assembly of an _____ Society.

   *Answer:* Organized — “The Local Assembly of an Organized Society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:13 — “The Local Assembly of an Organized Society.”

**43.** `[C0012 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The assembly at the meetings of an organized permanent society existing as a local club or local branch is the type of assembly with which the average person is most likely to have direct _____.
   A. Reconsider
   B. experience
   C. authorizes
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* B) experience. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:13 — “The assembly at the meetings of an organized permanent society existing as a local club or local branch is the type of assembly with which the average person is most likely to have direct experience.”

**44.** `[C0012 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As the highest authority within such a society or branch (subject only to the provisions of the bylaws or other basic document _____ the organization), this body acts for the total membership in the transaction of its business.

   *Answer:* establishing — “As the highest authority within such a society or branch (subject only to the provisions of the bylaws or other basic document establishing the organization), this body acts for the total membership in the transaction of its business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:13 — “As the highest authority within such a society or branch (subject only to the provisions of the bylaws or other basic document establishing the organization), this body acts for the total membership in the transaction of its business.”

**45.** `[C0013 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is an assembly of delegates (other than a permanently established public lawmaking body) chosen, normally for one session only, as representatives of constituent units or subdivisions of a much larger body of people in whose name the convention sits and acts.

   *Answer:* convention — “A convention is an assembly of delegates (other than a permanently established public lawmaking body) chosen, normally for one session only, as representatives of constituent units or subdivisions of a much larger body of people in whose name the convention sits and acts.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:14 — “A convention is an assembly of delegates (other than a permanently established public lawmaking body) chosen, normally for one session only, as representatives of constituent units or subdivisions of a much larger body of people in whose name the convention sits and acts.”

**46.** `[C0013 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A convention is an assembly of _____ (other than a permanently established public lawmaking body) chosen, normally for one session only, as representatives of constituent units or subdivisions of a much larger body of people in whose name the convention sits and acts.
   A. execution
   B. delegates
   C. Precedent
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* B) delegates. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:14 — “A convention is an assembly of delegates (other than a permanently established public lawmaking body) chosen, normally for one session only, as representatives of constituent units or subdivisions of a much larger body of people in whose name the convention sits and acts.”

**47.** `[C0014 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The most common type of _____ is that of an organized state or national society—held, for example, annually or biennially—in which the delegates are selected by, and from among the members of, each local branch.

   *Answer:* convention — “The most common type of convention is that of an organized state or national society—held, for example, annually or biennially—in which the delegates are selected by, and from among the members of, each local branch.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:15 — “The most common type of convention is that of an organized state or national society—held, for example, annually or biennially—in which the delegates are selected by, and from among the members of, each local branch.”

**48.** `[C0014 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A convention is sometimes also called for the purpose of forming an _____ or federation;
   A. Particular
   B. authorizes
   C. Unfinished
   D. association

   *Answer:* D) association. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:15 — “A convention is sometimes also called for the purpose of forming an association or federation;”

**49.** `[C0014 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, like a mass meeting, it may be convened to draw interested parties or _____ of interested organizations together in acting upon a common problem.

   *Answer:* representatives — “or, like a mass meeting, it may be convened to draw interested parties or representatives of interested organizations together in acting upon a common problem.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:15 — “or, like a mass meeting, it may be convened to draw interested parties or representatives of interested organizations together in acting upon a common problem.”

**50.** `[C0014 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ convention, for example—convoked to draft a proposed new state constitution —may continue for weeks or months.
   A. constitutional
   B. representatives
   C. adjournment
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) constitutional. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:15 — “A constitutional convention, for example—convoked to draft a proposed new state constitution —may continue for weeks or months.”

**51.** `[C0014 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The most common type of _____ is that of an organized state or national society—held, for example, annually or biennially—in which the delegates are selected by, and from among the members of, each local branch.
   A. variations
   B. Particular
   C. committees
   D. convention

   *Answer:* D) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:15 — “The most common type of convention is that of an organized state or national society—held, for example, annually or biennially—in which the delegates are selected by, and from among the members of, each local branch.”

**52.** `[C0015 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The voting membership of a convention consists of persons who hold proper _____ as delegates or as persons in some other way entitled to such membership, which must be certified and reported to the convention by its Credentials Committee.
   A. adjournment
   B. credentials
   C. resolutions
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) credentials. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:16 — “The voting membership of a convention consists of persons who hold proper credentials as delegates or as persons in some other way entitled to such membership, which must be certified and reported to the convention by its Credentials Committee.”

**53.** `[C0015 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The voting membership of a convention consists of persons who hold proper _____ as delegates or as persons in some other way entitled to such membership, which must be certified and reported to the convention by its Credentials Committee.

   *Answer:* credentials — “The voting membership of a convention consists of persons who hold proper credentials as delegates or as persons in some other way entitled to such membership, which must be certified and reported to the convention by its Credentials Committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:16 — “The voting membership of a convention consists of persons who hold proper credentials as delegates or as persons in some other way entitled to such membership, which must be certified and reported to the convention by its Credentials Committee.”

**54.** `[C0016 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of the convention session normally dissolves the assembly.

   *Answer:* conclusion — “The conclusion of the convention session normally dissolves the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:17 — “The conclusion of the convention session normally dissolves the assembly.”

**55.** `[C0016 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The conclusion of the _____ session normally dissolves the assembly.
   A. recognized
   B. convention
   C. settlement
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* B) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:17 — “The conclusion of the convention session normally dissolves the assembly.”

**56.** `[C0017 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____ refers to a constitutionally established public lawmaking body of representatives chosen by the electorate for a fixed term of office—such as Congress or a state legislature.

   *Answer:* legislative body — “The term legislative body refers to a constitutionally established public lawmaking body of representatives chosen by the electorate for a fixed term of office—such as Congress or a state legislature.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:19 — “The term legislative body refers to a constitutionally established public lawmaking body of representatives chosen by the electorate for a fixed term of office—such as Congress or a state legislature.”

**57.** `[C0017 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term legislative body refers to a _____ established public lawmaking body of representatives chosen by the electorate for a fixed term of office—such as Congress or a state legislature.
   A. requirement
   B. Indefinitely
   C. constitutionally
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* C) constitutionally. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:19 — “The term legislative body refers to a constitutionally established public lawmaking body of representatives chosen by the electorate for a fixed term of office—such as Congress or a state legislature.”

**58.** `[C0017 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a body typically (though not always) consists of two _____, or “houses.” Its sessions may last for months, during which it meets daily and its members are paid to devote their full time to its work and can be legally compelled to attend its meetings.

   *Answer:* assemblies — “Such a body typically (though not always) consists of two assemblies, or “houses.” Its sessions may last for months, during which it meets daily and its members are paid to devote their full time to its work and can be legally compelled to attend its meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:19 — “Such a body typically (though not always) consists of two assemblies, or “houses.” Its sessions may last for months, during which it meets daily and its members are paid to devote their full time to its work and can be legally compelled to attend its meetings.”

**59.** `[C0017 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a body typically (though not always) consists of two _____, or “houses.” Its sessions may last for months, during which it meets daily and its members are paid to devote their full time to its work and can be legally compelled to attend its meetings.
   A. assemblies
   B. subsidiary
   C. convention
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* A) assemblies. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:19 — “Such a body typically (though not always) consists of two assemblies, or “houses.” Its sessions may last for months, during which it meets daily and its members are paid to devote their full time to its work and can be legally compelled to attend its meetings.”

**60.** `[C0018 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each state or national legislative assembly generally has its own well-developed body of rules, _____, and precedents, so that the exact procedure for a particular legislative house can be found only in its own manual.

   *Answer:* interpretations — “Each state or national legislative assembly generally has its own well-developed body of rules, interpretations, and precedents, so that the exact procedure for a particular legislative house can be found only in its own manual.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:20 — “Each state or national legislative assembly generally has its own well-developed body of rules, interpretations, and precedents, so that the exact procedure for a particular legislative house can be found only in its own manual.”

**61.** `[C0018 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each state or national legislative assembly generally has its own well-developed body of rules, _____, and precedents, so that the exact procedure for a particular legislative house can be found only in its own manual.
   A. Preparation
   B. subsequently
   C. recommendation
   D. interpretations

   *Answer:* D) interpretations. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:20 — “Each state or national legislative assembly generally has its own well-developed body of rules, interpretations, and precedents, so that the exact procedure for a particular legislative house can be found only in its own manual.”

**62.** `[C0019 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In this connection, however, it should be noted that certain smaller public bodies may serve a lawmaking function yet not assume the character of a full-scale _____ assembly, and instead may somewhat resemble a board or the assembly of a society.

   *Answer:* legislative — “In this connection, however, it should be noted that certain smaller public bodies may serve a lawmaking function yet not assume the character of a full-scale legislative assembly, and instead may somewhat resemble a board or the assembly of a society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:21 — “In this connection, however, it should be noted that certain smaller public bodies may serve a lawmaking function yet not assume the character of a full-scale legislative assembly, and instead may somewhat resemble a board or the assembly of a society.”

**63.** `[C0019 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An example of such a body might be a city council that meets weekly or monthly and whose members continue their own full-time _____ during their term of service.
   A. convention
   B. occupations
   C. Preparation
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) occupations. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:21 — “An example of such a body might be a city council that meets weekly or monthly and whose members continue their own full-time occupations during their term of service.”

**64.** `[C0019 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In this connection, however, it should be noted that certain smaller public bodies may serve a lawmaking function yet not assume the character of a full-scale _____ assembly, and instead may somewhat resemble a board or the assembly of a society.
   A. legislative
   B. resolutions
   C. Particular
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) legislative. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:21 — “In this connection, however, it should be noted that certain smaller public bodies may serve a lawmaking function yet not assume the character of a full-scale legislative assembly, and instead may somewhat resemble a board or the assembly of a society.”

   *Answer:* occupations — “An example of such a body might be a city council that meets weekly or monthly and whose members continue their own full-time occupations during their term of service.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:21 — “An example of such a body might be a city council that meets weekly or monthly and whose members continue their own full-time occupations during their term of service.”

**66.** `[C0020 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____, in the general sense of the term, is an administrative, managerial, or quasi-judicial body of elected or appointed persons that differs from several of the other principal types of deliberative assembly as follows:

   *Answer:* board — “A board, in the general sense of the term, is an administrative, managerial, or quasi-judicial body of elected or appointed persons that differs from several of the other principal types of deliberative assembly as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:22 — “A board, in the general sense of the term, is an administrative, managerial, or quasi-judicial body of elected or appointed persons that differs from several of the other principal types of deliberative assembly as follows:”

**67.** `[C0020 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A board, in the general sense of the term, is an _____, managerial, or quasi-judicial body of elected or appointed persons that differs from several of the other principal types of deliberative assembly as follows:
   A. administrative
   B. president-elect
   C. recommendation
   D. Responsibilities

   *Answer:* A) administrative. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:22 — “A board, in the general sense of the term, is an administrative, managerial, or quasi-judicial body of elected or appointed persons that differs from several of the other principal types of deliberative assembly as follows:”

**68.** `[C0021 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A board may be assigned a _____ function on behalf of a national, state, or local government, as a village board that operates like a small city council, a board of education, or a board of examiners.

   *Answer:* particular — “A board may be assigned a particular function on behalf of a national, state, or local government, as a village board that operates like a small city council, a board of education, or a board of examiners.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:23 — “A board may be assigned a particular function on behalf of a national, state, or local government, as a village board that operates like a small city council, a board of education, or a board of examiners.”

**69.** `[C0021 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a nonstock corporation that has no assembly or body of persons _____ a general voting membership, as a university or foundation, the board of directors, managers, trustees, or governors is the supreme governing body of the institution.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. resolutions
   C. Definitely
   D. constituting

   *Answer:* D) constituting. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:23 — “In a nonstock corporation that has no assembly or body of persons constituting a general voting membership, as a university or foundation, the board of directors, managers, trustees, or governors is the supreme governing body of the institution.”

**70.** `[C0021 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Similarly, in a stock corporation, although the board of directors is elected by _____ who hold an annual meeting, it constitutes the highest authority in the management of the corporation.

   *Answer:* stockholders — “Similarly, in a stock corporation, although the board of directors is elected by stockholders who hold an annual meeting, it constitutes the highest authority in the management of the corporation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:23 — “Similarly, in a stock corporation, although the board of directors is elected by stockholders who hold an annual meeting, it constitutes the highest authority in the management of the corporation.”

**71.** `[C0021 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A board within an organized society, on the other hand, is an _____ of the society’s full assembly, to which it is subordinate.
   A. Vice-President
   B. interrupted
   C. specification
   D. instrumentality

   *Answer:* D) instrumentality. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:23 — “A board within an organized society, on the other hand, is an instrumentality of the society’s full assembly, to which it is subordinate.”

**72.** `[C0021 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Boards are _____ in greater detail in 49.

   *Answer:* discussed — “Boards are discussed in greater detail in 49.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:23 — “Boards are discussed in greater detail in 49.”

**73.** `[C0022 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The distinction between a _____ and a committee must be briefly noted here for an understanding of what follows.

   *Answer:* board — “The distinction between a board and a committee must be briefly noted here for an understanding of what follows.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:24 — “The distinction between a board and a committee must be briefly noted here for an understanding of what follows.”

**74.** `[C0022 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The distinction between a board and a _____ must be briefly noted here for an understanding of what follows.
   A. Privilege
   B. president
   C. committee
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* C) committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:24 — “The distinction between a board and a committee must be briefly noted here for an understanding of what follows.”

**75.** `[C0022 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A board of any size is a form of assembly as just _____.
   A. committee
   B. explained
   C. secondary
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* B) explained. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:24 — “A board of any size is a form of assembly as just explained.”

**76.** `[C0022 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Committees, on the other hand, are bodies that are often, but not necessarily, very small, and that are subordinate instruments of an assembly or are accountable to a higher authority in some way not _____ of an assembly.

   *Answer:* characteristic — “Committees, on the other hand, are bodies that are often, but not necessarily, very small, and that are subordinate instruments of an assembly or are accountable to a higher authority in some way not characteristic of an assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:24 — “Committees, on the other hand, are bodies that are often, but not necessarily, very small, and that are subordinate instruments of an assembly or are accountable to a higher authority in some way not characteristic of an assembly.”

**77.** `[C0022 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Large boards generally follow _____ procedure in the same way as any other assembly.
   A. undebatable
   B. Preparation
   C. parliamentary
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §1:24 — “Large boards generally follow parliamentary procedure in the same way as any other assembly.”

**78.** `[C0023 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ society requires certain rules to establish its basic structure and manner of operation.

   *Answer:* organized — “An organized society requires certain rules to establish its basic structure and manner of operation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:1 — “An organized society requires certain rules to establish its basic structure and manner of operation.”

**79.** `[C0023 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition, a need for formally adopted rules of procedure arises in any assembly, principally because there may be disagreement or a lack of _____ as to what is parliamentary law regarding points that can affect the outcome of substantive issues.
   A. Announcements
   B. resolutions
   C. understanding
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* C) understanding. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:1 — “In addition, a need for formally adopted rules of procedure arises in any assembly, principally because there may be disagreement or a lack of understanding as to what is parliamentary law regarding points that can affect the outcome of substantive issues.”

**80.** `[C0023 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ society requires certain rules to establish its basic structure and manner of operation.
   A. Signature
   B. organized
   C. delegates
   D. Reporting

   *Answer:* B) organized. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:1 — “An organized society requires certain rules to establish its basic structure and manner of operation.”

**81.** `[C0023 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, a need for formally adopted rules of procedure arises in any assembly, principally because there may be disagreement or a lack of _____ as to what is parliamentary law regarding points that can affect the outcome of substantive issues.

   *Answer:* understanding — “In addition, a need for formally adopted rules of procedure arises in any assembly, principally because there may be disagreement or a lack of understanding as to what is parliamentary law regarding points that can affect the outcome of substantive issues.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:1 — “In addition, a need for formally adopted rules of procedure arises in any assembly, principally because there may be disagreement or a lack of understanding as to what is parliamentary law regarding points that can affect the outcome of substantive issues.”

**82.** `[C0024 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ has shown that some of the rules of a society should be made more difficult to change, or to suspend—that is, to set aside for a specific purpose—than others.

   *Answer:* Experience — “Experience has shown that some of the rules of a society should be made more difficult to change, or to suspend—that is, to set aside for a specific purpose—than others.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:2 — “Experience has shown that some of the rules of a society should be made more difficult to change, or to suspend—that is, to set aside for a specific purpose—than others.”

**83.** `[C0024 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Upon this principle, the rules which an established _____ may have are commonly divided into classes—some of which are needed by every society, while others may be required only as conditions warrant.
   A. Announcements
   B. Reconsider
   C. organization
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:2 — “Upon this principle, the rules which an established organization may have are commonly divided into classes—some of which are needed by every society, while others may be required only as conditions warrant.”

**84.** `[C0024 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Within this framework under the general _____ law, an assembly or society is free to adopt any rules it may wish (even rules deviating from parliamentary law) provided that, in the procedure of adopting them, it conforms to parliamentary law or its own existing rules.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Within this framework under the general parliamentary law, an assembly or society is free to adopt any rules it may wish (even rules deviating from parliamentary law) provided that, in the procedure of adopting them, it conforms to parliamentary law or its own existing rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:2 — “Within this framework under the general parliamentary law, an assembly or society is free to adopt any rules it may wish (even rules deviating from parliamentary law) provided that, in the procedure of adopting them, it conforms to parliamentary law or its own existing rules.”

**85.** `[C0024 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ has shown that some of the rules of a society should be made more difficult to change, or to suspend—that is, to set aside for a specific purpose—than others.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Experience
   C. subsidiary
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) Experience. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:2 — “Experience has shown that some of the rules of a society should be made more difficult to change, or to suspend—that is, to set aside for a specific purpose—than others.”

**86.** `[C0024 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Upon this principle, the rules which an established _____ may have are commonly divided into classes—some of which are needed by every society, while others may be required only as conditions warrant.

   *Answer:* organization — “Upon this principle, the rules which an established organization may have are commonly divided into classes—some of which are needed by every society, while others may be required only as conditions warrant.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:2 — “Upon this principle, the rules which an established organization may have are commonly divided into classes—some of which are needed by every society, while others may be required only as conditions warrant.”

**87.** `[C0025 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The various kinds of rules which a society may formally adopt include the following: Corporate Charter, Constitution and/or Bylaws, Rules of Order (which include a standard work on _____ law adopted as the society’s Parliamentary Authority, and any Special Rules of Order), and Standing Rules.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The various kinds of rules which a society may formally adopt include the following: Corporate Charter, Constitution and/or Bylaws, Rules of Order (which include a standard work on parliamentary law adopted as the society’s Parliamentary Authority, and any Special Rules of Order), and Standing Rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:3 — “The various kinds of rules which a society may formally adopt include the following: Corporate Charter, Constitution and/or Bylaws, Rules of Order (which include a standard work on parliamentary law adopted as the society’s Parliamentary Authority, and any Special Rules of Order), and Standing Rules.”

**88.** `[C0025 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each of these types of rules is _____ below.
   A. amendment
   B. discussed
   C. Objection
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* B) discussed. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:3 — “Each of these types of rules is discussed below.”

**89.** `[C0025 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The various kinds of rules which a society may formally adopt include the following: Corporate Charter, _____ and/or Bylaws, Rules of Order (which include a standard work on parliamentary law adopted as the society’s Parliamentary Authority, and any Special Rules of Order), and Standing Rules.

   *Answer:* Constitution — “The various kinds of rules which a society may formally adopt include the following: Corporate Charter, Constitution and/or Bylaws, Rules of Order (which include a standard work on parliamentary law adopted as the society’s Parliamentary Authority, and any Special Rules of Order), and Standing Rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:3 — “The various kinds of rules which a society may formally adopt include the following: Corporate Charter, Constitution and/or Bylaws, Rules of Order (which include a standard work on parliamentary law adopted as the society’s Parliamentary Authority, and any Special Rules of Order), and Standing Rules.”

**90.** `[C0025 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each of these types of rules is _____ below.

   *Answer:* discussed — “Each of these types of rules is discussed below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:3 — “Each of these types of rules is discussed below.”

**91.** `[C0026 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In matters not governed by any adopted rule, a society may be guided by _____ custom, also discussed below.

   *Answer:* established — “In matters not governed by any adopted rule, a society may be guided by established custom, also discussed below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:4 — “In matters not governed by any adopted rule, a society may be guided by established custom, also discussed below.”

**92.** `[C0026 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In matters not governed by any adopted rule, a society may be guided by _____ custom, also discussed below.
   A. established
   B. constitution
   C. Definitely
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:4 — “In matters not governed by any adopted rule, a society may be guided by established custom, also discussed below.”

**93.** `[C0027 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Corporate Charter (in different states variously called the _____, Articles of Incorporation, Articles of Association, etc.) is a legal instrument that sets forth the name and object of the society and whatever other information is needed for incorporating the society under the laws of the particular state—or under federal law in the case of a few special types of organizations.

   *Answer:* Certificate of Incorporation — “The Corporate Charter (in different states variously called the Certificate of Incorporation, Articles of Incorporation, Articles of Association, etc.) is a legal instrument that sets forth the name and object of the society and whatever other information is needed for incorporating the society under the laws of the particular state—or under federal law in the case of a few special types of organizations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:5 — “The Corporate Charter (in different states variously called the Certificate of Incorporation, Articles of Incorporation, Articles of Association, etc.) is a legal instrument that sets forth the name and object of the society and whatever other information is needed for incorporating the society under the laws of the particular state—or under federal law in the case of a few special types of organizations.”

**94.** `[C0027 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Corporate Charter (in different states variously called the Certificate of Incorporation, _____, Articles of Association, etc.) is a legal instrument that sets forth the name and object of the society and whatever other information is needed for incorporating the society under the laws of the particular state—or under federal law in the case of a few special types of organizations.
   A. Meetings of the Committee
   B. Additional Bylaw Articles
   C. Articles of Incorporation
   D. Debate on the Suggestions

   *Answer:* C) Articles of Incorporation. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:5 — “The Corporate Charter (in different states variously called the Certificate of Incorporation, Articles of Incorporation, Articles of Association, etc.) is a legal instrument that sets forth the name and object of the society and whatever other information is needed for incorporating the society under the laws of the particular state—or under federal law in the case of a few special types of organizations.”

**95.** `[C0027 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Apart from this _____, in general, a society need not be incorporated unless incorporation is dictated by a law relating to the society’s contemplated activities.

   *Answer:* consideration — “Apart from this consideration, in general, a society need not be incorporated unless incorporation is dictated by a law relating to the society’s contemplated activities.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:5 — “Apart from this consideration, in general, a society need not be incorporated unless incorporation is dictated by a law relating to the society’s contemplated activities.”

**96.** `[C0027 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Apart from this _____, in general, a society need not be incorporated unless incorporation is dictated by a law relating to the society’s contemplated activities.
   A. adjournment
   B. resolutions
   C. Parliamentary
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:5 — “Apart from this consideration, in general, a society need not be incorporated unless incorporation is dictated by a law relating to the society’s contemplated activities.”

**97.** `[C0028 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A corporate charter should be drafted by an attorney and must then be processed in accordance with the legal procedure for _____ in the state (or under federal law if applicable).

**98.** `[C0028 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any later amendments (that is, changes in the charter) are subject to the _____ of law and any limitations placed in the charter itself.
   A. presentation
   B. requirements
   C. Announcements
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) requirements. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:6 — “Any later amendments (that is, changes in the charter) are subject to the requirements of law and any limitations placed in the charter itself.”

**99.** `[C0028 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A corporate charter should be drafted by an attorney and must then be processed in accordance with the legal procedure for _____ in the state (or under federal law if applicable).
   A. Indefinitely
   B. incorporation
   C. President-Elect
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* B) incorporation. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:6 — “A corporate charter should be drafted by an attorney and must then be processed in accordance with the legal procedure for incorporation in the state (or under federal law if applicable).”

**100.** `[C0028 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any later amendments (that is, changes in the charter) are subject to the _____ of law and any limitations placed in the charter itself.

   *Answer:* requirements — “Any later amendments (that is, changes in the charter) are subject to the requirements of law and any limitations placed in the charter itself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:6 — “Any later amendments (that is, changes in the charter) are subject to the requirements of law and any limitations placed in the charter itself.”

**101.** `[C0029 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an _____ organization, the corporate charter supersedes all its other rules, none of which can legally contain anything in conflict with the charter.

   *Answer:* incorporated — “In an incorporated organization, the corporate charter supersedes all its other rules, none of which can legally contain anything in conflict with the charter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §2:7 — “In an incorporated organization, the corporate charter supersedes all its other rules, none of which can legally contain anything in conflict with the charter.”

**102.** `[C0029 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an incorporated _____, the corporate charter supersedes all its other rules, none of which can legally contain anything in conflict with the charter.
   A. Announcements
   B. organization
   C. consideration
   D. Particular
