   *Answer:* explanations — “(For more complete explanations of the general forms observed by the presiding officer and members in an assembly, see 42 and 43.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:14 — “(For more complete explanations of the general forms observed by the presiding officer and members in an assembly, see 42 and 43.)”

**220.** `[C0060 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For more complete _____ of the general forms observed by the presiding officer and members in an assembly, see 42 and 43.)
   A. immediately
   B. presentation
   C. Procedures
   D. explanations

   *Answer:* D) explanations. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:14 — “(For more complete explanations of the general forms observed by the presiding officer and members in an assembly, see 42 and 43.)”

**221.** `[C0061 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the time of a meeting has arrived, the presiding officer opens it, after he has _____ that a quorum is present, by calling the meeting to order.

   *Answer:* determined — “When the time of a meeting has arrived, the presiding officer opens it, after he has determined that a quorum is present, by calling the meeting to order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:15 — “When the time of a meeting has arrived, the presiding officer opens it, after he has determined that a quorum is present, by calling the meeting to order.”

**222.** `[C0061 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the time of a meeting has arrived, the presiding officer opens it, after he has _____ that a quorum is present, by calling the meeting to order.
   A. persuasive
   B. Reconsider
   C. Incidental
   D. determined

   *Answer:* D) determined. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:15 — “When the time of a meeting has arrived, the presiding officer opens it, after he has determined that a quorum is present, by calling the meeting to order.”

**223.** `[C0062 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Meetings of permanently organized bodies usually follow an established _____ that specifies the sequence in which certain general types or classes of business are to be brought up or permitted to be introduced.

   *Answer:* order of business — “Meetings of permanently organized bodies usually follow an established order of business that specifies the sequence in which certain general types or classes of business are to be brought up or permitted to be introduced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:16 — “Meetings of permanently organized bodies usually follow an established order of business that specifies the sequence in which certain general types or classes of business are to be brought up or permitted to be introduced.”

**224.** `[C0062 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The initial _____ in a mass meeting or in one called to form a society are described in 53 and 54.
   A. variations
   B. recognized
   C. procedures
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) procedures. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:16 — “The initial procedures in a mass meeting or in one called to form a society are described in 53 and 54.”

**225.** `[C0062 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Meetings of _____ organized bodies usually follow an established order of business that specifies the sequence in which certain general types or classes of business are to be brought up or permitted to be introduced.

   *Answer:* permanently — “Meetings of permanently organized bodies usually follow an established order of business that specifies the sequence in which certain general types or classes of business are to be brought up or permitted to be introduced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:16 — “Meetings of permanently organized bodies usually follow an established order of business that specifies the sequence in which certain general types or classes of business are to be brought up or permitted to be introduced.”

**226.** `[C0062 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A society may follow the order of business given in the manual that the bylaws of the organization designate as its _____ authority, or it may have adopted its own particular order of business.
   A. recommendation
   B. deliberative
   C. representatives
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:16 — “A society may follow the order of business given in the manual that the bylaws of the organization designate as its parliamentary authority, or it may have adopted its own particular order of business.”

**227.** `[C0062 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The initial _____ in a mass meeting or in one called to form a society are described in 53 and 54.

   *Answer:* procedures — “The initial procedures in a mass meeting or in one called to form a society are described in 53 and 54.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:16 — “The initial procedures in a mass meeting or in one called to form a society are described in 53 and 54.”

**228.** `[C0063 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a meeting where an _____ order of business is being followed, the chair calls for the different classes of business in the prescribed order.

   *Answer:* established — “In a meeting where an established order of business is being followed, the chair calls for the different classes of business in the prescribed order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:17 — “In a meeting where an established order of business is being followed, the chair calls for the different classes of business in the prescribed order.”

**229.** `[C0063 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a meeting where an _____ order of business is being followed, the chair calls for the different classes of business in the prescribed order.
   A. established
   B. authorizes
   C. Substitute
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:17 — “In a meeting where an established order of business is being followed, the chair calls for the different classes of business in the prescribed order.”

**230.** `[C0064 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A mass meeting usually requires no order of business, since, _____ to the headings listed above, there is nothing but new business to be brought up (unless the meeting is one within a series).

   *Answer:* referring — “A mass meeting usually requires no order of business, since, referring to the headings listed above, there is nothing but new business to be brought up (unless the meeting is one within a series).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:18 — “A mass meeting usually requires no order of business, since, referring to the headings listed above, there is nothing but new business to be brought up (unless the meeting is one within a series).”

**231.** `[C0064 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A mass meeting usually requires no order of business, since, _____ to the headings listed above, there is nothing but new business to be brought up (unless the meeting is one within a series).
   A. delegates
   B. paragraph
   C. presiding
   D. referring

   *Answer:* D) referring. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:18 — “A mass meeting usually requires no order of business, since, referring to the headings listed above, there is nothing but new business to be brought up (unless the meeting is one within a series).”

**232.** `[C0065 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The order of business of a convention is known as the _____, or the agenda, depending on whether it is interwoven with, or separate from, the overall schedule of convention meetings, events, etc.

See RONR (12th ed.) §3:19 — “The order of business of a convention is known as the program, or the agenda, depending on whether it is interwoven with, or separate from, the overall schedule of convention meetings, events, etc.”

**233.** `[C0065 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The order of business of a convention is known as the program, or the _____, depending on whether it is interwoven with, or separate from, the overall schedule of convention meetings, events, etc.
   A. agenda
   B. action
   C. beyond
   D. caucus

   D. convention

   *Answer:* D) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:19 — “A convention commonly adopts its own order of business— which often specifies the exact hours at which certain important questions are to be taken up.”

**236.** `[C0066 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ body usually has a more elaborate order of business suited to its own needs.

**237.** `[C0066 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ body usually has a more elaborate order of business suited to its own needs.

   B. motion
   C. ballot
   D. caucus

**239.** `[C0067 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Business is _____ before an assembly by the motion of a member.

   *Answer:* brought — “Business is brought before an assembly by the motion of a member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:21 — “Business is brought before an assembly by the motion of a member.”

**240.** `[C0067 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion may itself bring its subject to the assembly’s attention, or the motion may follow upon the presentation of a report or other _____.
   A. specification
   B. Announcements
   C. communication
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) communication. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:21 — “A motion may itself bring its subject to the assembly’s attention, or the motion may follow upon the presentation of a report or other communication.”

**241.** `[C0067 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion may itself bring its subject to the assembly’s attention, or the motion may follow upon the presentation of a report or other _____.

**242.** `[C0068 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion is a formal _____ by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.

   *Answer:* proposal — “A motion is a formal proposal by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:22 — “A motion is a formal proposal by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.”

**243.** `[C0068 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The proposed action may be of a substantive nature, or it may express a certain view or direct that a particular _____ be conducted and the findings be reported to the assembly for possible further action, or the like.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. Preparation
   C. investigation
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* C) investigation. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:22 — “The proposed action may be of a substantive nature, or it may express a certain view or direct that a particular investigation be conducted and the findings be reported to the assembly for possible further action, or the like.”

**244.** `[C0068 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion is a formal _____ by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.
   A. Standard
   B. assembly
   C. limiting
   D. proposal

   *Answer:* D) proposal. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:22 — “A motion is a formal proposal by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.”

**245.** `[C0068 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The proposed action may be of a substantive nature, or it may express a certain view or direct that a particular _____ be conducted and the findings be reported to the assembly for possible further action, or the like.

**246.** `[C0069 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The basic form of motion—the only one whose introduction brings business before the assembly—is a _____.

**247.** `[C0069 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While all of these motions propose some form of action and while all of them are said to be brought “before the assembly” when they are placed under consideration, most of them do not _____ before it in the sense described above—as a main motion does.
   A. Standing rules
   B. The Framing of
   C. standing rules
   D. bring business

See RONR (12th ed.) §3:23 — “While all of these motions propose some form of action and while all of them are said to be brought “before the assembly” when they are placed under consideration, most of them do not bring business before it in the sense described above—as a main motion does.”

**248.** `[C0069 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The basic form of motion—the only one whose _____ brings business before the assembly—is a main motion.

**249.** `[C0069 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: There are also many other separate _____ motions that have evolved for specific purposes.

   D. recommendation

**257.** `[C0071 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the presentation of the report of an officer, a board, or a committee, one or more motions to carry out _____ contained in the report may be introduced.

   D. adjournment

**269.** `[C0074 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reading of a _____ does not in itself formally bring a question before the assembly.

   *Answer:* communication — “The reading of a communication does not in itself formally bring a question before the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:28 — “The reading of a communication does not in itself formally bring a question before the assembly.”

**270.** `[C0074 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the reading, or at the time provided by the order of business, a motion can be offered proposing _____ action.
   A. Preparation
   B. Substitute
   C. appropriate
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) appropriate. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:28 — “After the reading, or at the time provided by the order of business, a motion can be offered proposing appropriate action.”

**271.** `[C0074 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no member feels that _____ needs to be done, the matter is dropped without a motion.

   *Answer:* anything — “If no member feels that anything needs to be done, the matter is dropped without a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:28 — “If no member feels that anything needs to be done, the matter is dropped without a motion.”

**272.** `[C0074 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The reading of a _____ does not in itself formally bring a question before the assembly.
   A. Announcements
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. recommendation
   D. communication

   *Answer:* D) communication. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:28 — “The reading of a communication does not in itself formally bring a question before the assembly.”

**273.** `[C0074 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the reading, or at the time provided by the order of business, a motion can be offered proposing _____ action.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “After the reading, or at the time provided by the order of business, a motion can be offered proposing appropriate action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:28 — “After the reading, or at the time provided by the order of business, a motion can be offered proposing appropriate action.”

**274.** `[C0075 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Business That Comes Up Without a Motion, Because of _____.

   *Answer:* Previous Action — “Business That Comes Up Without a Motion, Because of Previous Action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:29 — “Business That Comes Up Without a Motion, Because of Previous Action.”

**275.** `[C0075 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Business That Comes Up Without a Motion, Because of _____ Action.
   A. Previous
   B. Nominees
   C. dilatory
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* A) Previous. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:29 — “Business That Comes Up Without a Motion, Because of Previous Action.”

**276.** `[C0075 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Business may come up _____ at a certain time or at a certain point in the order of business, if it has previously been postponed (14) or otherwise made a general or special order (41).

   *Answer:* automatically — “Business may come up automatically at a certain time or at a certain point in the order of business, if it has previously been postponed (14) or otherwise made a general or special order (41).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:29 — “Business may come up automatically at a certain time or at a certain point in the order of business, if it has previously been postponed (14) or otherwise made a general or special order (41).”

**277.** `[C0075 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such cases, the business is announced at the proper time by the chair, and, if it has already been _____ in the form of a motion, no additional motion is made at that time.
   A. Convention
   B. introduced
   C. Reconsider
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) introduced. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:29 — “In such cases, the business is announced at the proper time by the chair, and, if it has already been introduced in the form of a motion, no additional motion is made at that time.”

**278.** `[C0075 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Business may come up _____ at a certain time or at a certain point in the order of business, if it has previously been postponed (14) or otherwise made a general or special order (41).
   A. characteristics
   B. specification
   C. Preparation
   D. automatically

   *Answer:* D) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:29 — “Business may come up automatically at a certain time or at a certain point in the order of business, if it has previously been postponed (14) or otherwise made a general or special order (41).”

**279.** `[C0076 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in _____—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;

   *Answer:* debate — “Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:30 — “Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;”

**280.** `[C0076 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must _____;
   A. limits of debate
   B. Types of Reports
   C. chairman pro tem
   D. obtain the floor

   *Answer:* D) obtain the floor. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:30 — “Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;”

**281.** `[C0076 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: that is, he must be _____ by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.

   *Answer:* recognized — “that is, he must be recognized by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:30 — “that is, he must be recognized by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.”

**282.** `[C0076 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: that is, he must be _____ by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.
   A. Particular
   B. sustaining
   C. resolution
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* D) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:30 — “that is, he must be recognized by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.”

**283.** `[C0077 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To claim the floor, a member rises at his place when no one else has the floor (or goes to a _____ in a large hall), faces the chair, and says, “Mr.
   A. Unfinished
   B. Substitute
   C. microphone
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* C) microphone. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:31 — “To claim the floor, a member rises at his place when no one else has the floor (or goes to a microphone in a large hall), faces the chair, and says, “Mr.”

**284.** `[C0077 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Chairman,” or “Madam Chairman,” or _____ is the chair’s proper title.

   *Answer:* whatever — “Chairman,” or “Madam Chairman,” or whatever is the chair’s proper title.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:31 — “Chairman,” or “Madam Chairman,” or whatever is the chair’s proper title.”

**285.** `[C0077 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the member is entitled to the floor at the time, the chair _____ him—normally by announcing, as applicable, the person’s name or title, or the place or unit that he represents.
   A. subsidiary
   B. recognizes
   C. authorizes
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) recognizes. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:31 — “If the member is entitled to the floor at the time, the chair recognizes him—normally by announcing, as applicable, the person’s name or title, or the place or unit that he represents.”

**286.** `[C0077 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This member then has the floor and can remain standing and speak in debate or make a motion as permitted under the rules in this book depending on the _____ situation at the time.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “This member then has the floor and can remain standing and speak in debate or make a motion as permitted under the rules in this book depending on the parliamentary situation at the time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:31 — “This member then has the floor and can remain standing and speak in debate or make a motion as permitted under the rules in this book depending on the parliamentary situation at the time.”

**287.** `[C0077 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If only one person is seeking the floor in a small meeting where all present know and can clearly see one another, the chair can _____ the member merely by nodding to him.
   A. president
   B. Privilege
   C. rejecting
   D. recognize

   *Answer:* D) recognize. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:31 — “If only one person is seeking the floor in a small meeting where all present know and can clearly see one another, the chair can recognize the member merely by nodding to him.”

**288.** `[C0078 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In principle, it is out of order to rise or be standing while another person has the floor —except for the purpose of making one of the motions or taking one of the _____ steps that can legitimately interrupt at such a time (pages t44–t45).

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “In principle, it is out of order to rise or be standing while another person has the floor —except for the purpose of making one of the motions or taking one of the parliamentary steps that can legitimately interrupt at such a time (pages t44–t45).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:32 — “In principle, it is out of order to rise or be standing while another person has the floor —except for the purpose of making one of the motions or taking one of the parliamentary steps that can legitimately interrupt at such a time (pages t44–t45).”

**289.** `[C0078 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a very large assembly, if members must walk some distance to _____, it may be necessary to vary from the preceding rule as dictated by conditions in the particular hall.
   A. Definitely
   B. microphones
   C. Unfinished
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) microphones. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:32 — “In a very large assembly, if members must walk some distance to microphones, it may be necessary to vary from the preceding rule as dictated by conditions in the particular hall.”

**290.** `[C0078 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In principle, it is out of order to rise or be standing while another person has the floor —except for the purpose of making one of the motions or taking one of the _____ steps that can legitimately interrupt at such a time (pages t44–t45).
   A. immediately
   B. parliamentary
   C. Information
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:32 — “In principle, it is out of order to rise or be standing while another person has the floor —except for the purpose of making one of the motions or taking one of the parliamentary steps that can legitimately interrupt at such a time (pages t44–t45).”

**291.** `[C0078 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a very large assembly, if members must walk some distance to _____, it may be necessary to vary from the preceding rule as dictated by conditions in the particular hall.

   *Answer:* microphones — “In a very large assembly, if members must walk some distance to microphones, it may be necessary to vary from the preceding rule as dictated by conditions in the particular hall.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:32 — “In a very large assembly, if members must walk some distance to microphones, it may be necessary to vary from the preceding rule as dictated by conditions in the particular hall.”

**292.** `[C0079 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While a motion is open to debate, there are three important cases where the floor is properly assigned to a person who may not have been the first to rise and address the chair (but who did so before anyone had actually been _____).

   *Answer:* recognized — “While a motion is open to debate, there are three important cases where the floor is properly assigned to a person who may not have been the first to rise and address the chair (but who did so before anyone had actually been recognized).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:33 — “While a motion is open to debate, there are three important cases where the floor is properly assigned to a person who may not have been the first to rise and address the chair (but who did so before anyone had actually been recognized).”

**293.** `[C0079 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While a motion is open to debate, there are three important cases where the floor is properly assigned to a person who may not have been the first to rise and address the chair (but who did so before anyone had actually been _____).
   A. recognized
   B. Convention
   C. privileged
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* A) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:33 — “While a motion is open to debate, there are three important cases where the floor is properly assigned to a person who may not have been the first to rise and address the chair (but who did so before anyone had actually been recognized).”

**294.** `[C0080 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming _____ (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.

   *Answer:* preference in being recognized — “A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming preference in being recognized (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:34 — “A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming preference in being recognized (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.”

**295.** `[C0080 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If at any time the chair makes a mistake, however, and assigns the floor to the wrong person—when preference in recognition was timely claimed or in any other case—his attention can be called to it by raising a _____ (23), and he must immediately correct the error.
   A. special orders
   B. stated meeting
   C. Point of Order
   D. Insert, or Add

   *Answer:* C) Point of Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:34 — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake, however, and assigns the floor to the wrong person—when preference in recognition was timely claimed or in any other case—his attention can be called to it by raising a Point of Order (23), and he must immediately correct the error.”

**296.** `[C0080 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming _____ in being recognized (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.

   *Answer:* preference — “A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming preference in being recognized (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:34 — “A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming preference in being recognized (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.”

**297.** `[C0080 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If at any time the chair makes a mistake, however, and assigns the floor to the wrong person—when preference in _____ was timely claimed or in any other case—his attention can be called to it by raising a Point of Order (23), and he must immediately correct the error.
   A. Organization
   B. recognition
   C. Permission
   D. convention

   *Answer:* B) recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:34 — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake, however, and assigns the floor to the wrong person—when preference in recognition was timely claimed or in any other case—his attention can be called to it by raising a Point of Order (23), and he must immediately correct the error.”

**298.** `[C0081 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ rules usually are adequate for assigning the floor in most business meetings.

   *Answer:* preceding — “The preceding rules usually are adequate for assigning the floor in most business meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:35 — “The preceding rules usually are adequate for assigning the floor in most business meetings.”

**299.** `[C0081 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In great assemblies or _____, or in bodies that must handle a heavy agenda or complex issues, additional situations often occur where the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.
   A. Organization
   B. conventions
   C. resolutions
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:35 — “In great assemblies or conventions, or in bodies that must handle a heavy agenda or complex issues, additional situations often occur where the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.”

**300.** `[C0081 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (For the rules _____ these cases, see 42.)

   *Answer:* governing — “(For the rules governing these cases, see 42.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:35 — “(For the rules governing these cases, see 42.)”

**301.** `[C0081 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ rules usually are adequate for assigning the floor in most business meetings.
   A. committee
   B. preceding
   C. rejecting
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* B) preceding. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:35 — “The preceding rules usually are adequate for assigning the floor in most business meetings.”

**302.** `[C0081 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In great assemblies or _____, or in bodies that must handle a heavy agenda or complex issues, additional situations often occur where the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.

   *Answer:* conventions — “In great assemblies or conventions, or in bodies that must handle a heavy agenda or complex issues, additional situations often occur where the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:35 — “In great assemblies or conventions, or in bodies that must handle a heavy agenda or complex issues, additional situations often occur where the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.”

**303.** `[C0082 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is _____, and three in the consideration of the motion.

   *Answer:* brought before the assembly — “In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the consideration of the motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:1 — “In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the consideration of the motion.”

**304.** `[C0082 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the _____ of the motion.
   A. Organization
   B. consideration
   C. Preparation
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:1 — “In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the consideration of the motion.”

**305.** `[C0082 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The handling of a motion varies in certain details according to _____.

   *Answer:* conditions — “The handling of a motion varies in certain details according to conditions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:1 — “The handling of a motion varies in certain details according to conditions.”

**306.** `[C0082 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The handling of a motion varies in certain details according to _____.
   A. authorizes
   B. Definitely
   C. Procedures
   D. conditions

   *Answer:* D) conditions. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:1 — “The handling of a motion varies in certain details according to conditions.”

**307.** `[C0083 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the chair has stated the question, the motion is _____, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other debatable parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).

   *Answer:* pending — “When the chair has stated the question, the motion is pending, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other debatable parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “When the chair has stated the question, the motion is pending, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other debatable parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).”

**308.** `[C0083 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the chair has stated the question, the motion is pending, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other _____ parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).
   A. debatable
   B. Objection
   C. auditors’
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* A) debatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “When the chair has stated the question, the motion is pending, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other debatable parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).”

**309.** `[C0083 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it _____ the motion, or the motion is carried;

   *Answer:* adopts — “If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is carried;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is carried;”

**310.** `[C0083 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is _____;
   A. carried
   B. Suspend
   C. Reports
   D. adjourn

   *Answer:* A) carried. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is carried;”

**311.** `[C0083 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: if the assembly expressly decides against doing what the motion proposes, the motion is _____, or rejected.

   *Answer:* lost — “if the assembly expressly decides against doing what the motion proposes, the motion is lost, or rejected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “if the assembly expressly decides against doing what the motion proposes, the motion is lost, or rejected.”

**312.** `[C0084 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[announcing what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a _____.
   A. Permission
   B. Convention
   C. recognized
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[announcing what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a resolution.”

**313.** `[C0084 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘_____, That…’”;

   *Answer:* Resolved — “The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”

**314.** `[C0084 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To make a main motion, a member must obtain the floor, as explained above, when no other question is pending and when business of the kind _____ by the motion is in order.
   A. Particular
   B. represented
   C. Reconsider
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* B) represented. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “To make a main motion, a member must obtain the floor, as explained above, when no other question is pending and when business of the kind represented by the motion is in order.”

**315.** `[C0084 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[_____ what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a resolution.

   *Answer:* announcing — “The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[announcing what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a resolution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[announcing what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a resolution.”

**316.** `[C0084 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, “I offer the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That…’” (For additional _____ on the proper form for main motions and resolutions, see 10.)
   A. constitution
   B. Reconsider
   C. resolutions
   D. information

   *Answer:* D) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “or, “I offer the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That…’” (For additional information on the proper form for main motions and resolutions, see 10.)”

**317.** `[C0085 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A resolution or a long or _____ motion should be prepared in advance of the meeting, if possible, and should be put into writing before it is offered.

   *Answer:* complicated — “A resolution or a long or complicated motion should be prepared in advance of the meeting, if possible, and should be put into writing before it is offered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:5 — “A resolution or a long or complicated motion should be prepared in advance of the meeting, if possible, and should be put into writing before it is offered.”

**318.** `[C0085 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A resolution or a long or complicated motion should be prepared in advance of the meeting, if possible, and should be put into writing before it is _____.
   A. Suspend
   B. offered
   C. Reports
   D. carried

   *Answer:* B) offered. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:5 — “A resolution or a long or complicated motion should be prepared in advance of the meeting, if possible, and should be put into writing before it is offered.”

**319.** `[C0085 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If conditions make it _____ for a member offering a written resolution to read it himself, he signs it and passes or sends it to the chair ahead of time (in a large meeting, often by page or messenger), or he can deliver it to the secretary before the meeting.

   *Answer:* impractical — “If conditions make it impractical for a member offering a written resolution to read it himself, he signs it and passes or sends it to the chair ahead of time (in a large meeting, often by page or messenger), or he can deliver it to the secretary before the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:5 — “If conditions make it impractical for a member offering a written resolution to read it himself, he signs it and passes or sends it to the chair ahead of time (in a large meeting, often by page or messenger), or he can deliver it to the secretary before the meeting.”

**320.** `[C0085 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case the member offers his resolution by saying, “I move the adoption of the resolution relating to…, which I have sent to the chair [or “have delivered to the Secretary”],” _____ it by its subject matter;
   A. Permission
   B. Reconsider
   C. Substitute
   D. identifying

   *Answer:* D) identifying. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:5 — “In such a case the member offers his resolution by saying, “I move the adoption of the resolution relating to…, which I have sent to the chair [or “have delivered to the Secretary”],” identifying it by its subject matter;”

**321.** `[C0085 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the text of the resolution or motion has been _____ to the members in advance, however, it need not be read when moved.
   A. distributed
   B. Convention
   C. undebatable
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) distributed. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:5 — “If the text of the resolution or motion has been distributed to the members in advance, however, it need not be read when moved.”

**322.** `[C0086 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As soon as a member has made a motion, he _____ his seat.

   *Answer:* resumes — “As soon as a member has made a motion, he resumes his seat.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:6 — “As soon as a member has made a motion, he resumes his seat.”

**323.** `[C0086 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion has not been heard or is not clear, another member can ask that it be repeated, which the chair can request the maker or the _____ to do, or can do himself.
   A. secretary
   B. auditors’
   C. secondary
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* A) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:6 — “If the motion has not been heard or is not clear, another member can ask that it be repeated, which the chair can request the maker or the secretary to do, or can do himself.”

**324.** `[C0086 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As soon as a member has made a motion, he _____ his seat.
   A. Session
   B. Inquiry
   C. resumes
   D. Adjourn

   *Answer:* C) resumes. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:6 — “As soon as a member has made a motion, he resumes his seat.”

**325.** `[C0086 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion has not been heard or is not clear, another member can ask that it be repeated, which the chair can request the maker or the _____ to do, or can do himself.

   *Answer:* secretary — “If the motion has not been heard or is not clear, another member can ask that it be repeated, which the chair can request the maker or the secretary to do, or can do himself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:6 — “If the motion has not been heard or is not clear, another member can ask that it be repeated, which the chair can request the maker or the secretary to do, or can do himself.”

**326.** `[C0087 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under _____ procedure, strictly speaking, discussion of any subject is permitted only with reference to a pending motion.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Under parliamentary procedure, strictly speaking, discussion of any subject is permitted only with reference to a pending motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:7 — “Under parliamentary procedure, strictly speaking, discussion of any subject is permitted only with reference to a pending motion.”

**327.** `[C0087 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When necessary, a motion can be prefaced by a few words of _____, which must not become a speech;
   A. interrupted
   B. authorizes
   C. explanation
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) explanation. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:7 — “When necessary, a motion can be prefaced by a few words of explanation, which must not become a speech;”

**328.** `[C0087 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or a member can first request _____, or he can indicate briefly what he wishes to propose and can ask the chair to assist him in wording an appropriate motion.

   *Answer:* information — “or a member can first request information, or he can indicate briefly what he wishes to propose and can ask the chair to assist him in wording an appropriate motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:7 — “or a member can first request information, or he can indicate briefly what he wishes to propose and can ask the chair to assist him in wording an appropriate motion.”

**329.** `[C0087 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In general, however, when a member has obtained the floor while no motion is pending— unless it is for a special purpose, such as to ask a question— he makes a motion _____.
   A. Particular
   B. Substitute
   C. Preparation
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* D) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:7 — “In general, however, when a member has obtained the floor while no motion is pending— unless it is for a special purpose, such as to ask a question— he makes a motion immediately.”

**330.** `[C0087 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under _____ procedure, strictly speaking, discussion of any subject is permitted only with reference to a pending motion.
   A. requirement
   B. subsequently
   C. undebatable
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:7 — “Under parliamentary procedure, strictly speaking, discussion of any subject is permitted only with reference to a pending motion.”

**331.** `[C0088 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In larger assemblies, this rule requires firm _____.

   *Answer:* enforcement — “In larger assemblies, this rule requires firm enforcement.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:8 — “In larger assemblies, this rule requires firm enforcement.”

**332.** `[C0088 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In smaller meetings, it may sometimes be relaxed with _____ effect if the members are not accustomed to working under the standard rule.
   A. constructive
   B. undebatable
   C. subsequently
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) constructive. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:8 — “In smaller meetings, it may sometimes be relaxed with constructive effect if the members are not accustomed to working under the standard rule.”

**333.** `[C0088 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless the assembly has _____ authorized that a particular subject be discussed while no motion is pending, however, such a discussion can be entered into only at the sufferance of the chair or until a point of order is made;

   *Answer:* specifically — “Unless the assembly has specifically authorized that a particular subject be discussed while no motion is pending, however, such a discussion can be entered into only at the sufferance of the chair or until a point of order is made;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:8 — “Unless the assembly has specifically authorized that a particular subject be discussed while no motion is pending, however, such a discussion can be entered into only at the sufferance of the chair or until a point of order is made;”

**334.** `[C0088 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and in the latter case, the chair must _____ require that a motion be offered or the discussion cease.
   A. Substitute
   B. undebatable
   C. deliberative
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* D) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:8 — “and in the latter case, the chair must immediately require that a motion be offered or the discussion cease.”

**335.** `[C0088 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The general rule against discussion without a motion is one of _____ procedure’s powerful tools for keeping business “on track,” and an observance of its spirit can be an important factor in making even a very small meeting rapidly moving and interesting.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The general rule against discussion without a motion is one of parliamentary procedure’s powerful tools for keeping business “on track,” and an observance of its spirit can be an important factor in making even a very small meeting rapidly moving and interesting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:8 — “The general rule against discussion without a motion is one of parliamentary procedure’s powerful tools for keeping business “on track,” and an observance of its spirit can be an important factor in making even a very small meeting rapidly moving and interesting.”

**336.** `[C0089 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a motion has been made, another member who wishes it to be _____ says, “I second the motion,” or, “I second it,” or even, “Second!”—without obtaining the floor, and in small assemblies without rising.
   A. Unfinished
   B. considered
   C. landscaped
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:9 — “After a motion has been made, another member who wishes it to be considered says, “I second the motion,” or, “I second it,” or even, “Second!”—without obtaining the floor, and in small assemblies without rising.”

**337.** `[C0089 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In large assemblies, and especially in those where nonmembers may be seated in the hall, the seconder stands, and without waiting to be recognized states his name (with other _____, if appropriate) and says, “Mr.

   *Answer:* identification — “In large assemblies, and especially in those where nonmembers may be seated in the hall, the seconder stands, and without waiting to be recognized states his name (with other identification, if appropriate) and says, “Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:9 — “In large assemblies, and especially in those where nonmembers may be seated in the hall, the seconder stands, and without waiting to be recognized states his name (with other identification, if appropriate) and says, “Mr.”

**338.** `[C0089 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Chairman”], I second the motion.” In some _____, especially labor unions, the word “support” is used in place of “second.”
   A. organizations
   B. Indefinitely
   C. specification
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* A) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:9 — “Chairman”], I second the motion.” In some organizations, especially labor unions, the word “support” is used in place of “second.””

**339.** `[C0089 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a motion has been made, another member who wishes it to be _____ says, “I second the motion,” or, “I second it,” or even, “Second!”—without obtaining the floor, and in small assemblies without rising.

   *Answer:* considered — “After a motion has been made, another member who wishes it to be considered says, “I second the motion,” or, “I second it,” or even, “Second!”—without obtaining the floor, and in small assemblies without rising.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:9 — “After a motion has been made, another member who wishes it to be considered says, “I second the motion,” or, “I second it,” or even, “Second!”—without obtaining the floor, and in small assemblies without rising.”

**340.** `[C0089 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In large assemblies, and especially in those where nonmembers may be seated in the hall, the seconder stands, and without waiting to be recognized states his name (with other _____, if appropriate) and says, “Mr.
   A. identification
   B. Vice-President
   C. specification
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* A) identification. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:9 — “In large assemblies, and especially in those where nonmembers may be seated in the hall, the seconder stands, and without waiting to be recognized states his name (with other identification, if appropriate) and says, “Mr.”

**341.** `[C0090 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no member seconds the motion, the chair must be sure that all have heard it before _____ to other business.

   *Answer:* proceeding — “If no member seconds the motion, the chair must be sure that all have heard it before proceeding to other business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:10 — “If no member seconds the motion, the chair must be sure that all have heard it before proceeding to other business.”

**342.** `[C0090 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case the chair _____ asks, “Is there a second to the motion?” In a large hall he may repeat the motion before doing so.
   A. chairman
   B. Nominees
   C. normally
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* C) normally. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:10 — “In such a case the chair normally asks, “Is there a second to the motion?” In a large hall he may repeat the motion before doing so.”

**343.** `[C0090 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, if the text of the resolution has been _____ to the members in advance and was moved without being read, the chair may say, for example, “Miss A has moved the adoption of the resolution relating to…, as printed.

   *Answer:* distributed — “or, if the text of the resolution has been distributed to the members in advance and was moved without being read, the chair may say, for example, “Miss A has moved the adoption of the resolution relating to…, as printed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:10 — “or, if the text of the resolution has been distributed to the members in advance and was moved without being read, the chair may say, for example, “Miss A has moved the adoption of the resolution relating to…, as printed.”

**344.** `[C0090 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, if the text of the _____ has been distributed to the members in advance and was moved without being read, the chair may say, for example, “Miss A has moved the adoption of the resolution relating to…, as printed.
   A. Permission
   B. persuasive
   C. substitute
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:10 — “or, if the text of the resolution has been distributed to the members in advance and was moved without being read, the chair may say, for example, “Miss A has moved the adoption of the resolution relating to…, as printed.”

**345.** `[C0090 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, “Since there is no second, the motion is not before this meeting.” Then he _____ says, “The next item of business is…”;

   *Answer:* immediately — “or, “Since there is no second, the motion is not before this meeting.” Then he immediately says, “The next item of business is…”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:10 — “or, “Since there is no second, the motion is not before this meeting.” Then he immediately says, “The next item of business is…”;”

**346.** `[C0091 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should _____ and not that he necessarily favors the motion.

   *Answer:* come before the meeting — “A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should come before the meeting and not that he necessarily favors the motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:11 — “A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should come before the meeting and not that he necessarily favors the motion.”

**347.** `[C0091 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as indicated above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as _____ the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.
   A. exhausted
   B. Precedent
   C. rejecting
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* C) rejecting. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:11 — “A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as indicated above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as rejecting the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.”

**348.** `[C0091 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion made by direction of a board or duly appointed committee of the assembly requires no second from the floor (provided the subordinate group is composed of more than one person), since the motion’s introduction has been directed by a _____ vote within the board or committee and is therefore desired by at least two assembly members or elected or appointed persons to whose opinion the assembly is presumed to give weight regarding the board’s or committee’s concerns.

   *Answer:* majority — “A motion made by direction of a board or duly appointed committee of the assembly requires no second from the floor (provided the subordinate group is composed of more than one person), since the motion’s introduction has been directed by a majority vote within the board or committee and is therefore desired by at least two assembly members or elected or appointed persons to whose opinion the assembly is presumed to give weight regarding the board’s or committee’s concerns.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:11 — “A motion made by direction of a board or duly appointed committee of the assembly requires no second from the floor (provided the subordinate group is composed of more than one person), since the motion’s introduction has been directed by a majority vote within the board or committee and is therefore desired by at least two assembly members or elected or appointed persons to whose opinion the assembly is presumed to give weight regarding the board’s or committee’s concerns.”

**349.** `[C0091 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should come before the meeting and not that he _____ favors the motion.
   A. necessarily
   B. Preparation
   C. undebatable
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) necessarily. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:11 — “A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should come before the meeting and not that he necessarily favors the motion.”

**350.** `[C0091 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as _____ above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as rejecting the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.

   *Answer:* indicated — “A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as indicated above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as rejecting the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:11 — “A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as indicated above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as rejecting the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.”

**351.** `[C0092 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of a second is for the chair’s guidance whether to state the question on the motion, thus placing it before the assembly.

   *Answer:* requirement — “The requirement of a second is for the chair’s guidance whether to state the question on the motion, thus placing it before the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:12 — “The requirement of a second is for the chair’s guidance whether to state the question on the motion, thus placing it before the assembly.”

**352.** `[C0092 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Its purpose is to prevent time from being consumed by the assembly’s having to dispose of a motion that only one person wants to see _____.
   A. incidental
   B. Incidental
   C. introduced
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* C) introduced. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:12 — “Its purpose is to prevent time from being consumed by the assembly’s having to dispose of a motion that only one person wants to see introduced.”

**353.** `[C0092 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of a second is for the chair’s guidance whether to state the question on the motion, thus placing it before the assembly.
   A. requirement
   B. Information
   C. Incidental
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) requirement. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:12 — “The requirement of a second is for the chair’s guidance whether to state the question on the motion, thus placing it before the assembly.”

**354.** `[C0092 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Its purpose is to prevent time from being consumed by the assembly’s having to dispose of a motion that only one person wants to see _____.

   *Answer:* introduced — “Its purpose is to prevent time from being consumed by the assembly’s having to dispose of a motion that only one person wants to see introduced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:12 — “Its purpose is to prevent time from being consumed by the assembly’s having to dispose of a motion that only one person wants to see introduced.”

**355.** `[C0093 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a point of order should not be made only for the sake of form, if it is clear that more than _____ wishes to take up the motion.

   *Answer:* one member — “Such a point of order should not be made only for the sake of form, if it is clear that more than one member wishes to take up the motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:13 — “Such a point of order should not be made only for the sake of form, if it is clear that more than one member wishes to take up the motion.”

**356.** `[C0093 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In handling routine motions, less attention is paid to the _____ of a second.
   A. adjournment
   B. resolutions
   C. undebatable
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* D) requirement. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:13 — “In handling routine motions, less attention is paid to the requirement of a second.”

**357.** `[C0093 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair is certain that a motion meets with wide approval but members are slow in _____ it, he can state the question without waiting for a second.

   *Answer:* seconding — “If the chair is certain that a motion meets with wide approval but members are slow in seconding it, he can state the question without waiting for a second.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:13 — “If the chair is certain that a motion meets with wide approval but members are slow in seconding it, he can state the question without waiting for a second.”

**358.** `[C0093 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, until debate has begun in such a case—or, if there is no debate, until the chair begins to take the vote and any member has voted—a point of order (see 23) can be raised that the motion has not been _____;
   A. Standard
   B. Standing
   C. seconded
   D. Interest

   *Answer:* C) seconded. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:13 — “However, until debate has begun in such a case—or, if there is no debate, until the chair begins to take the vote and any member has voted—a point of order (see 23) can be raised that the motion has not been seconded;”

**359.** `[C0093 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and then the chair must proceed _____ and ask if there is a second.

   *Answer:* formally — “and then the chair must proceed formally and ask if there is a second.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:13 — “and then the chair must proceed formally and ask if there is a second.”

**360.** `[C0094 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (For lists of certain _____ motions that do not require a second, see pages t44–t45.)

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “(For lists of certain parliamentary motions that do not require a second, see pages t44–t45.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:14 — “(For lists of certain parliamentary motions that do not require a second, see pages t44–t45.)”

**361.** `[C0094 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For lists of certain _____ motions that do not require a second, see pages t44–t45.)
   A. Announcements
   B. specification
   C. parliamentary
   D. Information

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:14 — “(For lists of certain parliamentary motions that do not require a second, see pages t44–t45.)”

**362.** `[C0095 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by _____;

   *Answer:* stating the question — “When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by stating the question;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:15 — “When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by stating the question;”

**363.** `[C0095 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of the Question by the Chair.
   A. advance
   B. approve
   C. Another
   D. Stating

   *Answer:* D) Stating. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:15 — “The Stating of the Question by the Chair.”

**364.** `[C0095 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion that is in order has been made and _____, the chair formally places it before the assembly by stating the question;

   *Answer:* seconded — “When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by stating the question;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:15 — “When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by stating the question;”

**365.** `[C0095 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: that is, he states the exact motion and indicates that it is open to debate (and certain other _____ processes to be explained in 5 and 6) in the manner indicated below as appropriate to the case:
   A. parliamentary
   B. requirement
   C. specification
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:15 — “that is, he states the exact motion and indicates that it is open to debate (and certain other parliamentary processes to be explained in 5 and 6) in the manner indicated below as appropriate to the case:”

**366.** `[C0095 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of the Question by the Chair.

   *Answer:* Stating — “The Stating of the Question by the Chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:15 — “The Stating of the Question by the Chair.”

**367.** `[C0096 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In principle, the chair must state the question on a motion _____ after it has been made and seconded, unless he is obliged to rule that the motion is not in order or unless, in his opinion, the wording is not clear.

   *Answer:* immediately — “In principle, the chair must state the question on a motion immediately after it has been made and seconded, unless he is obliged to rule that the motion is not in order or unless, in his opinion, the wording is not clear.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:16 — “In principle, the chair must state the question on a motion immediately after it has been made and seconded, unless he is obliged to rule that the motion is not in order or unless, in his opinion, the wording is not clear.”

**368.** `[C0096 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In principle, the chair must state the question on a motion _____ after it has been made and seconded, unless he is obliged to rule that the motion is not in order or unless, in his opinion, the wording is not clear.
   A. requirement
   B. Substitute
   C. immediately
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:16 — “In principle, the chair must state the question on a motion immediately after it has been made and seconded, unless he is obliged to rule that the motion is not in order or unless, in his opinion, the wording is not clear.”

**369.** `[C0097 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which _____ be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.

   *Answer:* would — “When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.”

**370.** `[C0097 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a _____ who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.
   A. member
   B. amends
   C. Speech
   D. adhere

   *Answer:* A) member. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.”

**371.** `[C0097 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: also 61.) If the chair rules that a motion is not in order, his decision is subject to an _____ to the judgment of the assembly.

   *Answer:* appeal — “also 61.) If the chair rules that a motion is not in order, his decision is subject to an appeal to the judgment of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “also 61.) If the chair rules that a motion is not in order, his decision is subject to an appeal to the judgment of the assembly.”

**372.** `[C0097 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules and _____ relating to the conditions under which various motions are not in order will be found particularly in 5, 6, and 7;
   A. Announcements
   B. explanations
   C. subsequently
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) explanations. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “Rules and explanations relating to the conditions under which various motions are not in order will be found particularly in 5, 6, and 7;”

**373.** `[C0097 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and in the first three of the “Standard Descriptive _____” given in the sections on each of the parliamentary motions (11–37).

   *Answer:* Characteristics — “and in the first three of the “Standard Descriptive Characteristics” given in the sections on each of the parliamentary motions (11–37).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “and in the first three of the “Standard Descriptive Characteristics” given in the sections on each of the parliamentary motions (11–37).”

**374.** `[C0098 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing _____ it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the satisfaction of the mover— before the question is stated.

   *Answer:* before — “If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing before it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the satisfaction of the mover— before the question is stated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:18 — “If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing before it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the satisfaction of the mover— before the question is stated.”

**375.** `[C0098 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing before it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the _____ of the mover— before the question is stated.
   A. Definitely
   B. consideration
   C. constitution
   D. satisfaction

   *Answer:* D) satisfaction. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:18 — “If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing before it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the satisfaction of the mover— before the question is stated.”

**376.** `[C0098 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair must never admit a motion that the secretary would have to _____ for the record.

   *Answer:* paraphrase — “The chair must never admit a motion that the secretary would have to paraphrase for the record.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:18 — “The chair must never admit a motion that the secretary would have to paraphrase for the record.”

**377.** `[C0098 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair—either on his own initiative or at the secretary’s request—can require any main motion (10), amendment (12), or _____ to a committee to be in writing before he states the question.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. Indefinitely
   C. instructions
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:18 — “The chair—either on his own initiative or at the secretary’s request—can require any main motion (10), amendment (12), or instructions to a committee to be in writing before he states the question.”

**378.** `[C0098 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair must never admit a motion that the secretary would have to _____ for the record.
   A. recognized
   B. paraphrase
   C. landscaped
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) paraphrase. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:18 — “The chair must never admit a motion that the secretary would have to paraphrase for the record.”

**379.** `[C0099 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of _____ (12) before acting upon it.

   *Answer:* amendment — “but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of amendment (12) before acting upon it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:19 — “but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of amendment (12) before acting upon it.”

**380.** `[C0099 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Until the chair states the question, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to _____ it entirely.
   A. withdraw
   B. assembly
   C. dilatory
   D. decision

   *Answer:* A) withdraw. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:19 — “Until the chair states the question, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to withdraw it entirely.”

**381.** `[C0099 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of _____ (12) before acting upon it.
   A. Secretary
   B. Privilege
   C. amendment
   D. rejecting

   *Answer:* C) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:19 — “but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of amendment (12) before acting upon it.”

**382.** `[C0099 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Until the chair states the question, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to _____ it entirely.

   *Answer:* withdraw — “Until the chair states the question, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to withdraw it entirely.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:19 — “Until the chair states the question, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to withdraw it entirely.”

**383.** `[C0100 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At such a time, however, any member can quickly rise and, without waiting to be _____, can say, “Mr.

   *Answer:* recognized — “At such a time, however, any member can quickly rise and, without waiting to be recognized, can say, “Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:20 — “At such a time, however, any member can quickly rise and, without waiting to be recognized, can say, “Mr.”

**384.** `[C0100 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: President, I would like to ask the maker of the motion if he will accept the following _____:… [or, “… if he would be willing to change the words… to…”].” The maker then answers, “Mr.
   A. consideration
   B. immediately
   C. Permission
   D. modification

   *Answer:* D) modification. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:20 — “President, I would like to ask the maker of the motion if he will accept the following modification:… [or, “… if he would be willing to change the words… to…”].” The maker then answers, “Mr.”

**385.** `[C0100 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: President, I will modify the motion as _____:

   *Answer:* follows — “President, I will modify the motion as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:20 — “President, I will modify the motion as follows:”

**386.** `[C0100 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At such a time, however, any member can quickly rise and, without waiting to be _____, can say, “Mr.
   A. Substitute
   B. Unfinished
   C. introduced
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* D) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:20 — “At such a time, however, any member can quickly rise and, without waiting to be recognized, can say, “Mr.”

**387.** `[C0100 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: President, I would like to ask the maker of the motion if he will accept the following _____:… [or, “… if he would be willing to change the words… to…”].” The maker then answers, “Mr.

   *Answer:* modification — “President, I would like to ask the maker of the motion if he will accept the following modification:… [or, “… if he would be willing to change the words… to…”].” The maker then answers, “Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:20 — “President, I would like to ask the maker of the motion if he will accept the following modification:… [or, “… if he would be willing to change the words… to…”].” The maker then answers, “Mr.”

**388.** `[C0101 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if a modification is accepted _____ another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.

   *Answer:* as suggested by — “but if a modification is accepted as suggested by another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:21 — “but if a modification is accepted as suggested by another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.”

**389.** `[C0101 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but if a modification is accepted as suggested by _____—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.
   A. special orders
   B. Other Officers
   C. another member
   D. Conditions for

   *Answer:* C) another member. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:21 — “but if a modification is accepted as suggested by another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.”
