**390.** `[C0101 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the maker of a motion _____ it before the question is stated, a person who has seconded it has the right to withdraw his second;

   *Answer:* modifies — “If the maker of a motion modifies it before the question is stated, a person who has seconded it has the right to withdraw his second;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:21 — “If the maker of a motion modifies it before the question is stated, a person who has seconded it has the right to withdraw his second;”

**391.** `[C0101 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under any _____ where a second is withdrawn but it is clear that another member favors consideration of the motion in its modified form, the chair treats the motion as seconded.

   *Answer:* circumstances — “Under any circumstances where a second is withdrawn but it is clear that another member favors consideration of the motion in its modified form, the chair treats the motion as seconded.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:21 — “Under any circumstances where a second is withdrawn but it is clear that another member favors consideration of the motion in its modified form, the chair treats the motion as seconded.”

**392.** `[C0101 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the maker makes any change in his motion and it remains, in effect, seconded, or (if necessary) is then seconded, the chair says, “It is moved and seconded…,” stating the question on the modified motion just as if it had been so moved _____.
   A. originally
   B. Incidental
   C. Substitute
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) originally. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:21 — “If the maker makes any change in his motion and it remains, in effect, seconded, or (if necessary) is then seconded, the chair says, “It is moved and seconded…,” stating the question on the modified motion just as if it had been so moved originally.”

**393.** `[C0102 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This procedure for modifying a motion before the question is stated is useful primarily for quickly handling simple, _____ changes of the type that probably would not generate debate among the members present if proposed as amendments to a pending motion.

   *Answer:* uncontroversial — “This procedure for modifying a motion before the question is stated is useful primarily for quickly handling simple, uncontroversial changes of the type that probably would not generate debate among the members present if proposed as amendments to a pending motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:22 — “This procedure for modifying a motion before the question is stated is useful primarily for quickly handling simple, uncontroversial changes of the type that probably would not generate debate among the members present if proposed as amendments to a pending motion.”

**394.** `[C0102 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This procedure for modifying a motion before the question is stated is useful primarily for quickly handling simple, _____ changes of the type that probably would not generate debate among the members present if proposed as amendments to a pending motion.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. uncontroversial
   C. Announcements
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) uncontroversial. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:22 — “This procedure for modifying a motion before the question is stated is useful primarily for quickly handling simple, uncontroversial changes of the type that probably would not generate debate among the members present if proposed as amendments to a pending motion.”

**395.** `[C0103 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a similar manner, before the question on a motion has been stated, any member who believes that the maker will _____ withdraw the motion if a certain fact is pointed out to him can quickly rise and say (without waiting for recognition), “Mr.

   *Answer:* immediately — “In a similar manner, before the question on a motion has been stated, any member who believes that the maker will immediately withdraw the motion if a certain fact is pointed out to him can quickly rise and say (without waiting for recognition), “Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:23 — “In a similar manner, before the question on a motion has been stated, any member who believes that the maker will immediately withdraw the motion if a certain fact is pointed out to him can quickly rise and say (without waiting for recognition), “Mr.”

**396.** `[C0103 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Chairman, I would like to ask if the member would be willing to withdraw his motion in view of… [briefly stating the reasons for the suggested _____].” The maker responds, “I withdraw [or “decline to withdraw”] the motion.” If the maker withdraws his motion, the chair says, “The motion is withdrawn,” and proceeds to the next business.
   A. Unfinished
   B. subsidiary
   C. settlement
   D. withdrawal

   *Answer:* D) withdrawal. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:23 — “Chairman, I would like to ask if the member would be willing to withdraw his motion in view of… [briefly stating the reasons for the suggested withdrawal].” The maker responds, “I withdraw [or “decline to withdraw”] the motion.” If the maker withdraws his motion, the chair says, “The motion is withdrawn,” and proceeds to the next business.”

**397.** `[C0103 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the maker is _____ to withdraw his motion, the chair says, “The member declines to withdraw the motion,” and (if the motion has been seconded) he then states the question.

   *Answer:* unwilling — “If the maker is unwilling to withdraw his motion, the chair says, “The member declines to withdraw the motion,” and (if the motion has been seconded) he then states the question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:23 — “If the maker is unwilling to withdraw his motion, the chair says, “The member declines to withdraw the motion,” and (if the motion has been seconded) he then states the question.”

**398.** `[C0103 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a similar manner, before the question on a motion has been stated, any member who believes that the maker will _____ withdraw the motion if a certain fact is pointed out to him can quickly rise and say (without waiting for recognition), “Mr.
   A. immediately
   B. constitution
   C. Preparation
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:23 — “In a similar manner, before the question on a motion has been stated, any member who believes that the maker will immediately withdraw the motion if a certain fact is pointed out to him can quickly rise and say (without waiting for recognition), “Mr.”

**399.** `[C0103 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Chairman, I would like to ask if the member would be willing to withdraw his motion in view of… [briefly stating the reasons for the suggested _____].” The maker responds, “I withdraw [or “decline to withdraw”] the motion.” If the maker withdraws his motion, the chair says, “The motion is withdrawn,” and proceeds to the next business.

   *Answer:* withdrawal — “Chairman, I would like to ask if the member would be willing to withdraw his motion in view of… [briefly stating the reasons for the suggested withdrawal].” The maker responds, “I withdraw [or “decline to withdraw”] the motion.” If the maker withdraws his motion, the chair says, “The motion is withdrawn,” and proceeds to the next business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:23 — “Chairman, I would like to ask if the member would be willing to withdraw his motion in view of… [briefly stating the reasons for the suggested withdrawal].” The maker responds, “I withdraw [or “decline to withdraw”] the motion.” If the maker withdraws his motion, the chair says, “The motion is withdrawn,” and proceeds to the next business.”

**400.** `[C0104 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before the question is stated no debate may accompany _____ that a motion be modified or withdrawn.

   *Answer:* suggestions — “Before the question is stated no debate may accompany suggestions that a motion be modified or withdrawn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:24 — “Before the question is stated no debate may accompany suggestions that a motion be modified or withdrawn.”

**401.** `[C0104 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Time can often be saved, however, by brief informal _____—which the chair has discretion to permit, provided that he or she is careful to see that it does not develop into an extended colloquy between members or take on the semblance of debate.
   A. Organization
   B. resolutions
   C. consultation
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) consultation. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:24 — “Time can often be saved, however, by brief informal consultation—which the chair has discretion to permit, provided that he or she is careful to see that it does not develop into an extended colloquy between members or take on the semblance of debate.”

**402.** `[C0104 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before the question is stated no debate may accompany _____ that a motion be modified or withdrawn.
   A. resolutions
   B. assemblies
   C. suggestions
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) suggestions. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:24 — “Before the question is stated no debate may accompany suggestions that a motion be modified or withdrawn.”

**403.** `[C0104 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Time can often be saved, however, by brief informal _____—which the chair has discretion to permit, provided that he or she is careful to see that it does not develop into an extended colloquy between members or take on the semblance of debate.

   *Answer:* consultation — “Time can often be saved, however, by brief informal consultation—which the chair has discretion to permit, provided that he or she is careful to see that it does not develop into an extended colloquy between members or take on the semblance of debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:24 — “Time can often be saved, however, by brief informal consultation—which the chair has discretion to permit, provided that he or she is careful to see that it does not develop into an extended colloquy between members or take on the semblance of debate.”

**404.** `[C0106 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, while the motion is open to debate, the assembly may wish to take a number of actions as a part of the motion’s _____—which can themselves be the subject of certain parliamentary motions, as explained in 5 and 6.

   *Answer:* consideration — “In addition, while the motion is open to debate, the assembly may wish to take a number of actions as a part of the motion’s consideration—which can themselves be the subject of certain parliamentary motions, as explained in 5 and 6.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:26 — “In addition, while the motion is open to debate, the assembly may wish to take a number of actions as a part of the motion’s consideration—which can themselves be the subject of certain parliamentary motions, as explained in 5 and 6.”

**405.** `[C0106 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition, while the motion is open to debate, the assembly may wish to take a number of actions as a part of the motion’s _____—which can themselves be the subject of certain parliamentary motions, as explained in 5 and 6.
   A. constitution
   B. president-elect
   C. representatives
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:26 — “In addition, while the motion is open to debate, the assembly may wish to take a number of actions as a part of the motion’s consideration—which can themselves be the subject of certain parliamentary motions, as explained in 5 and 6.”

**406.** `[C0107 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ after stating the question, the chair turns toward the maker of the motion to see if he wishes to be assigned the floor first in debate—to which the maker has the right if he claims it before anyone else has been recognized, even though others may have risen and addressed the chair first.
   A. Permission
   B. committees
   C. Immediately
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) Immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:27 — “Immediately after stating the question, the chair turns toward the maker of the motion to see if he wishes to be assigned the floor first in debate—to which the maker has the right if he claims it before anyone else has been recognized, even though others may have risen and addressed the chair first.”

**407.** `[C0107 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ after stating the question, the chair turns toward the maker of the motion to see if he wishes to be assigned the floor first in debate—to which the maker has the right if he claims it before anyone else has been recognized, even though others may have risen and addressed the chair first.

   *Answer:* Immediately — “Immediately after stating the question, the chair turns toward the maker of the motion to see if he wishes to be assigned the floor first in debate—to which the maker has the right if he claims it before anyone else has been recognized, even though others may have risen and addressed the chair first.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:27 — “Immediately after stating the question, the chair turns toward the maker of the motion to see if he wishes to be assigned the floor first in debate—to which the maker has the right if he claims it before anyone else has been recognized, even though others may have risen and addressed the chair first.”

**408.** `[C0108 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In assigning the floor, the chair follows the rules explained in the same _____ and in 42.

   *Answer:* paragraphs — “In assigning the floor, the chair follows the rules explained in the same paragraphs and in 42.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:28 — “In assigning the floor, the chair follows the rules explained in the same paragraphs and in 42.”

**409.** `[C0108 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the debate, each member has the right to speak twice on the same question on the same day, but cannot make a second speech on the same question so long as any member who has not spoken on that question _____ the floor.
   A. desires
   B. Inquiry
   C. Example
   D. abstain

   *Answer:* A) desires. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:28 — “In the debate, each member has the right to speak twice on the same question on the same day, but cannot make a second speech on the same question so long as any member who has not spoken on that question desires the floor.”

**410.** `[C0108 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member who has spoken twice on a _____ question on the same day has exhausted his right to debate that question for that day.

   *Answer:* particular — “A member who has spoken twice on a particular question on the same day has exhausted his right to debate that question for that day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:28 — “A member who has spoken twice on a particular question on the same day has exhausted his right to debate that question for that day.”

**411.** `[C0108 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In assigning the floor, the chair follows the rules explained in the same _____ and in 42.
   A. Particular
   B. settlement
   C. paragraphs
   D. committees

   *Answer:* C) paragraphs. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:28 — “In assigning the floor, the chair follows the rules explained in the same paragraphs and in 42.”

**412.** `[C0108 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the debate, each member has the right to speak twice on the same question on the same day, but cannot make a second speech on the same question so long as any member who has not spoken on that question _____ the floor.

   *Answer:* desires — “In the debate, each member has the right to speak twice on the same question on the same day, but cannot make a second speech on the same question so long as any member who has not spoken on that question desires the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:28 — “In the debate, each member has the right to speak twice on the same question on the same day, but cannot make a second speech on the same question so long as any member who has not spoken on that question desires the floor.”

**413.** `[C0109 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Without the permission of the assembly, no one can speak longer than permitted by the rules of the body—or, in a nonlegislative assembly that has no rule of its own relating to the length of speeches, longer than _____.

   *Answer:* ten minutes — “Without the permission of the assembly, no one can speak longer than permitted by the rules of the body—or, in a nonlegislative assembly that has no rule of its own relating to the length of speeches, longer than ten minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:29 — “Without the permission of the assembly, no one can speak longer than permitted by the rules of the body—or, in a nonlegislative assembly that has no rule of its own relating to the length of speeches, longer than ten minutes.”

**414.** `[C0109 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Without the permission of the assembly, no one can speak longer than permitted by the rules of the body—or, in a _____ assembly that has no rule of its own relating to the length of speeches, longer than ten minutes.
   A. Responsibilities
   B. requirement
   C. nonlegislative
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* C) nonlegislative. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:29 — “Without the permission of the assembly, no one can speak longer than permitted by the rules of the body—or, in a nonlegislative assembly that has no rule of its own relating to the length of speeches, longer than ten minutes.”

**415.** `[C0110 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debate must be _____ to the merits of the pending question.

   *Answer:* confined — “Debate must be confined to the merits of the pending question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:30 — “Debate must be confined to the merits of the pending question.”

**416.** `[C0110 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Speakers must address their remarks to the chair, maintain a courteous tone, and—_____ in reference to any divergence of opinion—avoid injecting a personal note into debate.
   A. convention
   B. especially
   C. Permission
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) especially. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:30 — “Speakers must address their remarks to the chair, maintain a courteous tone, and—especially in reference to any divergence of opinion—avoid injecting a personal note into debate.”

**417.** `[C0110 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To this end, they must never attack or make any _____ to the motives of members.

   *Answer:* allusion — “To this end, they must never attack or make any allusion to the motives of members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:30 — “To this end, they must never attack or make any allusion to the motives of members.”

**418.** `[C0110 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ must address their remarks to the chair, maintain a courteous tone, and—especially in reference to any divergence of opinion—avoid injecting a personal note into debate.
   A. Previous
   B. Speakers
   C. Approved
   D. Business

   *Answer:* B) Speakers. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:30 — “Speakers must address their remarks to the chair, maintain a courteous tone, and—especially in reference to any divergence of opinion—avoid injecting a personal note into debate.”

**419.** `[C0110 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Debate must be _____ to the merits of the pending question.
   A. division
   B. Nominees
   C. confined
   D. decision

   *Answer:* C) confined. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:30 — “Debate must be confined to the merits of the pending question.”

**420.** `[C0111 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While members are speaking in debate, the presiding officer normally remains seated unless the view between him and the members would be _____.

   *Answer:* obstructed — “While members are speaking in debate, the presiding officer normally remains seated unless the view between him and the members would be obstructed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:31 — “While members are speaking in debate, the presiding officer normally remains seated unless the view between him and the members would be obstructed.”

**421.** `[C0111 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the latter case, he should step back _____ while a member is speaking.
   A. slightly
   B. Interest
   C. Standard
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* A) slightly. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:31 — “In the latter case, he should step back slightly while a member is speaking.”

**422.** `[C0111 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While members are speaking in debate, the _____ officer normally remains seated unless the view between him and the members would be obstructed.

   *Answer:* presiding — “While members are speaking in debate, the presiding officer normally remains seated unless the view between him and the members would be obstructed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:31 — “While members are speaking in debate, the presiding officer normally remains seated unless the view between him and the members would be obstructed.”

**423.** `[C0111 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the latter case, he should step back _____ while a member is speaking.

   *Answer:* slightly — “In the latter case, he should step back slightly while a member is speaking.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:31 — “In the latter case, he should step back slightly while a member is speaking.”

**424.** `[C0112 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ officer cannot close debate so long as any member who has not exhausted his right to debate desires the floor, except by order of the assembly, which requires a two- thirds vote (15, 16, 43).

   *Answer:* presiding — “The presiding officer cannot close debate so long as any member who has not exhausted his right to debate desires the floor, except by order of the assembly, which requires a two- thirds vote (15, 16, 43).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:32 — “The presiding officer cannot close debate so long as any member who has not exhausted his right to debate desires the floor, except by order of the assembly, which requires a two- thirds vote (15, 16, 43).”

**425.** `[C0112 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ officer cannot close debate so long as any member who has not exhausted his right to debate desires the floor, except by order of the assembly, which requires a two- thirds vote (15, 16, 43).
   A. presiding
   B. expressly
   C. exhausted
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* A) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:32 — “The presiding officer cannot close debate so long as any member who has not exhausted his right to debate desires the floor, except by order of the assembly, which requires a two- thirds vote (15, 16, 43).”

**426.** `[C0113 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except as it may be corrected in response to such a point of order, the exact wording the chair uses in _____ is definitive, and the wording in the minutes must be the same.

   *Answer:* putting the question — “Except as it may be corrected in response to such a point of order, the exact wording the chair uses in putting the question is definitive, and the wording in the minutes must be the same.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:34 — “Except as it may be corrected in response to such a point of order, the exact wording the chair uses in putting the question is definitive, and the wording in the minutes must be the same.”

**427.** `[C0113 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the debate appears to have closed, the chair may ask, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any further debate?” If no one then rises to claim the floor, the chair _____ to put the question—that is, he puts it to a vote after once more making clear the exact question the assembly is called upon to decide.
   A. chairman
   B. proceeds
   C. Postpone
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* B) proceeds. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:34 — “When the debate appears to have closed, the chair may ask, “Are you ready for the question?” or “Is there any further debate?” If no one then rises to claim the floor, the chair proceeds to put the question—that is, he puts it to a vote after once more making clear the exact question the assembly is called upon to decide.”

**428.** `[C0113 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair’s wording of the question is _____, a point of order may be made until any member has actually voted.

   *Answer:* erroneous — “If the chair’s wording of the question is erroneous, a point of order may be made until any member has actually voted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:34 — “If the chair’s wording of the question is erroneous, a point of order may be made until any member has actually voted.”

**429.** `[C0113 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except as it may be corrected in response to such a point of order, the exact wording the chair uses in putting the question is _____, and the wording in the minutes must be the same.
   A. Definitely
   B. authorizes
   C. definitive
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) definitive. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:34 — “Except as it may be corrected in response to such a point of order, the exact wording the chair uses in putting the question is definitive, and the wording in the minutes must be the same.”

**430.** `[C0113 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Where there is any _____ of confusion, the chair, before calling for the vote, should make sure that the members understand the effect of an “aye” vote and of a “no” vote.

   *Answer:* possibility — “Where there is any possibility of confusion, the chair, before calling for the vote, should make sure that the members understand the effect of an “aye” vote and of a “no” vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:34 — “Where there is any possibility of confusion, the chair, before calling for the vote, should make sure that the members understand the effect of an “aye” vote and of a “no” vote.”

**431.** `[C0114 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The vote on a motion is normally taken by _____ (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.

   *Answer:* voice — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:35 — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”

**432.** `[C0114 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or _____ voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.
   A. legal
   B. Rules
   C. clerk
   D. viva

   *Answer:* D) viva. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:35 — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”

**433.** `[C0114 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair must always call for the negative vote, no matter how nearly _____ the affirmative vote may appear, except that this rule is commonly relaxed in the case of noncontroversial motions of a complimentary or courtesy nature;
   A. A majority vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* C) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:35 — “The chair must always call for the negative vote, no matter how nearly unanimous the affirmative vote may appear, except that this rule is commonly relaxed in the case of noncontroversial motions of a complimentary or courtesy nature;”

**434.** `[C0114 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair must always call for the negative vote, no matter how nearly _____ the affirmative vote may appear, except that this rule is commonly relaxed in the case of noncontroversial motions of a complimentary or courtesy nature;

   *Answer:* unanimous — “The chair must always call for the negative vote, no matter how nearly unanimous the affirmative vote may appear, except that this rule is commonly relaxed in the case of noncontroversial motions of a complimentary or courtesy nature;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:35 — “The chair must always call for the negative vote, no matter how nearly unanimous the affirmative vote may appear, except that this rule is commonly relaxed in the case of noncontroversial motions of a complimentary or courtesy nature;”

**435.** `[C0115 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The three methods of putting the question stated in the _____ paragraph, as well as the forms used when a vote taken by rising or by a show of hands is counted, are described below.
   A. expressly
   B. preceding
   C. amendment
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* B) preceding. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:36 — “The three methods of putting the question stated in the preceding paragraph, as well as the forms used when a vote taken by rising or by a show of hands is counted, are described below.”

**436.** `[C0115 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The three methods of putting the question stated in the _____ paragraph, as well as the forms used when a vote taken by rising or by a show of hands is counted, are described below.

   *Answer:* preceding — “The three methods of putting the question stated in the preceding paragraph, as well as the forms used when a vote taken by rising or by a show of hands is counted, are described below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:36 — “The three methods of putting the question stated in the preceding paragraph, as well as the forms used when a vote taken by rising or by a show of hands is counted, are described below.”

**437.** `[C0116 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Those in favor of adopting the resolution that was just read, say _____.… Those opposed, say no.” If the resolution has been read very recently and there appears to be no desire to have it read again, the chair may use this form: “The question is on the adoption of the resolution last read.

   *Answer:* aye — “Those in favor of adopting the resolution that was just read, say aye.… Those opposed, say no.” If the resolution has been read very recently and there appears to be no desire to have it read again, the chair may use this form: “The question is on the adoption of the resolution last read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:37 — “Those in favor of adopting the resolution that was just read, say aye.… Those opposed, say no.” If the resolution has been read very recently and there appears to be no desire to have it read again, the chair may use this form: “The question is on the adoption of the resolution last read.”

**438.** `[C0116 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In taking a voice vote, the chair puts the question by saying, “The question is on the adoption of the motion to [or “that”]… [repeating or clearly _____ the motion].

   *Answer:* identifying — “In taking a voice vote, the chair puts the question by saying, “The question is on the adoption of the motion to [or “that”]… [repeating or clearly identifying the motion].”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:37 — “In taking a voice vote, the chair puts the question by saying, “The question is on the adoption of the motion to [or “that”]… [repeating or clearly identifying the motion].”

**439.** `[C0116 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: [Pausing for response.]… Those opposed, say no.” (_____ forms are: “All those in favor…”;
   A. resolutions
   B. assemblies
   C. Alternative
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) Alternative. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:37 — “[Pausing for response.]… Those opposed, say no.” (Alternative forms are: “All those in favor…”;”

**440.** `[C0116 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or the wording formerly _____ in Congress, “As many as are in favor…”) In the case of a resolution, the question may be put as follows: “The question is on the adoption of the following resolution: [reading it].

   *Answer:* prescribed — “or the wording formerly prescribed in Congress, “As many as are in favor…”) In the case of a resolution, the question may be put as follows: “The question is on the adoption of the following resolution: [reading it].”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:37 — “or the wording formerly prescribed in Congress, “As many as are in favor…”) In the case of a resolution, the question may be put as follows: “The question is on the adoption of the following resolution: [reading it].”

**441.** `[C0116 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or the wording formerly prescribed in Congress, “As many as are in favor…”) In the case of a _____, the question may be put as follows: “The question is on the adoption of the following resolution: [reading it].
   A. sustaining
   B. convention
   C. resolution
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* C) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:37 — “or the wording formerly prescribed in Congress, “As many as are in favor…”) In the case of a resolution, the question may be put as follows: “The question is on the adoption of the following resolution: [reading it].”

**442.** `[C0117 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When only a majority is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote _____ inconclusive.

   *Answer:* will be — “When only a majority is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote will be inconclusive.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:38 — “When only a majority is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote will be inconclusive.”

**443.** `[C0117 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When only a _____ is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote will be inconclusive.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* D) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:38 — “When only a majority is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote will be inconclusive.”

**444.** `[C0117 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The simple rising vote (in which the number of members voting on each side is not counted) is used principally in cases where a voice vote has been taken with an inconclusive result, and as the normal method of voting on motions requiring a _____ vote for adoption (see Chair’s Announcement of the Voting Result, etc., below).

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “The simple rising vote (in which the number of members voting on each side is not counted) is used principally in cases where a voice vote has been taken with an inconclusive result, and as the normal method of voting on motions requiring a two-thirds vote for adoption (see Chair’s Announcement of the Voting Result, etc., below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:38 — “The simple rising vote (in which the number of members voting on each side is not counted) is used principally in cases where a voice vote has been taken with an inconclusive result, and as the normal method of voting on motions requiring a two-thirds vote for adoption (see Chair’s Announcement of the Voting Result, etc., below).”

**445.** `[C0117 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Form for taking a rising (_____) vote.
   A. Nominees
   B. Business
   C. Assembly
   D. division

   *Answer:* D) division. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:38 — “Form for taking a rising (division) vote.”

**446.** `[C0117 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The simple rising vote (in which the number of members voting on each side is not counted) is used principally in cases where a voice vote has been taken with an _____ result, and as the normal method of voting on motions requiring a two-thirds vote for adoption (see Chair’s Announcement of the Voting Result, etc., below).

   *Answer:* inconclusive — “The simple rising vote (in which the number of members voting on each side is not counted) is used principally in cases where a voice vote has been taken with an inconclusive result, and as the normal method of voting on motions requiring a two-thirds vote for adoption (see Chair’s Announcement of the Voting Result, etc., below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:38 — “The simple rising vote (in which the number of members voting on each side is not counted) is used principally in cases where a voice vote has been taken with an inconclusive result, and as the normal method of voting on motions requiring a two-thirds vote for adoption (see Chair’s Announcement of the Voting Result, etc., below).”

**447.** `[C0118 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The form then used is, for example: “The question is on the motion to limit all speeches at this meeting to _____.

   *Answer:* two minutes — “The form then used is, for example: “The question is on the motion to limit all speeches at this meeting to two minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:39 — “The form then used is, for example: “The question is on the motion to limit all speeches at this meeting to two minutes.”

**448.** `[C0118 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The form then used is, for example: “The question is on the motion to limit all _____ at this meeting to two minutes.
   A. Standard
   B. Assembly
   C. speeches
   D. Business

   *Answer:* C) speeches. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:39 — “The form then used is, for example: “The question is on the motion to limit all speeches at this meeting to two minutes.”

**449.** `[C0118 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Those in favor of the motion will rise and remain _____ until counted.… Be seated.

   *Answer:* standing — “Those in favor of the motion will rise and remain standing until counted.… Be seated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:39 — “Those in favor of the motion will rise and remain standing until counted.… Be seated.”

**450.** `[C0118 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Those in favor of the motion will rise and remain _____ until counted.… Be seated.
   A. meetings
   B. business
   C. assuming
   D. standing

   *Answer:* D) standing. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:39 — “Those in favor of the motion will rise and remain standing until counted.… Be seated.”

**451.** `[C0119 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an alternative to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some _____.

   *Answer:* assemblies — “As an alternative to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some assemblies.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:40 — “As an alternative to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some assemblies.”

**452.** `[C0119 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As an _____ to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some assemblies.
   A. alternative
   B. Reconsider
   C. convention
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) alternative. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:40 — “As an alternative to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some assemblies.”

**453.** `[C0119 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ voice vote is also sometimes verified by this method.

   *Answer:* inconclusive — “An inconclusive voice vote is also sometimes verified by this method.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:40 — “An inconclusive voice vote is also sometimes verified by this method.”

**454.** `[C0119 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In voting by this method, the question can be put, for example, as follows: “The question is on the motion that the bill for _____ repairs be paid as rendered.
   A. building
   B. Resolved
   C. adoption
   D. Interest

   *Answer:* A) building. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:40 — “In voting by this method, the question can be put, for example, as follows: “The question is on the motion that the bill for building repairs be paid as rendered.”

**455.** `[C0119 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: [Or, _____, “Thank you.”] Those opposed will raise the right hand.… Lower hands.”

   *Answer:* nodding — “[Or, nodding, “Thank you.”] Those opposed will raise the right hand.… Lower hands.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:40 — “[Or, nodding, “Thank you.”] Those opposed will raise the right hand.… Lower hands.””

**456.** `[C0120 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ vote in the affirmative adopts any motion unless it is one of the particular motions that require a larger vote under parliamentary law or the rules of the organization.

   *Answer:* majority — “A majority vote in the affirmative adopts any motion unless it is one of the particular motions that require a larger vote under parliamentary law or the rules of the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:41 — “A majority vote in the affirmative adopts any motion unless it is one of the particular motions that require a larger vote under parliamentary law or the rules of the organization.”

**457.** `[C0120 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For the parliamentary motions that require a _____ vote, see pages t48–t49.) Under all of the voting methods described above except a counted rising vote (or a counted show of hands), the result is determined by the chair’s judgment as to the prevailing side—which it is his duty, in doubtful cases, to verify beyond reasonable doubt, and to the satisfaction of the members, by the procedures described below.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* C) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:41 — “(For the parliamentary motions that require a two-thirds vote, see pages t48–t49.) Under all of the voting methods described above except a counted rising vote (or a counted show of hands), the result is determined by the chair’s judgment as to the prevailing side—which it is his duty, in doubtful cases, to verify beyond reasonable doubt, and to the satisfaction of the members, by the procedures described below.”

**458.** `[C0120 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Chair’s _____ of the Voting Result;

   *Answer:* Announcement — “Chair’s Announcement of the Voting Result;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:41 — “Chair’s Announcement of the Voting Result;”

**459.** `[C0120 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Procedures and Cases Where the Chair Votes.
   A. Verification
   B. Particular
   C. Procedures
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* A) Verification. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:41 — “Verification Procedures and Cases Where the Chair Votes.”

**460.** `[C0120 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair, remaining standing, announces the result of the vote _____ after putting the question—that is, as soon as he has paused to permit response to his call for the negative vote.

   *Answer:* immediately — “The chair, remaining standing, announces the result of the vote immediately after putting the question—that is, as soon as he has paused to permit response to his call for the negative vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:41 — “The chair, remaining standing, announces the result of the vote immediately after putting the question—that is, as soon as he has paused to permit response to his call for the negative vote.”

**461.** `[C0121 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After that, he can make the change only by _____ permission of the assembly.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “After that, he can make the change only by unanimous permission of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:42 — “After that, he can make the change only by unanimous permission of the assembly.”

**462.** `[C0121 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In voting by any of these methods (_____ a counted rising vote), a member has the right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced.
   A. debatable
   B. including
   C. Secretary
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* B) including. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:42 — “In voting by any of these methods (including a counted rising vote), a member has the right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced.”

**463.** `[C0121 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After that, he can make the change only by unanimous _____ of the assembly.

   *Answer:* permission — “After that, he can make the change only by unanimous permission of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:42 — “After that, he can make the change only by unanimous permission of the assembly.”

**464.** `[C0121 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In voting by any of these methods (_____ a counted rising vote), a member has the right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced.

   *Answer:* including — “In voting by any of these methods (including a counted rising vote), a member has the right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:42 — “In voting by any of these methods (including a counted rising vote), a member has the right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced.”

**465.** `[C0122 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In general (that is, as applying to main motions and other types of motions explained in later chapters), the chair’s _____ of the result of the vote includes the following:
   A. Incidental
   B. undebatable
   C. resolutions
   D. announcement

   *Answer:* D) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:43 — “In general (that is, as applying to main motions and other types of motions explained in later chapters), the chair’s announcement of the result of the vote includes the following:”

**466.** `[C0122 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In general (that is, as applying to main motions and other types of motions explained in later chapters), the chair’s _____ of the result of the vote includes the following:

   *Answer:* announcement — “In general (that is, as applying to main motions and other types of motions explained in later chapters), the chair’s announcement of the result of the vote includes the following:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:43 — “In general (that is, as applying to main motions and other types of motions explained in later chapters), the chair’s announcement of the result of the vote includes the following:”

**467.** `[C0123 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The three points listed immediately above generally complete the chair’s _____ of the voting result.

   *Answer:* announcement — “The three points listed immediately above generally complete the chair’s announcement of the voting result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:44 — “The three points listed immediately above generally complete the chair’s announcement of the voting result.”

**468.** `[C0123 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The three points listed immediately above generally complete the chair’s _____ of the voting result.
   A. announcement
   B. Indefinitely
   C. specification
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:44 — “The three points listed immediately above generally complete the chair’s announcement of the voting result.”

**469.** `[C0124 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Form of announcement of voting result and the business _____.

   *Answer:* that follows — “Form of announcement of voting result and the business that follows.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:45 — “Form of announcement of voting result and the business that follows.”

**470.** `[C0124 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Form of _____ of voting result and the business that follows.
   A. announcement
   B. subsequently
   C. Definitely
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:45 — “Form of announcement of voting result and the business that follows.”

**471.** `[C0124 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and, where applicable, they are immediately followed without pause by announcement of the next item of business, or (in the case of “secondary” motions, which are described in the next chapter) by the stating of the next motion that consequently comes up for _____.

   *Answer:* consideration — “and, where applicable, they are immediately followed without pause by announcement of the next item of business, or (in the case of “secondary” motions, which are described in the next chapter) by the stating of the next motion that consequently comes up for consideration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:45 — “and, where applicable, they are immediately followed without pause by announcement of the next item of business, or (in the case of “secondary” motions, which are described in the next chapter) by the stating of the next motion that consequently comes up for consideration.”

**472.** `[C0124 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and, where applicable, they are immediately followed without pause by announcement of the next item of business, or (in the case of “secondary” motions, which are described in the next chapter) by the stating of the next motion that consequently comes up for _____.
   A. consideration
   B. interrupted
   C. undebatable
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:45 — “and, where applicable, they are immediately followed without pause by announcement of the next item of business, or (in the case of “secondary” motions, which are described in the next chapter) by the stating of the next motion that consequently comes up for consideration.”

**473.** `[C0125 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be _____—is determined by the particular motion.

   *Answer:* adopted — “The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be adopted—is determined by the particular motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:46 — “The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be adopted—is determined by the particular motion.”

**474.** `[C0125 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Standard forms, as shown in the bulleted list below, can be given only for the part of the _____ covered by the first two points listed above.
   A. announcement
   B. constitution
   C. Convention
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:46 — “Standard forms, as shown in the bulleted list below, can be given only for the part of the announcement covered by the first two points listed above.”

**475.** `[C0125 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be adopted—is _____ by the particular motion.

   *Answer:* determined — “The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be adopted—is determined by the particular motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:46 — “The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be adopted—is determined by the particular motion.”

**476.** `[C0125 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, after declaring that the motion is adopted, the chair might indicate its effect by saying, “The Secretary will send to the bank a certified copy of the _____ naming Mr.
   A. Definitely
   B. resolution
   C. Particular
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:46 — “For example, after declaring that the motion is adopted, the chair might indicate its effect by saying, “The Secretary will send to the bank a certified copy of the resolution naming Mr.”

**477.** `[C0125 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, “The question is _____ to the next meeting of the Society.”

   *Answer:* postponed — “or, “The question is postponed to the next meeting of the Society.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:46 — “or, “The question is postponed to the next meeting of the Society.””

**478.** `[C0126 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Immediately after completing the _____ of the voting result, to announce the next business in order, the chair might say, for example, “The next item of business is the report of the Treasurer,” or, “Is there any further new business?” or, to state the question on the motion that comes up next as a result of the vote, “The question is now on the main motion as amended.”

   *Answer:* announcement — “Immediately after completing the announcement of the voting result, to announce the next business in order, the chair might say, for example, “The next item of business is the report of the Treasurer,” or, “Is there any further new business?” or, to state the question on the motion that comes up next as a result of the vote, “The question is now on the main motion as amended.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:47 — “Immediately after completing the announcement of the voting result, to announce the next business in order, the chair might say, for example, “The next item of business is the report of the Treasurer,” or, “Is there any further new business?” or, to state the question on the motion that comes up next as a result of the vote, “The question is now on the main motion as amended.””

**479.** `[C0126 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Immediately after completing the _____ of the voting result, to announce the next business in order, the chair might say, for example, “The next item of business is the report of the Treasurer,” or, “Is there any further new business?” or, to state the question on the motion that comes up next as a result of the vote, “The question is now on the main motion as amended.”
   A. subsequently
   B. Definitely
   C. announcement
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:47 — “Immediately after completing the announcement of the voting result, to announce the next business in order, the chair might say, for example, “The next item of business is the report of the Treasurer,” or, “Is there any further new business?” or, to state the question on the motion that comes up next as a result of the vote, “The question is now on the main motion as amended.””

**480.** `[C0129 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] _____ to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.
   A. exact
   B. hours
   C. Rules
   D. seem

   *Answer:* D) seem. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:50 — “If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.”

**481.** `[C0129 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a _____, as explained below.

   *Answer:* division — “If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:50 — “If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.”

**482.** `[C0129 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A voice vote—or, in larger meetings, even a vote by show of hands—may sometimes be _____, either because the voting is close or because a significant number of members have failed to vote.
   A. inconclusive
   B. adjournment
   C. presentation
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) inconclusive. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:50 — “A voice vote—or, in larger meetings, even a vote by show of hands—may sometimes be inconclusive, either because the voting is close or because a significant number of members have failed to vote.”

**483.** `[C0129 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If no member makes such a demand or states that he doubts the result, the chair _____, “The ayes have it…,” as shown above.
   A. paragraph
   B. amendment
   C. Signature
   D. continues

   *Answer:* D) continues. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:50 — “If no member makes such a demand or states that he doubts the result, the chair continues, “The ayes have it…,” as shown above.”

**484.** `[C0129 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A voice vote—or, in larger meetings, even a vote by show of hands—may sometimes be _____, either because the voting is close or because a significant number of members have failed to vote.

   *Answer:* inconclusive — “A voice vote—or, in larger meetings, even a vote by show of hands—may sometimes be inconclusive, either because the voting is close or because a significant number of members have failed to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:50 — “A voice vote—or, in larger meetings, even a vote by show of hands—may sometimes be inconclusive, either because the voting is close or because a significant number of members have failed to vote.”

**485.** `[C0130 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, after a vote has been retaken as an un_____ rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.

   *Answer:* counted — “If, after a vote has been retaken as an uncounted rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:51 — “If, after a vote has been retaken as an uncounted rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.”

**486.** `[C0130 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair is in actual doubt in the case of such a vote, however, he does not announce a result, but instead _____ retakes the vote—strictly speaking, always as a rising vote.
   A. Convention
   B. immediately
   C. Indefinitely
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:51 — “If the chair is in actual doubt in the case of such a vote, however, he does not announce a result, but instead immediately retakes the vote—strictly speaking, always as a rising vote.”

**487.** `[C0130 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (Regarding use of a show of hands as a method of verifying an _____ voice vote, however, see below.) If it appears when those in the affirmative rise that the vote will be close enough to require a count, the chair should count the vote, or direct the secretary to do so, or (in a large assembly) appoint a convenient number of tellers—preferably an even number equally divided between members known to be in favor of the motion and those opposed to it.

   *Answer:* inconclusive — “(Regarding use of a show of hands as a method of verifying an inconclusive voice vote, however, see below.) If it appears when those in the affirmative rise that the vote will be close enough to require a count, the chair should count the vote, or direct the secretary to do so, or (in a large assembly) appoint a convenient number of tellers—preferably an even number equally divided between members known to be in favor of the motion and those opposed to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:51 — “(Regarding use of a show of hands as a method of verifying an inconclusive voice vote, however, see below.) If it appears when those in the affirmative rise that the vote will be close enough to require a count, the chair should count the vote, or direct the secretary to do so, or (in a large assembly) appoint a convenient number of tellers—preferably an even number equally divided between members known to be in favor of the motion and those opposed to it.”

**488.** `[C0130 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, after a vote has been retaken as an _____ rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.
   A. exhausted
   B. uncounted
   C. secondary
   D. extending

   *Answer:* B) uncounted. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:51 — “If, after a vote has been retaken as an uncounted rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.”

**489.** `[C0130 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair is in actual doubt in the case of such a vote, however, he does not announce a result, but instead _____ retakes the vote—strictly speaking, always as a rising vote.

   *Answer:* immediately — “If the chair is in actual doubt in the case of such a vote, however, he does not announce a result, but instead immediately retakes the vote—strictly speaking, always as a rising vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:51 — “If the chair is in actual doubt in the case of such a vote, however, he does not announce a result, but instead immediately retakes the vote—strictly speaking, always as a rising vote.”

**490.** `[C0131 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whether or not the chair pauses to say, “The ayes seem to have it…,” any member (without a second) has the right to require that a voice vote (or even a vote by show of hands) be retaken as a rising vote, so long as he does not use the procedure as a dilatory tactic when there clearly has been a full vote and there can be no _____ doubt of the result.
   A. resolution
   B. reasonable
   C. persuasive
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* B) reasonable. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:52 — “Whether or not the chair pauses to say, “The ayes seem to have it…,” any member (without a second) has the right to require that a voice vote (or even a vote by show of hands) be retaken as a rising vote, so long as he does not use the procedure as a dilatory tactic when there clearly has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt of the result.”

**491.** `[C0131 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To do so, the member, without obtaining the floor, calls out the single word “Division!” or “I call for [or “demand”] a division,” or “I doubt the result of the vote.” The chair must then _____ take the rising vote.

   *Answer:* immediately — “To do so, the member, without obtaining the floor, calls out the single word “Division!” or “I call for [or “demand”] a division,” or “I doubt the result of the vote.” The chair must then immediately take the rising vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:52 — “To do so, the member, without obtaining the floor, calls out the single word “Division!” or “I call for [or “demand”] a division,” or “I doubt the result of the vote.” The chair must then immediately take the rising vote.”

**492.** `[C0131 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whether or not the chair pauses to say, “The ayes seem to have it…,” any member (without a second) has the right to require that a voice vote (or even a vote by show of hands) be retaken as a rising vote, so long as he does not use the procedure as a dilatory tactic when there clearly has been a full vote and there can be no _____ doubt of the result.

   *Answer:* reasonable — “Whether or not the chair pauses to say, “The ayes seem to have it…,” any member (without a second) has the right to require that a voice vote (or even a vote by show of hands) be retaken as a rising vote, so long as he does not use the procedure as a dilatory tactic when there clearly has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt of the result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:52 — “Whether or not the chair pauses to say, “The ayes seem to have it…,” any member (without a second) has the right to require that a voice vote (or even a vote by show of hands) be retaken as a rising vote, so long as he does not use the procedure as a dilatory tactic when there clearly has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt of the result.”

**493.** `[C0131 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To do so, the member, without obtaining the floor, calls out the single word “Division!” or “I call for [or “demand”] a division,” or “I doubt the result of the vote.” The chair must then _____ take the rising vote.
   A. Permission
   B. adjournment
   C. Substitute
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* D) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:52 — “To do so, the member, without obtaining the floor, calls out the single word “Division!” or “I call for [or “demand”] a division,” or “I doubt the result of the vote.” The chair must then immediately take the rising vote.”

**494.** `[C0132 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If such a motion is made and is seconded, the chair states it, and puts the question (by a voice vote, or by an _____ rising vote or show of hands) on whether a count shall be ordered.

   *Answer:* uncounted — “If such a motion is made and is seconded, the chair states it, and puts the question (by a voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands) on whether a count shall be ordered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:53 — “If such a motion is made and is seconded, the chair states it, and puts the question (by a voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands) on whether a count shall be ordered.”

**495.** `[C0132 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Either the chair on his own initiative or the assembly by a _____ vote can order such a vote to be counted.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:53 — “Either the chair on his own initiative or the assembly by a majority vote can order such a vote to be counted.”

**496.** `[C0132 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Either the chair on his own _____ or the assembly by a majority vote can order such a vote to be counted.

   *Answer:* initiative — “Either the chair on his own initiative or the assembly by a majority vote can order such a vote to be counted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:53 — “Either the chair on his own initiative or the assembly by a majority vote can order such a vote to be counted.”

**497.** `[C0132 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a division appears doubtfully close and the chair does not order a count, a member, as soon as the chair has announced the result, can rise and address the chair, and is entitled to preference in _____ for the purpose of moving that the vote be counted.
   A. Preparation
   B. resolutions
   C. recognition
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:53 — “If a division appears doubtfully close and the chair does not order a count, a member, as soon as the chair has announced the result, can rise and address the chair, and is entitled to preference in recognition for the purpose of moving that the vote be counted.”

**498.** `[C0132 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a division appears _____ close and the chair does not order a count, a member, as soon as the chair has announced the result, can rise and address the chair, and is entitled to preference in recognition for the purpose of moving that the vote be counted.

   *Answer:* doubtfully — “If a division appears doubtfully close and the chair does not order a count, a member, as soon as the chair has announced the result, can rise and address the chair, and is entitled to preference in recognition for the purpose of moving that the vote be counted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:53 — “If a division appears doubtfully close and the chair does not order a count, a member, as soon as the chair has announced the result, can rise and address the chair, and is entitled to preference in recognition for the purpose of moving that the vote be counted.”

**499.** `[C0133 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (For additional information regarding _____ Assembly and motions relating to voting, see 29 and 30.)

   *Answer:* Division of the — “(For additional information regarding Division of the Assembly and motions relating to voting, see 29 and 30.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:54 — “(For additional information regarding Division of the Assembly and motions relating to voting, see 29 and 30.)”

**500.** `[C0133 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For additional information regarding Division of the _____ and motions relating to voting, see 29 and 30.)
   A. decision
   B. Postpone
   C. Assembly
   D. dilatory

   *Answer:* C) Assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:54 — “(For additional information regarding Division of the Assembly and motions relating to voting, see 29 and 30.)”

**501.** `[C0134 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In very small assemblies where everyone present can clearly see everyone else, an inconclusive voice vote may sometimes be verified _____ by a show of hands if no member objects.

   *Answer:* satisfactorily — “In very small assemblies where everyone present can clearly see everyone else, an inconclusive voice vote may sometimes be verified satisfactorily by a show of hands if no member objects.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:55 — “In very small assemblies where everyone present can clearly see everyone else, an inconclusive voice vote may sometimes be verified satisfactorily by a show of hands if no member objects.”

**502.** `[C0134 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A show of hands is not a division, however, and it is not always as _____ in causing a maximum number of members to vote when some have not done so.
   A. Signature
   B. Secretary
   C. exhausted
   D. effective

   *Answer:* D) effective. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:55 — “A show of hands is not a division, however, and it is not always as effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote when some have not done so.”

**503.** `[C0134 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In small meetings, a voice vote can be retaken by a show of hands at the _____ of the chair;

   *Answer:* initiative — “In small meetings, a voice vote can be retaken by a show of hands at the initiative of the chair;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:55 — “In small meetings, a voice vote can be retaken by a show of hands at the initiative of the chair;”

**504.** `[C0134 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A show of hands is not a _____, however, and it is not always as effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote when some have not done so.
   A. dilatory
   B. Resolved
   C. meetings
   D. division

   *Answer:* D) division. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:55 — “A show of hands is not a division, however, and it is not always as effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote when some have not done so.”

**505.** `[C0134 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair can also _____ take a rising vote in response to a request for a show of hands.

   *Answer:* immediately — “The chair can also immediately take a rising vote in response to a request for a show of hands.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:55 — “The chair can also immediately take a rising vote in response to a request for a show of hands.”

**506.** `[C0135 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Chair’s vote as part of the announcement, where it affects _____.

   *Answer:* the result — “Chair’s vote as part of the announcement, where it affects the result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:56 — “Chair’s vote as part of the announcement, where it affects the result.”

**507.** `[C0135 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the presiding officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting _____ as any other member.
   A. clerk
   B. Point
   C. right
   D. Other

   *Answer:* C) right. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:56 — “If the presiding officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting right as any other member.”

**508.** `[C0135 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a counted rising vote (or a count of hands) on a motion requiring a _____ vote for adoption, the outcome will be determined by the chair’s action in cases where, without his vote, there is (a) a tie, or (b) one more in the affirmative than in the negative.

   *Answer:* majority — “In a counted rising vote (or a count of hands) on a motion requiring a majority vote for adoption, the outcome will be determined by the chair’s action in cases where, without his vote, there is (a) a tie, or (b) one more in the affirmative than in the negative.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:56 — “In a counted rising vote (or a count of hands) on a motion requiring a majority vote for adoption, the outcome will be determined by the chair’s action in cases where, without his vote, there is (a) a tie, or (b) one more in the affirmative than in the negative.”

**509.** `[C0135 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Chair’s vote as part of the _____, where it affects the result.
   A. resolutions
   B. announcement
   C. Incidental
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:56 — “Chair’s vote as part of the announcement, where it affects the result.”

**510.** `[C0135 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting right as any other member.

   *Answer:* presiding — “If the presiding officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting right as any other member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:56 — “If the presiding officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting right as any other member.”

**511.** `[C0136 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of _____, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.

   *Answer:* unanimous consent — “In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:58 — “In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.”

**512.** `[C0136 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, _____.
   A. first paragraph
   B. Resolved by the
   C. general consent
   D. by paragraph or

   *Answer:* C) general consent. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:58 — “In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.”

**513.** `[C0136 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Action in this manner is in accord with the principle that rules are designed for the _____ of the minority and generally need not be strictly enforced when there is no minority to protect.

   *Answer:* protection — “Action in this manner is in accord with the principle that rules are designed for the protection of the minority and generally need not be strictly enforced when there is no minority to protect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:58 — “Action in this manner is in accord with the principle that rules are designed for the protection of the minority and generally need not be strictly enforced when there is no minority to protect.”

**514.** `[C0136 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under these _____, the method of unanimous consent can be used either to adopt a motion without the steps of stating the question and putting the motion to a formal vote, or it can be used to take action without even the formality of a motion.
   A. landscaped
   B. privileged
   C. subsidiary
   D. conditions

   *Answer:* D) conditions. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:58 — “Under these conditions, the method of unanimous consent can be used either to adopt a motion without the steps of stating the question and putting the motion to a formal vote, or it can be used to take action without even the formality of a motion.”

**515.** `[C0136 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Action in this manner is in accord with the principle that rules are designed for the _____ of the minority and generally need not be strictly enforced when there is no minority to protect.
   A. Reconsider
   B. protection
   C. Unfinished
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* B) protection. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:58 — “Action in this manner is in accord with the principle that rules are designed for the protection of the minority and generally need not be strictly enforced when there is no minority to protect.”

**516.** `[C0137 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To obtain _____ consent in either case, the chair states that “If there is no objection… [or, “Without objection…”],” the action that he mentions will be taken;

   *Answer:* unanimous — “To obtain unanimous consent in either case, the chair states that “If there is no objection… [or, “Without objection…”],” the action that he mentions will be taken;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:59 — “To obtain unanimous consent in either case, the chair states that “If there is no objection… [or, “Without objection…”],” the action that he mentions will be taken;”

**517.** `[C0137 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To obtain unanimous consent in either case, the chair states that “If there is no _____… [or, “Without objection…”],” the action that he mentions will be taken;
   A. objection
   B. debatable
   C. extending
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) objection. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:59 — “To obtain unanimous consent in either case, the chair states that “If there is no objection… [or, “Without objection…”],” the action that he mentions will be taken;”

**518.** `[C0137 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If any member objects, the chair must state the question on the motion, allow any desired debate (unless it is an “undebatable” _____ motion—see 6 and pages t46–t47), and put the question in the regular manner.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “If any member objects, the chair must state the question on the motion, allow any desired debate (unless it is an “undebatable” parliamentary motion—see 6 and pages t46–t47), and put the question in the regular manner.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:59 — “If any member objects, the chair must state the question on the motion, allow any desired debate (unless it is an “undebatable” parliamentary motion—see 6 and pages t46–t47), and put the question in the regular manner.”

**519.** `[C0137 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Or—if no motion has been made—the chair must first ask, “Is there a motion to… [stating the _____ action]”;
   A. Resolved
   B. proposed
   C. Standing
   D. chairman
