   *Answer:* B) proposed. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:59 — “Or—if no motion has been made—the chair must first ask, “Is there a motion to… [stating the proposed action]”;”

**520.** `[C0137 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or he must at least put the question, _____ such a motion.

   *Answer:* assuming — “or he must at least put the question, assuming such a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:59 — “or he must at least put the question, assuming such a motion.”

**521.** `[C0138 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: “_____ consent” does not necessarily imply that every member present is in favor of the proposed action;

   *Answer:* Unanimous — ““Unanimous consent” does not necessarily imply that every member present is in favor of the proposed action;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:60 — ““Unanimous consent” does not necessarily imply that every member present is in favor of the proposed action;”

**522.** `[C0138 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: “Unanimous consent” does not _____ imply that every member present is in favor of the proposed action;
   A. necessarily
   B. authorizes
   C. convention
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) necessarily. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:60 — ““Unanimous consent” does not necessarily imply that every member present is in favor of the proposed action;”

**523.** `[C0138 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: it may only mean that the _____, feeling that it is useless to oppose or discuss the matter, simply acquiesces.

   *Answer:* opposition — “it may only mean that the opposition, feeling that it is useless to oppose or discuss the matter, simply acquiesces.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:60 — “it may only mean that the opposition, feeling that it is useless to oppose or discuss the matter, simply acquiesces.”

**524.** `[C0138 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Similarly, when a member responds to the chair’s inquiry, “Is there any objection…?” with “I object,” he may not necessarily oppose the motion itself, but may believe that it is wise to take a formal vote under the _____.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. requirement
   C. circumstances
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) circumstances. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:60 — “Similarly, when a member responds to the chair’s inquiry, “Is there any objection…?” with “I object,” he may not necessarily oppose the motion itself, but may believe that it is wise to take a formal vote under the circumstances.”

**525.** `[C0138 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Similarly, when a member responds to the chair’s inquiry, “Is there any _____…?” with “I object,” he may not necessarily oppose the motion itself, but may believe that it is wise to take a formal vote under the circumstances.

   *Answer:* objection — “Similarly, when a member responds to the chair’s inquiry, “Is there any objection…?” with “I object,” he may not necessarily oppose the motion itself, but may believe that it is wise to take a formal vote under the circumstances.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:60 — “Similarly, when a member responds to the chair’s inquiry, “Is there any objection…?” with “I object,” he may not necessarily oppose the motion itself, but may believe that it is wise to take a formal vote under the circumstances.”

**526.** `[C0139 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As a second example, assume that a speaker whose time has expired in debate on a motion asks for two _____ minutes.
   A. privileged
   B. additional
   C. incidental
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) additional. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:61 — “As a second example, assume that a speaker whose time has expired in debate on a motion asks for two additional minutes.”

**527.** `[C0139 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair thinks that all members will _____, he may handle the matter as follows:

   *Answer:* approve — “If the chair thinks that all members will approve, he may handle the matter as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:61 — “If the chair thinks that all members will approve, he may handle the matter as follows:”

**528.** `[C0139 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a second example, assume that a speaker whose time has expired in debate on a motion asks for two _____ minutes.

   *Answer:* additional — “As a second example, assume that a speaker whose time has expired in debate on a motion asks for two additional minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:61 — “As a second example, assume that a speaker whose time has expired in debate on a motion asks for two additional minutes.”

**529.** `[C0139 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair thinks that all members will _____, he may handle the matter as follows:
   A. Adopted
   B. Rescind
   C. abstain
   D. approve

   *Answer:* D) approve. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:61 — “If the chair thinks that all members will approve, he may handle the matter as follows:”

**530.** `[C0140 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases where _____ consent is already apparent, the chair may sometimes assume it.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “In cases where unanimous consent is already apparent, the chair may sometimes assume it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:62 — “In cases where unanimous consent is already apparent, the chair may sometimes assume it.”

**531.** `[C0140 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, if everyone is obviously absorbed in listening to a speaker who seems near the end of his remarks, the chair may allow him to conclude without _____, although his time has expired.
   A. interruption
   B. deliberative
   C. requirement
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) interruption. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:62 — “For example, if everyone is obviously absorbed in listening to a speaker who seems near the end of his remarks, the chair may allow him to conclude without interruption, although his time has expired.”

**532.** `[C0140 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where _____ consent is already apparent, the chair may sometimes assume it.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* A) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:62 — “In cases where unanimous consent is already apparent, the chair may sometimes assume it.”

**533.** `[C0140 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, if everyone is obviously absorbed in listening to a speaker who seems near the end of his remarks, the chair may allow him to conclude without _____, although his time has expired.

   *Answer:* interruption — “For example, if everyone is obviously absorbed in listening to a speaker who seems near the end of his remarks, the chair may allow him to conclude without interruption, although his time has expired.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:62 — “For example, if everyone is obviously absorbed in listening to a speaker who seems near the end of his remarks, the chair may allow him to conclude without interruption, although his time has expired.”

**534.** `[C0141 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Action by _____ consent requires the presence of a quorum, just as for the transaction of business by any other method.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* A) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:63 — “Action by unanimous consent requires the presence of a quorum, just as for the transaction of business by any other method.”

**535.** `[C0141 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If much hinges on the _____, however, it is usually better to take a formal vote.

   *Answer:* outcome — “If much hinges on the outcome, however, it is usually better to take a formal vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:63 — “If much hinges on the outcome, however, it is usually better to take a formal vote.”

**536.** `[C0141 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Action by unanimous consent requires the presence of a quorum, just as for the _____ of business by any other method.
   A. authorizes
   B. transaction
   C. convention
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) transaction. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:63 — “Action by unanimous consent requires the presence of a quorum, just as for the transaction of business by any other method.”

**537.** `[C0141 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If much hinges on the _____, however, it is usually better to take a formal vote.
   A. Charges
   B. Reports
   C. Meeting
   D. outcome

   *Answer:* D) outcome. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:63 — “If much hinges on the outcome, however, it is usually better to take a formal vote.”

**538.** `[C0142 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As already noted, the foregoing initial description of the handling of motions refers principally to the _____—the basic form of motion by which business is brought up and by which the assembly takes substantive action.

   *Answer:* main motion — “As already noted, the foregoing initial description of the handling of motions refers principally to the main motion—the basic form of motion by which business is brought up and by which the assembly takes substantive action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:64 — “As already noted, the foregoing initial description of the handling of motions refers principally to the main motion—the basic form of motion by which business is brought up and by which the assembly takes substantive action.”

**539.** `[C0142 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As already noted, the foregoing initial _____ of the handling of motions refers principally to the main motion—the basic form of motion by which business is brought up and by which the assembly takes substantive action.
   A. requirement
   B. description
   C. Organization
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) description. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:64 — “As already noted, the foregoing initial description of the handling of motions refers principally to the main motion—the basic form of motion by which business is brought up and by which the assembly takes substantive action.”

**540.** `[C0142 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As also stated above, the _____ of a main motion can involve a number of other procedures not yet described—which are nevertheless in the nature of action by the assembly and are themselves properly the subject of motions.

   *Answer:* consideration — “As also stated above, the consideration of a main motion can involve a number of other procedures not yet described—which are nevertheless in the nature of action by the assembly and are themselves properly the subject of motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:64 — “As also stated above, the consideration of a main motion can involve a number of other procedures not yet described—which are nevertheless in the nature of action by the assembly and are themselves properly the subject of motions.”

**541.** `[C0142 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the same way there are a number of “privileged” motions, which are not associated with the main question but can nevertheless be introduced while it is pending because they relate to certain urgent matters that may arise and warrant immediate _____ at such a time.
   A. determination
   B. President-Elect
   C. Announcements
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* A) determination. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:64 — “In the same way there are a number of “privileged” motions, which are not associated with the main question but can nevertheless be introduced while it is pending because they relate to certain urgent matters that may arise and warrant immediate determination at such a time.”

**542.** `[C0142 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Finally, there are motions by which business can be brought before the assembly under a number of special _____ involving an earlier question.

   *Answer:* circumstances — “Finally, there are motions by which business can be brought before the assembly under a number of special circumstances involving an earlier question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:64 — “Finally, there are motions by which business can be brought before the assembly under a number of special circumstances involving an earlier question.”

**543.** `[C0143 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The next chapter contains a brief statement of the purpose of each of the _____ motions, together with an explanation of the classes into which all motions are divided.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The next chapter contains a brief statement of the purpose of each of the parliamentary motions, together with an explanation of the classes into which all motions are divided.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:65 — “The next chapter contains a brief statement of the purpose of each of the parliamentary motions, together with an explanation of the classes into which all motions are divided.”

**544.** `[C0143 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The main motion is more fully _____ in 10, as are each of the other motions in 11–37.
   A. Example
   B. Adjourn
   C. Pending
   D. treated

   *Answer:* D) treated. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:65 — “The main motion is more fully treated in 10, as are each of the other motions in 11–37.”

**545.** `[C0143 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The next chapter contains a brief statement of the purpose of each of the _____ motions, together with an explanation of the classes into which all motions are divided.
   A. Announcements
   B. parliamentary
   C. specification
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:65 — “The next chapter contains a brief statement of the purpose of each of the parliamentary motions, together with an explanation of the classes into which all motions are divided.”

**546.** `[C0143 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The main motion is more fully _____ in 10, as are each of the other motions in 11–37.

   *Answer:* treated — “The main motion is more fully treated in 10, as are each of the other motions in 11–37.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:65 — “The main motion is more fully treated in 10, as are each of the other motions in 11–37.”

**547.** `[C0144 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As noted in Chapter II, the word _____ refers to a formal proposal by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.

   *Answer:* motion — “As noted in Chapter II, the word motion refers to a formal proposal by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:1 — “As noted in Chapter II, the word motion refers to a formal proposal by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.”

**548.** `[C0144 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As noted in Chapter II, the word motion refers to a formal _____ by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.
   A. proposal
   B. Approved
   C. Interest
   D. division

   *Answer:* A) proposal. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:1 — “As noted in Chapter II, the word motion refers to a formal proposal by a member, in a meeting, that the assembly take certain action.”

**549.** `[C0144 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before a subject can be _____, it must be placed before the assembly in the form of a motion.

   *Answer:* considered — “Before a subject can be considered, it must be placed before the assembly in the form of a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:1 — “Before a subject can be considered, it must be placed before the assembly in the form of a motion.”

**550.** `[C0144 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: From the basic type of motion known as the main motion, as also noted in the preceding chapter, many other specific motions have been derived and have become defined under _____ law.
   A. recommendation
   B. deliberative
   C. parliamentary
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:1 — “From the basic type of motion known as the main motion, as also noted in the preceding chapter, many other specific motions have been derived and have become defined under parliamentary law.”

**551.** `[C0144 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before a subject can be _____, it must be placed before the assembly in the form of a motion.
   A. Definitely
   B. substitute
   C. considered
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* C) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:1 — “Before a subject can be considered, it must be placed before the assembly in the form of a motion.”

**552.** `[C0145 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For _____ in description, motions may be classified as shown in the following list.

   *Answer:* convenience — “For convenience in description, motions may be classified as shown in the following list.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:2 — “For convenience in description, motions may be classified as shown in the following list.”

**553.** `[C0145 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (As indicated to the right of the list, the motions in the second, third, and fourth classes— _____, privileged, and incidental motions taken together— are also called “secondary motions.”)
   A. subsidiary
   B. introduced
   C. committees
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* A) subsidiary. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:2 — “(As indicated to the right of the list, the motions in the second, third, and fourth classes— subsidiary, privileged, and incidental motions taken together— are also called “secondary motions.”)”

**554.** `[C0145 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For _____ in description, motions may be classified as shown in the following list.
   A. adjournment
   B. convenience
   C. Incidental
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) convenience. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:2 — “For convenience in description, motions may be classified as shown in the following list.”

**555.** `[C0145 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (As indicated to the right of the list, the motions in the second, third, and fourth classes— _____, privileged, and incidental motions taken together— are also called “secondary motions.”)

   *Answer:* subsidiary — “(As indicated to the right of the list, the motions in the second, third, and fourth classes— subsidiary, privileged, and incidental motions taken together— are also called “secondary motions.”)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:2 — “(As indicated to the right of the list, the motions in the second, third, and fourth classes— subsidiary, privileged, and incidental motions taken together— are also called “secondary motions.”)”

**556.** `[C0146 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The concept of _____ motions serves as a starting point for the division of motions into the classes shown.
   A. Crestwood
   B. postponed
   C. secondary
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* C) secondary. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:3 — “The concept of secondary motions serves as a starting point for the division of motions into the classes shown.”

**557.** `[C0146 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The concept of secondary _____ serves as a starting point for the division of motions into the classes shown.

   *Answer:* motions — “The concept of secondary motions serves as a starting point for the division of motions into the classes shown.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:3 — “The concept of secondary motions serves as a starting point for the division of motions into the classes shown.”

**558.** `[C0147 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Secondary motions may be seen as related to the following fundamental principle of parliamentary law: _____ can be considered at a time;

   *Answer:* Only one question — “Secondary motions may be seen as related to the following fundamental principle of parliamentary law: Only one question can be considered at a time;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:4 — “Secondary motions may be seen as related to the following fundamental principle of parliamentary law: Only one question can be considered at a time;”

**559.** `[C0147 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ motions may be seen as related to the following fundamental principle of parliamentary law: Only one question can be considered at a time;
   A. Secondary
   B. Secretary
   C. postponed
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* A) Secondary. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:4 — “Secondary motions may be seen as related to the following fundamental principle of parliamentary law: Only one question can be considered at a time;”

**560.** `[C0147 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: once a motion is before the assembly, it must be adopted or rejected by a vote, or the assembly must take action disposing of the question in some other way, before any other business (except certain matters called “_____ questions”) can be introduced.
   A. persuasive
   B. privileged
   C. Procedures
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:4 — “once a motion is before the assembly, it must be adopted or rejected by a vote, or the assembly must take action disposing of the question in some other way, before any other business (except certain matters called “privileged questions”) can be introduced.”

**561.** `[C0147 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, however, the need for a number of _____ secondary motions arises.
   A. recognized
   B. Unfinished
   C. particular
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* C) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:4 — “Thus, however, the need for a number of particular secondary motions arises.”

**562.** `[C0147 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: once a motion is before the assembly, it must be adopted or rejected by a vote, or the assembly must take action disposing of the question in some other way, before any other business (except certain matters called “_____ questions”) can be introduced.

   *Answer:* privileged — “once a motion is before the assembly, it must be adopted or rejected by a vote, or the assembly must take action disposing of the question in some other way, before any other business (except certain matters called “privileged questions”) can be introduced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:4 — “once a motion is before the assembly, it must be adopted or rejected by a vote, or the assembly must take action disposing of the question in some other way, before any other business (except certain matters called “privileged questions”) can be introduced.”

**563.** `[C0148 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A secondary motion is one whose _____ to the main question, or whose procedural character or urgency, is such that:

   *Answer:* relationship — “A secondary motion is one whose relationship to the main question, or whose procedural character or urgency, is such that:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:5 — “A secondary motion is one whose relationship to the main question, or whose procedural character or urgency, is such that:”

**564.** `[C0148 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A secondary motion is one whose _____ to the main question, or whose procedural character or urgency, is such that:
   A. interrupted
   B. adjournment
   C. Preparation
   D. relationship

   *Answer:* D) relationship. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:5 — “A secondary motion is one whose relationship to the main question, or whose procedural character or urgency, is such that:”

**565.** `[C0149 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ motions generally are made and seconded and are stated by the chair, as a main motion would be—except that certain of them are in order while another member has the floor, and most of the motions in this latter group do not require a second (see pages t44–t45).

   *Answer:* Secondary — “Secondary motions generally are made and seconded and are stated by the chair, as a main motion would be—except that certain of them are in order while another member has the floor, and most of the motions in this latter group do not require a second (see pages t44–t45).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:6 — “Secondary motions generally are made and seconded and are stated by the chair, as a main motion would be—except that certain of them are in order while another member has the floor, and most of the motions in this latter group do not require a second (see pages t44–t45).”

**566.** `[C0149 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ motions generally are made and seconded and are stated by the chair, as a main motion would be—except that certain of them are in order while another member has the floor, and most of the motions in this latter group do not require a second (see pages t44–t45).
   A. Signature
   B. delegates
   C. committee
   D. Secondary

   *Answer:* D) Secondary. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:6 — “Secondary motions generally are made and seconded and are stated by the chair, as a main motion would be—except that certain of them are in order while another member has the floor, and most of the motions in this latter group do not require a second (see pages t44–t45).”

**567.** `[C0150 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a secondary motion is placed before the assembly, it becomes the _____;

   *Answer:* immediately pending question — “When a secondary motion is placed before the assembly, it becomes the immediately pending question;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:7 — “When a secondary motion is placed before the assembly, it becomes the immediately pending question;”

**568.** `[C0150 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a secondary motion is placed before the assembly, it becomes the _____ pending question;
   A. adjournment
   B. immediately
   C. convention
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:7 — “When a secondary motion is placed before the assembly, it becomes the immediately pending question;”

**569.** `[C0150 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whenever the chair has occasion to inform the assembly as to what is the immediately pending question, however, he does not use this phrase, but employs the _____ form, “The question is on the motion to…” The latter form is used even when more than one motion is pending.
   A. parliamentary
   B. interrupted
   C. resolutions
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:7 — “Whenever the chair has occasion to inform the assembly as to what is the immediately pending question, however, he does not use this phrase, but employs the parliamentary form, “The question is on the motion to…” The latter form is used even when more than one motion is pending.”

**570.** `[C0150 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whenever the chair has occasion to inform the assembly as to what is the immediately pending question, however, he does not use this phrase, but employs the _____ form, “The question is on the motion to…” The latter form is used even when more than one motion is pending.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Whenever the chair has occasion to inform the assembly as to what is the immediately pending question, however, he does not use this phrase, but employs the parliamentary form, “The question is on the motion to…” The latter form is used even when more than one motion is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:7 — “Whenever the chair has occasion to inform the assembly as to what is the immediately pending question, however, he does not use this phrase, but employs the parliamentary form, “The question is on the motion to…” The latter form is used even when more than one motion is pending.”

**571.** `[C0152 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, the motion most recently stated by the chair (among those that have not been voted on) is the _____ pending question.

   *Answer:* immediately — “In such a case, the motion most recently stated by the chair (among those that have not been voted on) is the immediately pending question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:9 — “In such a case, the motion most recently stated by the chair (among those that have not been voted on) is the immediately pending question.”

**572.** `[C0152 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, the motion most recently stated by the chair (among those that have not been voted on) is the _____ pending question.
   A. immediately
   B. Preparation
   C. Indefinitely
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §5:9 — “In such a case, the motion most recently stated by the chair (among those that have not been voted on) is the immediately pending question.”

**573.** `[C0153 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While these rules are proper to each of the specific motions, they follow patterns that are related to the division of secondary motions into the classes of _____, privileged, and incidental motions.

**574.** `[C0153 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While these rules are proper to each of the specific motions, they follow patterns that are related to the division of secondary motions into the classes of subsidiary, _____, and incidental motions.

**575.** `[C0154 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The privileged motions _____ above all other motions.

   D. Preparation

**583.** `[C0184 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ background for the detailed treatment of the motions in 10–37 is provided by the discussion of the topics “meeting” and “session” in 8 and 9.

**585.** `[C0185 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but the complete unit of engagement in proceedings by the assembly is a _____, which (in the general case covering all types of assemblies) consists of one or more connected meetings.
   A. Adjourn
   B. adjourn
   C. applied
   D. session

   *Answer:* D) session. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:1 — “but the complete unit of engagement in proceedings by the assembly is a session, which (in the general case covering all types of assemblies) consists of one or more connected meetings.”

**586.** `[C0185 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but the complete unit of engagement in _____ by the assembly is a session, which (in the general case covering all types of assemblies) consists of one or more connected meetings.

**591.** `[C0187 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The length of a session or the number of meetings included within it varies _____ on the type of assembly.

   *Answer:* depending — “The length of a session or the number of meetings included within it varies depending on the type of assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:3 — “The length of a session or the number of meetings included within it varies depending on the type of assembly.”

**592.** `[C0187 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The length of a session or the number of meetings included within it varies _____ on the type of assembly.

   D. reception

**593.** `[C0188 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This rule is the common _____ law and holds except where the bylaws provide otherwise.
   A. undebatable
   B. parliamentary
   C. subsequently
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:4 — “This rule is the common parliamentary law and holds except where the bylaws provide otherwise.”

**594.** `[C0188 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although any society has the right to define, in its bylaws, what shall constitute a session of the _____, it is usually unwise in ordinary societies to adopt a rule making regular sessions last over a long period of time.

   *Answer:* organization — “Although any society has the right to define, in its bylaws, what shall constitute a session of the organization, it is usually unwise in ordinary societies to adopt a rule making regular sessions last over a long period of time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:4 — “Although any society has the right to define, in its bylaws, what shall constitute a session of the organization, it is usually unwise in ordinary societies to adopt a rule making regular sessions last over a long period of time.”

**595.** `[C0188 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a rule would make it possible for the hands of the organization to be tied during that time, since the same question cannot be brought up again during the same session after it is too late to reconsider (37) a vote that has finally disposed of a motion without adopting it (that is, a vote that has rejected or indefinitely postponed it or has sustained an objection to its _____;
   A. Vice-President
   B. consideration
   C. subsequently
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:4 — “Such a rule would make it possible for the hands of the organization to be tied during that time, since the same question cannot be brought up again during the same session after it is too late to reconsider (37) a vote that has finally disposed of a motion without adopting it (that is, a vote that has rejected or indefinitely postponed it or has sustained an objection to its consideration;”

**596.** `[C0188 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This rule is the common _____ law and holds except where the bylaws provide otherwise.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “This rule is the common parliamentary law and holds except where the bylaws provide otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:4 — “This rule is the common parliamentary law and holds except where the bylaws provide otherwise.”

**597.** `[C0188 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although any society has the right to define, in its bylaws, what shall constitute a session of the _____, it is usually unwise in ordinary societies to adopt a rule making regular sessions last over a long period of time.
   A. Procedures
   B. organization
   C. Announcements
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:4 — “Although any society has the right to define, in its bylaws, what shall constitute a session of the organization, it is usually unwise in ordinary societies to adopt a rule making regular sessions last over a long period of time.”

**598.** `[C0189 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of a state or national _____ that holds annual or biennial conventions, each convention constitutes a session of the organization—having one agenda or program— which may be broken up into separate meetings in the morning, afternoon, and evening, or into many meetings held over several days.

   *Answer:* organization — “In the case of a state or national organization that holds annual or biennial conventions, each convention constitutes a session of the organization—having one agenda or program— which may be broken up into separate meetings in the morning, afternoon, and evening, or into many meetings held over several days.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:5 — “In the case of a state or national organization that holds annual or biennial conventions, each convention constitutes a session of the organization—having one agenda or program— which may be broken up into separate meetings in the morning, afternoon, and evening, or into many meetings held over several days.”

**599.** `[C0189 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In Congress a session may comprise hundreds of almost daily meetings, sometimes _____ for nearly a year.
   A. continuing
   B. committees
   C. recognized
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) continuing. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:5 — “In Congress a session may comprise hundreds of almost daily meetings, sometimes continuing for nearly a year.”

**600.** `[C0189 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of a state or national _____ that holds annual or biennial conventions, each convention constitutes a session of the organization—having one agenda or program— which may be broken up into separate meetings in the morning, afternoon, and evening, or into many meetings held over several days.
   A. Preparation
   B. deliberative
   C. organization
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:5 — “In the case of a state or national organization that holds annual or biennial conventions, each convention constitutes a session of the organization—having one agenda or program— which may be broken up into separate meetings in the morning, afternoon, and evening, or into many meetings held over several days.”

**601.** `[C0189 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In Congress a session may comprise hundreds of almost daily meetings, sometimes _____ for nearly a year.

   *Answer:* continuing — “In Congress a session may comprise hundreds of almost daily meetings, sometimes continuing for nearly a year.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:5 — “In Congress a session may comprise hundreds of almost daily meetings, sometimes continuing for nearly a year.”

**602.** `[C0190 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Departure from Parliamentary Meanings in Ordinary _____.

   *Answer:* Speech — “Departure from Parliamentary Meanings in Ordinary Speech.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:6 — “Departure from Parliamentary Meanings in Ordinary Speech.”

**603.** `[C0190 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A similar situation exists regarding the terms _____, adjournment, and adjournment sine die.
   A. recess
   B. Motion
   C. common
   D. beyond

   *Answer:* A) recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:6 — “A similar situation exists regarding the terms recess, adjournment, and adjournment sine die.”

**604.** `[C0190 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A similar situation exists regarding the terms recess, _____, and adjournment sine die.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “A similar situation exists regarding the terms recess, adjournment, and adjournment sine die.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:6 — “A similar situation exists regarding the terms recess, adjournment, and adjournment sine die.”

**605.** `[C0190 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is common, especially in conventions, to hear the word recess also applied to a longer break that _____ terminate a meeting and that consequently should be understood as an adjournment, as in “to recess until tomorrow.” On the other hand, assemblies sometimes “adjourn” or provide in the program or agenda for an “adjournment” when only a short recess is intended.

   *Answer:* does — “It is common, especially in conventions, to hear the word recess also applied to a longer break that does terminate a meeting and that consequently should be understood as an adjournment, as in “to recess until tomorrow.” On the other hand, assemblies sometimes “adjourn” or provide in the program or agenda for an “adjournment” when only a short recess is intended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:6 — “It is common, especially in conventions, to hear the word recess also applied to a longer break that does terminate a meeting and that consequently should be understood as an adjournment, as in “to recess until tomorrow.” On the other hand, assemblies sometimes “adjourn” or provide in the program or agenda for an “adjournment” when only a short recess is intended.”

**606.** `[C0190 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Departure from _____ Meanings in Ordinary Speech.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. Announcements
   C. subsequently
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* A) Parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:6 — “Departure from Parliamentary Meanings in Ordinary Speech.”

**607.** `[C0191 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Comparative Effects of Recess and Adjournment Within a _____.

   *Answer:* Session — “Comparative Effects of Recess and Adjournment Within a Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:7 — “Comparative Effects of Recess and Adjournment Within a Session.”

**608.** `[C0191 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Comparative Effects of _____ and Adjournment Within a Session.
   A. Motion
   B. amends
   C. Recess
   D. Speech

   *Answer:* C) Recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:7 — “Comparative Effects of Recess and Adjournment Within a Session.”

**609.** `[C0191 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ between recess and adjournment may in some cases become thin so that it must be judged in the individual context.

   *Answer:* distinction — “The distinction between recess and adjournment may in some cases become thin so that it must be judged in the individual context.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:7 — “The distinction between recess and adjournment may in some cases become thin so that it must be judged in the individual context.”

**610.** `[C0191 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, according to the _____ given above, a break in the proceedings of a convention for lunch may be more in the nature of a recess, or of an adjournment, depending on the time and the extent of dispersion of the members that is required for them to be served.
   A. Definitely
   B. Incidental
   C. interrupted
   D. definitions

   *Answer:* D) definitions. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:7 — “For example, according to the definitions given above, a break in the proceedings of a convention for lunch may be more in the nature of a recess, or of an adjournment, depending on the time and the extent of dispersion of the members that is required for them to be served.”

**611.** `[C0191 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ between recess and adjournment may in some cases become thin so that it must be judged in the individual context.
   A. Definitely
   B. Procedures
   C. distinction
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) distinction. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:7 — “The distinction between recess and adjournment may in some cases become thin so that it must be judged in the individual context.”

**612.** `[C0192 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: How Meetings to Continue a Session Are _____.

   *Answer:* Scheduled — “How Meetings to Continue a Session Are Scheduled.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:8 — “How Meetings to Continue a Session Are Scheduled.”

**613.** `[C0192 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a meeting adjourns without ending the session, this _____ means that the time for another meeting to continue the same business or order of business has already been set (or that provision has been made for such a meeting to be held “at the call of the chair”).
   A. requirement
   B. Convention
   C. necessarily
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) necessarily. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:8 — “When a meeting adjourns without ending the session, this necessarily means that the time for another meeting to continue the same business or order of business has already been set (or that provision has been made for such a meeting to be held “at the call of the chair”).”

**614.** `[C0192 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The time or provision for this next meeting of the session may have been _____ by one of the following methods (which are listed in order of frequency of occurrence): (a) through a program adopted at the beginning of a convention;

   *Answer:* established — “The time or provision for this next meeting of the session may have been established by one of the following methods (which are listed in order of frequency of occurrence): (a) through a program adopted at the beginning of a convention;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:8 — “The time or provision for this next meeting of the session may have been established by one of the following methods (which are listed in order of frequency of occurrence): (a) through a program adopted at the beginning of a convention;”

**615.** `[C0192 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (b) by the adoption in the present meeting of a motion (main or _____, depending on when it is moved) to fix the time to which to adjourn;
   A. assemblies
   B. Unfinished
   C. committees
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* D) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:8 — “(b) by the adoption in the present meeting of a motion (main or privileged, depending on when it is moved) to fix the time to which to adjourn;”

**616.** `[C0192 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (c) by a _____ in the motion to adjourn, if that motion was made as a main motion while no other question was pending.

   *Answer:* specification — “or (c) by a specification in the motion to adjourn, if that motion was made as a main motion while no other question was pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:8 — “or (c) by a specification in the motion to adjourn, if that motion was made as a main motion while no other question was pending.”

**617.** `[C0193 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ practice a meeting is closed by adopting a motion simply “to adjourn”;
   A. ordinary
   B. Question
   C. chairman
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* A) ordinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:9 — “In ordinary practice a meeting is closed by adopting a motion simply “to adjourn”;”

**618.** `[C0193 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The society meets again at the time provided in its bylaws or other rules, or as already _____ by the adoption of an earlier motion.

   *Answer:* established — “The society meets again at the time provided in its bylaws or other rules, or as already established by the adoption of an earlier motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:9 — “The society meets again at the time provided in its bylaws or other rules, or as already established by the adoption of an earlier motion.”

**619.** `[C0193 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it does not expect to convene until the next “regular meeting” prescribed by rule or bylaw, the chair declares that the meeting “is adjourned,” and such an _____ closes the session.
   A. Preparation
   B. resolutions
   C. adjournment
   D. committees

   *Answer:* C) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:9 — “If it does not expect to convene until the next “regular meeting” prescribed by rule or bylaw, the chair declares that the meeting “is adjourned,” and such an adjournment closes the session.”

**620.** `[C0193 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ practice a meeting is closed by adopting a motion simply “to adjourn”;

   *Answer:* ordinary — “In ordinary practice a meeting is closed by adopting a motion simply “to adjourn”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:9 — “In ordinary practice a meeting is closed by adopting a motion simply “to adjourn”;”

**621.** `[C0193 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The society meets again at the time provided in its bylaws or other rules, or as already _____ by the adoption of an earlier motion.
   A. Permission
   B. resolutions
   C. Particular
   D. established

   *Answer:* D) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:9 — “The society meets again at the time provided in its bylaws or other rules, or as already established by the adoption of an earlier motion.”

**622.** `[C0194 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the event of fire, riot, or other extreme emergency, if the chair believes taking time for a vote on _____ would be dangerous to those present, he should declare the meeting adjourned—to a suitable time and place for an adjourned meeting (if he is able), or to meet at the call of the chair.

   *Answer:* adjourning — “In the event of fire, riot, or other extreme emergency, if the chair believes taking time for a vote on adjourning would be dangerous to those present, he should declare the meeting adjourned—to a suitable time and place for an adjourned meeting (if he is able), or to meet at the call of the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:10 — “In the event of fire, riot, or other extreme emergency, if the chair believes taking time for a vote on adjourning would be dangerous to those present, he should declare the meeting adjourned—to a suitable time and place for an adjourned meeting (if he is able), or to meet at the call of the chair.”

**623.** `[C0194 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the event of fire, riot, or other extreme emergency, if the chair believes taking time for a vote on _____ would be dangerous to those present, he should declare the meeting adjourned—to a suitable time and place for an adjourned meeting (if he is able), or to meet at the call of the chair.
   A. introduced
   B. substitute
   C. committees
   D. adjourning

   *Answer:* D) adjourning. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:10 — “In the event of fire, riot, or other extreme emergency, if the chair believes taking time for a vote on adjourning would be dangerous to those present, he should declare the meeting adjourned—to a suitable time and place for an adjourned meeting (if he is able), or to meet at the call of the chair.”

**624.** `[C0195 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The principal _____ of the session as a complete unit of an assembly’s engagement in proceedings lies in the freedom of each new session, as contrasted with the limitations placed upon a session in progress by decisions it has made.

   *Answer:* significance — “The principal significance of the session as a complete unit of an assembly’s engagement in proceedings lies in the freedom of each new session, as contrasted with the limitations placed upon a session in progress by decisions it has made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:11 — “The principal significance of the session as a complete unit of an assembly’s engagement in proceedings lies in the freedom of each new session, as contrasted with the limitations placed upon a session in progress by decisions it has made.”

**625.** `[C0195 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some of the consequences of this _____ of the session are described in the following paragraphs.
   A. Responsibilities
   B. characteristic
   C. Preparation
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) characteristic. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:11 — “Some of the consequences of this characteristic of the session are described in the following paragraphs.”

**626.** `[C0195 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The principal _____ of the session as a complete unit of an assembly’s engagement in proceedings lies in the freedom of each new session, as contrasted with the limitations placed upon a session in progress by decisions it has made.
   A. consideration
   B. significance
   C. adjournment
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* B) significance. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:11 — “The principal significance of the session as a complete unit of an assembly’s engagement in proceedings lies in the freedom of each new session, as contrasted with the limitations placed upon a session in progress by decisions it has made.”

**627.** `[C0195 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some of the consequences of this _____ of the session are described in the following paragraphs.

   *Answer:* characteristic — “Some of the consequences of this characteristic of the session are described in the following paragraphs.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:11 — “Some of the consequences of this characteristic of the session are described in the following paragraphs.”

**628.** `[C0196 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a general rule, one session cannot place a question beyond the reach of a _____ at a later session except through the process of adopting a special rule of order or an amendment to the bylaws (either of which requires more than a majority vote;

   *Answer:* majority — “As a general rule, one session cannot place a question beyond the reach of a majority at a later session except through the process of adopting a special rule of order or an amendment to the bylaws (either of which requires more than a majority vote;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:12 — “As a general rule, one session cannot place a question beyond the reach of a majority at a later session except through the process of adopting a special rule of order or an amendment to the bylaws (either of which requires more than a majority vote;”

**629.** `[C0196 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As a general rule, one session cannot place a question beyond the reach of a majority at a later session except through the process of adopting a special rule of order or an _____ to the bylaws (either of which requires more than a majority vote;
   A. Reporting
   B. Objection
   C. paragraph
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* D) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:12 — “As a general rule, one session cannot place a question beyond the reach of a majority at a later session except through the process of adopting a special rule of order or an amendment to the bylaws (either of which requires more than a majority vote;”

**630.** `[C0196 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is improper, for example, to postpone anything beyond the next regular session—which would be an attempt to prevent that session from _____ the question.

   *Answer:* considering — “It is improper, for example, to postpone anything beyond the next regular session—which would be an attempt to prevent that session from considering the question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:12 — “It is improper, for example, to postpone anything beyond the next regular session—which would be an attempt to prevent that session from considering the question.”

**631.** `[C0196 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The principle stated applies in qualified form to cases in which a majority rescinds or amends something adopted at an earlier session, or discharges a committee from further _____ of a question referred to it at an earlier session—which a majority can do provided that previous notice was given.
   A. recommendation
   B. consideration
   C. Preparation
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:12 — “The principle stated applies in qualified form to cases in which a majority rescinds or amends something adopted at an earlier session, or discharges a committee from further consideration of a question referred to it at an earlier session—which a majority can do provided that previous notice was given.”

**632.** `[C0196 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is improper, for example, to postpone anything beyond the next regular session—which would be an attempt to prevent that session from _____ the question.
   A. authorizes
   B. Unfinished
   C. assemblies
   D. considering

   *Answer:* D) considering. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:12 — “It is improper, for example, to postpone anything beyond the next regular session—which would be an attempt to prevent that session from considering the question.”

**633.** `[C0197 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Relation of a Session’s Freedom to the Rules of an _____.

   *Answer:* Organization — “Relation of a Session’s Freedom to the Rules of an Organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:13 — “Relation of a Session’s Freedom to the Rules of an Organization.”

**634.** `[C0197 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The application of the same principle to the case of _____ (which, as the term is understood in this book, do not deal with parliamentary procedure;
   A. body of people
   B. standing rules
   C. Insert, or Add
   D. Point of Order

   *Answer:* B) standing rules. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:13 — “The application of the same principle to the case of standing rules (which, as the term is understood in this book, do not deal with parliamentary procedure;”

**635.** `[C0197 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: see 2) is as follows: Although a standing rule can be adopted by a _____ vote at any session and continues in force until it is rescinded or amended, such a rule does not interfere materially with the freedom of a later session, since it can be suspended for the duration of any session (but not for longer) by a majority vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “see 2) is as follows: Although a standing rule can be adopted by a majority vote at any session and continues in force until it is rescinded or amended, such a rule does not interfere materially with the freedom of a later session, since it can be suspended for the duration of any session (but not for longer) by a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:13 — “see 2) is as follows: Although a standing rule can be adopted by a majority vote at any session and continues in force until it is rescinded or amended, such a rule does not interfere materially with the freedom of a later session, since it can be suspended for the duration of any session (but not for longer) by a majority vote.”

**636.** `[C0197 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The application of the same principle to the case of standing rules (which, as the term is understood in this book, do not deal with _____ procedure;
   A. president-elect
   B. undebatable
   C. parliamentary
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:13 — “The application of the same principle to the case of standing rules (which, as the term is understood in this book, do not deal with parliamentary procedure;”

**637.** `[C0197 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: see 2) is as follows: Although a standing rule can be adopted by a majority vote at any session and continues in force until it is rescinded or amended, such a rule does not interfere _____ with the freedom of a later session, since it can be suspended for the duration of any session (but not for longer) by a majority vote.

   *Answer:* materially — “see 2) is as follows: Although a standing rule can be adopted by a majority vote at any session and continues in force until it is rescinded or amended, such a rule does not interfere materially with the freedom of a later session, since it can be suspended for the duration of any session (but not for longer) by a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:13 — “see 2) is as follows: Although a standing rule can be adopted by a majority vote at any session and continues in force until it is rescinded or amended, such a rule does not interfere materially with the freedom of a later session, since it can be suspended for the duration of any session (but not for longer) by a majority vote.”

**638.** `[C0198 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, on the other hand—and special rules of order, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.

   *Answer:* Bylaws — “Bylaws, on the other hand—and special rules of order, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:14 — “Bylaws, on the other hand—and special rules of order, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.”

**639.** `[C0198 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Bylaws, on the other hand—and _____, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.
   A. Chairman or President
   B. committee of the whole
   C. Presentation of Report
   D. special rules of order

   *Answer:* D) special rules of order. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:14 — “Bylaws, on the other hand—and special rules of order, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.”

**640.** `[C0198 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and rules of order require a _____ vote for suspension, while bylaws normally cannot be suspended (see 2, 25).
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* A) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:14 — “and rules of order require a two-thirds vote for suspension, while bylaws normally cannot be suspended (see 2, 25).”

**641.** `[C0198 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: These rules therefore require both previous notice and a two-thirds vote for amendment (with a vote of a majority of the entire membership as an allowable _____);
   A. Substitute
   B. interrupted
   C. alternative
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) alternative. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:14 — “These rules therefore require both previous notice and a two-thirds vote for amendment (with a vote of a majority of the entire membership as an allowable alternative);”

**642.** `[C0198 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and rules of order require a _____ vote for suspension, while bylaws normally cannot be suspended (see 2, 25).

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “and rules of order require a two-thirds vote for suspension, while bylaws normally cannot be suspended (see 2, 25).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:14 — “and rules of order require a two-thirds vote for suspension, while bylaws normally cannot be suspended (see 2, 25).”

**643.** `[C0199 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Relation of a Session’s Freedom to the Renewability of _____.

   *Answer:* Motions — “Relation of a Session’s Freedom to the Renewability of Motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:15 — “Relation of a Session’s Freedom to the Renewability of Motions.”

**644.** `[C0199 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The conditions under which a motion can be _____—that is, can be introduced as if new after having previously been made and disposed of without adoption—are closely related to the freedom of each new session, and to the distinction between a meeting and a session.
   A. renewed
   B. Another
   C. Meeting
   D. Inquiry

   *Answer:* A) renewed. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:15 — “The conditions under which a motion can be renewed—that is, can be introduced as if new after having previously been made and disposed of without adoption—are closely related to the freedom of each new session, and to the distinction between a meeting and a session.”

**645.** `[C0199 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The conditions under which a motion can be renewed—that is, can be introduced as if new after having previously been made and disposed of without adoption—are closely related to the freedom of each new session, and to the distinction between a _____ and a session.
   A. Example
   B. Divided
   C. adopted
   D. meeting

   *Answer:* D) meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:15 — “The conditions under which a motion can be renewed—that is, can be introduced as if new after having previously been made and disposed of without adoption—are closely related to the freedom of each new session, and to the distinction between a meeting and a session.”

**646.** `[C0199 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Relation of a Session’s Freedom to the _____ of Motions.
   A. Renewability
   B. Preparation
   C. Parliamentary
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) Renewability. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:15 — “Relation of a Session’s Freedom to the Renewability of Motions.”

**647.** `[C0200 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Relation of a Session’s Freedom to Limitation on _____ as Chairman Pro Tem.

   *Answer:* Appointment — “Relation of a Session’s Freedom to Limitation on Appointment as Chairman Pro Tem.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:16 — “Relation of a Session’s Freedom to Limitation on Appointment as Chairman Pro Tem.”

**648.** `[C0200 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly is to elect a chairman pro tem to hold office beyond the current session (in the event of illness or _____ of both the regular presiding officer and his alternate), notice must be given at the preceding meeting or in the call of the meeting that elects him.
   A. authorizes
   B. persuasive
   C. disability
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) disability. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:16 — “If the assembly is to elect a chairman pro tem to hold office beyond the current session (in the event of illness or disability of both the regular presiding officer and his alternate), notice must be given at the preceding meeting or in the call of the meeting that elects him.”

**649.** `[C0200 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Relation of a Session’s Freedom to Limitation on _____ as Chairman Pro Tem.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Organization
   C. Appointment
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) Appointment. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:16 — “Relation of a Session’s Freedom to Limitation on Appointment as Chairman Pro Tem.”

**650.** `[C0200 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly is to elect a chairman pro tem to hold office beyond the current session (in the event of illness or _____ of both the regular presiding officer and his alternate), notice must be given at the preceding meeting or in the call of the meeting that elects him.

   *Answer:* disability — “If the assembly is to elect a chairman pro tem to hold office beyond the current session (in the event of illness or disability of both the regular presiding officer and his alternate), notice must be given at the preceding meeting or in the call of the meeting that elects him.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:16 — “If the assembly is to elect a chairman pro tem to hold office beyond the current session (in the event of illness or disability of both the regular presiding officer and his alternate), notice must be given at the preceding meeting or in the call of the meeting that elects him.”

**651.** `[C0201 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____ (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.

   *Answer:* regular meeting — “The term regular meeting (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:1 — “The term regular meeting (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.”

**652.** `[C0201 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term regular meeting (or _____) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.
   A. Filling Blanks
   B. Point of Order
   C. general orders
   D. stated meeting

   *Answer:* D) stated meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:1 — “The term regular meeting (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.”

**653.** `[C0202 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, instead, an _____ follows the practice of scheduling the day, hour, or place of its regular meetings by resolution, notice (also referred to as the call of the meeting) must be sent to all members a reasonable time in advance of each regular meeting.

   *Answer:* organization — “If, instead, an organization follows the practice of scheduling the day, hour, or place of its regular meetings by resolution, notice (also referred to as the call of the meeting) must be sent to all members a reasonable time in advance of each regular meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:2 — “If, instead, an organization follows the practice of scheduling the day, hour, or place of its regular meetings by resolution, notice (also referred to as the call of the meeting) must be sent to all members a reasonable time in advance of each regular meeting.”

**654.** `[C0202 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, instead, an _____ follows the practice of scheduling the day, hour, or place of its regular meetings by resolution, notice (also referred to as the call of the meeting) must be sent to all members a reasonable time in advance of each regular meeting.
   A. presentation
   B. Preparation
   C. Permission
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:2 — “If, instead, an organization follows the practice of scheduling the day, hour, or place of its regular meetings by resolution, notice (also referred to as the call of the meeting) must be sent to all members a reasonable time in advance of each regular meeting.”

**655.** `[C0203 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Notice must also be sent a _____ time in advance of a convention of delegates.

   *Answer:* reasonable — “Notice must also be sent a reasonable time in advance of a convention of delegates.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:3 — “Notice must also be sent a reasonable time in advance of a convention of delegates.”

**656.** `[C0203 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In many _____, a call is sent to all members in advance of each meeting.
   A. interrupted
   B. organizations
   C. representatives
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* B) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:3 — “In many organizations, a call is sent to all members in advance of each meeting.”

**657.** `[C0203 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Notice must also be sent a _____ time in advance of a convention of delegates.
   A. Convention
   B. introduced
   C. reasonable
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* C) reasonable. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:3 — “Notice must also be sent a reasonable time in advance of a convention of delegates.”

**658.** `[C0203 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In many _____, a call is sent to all members in advance of each meeting.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In many organizations, a call is sent to all members in advance of each meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:3 — “In many organizations, a call is sent to all members in advance of each meeting.”

**659.** `[C0204 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless _____ provided in the bylaws, the number of days is computed by counting all calendar days (including holidays and weekends), excluding the day of the meeting but including the day the notice is sent.

   *Answer:* otherwise — “Unless otherwise provided in the bylaws, the number of days is computed by counting all calendar days (including holidays and weekends), excluding the day of the meeting but including the day the notice is sent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:4 — “Unless otherwise provided in the bylaws, the number of days is computed by counting all calendar days (including holidays and weekends), excluding the day of the meeting but including the day the notice is sent.”

**660.** `[C0204 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless _____ provided in the bylaws, the number of days is computed by counting all calendar days (including holidays and weekends), excluding the day of the meeting but including the day the notice is sent.
   A. otherwise
   B. Precedent
   C. delegates
   D. committee

   *Answer:* A) otherwise. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:4 — “Unless otherwise provided in the bylaws, the number of days is computed by counting all calendar days (including holidays and weekends), excluding the day of the meeting but including the day the notice is sent.”

**661.** `[C0205 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When notice is required to be sent, unless a different standard is specified that _____ is met if written notice is sent to each member either:

   *Answer:* requirement — “When notice is required to be sent, unless a different standard is specified that requirement is met if written notice is sent to each member either:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:5 — “When notice is required to be sent, unless a different standard is specified that requirement is met if written notice is sent to each member either:”

**662.** `[C0205 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When notice is required to be sent, unless a different standard is specified that _____ is met if written notice is sent to each member either:
   A. requirement
   B. Information
   C. Incidental
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) requirement. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:5 — “When notice is required to be sent, unless a different standard is specified that requirement is met if written notice is sent to each member either:”

**663.** `[C0206 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such societies, the term _____ meeting applies particularly to the regular business session.
   A. regular
   B. approve
   C. Ordered
   D. censure

   *Answer:* A) regular. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:6 — “In such societies, the term regular meeting applies particularly to the regular business session.”

**664.** `[C0206 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such societies, the term regular _____ applies particularly to the regular business session.

   *Answer:* meeting — “In such societies, the term regular meeting applies particularly to the regular business session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:6 — “In such societies, the term regular meeting applies particularly to the regular business session.”

**665.** `[C0206 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some societies have frequent meetings for social or cultural purposes at which business may be _____, and also hold a session every month or quarter especially for business.
   A. Incidental
   B. Particular
   C. assemblies
   D. transacted

   *Answer:* D) transacted. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:6 — “Some societies have frequent meetings for social or cultural purposes at which business may be transacted, and also hold a session every month or quarter especially for business.”

**666.** `[C0206 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some societies have frequent meetings for social or cultural purposes at which business may be _____, and also hold a session every month or quarter especially for business.

   *Answer:* transacted — “Some societies have frequent meetings for social or cultural purposes at which business may be transacted, and also hold a session every month or quarter especially for business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:6 — “Some societies have frequent meetings for social or cultural purposes at which business may be transacted, and also hold a session every month or quarter especially for business.”

**667.** `[C0207 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether _____ quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.

   *Answer:* no more than a — “Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:7 — “Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.”

**668.** `[C0207 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a _____ intervenes between the two sessions.
   A. and Example: 1(a), 1(b)
   B. control of the assembly
   C. Article VII: Committees
   D. quarterly time interval

   *Answer:* D) quarterly time interval. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:7 — “Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.”

**669.** `[C0207 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In this book, it is _____ that no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between two sessions if the second session begins at any time during or before the third calendar month after the calendar month in which the first session ends.
   A. understood
   B. convention
   C. Substitute
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* A) understood. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:7 — “In this book, it is understood that no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between two sessions if the second session begins at any time during or before the third calendar month after the calendar month in which the first session ends.”

**670.** `[C0207 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, with _____ to a session held in January, no more than a quarterly time interval has elapsed since the previous session if that session ended on or after October 1st of the preceding calendar year;

   *Answer:* reference — “For example, with reference to a session held in January, no more than a quarterly time interval has elapsed since the previous session if that session ended on or after October 1st of the preceding calendar year;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:7 — “For example, with reference to a session held in January, no more than a quarterly time interval has elapsed since the previous session if that session ended on or after October 1st of the preceding calendar year;”

**671.** `[C0207 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In this book, it is _____ that no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between two sessions if the second session begins at any time during or before the third calendar month after the calendar month in which the first session ends.

   *Answer:* understood — “In this book, it is understood that no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between two sessions if the second session begins at any time during or before the third calendar month after the calendar month in which the first session ends.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:7 — “In this book, it is understood that no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between two sessions if the second session begins at any time during or before the third calendar month after the calendar month in which the first session ends.”

**672.** `[C0208 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If two business sessions are separated by _____ quarterly time interval—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).

   *Answer:* more than a — “If two business sessions are separated by more than a quarterly time interval—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:8 — “If two business sessions are separated by more than a quarterly time interval—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).”

**673.** `[C0208 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If two business sessions are separated by more than a _____—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).
   A. and Example: 1(a), 1(b)
   B. quarterly time interval
   C. ARTICLE VII: COMMITTEES
   D. Form of Detailed Report

   *Answer:* B) quarterly time interval.
