See RONR (12th ed.) §9:8 — “If two business sessions are separated by more than a quarterly time interval—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).”

**674.** `[C0209 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If two _____ regular business sessions are separated by no more than a quarterly time interval, then—provided that there is no specified portion of the membership whose term expires before the start of the later session—there are several ways in which business can go over from the earlier session to the later one:

   *Answer:* consecutive — “If two consecutive regular business sessions are separated by no more than a quarterly time interval, then—provided that there is no specified portion of the membership whose term expires before the start of the later session—there are several ways in which business can go over from the earlier session to the later one:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:9 — “If two consecutive regular business sessions are separated by no more than a quarterly time interval, then—provided that there is no specified portion of the membership whose term expires before the start of the later session—there are several ways in which business can go over from the earlier session to the later one:”

**675.** `[C0209 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If two _____ regular business sessions are separated by no more than a quarterly time interval, then—provided that there is no specified portion of the membership whose term expires before the start of the later session—there are several ways in which business can go over from the earlier session to the later one:
   A. constitution
   B. Unfinished
   C. Information
   D. consecutive

   *Answer:* D) consecutive. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:9 — “If two consecutive regular business sessions are separated by no more than a quarterly time interval, then—provided that there is no specified portion of the membership whose term expires before the start of the later session—there are several ways in which business can go over from the earlier session to the later one:”

**676.** `[C0210 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The only way for business to be carried over directly from one session to some later regular session _____ the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.

   *Answer:* beyond — “The only way for business to be carried over directly from one session to some later regular session beyond the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:10 — “The only way for business to be carried over directly from one session to some later regular session beyond the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.”

**677.** `[C0210 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The only way for business to be carried over _____ from one session to some later regular session beyond the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.
   A. division
   B. Question
   C. directly
   D. dilatory

   *Answer:* C) directly. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:10 — “The only way for business to be carried over directly from one session to some later regular session beyond the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.”

**678.** `[C0211 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains _____ control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.

   *Answer:* within the — “When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:11 — “When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.”

**679.** `[C0211 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the _____ as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.
   A. Appointment of Committee
   B. Recommending amendments
   C. ARTICLE VII: COMMITTEES
   D. control of the assembly

   *Answer:* D) control of the assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:11 — “When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.”

**680.** `[C0212 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any business that falls within the objects of the society as defined in its bylaws (or, in the case of a board, any business within the authority of the board) can be transacted at any regular meeting (provided that the _____ rules relating to action already taken, or to matters not finally disposed of and remaining within the control of the assembly, are complied with in cases where they apply;

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Any business that falls within the objects of the society as defined in its bylaws (or, in the case of a board, any business within the authority of the board) can be transacted at any regular meeting (provided that the parliamentary rules relating to action already taken, or to matters not finally disposed of and remaining within the control of the assembly, are complied with in cases where they apply;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:12 — “Any business that falls within the objects of the society as defined in its bylaws (or, in the case of a board, any business within the authority of the board) can be transacted at any regular meeting (provided that the parliamentary rules relating to action already taken, or to matters not finally disposed of and remaining within the control of the assembly, are complied with in cases where they apply;”

**681.** `[C0212 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any business that falls within the objects of the society as defined in its bylaws (or, in the case of a board, any business within the authority of the board) can be transacted at any regular meeting (provided that the _____ rules relating to action already taken, or to matters not finally disposed of and remaining within the control of the assembly, are complied with in cases where they apply;
   A. parliamentary
   B. Organization
   C. representatives
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:12 — “Any business that falls within the objects of the society as defined in its bylaws (or, in the case of a board, any business within the authority of the board) can be transacted at any regular meeting (provided that the parliamentary rules relating to action already taken, or to matters not finally disposed of and remaining within the control of the assembly, are complied with in cases where they apply;”

**682.** `[C0213 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.

   *Answer:* special meeting — “A special meeting (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:13 — “A special meeting (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.”

**683.** `[C0213 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A special meeting (or _____) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.
   A. Insert, or Add
   B. The Framing of
   C. special orders
   D. called meeting

   *Answer:* D) called meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:13 — “A special meeting (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.”

**684.** `[C0213 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Notice of the time, place, and purpose of the meeting, clearly and _____ describing the subject matter of the motions or items of business to be brought up, must be sent to all members a reasonable number of days in advance.
   A. Convention
   B. deliberative
   C. constitution
   D. specifically

   *Answer:* D) specifically. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:13 — “Notice of the time, place, and purpose of the meeting, clearly and specifically describing the subject matter of the motions or items of business to be brought up, must be sent to all members a reasonable number of days in advance.”

**685.** `[C0213 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reason for special meetings is to deal with matters that may arise between regular meetings and that require action by the society before the next regular meeting, or to dedicate an entire session to one or more _____ matters.

   *Answer:* particular — “The reason for special meetings is to deal with matters that may arise between regular meetings and that require action by the society before the next regular meeting, or to dedicate an entire session to one or more particular matters.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:13 — “The reason for special meetings is to deal with matters that may arise between regular meetings and that require action by the society before the next regular meeting, or to dedicate an entire session to one or more particular matters.”

**686.** `[C0213 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As in the case of a regular meeting, the session of a special meeting in an ordinary society is normally _____ in a single meeting, unless the assembly at the special meeting schedules an adjourned meeting (see below).
   A. reception
   B. concluded
   C. amendment
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* B) concluded. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:13 — “As in the case of a regular meeting, the session of a special meeting in an ordinary society is normally concluded in a single meeting, unless the assembly at the special meeting schedules an adjourned meeting (see below).”

**687.** `[C0214 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (b) when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal _____ procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “or (b) when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal disciplinary procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:14 — “or (b) when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal disciplinary procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).”

**688.** `[C0214 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A section of the bylaws that _____ the calling of special meetings should prescribe:
   A. authorizes
   B. subsidiary
   C. recognized
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* A) authorizes. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:14 — “A section of the bylaws that authorizes the calling of special meetings should prescribe:”

**689.** `[C0214 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or (b) when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal _____ procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).
   A. undebatable
   B. disciplinary
   C. Announcements
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:14 — “or (b) when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal disciplinary procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).”

**690.** `[C0214 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A section of the bylaws that _____ the calling of special meetings should prescribe:

   *Answer:* authorizes — “A section of the bylaws that authorizes the calling of special meetings should prescribe:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:14 — “A section of the bylaws that authorizes the calling of special meetings should prescribe:”

**691.** `[C0215 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The only business that can be _____ at a special meeting is that which has been specified in the call of the meeting.

   *Answer:* transacted — “The only business that can be transacted at a special meeting is that which has been specified in the call of the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:15 — “The only business that can be transacted at a special meeting is that which has been specified in the call of the meeting.”

**692.** `[C0215 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This rule, however, does not preclude the _____ of privileged motions, or of any subsidiary, incidental, or other motions that may arise in connection with the transaction of such business or the conduct of the meeting.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. requirement
   C. Preparation
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:15 — “This rule, however, does not preclude the consideration of privileged motions, or of any subsidiary, incidental, or other motions that may arise in connection with the transaction of such business or the conduct of the meeting.”

**693.** `[C0215 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The only business that can be _____ at a special meeting is that which has been specified in the call of the meeting.
   A. authorizes
   B. transacted
   C. introduced
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) transacted. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:15 — “The only business that can be transacted at a special meeting is that which has been specified in the call of the meeting.”

**694.** `[C0215 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This rule, however, does not preclude the _____ of privileged motions, or of any subsidiary, incidental, or other motions that may arise in connection with the transaction of such business or the conduct of the meeting.

   *Answer:* consideration — “This rule, however, does not preclude the consideration of privileged motions, or of any subsidiary, incidental, or other motions that may arise in connection with the transaction of such business or the conduct of the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:15 — “This rule, however, does not preclude the consideration of privileged motions, or of any subsidiary, incidental, or other motions that may arise in connection with the transaction of such business or the conduct of the meeting.”

**695.** `[C0216 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ that business transacted at a special meeting be specified in the call should not be confused with a requirement that previous notice of a motion be given.

   *Answer:* requirement — “The requirement that business transacted at a special meeting be specified in the call should not be confused with a requirement that previous notice of a motion be given.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:16 — “The requirement that business transacted at a special meeting be specified in the call should not be confused with a requirement that previous notice of a motion be given.”

**696.** `[C0216 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although the call of a special meeting must state the purpose of the meeting, it need not give the exact content of _____ motions that will be considered.
   A. resolution
   B. convention
   C. individual
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* C) individual. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:16 — “Although the call of a special meeting must state the purpose of the meeting, it need not give the exact content of individual motions that will be considered.”

**697.** `[C0216 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The requirement that business transacted at a special meeting be _____ in the call should not be confused with a requirement that previous notice of a motion be given.

   *Answer:* specified — “The requirement that business transacted at a special meeting be specified in the call should not be confused with a requirement that previous notice of a motion be given.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:16 — “The requirement that business transacted at a special meeting be specified in the call should not be confused with a requirement that previous notice of a motion be given.”

**698.** `[C0216 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the call of a special meeting must state the purpose of the meeting, it need not give the exact content of _____ motions that will be considered.

   *Answer:* individual — “Although the call of a special meeting must state the purpose of the meeting, it need not give the exact content of individual motions that will be considered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:16 — “Although the call of a special meeting must state the purpose of the meeting, it need not give the exact content of individual motions that will be considered.”

**699.** `[C0217 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.

   *Answer:* adjourned meeting — “An adjourned meeting is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:17 — “An adjourned meeting is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.”

**700.** `[C0217 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An adjourned meeting is a meeting in _____ of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.
   A. continuation
   B. presentation
   C. immediately
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) continuation. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:17 — “An adjourned meeting is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.”

**701.** `[C0217 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The name adjourned meeting means that the meeting is scheduled for a particular time (and place, if it is not otherwise _____) by the assembly’s “adjourning to” or “adjourning until” that time and place.

   *Answer:* established — “The name adjourned meeting means that the meeting is scheduled for a particular time (and place, if it is not otherwise established) by the assembly’s “adjourning to” or “adjourning until” that time and place.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:17 — “The name adjourned meeting means that the meeting is scheduled for a particular time (and place, if it is not otherwise established) by the assembly’s “adjourning to” or “adjourning until” that time and place.”

**702.** `[C0217 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a regular meeting or a special meeting is unable to complete its work, an adjourned meeting can be scheduled for later the same day or some other _____ time before the next regular meeting, by the adoption (as applicable) of a main or a privileged motion to fix the time to which to adjourn, or a main motion to adjourn until the specified time (see 21, 22).
   A. authorizes
   B. privileged
   C. Particular
   D. convenient

   *Answer:* D) convenient. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:17 — “If a regular meeting or a special meeting is unable to complete its work, an adjourned meeting can be scheduled for later the same day or some other convenient time before the next regular meeting, by the adoption (as applicable) of a main or a privileged motion to fix the time to which to adjourn, or a main motion to adjourn until the specified time (see 21, 22).”

**703.** `[C0217 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, the adjourned meeting is sometimes spoken of as “an _____ of” the regular or special meeting.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “In such a case, the adjourned meeting is sometimes spoken of as “an adjournment of” the regular or special meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:17 — “In such a case, the adjourned meeting is sometimes spoken of as “an adjournment of” the regular or special meeting.”

**704.** `[C0218 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] _____,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.

   *Answer:* meeting — “When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:18 — “When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.”

**705.** `[C0218 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean _____ in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.
   A. Suspend
   B. carried
   C. session
   D. Charges

   *Answer:* C) session. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:18 — “When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.”

**706.** `[C0219 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An adjourned meeting takes up its work at the point where it was interrupted in the order of business or in the _____ of the question that was postponed to the adjourned meeting, except that the minutes of the preceding meeting are first read.

   *Answer:* consideration — “An adjourned meeting takes up its work at the point where it was interrupted in the order of business or in the consideration of the question that was postponed to the adjourned meeting, except that the minutes of the preceding meeting are first read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:19 — “An adjourned meeting takes up its work at the point where it was interrupted in the order of business or in the consideration of the question that was postponed to the adjourned meeting, except that the minutes of the preceding meeting are first read.”

**707.** `[C0219 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An adjourned meeting takes up its work at the point where it was interrupted in the order of business or in the _____ of the question that was postponed to the adjourned meeting, except that the minutes of the preceding meeting are first read.
   A. representatives
   B. specification
   C. consideration
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:19 — “An adjourned meeting takes up its work at the point where it was interrupted in the order of business or in the consideration of the question that was postponed to the adjourned meeting, except that the minutes of the preceding meeting are first read.”

**708.** `[C0220 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Certain types of societies may hold only one business meeting of the general membership each year, perhaps leaving the management of the _____’s affairs in the meantime to a board.

   *Answer:* organization — “Certain types of societies may hold only one business meeting of the general membership each year, perhaps leaving the management of the organization’s affairs in the meantime to a board.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:21 — “Certain types of societies may hold only one business meeting of the general membership each year, perhaps leaving the management of the organization’s affairs in the meantime to a board.”

**709.** `[C0220 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Certain types of societies may hold only one business meeting of the general membership each year, perhaps leaving the management of the _____’s affairs in the meantime to a board.
   A. organization
   B. subsequently
   C. Convention
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:21 — “Certain types of societies may hold only one business meeting of the general membership each year, perhaps leaving the management of the organization’s affairs in the meantime to a board.”

**710.** `[C0221 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In local _____ that hold regular business meetings throughout the year, however, the bylaws may provide that one of these regular meetings held at a specified time each year shall be known as the annual meeting.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In local organizations that hold regular business meetings throughout the year, however, the bylaws may provide that one of these regular meetings held at a specified time each year shall be known as the annual meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:22 — “In local organizations that hold regular business meetings throughout the year, however, the bylaws may provide that one of these regular meetings held at a specified time each year shall be known as the annual meeting.”

**711.** `[C0221 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The only _____ between this kind of annual meeting and the other regular meetings is that the annual reports of officers and standing committees, the election of officers, and any other items of business that the bylaws may prescribe for the annual meeting are in order, besides the ordinary business that may come up.
   A. Substitute
   B. Definitely
   C. landscaped
   D. difference

   *Answer:* D) difference. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:22 — “The only difference between this kind of annual meeting and the other regular meetings is that the annual reports of officers and standing committees, the election of officers, and any other items of business that the bylaws may prescribe for the annual meeting are in order, besides the ordinary business that may come up.”

**712.** `[C0221 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The minutes of the _____ regular meeting are read and approved as usual at the annual meeting, and the minutes of the annual meeting are read and approved at the next regular meeting.

   *Answer:* previous — “The minutes of the previous regular meeting are read and approved as usual at the annual meeting, and the minutes of the annual meeting are read and approved at the next regular meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:22 — “The minutes of the previous regular meeting are read and approved as usual at the annual meeting, and the minutes of the annual meeting are read and approved at the next regular meeting.”

**713.** `[C0221 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of the previous regular meeting are read and approved as usual at the annual meeting, and the minutes of the annual meeting are read and approved at the next regular meeting.
   A. minutes
   B. Session
   C. Adopted
   D. Motions

   *Answer:* A) minutes. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:22 — “The minutes of the previous regular meeting are read and approved as usual at the annual meeting, and the minutes of the annual meeting are read and approved at the next regular meeting.”

**714.** `[C0221 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In local _____ that hold regular business meetings throughout the year, however, the bylaws may provide that one of these regular meetings held at a specified time each year shall be known as the annual meeting.
   A. Vice-President
   B. requirement
   C. constitution
   D. organizations

   *Answer:* D) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:22 — “In local organizations that hold regular business meetings throughout the year, however, the bylaws may provide that one of these regular meetings held at a specified time each year shall be known as the annual meeting.”

**715.** `[C0222 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Business that is required to be attended to “at the annual meeting” can be taken up at any time (when it is in order) during the session of the annual meeting, or, in other words, either at that meeting as originally convened or at any _____ of it.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “Business that is required to be attended to “at the annual meeting” can be taken up at any time (when it is in order) during the session of the annual meeting, or, in other words, either at that meeting as originally convened or at any adjournment of it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:23 — “Business that is required to be attended to “at the annual meeting” can be taken up at any time (when it is in order) during the session of the annual meeting, or, in other words, either at that meeting as originally convened or at any adjournment of it.”

**716.** `[C0222 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This is usually unwise, however, unless completing the item of business during the session proves impossible or _____.
   A. Reconsider
   B. requirement
   C. Unfinished
   D. impractical

   *Answer:* D) impractical. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:23 — “This is usually unwise, however, unless completing the item of business during the session proves impossible or impractical.”

**717.** `[C0222 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Business that is required to be attended to “at the annual meeting” can be taken up at any time (when it is in order) during the session of the annual meeting, or, in other words, either at that meeting as originally convened or at any _____ of it.
   A. requirement
   B. resolutions
   C. Information
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* D) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:23 — “Business that is required to be attended to “at the annual meeting” can be taken up at any time (when it is in order) during the session of the annual meeting, or, in other words, either at that meeting as originally convened or at any adjournment of it.”

**718.** `[C0222 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This is usually unwise, however, unless completing the item of business during the session proves impossible or _____.

   *Answer:* impractical — “This is usually unwise, however, unless completing the item of business during the session proves impossible or impractical.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:23 — “This is usually unwise, however, unless completing the item of business during the session proves impossible or impractical.”

**719.** `[C0223 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.

   *Answer:* executive session — “An executive session in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:24 — “An executive session in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.”

**720.** `[C0223 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to go into (or out of) executive session is a question of privilege (19), and is adopted by a _____ vote.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* D) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:24 — “A motion to go into (or out of) executive session is a question of privilege (19), and is adopted by a majority vote.”

**721.** `[C0223 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An executive session in general _____ usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “An executive session in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:24 — “An executive session in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.”

**722.** `[C0223 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This term originally referred to the _____ of executive business—that is, presidential nominations to appointive offices, and treaties—behind closed doors in the United States Senate.
   A. specification
   B. consideration
   C. deliberative
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:24 — “This term originally referred to the consideration of executive business—that is, presidential nominations to appointive offices, and treaties—behind closed doors in the United States Senate.”

**723.** `[C0223 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The practice of _____ operating under the lodge system is equivalent to holding all regular meetings in executive session.

   *Answer:* organizations — “The practice of organizations operating under the lodge system is equivalent to holding all regular meetings in executive session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:24 — “The practice of organizations operating under the lodge system is equivalent to holding all regular meetings in executive session.”

**724.** `[C0224 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Attendance at an Executive _____ or Other Closed Session.

   *Answer:* Session — “Attendance at an Executive Session or Other Closed Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:25 — “Attendance at an Executive Session or Other Closed Session.”

**725.** `[C0224 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ at an Executive Session or Other Closed Session.

   *Answer:* Attendance — “Attendance at an Executive Session or Other Closed Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:25 — “Attendance at an Executive Session or Other Closed Session.”

**726.** `[C0224 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, in the case of a board or committee meeting being held in executive session, all persons—whether or not they are members of the _____—who are not members of the board or committee (and who are not otherwise specifically invited or entitled to attend) are excluded from the meeting.

   *Answer:* organization — “Thus, in the case of a board or committee meeting being held in executive session, all persons—whether or not they are members of the organization—who are not members of the board or committee (and who are not otherwise specifically invited or entitled to attend) are excluded from the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:25 — “Thus, in the case of a board or committee meeting being held in executive session, all persons—whether or not they are members of the organization—who are not members of the board or committee (and who are not otherwise specifically invited or entitled to attend) are excluded from the meeting.”

**727.** `[C0224 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, in the case of a board or committee meeting being held in executive session, all persons—whether or not they are members of the _____—who are not members of the board or committee (and who are not otherwise specifically invited or entitled to attend) are excluded from the meeting.
   A. interrupted
   B. consideration
   C. Particular
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:25 — “Thus, in the case of a board or committee meeting being held in executive session, all persons—whether or not they are members of the organization—who are not members of the board or committee (and who are not otherwise specifically invited or entitled to attend) are excluded from the meeting.”

**728.** `[C0225 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to _____ Previously Adopted (35).

   *Answer:* Amend Something — “If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:26 — “If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**729.** `[C0225 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something _____ (35).
   A. Previously Adopted
   B. obtained the floor
   C. Article II: Object
   D. standing committee

   *Answer:* A) Previously Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:26 — “If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**730.** `[C0225 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules Relating to the Secrecy of an _____ Session.

   *Answer:* Executive — “Rules Relating to the Secrecy of an Executive Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:26 — “Rules Relating to the Secrecy of an Executive Session.”

**731.** `[C0225 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The general rule is that anything that occurs in executive session may not be divulged to _____ (except any entitled to attend).
   A. sustaining
   B. persuasive
   C. introduced
   D. nonmembers

   *Answer:* D) nonmembers. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:26 — “The general rule is that anything that occurs in executive session may not be divulged to nonmembers (except any entitled to attend).”

**732.** `[C0225 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, action taken, as distinct from that which was said in debate, may be divulged to the extent—and only to the extent—_____ to carry it out.

   *Answer:* necessary — “However, action taken, as distinct from that which was said in debate, may be divulged to the extent—and only to the extent—necessary to carry it out.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:26 — “However, action taken, as distinct from that which was said in debate, may be divulged to the extent—and only to the extent—necessary to carry it out.”

**733.** `[C0226 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member of a society can be punished under _____ procedure if he violates the secrecy of an executive session.

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “A member of a society can be punished under disciplinary procedure if he violates the secrecy of an executive session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:27 — “A member of a society can be punished under disciplinary procedure if he violates the secrecy of an executive session.”

**734.** `[C0226 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Anyone else permitted to be present is _____ not to divulge anything that occurred.
   A. interrupted
   B. honor-bound
   C. Substitute
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) honor-bound. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:27 — “Anyone else permitted to be present is honor-bound not to divulge anything that occurred.”

**735.** `[C0226 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member of a society can be punished under disciplinary procedure if he violates the secrecy of an _____ session.

   *Answer:* executive — “A member of a society can be punished under disciplinary procedure if he violates the secrecy of an executive session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:27 — “A member of a society can be punished under disciplinary procedure if he violates the secrecy of an executive session.”

**736.** `[C0226 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the minutes of an executive session must be _____ for approval at an executive session held solely for that purpose, the brief minutes of the latter meeting are, or are assumed to be, approved by that meeting.
   A. considered
   B. substitute
   C. landscaped
   D. committees

   *Answer:* A) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:27 — “When the minutes of an executive session must be considered for approval at an executive session held solely for that purpose, the brief minutes of the latter meeting are, or are assumed to be, approved by that meeting.”

**737.** `[C0226 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (For _____ rules regarding access to minutes and other records, including those kept by boards and committees, that are protected by the secrecy of an executive session, see

   *Answer:* additional — “(For additional rules regarding access to minutes and other records, including those kept by boards and committees, that are protected by the secrecy of an executive session, see”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:27 — “(For additional rules regarding access to minutes and other records, including those kept by boards and committees, that are protected by the secrecy of an executive session, see”

**738.** `[C0227 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ assembly or committee is normally entitled to determine whether nonmembers may attend or be excluded from its meetings (even when not in executive session).

   *Answer:* deliberative — “A deliberative assembly or committee is normally entitled to determine whether nonmembers may attend or be excluded from its meetings (even when not in executive session).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:28 — “A deliberative assembly or committee is normally entitled to determine whether nonmembers may attend or be excluded from its meetings (even when not in executive session).”

**739.** `[C0227 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Many public and _____ bodies, however, are governed by sunshine laws—that is, their meetings must be open to the public.
   A. Reconsider
   B. introduced
   C. settlement
   D. semipublic

   *Answer:* D) semipublic. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:28 — “Many public and semipublic bodies, however, are governed by sunshine laws—that is, their meetings must be open to the public.”

**740.** `[C0227 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Normally, such laws have no application to private, _____ bodies.

   *Answer:* nongovernmental — “Normally, such laws have no application to private, nongovernmental bodies.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:28 — “Normally, such laws have no application to private, nongovernmental bodies.”

**741.** `[C0227 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ assembly or committee is normally entitled to determine whether nonmembers may attend or be excluded from its meetings (even when not in executive session).
   A. Preparation
   B. deliberative
   C. subsequently
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:28 — “A deliberative assembly or committee is normally entitled to determine whether nonmembers may attend or be excluded from its meetings (even when not in executive session).”

**742.** `[C0227 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Many public and _____ bodies, however, are governed by sunshine laws—that is, their meetings must be open to the public.

   *Answer:* semipublic — “Many public and semipublic bodies, however, are governed by sunshine laws—that is, their meetings must be open to the public.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:28 — “Many public and semipublic bodies, however, are governed by sunshine laws—that is, their meetings must be open to the public.”

**743.** `[C0228 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Similarly, in some private _____ such as church councils, parishioners may be permitted to attend.

   *Answer:* organizations — “Similarly, in some private organizations such as church councils, parishioners may be permitted to attend.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:29 — “Similarly, in some private organizations such as church councils, parishioners may be permitted to attend.”

**744.** `[C0228 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: These attendees are not members of the meeting body and ordinarily have no right to _____.
   A. participate
   B. Incidental
   C. immediately
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) participate. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:29 — “These attendees are not members of the meeting body and ordinarily have no right to participate.”

**745.** `[C0228 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some bodies, _____ public ones, may invite nonmembers to express their views, but this is done under the control of the presiding officer subject to any relevant rules adopted by the body and subject to appeal by a member.

   *Answer:* especially — “Some bodies, especially public ones, may invite nonmembers to express their views, but this is done under the control of the presiding officer subject to any relevant rules adopted by the body and subject to appeal by a member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:29 — “Some bodies, especially public ones, may invite nonmembers to express their views, but this is done under the control of the presiding officer subject to any relevant rules adopted by the body and subject to appeal by a member.”

**746.** `[C0228 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Often, by rule or practice, time limits are placed on speakers and _____ is closely monitored.
   A. committee
   B. relevance
   C. delegates
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* B) relevance. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:29 — “Often, by rule or practice, time limits are placed on speakers and relevance is closely monitored.”

**747.** `[C0228 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Similarly, in some private _____ such as church councils, parishioners may be permitted to attend.
   A. resolutions
   B. organizations
   C. immediately
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* B) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:29 — “Similarly, in some private organizations such as church councils, parishioners may be permitted to attend.”

**748.** `[C0229 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Extension of _____ Law to Electronic Meetings.

   *Answer:* Parliamentary — “Extension of Parliamentary Law to Electronic Meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:30 — “Extension of Parliamentary Law to Electronic Meetings.”

**749.** `[C0229 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Extension of _____ Law to Electronic Meetings.
   A. Organization
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. Parliamentary
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* C) Parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:30 — “Extension of Parliamentary Law to Electronic Meetings.”

**750.** `[C0230 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Among some organizations, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at _____— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.

   *Answer:* electronic meetings — “Among some organizations, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at electronic meetings— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:31 — “Among some organizations, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at electronic meetings— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.”

**751.** `[C0230 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Among some _____, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at electronic meetings— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.
   A. characteristics
   B. undebatable
   C. organizations
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:31 — “Among some organizations, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at electronic meetings— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.”

**752.** `[C0231 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If _____ meetings are to be authorized, it is advisable to adopt additional rules pertaining to their conduct (see Additional Rules for the Conduct of Electronic Meetings, below).

   *Answer:* electronic — “If electronic meetings are to be authorized, it is advisable to adopt additional rules pertaining to their conduct (see Additional Rules for the Conduct of Electronic Meetings, below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:32 — “If electronic meetings are to be authorized, it is advisable to adopt additional rules pertaining to their conduct (see Additional Rules for the Conduct of Electronic Meetings, below).”

**753.** `[C0231 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If _____ meetings are to be authorized, it is advisable to adopt additional rules pertaining to their conduct (see Additional Rules for the Conduct of Electronic Meetings, below).
   A. assemblies
   B. landscaped
   C. electronic
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) electronic. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:32 — “If electronic meetings are to be authorized, it is advisable to adopt additional rules pertaining to their conduct (see Additional Rules for the Conduct of Electronic Meetings, below).”

**754.** `[C0232 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Various provisions for electronic meetings are possible, so that more than the minimum standard of an _____ may be required.
   A. audioconference
   B. recommendation
   C. constitution
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) audioconference. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:33 — “Various provisions for electronic meetings are possible, so that more than the minimum standard of an audioconference may be required.”

**755.** `[C0232 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Various provisions for electronic meetings are possible, so that more than the minimum standard of an _____ may be required.

   *Answer:* audioconference — “Various provisions for electronic meetings are possible, so that more than the minimum standard of an audioconference may be required.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:33 — “Various provisions for electronic meetings are possible, so that more than the minimum standard of an audioconference may be required.”

**756.** `[C0233 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is important to understand that, regardless of the technology used, the opportunity for simultaneous aural _____ is essential to the deliberative character of the meeting.

   *Answer:* communication — “It is important to understand that, regardless of the technology used, the opportunity for simultaneous aural communication is essential to the deliberative character of the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:34 — “It is important to understand that, regardless of the technology used, the opportunity for simultaneous aural communication is essential to the deliberative character of the meeting.”

**757.** `[C0233 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is important to understand that, regardless of the technology used, the opportunity for simultaneous aural communication is essential to the _____ character of the meeting.
   A. Definitely
   B. deliberative
   C. Convention
   D. specification

   *Answer:* B) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:34 — “It is important to understand that, regardless of the technology used, the opportunity for simultaneous aural communication is essential to the deliberative character of the meeting.”

**758.** `[C0233 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any such effort may achieve a consultative character, but it is foreign to the deliberative process as understood under _____ law.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Any such effort may achieve a consultative character, but it is foreign to the deliberative process as understood under parliamentary law.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:34 — “Any such effort may achieve a consultative character, but it is foreign to the deliberative process as understood under parliamentary law.”

**759.** `[C0233 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any such effort may achieve a consultative character, but it is foreign to the deliberative process as understood under _____ law.
   A. parliamentary
   B. adjournment
   C. President-Elect
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:34 — “Any such effort may achieve a consultative character, but it is foreign to the deliberative process as understood under parliamentary law.”

**760.** `[C0234 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As in the case of a board or any assembly, committees that are expressly _____ by the bylaws can hold a valid electronic meeting only if authorized in the bylaws to do so.
   A. Particular
   B. convention
   C. Preparation
   D. established

   *Answer:* D) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:35 — “As in the case of a board or any assembly, committees that are expressly established by the bylaws can hold a valid electronic meeting only if authorized in the bylaws to do so.”

**761.** `[C0234 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As in the case of a board or any assembly, committees that are expressly _____ by the bylaws can hold a valid electronic meeting only if authorized in the bylaws to do so.

   *Answer:* established — “As in the case of a board or any assembly, committees that are expressly established by the bylaws can hold a valid electronic meeting only if authorized in the bylaws to do so.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:35 — “As in the case of a board or any assembly, committees that are expressly established by the bylaws can hold a valid electronic meeting only if authorized in the bylaws to do so.”

**762.** `[C0235 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the _____ to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;

   *Answer:* right — “If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the right to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:36 — “If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the right to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;”

**763.** `[C0235 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Rules for the Conduct of Electronic Meetings.
   A. resolution
   B. privileged
   C. Additional
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) Additional. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:36 — “Additional Rules for the Conduct of Electronic Meetings.”

**764.** `[C0235 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the right to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such _____;

   *Answer:* participation — “If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the right to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:36 — “If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the right to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;”

**765.** `[C0235 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and, _____, whether there is required to be a central location for members who wish to attend meetings in person.
   A. conversely
   B. Unfinished
   C. settlement
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* A) conversely. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:36 — “and, conversely, whether there is required to be a central location for members who wish to attend meetings in person.”

**766.** `[C0235 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The notice of an electronic meeting must include an adequate description of how to participate in it (for example, the telephone number to call for a _____ must be provided).

   *Answer:* teleconference — “The notice of an electronic meeting must include an adequate description of how to participate in it (for example, the telephone number to call for a teleconference must be provided).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:36 — “The notice of an electronic meeting must include an adequate description of how to participate in it (for example, the telephone number to call for a teleconference must be provided).”

**767.** `[C0785 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in essentially the same connection, the motion is said to be _____.

   *Answer:* renewed — “If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in essentially the same connection, the motion is said to be renewed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:1 — “If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in essentially the same connection, the motion is said to be renewed.”

**768.** `[C0785 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in _____ the same connection, the motion is said to be renewed.
   A. Information
   B. Convention
   C. essentially
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) essentially. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:1 — “If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in essentially the same connection, the motion is said to be renewed.”

**769.** `[C0785 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A previously considered motion may become a _____ different question through a significant change in the wording or because of a difference in the time or circumstances in which it is proposed, and such a motion may thus be in order when it could not otherwise be renewed.

   *Answer:* substantially — “A previously considered motion may become a substantially different question through a significant change in the wording or because of a difference in the time or circumstances in which it is proposed, and such a motion may thus be in order when it could not otherwise be renewed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:1 — “A previously considered motion may become a substantially different question through a significant change in the wording or because of a difference in the time or circumstances in which it is proposed, and such a motion may thus be in order when it could not otherwise be renewed.”

**770.** `[C0785 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A previously considered motion may become a _____ different question through a significant change in the wording or because of a difference in the time or circumstances in which it is proposed, and such a motion may thus be in order when it could not otherwise be renewed.
   A. substantially
   B. constitution
   C. Organization
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) substantially. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:1 — “A previously considered motion may become a substantially different question through a significant change in the wording or because of a difference in the time or circumstances in which it is proposed, and such a motion may thus be in order when it could not otherwise be renewed.”

**771.** `[C0786 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rules _____ renewal of motions do not apply to any motion that was last disposed of by being withdrawn.

   *Answer:* restricting — “The rules restricting renewal of motions do not apply to any motion that was last disposed of by being withdrawn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:2 — “The rules restricting renewal of motions do not apply to any motion that was last disposed of by being withdrawn.”

**772.** `[C0786 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion that is withdrawn becomes as if it had never been made and can be renewed whenever it would be _____ in order.
   A. recognized
   B. Definitely
   C. incidental
   D. originally

   *Answer:* D) originally. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:2 — “A motion that is withdrawn becomes as if it had never been made and can be renewed whenever it would be originally in order.”

**773.** `[C0786 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rules _____ renewal of motions do not apply to any motion that was last disposed of by being withdrawn.
   A. Definitely
   B. restricting
   C. resolutions
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) restricting. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:2 — “The rules restricting renewal of motions do not apply to any motion that was last disposed of by being withdrawn.”

**774.** `[C0786 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion that is withdrawn becomes as if it had never been made and can be renewed whenever it would be _____ in order.

   *Answer:* originally — “A motion that is withdrawn becomes as if it had never been made and can be renewed whenever it would be originally in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:2 — “A motion that is withdrawn becomes as if it had never been made and can be renewed whenever it would be originally in order.”

**775.** `[C0787 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ rules are derived from the first principle stated above, by which a motion is not renewable at the same session unless the question has become somehow different.

   *Answer:* following — “The following rules are derived from the first principle stated above, by which a motion is not renewable at the same session unless the question has become somehow different.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:4 — “The following rules are derived from the first principle stated above, by which a motion is not renewable at the same session unless the question has become somehow different.”

**776.** `[C0787 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ rules are derived from the first principle stated above, by which a motion is not renewable at the same session unless the question has become somehow different.
   A. following
   B. postponed
   C. extending
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* A) following. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:4 — “The following rules are derived from the first principle stated above, by which a motion is not renewable at the same session unless the question has become somehow different.”

**777.** `[C0788 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ Cases of the General Rule Against Renewal.

   *Answer:* Particular — “Particular Cases of the General Rule Against Renewal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:5 — “Particular Cases of the General Rule Against Renewal.”

**778.** `[C0788 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of the general rule against renewal during the same session include the following:
   A. Applications
   B. Information
   C. specification
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) Applications. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:5 — “Applications of the general rule against renewal during the same session include the following:”

**779.** `[C0788 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Cases of the General Rule Against Renewal.
   A. convention
   B. assemblies
   C. Substitute
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* D) Particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:5 — “Particular Cases of the General Rule Against Renewal.”

**780.** `[C0788 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of the general rule against renewal during the same session include the following:

   *Answer:* Applications — “Applications of the general rule against renewal during the same session include the following:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:5 — “Applications of the general rule against renewal during the same session include the following:”

**781.** `[C0789 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions That Can Be Renewed at a Later Meeting of the _____.

   *Answer:* Same Session — “Motions That Can Be Renewed at a Later Meeting of the Same Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:6 — “Motions That Can Be Renewed at a Later Meeting of the Same Session.”

**782.** `[C0789 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions That Can Be Renewed at a Later _____ of the Same Session.
   A. Session
   B. carried
   C. Meeting
   D. Pending

   *Answer:* C) Meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:6 — “Motions That Can Be Renewed at a Later Meeting of the Same Session.”

**783.** `[C0789 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Following are two cases of motions which cannot be renewed at the same meeting, but which may have become different questions—and _____ are renewable —at another meeting of the same session (see 8):
   A. consequently
   B. constitution
   C. immediately
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) consequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:6 — “Following are two cases of motions which cannot be renewed at the same meeting, but which may have become different questions—and consequently are renewable —at another meeting of the same session (see 8):”

   *Answer:* consequently — “Following are two cases of motions which cannot be renewed at the same meeting, but which may have become different questions—and consequently are renewable —at another meeting of the same session (see 8):”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:6 — “Following are two cases of motions which cannot be renewed at the same meeting, but which may have become different questions—and consequently are renewable —at another meeting of the same session (see 8):”

**785.** `[C0790 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions That Can Be Renewed After Material Progress in _____.

   *Answer:* Business or Debate — “Motions That Can Be Renewed After Material Progress in Business or Debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:7 — “Motions That Can Be Renewed After Material Progress in Business or Debate.”

**786.** `[C0790 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions That Can Be Renewed After _____ Progress in Business or Debate.
   A. business
   B. Material
   C. meetings
   D. Division

   *Answer:* B) Material. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:7 — “Motions That Can Be Renewed After Material Progress in Business or Debate.”

**787.** `[C0790 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ motions are renewable if they become new questions as described, even within the same meeting:

   *Answer:* following — “The following motions are renewable if they become new questions as described, even within the same meeting:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:7 — “The following motions are renewable if they become new questions as described, even within the same meeting:”

**788.** `[C0790 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ motions are renewable if they become new questions as described, even within the same meeting:
   A. following
   B. expressly
   C. Reporting
   D. Secretary

   *Answer:* A) following. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:7 — “The following motions are renewable if they become new questions as described, even within the same meeting:”

**789.** `[C0791 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions Within the Control of the Assembly, Because Not Finally _____.

   *Answer:* Disposed Of — “Motions Within the Control of the Assembly, Because Not Finally Disposed Of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Motions Within the Control of the Assembly, Because Not Finally Disposed Of.”

**790.** `[C0791 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, _____ finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.
   A. the pending business
   B. Pattern of Formality
   C. Rising and Reporting
   D. temporarily, but not

   *Answer:* D) temporarily, but not. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”

**791.** `[C0791 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not _____), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.

   *Answer:* finally, disposed of — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”

**792.** `[C0791 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Main Motions That Go Over to Another _____;

   *Answer:* Session — “Main Motions That Go Over to Another Session;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Main Motions That Go Over to Another Session;”

**793.** `[C0791 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions Within the Control of the Assembly, Because Not Finally _____ Of.
   A. assuming
   B. business
   C. Disposed
   D. Standard

   *Answer:* C) Disposed. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Motions Within the Control of the Assembly, Because Not Finally Disposed Of.”

**794.** `[C0792 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the _____.

   *Answer:* Consideration of a Question — “Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the Consideration of a Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:9 — “Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the Consideration of a Question.”

**795.** `[C0792 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Nonrenewability of Unsustained _____ the Consideration of a Question.
   A. Objection to
   B. New Business
   C. Vote by Mail
   D. of Privilege

   *Answer:* A) Objection to. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:9 — “Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the Consideration of a Question.”

**796.** `[C0792 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By deciding to consider the question, the assembly has already begun its _____, and it is too late to make an objection.

   *Answer:* involvement — “By deciding to consider the question, the assembly has already begun its involvement, and it is too late to make an objection.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:9 — “By deciding to consider the question, the assembly has already begun its involvement, and it is too late to make an objection.”

**797.** `[C0792 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By deciding to consider the question, the assembly has already begun its _____, and it is too late to make an objection.
   A. Permission
   B. involvement
   C. interrupted
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) involvement. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:9 — “By deciding to consider the question, the assembly has already begun its involvement, and it is too late to make an objection.”

**798.** `[C0793 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion is _____ if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing parliamentary situation.

   *Answer:* dilatory — “A motion is dilatory if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing parliamentary situation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:1 — “A motion is dilatory if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing parliamentary situation.”

**799.** `[C0793 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion is dilatory if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing _____ situation.
   A. constitution
   B. characteristics
   C. representatives
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:1 — “A motion is dilatory if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing parliamentary situation.”

**800.** `[C0794 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ forms are designed to assist in the transaction of business.

   *Answer:* Parliamentary — “Parliamentary forms are designed to assist in the transaction of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:2 — “Parliamentary forms are designed to assist in the transaction of business.”

**801.** `[C0794 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even without adopting a rule on the subject, every _____ assembly has the right to protect itself from the dilatory use of these forms.
   A. Organization
   B. deliberative
   C. Procedures
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:2 — “Even without adopting a rule on the subject, every deliberative assembly has the right to protect itself from the dilatory use of these forms.”

**802.** `[C0794 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ forms are designed to assist in the transaction of business.
   A. Preparation
   B. Announcements
   C. Parliamentary
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* C) Parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:2 — “Parliamentary forms are designed to assist in the transaction of business.”

**803.** `[C0794 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even without adopting a rule on the subject, every _____ assembly has the right to protect itself from the dilatory use of these forms.

   *Answer:* deliberative — “Even without adopting a rule on the subject, every deliberative assembly has the right to protect itself from the dilatory use of these forms.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:2 — “Even without adopting a rule on the subject, every deliberative assembly has the right to protect itself from the dilatory use of these forms.”

**804.** `[C0795 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By use of such tactics, a minority of two or _____ could bring business to a standstill.

   *Answer:* three members — “By use of such tactics, a minority of two or three members could bring business to a standstill.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:3 — “By use of such tactics, a minority of two or three members could bring business to a standstill.”

**805.** `[C0795 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any main or other motion that is frivolous or absurd or that contains no rational _____ is dilatory and cannot be introduced.
   A. Organization
   B. authorizes
   C. interrupted
   D. proposition

   *Answer:* D) proposition. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:3 — “Any main or other motion that is frivolous or absurd or that contains no rational proposition is dilatory and cannot be introduced.”

**806.** `[C0795 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As further examples, it is dilatory to obstruct business by appealing from a ruling of the chair on a question about which there cannot possibly be two _____ opinions, by demanding a division (29) on a vote even when there has been a full vote and the result is clear, by moving to lay on the table the matter for which a special meeting has been called, by constantly raising points of order and appealing from the chair’s decision on them, or by moving to adjourn again and again when nothing has happened to justify renewal of such a motion.

   *Answer:* reasonable — “As further examples, it is dilatory to obstruct business by appealing from a ruling of the chair on a question about which there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions, by demanding a division (29) on a vote even when there has been a full vote and the result is clear, by moving to lay on the table the matter for which a special meeting has been called, by constantly raising points of order and appealing from the chair’s decision on them, or by moving to adjourn again and again when nothing has happened to justify renewal of such a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:3 — “As further examples, it is dilatory to obstruct business by appealing from a ruling of the chair on a question about which there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions, by demanding a division (29) on a vote even when there has been a full vote and the result is clear, by moving to lay on the table the matter for which a special meeting has been called, by constantly raising points of order and appealing from the chair’s decision on them, or by moving to adjourn again and again when nothing has happened to justify renewal of such a motion.”

**807.** `[C0795 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By use of such tactics, a minority of two or three members could bring business to a _____.
   A. Definitely
   B. standstill
   C. committees
   D. convention

   *Answer:* B) standstill. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:3 — “By use of such tactics, a minority of two or three members could bring business to a standstill.”

**808.** `[C0795 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any main or other motion that is frivolous or absurd or that contains no rational _____ is dilatory and cannot be introduced.

   *Answer:* proposition — “Any main or other motion that is frivolous or absurd or that contains no rational proposition is dilatory and cannot be introduced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:3 — “Any main or other motion that is frivolous or absurd or that contains no rational proposition is dilatory and cannot be introduced.”

**809.** `[C0796 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using parliamentary forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to _____ business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.
