   *Answer:* speed up — “Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using parliamentary forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to speed up business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:4 — “Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using parliamentary forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to speed up business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.”

**810.** `[C0796 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair only _____ that a motion is not made in good faith, he should give the maker of the motion the benefit of the doubt.
   A. business
   B. Nominees
   C. Business
   D. suspects

   *Answer:* D) suspects. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:4 — “If the chair only suspects that a motion is not made in good faith, he should give the maker of the motion the benefit of the doubt.”

**811.** `[C0796 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is the duty of the presiding officer to prevent members from misusing the _____ forms of motions, or abusing the privilege of renewing certain motions, merely to obstruct business.

   *Answer:* legitimate — “It is the duty of the presiding officer to prevent members from misusing the legitimate forms of motions, or abusing the privilege of renewing certain motions, merely to obstruct business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:4 — “It is the duty of the presiding officer to prevent members from misusing the legitimate forms of motions, or abusing the privilege of renewing certain motions, merely to obstruct business.”

**812.** `[C0796 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using _____ forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to speed up business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.
   A. undebatable
   B. parliamentary
   C. presentation
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:4 — “Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using parliamentary forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to speed up business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.”

**813.** `[C0796 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair should always be courteous and fair, but at the same time he should be firm in _____ the assembly from imposition.

   *Answer:* protecting — “The chair should always be courteous and fair, but at the same time he should be firm in protecting the assembly from imposition.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:4 — “The chair should always be courteous and fair, but at the same time he should be firm in protecting the assembly from imposition.”

**814.** `[C0797 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Likewise, apart from motions to _____ or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.

   *Answer:* Rescind — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:5 — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”

**815.** `[C0797 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to _____ Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.
   A. Modify a Motion
   B. Amend Something
   C. by paragraph or
   D. Division of the

   *Answer:* B) Amend Something. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:5 — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”

**816.** `[C0797 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions that conflict with the corporate charter, _____, or bylaws of a society, or with procedural rules prescribed by national, state, or local laws, are not in order, and if any motion of this kind is adopted, it is null and void.
   A. Announcements
   B. adjournment
   C. Indefinitely
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* D) constitution. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:5 — “Motions that conflict with the corporate charter, constitution, or bylaws of a society, or with procedural rules prescribed by national, state, or local laws, are not in order, and if any motion of this kind is adopted, it is null and void.”

**817.** `[C0797 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such _____ motions, if adopted, are null and void unless adopted by the vote required to rescind or amend the motion previously adopted.
   A. authorizes
   B. Incidental
   C. conflicting
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) conflicting. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:5 — “Such conflicting motions, if adopted, are null and void unless adopted by the vote required to rescind or amend the motion previously adopted.”

**818.** `[C0797 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions that conflict with the corporate charter, _____, or bylaws of a society, or with procedural rules prescribed by national, state, or local laws, are not in order, and if any motion of this kind is adopted, it is null and void.

   *Answer:* constitution — “Motions that conflict with the corporate charter, constitution, or bylaws of a society, or with procedural rules prescribed by national, state, or local laws, are not in order, and if any motion of this kind is adopted, it is null and void.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:5 — “Motions that conflict with the corporate charter, constitution, or bylaws of a society, or with procedural rules prescribed by national, state, or local laws, are not in order, and if any motion of this kind is adopted, it is null and void.”

**819.** `[C0798 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions are also improper when they present _____ the same question as a motion previously decided at the same session.

   *Answer:* practically — “Motions are also improper when they present practically the same question as a motion previously decided at the same session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:6 — “Motions are also improper when they present practically the same question as a motion previously decided at the same session.”

**820.** `[C0798 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a conflicting motion were allowed in such cases, it would interfere with the freedom of the assembly in acting on the earlier motion when its _____ is resumed.
   A. consideration
   B. constitution
   C. Vice-President
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:6 — “If a conflicting motion were allowed in such cases, it would interfere with the freedom of the assembly in acting on the earlier motion when its consideration is resumed.”

**821.** `[C0798 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions are also improper when they present _____ the same question as a motion previously decided at the same session.
   A. practically
   B. assemblies
   C. Organization
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) practically. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:6 — “Motions are also improper when they present practically the same question as a motion previously decided at the same session.”

**822.** `[C0798 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a conflicting motion were allowed in such cases, it would interfere with the freedom of the assembly in acting on the earlier motion when its _____ is resumed.

   *Answer:* consideration — “If a conflicting motion were allowed in such cases, it would interfere with the freedom of the assembly in acting on the earlier motion when its consideration is resumed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:6 — “If a conflicting motion were allowed in such cases, it would interfere with the freedom of the assembly in acting on the earlier motion when its consideration is resumed.”

**823.** `[C0799 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except as may be necessary in the case of a motion of censure or a motion related to disciplinary procedures (61, 63), a motion must not use language that reflects on a member’s conduct or character, or is discourteous, _____ harsh, or not allowed in debate (see 43).
   A. President-Elect
   B. resolutions
   C. unnecessarily
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) unnecessarily. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:7 — “Except as may be necessary in the case of a motion of censure or a motion related to disciplinary procedures (61, 63), a motion must not use language that reflects on a member’s conduct or character, or is discourteous, unnecessarily harsh, or not allowed in debate (see 43).”

**824.** `[C0799 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except as may be necessary in the case of a motion of censure or a motion related to disciplinary procedures (61, 63), a motion must not use language that reflects on a member’s conduct or character, or is discourteous, _____ harsh, or not allowed in debate (see 43).

   *Answer:* unnecessarily — “Except as may be necessary in the case of a motion of censure or a motion related to disciplinary procedures (61, 63), a motion must not use language that reflects on a member’s conduct or character, or is discourteous, unnecessarily harsh, or not allowed in debate (see 43).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:7 — “Except as may be necessary in the case of a motion of censure or a motion related to disciplinary procedures (61, 63), a motion must not use language that reflects on a member’s conduct or character, or is discourteous, unnecessarily harsh, or not allowed in debate (see 43).”

**825.** `[C0800 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The quorum refers to the number of members present, not to the number actually voting on a _____ question.

   *Answer:* particular — “The quorum refers to the number of members present, not to the number actually voting on a particular question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:1 — “The quorum refers to the number of members present, not to the number actually voting on a particular question.”

**826.** `[C0800 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The quorum refers to the number of members present, not to the number actually voting on a _____ question.
   A. Procedures
   B. Definitely
   C. particular
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* C) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:1 — “The quorum refers to the number of members present, not to the number actually voting on a particular question.”

**827.** `[C0801 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Number of Members _____ a Quorum.

   *Answer:* Constituting — “Number of Members Constituting a Quorum.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:2 — “Number of Members Constituting a Quorum.”

**828.** `[C0801 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Depending on the _____ and the provision it adopts in this regard, the number of members constituting a quorum may vary.
   A. consideration
   B. organization
   C. immediately
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:2 — “Depending on the organization and the provision it adopts in this regard, the number of members constituting a quorum may vary.”

**829.** `[C0801 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As discussed below, most voluntary societies should provide for a quorum in their bylaws, but where there is no such provision, the quorum, in accordance with the common _____ law, is as follows:

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “As discussed below, most voluntary societies should provide for a quorum in their bylaws, but where there is no such provision, the quorum, in accordance with the common parliamentary law, is as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:2 — “As discussed below, most voluntary societies should provide for a quorum in their bylaws, but where there is no such provision, the quorum, in accordance with the common parliamentary law, is as follows:”

**830.** `[C0801 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Number of Members _____ a Quorum.
   A. Organization
   B. Procedures
   C. Announcements
   D. Constituting

   *Answer:* D) Constituting. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:2 — “Number of Members Constituting a Quorum.”

**831.** `[C0801 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Depending on the _____ and the provision it adopts in this regard, the number of members constituting a quorum may vary.

   *Answer:* organization — “Depending on the organization and the provision it adopts in this regard, the number of members constituting a quorum may vary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:2 — “Depending on the organization and the provision it adopts in this regard, the number of members constituting a quorum may vary.”

**832.** `[C0802 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To accomplish their work, voluntary societies that have an enrolled membership generally need a provision in their bylaws establishing a relatively small quorum—considerably less than a _____ of all the members.

   *Answer:* majority — “To accomplish their work, voluntary societies that have an enrolled membership generally need a provision in their bylaws establishing a relatively small quorum—considerably less than a majority of all the members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:3 — “To accomplish their work, voluntary societies that have an enrolled membership generally need a provision in their bylaws establishing a relatively small quorum—considerably less than a majority of all the members.”

**833.** `[C0802 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To accomplish their work, voluntary societies that have an enrolled membership generally need a provision in their bylaws _____ a relatively small quorum—considerably less than a majority of all the members.
   A. constitution
   B. Permission
   C. establishing
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) establishing. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:3 — “To accomplish their work, voluntary societies that have an enrolled membership generally need a provision in their bylaws establishing a relatively small quorum—considerably less than a majority of all the members.”

**834.** `[C0802 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In most such _____, it is rarely possible to obtain the attendance of a majority of the membership at a meeting.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In most such organizations, it is rarely possible to obtain the attendance of a majority of the membership at a meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:3 — “In most such organizations, it is rarely possible to obtain the attendance of a majority of the membership at a meeting.”

**835.** `[C0802 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes the _____ of a quorum is based on a percentage of the membership;
   A. constitution
   B. specification
   C. President-Elect
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) specification. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:3 — “Sometimes the specification of a quorum is based on a percentage of the membership;”

**836.** `[C0802 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but such a method has the disadvantage of requiring _____ and may lead to confusion—for example, when the secretary, or other officer who is in a position to certify as to the current number of members for purposes of the percentage calculation, is absent.

   *Answer:* recomputation — “but such a method has the disadvantage of requiring recomputation and may lead to confusion—for example, when the secretary, or other officer who is in a position to certify as to the current number of members for purposes of the percentage calculation, is absent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:3 — “but such a method has the disadvantage of requiring recomputation and may lead to confusion—for example, when the secretary, or other officer who is in a position to certify as to the current number of members for purposes of the percentage calculation, is absent.”

**837.** `[C0803 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Note on Procedure in Changing the Quorum Provision in _____.

   *Answer:* Bylaws — “Note on Procedure in Changing the Quorum Provision in Bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:4 — “Note on Procedure in Changing the Quorum Provision in Bylaws.”

**838.** `[C0803 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it becomes necessary to change the quorum provision in a society’s bylaws, care should be taken, because if the rule is struck out first, the quorum will instantly become a _____ of the membership, so that in many cases a quorum could not be obtained to adopt a new rule.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:4 — “If it becomes necessary to change the quorum provision in a society’s bylaws, care should be taken, because if the rule is struck out first, the quorum will instantly become a majority of the membership, so that in many cases a quorum could not be obtained to adopt a new rule.”

**839.** `[C0803 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Note on _____ in Changing the Quorum Provision in Bylaws.

   *Answer:* Procedure — “Note on Procedure in Changing the Quorum Provision in Bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:4 — “Note on Procedure in Changing the Quorum Provision in Bylaws.”

**840.** `[C0803 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it becomes necessary to change the quorum provision in a society’s bylaws, care should be taken, because if the rule is struck out first, the quorum will instantly become a majority of the _____, so that in many cases a quorum could not be obtained to adopt a new rule.
   A. settlement
   B. persuasive
   C. Convention
   D. membership

   *Answer:* D) membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:4 — “If it becomes necessary to change the quorum provision in a society’s bylaws, care should be taken, because if the rule is struck out first, the quorum will instantly become a majority of the membership, so that in many cases a quorum could not be obtained to adopt a new rule.”

**841.** `[C0804 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In all other committees and in boards, the quorum is a _____ of the members of the board or committee unless a different quorum is provided for: (a) by the bylaws, in the case of a board or standing committee that the bylaws specifically establish;

   *Answer:* majority — “In all other committees and in boards, the quorum is a majority of the members of the board or committee unless a different quorum is provided for: (a) by the bylaws, in the case of a board or standing committee that the bylaws specifically establish;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:5 — “In all other committees and in boards, the quorum is a majority of the members of the board or committee unless a different quorum is provided for: (a) by the bylaws, in the case of a board or standing committee that the bylaws specifically establish;”

**842.** `[C0804 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a committee of the whole or its variations (52), the quorum is the same as in the assembly unless the rules of the assembly or the _____ (that is, either its bylaws or its rules of order) specify otherwise.
   A. Procedures
   B. deliberative
   C. Announcements
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:5 — “In a committee of the whole or its variations (52), the quorum is the same as in the assembly unless the rules of the assembly or the organization (that is, either its bylaws or its rules of order) specify otherwise.”

**843.** `[C0804 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In all other committees and in boards, the quorum is a majority of the members of the board or committee unless a different quorum is provided for: (a) by the bylaws, in the case of a board or standing committee that the bylaws _____ establish;

   *Answer:* specifically — “In all other committees and in boards, the quorum is a majority of the members of the board or committee unless a different quorum is provided for: (a) by the bylaws, in the case of a board or standing committee that the bylaws specifically establish;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:5 — “In all other committees and in boards, the quorum is a majority of the members of the board or committee unless a different quorum is provided for: (a) by the bylaws, in the case of a board or standing committee that the bylaws specifically establish;”

**844.** `[C0804 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a committee of the whole or its variations (52), the quorum is the same as in the assembly unless the rules of the assembly or the _____ (that is, either its bylaws or its rules of order) specify otherwise.

   *Answer:* organization — “In a committee of the whole or its variations (52), the quorum is the same as in the assembly unless the rules of the assembly or the organization (that is, either its bylaws or its rules of order) specify otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:5 — “In a committee of the whole or its variations (52), the quorum is the same as in the assembly unless the rules of the assembly or the organization (that is, either its bylaws or its rules of order) specify otherwise.”

**845.** `[C0805 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the absence of a quorum, any business _____ (except for the procedural actions noted in the next paragraph) is null and void.

   *Answer:* transacted — “In the absence of a quorum, any business transacted (except for the procedural actions noted in the next paragraph) is null and void.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:6 — “In the absence of a quorum, any business transacted (except for the procedural actions noted in the next paragraph) is null and void.”

**846.** `[C0805 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But if a quorum fails to appear at a regular or properly called meeting, the inability to transact business does not detract from the fact that the society’s rules requiring the meeting to be held were complied with and the meeting was convened—even though it had to adjourn _____.
   A. immediately
   B. Substitute
   C. requirement
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:6 — “But if a quorum fails to appear at a regular or properly called meeting, the inability to transact business does not detract from the fact that the society’s rules requiring the meeting to be held were complied with and the meeting was convened—even though it had to adjourn immediately.”

**847.** `[C0805 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the absence of a quorum, any business _____ (except for the procedural actions noted in the next paragraph) is null and void.
   A. convention
   B. Incidental
   C. Procedures
   D. transacted

   *Answer:* D) transacted. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:6 — “In the absence of a quorum, any business transacted (except for the procedural actions noted in the next paragraph) is null and void.”

**848.** `[C0805 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But if a quorum fails to appear at a regular or properly called meeting, the inability to transact business does not detract from the fact that the society’s rules requiring the meeting to be held were complied with and the meeting was convened—even though it had to adjourn _____.

   *Answer:* immediately — “But if a quorum fails to appear at a regular or properly called meeting, the inability to transact business does not detract from the fact that the society’s rules requiring the meeting to be held were complied with and the meeting was convened—even though it had to adjourn immediately.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:6 — “But if a quorum fails to appear at a regular or properly called meeting, the inability to transact business does not detract from the fact that the society’s rules requiring the meeting to be held were complied with and the meeting was convened—even though it had to adjourn immediately.”

**849.** `[C0806 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to _____ Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.

   *Answer:* Raise a Question of — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:7 — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”

**850.** `[C0806 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of _____, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.
   A. presiding
   B. privilege
   C. expressly
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* B) privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:7 — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”

**851.** `[C0806 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even in the absence of a quorum, the assembly may fix the time to which to adjourn (22), adjourn (21), recess (20), or take _____ to obtain a quorum.

   *Answer:* measures — “Even in the absence of a quorum, the assembly may fix the time to which to adjourn (22), adjourn (21), recess (20), or take measures to obtain a quorum.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:7 — “Even in the absence of a quorum, the assembly may fix the time to which to adjourn (22), adjourn (21), recess (20), or take measures to obtain a quorum.”

**852.** `[C0806 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even in the absence of a quorum, the assembly may fix the time to which to adjourn (22), adjourn (21), recess (20), or take _____ to obtain a quorum.
   A. Standing
   B. division
   C. Approved
   D. measures

   *Answer:* D) measures. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:7 — “Even in the absence of a quorum, the assembly may fix the time to which to adjourn (22), adjourn (21), recess (20), or take measures to obtain a quorum.”

**853.** `[C0807 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion that absent members be contacted during a _____ would represent a measure to obtain a quorum.

   *Answer:* recess — “A motion that absent members be contacted during a recess would represent a measure to obtain a quorum.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:8 — “A motion that absent members be contacted during a recess would represent a measure to obtain a quorum.”

**854.** `[C0807 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a _____, which can be ordered in assemblies having the power to compel attendance (see below).
   A. Consider Seriatim
   B. Suspend the Rules
   C. Adjourned Meeting
   D. Call of the House

   *Answer:* D) Call of the House. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:8 — “Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a Call of the House, which can be ordered in assemblies having the power to compel attendance (see below).”

**855.** `[C0807 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion that absent members be _____ during a recess would represent a measure to obtain a quorum.
   A. contacted
   B. Precedent
   C. committee
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* A) contacted. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:8 — “A motion that absent members be contacted during a recess would represent a measure to obtain a quorum.”

**856.** `[C0807 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a Call of the House, which can be ordered in _____ having the power to compel attendance (see below).

   *Answer:* assemblies — “Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a Call of the House, which can be ordered in assemblies having the power to compel attendance (see below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:8 — “Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a Call of the House, which can be ordered in assemblies having the power to compel attendance (see below).”

**857.** `[C0808 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is _____ business that should not be delayed until the next regular meeting, the assembly should fix the time for an adjourned meeting and then adjourn.
   A. Crestwood
   B. important
   C. Objection
   D. execution

   *Answer:* B) important. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:9 — “If there is important business that should not be delayed until the next regular meeting, the assembly should fix the time for an adjourned meeting and then adjourn.”

**858.** `[C0808 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, instead, the members present take action _____ in the absence of a quorum, they do so at their own risk.

   *Answer:* informally — “If, instead, the members present take action informally in the absence of a quorum, they do so at their own risk.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:9 — “If, instead, the members present take action informally in the absence of a quorum, they do so at their own risk.”

**859.** `[C0808 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is _____ business that should not be delayed until the next regular meeting, the assembly should fix the time for an adjourned meeting and then adjourn.

   *Answer:* important — “If there is important business that should not be delayed until the next regular meeting, the assembly should fix the time for an adjourned meeting and then adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:9 — “If there is important business that should not be delayed until the next regular meeting, the assembly should fix the time for an adjourned meeting and then adjourn.”

**860.** `[C0808 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, instead, the members present take action _____ in the absence of a quorum, they do so at their own risk.
   A. subsidiary
   B. informally
   C. Permission
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) informally. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:9 — “If, instead, the members present take action informally in the absence of a quorum, they do so at their own risk.”

**861.** `[C0809 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a committee of the whole finds itself without a quorum, it can do nothing but rise and report to the assembly, which can then proceed as _____ above.

   *Answer:* described — “If a committee of the whole finds itself without a quorum, it can do nothing but rise and report to the assembly, which can then proceed as described above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:10 — “If a committee of the whole finds itself without a quorum, it can do nothing but rise and report to the assembly, which can then proceed as described above.”

**862.** `[C0809 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A quasi committee of the whole or a meeting in informal _____ of a question can itself take any of the actions permitted an assembly in the absence of a quorum, but a quasi committee of the whole is thereby ended (see 52).
   A. requirement
   B. deliberative
   C. consideration
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:10 — “A quasi committee of the whole or a meeting in informal consideration of a question can itself take any of the actions permitted an assembly in the absence of a quorum, but a quasi committee of the whole is thereby ended (see 52).”

**863.** `[C0809 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a committee of the whole finds itself without a quorum, it can do nothing but rise and report to the assembly, which can then proceed as _____ above.
   A. extending
   B. Precedent
   C. amendment
   D. described

   *Answer:* D) described. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:10 — “If a committee of the whole finds itself without a quorum, it can do nothing but rise and report to the assembly, which can then proceed as described above.”

**864.** `[C0809 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A quasi committee of the whole or a meeting in informal _____ of a question can itself take any of the actions permitted an assembly in the absence of a quorum, but a quasi committee of the whole is thereby ended (see 52).

   *Answer:* consideration — “A quasi committee of the whole or a meeting in informal consideration of a question can itself take any of the actions permitted an assembly in the absence of a quorum, but a quasi committee of the whole is thereby ended (see 52).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:10 — “A quasi committee of the whole or a meeting in informal consideration of a question can itself take any of the actions permitted an assembly in the absence of a quorum, but a quasi committee of the whole is thereby ended (see 52).”

**865.** `[C0810 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before the _____ officer calls a meeting to order, it is his duty to determine, although he need not announce, that a quorum is present.

   *Answer:* presiding — “Before the presiding officer calls a meeting to order, it is his duty to determine, although he need not announce, that a quorum is present.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:11 — “Before the presiding officer calls a meeting to order, it is his duty to determine, although he need not announce, that a quorum is present.”

**866.** `[C0810 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a quorum is not present, the chair waits until there is one, or until, after a _____ time, there appears to be no prospect that a quorum will assemble.
   A. reasonable
   B. Unfinished
   C. landscaped
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) reasonable. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:11 — “If a quorum is not present, the chair waits until there is one, or until, after a reasonable time, there appears to be no prospect that a quorum will assemble.”

**867.** `[C0810 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a quorum cannot be obtained, the chair calls the meeting to order, announces the absence of a quorum, and _____ a motion to adjourn or one of the other motions allowed, as described above.

   *Answer:* entertains — “If a quorum cannot be obtained, the chair calls the meeting to order, announces the absence of a quorum, and entertains a motion to adjourn or one of the other motions allowed, as described above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:11 — “If a quorum cannot be obtained, the chair calls the meeting to order, announces the absence of a quorum, and entertains a motion to adjourn or one of the other motions allowed, as described above.”

**868.** `[C0810 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before the _____ officer calls a meeting to order, it is his duty to determine, although he need not announce, that a quorum is present.
   A. execution
   B. presiding
   C. delegates
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* B) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:11 — “Before the presiding officer calls a meeting to order, it is his duty to determine, although he need not announce, that a quorum is present.”

**869.** `[C0810 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a quorum is not present, the chair waits until there is one, or until, after a _____ time, there appears to be no prospect that a quorum will assemble.

   *Answer:* reasonable — “If a quorum is not present, the chair waits until there is one, or until, after a reasonable time, there appears to be no prospect that a quorum will assemble.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:11 — “If a quorum is not present, the chair waits until there is one, or until, after a reasonable time, there appears to be no prospect that a quorum will assemble.”

**870.** `[C0811 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.

   *Answer:* Debate — “Debate on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:12 — “Debate on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.”

**871.** `[C0811 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the chair has called a meeting to order after finding that a quorum is present, the _____ presence of a quorum is presumed unless the chair or a member notices that a quorum is no longer present.
   A. paragraph
   B. auditors’
   C. Reporting
   D. continued

   *Answer:* D) continued. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:12 — “When the chair has called a meeting to order after finding that a quorum is present, the continued presence of a quorum is presumed unless the chair or a member notices that a quorum is no longer present.”

**872.** `[C0811 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair notices the absence of a quorum, it is his duty to declare the fact, at least before taking any vote or stating the question on any new motion— which he can no longer do except in connection with the _____ proceedings related to the absence of a quorum, as explained above.

   *Answer:* permissible — “If the chair notices the absence of a quorum, it is his duty to declare the fact, at least before taking any vote or stating the question on any new motion— which he can no longer do except in connection with the permissible proceedings related to the absence of a quorum, as explained above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:12 — “If the chair notices the absence of a quorum, it is his duty to declare the fact, at least before taking any vote or stating the question on any new motion— which he can no longer do except in connection with the permissible proceedings related to the absence of a quorum, as explained above.”

**873.** `[C0811 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any member noticing the apparent absence of a quorum can make a point of order to that effect at any time so long as he does not _____ a person who is speaking.
   A. Crestwood
   B. expressly
   C. interrupt
   D. Reporting

   *Answer:* C) interrupt. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:12 — “Any member noticing the apparent absence of a quorum can make a point of order to that effect at any time so long as he does not interrupt a person who is speaking.”

**874.** `[C0811 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debate on a question already pending can be allowed to _____ at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.

   *Answer:* continue — “Debate on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:12 — “Debate on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.”

**875.** `[C0812 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a _____.

   *Answer:* Call of the House — “In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:13 — “In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.”

**876.** `[C0812 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.
   A. Incidental
   B. requirement
   C. legislative
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) legislative. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:13 — “In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.”

**877.** `[C0812 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This is a motion that _____ absent members be brought to the meeting under arrest.

   *Answer:* unexcused — “This is a motion that unexcused absent members be brought to the meeting under arrest.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:13 — “This is a motion that unexcused absent members be brought to the meeting under arrest.”

**878.** `[C0812 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A Call of the House is not _____ in voluntary societies.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Substitute
   C. Unfinished
   D. applicable

   *Answer:* D) applicable. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:13 — “A Call of the House is not applicable in voluntary societies.”

**879.** `[C0812 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This is a motion that _____ absent members be brought to the meeting under arrest.
   A. unexcused
   B. Precedent
   C. postponed
   D. reception

   *Answer:* A) unexcused. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:13 — “This is a motion that unexcused absent members be brought to the meeting under arrest.”

**880.** `[C0813 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a _____ of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.

   *Answer:* Call — “Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:14 — “Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.”

**881.** `[C0813 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call _____ should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.
   A. of the House
   B. the Assembly
   C. ceases to be
   D. Dilatory Use

   *Answer:* A) of the House. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:14 — “Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.”

**882.** `[C0813 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the rule allows the call to be moved while a quorum is actually present (for the purpose of obtaining a _____ attendance), the motion at such times should rank only with questions of privilege, should require a majority vote for adoption, and, if rejected, should not be allowed to be renewed while a quorum is present.

   *Answer:* greater — “If the rule allows the call to be moved while a quorum is actually present (for the purpose of obtaining a greater attendance), the motion at such times should rank only with questions of privilege, should require a majority vote for adoption, and, if rejected, should not be allowed to be renewed while a quorum is present.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:14 — “If the rule allows the call to be moved while a quorum is actually present (for the purpose of obtaining a greater attendance), the motion at such times should rank only with questions of privilege, should require a majority vote for adoption, and, if rejected, should not be allowed to be renewed while a quorum is present.”

**883.** `[C0813 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the rule allows the call to be moved while a quorum is actually present (for the purpose of obtaining a _____ attendance), the motion at such times should rank only with questions of privilege, should require a majority vote for adoption, and, if rejected, should not be allowed to be renewed while a quorum is present.
   A. Rescind
   B. Pending
   C. Adjourn
   D. greater

   *Answer:* D) greater. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:14 — “If the rule allows the call to be moved while a quorum is actually present (for the purpose of obtaining a greater attendance), the motion at such times should rank only with questions of privilege, should require a majority vote for adoption, and, if rejected, should not be allowed to be renewed while a quorum is present.”

**884.** `[C0814 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a _____ is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf explanations of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.

   *Answer:* Call of the House — “When a Call of the House is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf explanations of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:15 — “When a Call of the House is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf explanations of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.”

**885.** `[C0814 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a Call of the House is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf _____ of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.
   A. Permission
   B. explanations
   C. Parliamentary
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) explanations. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:15 — “When a Call of the House is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf explanations of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.”

**886.** `[C0814 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After this, no member is permitted to leave, the doors are locked, and the _____, chief of police, or other arresting officer is ordered to take into custody absentees who have not been excused from attendance and bring them before the house.

   *Answer:* sergeant-at-arms — “After this, no member is permitted to leave, the doors are locked, and the sergeant-at-arms, chief of police, or other arresting officer is ordered to take into custody absentees who have not been excused from attendance and bring them before the house.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:15 — “After this, no member is permitted to leave, the doors are locked, and the sergeant-at-arms, chief of police, or other arresting officer is ordered to take into custody absentees who have not been excused from attendance and bring them before the house.”

**887.** `[C0814 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: He does this on a warrant signed by the _____ officer and attested by the clerk.
   A. Crestwood
   B. secretary
   C. presiding
   D. Secretary

   *Answer:* C) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:15 — “He does this on a warrant signed by the presiding officer and attested by the clerk.”

**888.** `[C0814 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Until a member has paid such a fee assessed against him, he cannot vote or be _____ by the chair for any purpose.

   *Answer:* recognized — “Until a member has paid such a fee assessed against him, he cannot vote or be recognized by the chair for any purpose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:15 — “Until a member has paid such a fee assessed against him, he cannot vote or be recognized by the chair for any purpose.”

**889.** `[C0815 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a _____ has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.

   *Answer:* Call of the House — “After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:16 — “After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.”

**890.** `[C0815 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by _____ consent, except motions relating to the call.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* C) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:16 — “After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.”

**891.** `[C0815 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to adjourn or dispense with further _____ under the call, however, can be entertained after a quorum is present, or after the arresting officer reports that in his opinion a quorum cannot be obtained.

   *Answer:* proceedings — “Motions to adjourn or dispense with further proceedings under the call, however, can be entertained after a quorum is present, or after the arresting officer reports that in his opinion a quorum cannot be obtained.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:16 — “Motions to adjourn or dispense with further proceedings under the call, however, can be entertained after a quorum is present, or after the arresting officer reports that in his opinion a quorum cannot be obtained.”

**892.** `[C0815 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ terminates all proceedings under the Call of the House.
   A. Preparation
   B. adjournment
   C. assemblies
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:16 — “An adjournment terminates all proceedings under the Call of the House.”

**893.** `[C0815 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions to adjourn or dispense with further _____ under the call, however, can be entertained after a quorum is present, or after the arresting officer reports that in his opinion a quorum cannot be obtained.
   A. proceedings
   B. committees
   C. Substitute
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) proceedings. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:16 — “Motions to adjourn or dispense with further proceedings under the call, however, can be entertained after a quorum is present, or after the arresting officer reports that in his opinion a quorum cannot be obtained.”

**894.** `[C0816 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and _____ refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the scheduling of particular business.

   *Answer:* program — “The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and program refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the scheduling of particular business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:1 — “The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and program refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the scheduling of particular business.”

**895.** `[C0816 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and program refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the _____ of particular business.
   A. incidental
   B. Reconsider
   C. convention
   D. scheduling

   *Answer:* D) scheduling. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:1 — “The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and program refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the scheduling of particular business.”

**896.** `[C0816 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The meaning of these terms often coincides, although each has its own _____ in common usage.

   *Answer:* applications — “The meaning of these terms often coincides, although each has its own applications in common usage.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:1 — “The meaning of these terms often coincides, although each has its own applications in common usage.”

**897.** `[C0816 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The meaning of these terms often coincides, although each has its own _____ in common usage.
   A. applications
   B. Announcements
   C. subsequently
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* A) applications. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:1 — “The meaning of these terms often coincides, although each has its own applications in common usage.”

**898.** `[C0817 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.

   *Answer:* order of business — “An order of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:2 — “An order of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.”

**899.** `[C0817 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.
   A. floor
   B. order
   C. legal
   D. after

   *Answer:* B) order. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:2 — “An order of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.”

**900.** `[C0817 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the terms order of business, _____, and program relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.

   *Answer:* agenda — “Although the terms order of business, agenda, and program relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:2 — “Although the terms order of business, agenda, and program relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.”

**901.** `[C0817 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although the terms order of business, agenda, and _____ relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.
   A. Ordered
   B. program
   C. advance
   D. Another

   *Answer:* B) program. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:2 — “Although the terms order of business, agenda, and program relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.”

**902.** `[C0818 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless designated for particular hours or assigned positions item by item in an agenda or program formally adopted for a given session, _____ and special orders are taken up under assigned headings or in customary positions allotted to each of these categories in the order of business.
   A. plurality vote
   B. rules of order
   C. Roll-Call Vote
   D. general orders

   *Answer:* D) general orders. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:3 — “Unless designated for particular hours or assigned positions item by item in an agenda or program formally adopted for a given session, general orders and special orders are taken up under assigned headings or in customary positions allotted to each of these categories in the order of business.”

**903.** `[C0818 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless designated for particular hours or assigned positions item by item in an agenda or program formally adopted for a given session, general orders and _____ are taken up under assigned headings or in customary positions allotted to each of these categories in the order of business.

   *Answer:* special orders — “Unless designated for particular hours or assigned positions item by item in an agenda or program formally adopted for a given session, general orders and special orders are taken up under assigned headings or in customary positions allotted to each of these categories in the order of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:3 — “Unless designated for particular hours or assigned positions item by item in an agenda or program formally adopted for a given session, general orders and special orders are taken up under assigned headings or in customary positions allotted to each of these categories in the order of business.”

**904.** `[C0818 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (Note such headings in the “standard” order of business _____ below.)
   A. extending
   B. Secretary
   C. described
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* C) described. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:3 — “(Note such headings in the “standard” order of business described below.)”

**905.** `[C0818 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (Note such headings in the “standard” order of business _____ below.)

   *Answer:* described — “(Note such headings in the “standard” order of business described below.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:3 — “(Note such headings in the “standard” order of business described below.)”

**906.** `[C0819 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Within a meeting in which the only items of business that are in order have been specified and set in sequence in advance—as might occur, for example, in a _____ meeting of a convention—the orders of the day are identical with the order of business (which, in such a case, is in the form of an agenda or program).

   *Answer:* particular — “Within a meeting in which the only items of business that are in order have been specified and set in sequence in advance—as might occur, for example, in a particular meeting of a convention—the orders of the day are identical with the order of business (which, in such a case, is in the form of an agenda or program).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:4 — “Within a meeting in which the only items of business that are in order have been specified and set in sequence in advance—as might occur, for example, in a particular meeting of a convention—the orders of the day are identical with the order of business (which, in such a case, is in the form of an agenda or program).”

**907.** `[C0819 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Within a meeting in which the only items of business that are in order have been specified and set in sequence in advance—as might occur, for example, in a _____ meeting of a convention—the orders of the day are identical with the order of business (which, in such a case, is in the form of an agenda or program).
   A. particular
   B. variations
   C. Substitute
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:4 — “Within a meeting in which the only items of business that are in order have been specified and set in sequence in advance—as might occur, for example, in a particular meeting of a convention—the orders of the day are identical with the order of business (which, in such a case, is in the form of an agenda or program).”

**908.** `[C0820 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Basic _____ Covering Business Proper.

   *Answer:* Headings — “Basic Headings Covering Business Proper.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:5 — “Basic Headings Covering Business Proper.”

**909.** `[C0820 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The customary or “standard” order of business comprises the following _____:
   A. subdivisions
   B. undebatable
   C. adjournment
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) subdivisions. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:5 — “The customary or “standard” order of business comprises the following subdivisions:”

**910.** `[C0820 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Basic _____ Covering Business Proper.
   A. Resolved
   B. Headings
   C. Business
   D. meetings

   *Answer:* B) Headings. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:5 — “Basic Headings Covering Business Proper.”

**911.** `[C0820 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The customary or “standard” order of business comprises the following _____:

   *Answer:* subdivisions — “The customary or “standard” order of business comprises the following subdivisions:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:5 — “The customary or “standard” order of business comprises the following subdivisions:”

**912.** `[C0821 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This standard order of business _____ only the sequence of the headings, not the time to be allotted to each—which may vary with every meeting.

   *Answer:* prescribes — “This standard order of business prescribes only the sequence of the headings, not the time to be allotted to each—which may vary with every meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:6 — “This standard order of business prescribes only the sequence of the headings, not the time to be allotted to each—which may vary with every meeting.”

**913.** `[C0821 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Certain optional headings are also described following the detailed _____ of the regular headings below.
   A. substitute
   B. discussion
   C. resolution
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* B) discussion. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:6 — “Certain optional headings are also described following the detailed discussion of the regular headings below.”

**914.** `[C0821 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This standard order of business _____ only the sequence of the headings, not the time to be allotted to each—which may vary with every meeting.
   A. Particular
   B. Convention
   C. Unfinished
   D. prescribes

   *Answer:* D) prescribes. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:6 — “This standard order of business prescribes only the sequence of the headings, not the time to be allotted to each—which may vary with every meeting.”

**915.** `[C0821 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Certain optional headings are also described following the detailed _____ of the regular headings below.

   *Answer:* discussion — “Certain optional headings are also described following the detailed discussion of the regular headings below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:6 — “Certain optional headings are also described following the detailed discussion of the regular headings below.”

**916.** `[C0822 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The presiding officer may find it helpful to have at hand a _____ of the complete order of business, listing, under headings (2) and (3) as explained below, all known reports which are expected to be presented, and under headings (4) and (5), all matters which the minutes show are due to come up, arranged in proper sequence or, where applicable, listed with the times for which they have been set.

   *Answer:* memorandum — “The presiding officer may find it helpful to have at hand a memorandum of the complete order of business, listing, under headings (2) and (3) as explained below, all known reports which are expected to be presented, and under headings (4) and (5), all matters which the minutes show are due to come up, arranged in proper sequence or, where applicable, listed with the times for which they have been set.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:7 — “The presiding officer may find it helpful to have at hand a memorandum of the complete order of business, listing, under headings (2) and (3) as explained below, all known reports which are expected to be presented, and under headings (4) and (5), all matters which the minutes show are due to come up, arranged in proper sequence or, where applicable, listed with the times for which they have been set.”

**917.** `[C0822 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The presiding officer may find it helpful to have at hand a _____ of the complete order of business, listing, under headings (2) and (3) as explained below, all known reports which are expected to be presented, and under headings (4) and (5), all matters which the minutes show are due to come up, arranged in proper sequence or, where applicable, listed with the times for which they have been set.
   A. memorandum
   B. incidental
   C. Permission
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* A) memorandum. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:7 — “The presiding officer may find it helpful to have at hand a memorandum of the complete order of business, listing, under headings (2) and (3) as explained below, all known reports which are expected to be presented, and under headings (4) and (5), all matters which the minutes show are due to come up, arranged in proper sequence or, where applicable, listed with the times for which they have been set.”

**918.** `[C0824 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair says, “The Secretary will read the minutes.” However, in _____ where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests that they be read.

   *Answer:* organizations — “The chair says, “The Secretary will read the minutes.” However, in organizations where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests that they be read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:9 — “The chair says, “The Secretary will read the minutes.” However, in organizations where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests that they be read.”

**919.** `[C0824 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair says, “The _____ will read the minutes.” However, in organizations where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests that they be read.
   A. debatable
   B. expressly
   C. Secretary
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* C) Secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:9 — “The chair says, “The Secretary will read the minutes.” However, in organizations where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests that they be read.”

**920.** `[C0825 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the minutes have been read (or after their reading has been omitted by _____ consent as described in the previous paragraph), and whether or not a motion for approval has been offered, the chair asks, “Are there any corrections to the minutes?” and pauses.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* C) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:10 — “After the minutes have been read (or after their reading has been omitted by unanimous consent as described in the previous paragraph), and whether or not a motion for approval has been offered, the chair asks, “Are there any corrections to the minutes?” and pauses.”

**921.** `[C0825 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A formal motion to approve the minutes is not _____, although such a motion is not out of order.

   *Answer:* necessary — “A formal motion to approve the minutes is not necessary, although such a motion is not out of order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:10 — “A formal motion to approve the minutes is not necessary, although such a motion is not out of order.”

**922.** `[C0825 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the minutes have been read (or after their reading has been omitted by unanimous consent as described in the previous paragraph), and whether or not a motion for approval has been offered, the chair asks, “Are there any _____ to the minutes?” and pauses.
   A. corrections
   B. Convention
   C. assemblies
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) corrections. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:10 — “After the minutes have been read (or after their reading has been omitted by unanimous consent as described in the previous paragraph), and whether or not a motion for approval has been offered, the chair asks, “Are there any corrections to the minutes?” and pauses.”

**923.** `[C0825 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A formal motion to approve the minutes is not _____, although such a motion is not out of order.
   A. expressly
   B. necessary
   C. debatable
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* B) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:10 — “A formal motion to approve the minutes is not necessary, although such a motion is not out of order.”

**924.** `[C0826 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After any proposed _____ have been disposed of, and when there is no response to the chair’s inquiry, “Are there any corrections [or “further corrections”] to the minutes?” the chair says, “There being no corrections [or “no further corrections”] to the minutes, the minutes stand [or “are”] approved [or “approved as read,” or “approved as corrected”].” The minutes are thus approved without any formal vote, even if a motion for their approval has been made.

   *Answer:* corrections — “After any proposed corrections have been disposed of, and when there is no response to the chair’s inquiry, “Are there any corrections [or “further corrections”] to the minutes?” the chair says, “There being no corrections [or “no further corrections”] to the minutes, the minutes stand [or “are”] approved [or “approved as read,” or “approved as corrected”].” The minutes are thus approved without any formal vote, even if a motion for their approval has been made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:11 — “After any proposed corrections have been disposed of, and when there is no response to the chair’s inquiry, “Are there any corrections [or “further corrections”] to the minutes?” the chair says, “There being no corrections [or “no further corrections”] to the minutes, the minutes stand [or “are”] approved [or “approved as read,” or “approved as corrected”].” The minutes are thus approved without any formal vote, even if a motion for their approval has been made.”

**925.** `[C0826 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The only proper way to object to the approval of the secretary’s draft of the minutes is to offer a _____ to it.
   A. recognized
   B. privileged
   C. correction
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) correction. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:11 — “The only proper way to object to the approval of the secretary’s draft of the minutes is to offer a correction to it.”

**926.** `[C0826 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that a member’s absence from the meeting for which minutes are being approved does not prevent the member from _____ in their correction or approval.

   *Answer:* participating — “It should be noted that a member’s absence from the meeting for which minutes are being approved does not prevent the member from participating in their correction or approval.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:11 — “It should be noted that a member’s absence from the meeting for which minutes are being approved does not prevent the member from participating in their correction or approval.”

**927.** `[C0826 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After any proposed _____ have been disposed of, and when there is no response to the chair’s inquiry, “Are there any corrections [or “further corrections”] to the minutes?” the chair says, “There being no corrections [or “no further corrections”] to the minutes, the minutes stand [or “are”] approved [or “approved as read,” or “approved as corrected”].” The minutes are thus approved without any formal vote, even if a motion for their approval has been made.
   A. Reconsider
   B. corrections
   C. Organization
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) corrections. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:11 — “After any proposed corrections have been disposed of, and when there is no response to the chair’s inquiry, “Are there any corrections [or “further corrections”] to the minutes?” the chair says, “There being no corrections [or “no further corrections”] to the minutes, the minutes stand [or “are”] approved [or “approved as read,” or “approved as corrected”].” The minutes are thus approved without any formal vote, even if a motion for their approval has been made.”

**928.** `[C0826 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The only proper way to object to the approval of the secretary’s draft of the minutes is to offer a _____ to it.

   *Answer:* correction — “The only proper way to object to the approval of the secretary’s draft of the minutes is to offer a correction to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:11 — “The only proper way to object to the approval of the secretary’s draft of the minutes is to offer a correction to it.”

**929.** `[C0827 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ secretary’s draft of the minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.

   *Answer:* The — “The secretary’s draft of the minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:12 — “The secretary’s draft of the minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.”

**930.** `[C0827 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____’s draft of the minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.
   A. president
   B. reception
   C. secretary
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* C) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:12 — “The secretary’s draft of the minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.”

**931.** `[C0827 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When this has been done, it is presumed that the members have had an _____ to review these draft minutes, and they are not read at the meeting unless a member requests it.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “When this has been done, it is presumed that the members have had an opportunity to review these draft minutes, and they are not read at the meeting unless a member requests it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:12 — “When this has been done, it is presumed that the members have had an opportunity to review these draft minutes, and they are not read at the meeting unless a member requests it.”

**932.** `[C0827 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ and approval, however, is handled in the usual way.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Definitely
   C. Correction
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) Correction. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:12 — “Correction and approval, however, is handled in the usual way.”

**933.** `[C0827 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A draft of the minutes _____ to members must be clearly marked as such.

   *Answer:* circulated — “A draft of the minutes circulated to members must be clearly marked as such.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:12 — “A draft of the minutes circulated to members must be clearly marked as such.”

**934.** `[C0828 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reports of Officers, Boards, and _____ Committees.

   *Answer:* Standing — “Reports of Officers, Boards, and Standing Committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:13 — “Reports of Officers, Boards, and Standing Committees.”

**935.** `[C0828 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Reports of Officers, Boards, and Standing _____.
   A. resolution
   B. Permission
   C. Unfinished
   D. Committees

   *Answer:* D) Committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:13 — “Reports of Officers, Boards, and Standing Committees.”

**936.** `[C0828 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At other meetings the chair calls only on those who have reports to make, as by saying (in calling upon the secretary), “Is there any _____?” Or, “May we have the Treasurer’s report.” Or, “The chair recognizes Mr.

   *Answer:* correspondence — “At other meetings the chair calls only on those who have reports to make, as by saying (in calling upon the secretary), “Is there any correspondence?” Or, “May we have the Treasurer’s report.” Or, “The chair recognizes Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:13 — “At other meetings the chair calls only on those who have reports to make, as by saying (in calling upon the secretary), “Is there any correspondence?” Or, “May we have the Treasurer’s report.” Or, “The chair recognizes Mr.”

**937.** `[C0828 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Downey, Chairman of the _____ Committee, for a report.” If the chair is uncertain, he may ask, for example, “Does the Program Committee have a report?” Standing committees listed in the bylaws are called upon in the order in which they are listed.
   A. landscaped
   B. sustaining
   C. Membership
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) Membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:13 — “Downey, Chairman of the Membership Committee, for a report.” If the chair is uncertain, he may ask, for example, “Does the Program Committee have a report?” Standing committees listed in the bylaws are called upon in the order in which they are listed.”

**938.** `[C0828 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At other meetings the chair calls only on those who have reports to make, as by saying (in calling upon the secretary), “Is there any _____?” Or, “May we have the Treasurer’s report.” Or, “The chair recognizes Mr.
   A. correspondence
   B. representatives
   C. Responsibilities
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) correspondence. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:13 — “At other meetings the chair calls only on those who have reports to make, as by saying (in calling upon the secretary), “Is there any correspondence?” Or, “May we have the Treasurer’s report.” Or, “The chair recognizes Mr.”

**939.** `[C0829 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an officer, in reporting, makes a _____, he should not himself move its implementation, but such a motion can be made by another member as soon as the officer has concluded his report.
