   *Answer:* recommendation — “If an officer, in reporting, makes a recommendation, he should not himself move its implementation, but such a motion can be made by another member as soon as the officer has concluded his report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:14 — “If an officer, in reporting, makes a recommendation, he should not himself move its implementation, but such a motion can be made by another member as soon as the officer has concluded his report.”

**940.** `[C0829 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of a committee report, on the other hand, the chairman or other reporting member should make any motion(s) necessary to bring the committee’s _____ before the assembly for consideration.
   A. immediately
   B. Announcements
   C. recommendations
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* C) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:14 — “In the case of a committee report, on the other hand, the chairman or other reporting member should make any motion(s) necessary to bring the committee’s recommendations before the assembly for consideration.”

**941.** `[C0829 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion arising out of an officer’s, a board’s, or a committee’s report is taken up _____, since the object of the order of business is to give priority to the classes of business in the order listed.

   *Answer:* immediately — “A motion arising out of an officer’s, a board’s, or a committee’s report is taken up immediately, since the object of the order of business is to give priority to the classes of business in the order listed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:14 — “A motion arising out of an officer’s, a board’s, or a committee’s report is taken up immediately, since the object of the order of business is to give priority to the classes of business in the order listed.”

**942.** `[C0829 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an officer, in reporting, makes a _____, he should not himself move its implementation, but such a motion can be made by another member as soon as the officer has concluded his report.
   A. specification
   B. recommendation
   C. President-Elect
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:14 — “If an officer, in reporting, makes a recommendation, he should not himself move its implementation, but such a motion can be made by another member as soon as the officer has concluded his report.”

**943.** `[C0829 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of a committee report, on the other hand, the chairman or other reporting member should make any motion(s) necessary to bring the committee’s _____ before the assembly for consideration.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “In the case of a committee report, on the other hand, the chairman or other reporting member should make any motion(s) necessary to bring the committee’s recommendations before the assembly for consideration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:14 — “In the case of a committee report, on the other hand, the chairman or other reporting member should make any motion(s) necessary to bring the committee’s recommendations before the assembly for consideration.”

**944.** `[C0831 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (For procedures to be followed in making reports and in handling _____ arising from reports, see 51.)

   *Answer:* recommendations — “(For procedures to be followed in making reports and in handling recommendations arising from reports, see 51.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:16 — “(For procedures to be followed in making reports and in handling recommendations arising from reports, see 51.)”

**945.** `[C0831 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For procedures to be followed in making reports and in handling _____ arising from reports, see 51.)
   A. subsequently
   B. Parliamentary
   C. Organization
   D. recommendations

   *Answer:* D) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:16 — “(For procedures to be followed in making reports and in handling recommendations arising from reports, see 51.)”

**946.** `[C0832 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Business incident to _____ that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading (17, 34).

   *Answer:* reports of special committees — “Business incident to reports of special committees that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading (17, 34).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:17 — “Business incident to reports of special committees that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading (17, 34).”

**947.** `[C0832 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The special _____ (50) that are to report are called on in the order in which they were appointed.
   A. Substitute
   B. settlement
   C. assemblies
   D. committees

   *Answer:* D) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:17 — “The special committees (50) that are to report are called on in the order in which they were appointed.”

**948.** `[C0832 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Business incident to _____ that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading (17, 34).
   A. originating in the committee
   B. Basis of This Classification
   C. Because of Intervening Action
   D. reports of special committees

   *Answer:* D) reports of special committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:17 — “Business incident to reports of special committees that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading (17, 34).”

**949.** `[C0832 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The special _____ (50) that are to report are called on in the order in which they were appointed.

   *Answer:* committees — “The special committees (50) that are to report are called on in the order in which they were appointed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:17 — “The special committees (50) that are to report are called on in the order in which they were appointed.”

**950.** `[C0833 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under this heading (referring to the explanation of _____ in 41:40ff.) are taken up the following in the order listed:
   A. Orders of the Day
   B. Cumulative Voting
   C. General Procedure
   D. committee reports

   *Answer:* A) Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:18 — “Under this heading (referring to the explanation of Orders of the Day in 41:40ff.) are taken up the following in the order listed:”

**951.** `[C0833 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under this heading (referring to the _____ of Orders of the Day in 41:40ff.) are taken up the following in the order listed:

   *Answer:* explanation — “Under this heading (referring to the explanation of Orders of the Day in 41:40ff.) are taken up the following in the order listed:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:18 — “Under this heading (referring to the explanation of Orders of the Day in 41:40ff.) are taken up the following in the order listed:”

**952.** `[C0834 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The resolution is as follows: ‘_____, That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.”

   *Answer:* Resolved — “The resolution is as follows: ‘Resolved, That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:19 — “The resolution is as follows: ‘Resolved, That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.””

**953.** `[C0834 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a special order that was so introduced comes up, the chair announces it as pending, thus: “At the last meeting, the resolution relating to funds for a new playground was made a special order for this meeting [or, if the special order was made by _____, “… was postponed to this meeting and made a special order.”].
   A. specification
   B. postponement
   C. Definitely
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) postponement. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:19 — “When a special order that was so introduced comes up, the chair announces it as pending, thus: “At the last meeting, the resolution relating to funds for a new playground was made a special order for this meeting [or, if the special order was made by postponement, “… was postponed to this meeting and made a special order.”].”

**954.** `[C0834 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a special order that was so introduced comes up, the chair announces it as pending, thus: “At the last meeting, the _____ relating to funds for a new playground was made a special order for this meeting [or, if the special order was made by postponement, “… was postponed to this meeting and made a special order.”].

   *Answer:* resolution — “When a special order that was so introduced comes up, the chair announces it as pending, thus: “At the last meeting, the resolution relating to funds for a new playground was made a special order for this meeting [or, if the special order was made by postponement, “… was postponed to this meeting and made a special order.”].”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:19 — “When a special order that was so introduced comes up, the chair announces it as pending, thus: “At the last meeting, the resolution relating to funds for a new playground was made a special order for this meeting [or, if the special order was made by postponement, “… was postponed to this meeting and made a special order.”].”

**955.** `[C0834 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The resolution is as follows: ‘_____, That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.”
   A. Postpone
   B. Approved
   C. majority
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* D) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:19 — “The resolution is as follows: ‘Resolved, That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.””

**956.** `[C0835 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Matters that the bylaws require to be considered at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as _____ for the meeting and be considered under the heading of Special Orders in the order of business.

   *Answer:* special orders — “Matters that the bylaws require to be considered at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as special orders for the meeting and be considered under the heading of Special Orders in the order of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:20 — “Matters that the bylaws require to be considered at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as special orders for the meeting and be considered under the heading of Special Orders in the order of business.”

**957.** `[C0835 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Matters that the bylaws require to be _____ at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as special orders for the meeting and be considered under the heading of Special Orders in the order of business.
   A. considered
   B. settlement
   C. Unfinished
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* A) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:20 — “Matters that the bylaws require to be considered at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as special orders for the meeting and be considered under the heading of Special Orders in the order of business.”

**958.** `[C0837 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ (as explained under Orders of the Day, below) is any question which, usually by postponement, has been made an order of the day without being made a special order.

   *Answer:* general order — “A general order (as explained under Orders of the Day, below) is any question which, usually by postponement, has been made an order of the day without being made a special order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:22 — “A general order (as explained under Orders of the Day, below) is any question which, usually by postponement, has been made an order of the day without being made a special order.”

**959.** `[C0837 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A general order (as explained under _____, below) is any question which, usually by postponement, has been made an order of the day without being made a special order.
   A. Consider Seriatim
   B. committee reports
   C. General Revisions
   D. Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* D) Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:22 — “A general order (as explained under Orders of the Day, below) is any question which, usually by postponement, has been made an order of the day without being made a special order.”

**960.** `[C0838 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The heading of _____ Business and General Orders includes items of business in the four categories that are listed below in the order in which they are taken up.

   *Answer:* Unfinished — “The heading of Unfinished Business and General Orders includes items of business in the four categories that are listed below in the order in which they are taken up.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:23 — “The heading of Unfinished Business and General Orders includes items of business in the four categories that are listed below in the order in which they are taken up.”

**961.** `[C0838 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Of these, the first three _____ “Unfinished Business,” while the fourth consists of “General Orders”:
   A. authorizes
   B. settlement
   C. Unfinished
   D. constitute

   *Answer:* D) constitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:23 — “Of these, the first three constitute “Unfinished Business,” while the fourth consists of “General Orders”:”

**962.** `[C0838 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The heading of _____ Business and General Orders includes items of business in the four categories that are listed below in the order in which they are taken up.
   A. Procedures
   B. Particular
   C. Unfinished
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) Unfinished. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:23 — “The heading of Unfinished Business and General Orders includes items of business in the four categories that are listed below in the order in which they are taken up.”

**963.** `[C0838 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Of these, the first three _____ “Unfinished Business,” while the fourth consists of “General Orders”:

   *Answer:* constitute — “Of these, the first three constitute “Unfinished Business,” while the fourth consists of “General Orders”:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:23 — “Of these, the first three constitute “Unfinished Business,” while the fourth consists of “General Orders”:”

**964.** `[C0839 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair should not announce the heading of _____ Business and General Orders unless the minutes show that there is some business to come up under it.

   *Answer:* Unfinished — “The chair should not announce the heading of Unfinished Business and General Orders unless the minutes show that there is some business to come up under it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:24 — “The chair should not announce the heading of Unfinished Business and General Orders unless the minutes show that there is some business to come up under it.”

**965.** `[C0839 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair should not announce the heading of Unfinished _____ unless the minutes show that there is some business to come up under it.
   A. Something Previously Adopted
   B. Agenda Provided in Advance
   C. Amend Something Previously
   D. Business and General Orders

   *Answer:* D) Business and General Orders. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:24 — “The chair should not announce the heading of Unfinished Business and General Orders unless the minutes show that there is some business to come up under it.”

**966.** `[C0839 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The resolution is as follows: ‘_____, That… [reading the resolution].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.”
   A. Resolved
   B. Meetings
   C. assembly
   D. Business

   *Answer:* A) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:24 — “The resolution is as follows: ‘Resolved, That… [reading the resolution].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.””

**967.** `[C0839 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the latter case, he should have all such subjects listed in correct sequence in a _____ prepared in advance of the meeting.

   *Answer:* memorandum — “In the latter case, he should have all such subjects listed in correct sequence in a memorandum prepared in advance of the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:24 — “In the latter case, he should have all such subjects listed in correct sequence in a memorandum prepared in advance of the meeting.”

**968.** `[C0839 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and when it has been disposed of, he should proceed through the _____ subjects in their proper order.
   A. Committee
   B. extending
   C. remaining
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* C) remaining. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:24 — “and when it has been disposed of, he should proceed through the remaining subjects in their proper order.”

**969.** `[C0840 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any item of business (in _____ class) that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading at any time when no question is pending (17, 34).

   *Answer:* whatever — “Any item of business (in whatever class) that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading at any time when no question is pending (17, 34).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:25 — “Any item of business (in whatever class) that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading at any time when no question is pending (17, 34).”

**970.** `[C0840 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To obtain the floor for the purpose of moving to take a question from the table at such a time, a member can rise and address the chair, _____ him as he starts to announce the next item of business after the previous one is disposed of.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. interrupting
   C. adjournment
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) interrupting. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:25 — “To obtain the floor for the purpose of moving to take a question from the table at such a time, a member can rise and address the chair, interrupting him as he starts to announce the next item of business after the previous one is disposed of.”

**971.** `[C0840 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any item of business (in _____ class) that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading at any time when no question is pending (17, 34).
   A. whatever
   B. Approved
   C. dilatory
   D. Previous

   *Answer:* A) whatever. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:25 — “Any item of business (in whatever class) that is on the table can be taken from the table under this heading at any time when no question is pending (17, 34).”

**972.** `[C0840 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To obtain the floor for the purpose of moving to take a question from the table at such a time, a member can rise and address the chair, _____ him as he starts to announce the next item of business after the previous one is disposed of.

   *Answer:* interrupting — “To obtain the floor for the purpose of moving to take a question from the table at such a time, a member can rise and address the chair, interrupting him as he starts to announce the next item of business after the previous one is disposed of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:25 — “To obtain the floor for the purpose of moving to take a question from the table at such a time, a member can rise and address the chair, interrupting him as he starts to announce the next item of business after the previous one is disposed of.”

**973.** `[C0841 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If brief consultation during a meeting leads to an informal _____ that a certain subject should be “brought up at the next meeting,” that does not make it unfinished business.

   *Answer:* understanding — “If brief consultation during a meeting leads to an informal understanding that a certain subject should be “brought up at the next meeting,” that does not make it unfinished business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:26 — “If brief consultation during a meeting leads to an informal understanding that a certain subject should be “brought up at the next meeting,” that does not make it unfinished business.”

**974.** `[C0841 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Instead, the matter would have to be _____ at the next meeting as new business, as explained below.
   A. Particular
   B. Substitute
   C. variations
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* D) introduced. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:26 — “Instead, the matter would have to be introduced at the next meeting as new business, as explained below.”

**975.** `[C0841 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If brief consultation during a meeting leads to an informal _____ that a certain subject should be “brought up at the next meeting,” that does not make it unfinished business.
   A. interrupted
   B. understanding
   C. resolutions
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* B) understanding. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:26 — “If brief consultation during a meeting leads to an informal understanding that a certain subject should be “brought up at the next meeting,” that does not make it unfinished business.”

**976.** `[C0841 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Instead, the matter would have to be _____ at the next meeting as new business, as explained below.

   *Answer:* introduced — “Instead, the matter would have to be introduced at the next meeting as new business, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:26 — “Instead, the matter would have to be introduced at the next meeting as new business, as explained below.”

**977.** `[C0842 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After unfinished business and general orders have been disposed of, the chair asks, “Is there any _____?” Members can then introduce new items of business, or can move to take from the table any matter that is on the table (17, 34), in the order in which they are able to obtain the floor when no question is pending, as explained in 3 and 4.

   *Answer:* new business — “After unfinished business and general orders have been disposed of, the chair asks, “Is there any new business?” Members can then introduce new items of business, or can move to take from the table any matter that is on the table (17, 34), in the order in which they are able to obtain the floor when no question is pending, as explained in 3 and 4.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:27 — “After unfinished business and general orders have been disposed of, the chair asks, “Is there any new business?” Members can then introduce new items of business, or can move to take from the table any matter that is on the table (17, 34), in the order in which they are able to obtain the floor when no question is pending, as explained in 3 and 4.”

**978.** `[C0842 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After _____ business and general orders have been disposed of, the chair asks, “Is there any new business?” Members can then introduce new items of business, or can move to take from the table any matter that is on the table (17, 34), in the order in which they are able to obtain the floor when no question is pending, as explained in 3 and 4.
   A. settlement
   B. convention
   C. unfinished
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) unfinished. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:27 — “After unfinished business and general orders have been disposed of, the chair asks, “Is there any new business?” Members can then introduce new items of business, or can move to take from the table any matter that is on the table (17, 34), in the order in which they are able to obtain the floor when no question is pending, as explained in 3 and 4.”

**979.** `[C0842 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: So long as members are reasonably prompt in claiming the floor, the chair cannot prevent the making of legitimate motions or deprive members of the right to introduce legitimate business, by hurrying through the _____.

   *Answer:* proceedings — “So long as members are reasonably prompt in claiming the floor, the chair cannot prevent the making of legitimate motions or deprive members of the right to introduce legitimate business, by hurrying through the proceedings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:27 — “So long as members are reasonably prompt in claiming the floor, the chair cannot prevent the making of legitimate motions or deprive members of the right to introduce legitimate business, by hurrying through the proceedings.”

**980.** `[C0842 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: So long as members are reasonably prompt in claiming the floor, the chair cannot prevent the making of legitimate motions or deprive members of the right to introduce legitimate business, by hurrying through the _____.
   A. Particular
   B. proceedings
   C. Organization
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) proceedings. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:27 — “So long as members are reasonably prompt in claiming the floor, the chair cannot prevent the making of legitimate motions or deprive members of the right to introduce legitimate business, by hurrying through the proceedings.”

**981.** `[C0843 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition to the standard order of business as just described, regular meetings of _____ sometimes include proceedings in the categories listed below, which may be regarded as optional in the order of business prescribed by this book.
   A. organizations
   B. constitution
   C. Announcements
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:28 — “In addition to the standard order of business as just described, regular meetings of organizations sometimes include proceedings in the categories listed below, which may be regarded as optional in the order of business prescribed by this book.”

**982.** `[C0843 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition to the standard order of business as just described, regular meetings of _____ sometimes include proceedings in the categories listed below, which may be regarded as optional in the order of business prescribed by this book.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In addition to the standard order of business as just described, regular meetings of organizations sometimes include proceedings in the categories listed below, which may be regarded as optional in the order of business prescribed by this book.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:28 — “In addition to the standard order of business as just described, regular meetings of organizations sometimes include proceedings in the categories listed below, which may be regarded as optional in the order of business prescribed by this book.”

**983.** `[C0844 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the call to order and before the reading of the _____, the next two headings may be included:

   *Answer:* minutes — “After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two headings may be included:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:29 — “After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two headings may be included:”

**984.** `[C0844 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two _____ may be included:
   A. headings
   B. Nominees
   C. limiting
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* A) headings. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:29 — “After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two headings may be included:”

**985.** `[C0845 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Opening ceremonies immediately after the meeting is called to order may include the invocation (which, if offered, should always be placed first), the singing of the national anthem, the reciting of the Pledge of Allegiance to the flag, a ritual briefly recalling the objects or ideals of the _____, or the like.
   A. organization
   B. undebatable
   C. Permission
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:30 — “Opening ceremonies immediately after the meeting is called to order may include the invocation (which, if offered, should always be placed first), the singing of the national anthem, the reciting of the Pledge of Allegiance to the flag, a ritual briefly recalling the objects or ideals of the organization, or the like.”

**986.** `[C0845 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Opening ceremonies immediately after the meeting is called to order may include the invocation (which, if offered, should always be placed first), the singing of the national anthem, the reciting of the Pledge of Allegiance to the flag, a ritual briefly recalling the objects or ideals of the _____, or the like.

   *Answer:* organization — “Opening ceremonies immediately after the meeting is called to order may include the invocation (which, if offered, should always be placed first), the singing of the national anthem, the reciting of the Pledge of Allegiance to the flag, a ritual briefly recalling the objects or ideals of the organization, or the like.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:30 — “Opening ceremonies immediately after the meeting is called to order may include the invocation (which, if offered, should always be placed first), the singing of the national anthem, the reciting of the Pledge of Allegiance to the flag, a ritual briefly recalling the objects or ideals of the organization, or the like.”

**987.** `[C0846 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is a _____ of this nature, it should take place at the end of the opening ceremonies unless a special rule of the organization assigns it a different position in the order of business.

   *Answer:* roll call — “If there is a roll call of this nature, it should take place at the end of the opening ceremonies unless a special rule of the organization assigns it a different position in the order of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:31 — “If there is a roll call of this nature, it should take place at the end of the opening ceremonies unless a special rule of the organization assigns it a different position in the order of business.”

**988.** `[C0846 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some _____ it is customary at meetings to call the roll of officers in order to verify their attendance—or, sometimes in very small societies, even to call the roll of members.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. subsequently
   C. Indefinitely
   D. organizations

   *Answer:* D) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:31 — “In some organizations it is customary at meetings to call the roll of officers in order to verify their attendance—or, sometimes in very small societies, even to call the roll of members.”

**989.** `[C0846 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is a roll call of this nature, it should take place at the end of the opening ceremonies unless a special rule of the _____ assigns it a different position in the order of business.

   *Answer:* organization — “If there is a roll call of this nature, it should take place at the end of the opening ceremonies unless a special rule of the organization assigns it a different position in the order of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:31 — “If there is a roll call of this nature, it should take place at the end of the opening ceremonies unless a special rule of the organization assigns it a different position in the order of business.”

**990.** `[C0846 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair _____ it by saying, “The Secretary will call the roll of officers [or “will call the roll”].”
   A. Privilege
   B. presiding
   C. auditors’
   D. announces

   *Answer:* D) announces. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:31 — “The chair announces it by saying, “The Secretary will call the roll of officers [or “will call the roll”].””

**991.** `[C0846 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some _____ it is customary at meetings to call the roll of officers in order to verify their attendance—or, sometimes in very small societies, even to call the roll of members.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In some organizations it is customary at meetings to call the roll of officers in order to verify their attendance—or, sometimes in very small societies, even to call the roll of members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:31 — “In some organizations it is customary at meetings to call the roll of officers in order to verify their attendance—or, sometimes in very small societies, even to call the roll of members.”

**992.** `[C0847 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Legislatures, city, town, or county councils, or other assemblies which have a heavy work load including a large number of routine or noncontroversial matters may find a _____ a useful tool for disposing of such items of business.

   *Answer:* consent calendar — “Legislatures, city, town, or county councils, or other assemblies which have a heavy work load including a large number of routine or noncontroversial matters may find a consent calendar a useful tool for disposing of such items of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “Legislatures, city, town, or county councils, or other assemblies which have a heavy work load including a large number of routine or noncontroversial matters may find a consent calendar a useful tool for disposing of such items of business.”

**993.** `[C0847 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing _____.
   A. committee reports
   B. Suspend the Rules
   C. Order of Business
   D. board of trustees

   *Answer:* A) committee reports. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.”

**994.** `[C0847 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Legislatures, city, town, or county councils, or other assemblies which have a heavy work load including a large number of routine or _____ matters may find a consent calendar a useful tool for disposing of such items of business.

   *Answer:* noncontroversial — “Legislatures, city, town, or county councils, or other assemblies which have a heavy work load including a large number of routine or noncontroversial matters may find a consent calendar a useful tool for disposing of such items of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “Legislatures, city, town, or county councils, or other assemblies which have a heavy work load including a large number of routine or noncontroversial matters may find a consent calendar a useful tool for disposing of such items of business.”

**995.** `[C0847 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Commonly, when such a matter has been introduced or reported by a committee for _____ in the assembly, its sponsor, or, sometimes, an administrator, may seek to have it placed on the consent calendar.
   A. consideration
   B. deliberative
   C. President-Elect
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “Commonly, when such a matter has been introduced or reported by a committee for consideration in the assembly, its sponsor, or, sometimes, an administrator, may seek to have it placed on the consent calendar.”

**996.** `[C0847 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This calendar is called over _____ at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.

   *Answer:* periodically — “This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.”

**997.** `[C0848 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the completion of new business—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “After the completion of new business—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:33 — “After the completion of new business—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the organization.”

**998.** `[C0848 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the completion of new business—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the _____.
   A. organization
   B. Preparation
   C. Indefinitely
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:33 — “After the completion of new business—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the organization.”

**999.** `[C0849 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Good of the Order, General Good and Welfare, or Open _____.

   *Answer:* Forum — “Good of the Order, General Good and Welfare, or Open Forum.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:34 — “Good of the Order, General Good and Welfare, or Open Forum.”

**1000.** `[C0849 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Good of the Order, _____ Good and Welfare, or Open Forum.
   A. General
   B. Example
   C. adopted
   D. Rescind

   *Answer:* A) General. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:34 — “Good of the Order, General Good and Welfare, or Open Forum.”

**1001.** `[C0849 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This heading, included by some types of societies in their order of business, refers to the general welfare of the _____, and may vary in character.

   *Answer:* organization — “This heading, included by some types of societies in their order of business, refers to the general welfare of the organization, and may vary in character.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:34 — “This heading, included by some types of societies in their order of business, refers to the general welfare of the organization, and may vary in character.”

**1002.** `[C0849 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under this heading (in contrast to the general _____ rule that allows discussion only with reference to a pending motion), members who obtain the floor commonly are permitted to offer informal observations regarding the work of the organization, the public reputation of the society or its membership, or the like.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Announcements
   C. president-elect
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:34 — “Under this heading (in contrast to the general parliamentary rule that allows discussion only with reference to a pending motion), members who obtain the floor commonly are permitted to offer informal observations regarding the work of the organization, the public reputation of the society or its membership, or the like.”

**1003.** `[C0849 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Certain types of _____ may tend to fall here.

   *Answer:* announcements — “Certain types of announcements may tend to fall here.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:34 — “Certain types of announcements may tend to fall here.”

**1004.** `[C0850 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair may make, or call upon other officers or members to make, any necessary _____;

   *Answer:* announcements — “The chair may make, or call upon other officers or members to make, any necessary announcements;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:35 — “The chair may make, or call upon other officers or members to make, any necessary announcements;”

**1005.** `[C0850 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, if the practice of the _____ permits it, members can briefly obtain the floor for such a purpose.
   A. presentation
   B. immediately
   C. organization
   D. specification

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:35 — “or, if the practice of the organization permits it, members can briefly obtain the floor for such a purpose.”

**1006.** `[C0850 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair may make, or call upon other officers or members to make, any necessary _____;
   A. resolutions
   B. specification
   C. undebatable
   D. announcements

   *Answer:* D) announcements. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:35 — “The chair may make, or call upon other officers or members to make, any necessary announcements;”

**1007.** `[C0850 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, if the practice of the _____ permits it, members can briefly obtain the floor for such a purpose.

   *Answer:* organization — “or, if the practice of the organization permits it, members can briefly obtain the floor for such a purpose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:35 — “or, if the practice of the organization permits it, members can briefly obtain the floor for such a purpose.”

**1008.** `[C0851 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is to be a talk, film, or other _____ of a cultural, educational, or civic nature, it is usually presented before the meeting is adjourned, since it may prompt a desire on the part of the assembly to take action.

   *Answer:* program — “If there is to be a talk, film, or other program of a cultural, educational, or civic nature, it is usually presented before the meeting is adjourned, since it may prompt a desire on the part of the assembly to take action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:36 — “If there is to be a talk, film, or other program of a cultural, educational, or civic nature, it is usually presented before the meeting is adjourned, since it may prompt a desire on the part of the assembly to take action.”

**1009.** `[C0851 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, in courtesy to a guest speaker who is present, the chair wishes the talk to be located at an unscheduled point within the business portion of the meeting, he can usually obtain _____ consent for a suspension of the rules by simply announcing, “If there is no objection, we will hear our speaker’s address at this time.”
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* D) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:36 — “If, in courtesy to a guest speaker who is present, the chair wishes the talk to be located at an unscheduled point within the business portion of the meeting, he can usually obtain unanimous consent for a suspension of the rules by simply announcing, “If there is no objection, we will hear our speaker’s address at this time.””

**1010.** `[C0851 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is to be a talk, film, or other program of a cultural, _____, or civic nature, it is usually presented before the meeting is adjourned, since it may prompt a desire on the part of the assembly to take action.

   *Answer:* educational — “If there is to be a talk, film, or other program of a cultural, educational, or civic nature, it is usually presented before the meeting is adjourned, since it may prompt a desire on the part of the assembly to take action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:36 — “If there is to be a talk, film, or other program of a cultural, educational, or civic nature, it is usually presented before the meeting is adjourned, since it may prompt a desire on the part of the assembly to take action.”

**1011.** `[C0851 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ the program is commonly placed at the end of the order of business in such cases, it can, by special rule or practice, be received before the minutes are read;
   A. meetings
   B. Postpone
   C. Although
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* C) Although. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:36 — “Although the program is commonly placed at the end of the order of business in such cases, it can, by special rule or practice, be received before the minutes are read;”

**1012.** `[C0851 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, by _____ the rules (25), it can be proceeded to at any time during the meeting.

   *Answer:* suspending — “or, by suspending the rules (25), it can be proceeded to at any time during the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:36 — “or, by suspending the rules (25), it can be proceeded to at any time during the meeting.”

**1013.** `[C0852 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If desired, before the completion of the advanced question the regular order of business can be returned to by a _____ vote—by adopting a motion to lay the pending question on the table (17).
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:37 — “If desired, before the completion of the advanced question the regular order of business can be returned to by a majority vote—by adopting a motion to lay the pending question on the table (17).”

**1014.** `[C0852 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Hence, an important committee report or an urgent item of new business can be advanced in order to assure its full and unhurried _____.

   *Answer:* consideration — “Hence, an important committee report or an urgent item of new business can be advanced in order to assure its full and unhurried consideration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:37 — “Hence, an important committee report or an urgent item of new business can be advanced in order to assure its full and unhurried consideration.”

**1015.** `[C0852 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If desired, before the _____ of the advanced question the regular order of business can be returned to by a majority vote—by adopting a motion to lay the pending question on the table (17).
   A. sustaining
   B. Definitely
   C. persuasive
   D. completion

   *Answer:* D) completion. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:37 — “If desired, before the completion of the advanced question the regular order of business can be returned to by a majority vote—by adopting a motion to lay the pending question on the table (17).”

**1016.** `[C0852 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Hence, an important committee report or an urgent item of new business can be advanced in order to assure its full and unhurried _____.
   A. recommendation
   B. president-elect
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:37 — “Hence, an important committee report or an urgent item of new business can be advanced in order to assure its full and unhurried consideration.”

**1017.** `[C0853 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a _____ of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.

   *Answer:* class — “It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a class of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:38 — “It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a class of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.”

**1018.** `[C0853 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To take up a motion out of its proper order—for example, to introduce an item of new business before that heading is reached—a member who has obtained the floor can say, “I ask unanimous consent to introduce at this time a resolution on financing better schools.” If there is any objection, or the member anticipates that there may be, he can say, “I move to suspend the rules that interfere with the _____ at this time of…” If unanimous consent is given or if this motion is adopted by a two-thirds vote, the member is immediately recognized to introduce the resolution.
   A. introduction
   B. immediately
   C. Announcements
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) introduction. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:38 — “To take up a motion out of its proper order—for example, to introduce an item of new business before that heading is reached—a member who has obtained the floor can say, “I ask unanimous consent to introduce at this time a resolution on financing better schools.” If there is any objection, or the member anticipates that there may be, he can say, “I move to suspend the rules that interfere with the introduction at this time of…” If unanimous consent is given or if this motion is adopted by a two-thirds vote, the member is immediately recognized to introduce the resolution.”

**1019.** `[C0853 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If only one or two items stand ahead of the item it is desired to reach, it may be just as simple to lay the intervening items on the table _____ (17), or to postpone them as they arise (14).

   *Answer:* individually — “If only one or two items stand ahead of the item it is desired to reach, it may be just as simple to lay the intervening items on the table individually (17), or to postpone them as they arise (14).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:38 — “If only one or two items stand ahead of the item it is desired to reach, it may be just as simple to lay the intervening items on the table individually (17), or to postpone them as they arise (14).”

**1020.** `[C0853 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If only one or two items stand ahead of the item it is desired to reach, it may be just as simple to lay the intervening items on the table individually (17), or to _____ them as they arise (14).
   A. dilatory
   B. Interest
   C. postpone
   D. Division

   *Answer:* C) postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:38 — “If only one or two items stand ahead of the item it is desired to reach, it may be just as simple to lay the intervening items on the table individually (17), or to postpone them as they arise (14).”

**1021.** `[C0853 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a _____ of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.
   A. Table
   B. class
   C. hours
   D. Limit

   *Answer:* B) class. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:38 — “It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a class of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.”

**1022.** `[C0854 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (for obtaining unanimous consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading _____ above).

   *Answer:* Program — “or (for obtaining unanimous consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading Program above).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:39 — “or (for obtaining unanimous consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading Program above).”

**1023.** `[C0854 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or (for obtaining _____ consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading Program above).
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* C) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:39 — “or (for obtaining unanimous consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading Program above).”

**1024.** `[C0854 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair himself cannot depart from the _____ order of business, which only the assembly can do by at least a twothirds vote.

   *Answer:* prescribed — “The chair himself cannot depart from the prescribed order of business, which only the assembly can do by at least a twothirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:39 — “The chair himself cannot depart from the prescribed order of business, which only the assembly can do by at least a twothirds vote.”

**1025.** `[C0854 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This is an important protection in cases where some of the members _____ involved in a particular question may be unable to be present through an entire meeting.
   A. Information
   B. Definitely
   C. principally
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) principally. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:39 — “This is an important protection in cases where some of the members principally involved in a particular question may be unable to be present through an entire meeting.”

**1026.** `[C0854 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When such a departure from the order of business is justified, however, it is usually easy for the chair to obtain the necessary _____ from the assembly.

   *Answer:* authorization — “When such a departure from the order of business is justified, however, it is usually easy for the chair to obtain the necessary authorization from the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:39 — “When such a departure from the order of business is justified, however, it is usually easy for the chair to obtain the necessary authorization from the assembly.”

**1027.** `[C0855 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the next regular business session will be held within a quarterly time interval, an _____ cannot be made beyond the end of that next session.

   *Answer:* order of the day — “If the next regular business session will be held within a quarterly time interval, an order of the day cannot be made beyond the end of that next session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:40 — “If the next regular business session will be held within a quarterly time interval, an order of the day cannot be made beyond the end of that next session.”

**1028.** `[C0855 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An order of the day cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote that established the order (so long as a reconsideration is possible), or by suspending the rules (25) by a _____ vote.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* D) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:40 — “An order of the day cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote that established the order (so long as a reconsideration is possible), or by suspending the rules (25) by a two-thirds vote.”

**1029.** `[C0855 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the next regular business session will be held within a _____ time interval, an order of the day cannot be made beyond the end of that next session.

   *Answer:* quarterly — “If the next regular business session will be held within a quarterly time interval, an order of the day cannot be made beyond the end of that next session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:40 — “If the next regular business session will be held within a quarterly time interval, an order of the day cannot be made beyond the end of that next session.”

**1030.** `[C0855 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An order of the day cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote that established the order (so long as a _____ is possible), or by suspending the rules (25) by a two-thirds vote.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. recommendation
   C. reconsideration
   D. Information

   *Answer:* C) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:40 — “An order of the day cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote that established the order (so long as a reconsideration is possible), or by suspending the rules (25) by a two-thirds vote.”

**1031.** `[C0856 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Orders of the day are divided into the classes of _____ orders and special orders.

   *Answer:* general — “Orders of the day are divided into the classes of general orders and special orders.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:41 — “Orders of the day are divided into the classes of general orders and special orders.”

**1032.** `[C0856 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of the day are divided into the classes of general orders and special orders.
   A. adopts
   B. Motion
   C. Appeal
   D. Orders

   *Answer:* D) Orders. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:41 — “Orders of the day are divided into the classes of general orders and special orders.”

**1033.** `[C0856 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since the making of a special order has the effect of suspending any interfering rules, it requires a _____ vote (except where such action is included in the adoption of an agenda or program for a session having no prescribed order of business).

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “Since the making of a special order has the effect of suspending any interfering rules, it requires a two-thirds vote (except where such action is included in the adoption of an agenda or program for a session having no prescribed order of business).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:41 — “Since the making of a special order has the effect of suspending any interfering rules, it requires a two-thirds vote (except where such action is included in the adoption of an agenda or program for a session having no prescribed order of business).”

**1034.** `[C0856 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since the making of a special order has the effect of suspending any interfering rules, it requires a _____ vote (except where such action is included in the adoption of an agenda or program for a session having no prescribed order of business).
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* A) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:41 — “Since the making of a special order has the effect of suspending any interfering rules, it requires a two-thirds vote (except where such action is included in the adoption of an agenda or program for a session having no prescribed order of business).”

**1035.** `[C0857 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Forms for Motions to Make _____ or Special Orders.

   *Answer:* General — “Forms for Motions to Make General or Special Orders.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:43 — “Forms for Motions to Make General or Special Orders.”

**1036.** `[C0857 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Forms for Motions to Make _____ or Special Orders.
   A. Program
   B. applied
   C. Regular
   D. General

   *Answer:* D) General. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:43 — “Forms for Motions to Make General or Special Orders.”

**1037.** `[C0858 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question that is _____ is made an order of the day, it is usually made a special order.

   *Answer:* not pending — “When a question that is not pending is made an order of the day, it is usually made a special order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:44 — “When a question that is not pending is made an order of the day, it is usually made a special order.”

**1038.** `[C0858 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It can be offered in this form: “I move that the following resolution be made a special order for the next meeting: ‘_____, That…’”;
   A. Standing
   B. Resolved
   C. majority
   D. assembly

   *Answer:* B) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:44 — “It can be offered in this form: “I move that the following resolution be made a special order for the next meeting: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”

**1039.** `[C0858 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question that is not _____ is made an order of the day, it is usually made a special order.

   *Answer:* pending — “When a question that is not pending is made an order of the day, it is usually made a special order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:44 — “When a question that is not pending is made an order of the day, it is usually made a special order.”

**1040.** `[C0858 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A main motion to make a _____ subject a special order can be introduced whenever business of its class or new business is in order and nothing is pending.
   A. resolution
   B. incidental
   C. particular
   D. variations

   *Answer:* C) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:44 — “A main motion to make a particular subject a special order can be introduced whenever business of its class or new business is in order and nothing is pending.”

**1041.** `[C0858 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It can be offered in this form: “I move that the following _____ be made a special order for the next meeting: ‘Resolved, That…’”;

   *Answer:* resolution — “It can be offered in this form: “I move that the following resolution be made a special order for the next meeting: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:44 — “It can be offered in this form: “I move that the following resolution be made a special order for the next meeting: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”

**1042.** `[C0859 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions in _____ forms can also be used to make a question that is not pending a general order.

   *Answer:* similar — “Motions in similar forms can also be used to make a question that is not pending a general order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:45 — “Motions in similar forms can also be used to make a question that is not pending a general order.”

**1043.** `[C0859 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In this connection, however, it should be noted that a majority can thus prevent a matter from coming before the assembly until a future time, but after a majority has taken such action, nothing less than a two-thirds vote can change it unless it is _____ (37).
   A. reconsidered
   B. Permission
   C. constitution
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:45 — “In this connection, however, it should be noted that a majority can thus prevent a matter from coming before the assembly until a future time, but after a majority has taken such action, nothing less than a two-thirds vote can change it unless it is reconsidered (37).”

**1044.** `[C0859 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a main motion to make a question that is not pending an order of the day for a future time is introduced, any member who would prefer to consider the matter _____ should speak in debate against the motion that would make it an order of the day.

   *Answer:* immediately — “If a main motion to make a question that is not pending an order of the day for a future time is introduced, any member who would prefer to consider the matter immediately should speak in debate against the motion that would make it an order of the day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:45 — “If a main motion to make a question that is not pending an order of the day for a future time is introduced, any member who would prefer to consider the matter immediately should speak in debate against the motion that would make it an order of the day.”

**1045.** `[C0859 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If that motion is voted down, he can then _____ the subject of the proposed order as a main question.
   A. introduce
   B. Reporting
   C. Secretary
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* A) introduce. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:45 — “If that motion is voted down, he can then introduce the subject of the proposed order as a main question.”

**1046.** `[C0859 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions in _____ forms can also be used to make a question that is not pending a general order.
   A. similar
   B. adjourn
   C. Session
   D. Another

   *Answer:* A) similar. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:45 — “Motions in similar forms can also be used to make a question that is not pending a general order.”

**1047.** `[C0860 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Relation of Orders of the Day to the Established Order of _____.

   *Answer:* Business — “Relation of Orders of the Day to the Established Order of Business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:46 — “Relation of Orders of the Day to the Established Order of Business.”

**1048.** `[C0860 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Where an organization’s order of business does not provide such headings, _____ not set for particular hours are taken up before unfinished business and general orders, or (if there are neither of these), at all events before new business.
   A. The Framing of
   B. bring business
   C. Absentee Voting
   D. special orders

   *Answer:* D) special orders. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:46 — “Where an organization’s order of business does not provide such headings, special orders not set for particular hours are taken up before unfinished business and general orders, or (if there are neither of these), at all events before new business.”

**1049.** `[C0860 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Relation of Orders of the Day to the _____ Order of Business.

   *Answer:* Established — “Relation of Orders of the Day to the Established Order of Business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:46 — “Relation of Orders of the Day to the Established Order of Business.”

**1050.** `[C0860 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where an _____ society has adopted its own order of business for regular meetings, it usually includes similar headings covering such orders of the day.
   A. Nominees
   B. Previous
   C. ordinary
   D. chairman

   *Answer:* C) ordinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:46 — “In cases where an ordinary society has adopted its own order of business for regular meetings, it usually includes similar headings covering such orders of the day.”

**1051.** `[C0860 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Where an _____’s order of business does not provide such headings, special orders not set for particular hours are taken up before unfinished business and general orders, or (if there are neither of these), at all events before new business.

   *Answer:* organization — “Where an organization’s order of business does not provide such headings, special orders not set for particular hours are taken up before unfinished business and general orders, or (if there are neither of these), at all events before new business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:46 — “Where an organization’s order of business does not provide such headings, special orders not set for particular hours are taken up before unfinished business and general orders, or (if there are neither of these), at all events before new business.”

**1052.** `[C0861 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The most common instances of orders of the day set for particular hours occur in _____.

   *Answer:* conventions — “The most common instances of orders of the day set for particular hours occur in conventions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:47 — “The most common instances of orders of the day set for particular hours occur in conventions.”

**1053.** `[C0861 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The most common instances of orders of the day set for particular hours occur in _____.
   A. Unfinished
   B. conventions
   C. Incidental
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:47 — “The most common instances of orders of the day set for particular hours occur in conventions.”

**1054.** `[C0862 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any type of assembly, in cases where orders of the day have been set for particular hours, their _____ at the proper time may cause interruption or modification of the order of business as it exists apart from these orders of the day;

   *Answer:* consideration — “In any type of assembly, in cases where orders of the day have been set for particular hours, their consideration at the proper time may cause interruption or modification of the order of business as it exists apart from these orders of the day;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:48 — “In any type of assembly, in cases where orders of the day have been set for particular hours, their consideration at the proper time may cause interruption or modification of the order of business as it exists apart from these orders of the day;”

**1055.** `[C0862 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and _____ orders of the day may come into conflict.
   A. different
   B. Objection
   C. postponed
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* A) different. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:48 — “and different orders of the day may come into conflict.”

**1056.** `[C0862 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules _____ such cases are as follows:

   *Answer:* governing — “Rules governing such cases are as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:48 — “Rules governing such cases are as follows:”

**1057.** `[C0862 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In any type of assembly, in cases where orders of the day have been set for particular hours, their _____ at the proper time may cause interruption or modification of the order of business as it exists apart from these orders of the day;
   A. recommendation
   B. consideration
   C. constitution
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:48 — “In any type of assembly, in cases where orders of the day have been set for particular hours, their consideration at the proper time may cause interruption or modification of the order of business as it exists apart from these orders of the day;”

**1058.** `[C0862 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and _____ orders of the day may come into conflict.

   *Answer:* different — “and different orders of the day may come into conflict.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:48 — “and different orders of the day may come into conflict.”

**1059.** `[C0863 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As stated above, a general order that has been set for a particular hour cannot be considered before that hour unless the rules are suspended by a _____ vote, or unless the vote that made the general order can still be reconsidered.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* A) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:49 — “As stated above, a general order that has been set for a particular hour cannot be considered before that hour unless the rules are suspended by a two-thirds vote, or unless the vote that made the general order can still be reconsidered.”

**1060.** `[C0863 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As stated above, a general order that has been set for a particular hour cannot be considered before that hour unless the rules are suspended by a two-thirds vote, or unless the vote that made the general order can still be _____.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “As stated above, a general order that has been set for a particular hour cannot be considered before that hour unless the rules are suspended by a two-thirds vote, or unless the vote that made the general order can still be reconsidered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:49 — “As stated above, a general order that has been set for a particular hour cannot be considered before that hour unless the rules are suspended by a two-thirds vote, or unless the vote that made the general order can still be reconsidered.”

**1061.** `[C0863 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since the making of a general order does not suspend any rules, even if it is designated for a particular hour, delay in its _____ when that hour arrives may arise from a number of causes.

   *Answer:* consideration — “Since the making of a general order does not suspend any rules, even if it is designated for a particular hour, delay in its consideration when that hour arrives may arise from a number of causes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:49 — “Since the making of a general order does not suspend any rules, even if it is designated for a particular hour, delay in its consideration when that hour arrives may arise from a number of causes.”

**1062.** `[C0863 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even though the hour fixed for a general order has arrived, the order can be taken up only when all of the following _____ conditions are fulfilled:
   A. recognized
   B. substitute
   C. landscaped
   D. additional

   *Answer:* D) additional. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:49 — “Even though the hour fixed for a general order has arrived, the order can be taken up only when all of the following additional conditions are fulfilled:”

**1063.** `[C0863 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since the making of a general order does not suspend any rules, even if it is designated for a particular hour, delay in its _____ when that hour arrives may arise from a number of causes.
   A. consideration
   B. Organization
   C. Preparation
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:49 — “Since the making of a general order does not suspend any rules, even if it is designated for a particular hour, delay in its consideration when that hour arrives may arise from a number of causes.”

**1064.** `[C0864 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rule that a general order for a _____ hour does not interrupt a pending question when that hour arrives holds even when the pending question is a general order that was made later.

   *Answer:* particular — “The rule that a general order for a particular hour does not interrupt a pending question when that hour arrives holds even when the pending question is a general order that was made later.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:50 — “The rule that a general order for a particular hour does not interrupt a pending question when that hour arrives holds even when the pending question is a general order that was made later.”

**1065.** `[C0864 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But if a general order for an earlier time is not reached by the time set for another general order that was made before it was, the general order that was made first is taken up in _____ to the one for the earlier time.
   A. preference
   B. introduced
   C. resolution
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* A) preference. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:50 — “But if a general order for an earlier time is not reached by the time set for another general order that was made before it was, the general order that was made first is taken up in preference to the one for the earlier time.”

**1066.** `[C0864 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rule that a general order for a _____ hour does not interrupt a pending question when that hour arrives holds even when the pending question is a general order that was made later.
   A. Substitute
   B. landscaped
   C. particular
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:50 — “The rule that a general order for a particular hour does not interrupt a pending question when that hour arrives holds even when the pending question is a general order that was made later.”

**1067.** `[C0864 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But if a general order for an earlier time is not reached by the time set for another general order that was made before it was, the general order that was made first is taken up in _____ to the one for the earlier time.

   *Answer:* preference — “But if a general order for an earlier time is not reached by the time set for another general order that was made before it was, the general order that was made first is taken up in preference to the one for the earlier time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:50 — “But if a general order for an earlier time is not reached by the time set for another general order that was made before it was, the general order that was made first is taken up in preference to the one for the earlier time.”

**1068.** `[C0866 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If _____ general orders were made for the same time, they are taken up in the order in which they were made.

   *Answer:* several — “If several general orders were made for the same time, they are taken up in the order in which they were made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:52 — “If several general orders were made for the same time, they are taken up in the order in which they were made.”

**1069.** `[C0866 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If _____ general orders were made for the same time, they are taken up in the order in which they were made.
   A. Meeting
   B. several
   C. Inquiry
   D. Divided

   *Answer:* B) several. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:52 — “If several general orders were made for the same time, they are taken up in the order in which they were made.”

**1070.** `[C0867 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But when _____ designated hour arrives, the special order automatically interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;
   A. Point
   B. the
   C. hours
   D. floor

   *Answer:* B) the. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:53 — “But when the designated hour arrives, the special order automatically interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;”

**1071.** `[C0867 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A special order for a particular hour cannot be considered before that hour except by a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “A special order for a particular hour cannot be considered before that hour except by a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:53 — “A special order for a particular hour cannot be considered before that hour except by a two-thirds vote.”

**1072.** `[C0867 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A special order for a _____ hour cannot be considered before that hour except by a two-thirds vote.
   A. Substitute
   B. particular
   C. Unfinished
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* B) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:53 — “A special order for a particular hour cannot be considered before that hour except by a two-thirds vote.”

**1073.** `[C0867 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But when the designated hour arrives, the special order _____ interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;

   *Answer:* automatically — “But when the designated hour arrives, the special order automatically interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:53 — “But when the designated hour arrives, the special order automatically interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;”

**1074.** `[C0868 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If several special orders have been made at _____ same time for the same hour, they rank in the order in which they are listed in the motion by which they were made.

   *Answer:* the — “If several special orders have been made at the same time for the same hour, they rank in the order in which they are listed in the motion by which they were made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:54 — “If several special orders have been made at the same time for the same hour, they rank in the order in which they are listed in the motion by which they were made.”

**1075.** `[C0868 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This rule holds even when special orders made later have been set for _____ at earlier hours.
   A. presentation
   B. consideration
   C. Announcements
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:54 — “This rule holds even when special orders made later have been set for consideration at earlier hours.”

**1076.** `[C0868 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No special order can _____ with one that was made earlier than itself.
