   *Answer:* interfere — “No special order can interfere with one that was made earlier than itself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:54 — “No special order can interfere with one that was made earlier than itself.”

**1077.** `[C0868 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If _____ special orders have been made at the same time for the same hour, they rank in the order in which they are listed in the motion by which they were made.
   A. adopted
   B. Divided
   C. several
   D. abstain

   *Answer:* C) several. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:54 — “If several special orders have been made at the same time for the same hour, they rank in the order in which they are listed in the motion by which they were made.”

**1078.** `[C0868 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This rule holds even when special orders made later have been set for _____ at earlier hours.

   *Answer:* consideration — “This rule holds even when special orders made later have been set for consideration at earlier hours.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:54 — “This rule holds even when special orders made later have been set for consideration at earlier hours.”

**1079.** `[C0869 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume that a _____ order has been made for 3 P.M.
   A. Motions
   B. Program
   C. Inquiry
   D. special

   *Answer:* D) special. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:55 — “Assume that a special order has been made for 3 P.M.”

**1080.** `[C0869 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At two o’clock, the special order for that time is taken up, even if it _____ a general order that is pending.

   *Answer:* interrupts — “At two o’clock, the special order for that time is taken up, even if it interrupts a general order that is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:55 — “At two o’clock, the special order for that time is taken up, even if it interrupts a general order that is pending.”

**1081.** `[C0869 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: special order is immediately taken up—_____ the one that is pending—because it was made first.
   A. interrupted
   B. Indefinitely
   C. interrupting
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) interrupting. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:55 — “special order is immediately taken up—interrupting the one that is pending—because it was made first.”

**1082.** `[C0869 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: order was made first, if it is still under _____ at four o’clock, it continues regardless of the order for that time.

   *Answer:* consideration — “order was made first, if it is still under consideration at four o’clock, it continues regardless of the order for that time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:55 — “order was made first, if it is still under consideration at four o’clock, it continues regardless of the order for that time.”

**1083.** `[C0869 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: order must await _____ of the prior-made 2 P.M.
   A. Convention
   B. sustaining
   C. completion
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* C) completion. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:55 — “order must await completion of the prior-made 2 P.M.”

**1084.** `[C0870 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that a special order does not interfere with a recess or _____ that is scheduled for a particular hour.
   A. immediately
   B. Preparation
   C. assemblies
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* D) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:56 — “It should be noted that a special order does not interfere with a recess or adjournment that is scheduled for a particular hour.”

**1085.** `[C0870 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that a special order does not interfere with a recess or _____ that is scheduled for a particular hour.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “It should be noted that a special order does not interfere with a recess or adjournment that is scheduled for a particular hour.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:56 — “It should be noted that a special order does not interfere with a recess or adjournment that is scheduled for a particular hour.”

**1086.** `[C0871 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of _____ meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as distinguished from a special order for the meeting;

   *Answer:* the — “When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of the meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as distinguished from a special order for the meeting;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:57 — “When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of the meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as distinguished from a special order for the meeting;”

**1087.** `[C0871 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of the meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as _____ from a special order for the meeting;
   A. Indefinitely
   B. subsequently
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. distinguished

   *Answer:* D) distinguished. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:57 — “When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of the meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as distinguished from a special order for the meeting;”

**1088.** `[C0871 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The special order for the meeting will then be taken up as soon as the minutes have been approved, and the _____ of the order of business will not be taken up until this special order has been disposed of.

   *Answer:* remainder — “The special order for the meeting will then be taken up as soon as the minutes have been approved, and the remainder of the order of business will not be taken up until this special order has been disposed of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:57 — “The special order for the meeting will then be taken up as soon as the minutes have been approved, and the remainder of the order of business will not be taken up until this special order has been disposed of.”

**1089.** `[C0871 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The special order for the meeting will then be taken up as soon as the minutes have been approved, and the _____ of the order of business will not be taken up until this special order has been disposed of.
   A. Objection
   B. expressly
   C. remainder
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* C) remainder. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:57 — “The special order for the meeting will then be taken up as soon as the minutes have been approved, and the remainder of the order of business will not be taken up until this special order has been disposed of.”

**1090.** `[C0872 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an hour is assigned to a particular subject in an _____, that subject is thereby made a special order unless, by footnote or other means, it is stated that the time is intended merely for guidance, in which case the subject is only a general order.

   *Answer:* agenda — “When an hour is assigned to a particular subject in an agenda, that subject is thereby made a special order unless, by footnote or other means, it is stated that the time is intended merely for guidance, in which case the subject is only a general order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:58 — “When an hour is assigned to a particular subject in an agenda, that subject is thereby made a special order unless, by footnote or other means, it is stated that the time is intended merely for guidance, in which case the subject is only a general order.”

**1091.** `[C0872 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By a single vote, a series of _____ orders or general orders—or a mixture of both—can be made;
   A. approve
   B. applied
   C. Reports
   D. special

   *Answer:* D) special. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:58 — “By a single vote, a series of special orders or general orders—or a mixture of both—can be made;”

**1092.** `[C0872 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an hour is assigned to a _____ subject in an agenda, that subject is thereby made a special order unless, by footnote or other means, it is stated that the time is intended merely for guidance, in which case the subject is only a general order.

   *Answer:* particular — “When an hour is assigned to a particular subject in an agenda, that subject is thereby made a special order unless, by footnote or other means, it is stated that the time is intended merely for guidance, in which case the subject is only a general order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:58 — “When an hour is assigned to a particular subject in an agenda, that subject is thereby made a special order unless, by footnote or other means, it is stated that the time is intended merely for guidance, in which case the subject is only a general order.”

**1093.** `[C0872 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Subjects for which no hour is _____ in an agenda are general orders.
   A. Reporting
   B. specified
   C. debatable
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* B) specified. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:58 — “Subjects for which no hour is specified in an agenda are general orders.”

**1094.** `[C0872 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By a single vote, a series of _____ orders or general orders—or a mixture of both—can be made;

   *Answer:* special — “By a single vote, a series of special orders or general orders—or a mixture of both—can be made;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:58 — “By a single vote, a series of special orders or general orders—or a mixture of both—can be made;”

**1095.** `[C0873 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an agenda, often an hour is assigned only to such subjects as the calls to order, recesses, _____, and particularly important items of business where it is desired to give the members greater assurance that the matter will not be considered before that time.

   *Answer:* adjournments — “In an agenda, often an hour is assigned only to such subjects as the calls to order, recesses, adjournments, and particularly important items of business where it is desired to give the members greater assurance that the matter will not be considered before that time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:59 — “In an agenda, often an hour is assigned only to such subjects as the calls to order, recesses, adjournments, and particularly important items of business where it is desired to give the members greater assurance that the matter will not be considered before that time.”

**1096.** `[C0873 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: These, then, are special orders for the time stated, and a strict adherence to these times provides a _____ to the members and invited speakers, who often come from great distances.
   A. landscaped
   B. incidental
   C. Substitute
   D. protection

   *Answer:* D) protection. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:59 — “These, then, are special orders for the time stated, and a strict adherence to these times provides a protection to the members and invited speakers, who often come from great distances.”

**1097.** `[C0873 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, a time is assigned for every item on the agenda.

   *Answer:* Occasionally — “Occasionally, a time is assigned for every item on the agenda.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:59 — “Occasionally, a time is assigned for every item on the agenda.”

**1098.** `[C0873 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While this practice may be necessary in some cases, the resulting loss of _____ often outweighs any benefits that may be gained.
   A. Permission
   B. Definitely
   C. interrupted
   D. flexibility

   *Answer:* D) flexibility. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:59 — “While this practice may be necessary in some cases, the resulting loss of flexibility often outweighs any benefits that may be gained.”

**1099.** `[C0873 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an agenda, often an hour is assigned only to such subjects as the calls to order, recesses, _____, and particularly important items of business where it is desired to give the members greater assurance that the matter will not be considered before that time.
   A. immediately
   B. adjournments
   C. interrupted
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) adjournments. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:59 — “In an agenda, often an hour is assigned only to such subjects as the calls to order, recesses, adjournments, and particularly important items of business where it is desired to give the members greater assurance that the matter will not be considered before that time.”

**1100.** `[C0874 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Organizations and Meetings in Which Adoption of an _____.

   *Answer:* Agenda Is Customary — “Organizations and Meetings in Which Adoption of an Agenda Is Customary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:60 — “Organizations and Meetings in Which Adoption of an Agenda Is Customary.”

**1101.** `[C0874 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ and Meetings in Which Adoption of an Agenda Is Customary.
   A. requirement
   B. Organizations
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) Organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:60 — “Organizations and Meetings in Which Adoption of an Agenda Is Customary.”

**1102.** `[C0874 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This is also frequently done when, for any reason, neither the standard order of business nor a special order of business established by rule of the _____ is practical or applicable.

   *Answer:* organization — “This is also frequently done when, for any reason, neither the standard order of business nor a special order of business established by rule of the organization is practical or applicable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:60 — “This is also frequently done when, for any reason, neither the standard order of business nor a special order of business established by rule of the organization is practical or applicable.”

**1103.** `[C0874 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This is also frequently done when, for any reason, neither the standard order of business nor a special order of business established by rule of the _____ is practical or applicable.
   A. immediately
   B. organization
   C. Particular
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:60 — “This is also frequently done when, for any reason, neither the standard order of business nor a special order of business established by rule of the organization is practical or applicable.”

**1104.** `[C0875 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At a session having no prescribed or adopted order of business, such an agenda is followed as a guide by the chair pending its formal adoption and can be adopted by _____ vote, even if it contains special orders;
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:61 — “At a session having no prescribed or adopted order of business, such an agenda is followed as a guide by the chair pending its formal adoption and can be adopted by majority vote, even if it contains special orders;”

**1105.** `[C0875 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases in which an agenda is adopted, usually this is done at the outset of a session and the agenda is _____ to cover the entire session.
   A. Interest
   B. decision
   C. intended
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* C) intended. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:61 — “In cases in which an agenda is adopted, usually this is done at the outset of a session and the agenda is intended to cover the entire session.”

**1106.** `[C0875 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At a session having no _____ or adopted order of business, such an agenda is followed as a guide by the chair pending its formal adoption and can be adopted by majority vote, even if it contains special orders;

   *Answer:* prescribed — “At a session having no prescribed or adopted order of business, such an agenda is followed as a guide by the chair pending its formal adoption and can be adopted by majority vote, even if it contains special orders;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:61 — “At a session having no prescribed or adopted order of business, such an agenda is followed as a guide by the chair pending its formal adoption and can be adopted by majority vote, even if it contains special orders;”

**1107.** `[C0875 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases in which an agenda is adopted, usually this is done at the outset of a _____ and the agenda is intended to cover the entire session.
   A. Divided
   B. adopted
   C. session
   D. approve

   *Answer:* C) session. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:61 — “In cases in which an agenda is adopted, usually this is done at the outset of a session and the agenda is intended to cover the entire session.”

**1108.** `[C0876 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some _____, it is customary to send each member, in advance of a meeting, an order of business or agenda, with some indication of the matters to be considered under each heading.
   A. organizations
   B. presentation
   C. adjournment
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* A) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:62 — “In some organizations, it is customary to send each member, in advance of a meeting, an order of business or agenda, with some indication of the matters to be considered under each heading.”

**1109.** `[C0876 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such an agenda is often provided for _____ only, with no intention or practice of submitting it for adoption.

   *Answer:* information — “Such an agenda is often provided for information only, with no intention or practice of submitting it for adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:62 — “Such an agenda is often provided for information only, with no intention or practice of submitting it for adoption.”

**1110.** `[C0876 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some _____, it is customary to send each member, in advance of a meeting, an order of business or agenda, with some indication of the matters to be considered under each heading.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In some organizations, it is customary to send each member, in advance of a meeting, an order of business or agenda, with some indication of the matters to be considered under each heading.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:62 — “In some organizations, it is customary to send each member, in advance of a meeting, an order of business or agenda, with some indication of the matters to be considered under each heading.”

**1111.** `[C0876 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such an agenda is often provided for _____ only, with no intention or practice of submitting it for adoption.
   A. Definitely
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Permission
   D. information

   *Answer:* D) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:62 — “Such an agenda is often provided for information only, with no intention or practice of submitting it for adoption.”

**1112.** `[C0877 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the adoption of a proposed agenda is pending, it is subject to amendment by _____ vote.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* A) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:63 — “When the adoption of a proposed agenda is pending, it is subject to amendment by majority vote.”

**1113.** `[C0877 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the adoption of a proposed agenda is pending, it is subject to _____ by majority vote.

   *Answer:* amendment — “When the adoption of a proposed agenda is pending, it is subject to amendment by majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:63 — “When the adoption of a proposed agenda is pending, it is subject to amendment by majority vote.”

**1114.** `[C0878 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In reference to an order of business specially adopted for a given session, the term _____ is often used instead of agenda;

   *Answer:* program — “In reference to an order of business specially adopted for a given session, the term program is often used instead of agenda;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:64 — “In reference to an order of business specially adopted for a given session, the term program is often used instead of agenda;”

**1115.** `[C0878 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In reference to an order of business specially adopted for a given session, the term program is often used instead of _____;
   A. charge
   B. agenda
   C. Debate
   D. Bylaws

   *Answer:* B) agenda. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:64 — “In reference to an order of business specially adopted for a given session, the term program is often used instead of agenda;”

**1116.** `[C0878 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but while the latter _____ includes only items of business, the former may include also the times for speakers, meals, and other nonbusiness matters.
   A. Preparation
   B. Substitute
   C. Definitely
   D. technically

   *Answer:* D) technically. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:64 — “but while the latter technically includes only items of business, the former may include also the times for speakers, meals, and other nonbusiness matters.”

**1117.** `[C0878 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but while the latter _____ includes only items of business, the former may include also the times for speakers, meals, and other nonbusiness matters.

   *Answer:* technically — “but while the latter technically includes only items of business, the former may include also the times for speakers, meals, and other nonbusiness matters.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:64 — “but while the latter technically includes only items of business, the former may include also the times for speakers, meals, and other nonbusiness matters.”

**1118.** `[C0879 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While an extension under these conditions is seldom desirable and is often unfair to the next topic, it is sometimes necessary, and a motion for the extension can be adopted without debate by a _____ vote (see also 18).

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “While an extension under these conditions is seldom desirable and is often unfair to the next topic, it is sometimes necessary, and a motion for the extension can be adopted without debate by a two-thirds vote (see also 18).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:65 — “While an extension under these conditions is seldom desirable and is often unfair to the next topic, it is sometimes necessary, and a motion for the extension can be adopted without debate by a two-thirds vote (see also 18).”

**1119.** `[C0879 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the assigned time for taking up a topic in an agenda arrives, the chair _____ that fact.
   A. president
   B. Precedent
   C. announces
   D. delegates

   *Answer:* C) announces. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:65 — “When the assigned time for taking up a topic in an agenda arrives, the chair announces that fact.”

**1120.** `[C0879 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Then he puts to a vote any pending questions without allowing further debate, unless someone _____ moves to lay the question on the table, postpone it, or refer it to a committee.

   *Answer:* immediately — “Then he puts to a vote any pending questions without allowing further debate, unless someone immediately moves to lay the question on the table, postpone it, or refer it to a committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:65 — “Then he puts to a vote any pending questions without allowing further debate, unless someone immediately moves to lay the question on the table, postpone it, or refer it to a committee.”

**1121.** `[C0879 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If any of these _____ motions are moved, they are likewise put to a vote, together with any amendment to them, without debate.
   A. variations
   B. subsidiary
   C. convention
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* B) subsidiary. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:65 — “If any of these subsidiary motions are moved, they are likewise put to a vote, together with any amendment to them, without debate.”

**1122.** `[C0879 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Besides these subsidiary motions, a motion to extend the time for _____ the pending question is in order.

   *Answer:* considering — “Besides these subsidiary motions, a motion to extend the time for considering the pending question is in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:65 — “Besides these subsidiary motions, a motion to extend the time for considering the pending question is in order.”

**1123.** `[C0880 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (However, if the _____ Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)

   *Answer:* Previous — “(However, if the Previous Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:66 — “(However, if the Previous Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)”

**1124.** `[C0880 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (However, if the Previous _____ has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)
   A. Question
   B. Standing
   C. Meetings
   D. chairman

   *Answer:* A) Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:66 — “(However, if the Previous Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)”

**1125.** `[C0880 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Declaring a Scheduled Recess or _____.

   *Answer:* Adjournment — “Declaring a Scheduled Recess or Adjournment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:66 — “Declaring a Scheduled Recess or Adjournment.”

**1126.** `[C0880 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Declaring a Scheduled Recess or _____.
   A. resolutions
   B. constitution
   C. assemblies
   D. Adjournment

   *Answer:* D) Adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:66 — “Declaring a Scheduled Recess or Adjournment.”

**1127.** `[C0881 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: These motions are undebatable and require a _____ vote;
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:67 — “These motions are undebatable and require a two-thirds vote;”

**1128.** `[C0881 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the chair announces the hour, any member can move to reschedule the time for recess or _____, or to extend the time of considering the pending question for a specified period.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “When the chair announces the hour, any member can move to reschedule the time for recess or adjournment, or to extend the time of considering the pending question for a specified period.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:67 — “When the chair announces the hour, any member can move to reschedule the time for recess or adjournment, or to extend the time of considering the pending question for a specified period.”

**1129.** `[C0881 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: These motions are _____ and require a two-thirds vote;
   A. Information
   B. Procedures
   C. convention
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* D) undebatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:67 — “These motions are undebatable and require a two-thirds vote;”

**1130.** `[C0881 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the chair announces the hour, any member can move to reschedule the time for recess or _____, or to extend the time of considering the pending question for a specified period.
   A. requirement
   B. convention
   C. adjournment
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* C) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:67 — “When the chair announces the hour, any member can move to reschedule the time for recess or adjournment, or to extend the time of considering the pending question for a specified period.”

**1131.** `[C0882 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of an adjournment, see _____ below.

   *Answer:* Carrying Over Unfinished Business — “In the case of an adjournment, see Carrying Over Unfinished Business below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:68 — “In the case of an adjournment, see Carrying Over Unfinished Business below.”

**1132.** `[C0882 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of a recess, any pending business is _____ for the recess, and taken up again after the recess.
   A. authorizes
   B. interrupted
   C. Definitely
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) interrupted. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:68 — “In the case of a recess, any pending business is interrupted for the recess, and taken up again after the recess.”

**1133.** `[C0882 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of an _____, see Carrying Over Unfinished Business below.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “In the case of an adjournment, see Carrying Over Unfinished Business below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:68 — “In the case of an adjournment, see Carrying Over Unfinished Business below.”

**1134.** `[C0882 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of a recess, any pending business is _____ for the recess, and taken up again after the recess.

   *Answer:* interrupted — “In the case of a recess, any pending business is interrupted for the recess, and taken up again after the recess.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:68 — “In the case of a recess, any pending business is interrupted for the recess, and taken up again after the recess.”

**1135.** `[C0883 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of Providing for Unfinished Business.

   *Answer:* Advisability — “Advisability of Providing for Unfinished Business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:69 — “Advisability of Providing for Unfinished Business.”

**1136.** `[C0883 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Advisability of Providing for _____ Business.
   A. substitute
   B. Unfinished
   C. convention
   D. subsidiary

   *Answer:* B) Unfinished. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:69 — “Advisability of Providing for Unfinished Business.”

**1137.** `[C0883 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In agendas in which most or all of the items are special orders for _____ times, it may be necessary to provide for unfinished business near the end of each day’s sitting.

   *Answer:* particular — “In agendas in which most or all of the items are special orders for particular times, it may be necessary to provide for unfinished business near the end of each day’s sitting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:69 — “In agendas in which most or all of the items are special orders for particular times, it may be necessary to provide for unfinished business near the end of each day’s sitting.”

**1138.** `[C0883 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such _____ give the assembly a recourse whenever it needs a little more time before voting on a question.
   A. provisions
   B. Definitely
   C. assemblies
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) provisions. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:69 — “Such provisions give the assembly a recourse whenever it needs a little more time before voting on a question.”

**1139.** `[C0883 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It can postpone a question to a time provided for unfinished business or, at all events, can conclude _____ of a question during such a period.

   *Answer:* consideration — “It can postpone a question to a time provided for unfinished business or, at all events, can conclude consideration of a question during such a period.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:69 — “It can postpone a question to a time provided for unfinished business or, at all events, can conclude consideration of a question during such a period.”

**1140.** `[C0884 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In sessions consisting of several meetings, if the time set for _____ of a meeting arrives before all of the matters scheduled for that meeting have been considered, the remaining items of business are carried over to the next meeting.
   A. interrupted
   B. requirement
   C. adjournment
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* C) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:70 — “In sessions consisting of several meetings, if the time set for adjournment of a meeting arrives before all of the matters scheduled for that meeting have been considered, the remaining items of business are carried over to the next meeting.”

**1141.** `[C0884 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ items of business are taken first in their order before the matters scheduled for the later meeting, provided that the agenda makes no special provision for unfinished business that day and no conflict arises with a special order.

   *Answer:* unfinished — “The unfinished items of business are taken first in their order before the matters scheduled for the later meeting, provided that the agenda makes no special provision for unfinished business that day and no conflict arises with a special order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:70 — “The unfinished items of business are taken first in their order before the matters scheduled for the later meeting, provided that the agenda makes no special provision for unfinished business that day and no conflict arises with a special order.”

**1142.** `[C0884 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Therefore, when most items in the agenda are general orders, it is wise to schedule the more important items of business at a _____ early meeting of a convention or to set them as special orders.
   A. Definitely
   B. reasonably
   C. assemblies
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) reasonably. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:70 — “Therefore, when most items in the agenda are general orders, it is wise to schedule the more important items of business at a reasonably early meeting of a convention or to set them as special orders.”

**1143.** `[C0884 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In sessions consisting of several meetings, if the time set for _____ of a meeting arrives before all of the matters scheduled for that meeting have been considered, the remaining items of business are carried over to the next meeting.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “In sessions consisting of several meetings, if the time set for adjournment of a meeting arrives before all of the matters scheduled for that meeting have been considered, the remaining items of business are carried over to the next meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:70 — “In sessions consisting of several meetings, if the time set for adjournment of a meeting arrives before all of the matters scheduled for that meeting have been considered, the remaining items of business are carried over to the next meeting.”

**1144.** `[C0884 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ items of business are taken first in their order before the matters scheduled for the later meeting, provided that the agenda makes no special provision for unfinished business that day and no conflict arises with a special order.
   A. authorizes
   B. unfinished
   C. Permission
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) unfinished. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:70 — “The unfinished items of business are taken first in their order before the matters scheduled for the later meeting, provided that the agenda makes no special provision for unfinished business that day and no conflict arises with a special order.”

**1145.** `[C0885 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: More complete rules affecting the _____ of the floor are contained in this section.

   *Answer:* assignment — “More complete rules affecting the assignment of the floor are contained in this section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:1 — “More complete rules affecting the assignment of the floor are contained in this section.”

**1146.** `[C0885 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: More complete rules affecting the _____ of the floor are contained in this section.
   A. convention
   B. assignment
   C. Incidental
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) assignment. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:1 — “More complete rules affecting the assignment of the floor are contained in this section.”

**1147.** `[C0886 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair must _____ any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it.

   *Answer:* recognize — “The chair must recognize any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:2 — “The chair must recognize any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it.”

**1148.** `[C0886 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair normally _____ a member (thereby assigning the floor to him) by announcing, as applicable, the member’s name or title, or the place or unit that he represents.
   A. landscaped
   B. privileged
   C. Procedures
   D. recognizes

   *Answer:* D) recognizes. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:2 — “The chair normally recognizes a member (thereby assigning the floor to him) by announcing, as applicable, the member’s name or title, or the place or unit that he represents.”

**1149.** `[C0886 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If necessary, the member— either on his own initiative or at the request of the chair— should state his name, with any appropriate additional _____, as soon as the presiding officer turns toward him after he has risen and addressed the chair.

   *Answer:* identification — “If necessary, the member— either on his own initiative or at the request of the chair— should state his name, with any appropriate additional identification, as soon as the presiding officer turns toward him after he has risen and addressed the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:2 — “If necessary, the member— either on his own initiative or at the request of the chair— should state his name, with any appropriate additional identification, as soon as the presiding officer turns toward him after he has risen and addressed the chair.”

**1150.** `[C0886 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Variations in the granting of _____ are as follows:
   A. recognition
   B. Substitute
   C. assemblies
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:2 — “Variations in the granting of recognition are as follows:”

**1151.** `[C0886 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair must _____ any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it.
   A. committee
   B. presiding
   C. Committee
   D. recognize

   *Answer:* D) recognize. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:2 — “The chair must recognize any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it.”

**1152.** `[C0888 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion is called out by anyone who has not obtained the floor, the chair must not treat it as having been properly moved if another member, by rising promptly and claiming the floor, shows that _____ consent has not been given.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “If a motion is called out by anyone who has not obtained the floor, the chair must not treat it as having been properly moved if another member, by rising promptly and claiming the floor, shows that unanimous consent has not been given.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:4 — “If a motion is called out by anyone who has not obtained the floor, the chair must not treat it as having been properly moved if another member, by rising promptly and claiming the floor, shows that unanimous consent has not been given.”

**1153.** `[C0888 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion is called out by anyone who has not obtained the floor, the chair must not treat it as having been properly moved if another member, by rising promptly and claiming the floor, shows that _____ consent has not been given.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* D) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:4 — “If a motion is called out by anyone who has not obtained the floor, the chair must not treat it as having been properly moved if another member, by rising promptly and claiming the floor, shows that unanimous consent has not been given.”

**1154.** `[C0889 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the _____ (16), that is in order at the time.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the Previous Question (16), that is in order at the time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:5 — “for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the Previous Question (16), that is in order at the time.”

**1155.** `[C0889 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When assigned the floor, a member may use it for any proper purpose, or a _____ of purposes;
   A. combination
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Reconsider
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) combination. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:5 — “When assigned the floor, a member may use it for any proper purpose, or a combination of purposes;”

**1156.** `[C0889 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any _____ motion, including the Previous Question (16), that is in order at the time.

   *Answer:* secondary — “for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the Previous Question (16), that is in order at the time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:5 — “for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the Previous Question (16), that is in order at the time.”

**1157.** `[C0889 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When assigned the floor, a member may use it for any proper purpose, or a _____ of purposes;

   *Answer:* combination — “When assigned the floor, a member may use it for any proper purpose, or a combination of purposes;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:5 — “When assigned the floor, a member may use it for any proper purpose, or a combination of purposes;”

**1158.** `[C0891 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under a variety of particular conditions, however, when more than _____ claims the floor at about the same time, the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.
   A. of the day
   B. Agreed To
   C. Suspend the
   D. one member

   *Answer:* D) one member. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:7 — “Under a variety of particular conditions, however, when more than one member claims the floor at about the same time, the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.”

**1159.** `[C0891 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under a variety of _____ conditions, however, when more than one member claims the floor at about the same time, the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.

   *Answer:* particular — “Under a variety of particular conditions, however, when more than one member claims the floor at about the same time, the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:7 — “Under a variety of particular conditions, however, when more than one member claims the floor at about the same time, the best interests of the assembly require the floor to be assigned to a claimant who was not the first to rise and address the chair.”

**1160.** `[C0891 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a claimant to the floor in these cases is said to be entitled to “preference in being recognized” or “preference in _____.” A member cannot rise to claim preference in recognition after the chair has actually recognized another member.
   A. committees
   B. constitution
   C. requirement
   D. recognition

   *Answer:* D) recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:7 — “Such a claimant to the floor in these cases is said to be entitled to “preference in being recognized” or “preference in recognition.” A member cannot rise to claim preference in recognition after the chair has actually recognized another member.”

**1161.** `[C0891 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a claimant to the floor in these cases is said to be entitled to “preference in being recognized” or “preference in _____.” A member cannot rise to claim preference in recognition after the chair has actually recognized another member.

   *Answer:* recognition — “Such a claimant to the floor in these cases is said to be entitled to “preference in being recognized” or “preference in recognition.” A member cannot rise to claim preference in recognition after the chair has actually recognized another member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:7 — “Such a claimant to the floor in these cases is said to be entitled to “preference in being recognized” or “preference in recognition.” A member cannot rise to claim preference in recognition after the chair has actually recognized another member.”

**1162.** `[C0892 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the chair has just reported a vote, a member is entitled to preference in _____ to make an appropriate motion that the vote be taken again by another method (30).

   *Answer:* recognition — “When the chair has just reported a vote, a member is entitled to preference in recognition to make an appropriate motion that the vote be taken again by another method (30).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:8 — “When the chair has just reported a vote, a member is entitled to preference in recognition to make an appropriate motion that the vote be taken again by another method (30).”

**1163.** `[C0892 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the chair has just reported a vote, a member is entitled to preference in _____ to make an appropriate motion that the vote be taken again by another method (30).
   A. recognition
   B. Definitely
   C. undebatable
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:8 — “When the chair has just reported a vote, a member is entitled to preference in recognition to make an appropriate motion that the vote be taken again by another method (30).”

**1164.** `[C0893 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Preference in Recognition When a Debatable Question Is _____.

   *Answer:* Immediately Pending — “Preference in Recognition When a Debatable Question Is Immediately Pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:9 — “Preference in Recognition When a Debatable Question Is Immediately Pending.”

**1165.** `[C0893 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Preference in _____ When a Debatable Question Is Immediately Pending.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Incidental
   C. Information
   D. Recognition

   *Answer:* D) Recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:9 — “Preference in Recognition When a Debatable Question Is Immediately Pending.”

**1166.** `[C0895 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a member has moved to reconsider the vote on a motion for the announced purpose of amending the motion, if the vote is _____ he must be recognized in preference to others in order to move his amendment.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “When a member has moved to reconsider the vote on a motion for the announced purpose of amending the motion, if the vote is reconsidered he must be recognized in preference to others in order to move his amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:11 — “When a member has moved to reconsider the vote on a motion for the announced purpose of amending the motion, if the vote is reconsidered he must be recognized in preference to others in order to move his amendment.”

**1167.** `[C0895 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This rule also applies to _____ of amendable motions that are not debatable (see list on page t47), as noted below.
   A. reconsiderations
   B. president-elect
   C. interrupted
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) reconsiderations. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:11 — “This rule also applies to reconsiderations of amendable motions that are not debatable (see list on page t47), as noted below.”

**1168.** `[C0895 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a member has moved to reconsider the vote on a motion for the announced purpose of amending the motion, if the vote is _____ he must be recognized in preference to others in order to move his amendment.
   A. requirement
   B. interrupted
   C. reconsidered
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:11 — “When a member has moved to reconsider the vote on a motion for the announced purpose of amending the motion, if the vote is reconsidered he must be recognized in preference to others in order to move his amendment.”

**1169.** `[C0895 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This rule also applies to _____ of amendable motions that are not debatable (see list on page t47), as noted below.

   *Answer:* reconsiderations — “This rule also applies to reconsiderations of amendable motions that are not debatable (see list on page t47), as noted below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:11 — “This rule also applies to reconsiderations of amendable motions that are not debatable (see list on page t47), as noted below.”

**1170.** `[C0896 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Preference in Recognition When an Undebatable Question _____.

   *Answer:* Is Immediately Pending — “Preference in Recognition When an Undebatable Question Is Immediately Pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:12 — “Preference in Recognition When an Undebatable Question Is Immediately Pending.”

**1171.** `[C0896 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When an undebatable motion that can be amended is reconsidered for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to _____ (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.
   A. Particular
   B. Unfinished
   C. Reconsider
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:12 — “When an undebatable motion that can be amended is reconsidered for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to Reconsider (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.”

**1172.** `[C0896 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Preference in _____ When an Undebatable Question Is Immediately Pending.

   *Answer:* Recognition — “Preference in Recognition When an Undebatable Question Is Immediately Pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:12 — “Preference in Recognition When an Undebatable Question Is Immediately Pending.”

**1173.** `[C0896 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an undebatable motion that can be amended is _____ for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to Reconsider (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “When an undebatable motion that can be amended is reconsidered for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to Reconsider (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:12 — “When an undebatable motion that can be amended is reconsidered for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to Reconsider (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.”

**1174.** `[C0897 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Preference in _____ When No Question Is Pending.

   *Answer:* Recognition — “Preference in Recognition When No Question Is Pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:13 — “Preference in Recognition When No Question Is Pending.”

**1175.** `[C0897 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Preference in _____ When No Question Is Pending.
   A. Recognition
   B. undebatable
   C. authorizes
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) Recognition. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:13 — “Preference in Recognition When No Question Is Pending.”

**1176.** `[C0898 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair is in doubt as to who is entitled to the floor, he can allow the assembly to decide the question by a vote, in which case the member _____ the largest vote is entitled to the floor.

   *Answer:* receiving — “If the chair is in doubt as to who is entitled to the floor, he can allow the assembly to decide the question by a vote, in which case the member receiving the largest vote is entitled to the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:14 — “If the chair is in doubt as to who is entitled to the floor, he can allow the assembly to decide the question by a vote, in which case the member receiving the largest vote is entitled to the floor.”

**1177.** `[C0898 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair is in doubt as to who is entitled to the floor, he can allow the assembly to decide the question by a vote, in which case the member _____ the largest vote is entitled to the floor.
   A. Privilege
   B. expressly
   C. receiving
   D. execution

   *Answer:* C) receiving. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:14 — “If the chair is in doubt as to who is entitled to the floor, he can allow the assembly to decide the question by a vote, in which case the member receiving the largest vote is entitled to the floor.”

**1178.** `[C0899 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than one member rose and addressed the chair promptly, a _____ can be raised.

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than one member rose and addressed the chair promptly, a Point of Order can be raised.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:15 — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than one member rose and addressed the chair promptly, a Point of Order can be raised.”

**1179.** `[C0899 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than _____ rose and addressed the chair promptly, a Point of Order can be raised.
   A. and Example
   B. Lay on the
   C. main motion
   D. one member

   *Answer:* D) one member. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:15 — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than one member rose and addressed the chair promptly, a Point of Order can be raised.”

**1180.** `[C0899 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except in a mass meeting, the decision of the chair in assigning the floor can be appealed from by any _____ —one making the appeal and the other seconding it (24).

   *Answer:* two members — “Except in a mass meeting, the decision of the chair in assigning the floor can be appealed from by any two members —one making the appeal and the other seconding it (24).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:15 — “Except in a mass meeting, the decision of the chair in assigning the floor can be appealed from by any two members —one making the appeal and the other seconding it (24).”

**1181.** `[C0899 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except in a mass meeting, the decision of the chair in _____ the floor can be appealed from by any two members —one making the appeal and the other seconding it (24).

   *Answer:* assigning — “Except in a mass meeting, the decision of the chair in assigning the floor can be appealed from by any two members —one making the appeal and the other seconding it (24).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:15 — “Except in a mass meeting, the decision of the chair in assigning the floor can be appealed from by any two members —one making the appeal and the other seconding it (24).”

**1182.** `[C0900 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the _____ or a motion to limit debate is moved, members who have been waiting in line cannot validly protest;
   A. Previous Question
   B. Robert’s Rules of
   C. Quorum of Members
   D. Suspend the Rules

   *Answer:* A) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:16 — “If the Previous Question or a motion to limit debate is moved, members who have been waiting in line cannot validly protest;”

**1183.** `[C0900 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In large _____ or similar bodies, some of the rules applicable to the assignment of the floor may require adaptation, which, pending the adoption of appropriate convention standing rules or special rules of order, the chair may direct.

   *Answer:* conventions — “In large conventions or similar bodies, some of the rules applicable to the assignment of the floor may require adaptation, which, pending the adoption of appropriate convention standing rules or special rules of order, the chair may direct.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:16 — “In large conventions or similar bodies, some of the rules applicable to the assignment of the floor may require adaptation, which, pending the adoption of appropriate convention standing rules or special rules of order, the chair may direct.”

**1184.** `[C0900 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, in a large hall where _____ are in use and members must walk some distance to reach one, members may be asked to line up at numbered microphones.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. microphones
   C. convention
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) microphones. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:16 — “For example, in a large hall where microphones are in use and members must walk some distance to reach one, members may be asked to line up at numbered microphones.”

**1185.** `[C0900 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: They may be recognized in numerical order, or someone may list them for the chair in the order in which assistant _____ turned on lights affixed to the microphones.

   *Answer:* sergeants-at-arms — “They may be recognized in numerical order, or someone may list them for the chair in the order in which assistant sergeants-at-arms turned on lights affixed to the microphones.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:16 — “They may be recognized in numerical order, or someone may list them for the chair in the order in which assistant sergeants-at-arms turned on lights affixed to the microphones.”

**1186.** `[C0900 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It may be provided that a member who has a priority matter, such as a point of order, may ask the assistant at the _____ to flash the light to so indicate.
   A. authorizes
   B. microphone
   C. persuasive
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) microphone. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:16 — “It may be provided that a member who has a priority matter, such as a point of order, may ask the assistant at the microphone to flash the light to so indicate.”

**1187.** `[C0902 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a member has been assigned the floor and has begun to speak—unless he begins to discuss a subject when no motion is pending or speaks longer in debate than the rules of the assembly allow—he cannot be _____ by another member or by the chair except for one of the following purposes, and then only when the urgency of the situation justifies it:

   *Answer:* interrupted — “When a member has been assigned the floor and has begun to speak—unless he begins to discuss a subject when no motion is pending or speaks longer in debate than the rules of the assembly allow—he cannot be interrupted by another member or by the chair except for one of the following purposes, and then only when the urgency of the situation justifies it:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:18 — “When a member has been assigned the floor and has begun to speak—unless he begins to discuss a subject when no motion is pending or speaks longer in debate than the rules of the assembly allow—he cannot be interrupted by another member or by the chair except for one of the following purposes, and then only when the urgency of the situation justifies it:”

**1188.** `[C0902 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a member has been assigned the floor and has begun to speak—unless he begins to discuss a subject when no motion is pending or speaks longer in debate than the rules of the assembly allow—he cannot be _____ by another member or by the chair except for one of the following purposes, and then only when the urgency of the situation justifies it:
   A. Unfinished
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Definitely
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* D) interrupted. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:18 — “When a member has been assigned the floor and has begun to speak—unless he begins to discuss a subject when no motion is pending or speaks longer in debate than the rules of the assembly allow—he cannot be interrupted by another member or by the chair except for one of the following purposes, and then only when the urgency of the situation justifies it:”

**1189.** `[C0903 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a member has been assigned the floor but before he has begun to speak, it is in order to take any of the steps listed above, and also, if there may be no other _____, to rise for the purpose of:

   *Answer:* opportunity — “After a member has been assigned the floor but before he has begun to speak, it is in order to take any of the steps listed above, and also, if there may be no other opportunity, to rise for the purpose of:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:19 — “After a member has been assigned the floor but before he has begun to speak, it is in order to take any of the steps listed above, and also, if there may be no other opportunity, to rise for the purpose of:”

**1190.** `[C0903 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a member has been assigned the floor but before he has begun to speak, it is in order to take any of the steps listed above, and also, if there may be no other _____, to rise for the purpose of:
   A. constitution
   B. opportunity
   C. Indefinitely
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:19 — “After a member has been assigned the floor but before he has begun to speak, it is in order to take any of the steps listed above, and also, if there may be no other opportunity, to rise for the purpose of:”

**1191.** `[C0904 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an _____ occurs for any of the reasons listed above, the member who had the floor does not lose it, although he takes his seat while the interrupting matter is being attended to.

   *Answer:* interruption — “If an interruption occurs for any of the reasons listed above, the member who had the floor does not lose it, although he takes his seat while the interrupting matter is being attended to.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:20 — “If an interruption occurs for any of the reasons listed above, the member who had the floor does not lose it, although he takes his seat while the interrupting matter is being attended to.”

**1192.** `[C0904 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an _____ occurs for any of the reasons listed above, the member who had the floor does not lose it, although he takes his seat while the interrupting matter is being attended to.
   A. interrupted
   B. resolutions
   C. subsequently
   D. interruption

   *Answer:* D) interruption. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:20 — “If an interruption occurs for any of the reasons listed above, the member who had the floor does not lose it, although he takes his seat while the interrupting matter is being attended to.”

**1193.** `[C0906 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a member has risen to claim the floor or has been _____ the floor, it is out of order for another to call out a motion to adjourn, or a motion to lay the pending question on the table.

   *Answer:* assigned — “When a member has risen to claim the floor or has been assigned the floor, it is out of order for another to call out a motion to adjourn, or a motion to lay the pending question on the table.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:22 — “When a member has risen to claim the floor or has been assigned the floor, it is out of order for another to call out a motion to adjourn, or a motion to lay the pending question on the table.”

**1194.** `[C0906 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If someone does so, or if calls of “Question!” are made, it is the duty of the chair to obtain order and protect the rights of the member who is _____ to the floor.
   A. entitled
   B. business
   C. Interest
   D. division

   *Answer:* A) entitled. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:22 — “If someone does so, or if calls of “Question!” are made, it is the duty of the chair to obtain order and protect the rights of the member who is entitled to the floor.”

**1195.** `[C0906 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a member has risen to claim the floor or has been _____ the floor, it is out of order for another to call out a motion to adjourn, or a motion to lay the pending question on the table.
   A. assigned
   B. division
   C. Previous
   D. Interest

   *Answer:* A) assigned. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:22 — “When a member has risen to claim the floor or has been assigned the floor, it is out of order for another to call out a motion to adjourn, or a motion to lay the pending question on the table.”

**1196.** `[C0906 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If someone does so, or if calls of “Question!” are made, it is the duty of the chair to obtain order and protect the rights of the member who is _____ to the floor.

   *Answer:* entitled — “If someone does so, or if calls of “Question!” are made, it is the duty of the chair to obtain order and protect the rights of the member who is entitled to the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:22 — “If someone does so, or if calls of “Question!” are made, it is the duty of the chair to obtain order and protect the rights of the member who is entitled to the floor.”

**1197.** `[C0907 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.

   *Answer:* Debate — “Debate, rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:1 — “Debate, rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.”

**1198.** `[C0907 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a _____ assembly, this term applies to discussion on the merits of a pending question—that is, whether the proposal under consideration should, or should not, be agreed to.
   A. adjournment
   B. Procedures
   C. deliberative
   D. specification

   *Answer:* C) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:1 — “In a deliberative assembly, this term applies to discussion on the merits of a pending question—that is, whether the proposal under consideration should, or should not, be agreed to.”

**1199.** `[C0907 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debate, rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of _____ by intelligent people.

   *Answer:* consequence — “Debate, rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:1 — “Debate, rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.”

**1200.** `[C0907 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a deliberative assembly, this term applies to discussion on the merits of a pending question—that is, whether the proposal under _____ should, or should not, be agreed to.
   A. subsequently
   B. consideration
   C. specification
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:1 — “In a deliberative assembly, this term applies to discussion on the merits of a pending question—that is, whether the proposal under consideration should, or should not, be agreed to.”

**1201.** `[C0908 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debatability is a _____ of all main motions and of certain other motions, depending on the parliamentary function they serve, according to principles summarized at the end of this section;

   *Answer:* characteristic — “Debatability is a characteristic of all main motions and of certain other motions, depending on the parliamentary function they serve, according to principles summarized at the end of this section;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:2 — “Debatability is a characteristic of all main motions and of certain other motions, depending on the parliamentary function they serve, according to principles summarized at the end of this section;”

**1202.** `[C0908 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Debatability is a _____ of all main motions and of certain other motions, depending on the parliamentary function they serve, according to principles summarized at the end of this section;
   A. Indefinitely
   B. characteristic
   C. recommendation
   D. Responsibilities

   *Answer:* B) characteristic. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:2 — “Debatability is a characteristic of all main motions and of certain other motions, depending on the parliamentary function they serve, according to principles summarized at the end of this section;”

**1203.** `[C0909 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While the amount of debate on a motion in actual practice will depend on such factors as its _____, how strongly it is contested, etc., every member of the assembly has the right to speak to every debatable motion before it is finally acted upon;

   *Answer:* importance — “While the amount of debate on a motion in actual practice will depend on such factors as its importance, how strongly it is contested, etc., every member of the assembly has the right to speak to every debatable motion before it is finally acted upon;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:3 — “While the amount of debate on a motion in actual practice will depend on such factors as its importance, how strongly it is contested, etc., every member of the assembly has the right to speak to every debatable motion before it is finally acted upon;”

**1204.** `[C0909 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and subject only to general limitations on debate established by _____ law or the rules of the body as explained below, this right cannot be interfered with except by a twothirds vote.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. parliamentary
   C. Vice-President
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:3 — “and subject only to general limitations on debate established by parliamentary law or the rules of the body as explained below, this right cannot be interfered with except by a twothirds vote.”

**1205.** `[C0909 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While the amount of debate on a motion in actual practice will depend on such factors as its _____, how strongly it is contested, etc., every member of the assembly has the right to speak to every debatable motion before it is finally acted upon;
   A. introduced
   B. committees
   C. importance
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) importance. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:3 — “While the amount of debate on a motion in actual practice will depend on such factors as its importance, how strongly it is contested, etc., every member of the assembly has the right to speak to every debatable motion before it is finally acted upon;”

**1206.** `[C0909 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and subject only to general limitations on debate established by _____ law or the rules of the body as explained below, this right cannot be interfered with except by a twothirds vote.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “and subject only to general limitations on debate established by parliamentary law or the rules of the body as explained below, this right cannot be interfered with except by a twothirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:3 — “and subject only to general limitations on debate established by parliamentary law or the rules of the body as explained below, this right cannot be interfered with except by a twothirds vote.”

**1207.** `[C0910 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Until a matter has been brought before the assembly in the form of a motion _____ a specific action, it cannot be debated.

   *Answer:* proposing — “Until a matter has been brought before the assembly in the form of a motion proposing a specific action, it cannot be debated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:4 — “Until a matter has been brought before the assembly in the form of a motion proposing a specific action, it cannot be debated.”

**1208.** `[C0910 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As _____ in 3 and 4, the motion must be made by a member who has obtained the floor while no question is pending (or while the motion is in order, if it is not a main motion), after which it must be seconded by another member (unless it is made by direction of a board or committee), and must be stated by the chair.
   A. Signature
   B. expressly
   C. execution
   D. explained

   *Answer:* D) explained. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:4 — “As explained in 3 and 4, the motion must be made by a member who has obtained the floor while no question is pending (or while the motion is in order, if it is not a main motion), after which it must be seconded by another member (unless it is made by direction of a board or committee), and must be stated by the chair.”

**1209.** `[C0910 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair may conclude his statement of the question on the debatable motion by asking, “Are you ready for the question?” or, less formally, “Is there any debate?” _____, he may simply pause and turn toward the maker of the motion to see if he desires the floor first in debate.

   *Answer:* Alternatively — “The chair may conclude his statement of the question on the debatable motion by asking, “Are you ready for the question?” or, less formally, “Is there any debate?” Alternatively, he may simply pause and turn toward the maker of the motion to see if he desires the floor first in debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:4 — “The chair may conclude his statement of the question on the debatable motion by asking, “Are you ready for the question?” or, less formally, “Is there any debate?” Alternatively, he may simply pause and turn toward the maker of the motion to see if he desires the floor first in debate.”

**1210.** `[C0910 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the maker of the motion has had the _____ to speak first if he wishes, other members can rise and address the chair to claim the floor for the purpose of debate, as explained in 3:30ff.
   A. Unfinished
   B. committees
   C. opportunity
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:4 — “After the maker of the motion has had the opportunity to speak first if he wishes, other members can rise and address the chair to claim the floor for the purpose of debate, as explained in 3:30ff.”

**1211.** `[C0910 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Until a matter has been brought before the assembly in the form of a motion _____ a specific action, it cannot be debated.
   A. Crestwood
   B. reception
   C. debatable
   D. proposing

   *Answer:* D) proposing. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:4 — “Until a matter has been brought before the assembly in the form of a motion proposing a specific action, it cannot be debated.”

**1212.** `[C0911 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for _____ legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.

   *Answer:* any — “A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:5 — “A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.”

**1213.** `[C0911 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for _____ legitimate purposes.
   A. clerk
   B. Other
   C. all
   D. Board

   *Answer:* C) all. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:5 — “A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.”

**1214.** `[C0912 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When debate appears to have concluded, the chair may again ask, “Are you ready for the question?” (or “Is there any further debate?”) or if, after a _____ pause, no one rises to claim the floor, the chair may assume that no member wishes to speak and, standing, may proceed to put the question.

   *Answer:* reasonable — “When debate appears to have concluded, the chair may again ask, “Are you ready for the question?” (or “Is there any further debate?”) or if, after a reasonable pause, no one rises to claim the floor, the chair may assume that no member wishes to speak and, standing, may proceed to put the question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:6 — “When debate appears to have concluded, the chair may again ask, “Are you ready for the question?” (or “Is there any further debate?”) or if, after a reasonable pause, no one rises to claim the floor, the chair may assume that no member wishes to speak and, standing, may proceed to put the question.”

**1215.** `[C0912 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When debate appears to have concluded, the chair may again ask, “Are you ready for the question?” (or “Is there any further debate?”) or if, after a _____ pause, no one rises to claim the floor, the chair may assume that no member wishes to speak and, standing, may proceed to put the question.
   A. landscaped
   B. substitute
   C. reasonable
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) reasonable. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:6 — “When debate appears to have concluded, the chair may again ask, “Are you ready for the question?” (or “Is there any further debate?”) or if, after a reasonable pause, no one rises to claim the floor, the chair may assume that no member wishes to speak and, standing, may proceed to put the question.”

**1216.** `[C0913 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that, under legitimate _____ procedure, there is no such thing as “gaveling through” a measure.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “It should be noted that, under legitimate parliamentary procedure, there is no such thing as “gaveling through” a measure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:7 — “It should be noted that, under legitimate parliamentary procedure, there is no such thing as “gaveling through” a measure.”
