**1217.** `[C0913 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The right of members to debate or introduce secondary motions cannot be cut off by the chair’s _____ to put a question to vote so quickly that no member can get the floor—either when the chair first states the question or when he believes debate is ended.
   A. introduced
   B. incidental
   C. subsidiary
   D. attempting

   *Answer:* D) attempting. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:7 — “The right of members to debate or introduce secondary motions cannot be cut off by the chair’s attempting to put a question to vote so quickly that no member can get the floor—either when the chair first states the question or when he believes debate is ended.”

**1218.** `[C0913 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Debate is not closed by the _____ officer’s rising to put the question.

   *Answer:* presiding — “Debate is not closed by the presiding officer’s rising to put the question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:7 — “Debate is not closed by the presiding officer’s rising to put the question.”

**1219.** `[C0913 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a vote has been taken or begun quickly and it is found that a member rose and addressed the chair with reasonable promptness after the chair asked, “Are you ready for the question?” or, by a pause or otherwise, indicated that the floor was open to assignment, then—even if the chair has announced the result of such a vote —the vote must be _____, the member is entitled to the floor, and debate begins or resumes.
   A. Particular
   B. disregarded
   C. Convention
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) disregarded. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:7 — “If a vote has been taken or begun quickly and it is found that a member rose and addressed the chair with reasonable promptness after the chair asked, “Are you ready for the question?” or, by a pause or otherwise, indicated that the floor was open to assignment, then—even if the chair has announced the result of such a vote —the vote must be disregarded, the member is entitled to the floor, and debate begins or resumes.”

**1220.** `[C0913 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that, under legitimate _____ procedure, there is no such thing as “gaveling through” a measure.
   A. subsequently
   B. representatives
   C. Announcements
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:7 — “It should be noted that, under legitimate parliamentary procedure, there is no such thing as “gaveling through” a measure.”

**1221.** `[C0914 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a nonlegislative body or organization that has no special rule relating to the length of speeches (2), a member, having obtained the floor while a debatable motion is immediately pending, can speak no longer than _____ unless he obtains the consent of the assembly.

   *Answer:* ten minutes — “In a nonlegislative body or organization that has no special rule relating to the length of speeches (2), a member, having obtained the floor while a debatable motion is immediately pending, can speak no longer than ten minutes unless he obtains the consent of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:8 — “In a nonlegislative body or organization that has no special rule relating to the length of speeches (2), a member, having obtained the floor while a debatable motion is immediately pending, can speak no longer than ten minutes unless he obtains the consent of the assembly.”

**1222.** `[C0914 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a _____ body or organization that has no special rule relating to the length of speeches (2), a member, having obtained the floor while a debatable motion is immediately pending, can speak no longer than ten minutes unless he obtains the consent of the assembly.
   A. requirement
   B. Organization
   C. Indefinitely
   D. nonlegislative

   *Answer:* D) nonlegislative. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:8 — “In a nonlegislative body or organization that has no special rule relating to the length of speeches (2), a member, having obtained the floor while a debatable motion is immediately pending, can speak no longer than ten minutes unless he obtains the consent of the assembly.”

**1223.** `[C0915 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it appears that a minute more will afford sufficient time for the member to conclude more gracefully, the chair can ask _____ consent to extend the member’s time for a short period, or any member can do so.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “If it appears that a minute more will afford sufficient time for the member to conclude more gracefully, the chair can ask unanimous consent to extend the member’s time for a short period, or any member can do so.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:9 — “If it appears that a minute more will afford sufficient time for the member to conclude more gracefully, the chair can ask unanimous consent to extend the member’s time for a short period, or any member can do so.”

**1224.** `[C0915 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a member’s time is exhausted, the chair rises and—if the member does not immediately conclude his remarks—calls his attention to the fact by an appropriate signal, or by _____ him if necessary.
   A. Incidental
   B. interrupting
   C. consideration
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) interrupting. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:9 — “When a member’s time is exhausted, the chair rises and—if the member does not immediately conclude his remarks—calls his attention to the fact by an appropriate signal, or by interrupting him if necessary.”

**1225.** `[C0915 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair may appoint timekeepers to provide assistance in fulfilling this _____.

   *Answer:* responsibility — “The chair may appoint timekeepers to provide assistance in fulfilling this responsibility.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:9 — “The chair may appoint timekeepers to provide assistance in fulfilling this responsibility.”

**1226.** `[C0915 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it appears that a minute more will afford _____ time for the member to conclude more gracefully, the chair can ask unanimous consent to extend the member’s time for a short period, or any member can do so.
   A. landscaped
   B. authorizes
   C. sustaining
   D. sufficient

   *Answer:* D) sufficient. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:9 — “If it appears that a minute more will afford sufficient time for the member to conclude more gracefully, the chair can ask unanimous consent to extend the member’s time for a short period, or any member can do so.”

**1227.** `[C0915 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a member’s time is exhausted, the chair rises and—if the member does not immediately conclude his remarks—calls his attention to the fact by an appropriate signal, or by _____ him if necessary.

   *Answer:* interrupting — “When a member’s time is exhausted, the chair rises and—if the member does not immediately conclude his remarks—calls his attention to the fact by an appropriate signal, or by interrupting him if necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:9 — “When a member’s time is exhausted, the chair rises and—if the member does not immediately conclude his remarks—calls his attention to the fact by an appropriate signal, or by interrupting him if necessary.”

**1228.** `[C0916 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rights in regard to debate are not _____.

   *Answer:* transferable — “Rights in regard to debate are not transferable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:10 — “Rights in regard to debate are not transferable.”

**1229.** `[C0916 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rights in regard to debate are not _____.
   A. transferable
   B. Organization
   C. immediately
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) transferable. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:10 — “Rights in regard to debate are not transferable.”

**1230.** `[C0917 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee chairman or reporting member is not _____ to be debating when presenting or reading the committee’s report, but he is bound to obey the assembly’s rules relating to debate in any speech made by him in support of the motion offered on behalf of the committee.

   *Answer:* considered — “A committee chairman or reporting member is not considered to be debating when presenting or reading the committee’s report, but he is bound to obey the assembly’s rules relating to debate in any speech made by him in support of the motion offered on behalf of the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:11 — “A committee chairman or reporting member is not considered to be debating when presenting or reading the committee’s report, but he is bound to obey the assembly’s rules relating to debate in any speech made by him in support of the motion offered on behalf of the committee.”

**1231.** `[C0917 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee chairman or reporting member is not _____ to be debating when presenting or reading the committee’s report, but he is bound to obey the assembly’s rules relating to debate in any speech made by him in support of the motion offered on behalf of the committee.
   A. authorizes
   B. considered
   C. assemblies
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* B) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:11 — “A committee chairman or reporting member is not considered to be debating when presenting or reading the committee’s report, but he is bound to obey the assembly’s rules relating to debate in any speech made by him in support of the motion offered on behalf of the committee.”

**1232.** `[C0918 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Number of Speeches on the Same Question per Member _____.

   *Answer:* per Day — “Number of Speeches on the Same Question per Member per Day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:12 — “Number of Speeches on the Same Question per Member per Day.”

**1233.** `[C0918 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise, no member can speak more than twice to the same question on the same day—except that in the case of an _____ (24), only the presiding officer can speak twice (the second time at the close of the debate), all other members being limited to one speech.
   A. amends
   B. ballot
   C. Appeal
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* C) Appeal. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:12 — “Unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise, no member can speak more than twice to the same question on the same day—except that in the case of an Appeal (24), only the presiding officer can speak twice (the second time at the close of the debate), all other members being limited to one speech.”

**1234.** `[C0918 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Number of _____ on the Same Question per Member per Day.

   *Answer:* Speeches — “Number of Speeches on the Same Question per Member per Day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:12 — “Number of Speeches on the Same Question per Member per Day.”

**1235.** `[C0918 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the assembly has a special rule _____ otherwise, no member can speak more than twice to the same question on the same day—except that in the case of an Appeal (24), only the presiding officer can speak twice (the second time at the close of the debate), all other members being limited to one speech.
   A. providing
   B. execution
   C. president
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* A) providing. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:12 — “Unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise, no member can speak more than twice to the same question on the same day—except that in the case of an Appeal (24), only the presiding officer can speak twice (the second time at the close of the debate), all other members being limited to one speech.”

**1236.** `[C0918 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Merely asking a question or making a brief _____ is not counted as speaking in debate;

   *Answer:* suggestion — “Merely asking a question or making a brief suggestion is not counted as speaking in debate;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:12 — “Merely asking a question or making a brief suggestion is not counted as speaking in debate;”

**1237.** `[C0919 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under this rule, each _____ motion is a separate question with respect to members’ rights to debate it.

   *Answer:* debatable — “Under this rule, each debatable motion is a separate question with respect to members’ rights to debate it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:13 — “Under this rule, each debatable motion is a separate question with respect to members’ rights to debate it.”

**1238.** `[C0919 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, if a series of debatable questions is pending and a member has, for example, spoken twice that day while the main motion is _____ pending, he has exhausted his right to debate the main motion;
   A. requirement
   B. immediately
   C. assemblies
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:13 — “Thus, if a series of debatable questions is pending and a member has, for example, spoken twice that day while the main motion is immediately pending, he has exhausted his right to debate the main motion;”

**1239.** `[C0919 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but, even on the same day, he can still speak twice on a motion to postpone the main question _____, and twice on each amendment that may be moved, and so on.

   *Answer:* indefinitely — “but, even on the same day, he can still speak twice on a motion to postpone the main question indefinitely, and twice on each amendment that may be moved, and so on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:13 — “but, even on the same day, he can still speak twice on a motion to postpone the main question indefinitely, and twice on each amendment that may be moved, and so on.”

**1240.** `[C0919 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As noted under the rules for assigning the floor (42), however, a member cannot make a second speech on the same question the same day until every member who desires to speak on it has had an _____ to do so once.
   A. opportunity
   B. Incidental
   C. Substitute
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:13 — “As noted under the rules for assigning the floor (42), however, a member cannot make a second speech on the same question the same day until every member who desires to speak on it has had an opportunity to do so once.”

**1241.** `[C0919 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under this rule, each _____ motion is a separate question with respect to members’ rights to debate it.
   A. delegates
   B. Objection
   C. rejecting
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* D) debatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:13 — “Under this rule, each debatable motion is a separate question with respect to members’ rights to debate it.”

**1242.** `[C0920 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The general rules _____ the length and number of speeches in debate that are stated above can be modified to serve the assembly’s needs as follows:

   *Answer:* limiting — “The general rules limiting the length and number of speeches in debate that are stated above can be modified to serve the assembly’s needs as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:14 — “The general rules limiting the length and number of speeches in debate that are stated above can be modified to serve the assembly’s needs as follows:”

**1243.** `[C0920 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The general rules _____ the length and number of speeches in debate that are stated above can be modified to serve the assembly’s needs as follows:
   A. chairman
   B. Approved
   C. limiting
   D. division

   *Answer:* C) limiting. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:14 — “The general rules limiting the length and number of speeches in debate that are stated above can be modified to serve the assembly’s needs as follows:”

**1244.** `[C0921 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rule allowing each member two speeches of ten minutes’ length per day on each debatable question can be made either more restrictive or more liberal for all meetings of a society by _____ of order by a two-thirds vote after notice, or by a vote of a majority of the entire membership (2:14ff.;

   *Answer:* adopting a special rule — “The rule allowing each member two speeches of ten minutes’ length per day on each debatable question can be made either more restrictive or more liberal for all meetings of a society by adopting a special rule of order by a two-thirds vote after notice, or by a vote of a majority of the entire membership (2:14ff.;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:15 — “The rule allowing each member two speeches of ten minutes’ length per day on each debatable question can be made either more restrictive or more liberal for all meetings of a society by adopting a special rule of order by a two-thirds vote after notice, or by a vote of a majority of the entire membership (2:14ff.;”

**1245.** `[C0921 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rule allowing each member two speeches of _____’ length per day on each debatable question can be made either more restrictive or more liberal for all meetings of a society by adopting a special rule of order by a two-thirds vote after notice, or by a vote of a majority of the entire membership (2:14ff.;
   A. main motion
   B. not commend
   C. Suspend the
   D. ten minutes

   *Answer:* D) ten minutes. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:15 — “The rule allowing each member two speeches of ten minutes’ length per day on each debatable question can be made either more restrictive or more liberal for all meetings of a society by adopting a special rule of order by a two-thirds vote after notice, or by a vote of a majority of the entire membership (2:14ff.;”

**1246.** `[C0921 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An example of a more restrictive rule might be one setting a limit of not more than one speech of _____’ length on the same question on the same day for each member.

   *Answer:* five minutes — “An example of a more restrictive rule might be one setting a limit of not more than one speech of five minutes’ length on the same question on the same day for each member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:15 — “An example of a more restrictive rule might be one setting a limit of not more than one speech of five minutes’ length on the same question on the same day for each member.”

**1247.** `[C0921 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An example of a more restrictive rule might be one setting a limit of not more than one speech of _____’ length on the same question on the same day for each member.
   A. five minutes
   B. one fifth of
   C. Same Session
   D. Vote by Mail

   *Answer:* A) five minutes. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:15 — “An example of a more restrictive rule might be one setting a limit of not more than one speech of five minutes’ length on the same question on the same day for each member.”

**1248.** `[C0922 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a _____ the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.

   *Answer:* standing rule of — “In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:16 — “In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.”

**1249.** `[C0922 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of _____ (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.
   A. the convention
   B. rules of order
   C. general orders
   D. called meeting

   *Answer:* A) the convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:16 — “In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.”

**1250.** `[C0922 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An assembly at any session can change the limits of debate, for that session only, by means of a main motion adopted by a _____ vote without notice.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “An assembly at any session can change the limits of debate, for that session only, by means of a main motion adopted by a two-thirds vote without notice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:16 — “An assembly at any session can change the limits of debate, for that session only, by means of a main motion adopted by a two-thirds vote without notice.”

**1251.** `[C0922 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An assembly at any session can change the limits of debate, for that session only, by means of a main motion adopted by a _____ vote without notice.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* C) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:16 — “An assembly at any session can change the limits of debate, for that session only, by means of a main motion adopted by a two-thirds vote without notice.”

**1252.** `[C0923 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the _____ (16).

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the Previous Question (16).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:17 — “If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the Previous Question (16).”

**1253.** `[C0923 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate (15), adopted by a _____ vote.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:17 — “While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate (15), adopted by a two-thirds vote.”

**1254.** `[C0923 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the _____ (16).
   A. Previous Question
   B. Order of Business
   C. adjourned meeting
   D. Optional Headings

   *Answer:* A) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:17 — “If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the Previous Question (16).”

**1255.** `[C0923 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate (15), adopted by a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate (15), adopted by a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:17 — “While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate (15), adopted by a two-thirds vote.”

**1256.** `[C0924 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Speeches made under these _____ do not count against a member’s right to debate the same question if it is further considered by the assembly on the same day under the regular rules.
   A. variations
   B. resolution
   C. procedures
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* C) procedures. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:18 — “Speeches made under these procedures do not count against a member’s right to debate the same question if it is further considered by the assembly on the same day under the regular rules.”

**1257.** `[C0924 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the question under _____ is composed of a number of sections or paragraphs—as in the case of bylaws, for example—the total number of speeches allowed each member can be greatly increased, but not made unlimited, by considering the document seriatim (28), in which case each member can speak twice on each paragraph, section, or unit that is taken up as a separate part.

   *Answer:* consideration — “If the question under consideration is composed of a number of sections or paragraphs—as in the case of bylaws, for example—the total number of speeches allowed each member can be greatly increased, but not made unlimited, by considering the document seriatim (28), in which case each member can speak twice on each paragraph, section, or unit that is taken up as a separate part.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:18 — “If the question under consideration is composed of a number of sections or paragraphs—as in the case of bylaws, for example—the total number of speeches allowed each member can be greatly increased, but not made unlimited, by considering the document seriatim (28), in which case each member can speak twice on each paragraph, section, or unit that is taken up as a separate part.”

**1258.** `[C0924 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Speeches made under these _____ do not count against a member’s right to debate the same question if it is further considered by the assembly on the same day under the regular rules.

   *Answer:* procedures — “Speeches made under these procedures do not count against a member’s right to debate the same question if it is further considered by the assembly on the same day under the regular rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:18 — “Speeches made under these procedures do not count against a member’s right to debate the same question if it is further considered by the assembly on the same day under the regular rules.”

**1259.** `[C0924 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the question under _____ is composed of a number of sections or paragraphs—as in the case of bylaws, for example—the total number of speeches allowed each member can be greatly increased, but not made unlimited, by considering the document seriatim (28), in which case each member can speak twice on each paragraph, section, or unit that is taken up as a separate part.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. Vice-President
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:18 — “If the question under consideration is composed of a number of sections or paragraphs—as in the case of bylaws, for example—the total number of speeches allowed each member can be greatly increased, but not made unlimited, by considering the document seriatim (28), in which case each member can speak twice on each paragraph, section, or unit that is taken up as a separate part.”

**1260.** `[C0925 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ practices and customs observed by speakers and other members in an assembly assist the carrying on of debate in a smooth and orderly manner.
   A. Secretary
   B. execution
   C. committee
   D. following

   *Answer:* D) following. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:19 — “The following practices and customs observed by speakers and other members in an assembly assist the carrying on of debate in a smooth and orderly manner.”

**1261.** `[C0925 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ practices and customs observed by speakers and other members in an assembly assist the carrying on of debate in a smooth and orderly manner.

   *Answer:* following — “The following practices and customs observed by speakers and other members in an assembly assist the carrying on of debate in a smooth and orderly manner.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:19 — “The following practices and customs observed by speakers and other members in an assembly assist the carrying on of debate in a smooth and orderly manner.”

**1262.** `[C0926 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ Remarks to the Merits of the Pending Question.

   *Answer:* Confining — “Confining Remarks to the Merits of the Pending Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:20 — “Confining Remarks to the Merits of the Pending Question.”

**1263.** `[C0926 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Remarks to the Merits of the Pending Question.
   A. reception
   B. Confining
   C. expressly
   D. Secretary

   *Answer:* B) Confining. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:20 — “Confining Remarks to the Merits of the Pending Question.”

**1264.** `[C0927 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ from Attacking a Member’s Motives.

   *Answer:* Refraining — “Refraining from Attacking a Member’s Motives.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:21 — “Refraining from Attacking a Member’s Motives.”

**1265.** `[C0927 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is pending, a member can condemn the nature or likely consequences of the proposed measure in strong terms, but he must avoid _____, and under no circumstances can he attack or question the motives of another member.
   A. specification
   B. personalities
   C. representatives
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* B) personalities. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:21 — “When a question is pending, a member can condemn the nature or likely consequences of the proposed measure in strong terms, but he must avoid personalities, and under no circumstances can he attack or question the motives of another member.”

**1266.** `[C0927 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is pending, a member can condemn the nature or likely consequences of the proposed _____ in strong terms, but he must avoid personalities, and under no circumstances can he attack or question the motives of another member.

   *Answer:* measure — “When a question is pending, a member can condemn the nature or likely consequences of the proposed measure in strong terms, but he must avoid personalities, and under no circumstances can he attack or question the motives of another member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:21 — “When a question is pending, a member can condemn the nature or likely consequences of the proposed measure in strong terms, but he must avoid personalities, and under no circumstances can he attack or question the motives of another member.”

**1267.** `[C0927 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a member disagrees with a statement by another in regard to an event that both witnessed, he cannot state in debate that the other’s statement “is false.” But he might say, “I believe there is strong evidence that the member is mistaken.” The moment the chair hears such words as “fraud,” “liar,” or “lie” used about a member in debate, he must act _____ and decisively to correct the matter and prevent its repetition (see 61).
   A. committees
   B. resolutions
   C. immediately
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:21 — “If a member disagrees with a statement by another in regard to an event that both witnessed, he cannot state in debate that the other’s statement “is false.” But he might say, “I believe there is strong evidence that the member is mistaken.” The moment the chair hears such words as “fraud,” “liar,” or “lie” used about a member in debate, he must act immediately and decisively to correct the matter and prevent its repetition (see 61).”

**1268.** `[C0927 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ from Attacking a Member’s Motives.
   A. Substitute
   B. Unfinished
   C. Refraining
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) Refraining. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:21 — “Refraining from Attacking a Member’s Motives.”

**1269.** `[C0928 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ All Remarks Through the Chair.

   *Answer:* Addressing — “Addressing All Remarks Through the Chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:22 — “Addressing All Remarks Through the Chair.”

**1270.** `[C0928 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Members of an assembly cannot address one another _____, but must address all remarks through the chair.
   A. assembly
   B. adoption
   C. Division
   D. directly

   *Answer:* D) directly. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:22 — “Members of an assembly cannot address one another directly, but must address all remarks through the chair.”

**1271.** `[C0928 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ All Remarks Through the Chair.
   A. Addressing
   B. incidental
   C. Substitute
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* A) Addressing. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:22 — “Addressing All Remarks Through the Chair.”

**1272.** `[C0928 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Members of an assembly cannot address one another _____, but must address all remarks through the chair.

   *Answer:* directly — “Members of an assembly cannot address one another directly, but must address all remarks through the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:22 — “Members of an assembly cannot address one another directly, but must address all remarks through the chair.”

**1273.** `[C0929 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As much as _____, the use of names of members should be avoided in debate.

   *Answer:* possible — “As much as possible, the use of names of members should be avoided in debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:23 — “As much as possible, the use of names of members should be avoided in debate.”

**1274.** `[C0929 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is better to _____ a member in some other way, as by saying, “the member who spoke last,” or, “the delegate from Mason County.” The officers of the society should always be referred to by their official titles.
   A. Standing
   B. business
   C. assuming
   D. describe

   *Answer:* D) describe. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:23 — “It is better to describe a member in some other way, as by saying, “the member who spoke last,” or, “the delegate from Mason County.” The officers of the society should always be referred to by their official titles.”

**1275.** `[C0929 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: There is no need, however, to refer to oneself in debate in the third person as by the use of such _____ as “this member.” A member’s debate is expected and intended to be partial, and the first person is quite acceptable.

   *Answer:* expressions — “There is no need, however, to refer to oneself in debate in the third person as by the use of such expressions as “this member.” A member’s debate is expected and intended to be partial, and the first person is quite acceptable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:23 — “There is no need, however, to refer to oneself in debate in the third person as by the use of such expressions as “this member.” A member’s debate is expected and intended to be partial, and the first person is quite acceptable.”

**1276.** `[C0929 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As much as _____, the use of names of members should be avoided in debate.
   A. business
   B. possible
   C. decision
   D. Previous

   *Answer:* B) possible. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:23 — “As much as possible, the use of names of members should be avoided in debate.”

**1277.** `[C0929 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is better to _____ a member in some other way, as by saying, “the member who spoke last,” or, “the delegate from Mason County.” The officers of the society should always be referred to by their official titles.

   *Answer:* describe — “It is better to describe a member in some other way, as by saying, “the member who spoke last,” or, “the delegate from Mason County.” The officers of the society should always be referred to by their official titles.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:23 — “It is better to describe a member in some other way, as by saying, “the member who spoke last,” or, “the delegate from Mason County.” The officers of the society should always be referred to by their official titles.”

**1278.** `[C0930 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Refraining from Speaking Adversely on a Prior Action Not _____.

   *Answer:* Pending — “Refraining from Speaking Adversely on a Prior Action Not Pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:24 — “Refraining from Speaking Adversely on a Prior Action Not Pending.”

**1279.** `[C0930 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ from Speaking Adversely on a Prior Action Not Pending.
   A. privileged
   B. subsidiary
   C. Refraining
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) Refraining. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:24 — “Refraining from Speaking Adversely on a Prior Action Not Pending.”

**1280.** `[C0930 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In debate, a member cannot reflect adversely on any prior act of the society that is not then pending, unless a motion to _____, rescind, or amend it is pending, or unless he intends to conclude his remarks by making or giving notice of one of these motions.

   *Answer:* reconsider — “In debate, a member cannot reflect adversely on any prior act of the society that is not then pending, unless a motion to reconsider, rescind, or amend it is pending, or unless he intends to conclude his remarks by making or giving notice of one of these motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:24 — “In debate, a member cannot reflect adversely on any prior act of the society that is not then pending, unless a motion to reconsider, rescind, or amend it is pending, or unless he intends to conclude his remarks by making or giving notice of one of these motions.”

**1281.** `[C0930 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In debate, a member cannot reflect adversely on any prior act of the society that is not then pending, unless a motion to _____, rescind, or amend it is pending, or unless he intends to conclude his remarks by making or giving notice of one of these motions.
   A. reconsider
   B. Procedures
   C. sustaining
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* A) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:24 — “In debate, a member cannot reflect adversely on any prior act of the society that is not then pending, unless a motion to reconsider, rescind, or amend it is pending, or unless he intends to conclude his remarks by making or giving notice of one of these motions.”

**1282.** `[C0931 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ from Speaking Against One’s Own Motion.

   *Answer:* Refraining — “Refraining from Speaking Against One’s Own Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:25 — “Refraining from Speaking Against One’s Own Motion.”

**1283.** `[C0931 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In debate, the maker of a motion, while he can vote against it, is not _____ to speak against his own motion.
   A. abstain
   B. allowed
   C. Divided
   D. Example

   *Answer:* B) allowed. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:25 — “In debate, the maker of a motion, while he can vote against it, is not allowed to speak against his own motion.”

**1284.** `[C0931 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: He need not speak at all, but if he does he is obliged to take a _____ position.

   *Answer:* favorable — “He need not speak at all, but if he does he is obliged to take a favorable position.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:25 — “He need not speak at all, but if he does he is obliged to take a favorable position.”

**1285.** `[C0931 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ from Speaking Against One’s Own Motion.
   A. Refraining
   B. settlement
   C. Permission
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* A) Refraining. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:25 — “Refraining from Speaking Against One’s Own Motion.”

**1286.** `[C0931 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In debate, the maker of a motion, while he can vote against it, is not _____ to speak against his own motion.

   *Answer:* allowed — “In debate, the maker of a motion, while he can vote against it, is not allowed to speak against his own motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:25 — “In debate, the maker of a motion, while he can vote against it, is not allowed to speak against his own motion.”

**1287.** `[C0932 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reading from Reports, Quotations, etc., Only Without _____.

   *Answer:* Objection or With Permission — “Reading from Reports, Quotations, etc., Only Without Objection or With Permission.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:26 — “Reading from Reports, Quotations, etc., Only Without Objection or With Permission.”

**1288.** `[C0932 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Reading from Reports, _____, etc., Only Without Objection or With Permission.
   A. Unfinished
   B. Reconsider
   C. persuasive
   D. Quotations

   *Answer:* D) Quotations. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:26 — “Reading from Reports, Quotations, etc., Only Without Objection or With Permission.”

**1289.** `[C0933 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Being Seated During an _____ by the Chair.

   *Answer:* Interruption — “Being Seated During an Interruption by the Chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:27 — “Being Seated During an Interruption by the Chair.”

**1290.** `[C0933 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If at any time the presiding officer rises to make a ruling, give _____, or otherwise speak within his privilege, any member who is speaking should be seated (or should step back slightly if he is standing at a microphone some distance from a seat) until the presiding officer has finished.
   A. adjournment
   B. committees
   C. undebatable
   D. information

   *Answer:* D) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:27 — “If at any time the presiding officer rises to make a ruling, give information, or otherwise speak within his privilege, any member who is speaking should be seated (or should step back slightly if he is standing at a microphone some distance from a seat) until the presiding officer has finished.”

**1291.** `[C0933 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At that time the member can resume his speech, unless he is denied the right as a _____ measure.

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “At that time the member can resume his speech, unless he is denied the right as a disciplinary measure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:27 — “At that time the member can resume his speech, unless he is denied the right as a disciplinary measure.”

**1292.** `[C0933 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (Questions of _____ arising from disorderly debate by members are treated in 61.)
   A. substitute
   B. convention
   C. assemblies
   D. discipline

   *Answer:* D) discipline. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:27 — “(Questions of discipline arising from disorderly debate by members are treated in 61.)”

**1293.** `[C0933 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Being Seated During an _____ by the Chair.
   A. Information
   B. Interruption
   C. Particular
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) Interruption. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:27 — “Being Seated During an Interruption by the Chair.”

**1294.** `[C0934 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to _____ by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.

   *Answer:* disturb the assembly — “During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to disturb the assembly by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:28 — “During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to disturb the assembly by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.”

**1295.** `[C0934 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ from Disturbing the Assembly.
   A. assemblies
   B. Refraining
   C. Reconsider
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) Refraining. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:28 — “Refraining from Disturbing the Assembly.”

**1296.** `[C0934 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to disturb the assembly by _____, walking across the floor, or in any other way.

   *Answer:* whispering — “During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to disturb the assembly by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:28 — “During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to disturb the assembly by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.”

**1297.** `[C0934 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This rule does not mean, therefore, that members can never whisper, or walk from one place to another in the hall during the _____ of the assembly.

   *Answer:* deliberations — “This rule does not mean, therefore, that members can never whisper, or walk from one place to another in the hall during the deliberations of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:28 — “This rule does not mean, therefore, that members can never whisper, or walk from one place to another in the hall during the deliberations of the assembly.”

**1298.** `[C0934 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At large meetings it would be _____ to enforce such a rule.
   A. impossible
   B. Incidental
   C. substitute
   D. committees

   *Answer:* A) impossible. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:28 — “At large meetings it would be impossible to enforce such a rule.”

**1299.** `[C0935 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an individual—the same _____ in debate as any other member;

   *Answer:* rights — “If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an individual—the same rights in debate as any other member;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:29 — “If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an individual—the same rights in debate as any other member;”

**1300.** `[C0935 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an _____—the same rights in debate as any other member;
   A. individual
   B. authorizes
   C. privileged
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) individual. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:29 — “If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an individual—the same rights in debate as any other member;”

**1301.** `[C0935 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but the _____ required of the chair in an assembly precludes his exercising these rights while he is presiding.

   *Answer:* impartiality — “but the impartiality required of the chair in an assembly precludes his exercising these rights while he is presiding.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:29 — “but the impartiality required of the chair in an assembly precludes his exercising these rights while he is presiding.”

**1302.** `[C0935 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Normally, _____ in a large body, he should have nothing to say on the merits of pending questions.
   A. subsidiary
   B. persuasive
   C. authorizes
   D. especially

   *Answer:* D) especially. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:29 — “Normally, especially in a large body, he should have nothing to say on the merits of pending questions.”

**1303.** `[C0935 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On certain occasions—which should be extremely rare—the presiding officer may believe that a crucial factor relating to such a question has been _____ and that his obligation as a member to call attention to the point outweighs his duty to preside at that time.

   *Answer:* overlooked — “On certain occasions—which should be extremely rare—the presiding officer may believe that a crucial factor relating to such a question has been overlooked and that his obligation as a member to call attention to the point outweighs his duty to preside at that time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:29 — “On certain occasions—which should be extremely rare—the presiding officer may believe that a crucial factor relating to such a question has been overlooked and that his obligation as a member to call attention to the point outweighs his duty to preside at that time.”

**1304.** `[C0937 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Allowable _____ and Requests When No Motion Is Pending or When an Undebatable Motion Is Pending.

   *Answer:* Explanations — “Allowable Explanations and Requests When No Motion Is Pending or When an Undebatable Motion Is Pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:31 — “Allowable Explanations and Requests When No Motion Is Pending or When an Undebatable Motion Is Pending.”

**1305.** `[C0937 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As already stated, debate in a _____ assembly is permitted only when it is germane to a debatable motion that has been stated by the chair as the immediately pending question.
   A. constitution
   B. Incidental
   C. deliberative
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:31 — “As already stated, debate in a deliberative assembly is permitted only when it is germane to a debatable motion that has been stated by the chair as the immediately pending question.”

**1306.** `[C0937 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Allowable Explanations and Requests When No Motion Is Pending or When an _____ Motion Is Pending.

   *Answer:* Undebatable — “Allowable Explanations and Requests When No Motion Is Pending or When an Undebatable Motion Is Pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:31 — “Allowable Explanations and Requests When No Motion Is Pending or When an Undebatable Motion Is Pending.”

**1307.** `[C0937 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or a member can first request _____, or briefly indicate the substance of a desired proposal and ask for the chair’s assistance in wording an appropriate motion.
   A. information
   B. Reconsider
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:31 — “or a member can first request information, or briefly indicate the substance of a desired proposal and ask for the chair’s assistance in wording an appropriate motion.”

**1308.** `[C0937 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, as explained in 4, the making of a motion of any kind —whether debatable or undebatable—may be prefaced, when necessary, by a few words of _____, which must not become a speech;

   *Answer:* explanation — “However, as explained in 4, the making of a motion of any kind —whether debatable or undebatable—may be prefaced, when necessary, by a few words of explanation, which must not become a speech;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:31 — “However, as explained in 4, the making of a motion of any kind —whether debatable or undebatable—may be prefaced, when necessary, by a few words of explanation, which must not become a speech;”

**1309.** `[C0938 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ between debate and asking questions or making brief suggestions should be kept in mind in this connection.

   *Answer:* distinction — “The distinction between debate and asking questions or making brief suggestions should be kept in mind in this connection.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:32 — “The distinction between debate and asking questions or making brief suggestions should be kept in mind in this connection.”

**1310.** `[C0938 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Especially in large assemblies, the chair must be careful not to allow this type of _____ to develop into an extended colloquy between members or to take on the semblance of debate, and should generally remain standing while the consultation takes place, to show that the floor has not been assigned.
   A. Incidental
   B. Definitely
   C. consultation
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) consultation. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:32 — “Especially in large assemblies, the chair must be careful not to allow this type of consultation to develop into an extended colloquy between members or to take on the semblance of debate, and should generally remain standing while the consultation takes place, to show that the floor has not been assigned.”

**1311.** `[C0938 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ between debate and asking questions or making brief suggestions should be kept in mind in this connection.
   A. convention
   B. Procedures
   C. distinction
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) distinction. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:32 — “The distinction between debate and asking questions or making brief suggestions should be kept in mind in this connection.”

**1312.** `[C0938 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Especially in large assemblies, the chair must be careful not to allow this type of _____ to develop into an extended colloquy between members or to take on the semblance of debate, and should generally remain standing while the consultation takes place, to show that the floor has not been assigned.

   *Answer:* consultation — “Especially in large assemblies, the chair must be careful not to allow this type of consultation to develop into an extended colloquy between members or to take on the semblance of debate, and should generally remain standing while the consultation takes place, to show that the floor has not been assigned.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:32 — “Especially in large assemblies, the chair must be careful not to allow this type of consultation to develop into an extended colloquy between members or to take on the semblance of debate, and should generally remain standing while the consultation takes place, to show that the floor has not been assigned.”

**1313.** `[C0939 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Informal _____ to Assist the Framing of a Motion.

   *Answer:* Consultation — “Informal Consultation to Assist the Framing of a Motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:33 — “Informal Consultation to Assist the Framing of a Motion.”

**1314.** `[C0939 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, brief informal consultation or discussion of a subject may assist a member in framing a proper motion.
   A. Definitely
   B. Particular
   C. subsequently
   D. Occasionally

   *Answer:* D) Occasionally. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:33 — “Occasionally, brief informal consultation or discussion of a subject may assist a member in framing a proper motion.”

**1315.** `[C0939 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Occasionally, brief informal consultation or _____ of a subject may assist a member in framing a proper motion.

   *Answer:* discussion — “Occasionally, brief informal consultation or discussion of a subject may assist a member in framing a proper motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:33 — “Occasionally, brief informal consultation or discussion of a subject may assist a member in framing a proper motion.”

**1316.** `[C0939 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Informal _____ to Assist the Framing of a Motion.
   A. interrupted
   B. presentation
   C. Information
   D. Consultation

   *Answer:* D) Consultation. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:33 — “Informal Consultation to Assist the Framing of a Motion.”

**1317.** `[C0940 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission _____ assembly.

   *Answer:* of the — “In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:34 — “In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the assembly.”

**1318.** `[C0940 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the _____.
   A. Meetings
   B. assembly
   C. adoption
   D. chairman

   *Answer:* B) assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:34 — “In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the assembly.”

**1319.** `[C0940 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But _____, in very small bodies, a member who has obtained the floor at such a time may state that, if there is no objection, he would like to give some explanations dealing with a specified subject and to conclude by offering a motion on that subject.
   A. occasionally
   B. subsequently
   C. constitution
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) occasionally. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:34 — “But occasionally, in very small bodies, a member who has obtained the floor at such a time may state that, if there is no objection, he would like to give some explanations dealing with a specified subject and to conclude by offering a motion on that subject.”

**1320.** `[C0940 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no one _____, the member can then proceed;

   *Answer:* objects — “If no one objects, the member can then proceed;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:34 — “If no one objects, the member can then proceed;”

**1321.** `[C0940 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But _____, in very small bodies, a member who has obtained the floor at such a time may state that, if there is no objection, he would like to give some explanations dealing with a specified subject and to conclude by offering a motion on that subject.

   *Answer:* occasionally — “But occasionally, in very small bodies, a member who has obtained the floor at such a time may state that, if there is no objection, he would like to give some explanations dealing with a specified subject and to conclude by offering a motion on that subject.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:34 — “But occasionally, in very small bodies, a member who has obtained the floor at such a time may state that, if there is no objection, he would like to give some explanations dealing with a specified subject and to conclude by offering a motion on that subject.”

**1322.** `[C0941 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules as to each motion’s debatability or _____ are given under Standard Characteristic 5 in 10–37 and in the Table of Rules Relating to Motions on pages t6–t33.

   *Answer:* undebatability — “Rules as to each motion’s debatability or undebatability are given under Standard Characteristic 5 in 10–37 and in the Table of Rules Relating to Motions on pages t6–t33.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:35 — “Rules as to each motion’s debatability or undebatability are given under Standard Characteristic 5 in 10–37 and in the Table of Rules Relating to Motions on pages t6–t33.”

**1323.** `[C0941 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The following is a brief summary of these rules in relation to the _____ on which they are based.
   A. principles
   B. subsidiary
   C. Substitute
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* A) principles. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:35 — “The following is a brief summary of these rules in relation to the principles on which they are based.”

**1324.** `[C0941 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules as to each motion’s debatability or _____ are given under Standard Characteristic 5 in 10–37 and in the Table of Rules Relating to Motions on pages t6–t33.
   A. adjournment
   B. consideration
   C. Organization
   D. undebatability

   *Answer:* D) undebatability. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:35 — “Rules as to each motion’s debatability or undebatability are given under Standard Characteristic 5 in 10–37 and in the Table of Rules Relating to Motions on pages t6–t33.”

**1325.** `[C0941 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The following is a brief summary of these rules in relation to the _____ on which they are based.

   *Answer:* principles — “The following is a brief summary of these rules in relation to the principles on which they are based.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:35 — “The following is a brief summary of these rules in relation to the principles on which they are based.”

**1326.** `[C0942 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: With the exception of the two _____ motions that have to do with debate, the degree to which each of the subsidiary motions can be debated depends on the extent to which its adoption would restrict the assembly in dealing with the main question.

   *Answer:* subsidiary — “With the exception of the two subsidiary motions that have to do with debate, the degree to which each of the subsidiary motions can be debated depends on the extent to which its adoption would restrict the assembly in dealing with the main question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:37 — “With the exception of the two subsidiary motions that have to do with debate, the degree to which each of the subsidiary motions can be debated depends on the extent to which its adoption would restrict the assembly in dealing with the main question.”

**1327.** `[C0942 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: With the exception of the two _____ motions that have to do with debate, the degree to which each of the subsidiary motions can be debated depends on the extent to which its adoption would restrict the assembly in dealing with the main question.
   A. subsidiary
   B. Reconsider
   C. Particular
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) subsidiary. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:37 — “With the exception of the two subsidiary motions that have to do with debate, the degree to which each of the subsidiary motions can be debated depends on the extent to which its adoption would restrict the assembly in dealing with the main question.”

**1328.** `[C0943 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The privileged motions are all _____ because, if they were debatable, their high privilege would allow them to interfere with business.

   *Answer:* undebatable — “The privileged motions are all undebatable because, if they were debatable, their high privilege would allow them to interfere with business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:38 — “The privileged motions are all undebatable because, if they were debatable, their high privilege would allow them to interfere with business.”

**1329.** `[C0943 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The right of debate is thus _____ with high privilege.
   A. requirement
   B. adjournment
   C. incompatible
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) incompatible. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:38 — “The right of debate is thus incompatible with high privilege.”

**1330.** `[C0943 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: With reference to the two _____ privileged motions, it is, of course, the “calling” for the orders of the day (18) or the “raising” of a question of privilege (19) that is undebatable.

   *Answer:* lowest-ranking — “With reference to the two lowest-ranking privileged motions, it is, of course, the “calling” for the orders of the day (18) or the “raising” of a question of privilege (19) that is undebatable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:38 — “With reference to the two lowest-ranking privileged motions, it is, of course, the “calling” for the orders of the day (18) or the “raising” of a question of privilege (19) that is undebatable.”

**1331.** `[C0943 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____d motions are all undebatable because, if they were debatable, their high privilege would allow them to interfere with business.
   A. privilege
   B. paragraph
   C. Secretary
   D. extending

   *Answer:* A) privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:38 — “The privileged motions are all undebatable because, if they were debatable, their high privilege would allow them to interfere with business.”

**1332.** `[C0943 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The right of debate is thus _____ with high privilege.

   *Answer:* incompatible — “The right of debate is thus incompatible with high privilege.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:38 — “The right of debate is thus incompatible with high privilege.”

**1333.** `[C0944 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of an _____ that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.

   *Answer:* Appeal — “In the case of an Appeal that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:39 — “In the case of an Appeal that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.”

**1334.** `[C0944 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except as noted in this paragraph, the incidental motions are _____, because they have high privilege to interrupt any motions or situations to which they are incidental.
   A. undebatable
   B. convention
   C. deliberative
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) undebatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:39 — “Except as noted in this paragraph, the incidental motions are undebatable, because they have high privilege to interrupt any motions or situations to which they are incidental.”

**1335.** `[C0944 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At all other times, an appeal is fully _____ so long as the debate is germane to the subject matter of the appeal.

   *Answer:* debatable — “At all other times, an appeal is fully debatable so long as the debate is germane to the subject matter of the appeal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:39 — “At all other times, an appeal is fully debatable so long as the debate is germane to the subject matter of the appeal.”

**1336.** `[C0944 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A Request to Be Excused from a Duty, such as a _____, may require some discussion for its proper decision, and for this reason it is debatable.
   A. Organization
   B. Reconsider
   C. requirement
   D. resignation

   *Answer:* D) resignation. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:39 — “A Request to Be Excused from a Duty, such as a resignation, may require some discussion for its proper decision, and for this reason it is debatable.”

**1337.** `[C0944 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of an _____ that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.
   A. Appeal
   B. Commit
   C. Debate
   D. Abuses

   *Answer:* A) Appeal. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:39 — “In the case of an Appeal that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.”

**1338.** `[C0945 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rules as to the _____ of motions that bring a question again before the assembly may be summarized as follows:

   *Answer:* debatability — “Rules as to the debatability of motions that bring a question again before the assembly may be summarized as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:40 — “Rules as to the debatability of motions that bring a question again before the assembly may be summarized as follows:”

**1339.** `[C0945 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rules as to the _____ of motions that bring a question again before the assembly may be summarized as follows:
   A. adjournment
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Permission
   D. debatability

   *Answer:* D) debatability. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:40 — “Rules as to the debatability of motions that bring a question again before the assembly may be summarized as follows:”

**1340.** `[C1078 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The usual duties of these and other officers generally required in an organized society are _____ and discussed in this section.

   *Answer:* summarized — “The usual duties of these and other officers generally required in an organized society are summarized and discussed in this section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:1 — “The usual duties of these and other officers generally required in an organized society are summarized and discussed in this section.”

**1341.** `[C1078 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Every society should specify in its bylaws what officers it requires, how they shall be elected or appointed, their term of office, and any _____ for holding office or any duties different from or in addition to those stated in the parliamentary authority.
   A. qualifications
   B. requirement
   C. immediately
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) qualifications. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:1 — “Every society should specify in its bylaws what officers it requires, how they shall be elected or appointed, their term of office, and any qualifications for holding office or any duties different from or in addition to those stated in the parliamentary authority.”

**1342.** `[C1078 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The usual duties of these and other officers generally required in an organized society are _____ and discussed in this section.
   A. summarized
   B. Reconsider
   C. assemblies
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* A) summarized. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:1 — “The usual duties of these and other officers generally required in an organized society are summarized and discussed in this section.”

**1343.** `[C1078 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Every society should specify in its bylaws what officers it requires, how they shall be elected or appointed, their term of office, and any _____ for holding office or any duties different from or in addition to those stated in the parliamentary authority.

   *Answer:* qualifications — “Every society should specify in its bylaws what officers it requires, how they shall be elected or appointed, their term of office, and any qualifications for holding office or any duties different from or in addition to those stated in the parliamentary authority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:1 — “Every society should specify in its bylaws what officers it requires, how they shall be elected or appointed, their term of office, and any qualifications for holding office or any duties different from or in addition to those stated in the parliamentary authority.”

**1344.** `[C1079 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In most _____ it is usual to elect the officers from among the members;

   *Answer:* societies — “In most societies it is usual to elect the officers from among the members;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:2 — “In most societies it is usual to elect the officers from among the members;”

**1345.** `[C1079 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but in all except secret societies, unless the bylaws provide otherwise, it is possible for an _____ to choose its officers from outside its membership.
   A. Particular
   B. organization
   C. interrupted
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:2 — “but in all except secret societies, unless the bylaws provide otherwise, it is possible for an organization to choose its officers from outside its membership.”

**1346.** `[C1079 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In many _____ bodies the presiding officer is not a member of the body.

   *Answer:* legislative — “In many legislative bodies the presiding officer is not a member of the body.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:2 — “In many legislative bodies the presiding officer is not a member of the body.”

**1347.** `[C1079 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A large society with complex financial affairs may wish to employ a _____ as treasurer.
   A. immediately
   B. Information
   C. Permission
   D. professional

   *Answer:* D) professional. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:2 — “A large society with complex financial affairs may wish to employ a professional as treasurer.”

**1348.** `[C1079 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In most _____ it is usual to elect the officers from among the members;
   A. delegates
   B. reception
   C. postponed
   D. societies

   *Answer:* D) societies. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:2 — “In most societies it is usual to elect the officers from among the members;”

**1349.** `[C1080 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An office carries with it only the rights _____ for executing the duties of the office, and it does not deprive a member of the society of his rights as a member.

   *Answer:* necessary — “An office carries with it only the rights necessary for executing the duties of the office, and it does not deprive a member of the society of his rights as a member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:3 — “An office carries with it only the rights necessary for executing the duties of the office, and it does not deprive a member of the society of his rights as a member.”

**1350.** `[C1080 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a person holds an office in a society of which he is not a member and the bylaws make that officer an ex-officio member of the board, the nonmember is thereby a _____ board member with all the accompanying rights;
   A. Procedures
   B. full-fledged
   C. specification
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* B) full-fledged. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:3 — “If a person holds an office in a society of which he is not a member and the bylaws make that officer an ex-officio member of the board, the nonmember is thereby a full-fledged board member with all the accompanying rights;”

**1351.** `[C1080 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An office carries with it only the rights _____ for executing the duties of the office, and it does not deprive a member of the society of his rights as a member.
   A. extending
   B. necessary
   C. committee
   D. Secretary

   *Answer:* B) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:3 — “An office carries with it only the rights necessary for executing the duties of the office, and it does not deprive a member of the society of his rights as a member.”

**1352.** `[C1080 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a person holds an office in a society of which he is not a member and the bylaws make that officer an ex-officio member of the board, the nonmember is thereby a _____ board member with all the accompanying rights;

   *Answer:* full-fledged — “If a person holds an office in a society of which he is not a member and the bylaws make that officer an ex-officio member of the board, the nonmember is thereby a full-fledged board member with all the accompanying rights;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:3 — “If a person holds an office in a society of which he is not a member and the bylaws make that officer an ex-officio member of the board, the nonmember is thereby a full-fledged board member with all the accompanying rights;”

**1353.** `[C1081 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The bylaws may contain a provision that “No person shall be eligible to serve _____ terms in the same office.” In filling vacancies for unexpired terms, an officer who has served more than half a term in an office is considered to have served a full term.

   *Answer:* consecutive — “The bylaws may contain a provision that “No person shall be eligible to serve consecutive terms in the same office.” In filling vacancies for unexpired terms, an officer who has served more than half a term in an office is considered to have served a full term.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:4 — “The bylaws may contain a provision that “No person shall be eligible to serve consecutive terms in the same office.” In filling vacancies for unexpired terms, an officer who has served more than half a term in an office is considered to have served a full term.”

**1354.** `[C1081 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The bylaws may contain a provision that “No person shall be eligible to serve _____ terms in the same office.” In filling vacancies for unexpired terms, an officer who has served more than half a term in an office is considered to have served a full term.
   A. interrupted
   B. authorizes
   C. consecutive
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) consecutive. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:4 — “The bylaws may contain a provision that “No person shall be eligible to serve consecutive terms in the same office.” In filling vacancies for unexpired terms, an officer who has served more than half a term in an office is considered to have served a full term.”

**1355.** `[C1082 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The presiding officer of an assembly ordinarily is called the _____ when no special title has been assigned, or in a body not permanently organized, such as a mass meeting (53).
   A. chairman
   B. assembly
   C. Business
   D. Standing

   *Answer:* A) chairman. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:5 — “The presiding officer of an assembly ordinarily is called the chairman when no special title has been assigned, or in a body not permanently organized, such as a mass meeting (53).”

**1356.** `[C1082 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In organized societies the presiding officer’s title is usually prescribed by the bylaws, that of _____ being most common.

   *Answer:* president — “In organized societies the presiding officer’s title is usually prescribed by the bylaws, that of president being most common.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:5 — “In organized societies the presiding officer’s title is usually prescribed by the bylaws, that of president being most common.”

**1357.** `[C1082 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term _____ refers to the person in a meeting who is actually presiding at the time, whether that person is the regular presiding officer or not.
   A. the chair
   B. Form and
   C. of the day
   D. a Session

   *Answer:* A) the chair. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:5 — “The term the chair refers to the person in a meeting who is actually presiding at the time, whether that person is the regular presiding officer or not.”

**1358.** `[C1082 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The presiding officer of an assembly ordinarily is called the chairman when no special title has been assigned, or in a body not _____ organized, such as a mass meeting (53).

   *Answer:* permanently — “The presiding officer of an assembly ordinarily is called the chairman when no special title has been assigned, or in a body not permanently organized, such as a mass meeting (53).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:5 — “The presiding officer of an assembly ordinarily is called the chairman when no special title has been assigned, or in a body not permanently organized, such as a mass meeting (53).”

**1359.** `[C1082 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In organized societies the presiding officer’s title is usually _____ by the bylaws, that of president being most common.
   A. subsidiary
   B. prescribed
   C. incidental
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) prescribed. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:5 — “In organized societies the presiding officer’s title is usually prescribed by the bylaws, that of president being most common.”

**1360.** `[C1083 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The presiding officer of an assembly—especially of a large one—should be chosen _____ for the ability to preside.

   *Answer:* principally — “The presiding officer of an assembly—especially of a large one—should be chosen principally for the ability to preside.”.
