See RONR (12th ed.) §47:6 — “The presiding officer of an assembly—especially of a large one—should be chosen principally for the ability to preside.”

**1361.** `[C1083 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This person should be well versed in parliamentary law and should be thoroughly familiar with the bylaws and other rules of the organization—even if he or she is to have the assistance of a _____.
   A. Responsibilities
   B. immediately
   C. Announcements
   D. parliamentarian

   *Answer:* D) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:6 — “This person should be well versed in parliamentary law and should be thoroughly familiar with the bylaws and other rules of the organization—even if he or she is to have the assistance of a parliamentarian.”

**1362.** `[C1083 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ officer of an assembly—especially of a large one—should be chosen principally for the ability to preside.

   *Answer:* presiding — “The presiding officer of an assembly—especially of a large one—should be chosen principally for the ability to preside.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:6 — “The presiding officer of an assembly—especially of a large one—should be chosen principally for the ability to preside.”

**1363.** `[C1083 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This person should be well versed in parliamentary law and should be thoroughly familiar with the bylaws and other rules of the organization—even if he or she is to have the assistance of a _____.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “This person should be well versed in parliamentary law and should be thoroughly familiar with the bylaws and other rules of the organization—even if he or she is to have the assistance of a parliamentarian.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:6 — “This person should be well versed in parliamentary law and should be thoroughly familiar with the bylaws and other rules of the organization—even if he or she is to have the assistance of a parliamentarian.”

**1364.** `[C1084 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Duties of the _____ officer of an assembly.

   *Answer:* presiding — “Duties of the presiding officer of an assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:7 — “Duties of the presiding officer of an assembly.”

**1365.** `[C1084 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The principal duties of the presiding officer of an assembly under _____ law are listed below—with references, where appropriate, to fuller descriptions elsewhere in this book.
   A. president-elect
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. parliamentary
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:7 — “The principal duties of the presiding officer of an assembly under parliamentary law are listed below—with references, where appropriate, to fuller descriptions elsewhere in this book.”

**1366.** `[C1084 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Additional _____ relating to the duties of the chair in particular cases will be found in the treatment of the subjects involved.

   *Answer:* information — “Additional information relating to the duties of the chair in particular cases will be found in the treatment of the subjects involved.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:7 — “Additional information relating to the duties of the chair in particular cases will be found in the treatment of the subjects involved.”

**1367.** `[C1084 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Duties of the _____ officer of an assembly.
   A. presiding
   B. delegates
   C. execution
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* A) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:7 — “Duties of the presiding officer of an assembly.”

**1368.** `[C1084 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The principal duties of the presiding officer of an assembly under _____ law are listed below—with references, where appropriate, to fuller descriptions elsewhere in this book.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The principal duties of the presiding officer of an assembly under parliamentary law are listed below—with references, where appropriate, to fuller descriptions elsewhere in this book.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:7 — “The principal duties of the presiding officer of an assembly under parliamentary law are listed below—with references, where appropriate, to fuller descriptions elsewhere in this book.”

**1369.** `[C1085 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At each meeting, in addition to the _____ papers proper to that meeting’s business, the presiding officer should have at hand:

   *Answer:* necessary — “At each meeting, in addition to the necessary papers proper to that meeting’s business, the presiding officer should have at hand:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:8 — “At each meeting, in addition to the necessary papers proper to that meeting’s business, the presiding officer should have at hand:”

**1370.** `[C1085 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At each meeting, in addition to the _____ papers proper to that meeting’s business, the presiding officer should have at hand:
   A. debatable
   B. Reporting
   C. necessary
   D. president

   *Answer:* C) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:8 — “At each meeting, in addition to the necessary papers proper to that meeting’s business, the presiding officer should have at hand:”

**1371.** `[C1086 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except in a small board or a committee, the _____ officer should stand while calling a meeting to order or declaring it adjourned, and while putting a question to vote.

   *Answer:* presiding — “Except in a small board or a committee, the presiding officer should stand while calling a meeting to order or declaring it adjourned, and while putting a question to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:9 — “Except in a small board or a committee, the presiding officer should stand while calling a meeting to order or declaring it adjourned, and while putting a question to vote.”

**1372.** `[C1086 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: He should also stand—without leaving the chair—while explaining his reasons for a ruling on a point of order (if the _____ entails more than a few words) or when speaking during debate on an appeal or a point of order that he has submitted to the judgment of the assembly (23, 24).
   A. adjournment
   B. Unfinished
   C. explanation
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) explanation. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:9 — “He should also stand—without leaving the chair—while explaining his reasons for a ruling on a point of order (if the explanation entails more than a few words) or when speaking during debate on an appeal or a point of order that he has submitted to the judgment of the assembly (23, 24).”

**1373.** `[C1086 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While a member is speaking in debate on any question, the presiding officer should remain seated—unless the view between him and the members would be _____, in which case he should step back slightly during the member’s speech.

   *Answer:* obstructed — “While a member is speaking in debate on any question, the presiding officer should remain seated—unless the view between him and the members would be obstructed, in which case he should step back slightly during the member’s speech.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:9 — “While a member is speaking in debate on any question, the presiding officer should remain seated—unless the view between him and the members would be obstructed, in which case he should step back slightly during the member’s speech.”

**1374.** `[C1086 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except in a small board or a committee, the _____ officer should stand while calling a meeting to order or declaring it adjourned, and while putting a question to vote.
   A. rejecting
   B. exhausted
   C. Precedent
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* D) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:9 — “Except in a small board or a committee, the presiding officer should stand while calling a meeting to order or declaring it adjourned, and while putting a question to vote.”

**1375.** `[C1086 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: He should also stand—without leaving the chair—while explaining his reasons for a ruling on a point of order (if the _____ entails more than a few words) or when speaking during debate on an appeal or a point of order that he has submitted to the judgment of the assembly (23, 24).

   *Answer:* explanation — “He should also stand—without leaving the chair—while explaining his reasons for a ruling on a point of order (if the explanation entails more than a few words) or when speaking during debate on an appeal or a point of order that he has submitted to the judgment of the assembly (23, 24).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:9 — “He should also stand—without leaving the chair—while explaining his reasons for a ruling on a point of order (if the explanation entails more than a few words) or when speaking during debate on an appeal or a point of order that he has submitted to the judgment of the assembly (23, 24).”

**1376.** `[C1087 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair, however, should not hesitate to put the question on a motion to elect officers or appoint _____ or a committee even if he is included.

   *Answer:* delegates — “The chair, however, should not hesitate to put the question on a motion to elect officers or appoint delegates or a committee even if he is included.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:10 — “The chair, however, should not hesitate to put the question on a motion to elect officers or appoint delegates or a committee even if he is included.”

**1377.** `[C1087 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair, however, should not hesitate to put the question on a motion to elect officers or appoint _____ or a committee even if he is included.
   A. exhausted
   B. president
   C. presiding
   D. delegates

   *Answer:* D) delegates. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:10 — “The chair, however, should not hesitate to put the question on a motion to elect officers or appoint delegates or a committee even if he is included.”

**1378.** `[C1088 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is necessary for the president to vacate the chair during a meeting, or if the president is absent, the chair is occupied _____ by another—who also must not be precluded from presiding by any of the impediments mentioned in the preceding paragraph —as follows:

   *Answer:* temporarily — “If it is necessary for the president to vacate the chair during a meeting, or if the president is absent, the chair is occupied temporarily by another—who also must not be precluded from presiding by any of the impediments mentioned in the preceding paragraph —as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:11 — “If it is necessary for the president to vacate the chair during a meeting, or if the president is absent, the chair is occupied temporarily by another—who also must not be precluded from presiding by any of the impediments mentioned in the preceding paragraph —as follows:”

**1379.** `[C1088 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is necessary for the president to vacate the chair during a meeting, or if the president is absent, the chair is occupied _____ by another—who also must not be precluded from presiding by any of the impediments mentioned in the preceding paragraph —as follows:
   A. Definitely
   B. Convention
   C. committees
   D. temporarily

   *Answer:* D) temporarily. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:11 — “If it is necessary for the president to vacate the chair during a meeting, or if the president is absent, the chair is occupied temporarily by another—who also must not be precluded from presiding by any of the impediments mentioned in the preceding paragraph —as follows:”

**1380.** `[C1090 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (Sometimes this may be a professional presiding officer.) If the president and vice-president(s) do not object, the assembly, by _____ vote, can adopt an incidental main motion to effect such an arrangement for all or part of a session.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* D) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:13 — “(Sometimes this may be a professional presiding officer.) If the president and vice-president(s) do not object, the assembly, by majority vote, can adopt an incidental main motion to effect such an arrangement for all or part of a session.”

**1381.** `[C1090 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In certain instances in an ordinary society—for example, if an adjourned meeting or a special meeting (9) must deal with a problem that has intensely divided the _____—it may be that such a meeting can accomplish more under the chairmanship of an invited nonmember who is skilled in presiding.

   *Answer:* organization — “In certain instances in an ordinary society—for example, if an adjourned meeting or a special meeting (9) must deal with a problem that has intensely divided the organization—it may be that such a meeting can accomplish more under the chairmanship of an invited nonmember who is skilled in presiding.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:13 — “In certain instances in an ordinary society—for example, if an adjourned meeting or a special meeting (9) must deal with a problem that has intensely divided the organization—it may be that such a meeting can accomplish more under the chairmanship of an invited nonmember who is skilled in presiding.”

**1382.** `[C1090 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (Sometimes this may be a professional presiding officer.) If the president and _____(s) do not object, the assembly, by majority vote, can adopt an incidental main motion to effect such an arrangement for all or part of a session.
   A. vice-president
   B. Organization
   C. deliberative
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:13 — “(Sometimes this may be a professional presiding officer.) If the president and vice-president(s) do not object, the assembly, by majority vote, can adopt an incidental main motion to effect such an arrangement for all or part of a session.”

**1383.** `[C1090 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This motion is a question of _____ affecting the assembly (19).

   *Answer:* privilege — “This motion is a question of privilege affecting the assembly (19).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:13 — “This motion is a question of privilege affecting the assembly (19).”

**1384.** `[C1090 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In certain instances in an ordinary society—for example, if an adjourned meeting or a special meeting (9) must deal with a problem that has intensely divided the _____—it may be that such a meeting can accomplish more under the chairmanship of an invited nonmember who is skilled in presiding.
   A. organization
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Particular
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:13 — “In certain instances in an ordinary society—for example, if an adjourned meeting or a special meeting (9) must deal with a problem that has intensely divided the organization—it may be that such a meeting can accomplish more under the chairmanship of an invited nonmember who is skilled in presiding.”

**1385.** `[C1091 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Suggestions for _____ presiding officers.

   *Answer:* inexperienced — “Suggestions for inexperienced presiding officers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:14 — “Suggestions for inexperienced presiding officers.”

**1386.** `[C1091 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The larger the assembly, the more readily it will detect the _____ weakness in a presiding officer.
   A. reception
   B. slightest
   C. rejecting
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* B) slightest. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:14 — “The larger the assembly, the more readily it will detect the slightest weakness in a presiding officer.”

**1387.** `[C1091 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Efforts to _____ on any such failing may follow with sometimes disastrous results.

   *Answer:* capitalize — “Efforts to capitalize on any such failing may follow with sometimes disastrous results.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:14 — “Efforts to capitalize on any such failing may follow with sometimes disastrous results.”

**1388.** `[C1091 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is often said that _____ is strength, and certainly that is true in this case.
   A. knowledge
   B. Committee
   C. rejecting
   D. reception

   *Answer:* A) knowledge. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:14 — “It is often said that knowledge is strength, and certainly that is true in this case.”

**1389.** `[C1091 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: There is no acceptable alternative to _____ procedure for the conduct of business in a deliberative assembly;

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “There is no acceptable alternative to parliamentary procedure for the conduct of business in a deliberative assembly;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:14 — “There is no acceptable alternative to parliamentary procedure for the conduct of business in a deliberative assembly;”

**1390.** `[C1092 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A good first step is to read through Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised _____—a concise introductory guide prepared by the authors of this book and fully compatible with it—paying particular attention to Chapter 15 (“President or Vice-President”).

   *Answer:* In Brief — “A good first step is to read through Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised In Brief—a concise introductory guide prepared by the authors of this book and fully compatible with it—paying particular attention to Chapter 15 (“President or Vice-President”).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:15 — “A good first step is to read through Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised In Brief—a concise introductory guide prepared by the authors of this book and fully compatible with it—paying particular attention to Chapter 15 (“President or Vice-President”).”

**1391.** `[C1092 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A presiding officer should make every effort to know more _____ procedure than other members.
   A. parliamentary
   B. requirement
   C. subsequently
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:15 — “A presiding officer should make every effort to know more parliamentary procedure than other members.”

**1392.** `[C1092 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A good first step is to read through Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised In Brief—a concise introductory guide prepared by the authors of this book and fully compatible with it—paying particular attention to Chapter 15 (“President or _____”).

   *Answer:* Vice-President — “A good first step is to read through Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised In Brief—a concise introductory guide prepared by the authors of this book and fully compatible with it—paying particular attention to Chapter 15 (“President or Vice-President”).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:15 — “A good first step is to read through Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised In Brief—a concise introductory guide prepared by the authors of this book and fully compatible with it—paying particular attention to Chapter 15 (“President or Vice-President”).”

**1393.** `[C1092 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair should be able to refer to the table of rules relating to motions on pages t6– t33 quickly enough that there will be no delay in deciding all points _____ there.
   A. contained
   B. Precedent
   C. rejecting
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* A) contained. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:15 — “The chair should be able to refer to the table of rules relating to motions on pages t6– t33 quickly enough that there will be no delay in deciding all points contained there.”

**1394.** `[C1092 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As more difficult points arise, a careful reading of the detailed treatment of such points in the body of this book will make them readily _____ and mastered.

   *Answer:* understood — “As more difficult points arise, a careful reading of the detailed treatment of such points in the body of this book will make them readily understood and mastered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:15 — “As more difficult points arise, a careful reading of the detailed treatment of such points in the body of this book will make them readily understood and mastered.”

**1395.** `[C1093 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The presiding officer must not permit members to press on so rapidly that the _____ steps are abridged or go unobserved.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The presiding officer must not permit members to press on so rapidly that the parliamentary steps are abridged or go unobserved.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:16 — “The presiding officer must not permit members to press on so rapidly that the parliamentary steps are abridged or go unobserved.”

**1396.** `[C1093 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion is made, he must not _____ any member or allow anyone to speak until the motion is seconded (where that is required) and he has stated the question.
   A. Committee
   B. recognize
   C. exhausted
   D. Reporting

   *Answer:* B) recognize. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:16 — “When a motion is made, he must not recognize any member or allow anyone to speak until the motion is seconded (where that is required) and he has stated the question.”

**1397.** `[C1093 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The presiding officer must not permit members to press on so rapidly that the _____ steps are abridged or go unobserved.
   A. adjournment
   B. consideration
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:16 — “The presiding officer must not permit members to press on so rapidly that the parliamentary steps are abridged or go unobserved.”

**1398.** `[C1093 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion is made, he must not _____ any member or allow anyone to speak until the motion is seconded (where that is required) and he has stated the question.

   *Answer:* recognize — “When a motion is made, he must not recognize any member or allow anyone to speak until the motion is seconded (where that is required) and he has stated the question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:16 — “When a motion is made, he must not recognize any member or allow anyone to speak until the motion is seconded (where that is required) and he has stated the question.”

**1399.** `[C1094 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the immediately pending business—the _____ question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.

   *Answer:* exact — “The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the immediately pending business—the exact question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:17 — “The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the immediately pending business—the exact question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.”

**1400.** `[C1094 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the _____ pending business—the exact question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.
   A. Particular
   B. Substitute
   C. adjournment
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* D) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:17 — “The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the immediately pending business—the exact question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.”

**1401.** `[C1094 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Failure of _____ officers to do so is one of the greatest causes of confusion in meetings.

   *Answer:* presiding — “Failure of presiding officers to do so is one of the greatest causes of confusion in meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:17 — “Failure of presiding officers to do so is one of the greatest causes of confusion in meetings.”

**1402.** `[C1094 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ in stating the question on an unwritten motion, the chair should always say, “It is moved and seconded that” and then give the precise words of the motion fully, no matter how clearly the motion may have been framed when moved by its maker.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. Convention
   C. Particularly
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) Particularly. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:17 — “Particularly in stating the question on an unwritten motion, the chair should always say, “It is moved and seconded that” and then give the precise words of the motion fully, no matter how clearly the motion may have been framed when moved by its maker.”

**1403.** `[C1094 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair should never try to avoid this _____ important duty by saying, “You have heard the motion” or by saying, “The motion is moved and seconded” without repeating its words.

   *Answer:* critically — “The chair should never try to avoid this critically important duty by saying, “You have heard the motion” or by saying, “The motion is moved and seconded” without repeating its words.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:17 — “The chair should never try to avoid this critically important duty by saying, “You have heard the motion” or by saying, “The motion is moved and seconded” without repeating its words.”

**1404.** `[C1095 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a vote is taken, the result should be announced and also what question, if any, is then pending, before any member who addresses the chair is _____.

   *Answer:* recognized — “When a vote is taken, the result should be announced and also what question, if any, is then pending, before any member who addresses the chair is recognized.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:18 — “When a vote is taken, the result should be announced and also what question, if any, is then pending, before any member who addresses the chair is recognized.”

**1405.** `[C1095 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a large assembly where a _____ is required, the chair should insist that a member go to it and identify himself.
   A. introduced
   B. sustaining
   C. microphone
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) microphone. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:18 — “In a large assembly where a microphone is required, the chair should insist that a member go to it and identify himself.”

**1406.** `[C1095 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This brief delay is often very _____ in quieting heated feelings.

   *Answer:* salutary — “This brief delay is often very salutary in quieting heated feelings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:18 — “This brief delay is often very salutary in quieting heated feelings.”

**1407.** `[C1095 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Efforts to abbreviate the requirements of _____ procedure often signal an effort to substitute the member’s will for the parliamentary leadership of the presiding officer.
   A. consideration
   B. Vice-President
   C. specification
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:18 — “Efforts to abbreviate the requirements of parliamentary procedure often signal an effort to substitute the member’s will for the parliamentary leadership of the presiding officer.”

**1408.** `[C1095 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Firmness and, at the same time, calm insistence on the regular order is a technique essential to the _____ of a skilled presiding officer.

   *Answer:* development — “Firmness and, at the same time, calm insistence on the regular order is a technique essential to the development of a skilled presiding officer.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:18 — “Firmness and, at the same time, calm insistence on the regular order is a technique essential to the development of a skilled presiding officer.”

**1409.** `[C1096 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While a commanding presence and knowledge are essential in procedural matters, the president of an ordinary _____ assembly, especially a large one, should, of all the members, have the least to say upon the substance of pending questions.

   *Answer:* deliberative — “While a commanding presence and knowledge are essential in procedural matters, the president of an ordinary deliberative assembly, especially a large one, should, of all the members, have the least to say upon the substance of pending questions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:19 — “While a commanding presence and knowledge are essential in procedural matters, the president of an ordinary deliberative assembly, especially a large one, should, of all the members, have the least to say upon the substance of pending questions.”

**1410.** `[C1096 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While providing strong _____, he should be fair.
   A. Procedures
   B. persuasive
   C. leadership
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* C) leadership. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:19 — “While providing strong leadership, he should be fair.”

**1411.** `[C1096 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: he should never be unjust to even the most troublesome member, or take advantage of such member’s lack of knowledge of _____ law, even though a temporary good might be accomplished thereby.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “he should never be unjust to even the most troublesome member, or take advantage of such member’s lack of knowledge of parliamentary law, even though a temporary good might be accomplished thereby.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:19 — “he should never be unjust to even the most troublesome member, or take advantage of such member’s lack of knowledge of parliamentary law, even though a temporary good might be accomplished thereby.”

**1412.** `[C1096 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The president should never be _____ or more strict than is necessary for the good of the meeting.
   A. Privilege
   B. Objection
   C. Crestwood
   D. technical

   *Answer:* D) technical. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:19 — “The president should never be technical or more strict than is necessary for the good of the meeting.”

**1413.** `[C1096 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: the assembly may be of such a nature, through its _____ with parliamentary usage and its peaceable disposition, that strict enforcement of the rules, instead of assisting, would greatly hinder business.

   *Answer:* unfamiliarity — “the assembly may be of such a nature, through its unfamiliarity with parliamentary usage and its peaceable disposition, that strict enforcement of the rules, instead of assisting, would greatly hinder business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:19 — “the assembly may be of such a nature, through its unfamiliarity with parliamentary usage and its peaceable disposition, that strict enforcement of the rules, instead of assisting, would greatly hinder business.”

**1414.** `[C1097 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ duties of the president of a society.

   *Answer:* Administrative — “Administrative duties of the president of a society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:20 — “Administrative duties of the president of a society.”

**1415.** `[C1097 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: All of the duties of the _____ officer described above relate to the function of presiding over the assembly at its meetings.
   A. committee
   B. president
   C. delegates
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* D) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:20 — “All of the duties of the presiding officer described above relate to the function of presiding over the assembly at its meetings.”

**1416.** `[C1097 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but these are outside the scope of _____ law, and the president has such authority only insofar as the bylaws provide it.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “but these are outside the scope of parliamentary law, and the president has such authority only insofar as the bylaws provide it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:20 — “but these are outside the scope of parliamentary law, and the president has such authority only insofar as the bylaws provide it.”

**1417.** `[C1097 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some _____, the president is responsible for appointing, and is ex officio a member of, all committees (with the exception of the nominating committee, which should be expressly excluded from such a provision, and with the further possible exception of all disciplinary committees;
   A. president-elect
   B. President-Elect
   C. Parliamentary
   D. organizations

   *Answer:* D) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:20 — “In some organizations, the president is responsible for appointing, and is ex officio a member of, all committees (with the exception of the nominating committee, which should be expressly excluded from such a provision, and with the further possible exception of all disciplinary committees;”

**1418.** `[C1097 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But only when he is so _____ by the bylaws—or, in the case of a particular committee, by vote of the assembly—does he have this authority and status.

   *Answer:* authorized — “But only when he is so authorized by the bylaws—or, in the case of a particular committee, by vote of the assembly—does he have this authority and status.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:20 — “But only when he is so authorized by the bylaws—or, in the case of a particular committee, by vote of the assembly—does he have this authority and status.”

**1419.** `[C1098 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some organizations desire to elect their president one entire term in advance, and in such cases, during the term following the election, the person chosen is called the _____.

   *Answer:* president-elect — “Some organizations desire to elect their president one entire term in advance, and in such cases, during the term following the election, the person chosen is called the president-elect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:21 — “Some organizations desire to elect their president one entire term in advance, and in such cases, during the term following the election, the person chosen is called the president-elect.”

**1420.** `[C1098 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some organizations desire to elect their president one entire term in advance, and in such cases, during the term following the election, the person chosen is called the _____.
   A. Organization
   B. Vice-President
   C. Information
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* D) president-elect. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:21 — “Some organizations desire to elect their president one entire term in advance, and in such cases, during the term following the election, the person chosen is called the president-elect.”

**1421.** `[C1099 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the bylaws of an organization provide for a _____, it is usual to provide also that if the president should be absent, or if the office of the president should become vacant between elections, the president-elect shall preside, if present, or shall fill the vacancy.

   *Answer:* president-elect — “When the bylaws of an organization provide for a president-elect, it is usual to provide also that if the president should be absent, or if the office of the president should become vacant between elections, the president-elect shall preside, if present, or shall fill the vacancy.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:22 — “When the bylaws of an organization provide for a president-elect, it is usual to provide also that if the president should be absent, or if the office of the president should become vacant between elections, the president-elect shall preside, if present, or shall fill the vacancy.”

**1422.** `[C1099 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless such provision is made, the first _____ would preside or complete the president’s term.
   A. President-Elect
   B. vice-president
   C. characteristics
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:22 — “Unless such provision is made, the first vice-president would preside or complete the president’s term.”

**1423.** `[C1099 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is important to consider these _____ with great care.

   *Answer:* provisions — “It is important to consider these provisions with great care.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:22 — “It is important to consider these provisions with great care.”

**1424.** `[C1099 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The bylaws can assign the president-elect specific _____.
   A. resolutions
   B. responsibilities
   C. deliberative
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) responsibilities. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:22 — “The bylaws can assign the president-elect specific responsibilities.”

**1425.** `[C1099 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the bylaws of an organization provide for a _____, it is usual to provide also that if the president should be absent, or if the office of the president should become vacant between elections, the president-elect shall preside, if present, or shall fill the vacancy.
   A. adjournment
   B. representatives
   C. president-elect
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* C) president-elect. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:22 — “When the bylaws of an organization provide for a president-elect, it is usual to provide also that if the president should be absent, or if the office of the president should become vacant between elections, the president-elect shall preside, if present, or shall fill the vacancy.”

**1426.** `[C1100 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, it is important to elect a _____ who is competent to perform the duties of president.

   *Answer:* vice-president — “Thus, it is important to elect a vice-president who is competent to perform the duties of president.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:23 — “Thus, it is important to elect a vice-president who is competent to perform the duties of president.”

**1427.** `[C1100 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, it is important to elect a _____ who is competent to perform the duties of president.
   A. representatives
   B. vice-president
   C. immediately
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:23 — “Thus, it is important to elect a vice-president who is competent to perform the duties of president.”

   A. Reporting
   B. confusion
   C. Signature
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* B) confusion. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:24 — “President” or “Madam President” (unless confusion might result—for example, when the president is also on the platform—in which case the form “Mr.”

   C. consideration
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:24 — “When a vice-president is presiding over the assembly, he or she is addressed as “Mr.”

**1431.** `[C1101 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: President” or “Madam President” (unless _____ might result—for example, when the president is also on the platform—in which case the form “Mr.

   *Answer:* confusion — “President” or “Madam President” (unless confusion might result—for example, when the president is also on the platform—in which case the form “Mr.”. **1432.** `[C1104 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the president has prepared a report but cannot attend the meeting at which it is to be presented, the _____ should present it for him.

   *Answer:* vice-president — “If the president has prepared a report but cannot attend the meeting at which it is to be presented, the vice-president should present it for him.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:27 — “If the president has prepared a report but cannot attend the meeting at which it is to be presented, the vice-president should present it for him.”

**1433.** `[C1104 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the president has prepared a report but cannot attend the meeting at which it is to be presented, the _____ should present it for him.
   A. President-Elect
   B. adjournment
   C. vice-president
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* C) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:27 — “If the president has prepared a report but cannot attend the meeting at which it is to be presented, the vice-president should present it for him.”

**1434.** `[C1106 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In case of the president’s resignation, death, or removal, the first _____ then automatically becomes president (unless, as indicated above, the bylaws expressly provide otherwise for the office of president).

   *Answer:* vice-president — “In case of the president’s resignation, death, or removal, the first vice-president then automatically becomes president (unless, as indicated above, the bylaws expressly provide otherwise for the office of president).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:29 — “In case of the president’s resignation, death, or removal, the first vice-president then automatically becomes president (unless, as indicated above, the bylaws expressly provide otherwise for the office of president).”

**1435.** `[C1106 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Likewise, in case of any vice-president’s resignation, death, or removal, or upon his or her automatic promotion to a higher office, the _____ vicepresident, if there is one, is automatically promoted (unless the bylaws expressly provide otherwise).
   A. subsequently
   B. presentation
   C. constitution
   D. next-highest-ranking

   *Answer:* D) next-highest-ranking. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:29 — “Likewise, in case of any vice-president’s resignation, death, or removal, or upon his or her automatic promotion to a higher office, the next-highest-ranking vicepresident, if there is one, is automatically promoted (unless the bylaws expressly provide otherwise).”

**1436.** `[C1106 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Likewise, in case of any vice-president’s resignation, death, or removal, or upon his or her automatic promotion to a higher office, the next-highest-ranking _____, if there is one, is automatically promoted (unless the bylaws expressly provide otherwise).
   A. vicepresident
   B. immediately
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* A) vicepresident. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:29 — “Likewise, in case of any vice-president’s resignation, death, or removal, or upon his or her automatic promotion to a higher office, the next-highest-ranking vicepresident, if there is one, is automatically promoted (unless the bylaws expressly provide otherwise).”

**1437.** `[C1106 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In case of the president’s _____, death, or removal, the first vice-president then automatically becomes president (unless, as indicated above, the bylaws expressly provide otherwise for the office of president).

   *Answer:* resignation — “In case of the president’s resignation, death, or removal, the first vice-president then automatically becomes president (unless, as indicated above, the bylaws expressly provide otherwise for the office of president).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:29 — “In case of the president’s resignation, death, or removal, the first vice-president then automatically becomes president (unless, as indicated above, the bylaws expressly provide otherwise for the office of president).”

**1438.** `[C1107 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes the bylaws provide that the different _____ shall have administrative charge of different departments.

   *Answer:* vicepresidents — “Sometimes the bylaws provide that the different vicepresidents shall have administrative charge of different departments.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:30 — “Sometimes the bylaws provide that the different vicepresidents shall have administrative charge of different departments.”

**1439.** `[C1107 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In many such cases, it is inadvisable for the _____ to have to change their duties whenever a vacancy occurs among them, and the bylaws should therefore also provide a method for filling vacancies that expressly applies to the offices of president and all vice-presidents.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Announcements
   C. vice-presidents
   D. specification

   *Answer:* C) vice-presidents. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:30 — “In many such cases, it is inadvisable for the vice-presidents to have to change their duties whenever a vacancy occurs among them, and the bylaws should therefore also provide a method for filling vacancies that expressly applies to the offices of president and all vice-presidents.”

**1440.** `[C1107 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes the bylaws provide that the different _____ shall have administrative charge of different departments.
   A. vicepresidents
   B. Announcements
   C. adjournment
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) vicepresidents. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:30 — “Sometimes the bylaws provide that the different vicepresidents shall have administrative charge of different departments.”

**1441.** `[C1107 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In many such cases, it is inadvisable for the _____ to have to change their duties whenever a vacancy occurs among them, and the bylaws should therefore also provide a method for filling vacancies that expressly applies to the offices of president and all vice-presidents.

   *Answer:* vice-presidents — “In many such cases, it is inadvisable for the vice-presidents to have to change their duties whenever a vacancy occurs among them, and the bylaws should therefore also provide a method for filling vacancies that expressly applies to the offices of president and all vice-presidents.”.    A. Organization
   B. immediately
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. vice-president

   *Answer:* D) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:31 — “Although in many instances the outgoing vice-president (or first vice-president) will be the logical nominee for president in the next term, the society has the freedom to make its own choice and to elect the most promising candidate at that particular time, unless stated otherwise in the bylaws (cf.”

**1444.** `[C1109 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ is the recording officer of the assembly and the custodian of its records, except those specifically assigned to others, such as the treasurer’s books.

   *Answer:* secretary — “The secretary is the recording officer of the assembly and the custodian of its records, except those specifically assigned to others, such as the treasurer’s books.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:32 — “The secretary is the recording officer of the assembly and the custodian of its records, except those specifically assigned to others, such as the treasurer’s books.”

**1445.** `[C1109 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The recording officer is sometimes called the _____, the recording secretary (when there is also, for example, a corresponding secretary or financial secretary), the recorder, or the scribe.
   A. clerk
   B. legal
   C. amend
   D. Point

   *Answer:* A) clerk. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:32 — “The recording officer is sometimes called the clerk, the recording secretary (when there is also, for example, a corresponding secretary or financial secretary), the recorder, or the scribe.”

**1446.** `[C1109 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The recording officer is sometimes called the clerk, the _____ (when there is also, for example, a corresponding secretary or financial secretary), the recorder, or the scribe.

   *Answer:* recording secretary — “The recording officer is sometimes called the clerk, the recording secretary (when there is also, for example, a corresponding secretary or financial secretary), the recorder, or the scribe.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:32 — “The recording officer is sometimes called the clerk, the recording secretary (when there is also, for example, a corresponding secretary or financial secretary), the recorder, or the scribe.”

**1447.** `[C1109 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The secretary is the recording officer of the assembly and the custodian of its records, except those _____ assigned to others, such as the treasurer’s books.
   A. specifically
   B. Parliamentary
   C. consideration
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) specifically. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:32 — “The secretary is the recording officer of the assembly and the custodian of its records, except those specifically assigned to others, such as the treasurer’s books.”

**1448.** `[C1110 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the absence of the _____, a secretary pro tem must be elected;

   *Answer:* secretary — “In the absence of the secretary, a secretary pro tem must be elected;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:34 — “In the absence of the secretary, a secretary pro tem must be elected;”

**1449.** `[C1110 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: the _____, financial, or executive secretary in organizations having such officers is not an automatic replacement.
   A. president-elect
   B. resolutions
   C. adjournment
   D. corresponding

   *Answer:* D) corresponding. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:34 — “the corresponding, financial, or executive secretary in organizations having such officers is not an automatic replacement.”

**1450.** `[C1110 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, under “Reports of Officers” in the order of business, _____ of an official character is to be read, it is normally read by the recording secretary and not by the corresponding secretary.

   *Answer:* correspondence — “If, under “Reports of Officers” in the order of business, correspondence of an official character is to be read, it is normally read by the recording secretary and not by the corresponding secretary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:34 — “If, under “Reports of Officers” in the order of business, correspondence of an official character is to be read, it is normally read by the recording secretary and not by the corresponding secretary.”

**1451.** `[C1110 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the absence of the _____, a secretary pro tem must be elected;
   A. postponed
   B. reception
   C. secretary
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* C) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:34 — “In the absence of the secretary, a secretary pro tem must be elected;”

**1452.** `[C1110 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: the _____, financial, or executive secretary in organizations having such officers is not an automatic replacement.

   *Answer:* corresponding — “the corresponding, financial, or executive secretary in organizations having such officers is not an automatic replacement.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:34 — “the corresponding, financial, or executive secretary in organizations having such officers is not an automatic replacement.”

**1453.** `[C1111 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When written reports are received from boards or _____, the secretary records on them the date they were received and what further action was taken on them, and preserves them among his records.
   A. resolution
   B. committees
   C. recognized
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* B) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:35 — “When written reports are received from boards or committees, the secretary records on them the date they were received and what further action was taken on them, and preserves them among his records.”

**1454.** `[C1111 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is not _____ for an assembly to vote that a board or committee report be “placed on file,” as that is done without a vote.

   *Answer:* necessary — “It is not necessary for an assembly to vote that a board or committee report be “placed on file,” as that is done without a vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:35 — “It is not necessary for an assembly to vote that a board or committee report be “placed on file,” as that is done without a vote.”

**1455.** `[C1111 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When written reports are received from boards or _____, the secretary records on them the date they were received and what further action was taken on them, and preserves them among his records.

   *Answer:* committees — “When written reports are received from boards or committees, the secretary records on them the date they were received and what further action was taken on them, and preserves them among his records.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:35 — “When written reports are received from boards or committees, the secretary records on them the date they were received and what further action was taken on them, and preserves them among his records.”

**1456.** `[C1111 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is not _____ for an assembly to vote that a board or committee report be “placed on file,” as that is done without a vote.
   A. Signature
   B. auditors’
   C. amendment
   D. necessary

   *Answer:* D) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:35 — “It is not necessary for an assembly to vote that a board or committee report be “placed on file,” as that is done without a vote.”
