**1458.** `[C1112 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The corporation law of each state frequently provides for the _____ of records of any group incorporated in that state.
   A. consideration
   B. immediately
   C. subsequently
   D. availability

   *Answer:* D) availability. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:36 — “The corporation law of each state frequently provides for the availability of records of any group incorporated in that state.”

**1459.** `[C1112 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee requires certain records for the proper _____ of its duties, the secretary turns them over to the committee chairman—after consulting with the president in any cases where he or she is in doubt.
   A. Definitely
   B. Preparation
   C. performance
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) performance. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:36 — “When a committee requires certain records for the proper performance of its duties, the secretary turns them over to the committee chairman—after consulting with the president in any cases where he or she is in doubt.”

**1460.** `[C1112 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The corporation law of each state frequently provides for the _____ of records of any group incorporated in that state.

   *Answer:* availability — “The corporation law of each state frequently provides for the availability of records of any group incorporated in that state.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:36 — “The corporation law of each state frequently provides for the availability of records of any group incorporated in that state.”

**1461.** `[C1113 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When there is a _____, the unqualified word secretary used alone refers to the recording officer.

   *Answer:* corresponding secretary — “When there is a corresponding secretary, the unqualified word secretary used alone refers to the recording officer.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:37 — “When there is a corresponding secretary, the unqualified word secretary used alone refers to the recording officer.”

**1462.** `[C1113 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When there is a corresponding _____, the unqualified word secretary used alone refers to the recording officer.
   A. secretary
   B. postponed
   C. Committee
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:37 — “When there is a corresponding secretary, the unqualified word secretary used alone refers to the recording officer.”

**1463.** `[C1114 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The treasurer of an _____ is the officer entrusted with the custody of its funds.
   A. immediately
   B. organization
   C. Permission
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:38 — “The treasurer of an organization is the officer entrusted with the custody of its funds.”

**1464.** `[C1114 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The treasurer, and any other officers who handle funds of the society, should be bonded for a sum _____ to protect the society from loss.

   *Answer:* sufficient — “The treasurer, and any other officers who handle funds of the society, should be bonded for a sum sufficient to protect the society from loss.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:38 — “The treasurer, and any other officers who handle funds of the society, should be bonded for a sum sufficient to protect the society from loss.”

**1465.** `[C1114 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The specific duties of the treasurer will vary depending on the size and _____ of the society;
   A. Reconsider
   B. landscaped
   C. authorizes
   D. complexity

   *Answer:* D) complexity. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:38 — “The specific duties of the treasurer will vary depending on the size and complexity of the society;”

**1466.** `[C1114 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but this officer cannot disburse funds except by _____ of the society or as the bylaws prescribe.

   *Answer:* authority — “but this officer cannot disburse funds except by authority of the society or as the bylaws prescribe.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:38 — “but this officer cannot disburse funds except by authority of the society or as the bylaws prescribe.”

**1467.** `[C1114 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of an organization is the officer entrusted with the custody of its funds.
   A. delegates
   B. Crestwood
   C. treasurer
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* C) treasurer. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:38 — “The treasurer of an organization is the officer entrusted with the custody of its funds.”

**1468.** `[C1115 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a _____—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.

   *Answer:* financial secretary — “In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a financial secretary—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:39 — “In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a financial secretary—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.”

**1469.** `[C1115 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In ordinary societies, tasks incident to the _____ of dues from members are a part of the treasurer’s duties unless the bylaws provide otherwise.
   A. committees
   B. collection
   C. Convention
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) collection. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:39 — “In ordinary societies, tasks incident to the collection of dues from members are a part of the treasurer’s duties unless the bylaws provide otherwise.”

**1470.** `[C1115 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases some _____ have, in addition to the treasurer, a financial secretary—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a financial secretary—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:39 — “In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a financial secretary—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.”

**1471.** `[C1115 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In ordinary societies, tasks incident to the _____ of dues from members are a part of the treasurer’s duties unless the bylaws provide otherwise.

   *Answer:* collection — “In ordinary societies, tasks incident to the collection of dues from members are a part of the treasurer’s duties unless the bylaws provide otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:39 — “In ordinary societies, tasks incident to the collection of dues from members are a part of the treasurer’s duties unless the bylaws provide otherwise.”

**1472.** `[C1116 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition to the officers described above, an organization can provide in its bylaws for any _____ it may wish—including assistant officers.

   *Answer:* other officers — “In addition to the officers described above, an organization can provide in its bylaws for any other officers it may wish—including assistant officers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:40 — “In addition to the officers described above, an organization can provide in its bylaws for any other officers it may wish—including assistant officers.”

**1473.** `[C1116 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition to the officers described above, an _____ can provide in its bylaws for any other officers it may wish—including assistant officers.
   A. undebatable
   B. organization
   C. Preparation
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:40 — “In addition to the officers described above, an organization can provide in its bylaws for any other officers it may wish—including assistant officers.”

**1474.** `[C1116 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Officers _____ included, and their usual duties, are:

   *Answer:* sometimes — “Officers sometimes included, and their usual duties, are:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:40 — “Officers sometimes included, and their usual duties, are:”

**1475.** `[C1116 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Officers _____ included, and their usual duties, are:
   A. expressly
   B. amendment
   C. delegates
   D. sometimes

   *Answer:* D) sometimes. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:40 — “Officers sometimes included, and their usual duties, are:”

**1476.** `[C1117 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The other officers mentioned above are usually elected also, but the bylaws can provide for their _____.

   *Answer:* appointment — “The other officers mentioned above are usually elected also, but the bylaws can provide for their appointment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:41 — “The other officers mentioned above are usually elected also, but the bylaws can provide for their appointment.”

**1477.** `[C1117 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The other officers mentioned above are usually elected also, but the bylaws can provide for their _____.
   A. undebatable
   B. Procedures
   C. Reconsider
   D. appointment

   *Answer:* D) appointment. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:41 — “The other officers mentioned above are usually elected also, but the bylaws can provide for their appointment.”

**1478.** `[C1118 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An honorary office is in fact not an office but—like honorary membership—a _____ title that may be bestowed on members or nonmembers.

   *Answer:* complimentary — “An honorary office is in fact not an office but—like honorary membership—a complimentary title that may be bestowed on members or nonmembers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:42 — “An honorary office is in fact not an office but—like honorary membership—a complimentary title that may be bestowed on members or nonmembers.”

**1479.** `[C1118 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An honorary office is in fact not an office but—like honorary _____—a complimentary title that may be bestowed on members or nonmembers.
   A. Incidental
   B. membership
   C. Reconsider
   D. subsidiary

   *Answer:* B) membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:42 — “An honorary office is in fact not an office but—like honorary membership—a complimentary title that may be bestowed on members or nonmembers.”

**1480.** `[C1118 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An honorary officer—for example, an honorary president or an honorary treasurer—is often elected at the time of retirement from the _____ actual office, particularly when the person has filled it creditably for a long time.

   *Answer:* corresponding — “An honorary officer—for example, an honorary president or an honorary treasurer—is often elected at the time of retirement from the corresponding actual office, particularly when the person has filled it creditably for a long time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:42 — “An honorary officer—for example, an honorary president or an honorary treasurer—is often elected at the time of retirement from the corresponding actual office, particularly when the person has filled it creditably for a long time.”

**1481.** `[C1118 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there are to be honorary officers or honorary members, they must be _____ by the bylaws.
   A. subsidiary
   B. authorized
   C. introduced
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) authorized. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:42 — “If there are to be honorary officers or honorary members, they must be authorized by the bylaws.”

**1482.** `[C1118 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Like an honorary degree conferred by a college or _____, an honorary office or membership is perpetual—unless rescinded or unless its duration is limited by the bylaws.

   *Answer:* university — “Like an honorary degree conferred by a college or university, an honorary office or membership is perpetual—unless rescinded or unless its duration is limited by the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:42 — “Like an honorary degree conferred by a college or university, an honorary office or membership is perpetual—unless rescinded or unless its duration is limited by the bylaws.”

**1483.** `[C1119 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;

   *Answer:* executive secretary — “The term executive secretary, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:43 — “The term executive secretary, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;”

**1484.** `[C1119 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term executive secretary, or _____, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;
   A. Previously Adopted
   B. executive director
   C. Seconding a Motion
   D. Viva-Voce Election

   *Answer:* B) executive director. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:43 — “The term executive secretary, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;”

**1485.** `[C1119 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and unless _____ indicated the term is used in that sense in this book.
   A. Privilege
   B. otherwise
   C. secondary
   D. execution

   *Answer:* B) otherwise. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:43 — “and unless otherwise indicated the term is used in that sense in this book.”

**1486.** `[C1119 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In most _____, the executive secretary is employed by the board of directors under contract, but in some this officer is elected by the convention.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In most organizations, the executive secretary is employed by the board of directors under contract, but in some this officer is elected by the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:43 — “In most organizations, the executive secretary is employed by the board of directors under contract, but in some this officer is elected by the convention.”

**1487.** `[C1119 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and unless _____ indicated the term is used in that sense in this book.

   *Answer:* otherwise — “and unless otherwise indicated the term is used in that sense in this book.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:43 — “and unless otherwise indicated the term is used in that sense in this book.”

**1488.** `[C1120 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: He is sometimes ex officio the secretary of the executive committee (and sometimes of the board) and is responsible for seeing that the committee’s _____ are carried out.

   *Answer:* instructions — “He is sometimes ex officio the secretary of the executive committee (and sometimes of the board) and is responsible for seeing that the committee’s instructions are carried out.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:44 — “He is sometimes ex officio the secretary of the executive committee (and sometimes of the board) and is responsible for seeing that the committee’s instructions are carried out.”

**1489.** `[C1120 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: He is expected to recommend plans of work and to conduct the day-to-day business of the _____.
   A. Information
   B. presentation
   C. undebatable
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:44 — “He is expected to recommend plans of work and to conduct the day-to-day business of the organization.”

**1490.** `[C1120 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: He is often responsible for the work that would otherwise be carried out by an elected _____ secretary.

   *Answer:* corresponding — “He is often responsible for the work that would otherwise be carried out by an elected corresponding secretary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:44 — “He is often responsible for the work that would otherwise be carried out by an elected corresponding secretary.”

**1491.** `[C1120 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: He usually hires, fires, and _____ the salaries of other staff members with the approval of the board or executive committee, which may regulate this function by adopting personnel policies.
   A. Permission
   B. determines
   C. Particular
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* B) determines. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:44 — “He usually hires, fires, and determines the salaries of other staff members with the approval of the board or executive committee, which may regulate this function by adopting personnel policies.”

**1492.** `[C1120 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: He is sometimes ex officio the secretary of the _____ committee (and sometimes of the board) and is responsible for seeing that the committee’s instructions are carried out.

   *Answer:* executive — “He is sometimes ex officio the secretary of the executive committee (and sometimes of the board) and is responsible for seeing that the committee’s instructions are carried out.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:44 — “He is sometimes ex officio the secretary of the executive committee (and sometimes of the board) and is responsible for seeing that the committee’s instructions are carried out.”

**1493.** `[C1121 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ between the office of executive secretary and that of president depends on the duties and authority of these officers as defined in the bylaws.
   A. Permission
   B. relationship
   C. Organization
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) relationship. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:45 — “The relationship between the office of executive secretary and that of president depends on the duties and authority of these officers as defined in the bylaws.”

**1494.** `[C1121 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some _____, the executive and managerial function that would otherwise be exercised by the president is entirely split off and vested in the executive secretary.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In some organizations, the executive and managerial function that would otherwise be exercised by the president is entirely split off and vested in the executive secretary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:45 — “In some organizations, the executive and managerial function that would otherwise be exercised by the president is entirely split off and vested in the executive secretary.”

**1495.** `[C1121 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This arrangement leaves the president his duties as presiding officer and spokesman for the _____.
   A. Definitely
   B. Parliamentary
   C. organization
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:45 — “This arrangement leaves the president his duties as presiding officer and spokesman for the organization.”

**1496.** `[C1121 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any case, the president may not give orders to the executive secretary _____ unless the bylaws so authorize;

   *Answer:* independently — “In any case, the president may not give orders to the executive secretary independently unless the bylaws so authorize;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:45 — “In any case, the president may not give orders to the executive secretary independently unless the bylaws so authorize;”

**1497.** `[C1121 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: in the absence of such a _____, the executive secretary receives his direction from the board or executive committee.
   A. postponed
   B. committee
   C. Reporting
   D. provision

   *Answer:* D) provision. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:45 — “in the absence of such a provision, the executive secretary receives his direction from the board or executive committee.”

**1498.** `[C1122 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ is a consultant, commonly a professional, who advises the president and other officers, committees, and members on matters of parliamentary procedure.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “The parliamentarian is a consultant, commonly a professional, who advises the president and other officers, committees, and members on matters of parliamentary procedure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:46 — “The parliamentarian is a consultant, commonly a professional, who advises the president and other officers, committees, and members on matters of parliamentary procedure.”

**1499.** `[C1122 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ is a consultant, commonly a professional, who advises the president and other officers, committees, and members on matters of parliamentary procedure.
   A. interrupted
   B. parliamentarian
   C. immediately
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:46 — “The parliamentarian is a consultant, commonly a professional, who advises the president and other officers, committees, and members on matters of parliamentary procedure.”

**1500.** `[C1123 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A small local organization should rarely require the services of a _____, unless it undertakes a general revision of its bylaws;

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “A small local organization should rarely require the services of a parliamentarian, unless it undertakes a general revision of its bylaws;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:47 — “A small local organization should rarely require the services of a parliamentarian, unless it undertakes a general revision of its bylaws;”

**1501.** `[C1123 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but for large assemblies and conventions or _____ where the transaction of business is apt to be complex, it is advisable to engage one.
   A. immediately
   B. organizations
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* B) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:47 — “but for large assemblies and conventions or organizations where the transaction of business is apt to be complex, it is advisable to engage one.”

**1502.** `[C1123 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A small local organization should rarely require the services of a _____, unless it undertakes a general revision of its bylaws;
   A. immediately
   B. Parliamentary
   C. parliamentarian
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:47 — “A small local organization should rarely require the services of a parliamentarian, unless it undertakes a general revision of its bylaws;”

**1503.** `[C1123 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but for large assemblies and conventions or _____ where the transaction of business is apt to be complex, it is advisable to engage one.

   *Answer:* organizations — “but for large assemblies and conventions or organizations where the transaction of business is apt to be complex, it is advisable to engage one.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:47 — “but for large assemblies and conventions or organizations where the transaction of business is apt to be complex, it is advisable to engage one.”

**1504.** `[C1124 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a _____ is needed by an organization, the president should be free to appoint one in whom he has confidence.
   A. parliamentarian
   B. characteristics
   C. resolutions
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:48 — “If a parliamentarian is needed by an organization, the president should be free to appoint one in whom he has confidence.”

**1505.** `[C1124 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The board or society must approve any fee that will be _____, however.

   *Answer:* required — “The board or society must approve any fee that will be required, however.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:48 — “The board or society must approve any fee that will be required, however.”

**1506.** `[C1124 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a _____ is needed by an organization, the president should be free to appoint one in whom he has confidence.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “If a parliamentarian is needed by an organization, the president should be free to appoint one in whom he has confidence.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:48 — “If a parliamentarian is needed by an organization, the president should be free to appoint one in whom he has confidence.”

**1507.** `[C1124 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The board or society must approve any fee that will be _____, however.
   A. Question
   B. Resolved
   C. required
   D. Nominees

   *Answer:* C) required. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:48 — “The board or society must approve any fee that will be required, however.”

**1508.** `[C1125 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The president, knowing in advance the business to come before the assembly, should confer with the _____ before the meetings open, and during recesses, in order to anticipate any problems that may arise and to avoid, as much as possible, frequent consultation during the meetings.
   A. subsequently
   B. Vice-President
   C. parliamentarian
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* C) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:49 — “The president, knowing in advance the business to come before the assembly, should confer with the parliamentarian before the meetings open, and during recesses, in order to anticipate any problems that may arise and to avoid, as much as possible, frequent consultation during the meetings.”

**1509.** `[C1125 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The president, knowing in advance the business to come before the assembly, should confer with the _____ before the meetings open, and during recesses, in order to anticipate any problems that may arise and to avoid, as much as possible, frequent consultation during the meetings.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “The president, knowing in advance the business to come before the assembly, should confer with the parliamentarian before the meetings open, and during recesses, in order to anticipate any problems that may arise and to avoid, as much as possible, frequent consultation during the meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:49 — “The president, knowing in advance the business to come before the assembly, should confer with the parliamentarian before the meetings open, and during recesses, in order to anticipate any problems that may arise and to avoid, as much as possible, frequent consultation during the meetings.”

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “During a meeting the work of the parliamentarian should be limited to giving advice to the chair and, when requested, to any other member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:50 — “During a meeting the work of the parliamentarian should be limited to giving advice to the chair and, when requested, to any other member.”

   A. subsequently
   B. presentation
   C. consideration
   D. parliamentarian

   *Answer:* D) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:50 — “During a meeting the work of the parliamentarian should be limited to giving advice to the chair and, when requested, to any other member.”

**1512.** `[C1127 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This _____ will enable the chair to be in a position to act promptly at the correct time and be fully informed.
   A. Postpone
   B. practice
   C. Nominees
   D. Meetings

   *Answer:* B) practice. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:51 — “This practice will enable the chair to be in a position to act promptly at the correct time and be fully informed.”

**1513.** `[C1127 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In advising the chair, the _____ should not wait until asked for advice—that may be too late.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “In advising the chair, the parliamentarian should not wait until asked for advice—that may be too late.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:51 — “In advising the chair, the parliamentarian should not wait until asked for advice—that may be too late.”

**1514.** `[C1127 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This _____ will enable the chair to be in a position to act promptly at the correct time and be fully informed.

   D. specification

   *Answer:* B) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:51 — “In advising the chair, the parliamentarian should not wait until asked for advice—that may be too late.”

**1520.** `[C1129 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ should be assigned a seat next to the chair, so as to be convenient for consultation in a low voice, but the chair should try to avoid checking with the parliamentarian too frequently or too obviously.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “The parliamentarian should be assigned a seat next to the chair, so as to be convenient for consultation in a low voice, but the chair should try to avoid checking with the parliamentarian too frequently or too obviously.”. **1521.** `[C1129 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ should be assigned a seat next to the chair, so as to be convenient for consultation in a low voice, but the chair should try to avoid checking with the parliamentarian too frequently or too obviously.
   A. consideration
   B. Responsibilities

**1522.** `[C1130 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the _____ has expressed an opinion on a point, the chair has the duty to make the final ruling and, in doing so, has the right to follow the advice of the parliamentarian or to disregard it.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “After the parliamentarian has expressed an opinion on a point, the chair has the duty to make the final ruling and, in doing so, has the right to follow the advice of the parliamentarian or to disregard it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:54 — “After the parliamentarian has expressed an opinion on a point, the chair has the duty to make the final ruling and, in doing so, has the right to follow the advice of the parliamentarian or to disregard it.”

**1523.** `[C1130 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the _____ has expressed an opinion on a point, the chair has the duty to make the final ruling and, in doing so, has the right to follow the advice of the parliamentarian or to disregard it.
   A. Information
   B. interrupted
   C. specification
   D. parliamentarian

   *Answer:* D) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:54 — “After the parliamentarian has expressed an opinion on a point, the chair has the duty to make the final ruling and, in doing so, has the right to follow the advice of the parliamentarian or to disregard it.”

**1524.** `[C1131 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member of an assembly who acts as its _____ has the same duty as the presiding officer to maintain a position of impartiality, and therefore does not make motions, participate in debate, or vote on any question except in the case of a ballot vote.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “A member of an assembly who acts as its parliamentarian has the same duty as the presiding officer to maintain a position of impartiality, and therefore does not make motions, participate in debate, or vote on any question except in the case of a ballot vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:55 — “A member of an assembly who acts as its parliamentarian has the same duty as the presiding officer to maintain a position of impartiality, and therefore does not make motions, participate in debate, or vote on any question except in the case of a ballot vote.”

**1525.** `[C1131 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: He does not cast a deciding vote, even if his vote would affect the result, since that would interfere with the chair’s _____ of doing so.
   A. prerogative
   B. Organization
   C. Substitute
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) prerogative. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:55 — “He does not cast a deciding vote, even if his vote would affect the result, since that would interfere with the chair’s prerogative of doing so.”

**1526.** `[C1131 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member of an assembly who acts as its _____ has the same duty as the presiding officer to maintain a position of impartiality, and therefore does not make motions, participate in debate, or vote on any question except in the case of a ballot vote.
   A. Announcements
   B. parliamentarian
   C. constitution
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* B) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:55 — “A member of an assembly who acts as its parliamentarian has the same duty as the presiding officer to maintain a position of impartiality, and therefore does not make motions, participate in debate, or vote on any question except in the case of a ballot vote.”

**1527.** `[C1131 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: He does not cast a deciding vote, even if his vote would affect the result, since that would interfere with the chair’s _____ of doing so.

   *Answer:* prerogative — “He does not cast a deciding vote, even if his vote would affect the result, since that would interfere with the chair’s prerogative of doing so.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:55 — “He does not cast a deciding vote, even if his vote would affect the result, since that would interfere with the chair’s prerogative of doing so.”

**1528.** `[C1133 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The power to appoint or elect persons to any office or board carries with it the power to accept their _____, and also the power to fill any vacancy occurring in it, unless the bylaws expressly provide otherwise.

   *Answer:* resignations — “The power to appoint or elect persons to any office or board carries with it the power to accept their resignations, and also the power to fill any vacancy occurring in it, unless the bylaws expressly provide otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:57 — “The power to appoint or elect persons to any office or board carries with it the power to accept their resignations, and also the power to fill any vacancy occurring in it, unless the bylaws expressly provide otherwise.”

**1529.** `[C1133 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The power to appoint or elect persons to any office or board carries with it the power to accept their _____, and also the power to fill any vacancy occurring in it, unless the bylaws expressly provide otherwise.
   A. resignations
   B. immediately
   C. Incidental
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) resignations. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:57 — “The power to appoint or elect persons to any office or board carries with it the power to accept their resignations, and also the power to fill any vacancy occurring in it, unless the bylaws expressly provide otherwise.”

**1530.** `[C1134 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Notice of filling a vacancy in an office (_____ a vacancy in an executive board or executive committee) must always be given to the members of the body that will elect the person to fill it, unless the bylaws or special rules of order clearly provide otherwise.

   *Answer:* including — “Notice of filling a vacancy in an office (including a vacancy in an executive board or executive committee) must always be given to the members of the body that will elect the person to fill it, unless the bylaws or special rules of order clearly provide otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:58 — “Notice of filling a vacancy in an office (including a vacancy in an executive board or executive committee) must always be given to the members of the body that will elect the person to fill it, unless the bylaws or special rules of order clearly provide otherwise.”

**1531.** `[C1134 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Notice of filling a vacancy in an office (_____ a vacancy in an executive board or executive committee) must always be given to the members of the body that will elect the person to fill it, unless the bylaws or special rules of order clearly provide otherwise.
   A. including
   B. reception
   C. expressly
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* A) including. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:58 — “Notice of filling a vacancy in an office (including a vacancy in an executive board or executive committee) must always be given to the members of the body that will elect the person to fill it, unless the bylaws or special rules of order clearly provide otherwise.”

**1532.** `[C1135 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the _____, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.

   *Answer:* minutes — “The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:1 — “The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.”

**1533.** `[C1135 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the _____.
   A. Program
   B. Suspend
   C. Adopted
   D. journal

   *Answer:* D) journal. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:1 — “The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.”

**1534.** `[C1135 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The minutes should be kept in a _____ book or binder.
   A. substantial
   B. authorizes
   C. Definitely
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) substantial. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:1 — “The minutes should be kept in a substantial book or binder.”

**1535.** `[C1135 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The minutes should be kept in a _____ book or binder.

   *Answer:* substantial — “The minutes should be kept in a substantial book or binder.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:1 — “The minutes should be kept in a substantial book or binder.”

**1536.** `[C1136 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was _____ at the meeting, not what was said by the members.
   A. hours
   B. done
   C. Other
   D. adopt

   *Answer:* B) done. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:2 — “In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was said by the members.”

**1537.** `[C1136 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was _____ by the members.

   *Answer:* said — “In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was said by the members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:2 — “In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was said by the members.”

**1538.** `[C1136 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The minutes must never reflect the _____’s opinion, favorable or otherwise, on anything said or done.

   *Answer:* secretary — “The minutes must never reflect the secretary’s opinion, favorable or otherwise, on anything said or done.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:2 — “The minutes must never reflect the secretary’s opinion, favorable or otherwise, on anything said or done.”

**1539.** `[C1136 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The minutes must never reflect the _____’s opinion, favorable or otherwise, on anything said or done.
   A. paragraph
   B. secondary
   C. Precedent
   D. secretary

   *Answer:* D) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:2 — “The minutes must never reflect the secretary’s opinion, favorable or otherwise, on anything said or done.”

**1540.** `[C1137 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To modify the rules governing what is regularly to be included in the minutes requires adoption of a special rule of order, although a _____ vote may direct the inclusion of specific additional information in the minutes of a particular meeting.

   *Answer:* majority — “To modify the rules governing what is regularly to be included in the minutes requires adoption of a special rule of order, although a majority vote may direct the inclusion of specific additional information in the minutes of a particular meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:3 — “To modify the rules governing what is regularly to be included in the minutes requires adoption of a special rule of order, although a majority vote may direct the inclusion of specific additional information in the minutes of a particular meeting.”

**1541.** `[C1137 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To modify the rules governing what is regularly to be included in the minutes requires adoption of a special rule of order, although a majority vote may direct the inclusion of specific additional _____ in the minutes of a particular meeting.
   A. Unfinished
   B. information
   C. Reconsider
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:3 — “To modify the rules governing what is regularly to be included in the minutes requires adoption of a special rule of order, although a majority vote may direct the inclusion of specific additional information in the minutes of a particular meeting.”

**1542.** `[C1138 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly corresponding to those below):

   *Answer:* first paragraph — “The first paragraph of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly corresponding to those below):”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:4 — “The first paragraph of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly corresponding to those below):”

**1543.** `[C1138 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The first paragraph of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly _____ to those below):
   A. Vice-President
   B. consideration
   C. presentation
   D. corresponding

   *Answer:* D) corresponding. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:4 — “The first paragraph of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly corresponding to those below):”

**1544.** `[C1139 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The use by the secretary of a recording device can be of great benefit in preparing the minutes, but a _____ from it should never be used as the minutes themselves.

   *Answer:* transcription — “The use by the secretary of a recording device can be of great benefit in preparing the minutes, but a transcription from it should never be used as the minutes themselves.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:6 — “The use by the secretary of a recording device can be of great benefit in preparing the minutes, but a transcription from it should never be used as the minutes themselves.”

**1545.** `[C1139 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The use by the secretary of a recording device can be of great benefit in preparing the minutes, but a _____ from it should never be used as the minutes themselves.
   A. Information
   B. subsequently
   C. transcription
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) transcription. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:6 — “The use by the secretary of a recording device can be of great benefit in preparing the minutes, but a transcription from it should never be used as the minutes themselves.”

**1546.** `[C1140 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The words _____—although occasionally used —represent an older practice that is not essential in signing the minutes.
   A. of Order Newly Revised
   B. Methods of Appointment
   C. Respectfully submitted
   D. consideration as if in

   *Answer:* C) Respectfully submitted. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:7 — “The words Respectfully submitted—although occasionally used —represent an older practice that is not essential in signing the minutes.”

**1547.** `[C1140 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Minutes should be signed by the _____ and can also be signed, if the assembly wishes, by the president.

   *Answer:* secretary — “Minutes should be signed by the secretary and can also be signed, if the assembly wishes, by the president.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:7 — “Minutes should be signed by the secretary and can also be signed, if the assembly wishes, by the president.”

**1548.** `[C1140 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The words _____ submitted—although occasionally used —represent an older practice that is not essential in signing the minutes.
   A. adjournment
   B. Particular
   C. Respectfully
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) Respectfully. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:7 — “The words Respectfully submitted—although occasionally used —represent an older practice that is not essential in signing the minutes.”

**1549.** `[C1140 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Minutes should be signed by the _____ and can also be signed, if the assembly wishes, by the president.
   A. auditors’
   B. paragraph
   C. secretary
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* C) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:7 — “Minutes should be signed by the secretary and can also be signed, if the assembly wishes, by the president.”

**1550.** `[C1141 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The principles stated above are _____ in the following model form for minutes:
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Substitute
   C. illustrated
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) illustrated. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:8 — “The principles stated above are illustrated in the following model form for minutes:”

**1551.** `[C1141 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The principles stated above are _____ in the following model form for minutes:

   *Answer:* illustrated — “The principles stated above are illustrated in the following model form for minutes:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:8 — “The principles stated above are illustrated in the following model form for minutes:”

**1552.** `[C1142 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a session lasting longer than _____, the minutes of meetings held the preceding day are read and approved at the beginning of each day’s business after the first.
   A. house of
   B. one day
   C. at the
   D. Lay on

   *Answer:* B) one day. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:9 — “In a session lasting longer than one day, the minutes of meetings held the preceding day are read and approved at the beginning of each day’s business after the first.”

**1553.** `[C1142 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an _____ that holds regular meetings at frequent intervals, such as weekly, monthly, or quarterly, the minutes of each regular or special meeting are normally read and approved at the beginning of the next regular meeting, immediately after the call to order and any opening ceremonies.

   *Answer:* organization — “In an organization that holds regular meetings at frequent intervals, such as weekly, monthly, or quarterly, the minutes of each regular or special meeting are normally read and approved at the beginning of the next regular meeting, immediately after the call to order and any opening ceremonies.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:9 — “In an organization that holds regular meetings at frequent intervals, such as weekly, monthly, or quarterly, the minutes of each regular or special meeting are normally read and approved at the beginning of the next regular meeting, immediately after the call to order and any opening ceremonies.”

**1554.** `[C1142 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A special meeting does not approve minutes of a previous session unless the meeting was called for that purpose, which is not _____ the case.
   A. sustaining
   B. settlement
   C. ordinarily
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* C) ordinarily. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:9 — “A special meeting does not approve minutes of a previous session unless the meeting was called for that purpose, which is not ordinarily the case.”

**1555.** `[C1142 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a session lasting longer than one day, the minutes of meetings held the _____ day are read and approved at the beginning of each day’s business after the first.

   *Answer:* preceding — “In a session lasting longer than one day, the minutes of meetings held the preceding day are read and approved at the beginning of each day’s business after the first.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:9 — “In a session lasting longer than one day, the minutes of meetings held the preceding day are read and approved at the beginning of each day’s business after the first.”

**1556.** `[C1142 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In particular, an adjourned meeting of an ordinary society approves the minutes of the meeting that _____ the adjourned meeting;
   A. interrupted
   B. established
   C. authorizes
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:9 — “In particular, an adjourned meeting of an ordinary society approves the minutes of the meeting that established the adjourned meeting;”

**1557.** `[C1144 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by majority vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing _____ (34).

   *Answer:* Take from the Table — “If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by majority vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing Take from the Table (34).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:11 — “If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by majority vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing Take from the Table (34).”

**1558.** `[C1144 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by _____ vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing Take from the Table (34).
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:11 — “If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by majority vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing Take from the Table (34).”

**1559.** `[C1144 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the minutes are not thus taken up before _____, they are read and approved at the following meeting, before the later minutes are taken up.
   A. Particular
   B. adjournment
   C. Convention
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* B) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:11 — “If the minutes are not thus taken up before adjournment, they are read and approved at the following meeting, before the later minutes are taken up.”

**1560.** `[C1144 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to “dispense with the reading of the minutes” is not a request to omit their reading _____;

   *Answer:* altogether — “A motion to “dispense with the reading of the minutes” is not a request to omit their reading altogether;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:11 — “A motion to “dispense with the reading of the minutes” is not a request to omit their reading altogether;”

**1561.** `[C1144 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: it can be made at any time while the minutes are actually pending for approval _____ of whether the minutes have already been read or corrected in some respect.
   A. settlement
   B. Procedures
   C. regardless
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) regardless. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:11 — “it can be made at any time while the minutes are actually pending for approval regardless of whether the minutes have already been read or corrected in some respect.”

**1562.** `[C1145 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ to the rule that minutes are approved at the next regular meeting (or at the next meeting within the session) arise when the next meeting will not be held within a quarterly time interval, when the term of a specified portion of the membership will expire before the start of the next meeting, or when, as at the final meeting of a convention, the assembly will be dissolved at the close of the present meeting.

   *Answer:* Exceptions — “Exceptions to the rule that minutes are approved at the next regular meeting (or at the next meeting within the session) arise when the next meeting will not be held within a quarterly time interval, when the term of a specified portion of the membership will expire before the start of the next meeting, or when, as at the final meeting of a convention, the assembly will be dissolved at the close of the present meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:12 — “Exceptions to the rule that minutes are approved at the next regular meeting (or at the next meeting within the session) arise when the next meeting will not be held within a quarterly time interval, when the term of a specified portion of the membership will expire before the start of the next meeting, or when, as at the final meeting of a convention, the assembly will be dissolved at the close of the present meeting.”

**1563.** `[C1145 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ to the rule that minutes are approved at the next regular meeting (or at the next meeting within the session) arise when the next meeting will not be held within a quarterly time interval, when the term of a specified portion of the membership will expire before the start of the next meeting, or when, as at the final meeting of a convention, the assembly will be dissolved at the close of the present meeting.
   A. Exceptions
   B. subsidiary
   C. substitute
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* A) Exceptions. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:12 — “Exceptions to the rule that minutes are approved at the next regular meeting (or at the next meeting within the session) arise when the next meeting will not be held within a quarterly time interval, when the term of a specified portion of the membership will expire before the start of the next meeting, or when, as at the final meeting of a convention, the assembly will be dissolved at the close of the present meeting.”

**1564.** `[C1147 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the minutes are _____, the word Approved, with the secretary’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.

   *Answer:* approved — “When the minutes are approved, the word Approved, with the secretary’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:14 — “When the minutes are approved, the word Approved, with the secretary’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.”

**1565.** `[C1147 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the minutes are approved, the word Approved, with the _____’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.
   A. secretary
   B. rejecting
   C. debatable
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:14 — “When the minutes are approved, the word Approved, with the secretary’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.”

**1566.** `[C1147 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the minutes are approved with _____, the secretary should prepare a fully corrected version and distribute copies to the members as well as placing it in the minute book.

   *Answer:* corrections — “If the minutes are approved with corrections, the secretary should prepare a fully corrected version and distribute copies to the members as well as placing it in the minute book.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:14 — “If the minutes are approved with corrections, the secretary should prepare a fully corrected version and distribute copies to the members as well as placing it in the minute book.”

**1567.** `[C1147 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the minutes are approved with _____, the secretary should prepare a fully corrected version and distribute copies to the members as well as placing it in the minute book.
   A. corrections
   B. Incidental
   C. assemblies
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) corrections. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:14 — “If the minutes are approved with corrections, the secretary should prepare a fully corrected version and distribute copies to the members as well as placing it in the minute book.”

**1568.** `[C1148 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to _____ Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.

   *Answer:* Amend Something Previously — “If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:15 — “If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.”

**1569.** `[C1148 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously _____ (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.
   A. Example
   B. applied
   C. censure
   D. Adopted

   *Answer:* D) Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:15 — “If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.”

**1570.** `[C1149 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of an Assembly’s Proceedings.

   *Answer:* Publication — “Publication of an Assembly’s Proceedings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:16 — “Publication of an Assembly’s Proceedings.”

**1571.** `[C1149 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes a society wishes to have a full record of its proceedings made available to the public, and when such a record of the proceedings is to be published (in which case it is often called “proceedings,” “_____,” or the like), it frequently contains, in addition to the information described above for inclusion in the minutes, a list of the speakers on each side of every question, with an abstract or the text of each address.
   A. Organization
   B. transactions
   C. deliberative
   D. specification

   *Answer:* B) transactions. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:16 — “Sometimes a society wishes to have a full record of its proceedings made available to the public, and when such a record of the proceedings is to be published (in which case it is often called “proceedings,” “transactions,” or the like), it frequently contains, in addition to the information described above for inclusion in the minutes, a list of the speakers on each side of every question, with an abstract or the text of each address.”

**1572.** `[C1149 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases the _____ should have an assistant.

   *Answer:* secretary — “In such cases the secretary should have an assistant.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:16 — “In such cases the secretary should have an assistant.”

**1573.** `[C1149 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is desired, as in some conventions, to publish the proceedings in full, the secretary’s assistant should be a _____ reporter or recording technician.
   A. immediately
   B. stenographic
   C. interrupted
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) stenographic. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:16 — “When it is desired, as in some conventions, to publish the proceedings in full, the secretary’s assistant should be a stenographic reporter or recording technician.”

**1574.** `[C1149 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The presiding officer should then take _____ care that everyone to whom he assigns the floor is fully identified.

   *Answer:* particular — “The presiding officer should then take particular care that everyone to whom he assigns the floor is fully identified.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:16 — “The presiding officer should then take particular care that everyone to whom he assigns the floor is fully identified.”

**1575.** `[C1150 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In principle, all reports of officers in a society are incident to _____ duties that these officers have by virtue of provisions in the bylaws or other rules.

   *Answer:* administrative — “In principle, all reports of officers in a society are incident to administrative duties that these officers have by virtue of provisions in the bylaws or other rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:17 — “In principle, all reports of officers in a society are incident to administrative duties that these officers have by virtue of provisions in the bylaws or other rules.”

**1576.** `[C1150 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Strictly speaking, in a purely _____ assembly, the officers make no reports.
   A. specification
   B. deliberative
   C. Convention
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:17 — “Strictly speaking, in a purely deliberative assembly, the officers make no reports.”

**1577.** `[C1150 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In principle, all reports of officers in a society are incident to _____ duties that these officers have by virtue of provisions in the bylaws or other rules.
   A. administrative
   B. representatives
   C. president-elect
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* A) administrative. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:17 — “In principle, all reports of officers in a society are incident to administrative duties that these officers have by virtue of provisions in the bylaws or other rules.”

**1578.** `[C1150 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Strictly speaking, in a purely _____ assembly, the officers make no reports.

   *Answer:* deliberative — “Strictly speaking, in a purely deliberative assembly, the officers make no reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:17 — “Strictly speaking, in a purely deliberative assembly, the officers make no reports.”

**1579.** `[C1151 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an _____ society, the bylaws may require each of the principal officers to make a report of the year’s work at the annual meeting (9).

   *Answer:* organized — “In an organized society, the bylaws may require each of the principal officers to make a report of the year’s work at the annual meeting (9).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:18 — “In an organized society, the bylaws may require each of the principal officers to make a report of the year’s work at the annual meeting (9).”

**1580.** `[C1151 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At any meeting at which officers’ reports are made, they _____ follow the reading and approval of the minutes.
   A. Incidental
   B. immediately
   C. constitution
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:18 — “At any meeting at which officers’ reports are made, they immediately follow the reading and approval of the minutes.”

**1581.** `[C1151 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an _____ society, the bylaws may require each of the principal officers to make a report of the year’s work at the annual meeting (9).
   A. Signature
   B. paragraph
   C. Secretary
   D. organized

   *Answer:* D) organized. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:18 — “In an organized society, the bylaws may require each of the principal officers to make a report of the year’s work at the annual meeting (9).”

**1582.** `[C1151 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At any meeting at which officers’ reports are made, they _____ follow the reading and approval of the minutes.

   *Answer:* immediately — “At any meeting at which officers’ reports are made, they immediately follow the reading and approval of the minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:18 — “At any meeting at which officers’ reports are made, they immediately follow the reading and approval of the minutes.”

**1583.** `[C1152 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition to their annual reports, the president and _____ from time to time may wish or need to report on their activities in connection with administrative duties.
   A. representatives
   B. consideration
   C. characteristics
   D. vice-president

   *Answer:* D) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:19 — “In addition to their annual reports, the president and vice-president from time to time may wish or need to report on their activities in connection with administrative duties.”

**1584.** `[C1152 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such reports are usually for purposes of information only, but may sometimes contain _____ calling for action by the assembly.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “Such reports are usually for purposes of information only, but may sometimes contain recommendations calling for action by the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:19 — “Such reports are usually for purposes of information only, but may sometimes contain recommendations calling for action by the assembly.”

**1585.** `[C1152 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In either case, the reports should generally conform to the rules as to form, substance, and _____ that govern committee reports (51).
   A. disposition
   B. interrupted
   C. Reconsider
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) disposition. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:19 — “In either case, the reports should generally conform to the rules as to form, substance, and disposition that govern committee reports (51).”

**1586.** `[C1152 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition to their annual reports, the president and _____ from time to time may wish or need to report on their activities in connection with administrative duties.

   *Answer:* vice-president — “In addition to their annual reports, the president and vice-president from time to time may wish or need to report on their activities in connection with administrative duties.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:19 — “In addition to their annual reports, the president and vice-president from time to time may wish or need to report on their activities in connection with administrative duties.”

**1587.** `[C1152 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such reports are usually for purposes of information only, but may sometimes contain _____ calling for action by the assembly.
   A. President-Elect
   B. recommendations
   C. subsequently
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:19 — “Such reports are usually for purposes of information only, but may sometimes contain recommendations calling for action by the assembly.”

**1588.** `[C1153 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At each meeting of a society, the chair may ask for a “Treasurer’s report,” which may consist simply of a verbal statement of the cash balance on hand—or of this balance less _____ obligations.
   A. outstanding
   B. convention
   C. Convention
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) outstanding. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:20 — “At each meeting of a society, the chair may ask for a “Treasurer’s report,” which may consist simply of a verbal statement of the cash balance on hand—or of this balance less outstanding obligations.”

**1589.** `[C1153 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a report _____ no action by the assembly.

   *Answer:* requires — “Such a report requires no action by the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:20 — “Such a report requires no action by the assembly.”

**1590.** `[C1153 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At each meeting of a society, the chair may ask for a “Treasurer’s report,” which may consist simply of a verbal statement of the cash balance on hand—or of this balance less _____ obligations.

   *Answer:* outstanding — “At each meeting of a society, the chair may ask for a “Treasurer’s report,” which may consist simply of a verbal statement of the cash balance on hand—or of this balance less outstanding obligations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:20 — “At each meeting of a society, the chair may ask for a “Treasurer’s report,” which may consist simply of a verbal statement of the cash balance on hand—or of this balance less outstanding obligations.”

**1591.** `[C1153 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a report _____ no action by the assembly.
   A. chairman
   B. Previous
   C. Postpone
   D. requires

   *Answer:* D) requires. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:20 — “Such a report requires no action by the assembly.”

**1592.** `[C1154 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, the _____ is required to make a full financial report annually, and in some societies more often.

   *Answer:* treasurer — “In addition, the treasurer is required to make a full financial report annually, and in some societies more often.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:21 — “In addition, the treasurer is required to make a full financial report annually, and in some societies more often.”

**1593.** `[C1154 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such an annual report should always be _____.
   A. Motions
   B. Example
   C. Another
   D. audited

   *Answer:* D) audited. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:21 — “Such an annual report should always be audited.”

**1594.** `[C1154 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is compiled and dated as of the last day of the fiscal year, if there is one, or December 31 if no _____ financial year is stated in the bylaws.

   *Answer:* different — “It is compiled and dated as of the last day of the fiscal year, if there is one, or December 31 if no different financial year is stated in the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:21 — “It is compiled and dated as of the last day of the fiscal year, if there is one, or December 31 if no different financial year is stated in the bylaws.”

**1595.** `[C1154 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition, the _____ is required to make a full financial report annually, and in some societies more often.
   A. treasurer
   B. execution
   C. Reporting
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) treasurer. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:21 — “In addition, the treasurer is required to make a full financial report annually, and in some societies more often.”

**1596.** `[C1154 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such an annual report should always be _____.

   *Answer:* audited — “Such an annual report should always be audited.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:21 — “Such an annual report should always be audited.”

**1597.** `[C1155 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Form and content of the _____ report.

   *Answer:* financial — “Form and content of the financial report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:22 — “Form and content of the financial report.”

**1598.** `[C1155 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The best form for the financial report depends on _____ conditions, such as the kind and size of the society, the nature of its activities, the frequency of reporting, and so on.
   A. Substitute
   B. sustaining
   C. variations
   D. particular

   *Answer:* D) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:22 — “The best form for the financial report depends on particular conditions, such as the kind and size of the society, the nature of its activities, the frequency of reporting, and so on.”

**1599.** `[C1155 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The form used should be patterned after reports in similar _____.

   *Answer:* organizations — “The form used should be patterned after reports in similar organizations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:22 — “The form used should be patterned after reports in similar organizations.”

**1600.** `[C1155 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In any case, since the financial report is made for the _____ of the members, it should not contain details of dates and separate payments, which are a hindrance to the report’s being understood.
   A. immediately
   B. Reconsider
   C. Preparation
   D. information

   *Answer:* D) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:22 — “In any case, since the financial report is made for the information of the members, it should not contain details of dates and separate payments, which are a hindrance to the report’s being understood.”

**1601.** `[C1155 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Form and content of the _____ report.
   A. debatable
   B. Objection
   C. Signature
   D. financial

   *Answer:* D) financial. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:22 — “Form and content of the financial report.”

**1602.** `[C1156 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The brief model report below is in a form suitable for most small _____ whose financial affairs are simple and primarily involve cash.

   *Answer:* societies — “The brief model report below is in a form suitable for most small societies whose financial affairs are simple and primarily involve cash.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:23 — “The brief model report below is in a form suitable for most small societies whose financial affairs are simple and primarily involve cash.”

**1603.** `[C1156 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ whose finances are more involved, a double-entry set of books may be advisable or required.
   A. characteristics
   B. organizations
   C. recommendation
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:23 — “In organizations whose finances are more involved, a double-entry set of books may be advisable or required.”

**1604.** `[C1156 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a system should be set up with the assistance of an accountant, and the report would normally consist of a balance sheet showing the society’s assets, _____, and fund balance (or members’ equity) as well as an income statement similar to the report below without the opening and closing cash balances.

   *Answer:* liabilities — “Such a system should be set up with the assistance of an accountant, and the report would normally consist of a balance sheet showing the society’s assets, liabilities, and fund balance (or members’ equity) as well as an income statement similar to the report below without the opening and closing cash balances.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:23 — “Such a system should be set up with the assistance of an accountant, and the report would normally consist of a balance sheet showing the society’s assets, liabilities, and fund balance (or members’ equity) as well as an income statement similar to the report below without the opening and closing cash balances.”

**1605.** `[C1156 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Other statements may be included as needed, such as a statement of changes in members’ equity, a statement of sources and _____ of funds, and a cash forecast.
   A. convention
   B. authorizes
   C. application
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) application. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:23 — “Other statements may be included as needed, such as a statement of changes in members’ equity, a statement of sources and application of funds, and a cash forecast.”

**1606.** `[C1156 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a system should be set up with the assistance of an _____, and the report would normally consist of a balance sheet showing the society’s assets, liabilities, and fund balance (or members’ equity) as well as an income statement similar to the report below without the opening and closing cash balances.

   *Answer:* accountant — “Such a system should be set up with the assistance of an accountant, and the report would normally consist of a balance sheet showing the society’s assets, liabilities, and fund balance (or members’ equity) as well as an income statement similar to the report below without the opening and closing cash balances.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:23 — “Such a system should be set up with the assistance of an accountant, and the report would normally consist of a balance sheet showing the society’s assets, liabilities, and fund balance (or members’ equity) as well as an income statement similar to the report below without the opening and closing cash balances.”

**1607.** `[C1157 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No action of _____ by the assembly is required—or proper—on a financial report of the treasurer unless it is of sufficient importance, as an annual report, to be referred to auditors.

   *Answer:* acceptance — “No action of acceptance by the assembly is required—or proper—on a financial report of the treasurer unless it is of sufficient importance, as an annual report, to be referred to auditors.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:24 — “No action of acceptance by the assembly is required—or proper—on a financial report of the treasurer unless it is of sufficient importance, as an annual report, to be referred to auditors.”
