**1608.** `[C1157 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No action of acceptance by the assembly is required—or proper—on a financial report of the treasurer unless it is of sufficient importance, as an annual report, to be referred to _____.
   A. assembly
   B. auditors
   C. Approved
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* B) auditors. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:24 — “No action of acceptance by the assembly is required—or proper—on a financial report of the treasurer unless it is of sufficient importance, as an annual report, to be referred to auditors.”

**1609.** `[C1158 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the amounts involved are very large and the reports complicated, or the _____’s contributors or others require it, it is desirable to have the audits made by independent certified accountants.
   A. Announcements
   B. deliberative
   C. Preparation
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:25 — “When the amounts involved are very large and the reports complicated, or the organization’s contributors or others require it, it is desirable to have the audits made by independent certified accountants.”

**1610.** `[C1158 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But in ordinary societies and those in which the expense of a _____ cannot be justified, it is practical to have the financial reports audited by an auditing committee of two or more members of the society —appointed in advance if there is not a standing auditing committee.

   *Answer:* professional — “But in ordinary societies and those in which the expense of a professional cannot be justified, it is practical to have the financial reports audited by an auditing committee of two or more members of the society —appointed in advance if there is not a standing auditing committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:25 — “But in ordinary societies and those in which the expense of a professional cannot be justified, it is practical to have the financial reports audited by an auditing committee of two or more members of the society —appointed in advance if there is not a standing auditing committee.”

**1611.** `[C1158 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some _____ the financial reports are audited by elected officers known as “trustees.” Where professional examination is desired but the organization does not require a full audit, a “review” (as distinct from a mere “compilation”) by an accountant may supply sufficient testing and verification to satisfy the organization.
   A. specification
   B. organizations
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* B) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:25 — “In some organizations the financial reports are audited by elected officers known as “trustees.” Where professional examination is desired but the organization does not require a full audit, a “review” (as distinct from a mere “compilation”) by an accountant may supply sufficient testing and verification to satisfy the organization.”

**1612.** `[C1158 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the auditors’ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the treasurer can simply read out this _____ as he concludes the presentation of his own report.

   *Answer:* certification — “If the auditors’ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the treasurer can simply read out this certification as he concludes the presentation of his own report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:25 — “If the auditors’ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the treasurer can simply read out this certification as he concludes the presentation of his own report.”

**1613.** `[C1158 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the auditors’ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the _____ can simply read out this certification as he concludes the presentation of his own report.
   A. reception
   B. execution
   C. treasurer
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* C) treasurer. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:25 — “If the auditors’ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the treasurer can simply read out this certification as he concludes the presentation of his own report.”

**1614.** `[C1159 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the treasurer presents an unaudited annual report or other financial report that the bylaws require to be audited, and if there is a standing auditing committee or if auditors have already been chosen in some other manner, the chair, without waiting for a motion when the treasurer has finished reading his report, _____ says, “The report is referred to the Auditing Committee [or “to the auditors,” or “to the Trustees for audit”].” If no auditors have been chosen, the proper procedure is to adopt a motion to refer the report to an auditing committee to be appointed by one of the methods described in 50.

   *Answer:* immediately — “If the treasurer presents an unaudited annual report or other financial report that the bylaws require to be audited, and if there is a standing auditing committee or if auditors have already been chosen in some other manner, the chair, without waiting for a motion when the treasurer has finished reading his report, immediately says, “The report is referred to the Auditing Committee [or “to the auditors,” or “to the Trustees for audit”].” If no auditors have been chosen, the proper procedure is to adopt a motion to refer the report to an auditing committee to be appointed by one of the methods described in 50.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:26 — “If the treasurer presents an unaudited annual report or other financial report that the bylaws require to be audited, and if there is a standing auditing committee or if auditors have already been chosen in some other manner, the chair, without waiting for a motion when the treasurer has finished reading his report, immediately says, “The report is referred to the Auditing Committee [or “to the auditors,” or “to the Trustees for audit”].” If no auditors have been chosen, the proper procedure is to adopt a motion to refer the report to an auditing committee to be appointed by one of the methods described in 50.”

**1615.** `[C1159 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the treasurer presents an unaudited annual report or other financial report that the bylaws require to be audited, and if there is a standing auditing committee or if auditors have already been chosen in some other manner, the chair, without waiting for a motion when the treasurer has finished reading his report, _____ says, “The report is referred to the Auditing Committee [or “to the auditors,” or “to the Trustees for audit”].” If no auditors have been chosen, the proper procedure is to adopt a motion to refer the report to an auditing committee to be appointed by one of the methods described in 50.
   A. resolutions
   B. Information
   C. immediately
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:26 — “If the treasurer presents an unaudited annual report or other financial report that the bylaws require to be audited, and if there is a standing auditing committee or if auditors have already been chosen in some other manner, the chair, without waiting for a motion when the treasurer has finished reading his report, immediately says, “The report is referred to the Auditing Committee [or “to the auditors,” or “to the Trustees for audit”].” If no auditors have been chosen, the proper procedure is to adopt a motion to refer the report to an auditing committee to be appointed by one of the methods described in 50.”

**1616.** `[C1160 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Other officers as may be _____ in the bylaws, such as a historian or a librarian, may also have occasion to report to the assembly.
   A. prescribed
   B. privileged
   C. incidental
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* A) prescribed. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:27 — “Other officers as may be prescribed in the bylaws, such as a historian or a librarian, may also have occasion to report to the assembly.”

**1617.** `[C1160 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: These reports are usually made annually and, like those of the executive officers, are generally for purposes of _____ only.

   *Answer:* information — “These reports are usually made annually and, like those of the executive officers, are generally for purposes of information only.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:27 — “These reports are usually made annually and, like those of the executive officers, are generally for purposes of information only.”

**1618.** `[C1160 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: They can, however, contain _____ upon which it is hoped the assembly will act.
   A. interrupted
   B. Organization
   C. recommendations
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:27 — “They can, however, contain recommendations upon which it is hoped the assembly will act.”

**1619.** `[C1160 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the report is to become a permanent official document of the _____, it must be formally adopted by the assembly.

   *Answer:* organization — “If the report is to become a permanent official document of the organization, it must be formally adopted by the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:27 — “If the report is to become a permanent official document of the organization, it must be formally adopted by the assembly.”

**1620.** `[C1160 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, for example, historical accounts prepared by the historian do not become part of the official history of the society until the assembly formally adopts them, with any desired changes, after their _____ by the historian.
   A. Organization
   B. Parliamentary
   C. presentation
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:27 — “Thus, for example, historical accounts prepared by the historian do not become part of the official history of the society until the assembly formally adopts them, with any desired changes, after their presentation by the historian.”

---

## Domain 5: Voting, Nominations, and Elections

**1.** `[C0946 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and when the term _____ is used without qualification—as in the case of the basic requirement—it means more than half of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.

   *Answer:* majority vote — “and when the term majority vote is used without qualification—as in the case of the basic requirement—it means more than half of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:1 — “and when the term majority vote is used without qualification—as in the case of the basic requirement—it means more than half of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”

**2.** `[C0946 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The word _____ means “more than half”;
   A. Question
   B. Nominees
   C. Previous
   D. majority

   *Answer:* D) majority. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:1 — “The word majority means “more than half”;”

**3.** `[C0946 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and when the term majority vote is used without _____—as in the case of the basic requirement—it means more than half of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.

   *Answer:* qualification — “and when the term majority vote is used without qualification—as in the case of the basic requirement—it means more than half of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:1 — “and when the term majority vote is used without qualification—as in the case of the basic requirement—it means more than half of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”

**4.** `[C0946 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example (assuming that there are no voters having fractions of a vote, as may occur in some _____):
   A. Substitute
   B. conventions
   C. committees
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:1 — “For example (assuming that there are no voters having fractions of a vote, as may occur in some conventions):”

**5.** `[C0946 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The word _____ means “more than half”;

   *Answer:* majority — “The word majority means “more than half”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:1 — “The word majority means “more than half”;”

**6.** `[C0947 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of the basis required, a decision can be validly made only when a quorum is present (unless otherwise specified in the rules, as in the case of certain procedural actions);

   *Answer:* Regardless — “Regardless of the basis required, a decision can be validly made only when a quorum is present (unless otherwise specified in the rules, as in the case of certain procedural actions);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:2 — “Regardless of the basis required, a decision can be validly made only when a quorum is present (unless otherwise specified in the rules, as in the case of certain procedural actions);”

**7.** `[C0947 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of the basis required, a decision can be validly made only when a quorum is present (unless otherwise specified in the rules, as in the case of certain procedural actions);
   A. Unfinished
   B. Regardless
   C. privileged
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* B) Regardless. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:2 — “Regardless of the basis required, a decision can be validly made only when a quorum is present (unless otherwise specified in the rules, as in the case of certain procedural actions);”

**8.** `[C0948 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.

   *Answer:* two-thirds vote — “A two-thirds vote—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:3 — “A two-thirds vote—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”

**9.** `[C0948 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ vote—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* C) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:3 — “A two-thirds vote—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”

**10.** `[C0948 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example (assuming that there are no _____ of votes):
   A. postponed
   B. fractions
   C. Crestwood
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* B) fractions. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:3 — “For example (assuming that there are no fractions of votes):”

**11.** `[C0948 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example (assuming that there are no _____ of votes):

   *Answer:* fractions — “For example (assuming that there are no fractions of votes):”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:3 — “For example (assuming that there are no fractions of votes):”

**12.** `[C0949 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a compromise between the rights of the individual and the rights of the assembly, the principle has been established that a _____ vote is required to adopt any motion that: (a) suspends or modifies a rule of order previously adopted;

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “As a compromise between the rights of the individual and the rights of the assembly, the principle has been established that a two-thirds vote is required to adopt any motion that: (a) suspends or modifies a rule of order previously adopted;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:4 — “As a compromise between the rights of the individual and the rights of the assembly, the principle has been established that a two-thirds vote is required to adopt any motion that: (a) suspends or modifies a rule of order previously adopted;”

**13.** `[C0949 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As a compromise between the rights of the individual and the rights of the assembly, the principle has been _____ that a two-thirds vote is required to adopt any motion that: (a) suspends or modifies a rule of order previously adopted;
   A. established
   B. constitution
   C. requirement
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:4 — “As a compromise between the rights of the individual and the rights of the assembly, the principle has been established that a two-thirds vote is required to adopt any motion that: (a) suspends or modifies a rule of order previously adopted;”

**14.** `[C0949 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (b) prevents the introduction of a question for _____;

   *Answer:* consideration — “(b) prevents the introduction of a question for consideration;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:4 — “(b) prevents the introduction of a question for consideration;”

**15.** `[C0949 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (c) closes, limits, or _____ the limits of debate;
   A. extends
   B. adjourn
   C. applied
   D. Session

   *Answer:* A) extends. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:4 — “(c) closes, limits, or extends the limits of debate;”

**16.** `[C0949 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (d) closes _____ or the polls, or otherwise limits the freedom of nominating or voting;

   *Answer:* nominations — “(d) closes nominations or the polls, or otherwise limits the freedom of nominating or voting;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:4 — “(d) closes nominations or the polls, or otherwise limits the freedom of nominating or voting;”

**17.** `[C0950 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ whether a question has obtained two thirds of the votes cast, the chair takes a rising vote (or, in a very small assembly, if the chair prefers and no one objects, a vote by show of hands), and it is the chair’s duty to obtain a count of the vote whenever he is in doubt concerning the result.

   *Answer:* determining — “In determining whether a question has obtained two thirds of the votes cast, the chair takes a rising vote (or, in a very small assembly, if the chair prefers and no one objects, a vote by show of hands), and it is the chair’s duty to obtain a count of the vote whenever he is in doubt concerning the result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:5 — “In determining whether a question has obtained two thirds of the votes cast, the chair takes a rising vote (or, in a very small assembly, if the chair prefers and no one objects, a vote by show of hands), and it is the chair’s duty to obtain a count of the vote whenever he is in doubt concerning the result.”

**18.** `[C0950 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ whether a question has obtained two thirds of the votes cast, the chair takes a rising vote (or, in a very small assembly, if the chair prefers and no one objects, a vote by show of hands), and it is the chair’s duty to obtain a count of the vote whenever he is in doubt concerning the result.
   A. determining
   B. Particular
   C. Convention
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) determining. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:5 — “In determining whether a question has obtained two thirds of the votes cast, the chair takes a rising vote (or, in a very small assembly, if the chair prefers and no one objects, a vote by show of hands), and it is the chair’s duty to obtain a count of the vote whenever he is in doubt concerning the result.”

**19.** `[C0951 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly c_____ful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.

   *Answer:* are — “In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly careful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:6 — “In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly careful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.”

**20.** `[C0951 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The reason is that—whatever may be the true result in view of the closeness of the vote in such a case—those declared the losers are no more than approximately one third of those voting, and therefore cannot command the _____ necessary to order the vote counted.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:6 — “The reason is that—whatever may be the true result in view of the closeness of the vote in such a case—those declared the losers are no more than approximately one third of those voting, and therefore cannot command the majority necessary to order the vote counted.”

**21.** `[C0951 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair can obtain a count of the vote initially if it appears —when those in the _____ rise—that the result will be close;

   *Answer:* affirmative — “The chair can obtain a count of the vote initially if it appears —when those in the affirmative rise—that the result will be close;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:6 — “The chair can obtain a count of the vote initially if it appears —when those in the affirmative rise—that the result will be close;”

**22.** `[C0951 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or he can retake it as a counted rising vote if he is _____ in doubt.
   A. Secretary
   B. president
   C. amendment
   D. afterward

   *Answer:* D) afterward. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:6 — “or he can retake it as a counted rising vote if he is afterward in doubt.”

**23.** `[C0951 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be _____ careful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.

   *Answer:* particularly — “In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly careful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:6 — “In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly careful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.”

**24.** `[C0952 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and (2) the set of members to which the proportion applies—which (a) when not stated, is always the number of members _____, but (b) can be specified by rule as the number of members present, the total membership, or some other grouping.

   *Answer:* present and voting — “and (2) the set of members to which the proportion applies—which (a) when not stated, is always the number of members present and voting, but (b) can be specified by rule as the number of members present, the total membership, or some other grouping.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:7 — “and (2) the set of members to which the proportion applies—which (a) when not stated, is always the number of members present and voting, but (b) can be specified by rule as the number of members present, the total membership, or some other grouping.”

**25.** `[C0952 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By modifying the concepts of a _____ vote and a twothirds vote, other bases for determining a voting result can be defined and are sometimes prescribed by rule.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:7 — “By modifying the concepts of a majority vote and a twothirds vote, other bases for determining a voting result can be defined and are sometimes prescribed by rule.”

**26.** `[C0952 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By modifying the concepts of a majority vote and a twothirds vote, other bases for _____ a voting result can be defined and are sometimes prescribed by rule.

   *Answer:* determining — “By modifying the concepts of a majority vote and a twothirds vote, other bases for determining a voting result can be defined and are sometimes prescribed by rule.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:7 — “By modifying the concepts of a majority vote and a twothirds vote, other bases for determining a voting result can be defined and are sometimes prescribed by rule.”

**27.** `[C0952 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Two elements enter into the _____ of such bases for decision: (1) the proportion that must concur—as a majority, two thirds, three fourths, etc.;
   A. definition
   B. Substitute
   C. assemblies
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* A) definition. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:7 — “Two elements enter into the definition of such bases for decision: (1) the proportion that must concur—as a majority, two thirds, three fourths, etc.;”

**28.** `[C0952 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Two elements enter into the definition of such bases for decision: (1) the _____ that must concur—as a majority, two thirds, three fourths, etc.;

   *Answer:* proportion — “Two elements enter into the definition of such bases for decision: (1) the proportion that must concur—as a majority, two thirds, three fourths, etc.;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:7 — “Two elements enter into the definition of such bases for decision: (1) the proportion that must concur—as a majority, two thirds, three fourths, etc.;”

**29.** `[C0953 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assume, for example, that at a meeting of a society with a total membership of 150 and a quorum of 10, there are _____ present, of whom 25 participate in a given counted vote (taken by rising, by show of hands, by roll call, or by ballot).

   *Answer:* 30 members — “Assume, for example, that at a meeting of a society with a total membership of 150 and a quorum of 10, there are 30 members present, of whom 25 participate in a given counted vote (taken by rising, by show of hands, by roll call, or by ballot).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:8 — “Assume, for example, that at a meeting of a society with a total membership of 150 and a quorum of 10, there are 30 members present, of whom 25 participate in a given counted vote (taken by rising, by show of hands, by roll call, or by ballot).”

**30.** `[C0953 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assume, for example, that at a meeting of a society with a total membership of 150 and a quorum of 10, there are 30 members present, of whom 25 _____ in a given counted vote (taken by rising, by show of hands, by roll call, or by ballot).
   A. participate
   B. resolutions
   C. Definitely
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) participate. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:8 — “Assume, for example, that at a meeting of a society with a total membership of 150 and a quorum of 10, there are 30 members present, of whom 25 participate in a given counted vote (taken by rising, by show of hands, by roll call, or by ballot).”

**31.** `[C0954 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Regarding these bases for _____ a voting result, the following points should be noted:

   *Answer:* determining — “Regarding these bases for determining a voting result, the following points should be noted:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:9 — “Regarding these bases for determining a voting result, the following points should be noted:”

**32.** `[C0954 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Regarding these bases for _____ a voting result, the following points should be noted:
   A. determining
   B. Unfinished
   C. Permission
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) determining. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:9 — “Regarding these bases for determining a voting result, the following points should be noted:”

**33.** `[C0955 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of _____ for specified types of action.

   *Answer:* previous notice — “Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of previous notice for specified types of action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:10 — “Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of previous notice for specified types of action.”

**34.** `[C0955 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whenever it is desired that the basis for decision be other than a majority vote or (where the normal rules of _____ law require it) a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, the desired basis should be precisely defined in the bylaws or in a special rule of order.
   A. constitution
   B. parliamentary
   C. undebatable
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:10 — “Whenever it is desired that the basis for decision be other than a majority vote or (where the normal rules of parliamentary law require it) a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, the desired basis should be precisely defined in the bylaws or in a special rule of order.”

**35.** `[C0955 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a _____ of previous notice for specified types of action.

   *Answer:* requirement — “Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of previous notice for specified types of action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:10 — “Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of previous notice for specified types of action.”

**36.** `[C0955 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whenever it is desired that the basis for decision be other than a majority vote or (where the normal rules of _____ law require it) a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, the desired basis should be precisely defined in the bylaws or in a special rule of order.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Whenever it is desired that the basis for decision be other than a majority vote or (where the normal rules of parliamentary law require it) a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, the desired basis should be precisely defined in the bylaws or in a special rule of order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:10 — “Whenever it is desired that the basis for decision be other than a majority vote or (where the normal rules of parliamentary law require it) a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, the desired basis should be precisely defined in the bylaws or in a special rule of order.”

**37.** `[C0956 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;

   *Answer:* plurality vote — “A plurality vote is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:11 — “A plurality vote is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;”

**38.** `[C0956 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ vote is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;

   *Answer:* plurality — “A plurality vote is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:11 — “A plurality vote is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;”

**39.** `[C0956 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If such a rule is to apply to the election of officers, it must be _____ in the bylaws.

   *Answer:* prescribed — “If such a rule is to apply to the election of officers, it must be prescribed in the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:11 — “If such a rule is to apply to the election of officers, it must be prescribed in the bylaws.”

**40.** `[C0956 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A rule that a plurality shall elect is unlikely to be in the best interests of the average _____.
   A. interrupted
   B. Information
   C. organization
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:11 — “A rule that a plurality shall elect is unlikely to be in the best interests of the average organization.”

**41.** `[C0956 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If such a rule is to apply to the election of officers, it must be _____ in the bylaws.
   A. Particular
   B. prescribed
   C. persuasive
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) prescribed. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:11 — “If such a rule is to apply to the election of officers, it must be prescribed in the bylaws.”

**42.** `[C0957 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, in a case where a _____ vote is required, he can vote either to cause or to block the attainment of the necessary two thirds.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “or, in a case where a two-thirds vote is required, he can vote either to cause or to block the attainment of the necessary two thirds.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:12 — “or, in a case where a two-thirds vote is required, he can vote either to cause or to block the attainment of the necessary two thirds.”

**43.** `[C0957 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In all other cases the _____ officer, if a member of the assembly, can (but is not obliged to) vote whenever his vote will affect the result—that is, he can vote either to break or to cause a tie;
   A. expressly
   B. Reporting
   C. Privilege
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* D) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:12 — “In all other cases the presiding officer, if a member of the assembly, can (but is not obliged to) vote whenever his vote will affect the result—that is, he can vote either to break or to cause a tie;”

**44.** `[C0957 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, in a case where a _____ vote is required, he can vote either to cause or to block the attainment of the necessary two thirds.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:12 — “or, in a case where a two-thirds vote is required, he can vote either to cause or to block the attainment of the necessary two thirds.”

**45.** `[C0957 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In all other cases the _____ officer, if a member of the assembly, can (but is not obliged to) vote whenever his vote will affect the result—that is, he can vote either to break or to cause a tie;

   *Answer:* presiding — “In all other cases the presiding officer, if a member of the assembly, can (but is not obliged to) vote whenever his vote will affect the result—that is, he can vote either to break or to cause a tie;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:12 — “In all other cases the presiding officer, if a member of the assembly, can (but is not obliged to) vote whenever his vote will affect the result—that is, he can vote either to break or to cause a tie;”

**46.** `[C0958 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an appeal from the decision of the chair, a tie vote sustains the chair’s decision, even though his vote created the tie, on the principle that the decision of the chair can be reversed only by a _____.

   *Answer:* majority — “In an appeal from the decision of the chair, a tie vote sustains the chair’s decision, even though his vote created the tie, on the principle that the decision of the chair can be reversed only by a majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:13 — “In an appeal from the decision of the chair, a tie vote sustains the chair’s decision, even though his vote created the tie, on the principle that the decision of the chair can be reversed only by a majority.”

**47.** `[C0958 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an appeal from the decision of the chair, a tie vote sustains the chair’s decision, even though his vote created the tie, on the _____ that the decision of the chair can be reversed only by a majority.
   A. principle
   B. secondary
   C. rejecting
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* A) principle. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:13 — “In an appeal from the decision of the chair, a tie vote sustains the chair’s decision, even though his vote created the tie, on the principle that the decision of the chair can be reversed only by a majority.”

**48.** `[C0959 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member of a society who is in arrears in payment of his dues, but who has not been formally dropped from the membership rolls and is not under a _____ suspension, retains the full rights of a voting member and is entitled to vote except as the bylaws may otherwise provide.

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “A member of a society who is in arrears in payment of his dues, but who has not been formally dropped from the membership rolls and is not under a disciplinary suspension, retains the full rights of a voting member and is entitled to vote except as the bylaws may otherwise provide.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:1 — “A member of a society who is in arrears in payment of his dues, but who has not been formally dropped from the membership rolls and is not under a disciplinary suspension, retains the full rights of a voting member and is entitled to vote except as the bylaws may otherwise provide.”

**49.** `[C0959 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member of a society who is in arrears in payment of his dues, but who has not been formally dropped from the membership rolls and is not under a _____ suspension, retains the full rights of a voting member and is entitled to vote except as the bylaws may otherwise provide.
   A. Permission
   B. disciplinary
   C. constitution
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:1 — “A member of a society who is in arrears in payment of his dues, but who has not been formally dropped from the membership rolls and is not under a disciplinary suspension, retains the full rights of a voting member and is entitled to vote except as the bylaws may otherwise provide.”

**50.** `[C0960 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, in a convention, a person selected as delegate by more than one constituent body may cast only _____.
   A. one vote
   B. order of
   C. a Session
   D. In Brief

   *Answer:* A) one vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:2 — “For example, in a convention, a person selected as delegate by more than one constituent body may cast only one vote.”

**51.** `[C0960 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is a fundamental principle of _____ law that each person who is a member of a deliberative assembly is entitled to one—and only one—vote on a question.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “It is a fundamental principle of parliamentary law that each person who is a member of a deliberative assembly is entitled to one—and only one—vote on a question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:2 — “It is a fundamental principle of parliamentary law that each person who is a member of a deliberative assembly is entitled to one—and only one—vote on a question.”

**52.** `[C0960 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This is true even if a person is elected or _____ to more than one position, each of which would entitle the holder to a vote.
   A. Precedent
   B. appointed
   C. postponed
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* B) appointed. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:2 — “This is true even if a person is elected or appointed to more than one position, each of which would entitle the holder to a vote.”

**53.** `[C0960 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, in a convention, a person selected as delegate by more than one _____ body may cast only one vote.

   *Answer:* constituent — “For example, in a convention, a person selected as delegate by more than one constituent body may cast only one vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:2 — “For example, in a convention, a person selected as delegate by more than one constituent body may cast only one vote.”

**54.** `[C0960 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An individual member’s right to vote may not be _____ to another person (for example, by the use of proxies).
   A. Reconsider
   B. transferred
   C. Indefinitely
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) transferred. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:2 — “An individual member’s right to vote may not be transferred to another person (for example, by the use of proxies).”

**55.** `[C0961 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although it is the duty of every member who has an opinion on a question to express it by his vote, he can abstain, since he cannot be _____ to vote.
   A. extending
   B. exhausted
   C. compelled
   D. committee

   *Answer:* C) compelled. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:3 — “Although it is the duty of every member who has an opinion on a question to express it by his vote, he can abstain, since he cannot be compelled to vote.”

**56.** `[C0961 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By the same token, when an office or position is to be filled by a number of members, as in the case of a committee, or _____ on a board, a member may partially abstain by voting for less than all of those for whom he is entitled to vote.

   *Answer:* positions — “By the same token, when an office or position is to be filled by a number of members, as in the case of a committee, or positions on a board, a member may partially abstain by voting for less than all of those for whom he is entitled to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:3 — “By the same token, when an office or position is to be filled by a number of members, as in the case of a committee, or positions on a board, a member may partially abstain by voting for less than all of those for whom he is entitled to vote.”

**57.** `[C0961 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although it is the duty of every member who has an opinion on a question to express it by his vote, he can abstain, since he cannot be _____ to vote.

   *Answer:* compelled — “Although it is the duty of every member who has an opinion on a question to express it by his vote, he can abstain, since he cannot be compelled to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:3 — “Although it is the duty of every member who has an opinion on a question to express it by his vote, he can abstain, since he cannot be compelled to vote.”

**58.** `[C0961 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By the same token, when an office or position is to be filled by a number of members, as in the case of a committee, or _____ on a board, a member may partially abstain by voting for less than all of those for whom he is entitled to vote.
   A. positions
   B. auditors’
   C. president
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* A) positions. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:3 — “By the same token, when an office or position is to be filled by a number of members, as in the case of a committee, or positions on a board, a member may partially abstain by voting for less than all of those for whom he is entitled to vote.”

**59.** `[C0962 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Abstaining from Voting on a Question of Direct Personal _____.

   *Answer:* Interest — “Abstaining from Voting on a Question of Direct Personal Interest.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:4 — “Abstaining from Voting on a Question of Direct Personal Interest.”

**60.** `[C0962 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ from Voting on a Question of Direct Personal Interest.
   A. convention
   B. Reconsider
   C. Abstaining
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) Abstaining. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:4 — “Abstaining from Voting on a Question of Direct Personal Interest.”

**61.** `[C0962 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No member should vote on a question in which he has a direct personal or pecuniary interest not common to other members of the _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “No member should vote on a question in which he has a direct personal or pecuniary interest not common to other members of the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:4 — “No member should vote on a question in which he has a direct personal or pecuniary interest not common to other members of the organization.”

**62.** `[C0962 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, no member can be compelled to refrain from voting in such _____.
   A. deliberative
   B. specification
   C. recommendation
   D. circumstances

   *Answer:* D) circumstances. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:4 — “However, no member can be compelled to refrain from voting in such circumstances.”

**63.** `[C0962 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No member should vote on a question in which he has a direct personal or pecuniary interest not common to other members of the _____.
   A. organization
   B. constitution
   C. subsequently
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:4 — “No member should vote on a question in which he has a direct personal or pecuniary interest not common to other members of the organization.”

**64.** `[C0963 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member never voted on a question affecting himself, it would be impossible for a society to vote to hold a banquet, or for the _____ to prevent a small minority from preferring charges against them and suspending or expelling them (61, 63).

   *Answer:* majority — “If a member never voted on a question affecting himself, it would be impossible for a society to vote to hold a banquet, or for the majority to prevent a small minority from preferring charges against them and suspending or expelling them (61, 63).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:5 — “If a member never voted on a question affecting himself, it would be impossible for a society to vote to hold a banquet, or for the majority to prevent a small minority from preferring charges against them and suspending or expelling them (61, 63).”

**65.** `[C0963 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rule on _____ from voting on a question of direct personal interest does not mean that a member should not vote for himself for an office or other position to which members generally are eligible, or should not vote when other members are included with him in a motion.
   A. privileged
   B. Particular
   C. abstaining
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) abstaining. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:5 — “The rule on abstaining from voting on a question of direct personal interest does not mean that a member should not vote for himself for an office or other position to which members generally are eligible, or should not vote when other members are included with him in a motion.”

**66.** `[C0963 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member never voted on a question affecting himself, it would be _____ for a society to vote to hold a banquet, or for the majority to prevent a small minority from preferring charges against them and suspending or expelling them (61, 63).

   *Answer:* impossible — “If a member never voted on a question affecting himself, it would be impossible for a society to vote to hold a banquet, or for the majority to prevent a small minority from preferring charges against them and suspending or expelling them (61, 63).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:5 — “If a member never voted on a question affecting himself, it would be impossible for a society to vote to hold a banquet, or for the majority to prevent a small minority from preferring charges against them and suspending or expelling them (61, 63).”

**67.** `[C0963 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rule on _____ from voting on a question of direct personal interest does not mean that a member should not vote for himself for an office or other position to which members generally are eligible, or should not vote when other members are included with him in a motion.

   *Answer:* abstaining — “The rule on abstaining from voting on a question of direct personal interest does not mean that a member should not vote for himself for an office or other position to which members generally are eligible, or should not vote when other members are included with him in a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:5 — “The rule on abstaining from voting on a question of direct personal interest does not mean that a member should not vote for himself for an office or other position to which members generally are eligible, or should not vote when other members are included with him in a motion.”

**68.** `[C0964 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a vote is being taken, no _____ is permitted from the time that any member has actually voted until all have presumably voted, unless as sometimes occurs in ballot voting, other business is being transacted during voting.
   A. constitution
   B. immediately
   C. Permission
   D. interruption

   *Answer:* D) interruption. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:6 — “When a vote is being taken, no interruption is permitted from the time that any member has actually voted until all have presumably voted, unless as sometimes occurs in ballot voting, other business is being transacted during voting.”

**69.** `[C0964 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a vote is being taken, no _____ is permitted from the time that any member has actually voted until all have presumably voted, unless as sometimes occurs in ballot voting, other business is being transacted during voting.

   *Answer:* interruption — “When a vote is being taken, no interruption is permitted from the time that any member has actually voted until all have presumably voted, unless as sometimes occurs in ballot voting, other business is being transacted during voting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:6 — “When a vote is being taken, no interruption is permitted from the time that any member has actually voted until all have presumably voted, unless as sometimes occurs in ballot voting, other business is being transacted during voting.”

**70.** `[C0965 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rule Against _____ by Members During Voting.

   *Answer:* Explanation — “Rule Against Explanation by Members During Voting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:7 — “Rule Against Explanation by Members During Voting.”

**71.** `[C0965 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member has no right to “_____ his vote” during voting, which would be the same as debate at such a time.
   A. Regular
   B. Pending
   C. explain
   D. Adopted

   *Answer:* C) explain. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:7 — “A member has no right to “explain his vote” during voting, which would be the same as debate at such a time.”

**72.** `[C0965 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rule Against _____ by Members During Voting.
   A. Information
   B. Definitely
   C. Unfinished
   D. Explanation

   *Answer:* D) Explanation. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:7 — “Rule Against Explanation by Members During Voting.”

**73.** `[C0965 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member has no right to “_____ his vote” during voting, which would be the same as debate at such a time.

   *Answer:* explain — “A member has no right to “explain his vote” during voting, which would be the same as debate at such a time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:7 — “A member has no right to “explain his vote” during voting, which would be the same as debate at such a time.”

**74.** `[C0966 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except when the vote has been taken by ballot (or some other method that provides secrecy), a member has a right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced but afterward can make the change only by the _____ consent of the assembly requested and granted, without debate, immediately following the chair’s announcement of the result of the vote (see below).
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* B) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:8 — “Except when the vote has been taken by ballot (or some other method that provides secrecy), a member has a right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced but afterward can make the change only by the unanimous consent of the assembly requested and granted, without debate, immediately following the chair’s announcement of the result of the vote (see below).”

**75.** `[C0966 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except when the vote has been taken by ballot (or some other method that provides secrecy), a member has a right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced but afterward can make the change only by the unanimous consent of the assembly requested and granted, without debate, immediately following the chair’s _____ of the result of the vote (see below).

   *Answer:* announcement — “Except when the vote has been taken by ballot (or some other method that provides secrecy), a member has a right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced but afterward can make the change only by the unanimous consent of the assembly requested and granted, without debate, immediately following the chair’s announcement of the result of the vote (see below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:8 — “Except when the vote has been taken by ballot (or some other method that provides secrecy), a member has a right to change his vote up to the time the result is announced but afterward can make the change only by the unanimous consent of the assembly requested and granted, without debate, immediately following the chair’s announcement of the result of the vote (see below).”

**76.** `[C0967 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Time Limits on Efforts to Challenge, Retake, or Change a _____.

   *Answer:* Vote — “Time Limits on Efforts to Challenge, Retake, or Change a Vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:9 — “Time Limits on Efforts to Challenge, Retake, or Change a Vote.”

**77.** `[C0967 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member may raise a point of order regarding the conduct of the vote, demand a division of the assembly, move to retake the vote under another method, move for a recapitulation of a roll-call vote, or request _____ consent to change his vote.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* C) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:9 — “A member may raise a point of order regarding the conduct of the vote, demand a division of the assembly, move to retake the vote under another method, move for a recapitulation of a roll-call vote, or request unanimous consent to change his vote.”

**78.** `[C0967 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Time Limits on Efforts to _____, Retake, or Change a Vote.

   *Answer:* Challenge — “Time Limits on Efforts to Challenge, Retake, or Change a Vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:9 — “Time Limits on Efforts to Challenge, Retake, or Change a Vote.”

**79.** `[C0967 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the result of a vote has been _____, members can still propose or demand certain actions that may change the result.
   A. Committee
   B. extending
   C. paragraph
   D. announced

   *Answer:* D) announced. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:9 — “After the result of a vote has been announced, members can still propose or demand certain actions that may change the result.”

**80.** `[C0967 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member may raise a point of order regarding the conduct of the vote, demand a division of the assembly, move to retake the vote under another method, move for a _____ of a roll-call vote, or request unanimous consent to change his vote.

   *Answer:* recapitulation — “A member may raise a point of order regarding the conduct of the vote, demand a division of the assembly, move to retake the vote under another method, move for a recapitulation of a roll-call vote, or request unanimous consent to change his vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:9 — “A member may raise a point of order regarding the conduct of the vote, demand a division of the assembly, move to retake the vote under another method, move for a recapitulation of a roll-call vote, or request unanimous consent to change his vote.”

**81.** `[C0968 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assembly’s _____ in Judging Voting Procedures.

   *Answer:* Prerogative — “Assembly’s Prerogative in Judging Voting Procedures.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:10 — “Assembly’s Prerogative in Judging Voting Procedures.”

**82.** `[C0968 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The assembly itself is the judge of all questions arising that are _____ to the voting or the counting of the votes.
   A. assemblies
   B. Definitely
   C. Permission
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* D) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:10 — “The assembly itself is the judge of all questions arising that are incidental to the voting or the counting of the votes.”

**83.** `[C0968 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assembly’s _____ in Judging Voting Procedures.
   A. Prerogative
   B. requirement
   C. Preparation
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) Prerogative. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:10 — “Assembly’s Prerogative in Judging Voting Procedures.”

**84.** `[C0968 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The assembly itself is the judge of all questions arising that are _____ to the voting or the counting of the votes.

   *Answer:* incidental — “The assembly itself is the judge of all questions arising that are incidental to the voting or the counting of the votes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:10 — “The assembly itself is the judge of all questions arising that are incidental to the voting or the counting of the votes.”

**85.** `[C0969 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by _____.

   *Answer:* unanimous consent — “Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by unanimous consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:12 — “Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by unanimous consent.”

**86.** `[C0969 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by _____ consent.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* C) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:12 — “Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by unanimous consent.”

**87.** `[C0970 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A characteristic that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say _____,” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.

   *Answer:* aye — “A characteristic that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say aye,” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:13 — “A characteristic that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say aye,” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.”

**88.** `[C0970 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say aye,” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.
   A. Preparation
   B. immediately
   C. Indefinitely
   D. characteristic

   *Answer:* D) characteristic. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:13 — “A characteristic that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say aye,” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.”

**89.** `[C0971 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any member, by demanding a _____ (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.
   A. position
   B. dilatory
   C. majority
   D. Division

   *Answer:* D) Division. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:14 — “Any member, by demanding a Division (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.”

**90.** `[C0971 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If this motion is seconded, the question as to whether a count shall be ordered is voted on by voice vote, or by an _____ rising vote or show of hands.

   *Answer:* uncounted — “If this motion is seconded, the question as to whether a count shall be ordered is voted on by voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:14 — “If this motion is seconded, the question as to whether a count shall be ordered is voted on by voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands.”

**91.** `[C0971 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Where no special rule has been adopted, a _____ vote is required to order a count.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* D) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:14 — “Where no special rule has been adopted, a majority vote is required to order a count.”

**92.** `[C0971 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ with the methods of voting by voice, by rising, or by show of hands, as explained in Chapter II, if the chair is in doubt on a voice vote or a vote by show of hands, he should retake it as a rising vote and, if necessary to satisfy himself of the result, he should obtain a count of it.
   A. Particular
   B. Substitute
   C. subsidiary
   D. connection

   *Answer:* D) connection. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:14 — “In connection with the methods of voting by voice, by rising, or by show of hands, as explained in Chapter II, if the chair is in doubt on a voice vote or a vote by show of hands, he should retake it as a rising vote and, if necessary to satisfy himself of the result, he should obtain a count of it.”

**93.** `[C0971 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any member, by demanding a _____ (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.

   *Answer:* Division — “Any member, by demanding a Division (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:14 — “Any member, by demanding a Division (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.”

**94.** `[C0972 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In small meetings, the chair can take such a count himself—with or without directing the secretary to make an independent count for _____.

   *Answer:* verification — “In small meetings, the chair can take such a count himself—with or without directing the secretary to make an independent count for verification.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:15 — “In small meetings, the chair can take such a count himself—with or without directing the secretary to make an independent count for verification.”

**95.** `[C0972 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a large assembly, the chair should _____ tellers to take the count.
   A. adjourn
   B. Rescind
   C. appoint
   D. Charges

   *Answer:* C) appoint. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:15 — “In a large assembly, the chair should appoint tellers to take the count.”

**96.** `[C0972 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The count is taken by having those in the _____ rise and stand until counted, then having those in the negative rise and stand until counted.

   *Answer:* affirmative — “The count is taken by having those in the affirmative rise and stand until counted, then having those in the negative rise and stand until counted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:15 — “The count is taken by having those in the affirmative rise and stand until counted, then having those in the negative rise and stand until counted.”

**97.** `[C0972 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The votes can also be counted by having the members pass between tellers, or having them count off by rows and be seated one at a time, although the latter process is _____ subject to confusion if great care is not exercised by the tellers.
   A. consideration
   B. particularly
   C. immediately
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) particularly. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:15 — “The votes can also be counted by having the members pass between tellers, or having them count off by rows and be seated one at a time, although the latter process is particularly subject to confusion if great care is not exercised by the tellers.”

**98.** `[C0972 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In all but small _____, the doors should be closed and no one should enter or leave the hall while a count is being taken.

   *Answer:* assemblies — “In all but small assemblies, the doors should be closed and no one should enter or leave the hall while a count is being taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:15 — “In all but small assemblies, the doors should be closed and no one should enter or leave the hall while a count is being taken.”

**99.** `[C0973 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some _____ like to use a brightly colored cardboard card, approximately three inches wide and a foot long, in voting on most or all occasions by raising it when asked to do so by the chair.
   A. Preparation
   B. consideration
   C. organizations
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:16 — “Some organizations like to use a brightly colored cardboard card, approximately three inches wide and a foot long, in voting on most or all occasions by raising it when asked to do so by the chair.”

**100.** `[C0973 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of the use of these devices in voting, however, depends on expected conditions in the meeting.

   *Answer:* authorization — “The authorization of the use of these devices in voting, however, depends on expected conditions in the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:16 — “The authorization of the use of these devices in voting, however, depends on expected conditions in the meeting.”

**101.** `[C0973 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Those _____, raise your voters’ cards.
   A. opposed
   B. Meeting
   C. Regular
   D. Charges

   *Answer:* A) opposed. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:16 — “Those opposed, raise your voters’ cards.”

**102.** `[C0973 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: see also 30, Motions _____ to Methods of Voting and the Polls).

   *Answer:* Relating — “see also 30, Motions Relating to Methods of Voting and the Polls).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:16 — “see also 30, Motions Relating to Methods of Voting and the Polls).”

**103.** `[C0973 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If this method of voting is to be used, it must be _____ by a special rule of order or, in a convention, by a convention standing rule.
   A. settlement
   B. Procedures
   C. Substitute
   D. authorized

   *Answer:* D) authorized. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:16 — “If this method of voting is to be used, it must be authorized by a special rule of order or, in a convention, by a convention standing rule.”

**104.** `[C0974 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In contrast to the methods of voting mentioned in the preceding _____, the voting methods described below are used only when expressly ordered by the assembly or prescribed by its rules.

   *Answer:* subsection — “In contrast to the methods of voting mentioned in the preceding subsection, the voting methods described below are used only when expressly ordered by the assembly or prescribed by its rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:17 — “In contrast to the methods of voting mentioned in the preceding subsection, the voting methods described below are used only when expressly ordered by the assembly or prescribed by its rules.”

**105.** `[C0974 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In contrast to the methods of voting mentioned in the preceding _____, the voting methods described below are used only when expressly ordered by the assembly or prescribed by its rules.
   A. settlement
   B. subsection
   C. Particular
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* B) subsection. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:17 — “In contrast to the methods of voting mentioned in the preceding subsection, the voting methods described below are used only when expressly ordered by the assembly or prescribed by its rules.”

**106.** `[C0975 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.

   *Answer:* Voting by ballot — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”

**107.** `[C0975 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Voting by _____ (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.
   A. Recess
   B. Speech
   C. charge
   D. ballot

   *Answer:* D) ballot. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”

**108.** `[C0975 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A ballot vote is a vote taken by _____, such as slips of paper or electronic devices, by which members can indicate their choices without revealing how individual members have voted.

   *Answer:* instruments — “A ballot vote is a vote taken by instruments, such as slips of paper or electronic devices, by which members can indicate their choices without revealing how individual members have voted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “A ballot vote is a vote taken by instruments, such as slips of paper or electronic devices, by which members can indicate their choices without revealing how individual members have voted.”

**109.** `[C0975 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On a ballot vote in an election or other vote involving multiple possible choices, members are able to write in or fill in a vote for any eligible person or choice and are not confined to voting for or against _____ that appear on the ballot.
   A. subsidiary
   B. resolution
   C. candidates
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) candidates. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “On a ballot vote in an election or other vote involving multiple possible choices, members are able to write in or fill in a vote for any eligible person or choice and are not confined to voting for or against candidates that appear on the ballot.”

**110.** `[C0975 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A ballot vote is a vote taken by _____, such as slips of paper or electronic devices, by which members can indicate their choices without revealing how individual members have voted.
   A. Reconsider
   B. adjournment
   C. convention
   D. instruments

   *Answer:* D) instruments. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “A ballot vote is a vote taken by instruments, such as slips of paper or electronic devices, by which members can indicate their choices without revealing how individual members have voted.”

**111.** `[C0976 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases in which there is no requirement that a vote be by ballot, a ballot vote can be ordered by a _____ vote—which may be desirable whenever it is believed that members may thereby be more likely to vote their true sentiments.

   *Answer:* majority — “In cases in which there is no requirement that a vote be by ballot, a ballot vote can be ordered by a majority vote—which may be desirable whenever it is believed that members may thereby be more likely to vote their true sentiments.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:19 — “In cases in which there is no requirement that a vote be by ballot, a ballot vote can be ordered by a majority vote—which may be desirable whenever it is believed that members may thereby be more likely to vote their true sentiments.”

**112.** `[C0976 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The bylaws of the _____ may prescribe that the vote be by ballot in certain cases, as in the election of officers and in admission to membership.
   A. organization
   B. Incidental
   C. specification
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:19 — “The bylaws of the organization may prescribe that the vote be by ballot in certain cases, as in the election of officers and in admission to membership.”

**113.** `[C0976 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any vote related to charges or _____ charges before or after a trial of a member or any officer should always be by ballot.

   *Answer:* proposed — “Any vote related to charges or proposed charges before or after a trial of a member or any officer should always be by ballot.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:19 — “Any vote related to charges or proposed charges before or after a trial of a member or any officer should always be by ballot.”

**114.** `[C0976 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases in which there is no _____ that a vote be by ballot, a ballot vote can be ordered by a majority vote—which may be desirable whenever it is believed that members may thereby be more likely to vote their true sentiments.
   A. Incidental
   B. Preparation
   C. requirement
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) requirement. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:19 — “In cases in which there is no requirement that a vote be by ballot, a ballot vote can be ordered by a majority vote—which may be desirable whenever it is believed that members may thereby be more likely to vote their true sentiments.”

**115.** `[C0976 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The bylaws of the _____ may prescribe that the vote be by ballot in certain cases, as in the election of officers and in admission to membership.

   *Answer:* organization — “The bylaws of the organization may prescribe that the vote be by ballot in certain cases, as in the election of officers and in admission to membership.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:19 — “The bylaws of the organization may prescribe that the vote be by ballot in certain cases, as in the election of officers and in admission to membership.”

**116.** `[C0977 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the bylaws require a vote to be taken by ballot, this requirement cannot be suspended—even by a _____ vote—so as to take the vote by a nonsecret method.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “When the bylaws require a vote to be taken by ballot, this requirement cannot be suspended—even by a unanimous vote—so as to take the vote by a nonsecret method.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:20 — “When the bylaws require a vote to be taken by ballot, this requirement cannot be suspended—even by a unanimous vote—so as to take the vote by a nonsecret method.”

**117.** `[C0977 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the bylaws require a vote to be taken by ballot, this _____ cannot be suspended—even by a unanimous vote—so as to take the vote by a nonsecret method.
   A. requirement
   B. committees
   C. assemblies
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) requirement. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:20 — “When the bylaws require a vote to be taken by ballot, this requirement cannot be suspended—even by a unanimous vote—so as to take the vote by a nonsecret method.”

**118.** `[C0977 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A vote ordering a ballot vote on a particular question (see 30) can, however, be _____ as long as the balloting has not yet begun.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “A vote ordering a ballot vote on a particular question (see 30) can, however, be reconsidered as long as the balloting has not yet begun.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:20 — “A vote ordering a ballot vote on a particular question (see 30) can, however, be reconsidered as long as the balloting has not yet begun.”

**119.** `[C0977 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A vote ordering a ballot vote on a particular question (see 30) can, however, be _____ as long as the balloting has not yet begun.
   A. reconsidered
   B. undebatable
   C. Announcements
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:20 — “A vote ordering a ballot vote on a particular question (see 30) can, however, be reconsidered as long as the balloting has not yet begun.”

**120.** `[C0978 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Likewise, a motion to make _____ a ballot vote that was not unanimous must itself be voted on by ballot;
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* C) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:21 — “Likewise, a motion to make unanimous a ballot vote that was not unanimous must itself be voted on by ballot;”
