**121.** `[C0978 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a vote is to be taken, or has been taken, by ballot, whether or not the bylaws require that form of voting, no action is in order that would force the _____ of a member’s vote or views on the matter.

   *Answer:* disclosure — “When a vote is to be taken, or has been taken, by ballot, whether or not the bylaws require that form of voting, no action is in order that would force the disclosure of a member’s vote or views on the matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:21 — “When a vote is to be taken, or has been taken, by ballot, whether or not the bylaws require that form of voting, no action is in order that would force the disclosure of a member’s vote or views on the matter.”

**122.** `[C0978 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: even a single _____ vote in such a case defeats the motion.
   A. negative
   B. Postpone
   C. Interest
   D. Previous

   *Answer:* A) negative. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:21 — “even a single negative vote in such a case defeats the motion.”

**123.** `[C0978 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Likewise, a motion to make _____ a ballot vote that was not unanimous must itself be voted on by ballot;

   *Answer:* unanimous — “Likewise, a motion to make unanimous a ballot vote that was not unanimous must itself be voted on by ballot;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:21 — “Likewise, a motion to make unanimous a ballot vote that was not unanimous must itself be voted on by ballot;”

**124.** `[C0978 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a vote is to be taken, or has been taken, by ballot, whether or not the bylaws require that form of voting, no action is in order that would force the _____ of a member’s vote or views on the matter.
   A. privileged
   B. incidental
   C. disclosure
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) disclosure. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:21 — “When a vote is to be taken, or has been taken, by ballot, whether or not the bylaws require that form of voting, no action is in order that would force the disclosure of a member’s vote or views on the matter.”

**125.** `[C0979 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whenever a vote is to be taken by ballot, it is not in order to move that one person—the _____, for example—cast the ballot of the assembly.

   *Answer:* secretary — “Whenever a vote is to be taken by ballot, it is not in order to move that one person—the secretary, for example—cast the ballot of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:22 — “Whenever a vote is to be taken by ballot, it is not in order to move that one person—the secretary, for example—cast the ballot of the assembly.”

**126.** `[C0979 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whenever a vote is to be taken by ballot, it is not in order to move that one person—the _____, for example—cast the ballot of the assembly.
   A. postponed
   B. secretary
   C. Precedent
   D. execution

   *Answer:* B) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:22 — “Whenever a vote is to be taken by ballot, it is not in order to move that one person—the secretary, for example—cast the ballot of the assembly.”

**127.** `[C0981 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A ballot can consist of simply a small slip of paper on which the voter writes his choice in a manner _____ by the chair;
   A. assembly
   B. division
   C. Division
   D. directed

   *Answer:* D) directed. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:24 — “A ballot can consist of simply a small slip of paper on which the voter writes his choice in a manner directed by the chair;”

**128.** `[C0981 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if it is known ahead of time that a vote is to be by ballot and what the exact questions are, the ballots should be prepared in advance for _____ at the proper time.

   *Answer:* distribution — “but if it is known ahead of time that a vote is to be by ballot and what the exact questions are, the ballots should be prepared in advance for distribution at the proper time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:24 — “but if it is known ahead of time that a vote is to be by ballot and what the exact questions are, the ballots should be prepared in advance for distribution at the proper time.”

**129.** `[C0981 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, each question to be voted on appears on the ballot with a list of the _____ answers beside blank spaces or boxes, so that the voter can check the answer he desires.
   A. possible
   B. Interest
   C. Division
   D. assembly

   *Answer:* A) possible. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:24 — “In such a case, each question to be voted on appears on the ballot with a list of the possible answers beside blank spaces or boxes, so that the voter can check the answer he desires.”

**130.** `[C0981 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Two or more questions can be listed on the same sheet, provided that each is marked in such a way that there can be no confusion, as _____ below.

   *Answer:* illustrated — “Two or more questions can be listed on the same sheet, provided that each is marked in such a way that there can be no confusion, as illustrated below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:24 — “Two or more questions can be listed on the same sheet, provided that each is marked in such a way that there can be no confusion, as illustrated below.”

**131.** `[C0981 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A ballot can consist of simply a small slip of paper on which the voter writes his choice in a manner _____ by the chair;

   *Answer:* directed — “A ballot can consist of simply a small slip of paper on which the voter writes his choice in a manner directed by the chair;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:24 — “A ballot can consist of simply a small slip of paper on which the voter writes his choice in a manner directed by the chair;”

**132.** `[C0982 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____, “for” and “against” spaces or boxes may not be used.

   *Answer:* elections — “In elections, “for” and “against” spaces or boxes may not be used.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:25 — “In elections, “for” and “against” spaces or boxes may not be used.”

**133.** `[C0982 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: They are _____ only with respect to votes on motions.
   A. Convention
   B. substitute
   C. Permission
   D. applicable

   *Answer:* D) applicable. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:25 — “They are applicable only with respect to votes on motions.”

**134.** `[C0982 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an election, a voter can vote against one _____ only by voting for another who has been nominated or by writing in the name of another candidate.

   *Answer:* candidate — “In an election, a voter can vote against one candidate only by voting for another who has been nominated or by writing in the name of another candidate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:25 — “In an election, a voter can vote against one candidate only by voting for another who has been nominated or by writing in the name of another candidate.”

**135.** `[C0982 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____, “for” and “against” spaces or boxes may not be used.
   A. committee
   B. reception
   C. elections
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* C) elections. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:25 — “In elections, “for” and “against” spaces or boxes may not be used.”

**136.** `[C0982 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: They are _____ only with respect to votes on motions.

   *Answer:* applicable — “They are applicable only with respect to votes on motions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:25 — “They are applicable only with respect to votes on motions.”

**137.** `[C0983 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In balloting in a meeting where the voting is in the same room as the meeting, the chair appoints tellers to _____, collect, and count the ballots, and to report the vote.
   A. convention
   B. Definitely
   C. Procedures
   D. distribute

   *Answer:* D) distribute. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:26 — “In balloting in a meeting where the voting is in the same room as the meeting, the chair appoints tellers to distribute, collect, and count the ballots, and to report the vote.”

**138.** `[C0983 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The number of tellers is dependent on the number of voters, and the number of offices to be filled or questions to be answered, or the number of _____.

   *Answer:* candidates — “The number of tellers is dependent on the number of voters, and the number of offices to be filled or questions to be answered, or the number of candidates.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:26 — “The number of tellers is dependent on the number of voters, and the number of offices to be filled or questions to be answered, or the number of candidates.”

**139.** `[C0983 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For a small group, two or three tellers are usually _____.
   A. settlement
   B. sufficient
   C. Unfinished
   D. convention

   *Answer:* B) sufficient. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:26 — “For a small group, two or three tellers are usually sufficient.”

**140.** `[C0983 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Often their position with regard to the issue involved is well known, however, and they are _____ chosen to protect the interests of each opposing side.

   *Answer:* frequently — “Often their position with regard to the issue involved is well known, however, and they are frequently chosen to protect the interests of each opposing side.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:26 — “Often their position with regard to the issue involved is well known, however, and they are frequently chosen to protect the interests of each opposing side.”

**141.** `[C0983 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In balloting in a meeting where the voting is in the same room as the meeting, the chair appoints tellers to _____, collect, and count the ballots, and to report the vote.

   *Answer:* distribute — “In balloting in a meeting where the voting is in the same room as the meeting, the chair appoints tellers to distribute, collect, and count the ballots, and to report the vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:26 — “In balloting in a meeting where the voting is in the same room as the meeting, the chair appoints tellers to distribute, collect, and count the ballots, and to report the vote.”

**142.** `[C0984 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To ensure accuracy and to enable the tellers when _____ the ballots to detect any error, each ballot should be folded in a manner announced in advance or stated on the ballot itself.

   *Answer:* unfolding — “To ensure accuracy and to enable the tellers when unfolding the ballots to detect any error, each ballot should be folded in a manner announced in advance or stated on the ballot itself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:27 — “To ensure accuracy and to enable the tellers when unfolding the ballots to detect any error, each ballot should be folded in a manner announced in advance or stated on the ballot itself.”

**143.** `[C0984 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To ensure accuracy and to enable the tellers when _____ the ballots to detect any error, each ballot should be folded in a manner announced in advance or stated on the ballot itself.
   A. expressly
   B. postponed
   C. unfolding
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* C) unfolding. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:27 — “To ensure accuracy and to enable the tellers when unfolding the ballots to detect any error, each ballot should be folded in a manner announced in advance or stated on the ballot itself.”

**144.** `[C0985 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ officer, if a member of the assembly, can always vote in the case of a ballot at the time other members do.

   *Answer:* presiding — “The presiding officer, if a member of the assembly, can always vote in the case of a ballot at the time other members do.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:28 — “The presiding officer, if a member of the assembly, can always vote in the case of a ballot at the time other members do.”

**145.** `[C0985 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Should he fail to vote before the polls are closed, he cannot then do so without the _____ of the assembly.
   A. convention
   B. permission
   C. Incidental
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) permission. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:28 — “Should he fail to vote before the polls are closed, he cannot then do so without the permission of the assembly.”

**146.** `[C0985 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ officer, if a member of the assembly, can always vote in the case of a ballot at the time other members do.
   A. execution
   B. Crestwood
   C. presiding
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* C) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:28 — “The presiding officer, if a member of the assembly, can always vote in the case of a ballot at the time other members do.”

**147.** `[C0985 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Should he fail to vote before the polls are closed, he cannot then do so without the _____ of the assembly.

   *Answer:* permission — “Should he fail to vote before the polls are closed, he cannot then do so without the permission of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:28 — “Should he fail to vote before the polls are closed, he cannot then do so without the permission of the assembly.”

**148.** `[C0986 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the _____ is completed, the chair directs the tellers to collect the ballots.

   *Answer:* balloting — “When the balloting is completed, the chair directs the tellers to collect the ballots.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:29 — “When the balloting is completed, the chair directs the tellers to collect the ballots.”

**149.** `[C0986 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In collecting the ballots, it is the tellers’ _____ to see that no member votes more than once—for the assurance of which the assembly should adopt some reasonable and orderly method.
   A. responsibility
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. president-elect
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* A) responsibility. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:29 — “In collecting the ballots, it is the tellers’ responsibility to see that no member votes more than once—for the assurance of which the assembly should adopt some reasonable and orderly method.”

**150.** `[C0986 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example: (a) In meetings where only voters are present, members can remain in their seats and drop their ballots into a receptacle passed by a teller, _____ by another teller as watcher and checker;

   *Answer:* accompanied — “For example: (a) In meetings where only voters are present, members can remain in their seats and drop their ballots into a receptacle passed by a teller, accompanied by another teller as watcher and checker;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:29 — “For example: (a) In meetings where only voters are present, members can remain in their seats and drop their ballots into a receptacle passed by a teller, accompanied by another teller as watcher and checker;”

**151.** `[C0986 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (b) they can go to a central ballot box _____ by at least two tellers and deposit their ballots;
   A. Precedent
   B. rejecting
   C. monitored
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* C) monitored. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:29 — “(b) they can go to a central ballot box monitored by at least two tellers and deposit their ballots;”

**152.** `[C0986 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (c) they can hand their ballots to a teller—who judges by the _____ and feel of the paper that only one ballot is being cast, and who deposits them in a central ballot box.

   *Answer:* thickness — “or (c) they can hand their ballots to a teller—who judges by the thickness and feel of the paper that only one ballot is being cast, and who deposits them in a central ballot box.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:29 — “or (c) they can hand their ballots to a teller—who judges by the thickness and feel of the paper that only one ballot is being cast, and who deposits them in a central ballot box.”

**153.** `[C0987 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thereafter, if other members arrive who wish to vote, a _____ vote is required to reopen the polls (30).

   *Answer:* majority — “Thereafter, if other members arrive who wish to vote, a majority vote is required to reopen the polls (30).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:30 — “Thereafter, if other members arrive who wish to vote, a majority vote is required to reopen the polls (30).”

**154.** `[C0987 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After all have voted who wish to, the polls can be closed on the motion of a member by a _____ vote (30);
   A. A majority vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* D) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:30 — “After all have voted who wish to, the polls can be closed on the motion of a member by a two-thirds vote (30);”

**155.** `[C0987 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When everyone appears to have voted, the chair inquires, “Have all voted who wish to do so?” If there is no response, he says, “If no one else wishes to vote… [pause], the polls are closed,” thus in effect declaring the polls closed by _____ consent.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “When everyone appears to have voted, the chair inquires, “Have all voted who wish to do so?” If there is no response, he says, “If no one else wishes to vote… [pause], the polls are closed,” thus in effect declaring the polls closed by unanimous consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:30 — “When everyone appears to have voted, the chair inquires, “Have all voted who wish to do so?” If there is no response, he says, “If no one else wishes to vote… [pause], the polls are closed,” thus in effect declaring the polls closed by unanimous consent.”

**156.** `[C0987 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When everyone appears to have voted, the chair inquires, “Have all voted who wish to do so?” If there is no response, he says, “If no one else wishes to vote… [pause], the polls are closed,” thus in effect _____ the polls closed by unanimous consent.
   A. auditors’
   B. postponed
   C. presiding
   D. declaring

   *Answer:* D) declaring. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:30 — “When everyone appears to have voted, the chair inquires, “Have all voted who wish to do so?” If there is no response, he says, “If no one else wishes to vote… [pause], the polls are closed,” thus in effect declaring the polls closed by unanimous consent.”

**157.** `[C0987 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, if other members arrive who wish to vote, a majority vote is required to reopen the polls (30).

   *Answer:* Thereafter — “Thereafter, if other members arrive who wish to vote, a majority vote is required to reopen the polls (30).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:30 — “Thereafter, if other members arrive who wish to vote, a majority vote is required to reopen the polls (30).”

**158.** `[C0988 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ cast, the principle followed is that a choice has no mandate from the voting body unless approval is expressed by more than half of those entitled to vote and registering any evidence of having some preference.

   *Answer:* recording the votes — “In recording the votes cast, the principle followed is that a choice has no mandate from the voting body unless approval is expressed by more than half of those entitled to vote and registering any evidence of having some preference.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:31 — “In recording the votes cast, the principle followed is that a choice has no mandate from the voting body unless approval is expressed by more than half of those entitled to vote and registering any evidence of having some preference.”

**159.** `[C0988 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In recording the votes cast, the principle followed is that a choice has no mandate from the voting body unless approval is expressed by more than half of those entitled to vote and _____ any evidence of having some preference.
   A. registering
   B. Organization
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) registering. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:31 — “In recording the votes cast, the principle followed is that a choice has no mandate from the voting body unless approval is expressed by more than half of those entitled to vote and registering any evidence of having some preference.”

**160.** `[C0988 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, the tellers ignore blank ballots and other ballots that indicate no preference, treating them as abstentions.

   *Answer:* Accordingly — “Accordingly, the tellers ignore blank ballots and other ballots that indicate no preference, treating them as abstentions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:31 — “Accordingly, the tellers ignore blank ballots and other ballots that indicate no preference, treating them as abstentions.”

**161.** `[C0988 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (Blank ballots are _____ cast by members to conceal the fact that they do not wish to vote.)
   A. expressly
   B. sometimes
   C. Reporting
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* B) sometimes. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:31 — “(Blank ballots are sometimes cast by members to conceal the fact that they do not wish to vote.)”

**162.** `[C0988 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, the tellers ignore blank ballots and other ballots that indicate no preference, treating them as abstentions.
   A. resolutions
   B. Accordingly
   C. committees
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) Accordingly. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:31 — “Accordingly, the tellers ignore blank ballots and other ballots that indicate no preference, treating them as abstentions.”

**163.** `[C0989 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as _____—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.

   *Answer:* illegal votes — “Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as illegal votes—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:32 — “Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as illegal votes—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.”

**164.** `[C0989 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: All ballots that indicate a preference—provided they have been cast by persons entitled to vote—are taken into account in determining the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the _____.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* D) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:32 — “All ballots that indicate a preference—provided they have been cast by persons entitled to vote—are taken into account in determining the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority.”

**165.** `[C0989 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: All ballots that indicate a preference—provided they have been cast by persons entitled to vote—are taken into account in _____ the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority.

   *Answer:* determining — “All ballots that indicate a preference—provided they have been cast by persons entitled to vote—are taken into account in determining the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:32 — “All ballots that indicate a preference—provided they have been cast by persons entitled to vote—are taken into account in determining the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority.”

**166.** `[C0989 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each such ballot is credited to the voter’s _____ candidate or choice if the meaning of the ballot is clear and the choice is valid.
   A. Secretary
   B. auditors’
   C. preferred
   D. president

   *Answer:* C) preferred. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:32 — “Each such ballot is credited to the voter’s preferred candidate or choice if the meaning of the ballot is clear and the choice is valid.”

**167.** `[C0989 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as illegal votes—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.

   *Answer:* Unintelligible — “Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as illegal votes—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:32 — “Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as illegal votes—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.”

**168.** `[C0990 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Technical errors, like the _____ of a word or name, do not make a vote illegal if the meaning of the ballot is clear.

   *Answer:* misspelling — “Technical errors, like the misspelling of a word or name, do not make a vote illegal if the meaning of the ballot is clear.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:33 — “Technical errors, like the misspelling of a word or name, do not make a vote illegal if the meaning of the ballot is clear.”

**169.** `[C0990 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the meaning of one or more ballots is doubtful, they can be treated as illegal if it is _____ for them to affect the result;
   A. Convention
   B. introduced
   C. recognized
   D. impossible

   *Answer:* D) impossible. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:33 — “If the meaning of one or more ballots is doubtful, they can be treated as illegal if it is impossible for them to affect the result;”

**170.** `[C0990 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if they may affect the result, the tellers report them to the chair, who _____ submits to the assembly the question of how these ballots should be recorded.

   *Answer:* immediately — “but if they may affect the result, the tellers report them to the chair, who immediately submits to the assembly the question of how these ballots should be recorded.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:33 — “but if they may affect the result, the tellers report them to the chair, who immediately submits to the assembly the question of how these ballots should be recorded.”

**171.** `[C0990 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When reporting doubtful ballots, the tellers must be careful whenever possible not to show how the decision would affect any of the _____.
   A. persuasive
   B. recognized
   C. candidates
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) candidates. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:33 — “When reporting doubtful ballots, the tellers must be careful whenever possible not to show how the decision would affect any of the candidates.”

**172.** `[C0990 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Technical errors, like the _____ of a word or name, do not make a vote illegal if the meaning of the ballot is clear.
   A. Permission
   B. undebatable
   C. Convention
   D. misspelling

   *Answer:* D) misspelling. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:33 — “Technical errors, like the misspelling of a word or name, do not make a vote illegal if the meaning of the ballot is clear.”

**173.** `[C0991 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, in _____ the ballots, it is found that two or more filledout ballots are folded together, they are recorded as illegal votes—that is, each set of ballots folded together is reported as one illegal vote, and is not credited.

   *Answer:* unfolding — “If, in unfolding the ballots, it is found that two or more filledout ballots are folded together, they are recorded as illegal votes—that is, each set of ballots folded together is reported as one illegal vote, and is not credited.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:34 — “If, in unfolding the ballots, it is found that two or more filledout ballots are folded together, they are recorded as illegal votes—that is, each set of ballots folded together is reported as one illegal vote, and is not credited.”

**174.** `[C0991 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, a blank ballot folded in with one that is properly filled out is ignored and does not cause the _____ of the ballot with which it was folded.
   A. rejection
   B. postponed
   C. Reporting
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) rejection. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:34 — “On the other hand, a blank ballot folded in with one that is properly filled out is ignored and does not cause the rejection of the ballot with which it was folded.”

**175.** `[C0991 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, in _____ the ballots, it is found that two or more filledout ballots are folded together, they are recorded as illegal votes—that is, each set of ballots folded together is reported as one illegal vote, and is not credited.
   A. Precedent
   B. unfolding
   C. presiding
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* B) unfolding. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:34 — “If, in unfolding the ballots, it is found that two or more filledout ballots are folded together, they are recorded as illegal votes—that is, each set of ballots folded together is reported as one illegal vote, and is not credited.”

**176.** `[C0991 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, a blank ballot folded in with one that is properly filled out is ignored and does not cause the _____ of the ballot with which it was folded.

   *Answer:* rejection — “On the other hand, a blank ballot folded in with one that is properly filled out is ignored and does not cause the rejection of the ballot with which it was folded.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:34 — “On the other hand, a blank ballot folded in with one that is properly filled out is ignored and does not cause the rejection of the ballot with which it was folded.”

**177.** `[C0992 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If one or more ballots are identifiable as cast by persons not entitled to vote, these ballots are excluded in determining the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the _____.

   *Answer:* majority — “If one or more ballots are identifiable as cast by persons not entitled to vote, these ballots are excluded in determining the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:35 — “If one or more ballots are identifiable as cast by persons not entitled to vote, these ballots are excluded in determining the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority.”

**178.** `[C0992 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If one or more ballots are _____ as cast by persons not entitled to vote, these ballots are excluded in determining the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority.
   A. Convention
   B. Permission
   C. identifiable
   D. Information

   *Answer:* C) identifiable. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:35 — “If one or more ballots are identifiable as cast by persons not entitled to vote, these ballots are excluded in determining the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority.”

**179.** `[C0992 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is evidence that any _____ ballots were cast by persons not entitled to vote, and if there is any possibility that such ballots might affect the result, the entire ballot vote is null and void, and a new ballot vote must be taken.

   *Answer:* unidentifiable — “If there is evidence that any unidentifiable ballots were cast by persons not entitled to vote, and if there is any possibility that such ballots might affect the result, the entire ballot vote is null and void, and a new ballot vote must be taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:35 — “If there is evidence that any unidentifiable ballots were cast by persons not entitled to vote, and if there is any possibility that such ballots might affect the result, the entire ballot vote is null and void, and a new ballot vote must be taken.”

**180.** `[C0992 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is evidence that any _____ ballots were cast by persons not entitled to vote, and if there is any possibility that such ballots might affect the result, the entire ballot vote is null and void, and a new ballot vote must be taken.
   A. presentation
   B. Information
   C. President-Elect
   D. unidentifiable

   *Answer:* D) unidentifiable. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:35 — “If there is evidence that any unidentifiable ballots were cast by persons not entitled to vote, and if there is any possibility that such ballots might affect the result, the entire ballot vote is null and void, and a new ballot vote must be taken.”

**181.** `[C0993 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Therefore, the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the _____ must be tallied independently for each section.

   *Answer:* majority — “Therefore, the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority must be tallied independently for each section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:36 — “Therefore, the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority must be tallied independently for each section.”

**182.** `[C0993 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On a ballot containing several questions or several _____ offices or positions to be filled, each section of the ballot—that is, each portion that contains one question or position to be filled, or one group of identical positions (such as on a board or committee) to be filled—is treated for all purposes as if it were a separate ballot, which is counted in accordance with the rules given above.
   A. constitution
   B. Substitute
   C. independent
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) independent. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:36 — “On a ballot containing several questions or several independent offices or positions to be filled, each section of the ballot—that is, each portion that contains one question or position to be filled, or one group of identical positions (such as on a board or committee) to be filled—is treated for all purposes as if it were a separate ballot, which is counted in accordance with the rules given above.”

**183.** `[C0993 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Therefore, the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority must be tallied _____ for each section.

   *Answer:* independently — “Therefore, the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority must be tallied independently for each section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:36 — “Therefore, the number of votes cast for purposes of computing the majority must be tallied independently for each section.”

**184.** `[C0993 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: So, too, if a member leaves one or more of the _____ blank, the blank sections in no way affect the validity of the sections that are filled;
   A. limiting
   B. Nominees
   C. Resolved
   D. sections

   *Answer:* D) sections. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:36 — “So, too, if a member leaves one or more of the sections blank, the blank sections in no way affect the validity of the sections that are filled;”

**185.** `[C0993 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: nor do illegal votes on one section affect the validity of the _____ sections.

   *Answer:* remaining — “nor do illegal votes on one section affect the validity of the remaining sections.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:36 — “nor do illegal votes on one section affect the validity of the remaining sections.”

**186.** `[C0994 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Tellers’ report and the chair’s _____ of the result.

   *Answer:* declaration — “Tellers’ report and the chair’s declaration of the result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:37 — “Tellers’ report and the chair’s declaration of the result.”

**187.** `[C0994 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chairman of tellers, standing, _____ the chair, reads the tellers’ report, and hands it to the chair without declaring the result.
   A. addresses
   B. Objection
   C. rejecting
   D. extending

   *Answer:* A) addresses. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:37 — “The chairman of tellers, standing, addresses the chair, reads the tellers’ report, and hands it to the chair without declaring the result.”

**188.** `[C0994 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of an _____, the report follows this form:

   *Answer:* election — “In the case of an election, the report follows this form:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:37 — “In the case of an election, the report follows this form:”

**189.** `[C0994 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Tellers’ report and the chair’s _____ of the result.
   A. convention
   B. Convention
   C. Procedures
   D. declaration

   *Answer:* D) declaration. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:37 — “Tellers’ report and the chair’s declaration of the result.”

**190.** `[C0994 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chairman of tellers, standing, _____ the chair, reads the tellers’ report, and hands it to the chair without declaring the result.

   *Answer:* addresses — “The chairman of tellers, standing, addresses the chair, reads the tellers’ report, and hands it to the chair without declaring the result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:37 — “The chairman of tellers, standing, addresses the chair, reads the tellers’ report, and hands it to the chair without declaring the result.”

**191.** `[C0995 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The tellers’ report does not include the number of members eligible to vote nor the number _____.

   *Answer:* abstaining — “The tellers’ report does not include the number of members eligible to vote nor the number abstaining.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:39 — “The tellers’ report does not include the number of members eligible to vote nor the number abstaining.”

**192.** `[C0995 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a local society or other body in which membership continues on a long-term basis, only the officer _____ for maintaining the membership roll, and in a convention only an immediate updated report of the credentials committee, can validly determine the number of members eligible to vote if this figure becomes needed.
   A. convention
   B. responsible
   C. assemblies
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) responsible. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:39 — “In a local society or other body in which membership continues on a long-term basis, only the officer responsible for maintaining the membership roll, and in a convention only an immediate updated report of the credentials committee, can validly determine the number of members eligible to vote if this figure becomes needed.”

**193.** `[C0995 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ teller never declares the result of a ballot vote.

   *Answer:* reporting — “The reporting teller never declares the result of a ballot vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:39 — “The reporting teller never declares the result of a ballot vote.”

**194.** `[C0995 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The result is always _____ by the chair, who also reads the tellers’ report before he does so.
   A. Business
   B. assuming
   C. dilatory
   D. declared

   *Answer:* D) declared. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:39 — “The result is always declared by the chair, who also reads the tellers’ report before he does so.”

**195.** `[C0995 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an election, the chair _____ declares the election of each officer (see also 46).

   *Answer:* separately — “In an election, the chair separately declares the election of each officer (see also 46).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:39 — “In an election, the chair separately declares the election of each officer (see also 46).”

**196.** `[C0996 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The tellers’ report is entered in full in the minutes, becoming a part of the official records of the _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “The tellers’ report is entered in full in the minutes, becoming a part of the official records of the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:40 — “The tellers’ report is entered in full in the minutes, becoming a part of the official records of the organization.”

**197.** `[C0996 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under no _____ may this be omitted in an election or in a vote on a critical motion out of a mistaken deference to the feelings of unsuccessful candidates or members of the losing side.
   A. Preparation
   B. Vice-President
   C. circumstances
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* C) circumstances. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:40 — “Under no circumstances may this be omitted in an election or in a vote on a critical motion out of a mistaken deference to the feelings of unsuccessful candidates or members of the losing side.”

**198.** `[C0996 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The tellers’ report is entered in full in the minutes, becoming a part of the official records of the _____.
   A. Announcements
   B. organization
   C. Parliamentary
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:40 — “The tellers’ report is entered in full in the minutes, becoming a part of the official records of the organization.”

**199.** `[C0996 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under no _____ may this be omitted in an election or in a vote on a critical motion out of a mistaken deference to the feelings of unsuccessful candidates or members of the losing side.

   *Answer:* circumstances — “Under no circumstances may this be omitted in an election or in a vote on a critical motion out of a mistaken deference to the feelings of unsuccessful candidates or members of the losing side.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:40 — “Under no circumstances may this be omitted in an election or in a vote on a critical motion out of a mistaken deference to the feelings of unsuccessful candidates or members of the losing side.”

**200.** `[C0997 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After _____ of an election or balloting on a motion, unless the voting body adopts an incidental main motion directing otherwise, the tellers place the ballots and tally sheets in the custody of the secretary, who keeps them under seal until the time within which a recount may be ordered expires, and then destroys them.
   A. completion
   B. Definitely
   C. settlement
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* A) completion. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:41 — “After completion of an election or balloting on a motion, unless the voting body adopts an incidental main motion directing otherwise, the tellers place the ballots and tally sheets in the custody of the secretary, who keeps them under seal until the time within which a recount may be ordered expires, and then destroys them.”

**201.** `[C0997 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A recount may also be ordered at a special session properly called for that purpose, if held within a _____ time interval of the session at which the voting result was announced and before the next regular session.

   *Answer:* quarterly — “A recount may also be ordered at a special session properly called for that purpose, if held within a quarterly time interval of the session at which the voting result was announced and before the next regular session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:41 — “A recount may also be ordered at a special session properly called for that purpose, if held within a quarterly time interval of the session at which the voting result was announced and before the next regular session.”

**202.** `[C0997 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After completion of an election or balloting on a motion, unless the voting body adopts an _____ main motion directing otherwise, the tellers place the ballots and tally sheets in the custody of the secretary, who keeps them under seal until the time within which a recount may be ordered expires, and then destroys them.
   A. authorizes
   B. subsidiary
   C. incidental
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* C) incidental. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:41 — “After completion of an election or balloting on a motion, unless the voting body adopts an incidental main motion directing otherwise, the tellers place the ballots and tally sheets in the custody of the secretary, who keeps them under seal until the time within which a recount may be ordered expires, and then destroys them.”

**203.** `[C0997 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A recount may also be ordered at a special session properly called for that purpose, if held within a _____ time interval of the session at which the voting result was announced and before the next regular session.
   A. Objection
   B. committee
   C. Reporting
   D. quarterly

   *Answer:* D) quarterly. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:41 — “A recount may also be ordered at a special session properly called for that purpose, if held within a quarterly time interval of the session at which the voting result was announced and before the next regular session.”

**204.** `[C0998 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In many _____— especially in those comprising hundreds of voters—the process of verifying and counting votes is greatly simplified by the use of electronic or mechanical voting devices, such as handheld keypads or standalone voting machines.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In many organizations— especially in those comprising hundreds of voters—the process of verifying and counting votes is greatly simplified by the use of electronic or mechanical voting devices, such as handheld keypads or standalone voting machines.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:42 — “In many organizations— especially in those comprising hundreds of voters—the process of verifying and counting votes is greatly simplified by the use of electronic or mechanical voting devices, such as handheld keypads or standalone voting machines.”

**205.** `[C0998 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The use of such devices to conduct voting may be directed by a special rule of order or _____ standing rule or, for a particular vote, by a motion relating to methods of voting and the polls (30).
   A. convention
   B. Reconsider
   C. subsidiary
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:42 — “The use of such devices to conduct voting may be directed by a special rule of order or convention standing rule or, for a particular vote, by a motion relating to methods of voting and the polls (30).”

**206.** `[C0998 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Members must be able to indicate their choices without _____ how they have voted.

   *Answer:* revealing — “Members must be able to indicate their choices without revealing how they have voted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:42 — “Members must be able to indicate their choices without revealing how they have voted.”

**207.** `[C0998 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the devices are to be used for an election, _____ must be made to allow voters to cast write-in votes.
   A. rejecting
   B. postponed
   C. provision
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* C) provision. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:42 — “If the devices are to be used for an election, provision must be made to allow voters to cast write-in votes.”

**208.** `[C0998 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In many _____— especially in those comprising hundreds of voters—the process of verifying and counting votes is greatly simplified by the use of electronic or mechanical voting devices, such as handheld keypads or standalone voting machines.
   A. deliberative
   B. representatives
   C. organizations
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:42 — “In many organizations— especially in those comprising hundreds of voters—the process of verifying and counting votes is greatly simplified by the use of electronic or mechanical voting devices, such as handheld keypads or standalone voting machines.”

**209.** `[C0999 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Taking a vote by _____ (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;
   A. Form and
   B. and Void
   C. roll call
   D. the Rules

   *Answer:* C) roll call. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:45 — “Taking a vote by roll call (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;”

**210.** `[C0999 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Taking a vote by roll call (or by _____, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;

   *Answer:* yeas and nays — “Taking a vote by roll call (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:45 — “Taking a vote by roll call (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;”

**211.** `[C0999 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: therefore, it has exactly the _____ effect of a ballot vote.

   *Answer:* opposite — “therefore, it has exactly the opposite effect of a ballot vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:45 — “therefore, it has exactly the opposite effect of a ballot vote.”

**212.** `[C0999 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is usually confined to representative bodies, where the proceedings are published, since it enables constituents to know how their _____ voted on certain measures.
   A. recommendation
   B. characteristics
   C. immediately
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* D) representatives. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:45 — “It is usually confined to representative bodies, where the proceedings are published, since it enables constituents to know how their representatives voted on certain measures.”

**213.** `[C0999 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: therefore, it has exactly the _____ effect of a ballot vote.
   A. Assembly
   B. Meetings
   C. opposite
   D. division

   *Answer:* C) opposite. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:45 — “therefore, it has exactly the opposite effect of a ballot vote.”

**214.** `[C1000 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, _____ those present, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.
   A. Discharge a
   B. New Business
   C. one fifth of
   D. Same Session

   *Answer:* C) one fifth of. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:46 — “In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of those present, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.”

**215.** `[C1000 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of _____, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.

   *Answer:* those present — “In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of those present, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:46 — “In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of those present, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.”

**216.** `[C1000 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In local societies having a large _____ but relatively small attendance at meetings, a motion to take a vote by roll call is generally dilatory.
   A. membership
   B. committees
   C. recognized
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* A) membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:46 — “In local societies having a large membership but relatively small attendance at meetings, a motion to take a vote by roll call is generally dilatory.”

**217.** `[C1000 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In local societies having a large _____ but relatively small attendance at meetings, a motion to take a vote by roll call is generally dilatory.

   *Answer:* membership — “In local societies having a large membership but relatively small attendance at meetings, a motion to take a vote by roll call is generally dilatory.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:46 — “In local societies having a large membership but relatively small attendance at meetings, a motion to take a vote by roll call is generally dilatory.”

**218.** `[C1001 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a vote is to be taken by roll call (see 30 for the motion), the chair puts the question in a form like the _____:
   A. following
   B. postponed
   C. Privilege
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* A) following. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:47 — “When a vote is to be taken by roll call (see 30 for the motion), the chair puts the question in a form like the following:”

**219.** `[C1001 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a vote is to be taken by roll call (see 30 for the motion), the chair puts the question in a form like the _____:

   *Answer:* following — “When a vote is to be taken by roll call (see 30 for the motion), the chair puts the question in a form like the following:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:47 — “When a vote is to be taken by roll call (see 30 for the motion), the chair puts the question in a form like the following:”

**220.** `[C1002 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If he does not wish to vote, he answers _____ (or abstain).

   *Answer:* present — “If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or abstain).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:48 — “If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or abstain).”

**221.** `[C1002 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or _____).
   A. approve
   B. Charges
   C. abstain
   D. carried

   *Answer:* C) abstain. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:48 — “If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or abstain).”

**222.** `[C1002 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If he is not ready to vote, but wishes to be called on again after the roll has been completely called, he answers _____.

   *Answer:* pass — “If he is not ready to vote, but wishes to be called on again after the roll has been completely called, he answers pass.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:48 — “If he is not ready to vote, but wishes to be called on again after the roll has been completely called, he answers pass.”

**223.** `[C1002 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The roll is called in _____ order except that the presiding officer’s name is called last, and only when his vote will affect the result.
   A. adjournment
   B. constitution
   C. alphabetical
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) alphabetical. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:48 — “The roll is called in alphabetical order except that the presiding officer’s name is called last, and only when his vote will affect the result.”

**224.** `[C1002 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is too late, after one person has _____ to the roll call, to renew the debate.

   *Answer:* answered — “It is too late, after one person has answered to the roll call, to renew the debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:48 — “It is too late, after one person has answered to the roll call, to renew the debate.”

**225.** `[C1003 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and those answering _____ are tallied in a third column, to the far right or left.

   *Answer:* present — “and those answering present are tallied in a third column, to the far right or left.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:49 — “and those answering present are tallied in a third column, to the far right or left.”

**226.** `[C1003 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ repeats each member’s name and answer aloud as it is given and notes the answers to the roll call in separate columns.
   A. auditors’
   B. secretary
   C. amendment
   D. committee

   *Answer:* B) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:49 — “The secretary repeats each member’s name and answer aloud as it is given and notes the answers to the roll call in separate columns.”

**227.** `[C1003 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A convenient method of noting the answers is to write the number 1 to the left of the name of the first member answering in the _____, the number 2 to the left of the second name in the affirmative, and so on.

   *Answer:* affirmative — “A convenient method of noting the answers is to write the number 1 to the left of the name of the first member answering in the affirmative, the number 2 to the left of the second name in the affirmative, and so on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:49 — “A convenient method of noting the answers is to write the number 1 to the left of the name of the first member answering in the affirmative, the number 2 to the left of the second name in the affirmative, and so on.”

**228.** `[C1003 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The negative answers are treated _____ in a column to the right of the names;
   A. exhausted
   B. Precedent
   C. auditors’
   D. similarly

   *Answer:* D) similarly. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:49 — “The negative answers are treated similarly in a column to the right of the names;”

**229.** `[C1003 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A convenient method of noting the answers is to write the number 1 to the left of the name of the first member _____ in the affirmative, the number 2 to the left of the second name in the affirmative, and so on.

   *Answer:* answering — “A convenient method of noting the answers is to write the number 1 to the left of the name of the first member answering in the affirmative, the number 2 to the left of the second name in the affirmative, and so on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:49 — “A convenient method of noting the answers is to write the number 1 to the left of the name of the first member answering in the affirmative, the number 2 to the left of the second name in the affirmative, and so on.”

**230.** `[C1004 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At the _____ of the roll call, the names of those who failed to answer can be called again, or the chair can ask if anyone entered the room after his or her name was called.

   *Answer:* conclusion — “At the conclusion of the roll call, the names of those who failed to answer can be called again, or the chair can ask if anyone entered the room after his or her name was called.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:50 — “At the conclusion of the roll call, the names of those who failed to answer can be called again, or the chair can ask if anyone entered the room after his or her name was called.”

**231.** `[C1004 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each of these members who then responds can be assigned the final number for his or her vote or _____ in the proper column, continuing from the last number previously in the column, although the member’s number will appear in a position out of sequence.
   A. sustaining
   B. Procedures
   C. abstention
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) abstention. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:50 — “Each of these members who then responds can be assigned the final number for his or her vote or abstention in the proper column, continuing from the last number previously in the column, although the member’s number will appear in a position out of sequence.”

**232.** `[C1004 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Changes of vote are also _____ at this time, before the result is announced.

   *Answer:* permitted — “Changes of vote are also permitted at this time, before the result is announced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:50 — “Changes of vote are also permitted at this time, before the result is announced.”

**233.** `[C1004 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When this happens, the number beside the member’s name is struck through, the total for that column is adjusted _____, and the next, final number in the proper column is entered.
   A. assemblies
   B. Indefinitely
   C. constitution
   D. accordingly

   *Answer:* D) accordingly. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:50 — “When this happens, the number beside the member’s name is struck through, the total for that column is adjusted accordingly, and the next, final number in the proper column is entered.”

**234.** `[C1004 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Changes of vote may be limited when some _____ equipment is used.

   *Answer:* electronic — “Changes of vote may be limited when some electronic equipment is used.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:50 — “Changes of vote may be limited when some electronic equipment is used.”

**235.** `[C1005 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering _____, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.

   *Answer:* present — “The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering present, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:51 — “The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering present, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.”

**236.** `[C1005 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering present, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.
   A. rejecting
   B. secretary
   C. Objection
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* B) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:51 — “The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering present, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.”

**237.** `[C1005 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair, at his or her discretion, may direct, or the assembly may order (see 30), a “_____”—a procedure in which the secretary calls out the names, first, of the members who voted in the affirmative, second, of the members who voted in the negative, and third, of the members who answered present, with the chair calling for any necessary corrections to each category after the names in that category have been called.

   *Answer:* recapitulation — “The chair, at his or her discretion, may direct, or the assembly may order (see 30), a “recapitulation”—a procedure in which the secretary calls out the names, first, of the members who voted in the affirmative, second, of the members who voted in the negative, and third, of the members who answered present, with the chair calling for any necessary corrections to each category after the names in that category have been called.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:51 — “The chair, at his or her discretion, may direct, or the assembly may order (see 30), a “recapitulation”—a procedure in which the secretary calls out the names, first, of the members who voted in the affirmative, second, of the members who voted in the negative, and third, of the members who answered present, with the chair calling for any necessary corrections to each category after the names in that category have been called.”

**238.** `[C1005 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair, at his or her discretion, may direct, or the assembly may order (see 30), a “_____”—a procedure in which the secretary calls out the names, first, of the members who voted in the affirmative, second, of the members who voted in the negative, and third, of the members who answered present, with the chair calling for any necessary corrections to each category after the names in that category have been called.
   A. immediately
   B. recapitulation
   C. President-Elect
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) recapitulation. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:51 — “The chair, at his or her discretion, may direct, or the assembly may order (see 30), a “recapitulation”—a procedure in which the secretary calls out the names, first, of the members who voted in the affirmative, second, of the members who voted in the negative, and third, of the members who answered present, with the chair calling for any necessary corrections to each category after the names in that category have been called.”

**239.** `[C1006 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ voting, a record of how each member voted, as well as the result of the vote, is entered in full in the journal or minutes.

   *Answer:* roll-call — “In roll-call voting, a record of how each member voted, as well as the result of the vote, is entered in full in the journal or minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:52 — “In roll-call voting, a record of how each member voted, as well as the result of the vote, is entered in full in the journal or minutes.”

**240.** `[C1006 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If those _____ to the roll call do not total a sufficient number to constitute a quorum, the chair must direct the secretary to enter the names of enough members who are present but not voting to reflect the attendance of a quorum during the vote.
   A. Definitely
   B. responding
   C. Convention
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) responding. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:52 — “If those responding to the roll call do not total a sufficient number to constitute a quorum, the chair must direct the secretary to enter the names of enough members who are present but not voting to reflect the attendance of a quorum during the vote.”

**241.** `[C1006 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ voting, a record of how each member voted, as well as the result of the vote, is entered in full in the journal or minutes.
   A. execution
   B. roll-call
   C. Committee
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* B) roll-call. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:52 — “In roll-call voting, a record of how each member voted, as well as the result of the vote, is entered in full in the journal or minutes.”

**242.** `[C1006 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If those _____ to the roll call do not total a sufficient number to constitute a quorum, the chair must direct the secretary to enter the names of enough members who are present but not voting to reflect the attendance of a quorum during the vote.

   *Answer:* responding — “If those responding to the roll call do not total a sufficient number to constitute a quorum, the chair must direct the secretary to enter the names of enough members who are present but not voting to reflect the attendance of a quorum during the vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:52 — “If those responding to the roll call do not total a sufficient number to constitute a quorum, the chair must direct the secretary to enter the names of enough members who are present but not voting to reflect the attendance of a quorum during the vote.”

**243.** `[C1007 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: 145: _____.” In such cases, the chairman or spokesman of each delegation, as it is called in alphabetical or numerical order, responds by giving its vote, as: “Local No.

   *Answer:* 8 votes — “145: 8 votes.” In such cases, the chairman or spokesman of each delegation, as it is called in alphabetical or numerical order, responds by giving its vote, as: “Local No.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:53 — “145: 8 votes.” In such cases, the chairman or spokesman of each delegation, as it is called in alphabetical or numerical order, responds by giving its vote, as: “Local No.”

**244.** `[C1007 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ 5 ‘for’ and 3 ‘against.’” The secretary repeats this for confirmation and calls the next delegation.
   A. 145 votes
   B. Lay on the
   C. roll call
   D. the Rules

   *Answer:* A) 145 votes. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:53 — “145 votes 5 ‘for’ and 3 ‘against.’” The secretary repeats this for confirmation and calls the next delegation.”

**245.** `[C1007 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In large _____, the roll is sometimes called of entire delegations rather than of the individual members.

   *Answer:* conventions — “In large conventions, the roll is sometimes called of entire delegations rather than of the individual members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:53 — “In large conventions, the roll is sometimes called of entire delegations rather than of the individual members.”

**246.** `[C1007 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The secretary, in calling for the votes of a delegation, states the vote _____, as: “Local No.
   A. constitution
   B. undebatable
   C. entitlement
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) entitlement. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:53 — “The secretary, in calling for the votes of a delegation, states the vote entitlement, as: “Local No.”

**247.** `[C1007 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: 145: 8 votes.” In such cases, the chairman or spokesman of each delegation, as it is called in _____ or numerical order, responds by giving its vote, as: “Local No.

   *Answer:* alphabetical — “145: 8 votes.” In such cases, the chairman or spokesman of each delegation, as it is called in alphabetical or numerical order, responds by giving its vote, as: “Local No.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:53 — “145: 8 votes.” In such cases, the chairman or spokesman of each delegation, as it is called in alphabetical or numerical order, responds by giving its vote, as: “Local No.”

**248.** `[C1009 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Various forms of _____ devices have become available to take the place of a roll-call vote.
   A. electronic
   B. Particular
   C. incidental
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) electronic. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:55 — “Various forms of electronic devices have become available to take the place of a roll-call vote.”

**249.** `[C1009 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any _____ body can use such a system with appropriate adjustments to conform as closely as possible with the rules given above for roll-call voting procedure.

   *Answer:* deliberative — “Any deliberative body can use such a system with appropriate adjustments to conform as closely as possible with the rules given above for roll-call voting procedure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:55 — “Any deliberative body can use such a system with appropriate adjustments to conform as closely as possible with the rules given above for roll-call voting procedure.”

**250.** `[C1009 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When used, there is usually a _____ of technical, mechanical accuracy of the electronic system if properly used by the members.
   A. interrupted
   B. committees
   C. assemblies
   D. presumption

   *Answer:* D) presumption. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:55 — “When used, there is usually a presumption of technical, mechanical accuracy of the electronic system if properly used by the members.”

**251.** `[C1009 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Changes of votes after the result has been announced by the chair on the allegation of machine error are not _____.

   *Answer:* entertained — “Changes of votes after the result has been announced by the chair on the allegation of machine error are not entertained.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:55 — “Changes of votes after the result has been announced by the chair on the allegation of machine error are not entertained.”

**252.** `[C1009 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Also, steps must be taken to prevent members from being able to vote more than once by using a _____’s keypad, or a member lending his keypad to a friend so that the friend can vote for him in his absence by “proxy.”
   A. neighbor
   B. dilatory
   C. business
   D. Standard

   *Answer:* A) neighbor. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:55 — “Also, steps must be taken to prevent members from being able to vote more than once by using a neighbor’s keypad, or a member lending his keypad to a friend so that the friend can vote for him in his absence by “proxy.””

**253.** `[C1010 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is a fundamental principle of _____ law that the right to vote is limited to the members of an organization who are actually present at the time the vote is taken in a regular or properly called meeting, although it should be noted that a member need not be present when the question is put.
   A. parliamentary
   B. Indefinitely
   C. constitution
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:56 — “It is a fundamental principle of parliamentary law that the right to vote is limited to the members of an organization who are actually present at the time the vote is taken in a regular or properly called meeting, although it should be noted that a member need not be present when the question is put.”

**254.** `[C1010 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ to this rule must be expressly stated in the bylaws.

   *Answer:* Exceptions — “Exceptions to this rule must be expressly stated in the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:56 — “Exceptions to this rule must be expressly stated in the bylaws.”

**255.** `[C1010 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is a fundamental principle of parliamentary law that the right to vote is limited to the members of an _____ who are actually present at the time the vote is taken in a regular or properly called meeting, although it should be noted that a member need not be present when the question is put.
   A. Particular
   B. interrupted
   C. undebatable
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:56 — “It is a fundamental principle of parliamentary law that the right to vote is limited to the members of an organization who are actually present at the time the vote is taken in a regular or properly called meeting, although it should be noted that a member need not be present when the question is put.”

**256.** `[C1010 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The votes of those present could be affected by debate, by _____, and perhaps by the need for repeated balloting, while those absent would be unable to adjust their votes to reflect these factors.

   *Answer:* amendments — “The votes of those present could be affected by debate, by amendments, and perhaps by the need for repeated balloting, while those absent would be unable to adjust their votes to reflect these factors.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:56 — “The votes of those present could be affected by debate, by amendments, and perhaps by the need for repeated balloting, while those absent would be unable to adjust their votes to reflect these factors.”

**257.** `[C1010 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, the absentee ballots would in most cases be on a somewhat different question than that on which those present were voting, leading to confusion, unfairness, and inaccuracy in determining the result.
   A. consideration
   B. undebatable
   C. Consequently
   D. Information

   *Answer:* C) Consequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:56 — “Consequently, the absentee ballots would in most cases be on a somewhat different question than that on which those present were voting, leading to confusion, unfairness, and inaccuracy in determining the result.”

**258.** `[C1011 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____, when authorized in the bylaws, is generally reserved for important issues, such as an amendment to the bylaws or an election of officers—on which a full vote of the membership is desirable even though only a small fraction of the members normally attend meetings.

   *Answer:* vote by mail — “A vote by mail, when authorized in the bylaws, is generally reserved for important issues, such as an amendment to the bylaws or an election of officers—on which a full vote of the membership is desirable even though only a small fraction of the members normally attend meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:57 — “A vote by mail, when authorized in the bylaws, is generally reserved for important issues, such as an amendment to the bylaws or an election of officers—on which a full vote of the membership is desirable even though only a small fraction of the members normally attend meetings.”

**259.** `[C1011 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A vote by mail, when _____ in the bylaws, is generally reserved for important issues, such as an amendment to the bylaws or an election of officers—on which a full vote of the membership is desirable even though only a small fraction of the members normally attend meetings.
   A. Particular
   B. introduced
   C. authorized
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) authorized. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:57 — “A vote by mail, when authorized in the bylaws, is generally reserved for important issues, such as an amendment to the bylaws or an election of officers—on which a full vote of the membership is desirable even though only a small fraction of the members normally attend meetings.”

**260.** `[C1011 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Situations of this kind frequently occur in scientific societies or in alumni _____ whose members may be in many countries.

   *Answer:* associations — “Situations of this kind frequently occur in scientific societies or in alumni associations whose members may be in many countries.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:57 — “Situations of this kind frequently occur in scientific societies or in alumni associations whose members may be in many countries.”

**261.** `[C1011 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Situations of this kind frequently occur in scientific societies or in alumni _____ whose members may be in many countries.
   A. Permission
   B. associations
   C. requirement
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) associations. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:57 — “Situations of this kind frequently occur in scientific societies or in alumni associations whose members may be in many countries.”

**262.** `[C1012 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For a vote by mail—so that there may be no question of the result in the event that the vote is close—it is important that the mailing list used exactly _____ to the current official roll of voting members.

   *Answer:* correspond — “For a vote by mail—so that there may be no question of the result in the event that the vote is close—it is important that the mailing list used exactly correspond to the current official roll of voting members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:58 — “For a vote by mail—so that there may be no question of the result in the event that the vote is close—it is important that the mailing list used exactly correspond to the current official roll of voting members.”

**263.** `[C1012 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For this purpose, the _____ furnishes to the chairman of tellers or other official in charge of issuing the ballots a list of the names and mailing addresses of record of all persons entitled to vote, which the secretary certifies as corrected to the date as of which the ballots are issued.
   A. exhausted
   B. auditors’
   C. secretary
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* C) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:58 — “For this purpose, the secretary furnishes to the chairman of tellers or other official in charge of issuing the ballots a list of the names and mailing addresses of record of all persons entitled to vote, which the secretary certifies as corrected to the date as of which the ballots are issued.”

**264.** `[C1012 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each nominee may be allowed to furnish for enclosure with the ballots a brief factual statement of his service and _____, provided that all nominees are accorded equal opportunity and space.
