   *Answer:* qualifications — “Each nominee may be allowed to furnish for enclosure with the ballots a brief factual statement of his service and qualifications, provided that all nominees are accorded equal opportunity and space.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:58 — “Each nominee may be allowed to furnish for enclosure with the ballots a brief factual statement of his service and qualifications, provided that all nominees are accorded equal opportunity and space.”

**265.** `[C1012 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For a vote by mail—so that there may be no question of the result in the event that the vote is close—it is important that the mailing list used exactly _____ to the current official roll of voting members.
   A. assemblies
   B. convention
   C. correspond
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* C) correspond. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:58 — “For a vote by mail—so that there may be no question of the result in the event that the vote is close—it is important that the mailing list used exactly correspond to the current official roll of voting members.”

**266.** `[C1012 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For this purpose, the _____ furnishes to the chairman of tellers or other official in charge of issuing the ballots a list of the names and mailing addresses of record of all persons entitled to vote, which the secretary certifies as corrected to the date as of which the ballots are issued.

   *Answer:* secretary — “For this purpose, the secretary furnishes to the chairman of tellers or other official in charge of issuing the ballots a list of the names and mailing addresses of record of all persons entitled to vote, which the secretary certifies as corrected to the date as of which the ballots are issued.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:58 — “For this purpose, the secretary furnishes to the chairman of tellers or other official in charge of issuing the ballots a list of the names and mailing addresses of record of all persons entitled to vote, which the secretary certifies as corrected to the date as of which the ballots are issued.”

**267.** `[C1013 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the vote is not to be secret, the following items should be sent to each qualified voter: (1) a printed ballot containing a space for the voter’s signature, to ensure against votes being cast by persons not entitled to vote, together with full _____ for marking and returning by the required date;

   *Answer:* instructions — “If the vote is not to be secret, the following items should be sent to each qualified voter: (1) a printed ballot containing a space for the voter’s signature, to ensure against votes being cast by persons not entitled to vote, together with full instructions for marking and returning by the required date;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:59 — “If the vote is not to be secret, the following items should be sent to each qualified voter: (1) a printed ballot containing a space for the voter’s signature, to ensure against votes being cast by persons not entitled to vote, together with full instructions for marking and returning by the required date;”

**268.** `[C1013 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and (2) a specially recognizable, _____ return envelope with the name and address of the secretary, the chairman of tellers, or other person designated to receive the marked ballot.
   A. requirement
   B. Announcements
   C. self-addressed
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* C) self-addressed. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:59 — “and (2) a specially recognizable, self-addressed return envelope with the name and address of the secretary, the chairman of tellers, or other person designated to receive the marked ballot.”

**269.** `[C1013 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: E-mail and other means of electronic _____ can be tailored to comply with these requirements.

   *Answer:* communication — “E-mail and other means of electronic communication can be tailored to comply with these requirements.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:59 — “E-mail and other means of electronic communication can be tailored to comply with these requirements.”

**270.** `[C1013 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the vote is not to be secret, the following items should be sent to each qualified voter: (1) a printed ballot containing a space for the voter’s signature, to ensure against votes being cast by persons not entitled to vote, together with full _____ for marking and returning by the required date;
   A. subsequently
   B. adjournment
   C. instructions
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:59 — “If the vote is not to be secret, the following items should be sent to each qualified voter: (1) a printed ballot containing a space for the voter’s signature, to ensure against votes being cast by persons not entitled to vote, together with full instructions for marking and returning by the required date;”

**271.** `[C1013 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and (2) a specially recognizable, _____ return envelope with the name and address of the secretary, the chairman of tellers, or other person designated to receive the marked ballot.

   *Answer:* self-addressed — “and (2) a specially recognizable, self-addressed return envelope with the name and address of the secretary, the chairman of tellers, or other person designated to receive the marked ballot.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:59 — “and (2) a specially recognizable, self-addressed return envelope with the name and address of the secretary, the chairman of tellers, or other person designated to receive the marked ballot.”

**272.** `[C1014 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the vote is to be secret, an inner return envelope—with a space for the voter’s signature placed on its face instead of on the ballot—should be sent to the voter with the ballot, in addition to the _____ outer return envelope described above.

   *Answer:* self-addressed — “If the vote is to be secret, an inner return envelope—with a space for the voter’s signature placed on its face instead of on the ballot—should be sent to the voter with the ballot, in addition to the self-addressed outer return envelope described above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:60 — “If the vote is to be secret, an inner return envelope—with a space for the voter’s signature placed on its face instead of on the ballot—should be sent to the voter with the ballot, in addition to the self-addressed outer return envelope described above.”

**273.** `[C1014 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The ballot sent to the voter should be prefolded a _____ number of times so that—when returned marked and refolded in the same manner and sealed in the inner envelope —there will be no chance of accidental observance of the member’s vote by the teller who removes the ballot from the inner envelope.
   A. sufficient
   B. privileged
   C. Substitute
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) sufficient. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:60 — “The ballot sent to the voter should be prefolded a sufficient number of times so that—when returned marked and refolded in the same manner and sealed in the inner envelope —there will be no chance of accidental observance of the member’s vote by the teller who removes the ballot from the inner envelope.”

**274.** `[C1014 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The person _____ as addressee for the returned ballots holds them in the outer envelopes for delivery, unopened, at the meeting of the tellers where the votes are to be counted.

   *Answer:* designated — “The person designated as addressee for the returned ballots holds them in the outer envelopes for delivery, unopened, at the meeting of the tellers where the votes are to be counted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:60 — “The person designated as addressee for the returned ballots holds them in the outer envelopes for delivery, unopened, at the meeting of the tellers where the votes are to be counted.”

**275.** `[C1014 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The person designated as addressee for the returned ballots holds them in the outer _____ for delivery, unopened, at the meeting of the tellers where the votes are to be counted.
   A. Crestwood
   B. envelopes
   C. Signature
   D. extending

   *Answer:* B) envelopes. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:60 — “The person designated as addressee for the returned ballots holds them in the outer envelopes for delivery, unopened, at the meeting of the tellers where the votes are to be counted.”

**276.** `[C1014 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the _____ by which the tellers remove the ballots from the inner envelopes, each envelope and ballot is handled in the following manner: (1) the signature on the envelope is checked against the list of qualified voters;

   *Answer:* procedure — “In the procedure by which the tellers remove the ballots from the inner envelopes, each envelope and ballot is handled in the following manner: (1) the signature on the envelope is checked against the list of qualified voters;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:60 — “In the procedure by which the tellers remove the ballots from the inner envelopes, each envelope and ballot is handled in the following manner: (1) the signature on the envelope is checked against the list of qualified voters;”

**277.** `[C1015 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In order to ensure the _____ and the secrecy of such a vote by mail, special care should be taken in all phases of handling the ballots.

   *Answer:* accuracy — “In order to ensure the accuracy and the secrecy of such a vote by mail, special care should be taken in all phases of handling the ballots.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:61 — “In order to ensure the accuracy and the secrecy of such a vote by mail, special care should be taken in all phases of handling the ballots.”

**278.** `[C1015 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chairman of tellers or other person _____ must be able to certify the results from both of these standpoints.
   A. authorizes
   B. Permission
   C. responsible
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) responsible. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:61 — “The chairman of tellers or other person responsible must be able to certify the results from both of these standpoints.”

**279.** `[C1015 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Should the recipient of the ballots receive two evidently sent in by the same voter, the above procedure permits the voter to be contacted for a _____ of which is the voter’s true vote and, if both are, which (the most recent) is to be counted.

   *Answer:* determination — “Should the recipient of the ballots receive two evidently sent in by the same voter, the above procedure permits the voter to be contacted for a determination of which is the voter’s true vote and, if both are, which (the most recent) is to be counted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:61 — “Should the recipient of the ballots receive two evidently sent in by the same voter, the above procedure permits the voter to be contacted for a determination of which is the voter’s true vote and, if both are, which (the most recent) is to be counted.”

**280.** `[C1015 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As with respect to nonsecret ballots, e-mail and other means of electronic _____ may be able to be tailored to comply with the above requirements for secret mail balloting.
   A. communication
   B. subsequently
   C. Parliamentary
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* A) communication. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:61 — “As with respect to nonsecret ballots, e-mail and other means of electronic communication may be able to be tailored to comply with the above requirements for secret mail balloting.”

**281.** `[C1015 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In order to ensure the _____ and the secrecy of such a vote by mail, special care should be taken in all phases of handling the ballots.
   A. accuracy
   B. meetings
   C. Standard
   D. Assembly

   *Answer:* A) accuracy. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:61 — “In order to ensure the accuracy and the secrecy of such a vote by mail, special care should be taken in all phases of handling the ballots.”

**282.** `[C1016 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____ refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a majority.

   *Answer:* preferential voting — “The term preferential voting refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:62 — “The term preferential voting refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a majority.”

**283.** `[C1016 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term preferential voting refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a _____.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:62 — “The term preferential voting refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a majority.”

**284.** `[C1016 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases it makes possible a more representative result than under a rule that a _____ shall elect.

   *Answer:* plurality — “In such cases it makes possible a more representative result than under a rule that a plurality shall elect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:62 — “In such cases it makes possible a more representative result than under a rule that a plurality shall elect.”

**285.** `[C1016 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such cases it makes possible a more _____ result than under a rule that a plurality shall elect.
   A. consideration
   B. presentation
   C. representative
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* C) representative. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:62 — “In such cases it makes possible a more representative result than under a rule that a plurality shall elect.”

**286.** `[C1017 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If more than half of the ballots show one candidate or proposition indicated as first choice, that choice has a _____ in the ordinary sense and the candidate is elected or the proposition is decided upon.

   *Answer:* majority — “If more than half of the ballots show one candidate or proposition indicated as first choice, that choice has a majority in the ordinary sense and the candidate is elected or the proposition is decided upon.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:63 — “If more than half of the ballots show one candidate or proposition indicated as first choice, that choice has a majority in the ordinary sense and the candidate is elected or the proposition is decided upon.”

**287.** `[C1017 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ voting has many variations.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. interrupted
   C. Preferential
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) Preferential. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:63 — “Preferential voting has many variations.”

**288.** `[C1017 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: One method is described here by way of _____.

   *Answer:* illustration — “One method is described here by way of illustration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:63 — “One method is described here by way of illustration.”

**289.** `[C1017 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the preferential ballot —for each office to be filled or _____ question to be decided—the voter is asked to indicate the order in which he prefers all the candidates or propositions, placing the numeral 1 beside his first preference, the numeral 2 beside his second preference, and so on for every possible choice.
   A. multiple-choice
   B. constitution
   C. representatives
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* A) multiple-choice. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:63 — “On the preferential ballot —for each office to be filled or multiple-choice question to be decided—the voter is asked to indicate the order in which he prefers all the candidates or propositions, placing the numeral 1 beside his first preference, the numeral 2 beside his second preference, and so on for every possible choice.”

**290.** `[C1017 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In counting the votes for a given office or question, the ballots are arranged in piles according to the indicated first _____— one pile for each candidate or proposition.

   *Answer:* preferences — “In counting the votes for a given office or question, the ballots are arranged in piles according to the indicated first preferences— one pile for each candidate or proposition.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:63 — “In counting the votes for a given office or question, the ballots are arranged in piles according to the indicated first preferences— one pile for each candidate or proposition.”

**291.** `[C1018 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The ballots in the thinnest pile—that is, those containing the name designated as first choice by the fewest number of voters—are _____ into the other piles according to the names marked as second choice on these ballots.

   *Answer:* redistributed — “The ballots in the thinnest pile—that is, those containing the name designated as first choice by the fewest number of voters—are redistributed into the other piles according to the names marked as second choice on these ballots.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:64 — “The ballots in the thinnest pile—that is, those containing the name designated as first choice by the fewest number of voters—are redistributed into the other piles according to the names marked as second choice on these ballots.”

**292.** `[C1018 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The number of ballots in each remaining pile after this _____ is again recorded.
   A. resolutions
   B. deliberative
   C. distribution
   D. Information

   *Answer:* C) distribution. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:64 — “The number of ballots in each remaining pile after this distribution is again recorded.”

**293.** `[C1018 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If more than half of the ballots are now in one pile, that candidate or _____ is elected or decided upon.

   *Answer:* proposition — “If more than half of the ballots are now in one pile, that candidate or proposition is elected or decided upon.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:64 — “If more than half of the ballots are now in one pile, that candidate or proposition is elected or decided upon.”

**294.** `[C1018 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If not, the next least popular candidate or proposition is similarly eliminated, by taking the thinnest remaining pile and _____ its ballots according to their second choices into the other piles, except that, if the name eliminated in the last distribution is indicated as second choice on a ballot, that ballot is placed according to its third choice.
   A. redistributing
   B. Information
   C. Responsibilities
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* A) redistributing. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:64 — “If not, the next least popular candidate or proposition is similarly eliminated, by taking the thinnest remaining pile and redistributing its ballots according to their second choices into the other piles, except that, if the name eliminated in the last distribution is indicated as second choice on a ballot, that ballot is placed according to its third choice.”

**295.** `[C1018 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The ballots in the thinnest pile—that is, those containing the name designated as first choice by the fewest number of voters—are _____ into the other piles according to the names marked as second choice on these ballots.
   A. Information
   B. redistributed
   C. Parliamentary
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) redistributed. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:64 — “The ballots in the thinnest pile—that is, those containing the name designated as first choice by the fewest number of voters—are redistributed into the other piles according to the names marked as second choice on these ballots.”

**296.** `[C1019 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The tellers’ report consists of a table listing all candidates or _____, with the number of ballots that were in each pile after each successive distribution.

   *Answer:* propositions — “The tellers’ report consists of a table listing all candidates or propositions, with the number of ballots that were in each pile after each successive distribution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:65 — “The tellers’ report consists of a table listing all candidates or propositions, with the number of ballots that were in each pile after each successive distribution.”

**297.** `[C1019 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The tellers’ report consists of a table listing all candidates or _____, with the number of ballots that were in each pile after each successive distribution.
   A. Particular
   B. propositions
   C. resolutions
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) propositions. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:65 — “The tellers’ report consists of a table listing all candidates or propositions, with the number of ballots that were in each pile after each successive distribution.”

**298.** `[C1020 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a ballot having one or more names not marked with any numeral comes up for placement at any stage of the counting and all of its marked names have been _____, it is not placed in any pile, but instead is set aside.

   *Answer:* eliminated — “If a ballot having one or more names not marked with any numeral comes up for placement at any stage of the counting and all of its marked names have been eliminated, it is not placed in any pile, but instead is set aside.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:66 — “If a ballot having one or more names not marked with any numeral comes up for placement at any stage of the counting and all of its marked names have been eliminated, it is not placed in any pile, but instead is set aside.”

**299.** `[C1020 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If at any point two or more candidates or propositions are tied for the least popular position, the ballots in their piles are _____ in a single step, all of the tied names being treated as eliminated.
   A. constitution
   B. redistributed
   C. subsequently
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* B) redistributed. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:66 — “If at any point two or more candidates or propositions are tied for the least popular position, the ballots in their piles are redistributed in a single step, all of the tied names being treated as eliminated.”

**300.** `[C1020 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the event of a tie in the winning position—which would imply that the elimination process is continued until the ballots are reduced to two or more equal piles—the election is resolved in favor of the candidate or proposition that was strongest in terms of first choices (by referring to the record of the first _____).

   *Answer:* distribution — “In the event of a tie in the winning position—which would imply that the elimination process is continued until the ballots are reduced to two or more equal piles—the election is resolved in favor of the candidate or proposition that was strongest in terms of first choices (by referring to the record of the first distribution).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:66 — “In the event of a tie in the winning position—which would imply that the elimination process is continued until the ballots are reduced to two or more equal piles—the election is resolved in favor of the candidate or proposition that was strongest in terms of first choices (by referring to the record of the first distribution).”

**301.** `[C1020 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a ballot having one or more names not marked with any numeral comes up for placement at any stage of the counting and all of its marked names have been _____, it is not placed in any pile, but instead is set aside.
   A. Particular
   B. authorizes
   C. Substitute
   D. eliminated

   *Answer:* D) eliminated. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:66 — “If a ballot having one or more names not marked with any numeral comes up for placement at any stage of the counting and all of its marked names have been eliminated, it is not placed in any pile, but instead is set aside.”

**302.** `[C1020 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If at any point two or more candidates or propositions are tied for the least popular position, the ballots in their piles are _____ in a single step, all of the tied names being treated as eliminated.

   *Answer:* redistributed — “If at any point two or more candidates or propositions are tied for the least popular position, the ballots in their piles are redistributed in a single step, all of the tied names being treated as eliminated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:66 — “If at any point two or more candidates or propositions are tied for the least popular position, the ballots in their piles are redistributed in a single step, all of the tied names being treated as eliminated.”

**303.** `[C1021 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If more than one person is to be elected to the same type of office—for example, if _____ of a board are to be chosen—the voters can indicate their order of preference among the names in a single list of candidates, just as if only one was to be elected.

   *Answer:* three members — “If more than one person is to be elected to the same type of office—for example, if three members of a board are to be chosen—the voters can indicate their order of preference among the names in a single list of candidates, just as if only one was to be elected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:67 — “If more than one person is to be elected to the same type of office—for example, if three members of a board are to be chosen—the voters can indicate their order of preference among the names in a single list of candidates, just as if only one was to be elected.”

**304.** `[C1021 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If more than one person is to be elected to the same type of office—for example, if three members of a board are to be chosen—the voters can indicate their order of _____ among the names in a single list of candidates, just as if only one was to be elected.
   A. sustaining
   B. resolution
   C. preference
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* C) preference. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:67 — “If more than one person is to be elected to the same type of office—for example, if three members of a board are to be chosen—the voters can indicate their order of preference among the names in a single list of candidates, just as if only one was to be elected.”

**305.** `[C1022 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In fact, it may prevent any candidate from receiving a _____ and require the voting to be repeated.

   *Answer:* majority — “In fact, it may prevent any candidate from receiving a majority and require the voting to be repeated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:68 — “In fact, it may prevent any candidate from receiving a majority and require the voting to be repeated.”

**306.** `[C1022 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When this or any other system of _____ voting is to be used, the voting and counting procedure must be precisely established in advance and should be prescribed in detail.
   A. consideration
   B. preferential
   C. subsequently
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) preferential. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:68 — “When this or any other system of preferential voting is to be used, the voting and counting procedure must be precisely established in advance and should be prescribed in detail.”

**307.** `[C1022 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The members must be thoroughly instructed as to how to mark the ballot, and should have sufficient _____ of the counting process to enable them to have confidence in the method.

   *Answer:* understanding — “The members must be thoroughly instructed as to how to mark the ballot, and should have sufficient understanding of the counting process to enable them to have confidence in the method.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:68 — “The members must be thoroughly instructed as to how to mark the ballot, and should have sufficient understanding of the counting process to enable them to have confidence in the method.”

**308.** `[C1022 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes, for instance, voters decline to indicate a second or other choice, _____ believing that such a course increases the chances of their first choice.
   A. Substitute
   B. mistakenly
   C. resolution
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) mistakenly. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:68 — “Sometimes, for instance, voters decline to indicate a second or other choice, mistakenly believing that such a course increases the chances of their first choice.”

**309.** `[C1022 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In fact, it may prevent any _____ from receiving a majority and require the voting to be repeated.

   *Answer:* candidate — “In fact, it may prevent any candidate from receiving a majority and require the voting to be repeated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:68 — “In fact, it may prevent any candidate from receiving a majority and require the voting to be repeated.”

**310.** `[C1023 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The system of preferential voting just described should not be used in cases where it is possible to follow the normal procedure of repeated balloting until one candidate or proposition attains a _____.

   *Answer:* majority — “The system of preferential voting just described should not be used in cases where it is possible to follow the normal procedure of repeated balloting until one candidate or proposition attains a majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:69 — “The system of preferential voting just described should not be used in cases where it is possible to follow the normal procedure of repeated balloting until one candidate or proposition attains a majority.”

**311.** `[C1023 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although this type of preferential ballot is preferable to an election by _____, it affords less freedom of choice than repeated balloting, because it denies voters the opportunity of basing their second or lesser choices on the results of earlier ballots, and because the candidate or proposition in last place is automatically eliminated and may thus be prevented from becoming a compromise choice.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A plurality

   *Answer:* D) A plurality. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:69 — “Although this type of preferential ballot is preferable to an election by plurality, it affords less freedom of choice than repeated balloting, because it denies voters the opportunity of basing their second or lesser choices on the results of earlier ballots, and because the candidate or proposition in last place is automatically eliminated and may thus be prevented from becoming a compromise choice.”

**312.** `[C1023 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The system of _____ voting just described should not be used in cases where it is possible to follow the normal procedure of repeated balloting until one candidate or proposition attains a majority.

   *Answer:* preferential — “The system of preferential voting just described should not be used in cases where it is possible to follow the normal procedure of repeated balloting until one candidate or proposition attains a majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:69 — “The system of preferential voting just described should not be used in cases where it is possible to follow the normal procedure of repeated balloting until one candidate or proposition attains a majority.”

**313.** `[C1023 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although this type of preferential ballot is preferable to an election by plurality, it affords less freedom of choice than repeated balloting, because it denies voters the opportunity of basing their second or lesser choices on the results of earlier ballots, and because the candidate or proposition in last place is _____ eliminated and may thus be prevented from becoming a compromise choice.
   A. presentation
   B. President-Elect
   C. constitution
   D. automatically

   *Answer:* D) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:69 — “Although this type of preferential ballot is preferable to an election by plurality, it affords less freedom of choice than repeated balloting, because it denies voters the opportunity of basing their second or lesser choices on the results of earlier ballots, and because the candidate or proposition in last place is automatically eliminated and may thus be prevented from becoming a compromise choice.”

**314.** `[C1024 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ is not permitted in ordinary deliberative assemblies unless the laws of the state in which the society is incorporated require it, or the charter or bylaws of the organization provide for it.

   *Answer:* Proxy voting — “Proxy voting is not permitted in ordinary deliberative assemblies unless the laws of the state in which the society is incorporated require it, or the charter or bylaws of the organization provide for it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:70 — “Proxy voting is not permitted in ordinary deliberative assemblies unless the laws of the state in which the society is incorporated require it, or the charter or bylaws of the organization provide for it.”

**315.** `[C1024 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ is a power of attorney given by one person to another to vote in his stead;
   A. proxy
   B. Again
   C. floor
   D. Forum

   *Answer:* A) proxy. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:70 — “A proxy is a power of attorney given by one person to another to vote in his stead;”

**316.** `[C1024 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A proxy is a power of _____ given by one person to another to vote in his stead;

   *Answer:* attorney — “A proxy is a power of attorney given by one person to another to vote in his stead;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:70 — “A proxy is a power of attorney given by one person to another to vote in his stead;”

**317.** `[C1024 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: the term also _____ the person who holds the power of attorney.
   A. recognized
   B. authorizes
   C. designates
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) designates. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:70 — “the term also designates the person who holds the power of attorney.”

**318.** `[C1024 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Proxy voting is not permitted in ordinary _____ assemblies unless the laws of the state in which the society is incorporated require it, or the charter or bylaws of the organization provide for it.

   *Answer:* deliberative — “Proxy voting is not permitted in ordinary deliberative assemblies unless the laws of the state in which the society is incorporated require it, or the charter or bylaws of the organization provide for it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:70 — “Proxy voting is not permitted in ordinary deliberative assemblies unless the laws of the state in which the society is incorporated require it, or the charter or bylaws of the organization provide for it.”

**319.** `[C1025 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the law under which an organization is incorporated allows proxy voting to be prohibited by a provision of the bylaws, the adoption of this book as _____ authority by prescription in the bylaws should be treated as sufficient provision to accomplish that result (cf.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “If the law under which an organization is incorporated allows proxy voting to be prohibited by a provision of the bylaws, the adoption of this book as parliamentary authority by prescription in the bylaws should be treated as sufficient provision to accomplish that result (cf.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:71 — “If the law under which an organization is incorporated allows proxy voting to be prohibited by a provision of the bylaws, the adoption of this book as parliamentary authority by prescription in the bylaws should be treated as sufficient provision to accomplish that result (cf.”

**320.** `[C1025 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the law under which an organization is incorporated allows proxy voting to be prohibited by a provision of the bylaws, the adoption of this book as _____ authority by prescription in the bylaws should be treated as sufficient provision to accomplish that result (cf.
   A. subsequently
   B. Announcements
   C. President-Elect
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:71 — “If the law under which an organization is incorporated allows proxy voting to be prohibited by a provision of the bylaws, the adoption of this book as parliamentary authority by prescription in the bylaws should be treated as sufficient provision to accomplish that result (cf.”

**321.** `[C1026 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to take an informal straw poll to “test the water” is not in order because it neither adopts nor rejects a measure and hence is _____ and dilatory.

   *Answer:* meaningless — “A motion to take an informal straw poll to “test the water” is not in order because it neither adopts nor rejects a measure and hence is meaningless and dilatory.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:72 — “A motion to take an informal straw poll to “test the water” is not in order because it neither adopts nor rejects a measure and hence is meaningless and dilatory.”

**322.** `[C1026 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly wishes to discuss and take a vote on a matter without the vote _____ final action by the assembly, it may instead vote to go into a committee of the whole or a quasi committee of the whole (52).
   A. constitution
   B. constituting
   C. interrupted
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* B) constituting. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:72 — “If the assembly wishes to discuss and take a vote on a matter without the vote constituting final action by the assembly, it may instead vote to go into a committee of the whole or a quasi committee of the whole (52).”

**323.** `[C1026 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under these procedures, the assembly considers the matter as would a committee, and its vote while in committee of the whole (or quasi committee of the whole) serves only as a _____ to the assembly, which the assembly is free to reject just as would be the case with regard to the report of any ordinary committee.

   *Answer:* recommendation — “Under these procedures, the assembly considers the matter as would a committee, and its vote while in committee of the whole (or quasi committee of the whole) serves only as a recommendation to the assembly, which the assembly is free to reject just as would be the case with regard to the report of any ordinary committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:72 — “Under these procedures, the assembly considers the matter as would a committee, and its vote while in committee of the whole (or quasi committee of the whole) serves only as a recommendation to the assembly, which the assembly is free to reject just as would be the case with regard to the report of any ordinary committee.”

**324.** `[C1026 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to take an informal straw poll to “test the water” is not in order because it neither adopts nor rejects a measure and hence is _____ and dilatory.
   A. meaningless
   B. committees
   C. assemblies
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) meaningless. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:72 — “A motion to take an informal straw poll to “test the water” is not in order because it neither adopts nor rejects a measure and hence is meaningless and dilatory.”

**325.** `[C1026 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly wishes to discuss and take a vote on a matter without the vote _____ final action by the assembly, it may instead vote to go into a committee of the whole or a quasi committee of the whole (52).

   *Answer:* constituting — “If the assembly wishes to discuss and take a vote on a matter without the vote constituting final action by the assembly, it may instead vote to go into a committee of the whole or a quasi committee of the whole (52).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:72 — “If the assembly wishes to discuss and take a vote on a matter without the vote constituting final action by the assembly, it may instead vote to go into a committee of the whole or a quasi committee of the whole (52).”

**326.** `[C1027 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is, in effect, a proposal to fill the blank in an assumed motion “that be elected” to the specified position.

   *Answer:* nomination — “A nomination is, in effect, a proposal to fill the blank in an assumed motion “that be elected” to the specified position.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:1 — “A nomination is, in effect, a proposal to fill the blank in an assumed motion “that be elected” to the specified position.”

**327.** `[C1027 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In choosing someone to fill an office or other elective position in a society or assembly, a more effective freedom of choice is _____ through the practice of nominating persons for the office, rather than moving that a given person be elected as in the older British procedure.
   A. maintained
   B. authorizes
   C. settlement
   D. subsidiary

   *Answer:* A) maintained. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:1 — “In choosing someone to fill an office or other elective position in a society or assembly, a more effective freedom of choice is maintained through the practice of nominating persons for the office, rather than moving that a given person be elected as in the older British procedure.”

**328.** `[C1027 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Hence, a form of ballot on which provision is made for voting “for” or “against” a candidate or candidates, as _____ from a motion, is not proper.

   *Answer:* distinguished — “Hence, a form of ballot on which provision is made for voting “for” or “against” a candidate or candidates, as distinguished from a motion, is not proper.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:1 — “Hence, a form of ballot on which provision is made for voting “for” or “against” a candidate or candidates, as distinguished from a motion, is not proper.”

**329.** `[C1027 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Hence, a form of ballot on which provision is made for voting “for” or “against” a _____ or candidates, as distinguished from a motion, is not proper.
   A. extending
   B. expressly
   C. Precedent
   D. candidate

   *Answer:* D) candidate. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:1 — “Hence, a form of ballot on which provision is made for voting “for” or “against” a candidate or candidates, as distinguished from a motion, is not proper.”

**330.** `[C1027 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ is, in effect, a proposal to fill the blank in an assumed motion “that be elected” to the specified position.
   A. nomination
   B. Unfinished
   C. Definitely
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* A) nomination. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:1 — “A nomination is, in effect, a proposal to fill the blank in an assumed motion “that be elected” to the specified position.”

**331.** `[C1028 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Without nominations, voting might have to be repeated many times before a candidate achieved the required _____.

   *Answer:* majority — “Without nominations, voting might have to be repeated many times before a candidate achieved the required majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:2 — “Without nominations, voting might have to be repeated many times before a candidate achieved the required majority.”

**332.** `[C1028 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Strictly speaking, _____ are not necessary when an election is by ballot or roll call, since each member is free to vote for any eligible person, whether he has been nominated or not.
   A. Particular
   B. Organization
   C. nominations
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:2 — “Strictly speaking, nominations are not necessary when an election is by ballot or roll call, since each member is free to vote for any eligible person, whether he has been nominated or not.”

**333.** `[C1028 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In most societies, however, it is _____ to proceed to an election without first making nominations.

   *Answer:* impractical — “In most societies, however, it is impractical to proceed to an election without first making nominations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:2 — “In most societies, however, it is impractical to proceed to an election without first making nominations.”

**334.** `[C1028 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While members are always free to “write in,” on a ballot, the name of an eligible person who has not been nominated, or to vote for an eligible _____ during a roll-call vote, under normal conditions it is likely that most members will confine their choice to the nominees.
   A. undebatable
   B. authorizes
   C. non-nominee
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) non-nominee. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:2 — “While members are always free to “write in,” on a ballot, the name of an eligible person who has not been nominated, or to vote for an eligible non-nominee during a roll-call vote, under normal conditions it is likely that most members will confine their choice to the nominees.”

**335.** `[C1028 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Without nominations, voting might have to be repeated many times before a candidate achieved the required _____.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:2 — “Without nominations, voting might have to be repeated many times before a candidate achieved the required majority.”

**336.** `[C1029 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Methods of _____ are: (a) by the chair;

   *Answer:* nomination — “Methods of nomination are: (a) by the chair;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:3 — “Methods of nomination are: (a) by the chair;”

**337.** `[C1029 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (b) from the floor (sometimes called “open _____”);
   A. Permission
   B. Indefinitely
   C. nominations
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:3 — “(b) from the floor (sometimes called “open nominations”);”

**338.** `[C1029 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no method of nominating has been specified in the bylaws and if the assembly has adopted no rule on the subject, any member can make a motion _____ the method (31).

   *Answer:* prescribing — “If no method of nominating has been specified in the bylaws and if the assembly has adopted no rule on the subject, any member can make a motion prescribing the method (31).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:3 — “If no method of nominating has been specified in the bylaws and if the assembly has adopted no rule on the subject, any member can make a motion prescribing the method (31).”

**339.** `[C1029 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Methods of _____ are: (a) by the chair;
   A. nomination
   B. persuasive
   C. authorizes
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* A) nomination. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:3 — “Methods of nomination are: (a) by the chair;”

**340.** `[C1029 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (b) from the floor (sometimes called “open _____”);

   *Answer:* nominations — “(b) from the floor (sometimes called “open nominations”);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:3 — “(b) from the floor (sometimes called “open nominations”);”

**341.** `[C1030 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As the following _____ of the six methods of nomination indicate, not all of them are appropriate or desirable in average societies.

   *Answer:* descriptions — “As the following descriptions of the six methods of nomination indicate, not all of them are appropriate or desirable in average societies.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:4 — “As the following descriptions of the six methods of nomination indicate, not all of them are appropriate or desirable in average societies.”

**342.** `[C1030 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The order in which they are listed _____ to that in which they would be voted on if all six were proposed in motions prescribing the method of nomination.
   A. Substitute
   B. corresponds
   C. constitution
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) corresponds. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:4 — “The order in which they are listed corresponds to that in which they would be voted on if all six were proposed in motions prescribing the method of nomination.”

**343.** `[C1030 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As the following _____ of the six methods of nomination indicate, not all of them are appropriate or desirable in average societies.
   A. subsequently
   B. deliberative
   C. descriptions
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) descriptions. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:4 — “As the following descriptions of the six methods of nomination indicate, not all of them are appropriate or desirable in average societies.”

**344.** `[C1030 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The order in which they are listed _____ to that in which they would be voted on if all six were proposed in motions prescribing the method of nomination.

   *Answer:* corresponds — “The order in which they are listed corresponds to that in which they would be voted on if all six were proposed in motions prescribing the method of nomination.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:4 — “The order in which they are listed corresponds to that in which they would be voted on if all six were proposed in motions prescribing the method of nomination.”

**345.** `[C1031 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At a mass meeting it is a common practice to have the chairman nominated by the person who was _____ to call the meeting to order, but an organized society should adopt other methods of nominating for office.
   A. convention
   B. settlement
   C. resolution
   D. designated

   *Answer:* D) designated. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:5 — “At a mass meeting it is a common practice to have the chairman nominated by the person who was designated to call the meeting to order, but an organized society should adopt other methods of nominating for office.”

**346.** `[C1031 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair, however, can make _____ for committee membership and similar positions (an exception being made in the case of the nominating committee), as may be provided in the bylaws or by the adoption of a motion.

   *Answer:* nominations — “The chair, however, can make nominations for committee membership and similar positions (an exception being made in the case of the nominating committee), as may be provided in the bylaws or by the adoption of a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:5 — “The chair, however, can make nominations for committee membership and similar positions (an exception being made in the case of the nominating committee), as may be provided in the bylaws or by the adoption of a motion.”

**347.** `[C1031 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At a mass meeting it is a common practice to have the chairman nominated by the person who was _____ to call the meeting to order, but an organized society should adopt other methods of nominating for office.

   *Answer:* designated — “At a mass meeting it is a common practice to have the chairman nominated by the person who was designated to call the meeting to order, but an organized society should adopt other methods of nominating for office.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:5 — “At a mass meeting it is a common practice to have the chairman nominated by the person who was designated to call the meeting to order, but an organized society should adopt other methods of nominating for office.”

**348.** `[C1031 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair, however, can make _____ for committee membership and similar positions (an exception being made in the case of the nominating committee), as may be provided in the bylaws or by the adoption of a motion.
   A. Unfinished
   B. adjournment
   C. nominations
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:5 — “The chair, however, can make nominations for committee membership and similar positions (an exception being made in the case of the nominating committee), as may be provided in the bylaws or by the adoption of a motion.”

**349.** `[C1032 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under the procedure of _____, the chair calls for nominations at the time established by rule or custom of the organization or assembly—which may be while the election is pending or earlier, but in any case is subsequent to the report of the nominating committee if there is such a committee.

   *Answer:* nominations from the floor — “Under the procedure of nominations from the floor, the chair calls for nominations at the time established by rule or custom of the organization or assembly—which may be while the election is pending or earlier, but in any case is subsequent to the report of the nominating committee if there is such a committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:6 — “Under the procedure of nominations from the floor, the chair calls for nominations at the time established by rule or custom of the organization or assembly—which may be while the election is pending or earlier, but in any case is subsequent to the report of the nominating committee if there is such a committee.”

**350.** `[C1032 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under the procedure of nominations from the floor, the chair calls for nominations at the time established by rule or custom of the _____ or assembly—which may be while the election is pending or earlier, but in any case is subsequent to the report of the nominating committee if there is such a committee.
   A. Particular
   B. immediately
   C. Reconsider
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:6 — “Under the procedure of nominations from the floor, the chair calls for nominations at the time established by rule or custom of the organization or assembly—which may be while the election is pending or earlier, but in any case is subsequent to the report of the nominating committee if there is such a committee.”

**351.** `[C1032 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No second is required, but sometimes one or more members will second a nomination to indicate _____.
   A. Procedures
   B. endorsement
   C. authorizes
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) endorsement. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:6 — “No second is required, but sometimes one or more members will second a nomination to indicate endorsement.”

**352.** `[C1032 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Where more than one person is to be elected to an office, such as to a board of directors or trustees, or to a position, such as to a committee, no one may nominate more than one person for the office or position, if an objection is made, until every member wishing to nominate has had an _____ to do so.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “Where more than one person is to be elected to an office, such as to a board of directors or trustees, or to a position, such as to a committee, no one may nominate more than one person for the office or position, if an objection is made, until every member wishing to nominate has had an opportunity to do so.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:6 — “Where more than one person is to be elected to an office, such as to a board of directors or trustees, or to a position, such as to a committee, no one may nominate more than one person for the office or position, if an objection is made, until every member wishing to nominate has had an opportunity to do so.”

**353.** `[C1032 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Where more than one person is to be elected to an office, such as to a board of directors or trustees, or to a position, such as to a committee, no one may _____ more than one person for the office or position, if an objection is made, until every member wishing to nominate has had an opportunity to do so.
   A. nominate
   B. assembly
   C. Standing
   D. Standard

   *Answer:* A) nominate. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:6 — “Where more than one person is to be elected to an office, such as to a board of directors or trustees, or to a position, such as to a committee, no one may nominate more than one person for the office or position, if an objection is made, until every member wishing to nominate has had an opportunity to do so.”

**354.** `[C1033 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The same person can be _____ for more than one office, even if voting for all offices is to take place at the same time on a single ballot.

   *Answer:* nominated — “The same person can be nominated for more than one office, even if voting for all offices is to take place at the same time on a single ballot.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:7 — “The same person can be nominated for more than one office, even if voting for all offices is to take place at the same time on a single ballot.”

**355.** `[C1033 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The same person can be _____ for more than one office, even if voting for all offices is to take place at the same time on a single ballot.
   A. Privilege
   B. nominated
   C. Precedent
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* B) nominated. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:7 — “The same person can be nominated for more than one office, even if voting for all offices is to take place at the same time on a single ballot.”

**356.** `[C1034 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the presiding officer has called for _____ from the floor, a member rises and makes a nomination as follows:

   *Answer:* nominations — “When the presiding officer has called for nominations from the floor, a member rises and makes a nomination as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:8 — “When the presiding officer has called for nominations from the floor, a member rises and makes a nomination as follows:”

**357.** `[C1034 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the presiding officer has called for _____ from the floor, a member rises and makes a nomination as follows:
   A. adjournment
   B. Particular
   C. nominations
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:8 — “When the presiding officer has called for nominations from the floor, a member rises and makes a nomination as follows:”

**358.** `[C1035 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the election of officers of an ordinary society, _____ often are made by a nominating committee.
   A. nominations
   B. Convention
   C. Particular
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:9 — “In the election of officers of an ordinary society, nominations often are made by a nominating committee.”

**359.** `[C1035 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the election of officers of an ordinary society, _____ often are made by a nominating committee.

   *Answer:* nominations — “In the election of officers of an ordinary society, nominations often are made by a nominating committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:9 — “In the election of officers of an ordinary society, nominations often are made by a nominating committee.”

**360.** `[C1036 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of the nominating committee.

   *Answer:* Designation — “Designation of the nominating committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:10 — “Designation of the nominating committee.”

**361.** `[C1036 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The nominating committee should be elected by the _____ wherever possible, or else by its executive board.
   A. resolutions
   B. organization
   C. constitution
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:10 — “The nominating committee should be elected by the organization wherever possible, or else by its executive board.”

**362.** `[C1036 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although in _____ a new society it may be feasible for the chair to appoint the nominating committee, in an organized society the president should not appoint this committee or be a member of it—ex officio or otherwise.

   *Answer:* organizing — “Although in organizing a new society it may be feasible for the chair to appoint the nominating committee, in an organized society the president should not appoint this committee or be a member of it—ex officio or otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:10 — “Although in organizing a new society it may be feasible for the chair to appoint the nominating committee, in an organized society the president should not appoint this committee or be a member of it—ex officio or otherwise.”

**363.** `[C1036 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The bylaws may provide that “the President shall appoint all _____ except the Nominating Committee…” and that “the President shall be ex officio a member of all committees except the Nominating Committee…”;
   A. persuasive
   B. committees
   C. landscaped
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* B) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:10 — “The bylaws may provide that “the President shall appoint all committees except the Nominating Committee…” and that “the President shall be ex officio a member of all committees except the Nominating Committee…”;”

**364.** `[C1036 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: the _____ should not be omitted in either case.

   *Answer:* exception — “the exception should not be omitted in either case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:10 — “the exception should not be omitted in either case.”

**365.** `[C1037 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although it is not common for the _____ committee to nominate more than one candidate for any office, the committee can do so unless the bylaws prohibit it.

   *Answer:* nominating — “Although it is not common for the nominating committee to nominate more than one candidate for any office, the committee can do so unless the bylaws prohibit it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:11 — “Although it is not common for the nominating committee to nominate more than one candidate for any office, the committee can do so unless the bylaws prohibit it.”

**366.** `[C1037 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although it is not common for the _____ committee to nominate more than one candidate for any office, the committee can do so unless the bylaws prohibit it.
   A. subsidiary
   B. Convention
   C. nominating
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* C) nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:11 — “Although it is not common for the nominating committee to nominate more than one candidate for any office, the committee can do so unless the bylaws prohibit it.”

**367.** `[C1038 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Members of the _____ committee are not barred from becoming nominees for office themselves.

   *Answer:* nominating — “Members of the nominating committee are not barred from becoming nominees for office themselves.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:12 — “Members of the nominating committee are not barred from becoming nominees for office themselves.”

**368.** `[C1038 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To make such a _____ would mean, first, that service on the nominating committee carried a penalty by depriving its members of one of their privileges;
   A. assemblies
   B. authorizes
   C. Particular
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* D) requirement. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:12 — “To make such a requirement would mean, first, that service on the nominating committee carried a penalty by depriving its members of one of their privileges;”

**369.** `[C1038 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and second, that _____ or election to the nominating committee could be used to prevent a member from becoming a nominee.

   *Answer:* appointment — “and second, that appointment or election to the nominating committee could be used to prevent a member from becoming a nominee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:12 — “and second, that appointment or election to the nominating committee could be used to prevent a member from becoming a nominee.”

**370.** `[C1038 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Members of the _____ committee are not barred from becoming nominees for office themselves.
   A. Unfinished
   B. nominating
   C. substitute
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* B) nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:12 — “Members of the nominating committee are not barred from becoming nominees for office themselves.”

**371.** `[C1038 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To make such a _____ would mean, first, that service on the nominating committee carried a penalty by depriving its members of one of their privileges;

   *Answer:* requirement — “To make such a requirement would mean, first, that service on the nominating committee carried a penalty by depriving its members of one of their privileges;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:12 — “To make such a requirement would mean, first, that service on the nominating committee carried a penalty by depriving its members of one of their privileges;”

**372.** `[C1039 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is desirable policy for the _____ committee, before making its report, to contact each person whom it wishes to nominate, in order to obtain his acceptance of nomination— that is, his assurance that he will serve in the specified office if elected.

   *Answer:* nominating — “It is desirable policy for the nominating committee, before making its report, to contact each person whom it wishes to nominate, in order to obtain his acceptance of nomination— that is, his assurance that he will serve in the specified office if elected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:13 — “It is desirable policy for the nominating committee, before making its report, to contact each person whom it wishes to nominate, in order to obtain his acceptance of nomination— that is, his assurance that he will serve in the specified office if elected.”

**373.** `[C1039 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The bylaws can make such a practice _____.
   A. secondary
   B. Secretary
   C. mandatory
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* C) mandatory. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:13 — “The bylaws can make such a practice mandatory.”

**374.** `[C1039 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is desirable policy for the _____ committee, before making its report, to contact each person whom it wishes to nominate, in order to obtain his acceptance of nomination— that is, his assurance that he will serve in the specified office if elected.
   A. recognized
   B. nominating
   C. sustaining
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* B) nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:13 — “It is desirable policy for the nominating committee, before making its report, to contact each person whom it wishes to nominate, in order to obtain his acceptance of nomination— that is, his assurance that he will serve in the specified office if elected.”

**375.** `[C1039 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The bylaws can make such a practice _____.

   *Answer:* mandatory — “The bylaws can make such a practice mandatory.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:13 — “The bylaws can make such a practice mandatory.”

**376.** `[C1040 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The time at which the nominating committee’s report is made is a matter to be determined by rule or established custom of the particular _____—depending on its own conditions.

   *Answer:* organization — “The time at which the nominating committee’s report is made is a matter to be determined by rule or established custom of the particular organization—depending on its own conditions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:14 — “The time at which the nominating committee’s report is made is a matter to be determined by rule or established custom of the particular organization—depending on its own conditions.”

**377.** `[C1040 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some _____ this report is not formally presented to the voting body until the election is pending;
   A. president
   B. Crestwood
   C. Privilege
   D. societies

   *Answer:* D) societies. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:14 — “In some societies this report is not formally presented to the voting body until the election is pending;”

**378.** `[C1040 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but in any organization where advance interest in the election may develop, the _____ submitted by the committee should be made known to the membership earlier.

   *Answer:* nominations — “but in any organization where advance interest in the election may develop, the nominations submitted by the committee should be made known to the membership earlier.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:14 — “but in any organization where advance interest in the election may develop, the nominations submitted by the committee should be made known to the membership earlier.”

**379.** `[C1040 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The report should always be formally presented at a regular meeting, even if the names of the committee’s nominees have been _____ to the members of the society beforehand.
   A. Permission
   B. Information
   C. requirement
   D. transmitted

   *Answer:* D) transmitted. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:14 — “The report should always be formally presented at a regular meeting, even if the names of the committee’s nominees have been transmitted to the members of the society beforehand.”

**380.** `[C1040 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The time at which the _____ committee’s report is made is a matter to be determined by rule or established custom of the particular organization—depending on its own conditions.

   *Answer:* nominating — “The time at which the nominating committee’s report is made is a matter to be determined by rule or established custom of the particular organization—depending on its own conditions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:14 — “The time at which the nominating committee’s report is made is a matter to be determined by rule or established custom of the particular organization—depending on its own conditions.”

**381.** `[C1041 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the _____ committee is called upon for its report at a meeting, its chairman rises and presents the report as follows:

   *Answer:* nominating — “When the nominating committee is called upon for its report at a meeting, its chairman rises and presents the report as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:15 — “When the nominating committee is called upon for its report at a meeting, its chairman rises and presents the report as follows:”

**382.** `[C1041 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the _____ committee is called upon for its report at a meeting, its chairman rises and presents the report as follows:
   A. subsidiary
   B. nominating
   C. Unfinished
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:15 — “When the nominating committee is called upon for its report at a meeting, its chairman rises and presents the report as follows:”

**383.** `[C1042 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A minority within a nominating committee, as a group, may propose other nominees for some or all of the offices in any case where _____ from the floor are permitted.

   *Answer:* nominations — “A minority within a nominating committee, as a group, may propose other nominees for some or all of the offices in any case where nominations from the floor are permitted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:16 — “A minority within a nominating committee, as a group, may propose other nominees for some or all of the offices in any case where nominations from the floor are permitted.”

**384.** `[C1042 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A minority within a nominating committee, as a group, may propose other nominees for some or all of the offices in any case where _____ from the floor are permitted.
   A. Procedures
   B. nominations
   C. interrupted
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:16 — “A minority within a nominating committee, as a group, may propose other nominees for some or all of the offices in any case where nominations from the floor are permitted.”

**385.** `[C1043 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A nominating committee is _____ discharged when its report is formally presented to the assembly, although if one of the nominees withdraws before the election, the committee is revived and should meet immediately to agree upon another nomination if there is time.

   *Answer:* automatically — “A nominating committee is automatically discharged when its report is formally presented to the assembly, although if one of the nominees withdraws before the election, the committee is revived and should meet immediately to agree upon another nomination if there is time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:17 — “A nominating committee is automatically discharged when its report is formally presented to the assembly, although if one of the nominees withdraws before the election, the committee is revived and should meet immediately to agree upon another nomination if there is time.”

**386.** `[C1043 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A nominating committee is _____ discharged when its report is formally presented to the assembly, although if one of the nominees withdraws before the election, the committee is revived and should meet immediately to agree upon another nomination if there is time.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. Organization
   C. automatically
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* C) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:17 — “A nominating committee is automatically discharged when its report is formally presented to the assembly, although if one of the nominees withdraws before the election, the committee is revived and should meet immediately to agree upon another nomination if there is time.”

**387.** `[C1044 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Call by the chair for further _____ from the floor.

   *Answer:* nominations — “Call by the chair for further nominations from the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:18 — “Call by the chair for further nominations from the floor.”

**388.** `[C1044 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This is another stage of nomination and election procedure for which a number of details should be established by rule or custom of the particular _____.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Definitely
   C. Announcements
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:18 — “This is another stage of nomination and election procedure for which a number of details should be established by rule or custom of the particular organization.”

**389.** `[C1044 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In many _____, nominations from the floor are called for immediately after the presentation of the nominating committee’s report—while the election is pending or earlier.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In many organizations, nominations from the floor are called for immediately after the presentation of the nominating committee’s report—while the election is pending or earlier.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:18 — “In many organizations, nominations from the floor are called for immediately after the presentation of the nominating committee’s report—while the election is pending or earlier.”

**390.** `[C1044 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In many organizations, nominations from the floor are called for immediately after the _____ of the nominating committee’s report—while the election is pending or earlier.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. presentation
   C. subsequently
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:18 — “In many organizations, nominations from the floor are called for immediately after the presentation of the nominating committee’s report—while the election is pending or earlier.”

**391.** `[C1044 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any case, if the nominating committee has for any reason failed to make its report at the _____ time, this does not prevent the assembly from proceeding to nominations from the floor.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “In any case, if the nominating committee has for any reason failed to make its report at the appropriate time, this does not prevent the assembly from proceeding to nominations from the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:18 — “In any case, if the nominating committee has for any reason failed to make its report at the appropriate time, this does not prevent the assembly from proceeding to nominations from the floor.”

**392.** `[C1045 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some _____ all nominations from the floor are completed and nominations are closed for each office before voting for any office takes place.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In some organizations all nominations from the floor are completed and nominations are closed for each office before voting for any office takes place.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:19 — “In some organizations all nominations from the floor are completed and nominations are closed for each office before voting for any office takes place.”

**393.** `[C1045 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In either case, the _____ offices are taken in the order in which they are listed in the bylaws.
   A. Objection
   B. Privilege
   C. execution
   D. different

   *Answer:* D) different. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:19 — “In either case, the different offices are taken in the order in which they are listed in the bylaws.”

**394.** `[C1045 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some organizations all _____ from the floor are completed and nominations are closed for each office before voting for any office takes place.

   *Answer:* nominations — “In some organizations all nominations from the floor are completed and nominations are closed for each office before voting for any office takes place.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:19 — “In some organizations all nominations from the floor are completed and nominations are closed for each office before voting for any office takes place.”

**395.** `[C1045 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In either case, the _____ offices are taken in the order in which they are listed in the bylaws.

   *Answer:* different — “In either case, the different offices are taken in the order in which they are listed in the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:19 — “In either case, the different offices are taken in the order in which they are listed in the bylaws.”

**396.** `[C1046 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it appears that no one else wishes to make a nomination, the chair again asks if there are any further _____;

   *Answer:* nominations — “When it appears that no one else wishes to make a nomination, the chair again asks if there are any further nominations;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:20 — “When it appears that no one else wishes to make a nomination, the chair again asks if there are any further nominations;”

**397.** `[C1046 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it appears that no one else wishes to make a nomination, the chair again asks if there are any further _____;
   A. Incidental
   B. Definitely
   C. nominations
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:20 — “When it appears that no one else wishes to make a nomination, the chair again asks if there are any further nominations;”
