**398.** `[C1047 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After nominations have been closed, voting for that office takes place, or nominations for the next office are called for by the chair, depending on the procedure being followed by the particular _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “After nominations have been closed, voting for that office takes place, or nominations for the next office are called for by the chair, depending on the procedure being followed by the particular organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:21 — “After nominations have been closed, voting for that office takes place, or nominations for the next office are called for by the chair, depending on the procedure being followed by the particular organization.”

**399.** `[C1047 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After nominations have been closed, voting for that office takes place, or nominations for the next office are called for by the chair, depending on the procedure being followed by the particular _____.
   A. organization
   B. interrupted
   C. resolutions
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:21 — “After nominations have been closed, voting for that office takes place, or nominations for the next office are called for by the chair, depending on the procedure being followed by the particular organization.”

**400.** `[C1048 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since each member has the opportunity to nominate on his ballot a candidate for every office, he does not have the right then to make nominations from the floor, unless the assembly by a _____ vote authorizes such nominations.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:22 — “Since each member has the opportunity to nominate on his ballot a candidate for every office, he does not have the right then to make nominations from the floor, unless the assembly by a majority vote authorizes such nominations.”

**401.** `[C1048 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The object of a _____ ballot is to provide the members with an indication of the sentiments of the voting body, which they may take into account in voting in the election.

   *Answer:* nominating — “The object of a nominating ballot is to provide the members with an indication of the sentiments of the voting body, which they may take into account in voting in the election.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:22 — “The object of a nominating ballot is to provide the members with an indication of the sentiments of the voting body, which they may take into account in voting in the election.”

**402.** `[C1048 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The value of the nominating ballot is that it shows the _____ without electing anyone.
   A. Substitute
   B. preferences
   C. Permission
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) preferences. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:22 — “The value of the nominating ballot is that it shows the preferences without electing anyone.”

**403.** `[C1048 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: the tellers’ report, therefore, does not state the number of votes necessary for _____.

   *Answer:* nomination — “the tellers’ report, therefore, does not state the number of votes necessary for nomination.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:22 — “the tellers’ report, therefore, does not state the number of votes necessary for nomination.”

**404.** `[C1048 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since each member has the _____ to nominate on his ballot a candidate for every office, he does not have the right then to make nominations from the floor, unless the assembly by a majority vote authorizes such nominations.
   A. committees
   B. immediately
   C. interrupted
   D. opportunity

   *Answer:* D) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:22 — “Since each member has the opportunity to nominate on his ballot a candidate for every office, he does not have the right then to make nominations from the floor, unless the assembly by a majority vote authorizes such nominations.”

**405.** `[C1049 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Impropriety of making the nominating _____ the electing ballot.

   *Answer:* ballot — “Impropriety of making the nominating ballot the electing ballot.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:23 — “Impropriety of making the nominating ballot the electing ballot.”

**406.** `[C1049 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of making the nominating ballot the electing ballot.
   A. Permission
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Incidental
   D. Impropriety

   *Answer:* D) Impropriety. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:23 — “Impropriety of making the nominating ballot the electing ballot.”

**407.** `[C1049 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such action negates all the advantages of a nominating ballot and is, in effect, the same as having an electing ballot without any _____.
   A. authorizes
   B. Indefinitely
   C. nominations
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:23 — “Such action negates all the advantages of a nominating ballot and is, in effect, the same as having an electing ballot without any nominations.”

**408.** `[C1049 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A nominating ballot cannot take the place of an electing ballot in an _____ whose bylaws require elections to be held by ballot.

   *Answer:* organization — “A nominating ballot cannot take the place of an electing ballot in an organization whose bylaws require elections to be held by ballot.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:23 — “A nominating ballot cannot take the place of an electing ballot in an organization whose bylaws require elections to be held by ballot.”

**409.** `[C1049 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such action negates all the advantages of a nominating ballot and is, in effect, the same as having an electing ballot without any _____.

   *Answer:* nominations — “Such action negates all the advantages of a nominating ballot and is, in effect, the same as having an electing ballot without any nominations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:23 — “Such action negates all the advantages of a nominating ballot and is, in effect, the same as having an electing ballot without any nominations.”

**410.** `[C1050 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Impropriety of limiting voting in the election to the two _____.

   *Answer:* leading candidates — “Impropriety of limiting voting in the election to the two leading candidates.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:24 — “Impropriety of limiting voting in the election to the two leading candidates.”

**411.** `[C1050 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of limiting voting in the election to the two leading candidates.
   A. immediately
   B. Preparation
   C. Impropriety
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) Impropriety. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:24 — “Impropriety of limiting voting in the election to the two leading candidates.”

**412.** `[C1050 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some _____ using the nominating ballot, an attempt is made to limit the voting on the electing ballot to the two nominees for each office receiving the highest number of votes on the nominating ballot.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In some organizations using the nominating ballot, an attempt is made to limit the voting on the electing ballot to the two nominees for each office receiving the highest number of votes on the nominating ballot.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:24 — “In some organizations using the nominating ballot, an attempt is made to limit the voting on the electing ballot to the two nominees for each office receiving the highest number of votes on the nominating ballot.”

**413.** `[C1050 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This—or any attempt to limit the number of candidates for an office to two, by whatever method they are nominated—is an _____ practice and should be discouraged.
   A. Incidental
   B. Definitely
   C. Information
   D. unfortunate

   *Answer:* D) unfortunate. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:24 — “This—or any attempt to limit the number of candidates for an office to two, by whatever method they are nominated—is an unfortunate practice and should be discouraged.”

**414.** `[C1050 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Often the two leading candidates for a position will represent two different factions, and division within the _____ may be deepened by limiting the election to them.

   *Answer:* organization — “Often the two leading candidates for a position will represent two different factions, and division within the organization may be deepened by limiting the election to them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:24 — “Often the two leading candidates for a position will represent two different factions, and division within the organization may be deepened by limiting the election to them.”

**415.** `[C1051 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ whose membership is widely scattered, the method of nominating by mail is often adopted.
   A. adjournment
   B. organizations
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:25 — “In organizations whose membership is widely scattered, the method of nominating by mail is often adopted.”

**416.** `[C1051 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ whose membership is widely scattered, the method of nominating by mail is often adopted.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In organizations whose membership is widely scattered, the method of nominating by mail is often adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:25 — “In organizations whose membership is widely scattered, the method of nominating by mail is often adopted.”

**417.** `[C1052 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The bylaws may provide that a member shall be a nominee upon the petition of a _____ number of members.
   A. debatable
   B. exhausted
   C. specified
   D. committee

   *Answer:* C) specified. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:26 — “The bylaws may provide that a member shall be a nominee upon the petition of a specified number of members.”

**418.** `[C1052 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes, a _____ petition blank is sent to the members with a copy of the list of nominees submitted by a nominating committee.

   *Answer:* nominating — “Sometimes, a nominating petition blank is sent to the members with a copy of the list of nominees submitted by a nominating committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:26 — “Sometimes, a nominating petition blank is sent to the members with a copy of the list of nominees submitted by a nominating committee.”

**419.** `[C1052 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In large state or national societies composed of local units, the blanks are sometimes sent to these units with _____ for their distribution or processing.
   A. constitution
   B. instructions
   C. deliberative
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:26 — “In large state or national societies composed of local units, the blanks are sometimes sent to these units with instructions for their distribution or processing.”

**420.** `[C1052 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The bylaws may provide that a member shall be a nominee upon the petition of a _____ number of members.

   *Answer:* specified — “The bylaws may provide that a member shall be a nominee upon the petition of a specified number of members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:26 — “The bylaws may provide that a member shall be a nominee upon the petition of a specified number of members.”

**421.** `[C1052 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes, a _____ petition blank is sent to the members with a copy of the list of nominees submitted by a nominating committee.
   A. incidental
   B. sustaining
   C. Permission
   D. nominating

   *Answer:* D) nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:26 — “Sometimes, a nominating petition blank is sent to the members with a copy of the list of nominees submitted by a nominating committee.”

**422.** `[C1053 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In large _____, nominations are sometimes accompanied by a speech advocating the nominee’s election.
   A. Permission
   B. Information
   C. conventions
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* C) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:27 — “In large conventions, nominations are sometimes accompanied by a speech advocating the nominee’s election.”

**423.** `[C1053 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ may then be seconded by one or more members also making speeches.

   *Answer:* nomination — “The nomination may then be seconded by one or more members also making speeches.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:27 — “The nomination may then be seconded by one or more members also making speeches.”

**424.** `[C1053 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In ordinary _____, however, such speeches are less common.
   A. execution
   B. rejecting
   C. societies
   D. delegates

   *Answer:* C) societies. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:27 — “In ordinary societies, however, such speeches are less common.”

**425.** `[C1053 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In large _____, nominations are sometimes accompanied by a speech advocating the nominee’s election.

   *Answer:* conventions — “In large conventions, nominations are sometimes accompanied by a speech advocating the nominee’s election.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:27 — “In large conventions, nominations are sometimes accompanied by a speech advocating the nominee’s election.”

**426.** `[C1053 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ may then be seconded by one or more members also making speeches.
   A. landscaped
   B. nomination
   C. committees
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) nomination. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:27 — “The nomination may then be seconded by one or more members also making speeches.”

**427.** `[C1054 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By a _____ vote, the assembly may adopt a motion limiting debate to any extent desired (15).

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “By a two-thirds vote, the assembly may adopt a motion limiting debate to any extent desired (15).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:28 — “By a two-thirds vote, the assembly may adopt a motion limiting debate to any extent desired (15).”

**428.** `[C1054 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To give a nominating or seconding speech, a member must first be recognized by the chair while the floor is open for _____.
   A. immediately
   B. Organization
   C. Procedures
   D. nominations

   *Answer:* D) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:28 — “To give a nominating or seconding speech, a member must first be recognized by the chair while the floor is open for nominations.”

**429.** `[C1054 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The member may then nominate a candidate and, without waiting for the chair to state the _____, speak in favor of the candidate, or he or she may speak in favor of a candidate who was nominated previously.

   *Answer:* nomination — “The member may then nominate a candidate and, without waiting for the chair to state the nomination, speak in favor of the candidate, or he or she may speak in favor of a candidate who was nominated previously.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:28 — “The member may then nominate a candidate and, without waiting for the chair to state the nomination, speak in favor of the candidate, or he or she may speak in favor of a candidate who was nominated previously.”

**430.** `[C1054 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If candidates are members of the _____, speakers must exercise caution to avoid making any personal criticisms of them in debate.
   A. specification
   B. Definitely
   C. organization
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:28 — “If candidates are members of the organization, speakers must exercise caution to avoid making any personal criticisms of them in debate.”

**431.** `[C1054 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rather than _____ a nominee, a speaker may advocate the election of a rival candidate.

   *Answer:* attacking — “Rather than attacking a nominee, a speaker may advocate the election of a rival candidate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:28 — “Rather than attacking a nominee, a speaker may advocate the election of a rival candidate.”

**432.** `[C1055 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some _____ adopt rules specifying that debate on nominations be conducted at a different time or in a different manner.

   *Answer:* organizations — “Some organizations adopt rules specifying that debate on nominations be conducted at a different time or in a different manner.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:29 — “Some organizations adopt rules specifying that debate on nominations be conducted at a different time or in a different manner.”

**433.** `[C1055 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a rule might, for example, allot each candidate (or his or her designee) equal time to state the candidate’s _____ and to argue for the candidate’s election.
   A. constitution
   B. Information
   C. credentials
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) credentials. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:29 — “Such a rule might, for example, allot each candidate (or his or her designee) equal time to state the candidate’s credentials and to argue for the candidate’s election.”

**434.** `[C1055 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some _____ adopt rules specifying that debate on nominations be conducted at a different time or in a different manner.
   A. organizations
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. adjournment
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:29 — “Some organizations adopt rules specifying that debate on nominations be conducted at a different time or in a different manner.”

**435.** `[C1055 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a rule might, for example, allot each candidate (or his or her designee) equal time to state the candidate’s _____ and to argue for the candidate’s election.

   *Answer:* credentials — “Such a rule might, for example, allot each candidate (or his or her designee) equal time to state the candidate’s credentials and to argue for the candidate’s election.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:29 — “Such a rule might, for example, allot each candidate (or his or her designee) equal time to state the candidate’s credentials and to argue for the candidate’s election.”

**436.** `[C1056 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an assembly or _____ that does not have a rule or established custom prescribing the method of voting in elections, the voting can be by any of the accepted methods.

   *Answer:* organization — “In an assembly or organization that does not have a rule or established custom prescribing the method of voting in elections, the voting can be by any of the accepted methods.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:30 — “In an assembly or organization that does not have a rule or established custom prescribing the method of voting in elections, the voting can be by any of the accepted methods.”

**437.** `[C1056 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While some form of election by ballot is generally _____ in organized societies, each assembly should adopt—and each society should prescribe in its bylaws—the procedure best suited to its purposes and needs.
   A. Incidental
   B. resolutions
   C. appropriate
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) appropriate. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:30 — “While some form of election by ballot is generally appropriate in organized societies, each assembly should adopt—and each society should prescribe in its bylaws—the procedure best suited to its purposes and needs.”

**438.** `[C1056 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Where there is no _____ rule, a motion to fix the method of voting (or any other detail of nomination or election procedure) is an incidental main motion if made before the election is pending, or an incidental motion if made while the election is pending (30, 31).

   *Answer:* determining — “Where there is no determining rule, a motion to fix the method of voting (or any other detail of nomination or election procedure) is an incidental main motion if made before the election is pending, or an incidental motion if made while the election is pending (30, 31).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:30 — “Where there is no determining rule, a motion to fix the method of voting (or any other detail of nomination or election procedure) is an incidental main motion if made before the election is pending, or an incidental motion if made while the election is pending (30, 31).”

**439.** `[C1056 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a motion can be offered _____ a blank so that different methods are voted on in succession;
   A. containing
   B. assemblies
   C. Substitute
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) containing. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:30 — “Such a motion can be offered containing a blank so that different methods are voted on in succession;”

**440.** `[C1056 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or the chair can take votes on the methods in this way, _____ the motion, if no member objects.

   *Answer:* assuming — “or the chair can take votes on the methods in this way, assuming the motion, if no member objects.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:30 — “or the chair can take votes on the methods in this way, assuming the motion, if no member objects.”

**441.** `[C1057 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Two _____ procedures for the sequence of nominating and voting in elections by ballot can be prescribed or adopted, as mentioned above.
   A. Particular
   B. Definitely
   C. alternative
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) alternative. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:31 — “Two alternative procedures for the sequence of nominating and voting in elections by ballot can be prescribed or adopted, as mentioned above.”

**442.** `[C1057 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The first method requires the least time, while the second affords greater _____ in choosing officers.

   *Answer:* flexibility — “The first method requires the least time, while the second affords greater flexibility in choosing officers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:31 — “The first method requires the least time, while the second affords greater flexibility in choosing officers.”

**443.** `[C1057 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Two _____ procedures for the sequence of nominating and voting in elections by ballot can be prescribed or adopted, as mentioned above.

   *Answer:* alternative — “Two alternative procedures for the sequence of nominating and voting in elections by ballot can be prescribed or adopted, as mentioned above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:31 — “Two alternative procedures for the sequence of nominating and voting in elections by ballot can be prescribed or adopted, as mentioned above.”

**444.** `[C1057 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The first method requires the least time, while the second affords greater _____ in choosing officers.
   A. flexibility
   B. Convention
   C. Permission
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) flexibility. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:31 — “The first method requires the least time, while the second affords greater flexibility in choosing officers.”

**445.** `[C1058 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whichever one of the preceding methods of election is used, if any office remains unfilled after the first ballot, the balloting is repeated for that office as many times as necessary to obtain a _____ vote for a single candidate.

   *Answer:* majority — “Whichever one of the preceding methods of election is used, if any office remains unfilled after the first ballot, the balloting is repeated for that office as many times as necessary to obtain a majority vote for a single candidate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:32 — “Whichever one of the preceding methods of election is used, if any office remains unfilled after the first ballot, the balloting is repeated for that office as many times as necessary to obtain a majority vote for a single candidate.”

**446.** `[C1058 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ one of the preceding methods of election is used, if any office remains unfilled after the first ballot, the balloting is repeated for that office as many times as necessary to obtain a majority vote for a single candidate.
   A. Reporting
   B. amendment
   C. Whichever
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* C) Whichever. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:32 — “Whichever one of the preceding methods of election is used, if any office remains unfilled after the first ballot, the balloting is repeated for that office as many times as necessary to obtain a majority vote for a single candidate.”

**447.** `[C1058 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When repeated balloting for an office is necessary, _____ are never removed from candidacy on the next ballot unless they voluntarily withdraw—which they are not obligated to do.

   *Answer:* individuals — “When repeated balloting for an office is necessary, individuals are never removed from candidacy on the next ballot unless they voluntarily withdraw—which they are not obligated to do.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:32 — “When repeated balloting for an office is necessary, individuals are never removed from candidacy on the next ballot unless they voluntarily withdraw—which they are not obligated to do.”

**448.** `[C1058 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When repeated balloting for an office is necessary, _____ are never removed from candidacy on the next ballot unless they voluntarily withdraw—which they are not obligated to do.
   A. individuals
   B. assemblies
   C. Information
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* A) individuals. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:32 — “When repeated balloting for an office is necessary, individuals are never removed from candidacy on the next ballot unless they voluntarily withdraw—which they are not obligated to do.”

**449.** `[C1059 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an election of members of a board or committee in which votes are cast in one section of the ballot for multiple positions on the board or committee, every ballot with a vote in that section for one or more candidates is counted as one vote cast, and a candidate must receive a _____ of the total of such votes to be elected.

   *Answer:* majority — “In an election of members of a board or committee in which votes are cast in one section of the ballot for multiple positions on the board or committee, every ballot with a vote in that section for one or more candidates is counted as one vote cast, and a candidate must receive a majority of the total of such votes to be elected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:33 — “In an election of members of a board or committee in which votes are cast in one section of the ballot for multiple positions on the board or committee, every ballot with a vote in that section for one or more candidates is counted as one vote cast, and a candidate must receive a majority of the total of such votes to be elected.”

**450.** `[C1059 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an election of members of a board or committee in which votes are cast in one section of the ballot for multiple positions on the board or committee, every ballot with a vote in that section for one or more candidates is counted as _____ cast, and a candidate must receive a majority of the total of such votes to be elected.
   A. In Brief
   B. the same
   C. Agreed To
   D. one vote

   *Answer:* D) one vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:33 — “In an election of members of a board or committee in which votes are cast in one section of the ballot for multiple positions on the board or committee, every ballot with a vote in that section for one or more candidates is counted as one vote cast, and a candidate must receive a majority of the total of such votes to be elected.”

**451.** `[C1060 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the multiple positions have varying terms (as may happen when terms are staggered or there is an election to fill the remainder of an unexpired term) and the differing term lengths have not been assigned different sections of the ballot, the longer terms are allocated among those receiving a _____ vote in the order in which they obtain greater numbers of votes.

   *Answer:* majority — “If the multiple positions have varying terms (as may happen when terms are staggered or there is an election to fill the remainder of an unexpired term) and the differing term lengths have not been assigned different sections of the ballot, the longer terms are allocated among those receiving a majority vote in the order in which they obtain greater numbers of votes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:34 — “If the multiple positions have varying terms (as may happen when terms are staggered or there is an election to fill the remainder of an unexpired term) and the differing term lengths have not been assigned different sections of the ballot, the longer terms are allocated among those receiving a majority vote in the order in which they obtain greater numbers of votes.”

**452.** `[C1060 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the multiple _____ have varying terms (as may happen when terms are staggered or there is an election to fill the remainder of an unexpired term) and the differing term lengths have not been assigned different sections of the ballot, the longer terms are allocated among those receiving a majority vote in the order in which they obtain greater numbers of votes.
   A. positions
   B. auditors’
   C. debatable
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* A) positions. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:34 — “If the multiple positions have varying terms (as may happen when terms are staggered or there is an election to fill the remainder of an unexpired term) and the differing term lengths have not been assigned different sections of the ballot, the longer terms are allocated among those receiving a majority vote in the order in which they obtain greater numbers of votes.”

**453.** `[C1060 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is a tie, the tied _____ may agree which of them will take a longer term;

   *Answer:* candidates — “If there is a tie, the tied candidates may agree which of them will take a longer term;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:34 — “If there is a tie, the tied candidates may agree which of them will take a longer term;”

**454.** `[C1060 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is a tie, the tied _____ may agree which of them will take a longer term;
   A. Permission
   B. candidates
   C. landscaped
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* B) candidates. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:34 — “If there is a tie, the tied candidates may agree which of them will take a longer term;”

**455.** `[C1061 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the bylaws require the election of officers to be by ballot and there is only one nominee for an office, the ballot must _____ be taken for that office unless the bylaws provide for an exception in such a case.

   *Answer:* nevertheless — “If the bylaws require the election of officers to be by ballot and there is only one nominee for an office, the ballot must nevertheless be taken for that office unless the bylaws provide for an exception in such a case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:35 — “If the bylaws require the election of officers to be by ballot and there is only one nominee for an office, the ballot must nevertheless be taken for that office unless the bylaws provide for an exception in such a case.”

**456.** `[C1061 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the absence of the latter _____, members still have the right, on the ballot, to cast “write-in votes” for other eligible persons.
   A. presiding
   B. rejecting
   C. provision
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* C) provision. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:35 — “In the absence of the latter provision, members still have the right, on the ballot, to cast “write-in votes” for other eligible persons.”

**457.** `[C1061 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the bylaws require the election of officers to be by ballot and there is only one nominee for an office, the ballot must _____ be taken for that office unless the bylaws provide for an exception in such a case.
   A. nevertheless
   B. Procedures
   C. Definitely
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) nevertheless. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:35 — “If the bylaws require the election of officers to be by ballot and there is only one nominee for an office, the ballot must nevertheless be taken for that office unless the bylaws provide for an exception in such a case.”

**458.** `[C1061 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the absence of the latter _____, members still have the right, on the ballot, to cast “write-in votes” for other eligible persons.

   *Answer:* provision — “In the absence of the latter provision, members still have the right, on the ballot, to cast “write-in votes” for other eligible persons.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:35 — “In the absence of the latter provision, members still have the right, on the ballot, to cast “write-in votes” for other eligible persons.”

**459.** `[C1062 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For such an election, however—unless repeated balloting by mail is feasible in cases where no candidate attains a majority—the bylaws should authorize the use of some form of _____ voting or should provide that a plurality shall elect, and should provide for a method of selection if there is a tie.

   *Answer:* preferential — “For such an election, however—unless repeated balloting by mail is feasible in cases where no candidate attains a majority—the bylaws should authorize the use of some form of preferential voting or should provide that a plurality shall elect, and should provide for a method of selection if there is a tie.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:36 — “For such an election, however—unless repeated balloting by mail is feasible in cases where no candidate attains a majority—the bylaws should authorize the use of some form of preferential voting or should provide that a plurality shall elect, and should provide for a method of selection if there is a tie.”

**460.** `[C1062 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For such an election, however—unless repeated balloting by mail is feasible in cases where no candidate attains a majority—the bylaws should authorize the use of some form of _____ voting or should provide that a plurality shall elect, and should provide for a method of selection if there is a tie.
   A. consideration
   B. Convention
   C. Definitely
   D. preferential

   *Answer:* D) preferential. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:36 — “For such an election, however—unless repeated balloting by mail is feasible in cases where no candidate attains a majority—the bylaws should authorize the use of some form of preferential voting or should provide that a plurality shall elect, and should provide for a method of selection if there is a tie.”

**461.** `[C1063 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The viva-voce method of election finds _____ principally in mass meetings—or when an election is not strongly contested and the bylaws do not require election by ballot.
   A. Substitute
   B. Information
   C. application
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) application. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:37 — “The viva-voce method of election finds application principally in mass meetings—or when an election is not strongly contested and the bylaws do not require election by ballot.”

**462.** `[C1063 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The viva-voce method of election finds _____ principally in mass meetings—or when an election is not strongly contested and the bylaws do not require election by ballot.

   *Answer:* application — “The viva-voce method of election finds application principally in mass meetings—or when an election is not strongly contested and the bylaws do not require election by ballot.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:37 — “The viva-voce method of election finds application principally in mass meetings—or when an election is not strongly contested and the bylaws do not require election by ballot.”

**463.** `[C1064 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When there is more than one nominee for a given office in a viva-voce election—or in an election by rising vote or by show of hands—the _____ are voted on in the order in which they were nominated.

   *Answer:* candidates — “When there is more than one nominee for a given office in a viva-voce election—or in an election by rising vote or by show of hands—the candidates are voted on in the order in which they were nominated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:38 — “When there is more than one nominee for a given office in a viva-voce election—or in an election by rising vote or by show of hands—the candidates are voted on in the order in which they were nominated.”

**464.** `[C1064 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the _____ have ended, the chair repeats the nominations and continues:
   A. Procedures
   B. nominations
   C. Particular
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:38 — “When the nominations have ended, the chair repeats the nominations and continues:”

**465.** `[C1064 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When there is more than one nominee for a given office in a viva-voce election—or in an election by rising vote or by show of hands—the _____ are voted on in the order in which they were nominated.
   A. subsidiary
   B. candidates
   C. committees
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) candidates. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:38 — “When there is more than one nominee for a given office in a viva-voce election—or in an election by rising vote or by show of hands—the candidates are voted on in the order in which they were nominated.”

**466.** `[C1064 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the _____ have ended, the chair repeats the nominations and continues:

   *Answer:* nominations — “When the nominations have ended, the chair repeats the nominations and continues:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:38 — “When the nominations have ended, the chair repeats the nominations and continues:”

**467.** `[C1065 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It will be seen that, under the _____ just described, it is necessary for members wishing to vote for a later nominee to vote against an earlier one.

   *Answer:* procedure — “It will be seen that, under the procedure just described, it is necessary for members wishing to vote for a later nominee to vote against an earlier one.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:39 — “It will be seen that, under the procedure just described, it is necessary for members wishing to vote for a later nominee to vote against an earlier one.”

**468.** `[C1065 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This fact gives an undue advantage to earlier nominees and, _____, a voice vote is not a generally suitable method for electing the officers of organized societies.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. constitution
   C. accordingly
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* C) accordingly. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:39 — “This fact gives an undue advantage to earlier nominees and, accordingly, a voice vote is not a generally suitable method for electing the officers of organized societies.”

**469.** `[C1065 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It will be seen that, under the _____ just described, it is necessary for members wishing to vote for a later nominee to vote against an earlier one.
   A. postponed
   B. presiding
   C. procedure
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* C) procedure. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:39 — “It will be seen that, under the procedure just described, it is necessary for members wishing to vote for a later nominee to vote against an earlier one.”

**470.** `[C1065 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This fact gives an undue advantage to earlier nominees and, _____, a voice vote is not a generally suitable method for electing the officers of organized societies.

   *Answer:* accordingly — “This fact gives an undue advantage to earlier nominees and, accordingly, a voice vote is not a generally suitable method for electing the officers of organized societies.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:39 — “This fact gives an undue advantage to earlier nominees and, accordingly, a voice vote is not a generally suitable method for electing the officers of organized societies.”

**471.** `[C1066 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If only one person is nominated and the bylaws do not require that a ballot vote be taken, the chair, after ensuring that, in fact, no members present wish to make further nominations, simply declares that the nominee is elected, thus effecting the election by _____ consent or “acclamation.” The motion to close nominations cannot be used as a means of moving the election of the candidate in such a case.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “If only one person is nominated and the bylaws do not require that a ballot vote be taken, the chair, after ensuring that, in fact, no members present wish to make further nominations, simply declares that the nominee is elected, thus effecting the election by unanimous consent or “acclamation.” The motion to close nominations cannot be used as a means of moving the election of the candidate in such a case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:40 — “If only one person is nominated and the bylaws do not require that a ballot vote be taken, the chair, after ensuring that, in fact, no members present wish to make further nominations, simply declares that the nominee is elected, thus effecting the election by unanimous consent or “acclamation.” The motion to close nominations cannot be used as a means of moving the election of the candidate in such a case.”

**472.** `[C1066 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If only one person is nominated and the bylaws do not require that a ballot vote be taken, the chair, after ensuring that, in fact, no members present wish to make further _____, simply declares that the nominee is elected, thus effecting the election by unanimous consent or “acclamation.” The motion to close nominations cannot be used as a means of moving the election of the candidate in such a case.
   A. Permission
   B. nominations
   C. authorizes
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* B) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:40 — “If only one person is nominated and the bylaws do not require that a ballot vote be taken, the chair, after ensuring that, in fact, no members present wish to make further nominations, simply declares that the nominee is elected, thus effecting the election by unanimous consent or “acclamation.” The motion to close nominations cannot be used as a means of moving the election of the candidate in such a case.”

**473.** `[C1067 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The assembly cannot make valid a _____ election if the bylaws require the election to be by ballot.

   *Answer:* viva-voce — “The assembly cannot make valid a viva-voce election if the bylaws require the election to be by ballot.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:41 — “The assembly cannot make valid a viva-voce election if the bylaws require the election to be by ballot.”

**474.** `[C1067 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The assembly cannot make valid a _____ election if the bylaws require the election to be by ballot.
   A. president
   B. secondary
   C. committee
   D. viva-voce

   *Answer:* D) viva-voce. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:41 — “The assembly cannot make valid a viva-voce election if the bylaws require the election to be by ballot.”

**475.** `[C1068 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ unusual, an election can be held by roll call.
   A. assembly
   B. Although
   C. Previous
   D. limiting

   *Answer:* B) Although. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:42 — “Although unusual, an election can be held by roll call.”

**476.** `[C1068 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Either the first or second _____ described in

   *Answer:* procedure — “Either the first or second procedure described in”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:42 — “Either the first or second procedure described in”

**477.** `[C1068 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ unusual, an election can be held by roll call.

   *Answer:* Although — “Although unusual, an election can be held by roll call.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:42 — “Although unusual, an election can be held by roll call.”

**478.** `[C1068 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Either the first or second _____ described in
   A. Committee
   B. Secretary
   C. extending
   D. procedure

   *Answer:* D) procedure. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:42 — “Either the first or second procedure described in”

**479.** `[C1069 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: for election by ballot can be generally followed, and the member (or the chairman of a _____, as the case may be), when called upon, declares his vote or the votes of the members of his delegation for each office to be filled.

   *Answer:* delegation — “for election by ballot can be generally followed, and the member (or the chairman of a delegation, as the case may be), when called upon, declares his vote or the votes of the members of his delegation for each office to be filled.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:31 — “for election by ballot can be generally followed, and the member (or the chairman of a delegation, as the case may be), when called upon, declares his vote or the votes of the members of his delegation for each office to be filled.”

**480.** `[C1069 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The secretary records the vote(s) and then repeats them to be sure of their accurate _____.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Information
   C. recordation
   D. committees

   *Answer:* C) recordation. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:31 — “The secretary records the vote(s) and then repeats them to be sure of their accurate recordation.”

**481.** `[C1069 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: for election by ballot can be generally followed, and the member (or the chairman of a _____, as the case may be), when called upon, declares his vote or the votes of the members of his delegation for each office to be filled.
   A. resolution
   B. substitute
   C. delegation
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) delegation. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:31 — “for election by ballot can be generally followed, and the member (or the chairman of a delegation, as the case may be), when called upon, declares his vote or the votes of the members of his delegation for each office to be filled.”

**482.** `[C1069 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The secretary records the vote(s) and then repeats them to be sure of their accurate _____.

   *Answer:* recordation — “The secretary records the vote(s) and then repeats them to be sure of their accurate recordation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:31 — “The secretary records the vote(s) and then repeats them to be sure of their accurate recordation.”

**483.** `[C1070 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, committees, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for _____.

   *Answer:* cumulative voting — “For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, committees, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for cumulative voting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:43 — “For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, committees, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for cumulative voting.”

**484.** `[C1070 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In this form of voting, each member is entitled to cast _____ for each position, so that if, for example, three directors are to be elected, each member may cast three votes.
   A. Form and
   B. Limit or
   C. a Session
   D. one vote

   *Answer:* D) one vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:43 — “In this form of voting, each member is entitled to cast one vote for each position, so that if, for example, three directors are to be elected, each member may cast three votes.”

**485.** `[C1070 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In this form of voting, each member is entitled to cast one vote for each position, so that if, for example, three directors are to be elected, each member may cast _____.

   *Answer:* three votes — “In this form of voting, each member is entitled to cast one vote for each position, so that if, for example, three directors are to be elected, each member may cast three votes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:43 — “In this form of voting, each member is entitled to cast one vote for each position, so that if, for example, three directors are to be elected, each member may cast three votes.”

**486.** `[C1070 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, _____, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for cumulative voting.
   A. assemblies
   B. committees
   C. Definitely
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* B) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:43 — “For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, committees, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for cumulative voting.”

**487.** `[C1070 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: These votes may all be cast for one, two, or three _____, as the voter chooses.
   A. candidates
   B. Definitely
   C. Incidental
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* A) candidates. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:43 — “These votes may all be cast for one, two, or three candidates, as the voter chooses.”

**488.** `[C1071 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Providing for _____ of an Election.

   *Answer:* Completion — “Providing for Completion of an Election.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:44 — “Providing for Completion of an Election.”

**489.** `[C1071 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An election should be completed at the session at which it is taken up, unless it is impossible or _____ to do so.
   A. impractical
   B. Preparation
   C. Unfinished
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) impractical. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:44 — “An election should be completed at the session at which it is taken up, unless it is impossible or impractical to do so.”

**490.** `[C1071 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an assembly wishes to adjourn when an election is _____, an adjourned meeting (9) should therefore be provided for.

   *Answer:* incomplete — “If an assembly wishes to adjourn when an election is incomplete, an adjourned meeting (9) should therefore be provided for.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:44 — “If an assembly wishes to adjourn when an election is incomplete, an adjourned meeting (9) should therefore be provided for.”

**491.** `[C1071 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Providing for _____ of an Election.
   A. Unfinished
   B. Reconsider
   C. incidental
   D. Completion

   *Answer:* D) Completion. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:44 — “Providing for Completion of an Election.”

**492.** `[C1071 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An election should be completed at the session at which it is taken up, unless it is impossible or _____ to do so.

   *Answer:* impractical — “An election should be completed at the session at which it is taken up, unless it is impossible or impractical to do so.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:44 — “An election should be completed at the session at which it is taken up, unless it is impossible or impractical to do so.”

**493.** `[C1072 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, for any reason, the assembly does not complete an election at the time for which it was scheduled, it should do so as soon as possible and may do so at any time until the _____ of the term the election is to fill.

   *Answer:* expiration — “If, for any reason, the assembly does not complete an election at the time for which it was scheduled, it should do so as soon as possible and may do so at any time until the expiration of the term the election is to fill.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:45 — “If, for any reason, the assembly does not complete an election at the time for which it was scheduled, it should do so as soon as possible and may do so at any time until the expiration of the term the election is to fill.”

**494.** `[C1072 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Once the election is _____, however, the person elected replaces anyone who filled the vacancy.
   A. auditors’
   B. reception
   C. paragraph
   D. completed

   *Answer:* D) completed. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:45 — “Once the election is completed, however, the person elected replaces anyone who filled the vacancy.”

**495.** `[C1072 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Failure to hold or to complete an election at the scheduled time does not deprive the _____ of its right to elect an officer of its choice.

   *Answer:* membership — “Failure to hold or to complete an election at the scheduled time does not deprive the membership of its right to elect an officer of its choice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:45 — “Failure to hold or to complete an election at the scheduled time does not deprive the membership of its right to elect an officer of its choice.”

**496.** `[C1072 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, for any reason, the assembly does not complete an election at the time for which it was scheduled, it should do so as soon as possible and may do so at any time until the _____ of the term the election is to fill.
   A. settlement
   B. expiration
   C. assemblies
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) expiration. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:45 — “If, for any reason, the assembly does not complete an election at the time for which it was scheduled, it should do so as soon as possible and may do so at any time until the expiration of the term the election is to fill.”

**497.** `[C1072 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Once the election is _____, however, the person elected replaces anyone who filled the vacancy.

   *Answer:* completed — “Once the election is completed, however, the person elected replaces anyone who filled the vacancy.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:45 — “Once the election is completed, however, the person elected replaces anyone who filled the vacancy.”

**498.** `[C1073 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An election to an office becomes final _____ if the candidate is present and does not decline, or if he is absent but has consented to his candidacy.

   *Answer:* immediately — “An election to an office becomes final immediately if the candidate is present and does not decline, or if he is absent but has consented to his candidacy.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:46 — “An election to an office becomes final immediately if the candidate is present and does not decline, or if he is absent but has consented to his candidacy.”

**499.** `[C1073 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After an election has become final as stated in this paragraph, it is too late to _____ (37) the vote on the election.
   A. Substitute
   B. Permission
   C. reconsider
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* C) reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:46 — “After an election has become final as stated in this paragraph, it is too late to reconsider (37) the vote on the election.”

**500.** `[C1073 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An election to an office becomes final _____ if the candidate is present and does not decline, or if he is absent but has consented to his candidacy.
   A. Unfinished
   B. immediately
   C. authorizes
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:46 — “An election to an office becomes final immediately if the candidate is present and does not decline, or if he is absent but has consented to his candidacy.”

**501.** `[C1073 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After an election has become final as stated in this paragraph, it is too late to _____ (37) the vote on the election.

   *Answer:* reconsider — “After an election has become final as stated in this paragraph, it is too late to reconsider (37) the vote on the election.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:46 — “After an election has become final as stated in this paragraph, it is too late to reconsider (37) the vote on the election.”

**502.** `[C1074 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a formal _____ ceremony is prescribed, failure to hold it does not affect the time at which the new officers assume office.

   *Answer:* installation — “If a formal installation ceremony is prescribed, failure to hold it does not affect the time at which the new officers assume office.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:47 — “If a formal installation ceremony is prescribed, failure to hold it does not affect the time at which the new officers assume office.”

**503.** `[C1074 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a formal _____ ceremony is prescribed, failure to hold it does not affect the time at which the new officers assume office.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. consideration
   C. installation
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) installation. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:47 — “If a formal installation ceremony is prescribed, failure to hold it does not affect the time at which the new officers assume office.”

**504.** `[C1075 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ the Announced Result of an Election.
   A. Contesting
   B. resolution
   C. persuasive
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) Contesting. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:48 — “Contesting the Announced Result of an Election.”

**505.** `[C1075 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ the Announced Result of an Election.

   *Answer:* Contesting — “Contesting the Announced Result of an Election.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:48 — “Contesting the Announced Result of an Election.”

**506.** `[C1076 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, an election may be contested only by raising a point of order.

   *Answer:* Otherwise — “Otherwise, an election may be contested only by raising a point of order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:49 — “Otherwise, an election may be contested only by raising a point of order.”

**507.** `[C1076 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, an election may be contested only by raising a point of order.
   A. Otherwise
   B. Signature
   C. extending
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* A) Otherwise. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:49 — “Otherwise, an election may be contested only by raising a point of order.”

**508.** `[C1077 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Because the voting body itself is the ultimate judge of election disputes, only that body has the authority to resolve them in the absence of a bylaw or special rule of order that _____ grants another body that authority.

   *Answer:* specifically — “Because the voting body itself is the ultimate judge of election disputes, only that body has the authority to resolve them in the absence of a bylaw or special rule of order that specifically grants another body that authority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:50 — “Because the voting body itself is the ultimate judge of election disputes, only that body has the authority to resolve them in the absence of a bylaw or special rule of order that specifically grants another body that authority.”

**509.** `[C1077 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, for example, when an election has been conducted at a membership meeting or in a convention of delegates, an executive board, even one that is given full power and authority over the society’s affairs between meetings of the body that conducted the election, may not entertain a point of order _____, or direct a recount concerning, the announced election result.
   A. authorizes
   B. Reconsider
   C. challenging
   D. Information

   *Answer:* C) challenging. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:50 — “Thus, for example, when an election has been conducted at a membership meeting or in a convention of delegates, an executive board, even one that is given full power and authority over the society’s affairs between meetings of the body that conducted the election, may not entertain a point of order challenging, or direct a recount concerning, the announced election result.”

**510.** `[C1077 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While an election dispute is _____ pending before the voting body, however, it may vote to refer the dispute to a committee or board to which it delegates power to resolve the dispute.

   *Answer:* immediately — “While an election dispute is immediately pending before the voting body, however, it may vote to refer the dispute to a committee or board to which it delegates power to resolve the dispute.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:50 — “While an election dispute is immediately pending before the voting body, however, it may vote to refer the dispute to a committee or board to which it delegates power to resolve the dispute.”

**511.** `[C1077 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Because the voting body itself is the ultimate judge of election disputes, only that body has the authority to resolve them in the absence of a bylaw or special rule of order that _____ grants another body that authority.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. interrupted
   C. specifically
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* C) specifically. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:50 — “Because the voting body itself is the ultimate judge of election disputes, only that body has the authority to resolve them in the absence of a bylaw or special rule of order that specifically grants another body that authority.”

**512.** `[C1077 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, for example, when an election has been conducted at a membership meeting or in a convention of delegates, an executive board, even one that is given full power and authority over the society’s affairs between meetings of the body that conducted the election, may not entertain a point of order _____, or direct a recount concerning, the announced election result.

   *Answer:* challenging — “Thus, for example, when an election has been conducted at a membership meeting or in a convention of delegates, an executive board, even one that is given full power and authority over the society’s affairs between meetings of the body that conducted the election, may not entertain a point of order challenging, or direct a recount concerning, the announced election result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:50 — “Thus, for example, when an election has been conducted at a membership meeting or in a convention of delegates, an executive board, even one that is given full power and authority over the society’s affairs between meetings of the body that conducted the election, may not entertain a point of order challenging, or direct a recount concerning, the announced election result.”

---

## Domain 6: Being and Serving as a Professional Parliamentarian and Teaching Parliamentary Procedure

**1.** `[C1562 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In most societies it is understood that members are required to be of honorable character and reputation, and certain types of _____ may have particular codes of ethics to enforce.

   *Answer:* associations — “In most societies it is understood that members are required to be of honorable character and reputation, and certain types of associations may have particular codes of ethics to enforce.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:1 — “In most societies it is understood that members are required to be of honorable character and reputation, and certain types of associations may have particular codes of ethics to enforce.”

**2.** `[C1562 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although ordinary societies seldom have occasion to discipline members, an _____ or assembly has the ultimate right to make and enforce its own rules, and to require that its members refrain from conduct injurious to the organization or its purposes.
   A. organization
   B. adjournment
   C. subsequently
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:1 — “Although ordinary societies seldom have occasion to discipline members, an organization or assembly has the ultimate right to make and enforce its own rules, and to require that its members refrain from conduct injurious to the organization or its purposes.”

**3.** `[C1562 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No one should be allowed to remain a member if his _____ will do this kind of harm.

   *Answer:* retention — “No one should be allowed to remain a member if his retention will do this kind of harm.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:1 — “No one should be allowed to remain a member if his retention will do this kind of harm.”

**4.** `[C1562 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In most societies it is understood that members are required to be of honorable character and reputation, and certain types of _____ may have particular codes of ethics to enforce.
   A. resolutions
   B. associations
   C. immediately
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) associations. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:1 — “In most societies it is understood that members are required to be of honorable character and reputation, and certain types of associations may have particular codes of ethics to enforce.”

**5.** `[C1562 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although ordinary societies seldom have occasion to discipline members, an _____ or assembly has the ultimate right to make and enforce its own rules, and to require that its members refrain from conduct injurious to the organization or its purposes.

   *Answer:* organization — “Although ordinary societies seldom have occasion to discipline members, an organization or assembly has the ultimate right to make and enforce its own rules, and to require that its members refrain from conduct injurious to the organization or its purposes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:1 — “Although ordinary societies seldom have occasion to discipline members, an organization or assembly has the ultimate right to make and enforce its own rules, and to require that its members refrain from conduct injurious to the organization or its purposes.”

**6.** `[C1563 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ that a society can impose generally fall under the headings of censure, fine (if authorized in the bylaws), suspension, or expulsion.

   *Answer:* Punishments — “Punishments that a society can impose generally fall under the headings of censure, fine (if authorized in the bylaws), suspension, or expulsion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:2 — “Punishments that a society can impose generally fall under the headings of censure, fine (if authorized in the bylaws), suspension, or expulsion.”

**7.** `[C1563 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The extreme penalty that an _____ or society can impose on a member is expulsion.
   A. resolutions
   B. organization
   C. Permission
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:2 — “The extreme penalty that an organization or society can impose on a member is expulsion.”

**8.** `[C1563 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ that a society can impose generally fall under the headings of censure, fine (if authorized in the bylaws), suspension, or expulsion.
   A. Permission
   B. authorizes
   C. convention
   D. Punishments

   *Answer:* D) Punishments. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:2 — “Punishments that a society can impose generally fall under the headings of censure, fine (if authorized in the bylaws), suspension, or expulsion.”

**9.** `[C1563 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The extreme penalty that an _____ or society can impose on a member is expulsion.

   *Answer:* organization — “The extreme penalty that an organization or society can impose on a member is expulsion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:2 — “The extreme penalty that an organization or society can impose on a member is expulsion.”

**10.** `[C1564 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Frequently, such an article provides for their imposition on any member found guilty of conduct described, for example, as “tending to injure the good name of the _____, disturb its well-being, or hamper it in its work.” In any society, behavior of this nature is a serious offense properly subject to disciplinary action, whether the bylaws make mention of it or not.

   *Answer:* organization — “Frequently, such an article provides for their imposition on any member found guilty of conduct described, for example, as “tending to injure the good name of the organization, disturb its well-being, or hamper it in its work.” In any society, behavior of this nature is a serious offense properly subject to disciplinary action, whether the bylaws make mention of it or not.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:3 — “Frequently, such an article provides for their imposition on any member found guilty of conduct described, for example, as “tending to injure the good name of the organization, disturb its well-being, or hamper it in its work.” In any society, behavior of this nature is a serious offense properly subject to disciplinary action, whether the bylaws make mention of it or not.”

**11.** `[C1564 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Frequently, such an article provides for their imposition on any member found guilty of conduct described, for example, as “tending to injure the good name of the _____, disturb its well-being, or hamper it in its work.” In any society, behavior of this nature is a serious offense properly subject to disciplinary action, whether the bylaws make mention of it or not.
   A. subsequently
   B. Permission
   C. Incidental
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:3 — “Frequently, such an article provides for their imposition on any member found guilty of conduct described, for example, as “tending to injure the good name of the organization, disturb its well-being, or hamper it in its work.” In any society, behavior of this nature is a serious offense properly subject to disciplinary action, whether the bylaws make mention of it or not.”

**12.** `[C1565 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Formal _____ procedures should generally be regarded as a drastic step reserved for serious situations or those potentially so.

See RONR (12th ed.) §61:4 — “Formal disciplinary procedures should generally be regarded as a drastic step reserved for serious situations or those potentially so.”

**13.** `[C1565 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it appears that such measures may become necessary, proper and tactful handling of the case is of prime _____.

   C. importance
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* C) importance. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:4 — “When it appears that such measures may become necessary, proper and tactful handling of the case is of prime importance.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §61:4 — “It is usually in the best interests of the organization first to make every effort to obtain a satisfactory solution of the matter quietly and informally.”

**15.** `[C1565 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Formal _____ procedures should generally be regarded as a drastic step reserved for serious situations or those potentially so.
   A. Incidental
   B. Convention
   C. disciplinary
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:4 — “Formal disciplinary procedures should generally be regarded as a drastic step reserved for serious situations or those potentially so.”

**16.** `[C1565 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it appears that such measures may become necessary, proper and tactful handling of the case is of prime _____.

   *Answer:* importance — “When it appears that such measures may become necessary, proper and tactful handling of the case is of prime importance.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:4 — “When it appears that such measures may become necessary, proper and tactful handling of the case is of prime importance.”

**19.** `[C1567 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ Governing Discipline at Meetings.

   *Answer:* Principles — “Principles Governing Discipline at Meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:6 — “Principles Governing Discipline at Meetings.”

**20.** `[C1567 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A society has the right to _____ who may be present at its meetings and to control its hall while meetings are in progress;
   A. president
   B. exhausted
   C. amendment
   D. determine

   *Answer:* D) determine. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:6 — “A society has the right to determine who may be present at its meetings and to control its hall while meetings are in progress;”

**21.** `[C1567 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but all members have the right to attend except in cases where the bylaws provide for the automatic _____ of members who fall in arrears in payment of their dues, or where the society has, by vote and as a penalty imposed for a specific offense, forbidden attendance.

   *Answer:* suspension — “but all members have the right to attend except in cases where the bylaws provide for the automatic suspension of members who fall in arrears in payment of their dues, or where the society has, by vote and as a penalty imposed for a specific offense, forbidden attendance.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:6 — “but all members have the right to attend except in cases where the bylaws provide for the automatic suspension of members who fall in arrears in payment of their dues, or where the society has, by vote and as a penalty imposed for a specific offense, forbidden attendance.”

   D. Principles

**24.** `[C1568 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, on the other hand—or a particular nonmember or group of nonmembers—can be excluded at any time from part or all of a meeting of a society, or from all of its meetings.

See RONR (12th ed.) §61:7 — “Nonmembers, on the other hand—or a particular nonmember or group of nonmembers—can be excluded at any time from part or all of a meeting of a society, or from all of its meetings.”

**25.** `[C1568 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, on the other hand—or a particular nonmember or group of nonmembers—can be excluded at any time from part or all of a meeting of a society, or from all of its meetings.

   C. Nonmembers
