**26.** `[C1569 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: All persons present at a meeting have an _____ to obey the legitimate orders of the presiding officer.

   *Answer:* obligation — “All persons present at a meeting have an obligation to obey the legitimate orders of the presiding officer.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:8 — “All persons present at a meeting have an obligation to obey the legitimate orders of the presiding officer.”

**27.** `[C1569 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Members, however, can appeal from the decision of the chair (24), move to suspend the rules (25), or move a _____ (37)— depending on the circumstances of the chair’s ruling.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Preparation
   C. requirement
   D. reconsideration

   *Answer:* D) reconsideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:8 — “Members, however, can appeal from the decision of the chair (24), move to suspend the rules (25), or move a reconsideration (37)— depending on the circumstances of the chair’s ruling.”

**28.** `[C1569 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member can make such an appeal or motion whether the order _____ applies to him or not.

   *Answer:* involved — “A member can make such an appeal or motion whether the order involved applies to him or not.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:8 — “A member can make such an appeal or motion whether the order involved applies to him or not.”

**29.** `[C1569 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: All persons present at a meeting have an _____ to obey the legitimate orders of the presiding officer.
   A. obligation
   B. convention
   C. landscaped
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* A) obligation. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:8 — “All persons present at a meeting have an obligation to obey the legitimate orders of the presiding officer.”

**30.** `[C1569 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Members, however, can appeal from the decision of the chair (24), move to suspend the rules (25), or move a _____ (37)— depending on the circumstances of the chair’s ruling.

   *Answer:* reconsideration — “Members, however, can appeal from the decision of the chair (24), move to suspend the rules (25), or move a reconsideration (37)— depending on the circumstances of the chair’s ruling.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:8 — “Members, however, can appeal from the decision of the chair (24), move to suspend the rules (25), or move a reconsideration (37)— depending on the circumstances of the chair’s ruling.”

**31.** `[C1570 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In dealing with any case of disorder in a meeting, the presiding officer should always maintain a calm, deliberate tone—although he may become _____ firm if a situation demands it.

   *Answer:* increasingly — “In dealing with any case of disorder in a meeting, the presiding officer should always maintain a calm, deliberate tone—although he may become increasingly firm if a situation demands it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:9 — “In dealing with any case of disorder in a meeting, the presiding officer should always maintain a calm, deliberate tone—although he may become increasingly firm if a situation demands it.”

**32.** `[C1570 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under no _____ should the chair attempt to drown out a disorderly member—either by his own voice or the gavel—or permit himself to be drawn into a verbal duel.
   A. Preparation
   B. circumstances
   C. Information
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) circumstances. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:9 — “Under no circumstances should the chair attempt to drown out a disorderly member—either by his own voice or the gavel—or permit himself to be drawn into a verbal duel.”

**33.** `[C1570 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If unavoidable, however, proper _____ proceedings to cope with immediate necessity can be conducted while a disorderly member continues to speak.

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “If unavoidable, however, proper disciplinary proceedings to cope with immediate necessity can be conducted while a disorderly member continues to speak.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:9 — “If unavoidable, however, proper disciplinary proceedings to cope with immediate necessity can be conducted while a disorderly member continues to speak.”

**34.** `[C1570 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In dealing with any case of disorder in a meeting, the presiding officer should always maintain a calm, deliberate tone—although he may become _____ firm if a situation demands it.
   A. requirement
   B. increasingly
   C. consideration
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* B) increasingly. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:9 — “In dealing with any case of disorder in a meeting, the presiding officer should always maintain a calm, deliberate tone—although he may become increasingly firm if a situation demands it.”

**35.** `[C1570 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under no _____ should the chair attempt to drown out a disorderly member—either by his own voice or the gavel—or permit himself to be drawn into a verbal duel.

   *Answer:* circumstances — “Under no circumstances should the chair attempt to drown out a disorderly member—either by his own voice or the gavel—or permit himself to be drawn into a verbal duel.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:9 — “Under no circumstances should the chair attempt to drown out a disorderly member—either by his own voice or the gavel—or permit himself to be drawn into a verbal duel.”

**36.** `[C1571 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of Order by Members in a Meeting.

   *Answer:* Breaches — “Breaches of Order by Members in a Meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:10 — “Breaches of Order by Members in a Meeting.”

**37.** `[C1571 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a member commits only a slight breach of order—such as _____ another member instead of the chair in debate, or, in a single instance, failing to confine his remarks to the merits of the pending question—the chair simply raps lightly, points out the fault, and advises the member to avoid it.
   A. addressing
   B. convention
   C. Particular
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) addressing. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:10 — “If a member commits only a slight breach of order—such as addressing another member instead of the chair in debate, or, in a single instance, failing to confine his remarks to the merits of the pending question—the chair simply raps lightly, points out the fault, and advises the member to avoid it.”

**38.** `[C1571 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The member can then _____ speaking if he commits no further breaches.

**41.** `[C1572 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the offense is more serious than in the case above—as when a member _____ questions the motives of other members whom he mentions by name, or persists in speaking on completely irrelevant matters in debate—the chair normally should first warn the member;

   A. Definitely

**44.** `[C1572 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair finds this point of order (23) well taken, he _____ the offender out of order and directs him to be seated, just as above.

   B. dilatory
   C. Meetings
   D. declares

   *Answer:* D) declares. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:11 — “If the chair finds this point of order (23) well taken, he declares the offender out of order and directs him to be seated, just as above.”

**45.** `[C1572 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the offender had the floor, then (_____ of who originated the proceeding) the chair clearly states the breach involved and puts the question to the assembly: “Shall the member be allowed to continue speaking?” This question is undebatable.

   *Answer:* irrespective — “If the offender had the floor, then (irrespective of who originated the proceeding) the chair clearly states the breach involved and puts the question to the assembly: “Shall the member be allowed to continue speaking?” This question is undebatable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:11 — “If the offender had the floor, then (irrespective of who originated the proceeding) the chair clearly states the breach involved and puts the question to the assembly: “Shall the member be allowed to continue speaking?” This question is undebatable.”

**46.** `[C1573 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases of obstinate or grave breach of order by a member, the chair can, after repeated warnings, “name” the offender, which amounts to preferring charges and should be resorted to only in extreme _____.

   *Answer:* circumstances — “In cases of obstinate or grave breach of order by a member, the chair can, after repeated warnings, “name” the offender, which amounts to preferring charges and should be resorted to only in extreme circumstances.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:12 — “In cases of obstinate or grave breach of order by a member, the chair can, after repeated warnings, “name” the offender, which amounts to preferring charges and should be resorted to only in extreme circumstances.”

**47.** `[C1573 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before taking such action, when it begins to appear that it may become necessary, the chair directs the secretary to take down _____ or disorderly words used by the member.

   B. objectionable
   C. subsequently
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) objectionable. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:12 — “Before taking such action, when it begins to appear that it may become necessary, the chair directs the secretary to take down objectionable or disorderly words used by the member.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §61:12 — “This direction by the chair, and the words taken down pursuant to it, are entered in the minutes only if the chair finds it necessary to name the offender.”

**49.** `[C1573 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases of obstinate or grave breach of order by a member, the chair can, after repeated warnings, “name” the offender, which amounts to preferring charges and should be resorted to only in extreme _____.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. adjournment
   C. representatives
   D. circumstances

See RONR (12th ed.) §61:13 — “Although the chair has no authority to impose a penalty or to order the offending member removed from the hall, the assembly has that power.”

**52.** `[C1574 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted in this _____ that in any case of an offense against the assembly occurring in a meeting, there is no need for a formal trial provided that any penalty is imposed promptly after the breach (cf.

   D. committees

   D. Secretary

**55.** `[C1575 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ made by the chair in naming a member is addressed to the offender by name and in the second person, and is entered in the minutes.

   *Answer:* declaration — “The declaration made by the chair in naming a member is addressed to the offender by name and in the second person, and is entered in the minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:14 — “The declaration made by the chair in naming a member is addressed to the offender by name and in the second person, and is entered in the minutes.”

**56.** `[C1575 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ made by the chair in naming a member is addressed to the offender by name and in the second person, and is entered in the minutes.
   A. authorizes
   B. undebatable
   C. Unfinished
   D. declaration

   *Answer:* D) declaration. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:14 — “The declaration made by the chair in naming a member is addressed to the offender by name and in the second person, and is entered in the minutes.”

**57.** `[C1576 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the member obeys at this point, the matter can be _____ or not, as the assembly chooses.

   *Answer:* dropped — “If the member obeys at this point, the matter can be dropped or not, as the assembly chooses.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:15 — “If the member obeys at this point, the matter can be dropped or not, as the assembly chooses.”

**58.** `[C1576 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The case may be sufficiently resolved by an apology or a withdrawal of _____ statements or remarks by the offender;
   A. characteristics
   B. President-Elect
   C. objectionable
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* C) objectionable. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:15 — “The case may be sufficiently resolved by an apology or a withdrawal of objectionable statements or remarks by the offender;”

**59.** `[C1576 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if not, any member can move to order a penalty, or the chair can first ask, “What penalty shall be imposed on the member?” A motion offered in a case of this kind can propose, for example, that the offender be required to make an apology, that he be censured, that he be required to leave the hall during the remainder of the meeting or until he is prepared to apologize, that his rights of membership be suspended for a time, or that he be expelled from the _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “but if not, any member can move to order a penalty, or the chair can first ask, “What penalty shall be imposed on the member?” A motion offered in a case of this kind can propose, for example, that the offender be required to make an apology, that he be censured, that he be required to leave the hall during the remainder of the meeting or until he is prepared to apologize, that his rights of membership be suspended for a time, or that he be expelled from the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:15 — “but if not, any member can move to order a penalty, or the chair can first ask, “What penalty shall be imposed on the member?” A motion offered in a case of this kind can propose, for example, that the offender be required to make an apology, that he be censured, that he be required to leave the hall during the remainder of the meeting or until he is prepared to apologize, that his rights of membership be suspended for a time, or that he be expelled from the organization.”

**60.** `[C1576 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the member obeys at this point, the matter can be _____ or not, as the assembly chooses.
   A. dropped
   B. Meeting
   C. applied
   D. Divided

   *Answer:* A) dropped. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:15 — “If the member obeys at this point, the matter can be dropped or not, as the assembly chooses.”

**61.** `[C1576 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The case may be sufficiently resolved by an apology or a withdrawal of _____ statements or remarks by the offender;

   *Answer:* objectionable — “The case may be sufficiently resolved by an apology or a withdrawal of objectionable statements or remarks by the offender;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:15 — “The case may be sufficiently resolved by an apology or a withdrawal of objectionable statements or remarks by the offender;”

**62.** `[C1577 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to require the member’s departure during consideration of the penalty— which may be assumed by the chair if he thinks it appropriate —is undebatable, is unamendable, and requires a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “A motion to require the member’s departure during consideration of the penalty— which may be assumed by the chair if he thinks it appropriate —is undebatable, is unamendable, and requires a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:16 — “A motion to require the member’s departure during consideration of the penalty— which may be assumed by the chair if he thinks it appropriate —is undebatable, is unamendable, and requires a majority vote.”

**63.** `[C1577 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The offending member can be required to leave the hall during the _____ of his penalty, but he must be allowed to present his defense briefly first.
   A. consideration
   B. representatives
   C. president-elect
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:16 — “The offending member can be required to leave the hall during the consideration of his penalty, but he must be allowed to present his defense briefly first.”

**64.** `[C1577 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to require the member’s departure during consideration of the penalty— which may be assumed by the chair if he thinks it appropriate —is undebatable, is unamendable, and requires a _____ vote.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* A) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:16 — “A motion to require the member’s departure during consideration of the penalty— which may be assumed by the chair if he thinks it appropriate —is undebatable, is unamendable, and requires a majority vote.”

**65.** `[C1577 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The offending member can be required to leave the hall during the _____ of his penalty, but he must be allowed to present his defense briefly first.

   *Answer:* consideration — “The offending member can be required to leave the hall during the consideration of his penalty, but he must be allowed to present his defense briefly first.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:16 — “The offending member can be required to leave the hall during the consideration of his penalty, but he must be allowed to present his defense briefly first.”

**66.** `[C1578 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Expulsion from membership requires a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “Expulsion from membership requires a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:17 — “Expulsion from membership requires a two-thirds vote.”

**67.** `[C1578 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the member denies having said anything improper, the words recorded by the _____ can be read to him and, if necessary, the assembly can decide by vote whether he was heard to say them.
   A. amendment
   B. extending
   C. Objection
   D. secretary

   *Answer:* D) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:17 — “If the member denies having said anything improper, the words recorded by the secretary can be read to him and, if necessary, the assembly can decide by vote whether he was heard to say them.”

**68.** `[C1578 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the demand of a single member—other than the named offender, who is not _____ to be a voting member while his case is pending—the vote on imposing a penalty must be taken by ballot, unless the penalty proposed is only that the offender be required to leave the hall for all or part of the remainder of the meeting.

   *Answer:* considered — “On the demand of a single member—other than the named offender, who is not considered to be a voting member while his case is pending—the vote on imposing a penalty must be taken by ballot, unless the penalty proposed is only that the offender be required to leave the hall for all or part of the remainder of the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:17 — “On the demand of a single member—other than the named offender, who is not considered to be a voting member while his case is pending—the vote on imposing a penalty must be taken by ballot, unless the penalty proposed is only that the offender be required to leave the hall for all or part of the remainder of the meeting.”

**69.** `[C1578 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Expulsion from _____ requires a two-thirds vote.
   A. Convention
   B. committees
   C. membership
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* C) membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:17 — “Expulsion from membership requires a two-thirds vote.”

**70.** `[C1578 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the member denies having said anything improper, the words recorded by the _____ can be read to him and, if necessary, the assembly can decide by vote whether he was heard to say them.

   *Answer:* secretary — “If the member denies having said anything improper, the words recorded by the secretary can be read to him and, if necessary, the assembly can decide by vote whether he was heard to say them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:17 — “If the member denies having said anything improper, the words recorded by the secretary can be read to him and, if necessary, the assembly can decide by vote whether he was heard to say them.”

**71.** `[C1579 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly orders an offending member to leave the hall during a meeting as described above and he refuses to do so, the _____ stated below regarding the removal of offenders apply;

   *Answer:* considerations — “If the assembly orders an offending member to leave the hall during a meeting as described above and he refuses to do so, the considerations stated below regarding the removal of offenders apply;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:18 — “If the assembly orders an offending member to leave the hall during a meeting as described above and he refuses to do so, the considerations stated below regarding the removal of offenders apply;”

**72.** `[C1579 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but such a member exposes himself to the possibility of more severe _____ action by the society.
   A. immediately
   B. presentation
   C. disciplinary
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:18 — “but such a member exposes himself to the possibility of more severe disciplinary action by the society.”

**73.** `[C1579 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly orders an offending member to leave the hall during a meeting as described above and he refuses to do so, the _____ stated below regarding the removal of offenders apply;
   A. Organization
   B. considerations
   C. subsequently
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* B) considerations. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:18 — “If the assembly orders an offending member to leave the hall during a meeting as described above and he refuses to do so, the considerations stated below regarding the removal of offenders apply;”

**74.** `[C1579 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but such a member exposes himself to the possibility of more severe _____ action by the society.

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “but such a member exposes himself to the possibility of more severe disciplinary action by the society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:18 — “but such a member exposes himself to the possibility of more severe disciplinary action by the society.”

**75.** `[C1580 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ from Annoyance by Nonmembers in a Meeting;

   *Answer:* Protection — “Protection from Annoyance by Nonmembers in a Meeting;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:19 — “Protection from Annoyance by Nonmembers in a Meeting;”

**76.** `[C1580 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An assembly has the right to protect itself from annoyance by nonmembers, and its full authority in this regard —as _____ from cases involving disorderly members— can be exercised by the chair acting alone.
   A. representatives
   B. distinguished
   C. Announcements
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) distinguished. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:19 — “An assembly has the right to protect itself from annoyance by nonmembers, and its full authority in this regard —as distinguished from cases involving disorderly members— can be exercised by the chair acting alone.”

**77.** `[C1580 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Protection from Annoyance by _____ in a Meeting;

   *Answer:* Nonmembers — “Protection from Annoyance by Nonmembers in a Meeting;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:19 — “Protection from Annoyance by Nonmembers in a Meeting;”

**78.** `[C1580 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, such an order may be _____ by a member.
   A. Nominees
   B. limiting
   C. appealed
   D. Interest

   *Answer:* C) appealed. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:19 — “However, such an order may be appealed by a member.”

**79.** `[C1580 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At a mass meeting (53), any person who attempts to disrupt the _____ in a manner obviously hostile to the announced purpose of the meeting can be treated as a nonmember under the provisions of this paragraph.

   *Answer:* proceedings — “At a mass meeting (53), any person who attempts to disrupt the proceedings in a manner obviously hostile to the announced purpose of the meeting can be treated as a nonmember under the provisions of this paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:19 — “At a mass meeting (53), any person who attempts to disrupt the proceedings in a manner obviously hostile to the announced purpose of the meeting can be treated as a nonmember under the provisions of this paragraph.”

**80.** `[C1581 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a person—whether a member of the assembly or not— refuses to obey the order of proper _____ to leave the hall during a meeting, the chair should take necessary measures to see that the order is enforced, but should be guided by a judicious appraisal of the situation.

   *Answer:* authority — “If a person—whether a member of the assembly or not— refuses to obey the order of proper authority to leave the hall during a meeting, the chair should take necessary measures to see that the order is enforced, but should be guided by a judicious appraisal of the situation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:20 — “If a person—whether a member of the assembly or not— refuses to obey the order of proper authority to leave the hall during a meeting, the chair should take necessary measures to see that the order is enforced, but should be guided by a judicious appraisal of the situation.”

**81.** `[C1581 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair can appoint a committee to escort the offender to the door, or the _____—if there is one—can be asked to do this.
   A. representatives
   B. subsequently
   C. immediately
   D. sergeantat-arms

   *Answer:* D) sergeantat-arms. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:20 — “The chair can appoint a committee to escort the offender to the door, or the sergeantat-arms—if there is one—can be asked to do this.”

**82.** `[C1581 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If those who are assigned that task are unable to persuade the offender to leave, it is usually preferable that he be removed by police— who may, however, be reluctant to intervene unless _____ of the organization are prepared to press charges.

   *Answer:* representatives — “If those who are assigned that task are unable to persuade the offender to leave, it is usually preferable that he be removed by police— who may, however, be reluctant to intervene unless representatives of the organization are prepared to press charges.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:20 — “If those who are assigned that task are unable to persuade the offender to leave, it is usually preferable that he be removed by police— who may, however, be reluctant to intervene unless representatives of the organization are prepared to press charges.”

**83.** `[C1581 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a person—whether a member of the assembly or not— refuses to obey the order of proper _____ to leave the hall during a meeting, the chair should take necessary measures to see that the order is enforced, but should be guided by a judicious appraisal of the situation.
   A. Committee
   B. rejecting
   C. auditors’
   D. authority

   *Answer:* D) authority. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:20 — “If a person—whether a member of the assembly or not— refuses to obey the order of proper authority to leave the hall during a meeting, the chair should take necessary measures to see that the order is enforced, but should be guided by a judicious appraisal of the situation.”

**84.** `[C1581 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair can appoint a committee to escort the offender to the door, or the _____—if there is one—can be asked to do this.

   *Answer:* sergeantat-arms — “The chair can appoint a committee to escort the offender to the door, or the sergeantat-arms—if there is one—can be asked to do this.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:20 — “The chair can appoint a committee to escort the offender to the door, or the sergeantat-arms—if there is one—can be asked to do this.”

**85.** `[C1582 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ or the members of the appointed committee themselves may attempt to remove the offender from the hall, using the minimum force necessary.

   *Answer:* sergeant-at-arms — “The sergeant-at-arms or the members of the appointed committee themselves may attempt to remove the offender from the hall, using the minimum force necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:21 — “The sergeant-at-arms or the members of the appointed committee themselves may attempt to remove the offender from the hall, using the minimum force necessary.”

**86.** `[C1582 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a step should generally be taken only as a last resort, since there may be adverse legal _____;
   A. Particular
   B. consequences
   C. subsequently
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) consequences. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:21 — “Such a step should generally be taken only as a last resort, since there may be adverse legal consequences;”

**87.** `[C1582 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and a person who would refuse to leave upon legitimate request may be the type most likely to bring suit, even if with little _____.

   *Answer:* justification — “and a person who would refuse to leave upon legitimate request may be the type most likely to bring suit, even if with little justification.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:21 — “and a person who would refuse to leave upon legitimate request may be the type most likely to bring suit, even if with little justification.”

**88.** `[C1582 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where possibly serious annoyance by hostile persons is _____—in some mass meetings, for example—it may be advisable to arrange in advance for the presence of police or guards from a security service agency.
   A. authorizes
   B. Incidental
   C. anticipated
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) anticipated. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:21 — “In cases where possibly serious annoyance by hostile persons is anticipated—in some mass meetings, for example—it may be advisable to arrange in advance for the presence of police or guards from a security service agency.”

**89.** `[C1582 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ or the members of the appointed committee themselves may attempt to remove the offender from the hall, using the minimum force necessary.
   A. sergeant-at-arms
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. Vice-President
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) sergeant-at-arms. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:21 — “The sergeant-at-arms or the members of the appointed committee themselves may attempt to remove the offender from the hall, using the minimum force necessary.”

**90.** `[C1583 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken _____ promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.

   *Answer:* other than — “In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:22 — “In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.”

**91.** `[C1583 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If improper conduct by a member of a society occurs elsewhere than at a meeting, the members generally have no _____ knowledge of the case.
   A. Permission
   B. settlement
   C. sustaining
   D. first-hand

   *Answer:* D) first-hand. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:22 — “If improper conduct by a member of a society occurs elsewhere than at a meeting, the members generally have no first-hand knowledge of the case.”

**92.** `[C1583 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Therefore, if disciplinary action is to be taken, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held before the assembly of the society, or before a committee—standing or special—which is then required to report its findings and _____ to the assembly for action.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “Therefore, if disciplinary action is to be taken, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held before the assembly of the society, or before a committee—standing or special—which is then required to report its findings and recommendations to the assembly for action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:22 — “Therefore, if disciplinary action is to be taken, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held before the assembly of the society, or before a committee—standing or special—which is then required to report its findings and recommendations to the assembly for action.”

**93.** `[C1583 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Therefore, if _____ action is to be taken, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held before the assembly of the society, or before a committee—standing or special—which is then required to report its findings and recommendations to the assembly for action.
   A. requirement
   B. Indefinitely
   C. immediately
   D. disciplinary

   *Answer:* D) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:22 — “Therefore, if disciplinary action is to be taken, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held before the assembly of the society, or before a committee—standing or special—which is then required to report its findings and recommendations to the assembly for action.”

**94.** `[C1583 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some societies (depending on _____ provisions of the bylaws, as explained in 62), the same steps must also be employed if an officer of the society is to be removed from office.

   *Answer:* particular — “In some societies (depending on particular provisions of the bylaws, as explained in 62), the same steps must also be employed if an officer of the society is to be removed from office.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:22 — “In some societies (depending on particular provisions of the bylaws, as explained in 62), the same steps must also be employed if an officer of the society is to be removed from office.”

**95.** `[C1584 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The presiding officer and other officers have the duties set forth in this manual (see 47) and in the _____’s bylaws.

   *Answer:* organization — “The presiding officer and other officers have the duties set forth in this manual (see 47) and in the organization’s bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:1 — “The presiding officer and other officers have the duties set forth in this manual (see 47) and in the organization’s bylaws.”

**96.** `[C1584 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This section covers _____ available if they neglect those duties, abuse their authority, or engage in other misconduct that calls into question their fitness for office.
   A. resolution
   B. introduced
   C. settlement
   D. procedures

   *Answer:* D) procedures. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:1 — “This section covers procedures available if they neglect those duties, abuse their authority, or engage in other misconduct that calls into question their fitness for office.”

**97.** `[C1584 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This section also covers removal from office at the _____ of the assembly when the bylaws permit such removal.

   *Answer:* pleasure — “This section also covers removal from office at the pleasure of the assembly when the bylaws permit such removal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:1 — “This section also covers removal from office at the pleasure of the assembly when the bylaws permit such removal.”

**98.** `[C1584 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The presiding officer and other officers have the duties set forth in this manual (see 47) and in the _____’s bylaws.
   A. Preparation
   B. subsequently
   C. organization
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:1 — “The presiding officer and other officers have the duties set forth in this manual (see 47) and in the organization’s bylaws.”

**99.** `[C1584 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This section covers _____ available if they neglect those duties, abuse their authority, or engage in other misconduct that calls into question their fitness for office.

   *Answer:* procedures — “This section covers procedures available if they neglect those duties, abuse their authority, or engage in other misconduct that calls into question their fitness for office.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:1 — “This section covers procedures available if they neglect those duties, abuse their authority, or engage in other misconduct that calls into question their fitness for office.”

**100.** `[C1585 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In attempting to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a _____ Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.

   *Answer:* Point of — “In attempting to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a Point of Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:2 — “In attempting to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a Point of Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.”

**101.** `[C1585 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Making and Enforcing Points of _____ and Appeals.
   A. Order
   B. Amend
   C. floor
   D. Forum

   *Answer:* A) Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:2 — “Making and Enforcing Points of Order and Appeals.”

**102.** `[C1585 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Making and _____ Points of Order and Appeals.

   *Answer:* Enforcing — “Making and Enforcing Points of Order and Appeals.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:2 — “Making and Enforcing Points of Order and Appeals.”

**103.** `[C1585 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a Point of Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.
   A. attempting
   B. committees
   C. Substitute
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* A) attempting. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:2 — “In attempting to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a Point of Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.”

**104.** `[C1586 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, some important rules of _____ procedure are (a) that the chair must recognize any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it (see 42);

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “For example, some important rules of parliamentary procedure are (a) that the chair must recognize any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it (see 42);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:3 — “For example, some important rules of parliamentary procedure are (a) that the chair must recognize any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it (see 42);”

**105.** `[C1586 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (b) that after a member has properly made a motion that is not dilatory (see 39), the chair must either state the question on it, or else rule it out of order for a _____ valid reason, require that the wording be clarified or be submitted in writing, or declare that it is not before the assembly for lack of a required second (see 4);
   A. presiding
   B. debatable
   C. specified
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* C) specified. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:3 — “(b) that after a member has properly made a motion that is not dilatory (see 39), the chair must either state the question on it, or else rule it out of order for a specified valid reason, require that the wording be clarified or be submitted in writing, or declare that it is not before the assembly for lack of a required second (see 4);”

**106.** `[C1586 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, some important rules of _____ procedure are (a) that the chair must recognize any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it (see 42);
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. recommendation
   C. requirement
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:3 — “For example, some important rules of parliamentary procedure are (a) that the chair must recognize any member who seeks the floor while entitled to it (see 42);”

**107.** `[C1586 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (b) that after a member has properly made a motion that is not dilatory (see 39), the chair must either state the question on it, or else rule it out of order for a _____ valid reason, require that the wording be clarified or be submitted in writing, or declare that it is not before the assembly for lack of a required second (see 4);

   *Answer:* specified — “(b) that after a member has properly made a motion that is not dilatory (see 39), the chair must either state the question on it, or else rule it out of order for a specified valid reason, require that the wording be clarified or be submitted in writing, or declare that it is not before the assembly for lack of a required second (see 4);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:3 — “(b) that after a member has properly made a motion that is not dilatory (see 39), the chair must either state the question on it, or else rule it out of order for a specified valid reason, require that the wording be clarified or be submitted in writing, or declare that it is not before the assembly for lack of a required second (see 4);”

**108.** `[C1587 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made nondilatory motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a _____.

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made nondilatory motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a Point of Order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:4 — “So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made nondilatory motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a Point of Order.”

**109.** `[C1587 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made _____ motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a Point of Order.
   A. undebatable
   B. Particular
   C. Substitute
   D. nondilatory

   *Answer:* D) nondilatory. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:4 — “So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made nondilatory motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a Point of Order.”

**110.** `[C1588 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To raise a _____, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, immediately addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “To raise a Point of Order, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, immediately addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:5 — “To raise a Point of Order, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, immediately addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;”

**111.** `[C1588 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To raise a Point of Order, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, _____ addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;
   A. Particular
   B. Preparation
   C. immediately
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:5 — “To raise a Point of Order, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, immediately addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;”

**112.** `[C1588 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and after the member does so, the chair is _____ either to rule whether the point is “well taken”—briefly stating the reasons for that ruling—or to submit the question to the assembly (see 23).

   *Answer:* obligated — “and after the member does so, the chair is obligated either to rule whether the point is “well taken”—briefly stating the reasons for that ruling—or to submit the question to the assembly (see 23).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:5 — “and after the member does so, the chair is obligated either to rule whether the point is “well taken”—briefly stating the reasons for that ruling—or to submit the question to the assembly (see 23).”

**113.** `[C1588 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and after the member does so, the chair is _____ either to rule whether the point is “well taken”—briefly stating the reasons for that ruling—or to submit the question to the assembly (see 23).
   A. delegates
   B. Objection
   C. expressly
   D. obligated

   *Answer:* D) obligated. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:5 — “and after the member does so, the chair is obligated either to rule whether the point is “well taken”—briefly stating the reasons for that ruling—or to submit the question to the assembly (see 23).”

**114.** `[C1589 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an _____ (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).

   *Answer:* Appeal — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:6 — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”

**115.** `[C1589 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).
   A. Incidental
   B. Immediately
   C. assemblies
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) Immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:6 — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”

**116.** `[C1589 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The member does this by rising and, without waiting to be recognized, addressing the chair, saying, “I appeal from the decision of the chair.” If the Appeal is seconded, the chair must state the question on it, clearly explain the exact _____ question at issue, allow any permissible debate, and put the question to a vote, as shown in 24.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The member does this by rising and, without waiting to be recognized, addressing the chair, saying, “I appeal from the decision of the chair.” If the Appeal is seconded, the chair must state the question on it, clearly explain the exact parliamentary question at issue, allow any permissible debate, and put the question to a vote, as shown in 24.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:6 — “The member does this by rising and, without waiting to be recognized, addressing the chair, saying, “I appeal from the decision of the chair.” If the Appeal is seconded, the chair must state the question on it, clearly explain the exact parliamentary question at issue, allow any permissible debate, and put the question to a vote, as shown in 24.”

**117.** `[C1589 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The member does this by rising and, without waiting to be recognized, addressing the chair, saying, “I appeal from the decision of the chair.” If the Appeal is seconded, the chair must state the question on it, clearly explain the exact _____ question at issue, allow any permissible debate, and put the question to a vote, as shown in 24.
   A. President-Elect
   B. specification
   C. Organization
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:6 — “The member does this by rising and, without waiting to be recognized, addressing the chair, saying, “I appeal from the decision of the chair.” If the Appeal is seconded, the chair must state the question on it, clearly explain the exact parliamentary question at issue, allow any permissible debate, and put the question to a vote, as shown in 24.”

**118.** `[C1590 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the _____, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:7 — “These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).”

**119.** `[C1590 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any _____, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).
   A. adhere
   B. adding
   C. adopts
   D. Appeal

   *Answer:* D) Appeal. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:7 — “These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).”

**120.** `[C1591 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair ignores a point of order that is not dilatory, the member can repeat the point of order a second and third time and if the chair still ignores it, the member, standing in his place, can _____ put the point of order to a vote without debate.

   *Answer:* immediately — “If the chair ignores a point of order that is not dilatory, the member can repeat the point of order a second and third time and if the chair still ignores it, the member, standing in his place, can immediately put the point of order to a vote without debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:8 — “If the chair ignores a point of order that is not dilatory, the member can repeat the point of order a second and third time and if the chair still ignores it, the member, standing in his place, can immediately put the point of order to a vote without debate.”

**121.** `[C1591 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The question may be put as, “Is the point of order that… well taken?” If the point of order was that the chair _____ ignored another motion, the member may, instead of repeating the point of order, repeat the original motion, and if it is seconded and the chair still ignores it, may, standing in his place, put the ignored motion to a vote without debate.
   A. Unfinished
   B. Particular
   C. improperly
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* C) improperly. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:8 — “The question may be put as, “Is the point of order that… well taken?” If the point of order was that the chair improperly ignored another motion, the member may, instead of repeating the point of order, repeat the original motion, and if it is seconded and the chair still ignores it, may, standing in his place, put the ignored motion to a vote without debate.”

**122.** `[C1591 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair ignores a point of order that is not dilatory, the member can repeat the point of order a second and third time and if the chair still ignores it, the member, standing in his place, can _____ put the point of order to a vote without debate.
   A. resolutions
   B. assemblies
   C. immediately
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:8 — “If the chair ignores a point of order that is not dilatory, the member can repeat the point of order a second and third time and if the chair still ignores it, the member, standing in his place, can immediately put the point of order to a vote without debate.”

**123.** `[C1591 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The question may be put as, “Is the point of order that… well taken?” If the point of order was that the chair _____ ignored another motion, the member may, instead of repeating the point of order, repeat the original motion, and if it is seconded and the chair still ignores it, may, standing in his place, put the ignored motion to a vote without debate.

   *Answer:* improperly — “The question may be put as, “Is the point of order that… well taken?” If the point of order was that the chair improperly ignored another motion, the member may, instead of repeating the point of order, repeat the original motion, and if it is seconded and the chair still ignores it, may, standing in his place, put the ignored motion to a vote without debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:8 — “The question may be put as, “Is the point of order that… well taken?” If the point of order was that the chair improperly ignored another motion, the member may, instead of repeating the point of order, repeat the original motion, and if it is seconded and the chair still ignores it, may, standing in his place, put the ignored motion to a vote without debate.”

**124.** `[C1592 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Likewise, if the chair ignores an appeal _____ made and seconded, a member can repeat the appeal and if, despite its being seconded, the chair ignores it again, the member can repeat it a third time and if it is again seconded but still ignored by the chair, the member can immediately, standing in his place, put the appeal to a vote without debate.

   *Answer:* appropriately — “Likewise, if the chair ignores an appeal appropriately made and seconded, a member can repeat the appeal and if, despite its being seconded, the chair ignores it again, the member can repeat it a third time and if it is again seconded but still ignored by the chair, the member can immediately, standing in his place, put the appeal to a vote without debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:9 — “Likewise, if the chair ignores an appeal appropriately made and seconded, a member can repeat the appeal and if, despite its being seconded, the chair ignores it again, the member can repeat it a third time and if it is again seconded but still ignored by the chair, the member can immediately, standing in his place, put the appeal to a vote without debate.”

**125.** `[C1592 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The question may be put as: “Shall the decision of the chair be _____?”
   A. sustained
   B. Signature
   C. Precedent
   D. president

   *Answer:* A) sustained. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:9 — “The question may be put as: “Shall the decision of the chair be sustained?””

**126.** `[C1592 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Likewise, if the chair ignores an appeal _____ made and seconded, a member can repeat the appeal and if, despite its being seconded, the chair ignores it again, the member can repeat it a third time and if it is again seconded but still ignored by the chair, the member can immediately, standing in his place, put the appeal to a vote without debate.
   A. appropriately
   B. Indefinitely
   C. consideration
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) appropriately. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:9 — “Likewise, if the chair ignores an appeal appropriately made and seconded, a member can repeat the appeal and if, despite its being seconded, the chair ignores it again, the member can repeat it a third time and if it is again seconded but still ignored by the chair, the member can immediately, standing in his place, put the appeal to a vote without debate.”

**127.** `[C1592 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The question may be put as: “Shall the decision of the chair be _____?”

   *Answer:* sustained — “The question may be put as: “Shall the decision of the chair be sustained?””. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:9 — “The question may be put as: “Shall the decision of the chair be sustained?””

**128.** `[C1593 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Removal of Presiding Officer from Chair for All or Part of _____.

   *Answer:* a Session — “Removal of Presiding Officer from Chair for All or Part of a Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:10 — “Removal of Presiding Officer from Chair for All or Part of a Session.”

**129.** `[C1593 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Removal of _____ Officer from Chair for All or Part of a Session.
   A. amendment
   B. delegates
   C. secondary
   D. Presiding

   *Answer:* D) Presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:10 — “Removal of Presiding Officer from Chair for All or Part of a Session.”

**130.** `[C1593 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on an appeal (or on a point of order submitted to a vote of the assembly) or otherwise culpably fails to perform the duties of the chair properly in a meeting, the assembly may employ measures _____ to replace the chair with another presiding officer expected to act in accordance with the will of the assembly.

   *Answer:* temporarily — “If the chair fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on an appeal (or on a point of order submitted to a vote of the assembly) or otherwise culpably fails to perform the duties of the chair properly in a meeting, the assembly may employ measures temporarily to replace the chair with another presiding officer expected to act in accordance with the will of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:10 — “If the chair fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on an appeal (or on a point of order submitted to a vote of the assembly) or otherwise culpably fails to perform the duties of the chair properly in a meeting, the assembly may employ measures temporarily to replace the chair with another presiding officer expected to act in accordance with the will of the assembly.”

**131.** `[C1593 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on an appeal (or on a point of order submitted to a vote of the assembly) or otherwise culpably fails to perform the duties of the chair properly in a meeting, the assembly may employ measures _____ to replace the chair with another presiding officer expected to act in accordance with the will of the assembly.
   A. Unfinished
   B. Particular
   C. temporarily
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) temporarily. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:10 — “If the chair fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on an appeal (or on a point of order submitted to a vote of the assembly) or otherwise culpably fails to perform the duties of the chair properly in a meeting, the assembly may employ measures temporarily to replace the chair with another presiding officer expected to act in accordance with the will of the assembly.”

**132.** `[C1595 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, a motion can be made to _____ so as to take away from him the authority to preside during all or part of a given session.

   *Answer:* Suspend the Rules — “However, a motion can be made to Suspend the Rules so as to take away from him the authority to preside during all or part of a given session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:12 — “However, a motion can be made to Suspend the Rules so as to take away from him the authority to preside during all or part of a given session.”

**133.** `[C1595 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair is not an _____ or elected chairman pro tem, a motion to declare the chair vacant is not in order.
   A. Signature
   B. reception
   C. exhausted
   D. appointed

   *Answer:* D) appointed. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:12 — “If the chair is not an appointed or elected chairman pro tem, a motion to declare the chair vacant is not in order.”

**134.** `[C1595 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, a motion can be made to Suspend the Rules so as to take away from him the _____ to preside during all or part of a given session.

   *Answer:* authority — “However, a motion can be made to Suspend the Rules so as to take away from him the authority to preside during all or part of a given session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:12 — “However, a motion can be made to Suspend the Rules so as to take away from him the authority to preside during all or part of a given session.”

**135.** `[C1595 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair is not an _____ or elected chairman pro tem, a motion to declare the chair vacant is not in order.

   *Answer:* appointed — “If the chair is not an appointed or elected chairman pro tem, a motion to declare the chair vacant is not in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:12 — “If the chair is not an appointed or elected chairman pro tem, a motion to declare the chair vacant is not in order.”

**136.** `[C1596 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any one motion to _____ that might limit the authority or duties of the presiding officer during a meeting can remain in effect, at most, for one session.

   *Answer:* Suspend the Rules — “Any one motion to Suspend the Rules that might limit the authority or duties of the presiding officer during a meeting can remain in effect, at most, for one session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:13 — “Any one motion to Suspend the Rules that might limit the authority or duties of the presiding officer during a meeting can remain in effect, at most, for one session.”

**137.** `[C1596 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any one motion to _____ the Rules that might limit the authority or duties of the presiding officer during a meeting can remain in effect, at most, for one session.
   A. Session
   B. adjourn
   C. Suspend
   D. carried

   *Answer:* C) Suspend. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:13 — “Any one motion to Suspend the Rules that might limit the authority or duties of the presiding officer during a meeting can remain in effect, at most, for one session.”

**138.** `[C1597 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to suspend the rules is adopted by a twothirds vote, then, unless the motion names a new occupant of the chair, the ranking _____ (or, in the absence of the vice-president, an elected temporary presiding officer;

   *Answer:* vice-president — “If the motion to suspend the rules is adopted by a twothirds vote, then, unless the motion names a new occupant of the chair, the ranking vice-president (or, in the absence of the vice-president, an elected temporary presiding officer;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:14 — “If the motion to suspend the rules is adopted by a twothirds vote, then, unless the motion names a new occupant of the chair, the ranking vice-president (or, in the absence of the vice-president, an elected temporary presiding officer;”

**139.** `[C1597 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to suspend the rules is adopted by a twothirds vote, then, unless the motion names a new occupant of the chair, the ranking _____ (or, in the absence of the vice-president, an elected temporary presiding officer;
   A. constitution
   B. interrupted
   C. Responsibilities
   D. vice-president

   *Answer:* D) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:14 — “If the motion to suspend the rules is adopted by a twothirds vote, then, unless the motion names a new occupant of the chair, the ranking vice-president (or, in the absence of the vice-president, an elected temporary presiding officer;”

**140.** `[C1598 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A permanent removal of the presiding officer, and removal of authority to exercise _____ duties conferred by the bylaws, requires the procedure described below.

   *Answer:* administrative — “A permanent removal of the presiding officer, and removal of authority to exercise administrative duties conferred by the bylaws, requires the procedure described below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:15 — “A permanent removal of the presiding officer, and removal of authority to exercise administrative duties conferred by the bylaws, requires the procedure described below.”

**141.** `[C1598 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A permanent removal of the presiding officer, and removal of authority to exercise _____ duties conferred by the bylaws, requires the procedure described below.
   A. Information
   B. administrative
   C. Organization
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* B) administrative. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:15 — “A permanent removal of the presiding officer, and removal of authority to exercise administrative duties conferred by the bylaws, requires the procedure described below.”

**142.** `[C1599 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except as the bylaws may provide _____, any regularly elected officer of a permanent society can be removed from office by the society’s assembly as follows:

   *Answer:* otherwise — “Except as the bylaws may provide otherwise, any regularly elected officer of a permanent society can be removed from office by the society’s assembly as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:16 — “Except as the bylaws may provide otherwise, any regularly elected officer of a permanent society can be removed from office by the society’s assembly as follows:”

**143.** `[C1599 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except as the bylaws may provide _____, any regularly elected officer of a permanent society can be removed from office by the society’s assembly as follows:
   A. committee
   B. Crestwood
   C. otherwise
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* C) otherwise. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:16 — “Except as the bylaws may provide otherwise, any regularly elected officer of a permanent society can be removed from office by the society’s assembly as follows:”

**144.** `[C1600 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As explained in 61 and 62, the removal of an officer for cause, or the _____ of a member for improper conduct, may require that charges be preferred and that a formal trial be held.

   *Answer:* discipline — “As explained in 61 and 62, the removal of an officer for cause, or the discipline of a member for improper conduct, may require that charges be preferred and that a formal trial be held.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:1 — “As explained in 61 and 62, the removal of an officer for cause, or the discipline of a member for improper conduct, may require that charges be preferred and that a formal trial be held.”

**145.** `[C1600 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The full _____ for such cases is described in this section.
   A. procedure
   B. secondary
   C. Privilege
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* A) procedure. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:1 — “The full procedure for such cases is described in this section.”

**146.** `[C1600 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As explained in 61 and 62, the removal of an officer for cause, or the _____ of a member for improper conduct, may require that charges be preferred and that a formal trial be held.
   A. discipline
   B. authorizes
   C. Unfinished
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) discipline. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:1 — “As explained in 61 and 62, the removal of an officer for cause, or the discipline of a member for improper conduct, may require that charges be preferred and that a formal trial be held.”

**147.** `[C1600 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The full _____ for such cases is described in this section.

   *Answer:* procedure — “The full procedure for such cases is described in this section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:1 — “The full procedure for such cases is described in this section.”

**148.** `[C1601 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A society has the right to _____ the character of its members and officers as may be necessary to the enforcement of its own standards.

   *Answer:* investigate — “A society has the right to investigate the character of its members and officers as may be necessary to the enforcement of its own standards.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:2 — “A society has the right to investigate the character of its members and officers as may be necessary to the enforcement of its own standards.”

**149.** `[C1601 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But neither the society nor any member has the right to make public any information obtained through such _____;
   A. interrupted
   B. subsequently
   C. investigation
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* C) investigation. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:2 — “But neither the society nor any member has the right to make public any information obtained through such investigation;”

**150.** `[C1601 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: if it becomes common _____ within the society, it may not be revealed to any persons outside the society.

   *Answer:* knowledge — “if it becomes common knowledge within the society, it may not be revealed to any persons outside the society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:2 — “if it becomes common knowledge within the society, it may not be revealed to any persons outside the society.”

**151.** `[C1601 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Consequently, a trial must always be held in executive session, as must the introduction and _____ of all resolutions leading up to the trial.
   A. president-elect
   B. consideration
   C. Announcements
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:2 — “Consequently, a trial must always be held in executive session, as must the introduction and consideration of all resolutions leading up to the trial.”

**152.** `[C1601 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A society has the right to _____ the character of its members and officers as may be necessary to the enforcement of its own standards.
   A. interrupted
   B. Reconsider
   C. investigate
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) investigate. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:2 — “A society has the right to investigate the character of its members and officers as may be necessary to the enforcement of its own standards.”

**153.** `[C1602 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If (after trial) a member is expelled or an officer is removed from office, the society has the right to disclose that fact— circulating it only to the extent required for the protection of the society or, possibly, of other _____.

   *Answer:* organizations — “If (after trial) a member is expelled or an officer is removed from office, the society has the right to disclose that fact— circulating it only to the extent required for the protection of the society or, possibly, of other organizations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:3 — “If (after trial) a member is expelled or an officer is removed from office, the society has the right to disclose that fact— circulating it only to the extent required for the protection of the society or, possibly, of other organizations.”

**154.** `[C1602 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Neither the society nor any of its members has the right to make public the charge of which an officer or member has been found guilty, or to reveal any other details _____ with the case.
   A. committee
   B. connected
   C. Committee
   D. rejecting

   *Answer:* B) connected. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:3 — “Neither the society nor any of its members has the right to make public the charge of which an officer or member has been found guilty, or to reveal any other details connected with the case.”

**155.** `[C1602 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To make any of the facts public may _____ libel.

   *Answer:* constitute — “To make any of the facts public may constitute libel.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:3 — “To make any of the facts public may constitute libel.”

**156.** `[C1602 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A trial by the society cannot legally establish the guilt of the accused, as _____ in a court of law;
   A. Procedures
   B. introduced
   C. committees
   D. understood

   *Answer:* D) understood. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:3 — “A trial by the society cannot legally establish the guilt of the accused, as understood in a court of law;”

**157.** `[C1602 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: it can only establish his guilt as affecting the society’s judgment of his fitness for _____ or office.

   *Answer:* membership — “it can only establish his guilt as affecting the society’s judgment of his fitness for membership or office.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:3 — “it can only establish his guilt as affecting the society’s judgment of his fitness for membership or office.”

**158.** `[C1603 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Ordinarily it is impossible for the society to obtain _____ proof of facts in disciplinary cases.

   *Answer:* legal — “Ordinarily it is impossible for the society to obtain legal proof of facts in disciplinary cases.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:4 — “Ordinarily it is impossible for the society to obtain legal proof of facts in disciplinary cases.”

**159.** `[C1603 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Ordinarily it is impossible for the society to obtain legal proof of facts in _____ cases.
   A. deliberative
   B. disciplinary
   C. Permission
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:4 — “Ordinarily it is impossible for the society to obtain legal proof of facts in disciplinary cases.”

**160.** `[C1603 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To get at the truth under the _____ of such a trial, hearsay evidence has to be admissible, and judgment as to the best interests of the society may have to be based on it.

   *Answer:* conditions — “To get at the truth under the conditions of such a trial, hearsay evidence has to be admissible, and judgment as to the best interests of the society may have to be based on it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:4 — “To get at the truth under the conditions of such a trial, hearsay evidence has to be admissible, and judgment as to the best interests of the society may have to be based on it.”

**161.** `[C1603 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The persons with _____ knowledge may be nonmembers, who probably will decline to testify, and may be willing only to reveal the facts privately to a single member on condition that their names in no way be connected with the case.
   A. authorizes
   B. assemblies
   C. incidental
   D. first-hand

   *Answer:* D) first-hand. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:4 — “The persons with first-hand knowledge may be nonmembers, who probably will decline to testify, and may be willing only to reveal the facts privately to a single member on condition that their names in no way be connected with the case.”

**162.** `[C1603 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even members may be _____ to give formal testimony against the accused.

   *Answer:* reluctant — “Even members may be reluctant to give formal testimony against the accused.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:4 — “Even members may be reluctant to give formal testimony against the accused.”

**163.** `[C1604 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member or officer has the right that _____ against his good name shall not be made except by charges brought on reasonable ground.

   *Answer:* allegations — “A member or officer has the right that allegations against his good name shall not be made except by charges brought on reasonable ground.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:5 — “A member or officer has the right that allegations against his good name shall not be made except by charges brought on reasonable ground.”

**164.** `[C1604 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If thus accused, he has the right to due process—that is, to be _____ of the charge and given time to prepare his defense, to appear and defend himself, and to be fairly treated.
   A. Question
   B. meetings
   C. informed
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* C) informed. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:5 — “If thus accused, he has the right to due process—that is, to be informed of the charge and given time to prepare his defense, to appear and defend himself, and to be fairly treated.”

**165.** `[C1604 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member or officer has the right that _____ against his good name shall not be made except by charges brought on reasonable ground.
   A. allegations
   B. adjournment
   C. Reconsider
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) allegations. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:5 — “A member or officer has the right that allegations against his good name shall not be made except by charges brought on reasonable ground.”

**166.** `[C1604 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If thus accused, he has the right to due process—that is, to be _____ of the charge and given time to prepare his defense, to appear and defend himself, and to be fairly treated.

   *Answer:* informed — “If thus accused, he has the right to due process—that is, to be informed of the charge and given time to prepare his defense, to appear and defend himself, and to be fairly treated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:5 — “If thus accused, he has the right to due process—that is, to be informed of the charge and given time to prepare his defense, to appear and defend himself, and to be fairly treated.”

**167.** `[C1605 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member or officer is guilty of a serious offense and knows that other members are in possession of the facts, he may wish to submit his _____.

   *Answer:* resignation — “If a member or officer is guilty of a serious offense and knows that other members are in possession of the facts, he may wish to submit his resignation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:6 — “If a member or officer is guilty of a serious offense and knows that other members are in possession of the facts, he may wish to submit his resignation.”

**168.** `[C1605 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the good of the society appears to demand the departure of an offender, it is usually best for all concerned to offer him the _____ to resign quietly before charges are preferred.
   A. Convention
   B. opportunity
   C. Procedures
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:6 — “When the good of the society appears to demand the departure of an offender, it is usually best for all concerned to offer him the opportunity to resign quietly before charges are preferred.”
