**169.** `[C1605 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a member or officer is guilty of a serious offense and knows that other members are in possession of the facts, he may wish to submit his _____.
   A. constitution
   B. resignation
   C. Convention
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) resignation. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:6 — “If a member or officer is guilty of a serious offense and knows that other members are in possession of the facts, he may wish to submit his resignation.”

**170.** `[C1605 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the good of the society appears to demand the departure of an offender, it is usually best for all concerned to offer him the _____ to resign quietly before charges are preferred.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “When the good of the society appears to demand the departure of an offender, it is usually best for all concerned to offer him the opportunity to resign quietly before charges are preferred.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:6 — “When the good of the society appears to demand the departure of an offender, it is usually best for all concerned to offer him the opportunity to resign quietly before charges are preferred.”

**171.** `[C1606 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Most ordinary societies should never have to hold a formal trial, and their bylaws need not be _____ with clauses on discipline.

   *Answer:* encumbered — “Most ordinary societies should never have to hold a formal trial, and their bylaws need not be encumbered with clauses on discipline.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:7 — “Most ordinary societies should never have to hold a formal trial, and their bylaws need not be encumbered with clauses on discipline.”

**172.** `[C1606 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For the protection both of the society and of its members and officers, however, the basic steps which, in any _____, make up the elements of fair disciplinary process should be understood.
   A. Preparation
   B. organization
   C. consideration
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:7 — “For the protection both of the society and of its members and officers, however, the basic steps which, in any organization, make up the elements of fair disciplinary process should be understood.”

**173.** `[C1606 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any special procedures _____ should be built essentially around them, and the steps must be followed in the absence of such provisions.

   *Answer:* established — “Any special procedures established should be built essentially around them, and the steps must be followed in the absence of such provisions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:7 — “Any special procedures established should be built essentially around them, and the steps must be followed in the absence of such provisions.”

**174.** `[C1606 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As set forth below, these are: (1) confidential _____ by a committee;
   A. subsequently
   B. Announcements
   C. investigation
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* C) investigation. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:7 — “As set forth below, these are: (1) confidential investigation by a committee;”

**175.** `[C1606 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (2) report of the committee, and _____ of charges if warranted;

   *Answer:* preferral — “(2) report of the committee, and preferral of charges if warranted;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:7 — “(2) report of the committee, and preferral of charges if warranted;”

**176.** `[C1607 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____.

   *Answer:* Confidential Investigation by Committee — “Confidential Investigation by Committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:8 — “Confidential Investigation by Committee.”

**177.** `[C1607 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Confidential _____ by Committee.
   A. specification
   B. representatives
   C. president-elect
   D. Investigation

   *Answer:* D) Investigation. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:8 — “Confidential Investigation by Committee.”

**178.** `[C1608 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Accordingly, if the rules of the organization do not otherwise provide for the method of charge and trial, a member may, at a time when nonmembers are not present, offer a resolution to appoint an _____ committee.

   *Answer:* investigating — “Accordingly, if the rules of the organization do not otherwise provide for the method of charge and trial, a member may, at a time when nonmembers are not present, offer a resolution to appoint an investigating committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:9 — “Accordingly, if the rules of the organization do not otherwise provide for the method of charge and trial, a member may, at a time when nonmembers are not present, offer a resolution to appoint an investigating committee.”

**179.** `[C1608 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Accordingly, if the rules of the organization do not otherwise provide for the method of charge and trial, a member may, at a time when nonmembers are not present, offer a _____ to appoint an investigating committee.
   A. sustaining
   B. landscaped
   C. resolution
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:9 — “Accordingly, if the rules of the organization do not otherwise provide for the method of charge and trial, a member may, at a time when nonmembers are not present, offer a resolution to appoint an investigating committee.”

**180.** `[C1609 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For the _____ of parties who may be innocent, the first resolution should avoid details as much as possible.

   *Answer:* protection — “For the protection of parties who may be innocent, the first resolution should avoid details as much as possible.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:11 — “For the protection of parties who may be innocent, the first resolution should avoid details as much as possible.”

**181.** `[C1609 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ member may not prefer charges, even if that member has proof of an officer’s or member’s wrongdoing.
   A. convention
   B. Unfinished
   C. individual
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) individual. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:11 — “An individual member may not prefer charges, even if that member has proof of an officer’s or member’s wrongdoing.”

**182.** `[C1609 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member introduces a resolution preferring charges unsupported by an investigating committee’s _____, the chair must rule the resolution out of order, informing the member that it would instead be in order to move the appointment of such a committee (by a resolution, as in the example above).

   *Answer:* recommendation — “If a member introduces a resolution preferring charges unsupported by an investigating committee’s recommendation, the chair must rule the resolution out of order, informing the member that it would instead be in order to move the appointment of such a committee (by a resolution, as in the example above).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:11 — “If a member introduces a resolution preferring charges unsupported by an investigating committee’s recommendation, the chair must rule the resolution out of order, informing the member that it would instead be in order to move the appointment of such a committee (by a resolution, as in the example above).”

**183.** `[C1609 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A resolution is improper if it implies the truth of specific rumors or contains _____ unfavorable to an officer or member, even one who is to be accused.
   A. Information
   B. adjournment
   C. Indefinitely
   D. insinuations

   *Answer:* D) insinuations. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:11 — “A resolution is improper if it implies the truth of specific rumors or contains insinuations unfavorable to an officer or member, even one who is to be accused.”

**184.** `[C1609 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For the protection of parties who may be innocent, the first _____ should avoid details as much as possible.

   *Answer:* resolution — “For the protection of parties who may be innocent, the first resolution should avoid details as much as possible.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:11 — “For the protection of parties who may be innocent, the first resolution should avoid details as much as possible.”

**185.** `[C1610 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ committee appointed as described above has no power to require the accused, or any other person, to appear before it, but it should quietly conduct a complete investigation, making an effort to learn all relevant facts.

   *Answer:* investigating — “An investigating committee appointed as described above has no power to require the accused, or any other person, to appear before it, but it should quietly conduct a complete investigation, making an effort to learn all relevant facts.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:12 — “An investigating committee appointed as described above has no power to require the accused, or any other person, to appear before it, but it should quietly conduct a complete investigation, making an effort to learn all relevant facts.”

**186.** `[C1610 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Information obtained in strict confidence may help the committee to form an opinion, but it may not be reported to the society or used in a trial—except as may be possible without bringing out the _____ particulars.
   A. adjournment
   B. Announcements
   C. confidential
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) confidential. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:12 — “Information obtained in strict confidence may help the committee to form an opinion, but it may not be reported to the society or used in a trial—except as may be possible without bringing out the confidential particulars.”

**187.** `[C1610 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before any action is taken, fairness demands that the committee or some of its members make a _____ attempt to meet with the accused for frank discussion and to hear his side of the story.

   *Answer:* reasonable — “Before any action is taken, fairness demands that the committee or some of its members make a reasonable attempt to meet with the accused for frank discussion and to hear his side of the story.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:12 — “Before any action is taken, fairness demands that the committee or some of its members make a reasonable attempt to meet with the accused for frank discussion and to hear his side of the story.”

**188.** `[C1610 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It may be possible at this stage to point out to the accused that if he does not rectify the _____ or resign, he probably will be brought to trial.
   A. Reporting
   B. presiding
   C. extending
   D. situation

   *Answer:* D) situation. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:12 — “It may be possible at this stage to point out to the accused that if he does not rectify the situation or resign, he probably will be brought to trial.”

**189.** `[C1610 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ committee appointed as described above has no power to require the accused, or any other person, to appear before it, but it should quietly conduct a complete investigation, making an effort to learn all relevant facts.
   A. Information
   B. investigating
   C. specification
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* B) investigating. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:12 — “An investigating committee appointed as described above has no power to require the accused, or any other person, to appear before it, but it should quietly conduct a complete investigation, making an effort to learn all relevant facts.”

**190.** `[C1611 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But if the committee from its investigations finds substance to the allegations and cannot resolve the matter satisfactorily in any other way, it makes a report in writing—which is signed by every committee member who agrees—outlining the course of its investigation and recommending in the report the adoption of resolutions preferring _____, arranging for a trial, and, if desired, suspending the rights of the accused, as in the following example:

   *Answer:* charges — “But if the committee from its investigations finds substance to the allegations and cannot resolve the matter satisfactorily in any other way, it makes a report in writing—which is signed by every committee member who agrees—outlining the course of its investigation and recommending in the report the adoption of resolutions preferring charges, arranging for a trial, and, if desired, suspending the rights of the accused, as in the following example:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:13 — “But if the committee from its investigations finds substance to the allegations and cannot resolve the matter satisfactorily in any other way, it makes a report in writing—which is signed by every committee member who agrees—outlining the course of its investigation and recommending in the report the adoption of resolutions preferring charges, arranging for a trial, and, if desired, suspending the rights of the accused, as in the following example:”

**191.** `[C1611 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If after investigation the committee’s opinion is favorable to the accused, or if it finds that the matter can be resolved _____ without a trial, it reports that fact.
   A. satisfactorily
   B. subsequently
   C. Announcements
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* A) satisfactorily. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:13 — “If after investigation the committee’s opinion is favorable to the accused, or if it finds that the matter can be resolved satisfactorily without a trial, it reports that fact.”

**192.** `[C1611 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But if the committee from its _____ finds substance to the allegations and cannot resolve the matter satisfactorily in any other way, it makes a report in writing—which is signed by every committee member who agrees—outlining the course of its investigation and recommending in the report the adoption of resolutions preferring charges, arranging for a trial, and, if desired, suspending the rights of the accused, as in the following example:

   *Answer:* investigations — “But if the committee from its investigations finds substance to the allegations and cannot resolve the matter satisfactorily in any other way, it makes a report in writing—which is signed by every committee member who agrees—outlining the course of its investigation and recommending in the report the adoption of resolutions preferring charges, arranging for a trial, and, if desired, suspending the rights of the accused, as in the following example:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:13 — “But if the committee from its investigations finds substance to the allegations and cannot resolve the matter satisfactorily in any other way, it makes a report in writing—which is signed by every committee member who agrees—outlining the course of its investigation and recommending in the report the adoption of resolutions preferring charges, arranging for a trial, and, if desired, suspending the rights of the accused, as in the following example:”

**193.** `[C1611 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If after investigation the committee’s opinion is favorable to the accused, or if it finds that the matter can be resolved _____ without a trial, it reports that fact.

   *Answer:* satisfactorily — “If after investigation the committee’s opinion is favorable to the accused, or if it finds that the matter can be resolved satisfactorily without a trial, it reports that fact.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:13 — “If after investigation the committee’s opinion is favorable to the accused, or if it finds that the matter can be resolved satisfactorily without a trial, it reports that fact.”

**194.** `[C1612 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and _____:
   A. consideration
   B. Information
   C. interrupted
   D. specifications

   *Answer:* D) specifications. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:15 — “is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and specifications:”

**195.** `[C1612 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and _____:

   *Answer:* specifications — “is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and specifications:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:15 — “is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and specifications:”

**196.** `[C1613 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, That from the time official notification of this resolution is delivered to J.M.’s address until disposition of the case, all of J.M.’s authority, rights, and duties pertaining to the office of treasurer are suspended.

   *Answer:* Resolved — “Resolved, That from the time official notification of this resolution is delivered to J.M.’s address until disposition of the case, all of J.M.’s authority, rights, and duties pertaining to the office of treasurer are suspended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:16 — “Resolved, That from the time official notification of this resolution is delivered to J.M.’s address until disposition of the case, all of J.M.’s authority, rights, and duties pertaining to the office of treasurer are suspended.”

**197.** `[C1613 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Resolved, That from the time official _____ of this resolution is delivered to J.M.’s address until disposition of the case, all of J.M.’s authority, rights, and duties pertaining to the office of treasurer are suspended.
   A. Preparation
   B. subsequently
   C. notification
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) notification. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:16 — “Resolved, That from the time official notification of this resolution is delivered to J.M.’s address until disposition of the case, all of J.M.’s authority, rights, and duties pertaining to the office of treasurer are suspended.”

**198.** `[C1614 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a _____ proceeding against a member, an example of the second and third resolutions is:

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “In a disciplinary proceeding against a member, an example of the second and third resolutions is:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:18 — “In a disciplinary proceeding against a member, an example of the second and third resolutions is:”

**199.** `[C1614 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a _____ proceeding against a member, an example of the second and third resolutions is:
   A. constitution
   B. deliberative
   C. disciplinary
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:18 — “In a disciplinary proceeding against a member, an example of the second and third resolutions is:”

**200.** `[C1615 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: N is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why he should not be expelled from the Society on the following charge and _____:
   A. presentation
   B. specifications
   C. constitution
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) specifications. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:19 — “N is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why he should not be expelled from the Society on the following charge and specifications:”

**201.** `[C1615 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: N is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why he should not be expelled from the Society on the following charge and _____:

   *Answer:* specifications — “N is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why he should not be expelled from the Society on the following charge and specifications:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:19 — “N is hereby cited to appear at said adjourned meeting for trial, to show cause why he should not be expelled from the Society on the following charge and specifications:”

**202.** `[C1616 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, That from the time official notification of this resolution is delivered to Mr.

   *Answer:* Resolved — “Resolved, That from the time official notification of this resolution is delivered to Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:20 — “Resolved, That from the time official notification of this resolution is delivered to Mr.”

**203.** `[C1616 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Resolved, That from the time official _____ of this resolution is delivered to Mr.
   A. Permission
   B. notification
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) notification. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:20 — “Resolved, That from the time official notification of this resolution is delivered to Mr.”

**204.** `[C1616 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: N’s address until _____ of the case, all of Mr.

   *Answer:* disposition — “N’s address until disposition of the case, all of Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:20 — “N’s address until disposition of the case, all of Mr.”

**205.** `[C1616 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: N’s address until _____ of the case, all of Mr.
   A. Substitute
   B. resolutions
   C. Permission
   D. disposition

   *Answer:* D) disposition. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:20 — “N’s address until disposition of the case, all of Mr.”

**206.** `[C1617 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: With reference to an _____ date for which to set the trial, thirty days is a reasonable time to allow the accused to prepare his defense.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “With reference to an appropriate date for which to set the trial, thirty days is a reasonable time to allow the accused to prepare his defense.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:21 — “With reference to an appropriate date for which to set the trial, thirty days is a reasonable time to allow the accused to prepare his defense.”

**207.** `[C1617 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a trial is to be before the assembly of the society, it is generally a good policy to hold it at a meeting devoted _____ to the matter, such as an adjourned meeting as in the example above.
   A. Definitely
   B. committees
   C. Organization
   D. exclusively

   *Answer:* D) exclusively. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:21 — “When a trial is to be before the assembly of the society, it is generally a good policy to hold it at a meeting devoted exclusively to the matter, such as an adjourned meeting as in the example above.”

**208.** `[C1617 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To devote a meeting to the trial when there is to be another regular meeting between the date of adoption of these _____ and the date desired for the trial, the first resolution would establish a special meeting instead of an adjourned meeting (see 9).

   *Answer:* resolutions — “To devote a meeting to the trial when there is to be another regular meeting between the date of adoption of these resolutions and the date desired for the trial, the first resolution would establish a special meeting instead of an adjourned meeting (see 9).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:21 — “To devote a meeting to the trial when there is to be another regular meeting between the date of adoption of these resolutions and the date desired for the trial, the first resolution would establish a special meeting instead of an adjourned meeting (see 9).”

**209.** `[C1617 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If believed advisable—and particularly when the trial is likely to be delicate, involve potential scandal, or be long and troublesome, or when the assembly of the organization is large—the resolutions reported by the investigating committee, instead of providing for trial before the entire assembly, can be worded so as to establish a committee to hear the trial and report its findings and _____ to the assembly for action.
   A. adjournment
   B. recommendations
   C. resolutions
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:21 — “If believed advisable—and particularly when the trial is likely to be delicate, involve potential scandal, or be long and troublesome, or when the assembly of the organization is large—the resolutions reported by the investigating committee, instead of providing for trial before the entire assembly, can be worded so as to establish a committee to hear the trial and report its findings and recommendations to the assembly for action.”

**210.** `[C1617 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: With reference to an _____ date for which to set the trial, thirty days is a reasonable time to allow the accused to prepare his defense.
   A. Substitute
   B. Organization
   C. committees
   D. appropriate

   *Answer:* D) appropriate. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:21 — “With reference to an appropriate date for which to set the trial, thirty days is a reasonable time to allow the accused to prepare his defense.”

**211.** `[C1618 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, That a trial committee consisting of Mr.

   *Answer:* Resolved — “Resolved, That a trial committee consisting of Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:22 — “Resolved, That a trial committee consisting of Mr.”

**212.** `[C1618 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Resolved, That a trial committee _____ of Mr.
   A. sustaining
   B. persuasive
   C. consisting
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) consisting. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:22 — “Resolved, That a trial committee consisting of Mr.”

**213.** `[C1618 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: H as chairman and members A, B, C, D, E, and F be _____ to try the case of J.M.

   *Answer:* appointed — “H as chairman and members A, B, C, D, E, and F be appointed to try the case of J.M.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:22 — “H as chairman and members A, B, C, D, E, and F be appointed to try the case of J.M.”

**214.** `[C1618 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and report its findings and _____.
   A. subsequently
   B. recommendations
   C. characteristics
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:22 — “and report its findings and recommendations.”

**215.** `[C1618 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: [A special committee appointed to hear a trial must be composed of persons different from those on the preliminary _____ committee.

   *Answer:* investigating — “[A special committee appointed to hear a trial must be composed of persons different from those on the preliminary investigating committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:22 — “[A special committee appointed to hear a trial must be composed of persons different from those on the preliminary investigating committee.”

**216.** `[C1619 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: on Wednesday, November 15, 20, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and _____:… [setting them forth, as above].
   A. Organization
   B. specifications
   C. interrupted
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* B) specifications. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:23 — “on Wednesday, November 15, 20, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and specifications:… [setting them forth, as above].”

**217.** `[C1619 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: on Wednesday, November 15, 20, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and _____:… [setting them forth, as above].

   *Answer:* specifications — “on Wednesday, November 15, 20, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and specifications:… [setting them forth, as above].”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:23 — “on Wednesday, November 15, 20, to show cause why she should not be removed from the office of treasurer on the following charge and specifications:… [setting them forth, as above].”

**218.** `[C1620 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ sets forth an offense—that is, a particular kind of act or conduct that entails liability to penalty under the governing rules—of which the accused is alleged to be guilty.

   *Answer:* charge — “A charge sets forth an offense—that is, a particular kind of act or conduct that entails liability to penalty under the governing rules—of which the accused is alleged to be guilty.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:24 — “A charge sets forth an offense—that is, a particular kind of act or conduct that entails liability to penalty under the governing rules—of which the accused is alleged to be guilty.”

**219.** `[C1620 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A charge sets forth an _____—that is, a particular kind of act or conduct that entails liability to penalty under the governing rules—of which the accused is alleged to be guilty.
   A. Regular
   B. Reports
   C. Charges
   D. offense

   *Answer:* D) offense. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:24 — “A charge sets forth an offense—that is, a particular kind of act or conduct that entails liability to penalty under the governing rules—of which the accused is alleged to be guilty.”

**220.** `[C1620 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ states what the accused is alleged to have done which, if true, constitutes an instance of the offense indicated in the charge.

   *Answer:* specification — “A specification states what the accused is alleged to have done which, if true, constitutes an instance of the offense indicated in the charge.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:24 — “A specification states what the accused is alleged to have done which, if true, constitutes an instance of the offense indicated in the charge.”

**221.** `[C1620 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A specification states what the accused is alleged to have _____ which, if true, constitutes an instance of the offense indicated in the charge.
   A. after
   B. done
   C. CHAIR
   D. clerk

   *Answer:* B) done. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:24 — “A specification states what the accused is alleged to have done which, if true, constitutes an instance of the offense indicated in the charge.”

**222.** `[C1621 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each separate charge contained in the resolutions must be accompanied by at least one _____, unless the investigating committee and the accused agree in preferring that this information not be disclosed outside the trial.

   *Answer:* specification — “Each separate charge contained in the resolutions must be accompanied by at least one specification, unless the investigating committee and the accused agree in preferring that this information not be disclosed outside the trial.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:25 — “Each separate charge contained in the resolutions must be accompanied by at least one specification, unless the investigating committee and the accused agree in preferring that this information not be disclosed outside the trial.”

**223.** `[C1621 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each separate charge contained in the resolutions must be accompanied by at least one _____, unless the investigating committee and the accused agree in preferring that this information not be disclosed outside the trial.
   A. deliberative
   B. specification
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* B) specification. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:25 — “Each separate charge contained in the resolutions must be accompanied by at least one specification, unless the investigating committee and the accused agree in preferring that this information not be disclosed outside the trial.”

**224.** `[C1622 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A resolution preferring charges may (although it need not) be accompanied by one suspending all or some specified portion of the accused’s authority, rights, and duties as an officer or rights as a member (except those rights that relate to the trial) pending disposition of the case, effective from the time official _____ of the resolution is delivered to the accused’s address.

   *Answer:* notification — “A resolution preferring charges may (although it need not) be accompanied by one suspending all or some specified portion of the accused’s authority, rights, and duties as an officer or rights as a member (except those rights that relate to the trial) pending disposition of the case, effective from the time official notification of the resolution is delivered to the accused’s address.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:26 — “A resolution preferring charges may (although it need not) be accompanied by one suspending all or some specified portion of the accused’s authority, rights, and duties as an officer or rights as a member (except those rights that relate to the trial) pending disposition of the case, effective from the time official notification of the resolution is delivered to the accused’s address.”

**225.** `[C1622 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A resolution preferring charges may (although it need not) be accompanied by one suspending all or some specified portion of the accused’s authority, rights, and duties as an officer or rights as a member (except those rights that relate to the trial) pending disposition of the case, effective from the time official _____ of the resolution is delivered to the accused’s address.
   A. Announcements
   B. notification
   C. Parliamentary
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) notification. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:26 — “A resolution preferring charges may (although it need not) be accompanied by one suspending all or some specified portion of the accused’s authority, rights, and duties as an officer or rights as a member (except those rights that relate to the trial) pending disposition of the case, effective from the time official notification of the resolution is delivered to the accused’s address.”

**226.** `[C1623 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The “managers” at the trial—referred to in the fourth _____ of the complete set shown above—have the task of presenting the evidence against the accused, and must be members of the society.

   *Answer:* resolution — “The “managers” at the trial—referred to in the fourth resolution of the complete set shown above—have the task of presenting the evidence against the accused, and must be members of the society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:27 — “The “managers” at the trial—referred to in the fourth resolution of the complete set shown above—have the task of presenting the evidence against the accused, and must be members of the society.”

**227.** `[C1623 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Their duty, however, is not to act as _____—in the sense of making every effort to secure conviction—but rather to strive that the trial will get at the truth and that, in the light of all facts brought out, the outcome will be just.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Preparation
   C. Definitely
   D. prosecutors

   *Answer:* D) prosecutors. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:27 — “Their duty, however, is not to act as prosecutors—in the sense of making every effort to secure conviction—but rather to strive that the trial will get at the truth and that, in the light of all facts brought out, the outcome will be just.”

**228.** `[C1623 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The “managers” at the trial—referred to in the fourth _____ of the complete set shown above—have the task of presenting the evidence against the accused, and must be members of the society.
   A. landscaped
   B. privileged
   C. resolution
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:27 — “The “managers” at the trial—referred to in the fourth resolution of the complete set shown above—have the task of presenting the evidence against the accused, and must be members of the society.”

**229.** `[C1623 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Their duty, however, is not to act as _____—in the sense of making every effort to secure conviction—but rather to strive that the trial will get at the truth and that, in the light of all facts brought out, the outcome will be just.

   *Answer:* prosecutors — “Their duty, however, is not to act as prosecutors—in the sense of making every effort to secure conviction—but rather to strive that the trial will get at the truth and that, in the light of all facts brought out, the outcome will be just.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:27 — “Their duty, however, is not to act as prosecutors—in the sense of making every effort to secure conviction—but rather to strive that the trial will get at the truth and that, in the light of all facts brought out, the outcome will be just.”

**230.** `[C1624 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Formal _____ of the Officer or Member.

   *Answer:* Notification — “Formal Notification of the Officer or Member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:28 — “Formal Notification of the Officer or Member.”

**231.** `[C1624 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the society adopts resolutions ordering trial before the assembly or a committee, the secretary immediately sends to the accused, by a method providing confirmation of delivery to his address (such as registered mail with delivery confirmation), a letter notifying him of the date, hour, and place of the trial, containing an exact copy of the charge(s) and _____ with the date of their adoption, and directing him to appear as cited—even if the accused officer or member was present when the resolutions were adopted.
   A. Information
   B. presentation
   C. specifications
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* C) specifications. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:28 — “If the society adopts resolutions ordering trial before the assembly or a committee, the secretary immediately sends to the accused, by a method providing confirmation of delivery to his address (such as registered mail with delivery confirmation), a letter notifying him of the date, hour, and place of the trial, containing an exact copy of the charge(s) and specifications with the date of their adoption, and directing him to appear as cited—even if the accused officer or member was present when the resolutions were adopted.”

**232.** `[C1624 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Formal _____ of the Officer or Member.
   A. Organization
   B. consideration
   C. Convention
   D. Notification

   *Answer:* D) Notification. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:28 — “Formal Notification of the Officer or Member.”

**233.** `[C1624 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the society adopts resolutions ordering trial before the assembly or a committee, the secretary immediately sends to the accused, by a method providing confirmation of delivery to his address (such as registered mail with delivery confirmation), a letter notifying him of the date, hour, and place of the trial, containing an exact copy of the charge(s) and _____ with the date of their adoption, and directing him to appear as cited—even if the accused officer or member was present when the resolutions were adopted.

   *Answer:* specifications — “If the society adopts resolutions ordering trial before the assembly or a committee, the secretary immediately sends to the accused, by a method providing confirmation of delivery to his address (such as registered mail with delivery confirmation), a letter notifying him of the date, hour, and place of the trial, containing an exact copy of the charge(s) and specifications with the date of their adoption, and directing him to appear as cited—even if the accused officer or member was present when the resolutions were adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:28 — “If the society adopts resolutions ordering trial before the assembly or a committee, the secretary immediately sends to the accused, by a method providing confirmation of delivery to his address (such as registered mail with delivery confirmation), a letter notifying him of the date, hour, and place of the trial, containing an exact copy of the charge(s) and specifications with the date of their adoption, and directing him to appear as cited—even if the accused officer or member was present when the resolutions were adopted.”

**234.** `[C1625 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is the duty of the secretary to have at hand at the trial a photocopy, printout, or other direct _____ of the letter of notification with the delivery confirmation attached, as proof that it was delivered to the accused’s address.

   *Answer:* reproduction — “It is the duty of the secretary to have at hand at the trial a photocopy, printout, or other direct reproduction of the letter of notification with the delivery confirmation attached, as proof that it was delivered to the accused’s address.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:29 — “It is the duty of the secretary to have at hand at the trial a photocopy, printout, or other direct reproduction of the letter of notification with the delivery confirmation attached, as proof that it was delivered to the accused’s address.”

**235.** `[C1625 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is the duty of the secretary to have at hand at the trial a photocopy, printout, or other direct _____ of the letter of notification with the delivery confirmation attached, as proof that it was delivered to the accused’s address.
   A. reproduction
   B. Organization
   C. Parliamentary
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) reproduction. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:29 — “It is the duty of the secretary to have at hand at the trial a photocopy, printout, or other direct reproduction of the letter of notification with the delivery confirmation attached, as proof that it was delivered to the accused’s address.”

**236.** `[C1626 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The trial is a formal hearing on the _____ of the charges.
   A. decision
   B. validity
   C. meetings
   D. Interest

   *Answer:* B) validity. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:30 — “The trial is a formal hearing on the validity of the charges.”

**237.** `[C1626 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At the trial, the evidence against the accused officer or member is presented by the managers for the society, and the officer or member has the right to be _____ by counsel and to speak and produce witnesses in his own defense.

   *Answer:* represented — “At the trial, the evidence against the accused officer or member is presented by the managers for the society, and the officer or member has the right to be represented by counsel and to speak and produce witnesses in his own defense.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:30 — “At the trial, the evidence against the accused officer or member is presented by the managers for the society, and the officer or member has the right to be represented by counsel and to speak and produce witnesses in his own defense.”

**238.** `[C1626 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the charges are found to be true, a penalty may be imposed or _____;
   A. interrupted
   B. Indefinitely
   C. authorizes
   D. recommended

   *Answer:* D) recommended. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:30 — “If the charges are found to be true, a penalty may be imposed or recommended;”

**239.** `[C1626 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if the charges are not _____, the officer or member is exonerated and any authority, rights, duties, and privileges of office or membership that had been suspended are automatically restored.

   *Answer:* substantiated — “but if the charges are not substantiated, the officer or member is exonerated and any authority, rights, duties, and privileges of office or membership that had been suspended are automatically restored.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:30 — “but if the charges are not substantiated, the officer or member is exonerated and any authority, rights, duties, and privileges of office or membership that had been suspended are automatically restored.”

**240.** `[C1626 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The managers, as _____ stated, must be members of the society.
   A. previously
   B. resolution
   C. sustaining
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) previously. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:30 — “The managers, as previously stated, must be members of the society.”

**241.** `[C1627 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the accused fails to appear for trial at the _____ time as directed, the trial proceeds without him.

   *Answer:* appointed — “If the accused fails to appear for trial at the appointed time as directed, the trial proceeds without him.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:31 — “If the accused fails to appear for trial at the appointed time as directed, the trial proceeds without him.”

**242.** `[C1627 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the accused fails to appear for trial at the _____ time as directed, the trial proceeds without him.
   A. presiding
   B. Objection
   C. appointed
   D. Secretary

   *Answer:* C) appointed. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:31 — “If the accused fails to appear for trial at the appointed time as directed, the trial proceeds without him.”

**243.** `[C1628 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At any time before the commencement of the trial with the first of the “preliminary steps” described below, the assembly may, by _____ vote, adopt a resolution to govern the trial specifying details not inconsistent with the procedures described here.

   *Answer:* majority — “At any time before the commencement of the trial with the first of the “preliminary steps” described below, the assembly may, by majority vote, adopt a resolution to govern the trial specifying details not inconsistent with the procedures described here.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:32 — “At any time before the commencement of the trial with the first of the “preliminary steps” described below, the assembly may, by majority vote, adopt a resolution to govern the trial specifying details not inconsistent with the procedures described here.”

**244.** `[C1628 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At any time before the _____ of the trial with the first of the “preliminary steps” described below, the assembly may, by majority vote, adopt a resolution to govern the trial specifying details not inconsistent with the procedures described here.
   A. deliberative
   B. Definitely
   C. Procedures
   D. commencement

   *Answer:* D) commencement. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:32 — “At any time before the commencement of the trial with the first of the “preliminary steps” described below, the assembly may, by majority vote, adopt a resolution to govern the trial specifying details not inconsistent with the procedures described here.”

**245.** `[C1628 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The resolution may include an agenda that _____ times for portions of the trial, such as time limits for opening and closing statements.

   *Answer:* establishes — “The resolution may include an agenda that establishes times for portions of the trial, such as time limits for opening and closing statements.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:32 — “The resolution may include an agenda that establishes times for portions of the trial, such as time limits for opening and closing statements.”

**246.** `[C1628 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If time limits are imposed, they must allow the defense at least equal time for each element of the trial as that allowed the managers, and this rule may not be _____ without the consent of the defense.
   A. delegates
   B. Reporting
   C. debatable
   D. suspended

   *Answer:* D) suspended. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:32 — “If time limits are imposed, they must allow the defense at least equal time for each element of the trial as that allowed the managers, and this rule may not be suspended without the consent of the defense.”

**247.** `[C1628 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The resolution may include an agenda that _____ times for portions of the trial, such as time limits for opening and closing statements.
   A. establishes
   B. committees
   C. interrupted
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) establishes. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:32 — “The resolution may include an agenda that establishes times for portions of the trial, such as time limits for opening and closing statements.”

**248.** `[C1629 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At the trial, in calling the meeting to order, the chair should call attention to the fact that the meeting is in executive session (9), and to the attendant _____ of secrecy.

   *Answer:* obligation — “At the trial, in calling the meeting to order, the chair should call attention to the fact that the meeting is in executive session (9), and to the attendant obligation of secrecy.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:33 — “At the trial, in calling the meeting to order, the chair should call attention to the fact that the meeting is in executive session (9), and to the attendant obligation of secrecy.”

**249.** `[C1629 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Preliminary steps then include the secretary’s reading from the minutes the resolutions adopted by the society relating to the trial, the chair’s _____—by inquiring of the secretary—that the accused was furnished with a copy of the charges, the chair’s announcement of the names of the managers for the society, and the chair’s inquiry of the accused as to whether he has counsel.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. adjournment
   C. immediately
   D. verification

   *Answer:* D) verification. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:33 — “Preliminary steps then include the secretary’s reading from the minutes the resolutions adopted by the society relating to the trial, the chair’s verification—by inquiring of the secretary—that the accused was furnished with a copy of the charges, the chair’s announcement of the names of the managers for the society, and the chair’s inquiry of the accused as to whether he has counsel.”

**250.** `[C1629 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At the trial, in calling the meeting to order, the chair should call attention to the fact that the meeting is in executive session (9), and to the attendant _____ of secrecy.
   A. obligation
   B. Unfinished
   C. convention
   D. subsidiary

   *Answer:* A) obligation. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:33 — “At the trial, in calling the meeting to order, the chair should call attention to the fact that the meeting is in executive session (9), and to the attendant obligation of secrecy.”

**251.** `[C1629 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Preliminary steps then include the secretary’s reading from the minutes the resolutions adopted by the society relating to the trial, the chair’s _____—by inquiring of the secretary—that the accused was furnished with a copy of the charges, the chair’s announcement of the names of the managers for the society, and the chair’s inquiry of the accused as to whether he has counsel.

   *Answer:* verification — “Preliminary steps then include the secretary’s reading from the minutes the resolutions adopted by the society relating to the trial, the chair’s verification—by inquiring of the secretary—that the accused was furnished with a copy of the charges, the chair’s announcement of the names of the managers for the society, and the chair’s inquiry of the accused as to whether he has counsel.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:33 — “Preliminary steps then include the secretary’s reading from the minutes the resolutions adopted by the society relating to the trial, the chair’s verification—by inquiring of the secretary—that the accused was furnished with a copy of the charges, the chair’s announcement of the names of the managers for the society, and the chair’s inquiry of the accused as to whether he has counsel.”

**252.** `[C1630 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member who votes for a finding of guilt at a trial should be morally _____, on the basis of the evidence he has heard, that the accused is guilty.

   *Answer:* convinced — “A member who votes for a finding of guilt at a trial should be morally convinced, on the basis of the evidence he has heard, that the accused is guilty.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:34 — “A member who votes for a finding of guilt at a trial should be morally convinced, on the basis of the evidence he has heard, that the accused is guilty.”

**253.** `[C1630 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member who votes for a finding of guilt at a trial should be morally _____, on the basis of the evidence he has heard, that the accused is guilty.
   A. Reporting
   B. extending
   C. convinced
   D. execution

   *Answer:* C) convinced. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:34 — “A member who votes for a finding of guilt at a trial should be morally convinced, on the basis of the evidence he has heard, that the accused is guilty.”

**254.** `[C1631 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the trial has been held before a trial committee instead of the assembly of the society, this committee reports to the assembly in executive session (9) the results of its trial of the case, with resolutions—in cases where its finding is one of _____—covering the penalty it recommends that the society impose.

   *Answer:* guilty — “If the trial has been held before a trial committee instead of the assembly of the society, this committee reports to the assembly in executive session (9) the results of its trial of the case, with resolutions—in cases where its finding is one of guilty—covering the penalty it recommends that the society impose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:35 — “If the trial has been held before a trial committee instead of the assembly of the society, this committee reports to the assembly in executive session (9) the results of its trial of the case, with resolutions—in cases where its finding is one of guilty—covering the penalty it recommends that the society impose.”

**255.** `[C1631 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assembly’s Review of a Trial Committee’s _____.
   A. Approved
   B. Nominees
   C. Findings
   D. meetings

   *Answer:* C) Findings. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:35 — “Assembly’s Review of a Trial Committee’s Findings.”

**256.** `[C1631 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the trial has been held before a trial committee instead of the assembly of the society, this committee reports to the assembly in executive session (9) the results of its trial of the case, with _____—in cases where its finding is one of guilty—covering the penalty it recommends that the society impose.

   *Answer:* resolutions — “If the trial has been held before a trial committee instead of the assembly of the society, this committee reports to the assembly in executive session (9) the results of its trial of the case, with resolutions—in cases where its finding is one of guilty—covering the penalty it recommends that the society impose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:35 — “If the trial has been held before a trial committee instead of the assembly of the society, this committee reports to the assembly in executive session (9) the results of its trial of the case, with resolutions—in cases where its finding is one of guilty—covering the penalty it recommends that the society impose.”

**257.** `[C1631 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The report is prepared in writing and includes, to the extent possible without disclosing _____ information which should be kept within the committee, a summary of the basis for the committee’s finding.
   A. adjournment
   B. confidential
   C. Convention
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) confidential. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:35 — “The report is prepared in writing and includes, to the extent possible without disclosing confidential information which should be kept within the committee, a summary of the basis for the committee’s finding.”

**258.** `[C1631 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assembly’s Review of a Trial Committee’s _____.

   *Answer:* Findings — “Assembly’s Review of a Trial Committee’s Findings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:35 — “Assembly’s Review of a Trial Committee’s Findings.”

**259.** `[C1632 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless the report exonerates the accused, he is then permitted—personally, through counsel, or both, as he prefers —to make a statement of the case, after which the committee is given the _____ to present a statement in rebuttal.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “Unless the report exonerates the accused, he is then permitted—personally, through counsel, or both, as he prefers —to make a statement of the case, after which the committee is given the opportunity to present a statement in rebuttal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:36 — “Unless the report exonerates the accused, he is then permitted—personally, through counsel, or both, as he prefers —to make a statement of the case, after which the committee is given the opportunity to present a statement in rebuttal.”

**260.** `[C1632 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The accused—and defense counsel if not member(s)—then leave the room, and the assembly acts upon the _____ submitted by the committee.
   A. Permission
   B. resolutions
   C. Information
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:36 — “The accused—and defense counsel if not member(s)—then leave the room, and the assembly acts upon the resolutions submitted by the committee.”

**261.** `[C1632 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the report exonerates the accused, he is then permitted—personally, through counsel, or both, as he prefers —to make a statement of the case, after which the committee is given the _____ to present a statement in rebuttal.
   A. adjournment
   B. opportunity
   C. Definitely
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:36 — “Unless the report exonerates the accused, he is then permitted—personally, through counsel, or both, as he prefers —to make a statement of the case, after which the committee is given the opportunity to present a statement in rebuttal.”

**262.** `[C1632 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The accused—and defense counsel if not member(s)—then leave the room, and the assembly acts upon the _____ submitted by the committee.

   *Answer:* resolutions — “The accused—and defense counsel if not member(s)—then leave the room, and the assembly acts upon the resolutions submitted by the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:36 — “The accused—and defense counsel if not member(s)—then leave the room, and the assembly acts upon the resolutions submitted by the committee.”

**263.** `[C1633 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under this procedure, the assembly can decline to impose any penalty, _____ the trial committee’s recommendation;

   *Answer:* notwithstanding — “Under this procedure, the assembly can decline to impose any penalty, notwithstanding the trial committee’s recommendation;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:37 — “Under this procedure, the assembly can decline to impose any penalty, notwithstanding the trial committee’s recommendation;”

**264.** `[C1633 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or it can reduce the _____ penalty;
   A. Incidental
   B. recommended
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) recommended. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:37 — “or it can reduce the recommended penalty;”

**265.** `[C1633 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under this procedure, the assembly can decline to impose any _____, notwithstanding the trial committee’s recommendation;

   *Answer:* penalty — “Under this procedure, the assembly can decline to impose any penalty, notwithstanding the trial committee’s recommendation;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:37 — “Under this procedure, the assembly can decline to impose any penalty, notwithstanding the trial committee’s recommendation;”

**266.** `[C1633 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or it can reduce the _____ penalty;

   *Answer:* recommended — “or it can reduce the recommended penalty;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:37 — “or it can reduce the recommended penalty;”

**267.** `[C1634 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some professional societies and other _____ where particular aspects of discipline are of special importance, the handling of such matters is simplified by providing in the bylaws for a standing Committee on Discipline (see 50, 56).

   *Answer:* organizations — “In some professional societies and other organizations where particular aspects of discipline are of special importance, the handling of such matters is simplified by providing in the bylaws for a standing Committee on Discipline (see 50, 56).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:38 — “In some professional societies and other organizations where particular aspects of discipline are of special importance, the handling of such matters is simplified by providing in the bylaws for a standing Committee on Discipline (see 50, 56).”

**268.** `[C1634 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Its prescribed duties are normally to be alert to _____ problems, to investigate them, to introduce all necessary resolutions, and—in event of a trial—to manage the case for the society.
   A. Announcements
   B. Definitely
   C. disciplinary
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:38 — “Its prescribed duties are normally to be alert to disciplinary problems, to investigate them, to introduce all necessary resolutions, and—in event of a trial—to manage the case for the society.”

**269.** `[C1634 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some professional societies and other _____ where particular aspects of discipline are of special importance, the handling of such matters is simplified by providing in the bylaws for a standing Committee on Discipline (see 50, 56).
   A. specification
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. Parliamentary
   D. organizations

   *Answer:* D) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:38 — “In some professional societies and other organizations where particular aspects of discipline are of special importance, the handling of such matters is simplified by providing in the bylaws for a standing Committee on Discipline (see 50, 56).”

**270.** `[C1634 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Its prescribed duties are normally to be alert to _____ problems, to investigate them, to introduce all necessary resolutions, and—in event of a trial—to manage the case for the society.

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “Its prescribed duties are normally to be alert to disciplinary problems, to investigate them, to introduce all necessary resolutions, and—in event of a trial—to manage the case for the society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:38 — “Its prescribed duties are normally to be alert to disciplinary problems, to investigate them, to introduce all necessary resolutions, and—in event of a trial—to manage the case for the society.”

**271.** `[C1635 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is generally best not to empower the committee to _____ a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.

   *Answer:* impose — “It is generally best not to empower the committee to impose a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:39 — “It is generally best not to empower the committee to impose a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.”

**272.** `[C1635 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under the latter practice, the full Committee on Discipline adopts the charge and _____, and the chairman of the committee sends the citation to the accused and presides at the trial, which is conducted just as it would be if held before the assembly.
   A. recommendation
   B. requirement
   C. President-Elect
   D. specifications

   *Answer:* D) specifications. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:39 — “Under the latter practice, the full Committee on Discipline adopts the charge and specifications, and the chairman of the committee sends the citation to the accused and presides at the trial, which is conducted just as it would be if held before the assembly.”

**273.** `[C1635 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is generally best not to empower the committee to impose a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended _____ measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “It is generally best not to empower the committee to impose a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:39 — “It is generally best not to empower the committee to impose a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.”

**274.** `[C1635 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under the latter practice, the full Committee on Discipline adopts the charge and _____, and the chairman of the committee sends the citation to the accused and presides at the trial, which is conducted just as it would be if held before the assembly.

   *Answer:* specifications — “Under the latter practice, the full Committee on Discipline adopts the charge and specifications, and the chairman of the committee sends the citation to the accused and presides at the trial, which is conducted just as it would be if held before the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:39 — “Under the latter practice, the full Committee on Discipline adopts the charge and specifications, and the chairman of the committee sends the citation to the accused and presides at the trial, which is conducted just as it would be if held before the assembly.”

**275.** `[C1636 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In organizations where disciplinary matters may arise with some frequency, the system of having a Committee on Discipline has the advantages of not unduly _____ the society, and of promoting the avoidance of scandal and the settlement of disciplinary problems without an actual trial.

   *Answer:* inconveniencing — “In organizations where disciplinary matters may arise with some frequency, the system of having a Committee on Discipline has the advantages of not unduly inconveniencing the society, and of promoting the avoidance of scandal and the settlement of disciplinary problems without an actual trial.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:40 — “In organizations where disciplinary matters may arise with some frequency, the system of having a Committee on Discipline has the advantages of not unduly inconveniencing the society, and of promoting the avoidance of scandal and the settlement of disciplinary problems without an actual trial.”

**276.** `[C1636 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In organizations where disciplinary matters may arise with some frequency, the system of having a Committee on Discipline has the advantages of not unduly _____ the society, and of promoting the avoidance of scandal and the settlement of disciplinary problems without an actual trial.
   A. Responsibilities
   B. immediately
   C. inconveniencing
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) inconveniencing. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:40 — “In organizations where disciplinary matters may arise with some frequency, the system of having a Committee on Discipline has the advantages of not unduly inconveniencing the society, and of promoting the avoidance of scandal and the settlement of disciplinary problems without an actual trial.”

---

## Domain 7: Boards and Committees, and Writing and Interpreting Bylaws

**1.** `[C1162 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The authority by which a board is _____ commonly prescribes the times at which it shall hold regular meetings, and the procedure by which special meetings of the board can be called;

   *Answer:* constituted — “The authority by which a board is constituted commonly prescribes the times at which it shall hold regular meetings, and the procedure by which special meetings of the board can be called;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:2 — “The authority by which a board is constituted commonly prescribes the times at which it shall hold regular meetings, and the procedure by which special meetings of the board can be called;”

**2.** `[C1162 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The authority by which a board is _____ commonly prescribes the times at which it shall hold regular meetings, and the procedure by which special meetings of the board can be called;
   A. Information
   B. constituted
   C. constitution
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* B) constituted. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:2 — “The authority by which a board is constituted commonly prescribes the times at which it shall hold regular meetings, and the procedure by which special meetings of the board can be called;”

**3.** `[C1163 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a board is usually known as the _____, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.

   *Answer:* executive board — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:3 — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”

**4.** `[C1163 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the _____ of managers, or board of trustees.
   A. board of directors, board
   B. Motions Relating to Voting
   C. Additional Bylaw Articles
   D. Nominations by the Chair

   *Answer:* A) board of directors, board. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:3 — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”

**5.** `[C1163 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except in the simplest and smallest local _____, or those holding very frequent regular meetings, it is generally found advisable to provide in the bylaws for a board to be empowered to act for the society when necessary between its regular meetings, and in some cases to have complete control over certain phases of the society’s business.

   *Answer:* societies — “Except in the simplest and smallest local societies, or those holding very frequent regular meetings, it is generally found advisable to provide in the bylaws for a board to be empowered to act for the society when necessary between its regular meetings, and in some cases to have complete control over certain phases of the society’s business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:3 — “Except in the simplest and smallest local societies, or those holding very frequent regular meetings, it is generally found advisable to provide in the bylaws for a board to be empowered to act for the society when necessary between its regular meetings, and in some cases to have complete control over certain phases of the society’s business.”

**6.** `[C1163 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except in the simplest and smallest local _____, or those holding very frequent regular meetings, it is generally found advisable to provide in the bylaws for a board to be empowered to act for the society when necessary between its regular meetings, and in some cases to have complete control over certain phases of the society’s business.
   A. societies
   B. postponed
   C. expressly
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) societies. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:3 — “Except in the simplest and smallest local societies, or those holding very frequent regular meetings, it is generally found advisable to provide in the bylaws for a board to be empowered to act for the society when necessary between its regular meetings, and in some cases to have complete control over certain phases of the society’s business.”

**7.** `[C1164 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a society is to have an executive board, the bylaws should specify the number of board members and how they are to be _____, should define the board’s duties and powers, and should make provision for meetings of the board as stated above.

   *Answer:* determined — “If a society is to have an executive board, the bylaws should specify the number of board members and how they are to be determined, should define the board’s duties and powers, and should make provision for meetings of the board as stated above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:4 — “If a society is to have an executive board, the bylaws should specify the number of board members and how they are to be determined, should define the board’s duties and powers, and should make provision for meetings of the board as stated above.”

**8.** `[C1164 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An executive board commonly consists of those of the society’s officers (47) who also have duties apart from the board, together with a number of directors, managers, or trustees who may or may not have other duties such as the _____ of important standing committees (50).
   A. Definitely
   B. chairmanship
   C. Announcements
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) chairmanship. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:4 — “An executive board commonly consists of those of the society’s officers (47) who also have duties apart from the board, together with a number of directors, managers, or trustees who may or may not have other duties such as the chairmanship of important standing committees (50).”

**9.** `[C1164 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Frequently it is provided that a specified percentage of the directors shall be chosen _____ in such a way that their terms of office overlap those of the others—as when, for example, there are six directors and it is provided that two shall be elected at each annual meeting for three-year terms.

   *Answer:* periodically — “Frequently it is provided that a specified percentage of the directors shall be chosen periodically in such a way that their terms of office overlap those of the others—as when, for example, there are six directors and it is provided that two shall be elected at each annual meeting for three-year terms.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:4 — “Frequently it is provided that a specified percentage of the directors shall be chosen periodically in such a way that their terms of office overlap those of the others—as when, for example, there are six directors and it is provided that two shall be elected at each annual meeting for three-year terms.”

**10.** `[C1164 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a society is to have an executive board, the bylaws should specify the number of board members and how they are to be _____, should define the board’s duties and powers, and should make provision for meetings of the board as stated above.
   A. Permission
   B. determined
   C. Particular
   D. convention

   *Answer:* B) determined. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:4 — “If a society is to have an executive board, the bylaws should specify the number of board members and how they are to be determined, should define the board’s duties and powers, and should make provision for meetings of the board as stated above.”

**11.** `[C1164 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An executive board commonly consists of those of the society’s officers (47) who also have duties apart from the board, together with a number of directors, managers, or trustees who may or may not have other duties such as the _____ of important standing committees (50).

   *Answer:* chairmanship — “An executive board commonly consists of those of the society’s officers (47) who also have duties apart from the board, together with a number of directors, managers, or trustees who may or may not have other duties such as the chairmanship of important standing committees (50).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:4 — “An executive board commonly consists of those of the society’s officers (47) who also have duties apart from the board, together with a number of directors, managers, or trustees who may or may not have other duties such as the chairmanship of important standing committees (50).”

**12.** `[C1165 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A society has no _____ board, nor can its officers act as a board, except as the bylaws may provide;

   *Answer:* executive — “A society has no executive board, nor can its officers act as a board, except as the bylaws may provide;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:5 — “A society has no executive board, nor can its officers act as a board, except as the bylaws may provide;”

**13.** `[C1165 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and when so _____, the board has only such power as is delegated to it by the bylaws or by vote of the society’s assembly referring individual matters to it.
   A. Unfinished
   B. Convention
   C. Definitely
   D. established

   *Answer:* D) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:5 — “and when so established, the board has only such power as is delegated to it by the bylaws or by vote of the society’s assembly referring individual matters to it.”

**14.** `[C1165 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A society has no _____ board, nor can its officers act as a board, except as the bylaws may provide;
   A. executive
   B. debatable
   C. Objection
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* A) executive. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:5 — “A society has no executive board, nor can its officers act as a board, except as the bylaws may provide;”

**15.** `[C1165 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and when so _____, the board has only such power as is delegated to it by the bylaws or by vote of the society’s assembly referring individual matters to it.

   *Answer:* established — “and when so established, the board has only such power as is delegated to it by the bylaws or by vote of the society’s assembly referring individual matters to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:5 — “and when so established, the board has only such power as is delegated to it by the bylaws or by vote of the society’s assembly referring individual matters to it.”

**16.** `[C1166 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The amount of regular power delegated to an executive board under the bylaws varies _____ from one organization to another.

   *Answer:* considerably — “The amount of regular power delegated to an executive board under the bylaws varies considerably from one organization to another.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:6 — “The amount of regular power delegated to an executive board under the bylaws varies considerably from one organization to another.”

**17.** `[C1166 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Usually in _____ meeting monthly or oftener, and sometimes in those meeting quarterly, the board is not given so much power, since the society can attend to much of its business at its regular meetings.
   A. resolutions
   B. president-elect
   C. Parliamentary
   D. organizations

   *Answer:* D) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:6 — “Usually in organizations meeting monthly or oftener, and sometimes in those meeting quarterly, the board is not given so much power, since the society can attend to much of its business at its regular meetings.”
