**18.** `[C1166 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The amount of regular power delegated to an executive board under the bylaws varies _____ from one organization to another.
   A. considerably
   B. Definitely
   C. Organization
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) considerably. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:6 — “The amount of regular power delegated to an executive board under the bylaws varies considerably from one organization to another.”

**19.** `[C1166 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Usually in _____ meeting monthly or oftener, and sometimes in those meeting quarterly, the board is not given so much power, since the society can attend to much of its business at its regular meetings.

   *Answer:* organizations — “Usually in organizations meeting monthly or oftener, and sometimes in those meeting quarterly, the board is not given so much power, since the society can attend to much of its business at its regular meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:6 — “Usually in organizations meeting monthly or oftener, and sometimes in those meeting quarterly, the board is not given so much power, since the society can attend to much of its business at its regular meetings.”

**20.** `[C1167 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except in matters placed by the bylaws exclusively under the control of the board, the society’s assembly can give the board _____ which it must carry out, and can rescind or amend any action of the board if it is not too late (see 35).

   *Answer:* instructions — “Except in matters placed by the bylaws exclusively under the control of the board, the society’s assembly can give the board instructions which it must carry out, and can rescind or amend any action of the board if it is not too late (see 35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:7 — “Except in matters placed by the bylaws exclusively under the control of the board, the society’s assembly can give the board instructions which it must carry out, and can rescind or amend any action of the board if it is not too late (see 35).”

**21.** `[C1167 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted, however, that exactly the opposite condition prevails in connection with boards of business _____, in which the board has exclusive power and authority to operate the business.
   A. corporations
   B. requirement
   C. Announcements
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) corporations. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:7 — “It should be noted, however, that exactly the opposite condition prevails in connection with boards of business corporations, in which the board has exclusive power and authority to operate the business.”

**22.** `[C1167 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except in matters placed by the bylaws exclusively under the control of the board, the society’s assembly can give the board _____ which it must carry out, and can rescind or amend any action of the board if it is not too late (see 35).
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Incidental
   C. instructions
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:7 — “Except in matters placed by the bylaws exclusively under the control of the board, the society’s assembly can give the board instructions which it must carry out, and can rescind or amend any action of the board if it is not too late (see 35).”

**23.** `[C1167 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted, however, that exactly the opposite condition prevails in connection with boards of business _____, in which the board has exclusive power and authority to operate the business.

   *Answer:* corporations — “It should be noted, however, that exactly the opposite condition prevails in connection with boards of business corporations, in which the board has exclusive power and authority to operate the business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:7 — “It should be noted, however, that exactly the opposite condition prevails in connection with boards of business corporations, in which the board has exclusive power and authority to operate the business.”

**24.** `[C1168 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Frequently boards include ex-officio members—that is, persons who are members of the board by virtue of an office or committee _____ held in the society, or in the parent state or national society or federation or some allied group;

   *Answer:* chairmanship — “Frequently boards include ex-officio members—that is, persons who are members of the board by virtue of an office or committee chairmanship held in the society, or in the parent state or national society or federation or some allied group;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:8 — “Frequently boards include ex-officio members—that is, persons who are members of the board by virtue of an office or committee chairmanship held in the society, or in the parent state or national society or federation or some allied group;”

**25.** `[C1168 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or —_____ in boards outside of organized societies—by virtue of a public office.
   A. Objection
   B. president
   C. exhausted
   D. sometimes

   *Answer:* D) sometimes. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:8 — “or —sometimes in boards outside of organized societies—by virtue of a public office.”

**26.** `[C1168 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the executive board of a society, if the ex-officio member of the board is under the authority of the society (that is, if he is a member, an employee, or an elected or appointed officer of the society), there is no _____ between him and the other board members.

   *Answer:* distinction — “In the executive board of a society, if the ex-officio member of the board is under the authority of the society (that is, if he is a member, an employee, or an elected or appointed officer of the society), there is no distinction between him and the other board members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:8 — “In the executive board of a society, if the ex-officio member of the board is under the authority of the society (that is, if he is a member, an employee, or an elected or appointed officer of the society), there is no distinction between him and the other board members.”

**27.** `[C1168 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the ex-officio member is not under the authority of the society, he has all the privileges of board membership, including the right to make motions and to vote, but none of the _____—just as in a case, for example, where the governor of a state is ex officio a trustee of a private academy.
   A. obligations
   B. requirement
   C. Unfinished
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* A) obligations. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:8 — “If the ex-officio member is not under the authority of the society, he has all the privileges of board membership, including the right to make motions and to vote, but none of the obligations—just as in a case, for example, where the governor of a state is ex officio a trustee of a private academy.”

**28.** `[C1168 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The latter class of ex-officio board member, who has no obligation to _____, is not counted in determining the number required for a quorum or whether a quorum is present at a meeting.

   *Answer:* participate — “The latter class of ex-officio board member, who has no obligation to participate, is not counted in determining the number required for a quorum or whether a quorum is present at a meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:8 — “The latter class of ex-officio board member, who has no obligation to participate, is not counted in determining the number required for a quorum or whether a quorum is present at a meeting.”

**29.** `[C1169 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an ex-officio member of a board ceases to hold the office that entitles him to such membership, his membership on the board terminates _____.

   *Answer:* automatically — “When an ex-officio member of a board ceases to hold the office that entitles him to such membership, his membership on the board terminates automatically.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:9 — “When an ex-officio member of a board ceases to hold the office that entitles him to such membership, his membership on the board terminates automatically.”

**30.** `[C1169 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When an ex-officio member of a board ceases to hold the office that entitles him to such membership, his membership on the board terminates _____.
   A. immediately
   B. Organization
   C. automatically
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* C) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:9 — “When an ex-officio member of a board ceases to hold the office that entitles him to such membership, his membership on the board terminates automatically.”

**31.** `[C1170 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A board that is not an _____ of a parent assembly or membership body is organized as any deliberative assembly, with a chairman or president, a secretary, and other officers as may be needed.

   *Answer:* instrumentality — “A board that is not an instrumentality of a parent assembly or membership body is organized as any deliberative assembly, with a chairman or president, a secretary, and other officers as may be needed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:11 — “A board that is not an instrumentality of a parent assembly or membership body is organized as any deliberative assembly, with a chairman or president, a secretary, and other officers as may be needed.”

**32.** `[C1170 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In general, such a board elects its own officers if the authority under which the board is _____ makes no other provision as to how the officers are to be determined.
   A. committees
   B. immediately
   C. constituted
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) constituted. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:11 — “In general, such a board elects its own officers if the authority under which the board is constituted makes no other provision as to how the officers are to be determined.”

**33.** `[C1170 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In ordinary societies having executive boards, on the other hand, the president and the secretary of the society serve in the same _____ within the board (and the executive committee, if there is one), unless the bylaws provide otherwise.

   *Answer:* capacities — “In ordinary societies having executive boards, on the other hand, the president and the secretary of the society serve in the same capacities within the board (and the executive committee, if there is one), unless the bylaws provide otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:11 — “In ordinary societies having executive boards, on the other hand, the president and the secretary of the society serve in the same capacities within the board (and the executive committee, if there is one), unless the bylaws provide otherwise.”

**34.** `[C1170 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A board that is not an _____ of a parent assembly or membership body is organized as any deliberative assembly, with a chairman or president, a secretary, and other officers as may be needed.
   A. president-elect
   B. instrumentality
   C. constitution
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* B) instrumentality. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:11 — “A board that is not an instrumentality of a parent assembly or membership body is organized as any deliberative assembly, with a chairman or president, a secretary, and other officers as may be needed.”

**35.** `[C1170 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In general, such a board elects its own officers if the authority under which the board is _____ makes no other provision as to how the officers are to be determined.

   *Answer:* constituted — “In general, such a board elects its own officers if the authority under which the board is constituted makes no other provision as to how the officers are to be determined.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:11 — “In general, such a board elects its own officers if the authority under which the board is constituted makes no other provision as to how the officers are to be determined.”

**36.** `[C1171 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the _____) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;

   *Answer:* society — “As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;”

**37.** `[C1171 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such committees _____ always report to the board.
   A. regular time
   B. of the board
   C. Voting Cards
   D. mass meeting

   *Answer:* B) of the board. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “Such committees of the board always report to the board.”

**38.** `[C1171 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which _____ is constituted;

   *Answer:* the board — “As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;”

**39.** `[C1171 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but any board can appoint committees to work under its supervision or according to its specific _____.

   *Answer:* instructions — “but any board can appoint committees to work under its supervision or according to its specific instructions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “but any board can appoint committees to work under its supervision or according to its specific instructions.”

**40.** `[C1171 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but any board can appoint _____ to work under its supervision or according to its specific instructions.
   A. substitute
   B. committees
   C. Definitely
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “but any board can appoint committees to work under its supervision or according to its specific instructions.”

**41.** `[C1172 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to establish an _____ composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).

   *Answer:* executive committee — “In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to establish an executive committee composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:13 — “In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to establish an executive committee composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).”

**42.** `[C1172 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to _____ an executive committee composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).
   A. reception
   B. establish
   C. extending
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* B) establish. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:13 — “In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to establish an executive committee composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).”

**43.** `[C1172 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The executive committee is thus in reality a “board within a board” and operates under the rules in this book _____ to boards rather than those applicable to committees.

   *Answer:* applicable — “The executive committee is thus in reality a “board within a board” and operates under the rules in this book applicable to boards rather than those applicable to committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:13 — “The executive committee is thus in reality a “board within a board” and operates under the rules in this book applicable to boards rather than those applicable to committees.”

**44.** `[C1172 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Usually the _____ of the executive committee is specified in the bylaws, rather than being left to the choice of the full board.
   A. Permission
   B. subsidiary
   C. membership
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* C) membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:13 — “Usually the membership of the executive committee is specified in the bylaws, rather than being left to the choice of the full board.”

**45.** `[C1172 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The executive committee is thus in reality a “board within a board” and operates under the rules in this book _____ to boards rather than those applicable to committees.
   A. applicable
   B. subsidiary
   C. Definitely
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) applicable. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:13 — “The executive committee is thus in reality a “board within a board” and operates under the rules in this book applicable to boards rather than those applicable to committees.”

**46.** `[C1173 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Where an organization is local—for example, a _____ for sustaining a foster home for children— the executive board usually divides itself into committees having charge of different branches of the work during the interval between the monthly or quarterly meetings of the board.
   A. Ordered
   B. adjourn
   C. Rescind
   D. society

   *Answer:* D) society. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:14 — “Where an organization is local—for example, a society for sustaining a foster home for children— the executive board usually divides itself into committees having charge of different branches of the work during the interval between the monthly or quarterly meetings of the board.”

**47.** `[C1173 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Where an _____ is local—for example, a society for sustaining a foster home for children— the executive board usually divides itself into committees having charge of different branches of the work during the interval between the monthly or quarterly meetings of the board.

   *Answer:* organization — “Where an organization is local—for example, a society for sustaining a foster home for children— the executive board usually divides itself into committees having charge of different branches of the work during the interval between the monthly or quarterly meetings of the board.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:14 — “Where an organization is local—for example, a society for sustaining a foster home for children— the executive board usually divides itself into committees having charge of different branches of the work during the interval between the monthly or quarterly meetings of the board.”

**48.** `[C1173 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At the board meetings these committees report on the fulfillment of their assigned _____.
   A. responsibilities
   B. Preparation
   C. constitution
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) responsibilities. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:14 — “At the board meetings these committees report on the fulfillment of their assigned responsibilities.”

**49.** `[C1173 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases the committees are genuinely _____ to the board and must ordinarily report back to it for authority to act (in contrast to an executive committee, which usually has power to act as the board, and in contrast to standing committees of the society, which are not subordinate to the board unless made so by a provision in the bylaws).

   *Answer:* subordinate — “In such cases the committees are genuinely subordinate to the board and must ordinarily report back to it for authority to act (in contrast to an executive committee, which usually has power to act as the board, and in contrast to standing committees of the society, which are not subordinate to the board unless made so by a provision in the bylaws).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:14 — “In such cases the committees are genuinely subordinate to the board and must ordinarily report back to it for authority to act (in contrast to an executive committee, which usually has power to act as the board, and in contrast to standing committees of the society, which are not subordinate to the board unless made so by a provision in the bylaws).”

**50.** `[C1173 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any board can appoint committees of the kind just described without _____ in the bylaws.
   A. Vice-President
   B. characteristics
   C. authorization
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* C) authorization. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:14 — “Any board can appoint committees of the kind just described without authorization in the bylaws.”

**51.** `[C1174 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The executive board of an organized society operates under the society’s bylaws, the society’s _____ authority, and any special rules of order or standing rules of the society which may be applicable to it.
   A. resolutions
   B. parliamentary
   C. immediately
   D. specification

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:15 — “The executive board of an organized society operates under the society’s bylaws, the society’s parliamentary authority, and any special rules of order or standing rules of the society which may be applicable to it.”

**52.** `[C1174 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The executive board of an organized society operates under the society’s bylaws, the society’s parliamentary authority, and any special rules of order or _____ rules of the society which may be applicable to it.

   *Answer:* standing — “The executive board of an organized society operates under the society’s bylaws, the society’s parliamentary authority, and any special rules of order or standing rules of the society which may be applicable to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:15 — “The executive board of an organized society operates under the society’s bylaws, the society’s parliamentary authority, and any special rules of order or standing rules of the society which may be applicable to it.”

**53.** `[C1174 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A board that is not a part of a society can adopt its own rules, provided that they do not conflict with anything in the legal instrument under which the board is _____.
   A. convention
   B. Substitute
   C. constituted
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) constituted. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:15 — “A board that is not a part of a society can adopt its own rules, provided that they do not conflict with anything in the legal instrument under which the board is constituted.”

**54.** `[C1174 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A board that is not a part of a society can adopt its own rules, provided that they do not conflict with anything in the legal instrument under which the board is _____.

   *Answer:* constituted — “A board that is not a part of a society can adopt its own rules, provided that they do not conflict with anything in the legal instrument under which the board is constituted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:15 — “A board that is not a part of a society can adopt its own rules, provided that they do not conflict with anything in the legal instrument under which the board is constituted.”

**55.** `[C1175 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under the general _____ law, business is transacted in large boards according to the same rules of procedure as in other deliberative assemblies.
   A. Announcements
   B. Information
   C. representatives
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:16 — “Under the general parliamentary law, business is transacted in large boards according to the same rules of procedure as in other deliberative assemblies.”

**56.** `[C1175 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In smaller boards, these rules apply as far as _____, with the exceptions noted below.

   *Answer:* practicable — “In smaller boards, these rules apply as far as practicable, with the exceptions noted below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:16 — “In smaller boards, these rules apply as far as practicable, with the exceptions noted below.”

**57.** `[C1175 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under the general parliamentary law, business is transacted in large boards according to the same rules of procedure as in other _____ assemblies.
   A. specification
   B. Organization
   C. deliberative
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:16 — “Under the general parliamentary law, business is transacted in large boards according to the same rules of procedure as in other deliberative assemblies.”

**58.** `[C1175 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In smaller boards, these rules apply as far as _____, with the exceptions noted below.
   A. Incidental
   B. Information
   C. interrupted
   D. practicable

   *Answer:* D) practicable. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:16 — “In smaller boards, these rules apply as far as practicable, with the exceptions noted below.”

**59.** `[C1176 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A record of the board’s _____ is kept by the secretary, just as in any other assembly.

   *Answer:* proceedings — “A record of the board’s proceedings is kept by the secretary, just as in any other assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:17 — “A record of the board’s proceedings is kept by the secretary, just as in any other assembly.”

**60.** `[C1176 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A record of the board’s proceedings is kept by the _____, just as in any other assembly.
   A. Signature
   B. debatable
   C. expressly
   D. secretary

   *Answer:* D) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:17 — “A record of the board’s proceedings is kept by the secretary, just as in any other assembly.”

**61.** `[C1177 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to do so is an incidental main motion, which can be adopted by a _____ vote if the minutes are not protected by executive-session secrecy.

   *Answer:* majority — “A motion to do so is an incidental main motion, which can be adopted by a majority vote if the minutes are not protected by executive-session secrecy.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:18 — “A motion to do so is an incidental main motion, which can be adopted by a majority vote if the minutes are not protected by executive-session secrecy.”

**62.** `[C1177 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The board can order that any _____ person(s)— including, for example, all members of the society—be permitted to view, or be furnished with copies of, board minutes.

   *Answer:* specified — “The board can order that any specified person(s)— including, for example, all members of the society—be permitted to view, or be furnished with copies of, board minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:18 — “The board can order that any specified person(s)— including, for example, all members of the society—be permitted to view, or be furnished with copies of, board minutes.”

**63.** `[C1177 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to do so is an incidental main motion, which can be adopted by a majority vote if the minutes are not protected by _____ secrecy.
   A. recommendation
   B. executive-session
   C. deliberative
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) executive-session. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:18 — “A motion to do so is an incidental main motion, which can be adopted by a majority vote if the minutes are not protected by executive-session secrecy.”

**64.** `[C1177 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The board can order that any _____ person(s)— including, for example, all members of the society—be permitted to view, or be furnished with copies of, board minutes.
   A. Signature
   B. postponed
   C. specified
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* C) specified. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:18 — “The board can order that any specified person(s)— including, for example, all members of the society—be permitted to view, or be furnished with copies of, board minutes.”

**65.** `[C1178 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whether or not board minutes are protected by the secrecy of an executive session, the assembly of the society can adopt a motion granting such _____, or can order that the board’s minutes be produced and read at a meeting of the assembly, by a two-thirds vote, the vote of a majority of the entire membership of the assembly, or a majority vote if previous notice has been given.

   *Answer:* permission — “Whether or not board minutes are protected by the secrecy of an executive session, the assembly of the society can adopt a motion granting such permission, or can order that the board’s minutes be produced and read at a meeting of the assembly, by a two-thirds vote, the vote of a majority of the entire membership of the assembly, or a majority vote if previous notice has been given.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:19 — “Whether or not board minutes are protected by the secrecy of an executive session, the assembly of the society can adopt a motion granting such permission, or can order that the board’s minutes be produced and read at a meeting of the assembly, by a two-thirds vote, the vote of a majority of the entire membership of the assembly, or a majority vote if previous notice has been given.”

**66.** `[C1178 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whether or not board minutes are protected by the secrecy of an executive session, the assembly of the society can adopt a motion granting such _____, or can order that the board’s minutes be produced and read at a meeting of the assembly, by a two-thirds vote, the vote of a majority of the entire membership of the assembly, or a majority vote if previous notice has been given.
   A. committees
   B. permission
   C. Substitute
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) permission. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:19 — “Whether or not board minutes are protected by the secrecy of an executive session, the assembly of the society can adopt a motion granting such permission, or can order that the board’s minutes be produced and read at a meeting of the assembly, by a two-thirds vote, the vote of a majority of the entire membership of the assembly, or a majority vote if previous notice has been given.”

**67.** `[C1179 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At regular board meetings the executive committee, if there is one, should be required to make a report of its _____ since the last board meeting.

   *Answer:* activities — “At regular board meetings the executive committee, if there is one, should be required to make a report of its activities since the last board meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:20 — “At regular board meetings the executive committee, if there is one, should be required to make a report of its activities since the last board meeting.”

**68.** `[C1179 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No action need be taken on this report, which is generally intended as _____ only.
   A. Reconsider
   B. convention
   C. information
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:20 — “No action need be taken on this report, which is generally intended as information only.”

**69.** `[C1179 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At regular board meetings the executive committee, if there is one, should be required to make a report of its _____ since the last board meeting.
   A. Reconsider
   B. committees
   C. Particular
   D. activities

   *Answer:* D) activities. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:20 — “At regular board meetings the executive committee, if there is one, should be required to make a report of its activities since the last board meeting.”

**70.** `[C1179 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No action need be taken on this report, which is generally intended as _____ only.

   *Answer:* information — “No action need be taken on this report, which is generally intended as information only.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:20 — “No action need be taken on this report, which is generally intended as information only.”

**71.** `[C1180 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a board meeting where there are not more than about a dozen members present, some of the _____ that is necessary in a large assembly would hinder business.
   A. debatable
   B. formality
   C. paragraph
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* B) formality. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:21 — “In a board meeting where there are not more than about a dozen members present, some of the formality that is necessary in a large assembly would hinder business.”

**72.** `[C1180 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rules governing such meetings are different from the rules that hold in other _____, in the following respects:

   *Answer:* assemblies — “The rules governing such meetings are different from the rules that hold in other assemblies, in the following respects:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:21 — “The rules governing such meetings are different from the rules that hold in other assemblies, in the following respects:”

**73.** `[C1180 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a board meeting where there are not more than about a dozen members present, some of the _____ that is necessary in a large assembly would hinder business.

   *Answer:* formality — “In a board meeting where there are not more than about a dozen members present, some of the formality that is necessary in a large assembly would hinder business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:21 — “In a board meeting where there are not more than about a dozen members present, some of the formality that is necessary in a large assembly would hinder business.”

**74.** `[C1180 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rules governing such meetings are different from the rules that hold in other _____, in the following respects:
   A. Permission
   B. Convention
   C. Substitute
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* D) assemblies. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:21 — “The rules governing such meetings are different from the rules that hold in other assemblies, in the following respects:”

**75.** `[C1181 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Effect of Periodic Partial Change in Board _____.

   *Answer:* Membership — “Effect of Periodic Partial Change in Board Membership.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:22 — “Effect of Periodic Partial Change in Board Membership.”

**76.** `[C1181 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where a board is constituted so that a specified portion of its membership is chosen _____ (as, for example, where one third of the board is elected annually for three-year terms), it becomes, in effect, a new board each time such a group assumes board membership.
   A. specification
   B. Definitely
   C. periodically
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) periodically. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:22 — “In cases where a board is constituted so that a specified portion of its membership is chosen periodically (as, for example, where one third of the board is elected annually for three-year terms), it becomes, in effect, a new board each time such a group assumes board membership.”

**77.** `[C1181 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The individual replacement of persons who may _____ vacate board membership at other times, however, does not have these effects.

   *Answer:* occasionally — “The individual replacement of persons who may occasionally vacate board membership at other times, however, does not have these effects.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:22 — “The individual replacement of persons who may occasionally vacate board membership at other times, however, does not have these effects.”

**78.** `[C1181 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Effect of Periodic Partial Change in Board _____.
   A. Permission
   B. Convention
   C. Membership
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) Membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:22 — “Effect of Periodic Partial Change in Board Membership.”

**79.** `[C1181 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases where a board is constituted so that a specified portion of its membership is chosen _____ (as, for example, where one third of the board is elected annually for three-year terms), it becomes, in effect, a new board each time such a group assumes board membership.

   *Answer:* periodically — “In cases where a board is constituted so that a specified portion of its membership is chosen periodically (as, for example, where one third of the board is elected annually for three-year terms), it becomes, in effect, a new board each time such a group assumes board membership.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:22 — “In cases where a board is constituted so that a specified portion of its membership is chosen periodically (as, for example, where one third of the board is elected annually for three-year terms), it becomes, in effect, a new board each time such a group assumes board membership.”

**80.** `[C1182 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee, as understood in _____ law, is a body of one or more persons, elected or appointed by (or by direction of) an assembly or society, to consider, investigate, or take action on certain matters or subjects, or to do all of these things.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “A committee, as understood in parliamentary law, is a body of one or more persons, elected or appointed by (or by direction of) an assembly or society, to consider, investigate, or take action on certain matters or subjects, or to do all of these things.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:1 — “A committee, as understood in parliamentary law, is a body of one or more persons, elected or appointed by (or by direction of) an assembly or society, to consider, investigate, or take action on certain matters or subjects, or to do all of these things.”

**81.** `[C1182 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unlike a board, a committee is not itself _____ to be a form of assembly.
   A. authorizes
   B. considered
   C. incidental
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:1 — “Unlike a board, a committee is not itself considered to be a form of assembly.”

**82.** `[C1182 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee, as understood in _____ law, is a body of one or more persons, elected or appointed by (or by direction of) an assembly or society, to consider, investigate, or take action on certain matters or subjects, or to do all of these things.
   A. immediately
   B. deliberative
   C. undebatable
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:1 — “A committee, as understood in parliamentary law, is a body of one or more persons, elected or appointed by (or by direction of) an assembly or society, to consider, investigate, or take action on certain matters or subjects, or to do all of these things.”

**83.** `[C1182 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike a board, a committee is not itself _____ to be a form of assembly.

   *Answer:* considered — “Unlike a board, a committee is not itself considered to be a form of assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:1 — “Unlike a board, a committee is not itself considered to be a form of assembly.”

**84.** `[C1183 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the term _____ commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.

   *Answer:* committee — “Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.”

**85.** `[C1183 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as _____.
   A. Before Its Adoption
   B. ordinary committees
   C. Duties of Delegates
   D. Form of the Minutes

   *Answer:* B) ordinary committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.”

**86.** `[C1183 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a _____ and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.

   *Answer:* committee of the whole — “An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a committee of the whole and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a committee of the whole and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.”

**87.** `[C1183 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a committee of the whole and is _____ from an ordinary committee.

   *Answer:* distinguished — “An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a committee of the whole and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a committee of the whole and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.”

**88.** `[C1183 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In large assemblies, the use of a committee of the whole is a convenient method of _____ a question when it is desired to allow each member to speak an unlimited number of times in debate.
   A. considering
   B. Preparation
   C. Reconsider
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) considering. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “In large assemblies, the use of a committee of the whole is a convenient method of considering a question when it is desired to allow each member to speak an unlimited number of times in debate.”

**89.** `[C1184 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Ordinary committees are of two types—_____ committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).

   *Answer:* standing — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:3 — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”

**90.** `[C1184 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Ordinary _____ are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).
   A. Permission
   B. assemblies
   C. incidental
   D. committees

   *Answer:* D) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:3 — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”

**91.** `[C1185 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Generally the term _____ implies that, within the area of its assigned responsibilities, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.

   *Answer:* committee — “Generally the term committee implies that, within the area of its assigned responsibilities, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:4 — “Generally the term committee implies that, within the area of its assigned responsibilities, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.”

**92.** `[C1185 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Generally the term committee implies that, within the area of its assigned _____, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.
   A. immediately
   B. President-Elect
   C. responsibilities
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) responsibilities. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:4 — “Generally the term committee implies that, within the area of its assigned responsibilities, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.”

**93.** `[C1185 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, if the committee is to do more than report its findings or _____ to the assembly, it may be empowered to act for the society only on specific instructions;

   *Answer:* recommendations — “Thus, if the committee is to do more than report its findings or recommendations to the assembly, it may be empowered to act for the society only on specific instructions;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:4 — “Thus, if the committee is to do more than report its findings or recommendations to the assembly, it may be empowered to act for the society only on specific instructions;”

**94.** `[C1185 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, if it is given _____ powers, its actions may be more closely subject to review than a board’s, or it may be required to report more fully.
   A. Postpone
   B. standing
   C. Nominees
   D. meetings

   *Answer:* B) standing. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:4 — “or, if it is given standing powers, its actions may be more closely subject to review than a board’s, or it may be required to report more fully.”

**95.** `[C1185 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Also, unlike most boards, a committee in general does not have regular meeting times _____ by rule;

   *Answer:* established — “Also, unlike most boards, a committee in general does not have regular meeting times established by rule;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:4 — “Also, unlike most boards, a committee in general does not have regular meeting times established by rule;”

**96.** `[C1186 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a committee is appointed “with power,” this means with power to take all the steps necessary to carry out its _____.

   *Answer:* instructions — “When a committee is appointed “with power,” this means with power to take all the steps necessary to carry out its instructions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:5 — “When a committee is appointed “with power,” this means with power to take all the steps necessary to carry out its instructions.”

**97.** `[C1186 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee is appointed “with power,” this means with power to take all the steps necessary to carry out its _____.
   A. instructions
   B. interrupted
   C. Convention
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* A) instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:5 — “When a committee is appointed “with power,” this means with power to take all the steps necessary to carry out its instructions.”

**98.** `[C1187 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In large assemblies or those doing a great volume of business, much of the preliminary work in the preparation of subjects for _____ is usually done by committees.

   *Answer:* consideration — “In large assemblies or those doing a great volume of business, much of the preliminary work in the preparation of subjects for consideration is usually done by committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:6 — “In large assemblies or those doing a great volume of business, much of the preliminary work in the preparation of subjects for consideration is usually done by committees.”

**99.** `[C1187 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In many such bodies, in fact, it is _____ to have every main question go to a committee before final action on it is taken by the assembly.
   A. reception
   B. execution
   C. Reporting
   D. advisable

   *Answer:* D) advisable. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:6 — “In many such bodies, in fact, it is advisable to have every main question go to a committee before final action on it is taken by the assembly.”

**100.** `[C1187 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In large assemblies or those doing a great volume of business, much of the preliminary work in the preparation of subjects for _____ is usually done by committees.
   A. consideration
   B. Organization
   C. representatives
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:6 — “In large assemblies or those doing a great volume of business, much of the preliminary work in the preparation of subjects for consideration is usually done by committees.”

**101.** `[C1187 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In many such bodies, in fact, it is _____ to have every main question go to a committee before final action on it is taken by the assembly.

   *Answer:* advisable — “In many such bodies, in fact, it is advisable to have every main question go to a committee before final action on it is taken by the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:6 — “In many such bodies, in fact, it is advisable to have every main question go to a committee before final action on it is taken by the assembly.”

**102.** `[C1188 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ are constituted to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.

   *Answer:* Standing committees — “Standing committees are constituted to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:7 — “Standing committees are constituted to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.”

**103.** `[C1188 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Standing committees are _____ to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.
   A. Permission
   B. constituted
   C. interrupted
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) constituted. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:7 — “Standing committees are constituted to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.”

**104.** `[C1188 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an ordinary society, the members of such a committee serve for a term _____ to that of the officers, or until their successors have been chosen, unless the bylaws or other rules otherwise expressly provide.

   *Answer:* corresponding — “In an ordinary society, the members of such a committee serve for a term corresponding to that of the officers, or until their successors have been chosen, unless the bylaws or other rules otherwise expressly provide.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:7 — “In an ordinary society, the members of such a committee serve for a term corresponding to that of the officers, or until their successors have been chosen, unless the bylaws or other rules otherwise expressly provide.”

**105.** `[C1188 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, a new body of committee members is normally appointed at the beginning of each _____.
   A. constitution
   B. administration
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) administration. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:7 — “Thus, a new body of committee members is normally appointed at the beginning of each administration.”

**106.** `[C1188 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an ordinary society, the members of such a committee serve for a term _____ to that of the officers, or until their successors have been chosen, unless the bylaws or other rules otherwise expressly provide.
   A. corresponding
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. Indefinitely
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* A) corresponding. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:7 — “In an ordinary society, the members of such a committee serve for a term corresponding to that of the officers, or until their successors have been chosen, unless the bylaws or other rules otherwise expressly provide.”

**107.** `[C1189 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A standing committee must be _____ by name (a) by a specific provision of the bylaws or (b) by a resolution which is in effect a special rule of order and therefore requires for its adoption either previous notice and a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, if any of the following conditions are to apply:

   *Answer:* constituted — “A standing committee must be constituted by name (a) by a specific provision of the bylaws or (b) by a resolution which is in effect a special rule of order and therefore requires for its adoption either previous notice and a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, if any of the following conditions are to apply:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:8 — “A standing committee must be constituted by name (a) by a specific provision of the bylaws or (b) by a resolution which is in effect a special rule of order and therefore requires for its adoption either previous notice and a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, if any of the following conditions are to apply:”

**108.** `[C1189 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A standing committee must be _____ by name (a) by a specific provision of the bylaws or (b) by a resolution which is in effect a special rule of order and therefore requires for its adoption either previous notice and a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, if any of the following conditions are to apply:
   A. constituted
   B. Unfinished
   C. Convention
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) constituted. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:8 — “A standing committee must be constituted by name (a) by a specific provision of the bylaws or (b) by a resolution which is in effect a special rule of order and therefore requires for its adoption either previous notice and a two-thirds vote or a vote of a majority of the entire membership, if any of the following conditions are to apply:”

**109.** `[C1190 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a standing committee’s assigned function does not affect a rule of parliamentary procedure in any of these three ways, it can be established by a standing rule adopted by a _____ vote without notice, although, even in such a case, the committee is frequently constituted by name in the bylaws as indicated above.

   *Answer:* majority — “If a standing committee’s assigned function does not affect a rule of parliamentary procedure in any of these three ways, it can be established by a standing rule adopted by a majority vote without notice, although, even in such a case, the committee is frequently constituted by name in the bylaws as indicated above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:9 — “If a standing committee’s assigned function does not affect a rule of parliamentary procedure in any of these three ways, it can be established by a standing rule adopted by a majority vote without notice, although, even in such a case, the committee is frequently constituted by name in the bylaws as indicated above.”

**110.** `[C1190 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a standing committee’s assigned function does not affect a rule of _____ procedure in any of these three ways, it can be established by a standing rule adopted by a majority vote without notice, although, even in such a case, the committee is frequently constituted by name in the bylaws as indicated above.
   A. Announcements
   B. specification
   C. presentation
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:9 — “If a standing committee’s assigned function does not affect a rule of parliamentary procedure in any of these three ways, it can be established by a standing rule adopted by a majority vote without notice, although, even in such a case, the committee is frequently constituted by name in the bylaws as indicated above.”

**111.** `[C1190 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A standing committee of a society reports to the assembly of the society, and not to the _____ board or board of directors, unless the bylaws provide otherwise.

   *Answer:* executive — “A standing committee of a society reports to the assembly of the society, and not to the executive board or board of directors, unless the bylaws provide otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:9 — “A standing committee of a society reports to the assembly of the society, and not to the executive board or board of directors, unless the bylaws provide otherwise.”

**112.** `[C1190 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some societies, standing committees in effect have charge of certain branches of the _____’s work, in which case these committees are really in the nature of boards.
   A. requirement
   B. organization
   C. constitution
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:9 — “In some societies, standing committees in effect have charge of certain branches of the organization’s work, in which case these committees are really in the nature of boards.”

**113.** `[C1190 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A standing committee of a society reports to the assembly of the society, and not to the _____ board or board of directors, unless the bylaws provide otherwise.
   A. Secretary
   B. executive
   C. expressly
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* B) executive. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:9 — “A standing committee of a society reports to the assembly of the society, and not to the executive board or board of directors, unless the bylaws provide otherwise.”

**114.** `[C1191 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.

   *Answer:* special — “A special (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:10 — “A special (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.”

**115.** `[C1191 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A special (_____, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.
   A. select
   B. Appeal
   C. action
   D. beyond

   *Answer:* A) select. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:10 — “A special (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.”

**116.** `[C1192 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (in the case of a special committee) the method can be _____ in the motion to establish the committee.

   *Answer:* specified — “or (in the case of a special committee) the method can be specified in the motion to establish the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:11 — “or (in the case of a special committee) the method can be specified in the motion to establish the committee.”

**117.** `[C1192 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The power to _____ a committee carries with it the power to appoint the chairman and to fill any vacancy that may arise in the committee.
   A. Motions
   B. appoint
   C. Ordered
   D. adopted

   *Answer:* B) appoint. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:11 — “The power to appoint a committee carries with it the power to appoint the chairman and to fill any vacancy that may arise in the committee.”

**118.** `[C1192 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or (in the case of a special committee) the method can be _____ in the motion to establish the committee.
   A. delegates
   B. specified
   C. secondary
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* B) specified. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:11 — “or (in the case of a special committee) the method can be specified in the motion to establish the committee.”

**119.** `[C1192 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The power to _____ a committee carries with it the power to appoint the chairman and to fill any vacancy that may arise in the committee.

   *Answer:* appoint — “The power to appoint a committee carries with it the power to appoint the chairman and to fill any vacancy that may arise in the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:11 — “The power to appoint a committee carries with it the power to appoint the chairman and to fill any vacancy that may arise in the committee.”

**120.** `[C1193 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is possible for persons who are not members of the assembly or the society to be appointed to _____s—even to the position of committee chairman—but control over each such appointment is reserved to the assembly in the individual case.

   *Answer:* committee — “It is possible for persons who are not members of the assembly or the society to be appointed to committees—even to the position of committee chairman—but control over each such appointment is reserved to the assembly in the individual case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:12 — “It is possible for persons who are not members of the assembly or the society to be appointed to committees—even to the position of committee chairman—but control over each such appointment is reserved to the assembly in the individual case.”

**121.** `[C1193 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is possible for persons who are not members of the assembly or the society to be appointed to committees—even to the position of committee chairman—but control over each such _____ is reserved to the assembly in the individual case.
   A. committees
   B. requirement
   C. appointment
   D. Information

   *Answer:* C) appointment. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:12 — “It is possible for persons who are not members of the assembly or the society to be appointed to committees—even to the position of committee chairman—but control over each such appointment is reserved to the assembly in the individual case.”

**122.** `[C1195 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee (except a committee of the whole, 52) can appoint _____, which are responsible to and report to the committee and not to the assembly.

   *Answer:* subcommittees — “A committee (except a committee of the whole, 52) can appoint subcommittees, which are responsible to and report to the committee and not to the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:15 — “A committee (except a committee of the whole, 52) can appoint subcommittees, which are responsible to and report to the committee and not to the assembly.”

**123.** `[C1195 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee (except a committee of the whole, 52) can appoint _____, which are responsible to and report to the committee and not to the assembly.
   A. requirement
   B. consideration
   C. subcommittees
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* C) subcommittees. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:15 — “A committee (except a committee of the whole, 52) can appoint subcommittees, which are responsible to and report to the committee and not to the assembly.”

**124.** `[C1196 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the bylaws provide that the president shall be ex officio a member of all _____ (or of all committees with the stated exception of those from which the president is best excluded;

   *Answer:* committees — “When the bylaws provide that the president shall be ex officio a member of all committees (or of all committees with the stated exception of those from which the president is best excluded;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:16 — “When the bylaws provide that the president shall be ex officio a member of all committees (or of all committees with the stated exception of those from which the president is best excluded;”

**125.** `[C1196 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the bylaws provide that the president shall be ex officio a member of all _____ (or of all committees with the stated exception of those from which the president is best excluded;
   A. landscaped
   B. variations
   C. committees
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:16 — “When the bylaws provide that the president shall be ex officio a member of all committees (or of all committees with the stated exception of those from which the president is best excluded;”

**126.** `[C1197 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The resignation of a member of a committee should be addressed to the appointing power, and it is the _____ of that power to fill the resulting vacancy (cf.

   *Answer:* responsibility — “The resignation of a member of a committee should be addressed to the appointing power, and it is the responsibility of that power to fill the resulting vacancy (cf.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:17 — “The resignation of a member of a committee should be addressed to the appointing power, and it is the responsibility of that power to fill the resulting vacancy (cf.”

**127.** `[C1197 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The resignation of a member of a committee should be addressed to the appointing power, and it is the _____ of that power to fill the resulting vacancy (cf.
   A. Vice-President
   B. Parliamentary
   C. president-elect
   D. responsibility

   *Answer:* D) responsibility. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:17 — “The resignation of a member of a committee should be addressed to the appointing power, and it is the responsibility of that power to fill the resulting vacancy (cf.”

**128.** `[C1198 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The members of a standing committee should be chosen so as to provide the _____ possible group for the handling of any task that may arise within the province of the committee.
   A. execution
   B. delegates
   C. extending
   D. strongest

   *Answer:* D) strongest. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:18 — “The members of a standing committee should be chosen so as to provide the strongest possible group for the handling of any task that may arise within the province of the committee.”

**129.** `[C1198 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of a special committee, the purpose for which it is appointed affects the desirable size and _____, as follows:

   *Answer:* composition — “In the case of a special committee, the purpose for which it is appointed affects the desirable size and composition, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:18 — “In the case of a special committee, the purpose for which it is appointed affects the desirable size and composition, as follows:”

**130.** `[C1198 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The members of a standing committee should be chosen so as to provide the _____ possible group for the handling of any task that may arise within the province of the committee.

   *Answer:* strongest — “The members of a standing committee should be chosen so as to provide the strongest possible group for the handling of any task that may arise within the province of the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:18 — “The members of a standing committee should be chosen so as to provide the strongest possible group for the handling of any task that may arise within the province of the committee.”

**131.** `[C1198 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of a special committee, the purpose for which it is appointed affects the desirable size and _____, as follows:
   A. interrupted
   B. composition
   C. Permission
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) composition. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:18 — “In the case of a special committee, the purpose for which it is appointed affects the desirable size and composition, as follows:”

**132.** `[C1199 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Information, _____, and Referred Papers.

   *Answer:* Instructions — “Information, Instructions, and Referred Papers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:19 — “Information, Instructions, and Referred Papers.”

**133.** `[C1199 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Upon the _____ of a committee, it is the duty of the secretary of the society to see that all persons appointed are notified, and to furnish a list of the members of the committee to its chairman or, in the chairman’s absence, to some other authorized committee member.
   A. resolutions
   B. appointment
   C. Substitute
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) appointment. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:19 — “Upon the appointment of a committee, it is the duty of the secretary of the society to see that all persons appointed are notified, and to furnish a list of the members of the committee to its chairman or, in the chairman’s absence, to some other authorized committee member.”

**134.** `[C1199 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a subject or item of business is referred to the committee (normally at the time of its appointment if it is a special committee, or at any time if it is a standing committee), the secretary provides the committee chairman or his _____ with copies of the papers, motion, or other matter formally referred to it, and whatever instructions the assembly has given.

   *Answer:* representative — “When a subject or item of business is referred to the committee (normally at the time of its appointment if it is a special committee, or at any time if it is a standing committee), the secretary provides the committee chairman or his representative with copies of the papers, motion, or other matter formally referred to it, and whatever instructions the assembly has given.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:19 — “When a subject or item of business is referred to the committee (normally at the time of its appointment if it is a special committee, or at any time if it is a standing committee), the secretary provides the committee chairman or his representative with copies of the papers, motion, or other matter formally referred to it, and whatever instructions the assembly has given.”

**135.** `[C1199 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Upon the committee’s request, any other papers or books necessary for the proper _____ of its duties must be made available to it by the appropriate officers of the society, who can first consult with the president if in doubt whether the requested papers or books are indeed needed by the committee.
   A. authorizes
   B. adjournment
   C. performance
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) performance. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:19 — “Upon the committee’s request, any other papers or books necessary for the proper performance of its duties must be made available to it by the appropriate officers of the society, who can first consult with the president if in doubt whether the requested papers or books are indeed needed by the committee.”

**136.** `[C1199 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Information, _____, and Referred Papers.
   A. consideration
   B. Convention
   C. Instructions
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) Instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:19 — “Information, Instructions, and Referred Papers.”

**137.** `[C1200 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee should take care to preserve the papers referred to it from the society, since, after its _____ is completed, they must be returned in the same condition as when received.

   *Answer:* assignment — “A committee should take care to preserve the papers referred to it from the society, since, after its assignment is completed, they must be returned in the same condition as when received.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:20 — “A committee should take care to preserve the papers referred to it from the society, since, after its assignment is completed, they must be returned in the same condition as when received.”

**138.** `[C1200 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee wishes to write on copies of the documents, therefore, it must obtain its own photocopies, unless it has been provided with extra copies specified in its _____ as for that purpose.
   A. instructions
   B. interrupted
   C. presentation
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* A) instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:20 — “If the committee wishes to write on copies of the documents, therefore, it must obtain its own photocopies, unless it has been provided with extra copies specified in its instructions as for that purpose.”

**139.** `[C1200 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any case, amendments prepared for _____ to the assembly when the committee reports should be drawn up on a separate sheet.

   *Answer:* recommendation — “In any case, amendments prepared for recommendation to the assembly when the committee reports should be drawn up on a separate sheet.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:20 — “In any case, amendments prepared for recommendation to the assembly when the committee reports should be drawn up on a separate sheet.”

**140.** `[C1200 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee should take care to preserve the papers referred to it from the society, since, after its _____ is completed, they must be returned in the same condition as when received.
   A. assemblies
   B. persuasive
   C. assignment
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) assignment. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:20 — “A committee should take care to preserve the papers referred to it from the society, since, after its assignment is completed, they must be returned in the same condition as when received.”

**141.** `[C1200 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee wishes to write on copies of the documents, therefore, it must obtain its own photocopies, unless it has been provided with extra copies specified in its _____ as for that purpose.

   *Answer:* instructions — “If the committee wishes to write on copies of the documents, therefore, it must obtain its own photocopies, unless it has been provided with extra copies specified in its instructions as for that purpose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:20 — “If the committee wishes to write on copies of the documents, therefore, it must obtain its own photocopies, unless it has been provided with extra copies specified in its instructions as for that purpose.”

**142.** `[C1201 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The quorum in a committee is a _____ of its membership unless the assembly has prescribed a different quorum (40).
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:21 — “The quorum in a committee is a majority of its membership unless the assembly has prescribed a different quorum (40).”

**143.** `[C1201 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If some members of the committee believe that the chairman has failed to call this initial meeting or any subsequent meeting when necessary, a meeting of the committee may be called by any two of its members, unless (such as for very large committees) the assembly’s rules or _____ prescribe, or empower the committee itself to require, a larger number.

   *Answer:* instructions — “If some members of the committee believe that the chairman has failed to call this initial meeting or any subsequent meeting when necessary, a meeting of the committee may be called by any two of its members, unless (such as for very large committees) the assembly’s rules or instructions prescribe, or empower the committee itself to require, a larger number.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:21 — “If some members of the committee believe that the chairman has failed to call this initial meeting or any subsequent meeting when necessary, a meeting of the committee may be called by any two of its members, unless (such as for very large committees) the assembly’s rules or instructions prescribe, or empower the committee itself to require, a larger number.”

**144.** `[C1201 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is the _____ of the person or persons calling a committee meeting to ensure that reasonable notice of its time and place is sent to every committee member.
   A. consideration
   B. Parliamentary
   C. immediately
   D. responsibility

   *Answer:* D) responsibility. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:21 — “It is the responsibility of the person or persons calling a committee meeting to ensure that reasonable notice of its time and place is sent to every committee member.”

**145.** `[C1201 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The quorum in a committee is a majority of its _____ unless the assembly has prescribed a different quorum (40).
