   *Answer:* membership — “The quorum in a committee is a majority of its membership unless the assembly has prescribed a different quorum (40).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:21 — “The quorum in a committee is a majority of its membership unless the assembly has prescribed a different quorum (40).”

**146.** `[C1201 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If some members of the committee believe that the chairman has failed to call this initial meeting or any subsequent meeting when necessary, a meeting of the committee may be called by any two of its members, unless (such as for very large committees) the assembly’s rules or _____ prescribe, or empower the committee itself to require, a larger number.
   A. Announcements
   B. Parliamentary
   C. Indefinitely
   D. instructions

   *Answer:* D) instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:21 — “If some members of the committee believe that the chairman has failed to call this initial meeting or any subsequent meeting when necessary, a meeting of the committee may be called by any two of its members, unless (such as for very large committees) the assembly’s rules or instructions prescribe, or empower the committee itself to require, a larger number.”

**147.** `[C1202 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a committee intends to _____, it can simply adjourn, or adjourn to meet at a later time.

   *Answer:* reconvene — “When a committee intends to reconvene, it can simply adjourn, or adjourn to meet at a later time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:22 — “When a committee intends to reconvene, it can simply adjourn, or adjourn to meet at a later time.”

**148.** `[C1202 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the first case—when it adjourns without _____ a time for another meeting—the next meeting is called as provided in the previous paragraph.
   A. landscaped
   B. appointing
   C. Definitely
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* B) appointing. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:22 — “In the first case—when it adjourns without appointing a time for another meeting—the next meeting is called as provided in the previous paragraph.”

**149.** `[C1202 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the second case—when it sets an adjourned meeting— notice of the adjourned meeting is not required (although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible), but _____ efforts must be made to inform absent members of its time and place.

   *Answer:* reasonable — “In the second case—when it sets an adjourned meeting— notice of the adjourned meeting is not required (although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible), but reasonable efforts must be made to inform absent members of its time and place.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:22 — “In the second case—when it sets an adjourned meeting— notice of the adjourned meeting is not required (although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible), but reasonable efforts must be made to inform absent members of its time and place.”

**150.** `[C1202 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee intends to _____, it can simply adjourn, or adjourn to meet at a later time.
   A. delegates
   B. expressly
   C. Secretary
   D. reconvene

   *Answer:* D) reconvene. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:22 — “When a committee intends to reconvene, it can simply adjourn, or adjourn to meet at a later time.”

**151.** `[C1202 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the first case—when it adjourns without _____ a time for another meeting—the next meeting is called as provided in the previous paragraph.

   *Answer:* appointing — “In the first case—when it adjourns without appointing a time for another meeting—the next meeting is called as provided in the previous paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:22 — “In the first case—when it adjourns without appointing a time for another meeting—the next meeting is called as provided in the previous paragraph.”

**152.** `[C1203 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn _____ (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.

   *Answer:* sine die — “When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn sine die (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:23 — “When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn sine die (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.”

**153.** `[C1203 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: All of the meetings of a special committee _____ one session (8).
   A. Unfinished
   B. Procedures
   C. constitute
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) constitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:23 — “All of the meetings of a special committee constitute one session (8).”

**154.** `[C1203 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is _____ to the motion to adjourn sine die (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.

   *Answer:* equivalent — “When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn sine die (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:23 — “When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn sine die (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.”

**155.** `[C1203 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to rise is never used in standing _____, or in special committees until they are ready to go out of existence.
   A. Convention
   B. recognized
   C. authorizes
   D. committees

   *Answer:* D) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:23 — “The motion to rise is never used in standing committees, or in special committees until they are ready to go out of existence.”

**156.** `[C1203 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: All of the meetings of a special committee _____ one session (8).

   *Answer:* constitute — “All of the meetings of a special committee constitute one session (8).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:23 — “All of the meetings of a special committee constitute one session (8).”

**157.** `[C1204 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In small _____, the chairman usually acts as secretary, but in large ones and many standing committees, a secretary may be chosen to keep a brief memorandum in the nature of minutes for the use of the committee.
   A. Reconsider
   B. Definitely
   C. committees
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:24 — “In small committees, the chairman usually acts as secretary, but in large ones and many standing committees, a secretary may be chosen to keep a brief memorandum in the nature of minutes for the use of the committee.”

**158.** `[C1204 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In small _____, the chairman usually acts as secretary, but in large ones and many standing committees, a secretary may be chosen to keep a brief memorandum in the nature of minutes for the use of the committee.

   *Answer:* committees — “In small committees, the chairman usually acts as secretary, but in large ones and many standing committees, a secretary may be chosen to keep a brief memorandum in the nature of minutes for the use of the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:24 — “In small committees, the chairman usually acts as secretary, but in large ones and many standing committees, a secretary may be chosen to keep a brief memorandum in the nature of minutes for the use of the committee.”

**159.** `[C1205 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In committees, the chairman is usually the most active _____ in the discussions and work of the committee.

   *Answer:* participant — “In committees, the chairman is usually the most active participant in the discussions and work of the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:25 — “In committees, the chairman is usually the most active participant in the discussions and work of the committee.”

**160.** `[C1205 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In order that there may be no _____ with the assembly’s having the benefit of its committees’ matured judgment, motions to close or limit debate (15, 16) are not allowed in committees.
   A. constitution
   B. immediately
   C. interference
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) interference. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:25 — “In order that there may be no interference with the assembly’s having the benefit of its committees’ matured judgment, motions to close or limit debate (15, 16) are not allowed in committees.”

**161.** `[C1205 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In committees, the chairman is usually the most active _____ in the discussions and work of the committee.
   A. requirement
   B. convention
   C. participant
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) participant. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:25 — “In committees, the chairman is usually the most active participant in the discussions and work of the committee.”

**162.** `[C1205 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In order that there may be no _____ with the assembly’s having the benefit of its committees’ matured judgment, motions to close or limit debate (15, 16) are not allowed in committees.

   *Answer:* interference — “In order that there may be no interference with the assembly’s having the benefit of its committees’ matured judgment, motions to close or limit debate (15, 16) are not allowed in committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:25 — “In order that there may be no interference with the assembly’s having the benefit of its committees’ matured judgment, motions to close or limit debate (15, 16) are not allowed in committees.”

**163.** `[C1206 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The parent assembly may adopt such instructions to the committee by _____ vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “The parent assembly may adopt such instructions to the committee by majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:26 — “The parent assembly may adopt such instructions to the committee by majority vote.”

**164.** `[C1206 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Committees of organized societies operate under the bylaws, the _____ authority, and any special rules of order or standing rules of the society which may be applicable to them.
   A. adjournment
   B. representatives
   C. Announcements
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:26 — “Committees of organized societies operate under the bylaws, the parliamentary authority, and any special rules of order or standing rules of the society which may be applicable to them.”

**165.** `[C1206 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee may not adopt its own rules except as authorized in the rules of the society or in _____ given to the committee by its parent assembly in a particular case.

   *Answer:* instructions — “A committee may not adopt its own rules except as authorized in the rules of the society or in instructions given to the committee by its parent assembly in a particular case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:26 — “A committee may not adopt its own rules except as authorized in the rules of the society or in instructions given to the committee by its parent assembly in a particular case.”

**166.** `[C1206 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The parent assembly may adopt such instructions to the committee by _____ vote.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:26 — “The parent assembly may adopt such instructions to the committee by majority vote.”

**167.** `[C1206 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Committees of organized societies operate under the bylaws, the _____ authority, and any special rules of order or standing rules of the society which may be applicable to them.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Committees of organized societies operate under the bylaws, the parliamentary authority, and any special rules of order or standing rules of the society which may be applicable to them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:26 — “Committees of organized societies operate under the bylaws, the parliamentary authority, and any special rules of order or standing rules of the society which may be applicable to them.”

**168.** `[C1207 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a meeting is usually called a _____.

   *Answer:* hearing — “Such a meeting is usually called a hearing.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:27 — “Such a meeting is usually called a hearing.”

**169.** `[C1207 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee is to make substantive _____ or decisions on an important matter, it should give members of the society an opportunity to appear before it and present their views on the subject at a time scheduled by the committee.
   A. resolutions
   B. President-Elect
   C. subsequently
   D. recommendations

   *Answer:* D) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:27 — “When a committee is to make substantive recommendations or decisions on an important matter, it should give members of the society an opportunity to appear before it and present their views on the subject at a time scheduled by the committee.”

**170.** `[C1207 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During actual _____ of the committee, only committee members have the right to be present.

   *Answer:* deliberations — “During actual deliberations of the committee, only committee members have the right to be present.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:27 — “During actual deliberations of the committee, only committee members have the right to be present.”

**171.** `[C1207 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a meeting is usually called a _____.
   A. hearing
   B. Charges
   C. Motions
   D. Pending

   *Answer:* A) hearing. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:27 — “Such a meeting is usually called a hearing.”

**172.** `[C1207 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a committee is to make substantive _____ or decisions on an important matter, it should give members of the society an opportunity to appear before it and present their views on the subject at a time scheduled by the committee.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “When a committee is to make substantive recommendations or decisions on an important matter, it should give members of the society an opportunity to appear before it and present their views on the subject at a time scheduled by the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:27 — “When a committee is to make substantive recommendations or decisions on an important matter, it should give members of the society an opportunity to appear before it and present their views on the subject at a time scheduled by the committee.”

**173.** `[C1208 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, if there will be no _____ for this to occur within the time needed to effectively resolve the problem and enable the committee to complete its assigned tasks, the committee may protect itself against such disruptive behavior by requiring the disorderly member to leave the meeting room during the remainder of the meeting.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “However, if there will be no opportunity for this to occur within the time needed to effectively resolve the problem and enable the committee to complete its assigned tasks, the committee may protect itself against such disruptive behavior by requiring the disorderly member to leave the meeting room during the remainder of the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:28 — “However, if there will be no opportunity for this to occur within the time needed to effectively resolve the problem and enable the committee to complete its assigned tasks, the committee may protect itself against such disruptive behavior by requiring the disorderly member to leave the meeting room during the remainder of the meeting.”

**174.** `[C1208 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, if there will be no _____ for this to occur within the time needed to effectively resolve the problem and enable the committee to complete its assigned tasks, the committee may protect itself against such disruptive behavior by requiring the disorderly member to leave the meeting room during the remainder of the meeting.
   A. opportunity
   B. Information
   C. Organization
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:28 — “However, if there will be no opportunity for this to occur within the time needed to effectively resolve the problem and enable the committee to complete its assigned tasks, the committee may protect itself against such disruptive behavior by requiring the disorderly member to leave the meeting room during the remainder of the meeting.”

**175.** `[C1209 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Continuity and Conclusion of _____ Assignment.

   *Answer:* Committee — “Continuity and Conclusion of Committee Assignment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:29 — “Continuity and Conclusion of Committee Assignment.”

**176.** `[C1209 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ and Conclusion of Committee Assignment.
   A. resolution
   B. Continuity
   C. authorizes
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) Continuity. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:29 — “Continuity and Conclusion of Committee Assignment.”

**177.** `[C1209 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since members of standing committees in ordinary societies are appointed for a term _____ to that of the officers, such a committee is generally required to report at least once a year, usually at the annual meeting, on its activities and everything referred to it during the year.

   *Answer:* corresponding — “Since members of standing committees in ordinary societies are appointed for a term corresponding to that of the officers, such a committee is generally required to report at least once a year, usually at the annual meeting, on its activities and everything referred to it during the year.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:29 — “Since members of standing committees in ordinary societies are appointed for a term corresponding to that of the officers, such a committee is generally required to report at least once a year, usually at the annual meeting, on its activities and everything referred to it during the year.”

**178.** `[C1209 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a standing committee submits such a report at the conclusion of its members’ term, the committee is not discharged from further _____ of referred matters on which it reports partially at that time, unless the assembly so votes (36);
   A. presentation
   B. Preparation
   C. consideration
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:29 — “When a standing committee submits such a report at the conclusion of its members’ term, the committee is not discharged from further consideration of referred matters on which it reports partially at that time, unless the assembly so votes (36);”

**179.** `[C1209 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: thus such matters _____ go over to the new committee.

   *Answer:* normally — “thus such matters normally go over to the new committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:29 — “thus such matters normally go over to the new committee.”

**180.** `[C1210 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A special committee—since it is appointed for a specific purpose—continues to exist until the duty assigned to it is _____, unless discharged sooner (see 36);

   *Answer:* accomplished — “A special committee—since it is appointed for a specific purpose—continues to exist until the duty assigned to it is accomplished, unless discharged sooner (see 36);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:30 — “A special committee—since it is appointed for a specific purpose—continues to exist until the duty assigned to it is accomplished, unless discharged sooner (see 36);”

**181.** `[C1210 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and it ceases to exist as soon as the assembly _____ its final report.
   A. assuming
   B. Postpone
   C. receives
   D. business

   *Answer:* C) receives. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:30 — “and it ceases to exist as soon as the assembly receives its final report.”

**182.** `[C1210 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The fact that an annual meeting _____ does not discharge a special committee.

   *Answer:* intervenes — “The fact that an annual meeting intervenes does not discharge a special committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:30 — “The fact that an annual meeting intervenes does not discharge a special committee.”

**183.** `[C1210 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But in a body which ceases to exist or in which the terms of some or all of its members expire at a definite time, like a _____ of delegates, a city council, or a board of directors, a special committee expires with the body that appointed it, unless it is appointed expressly to report at a later time.
   A. recognized
   B. subsidiary
   C. convention
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:30 — “But in a body which ceases to exist or in which the terms of some or all of its members expire at a definite time, like a convention of delegates, a city council, or a board of directors, a special committee expires with the body that appointed it, unless it is appointed expressly to report at a later time.”

**184.** `[C1210 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But in a body which ceases to exist or in which the terms of some or all of its members expire at a definite time, like a convention of delegates, a city council, or a board of directors, a special committee _____ with the body that appointed it, unless it is appointed expressly to report at a later time.

   *Answer:* expires — “But in a body which ceases to exist or in which the terms of some or all of its members expire at a definite time, like a convention of delegates, a city council, or a board of directors, a special committee expires with the body that appointed it, unless it is appointed expressly to report at a later time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:30 — “But in a body which ceases to exist or in which the terms of some or all of its members expire at a definite time, like a convention of delegates, a city council, or a board of directors, a special committee expires with the body that appointed it, unless it is appointed expressly to report at a later time.”

**185.** `[C1211 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A report of a _____ board or a committee is an official statement formally adopted by, and submitted in the name of, the reporting body, informing the parent assembly of action taken or recommended, or information obtained.

   *Answer:* subordinate — “A report of a subordinate board or a committee is an official statement formally adopted by, and submitted in the name of, the reporting body, informing the parent assembly of action taken or recommended, or information obtained.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:1 — “A report of a subordinate board or a committee is an official statement formally adopted by, and submitted in the name of, the reporting body, informing the parent assembly of action taken or recommended, or information obtained.”

**186.** `[C1211 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A report of a _____ board or a committee is an official statement formally adopted by, and submitted in the name of, the reporting body, informing the parent assembly of action taken or recommended, or information obtained.
   A. undebatable
   B. Permission
   C. subordinate
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) subordinate. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:1 — “A report of a subordinate board or a committee is an official statement formally adopted by, and submitted in the name of, the reporting body, informing the parent assembly of action taken or recommended, or information obtained.”

**187.** `[C1212 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Limitation of Report Content to What Has Been Properly _____.

   *Answer:* Agreed To — “Limitation of Report Content to What Has Been Properly Agreed To.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:2 — “Limitation of Report Content to What Has Been Properly Agreed To.”

**188.** `[C1212 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of Report Content to What Has Been Properly Agreed To.
   A. Convention
   B. Substitute
   C. Limitation
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* C) Limitation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:2 — “Limitation of Report Content to What Has Been Properly Agreed To.”

**189.** `[C1212 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of a committee, however, if it is _____ to bring its members together for a meeting, the report of the committee can contain what has been agreed to by every one of its members.

   *Answer:* impractical — “In the case of a committee, however, if it is impractical to bring its members together for a meeting, the report of the committee can contain what has been agreed to by every one of its members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:2 — “In the case of a committee, however, if it is impractical to bring its members together for a meeting, the report of the committee can contain what has been agreed to by every one of its members.”

**190.** `[C1212 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of a committee, however, if it is _____ to bring its members together for a meeting, the report of the committee can contain what has been agreed to by every one of its members.
   A. authorizes
   B. resolutions
   C. Particular
   D. impractical

   *Answer:* D) impractical. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:2 — “In the case of a committee, however, if it is impractical to bring its members together for a meeting, the report of the committee can contain what has been agreed to by every one of its members.”

**191.** `[C1213 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For _____ in the discussions in this section, reports may be divided into two general categories as follows:
   A. convenience
   B. interrupted
   C. convention
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) convenience. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:3 — “For convenience in the discussions in this section, reports may be divided into two general categories as follows:”

**192.** `[C1213 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For _____ in the discussions in this section, reports may be divided into two general categories as follows:

   *Answer:* convenience — “For convenience in the discussions in this section, reports may be divided into two general categories as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:3 — “For convenience in the discussions in this section, reports may be divided into two general categories as follows:”

**193.** `[C1214 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption _____ action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).
   A. recognized
   B. convention
   C. Definitely
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* D) authorizes. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:4 — “Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).”

**194.** `[C1214 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any report of a subordinate board or a committee (of type (a) or (b) above), specific recommendations for immediate _____ by the parent assembly should be grouped at the end—repeating them if they have already been noted at separate places in the report —and should generally be cast in the form of one or more proposed resolutions.

   *Answer:* action — “In any report of a subordinate board or a committee (of type (a) or (b) above), specific recommendations for immediate action by the parent assembly should be grouped at the end—repeating them if they have already been noted at separate places in the report —and should generally be cast in the form of one or more proposed resolutions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:4 — “In any report of a subordinate board or a committee (of type (a) or (b) above), specific recommendations for immediate action by the parent assembly should be grouped at the end—repeating them if they have already been noted at separate places in the report —and should generally be cast in the form of one or more proposed resolutions.”

**195.** `[C1214 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In any report of a subordinate board or a committee (of type (a) or (b) above), specific _____ for immediate action by the parent assembly should be grouped at the end—repeating them if they have already been noted at separate places in the report —and should generally be cast in the form of one or more proposed resolutions.
   A. adjournment
   B. immediately
   C. representatives
   D. recommendations

   *Answer:* D) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:4 — “In any report of a subordinate board or a committee (of type (a) or (b) above), specific recommendations for immediate action by the parent assembly should be grouped at the end—repeating them if they have already been noted at separate places in the report —and should generally be cast in the form of one or more proposed resolutions.”

**196.** `[C1214 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption _____ action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).

   *Answer:* authorizes — “Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:4 — “Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).”

**197.** `[C1215 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If this approach is followed and it is desired to bring supporting reasons to the attention of the assembly, the reporting member (that is, the person who presents the report) can include brief oral _____ with his presentation.

   *Answer:* explanations — “If this approach is followed and it is desired to bring supporting reasons to the attention of the assembly, the reporting member (that is, the person who presents the report) can include brief oral explanations with his presentation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:5 — “If this approach is followed and it is desired to bring supporting reasons to the attention of the assembly, the reporting member (that is, the person who presents the report) can include brief oral explanations with his presentation.”

**198.** `[C1215 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Or, supporting reasons can be explained at greater length during debate on the report —by the reporting member, who has the right to the floor first in debate, and also by other members of the board or committee, if _____, as the debate progresses.
   A. authorizes
   B. Reconsider
   C. appropriate
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) appropriate. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:5 — “Or, supporting reasons can be explained at greater length during debate on the report —by the reporting member, who has the right to the floor first in debate, and also by other members of the board or committee, if appropriate, as the debate progresses.”

**199.** `[C1215 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If this approach is followed and it is desired to bring supporting reasons to the attention of the assembly, the reporting member (that is, the person who presents the report) can include brief oral _____ with his presentation.
   A. explanations
   B. Definitely
   C. immediately
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) explanations. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:5 — “If this approach is followed and it is desired to bring supporting reasons to the attention of the assembly, the reporting member (that is, the person who presents the report) can include brief oral explanations with his presentation.”

**200.** `[C1215 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Or, supporting reasons can be explained at greater length during debate on the report —by the reporting member, who has the right to the floor first in debate, and also by other members of the board or committee, if _____, as the debate progresses.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “Or, supporting reasons can be explained at greater length during debate on the report —by the reporting member, who has the right to the floor first in debate, and also by other members of the board or committee, if appropriate, as the debate progresses.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:5 — “Or, supporting reasons can be explained at greater length during debate on the report —by the reporting member, who has the right to the floor first in debate, and also by other members of the board or committee, if appropriate, as the debate progresses.”

**201.** `[C1216 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the foregoing connection, it should be noted that under _____ conditions the inclusion of supporting facts or reasoning in a report proposing certain action may tend to work against the taking of that action, since some members who might otherwise have been willing to accept the proposals may be led to vote against them if they disagree with the factual background as reported or the reasoning of the reporting body.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “In the foregoing connection, it should be noted that under parliamentary conditions the inclusion of supporting facts or reasoning in a report proposing certain action may tend to work against the taking of that action, since some members who might otherwise have been willing to accept the proposals may be led to vote against them if they disagree with the factual background as reported or the reasoning of the reporting body.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:6 — “In the foregoing connection, it should be noted that under parliamentary conditions the inclusion of supporting facts or reasoning in a report proposing certain action may tend to work against the taking of that action, since some members who might otherwise have been willing to accept the proposals may be led to vote against them if they disagree with the factual background as reported or the reasoning of the reporting body.”

**202.** `[C1216 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the foregoing connection, it should be noted that under _____ conditions the inclusion of supporting facts or reasoning in a report proposing certain action may tend to work against the taking of that action, since some members who might otherwise have been willing to accept the proposals may be led to vote against them if they disagree with the factual background as reported or the reasoning of the reporting body.
   A. constitution
   B. deliberative
   C. undebatable
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:6 — “In the foregoing connection, it should be noted that under parliamentary conditions the inclusion of supporting facts or reasoning in a report proposing certain action may tend to work against the taking of that action, since some members who might otherwise have been willing to accept the proposals may be led to vote against them if they disagree with the factual background as reported or the reasoning of the reporting body.”

**203.** `[C1217 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If special conditions dictate that a report devoted to a single subject and presenting _____ should include a full account of the details involved in the case, the body of the report is best organized according to the following topics, as applicable:
   A. consideration
   B. president-elect
   C. recommendations
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* C) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:7 — “If special conditions dictate that a report devoted to a single subject and presenting recommendations should include a full account of the details involved in the case, the body of the report is best organized according to the following topics, as applicable:”

**204.** `[C1217 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If special conditions dictate that a report devoted to a single subject and presenting _____ should include a full account of the details involved in the case, the body of the report is best organized according to the following topics, as applicable:

   *Answer:* recommendations — “If special conditions dictate that a report devoted to a single subject and presenting recommendations should include a full account of the details involved in the case, the body of the report is best organized according to the following topics, as applicable:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:7 — “If special conditions dictate that a report devoted to a single subject and presenting recommendations should include a full account of the details involved in the case, the body of the report is best organized according to the following topics, as applicable:”

**205.** `[C1218 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A report of a board or committee to an assembly is _____ at the proper time by a “reporting member” of the board or committee.

   *Answer:* presented — “A report of a board or committee to an assembly is presented at the proper time by a “reporting member” of the board or committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:8 — “A report of a board or committee to an assembly is presented at the proper time by a “reporting member” of the board or committee.”

**206.** `[C1218 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For the report of a board whose chairman is also the _____ officer of the assembly, the secretary or another one of its members acts as reporting member.
   A. delegates
   B. paragraph
   C. presiding
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* C) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:8 — “For the report of a board whose chairman is also the presiding officer of the assembly, the secretary or another one of its members acts as reporting member.”

**207.** `[C1218 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A report of a board or committee to an assembly is presented at the proper time by a “_____ member” of the board or committee.

   *Answer:* reporting — “A report of a board or committee to an assembly is presented at the proper time by a “reporting member” of the board or committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:8 — “A report of a board or committee to an assembly is presented at the proper time by a “reporting member” of the board or committee.”

**208.** `[C1218 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For the report of a board whose chairman is also the _____ officer of the assembly, the secretary or another one of its members acts as reporting member.

   *Answer:* presiding — “For the report of a board whose chairman is also the presiding officer of the assembly, the secretary or another one of its members acts as reporting member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:8 — “For the report of a board whose chairman is also the presiding officer of the assembly, the secretary or another one of its members acts as reporting member.”

**209.** `[C1219 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A reporting member _____ or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;

   *Answer:* makes — “A reporting member makes or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “A reporting member makes or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;”

**210.** `[C1219 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A reporting member makes or _____ a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;
   A. adoption
   B. Standard
   C. decision
   D. presents

   *Answer:* D) presents. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “A reporting member makes or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;”

**211.** `[C1219 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the assembly hears the report thus read or orally rendered, it _____ the report.

   *Answer:* receives — “When the assembly hears the report thus read or orally rendered, it receives the report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “When the assembly hears the report thus read or orally rendered, it receives the report.”

**212.** `[C1219 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The terms _____ and reception accordingly describe one and the same event from the respective viewpoints of the reporting member and the assembly.
   A. subsequently
   B. presentation
   C. immediately
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “The terms presentation and reception accordingly describe one and the same event from the respective viewpoints of the reporting member and the assembly.”

**213.** `[C1219 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The terms presentation and _____ accordingly describe one and the same event from the respective viewpoints of the reporting member and the assembly.

   *Answer:* reception — “The terms presentation and reception accordingly describe one and the same event from the respective viewpoints of the reporting member and the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “The terms presentation and reception accordingly describe one and the same event from the respective viewpoints of the reporting member and the assembly.”

**214.** `[C1220 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the remainder of this section, it is explained how such action under various conditions may involve the introduction of motions—to implement _____ or, occasionally, to adopt the entire report.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “In the remainder of this section, it is explained how such action under various conditions may involve the introduction of motions—to implement recommendations or, occasionally, to adopt the entire report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:10 — “In the remainder of this section, it is explained how such action under various conditions may involve the introduction of motions—to implement recommendations or, occasionally, to adopt the entire report.”

**215.** `[C1220 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the remainder of this section, it is explained how such action under various conditions may involve the introduction of motions—to implement _____ or, occasionally, to adopt the entire report.
   A. immediately
   B. resolutions
   C. recommendations
   D. Responsibilities

   *Answer:* C) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:10 — “In the remainder of this section, it is explained how such action under various conditions may involve the introduction of motions—to implement recommendations or, occasionally, to adopt the entire report.”

**216.** `[C1221 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the report is read by the secretary or a reading clerk in such a case, the reporting member resumes the floor for the purpose of making the motion _____ after the reading is completed.
   A. adjournment
   B. immediately
   C. Convention
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:11 — “If the report is read by the secretary or a reading clerk in such a case, the reporting member resumes the floor for the purpose of making the motion immediately after the reading is completed.”

**217.** `[C1221 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the report is read by the secretary or a reading clerk in such a case, the reporting member resumes the floor for the purpose of making the motion _____ after the reading is completed.

   *Answer:* immediately — “If the report is read by the secretary or a reading clerk in such a case, the reporting member resumes the floor for the purpose of making the motion immediately after the reading is completed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:11 — “If the report is read by the secretary or a reading clerk in such a case, the reporting member resumes the floor for the purpose of making the motion immediately after the reading is completed.”

**218.** `[C1222 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by _____ the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.

   *Answer:* assuming — “Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by assuming the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:12 — “Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by assuming the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.”

**219.** `[C1222 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the person presenting the report is not a member of the assembly or for any other reason does not make the required motion to implement the _____ as just described, any member of the assembly can do so;
   A. recommendations
   B. characteristics
   C. requirement
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:12 — “If the person presenting the report is not a member of the assembly or for any other reason does not make the required motion to implement the recommendations as just described, any member of the assembly can do so;”

**220.** `[C1222 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must _____ be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by assuming the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.

   *Answer:* necessarily — “Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by assuming the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:12 — “Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by assuming the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.”

**221.** `[C1222 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the person presenting the report is not a member of the assembly or for any other reason does not make the required motion to implement the _____ as just described, any member of the assembly can do so;

   *Answer:* recommendations — “If the person presenting the report is not a member of the assembly or for any other reason does not make the required motion to implement the recommendations as just described, any member of the assembly can do so;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:12 — “If the person presenting the report is not a member of the assembly or for any other reason does not make the required motion to implement the recommendations as just described, any member of the assembly can do so;”

**222.** `[C1223 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In rare _____ after an assembly has received a report, it may have occasion to adopt the (entire) report;

   *Answer:* instances — “In rare instances after an assembly has received a report, it may have occasion to adopt the (entire) report;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:13 — “In rare instances after an assembly has received a report, it may have occasion to adopt the (entire) report;”

**223.** `[C1223 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unlike motions to take the action _____ in a report as described above, a motion “to adopt the report” should be made by someone other than the reporting member and requires a second.
   A. convention
   B. authorizes
   C. recommended
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) recommended. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:13 — “Unlike motions to take the action recommended in a report as described above, a motion “to adopt the report” should be made by someone other than the reporting member and requires a second.”

**224.** `[C1223 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Adoption of an entire report is seldom wise except when it is to be issued or published in the name of the whole _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “Adoption of an entire report is seldom wise except when it is to be issued or published in the name of the whole organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:13 — “Adoption of an entire report is seldom wise except when it is to be issued or published in the name of the whole organization.”

**225.** `[C1223 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In rare _____ after an assembly has received a report, it may have occasion to adopt the (entire) report;
   A. Reporting
   B. delegates
   C. postponed
   D. instances

   *Answer:* D) instances. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:13 — “In rare instances after an assembly has received a report, it may have occasion to adopt the (entire) report;”

**226.** `[C1223 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike motions to take the action _____ in a report as described above, a motion “to adopt the report” should be made by someone other than the reporting member and requires a second.

   *Answer:* recommended — “Unlike motions to take the action recommended in a report as described above, a motion “to adopt the report” should be made by someone other than the reporting member and requires a second.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:13 — “Unlike motions to take the action recommended in a report as described above, a motion “to adopt the report” should be made by someone other than the reporting member and requires a second.”

**227.** `[C1224 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions _____, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.

   *Answer:* adopt, accept — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:14 — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”

**228.** `[C1224 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and _____ are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.
   A. the same
   B. Form and
   C. speed up
   D. agree to

   *Answer:* D) agree to. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:14 — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”

**229.** `[C1224 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is usually best to use the word adopt, however, since it is the least likely to be _____.

   *Answer:* misunderstood — “It is usually best to use the word adopt, however, since it is the least likely to be misunderstood.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:14 — “It is usually best to use the word adopt, however, since it is the least likely to be misunderstood.”

**230.** `[C1224 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is usually best to use the word adopt, however, since it is the least likely to be _____.
   A. immediately
   B. constitution
   C. Organization
   D. misunderstood

   *Answer:* D) misunderstood. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:14 — “It is usually best to use the word adopt, however, since it is the least likely to be misunderstood.”

**231.** `[C1225 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A common error is to move that a report “be received” after it has been read—apparently on the supposition that such a motion is necessary in order for the report to be taken under _____ or to be recorded as having been made.

   *Answer:* consideration — “A common error is to move that a report “be received” after it has been read—apparently on the supposition that such a motion is necessary in order for the report to be taken under consideration or to be recorded as having been made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:15 — “A common error is to move that a report “be received” after it has been read—apparently on the supposition that such a motion is necessary in order for the report to be taken under consideration or to be recorded as having been made.”

**232.** `[C1225 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In fact, this motion is _____ and is therefore not in order, since the report has already been received.
   A. meaningless
   B. Information
   C. Unfinished
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) meaningless. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:15 — “In fact, this motion is meaningless and is therefore not in order, since the report has already been received.”

**233.** `[C1225 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even before a report has been read, a motion to receive it is _____ if the time for its reception is established by the order of business, or if no member objects (see also below).

   *Answer:* unnecessary — “Even before a report has been read, a motion to receive it is unnecessary if the time for its reception is established by the order of business, or if no member objects (see also below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:15 — “Even before a report has been read, a motion to receive it is unnecessary if the time for its reception is established by the order of business, or if no member objects (see also below).”

**234.** `[C1225 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A common error is to move that a report “be received” after it has been read—apparently on the supposition that such a motion is necessary in order for the report to be taken under _____ or to be recorded as having been made.
   A. recommendation
   B. consideration
   C. presentation
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:15 — “A common error is to move that a report “be received” after it has been read—apparently on the supposition that such a motion is necessary in order for the report to be taken under consideration or to be recorded as having been made.”

**235.** `[C1225 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In fact, this motion is _____ and is therefore not in order, since the report has already been received.

   *Answer:* meaningless — “In fact, this motion is meaningless and is therefore not in order, since the report has already been received.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:15 — “In fact, this motion is meaningless and is therefore not in order, since the report has already been received.”

**236.** `[C1226 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even _____ the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a legitimate motion to receive made before the report is read.

   *Answer:* before — “Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even before the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a legitimate motion to receive made before the report is read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:16 — “Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even before the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a legitimate motion to receive made before the report is read.”

**237.** `[C1226 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even before the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a _____ motion to receive made before the report is read.
   A. privileged
   B. legitimate
   C. settlement
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) legitimate. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:16 — “Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even before the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a legitimate motion to receive made before the report is read.”

**238.** `[C1226 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion “to accept” made under any of these circumstances is adopted and is given its proper _____, it implies that the assembly has endorsed the complete report.

   *Answer:* interpretation — “If a motion “to accept” made under any of these circumstances is adopted and is given its proper interpretation, it implies that the assembly has endorsed the complete report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:16 — “If a motion “to accept” made under any of these circumstances is adopted and is given its proper interpretation, it implies that the assembly has endorsed the complete report.”

**239.** `[C1226 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion “to accept” made under any of these circumstances is adopted and is given its proper _____, it implies that the assembly has endorsed the complete report.
   A. interpretation
   B. presentation
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Responsibilities

   *Answer:* A) interpretation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:16 — “If a motion “to accept” made under any of these circumstances is adopted and is given its proper interpretation, it implies that the assembly has endorsed the complete report.”

**240.** `[C1227 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Applicability of Regular Rules to Questions Arising out of _____.

   *Answer:* Reports — “Applicability of Regular Rules to Questions Arising out of Reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:17 — “Applicability of Regular Rules to Questions Arising out of Reports.”

**241.** `[C1227 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of Regular Rules to Questions Arising out of Reports.
   A. Applicability
   B. consideration
   C. resolutions
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* A) Applicability. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:17 — “Applicability of Regular Rules to Questions Arising out of Reports.”

**242.** `[C1227 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a board or committee report has been received and the chair has stated the question on the adoption of the motion, resolution(s), _____(s), or report— whether the question became pending automatically, or the proper motion was made or was assumed by the chair as explained above—the matter is treated as any other main question, is open to debate and amendment, and can have any of the subsidiary motions applied to it.

   *Answer:* recommendation — “When a board or committee report has been received and the chair has stated the question on the adoption of the motion, resolution(s), recommendation(s), or report— whether the question became pending automatically, or the proper motion was made or was assumed by the chair as explained above—the matter is treated as any other main question, is open to debate and amendment, and can have any of the subsidiary motions applied to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:17 — “When a board or committee report has been received and the chair has stated the question on the adoption of the motion, resolution(s), recommendation(s), or report— whether the question became pending automatically, or the proper motion was made or was assumed by the chair as explained above—the matter is treated as any other main question, is open to debate and amendment, and can have any of the subsidiary motions applied to it.”

**243.** `[C1227 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Similarly, if a committee to which the main question was referred has recommended that it be amended or definitely or _____ postponed, the motion to take such action is debatable and (for primary amendment or definite postponement) amendable, under the regular rules for these motions.
   A. Procedures
   B. requirement
   C. indefinitely
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:17 — “Similarly, if a committee to which the main question was referred has recommended that it be amended or definitely or indefinitely postponed, the motion to take such action is debatable and (for primary amendment or definite postponement) amendable, under the regular rules for these motions.”

**244.** `[C1227 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a board or committee report has been received and the chair has stated the question on the adoption of the motion, resolution(s), _____(s), or report— whether the question became pending automatically, or the proper motion was made or was assumed by the chair as explained above—the matter is treated as any other main question, is open to debate and amendment, and can have any of the subsidiary motions applied to it.
   A. adjournment
   B. recommendation
   C. characteristics
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* B) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:17 — “When a board or committee report has been received and the chair has stated the question on the adoption of the motion, resolution(s), recommendation(s), or report— whether the question became pending automatically, or the proper motion was made or was assumed by the chair as explained above—the matter is treated as any other main question, is open to debate and amendment, and can have any of the subsidiary motions applied to it.”

**245.** `[C1228 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the consideration of a matter _____ to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.

   *Answer:* that was referred — “In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the consideration of a matter that was referred to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:18 — “In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the consideration of a matter that was referred to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.”

**246.** `[C1228 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The reason is that an _____ of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;
   A. Variations in Large Assemblies
   B. Objection to the Consideration
   C. Reports of Executive Officers
   D. Other limitations or extensions

   *Answer:* B) Objection to the Consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:18 — “The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;”

**247.** `[C1228 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration _____ can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;

   *Answer:* of a Question — “The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:18 — “The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;”

**248.** `[C1228 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the _____ of a matter that was referred to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.
   A. consideration
   B. adjournment
   C. Parliamentary
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:18 — “In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the consideration of a matter that was referred to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.”

**249.** `[C1229 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Conditions for Amendment of a Report by the Assembly _____.

   *Answer:* Before Its Adoption — “Conditions for Amendment of a Report by the Assembly Before Its Adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:19 — “Conditions for Amendment of a Report by the Assembly Before Its Adoption.”

**250.** `[C1229 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ for Amendment of a Report by the Assembly Before Its Adoption.
   A. subsidiary
   B. Particular
   C. Permission
   D. Conditions

   *Answer:* D) Conditions. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:19 — “Conditions for Amendment of a Report by the Assembly Before Its Adoption.”

**251.** `[C1229 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An assembly that is to adopt an entire report which it has received can amend the report, but the text as _____ or recorded must not make the reporting board or committee appear to say anything different from the wording that was actually reported.

   *Answer:* published — “An assembly that is to adopt an entire report which it has received can amend the report, but the text as published or recorded must not make the reporting board or committee appear to say anything different from the wording that was actually reported.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:19 — “An assembly that is to adopt an entire report which it has received can amend the report, but the text as published or recorded must not make the reporting board or committee appear to say anything different from the wording that was actually reported.”

**252.** `[C1229 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An assembly that is to adopt an entire report which it has received can amend the report, but the text as _____ or recorded must not make the reporting board or committee appear to say anything different from the wording that was actually reported.
   A. reception
   B. Reporting
   C. Crestwood
   D. published

   *Answer:* D) published. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:19 — “An assembly that is to adopt an entire report which it has received can amend the report, but the text as published or recorded must not make the reporting board or committee appear to say anything different from the wording that was actually reported.”

**253.** `[C1230 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ board (or board of directors) of a society reports to the assembly annually on the work done during the year, and at such other times and upon such subjects as the bylaws may prescribe or the society’s business may require.

   *Answer:* executive — “The executive board (or board of directors) of a society reports to the assembly annually on the work done during the year, and at such other times and upon such subjects as the bylaws may prescribe or the society’s business may require.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:20 — “The executive board (or board of directors) of a society reports to the assembly annually on the work done during the year, and at such other times and upon such subjects as the bylaws may prescribe or the society’s business may require.”

**254.** `[C1230 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ board (or board of directors) of a society reports to the assembly annually on the work done during the year, and at such other times and upon such subjects as the bylaws may prescribe or the society’s business may require.
   A. executive
   B. Privilege
   C. debatable
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* A) executive. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:20 — “The executive board (or board of directors) of a society reports to the assembly annually on the work done during the year, and at such other times and upon such subjects as the bylaws may prescribe or the society’s business may require.”

**255.** `[C1231 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A board report should be signed by the president or chairman of the board and its _____ only.

   *Answer:* secretary — “A board report should be signed by the president or chairman of the board and its secretary only.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:21 — “A board report should be signed by the president or chairman of the board and its secretary only.”

**256.** `[C1231 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A board report should be signed by the president or chairman of the board and its _____ only.
   A. debatable
   B. extending
   C. auditors’
   D. secretary

   *Answer:* D) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:21 — “A board report should be signed by the president or chairman of the board and its secretary only.”

**257.** `[C1232 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reception and _____ of Board Reports.

   *Answer:* Disposition — “Reception and Disposition of Board Reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:22 — “Reception and Disposition of Board Reports.”

**258.** `[C1232 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In meetings at which the executive board is to make a report, the chair calls for it at the time provided in the order of business, or, if there is no such provision, before committee reports are received or _____ business is taken up.
   A. landscaped
   B. unfinished
   C. variations
   D. convention

   *Answer:* B) unfinished. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:22 — “In meetings at which the executive board is to make a report, the chair calls for it at the time provided in the order of business, or, if there is no such provision, before committee reports are received or unfinished business is taken up.”

**259.** `[C1232 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the reading of the report, the reporting member moves the adoption of any _____ included in it, which, as indicated above, should be grouped or repeated at the end.

   *Answer:* resolutions — “After the reading of the report, the reporting member moves the adoption of any resolutions included in it, which, as indicated above, should be grouped or repeated at the end.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:22 — “After the reading of the report, the reporting member moves the adoption of any resolutions included in it, which, as indicated above, should be grouped or repeated at the end.”

**260.** `[C1232 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the annual report of the board is to be formally adopted by the society before being published, an _____ wording for the minutes in such a case is the following: “The Executive Board [or “Board of Directors,” etc.] submitted its report, which, after debate and amendment [if any], was adopted as follows, the words in brackets having been struck out and those underlined [or, “in italics”] having been inserted before the report was adopted.” A society need not endorse the report of its board, and can even decline to allow the report to be printed, or it can adopt only a part;
   A. convention
   B. appropriate
   C. requirement
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) appropriate. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:22 — “If the annual report of the board is to be formally adopted by the society before being published, an appropriate wording for the minutes in such a case is the following: “The Executive Board [or “Board of Directors,” etc.] submitted its report, which, after debate and amendment [if any], was adopted as follows, the words in brackets having been struck out and those underlined [or, “in italics”] having been inserted before the report was adopted.” A society need not endorse the report of its board, and can even decline to allow the report to be printed, or it can adopt only a part;”

**261.** `[C1232 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but _____ it prints or records from the report must show any changes clearly marked.

   *Answer:* whatever — “but whatever it prints or records from the report must show any changes clearly marked.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:22 — “but whatever it prints or records from the report must show any changes clearly marked.”

**262.** `[C1233 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of such _____, a report can be given orally only if it is brief enough that the secretary can record its complete substance in the minutes on hearing it given—which he must do if no written copy is submitted for file.

   *Answer:* exceptions — “In the case of such exceptions, a report can be given orally only if it is brief enough that the secretary can record its complete substance in the minutes on hearing it given—which he must do if no written copy is submitted for file.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:23 — “In the case of such exceptions, a report can be given orally only if it is brief enough that the secretary can record its complete substance in the minutes on hearing it given—which he must do if no written copy is submitted for file.”

**263.** `[C1233 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of such _____, a report can be given orally only if it is brief enough that the secretary can record its complete substance in the minutes on hearing it given—which he must do if no written copy is submitted for file.
   A. exceptions
   B. Permission
   C. Procedures
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* A) exceptions. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:23 — “In the case of such exceptions, a report can be given orally only if it is brief enough that the secretary can record its complete substance in the minutes on hearing it given—which he must do if no written copy is submitted for file.”

**264.** `[C1234 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Usually a written committee report is not _____ or dated.

   *Answer:* addressed — “Usually a written committee report is not addressed or dated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:24 — “Usually a written committee report is not addressed or dated.”

**265.** `[C1234 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is _____ to be addressed to the assembly, and its date is that on which it is presented in a meeting of the assembly as recorded in the minutes.
   A. understood
   B. Substitute
   C. Permission
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* A) understood. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:24 — “It is understood to be addressed to the assembly, and its date is that on which it is presented in a meeting of the assembly as recorded in the minutes.”

**266.** `[C1234 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Usually a written committee report is not _____ or dated.
   A. Privilege
   B. addressed
   C. presiding
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* B) addressed. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:24 — “Usually a written committee report is not addressed or dated.”

**267.** `[C1234 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is _____ to be addressed to the assembly, and its date is that on which it is presented in a meeting of the assembly as recorded in the minutes.

   *Answer:* understood — “It is understood to be addressed to the assembly, and its date is that on which it is presented in a meeting of the assembly as recorded in the minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:24 — “It is understood to be addressed to the assembly, and its date is that on which it is presented in a meeting of the assembly as recorded in the minutes.”

**268.** `[C1235 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee report should always be worded in the third person—that is, as shown in the next _____ (not “I report…” or “We recommend…”).

   *Answer:* paragraph — “A committee report should always be worded in the third person—that is, as shown in the next paragraph (not “I report…” or “We recommend…”).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:25 — “A committee report should always be worded in the third person—that is, as shown in the next paragraph (not “I report…” or “We recommend…”).”

**269.** `[C1235 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, in an assembly a committee report is always spoken of by the chair and others as, for example, “the report of the Finance Committee” or “the report of the committee to which was referred… [stating the subject].” It should never be spoken of as “the report of the chairman of the Finance Committee” and never as “Mr.
   A. reception
   B. debatable
   C. Similarly
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* C) Similarly. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:25 — “Similarly, in an assembly a committee report is always spoken of by the chair and others as, for example, “the report of the Finance Committee” or “the report of the committee to which was referred… [stating the subject].” It should never be spoken of as “the report of the chairman of the Finance Committee” and never as “Mr.”
