**270.** `[C1235 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Smith’s report,” even though it is usually presented by the committee chairman and even if he may have _____ drafted it or done most of the work reported.

   *Answer:* personally — “Smith’s report,” even though it is usually presented by the committee chairman and even if he may have personally drafted it or done most of the work reported.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:25 — “Smith’s report,” even though it is usually presented by the committee chairman and even if he may have personally drafted it or done most of the work reported.”

**271.** `[C1235 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee report should always be worded in the third person—that is, as shown in the next _____ (not “I report…” or “We recommend…”).
   A. delegates
   B. auditors’
   C. rejecting
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* D) paragraph. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:25 — “A committee report should always be worded in the third person—that is, as shown in the next paragraph (not “I report…” or “We recommend…”).”

**272.** `[C1235 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, in an assembly a committee report is always spoken of by the chair and others as, for example, “the report of the Finance Committee” or “the report of the committee to which was referred… [stating the subject].” It should never be spoken of as “the report of the chairman of the Finance Committee” and never as “Mr.

   *Answer:* Similarly — “Similarly, in an assembly a committee report is always spoken of by the chair and others as, for example, “the report of the Finance Committee” or “the report of the committee to which was referred… [stating the subject].” It should never be spoken of as “the report of the chairman of the Finance Committee” and never as “Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:25 — “Similarly, in an assembly a committee report is always spoken of by the chair and others as, for example, “the report of the Finance Committee” or “the report of the committee to which was referred… [stating the subject].” It should never be spoken of as “the report of the chairman of the Finance Committee” and never as “Mr.”

**273.** `[C1236 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee report should begin with an _____ of the committee submitting it—the name of the committee in the case of a standing committee, or the subject that was referred in the case of a special committee;

   *Answer:* identification — “A committee report should begin with an identification of the committee submitting it—the name of the committee in the case of a standing committee, or the subject that was referred in the case of a special committee;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:26 — “A committee report should begin with an identification of the committee submitting it—the name of the committee in the case of a standing committee, or the subject that was referred in the case of a special committee;”

**274.** `[C1236 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee report should begin with an _____ of the committee submitting it—the name of the committee in the case of a standing committee, or the subject that was referred in the case of a special committee;
   A. Announcements
   B. identification
   C. recommendation
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) identification. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:26 — “A committee report should begin with an identification of the committee submitting it—the name of the committee in the case of a standing committee, or the subject that was referred in the case of a special committee;”

**275.** `[C1237 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a written committee report is of _____ importance, it should be signed by all the members concurring.

   *Answer:* considerable — “If a written committee report is of considerable importance, it should be signed by all the members concurring.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:27 — “If a written committee report is of considerable importance, it should be signed by all the members concurring.”

**276.** `[C1237 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a written committee report is of _____ importance, it should be signed by all the members concurring.
   A. consideration
   B. Procedures
   C. Permission
   D. considerable

   *Answer:* D) considerable. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:27 — “If a written committee report is of considerable importance, it should be signed by all the members concurring.”

**277.** `[C1238 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Reports of _____ are called for or can be presented in a meeting as follows:
   A. convention
   B. Reconsider
   C. committees
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:28 — “Reports of committees are called for or can be presented in a meeting as follows:”

**278.** `[C1238 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reports of _____ are called for or can be presented in a meeting as follows:

   *Answer:* committees — “Reports of committees are called for or can be presented in a meeting as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:28 — “Reports of committees are called for or can be presented in a meeting as follows:”

**279.** `[C1239 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The proper method of _____ and disposing of different types of committee reports is explained below, with sample reports being given in certain cases.

   *Answer:* presenting — “The proper method of presenting and disposing of different types of committee reports is explained below, with sample reports being given in certain cases.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:30 — “The proper method of presenting and disposing of different types of committee reports is explained below, with sample reports being given in certain cases.”

**280.** `[C1239 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The proper method of _____ and disposing of different types of committee reports is explained below, with sample reports being given in certain cases.
   A. Substitute
   B. convention
   C. presenting
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) presenting. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:30 — “The proper method of presenting and disposing of different types of committee reports is explained below, with sample reports being given in certain cases.”

**281.** `[C1240 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A Report at the Initiative of a Standing Committee _____.

   *Answer:* Recommending Action — “A Report at the Initiative of a Standing Committee Recommending Action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:31 — “A Report at the Initiative of a Standing Committee Recommending Action.”

**282.** `[C1240 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A Report at the Initiative of a Standing Committee _____ Action.
   A. adjournment
   B. Recommending
   C. subsequently
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) Recommending. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:31 — “A Report at the Initiative of a Standing Committee Recommending Action.”

**283.** `[C1240 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a standing committee wishes on its own initiative to recommend action by the assembly on a matter within the committee’s concern, it is generally desirable, as stated above, for the report to consist of or conclude with one or more proposed resolutions embodying the committee’s _____.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “If a standing committee wishes on its own initiative to recommend action by the assembly on a matter within the committee’s concern, it is generally desirable, as stated above, for the report to consist of or conclude with one or more proposed resolutions embodying the committee’s recommendations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:31 — “If a standing committee wishes on its own initiative to recommend action by the assembly on a matter within the committee’s concern, it is generally desirable, as stated above, for the report to consist of or conclude with one or more proposed resolutions embodying the committee’s recommendations.”

**284.** `[C1240 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a standing committee wishes on its own initiative to recommend action by the assembly on a matter within the committee’s concern, it is generally desirable, as stated above, for the report to consist of or conclude with one or more proposed _____ embodying the committee’s recommendations.
   A. resolutions
   B. Information
   C. convention
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:31 — “If a standing committee wishes on its own initiative to recommend action by the assembly on a matter within the committee’s concern, it is generally desirable, as stated above, for the report to consist of or conclude with one or more proposed resolutions embodying the committee’s recommendations.”

**285.** `[C1240 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the reporting member in a small assembly may sometimes give _____ explanations orally, it is usually better to submit a formal written report if it is to contain anything other than the resolutions themselves.

   *Answer:* accompanying — “Although the reporting member in a small assembly may sometimes give accompanying explanations orally, it is usually better to submit a formal written report if it is to contain anything other than the resolutions themselves.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:31 — “Although the reporting member in a small assembly may sometimes give accompanying explanations orally, it is usually better to submit a formal written report if it is to contain anything other than the resolutions themselves.”

**286.** `[C1241 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then states the question on the _____, and it is considered just as any other main motion.

   *Answer:* resolution — “The chair then states the question on the resolution, and it is considered just as any other main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:32 — “The chair then states the question on the resolution, and it is considered just as any other main motion.”

**287.** `[C1241 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then states the question on the _____, and it is considered just as any other main motion.
   A. recognized
   B. Incidental
   C. Substitute
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:32 — “The chair then states the question on the resolution, and it is considered just as any other main motion.”

**288.** `[C1242 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although it is not generally the best procedure, a report may sometimes contain _____ not in the form of motions or resolutions.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “Although it is not generally the best procedure, a report may sometimes contain recommendations not in the form of motions or resolutions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:33 — “Although it is not generally the best procedure, a report may sometimes contain recommendations not in the form of motions or resolutions.”

**289.** `[C1242 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A better method of treating a committee _____ that is not in the form of a resolution—in a case, for example, where the recommendation is to authorize a $2,000 expenditure for a personnel consultant’s fee—is to offer a motion like this: “In accordance with the recommendation in the committee’s report, I move that the expenditure of $2,000 for a personnel consultant’s fee be authorized.” The reporting member can make such a motion after reading the report, or another member can obtain the floor to do so.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. Announcements
   C. interrupted
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* D) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:33 — “A better method of treating a committee recommendation that is not in the form of a resolution—in a case, for example, where the recommendation is to authorize a $2,000 expenditure for a personnel consultant’s fee—is to offer a motion like this: “In accordance with the recommendation in the committee’s report, I move that the expenditure of $2,000 for a personnel consultant’s fee be authorized.” The reporting member can make such a motion after reading the report, or another member can obtain the floor to do so.”

**290.** `[C1242 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although it is not generally the best procedure, a report may sometimes contain _____ not in the form of motions or resolutions.
   A. Announcements
   B. recommendations
   C. adjournment
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* B) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:33 — “Although it is not generally the best procedure, a report may sometimes contain recommendations not in the form of motions or resolutions.”

**291.** `[C1242 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A better method of treating a committee _____ that is not in the form of a resolution—in a case, for example, where the recommendation is to authorize a $2,000 expenditure for a personnel consultant’s fee—is to offer a motion like this: “In accordance with the recommendation in the committee’s report, I move that the expenditure of $2,000 for a personnel consultant’s fee be authorized.” The reporting member can make such a motion after reading the report, or another member can obtain the floor to do so.

   *Answer:* recommendation — “A better method of treating a committee recommendation that is not in the form of a resolution—in a case, for example, where the recommendation is to authorize a $2,000 expenditure for a personnel consultant’s fee—is to offer a motion like this: “In accordance with the recommendation in the committee’s report, I move that the expenditure of $2,000 for a personnel consultant’s fee be authorized.” The reporting member can make such a motion after reading the report, or another member can obtain the floor to do so.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:33 — “A better method of treating a committee recommendation that is not in the form of a resolution—in a case, for example, where the recommendation is to authorize a $2,000 expenditure for a personnel consultant’s fee—is to offer a motion like this: “In accordance with the recommendation in the committee’s report, I move that the expenditure of $2,000 for a personnel consultant’s fee be authorized.” The reporting member can make such a motion after reading the report, or another member can obtain the floor to do so.”

**292.** `[C1243 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A Report on a Referred Subject on Which No Resolution _____.

   *Answer:* or Motion Was Pending — “A Report on a Referred Subject on Which No Resolution or Motion Was Pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:34 — “A Report on a Referred Subject on Which No Resolution or Motion Was Pending.”

**293.** `[C1243 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A Report on a Referred Subject on Which No _____ or Motion Was Pending.
   A. substitute
   B. Resolution
   C. convention
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) Resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:34 — “A Report on a Referred Subject on Which No Resolution or Motion Was Pending.”

**294.** `[C1243 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a subject on which no resolution or motion was pending has been referred to a committee for _____, the report usually should conclude with one or more resolutions, unless the committee recommends that no action be taken.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “If a subject on which no resolution or motion was pending has been referred to a committee for recommendations, the report usually should conclude with one or more resolutions, unless the committee recommends that no action be taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:34 — “If a subject on which no resolution or motion was pending has been referred to a committee for recommendations, the report usually should conclude with one or more resolutions, unless the committee recommends that no action be taken.”

**295.** `[C1243 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The committee’s report on the subject referred to it may be _____, for example, thus:
   A. presented
   B. Objection
   C. presiding
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* A) presented. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:34 — “The committee’s report on the subject referred to it may be presented, for example, thus:”

**296.** `[C1243 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a subject on which no resolution or motion was pending has been referred to a committee for _____, the report usually should conclude with one or more resolutions, unless the committee recommends that no action be taken.
   A. subsequently
   B. characteristics
   C. recommendations
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* C) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:34 — “If a subject on which no resolution or motion was pending has been referred to a committee for recommendations, the report usually should conclude with one or more resolutions, unless the committee recommends that no action be taken.”

**297.** `[C1244 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The resolution is treated as any other main question, just as in the preceding case dealing with the _____ of a standing committee.

   *Answer:* recommendation — “The resolution is treated as any other main question, just as in the preceding case dealing with the recommendation of a standing committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:35 — “The resolution is treated as any other main question, just as in the preceding case dealing with the recommendation of a standing committee.”

**298.** `[C1244 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ not in the form of resolutions are also handled as described in the preceding case.
   A. Recommendations
   B. deliberative
   C. characteristics
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) Recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:35 — “Recommendations not in the form of resolutions are also handled as described in the preceding case.”

**299.** `[C1244 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The resolution is treated as any other main question, just as in the preceding case dealing with the _____ of a standing committee.
   A. Organization
   B. specification
   C. recommendation
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:35 — “The resolution is treated as any other main question, just as in the preceding case dealing with the recommendation of a standing committee.”

**300.** `[C1244 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ not in the form of resolutions are also handled as described in the preceding case.

   *Answer:* Recommendations — “Recommendations not in the form of resolutions are also handled as described in the preceding case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:35 — “Recommendations not in the form of resolutions are also handled as described in the preceding case.”

**301.** `[C1245 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A Report on a Resolution Previously Referred to a _____.

   *Answer:* Committee — “A Report on a Resolution Previously Referred to a Committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:36 — “A Report on a Resolution Previously Referred to a Committee.”

**302.** `[C1245 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A Report on a _____ Previously Referred to a Committee.
   A. assemblies
   B. settlement
   C. Resolution
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) Resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:36 — “A Report on a Resolution Previously Referred to a Committee.”

**303.** `[C1245 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a committee reports on a resolution or other main question which was referred to it (13) or which the rules require to be considered by it before coming before the assembly with the committee’s _____, the form of the report and the type of action depends on the nature of the case, as follows:

   *Answer:* recommendation — “When a committee reports on a resolution or other main question which was referred to it (13) or which the rules require to be considered by it before coming before the assembly with the committee’s recommendation, the form of the report and the type of action depends on the nature of the case, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:36 — “When a committee reports on a resolution or other main question which was referred to it (13) or which the rules require to be considered by it before coming before the assembly with the committee’s recommendation, the form of the report and the type of action depends on the nature of the case, as follows:”

**304.** `[C1245 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee reports on a resolution or other main question which was referred to it (13) or which the rules require to be considered by it before coming before the assembly with the committee’s _____, the form of the report and the type of action depends on the nature of the case, as follows:
   A. immediately
   B. Information
   C. Announcements
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* D) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:36 — “When a committee reports on a resolution or other main question which was referred to it (13) or which the rules require to be considered by it before coming before the assembly with the committee’s recommendation, the form of the report and the type of action depends on the nature of the case, as follows:”

**305.** `[C1246 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Recommending adoption, or rejection, or (when a majority of the committee fail to agree) making no _____.

   *Answer:* recommendation — “Recommending adoption, or rejection, or (when a majority of the committee fail to agree) making no recommendation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:37 — “Recommending adoption, or rejection, or (when a majority of the committee fail to agree) making no recommendation.”

**306.** `[C1246 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Recommending adoption, or rejection, or (when a _____ of the committee fail to agree) making no recommendation.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:37 — “Recommending adoption, or rejection, or (when a majority of the committee fail to agree) making no recommendation.”

**307.** `[C1246 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a report in a small assembly can be given orally (provided that the _____ records it in the minutes);

   *Answer:* secretary — “Such a report in a small assembly can be given orally (provided that the secretary records it in the minutes);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:37 — “Such a report in a small assembly can be given orally (provided that the secretary records it in the minutes);”

**308.** `[C1246 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a report in a small assembly can be given orally (provided that the _____ records it in the minutes);
   A. secretary
   B. Crestwood
   C. expressly
   D. delegates

   *Answer:* A) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:37 — “Such a report in a small assembly can be given orally (provided that the secretary records it in the minutes);”

**309.** `[C1247 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ is too long to fit into the form given above, a form such as the following may be used:

   *Answer:* resolution — “If the resolution is too long to fit into the form given above, a form such as the following may be used:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:38 — “If the resolution is too long to fit into the form given above, a form such as the following may be used:”

**310.** `[C1247 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the _____ is too long to fit into the form given above, a form such as the following may be used:
   A. resolution
   B. incidental
   C. authorizes
   D. subsidiary

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:38 — “If the resolution is too long to fit into the form given above, a form such as the following may be used:”

**311.** `[C1248 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reporting member then hands to the chair or the secretary the copy of the _____ that was turned over to the committee.

   *Answer:* resolution — “The reporting member then hands to the chair or the secretary the copy of the resolution that was turned over to the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:39 — “The reporting member then hands to the chair or the secretary the copy of the resolution that was turned over to the committee.”

**312.** `[C1248 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The reporting member then hands to the chair or the secretary the copy of the _____ that was turned over to the committee.
   A. Unfinished
   B. resolution
   C. privileged
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:39 — “The reporting member then hands to the chair or the secretary the copy of the resolution that was turned over to the committee.”

**313.** `[C1249 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the presentation of the report is concluded in each of the above cases, the resolution or main question becomes pending _____ and the chair states the question accordingly, no motion being necessary.

   *Answer:* automatically — “When the presentation of the report is concluded in each of the above cases, the resolution or main question becomes pending automatically and the chair states the question accordingly, no motion being necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:40 — “When the presentation of the report is concluded in each of the above cases, the resolution or main question becomes pending automatically and the chair states the question accordingly, no motion being necessary.”

**314.** `[C1249 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee recommends adoption or makes no _____, the chair, as soon as the reporting member has resumed his seat, proceeds:
   A. Organization
   B. interrupted
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* D) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:40 — “If the committee recommends adoption or makes no recommendation, the chair, as soon as the reporting member has resumed his seat, proceeds:”

**315.** `[C1249 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the presentation of the report is concluded in each of the above cases, the resolution or main question becomes pending _____ and the chair states the question accordingly, no motion being necessary.
   A. Preparation
   B. representatives
   C. automatically
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:40 — “When the presentation of the report is concluded in each of the above cases, the resolution or main question becomes pending automatically and the chair states the question accordingly, no motion being necessary.”

**316.** `[C1249 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee recommends adoption or makes no _____, the chair, as soon as the reporting member has resumed his seat, proceeds:

   *Answer:* recommendation — “If the committee recommends adoption or makes no recommendation, the chair, as soon as the reporting member has resumed his seat, proceeds:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:40 — “If the committee recommends adoption or makes no recommendation, the chair, as soon as the reporting member has resumed his seat, proceeds:”

**317.** `[C1250 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The question is always stated and put on the _____ of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).

   *Answer:* adoption — “The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:41 — “The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).”

**318.** `[C1250 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of _____ is a vote in favor of it).
   A. after
   B. aye
   C. Refer
   D. amend

   *Answer:* B) aye. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:41 — “The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).”

**319.** `[C1250 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, when the committee’s _____ is in the negative, the chair states the question as follows:
   A. interrupted
   B. Organization
   C. President-Elect
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* D) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:41 — “Thus, when the committee’s recommendation is in the negative, the chair states the question as follows:”

**320.** `[C1250 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, when the committee’s _____ is in the negative, the chair states the question as follows:

   *Answer:* recommendation — “Thus, when the committee’s recommendation is in the negative, the chair states the question as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:41 — “Thus, when the committee’s recommendation is in the negative, the chair states the question as follows:”

**321.** `[C1251 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ action on a resolution and an amendment that were pending when referred.

   *Answer:* Recommending — “Recommending action on a resolution and an amendment that were pending when referred.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:42 — “Recommending action on a resolution and an amendment that were pending when referred.”

**322.** `[C1251 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Recommending action on a _____ and an amendment that were pending when referred.
   A. introduced
   B. recognized
   C. resolution
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:42 — “Recommending action on a resolution and an amendment that were pending when referred.”

**323.** `[C1251 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and it should first state the committee’s _____ as to the disposition of the amendment, then as to the disposition of the resolution.

   *Answer:* recommendation — “and it should first state the committee’s recommendation as to the disposition of the amendment, then as to the disposition of the resolution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:42 — “and it should first state the committee’s recommendation as to the disposition of the amendment, then as to the disposition of the resolution.”

**324.** `[C1251 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and it should first state the committee’s _____ as to the disposition of the amendment, then as to the disposition of the resolution.
   A. representatives
   B. characteristics
   C. recommendation
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* C) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:42 — “and it should first state the committee’s recommendation as to the disposition of the amendment, then as to the disposition of the resolution.”

**325.** `[C1252 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As in the preceding case, no motions are necessary, and the chair states the question first on the amendment, and after it is voted on, then on the _____.

   *Answer:* resolution — “As in the preceding case, no motions are necessary, and the chair states the question first on the amendment, and after it is voted on, then on the resolution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:43 — “As in the preceding case, no motions are necessary, and the chair states the question first on the amendment, and after it is voted on, then on the resolution.”

**326.** `[C1252 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The same _____ apply if a primary amendment and a secondary amendment were pending when the resolution was referred.
   A. recognized
   B. incidental
   C. resolution
   D. principles

   *Answer:* D) principles. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:43 — “The same principles apply if a primary amendment and a secondary amendment were pending when the resolution was referred.”

**327.** `[C1252 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The report states the committee’s _____ first on the secondary amendment, then on the primary amendment, and finally on the resolution;

   *Answer:* recommendations — “The report states the committee’s recommendations first on the secondary amendment, then on the primary amendment, and finally on the resolution;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:43 — “The report states the committee’s recommendations first on the secondary amendment, then on the primary amendment, and finally on the resolution;”

**328.** `[C1252 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As in the preceding case, no motions are necessary, and the chair states the question first on the amendment, and after it is voted on, then on the _____.
   A. settlement
   B. convention
   C. Particular
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:43 — “As in the preceding case, no motions are necessary, and the chair states the question first on the amendment, and after it is voted on, then on the resolution.”

**329.** `[C1252 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The same _____ apply if a primary amendment and a secondary amendment were pending when the resolution was referred.

   *Answer:* principles — “The same principles apply if a primary amendment and a secondary amendment were pending when the resolution was referred.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:43 — “The same principles apply if a primary amendment and a secondary amendment were pending when the resolution was referred.”

**330.** `[C1253 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to _____ (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:44 — “If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.”

**331.** `[C1253 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ definite or indefinite postponement.
   A. Particular
   B. resolutions
   C. Recommending
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) Recommending. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:44 — “Recommending definite or indefinite postponement.”

**332.** `[C1253 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it _____ is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.

   *Answer:* indefinitely — “If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:44 — “If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.”

**333.** `[C1253 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But whether or not such a motion was pending at the time of referral, the committee can report the main question with a _____ that it be postponed indefinitely or that it be postponed to a certain time (14).
   A. President-Elect
   B. adjournment
   C. recommendation
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* C) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:44 — “But whether or not such a motion was pending at the time of referral, the committee can report the main question with a recommendation that it be postponed indefinitely or that it be postponed to a certain time (14).”

**334.** `[C1253 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Recommending definite or indefinite _____.

   *Answer:* postponement — “Recommending definite or indefinite postponement.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:44 — “Recommending definite or indefinite postponement.”

**335.** `[C1254 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee reports back a _____ or paper with amendments that it proposes, the amendments, at least, should be in writing unless they are very simple.
   A. recognized
   B. privileged
   C. resolution
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* C) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:45 — “When a committee reports back a resolution or paper with amendments that it proposes, the amendments, at least, should be in writing unless they are very simple.”

**336.** `[C1254 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a small assembly, depending on the complexity of the case, the amendments can be written out on a separate sheet that is handed to the chair or the secretary at the conclusion of an oral _____, or a more formal written report can be submitted;

   *Answer:* presentation — “In a small assembly, depending on the complexity of the case, the amendments can be written out on a separate sheet that is handed to the chair or the secretary at the conclusion of an oral presentation, or a more formal written report can be submitted;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:45 — “In a small assembly, depending on the complexity of the case, the amendments can be written out on a separate sheet that is handed to the chair or the secretary at the conclusion of an oral presentation, or a more formal written report can be submitted;”

**337.** `[C1254 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: the latter _____ should be followed in a large body.
   A. secondary
   B. auditors’
   C. procedure
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* C) procedure. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:45 — “the latter procedure should be followed in a large body.”

**338.** `[C1254 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a committee reports back a _____ or paper with amendments that it proposes, the amendments, at least, should be in writing unless they are very simple.

   *Answer:* resolution — “When a committee reports back a resolution or paper with amendments that it proposes, the amendments, at least, should be in writing unless they are very simple.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:45 — “When a committee reports back a resolution or paper with amendments that it proposes, the amendments, at least, should be in writing unless they are very simple.”

**339.** `[C1254 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a small assembly, depending on the complexity of the case, the amendments can be written out on a separate sheet that is handed to the chair or the secretary at the conclusion of an oral _____, or a more formal written report can be submitted;
   A. presentation
   B. adjournment
   C. consideration
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:45 — “In a small assembly, depending on the complexity of the case, the amendments can be written out on a separate sheet that is handed to the chair or the secretary at the conclusion of an oral presentation, or a more formal written report can be submitted;”

**340.** `[C1255 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no amendment was pending at the time of referral, the reporting member at the conclusion of his _____ moves the adoption of the amendments proposed by the committee, making a single motion covering them all.

   *Answer:* presentation — “If no amendment was pending at the time of referral, the reporting member at the conclusion of his presentation moves the adoption of the amendments proposed by the committee, making a single motion covering them all.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:46 — “If no amendment was pending at the time of referral, the reporting member at the conclusion of his presentation moves the adoption of the amendments proposed by the committee, making a single motion covering them all.”

**341.** `[C1255 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But if an amendment was pending at the time of referral, the chair (unless the committee _____ a secondary amendment, whose adoption the reporting member would first move) states the question on the referred amendment first—the reporting member making no motion until after that amendment has been voted on.
   A. recommends
   B. recognized
   C. Definitely
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* A) recommends. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:46 — “But if an amendment was pending at the time of referral, the chair (unless the committee recommends a secondary amendment, whose adoption the reporting member would first move) states the question on the referred amendment first—the reporting member making no motion until after that amendment has been voted on.”

**342.** `[C1255 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If no amendment was pending at the time of referral, the reporting member at the conclusion of his _____ moves the adoption of the amendments proposed by the committee, making a single motion covering them all.
   A. Procedures
   B. Reconsider
   C. subsequently
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* D) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:46 — “If no amendment was pending at the time of referral, the reporting member at the conclusion of his presentation moves the adoption of the amendments proposed by the committee, making a single motion covering them all.”

**343.** `[C1255 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But if an amendment was pending at the time of referral, the chair (unless the committee _____ a secondary amendment, whose adoption the reporting member would first move) states the question on the referred amendment first—the reporting member making no motion until after that amendment has been voted on.

   *Answer:* recommends — “But if an amendment was pending at the time of referral, the chair (unless the committee recommends a secondary amendment, whose adoption the reporting member would first move) states the question on the referred amendment first—the reporting member making no motion until after that amendment has been voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:46 — “But if an amendment was pending at the time of referral, the chair (unless the committee recommends a secondary amendment, whose adoption the reporting member would first move) states the question on the referred amendment first—the reporting member making no motion until after that amendment has been voted on.”

**344.** `[C1257 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the chair states the question on the adoption of the _____ proposed by the committee, the procedure is by one of the following methods:

   *Answer:* amendments — “After the chair states the question on the adoption of the amendments proposed by the committee, the procedure is by one of the following methods:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:48 — “After the chair states the question on the adoption of the amendments proposed by the committee, the procedure is by one of the following methods:”

**345.** `[C1257 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the chair states the question on the adoption of the _____ proposed by the committee, the procedure is by one of the following methods:
   A. substitute
   B. resolution
   C. introduced
   D. amendments

   *Answer:* D) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:48 — “After the chair states the question on the adoption of the amendments proposed by the committee, the procedure is by one of the following methods:”

**346.** `[C1258 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a committee reports back a _____ with a substitute that it recommends for adoption, at least the substitute should be in writing, just as in the case of any other report proposing amendments.
   A. resolution
   B. Definitely
   C. Substitute
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:49 — “If a committee reports back a resolution with a substitute that it recommends for adoption, at least the substitute should be in writing, just as in the case of any other report proposing amendments.”

**347.** `[C1258 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a committee reports back a _____ with a substitute that it recommends for adoption, at least the substitute should be in writing, just as in the case of any other report proposing amendments.

   *Answer:* resolution — “If a committee reports back a resolution with a substitute that it recommends for adoption, at least the substitute should be in writing, just as in the case of any other report proposing amendments.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:49 — “If a committee reports back a resolution with a substitute that it recommends for adoption, at least the substitute should be in writing, just as in the case of any other report proposing amendments.”

**348.** `[C1259 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no amendment was pending when the resolution was referred, the reporting member concludes his _____ by making the motion to substitute;

   *Answer:* presentation — “If no amendment was pending when the resolution was referred, the reporting member concludes his presentation by making the motion to substitute;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:50 — “If no amendment was pending when the resolution was referred, the reporting member concludes his presentation by making the motion to substitute;”

**349.** `[C1259 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If no amendment was pending when the resolution was referred, the reporting member concludes his _____ by making the motion to substitute;
   A. constitution
   B. undebatable
   C. resolutions
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* D) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:50 — “If no amendment was pending when the resolution was referred, the reporting member concludes his presentation by making the motion to substitute;”

**350.** `[C1260 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by _____.

   *Answer:* adding — “but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by adding.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:51 — “but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by adding.”

**351.** `[C1260 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then states the question on the motion to _____.
   A. substitute
   B. Unfinished
   C. Reconsider
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* A) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:51 — “The chair then states the question on the motion to substitute.”

**352.** `[C1260 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But if amendment(s) were pending when the _____ was referred, then, before the motion to substitute can be made, the question is first on the secondary amendment if one was pending, then on the primary amendment that was pending.

   *Answer:* resolution — “But if amendment(s) were pending when the resolution was referred, then, before the motion to substitute can be made, the question is first on the secondary amendment if one was pending, then on the primary amendment that was pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:51 — “But if amendment(s) were pending when the resolution was referred, then, before the motion to substitute can be made, the question is first on the secondary amendment if one was pending, then on the primary amendment that was pending.”

**353.** `[C1260 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When these _____ have been voted on, the reporting member makes the motion to substitute and the chair states the question on it (or the chair can state the question assuming the motion).
   A. Unfinished
   B. incidental
   C. privileged
   D. amendments

   *Answer:* D) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:51 — “When these amendments have been voted on, the reporting member makes the motion to substitute and the chair states the question on it (or the chair can state the question assuming the motion).”

**354.** `[C1260 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus _____ can be amended only by adding.

   *Answer:* substituted — “but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by adding.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:51 — “but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by adding.”

**355.** `[C1262 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even if a report contains only an account of work done or a statement of fact or opinion for the assembly’s _____, it should be in writing.

   *Answer:* information — “Even if a report contains only an account of work done or a statement of fact or opinion for the assembly’s information, it should be in writing.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:53 — “Even if a report contains only an account of work done or a statement of fact or opinion for the assembly’s information, it should be in writing.”

**356.** `[C1262 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Apart from filing such a report, however, no action on it is _____ and usually none should be taken.
   A. exhausted
   B. debatable
   C. Secretary
   D. necessary

   *Answer:* D) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:53 — “Apart from filing such a report, however, no action on it is necessary and usually none should be taken.”

**357.** `[C1262 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even if a report contains only an account of work done or a statement of fact or opinion for the assembly’s _____, it should be in writing.
   A. Particular
   B. information
   C. undebatable
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:53 — “Even if a report contains only an account of work done or a statement of fact or opinion for the assembly’s information, it should be in writing.”

**358.** `[C1262 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Apart from filing such a report, however, no action on it is _____ and usually none should be taken.

   *Answer:* necessary — “Apart from filing such a report, however, no action on it is necessary and usually none should be taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:53 — “Apart from filing such a report, however, no action on it is necessary and usually none should be taken.”

**359.** `[C1263 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ and Nominating Committee Reports;

   *Answer:* Membership — “Membership and Nominating Committee Reports;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:54 — “Membership and Nominating Committee Reports;”

**360.** `[C1263 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a membership committee reports on names of persons referred to the committee as applicants for society membership, the report can be rendered orally, but a list of the names of the persons _____ for membership should be submitted in writing.
   A. Particular
   B. undebatable
   C. recommended
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) recommended. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:54 — “When a membership committee reports on names of persons referred to the committee as applicants for society membership, the report can be rendered orally, but a list of the names of the persons recommended for membership should be submitted in writing.”

**361.** `[C1263 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ and Nominating Committee Reports;
   A. resolution
   B. Membership
   C. Incidental
   D. convention

   *Answer:* B) Membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:54 — “Membership and Nominating Committee Reports;”

**362.** `[C1263 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a membership committee reports on names of persons referred to the committee as applicants for society membership, the report can be rendered orally, but a list of the names of the persons _____ for membership should be submitted in writing.

   *Answer:* recommended — “When a membership committee reports on names of persons referred to the committee as applicants for society membership, the report can be rendered orally, but a list of the names of the persons recommended for membership should be submitted in writing.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:54 — “When a membership committee reports on names of persons referred to the committee as applicants for society membership, the report can be rendered orally, but a list of the names of the persons recommended for membership should be submitted in writing.”

**363.** `[C1264 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The report of the _____ committee consists of a written list of candidates for office, just as in the case of the membership committee’s report.

   *Answer:* nominating — “The report of the nominating committee consists of a written list of candidates for office, just as in the case of the membership committee’s report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:55 — “The report of the nominating committee consists of a written list of candidates for office, just as in the case of the membership committee’s report.”

**364.** `[C1264 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The report of the _____ committee consists of a written list of candidates for office, just as in the case of the membership committee’s report.
   A. Convention
   B. privileged
   C. Permission
   D. nominating

   *Answer:* D) nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:55 — “The report of the nominating committee consists of a written list of candidates for office, just as in the case of the membership committee’s report.”

**365.** `[C1265 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For the reports of the three principal committees that perform _____ functions in the organization of a convention—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee—see 59.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “For the reports of the three principal committees that perform parliamentary functions in the organization of a convention—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee—see 59.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:58 — “For the reports of the three principal committees that perform parliamentary functions in the organization of a convention—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee—see 59.”

**366.** `[C1265 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For the reports of the three principal committees that perform _____ functions in the organization of a convention—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee—see 59.
   A. characteristics
   B. deliberative
   C. President-Elect
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:58 — “For the reports of the three principal committees that perform parliamentary functions in the organization of a convention—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee—see 59.”

**367.** `[C1266 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For the report of an _____ committee appointed under disciplinary procedures, see 63.

   *Answer:* investigating — “For the report of an investigating committee appointed under disciplinary procedures, see 63.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:59 — “For the report of an investigating committee appointed under disciplinary procedures, see 63.”

**368.** `[C1266 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For the report of an _____ committee appointed under disciplinary procedures, see 63.
   A. immediately
   B. representatives
   C. investigating
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* C) investigating. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:59 — “For the report of an investigating committee appointed under disciplinary procedures, see 63.”

**369.** `[C1267 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Summary of Types of Reports That Can Be _____ Orally in a Small Assembly.

   *Answer:* Rendered — “Summary of Types of Reports That Can Be Rendered Orally in a Small Assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:60 — “Summary of Types of Reports That Can Be Rendered Orally in a Small Assembly.”

**370.** `[C1267 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Summary of Types of Reports That Can Be _____ Orally in a Small Assembly.
   A. Nominees
   B. Rendered
   C. limiting
   D. decision

   *Answer:* B) Rendered. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:60 — “Summary of Types of Reports That Can Be Rendered Orally in a Small Assembly.”

**371.** `[C1268 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a subject that was not in the form of a pending resolution or motion was referred to a committee for _____, and if the committee in its report presents the recommendations in one or more resolutions offered with no comment, only the resolutions need be in writing and the statement that the committee recommends their adoption can be given orally.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “If a subject that was not in the form of a pending resolution or motion was referred to a committee for recommendations, and if the committee in its report presents the recommendations in one or more resolutions offered with no comment, only the resolutions need be in writing and the statement that the committee recommends their adoption can be given orally.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:62 — “If a subject that was not in the form of a pending resolution or motion was referred to a committee for recommendations, and if the committee in its report presents the recommendations in one or more resolutions offered with no comment, only the resolutions need be in writing and the statement that the committee recommends their adoption can be given orally.”

**372.** `[C1268 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a subject that was not in the form of a pending resolution or motion was referred to a committee for _____, and if the committee in its report presents the recommendations in one or more resolutions offered with no comment, only the resolutions need be in writing and the statement that the committee recommends their adoption can be given orally.
   A. recommendations
   B. Responsibilities
   C. adjournment
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:62 — “If a subject that was not in the form of a pending resolution or motion was referred to a committee for recommendations, and if the committee in its report presents the recommendations in one or more resolutions offered with no comment, only the resolutions need be in writing and the statement that the committee recommends their adoption can be given orally.”

**373.** `[C1269 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee can be discharged (36) by a _____ vote at the time at which it makes a partial report.

   *Answer:* majority — “A committee can be discharged (36) by a majority vote at the time at which it makes a partial report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:63 — “A committee can be discharged (36) by a majority vote at the time at which it makes a partial report.”

**374.** `[C1269 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ report of a committee is handled in the same way as the final report.
   A. carried
   B. Divided
   C. partial
   D. abstain

   *Answer:* C) partial. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:63 — “A partial report of a committee is handled in the same way as the final report.”

**375.** `[C1269 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is a progress report only, with no _____ or conclusions, it is treated as any other report for information only, and no action need be taken.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “If it is a progress report only, with no recommendations or conclusions, it is treated as any other report for information only, and no action need be taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:63 — “If it is a progress report only, with no recommendations or conclusions, it is treated as any other report for information only, and no action need be taken.”

**376.** `[C1269 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee can be _____ (36) by a majority vote at the time at which it makes a partial report.
   A. Substitute
   B. recognized
   C. Incidental
   D. discharged

   *Answer:* D) discharged. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:63 — “A committee can be discharged (36) by a majority vote at the time at which it makes a partial report.”

**377.** `[C1269 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ report of a committee is handled in the same way as the final report.

   *Answer:* partial — “A partial report of a committee is handled in the same way as the final report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:63 — “A partial report of a committee is handled in the same way as the final report.”

**378.** `[C1270 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Regardless of whether a minority report is submitted, however, the report adopted by vote of a _____ in the committee should always be referred to as “the committee report,” never “the majority report.”

   *Answer:* majority — “Regardless of whether a minority report is submitted, however, the report adopted by vote of a majority in the committee should always be referred to as “the committee report,” never “the majority report.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:64 — “Regardless of whether a minority report is submitted, however, the report adopted by vote of a majority in the committee should always be referred to as “the committee report,” never “the majority report.””

**379.** `[C1270 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Formal _____ of Minority View (“Minority Report”).
   A. privileged
   B. introduced
   C. subsidiary
   D. Expression

   *Answer:* D) Expression. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:64 — “Formal Expression of Minority View (“Minority Report”).”

**380.** `[C1270 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The formal _____ of a so-called “minority report”—that is, the presentation of an expression of views in the name of a group of committee members not concurring with the committee report—is usually allowed by the assembly when such permission is requested, as explained below.

   *Answer:* presentation — “The formal presentation of a so-called “minority report”—that is, the presentation of an expression of views in the name of a group of committee members not concurring with the committee report—is usually allowed by the assembly when such permission is requested, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:64 — “The formal presentation of a so-called “minority report”—that is, the presentation of an expression of views in the name of a group of committee members not concurring with the committee report—is usually allowed by the assembly when such permission is requested, as explained below.”

**381.** `[C1270 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of whether a minority report is submitted, however, the report adopted by vote of a majority in the committee should always be referred to as “the committee report,” never “the majority report.”
   A. Permission
   B. Convention
   C. Regardless
   D. committees

   *Answer:* C) Regardless. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:64 — “Regardless of whether a minority report is submitted, however, the report adopted by vote of a majority in the committee should always be referred to as “the committee report,” never “the majority report.””

**382.** `[C1270 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Formal _____ of Minority View (“Minority Report”).

   *Answer:* Expression — “Formal Expression of Minority View (“Minority Report”).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:64 — “Formal Expression of Minority View (“Minority Report”).”

**383.** `[C1271 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Nature of committee minority’s rights with respect to _____.

   *Answer:* reports — “Nature of committee minority’s rights with respect to reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:65 — “Nature of committee minority’s rights with respect to reports.”

**384.** `[C1271 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As indicated above, the formal presentation of a “minority report” is a privilege that the assembly may accord, not a matter of right—since the appointment of the committee implies that the assembly is primarily interested in the findings of the _____ of the committee’s members.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* A) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:65 — “As indicated above, the formal presentation of a “minority report” is a privilege that the assembly may accord, not a matter of right—since the appointment of the committee implies that the assembly is primarily interested in the findings of the majority of the committee’s members.”

**385.** `[C1271 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No one can make allusion in the assembly to what has occurred during the deliberations of the committee, however, unless it is by report of the committee or by _____ consent.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “No one can make allusion in the assembly to what has occurred during the deliberations of the committee, however, unless it is by report of the committee or by unanimous consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:65 — “No one can make allusion in the assembly to what has occurred during the deliberations of the committee, however, unless it is by report of the committee or by unanimous consent.”

**386.** `[C1271 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Nature of committee _____’s rights with respect to reports.
   A. minority
   B. dilatory
   C. Approved
   D. Standard

   *Answer:* A) minority. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:65 — “Nature of committee minority’s rights with respect to reports.”

**387.** `[C1271 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As indicated above, the formal _____ of a “minority report” is a privilege that the assembly may accord, not a matter of right—since the appointment of the committee implies that the assembly is primarily interested in the findings of the majority of the committee’s members.

   *Answer:* presentation — “As indicated above, the formal presentation of a “minority report” is a privilege that the assembly may accord, not a matter of right—since the appointment of the committee implies that the assembly is primarily interested in the findings of the majority of the committee’s members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:65 — “As indicated above, the formal presentation of a “minority report” is a privilege that the assembly may accord, not a matter of right—since the appointment of the committee implies that the assembly is primarily interested in the findings of the majority of the committee’s members.”

**388.** `[C1272 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A “_____ report” in writing may begin:

   *Answer:* minority — “A “minority report” in writing may begin:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:66 — “A “minority report” in writing may begin:”

**389.** `[C1272 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A “_____ report” in writing may begin:
   A. Resolved
   B. assembly
   C. minority
   D. Division

   *Answer:* C) minority. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:66 — “A “minority report” in writing may begin:”

**390.** `[C1273 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee report concludes with a proposed _____, the minority can (a) recommend rejection of the resolution;

   *Answer:* resolution — “If the committee report concludes with a proposed resolution, the minority can (a) recommend rejection of the resolution;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:67 — “If the committee report concludes with a proposed resolution, the minority can (a) recommend rejection of the resolution;”

**391.** `[C1273 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or (c) recommend adoption of some other suitable motion designed to dispose of the resolution _____.
   A. presentation
   B. representatives
   C. appropriately
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) appropriately. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:67 — “or (c) recommend adoption of some other suitable motion designed to dispose of the resolution appropriately.”

**392.** `[C1273 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee report concludes with a proposed _____, the minority can (a) recommend rejection of the resolution;
   A. introduced
   B. Permission
   C. resolution
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* C) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:67 — “If the committee report concludes with a proposed resolution, the minority can (a) recommend rejection of the resolution;”

**393.** `[C1273 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (c) recommend adoption of some other suitable motion designed to dispose of the resolution _____.

   *Answer:* appropriately — “or (c) recommend adoption of some other suitable motion designed to dispose of the resolution appropriately.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:67 — “or (c) recommend adoption of some other suitable motion designed to dispose of the resolution appropriately.”

**394.** `[C1274 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee report is for _____ only, the views of the minority may be similarly constructed or may conclude with a motion.

   *Answer:* information — “If the committee report is for information only, the views of the minority may be similarly constructed or may conclude with a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:68 — “If the committee report is for information only, the views of the minority may be similarly constructed or may conclude with a motion.”

**395.** `[C1274 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee report is for _____ only, the views of the minority may be similarly constructed or may conclude with a motion.
   A. requirement
   B. information
   C. Definitely
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:68 — “If the committee report is for information only, the views of the minority may be similarly constructed or may conclude with a motion.”

**396.** `[C1275 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ vote is required to receive a minority report;
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A three-fourths vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* D) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:69 — “A majority vote is required to receive a minority report;”

**397.** `[C1275 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the minority of a committee wishes to make a formal _____ of its views, it is customary, unless the assembly refuses permission, to receive its report immediately after the report of the committee.

   *Answer:* presentation — “When the minority of a committee wishes to make a formal presentation of its views, it is customary, unless the assembly refuses permission, to receive its report immediately after the report of the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:69 — “When the minority of a committee wishes to make a formal presentation of its views, it is customary, unless the assembly refuses permission, to receive its report immediately after the report of the committee.”

**398.** `[C1275 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, the member _____ the committee report can properly notify the assembly that the minority wishes to submit its views in a separate report.
   A. substitute
   B. presenting
   C. Permission
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) presenting. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:69 — “In such a case, the member presenting the committee report can properly notify the assembly that the minority wishes to submit its views in a separate report.”

**399.** `[C1275 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ vote is required to receive a minority report;

   *Answer:* majority — “A majority vote is required to receive a minority report;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:69 — “A majority vote is required to receive a minority report;”

**400.** `[C1275 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the minority of a committee wishes to make a formal _____ of its views, it is customary, unless the assembly refuses permission, to receive its report immediately after the report of the committee.
   A. presentation
   B. adjournment
   C. consideration
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:69 — “When the minority of a committee wishes to make a formal presentation of its views, it is customary, unless the assembly refuses permission, to receive its report immediately after the report of the committee.”

**401.** `[C1276 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the minority report is presented, it is for _____, and it cannot be acted upon except by a motion to substitute it for the report of the committee.

   *Answer:* information — “When the minority report is presented, it is for information, and it cannot be acted upon except by a motion to substitute it for the report of the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:70 — “When the minority report is presented, it is for information, and it cannot be acted upon except by a motion to substitute it for the report of the committee.”

**402.** `[C1276 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whether the views of the minority are formally presented or not, however, any member can move that resolutions proposed by the committee be amended, or that they be postponed _____, or that some other appropriate action be taken.
   A. indefinitely
   B. Preparation
   C. constitution
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:70 — “Whether the views of the minority are formally presented or not, however, any member can move that resolutions proposed by the committee be amended, or that they be postponed indefinitely, or that some other appropriate action be taken.”

**403.** `[C1276 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the minority report is presented, it is for _____, and it cannot be acted upon except by a motion to substitute it for the report of the committee.
   A. deliberative
   B. Permission
   C. authorizes
   D. information

   *Answer:* D) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:70 — “When the minority report is presented, it is for information, and it cannot be acted upon except by a motion to substitute it for the report of the committee.”

**404.** `[C1276 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whether the views of the minority are formally presented or not, however, any member can move that resolutions proposed by the committee be amended, or that they be postponed _____, or that some other appropriate action be taken.

   *Answer:* indefinitely — “Whether the views of the minority are formally presented or not, however, any member can move that resolutions proposed by the committee be amended, or that they be postponed indefinitely, or that some other appropriate action be taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:70 — “Whether the views of the minority are formally presented or not, however, any member can move that resolutions proposed by the committee be amended, or that they be postponed indefinitely, or that some other appropriate action be taken.”

**405.** `[C1277 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Indicating Agreement with a Report Except in a Specified _____.

   *Answer:* Particular — “Indicating Agreement with a Report Except in a Specified Particular.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:71 — “Indicating Agreement with a Report Except in a Specified Particular.”

**406.** `[C1277 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Agreement with a Report Except in a Specified Particular.
   A. Definitely
   B. Permission
   C. assemblies
   D. Indicating

   *Answer:* D) Indicating. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:71 — “Indicating Agreement with a Report Except in a Specified Particular.”

**407.** `[C1277 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a written report of a committee is signed by all who concur and a committee member is in agreement with the report except in one particular, he can, after all who agree to the report have signed, add a statement that he concurs with the report except the part that he specifies, and then sign the statement—regardless of whether a minority report is to be _____.

   *Answer:* submitted — “If a written report of a committee is signed by all who concur and a committee member is in agreement with the report except in one particular, he can, after all who agree to the report have signed, add a statement that he concurs with the report except the part that he specifies, and then sign the statement—regardless of whether a minority report is to be submitted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:71 — “If a written report of a committee is signed by all who concur and a committee member is in agreement with the report except in one particular, he can, after all who agree to the report have signed, add a statement that he concurs with the report except the part that he specifies, and then sign the statement—regardless of whether a minority report is to be submitted.”

**408.** `[C1277 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a written report of a committee is signed by all who concur and a committee member is in agreement with the report except in one particular, he can, after all who agree to the report have signed, add a statement that he concurs with the report except the part that he specifies, and then sign the statement—regardless of whether a minority report is to be _____.
   A. submitted
   B. Privilege
   C. Crestwood
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* A) submitted. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:71 — “If a written report of a committee is signed by all who concur and a committee member is in agreement with the report except in one particular, he can, after all who agree to the report have signed, add a statement that he concurs with the report except the part that he specifies, and then sign the statement—regardless of whether a minority report is to be submitted.”

**409.** `[C1278 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.

   *Answer:* committee of the whole — “The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:1 — “The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.”

**410.** `[C1278 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or _____ committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.
   A. consideration as if in
   B. incidental main motion
   C. ARTICLE VII: COMMITTEES
   D. of Order Newly Revised

   *Answer:* A) consideration as if in. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:1 — “The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.”

**411.** `[C1278 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under each of these _____, any member can speak in debate on the main question or any amendment—for the same length of time as allowed by the assembly’s rules—as often as he is able to get the floor.

   *Answer:* procedures — “Under each of these procedures, any member can speak in debate on the main question or any amendment—for the same length of time as allowed by the assembly’s rules—as often as he is able to get the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:1 — “Under each of these procedures, any member can speak in debate on the main question or any amendment—for the same length of time as allowed by the assembly’s rules—as often as he is able to get the floor.”

**412.** `[C1278 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As under the _____ rules of debate, however, he cannot speak another time on the same question so long as a member who has not spoken on it is seeking the floor.
   A. Pending
   B. regular
   C. advance
   D. Meeting

   *Answer:* B) regular. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:1 — “As under the regular rules of debate, however, he cannot speak another time on the same question so long as a member who has not spoken on it is seeking the floor.”
