**413.** `[C1278 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under each of these _____, any member can speak in debate on the main question or any amendment—for the same length of time as allowed by the assembly’s rules—as often as he is able to get the floor.
   A. Permission
   B. committees
   C. incidental
   D. procedures

   *Answer:* D) procedures. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:1 — “Under each of these procedures, any member can speak in debate on the main question or any amendment—for the same length of time as allowed by the assembly’s rules—as often as he is able to get the floor.”

**414.** `[C1279 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each of these three devices is best suited to assemblies of a particular range in size and provides a different degree of protection against _____ and its possible consequences—which are risked when each member is allowed to speak an unlimited number of times in debate, such risk increasing in proportion to the size of the assembly.

   *Answer:* disorderliness — “Each of these three devices is best suited to assemblies of a particular range in size and provides a different degree of protection against disorderliness and its possible consequences—which are risked when each member is allowed to speak an unlimited number of times in debate, such risk increasing in proportion to the size of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:2 — “Each of these three devices is best suited to assemblies of a particular range in size and provides a different degree of protection against disorderliness and its possible consequences—which are risked when each member is allowed to speak an unlimited number of times in debate, such risk increasing in proportion to the size of the assembly.”

**415.** `[C1279 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: With respect to this type of protection, the essential _____ between the three procedures may be summarized as follows:
   A. specification
   B. Incidental
   C. consideration
   D. distinctions

   *Answer:* D) distinctions. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:2 — “With respect to this type of protection, the essential distinctions between the three procedures may be summarized as follows:”

**416.** `[C1279 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each of these three devices is best suited to assemblies of a particular range in size and provides a different degree of protection against _____ and its possible consequences—which are risked when each member is allowed to speak an unlimited number of times in debate, such risk increasing in proportion to the size of the assembly.
   A. Responsibilities
   B. Preparation
   C. disorderliness
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* C) disorderliness. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:2 — “Each of these three devices is best suited to assemblies of a particular range in size and provides a different degree of protection against disorderliness and its possible consequences—which are risked when each member is allowed to speak an unlimited number of times in debate, such risk increasing in proportion to the size of the assembly.”

**417.** `[C1279 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: With respect to this type of protection, the essential _____ between the three procedures may be summarized as follows:

   *Answer:* distinctions — “With respect to this type of protection, the essential distinctions between the three procedures may be summarized as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:2 — “With respect to this type of protection, the essential distinctions between the three procedures may be summarized as follows:”

**418.** `[C1280 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The complete rules governing committees of the whole, proceedings in quasi committee of the whole, and informal _____ are given below.

   *Answer:* consideration — “The complete rules governing committees of the whole, proceedings in quasi committee of the whole, and informal consideration are given below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:3 — “The complete rules governing committees of the whole, proceedings in quasi committee of the whole, and informal consideration are given below.”

**419.** `[C1280 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The complete rules governing committees of the whole, proceedings in quasi committee of the whole, and informal _____ are given below.
   A. President-Elect
   B. consideration
   C. representatives
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:3 — “The complete rules governing committees of the whole, proceedings in quasi committee of the whole, and informal consideration are given below.”

**420.** `[C1281 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the committee of the whole is not used _____ except in legislative bodies, it is the oldest of the three devices described above and is the prototype from which the other two are derived.

   *Answer:* extensively — “Although the committee of the whole is not used extensively except in legislative bodies, it is the oldest of the three devices described above and is the prototype from which the other two are derived.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:4 — “Although the committee of the whole is not used extensively except in legislative bodies, it is the oldest of the three devices described above and is the prototype from which the other two are derived.”

**421.** `[C1281 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unlike the processes of quasi committee of the whole and informal _____, a committee of the whole is a real committee in the parliamentary sense.
   A. president-elect
   B. specification
   C. recommendation
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:4 — “Unlike the processes of quasi committee of the whole and informal consideration, a committee of the whole is a real committee in the parliamentary sense.”

**422.** `[C1281 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Therefore, during the time that a meeting is “in committee of the whole,” even though the committee consists of the entire body of members in attendance at the assembly’s meeting, it is _____ not “the assembly.”

   *Answer:* technically — “Therefore, during the time that a meeting is “in committee of the whole,” even though the committee consists of the entire body of members in attendance at the assembly’s meeting, it is technically not “the assembly.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:4 — “Therefore, during the time that a meeting is “in committee of the whole,” even though the committee consists of the entire body of members in attendance at the assembly’s meeting, it is technically not “the assembly.””

**423.** `[C1281 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although the committee of the whole is not used _____ except in legislative bodies, it is the oldest of the three devices described above and is the prototype from which the other two are derived.
   A. convention
   B. adjournment
   C. Indefinitely
   D. extensively

   *Answer:* D) extensively. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:4 — “Although the committee of the whole is not used extensively except in legislative bodies, it is the oldest of the three devices described above and is the prototype from which the other two are derived.”

**424.** `[C1281 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike the processes of quasi committee of the whole and informal _____, a committee of the whole is a real committee in the parliamentary sense.

   *Answer:* consideration — “Unlike the processes of quasi committee of the whole and informal consideration, a committee of the whole is a real committee in the parliamentary sense.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:4 — “Unlike the processes of quasi committee of the whole and informal consideration, a committee of the whole is a real committee in the parliamentary sense.”

**425.** `[C1282 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ steps in making use of a committee of the whole are essentially the same as those involved in referring a subject to an ordinary committee.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The parliamentary steps in making use of a committee of the whole are essentially the same as those involved in referring a subject to an ordinary committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:5 — “The parliamentary steps in making use of a committee of the whole are essentially the same as those involved in referring a subject to an ordinary committee.”

**426.** `[C1282 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The assembly votes to go into a committee of the whole (which is _____ to voting to refer the matter to the committee), and a chairman of the committee is appointed.
   A. equivalent
   B. Incidental
   C. Permission
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) equivalent. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:5 — “The assembly votes to go into a committee of the whole (which is equivalent to voting to refer the matter to the committee), and a chairman of the committee is appointed.”

**427.** `[C1282 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The committee considers the referred matter, adopts a report to be made to the assembly, then votes to “rise and report.” Finally, the committee chairman presents the report and the assembly considers the committee’s _____—all as in the case of an ordinary committee.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “The committee considers the referred matter, adopts a report to be made to the assembly, then votes to “rise and report.” Finally, the committee chairman presents the report and the assembly considers the committee’s recommendations—all as in the case of an ordinary committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:5 — “The committee considers the referred matter, adopts a report to be made to the assembly, then votes to “rise and report.” Finally, the committee chairman presents the report and the assembly considers the committee’s recommendations—all as in the case of an ordinary committee.”

**428.** `[C1282 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ steps in making use of a committee of the whole are essentially the same as those involved in referring a subject to an ordinary committee.
   A. representatives
   B. undebatable
   C. President-Elect
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:5 — “The parliamentary steps in making use of a committee of the whole are essentially the same as those involved in referring a subject to an ordinary committee.”

**429.** `[C1282 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The assembly votes to go into a committee of the whole (which is _____ to voting to refer the matter to the committee), and a chairman of the committee is appointed.

   *Answer:* equivalent — “The assembly votes to go into a committee of the whole (which is equivalent to voting to refer the matter to the committee), and a chairman of the committee is appointed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:5 — “The assembly votes to go into a committee of the whole (which is equivalent to voting to refer the matter to the committee), and a chairman of the committee is appointed.”

**430.** `[C1283 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As _____ above, the motion to go into a committee of the whole is a form of the motion to commit (13).

   *Answer:* implied — “As implied above, the motion to go into a committee of the whole is a form of the motion to commit (13).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:6 — “As implied above, the motion to go into a committee of the whole is a form of the motion to commit (13).”

**431.** `[C1283 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As _____ above, the motion to go into a committee of the whole is a form of the motion to commit (13).
   A. approve
   B. Ordered
   C. implied
   D. carried

   *Answer:* C) implied. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:6 — “As implied above, the motion to go into a committee of the whole is a form of the motion to commit (13).”

**432.** `[C1284 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion of referral to the committee of the whole is adopted, the presiding officer immediately calls another member to the chair—frequently, but not necessarily, the _____—and takes his place as a member of the committee.

   *Answer:* vicepresident — “If the motion of referral to the committee of the whole is adopted, the presiding officer immediately calls another member to the chair—frequently, but not necessarily, the vicepresident—and takes his place as a member of the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:7 — “If the motion of referral to the committee of the whole is adopted, the presiding officer immediately calls another member to the chair—frequently, but not necessarily, the vicepresident—and takes his place as a member of the committee.”

**433.** `[C1284 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In large _____, the secretary may also leave his seat, the committee chairman may preside from that position, and an assistant secretary may act as secretary of the committee.
   A. recognized
   B. assemblies
   C. subsidiary
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) assemblies. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:7 — “In large assemblies, the secretary may also leave his seat, the committee chairman may preside from that position, and an assistant secretary may act as secretary of the committee.”

**434.** `[C1284 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For the committee’s use until it reports, its secretary keeps a temporary memorandum of the business it transacts, but the committee’s _____ are not entered in the minutes of the assembly.

   *Answer:* proceedings — “For the committee’s use until it reports, its secretary keeps a temporary memorandum of the business it transacts, but the committee’s proceedings are not entered in the minutes of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:7 — “For the committee’s use until it reports, its secretary keeps a temporary memorandum of the business it transacts, but the committee’s proceedings are not entered in the minutes of the assembly.”

**435.** `[C1284 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion of _____ to the committee of the whole is adopted, the presiding officer immediately calls another member to the chair—frequently, but not necessarily, the vicepresident—and takes his place as a member of the committee.
   A. Nominees
   B. referral
   C. Postpone
   D. Standing

   *Answer:* B) referral. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:7 — “If the motion of referral to the committee of the whole is adopted, the presiding officer immediately calls another member to the chair—frequently, but not necessarily, the vicepresident—and takes his place as a member of the committee.”

**436.** `[C1284 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In large _____, the secretary may also leave his seat, the committee chairman may preside from that position, and an assistant secretary may act as secretary of the committee.

   *Answer:* assemblies — “In large assemblies, the secretary may also leave his seat, the committee chairman may preside from that position, and an assistant secretary may act as secretary of the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:7 — “In large assemblies, the secretary may also leave his seat, the committee chairman may preside from that position, and an assistant secretary may act as secretary of the committee.”

**437.** `[C1285 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Amendments to a resolution _____ are in order, however;
   A. Reports of Special Committees
   B. immediately pending question
   C. originating in the committee
   D. Reports of Executive Officers

   *Answer:* C) originating in the committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:8 — “Amendments to a resolution originating in the committee are in order, however;”

**438.** `[C1285 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Like ordinary _____, a committee of the whole cannot alter the text of any resolution referred to it;

   *Answer:* committees — “Like ordinary committees, a committee of the whole cannot alter the text of any resolution referred to it;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:8 — “Like ordinary committees, a committee of the whole cannot alter the text of any resolution referred to it;”

**439.** `[C1285 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but it can propose amendments, which it must report in the form of _____ to the assembly.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. recommendations
   C. subsequently
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* B) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:8 — “but it can propose amendments, which it must report in the form of recommendations to the assembly.”

**440.** `[C1285 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Amendments to a resolution _____ in the committee are in order, however;

   *Answer:* originating — “Amendments to a resolution originating in the committee are in order, however;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:8 — “Amendments to a resolution originating in the committee are in order, however;”

**441.** `[C1285 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and if such amendments are adopted within the committee, they are _____ in the resolution before it is reported to the assembly for action.
   A. Organization
   B. interrupted
   C. subsequently
   D. incorporated

   *Answer:* D) incorporated. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:8 — “and if such amendments are adopted within the committee, they are incorporated in the resolution before it is reported to the assembly for action.”

**442.** `[C1286 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee of the whole has completed its _____ of the matter referred to it, or when it wishes to bring the meeting to an end, or wishes the assembly to take any other action requiring the adoption of a motion which is not in order in the committee, the committee rises and reports.
   A. subsequently
   B. president-elect
   C. Announcements
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:11 — “When a committee of the whole has completed its consideration of the matter referred to it, or when it wishes to bring the meeting to an end, or wishes the assembly to take any other action requiring the adoption of a motion which is not in order in the committee, the committee rises and reports.”

**443.** `[C1286 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a committee of the whole has completed its _____ of the matter referred to it, or when it wishes to bring the meeting to an end, or wishes the assembly to take any other action requiring the adoption of a motion which is not in order in the committee, the committee rises and reports.

   *Answer:* consideration — “When a committee of the whole has completed its consideration of the matter referred to it, or when it wishes to bring the meeting to an end, or wishes the assembly to take any other action requiring the adoption of a motion which is not in order in the committee, the committee rises and reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:11 — “When a committee of the whole has completed its consideration of the matter referred to it, or when it wishes to bring the meeting to an end, or wishes the assembly to take any other action requiring the adoption of a motion which is not in order in the committee, the committee rises and reports.”

**444.** `[C1287 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On a motion, or by _____ consent, the committee rises and the presiding officer of the assembly resumes the chair.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “On a motion, or by unanimous consent, the committee rises and the presiding officer of the assembly resumes the chair.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:12 — “On a motion, or by unanimous consent, the committee rises and the presiding officer of the assembly resumes the chair.”

**445.** `[C1287 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee _____ a resolution, it concludes by voting to report the resolution, as perfected.
   A. Definitely
   B. landscaped
   C. convention
   D. originates

   *Answer:* D) originates. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:12 — “If the committee originates a resolution, it concludes by voting to report the resolution, as perfected.”

**446.** `[C1287 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a resolution was referred to the committee, however, it votes only on any amendments that it will recommend, not on the resolution, which it reports back to the assembly with the _____ amendments.

   *Answer:* recommended — “If a resolution was referred to the committee, however, it votes only on any amendments that it will recommend, not on the resolution, which it reports back to the assembly with the recommended amendments.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:12 — “If a resolution was referred to the committee, however, it votes only on any amendments that it will recommend, not on the resolution, which it reports back to the assembly with the recommended amendments.”

**447.** `[C1287 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On a motion, or by unanimous consent, the committee rises and the _____ officer of the assembly resumes the chair.
   A. Precedent
   B. amendment
   C. Reporting
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* D) presiding. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:12 — “On a motion, or by unanimous consent, the committee rises and the presiding officer of the assembly resumes the chair.”

**448.** `[C1287 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee _____ a resolution, it concludes by voting to report the resolution, as perfected.

   *Answer:* originates — “If the committee originates a resolution, it concludes by voting to report the resolution, as perfected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:12 — “If the committee originates a resolution, it concludes by voting to report the resolution, as perfected.”

**449.** `[C1288 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If no _____ are reported, the chair states the question on the resolution that was referred to the committee or that it recommends for adoption;
   A. sustaining
   B. incidental
   C. amendments
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* C) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:13 — “If no amendments are reported, the chair states the question on the resolution that was referred to the committee or that it recommends for adoption;”

**450.** `[C1288 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and this question is then open to debate and _____ in the assembly.

   *Answer:* amendment — “and this question is then open to debate and amendment in the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:13 — “and this question is then open to debate and amendment in the assembly.”

**451.** `[C1288 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no _____ are reported, the chair states the question on the resolution that was referred to the committee or that it recommends for adoption;

   *Answer:* amendments — “If no amendments are reported, the chair states the question on the resolution that was referred to the committee or that it recommends for adoption;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:13 — “If no amendments are reported, the chair states the question on the resolution that was referred to the committee or that it recommends for adoption;”

**452.** `[C1288 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and this question is then open to debate and _____ in the assembly.
   A. Signature
   B. expressly
   C. Precedent
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* D) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:13 — “and this question is then open to debate and amendment in the assembly.”

**453.** `[C1289 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee, to facilitate completion of its work, wishes the assembly to take an action outside the committee’s powers that requires the adoption of an _____ motion—for example, to limit debate in the committee—a motion to rise should be made in a form like the following:

   *Answer:* undebatable — “If the committee, to facilitate completion of its work, wishes the assembly to take an action outside the committee’s powers that requires the adoption of an undebatable motion—for example, to limit debate in the committee—a motion to rise should be made in a form like the following:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:14 — “If the committee, to facilitate completion of its work, wishes the assembly to take an action outside the committee’s powers that requires the adoption of an undebatable motion—for example, to limit debate in the committee—a motion to rise should be made in a form like the following:”

**454.** `[C1289 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee, to facilitate completion of its work, wishes the assembly to take an action outside the committee’s powers that requires the adoption of an _____ motion—for example, to limit debate in the committee—a motion to rise should be made in a form like the following:
   A. resolutions
   B. undebatable
   C. Unfinished
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) undebatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:14 — “If the committee, to facilitate completion of its work, wishes the assembly to take an action outside the committee’s powers that requires the adoption of an undebatable motion—for example, to limit debate in the committee—a motion to rise should be made in a form like the following:”

**455.** `[C1290 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee of the whole can also rise, before completing its work, to request _____, in which case the nature or wording of the request needs to be agreed to before the motion to rise is made.

   *Answer:* instructions — “A committee of the whole can also rise, before completing its work, to request instructions, in which case the nature or wording of the request needs to be agreed to before the motion to rise is made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:15 — “A committee of the whole can also rise, before completing its work, to request instructions, in which case the nature or wording of the request needs to be agreed to before the motion to rise is made.”

**456.** `[C1290 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee of the whole can also rise, before completing its work, to request _____, in which case the nature or wording of the request needs to be agreed to before the motion to rise is made.
   A. consideration
   B. Definitely
   C. Particular
   D. instructions

   *Answer:* D) instructions. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:15 — “A committee of the whole can also rise, before completing its work, to request instructions, in which case the nature or wording of the request needs to be agreed to before the motion to rise is made.”

**457.** `[C1291 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee wishes to bring its _____ to an end because it believes the matter can be better handled under the assembly’s rules, or because it wishes the meeting to be adjourned, the motion to rise can be made in this form:

   *Answer:* proceedings — “If the committee wishes to bring its proceedings to an end because it believes the matter can be better handled under the assembly’s rules, or because it wishes the meeting to be adjourned, the motion to rise can be made in this form:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:16 — “If the committee wishes to bring its proceedings to an end because it believes the matter can be better handled under the assembly’s rules, or because it wishes the meeting to be adjourned, the motion to rise can be made in this form:”

**458.** `[C1291 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee wishes to bring its _____ to an end because it believes the matter can be better handled under the assembly’s rules, or because it wishes the meeting to be adjourned, the motion to rise can be made in this form:
   A. Organization
   B. proceedings
   C. constitution
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) proceedings. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:16 — “If the committee wishes to bring its proceedings to an end because it believes the matter can be better handled under the assembly’s rules, or because it wishes the meeting to be adjourned, the motion to rise can be made in this form:”

**459.** `[C1292 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an hour for _____ has been preset in the assembly and it arrives while the meeting is in committee of the whole, the committee chairman announces, “The hour for adjournment of the assembly has arrived and the committee will rise.” The committee chairman then reports that the committee has come to no conclusion on the referred matter, as described in the preceding paragraph.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “If an hour for adjournment has been preset in the assembly and it arrives while the meeting is in committee of the whole, the committee chairman announces, “The hour for adjournment of the assembly has arrived and the committee will rise.” The committee chairman then reports that the committee has come to no conclusion on the referred matter, as described in the preceding paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:17 — “If an hour for adjournment has been preset in the assembly and it arrives while the meeting is in committee of the whole, the committee chairman announces, “The hour for adjournment of the assembly has arrived and the committee will rise.” The committee chairman then reports that the committee has come to no conclusion on the referred matter, as described in the preceding paragraph.”

**460.** `[C1292 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an hour for _____ has been preset in the assembly and it arrives while the meeting is in committee of the whole, the committee chairman announces, “The hour for adjournment of the assembly has arrived and the committee will rise.” The committee chairman then reports that the committee has come to no conclusion on the referred matter, as described in the preceding paragraph.
   A. Substitute
   B. adjournment
   C. requirement
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:17 — “If an hour for adjournment has been preset in the assembly and it arrives while the meeting is in committee of the whole, the committee chairman announces, “The hour for adjournment of the assembly has arrived and the committee will rise.” The committee chairman then reports that the committee has come to no conclusion on the referred matter, as described in the preceding paragraph.”

**461.** `[C1293 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The quorum of a committee of the whole is the same as that of the assembly unless the bylaws provide, or the assembly _____, a different quorum for the committee.

   *Answer:* establishes — “The quorum of a committee of the whole is the same as that of the assembly unless the bylaws provide, or the assembly establishes, a different quorum for the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:18 — “The quorum of a committee of the whole is the same as that of the assembly unless the bylaws provide, or the assembly establishes, a different quorum for the committee.”

**462.** `[C1293 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the bylaws do not provide a different quorum for the committee, the assembly can establish one in the _____ case before going into committee of the whole, regardless of the quorum that the bylaws require for the assembly.
   A. substitute
   B. settlement
   C. committees
   D. particular

   *Answer:* D) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:18 — “If the bylaws do not provide a different quorum for the committee, the assembly can establish one in the particular case before going into committee of the whole, regardless of the quorum that the bylaws require for the assembly.”

**463.** `[C1293 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The quorum of a committee of the whole is the same as that of the assembly unless the bylaws provide, or the assembly _____, a different quorum for the committee.
   A. Organization
   B. Procedures
   C. Preparation
   D. establishes

   *Answer:* D) establishes. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:18 — “The quorum of a committee of the whole is the same as that of the assembly unless the bylaws provide, or the assembly establishes, a different quorum for the committee.”

**464.** `[C1293 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the bylaws do not provide a different quorum for the committee, the assembly can establish one in the _____ case before going into committee of the whole, regardless of the quorum that the bylaws require for the assembly.

   *Answer:* particular — “If the bylaws do not provide a different quorum for the committee, the assembly can establish one in the particular case before going into committee of the whole, regardless of the quorum that the bylaws require for the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:18 — “If the bylaws do not provide a different quorum for the committee, the assembly can establish one in the particular case before going into committee of the whole, regardless of the quorum that the bylaws require for the assembly.”

**465.** `[C1294 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A somewhat simpler version of the committee of the whole, in effect, is the procedure of _____ in quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole), which is convenient in assemblies of medium size.

   *Answer:* consideration — “A somewhat simpler version of the committee of the whole, in effect, is the procedure of consideration in quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole), which is convenient in assemblies of medium size.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:19 — “A somewhat simpler version of the committee of the whole, in effect, is the procedure of consideration in quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole), which is convenient in assemblies of medium size.”

**466.** `[C1294 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ to a committee of the whole, the quasi committee of the whole is not a real committee, but is “the assembly acting as if in committee of the whole.”
   A. contrast
   B. business
   C. Resolved
   D. meetings

   *Answer:* A) contrast. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:19 — “In contrast to a committee of the whole, the quasi committee of the whole is not a real committee, but is “the assembly acting as if in committee of the whole.””

**467.** `[C1294 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A somewhat simpler version of the committee of the whole, in effect, is the procedure of _____ in quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole), which is convenient in assemblies of medium size.
   A. resolutions
   B. Preparation
   C. deliberative
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:19 — “A somewhat simpler version of the committee of the whole, in effect, is the procedure of consideration in quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole), which is convenient in assemblies of medium size.”

**468.** `[C1294 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ to a committee of the whole, the quasi committee of the whole is not a real committee, but is “the assembly acting as if in committee of the whole.”

   *Answer:* contrast — “In contrast to a committee of the whole, the quasi committee of the whole is not a real committee, but is “the assembly acting as if in committee of the whole.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:19 — “In contrast to a committee of the whole, the quasi committee of the whole is not a real committee, but is “the assembly acting as if in committee of the whole.””

**469.** `[C1295 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion for _____ in quasi committee is made in a form like this:

   *Answer:* consideration — “The motion for consideration in quasi committee is made in a form like this:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:20 — “The motion for consideration in quasi committee is made in a form like this:”

**470.** `[C1295 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion for _____ in quasi committee is made in a form like this:
   A. immediately
   B. characteristics
   C. consideration
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:20 — “The motion for consideration in quasi committee is made in a form like this:”

**471.** `[C1296 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The presiding officer of the assembly does not appoint a chairman of the quasi committee, but remains in the chair himself throughout its _____.

   *Answer:* proceedings — “The presiding officer of the assembly does not appoint a chairman of the quasi committee, but remains in the chair himself throughout its proceedings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:21 — “The presiding officer of the assembly does not appoint a chairman of the quasi committee, but remains in the chair himself throughout its proceedings.”

**472.** `[C1296 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The assembly’s secretary keeps a temporary _____ of the business transacted in quasi committee;
   A. introduced
   B. landscaped
   C. memorandum
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) memorandum. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:21 — “The assembly’s secretary keeps a temporary memorandum of the business transacted in quasi committee;”

**473.** `[C1296 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but, just as in the case of a real committee of the whole, the _____ of the assembly carry only a record of the report from quasi committee and the action thereon, as shown below.

   *Answer:* minutes — “but, just as in the case of a real committee of the whole, the minutes of the assembly carry only a record of the report from quasi committee and the action thereon, as shown below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:21 — “but, just as in the case of a real committee of the whole, the minutes of the assembly carry only a record of the report from quasi committee and the action thereon, as shown below.”

**474.** `[C1296 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The presiding officer of the assembly does not appoint a chairman of the quasi committee, but remains in the chair himself throughout its _____.
   A. Unfinished
   B. requirement
   C. Permission
   D. proceedings

   *Answer:* D) proceedings. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:21 — “The presiding officer of the assembly does not appoint a chairman of the quasi committee, but remains in the chair himself throughout its proceedings.”

**475.** `[C1296 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The assembly’s secretary keeps a temporary _____ of the business transacted in quasi committee;

   *Answer:* memorandum — “The assembly’s secretary keeps a temporary memorandum of the business transacted in quasi committee;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:21 — “The assembly’s secretary keeps a temporary memorandum of the business transacted in quasi committee;”

**476.** `[C1297 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, for example, if a motion to refer the main question to an ordinary committee is made in quasi committee of the whole, such a motion to _____ would be equivalent to the following series of motions if the matter were being considered in a real committee of the whole: (1) that the committee of the whole rise;
   A. agenda
   B. attend
   C. Appeal
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* D) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:22 — “Thus, for example, if a motion to refer the main question to an ordinary committee is made in quasi committee of the whole, such a motion to Commit would be equivalent to the following series of motions if the matter were being considered in a real committee of the whole: (1) that the committee of the whole rise;”

**477.** `[C1297 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In contrast to the case of a real committee of the whole, however, any motion that would be in order in the assembly is also in order in the quasi committee, where it is _____ only to the extent permitted under the assembly’s rules.

   *Answer:* debatable — “In contrast to the case of a real committee of the whole, however, any motion that would be in order in the assembly is also in order in the quasi committee, where it is debatable only to the extent permitted under the assembly’s rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:22 — “In contrast to the case of a real committee of the whole, however, any motion that would be in order in the assembly is also in order in the quasi committee, where it is debatable only to the extent permitted under the assembly’s rules.”

**478.** `[C1297 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But if any such motion except an amendment or other motion that would be in order in a real committee of the whole is adopted, it _____ puts an end to the proceedings in quasi committee.
   A. adjournment
   B. automatically
   C. representatives
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:22 — “But if any such motion except an amendment or other motion that would be in order in a real committee of the whole is adopted, it automatically puts an end to the proceedings in quasi committee.”

**479.** `[C1297 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, for example, if a motion to refer the main question to an ordinary committee is made in quasi committee of the whole, such a motion to Commit would be _____ to the following series of motions if the matter were being considered in a real committee of the whole: (1) that the committee of the whole rise;

   *Answer:* equivalent — “Thus, for example, if a motion to refer the main question to an ordinary committee is made in quasi committee of the whole, such a motion to Commit would be equivalent to the following series of motions if the matter were being considered in a real committee of the whole: (1) that the committee of the whole rise;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:22 — “Thus, for example, if a motion to refer the main question to an ordinary committee is made in quasi committee of the whole, such a motion to Commit would be equivalent to the following series of motions if the matter were being considered in a real committee of the whole: (1) that the committee of the whole rise;”

**480.** `[C1297 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (2) that the committee of the whole be discharged from further _____ of the subject;
   A. undebatable
   B. interrupted
   C. consideration
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:22 — “(2) that the committee of the whole be discharged from further consideration of the subject;”

**481.** `[C1298 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the quasi committee is not brought to an end as described in the preceding paragraph, then, when no further amendments are offered in response to the chair’s call for them, the presiding officer _____ proceeds to report to the assembly and to state the question on the amendments as follows:
   A. authorizes
   B. immediately
   C. interrupted
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:23 — “If the quasi committee is not brought to an end as described in the preceding paragraph, then, when no further amendments are offered in response to the chair’s call for them, the presiding officer immediately proceeds to report to the assembly and to state the question on the amendments as follows:”

**482.** `[C1298 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the quasi committee is not brought to an end as described in the preceding paragraph, then, when no further amendments are offered in response to the chair’s call for them, the presiding officer _____ proceeds to report to the assembly and to state the question on the amendments as follows:

   *Answer:* immediately — “If the quasi committee is not brought to an end as described in the preceding paragraph, then, when no further amendments are offered in response to the chair’s call for them, the presiding officer immediately proceeds to report to the assembly and to state the question on the amendments as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:23 — “If the quasi committee is not brought to an end as described in the preceding paragraph, then, when no further amendments are offered in response to the chair’s call for them, the presiding officer immediately proceeds to report to the assembly and to state the question on the amendments as follows:”

**483.** `[C1299 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As explained above, if a question is considered in either a real committee of the whole or in quasi committee of the whole, the _____ of the committee or quasi committee must be reported to the assembly and then the assembly must take action on these recommendations.

   *Answer:* recommendations — “As explained above, if a question is considered in either a real committee of the whole or in quasi committee of the whole, the recommendations of the committee or quasi committee must be reported to the assembly and then the assembly must take action on these recommendations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:24 — “As explained above, if a question is considered in either a real committee of the whole or in quasi committee of the whole, the recommendations of the committee or quasi committee must be reported to the assembly and then the assembly must take action on these recommendations.”

**484.** `[C1299 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In ordinary societies whose meetings are not large, a much simpler method is to consider the question _____, which in effect only suspends the rule limiting the number of times a member can speak in debate on the main question and any amendments to it.
   A. subsidiary
   B. committees
   C. informally
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* C) informally. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:24 — “In ordinary societies whose meetings are not large, a much simpler method is to consider the question informally, which in effect only suspends the rule limiting the number of times a member can speak in debate on the main question and any amendments to it.”

**485.** `[C1299 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As explained above, if a question is considered in either a real committee of the whole or in quasi committee of the whole, the _____ of the committee or quasi committee must be reported to the assembly and then the assembly must take action on these recommendations.
   A. specification
   B. recommendations
   C. Vice-President
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) recommendations. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:24 — “As explained above, if a question is considered in either a real committee of the whole or in quasi committee of the whole, the recommendations of the committee or quasi committee must be reported to the assembly and then the assembly must take action on these recommendations.”

**486.** `[C1299 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In ordinary societies whose meetings are not large, a much simpler method is to consider the question _____, which in effect only suspends the rule limiting the number of times a member can speak in debate on the main question and any amendments to it.

   *Answer:* informally — “In ordinary societies whose meetings are not large, a much simpler method is to consider the question informally, which in effect only suspends the rule limiting the number of times a member can speak in debate on the main question and any amendments to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:24 — “In ordinary societies whose meetings are not large, a much simpler method is to consider the question informally, which in effect only suspends the rule limiting the number of times a member can speak in debate on the main question and any amendments to it.”

**487.** `[C1300 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is desired to consider a question _____, a member makes the motion that this be done:

   *Answer:* informally — “When it is desired to consider a question informally, a member makes the motion that this be done:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:25 — “When it is desired to consider a question informally, a member makes the motion that this be done:”

**488.** `[C1300 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is desired to consider a question _____, a member makes the motion that this be done:
   A. Permission
   B. informally
   C. convention
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) informally. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:25 — “When it is desired to consider a question informally, a member makes the motion that this be done:”

**489.** `[C1301 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The “informal” aspect of the _____ applies only to the number of speeches allowed in debate on the main question and its amendments;

   *Answer:* consideration — “The “informal” aspect of the consideration applies only to the number of speeches allowed in debate on the main question and its amendments;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:26 — “The “informal” aspect of the consideration applies only to the number of speeches allowed in debate on the main question and its amendments;”

**490.** `[C1301 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: all votes are formal, and any other motion that is made is under the _____ rules of debate.
   A. regular
   B. Program
   C. Ordered
   D. Suspend

   *Answer:* A) regular. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:26 — “all votes are formal, and any other motion that is made is under the regular rules of debate.”

**491.** `[C1301 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While _____ a question informally the assembly can, by a twothirds vote, limit the number or length of speeches, or in any other way limit or close debate.

   *Answer:* considering — “While considering a question informally the assembly can, by a twothirds vote, limit the number or length of speeches, or in any other way limit or close debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:26 — “While considering a question informally the assembly can, by a twothirds vote, limit the number or length of speeches, or in any other way limit or close debate.”

**492.** `[C1301 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The “informal” aspect of the _____ applies only to the number of speeches allowed in debate on the main question and its amendments;
   A. constitution
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. consideration
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:26 — “The “informal” aspect of the consideration applies only to the number of speeches allowed in debate on the main question and its amendments;”

**493.** `[C1301 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: all votes are formal, and any other motion that is made is under the _____ rules of debate.

   *Answer:* regular — “all votes are formal, and any other motion that is made is under the regular rules of debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:26 — “all votes are formal, and any other motion that is made is under the regular rules of debate.”

**494.** `[C1302 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before the main question is disposed of, the informal consideration can be brought to an end, if desired, by adopting by _____ vote a motion “that the regular rules of debate be in force,” or “that the question be considered formally.”

   *Answer:* majority — “Before the main question is disposed of, the informal consideration can be brought to an end, if desired, by adopting by majority vote a motion “that the regular rules of debate be in force,” or “that the question be considered formally.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:27 — “Before the main question is disposed of, the informal consideration can be brought to an end, if desired, by adopting by majority vote a motion “that the regular rules of debate be in force,” or “that the question be considered formally.””

**495.** `[C1302 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before the main question is disposed of, the informal _____ can be brought to an end, if desired, by adopting by majority vote a motion “that the regular rules of debate be in force,” or “that the question be considered formally.”
   A. interrupted
   B. Organization
   C. Preparation
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:27 — “Before the main question is disposed of, the informal consideration can be brought to an end, if desired, by adopting by majority vote a motion “that the regular rules of debate be in force,” or “that the question be considered formally.””

**496.** `[C1303 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The more traditional aids to the crystallization of opinion in societies have been, simply, to take a _____ or to refer the matter to a committee—often a large committee composed of members representing differing views in the society, such as a committee of the whole or one of its alternate forms.

   *Answer:* Recess — “The more traditional aids to the crystallization of opinion in societies have been, simply, to take a Recess or to refer the matter to a committee—often a large committee composed of members representing differing views in the society, such as a committee of the whole or one of its alternate forms.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:28 — “The more traditional aids to the crystallization of opinion in societies have been, simply, to take a Recess or to refer the matter to a committee—often a large committee composed of members representing differing views in the society, such as a committee of the whole or one of its alternate forms.”

**497.** `[C1303 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The more traditional aids to the _____ of opinion in societies have been, simply, to take a Recess or to refer the matter to a committee—often a large committee composed of members representing differing views in the society, such as a committee of the whole or one of its alternate forms.
   A. representatives
   B. President-Elect
   C. presentation
   D. crystallization

   *Answer:* D) crystallization. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:28 — “The more traditional aids to the crystallization of opinion in societies have been, simply, to take a Recess or to refer the matter to a committee—often a large committee composed of members representing differing views in the society, such as a committee of the whole or one of its alternate forms.”

**498.** `[C1303 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In more recent years, a practice has developed of _____ breakout groups with every member in attendance being urged to participate in a group.

   *Answer:* establishing — “In more recent years, a practice has developed of establishing breakout groups with every member in attendance being urged to participate in a group.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:28 — “In more recent years, a practice has developed of establishing breakout groups with every member in attendance being urged to participate in a group.”

**499.** `[C1303 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each breakout group, of which there may be many, is usually kept small—_____ ten or twelve persons—and a moderator is appointed for each group.
   A. convention
   B. sustaining
   C. frequently
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* C) frequently. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:28 — “Each breakout group, of which there may be many, is usually kept small—frequently ten or twelve persons—and a moderator is appointed for each group.”

**500.** `[C1303 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Often, the groups meet during a recess or _____ of the assembly.

   *Answer:* adjournment — “Often, the groups meet during a recess or adjournment of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:28 — “Often, the groups meet during a recess or adjournment of the assembly.”

**501.** `[C1304 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____, as understood in parliamentary law, is a meeting of an unorganized group, which—in a publicized or selectively distributed notice known as the call of the meeting— has been announced:

   *Answer:* mass meeting — “A mass meeting, as understood in parliamentary law, is a meeting of an unorganized group, which—in a publicized or selectively distributed notice known as the call of the meeting— has been announced:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:1 — “A mass meeting, as understood in parliamentary law, is a meeting of an unorganized group, which—in a publicized or selectively distributed notice known as the call of the meeting— has been announced:”

**502.** `[C1304 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A mass meeting, as understood in parliamentary law, is a meeting of an unorganized group, which—in a publicized or selectively distributed notice known as the _____ of the meeting— has been announced:
   A. Limit
   B. Annul
   C. Point
   D. call

   *Answer:* D) call. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:1 — “A mass meeting, as understood in parliamentary law, is a meeting of an unorganized group, which—in a publicized or selectively distributed notice known as the call of the meeting— has been announced:”

**503.** `[C1305 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To the extent that persons in the invited category are clearly _____—as, for example, registered voters of a particular political party, or residents of a certain area—only such persons have the right to make motions, to speak, and to vote at the meeting, and none others need be admitted if the sponsors so choose.

   *Answer:* identifiable — “To the extent that persons in the invited category are clearly identifiable—as, for example, registered voters of a particular political party, or residents of a certain area—only such persons have the right to make motions, to speak, and to vote at the meeting, and none others need be admitted if the sponsors so choose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:2 — “To the extent that persons in the invited category are clearly identifiable—as, for example, registered voters of a particular political party, or residents of a certain area—only such persons have the right to make motions, to speak, and to vote at the meeting, and none others need be admitted if the sponsors so choose.”

**504.** `[C1305 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In any event, a mass meeting is convened—and those who attend are admitted—upon the implied _____ that the sponsors (who have engaged the hall and assumed the expenses of promoting the meeting) have the right to have the proceedings confined to the overall object they have announced;
   A. Indefinitely
   B. understanding
   C. presentation
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) understanding. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:2 — “In any event, a mass meeting is convened—and those who attend are admitted—upon the implied understanding that the sponsors (who have engaged the hall and assumed the expenses of promoting the meeting) have the right to have the proceedings confined to the overall object they have announced;”

**505.** `[C1305 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but that the entire assembly (which is made up of persons whose help the sponsors are seeking) has the right to _____ the action to be taken in pursuit of the stated object.

   *Answer:* determine — “but that the entire assembly (which is made up of persons whose help the sponsors are seeking) has the right to determine the action to be taken in pursuit of the stated object.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:2 — “but that the entire assembly (which is made up of persons whose help the sponsors are seeking) has the right to determine the action to be taken in pursuit of the stated object.”

**506.** `[C1305 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To the extent that persons in the invited category are clearly _____—as, for example, registered voters of a particular political party, or residents of a certain area—only such persons have the right to make motions, to speak, and to vote at the meeting, and none others need be admitted if the sponsors so choose.
   A. consideration
   B. identifiable
   C. Procedures
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) identifiable. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:2 — “To the extent that persons in the invited category are clearly identifiable—as, for example, registered voters of a particular political party, or residents of a certain area—only such persons have the right to make motions, to speak, and to vote at the meeting, and none others need be admitted if the sponsors so choose.”

**507.** `[C1305 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any event, a mass meeting is convened—and those who attend are admitted—upon the implied _____ that the sponsors (who have engaged the hall and assumed the expenses of promoting the meeting) have the right to have the proceedings confined to the overall object they have announced;

   *Answer:* understanding — “In any event, a mass meeting is convened—and those who attend are admitted—upon the implied understanding that the sponsors (who have engaged the hall and assumed the expenses of promoting the meeting) have the right to have the proceedings confined to the overall object they have announced;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:2 — “In any event, a mass meeting is convened—and those who attend are admitted—upon the implied understanding that the sponsors (who have engaged the hall and assumed the expenses of promoting the meeting) have the right to have the proceedings confined to the overall object they have announced;”

**508.** `[C1306 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The call or _____ of a mass meeting should specify the date, hour, and place of the meeting, its purpose, and—where applicable—who is invited to attend.
   A. announcement
   B. Information
   C. adjournment
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* A) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:3 — “The call or announcement of a mass meeting should specify the date, hour, and place of the meeting, its purpose, and—where applicable—who is invited to attend.”

**509.** `[C1306 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It may also carry an _____ of the sponsorship.

   *Answer:* identification — “It may also carry an identification of the sponsorship.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:3 — “It may also carry an identification of the sponsorship.”

**510.** `[C1306 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Depending on the funds available and the people to be reached, the call can be given the desired publicity or distribution by whatever means are expected to be most effective—_____ in the newspapers or by radio or television, a website, mailings, posters, handbills or flyers, or the like.
   A. announcements
   B. specification
   C. presentation
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* A) announcements. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:3 — “Depending on the funds available and the people to be reached, the call can be given the desired publicity or distribution by whatever means are expected to be most effective—announcements in the newspapers or by radio or television, a website, mailings, posters, handbills or flyers, or the like.”

**511.** `[C1306 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The call or _____ of a mass meeting should specify the date, hour, and place of the meeting, its purpose, and—where applicable—who is invited to attend.

   *Answer:* announcement — “The call or announcement of a mass meeting should specify the date, hour, and place of the meeting, its purpose, and—where applicable—who is invited to attend.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:3 — “The call or announcement of a mass meeting should specify the date, hour, and place of the meeting, its purpose, and—where applicable—who is invited to attend.”

**512.** `[C1306 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It may also carry an _____ of the sponsorship.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. presentation
   C. representatives
   D. identification

   *Answer:* D) identification. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:3 — “It may also carry an identification of the sponsorship.”

**513.** `[C1307 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While a mass meeting should be conducted so as to accord the assembly its proper role in _____ the outcome as described above, at the same time, a certain amount of planning by the sponsors is advisable to avoid the risk of the meeting’s foundering.
   A. Reconsider
   B. constitution
   C. adjournment
   D. determining

   *Answer:* D) determining. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:4 — “While a mass meeting should be conducted so as to accord the assembly its proper role in determining the outcome as described above, at the same time, a certain amount of planning by the sponsors is advisable to avoid the risk of the meeting’s foundering.”

**514.** `[C1307 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While a mass meeting should be conducted so as to accord the assembly its proper role in _____ the outcome as described above, at the same time, a certain amount of planning by the sponsors is advisable to avoid the risk of the meeting’s foundering.

   *Answer:* determining — “While a mass meeting should be conducted so as to accord the assembly its proper role in determining the outcome as described above, at the same time, a certain amount of planning by the sponsors is advisable to avoid the risk of the meeting’s foundering.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:4 — “While a mass meeting should be conducted so as to accord the assembly its proper role in determining the outcome as described above, at the same time, a certain amount of planning by the sponsors is advisable to avoid the risk of the meeting’s foundering.”

**515.** `[C1308 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The person chosen as chairman should be competent as a presiding officer and in sympathy with the object of the meeting, and it is an advantage if he _____ knows many of the people who may attend.

   *Answer:* personally — “The person chosen as chairman should be competent as a presiding officer and in sympathy with the object of the meeting, and it is an advantage if he personally knows many of the people who may attend.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:6 — “The person chosen as chairman should be competent as a presiding officer and in sympathy with the object of the meeting, and it is an advantage if he personally knows many of the people who may attend.”

**516.** `[C1308 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Depending on conditions, it is sometimes good policy to have a set of _____ drafted in advance to submit to the meeting.
   A. undebatable
   B. resolutions
   C. deliberative
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:6 — “Depending on conditions, it is sometimes good policy to have a set of resolutions drafted in advance to submit to the meeting.”

**517.** `[C1308 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The person chosen as chairman should be competent as a presiding officer and in sympathy with the object of the meeting, and it is an advantage if he _____ knows many of the people who may attend.
   A. Convention
   B. resolution
   C. personally
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) personally. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:6 — “The person chosen as chairman should be competent as a presiding officer and in sympathy with the object of the meeting, and it is an advantage if he personally knows many of the people who may attend.”

**518.** `[C1308 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Depending on conditions, it is sometimes good policy to have a set of _____ drafted in advance to submit to the meeting.

   *Answer:* resolutions — “Depending on conditions, it is sometimes good policy to have a set of resolutions drafted in advance to submit to the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:6 — “Depending on conditions, it is sometimes good policy to have a set of resolutions drafted in advance to submit to the meeting.”

**519.** `[C1309 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At a mass meeting, the “_____” consists of all persons in the invited category who attend.

   *Answer:* membership — “At a mass meeting, the “membership” consists of all persons in the invited category who attend.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:7 — “At a mass meeting, the “membership” consists of all persons in the invited category who attend.”

**520.** `[C1309 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If no _____ was placed in the call, anyone who attends is regarded as a member and has the same rights as members in other assemblies—to make motions, to speak in debate, and to vote.
   A. deliberative
   B. qualification
   C. resolutions
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* B) qualification. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:7 — “If no qualification was placed in the call, anyone who attends is regarded as a member and has the same rights as members in other assemblies—to make motions, to speak in debate, and to vote.”

**521.** `[C1309 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the call specified only a particular category of persons as invited and if no attempt is made to screen the attendance at the door, anyone attending is presumed to be entitled to participate as a member, subject only to his subsequent _____ to the contrary.

   *Answer:* identification — “If the call specified only a particular category of persons as invited and if no attempt is made to screen the attendance at the door, anyone attending is presumed to be entitled to participate as a member, subject only to his subsequent identification to the contrary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:7 — “If the call specified only a particular category of persons as invited and if no attempt is made to screen the attendance at the door, anyone attending is presumed to be entitled to participate as a member, subject only to his subsequent identification to the contrary.”

**522.** `[C1309 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If only those invited are admitted, anyone _____ admitted has the rights of a member, and a person who is discovered to have entered fraudulently can be asked to leave.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. deliberative
   C. legitimately
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) legitimately. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:7 — “If only those invited are admitted, anyone legitimately admitted has the rights of a member, and a person who is discovered to have entered fraudulently can be asked to leave.”

**523.** `[C1309 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At a mass meeting, the “_____” consists of all persons in the invited category who attend.
   A. assemblies
   B. settlement
   C. Particular
   D. membership

   *Answer:* D) membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:7 — “At a mass meeting, the “membership” consists of all persons in the invited category who attend.”

**524.** `[C1310 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Depending on the probable character of the assembly, however, it may be wise to adopt a standard parliamentary authority, which can be done by a _____ vote on the motion of a member—made, as prearranged by the sponsors, immediately after the election of the secretary (see below).

   *Answer:* majority — “Depending on the probable character of the assembly, however, it may be wise to adopt a standard parliamentary authority, which can be done by a majority vote on the motion of a member—made, as prearranged by the sponsors, immediately after the election of the secretary (see below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:8 — “Depending on the probable character of the assembly, however, it may be wise to adopt a standard parliamentary authority, which can be done by a majority vote on the motion of a member—made, as prearranged by the sponsors, immediately after the election of the secretary (see below).”

**525.** `[C1310 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Depending on the probable character of the assembly, however, it may be wise to adopt a standard _____ authority, which can be done by a majority vote on the motion of a member—made, as prearranged by the sponsors, immediately after the election of the secretary (see below).
   A. parliamentary
   B. interrupted
   C. Organization
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:8 — “Depending on the probable character of the assembly, however, it may be wise to adopt a standard parliamentary authority, which can be done by a majority vote on the motion of a member—made, as prearranged by the sponsors, immediately after the election of the secretary (see below).”

**526.** `[C1311 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any event, without adoption at a mass meeting and _____ of what rules the meeting may adopt, the provisions of the call, specifying the meeting’s purpose and those invited to attend it, have a force equivalent to bylaws of an organized society;

   *Answer:* regardless — “In any event, without adoption at a mass meeting and regardless of what rules the meeting may adopt, the provisions of the call, specifying the meeting’s purpose and those invited to attend it, have a force equivalent to bylaws of an organized society;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:9 — “In any event, without adoption at a mass meeting and regardless of what rules the meeting may adopt, the provisions of the call, specifying the meeting’s purpose and those invited to attend it, have a force equivalent to bylaws of an organized society;”

**527.** `[C1311 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This effect is a _____ of the sponsors’ rights as explained in the first paragraph of this section.
   A. constitution
   B. Substitute
   C. consequence
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) consequence. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:9 — “This effect is a consequence of the sponsors’ rights as explained in the first paragraph of this section.”

**528.** `[C1311 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In any event, without adoption at a mass meeting and _____ of what rules the meeting may adopt, the provisions of the call, specifying the meeting’s purpose and those invited to attend it, have a force equivalent to bylaws of an organized society;
   A. regardless
   B. Substitute
   C. incidental
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* A) regardless. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:9 — “In any event, without adoption at a mass meeting and regardless of what rules the meeting may adopt, the provisions of the call, specifying the meeting’s purpose and those invited to attend it, have a force equivalent to bylaws of an organized society;”

**529.** `[C1311 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This effect is a _____ of the sponsors’ rights as explained in the first paragraph of this section.

   *Answer:* consequence — “This effect is a consequence of the sponsors’ rights as explained in the first paragraph of this section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:9 — “This effect is a consequence of the sponsors’ rights as explained in the first paragraph of this section.”

**530.** `[C1312 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any person at a mass meeting who, after being advised, persists in an obvious attempt to divert the meeting to a different purpose from that for which it was called, or who otherwise tries to disrupt the proceedings, becomes subject to the _____ procedures described in 61.

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “Any person at a mass meeting who, after being advised, persists in an obvious attempt to divert the meeting to a different purpose from that for which it was called, or who otherwise tries to disrupt the proceedings, becomes subject to the disciplinary procedures described in 61.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:10 — “Any person at a mass meeting who, after being advised, persists in an obvious attempt to divert the meeting to a different purpose from that for which it was called, or who otherwise tries to disrupt the proceedings, becomes subject to the disciplinary procedures described in 61.”

**531.** `[C1312 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any person at a mass meeting who, after being advised, persists in an obvious attempt to divert the meeting to a different purpose from that for which it was called, or who otherwise tries to disrupt the proceedings, becomes subject to the _____ procedures described in 61.
   A. Particular
   B. constitution
   C. disciplinary
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* C) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:10 — “Any person at a mass meeting who, after being advised, persists in an obvious attempt to divert the meeting to a different purpose from that for which it was called, or who otherwise tries to disrupt the proceedings, becomes subject to the disciplinary procedures described in 61.”

**532.** `[C1313 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A chairman and a _____ are in general the only officers required by a mass meeting.

   *Answer:* secretary — “A chairman and a secretary are in general the only officers required by a mass meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:11 — “A chairman and a secretary are in general the only officers required by a mass meeting.”

**533.** `[C1313 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Their election takes place _____ after the meeting is called to order, a convenient method of electing them being by voice vote.
   A. Substitute
   B. interrupted
   C. immediately
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:11 — “Their election takes place immediately after the meeting is called to order, a convenient method of electing them being by voice vote.”

**534.** `[C1313 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As _____ in 46, the first person nominated is voted on first under this method.

   *Answer:* explained — “As explained in 46, the first person nominated is voted on first under this method.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:11 — “As explained in 46, the first person nominated is voted on first under this method.”

**535.** `[C1313 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the interest of electing competent persons, it is therefore advisable that a name chosen by the sponsors be placed in _____ first for each office.
   A. nomination
   B. recognized
   C. Convention
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* A) nomination. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:11 — “In the interest of electing competent persons, it is therefore advisable that a name chosen by the sponsors be placed in nomination first for each office.”

**536.** `[C1313 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Additional nominations can be made from the floor and the assembly can elect anyone it wishes, but except under unusual _____ it is likely to elect the apparent choices of the sponsors.

   *Answer:* circumstances — “Additional nominations can be made from the floor and the assembly can elect anyone it wishes, but except under unusual circumstances it is likely to elect the apparent choices of the sponsors.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:11 — “Additional nominations can be made from the floor and the assembly can elect anyone it wishes, but except under unusual circumstances it is likely to elect the apparent choices of the sponsors.”

**537.** `[C1314 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the latter case, at the scheduled hour or shortly _____, this person steps to the chair and, after waiting or signaling for quiet, says, “The meeting will come to order.

   *Answer:* thereafter — “In the latter case, at the scheduled hour or shortly thereafter, this person steps to the chair and, after waiting or signaling for quiet, says, “The meeting will come to order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:12 — “In the latter case, at the scheduled hour or shortly thereafter, this person steps to the chair and, after waiting or signaling for quiet, says, “The meeting will come to order.”

**538.** `[C1314 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the latter case, at the scheduled hour or shortly _____, this person steps to the chair and, after waiting or signaling for quiet, says, “The meeting will come to order.
   A. Procedures
   B. thereafter
   C. convention
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) thereafter. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:12 — “In the latter case, at the scheduled hour or shortly thereafter, this person steps to the chair and, after waiting or signaling for quiet, says, “The meeting will come to order.”

**539.** `[C1315 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the chairman of the meeting has been elected, he takes the chair and may say a few words of _____ if he wishes, after which he says, “Nominations are now in order for secretary of this meeting.” The person agreed upon to nominate the sponsors’ choice for secretary should promptly place that name in nomination;

   *Answer:* acknowledgment — “When the chairman of the meeting has been elected, he takes the chair and may say a few words of acknowledgment if he wishes, after which he says, “Nominations are now in order for secretary of this meeting.” The person agreed upon to nominate the sponsors’ choice for secretary should promptly place that name in nomination;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:13 — “When the chairman of the meeting has been elected, he takes the chair and may say a few words of acknowledgment if he wishes, after which he says, “Nominations are now in order for secretary of this meeting.” The person agreed upon to nominate the sponsors’ choice for secretary should promptly place that name in nomination;”
