**540.** `[C1315 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the chairman of the meeting has been elected, he takes the chair and may say a few words of acknowledgment if he wishes, after which he says, “_____ are now in order for secretary of this meeting.” The person agreed upon to nominate the sponsors’ choice for secretary should promptly place that name in nomination;
   A. Preparation
   B. Nominations
   C. Substitute
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) Nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:13 — “When the chairman of the meeting has been elected, he takes the chair and may say a few words of acknowledgment if he wishes, after which he says, “Nominations are now in order for secretary of this meeting.” The person agreed upon to nominate the sponsors’ choice for secretary should promptly place that name in nomination;”

**541.** `[C1315 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the secretary has been elected, he takes his seat near the chairman and keeps a record of the _____.
   A. undebatable
   B. proceedings
   C. constitution
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) proceedings. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:13 — “When the secretary has been elected, he takes his seat near the chairman and keeps a record of the proceedings.”

**542.** `[C1315 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: members can also make _____ nominations.

   *Answer:* additional — “members can also make additional nominations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:13 — “members can also make additional nominations.”

**543.** `[C1315 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the secretary has been elected, he takes his seat near the chairman and keeps a record of the _____.

   *Answer:* proceedings — “When the secretary has been elected, he takes his seat near the chairman and keeps a record of the proceedings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:13 — “When the secretary has been elected, he takes his seat near the chairman and keeps a record of the proceedings.”

**544.** `[C1316 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the _____ are completed, the chair says, “The Secretary will now read the call of this meeting.” This reading of the call should include the names of the sponsors.

   *Answer:* elections — “When the elections are completed, the chair says, “The Secretary will now read the call of this meeting.” This reading of the call should include the names of the sponsors.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:14 — “When the elections are completed, the chair says, “The Secretary will now read the call of this meeting.” This reading of the call should include the names of the sponsors.”

**545.** `[C1316 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then recognizes the person who is to explain the purpose of the meeting more fully, or the chairman gives this _____ himself if he has been selected to do so.
   A. interrupted
   B. undebatable
   C. presentation
   D. specification

   *Answer:* C) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:14 — “The chair then recognizes the person who is to explain the purpose of the meeting more fully, or the chairman gives this presentation himself if he has been selected to do so.”

**546.** `[C1316 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the _____ are completed, the chair says, “The Secretary will now read the call of this meeting.” This reading of the call should include the names of the sponsors.
   A. debatable
   B. elections
   C. auditors’
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* B) elections. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:14 — “When the elections are completed, the chair says, “The Secretary will now read the call of this meeting.” This reading of the call should include the names of the sponsors.”

**547.** `[C1316 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then recognizes the person who is to explain the purpose of the meeting more fully, or the chairman gives this _____ himself if he has been selected to do so.

   *Answer:* presentation — “The chair then recognizes the person who is to explain the purpose of the meeting more fully, or the chairman gives this presentation himself if he has been selected to do so.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:14 — “The chair then recognizes the person who is to explain the purpose of the meeting more fully, or the chairman gives this presentation himself if he has been selected to do so.”

**548.** `[C1317 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the purpose of the meeting has been explained, it is in order for a member to offer a resolution, or a series of _____, to accomplish this purpose;

   *Answer:* resolutions — “After the purpose of the meeting has been explained, it is in order for a member to offer a resolution, or a series of resolutions, to accomplish this purpose;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:15 — “After the purpose of the meeting has been explained, it is in order for a member to offer a resolution, or a series of resolutions, to accomplish this purpose;”

**549.** `[C1317 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, if believed more suitable to the _____, it can be moved that a committee be appointed to draft such resolution(s).
   A. Particular
   B. conditions
   C. substitute
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* B) conditions. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:15 — “or, if believed more suitable to the conditions, it can be moved that a committee be appointed to draft such resolution(s).”

**550.** `[C1317 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the purpose of the meeting has been explained, it is in order for a member to offer a resolution, or a series of _____, to accomplish this purpose;
   A. Incidental
   B. Definitely
   C. resolutions
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:15 — “After the purpose of the meeting has been explained, it is in order for a member to offer a resolution, or a series of resolutions, to accomplish this purpose;”

**551.** `[C1317 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, if believed more suitable to the _____, it can be moved that a committee be appointed to draft such resolution(s).

   *Answer:* conditions — “or, if believed more suitable to the conditions, it can be moved that a committee be appointed to draft such resolution(s).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:15 — “or, if believed more suitable to the conditions, it can be moved that a committee be appointed to draft such resolution(s).”

**552.** `[C1318 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the plan is for the resolution(s) to be offered immediately after the initial explanation, they can have been prepared in advance—with _____ reproduced copies for distribution to those in attendance—and a preselected member can now move their adoption.

   *Answer:* double-spaced — “If the plan is for the resolution(s) to be offered immediately after the initial explanation, they can have been prepared in advance—with double-spaced reproduced copies for distribution to those in attendance—and a preselected member can now move their adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:16 — “If the plan is for the resolution(s) to be offered immediately after the initial explanation, they can have been prepared in advance—with double-spaced reproduced copies for distribution to those in attendance—and a preselected member can now move their adoption.”

**553.** `[C1318 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the plan is for the resolution(s) to be offered immediately after the initial explanation, they can have been prepared in advance—with _____ reproduced copies for distribution to those in attendance—and a preselected member can now move their adoption.
   A. consideration
   B. President-Elect
   C. specification
   D. double-spaced

   *Answer:* D) double-spaced. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:16 — “If the plan is for the resolution(s) to be offered immediately after the initial explanation, they can have been prepared in advance—with double-spaced reproduced copies for distribution to those in attendance—and a preselected member can now move their adoption.”

**554.** `[C1319 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: With reference to the proposal of _____ or other amendments—or alternative resolutions if those first introduced are rejected—any motion within the scope of the meeting’s purpose as announced in the call is in order;

   *Answer:* substitutes — “With reference to the proposal of substitutes or other amendments—or alternative resolutions if those first introduced are rejected—any motion within the scope of the meeting’s purpose as announced in the call is in order;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:17 — “With reference to the proposal of substitutes or other amendments—or alternative resolutions if those first introduced are rejected—any motion within the scope of the meeting’s purpose as announced in the call is in order;”

**555.** `[C1319 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but any motion outside of or _____ to that purpose is not in order.
   A. decision
   B. Standard
   C. chairman
   D. contrary

   *Answer:* D) contrary. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:17 — “but any motion outside of or contrary to that purpose is not in order.”

**556.** `[C1319 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, if the announced purpose of a mass meeting is to oppose the _____ of a proposed airport in a particular location, any motion or resolution directed toward preventing the airport’s construction can be brought up at the meeting;

   *Answer:* construction — “For example, if the announced purpose of a mass meeting is to oppose the construction of a proposed airport in a particular location, any motion or resolution directed toward preventing the airport’s construction can be brought up at the meeting;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:17 — “For example, if the announced purpose of a mass meeting is to oppose the construction of a proposed airport in a particular location, any motion or resolution directed toward preventing the airport’s construction can be brought up at the meeting;”

**557.** `[C1319 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: With reference to the proposal of _____ or other amendments—or alternative resolutions if those first introduced are rejected—any motion within the scope of the meeting’s purpose as announced in the call is in order;
   A. substitutes
   B. Organization
   C. Convention
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) substitutes. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:17 — “With reference to the proposal of substitutes or other amendments—or alternative resolutions if those first introduced are rejected—any motion within the scope of the meeting’s purpose as announced in the call is in order;”

**558.** `[C1319 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but any motion outside of or _____ to that purpose is not in order.

   *Answer:* contrary — “but any motion outside of or contrary to that purpose is not in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:17 — “but any motion outside of or contrary to that purpose is not in order.”

**559.** `[C1320 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rule requiring the assembly’s permission to speak for longer than _____ at a time in debate on pending questions applies if the meeting has adopted no other rule.

   *Answer:* ten minutes — “The rule requiring the assembly’s permission to speak for longer than ten minutes at a time in debate on pending questions applies if the meeting has adopted no other rule.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:18 — “The rule requiring the assembly’s permission to speak for longer than ten minutes at a time in debate on pending questions applies if the meeting has adopted no other rule.”

**560.** `[C1320 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rules governing the _____ of the floor and debate, as given in 42 and 43, are generally applicable.
   A. recognized
   B. assignment
   C. persuasive
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* B) assignment. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:18 — “The rules governing the assignment of the floor and debate, as given in 42 and 43, are generally applicable.”

**561.** `[C1320 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a mass meeting, however, there is no appeal from the chair’s decision in _____ the floor.

   *Answer:* assigning — “In a mass meeting, however, there is no appeal from the chair’s decision in assigning the floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:18 — “In a mass meeting, however, there is no appeal from the chair’s decision in assigning the floor.”

**562.** `[C1320 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rule requiring the assembly’s _____ to speak for longer than ten minutes at a time in debate on pending questions applies if the meeting has adopted no other rule.
   A. Incidental
   B. Definitely
   C. Particular
   D. permission

   *Answer:* D) permission. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:18 — “The rule requiring the assembly’s permission to speak for longer than ten minutes at a time in debate on pending questions applies if the meeting has adopted no other rule.”

**563.** `[C1320 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rules governing the _____ of the floor and debate, as given in 42 and 43, are generally applicable.

   *Answer:* assignment — “The rules governing the assignment of the floor and debate, as given in 42 and 43, are generally applicable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:18 — “The rules governing the assignment of the floor and debate, as given in 42 and 43, are generally applicable.”

**564.** `[C1321 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Resolutions Drafted by a Committee Appointed at the _____.

   *Answer:* Meeting — “Resolutions Drafted by a Committee Appointed at the Meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:19 — “Resolutions Drafted by a Committee Appointed at the Meeting.”

**565.** `[C1321 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ Drafted by a Committee Appointed at the Meeting.
   A. Definitely
   B. constitution
   C. Convention
   D. Resolutions

   *Answer:* D) Resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:19 — “Resolutions Drafted by a Committee Appointed at the Meeting.”

**566.** `[C1321 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a procedure is _____ when it is believed advisable to obtain expressions of opinion from persons who attend the meeting, before attempting to frame resolutions.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “Such a procedure is appropriate when it is believed advisable to obtain expressions of opinion from persons who attend the meeting, before attempting to frame resolutions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:19 — “Such a procedure is appropriate when it is believed advisable to obtain expressions of opinion from persons who attend the meeting, before attempting to frame resolutions.”

**567.** `[C1321 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a small mass meeting, the chair at his discretion can permit those present to make brief statements of this nature after the initial _____ of the meeting’s purpose and before the motion to appoint the committee is made.
   A. requirement
   B. Permission
   C. explanation
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) explanation. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:19 — “In a small mass meeting, the chair at his discretion can permit those present to make brief statements of this nature after the initial explanation of the meeting’s purpose and before the motion to appoint the committee is made.”

**568.** `[C1321 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any event, the same kind of discussion can also take place in regular debate on the motion to appoint the committee, since it is relevant to the committee’s _____ to draft resolution(s) “expressing the sense of the meeting” on the specified subject, as explained below.

   *Answer:* instructions — “In any event, the same kind of discussion can also take place in regular debate on the motion to appoint the committee, since it is relevant to the committee’s instructions to draft resolution(s) “expressing the sense of the meeting” on the specified subject, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:19 — “In any event, the same kind of discussion can also take place in regular debate on the motion to appoint the committee, since it is relevant to the committee’s instructions to draft resolution(s) “expressing the sense of the meeting” on the specified subject, as explained below.”

**569.** `[C1322 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The person who is to make the motion to appoint the committee should be agreed upon by the sponsors in advance, as well as the matter of whether _____ discussion is to be permitted before the motion is made.

   *Answer:* preliminary — “The person who is to make the motion to appoint the committee should be agreed upon by the sponsors in advance, as well as the matter of whether preliminary discussion is to be permitted before the motion is made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:20 — “The person who is to make the motion to appoint the committee should be agreed upon by the sponsors in advance, as well as the matter of whether preliminary discussion is to be permitted before the motion is made.”

**570.** `[C1322 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion may be made in a form such as the _____:
   A. committee
   B. following
   C. rejecting
   D. Secretary

   *Answer:* B) following. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:20 — “The motion may be made in a form such as the following:”

**571.** `[C1322 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The person who is to make the motion to appoint the committee should be agreed upon by the sponsors in advance, as well as the matter of whether _____ discussion is to be permitted before the motion is made.
   A. adjournment
   B. interrupted
   C. committees
   D. preliminary

   *Answer:* D) preliminary. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:20 — “The person who is to make the motion to appoint the committee should be agreed upon by the sponsors in advance, as well as the matter of whether preliminary discussion is to be permitted before the motion is made.”

**572.** `[C1322 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion may be made in a form such as the _____:

   *Answer:* following — “The motion may be made in a form such as the following:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:20 — “The motion may be made in a form such as the following:”

**573.** `[C1323 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a mass meeting it is usually advisable to have all _____ appointed by the chair—assuming that the chairman has been well chosen.

   *Answer:* committees — “In a mass meeting it is usually advisable to have all committees appointed by the chair—assuming that the chairman has been well chosen.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:21 — “In a mass meeting it is usually advisable to have all committees appointed by the chair—assuming that the chairman has been well chosen.”

**574.** `[C1323 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly prefers a different method of _____, however, the procedures that can be followed are as described in 50.
   A. appointment
   B. Organization
   C. requirement
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) appointment. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:21 — “If the assembly prefers a different method of appointment, however, the procedures that can be followed are as described in 50.”

**575.** `[C1323 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the committee has been appointed, it should _____ retire and prepare the resolution(s).

   *Answer:* immediately — “When the committee has been appointed, it should immediately retire and prepare the resolution(s).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:21 — “When the committee has been appointed, it should immediately retire and prepare the resolution(s).”

**576.** `[C1323 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a mass meeting it is usually advisable to have all _____ appointed by the chair—assuming that the chairman has been well chosen.
   A. sustaining
   B. committees
   C. subsidiary
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* B) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:21 — “In a mass meeting it is usually advisable to have all committees appointed by the chair—assuming that the chairman has been well chosen.”

**577.** `[C1323 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly prefers a different method of _____, however, the procedures that can be followed are as described in 50.

   *Answer:* appointment — “If the assembly prefers a different method of appointment, however, the procedures that can be followed are as described in 50.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:21 — “If the assembly prefers a different method of appointment, however, the procedures that can be followed are as described in 50.”

**578.** `[C1324 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During the committee’s _____ from the hall, the assembly can attend to any other business related to the object of the meeting;

   *Answer:* absence — “During the committee’s absence from the hall, the assembly can attend to any other business related to the object of the meeting;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:22 — “During the committee’s absence from the hall, the assembly can attend to any other business related to the object of the meeting;”

**579.** `[C1324 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or it can occupy the time in listening to talks, in engaging in forums or _____ discussions, or in watching a relevant film;
   A. specification
   B. seminar-type
   C. presentation
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* B) seminar-type. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:22 — “or it can occupy the time in listening to talks, in engaging in forums or seminar-type discussions, or in watching a relevant film;”

**580.** `[C1324 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: During the committee’s _____ from the hall, the assembly can attend to any other business related to the object of the meeting;
   A. absence
   B. Divided
   C. Another
   D. approve

   *Answer:* A) absence. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:22 — “During the committee’s absence from the hall, the assembly can attend to any other business related to the object of the meeting;”

**581.** `[C1324 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or it can occupy the time in listening to talks, in engaging in forums or _____ discussions, or in watching a relevant film;

   *Answer:* seminar-type — “or it can occupy the time in listening to talks, in engaging in forums or seminar-type discussions, or in watching a relevant film;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:22 — “or it can occupy the time in listening to talks, in engaging in forums or seminar-type discussions, or in watching a relevant film;”

**582.** `[C1325 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly does not recess and the chair sees the committee return to the room, he asks, as soon as the pending business is disposed of (or as soon as the person giving a talk closes, etc.), “Is the committee that was appointed to draft _____ prepared to report?”

   *Answer:* resolutions — “If the assembly does not recess and the chair sees the committee return to the room, he asks, as soon as the pending business is disposed of (or as soon as the person giving a talk closes, etc.), “Is the committee that was appointed to draft resolutions prepared to report?””. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:23 — “If the assembly does not recess and the chair sees the committee return to the room, he asks, as soon as the pending business is disposed of (or as soon as the person giving a talk closes, etc.), “Is the committee that was appointed to draft resolutions prepared to report?””

**583.** `[C1325 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly does not recess and the chair sees the committee return to the room, he asks, as soon as the pending business is disposed of (or as soon as the person giving a talk closes, etc.), “Is the committee that was appointed to draft _____ prepared to report?”
   A. Organization
   B. constitution
   C. adjournment
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* D) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:23 — “If the assembly does not recess and the chair sees the committee return to the room, he asks, as soon as the pending business is disposed of (or as soon as the person giving a talk closes, etc.), “Is the committee that was appointed to draft resolutions prepared to report?””

**584.** `[C1326 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the committee chairman has answered _____, the chair says, “If there is no objection, the meeting will now hear the committee’s report.

   *Answer:* affirmatively — “When the committee chairman has answered affirmatively, the chair says, “If there is no objection, the meeting will now hear the committee’s report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:24 — “When the committee chairman has answered affirmatively, the chair says, “If there is no objection, the meeting will now hear the committee’s report.”

**585.** `[C1326 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the committee chairman has answered _____, the chair says, “If there is no objection, the meeting will now hear the committee’s report.
   A. requirement
   B. constitution
   C. affirmatively
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* C) affirmatively. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:24 — “When the committee chairman has answered affirmatively, the chair says, “If there is no objection, the meeting will now hear the committee’s report.”

**586.** `[C1327 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair does not notice the committee’s return, the committee chairman, at the first _____, obtains the floor and says, “The committee appointed to draft resolutions is prepared to report.” Unless objection is then made, the chair directs the committee chairman to proceed.

   *Answer:* opportunity — “If the chair does not notice the committee’s return, the committee chairman, at the first opportunity, obtains the floor and says, “The committee appointed to draft resolutions is prepared to report.” Unless objection is then made, the chair directs the committee chairman to proceed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:25 — “If the chair does not notice the committee’s return, the committee chairman, at the first opportunity, obtains the floor and says, “The committee appointed to draft resolutions is prepared to report.” Unless objection is then made, the chair directs the committee chairman to proceed.”

**587.** `[C1327 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair does not notice the committee’s return, the committee chairman, at the first _____, obtains the floor and says, “The committee appointed to draft resolutions is prepared to report.” Unless objection is then made, the chair directs the committee chairman to proceed.
   A. Information
   B. requirement
   C. opportunity
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:25 — “If the chair does not notice the committee’s return, the committee chairman, at the first opportunity, obtains the floor and says, “The committee appointed to draft resolutions is prepared to report.” Unless objection is then made, the chair directs the committee chairman to proceed.”

**588.** `[C1328 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The committee chairman, _____ the presiding officer of the assembly, presents the report as follows:

   *Answer:* addressing — “The committee chairman, addressing the presiding officer of the assembly, presents the report as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:26 — “The committee chairman, addressing the presiding officer of the assembly, presents the report as follows:”

**589.** `[C1328 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The committee chairman, _____ the presiding officer of the assembly, presents the report as follows:
   A. Reconsider
   B. addressing
   C. Substitute
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) addressing. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:26 — “The committee chairman, addressing the presiding officer of the assembly, presents the report as follows:”

**590.** `[C1329 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the presentation of this report, the committee is discharged _____.

   *Answer:* automatically — “On the presentation of this report, the committee is discharged automatically.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:27 — “On the presentation of this report, the committee is discharged automatically.”

**591.** `[C1329 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then states the question on the _____, and they are considered in the same way as summarized above for the case in which resolutions are offered by a member from the floor (see also 51).
   A. authorizes
   B. undebatable
   C. resolutions
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:27 — “The chair then states the question on the resolutions, and they are considered in the same way as summarized above for the case in which resolutions are offered by a member from the floor (see also 51).”

**592.** `[C1329 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the presentation of this report, the committee is discharged _____.
   A. automatically
   B. consideration
   C. president-elect
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* A) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:27 — “On the presentation of this report, the committee is discharged automatically.”

**593.** `[C1329 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then states the question on the _____, and they are considered in the same way as summarized above for the case in which resolutions are offered by a member from the floor (see also 51).

   *Answer:* resolutions — “The chair then states the question on the resolutions, and they are considered in the same way as summarized above for the case in which resolutions are offered by a member from the floor (see also 51).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:27 — “The chair then states the question on the resolutions, and they are considered in the same way as summarized above for the case in which resolutions are offered by a member from the floor (see also 51).”

**594.** `[C1331 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the business for which the mass meeting was called has _____ been completed and no question is pending, someone should move “to adjourn,” or the chair can call for such a motion.

   *Answer:* apparently — “When the business for which the mass meeting was called has apparently been completed and no question is pending, someone should move “to adjourn,” or the chair can call for such a motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:29 — “When the business for which the mass meeting was called has apparently been completed and no question is pending, someone should move “to adjourn,” or the chair can call for such a motion.”

**595.** `[C1331 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless a time has been set for another meeting, the adoption of this motion _____ the assembly— so that, as explained in 21, it is a main motion and can be debated and amended just as any other main motion.
   A. dissolves
   B. Committee
   C. secondary
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* A) dissolves. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:29 — “Unless a time has been set for another meeting, the adoption of this motion dissolves the assembly— so that, as explained in 21, it is a main motion and can be debated and amended just as any other main motion.”

**596.** `[C1331 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An example of an _____ to a main motion “to adjourn” might be “to add the words ‘until eight o’clock Wednesday evening,’” which would thereby include in the motion a provision to set a time for another meeting.

   *Answer:* amendment — “An example of an amendment to a main motion “to adjourn” might be “to add the words ‘until eight o’clock Wednesday evening,’” which would thereby include in the motion a provision to set a time for another meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:29 — “An example of an amendment to a main motion “to adjourn” might be “to add the words ‘until eight o’clock Wednesday evening,’” which would thereby include in the motion a provision to set a time for another meeting.”

**597.** `[C1331 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the business for which the mass meeting was called has _____ been completed and no question is pending, someone should move “to adjourn,” or the chair can call for such a motion.
   A. apparently
   B. incidental
   C. Substitute
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* A) apparently. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:29 — “When the business for which the mass meeting was called has apparently been completed and no question is pending, someone should move “to adjourn,” or the chair can call for such a motion.”

**598.** `[C1331 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless a time has been set for another meeting, the adoption of this motion _____ the assembly— so that, as explained in 21, it is a main motion and can be debated and amended just as any other main motion.

   *Answer:* dissolves — “Unless a time has been set for another meeting, the adoption of this motion dissolves the assembly— so that, as explained in 21, it is a main motion and can be debated and amended just as any other main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:29 — “Unless a time has been set for another meeting, the adoption of this motion dissolves the assembly— so that, as explained in 21, it is a main motion and can be debated and amended just as any other main motion.”

**599.** `[C1332 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases where it is desired to close the meeting before its business has been _____, the rules are as follows:

   *Answer:* completed — “In cases where it is desired to close the meeting before its business has been completed, the rules are as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:30 — “In cases where it is desired to close the meeting before its business has been completed, the rules are as follows:”

**600.** `[C1332 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where it is desired to close the meeting before its business has been _____, the rules are as follows:
   A. completed
   B. president
   C. secondary
   D. exhausted

   *Answer:* A) completed. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:30 — “In cases where it is desired to close the meeting before its business has been completed, the rules are as follows:”

**601.** `[C1333 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion to adjourn a mass meeting has been adopted and no time is set for another meeting, the chair says, “The ayes have it and the meeting is adjourned.” This _____ in effect declares the assembly dissolved.

   *Answer:* announcement — “When a motion to adjourn a mass meeting has been adopted and no time is set for another meeting, the chair says, “The ayes have it and the meeting is adjourned.” This announcement in effect declares the assembly dissolved.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:31 — “When a motion to adjourn a mass meeting has been adopted and no time is set for another meeting, the chair says, “The ayes have it and the meeting is adjourned.” This announcement in effect declares the assembly dissolved.”

**602.** `[C1333 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a time for an adjourned meeting has been set, on the other hand —either previously or by means of a provision included in the motion to adjourn—the chair announces the result by saying, “The ayes have it and this meeting is [or “stands”] adjourned until eight o’clock Wednesday evening.” Before declaring the adjournment, or even taking a vote on adjourning, the chair should make sure that all necessary _____ have been made.
   A. announcements
   B. Preparation
   C. adjournment
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* A) announcements. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:31 — “If a time for an adjourned meeting has been set, on the other hand —either previously or by means of a provision included in the motion to adjourn—the chair announces the result by saying, “The ayes have it and this meeting is [or “stands”] adjourned until eight o’clock Wednesday evening.” Before declaring the adjournment, or even taking a vote on adjourning, the chair should make sure that all necessary announcements have been made.”

**603.** `[C1333 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion to adjourn a mass meeting has been adopted and no time is set for another meeting, the chair says, “The ayes have it and the meeting is adjourned.” This _____ in effect declares the assembly dissolved.
   A. announcement
   B. Indefinitely
   C. constitution
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:31 — “When a motion to adjourn a mass meeting has been adopted and no time is set for another meeting, the chair says, “The ayes have it and the meeting is adjourned.” This announcement in effect declares the assembly dissolved.”

**604.** `[C1333 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a time for an adjourned meeting has been set, on the other hand —either previously or by means of a provision included in the motion to adjourn—the chair announces the result by saying, “The ayes have it and this meeting is [or “stands”] adjourned until eight o’clock Wednesday evening.” Before declaring the adjournment, or even taking a vote on adjourning, the chair should make sure that all necessary _____ have been made.

   *Answer:* announcements — “If a time for an adjourned meeting has been set, on the other hand —either previously or by means of a provision included in the motion to adjourn—the chair announces the result by saying, “The ayes have it and this meeting is [or “stands”] adjourned until eight o’clock Wednesday evening.” Before declaring the adjournment, or even taking a vote on adjourning, the chair should make sure that all necessary announcements have been made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:31 — “If a time for an adjourned meeting has been set, on the other hand —either previously or by means of a provision included in the motion to adjourn—the chair announces the result by saying, “The ayes have it and this meeting is [or “stands”] adjourned until eight o’clock Wednesday evening.” Before declaring the adjournment, or even taking a vote on adjourning, the chair should make sure that all necessary announcements have been made.”

**605.** `[C1334 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If so, the officers elected at the first meeting are designated _____ and secretary pro tem— although the words pro tem are not used in addressing these officers.

   *Answer:* chairman pro tem — “If so, the officers elected at the first meeting are designated chairman pro tem and secretary pro tem— although the words pro tem are not used in addressing these officers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:32 — “If so, the officers elected at the first meeting are designated chairman pro tem and secretary pro tem— although the words pro tem are not used in addressing these officers.”

**606.** `[C1334 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If so, the officers elected at the first meeting are designated chairman pro tem and _____— although the words pro tem are not used in addressing these officers.
   A. adjourned meeting
   B. committee reports
   C. Agenda or Program
   D. secretary pro tem

   *Answer:* D) secretary pro tem. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:32 — “If so, the officers elected at the first meeting are designated chairman pro tem and secretary pro tem— although the words pro tem are not used in addressing these officers.”

**607.** `[C1334 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If more than one mass meeting is necessary to achieve a certain objective, or if the group is working toward the formation of an organized society, a temporary _____ to continue beyond a single mass meeting may become necessary.
   A. Reconsider
   B. organization
   C. specification
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:32 — “If more than one mass meeting is necessary to achieve a certain objective, or if the group is working toward the formation of an organized society, a temporary organization to continue beyond a single mass meeting may become necessary.”

**608.** `[C1334 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a _____ society is the aim of the group, the temporary officers serve until the election of permanent officers.
   A. permanent
   B. Signature
   C. president
   D. reception

   *Answer:* A) permanent. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:32 — “If a permanent society is the aim of the group, the temporary officers serve until the election of permanent officers.”

**609.** `[C1334 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If more than one mass meeting is necessary to achieve a certain objective, or if the group is working toward the formation of an organized society, a temporary _____ to continue beyond a single mass meeting may become necessary.

   *Answer:* organization — “If more than one mass meeting is necessary to achieve a certain objective, or if the group is working toward the formation of an organized society, a temporary organization to continue beyond a single mass meeting may become necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §53:32 — “If more than one mass meeting is necessary to achieve a certain objective, or if the group is working toward the formation of an organized society, a temporary organization to continue beyond a single mass meeting may become necessary.”

**610.** `[C1335 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is desired to form a permanent society, the organizers proceed in much the same way as for a mass meeting, except that the meetings while the _____ is being formed should usually be carefully limited to persons whose interest in the project is known.

   *Answer:* organization — “When it is desired to form a permanent society, the organizers proceed in much the same way as for a mass meeting, except that the meetings while the organization is being formed should usually be carefully limited to persons whose interest in the project is known.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:1 — “When it is desired to form a permanent society, the organizers proceed in much the same way as for a mass meeting, except that the meetings while the organization is being formed should usually be carefully limited to persons whose interest in the project is known.”

**611.** `[C1335 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For this reason, it may be desirable to solicit attendance for these meetings by personal contact or by letter, rather than by public _____.
   A. announcement
   B. interrupted
   C. immediately
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* A) announcement. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:1 — “For this reason, it may be desirable to solicit attendance for these meetings by personal contact or by letter, rather than by public announcement.”

**612.** `[C1335 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is desired to form a permanent society, the organizers proceed in much the same way as for a mass meeting, except that the meetings while the _____ is being formed should usually be carefully limited to persons whose interest in the project is known.
   A. organization
   B. consideration
   C. constitution
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:1 — “When it is desired to form a permanent society, the organizers proceed in much the same way as for a mass meeting, except that the meetings while the organization is being formed should usually be carefully limited to persons whose interest in the project is known.”

**613.** `[C1335 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For this reason, it may be desirable to solicit attendance for these meetings by personal contact or by letter, rather than by public _____.

   *Answer:* announcement — “For this reason, it may be desirable to solicit attendance for these meetings by personal contact or by letter, rather than by public announcement.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:1 — “For this reason, it may be desirable to solicit attendance for these meetings by personal contact or by letter, rather than by public announcement.”

**614.** `[C1336 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The first meeting, at which the business portion should be kept brief, _____ follows a luncheon or dinner.

   *Answer:* sometimes — “The first meeting, at which the business portion should be kept brief, sometimes follows a luncheon or dinner.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:2 — “The first meeting, at which the business portion should be kept brief, sometimes follows a luncheon or dinner.”

**615.** `[C1336 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At these meetings for purposes of _____, the call to order can be delayed a few minutes beyond the scheduled time, if desired.
   A. Particular
   B. resolutions
   C. organization
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:2 — “At these meetings for purposes of organization, the call to order can be delayed a few minutes beyond the scheduled time, if desired.”

**616.** `[C1336 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The first meeting, at which the business portion should be kept brief, _____ follows a luncheon or dinner.
   A. sometimes
   B. exhausted
   C. Committee
   D. paragraph

   *Answer:* A) sometimes. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:2 — “The first meeting, at which the business portion should be kept brief, sometimes follows a luncheon or dinner.”

**617.** `[C1336 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At these meetings for purposes of _____, the call to order can be delayed a few minutes beyond the scheduled time, if desired.

   *Answer:* organization — “At these meetings for purposes of organization, the call to order can be delayed a few minutes beyond the scheduled time, if desired.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:2 — “At these meetings for purposes of organization, the call to order can be delayed a few minutes beyond the scheduled time, if desired.”

**618.** `[C1337 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Election of Temporary Officers, and _____ Talks.

   *Answer:* Introductory — “Election of Temporary Officers, and Introductory Talks.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:3 — “Election of Temporary Officers, and Introductory Talks.”

**619.** `[C1337 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the person designated for the purpose has called the meeting to order, he announces, “The first business is the election of a chairman.” As in a mass meeting, the one who calls the meeting to order can either nominate a chairman pro tem or _____ call for nominations from the floor, and the nominees are voted on by voice.
   A. immediately
   B. convention
   C. Unfinished
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:3 — “When the person designated for the purpose has called the meeting to order, he announces, “The first business is the election of a chairman.” As in a mass meeting, the one who calls the meeting to order can either nominate a chairman pro tem or immediately call for nominations from the floor, and the nominees are voted on by voice.”

**620.** `[C1337 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Election of Temporary Officers, and _____ Talks.
   A. Procedures
   B. Introductory
   C. Particular
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* B) Introductory. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:3 — “Election of Temporary Officers, and Introductory Talks.”

**621.** `[C1337 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the person designated for the purpose has called the meeting to order, he announces, “The first business is the election of a chairman.” As in a mass meeting, the one who calls the meeting to order can either nominate a chairman pro tem or _____ call for nominations from the floor, and the nominees are voted on by voice.

   *Answer:* immediately — “When the person designated for the purpose has called the meeting to order, he announces, “The first business is the election of a chairman.” As in a mass meeting, the one who calls the meeting to order can either nominate a chairman pro tem or immediately call for nominations from the floor, and the nominees are voted on by voice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:3 — “When the person designated for the purpose has called the meeting to order, he announces, “The first business is the election of a chairman.” As in a mass meeting, the one who calls the meeting to order can either nominate a chairman pro tem or immediately call for nominations from the floor, and the nominees are voted on by voice.”

**622.** `[C1338 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then calls on the member most interested in the formation of the society to provide background _____, or he himself can make the talk.

   *Answer:* information — “The chair then calls on the member most interested in the formation of the society to provide background information, or he himself can make the talk.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:4 — “The chair then calls on the member most interested in the formation of the society to provide background information, or he himself can make the talk.”

**623.** `[C1338 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Others can also be asked to give their opinions on the subject, but the chair should not permit any one person to _____ the meeting.
   A. resolution
   B. authorizes
   C. incidental
   D. monopolize

   *Answer:* D) monopolize. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:4 — “Others can also be asked to give their opinions on the subject, but the chair should not permit any one person to monopolize the meeting.”

**624.** `[C1338 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then calls on the member most interested in the formation of the society to provide background _____, or he himself can make the talk.
   A. information
   B. Unfinished
   C. Definitely
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:4 — “The chair then calls on the member most interested in the formation of the society to provide background information, or he himself can make the talk.”

**625.** `[C1338 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Others can also be asked to give their opinions on the subject, but the chair should not permit any one person to _____ the meeting.

   *Answer:* monopolize — “Others can also be asked to give their opinions on the subject, but the chair should not permit any one person to monopolize the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:4 — “Others can also be asked to give their opinions on the subject, but the chair should not permit any one person to monopolize the meeting.”

**626.** `[C1339 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Adoption of a _____ to Form a Society.

   *Answer:* Resolution — “Adoption of a Resolution to Form a Society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:5 — “Adoption of a Resolution to Form a Society.”

**627.** `[C1339 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a _____ time for such informal discussion, someone should offer a resolution proposing definite action.
   A. Incidental
   B. Permission
   C. resolution
   D. reasonable

   *Answer:* D) reasonable. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:5 — “After a reasonable time for such informal discussion, someone should offer a resolution proposing definite action.”

**628.** `[C1339 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Those who planned the meeting should have prepared in advance a suitable resolution, which may be in a form _____ as follows:

   *Answer:* essentially — “Those who planned the meeting should have prepared in advance a suitable resolution, which may be in a form essentially as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:5 — “Those who planned the meeting should have prepared in advance a suitable resolution, which may be in a form essentially as follows:”

**629.** `[C1339 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Adoption of a _____ to Form a Society.
   A. incidental
   B. Particular
   C. assemblies
   D. Resolution

   *Answer:* D) Resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:5 — “Adoption of a Resolution to Form a Society.”

**630.** `[C1339 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a _____ time for such informal discussion, someone should offer a resolution proposing definite action.

   *Answer:* reasonable — “After a reasonable time for such informal discussion, someone should offer a resolution proposing definite action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:5 — “After a reasonable time for such informal discussion, someone should offer a resolution proposing definite action.”

**631.** `[C1340 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, That it is the sense of this meeting that a society for… [the object of the proposed society] now be formed [or “shall now be formed”].

   *Answer:* Resolved — “Resolved, That it is the sense of this meeting that a society for… [the object of the proposed society] now be formed [or “shall now be formed”].”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:6 — “Resolved, That it is the sense of this meeting that a society for… [the object of the proposed society] now be formed [or “shall now be formed”].”

**632.** `[C1340 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, That it is the sense of this meeting that a society for… [the object of the proposed society] now be formed [or “shall now be formed”].
   A. Resolved
   B. business
   C. dilatory
   D. Standing

   *Answer:* A) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:6 — “Resolved, That it is the sense of this meeting that a society for… [the object of the proposed society] now be formed [or “shall now be formed”].”

**633.** `[C1341 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This _____, when seconded, is stated by the chair, and is then open to debate and amendment.

   *Answer:* resolution — “This resolution, when seconded, is stated by the chair, and is then open to debate and amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:7 — “This resolution, when seconded, is stated by the chair, and is then open to debate and amendment.”

**634.** `[C1341 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a resolution, it should be noted, is only a _____ of intention;
   A. declaration
   B. adjournment
   C. Indefinitely
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) declaration. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:7 — “Such a resolution, it should be noted, is only a declaration of intention;”

**635.** `[C1341 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: its adoption does not bring the _____ into being, which is accomplished by the adoption of bylaws and the signing of the membership roll by those who initially join the society, as described below.

   *Answer:* organization — “its adoption does not bring the organization into being, which is accomplished by the adoption of bylaws and the signing of the membership roll by those who initially join the society, as described below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:7 — “its adoption does not bring the organization into being, which is accomplished by the adoption of bylaws and the signing of the membership roll by those who initially join the society, as described below.”

**636.** `[C1341 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the meeting is a large one, it is usually better that, except for a brief statement of purpose, the resolution be offered before the _____ talks mentioned above.
   A. introductory
   B. Incidental
   C. constitution
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) introductory. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:7 — “If the meeting is a large one, it is usually better that, except for a brief statement of purpose, the resolution be offered before the introductory talks mentioned above.”

**637.** `[C1341 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This _____, when seconded, is stated by the chair, and is then open to debate and amendment.
   A. incidental
   B. Reconsider
   C. substitute
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* D) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:7 — “This resolution, when seconded, is stated by the chair, and is then open to debate and amendment.”

**638.** `[C1342 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Further Business Relating to _____.

   *Answer:* Organization — “Further Business Relating to Organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:8 — “Further Business Relating to Organization.”

**639.** `[C1342 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the _____ to organize the society is adopted, the succeeding steps generally are:
   A. landscaped
   B. introduced
   C. resolution
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:8 — “After the resolution to organize the society is adopted, the succeeding steps generally are:”

**640.** `[C1342 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Further Business Relating to _____.
   A. Particular
   B. constitution
   C. Information
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* D) Organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:8 — “Further Business Relating to Organization.”

**641.** `[C1342 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the _____ to organize the society is adopted, the succeeding steps generally are:

   *Answer:* resolution — “After the resolution to organize the society is adopted, the succeeding steps generally are:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:8 — “After the resolution to organize the society is adopted, the succeeding steps generally are:”

**642.** `[C1343 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Other business before _____ may include informal discussion of aims and structure of the proposed society— which may serve to guide the bylaws committee (see also below).

   *Answer:* adjournment — “Other business before adjournment may include informal discussion of aims and structure of the proposed society— which may serve to guide the bylaws committee (see also below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:9 — “Other business before adjournment may include informal discussion of aims and structure of the proposed society— which may serve to guide the bylaws committee (see also below).”

**643.** `[C1343 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Other business before _____ may include informal discussion of aims and structure of the proposed society— which may serve to guide the bylaws committee (see also below).
   A. adjournment
   B. Organization
   C. Reconsider
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) adjournment. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:9 — “Other business before adjournment may include informal discussion of aims and structure of the proposed society— which may serve to guide the bylaws committee (see also below).”

**644.** `[C1344 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (2) “The meeting is _____ to meet again at the call of the chair.”

   *Answer:* adjourned — “or (2) “The meeting is adjourned to meet again at the call of the chair.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:10 — “or (2) “The meeting is adjourned to meet again at the call of the chair.””

**645.** `[C1344 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or (2) “The meeting is _____ to meet again at the call of the chair.”
   A. Precedent
   B. adjourned
   C. Reporting
   D. committee

   *Answer:* B) adjourned. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:10 — “or (2) “The meeting is adjourned to meet again at the call of the chair.””

**646.** `[C1345 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: General _____ for guidance in the drafting of bylaws are given in 56.

   *Answer:* principles — “General principles for guidance in the drafting of bylaws are given in 56.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:11 — “General principles for guidance in the drafting of bylaws are given in 56.”

**647.** `[C1345 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The drafting committee may find it helpful to procure and study copies of the bylaws of other _____ similar to the one being formed, although the possible applicability of their provisions must be carefully evaluated in the light of expected conditions within the new society.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Parliamentary
   C. requirement
   D. organizations

   *Answer:* D) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:11 — “The drafting committee may find it helpful to procure and study copies of the bylaws of other organizations similar to the one being formed, although the possible applicability of their provisions must be carefully evaluated in the light of expected conditions within the new society.”

**648.** `[C1345 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The committee may also find it advisable to consult a professional _____.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “The committee may also find it advisable to consult a professional parliamentarian.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:11 — “The committee may also find it advisable to consult a professional parliamentarian.”

**649.** `[C1345 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: General _____ for guidance in the drafting of bylaws are given in 56.
   A. sustaining
   B. principles
   C. Convention
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) principles. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:11 — “General principles for guidance in the drafting of bylaws are given in 56.”

**650.** `[C1345 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The drafting committee may find it helpful to procure and study copies of the bylaws of other _____ similar to the one being formed, although the possible applicability of their provisions must be carefully evaluated in the light of expected conditions within the new society.

   *Answer:* organizations — “The drafting committee may find it helpful to procure and study copies of the bylaws of other organizations similar to the one being formed, although the possible applicability of their provisions must be carefully evaluated in the light of expected conditions within the new society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:11 — “The drafting committee may find it helpful to procure and study copies of the bylaws of other organizations similar to the one being formed, although the possible applicability of their provisions must be carefully evaluated in the light of expected conditions within the new society.”

**651.** `[C1346 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, the bylaws committee should be authorized to have one or more of its members consult an attorney to secure information and advice regarding the legal _____ that must be taken into account in drawing up the society’s bylaws.

   *Answer:* requirements — “In such a case, the bylaws committee should be authorized to have one or more of its members consult an attorney to secure information and advice regarding the legal requirements that must be taken into account in drawing up the society’s bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:12 — “In such a case, the bylaws committee should be authorized to have one or more of its members consult an attorney to secure information and advice regarding the legal requirements that must be taken into account in drawing up the society’s bylaws.”

**652.** `[C1346 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the society is to be incorporated, the same attorney should draft the charter or other instrument of incorporation, which the committee submits for approval at the second _____ meeting, before the bylaws are considered, unless there is some reason for delay (see below).
   A. organizational
   B. requirement
   C. resolutions
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* A) organizational. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:12 — “If the society is to be incorporated, the same attorney should draft the charter or other instrument of incorporation, which the committee submits for approval at the second organizational meeting, before the bylaws are considered, unless there is some reason for delay (see below).”

**653.** `[C1346 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, the bylaws committee should be authorized to have one or more of its members consult an attorney to secure information and advice regarding the legal _____ that must be taken into account in drawing up the society’s bylaws.
   A. Procedures
   B. requirements
   C. Announcements
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) requirements. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:12 — “In such a case, the bylaws committee should be authorized to have one or more of its members consult an attorney to secure information and advice regarding the legal requirements that must be taken into account in drawing up the society’s bylaws.”

**654.** `[C1346 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the society is to be incorporated, the same attorney should draft the charter or other instrument of incorporation, which the committee submits for approval at the second _____ meeting, before the bylaws are considered, unless there is some reason for delay (see below).

   *Answer:* organizational — “If the society is to be incorporated, the same attorney should draft the charter or other instrument of incorporation, which the committee submits for approval at the second organizational meeting, before the bylaws are considered, unless there is some reason for delay (see below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:12 — “If the society is to be incorporated, the same attorney should draft the charter or other instrument of incorporation, which the committee submits for approval at the second organizational meeting, before the bylaws are considered, unless there is some reason for delay (see below).”

**655.** `[C1347 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As indicated above, it is advisable to prepare doublespaced reproduced copies of the proposed bylaws—as drawn up by the committee—for distribution to each person entering the hall for the second _____ meeting.

   *Answer:* organizational — “As indicated above, it is advisable to prepare doublespaced reproduced copies of the proposed bylaws—as drawn up by the committee—for distribution to each person entering the hall for the second organizational meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:13 — “As indicated above, it is advisable to prepare doublespaced reproduced copies of the proposed bylaws—as drawn up by the committee—for distribution to each person entering the hall for the second organizational meeting.”

**656.** `[C1347 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If desired, such copies can be sent in advance to _____ who attended the first meeting.
   A. Business
   B. adoption
   C. everyone
   D. Standard

   *Answer:* C) everyone. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:13 — “If desired, such copies can be sent in advance to everyone who attended the first meeting.”

**657.** `[C1347 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As indicated above, it is advisable to prepare doublespaced reproduced copies of the proposed bylaws—as drawn up by the committee—for distribution to each person entering the hall for the second _____ meeting.
   A. Announcements
   B. organizational
   C. Responsibilities
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) organizational. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:13 — “As indicated above, it is advisable to prepare doublespaced reproduced copies of the proposed bylaws—as drawn up by the committee—for distribution to each person entering the hall for the second organizational meeting.”

**658.** `[C1347 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If desired, such copies can be sent in advance to _____ who attended the first meeting.

   *Answer:* everyone — “If desired, such copies can be sent in advance to everyone who attended the first meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:13 — “If desired, such copies can be sent in advance to everyone who attended the first meeting.”

**659.** `[C1348 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: With the temporary officers elected at the first _____ meeting serving until the regular officers are elected, the first item of business at the second meeting is the reading and approval of the minutes of the first meeting, with corrections if necessary.

   *Answer:* organizational — “With the temporary officers elected at the first organizational meeting serving until the regular officers are elected, the first item of business at the second meeting is the reading and approval of the minutes of the first meeting, with corrections if necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:14 — “With the temporary officers elected at the first organizational meeting serving until the regular officers are elected, the first item of business at the second meeting is the reading and approval of the minutes of the first meeting, with corrections if necessary.”

**660.** `[C1348 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: With the temporary officers elected at the first _____ meeting serving until the regular officers are elected, the first item of business at the second meeting is the reading and approval of the minutes of the first meeting, with corrections if necessary.
   A. Parliamentary
   B. organizational
   C. interrupted
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* B) organizational. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:14 — “With the temporary officers elected at the first organizational meeting serving until the regular officers are elected, the first item of business at the second meeting is the reading and approval of the minutes of the first meeting, with corrections if necessary.”

**661.** `[C1349 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ and Adoption of Proposed Bylaws.

   *Answer:* Consideration — “Consideration and Adoption of Proposed Bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:15 — “Consideration and Adoption of Proposed Bylaws.”

**662.** `[C1349 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the minutes are _____, the report of the bylaws committee normally is received.
   A. Meetings
   B. Interest
   C. approved
   D. limiting

   *Answer:* C) approved. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:15 — “After the minutes are approved, the report of the bylaws committee normally is received.”

**663.** `[C1349 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is a proposed _____ charter, that document is presented first.

   *Answer:* corporate — “If there is a proposed corporate charter, that document is presented first.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:15 — “If there is a proposed corporate charter, that document is presented first.”

**664.** `[C1349 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The assembly can amend the draft of the charter, but any resulting modification should be checked by the attorney, to whom the charter is returned after its adoption, for processing under the legal procedure for _____ in the particular state.
   A. characteristics
   B. incorporation
   C. Parliamentary
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) incorporation. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:15 — “The assembly can amend the draft of the charter, but any resulting modification should be checked by the attorney, to whom the charter is returned after its adoption, for processing under the legal procedure for incorporation in the particular state.”

**665.** `[C1349 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ and Adoption of Proposed Bylaws.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. immediately
   C. Consideration
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* C) Consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:15 — “Consideration and Adoption of Proposed Bylaws.”

**666.** `[C1350 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is no proposed corporate charter, the bylaws committee chairman, when _____ for the purpose of presenting the report, begins somewhat as follows:

   *Answer:* recognized — “If there is no proposed corporate charter, the bylaws committee chairman, when recognized for the purpose of presenting the report, begins somewhat as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:16 — “If there is no proposed corporate charter, the bylaws committee chairman, when recognized for the purpose of presenting the report, begins somewhat as follows:”

**667.** `[C1350 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is no proposed corporate charter, the bylaws committee chairman, when _____ for the purpose of presenting the report, begins somewhat as follows:
   A. recognized
   B. settlement
   C. resolution
   D. committees

   *Answer:* A) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:16 — “If there is no proposed corporate charter, the bylaws committee chairman, when recognized for the purpose of presenting the report, begins somewhat as follows:”

**668.** `[C1351 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since a complete set of bylaws is commonly considered _____ (see 28), the chair states the question as follows:

   *Answer:* by article or section — “Since a complete set of bylaws is commonly considered by article or section (see 28), the chair states the question as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:17 — “Since a complete set of bylaws is commonly considered by article or section (see 28), the chair states the question as follows:”

**669.** `[C1351 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No second is _____, since the motion is offered by a committee of more than one person.
   A. necessary
   B. paragraph
   C. committee
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* A) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:17 — “No second is necessary, since the motion is offered by a committee of more than one person.”

**670.** `[C1351 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since a complete set of bylaws is commonly _____ by article or section (see 28), the chair states the question as follows:

   *Answer:* considered — “Since a complete set of bylaws is commonly considered by article or section (see 28), the chair states the question as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:17 — “Since a complete set of bylaws is commonly considered by article or section (see 28), the chair states the question as follows:”

**671.** `[C1351 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No second is _____, since the motion is offered by a committee of more than one person.

   *Answer:* necessary — “No second is necessary, since the motion is offered by a committee of more than one person.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:17 — “No second is necessary, since the motion is offered by a committee of more than one person.”

**672.** `[C1352 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each article or section is read _____, each provision being carefully explained by the chairman of the bylaws committee, as described above;

   *Answer:* separately — “Each article or section is read separately, each provision being carefully explained by the chairman of the bylaws committee, as described above;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:18 — “Each article or section is read separately, each provision being carefully explained by the chairman of the bylaws committee, as described above;”

**673.** `[C1352 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and after the last one has been completed, the chair gives opportunity to insert additional paragraphs or sections and to correct any _____ or oversight that may have arisen during the process of amendment, as follows:
   A. inconsistency
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. consideration
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) inconsistency. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:18 — “and after the last one has been completed, the chair gives opportunity to insert additional paragraphs or sections and to correct any inconsistency or oversight that may have arisen during the process of amendment, as follows:”

**674.** `[C1352 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each article or section is read _____, each provision being carefully explained by the chairman of the bylaws committee, as described above;
   A. convention
   B. separately
   C. Particular
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) separately. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:18 — “Each article or section is read separately, each provision being carefully explained by the chairman of the bylaws committee, as described above;”

**675.** `[C1352 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and after the last one has been completed, the chair gives opportunity to insert additional paragraphs or sections and to correct any _____ or oversight that may have arisen during the process of amendment, as follows:

   *Answer:* inconsistency — “and after the last one has been completed, the chair gives opportunity to insert additional paragraphs or sections and to correct any inconsistency or oversight that may have arisen during the process of amendment, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:18 — “and after the last one has been completed, the chair gives opportunity to insert additional paragraphs or sections and to correct any inconsistency or oversight that may have arisen during the process of amendment, as follows:”

**676.** `[C1353 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, at any point during the _____ of the bylaws, it develops that important additions or amendments are desirable but will require time or investigation to prepare, it is in order to move to recommit (13) the proposed bylaws, with instructions that the committee report at another meeting for which the time can be fixed.

   *Answer:* consideration — “If, at any point during the consideration of the bylaws, it develops that important additions or amendments are desirable but will require time or investigation to prepare, it is in order to move to recommit (13) the proposed bylaws, with instructions that the committee report at another meeting for which the time can be fixed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:19 — “If, at any point during the consideration of the bylaws, it develops that important additions or amendments are desirable but will require time or investigation to prepare, it is in order to move to recommit (13) the proposed bylaws, with instructions that the committee report at another meeting for which the time can be fixed.”

**677.** `[C1353 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This third meeting in forming an organization, although in many cases unnecessary, in others often pays dividends in increased _____ and a larger membership.
   A. understanding
   B. Organization
   C. resolutions
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* A) understanding. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:19 — “This third meeting in forming an organization, although in many cases unnecessary, in others often pays dividends in increased understanding and a larger membership.”

**678.** `[C1353 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, at any point during the consideration of the bylaws, it develops that important additions or _____ are desirable but will require time or investigation to prepare, it is in order to move to recommit (13) the proposed bylaws, with instructions that the committee report at another meeting for which the time can be fixed.

   *Answer:* amendments — “If, at any point during the consideration of the bylaws, it develops that important additions or amendments are desirable but will require time or investigation to prepare, it is in order to move to recommit (13) the proposed bylaws, with instructions that the committee report at another meeting for which the time can be fixed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:19 — “If, at any point during the consideration of the bylaws, it develops that important additions or amendments are desirable but will require time or investigation to prepare, it is in order to move to recommit (13) the proposed bylaws, with instructions that the committee report at another meeting for which the time can be fixed.”

**679.** `[C1353 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This third meeting in forming an organization, although in many cases unnecessary, in others often pays dividends in increased _____ and a larger membership.

   *Answer:* understanding — “This third meeting in forming an organization, although in many cases unnecessary, in others often pays dividends in increased understanding and a larger membership.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:19 — “This third meeting in forming an organization, although in many cases unnecessary, in others often pays dividends in increased understanding and a larger membership.”

**680.** `[C1354 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike the case of amending or revising the bylaws of an organization already established (57), the adoption of the bylaws through which a society is brought into being requires only a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “Unlike the case of amending or revising the bylaws of an organization already established (57), the adoption of the bylaws through which a society is brought into being requires only a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:20 — “Unlike the case of amending or revising the bylaws of an organization already established (57), the adoption of the bylaws through which a society is brought into being requires only a majority vote.”

**681.** `[C1354 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In case of doubt, the chair calls for a rising vote and, if _____, directs that a count be made;
   A. secondary
   B. committee
   C. Secretary
   D. necessary

   *Answer:* D) necessary. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:20 — “In case of doubt, the chair calls for a rising vote and, if necessary, directs that a count be made;”

**682.** `[C1354 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike the case of amending or revising the bylaws of an _____ already established (57), the adoption of the bylaws through which a society is brought into being requires only a majority vote.
