   *Answer:* organization — “Unlike the case of amending or revising the bylaws of an organization already established (57), the adoption of the bylaws through which a society is brought into being requires only a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:20 — “Unlike the case of amending or revising the bylaws of an organization already established (57), the adoption of the bylaws through which a society is brought into being requires only a majority vote.”

**683.** `[C1354 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The bylaws take effect _____ upon their adoption.
   A. assemblies
   B. immediately
   C. Preparation
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:20 — “The bylaws take effect immediately upon their adoption.”

**684.** `[C1354 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A negative vote on their adoption can be _____, but not an affirmative one.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “A negative vote on their adoption can be reconsidered, but not an affirmative one.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:20 — “A negative vote on their adoption can be reconsidered, but not an affirmative one.”

**685.** `[C1355 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the adoption of the bylaws, only those who join the society are entitled to vote in further _____.
   A. Definitely
   B. Preparation
   C. proceedings
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) proceedings. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:21 — “After the adoption of the bylaws, only those who join the society are entitled to vote in further proceedings.”

**686.** `[C1355 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At this point, therefore, the meeting _____ to enroll initial members.

   *Answer:* recesses — “At this point, therefore, the meeting recesses to enroll initial members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:21 — “At this point, therefore, the meeting recesses to enroll initial members.”

**687.** `[C1355 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Immediate admission to membership is contingent upon signing a permanent record sheet provided in advance by the secretary pro tem—to be filed with the original papers of the _____.
   A. organization
   B. requirement
   C. specification
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:21 — “Immediate admission to membership is contingent upon signing a permanent record sheet provided in advance by the secretary pro tem—to be filed with the original papers of the organization.”

**688.** `[C1355 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This signature _____ agreement to abide by the bylaws, and is a commitment to prompt payment of the initiation fee (if there is one) and dues for the first year or other period prescribed by the bylaws.

   *Answer:* constitutes — “This signature constitutes agreement to abide by the bylaws, and is a commitment to prompt payment of the initiation fee (if there is one) and dues for the first year or other period prescribed by the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:21 — “This signature constitutes agreement to abide by the bylaws, and is a commitment to prompt payment of the initiation fee (if there is one) and dues for the first year or other period prescribed by the bylaws.”

**689.** `[C1355 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Immediate admission to membership is contingent upon signing a permanent record sheet provided in advance by the _____ pro tem—to be filed with the original papers of the organization.
   A. Crestwood
   B. Precedent
   C. secretary
   D. Committee

   *Answer:* C) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:21 — “Immediate admission to membership is contingent upon signing a permanent record sheet provided in advance by the secretary pro tem—to be filed with the original papers of the organization.”

**690.** `[C1356 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Reading of the Roll and Election of _____ Officers.

   *Answer:* Permanent — “Reading of the Roll and Election of Permanent Officers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:22 — “Reading of the Roll and Election of Permanent Officers.”

**691.** `[C1356 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the recess the chairman pro tem calls for the reading of the roll of members, and the _____ pro tem does so.
   A. secretary
   B. postponed
   C. presiding
   D. auditors’

   *Answer:* A) secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:22 — “After the recess the chairman pro tem calls for the reading of the roll of members, and the secretary pro tem does so.”

**692.** `[C1356 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then says, “The next business in order is the _____ and election of the permanent officers as prescribed in the bylaws.”

   *Answer:* nomination — “The chair then says, “The next business in order is the nomination and election of the permanent officers as prescribed in the bylaws.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:22 — “The chair then says, “The next business in order is the nomination and election of the permanent officers as prescribed in the bylaws.””

**693.** `[C1356 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Reading of the Roll and Election of _____ Officers.
   A. debatable
   B. presiding
   C. Permanent
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* C) Permanent. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:22 — “Reading of the Roll and Election of Permanent Officers.”

**694.** `[C1356 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the recess the chairman pro tem calls for the reading of the roll of members, and the _____ pro tem does so.

   *Answer:* secretary — “After the recess the chairman pro tem calls for the reading of the roll of members, and the secretary pro tem does so.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:22 — “After the recess the chairman pro tem calls for the reading of the roll of members, and the secretary pro tem does so.”

**695.** `[C1357 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ and election processes are as described in 46, the election being by ballot if the bylaws so prescribe, which they usually should.

   *Answer:* nomination — “The nomination and election processes are as described in 46, the election being by ballot if the bylaws so prescribe, which they usually should.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:23 — “The nomination and election processes are as described in 46, the election being by ballot if the bylaws so prescribe, which they usually should.”

**696.** `[C1357 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The members for whom one can vote are not limited to nominees, since each member is free to vote for any member who is not made _____ by the bylaws.
   A. Permission
   B. Unfinished
   C. committees
   D. ineligible

   *Answer:* D) ineligible. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:23 — “The members for whom one can vote are not limited to nominees, since each member is free to vote for any member who is not made ineligible by the bylaws.”

**697.** `[C1357 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the election is _____, the chair declares the results.

   *Answer:* completed — “After the election is completed, the chair declares the results.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:23 — “After the election is completed, the chair declares the results.”

**698.** `[C1357 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ and election processes are as described in 46, the election being by ballot if the bylaws so prescribe, which they usually should.
   A. authorizes
   B. nomination
   C. Unfinished
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) nomination. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:23 — “The nomination and election processes are as described in 46, the election being by ballot if the bylaws so prescribe, which they usually should.”

**699.** `[C1357 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The members for whom one can vote are not limited to nominees, since each member is free to vote for any member who is not made _____ by the bylaws.

   *Answer:* ineligible — “The members for whom one can vote are not limited to nominees, since each member is free to vote for any member who is not made ineligible by the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:23 — “The members for whom one can vote are not limited to nominees, since each member is free to vote for any member who is not made ineligible by the bylaws.”

**700.** `[C1358 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the offices have been filled and the new president has taken the chair, he should call for any business _____ immediate attention.
   A. reception
   B. delegates
   C. committee
   D. requiring

   *Answer:* D) requiring. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:24 — “When the offices have been filled and the new president has taken the chair, he should call for any business requiring immediate attention.”

**701.** `[C1358 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a new society it is generally important that the president have time to give careful thought to committee _____ after examining the list of members.

   *Answer:* appointments — “In a new society it is generally important that the president have time to give careful thought to committee appointments after examining the list of members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:24 — “In a new society it is generally important that the president have time to give careful thought to committee appointments after examining the list of members.”

**702.** `[C1358 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is therefore often advisable to provide for an adjourned meeting to complete the _____ before the first regular meeting.
   A. Preparation
   B. Information
   C. organization
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:24 — “It is therefore often advisable to provide for an adjourned meeting to complete the organization before the first regular meeting.”

**703.** `[C1358 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The president may find it essential, however, to name the chairmen of certain committees, such as the membership or program committees, _____.

   *Answer:* immediately — “The president may find it essential, however, to name the chairmen of certain committees, such as the membership or program committees, immediately.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:24 — “The president may find it essential, however, to name the chairmen of certain committees, such as the membership or program committees, immediately.”

**704.** `[C1358 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the offices have been filled and the new president has taken the chair, he should call for any business _____ immediate attention.

   *Answer:* requiring — “When the offices have been filled and the new president has taken the chair, he should call for any business requiring immediate attention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:24 — “When the offices have been filled and the new president has taken the chair, he should call for any business requiring immediate attention.”

**705.** `[C1359 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the business of the meeting has been _____, or when an adjourned meeting has been provided for, a motion to adjourn is in order.

   *Answer:* completed — “When the business of the meeting has been completed, or when an adjourned meeting has been provided for, a motion to adjourn is in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:25 — “When the business of the meeting has been completed, or when an adjourned meeting has been provided for, a motion to adjourn is in order.”

**706.** `[C1359 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is adopted, the chair _____ the result and declares the meeting adjourned.
   A. delegates
   B. announces
   C. rejecting
   D. expressly

   *Answer:* B) announces. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:25 — “If it is adopted, the chair announces the result and declares the meeting adjourned.”

**707.** `[C1359 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the business of the meeting has been _____, or when an adjourned meeting has been provided for, a motion to adjourn is in order.
   A. delegates
   B. completed
   C. presiding
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* B) completed. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:25 — “When the business of the meeting has been completed, or when an adjourned meeting has been provided for, a motion to adjourn is in order.”

**708.** `[C1359 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is adopted, the chair _____ the result and declares the meeting adjourned.

   *Answer:* announces — “If it is adopted, the chair announces the result and declares the meeting adjourned.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:25 — “If it is adopted, the chair announces the result and declares the meeting adjourned.”

**709.** `[C1360 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ meetings of the society are conducted as described in 3 and 4.

   C. organization
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §54:26 — “For additional information regarding the organization of a federation by a convention of delegates from prospective member societies, see 60.”

**711.** `[C1360 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ meetings of the society are conducted as described in 3 and 4.
   A. Subsequent
   B. Particular
   C. Procedures
   D. landscaped

**712.** `[C1360 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For additional information regarding the _____ of a federation by a convention of delegates from prospective member societies, see 60.

See RONR (12th ed.) §54:26 — “For additional information regarding the organization of a federation by a convention of delegates from prospective member societies, see 60.”

**714.** `[C1361 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where two existing societies wish to combine, there are two possible _____, which are legally distinct:

   D. variations

**717.** `[C1362 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If one or more of the _____ involved in a merger or a consolidation are incorporated, an attorney should be consulted to draw up the proper papers and advise as to all steps necessary to fulfill the legal requirements.

   B. specification
   C. Vice-President
   D. organizations

   *Answer:* D) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §55:2 — “If one or more of the organizations involved in a merger or a consolidation are incorporated, an attorney should be consulted to draw up the proper papers and advise as to all steps necessary to fulfill the legal requirements.”

   C. organization

See RONR (12th ed.) §55:6 — “In the case of an unincorporated society, a resolution should be prepared, such as: “Resolved, That the X Society be dissolved as of March 31, 20.” This resolution may be preceded by a preamble setting forth the reasons for the dissolution.”

   B. Unincorporated
   C. Organization
   D. Parliamentary

**731.** `[C1366 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ notice must be sent to all members of record.

   *Answer:* required — “The required notice must be sent to all members of record.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §55:6 — “The required notice must be sent to all members of record.”

**732.** `[C1366 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of an unincorporated society, a resolution should be prepared, such as: “_____, That the X Society be dissolved as of March 31, 20.” This resolution may be preceded by a preamble setting forth the reasons for the dissolution.

   C. Nominees

**735.** `[C1367 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In certain tax-exempt _____ of a charitable or educational character, federal and state tax laws must be adhered to in the disposal of the organization’s assets.

   C. characteristics
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* A) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §55:7 — “In certain tax-exempt organizations of a charitable or educational character, federal and state tax laws must be adhered to in the disposal of the organization’s assets.”

**736.** `[C1367 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Often such assets are _____ to societies with similar objectives, or to a superior body.

   *Answer:* distributed — “Often such assets are distributed to societies with similar objectives, or to a superior body.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §55:7 — “Often such assets are distributed to societies with similar objectives, or to a superior body.”

**737.** `[C1367 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a resolution can be coupled with other resolutions stating the manner in which the society’s assets shall be disposed of and attending to other _____ details—or these can be adopted separately.
   A. specification
   B. requirement
   C. administrative
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* C) administrative. See RONR (12th ed.) §55:7 — “Such a resolution can be coupled with other resolutions stating the manner in which the society’s assets shall be disposed of and attending to other administrative details—or these can be adopted separately.”

**738.** `[C1367 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In certain tax-exempt _____ of a charitable or educational character, federal and state tax laws must be adhered to in the disposal of the organization’s assets.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In certain tax-exempt organizations of a charitable or educational character, federal and state tax laws must be adhered to in the disposal of the organization’s assets.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §55:7 — “In certain tax-exempt organizations of a charitable or educational character, federal and state tax laws must be adhered to in the disposal of the organization’s assets.”

**739.** `[C1368 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The constitution and/or _____ of a society, as explained in 2, contains its own basic rules that relate to itself as an organization, except for what must be included in the corporate charter of an incorporated society.

   *Answer:* bylaws — “The constitution and/or bylaws of a society, as explained in 2, contains its own basic rules that relate to itself as an organization, except for what must be included in the corporate charter of an incorporated society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:1 — “The constitution and/or bylaws of a society, as explained in 2, contains its own basic rules that relate to itself as an organization, except for what must be included in the corporate charter of an incorporated society.”

**740.** `[C1368 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ and/or bylaws of a society, as explained in 2, contains its own basic rules that relate to itself as an organization, except for what must be included in the corporate charter of an incorporated society.
   A. constitution
   B. adjournment
   C. deliberative
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) constitution. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:1 — “The constitution and/or bylaws of a society, as explained in 2, contains its own basic rules that relate to itself as an organization, except for what must be included in the corporate charter of an incorporated society.”

**741.** `[C1368 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Under the preferred practice for ordinary societies today, the constitution and the bylaws— once usually separate—are now combined in a single instrument, referred to in this book as the bylaws (although in some organizations called the constitution, or—even though only one document—the _____).

   *Answer:* constitution and bylaws — “Under the preferred practice for ordinary societies today, the constitution and the bylaws— once usually separate—are now combined in a single instrument, referred to in this book as the bylaws (although in some organizations called the constitution, or—even though only one document—the constitution and bylaws).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:1 — “Under the preferred practice for ordinary societies today, the constitution and the bylaws— once usually separate—are now combined in a single instrument, referred to in this book as the bylaws (although in some organizations called the constitution, or—even though only one document—the constitution and bylaws).”

**742.** `[C1368 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Under the preferred practice for ordinary societies today, the constitution and the bylaws— once usually separate—are now combined in a single instrument, referred to in this book as the bylaws (although in some _____ called the constitution, or—even though only one document—the constitution and bylaws).
   A. deliberative
   B. Vice-President
   C. organizations
   D. specification

   *Answer:* C) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:1 — “Under the preferred practice for ordinary societies today, the constitution and the bylaws— once usually separate—are now combined in a single instrument, referred to in this book as the bylaws (although in some organizations called the constitution, or—even though only one document—the constitution and bylaws).”

**743.** `[C1368 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A precise statement of the essential characteristics of bylaws, in the sense of the _____ instrument, and their relation to the other kinds of rules that an organization may have is given in 2, which should be read in connection with this chapter.

   *Answer:* combination-type — “A precise statement of the essential characteristics of bylaws, in the sense of the combination-type instrument, and their relation to the other kinds of rules that an organization may have is given in 2, which should be read in connection with this chapter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:1 — “A precise statement of the essential characteristics of bylaws, in the sense of the combination-type instrument, and their relation to the other kinds of rules that an organization may have is given in 2, which should be read in connection with this chapter.”

**744.** `[C1369 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except as the rules of a society may provide otherwise, its assembly (that is, the members attending one of its regular or properly called meetings) has full and sole power to act for the entire organization, and does so by _____ vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “Except as the rules of a society may provide otherwise, its assembly (that is, the members attending one of its regular or properly called meetings) has full and sole power to act for the entire organization, and does so by majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:2 — “Except as the rules of a society may provide otherwise, its assembly (that is, the members attending one of its regular or properly called meetings) has full and sole power to act for the entire organization, and does so by majority vote.”

**745.** `[C1369 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The content of a society’s bylaws has important bearing on the rights and duties of members within the _____— whether present or absent from the assembly—and on the degree to which the general membership is to retain control of, or be relieved of detailed concern with, the society’s business.
   A. immediately
   B. Preparation
   C. organization
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:2 — “The content of a society’s bylaws has important bearing on the rights and duties of members within the organization— whether present or absent from the assembly—and on the degree to which the general membership is to retain control of, or be relieved of detailed concern with, the society’s business.”

**746.** `[C1369 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any limitation or standing delegation of the assembly’s power with respect to the society as a whole can only be by provision in the bylaws— or in the corporate charter or separate _____, if either of these exists.

   *Answer:* constitution — “Any limitation or standing delegation of the assembly’s power with respect to the society as a whole can only be by provision in the bylaws— or in the corporate charter or separate constitution, if either of these exists.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:2 — “Any limitation or standing delegation of the assembly’s power with respect to the society as a whole can only be by provision in the bylaws— or in the corporate charter or separate constitution, if either of these exists.”

**747.** `[C1369 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except as the rules of a society may provide otherwise, its assembly (that is, the members attending one of its regular or properly called meetings) has full and sole power to act for the entire organization, and does so by _____ vote.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* C) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:2 — “Except as the rules of a society may provide otherwise, its assembly (that is, the members attending one of its regular or properly called meetings) has full and sole power to act for the entire organization, and does so by majority vote.”

**748.** `[C1369 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The content of a society’s bylaws has important bearing on the rights and duties of members within the _____— whether present or absent from the assembly—and on the degree to which the general membership is to retain control of, or be relieved of detailed concern with, the society’s business.

   *Answer:* organization — “The content of a society’s bylaws has important bearing on the rights and duties of members within the organization— whether present or absent from the assembly—and on the degree to which the general membership is to retain control of, or be relieved of detailed concern with, the society’s business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:2 — “The content of a society’s bylaws has important bearing on the rights and duties of members within the organization— whether present or absent from the assembly—and on the degree to which the general membership is to retain control of, or be relieved of detailed concern with, the society’s business.”

**749.** `[C1370 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee to draw up proposed bylaws is usually appointed at the first _____ meeting when a new society is being formed, as described in 54;
   A. Parliamentary
   B. Indefinitely
   C. recommendation
   D. organizational

   *Answer:* D) organizational. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:3 — “A committee to draw up proposed bylaws is usually appointed at the first organizational meeting when a new society is being formed, as described in 54;”

**750.** `[C1370 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, if an existing society wishes to _____ a general revision of its bylaws, a committee to draw up the proposed revision can be appointed at any regular meeting, just as any other special committee.

   *Answer:* undertake — “or, if an existing society wishes to undertake a general revision of its bylaws, a committee to draw up the proposed revision can be appointed at any regular meeting, just as any other special committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:3 — “or, if an existing society wishes to undertake a general revision of its bylaws, a committee to draw up the proposed revision can be appointed at any regular meeting, just as any other special committee.”

**751.** `[C1370 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee to draw up proposed bylaws is usually appointed at the first _____ meeting when a new society is being formed, as described in 54;

   *Answer:* organizational — “A committee to draw up proposed bylaws is usually appointed at the first organizational meeting when a new society is being formed, as described in 54;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:3 — “A committee to draw up proposed bylaws is usually appointed at the first organizational meeting when a new society is being formed, as described in 54;”

**752.** `[C1370 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, if an existing society wishes to _____ a general revision of its bylaws, a committee to draw up the proposed revision can be appointed at any regular meeting, just as any other special committee.
   A. execution
   B. undertake
   C. Committee
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* B) undertake. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:3 — “or, if an existing society wishes to undertake a general revision of its bylaws, a committee to draw up the proposed revision can be appointed at any regular meeting, just as any other special committee.”

**753.** `[C1371 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A committee to draw up proposed bylaws should generally be large, and should include the most judicious persons available, those who have a special interest in the rules of the society, and those who would otherwise be likely to consume much time in _____ the bylaws when they come before the assembly for adoption.

   *Answer:* discussing — “A committee to draw up proposed bylaws should generally be large, and should include the most judicious persons available, those who have a special interest in the rules of the society, and those who would otherwise be likely to consume much time in discussing the bylaws when they come before the assembly for adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:4 — “A committee to draw up proposed bylaws should generally be large, and should include the most judicious persons available, those who have a special interest in the rules of the society, and those who would otherwise be likely to consume much time in discussing the bylaws when they come before the assembly for adoption.”

**754.** `[C1371 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Persons having writing ability of the kind required should also be included, unless a professional _____ is to do the actual drafting of the bylaws.
   A. deliberative
   B. immediately
   C. specification
   D. parliamentarian

   *Answer:* D) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:4 — “Persons having writing ability of the kind required should also be included, unless a professional parliamentarian is to do the actual drafting of the bylaws.”

**755.** `[C1371 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A committee to draw up proposed bylaws should generally be large, and should include the most judicious persons available, those who have a special interest in the rules of the society, and those who would otherwise be likely to consume much time in _____ the bylaws when they come before the assembly for adoption.
   A. Particular
   B. persuasive
   C. settlement
   D. discussing

   *Answer:* D) discussing. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:4 — “A committee to draw up proposed bylaws should generally be large, and should include the most judicious persons available, those who have a special interest in the rules of the society, and those who would otherwise be likely to consume much time in discussing the bylaws when they come before the assembly for adoption.”

**756.** `[C1371 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Persons having writing ability of the kind required should also be included, unless a professional _____ is to do the actual drafting of the bylaws.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “Persons having writing ability of the kind required should also be included, unless a professional parliamentarian is to do the actual drafting of the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:4 — “Persons having writing ability of the kind required should also be included, unless a professional parliamentarian is to do the actual drafting of the bylaws.”

**757.** `[C1372 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is to be _____, the committee works with the attorney to provide him with the necessary information for drafting an appropriate corporate charter, to which the bylaws must conform.

   *Answer:* incorporated — “If it is to be incorporated, the committee works with the attorney to provide him with the necessary information for drafting an appropriate corporate charter, to which the bylaws must conform.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:5 — “If it is to be incorporated, the committee works with the attorney to provide him with the necessary information for drafting an appropriate corporate charter, to which the bylaws must conform.”

**758.** `[C1372 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The committee should review the draft of the charter before _____ it to the assembly.
   A. settlement
   B. submitting
   C. privileged
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) submitting. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:5 — “The committee should review the draft of the charter before submitting it to the assembly.”

**759.** `[C1372 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is to be _____, the committee works with the attorney to provide him with the necessary information for drafting an appropriate corporate charter, to which the bylaws must conform.
   A. incorporated
   B. Definitely
   C. deliberative
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) incorporated. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:5 — “If it is to be incorporated, the committee works with the attorney to provide him with the necessary information for drafting an appropriate corporate charter, to which the bylaws must conform.”

**760.** `[C1372 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The committee should review the draft of the charter before _____ it to the assembly.

   *Answer:* submitting — “The committee should review the draft of the charter before submitting it to the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:5 — “The committee should review the draft of the charter before submitting it to the assembly.”

**761.** `[C1373 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The committee normally begins its work—with the entire committee present—in general _____ of the desired content of the bylaws.

   *Answer:* discussion — “The committee normally begins its work—with the entire committee present—in general discussion of the desired content of the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:6 — “The committee normally begins its work—with the entire committee present—in general discussion of the desired content of the bylaws.”

**762.** `[C1373 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Besides reviewing the existing bylaws (in the case of a revision), it is well for the committee to study the bylaws of a number of similar _____, or—if applicable—of other subordinate units within the same state or national society.
   A. organizations
   B. Parliamentary
   C. presentation
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* A) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:6 — “Besides reviewing the existing bylaws (in the case of a revision), it is well for the committee to study the bylaws of a number of similar organizations, or—if applicable—of other subordinate units within the same state or national society.”

**763.** `[C1373 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The committee normally begins its work—with the entire committee present—in general _____ of the desired content of the bylaws.
   A. substitute
   B. convention
   C. discussion
   D. incidental

   *Answer:* C) discussion. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:6 — “The committee normally begins its work—with the entire committee present—in general discussion of the desired content of the bylaws.”

**764.** `[C1373 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Besides reviewing the existing bylaws (in the case of a revision), it is well for the committee to study the bylaws of a number of similar _____, or—if applicable—of other subordinate units within the same state or national society.

   *Answer:* organizations — “Besides reviewing the existing bylaws (in the case of a revision), it is well for the committee to study the bylaws of a number of similar organizations, or—if applicable—of other subordinate units within the same state or national society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:6 — “Besides reviewing the existing bylaws (in the case of a revision), it is well for the committee to study the bylaws of a number of similar organizations, or—if applicable—of other subordinate units within the same state or national society.”

**765.** `[C1374 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, if the superior body limits the size of its subordinate units to _____, the bylaws may not contain a higher limit.

   *Answer:* 200 members — “For example, if the superior body limits the size of its subordinate units to 200 members, the bylaws may not contain a higher limit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:7 — “For example, if the superior body limits the size of its subordinate units to 200 members, the bylaws may not contain a higher limit.”

**766.** `[C1374 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the unit for which the bylaws are to be drawn up is subject to a parent _____ or superior body, such as a state or a national society (or both), or a federation, the bylaws governing at these higher levels should be studied for provisions which are binding upon subordinate units in a way that must be taken into account.
   A. organization
   B. constitution
   C. Definitely
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:7 — “If the unit for which the bylaws are to be drawn up is subject to a parent organization or superior body, such as a state or a national society (or both), or a federation, the bylaws governing at these higher levels should be studied for provisions which are binding upon subordinate units in a way that must be taken into account.”

**767.** `[C1374 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the unit for which the bylaws are to be drawn up is subject to a parent organization or superior body, such as a state or a national society (or both), or a federation, the bylaws governing at these higher levels should be studied for provisions which are binding upon _____ units in a way that must be taken into account.

   *Answer:* subordinate — “If the unit for which the bylaws are to be drawn up is subject to a parent organization or superior body, such as a state or a national society (or both), or a federation, the bylaws governing at these higher levels should be studied for provisions which are binding upon subordinate units in a way that must be taken into account.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:7 — “If the unit for which the bylaws are to be drawn up is subject to a parent organization or superior body, such as a state or a national society (or both), or a federation, the bylaws governing at these higher levels should be studied for provisions which are binding upon subordinate units in a way that must be taken into account.”

**768.** `[C1374 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, if the superior body limits the size of its subordinate units to _____, the bylaws may not contain a higher limit.
   A. Proxy Voting
   B. and Example
   C. 200 members
   D. Discharge a

   *Answer:* C) 200 members. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:7 — “For example, if the superior body limits the size of its subordinate units to 200 members, the bylaws may not contain a higher limit.”

**769.** `[C1375 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In order to give the organization the greatest freedom to act within its object, bylaws should be made no more restrictive nor more detailed in _____ than necessary.

   *Answer:* specification — “In order to give the organization the greatest freedom to act within its object, bylaws should be made no more restrictive nor more detailed in specification than necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:8 — “In order to give the organization the greatest freedom to act within its object, bylaws should be made no more restrictive nor more detailed in specification than necessary.”

**770.** `[C1375 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In order to give the organization the greatest freedom to act within its object, bylaws should be made no more restrictive nor more detailed in _____ than necessary.
   A. constitution
   B. Vice-President
   C. requirement
   D. specification

   *Answer:* D) specification. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:8 — “In order to give the organization the greatest freedom to act within its object, bylaws should be made no more restrictive nor more detailed in specification than necessary.”

**771.** `[C1376 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a model can only _____ how a typical document of this kind is put together, however;

   *Answer:* illustrate — “Such a model can only illustrate how a typical document of this kind is put together, however;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:9 — “Such a model can only illustrate how a typical document of this kind is put together, however;”

**772.** `[C1376 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and the provisions must be varied, additional ones inserted, or _____ ones omitted, as appropriate to the individual organization.
   A. specification
   B. deliberative
   C. inapplicable
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) inapplicable. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:9 — “and the provisions must be varied, additional ones inserted, or inapplicable ones omitted, as appropriate to the individual organization.”

**773.** `[C1376 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a model can only _____ how a typical document of this kind is put together, however;
   A. Convention
   B. Reconsider
   C. illustrate
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) illustrate. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:9 — “Such a model can only illustrate how a typical document of this kind is put together, however;”

**774.** `[C1376 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and the provisions must be varied, additional ones inserted, or _____ ones omitted, as appropriate to the individual organization.

   *Answer:* inapplicable — “and the provisions must be varied, additional ones inserted, or inapplicable ones omitted, as appropriate to the individual organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:9 — “and the provisions must be varied, additional ones inserted, or inapplicable ones omitted, as appropriate to the individual organization.”

**775.** `[C1377 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After conferences on the topics described above, the committee should appoint a drafting _____, or several of them for various articles if the bylaws are expected to be long and complex.

   *Answer:* subcommittee — “After conferences on the topics described above, the committee should appoint a drafting subcommittee, or several of them for various articles if the bylaws are expected to be long and complex.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:10 — “After conferences on the topics described above, the committee should appoint a drafting subcommittee, or several of them for various articles if the bylaws are expected to be long and complex.”

**776.** `[C1377 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Another subcommittee may be needed in the latter case to eliminate _____, make the style uniform, and make sure that, as far as possible, everything relating to a single subject is placed in the same or adjacent articles.
   A. characteristics
   B. recommendation
   C. immediately
   D. inconsistencies

   *Answer:* D) inconsistencies. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:10 — “Another subcommittee may be needed in the latter case to eliminate inconsistencies, make the style uniform, and make sure that, as far as possible, everything relating to a single subject is placed in the same or adjacent articles.”

**777.** `[C1377 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After conferences on the topics described above, the committee should appoint a drafting _____, or several of them for various articles if the bylaws are expected to be long and complex.
   A. Announcements
   B. Particular
   C. Procedures
   D. subcommittee

   *Answer:* D) subcommittee. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:10 — “After conferences on the topics described above, the committee should appoint a drafting subcommittee, or several of them for various articles if the bylaws are expected to be long and complex.”

**778.** `[C1377 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Another subcommittee may be needed in the latter case to eliminate _____, make the style uniform, and make sure that, as far as possible, everything relating to a single subject is placed in the same or adjacent articles.

   *Answer:* inconsistencies — “Another subcommittee may be needed in the latter case to eliminate inconsistencies, make the style uniform, and make sure that, as far as possible, everything relating to a single subject is placed in the same or adjacent articles.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:10 — “Another subcommittee may be needed in the latter case to eliminate inconsistencies, make the style uniform, and make sure that, as far as possible, everything relating to a single subject is placed in the same or adjacent articles.”

**779.** `[C1378 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of bylaws is somewhat different from ordinary expository writing, in that it places greater demand on a “tight” clarity and precision in word choice, sentence structure, and punctuation.

   *Answer:* composition — “The composition of bylaws is somewhat different from ordinary expository writing, in that it places greater demand on a “tight” clarity and precision in word choice, sentence structure, and punctuation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:11 — “The composition of bylaws is somewhat different from ordinary expository writing, in that it places greater demand on a “tight” clarity and precision in word choice, sentence structure, and punctuation.”

**780.** `[C1378 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The composition of bylaws is somewhat different from ordinary expository writing, in that it places greater demand on a “tight” clarity and precision in word choice, sentence structure, and _____.
   A. adjournment
   B. punctuation
   C. Incidental
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* B) punctuation. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:11 — “The composition of bylaws is somewhat different from ordinary expository writing, in that it places greater demand on a “tight” clarity and precision in word choice, sentence structure, and punctuation.”

**781.** `[C1378 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and what is omitted may carry as much _____ as what is included.

   *Answer:* significance — “and what is omitted may carry as much significance as what is included.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:11 — “and what is omitted may carry as much significance as what is included.”

**782.** `[C1378 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of meaning and application is a more important consideration than “readability,” and the latter must be sacrificed when both cannot be achieved.
   A. Indisputability
   B. consideration
   C. Responsibilities
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) Indisputability. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:11 — “Indisputability of meaning and application is a more important consideration than “readability,” and the latter must be sacrificed when both cannot be achieved.”

**783.** `[C1378 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each sentence should be written so as to be _____ to quote out of context;

   *Answer:* impossible — “Each sentence should be written so as to be impossible to quote out of context;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:11 — “Each sentence should be written so as to be impossible to quote out of context;”

**784.** `[C1381 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the first draft of the bylaws has been completed, it should be given thorough critical _____ in discussions by the full committee.

   *Answer:* examination — “After the first draft of the bylaws has been completed, it should be given thorough critical examination in discussions by the full committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:14 — “After the first draft of the bylaws has been completed, it should be given thorough critical examination in discussions by the full committee.”

**785.** `[C1381 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The probable long-range effect of each provision should be weighed, and particular care taken to detect and eliminate any remaining _____ or ambiguities.
   A. representatives
   B. adjournment
   C. inconsistencies
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* C) inconsistencies. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:14 — “The probable long-range effect of each provision should be weighed, and particular care taken to detect and eliminate any remaining inconsistencies or ambiguities.”

**786.** `[C1381 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is much better to take a good deal of time in _____ of bylaws before their adoption than to find an early need for extensive amendment.

   *Answer:* consideration — “It is much better to take a good deal of time in consideration of bylaws before their adoption than to find an early need for extensive amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:14 — “It is much better to take a good deal of time in consideration of bylaws before their adoption than to find an early need for extensive amendment.”

**787.** `[C1381 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the first draft of the bylaws has been completed, it should be given thorough critical _____ in discussions by the full committee.
   A. immediately
   B. Preparation
   C. resolutions
   D. examination

   *Answer:* D) examination. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:14 — “After the first draft of the bylaws has been completed, it should be given thorough critical examination in discussions by the full committee.”

**788.** `[C1381 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The probable long-range effect of each provision should be weighed, and particular care taken to detect and eliminate any remaining _____ or ambiguities.

   *Answer:* inconsistencies — “The probable long-range effect of each provision should be weighed, and particular care taken to detect and eliminate any remaining inconsistencies or ambiguities.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:14 — “The probable long-range effect of each provision should be weighed, and particular care taken to detect and eliminate any remaining inconsistencies or ambiguities.”

**789.** `[C1382 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the proposed bylaws are approved by the committee, the report of the committee is presented to the assembly and is _____ seriatim—article by article and, whenever an article consists of more than one section, section by section.
   A. landscaped
   B. considered
   C. Incidental
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* B) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:15 — “After the proposed bylaws are approved by the committee, the report of the committee is presented to the assembly and is considered seriatim—article by article and, whenever an article consists of more than one section, section by section.”

**790.** `[C1382 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the proposed bylaws are approved by the committee, the report of the committee is presented to the assembly and is _____ seriatim—article by article and, whenever an article consists of more than one section, section by section.

   *Answer:* considered — “After the proposed bylaws are approved by the committee, the report of the committee is presented to the assembly and is considered seriatim—article by article and, whenever an article consists of more than one section, section by section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:15 — “After the proposed bylaws are approved by the committee, the report of the committee is presented to the assembly and is considered seriatim—article by article and, whenever an article consists of more than one section, section by section.”

**791.** `[C1383 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While the number of bylaw articles will be determined by the size and activities of the _____ adopting them, and more than those listed below will be needed in some cases, the average society will find it sufficient to include articles on the following numbered headings.
   A. requirement
   B. organization
   C. specification
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:16 — “While the number of bylaw articles will be determined by the size and activities of the organization adopting them, and more than those listed below will be needed in some cases, the average society will find it sufficient to include articles on the following numbered headings.”

**792.** `[C1383 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of appropriate provisions in these articles, while in no sense exhaustive, should prove of help in framing bylaws.

   *Answer:* description — “The description of appropriate provisions in these articles, while in no sense exhaustive, should prove of help in framing bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:16 — “The description of appropriate provisions in these articles, while in no sense exhaustive, should prove of help in framing bylaws.”

**793.** `[C1383 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For ready reference, it may be helpful to precede lengthy bylaws with a table of contents when they are printed or copied for _____.
   A. interrupted
   B. Definitely
   C. Procedures
   D. distribution

   *Answer:* D) distribution. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:16 — “For ready reference, it may be helpful to precede lengthy bylaws with a table of contents when they are printed or copied for distribution.”

**794.** `[C1383 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While the number of bylaw articles will be determined by the size and activities of the _____ adopting them, and more than those listed below will be needed in some cases, the average society will find it sufficient to include articles on the following numbered headings.

   *Answer:* organization — “While the number of bylaw articles will be determined by the size and activities of the organization adopting them, and more than those listed below will be needed in some cases, the average society will find it sufficient to include articles on the following numbered headings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:16 — “While the number of bylaw articles will be determined by the size and activities of the organization adopting them, and more than those listed below will be needed in some cases, the average society will find it sufficient to include articles on the following numbered headings.”

**795.** `[C1383 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of appropriate provisions in these articles, while in no sense exhaustive, should prove of help in framing bylaws.
   A. constitution
   B. Organization
   C. description
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* C) description. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:16 — “The description of appropriate provisions in these articles, while in no sense exhaustive, should prove of help in framing bylaws.”

**796.** `[C1384 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ societies, the full, exact, and properly punctuated name of the society should be given.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. Organization
   C. Vice-President
   D. unincorporated

   *Answer:* D) unincorporated. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:17 — “In unincorporated societies, the full, exact, and properly punctuated name of the society should be given.”

**797.** `[C1384 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In incorporated societies or those with separate _____, however, the bylaws can omit this article, since the official name of the organization is then stated in the corporate charter or constitution.

   *Answer:* constitutions — “In incorporated societies or those with separate constitutions, however, the bylaws can omit this article, since the official name of the organization is then stated in the corporate charter or constitution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:17 — “In incorporated societies or those with separate constitutions, however, the bylaws can omit this article, since the official name of the organization is then stated in the corporate charter or constitution.”

**798.** `[C1384 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the name is in both _____, conflicts may creep in, and it is the name as stated in the superior document that is official.
   A. locations
   B. Signature
   C. Objection
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* A) locations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:17 — “If the name is in both locations, conflicts may creep in, and it is the name as stated in the superior document that is official.”

**799.** `[C1384 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ societies, the full, exact, and properly punctuated name of the society should be given.

   *Answer:* unincorporated — “In unincorporated societies, the full, exact, and properly punctuated name of the society should be given.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:17 — “In unincorporated societies, the full, exact, and properly punctuated name of the society should be given.”

**800.** `[C1384 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In incorporated societies or those with separate _____, however, the bylaws can omit this article, since the official name of the organization is then stated in the corporate charter or constitution.
   A. constitutions
   B. Vice-President
   C. deliberative
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* A) constitutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:17 — “In incorporated societies or those with separate constitutions, however, the bylaws can omit this article, since the official name of the organization is then stated in the corporate charter or constitution.”

**801.** `[C1385 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The statement should be general in its application, since it sets boundaries within which business can be introduced at the society’s meetings—a _____ vote being required to allow the introduction of a motion that falls outside the society’s object.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* B) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:18 — “The statement should be general in its application, since it sets boundaries within which business can be introduced at the society’s meetings—a two-thirds vote being required to allow the introduction of a motion that falls outside the society’s object.”

**802.** `[C1385 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ societies, the object of the society should be concisely expressed in a single sentence, the various aspects or phases being written in sequence, set off by semicolons, or in lettered subparagraphs, also set off by semicolons.

   *Answer:* unincorporated — “In unincorporated societies, the object of the society should be concisely expressed in a single sentence, the various aspects or phases being written in sequence, set off by semicolons, or in lettered subparagraphs, also set off by semicolons.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:18 — “In unincorporated societies, the object of the society should be concisely expressed in a single sentence, the various aspects or phases being written in sequence, set off by semicolons, or in lettered subparagraphs, also set off by semicolons.”

**803.** `[C1385 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The statement should be general in its application, since it sets boundaries within which business can be introduced at the society’s meetings—a two-thirds vote being required to allow the _____ of a motion that falls outside the society’s object.
   A. Particular
   B. Definitely
   C. undebatable
   D. introduction

   *Answer:* D) introduction. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:18 — “The statement should be general in its application, since it sets boundaries within which business can be introduced at the society’s meetings—a two-thirds vote being required to allow the introduction of a motion that falls outside the society’s object.”

**804.** `[C1385 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For the same reason stated above in reference to the society’s name, this article also can be omitted from the bylaws in _____ societies or in those having a separate constitution.

   *Answer:* incorporated — “For the same reason stated above in reference to the society’s name, this article also can be omitted from the bylaws in incorporated societies or in those having a separate constitution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:18 — “For the same reason stated above in reference to the society’s name, this article also can be omitted from the bylaws in incorporated societies or in those having a separate constitution.”

**805.** `[C1385 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In unincorporated _____, the object of the society should be concisely expressed in a single sentence, the various aspects or phases being written in sequence, set off by semicolons, or in lettered subparagraphs, also set off by semicolons.
   A. societies
   B. exhausted
   C. secondary
   D. Signature

   *Answer:* A) societies. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:18 — “In unincorporated societies, the object of the society should be concisely expressed in a single sentence, the various aspects or phases being written in sequence, set off by semicolons, or in lettered subparagraphs, also set off by semicolons.”

**806.** `[C1386 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Usually the article on members consists of several sections, covering, for example: (1) classes of members—as “active,” “associate,” and the like—with any _____ between them being set forth, and, as applicable, the rights of each, and any limitation on their number;
   A. interrupted
   B. requirement
   C. distinctions
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) distinctions. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:19 — “Usually the article on members consists of several sections, covering, for example: (1) classes of members—as “active,” “associate,” and the like—with any distinctions between them being set forth, and, as applicable, the rights of each, and any limitation on their number;”

**807.** `[C1386 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and (2) _____ or eligibility for membership, with application and acceptance procedures, including the method of reviewing and voting on applications.

   *Answer:* qualifications — “and (2) qualifications or eligibility for membership, with application and acceptance procedures, including the method of reviewing and voting on applications.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:19 — “and (2) qualifications or eligibility for membership, with application and acceptance procedures, including the method of reviewing and voting on applications.”

**808.** `[C1386 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the financial obligations of members are especially complicated, a section of this article should also state: (3) the required fees and dues, the date(s) when payable (whether annually, _____, quarterly, etc.), the time and prescribed procedure for notifying members if they become delinquent in payment, and the date thereafter on which a member will be dropped for nonpayment of dues.
   A. Permission
   B. immediately
   C. semiannually
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) semiannually. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:19 — “Unless the financial obligations of members are especially complicated, a section of this article should also state: (3) the required fees and dues, the date(s) when payable (whether annually, semiannually, quarterly, etc.), the time and prescribed procedure for notifying members if they become delinquent in payment, and the date thereafter on which a member will be dropped for nonpayment of dues.”

**809.** `[C1386 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before a member in arrears has been finally dropped under such a _____, his voting rights cannot be suspended unless the bylaws so provide.

   *Answer:* provision — “Before a member in arrears has been finally dropped under such a provision, his voting rights cannot be suspended unless the bylaws so provide.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:19 — “Before a member in arrears has been finally dropped under such a provision, his voting rights cannot be suspended unless the bylaws so provide.”

**810.** `[C1386 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the financial _____ of members are especially complicated, a section of this article should also state: (3) the required fees and dues, the date(s) when payable (whether annually, semiannually, quarterly, etc.), the time and prescribed procedure for notifying members if they become delinquent in payment, and the date thereafter on which a member will be dropped for nonpayment of dues.
   A. constitution
   B. convention
   C. Unfinished
   D. obligations

   *Answer:* D) obligations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:19 — “Unless the financial obligations of members are especially complicated, a section of this article should also state: (3) the required fees and dues, the date(s) when payable (whether annually, semiannually, quarterly, etc.), the time and prescribed procedure for notifying members if they become delinquent in payment, and the date thereafter on which a member will be dropped for nonpayment of dues.”

**811.** `[C1387 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some _____ require attendance at a certain proportion of the meetings or a specified minimum participation in the society’s activities as a requirement for continued membership;

   *Answer:* organizations — “Some organizations require attendance at a certain proportion of the meetings or a specified minimum participation in the society’s activities as a requirement for continued membership;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:20 — “Some organizations require attendance at a certain proportion of the meetings or a specified minimum participation in the society’s activities as a requirement for continued membership;”

**812.** `[C1387 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: this also can be done only by _____ in the bylaws.
   A. postponed
   B. provision
   C. rejecting
   D. Objection

   *Answer:* B) provision. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:20 — “this also can be done only by provision in the bylaws.”

**813.** `[C1387 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some _____ require attendance at a certain proportion of the meetings or a specified minimum participation in the society’s activities as a requirement for continued membership;
   A. presentation
   B. immediately
   C. organizations
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:20 — “Some organizations require attendance at a certain proportion of the meetings or a specified minimum participation in the society’s activities as a requirement for continued membership;”

**814.** `[C1387 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: this also can be done only by _____ in the bylaws.

   *Answer:* provision — “this also can be done only by provision in the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:20 — “this also can be done only by provision in the bylaws.”

**815.** `[C1388 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes this article also contains provisions for: (4) _____;

   *Answer:* resignations — “Sometimes this article also contains provisions for: (4) resignations;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:21 — “Sometimes this article also contains provisions for: (4) resignations;”

**816.** `[C1388 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes this article also contains provisions for: (4) _____;
   A. interrupted
   B. Information
   C. undebatable
   D. resignations

   *Answer:* D) resignations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:21 — “Sometimes this article also contains provisions for: (4) resignations;”

**817.** `[C1389 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a state or national body or a federation, local units or _____ clubs, rather than individuals, may be the “members” referred to in this article.

   *Answer:* constituent — “In a state or national body or a federation, local units or constituent clubs, rather than individuals, may be the “members” referred to in this article.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:22 — “In a state or national body or a federation, local units or constituent clubs, rather than individuals, may be the “members” referred to in this article.”

**818.** `[C1389 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a state or national body or a federation, local units or _____ clubs, rather than individuals, may be the “members” referred to in this article.
   A. constituent
   B. Convention
   C. Reconsider
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) constituent. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:22 — “In a state or national body or a federation, local units or constituent clubs, rather than individuals, may be the “members” referred to in this article.”

**819.** `[C1390 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As stated in 47, every society should specify in this article of its bylaws the officers it requires, _____ honorary ones, and how they shall be elected or appointed.
   A. Committee
   B. postponed
   C. including
   D. exhausted

   *Answer:* C) including. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:23 — “As stated in 47, every society should specify in this article of its bylaws the officers it requires, including honorary ones, and how they shall be elected or appointed.”

**820.** `[C1390 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The officers rank in the order listed, so that the president should be named first, the vice-president or first vice-president next (unless there is to be a _____;

   *Answer:* president-elect — “The officers rank in the order listed, so that the president should be named first, the vice-president or first vice-president next (unless there is to be a president-elect;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:23 — “The officers rank in the order listed, so that the president should be named first, the vice-president or first vice-president next (unless there is to be a president-elect;”

**821.** `[C1390 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ should be classed as officers.
   A. Directors
   B. president
   C. postponed
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* A) Directors. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:23 — “Directors should be classed as officers.”

**822.** `[C1390 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As stated in 47, every society should specify in this article of its bylaws the officers it requires, _____ honorary ones, and how they shall be elected or appointed.

   *Answer:* including — “As stated in 47, every society should specify in this article of its bylaws the officers it requires, including honorary ones, and how they shall be elected or appointed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:23 — “As stated in 47, every society should specify in this article of its bylaws the officers it requires, including honorary ones, and how they shall be elected or appointed.”

**823.** `[C1390 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The officers rank in the order listed, so that the president should be named first, the vice-president or first vice-president next (unless there is to be a _____;
   A. president-elect
   B. characteristics
   C. resolutions
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) president-elect. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:23 — “The officers rank in the order listed, so that the president should be named first, the vice-president or first vice-president next (unless there is to be a president-elect;”

**824.** `[C1391 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Normally all that need be said about the duties of officers (apart from occasional references in other articles, under the topics to which specific duties relate) can be included in the section designating the officers, to the effect that “These officers shall perform the duties prescribed by these bylaws and by the _____ authority adopted by the Society.” In cases where the extraordinary duties of officers are numerous, however, a separate article titled “Duties of Officers” may sometimes follow this article, and treat the duties for each office in a separate section.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Normally all that need be said about the duties of officers (apart from occasional references in other articles, under the topics to which specific duties relate) can be included in the section designating the officers, to the effect that “These officers shall perform the duties prescribed by these bylaws and by the parliamentary authority adopted by the Society.” In cases where the extraordinary duties of officers are numerous, however, a separate article titled “Duties of Officers” may sometimes follow this article, and treat the duties for each office in a separate section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:24 — “Normally all that need be said about the duties of officers (apart from occasional references in other articles, under the topics to which specific duties relate) can be included in the section designating the officers, to the effect that “These officers shall perform the duties prescribed by these bylaws and by the parliamentary authority adopted by the Society.” In cases where the extraordinary duties of officers are numerous, however, a separate article titled “Duties of Officers” may sometimes follow this article, and treat the duties for each office in a separate section.”

**825.** `[C1391 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a procedure is advantageous in collecting related information in one place, but it results in repetition and may occasion problems of _____.
   A. Organization
   B. interpretation
   C. specification
   D. Responsibilities

   *Answer:* B) interpretation. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:24 — “Such a procedure is advantageous in collecting related information in one place, but it results in repetition and may occasion problems of interpretation.”

**826.** `[C1391 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Great care must be taken in the writing of the article not to omit any duty, since an _____ that the duty is not required could be read into the omission.

   *Answer:* implication — “Great care must be taken in the writing of the article not to omit any duty, since an implication that the duty is not required could be read into the omission.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:24 — “Great care must be taken in the writing of the article not to omit any duty, since an implication that the duty is not required could be read into the omission.”

**827.** `[C1391 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Normally all that need be said about the duties of officers (apart from occasional references in other articles, under the topics to which specific duties relate) can be included in the section designating the officers, to the effect that “These officers shall perform the duties prescribed by these bylaws and by the _____ authority adopted by the Society.” In cases where the extraordinary duties of officers are numerous, however, a separate article titled “Duties of Officers” may sometimes follow this article, and treat the duties for each office in a separate section.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. characteristics
   C. requirement
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:24 — “Normally all that need be said about the duties of officers (apart from occasional references in other articles, under the topics to which specific duties relate) can be included in the section designating the officers, to the effect that “These officers shall perform the duties prescribed by these bylaws and by the parliamentary authority adopted by the Society.” In cases where the extraordinary duties of officers are numerous, however, a separate article titled “Duties of Officers” may sometimes follow this article, and treat the duties for each office in a separate section.”

**828.** `[C1391 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a procedure is advantageous in collecting related information in one place, but it results in repetition and may occasion problems of _____.

   *Answer:* interpretation — “Such a procedure is advantageous in collecting related information in one place, but it results in repetition and may occasion problems of interpretation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:24 — “Such a procedure is advantageous in collecting related information in one place, but it results in repetition and may occasion problems of interpretation.”

**829.** `[C1392 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A method of _____ officers (see 46) may be prescribed in a section of this article;

   *Answer:* nominating — “A method of nominating officers (see 46) may be prescribed in a section of this article;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:25 — “A method of nominating officers (see 46) may be prescribed in a section of this article;”

**830.** `[C1392 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: in the absence of such a provision or any rule adopted by the society, _____ are made in accordance with established custom (if any) or as otherwise directed by vote of the society at the time of each election (see also 31).
   A. Information
   B. authorizes
   C. nominations
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) nominations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:25 — “in the absence of such a provision or any rule adopted by the society, nominations are made in accordance with established custom (if any) or as otherwise directed by vote of the society at the time of each election (see also 31).”

**831.** `[C1392 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to impose such a _____, the provision should state that the committee shall nominate “a candidate for each office.”

   *Answer:* limitation — “If it is desired to impose such a limitation, the provision should state that the committee shall nominate “a candidate for each office.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:25 — “If it is desired to impose such a limitation, the provision should state that the committee shall nominate “a candidate for each office.””

**832.** `[C1392 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A method of _____ officers (see 46) may be prescribed in a section of this article;
   A. assemblies
   B. Procedures
   C. nominating
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* C) nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:25 — “A method of nominating officers (see 46) may be prescribed in a section of this article;”

**833.** `[C1392 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: in the absence of such a provision or any rule adopted by the society, _____ are made in accordance with established custom (if any) or as otherwise directed by vote of the society at the time of each election (see also 31).

   *Answer:* nominations — “in the absence of such a provision or any rule adopted by the society, nominations are made in accordance with established custom (if any) or as otherwise directed by vote of the society at the time of each election (see also 31).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:25 — “in the absence of such a provision or any rule adopted by the society, nominations are made in accordance with established custom (if any) or as otherwise directed by vote of the society at the time of each election (see also 31).”

**834.** `[C1393 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to elect by mail, by _____ vote, by preferential voting, or by cumulative voting, this must be expressly stated, and necessary details of the procedure should be prescribed (see 45).

   *Answer:* plurality — “If it is desired to elect by mail, by plurality vote, by preferential voting, or by cumulative voting, this must be expressly stated, and necessary details of the procedure should be prescribed (see 45).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:26 — “If it is desired to elect by mail, by plurality vote, by preferential voting, or by cumulative voting, this must be expressly stated, and necessary details of the procedure should be prescribed (see 45).”

**835.** `[C1393 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Election by ballot should usually be _____ in the section pertaining to elections and terms of office—often with additional details of election procedure as discussed in 46.
   A. Permission
   B. committees
   C. prescribed
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* C) prescribed. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:26 — “Election by ballot should usually be prescribed in the section pertaining to elections and terms of office—often with additional details of election procedure as discussed in 46.”

**836.** `[C1393 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A provision can be included to dispense with the ballot when there is only one candidate for an office, although this deprives members of the privilege of voting for “write-in” _____ in such a case.

   *Answer:* candidates — “A provision can be included to dispense with the ballot when there is only one candidate for an office, although this deprives members of the privilege of voting for “write-in” candidates in such a case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:26 — “A provision can be included to dispense with the ballot when there is only one candidate for an office, although this deprives members of the privilege of voting for “write-in” candidates in such a case.”

**837.** `[C1393 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is desired to elect by mail, by plurality vote, by _____ voting, or by cumulative voting, this must be expressly stated, and necessary details of the procedure should be prescribed (see 45).
   A. preferential
   B. Particular
   C. Procedures
   D. consideration
