   *Answer:* A) preferential. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:26 — “If it is desired to elect by mail, by plurality vote, by preferential voting, or by cumulative voting, this must be expressly stated, and necessary details of the procedure should be prescribed (see 45).”

**838.** `[C1393 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Election by ballot should usually be _____ in the section pertaining to elections and terms of office—often with additional details of election procedure as discussed in 46.

   *Answer:* prescribed — “Election by ballot should usually be prescribed in the section pertaining to elections and terms of office—often with additional details of election procedure as discussed in 46.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:26 — “Election by ballot should usually be prescribed in the section pertaining to elections and terms of office—often with additional details of election procedure as discussed in 46.”

**839.** `[C1394 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, suppose that the bylaws provide that the annual meeting for the election of officers shall take place “in October or November,” that their terms of office shall begin “at the close of the annual meeting,” and that they shall serve for a term of “_____ and until their successors are elected” (or simply for a term of “one year,” which is not recommended;

   *Answer:* one year — “For example, suppose that the bylaws provide that the annual meeting for the election of officers shall take place “in October or November,” that their terms of office shall begin “at the close of the annual meeting,” and that they shall serve for a term of “one year and until their successors are elected” (or simply for a term of “one year,” which is not recommended;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:27 — “For example, suppose that the bylaws provide that the annual meeting for the election of officers shall take place “in October or November,” that their terms of office shall begin “at the close of the annual meeting,” and that they shall serve for a term of “one year and until their successors are elected” (or simply for a term of “one year,” which is not recommended;”

**840.** `[C1394 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The length of the terms of office should be _____;
   A. Particular
   B. prescribed
   C. recognized
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) prescribed. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:27 — “The length of the terms of office should be prescribed;”

**841.** `[C1394 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and unless the terms are to begin at the instant the chair declares each officer elected, the time when they are to begin must be _____.

   *Answer:* specified — “and unless the terms are to begin at the instant the chair declares each officer elected, the time when they are to begin must be specified.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:27 — “and unless the terms are to begin at the instant the chair declares each officer elected, the time when they are to begin must be specified.”

**842.** `[C1394 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the bylaws specify the number of years in a term of office, it is understood that the actual term may be more or less than a whole number of calendar years, owing to _____ variation in the dates on which successive elections are scheduled.
   A. Information
   B. immediately
   C. permissible
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) permissible. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:27 — “When the bylaws specify the number of years in a term of office, it is understood that the actual term may be more or less than a whole number of calendar years, owing to permissible variation in the dates on which successive elections are scheduled.”

**843.** `[C1394 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, suppose that the bylaws provide that the annual meeting for the election of officers shall take place “in October or November,” that their terms of office shall begin “at the close of the annual meeting,” and that they shall serve for a term of “one year and until their successors are elected” (or simply for a term of “one year,” which is not _____;

   *Answer:* recommended — “For example, suppose that the bylaws provide that the annual meeting for the election of officers shall take place “in October or November,” that their terms of office shall begin “at the close of the annual meeting,” and that they shall serve for a term of “one year and until their successors are elected” (or simply for a term of “one year,” which is not recommended;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:27 — “For example, suppose that the bylaws provide that the annual meeting for the election of officers shall take place “in October or November,” that their terms of office shall begin “at the close of the annual meeting,” and that they shall serve for a term of “one year and until their successors are elected” (or simply for a term of “one year,” which is not recommended;”

**844.** `[C1395 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To ensure the continued services of officers in the event, for example, of public emergency or of difficulty in obtaining a nominee for an office, the _____ wording “for a term of… year(s)” should be avoided, because at the end of that time there would be no officers if new ones had not been elected.

   *Answer:* unqualified — “To ensure the continued services of officers in the event, for example, of public emergency or of difficulty in obtaining a nominee for an office, the unqualified wording “for a term of… year(s)” should be avoided, because at the end of that time there would be no officers if new ones had not been elected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:28 — “To ensure the continued services of officers in the event, for example, of public emergency or of difficulty in obtaining a nominee for an office, the unqualified wording “for a term of… year(s)” should be avoided, because at the end of that time there would be no officers if new ones had not been elected.”

**845.** `[C1395 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The exact wording that instead ought to be used depends on a further _____, namely, the manner in which the organization wants to make it possible to remove officers before the expiration of their normal term.
   A. representatives
   B. Vice-President
   C. deliberative
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:28 — “The exact wording that instead ought to be used depends on a further consideration, namely, the manner in which the organization wants to make it possible to remove officers before the expiration of their normal term.”

**846.** `[C1395 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To ensure the continued services of officers in the event, for example, of public emergency or of difficulty in obtaining a nominee for an office, the _____ wording “for a term of… year(s)” should be avoided, because at the end of that time there would be no officers if new ones had not been elected.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Reconsider
   C. constitution
   D. unqualified

   *Answer:* D) unqualified. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:28 — “To ensure the continued services of officers in the event, for example, of public emergency or of difficulty in obtaining a nominee for an office, the unqualified wording “for a term of… year(s)” should be avoided, because at the end of that time there would be no officers if new ones had not been elected.”

**847.** `[C1395 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The exact wording that instead ought to be used depends on a further _____, namely, the manner in which the organization wants to make it possible to remove officers before the expiration of their normal term.

   *Answer:* consideration — “The exact wording that instead ought to be used depends on a further consideration, namely, the manner in which the organization wants to make it possible to remove officers before the expiration of their normal term.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:28 — “The exact wording that instead ought to be used depends on a further consideration, namely, the manner in which the organization wants to make it possible to remove officers before the expiration of their normal term.”

**848.** `[C1397 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To accomplish the first alternative, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) _____ until their successors are elected.” To accomplish the second alternative, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) or until their successors are elected.” (Emphases added.) Because the significant difference in effect between the use of “and” and “or” is unlikely to be clear to most members, it may be desirable (although it is not essential) to add an explanatory sentence, such as:

   *Answer:* and — “To accomplish the first alternative, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) and until their successors are elected.” To accomplish the second alternative, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) or until their successors are elected.” (Emphases added.) Because the significant difference in effect between the use of “and” and “or” is unlikely to be clear to most members, it may be desirable (although it is not essential) to add an explanatory sentence, such as:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:30 — “To accomplish the first alternative, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) and until their successors are elected.” To accomplish the second alternative, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) or until their successors are elected.” (Emphases added.) Because the significant difference in effect between the use of “and” and “or” is unlikely to be clear to most members, it may be desirable (although it is not essential) to add an explanatory sentence, such as:”

**849.** `[C1397 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To accomplish the first _____, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) and until their successors are elected.” To accomplish the second alternative, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) or until their successors are elected.” (Emphases added.) Because the significant difference in effect between the use of “and” and “or” is unlikely to be clear to most members, it may be desirable (although it is not essential) to add an explanatory sentence, such as:
   A. Unfinished
   B. alternative
   C. convention
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) alternative. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:30 — “To accomplish the first alternative, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) and until their successors are elected.” To accomplish the second alternative, the bylaws may provide that officers “shall hold office for a term of year(s) or until their successors are elected.” (Emphases added.) Because the significant difference in effect between the use of “and” and “or” is unlikely to be clear to most members, it may be desirable (although it is not essential) to add an explanatory sentence, such as:”

**850.** `[C1398 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since a reasonable rotation in office is desirable in almost all _____, a section of this article may well provide that “No person shall be eligible to serve… consecutive terms [specifying the number] in the same office.” For purposes of determining eligibility to continue in office under such a provision, an officer who has served more than half a term is considered to have served a full term in that office.

   *Answer:* organizations — “Since a reasonable rotation in office is desirable in almost all organizations, a section of this article may well provide that “No person shall be eligible to serve… consecutive terms [specifying the number] in the same office.” For purposes of determining eligibility to continue in office under such a provision, an officer who has served more than half a term is considered to have served a full term in that office.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:31 — “Since a reasonable rotation in office is desirable in almost all organizations, a section of this article may well provide that “No person shall be eligible to serve… consecutive terms [specifying the number] in the same office.” For purposes of determining eligibility to continue in office under such a provision, an officer who has served more than half a term is considered to have served a full term in that office.”

**851.** `[C1398 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since a reasonable rotation in office is desirable in almost all _____, a section of this article may well provide that “No person shall be eligible to serve… consecutive terms [specifying the number] in the same office.” For purposes of determining eligibility to continue in office under such a provision, an officer who has served more than half a term is considered to have served a full term in that office.
   A. undebatable
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. organizations
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:31 — “Since a reasonable rotation in office is desirable in almost all organizations, a section of this article may well provide that “No person shall be eligible to serve… consecutive terms [specifying the number] in the same office.” For purposes of determining eligibility to continue in office under such a provision, an officer who has served more than half a term is considered to have served a full term in that office.”

**852.** `[C1399 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The method of filling _____ may also be provided (cf.

   *Answer:* vacancies — “The method of filling vacancies may also be provided (cf.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:32 — “The method of filling vacancies may also be provided (cf.”

**853.** `[C1399 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the bylaws clearly provide _____, notice of filling a vacancy in office must always be given to the members of the body that will elect the person to fill it.
   A. reception
   B. Signature
   C. otherwise
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* C) otherwise. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:32 — “Unless the bylaws clearly provide otherwise, notice of filling a vacancy in office must always be given to the members of the body that will elect the person to fill it.”

**854.** `[C1399 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the bylaws are silent as to the method of filling a vacancy in the specific case of the presidency, the vice-president or first vicepresident automatically becomes president for the remainder of the term, and the vacancy to be filled arises in the vicepresidency or lowest-ranking _____;

   *Answer:* vice-presidency — “If the bylaws are silent as to the method of filling a vacancy in the specific case of the presidency, the vice-president or first vicepresident automatically becomes president for the remainder of the term, and the vacancy to be filled arises in the vicepresidency or lowest-ranking vice-presidency;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:32 — “If the bylaws are silent as to the method of filling a vacancy in the specific case of the presidency, the vice-president or first vicepresident automatically becomes president for the remainder of the term, and the vacancy to be filled arises in the vicepresidency or lowest-ranking vice-presidency;”

**855.** `[C1399 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the bylaws are silent as to the method of filling a vacancy in the specific case of the _____, the vice-president or first vicepresident automatically becomes president for the remainder of the term, and the vacancy to be filled arises in the vicepresidency or lowest-ranking vice-presidency;
   A. settlement
   B. authorizes
   C. sustaining
   D. presidency

   *Answer:* D) presidency. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:32 — “If the bylaws are silent as to the method of filling a vacancy in the specific case of the presidency, the vice-president or first vicepresident automatically becomes president for the remainder of the term, and the vacancy to be filled arises in the vicepresidency or lowest-ranking vice-presidency;”

**856.** `[C1399 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The method of filling _____ may also be provided (cf.
   A. auditors’
   B. vacancies
   C. Crestwood
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* B) vacancies. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:32 — “The method of filling vacancies may also be provided (cf.”

**857.** `[C1400 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The first section of the article on meetings should fix the day on which regular meetings of the society are to be held—as by specifying, for example, “the first Friday of each month.” If the words “unless otherwise ordered by the Society [or “Executive Board”]” are added, the date can be changed in an unusual _____, but only for that single meeting on that particular occasion, and not for a period of time including several meetings.
   A. immediately
   B. Incidental
   C. circumstance
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* C) circumstance. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:33 — “The first section of the article on meetings should fix the day on which regular meetings of the society are to be held—as by specifying, for example, “the first Friday of each month.” If the words “unless otherwise ordered by the Society [or “Executive Board”]” are added, the date can be changed in an unusual circumstance, but only for that single meeting on that particular occasion, and not for a period of time including several meetings.”

**858.** `[C1400 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To change the general rule fixing the time for meetings would require _____ of the bylaws.

   *Answer:* amendment — “To change the general rule fixing the time for meetings would require amendment of the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:33 — “To change the general rule fixing the time for meetings would require amendment of the bylaws.”

**859.** `[C1400 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The hour and place at which meetings are to be held should not be specified in the bylaws, but should be _____ by a standing rule (2) adopted by the society or, if it is empowered to do so, by the executive board.
   A. established
   B. committees
   C. immediately
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* A) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:33 — “The hour and place at which meetings are to be held should not be specified in the bylaws, but should be established by a standing rule (2) adopted by the society or, if it is empowered to do so, by the executive board.”

**860.** `[C1400 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The first section of the article on meetings should fix the day on which regular meetings of the society are to be held—as by specifying, for example, “the first Friday of each month.” If the words “unless otherwise ordered by the Society [or “Executive Board”]” are added, the date can be changed in an unusual _____, but only for that single meeting on that particular occasion, and not for a period of time including several meetings.

   *Answer:* circumstance — “The first section of the article on meetings should fix the day on which regular meetings of the society are to be held—as by specifying, for example, “the first Friday of each month.” If the words “unless otherwise ordered by the Society [or “Executive Board”]” are added, the date can be changed in an unusual circumstance, but only for that single meeting on that particular occasion, and not for a period of time including several meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:33 — “The first section of the article on meetings should fix the day on which regular meetings of the society are to be held—as by specifying, for example, “the first Friday of each month.” If the words “unless otherwise ordered by the Society [or “Executive Board”]” are added, the date can be changed in an unusual circumstance, but only for that single meeting on that particular occasion, and not for a period of time including several meetings.”

**861.** `[C1400 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To change the general rule fixing the time for meetings would require _____ of the bylaws.
   A. committee
   B. Precedent
   C. president
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* D) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:33 — “To change the general rule fixing the time for meetings would require amendment of the bylaws.”

**862.** `[C1401 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some _____ prefer to schedule meetings by resolution.

   *Answer:* organizations — “Some organizations prefer to schedule meetings by resolution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:34 — “Some organizations prefer to schedule meetings by resolution.”

**863.** `[C1401 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If so, the bylaws should provide for the number of days’ notice required before regular meetings, since under such a practice members cannot determine the meeting dates by _____ the bylaws.
   A. Procedures
   B. incidental
   C. introduced
   D. consulting

   *Answer:* D) consulting. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:34 — “If so, the bylaws should provide for the number of days’ notice required before regular meetings, since under such a practice members cannot determine the meeting dates by consulting the bylaws.”

**864.** `[C1401 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless _____ provided in the bylaws, the number of days is computed by counting all calendar days (including holidays and weekends), excluding the day of the meeting but including the day the notice is sent.

   *Answer:* otherwise — “Unless otherwise provided in the bylaws, the number of days is computed by counting all calendar days (including holidays and weekends), excluding the day of the meeting but including the day the notice is sent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:34 — “Unless otherwise provided in the bylaws, the number of days is computed by counting all calendar days (including holidays and weekends), excluding the day of the meeting but including the day the notice is sent.”

**865.** `[C1401 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some _____ prefer to schedule meetings by resolution.
   A. specification
   B. characteristics
   C. organizations
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:34 — “Some organizations prefer to schedule meetings by resolution.”

**866.** `[C1401 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If so, the bylaws should provide for the number of days’ notice required before regular meetings, since under such a practice members cannot determine the meeting dates by _____ the bylaws.

   *Answer:* consulting — “If so, the bylaws should provide for the number of days’ notice required before regular meetings, since under such a practice members cannot determine the meeting dates by consulting the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:34 — “If so, the bylaws should provide for the number of days’ notice required before regular meetings, since under such a practice members cannot determine the meeting dates by consulting the bylaws.”

**867.** `[C1402 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a separate section it should be provided that “The regular meeting… [_____ which one, as “on the last Tuesday in May”] shall be known as the annual meeting.” As explained in 9, this meeting is conducted in the same way as any regular meeting, except that officers are elected and annual reports are received from officers and standing committees.

   *Answer:* specifying — “In a separate section it should be provided that “The regular meeting… [specifying which one, as “on the last Tuesday in May”] shall be known as the annual meeting.” As explained in 9, this meeting is conducted in the same way as any regular meeting, except that officers are elected and annual reports are received from officers and standing committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:35 — “In a separate section it should be provided that “The regular meeting… [specifying which one, as “on the last Tuesday in May”] shall be known as the annual meeting.” As explained in 9, this meeting is conducted in the same way as any regular meeting, except that officers are elected and annual reports are received from officers and standing committees.”

**868.** `[C1402 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a separate section it should be provided that “The regular meeting… [_____ which one, as “on the last Tuesday in May”] shall be known as the annual meeting.” As explained in 9, this meeting is conducted in the same way as any regular meeting, except that officers are elected and annual reports are received from officers and standing committees.
   A. introduced
   B. assemblies
   C. privileged
   D. specifying

   *Answer:* D) specifying. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:35 — “In a separate section it should be provided that “The regular meeting… [specifying which one, as “on the last Tuesday in May”] shall be known as the annual meeting.” As explained in 9, this meeting is conducted in the same way as any regular meeting, except that officers are elected and annual reports are received from officers and standing committees.”

**869.** `[C1403 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A section _____ the calling of special meetings should state by whom such meetings can be called—such as the president, the board, or a specified number of members nearly equal to a quorum—and the number of days’ notice required.

   *Answer:* authorizing — “A section authorizing the calling of special meetings should state by whom such meetings can be called—such as the president, the board, or a specified number of members nearly equal to a quorum—and the number of days’ notice required.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:36 — “A section authorizing the calling of special meetings should state by whom such meetings can be called—such as the president, the board, or a specified number of members nearly equal to a quorum—and the number of days’ notice required.”

**870.** `[C1403 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the bylaws do not authorize the calling of special meetings, such meetings are not permitted— except when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal _____ procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).
   A. undebatable
   B. requirement
   C. Organization
   D. disciplinary

   *Answer:* D) disciplinary. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:36 — “If the bylaws do not authorize the calling of special meetings, such meetings are not permitted— except when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal disciplinary procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).”

**871.** `[C1403 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A section _____ the calling of special meetings should state by whom such meetings can be called—such as the president, the board, or a specified number of members nearly equal to a quorum—and the number of days’ notice required.
   A. convention
   B. Permission
   C. Unfinished
   D. authorizing

   *Answer:* D) authorizing. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:36 — “A section authorizing the calling of special meetings should state by whom such meetings can be called—such as the president, the board, or a specified number of members nearly equal to a quorum—and the number of days’ notice required.”

**872.** `[C1403 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the bylaws do not authorize the calling of special meetings, such meetings are not permitted— except when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal _____ procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).

   *Answer:* disciplinary — “If the bylaws do not authorize the calling of special meetings, such meetings are not permitted— except when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal disciplinary procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:36 — “If the bylaws do not authorize the calling of special meetings, such meetings are not permitted— except when authorized by the assembly itself, as part of formal disciplinary procedures, for purposes of conducting a trial and determining a punishment (see 63:21n9).”

**873.** `[C1405 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Article VI: _____ Board (or Board of Directors).

   *Answer:* Executive — “Article VI: Executive Board (or Board of Directors).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:39 — “Article VI: Executive Board (or Board of Directors).”

**874.** `[C1405 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is to be such a board, _____ of this article should:
   A. majority
   B. assuming
   C. dilatory
   D. sections

   *Answer:* D) sections. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:39 — “If there is to be such a board, sections of this article should:”

**875.** `[C1405 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Article VI: _____ Board (or Board of Directors).
   A. execution
   B. exhausted
   C. Executive
   D. reception

   *Answer:* C) Executive. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:39 — “Article VI: Executive Board (or Board of Directors).”

**876.** `[C1405 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is to be such a board, _____ of this article should:

   *Answer:* sections — “If there is to be such a board, sections of this article should:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:39 — “If there is to be such a board, sections of this article should:”

**877.** `[C1406 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ is then established in a separate article following the one on the complete board, with similar provisions.

   *Answer:* Executive Committee — “The Executive Committee is then established in a separate article following the one on the complete board, with similar provisions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:40 — “The Executive Committee is then established in a separate article following the one on the complete board, with similar provisions.”

**878.** `[C1406 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Organizations may sometimes give varied names to their full boards, such as _____ of Trustees, Board of Governors, Administrative Council, etc.
   A. Board of Managers, Board
   B. Classification as to Form
   C. Approval of the Minutes
   D. Debate on the Suggestions

   *Answer:* A) Board of Managers, Board. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:40 — “Organizations may sometimes give varied names to their full boards, such as Board of Managers, Board of Trustees, Board of Governors, Administrative Council, etc.”

**879.** `[C1406 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Executive Committee is then _____ in a separate article following the one on the complete board, with similar provisions.

   *Answer:* established — “The Executive Committee is then established in a separate article following the one on the complete board, with similar provisions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:40 — “The Executive Committee is then established in a separate article following the one on the complete board, with similar provisions.”

**880.** `[C1406 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The bylaws may provide for how the presiding officer and the secretary of the board, and those of the Executive Committee, are to be _____.
   A. authorizes
   B. settlement
   C. Particular
   D. determined

   *Answer:* D) determined. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:40 — “The bylaws may provide for how the presiding officer and the secretary of the board, and those of the Executive Committee, are to be determined.”

**881.** `[C1406 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Organizations may sometimes give varied names to their full boards, such as _____ of Trustees, Board of Governors, Administrative Council, etc.

   *Answer:* Board of Managers, Board — “Organizations may sometimes give varied names to their full boards, such as Board of Managers, Board of Trustees, Board of Governors, Administrative Council, etc.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:40 — “Organizations may sometimes give varied names to their full boards, such as Board of Managers, Board of Trustees, Board of Governors, Administrative Council, etc.”

**882.** `[C1407 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, for example, if it is desired that the assembly adopt an annual budget but that the board be empowered to alter it to deal with _____ that may develop, the bylaws (or the budget resolution) must specifically confer this power on the board.

   *Answer:* contingencies — “Thus, for example, if it is desired that the assembly adopt an annual budget but that the board be empowered to alter it to deal with contingencies that may develop, the bylaws (or the budget resolution) must specifically confer this power on the board.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:41 — “Thus, for example, if it is desired that the assembly adopt an annual budget but that the board be empowered to alter it to deal with contingencies that may develop, the bylaws (or the budget resolution) must specifically confer this power on the board.”

**883.** `[C1407 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, for example, if it is desired that the assembly adopt an annual budget but that the board be empowered to alter it to deal with _____ that may develop, the bylaws (or the budget resolution) must specifically confer this power on the board.
   A. contingencies
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. president-elect
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) contingencies. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:41 — “Thus, for example, if it is desired that the assembly adopt an annual budget but that the board be empowered to alter it to deal with contingencies that may develop, the bylaws (or the budget resolution) must specifically confer this power on the board.”

**884.** `[C1409 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the organization desires to leave the entire _____ authority of the society to the board between the society’s meetings—as may occur, for example, in organizations that meet infrequently or whose main purpose is other than to transact business—the same section might read:

   *Answer:* administrative — “If the organization desires to leave the entire administrative authority of the society to the board between the society’s meetings—as may occur, for example, in organizations that meet infrequently or whose main purpose is other than to transact business—the same section might read:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:43 — “If the organization desires to leave the entire administrative authority of the society to the board between the society’s meetings—as may occur, for example, in organizations that meet infrequently or whose main purpose is other than to transact business—the same section might read:”

**885.** `[C1409 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the organization desires to leave the entire _____ authority of the society to the board between the society’s meetings—as may occur, for example, in organizations that meet infrequently or whose main purpose is other than to transact business—the same section might read:
   A. immediately
   B. Parliamentarian
   C. administrative
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) administrative. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:43 — “If the organization desires to leave the entire administrative authority of the society to the board between the society’s meetings—as may occur, for example, in organizations that meet infrequently or whose main purpose is other than to transact business—the same section might read:”

**886.** `[C1410 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The article on committees should provide for the _____ of each of the standing committees (50) that it is known will be required.
   A. establishment
   B. Preparation
   C. deliberative
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* A) establishment. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:44 — “The article on committees should provide for the establishment of each of the standing committees (50) that it is known will be required.”

**887.** `[C1410 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A separate section devoted to each of these committees should give its name, _____, manner of selection, and duties.

   *Answer:* composition — “A separate section devoted to each of these committees should give its name, composition, manner of selection, and duties.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:44 — “A separate section devoted to each of these committees should give its name, composition, manner of selection, and duties.”

**888.** `[C1410 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In any event, if a standing committee is to have standing authority to act for the society without specific instructions, if business of a certain class is to be _____ referred to it, or if some other rule of parliamentary procedure is affected by the committee’s assigned function, such procedure must be prescribed in a provision of the bylaws or in a special rule of order, establishing the committee by name.
   A. subsequently
   B. specification
   C. automatically
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) automatically. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:44 — “In any event, if a standing committee is to have standing authority to act for the society without specific instructions, if business of a certain class is to be automatically referred to it, or if some other rule of parliamentary procedure is affected by the committee’s assigned function, such procedure must be prescribed in a provision of the bylaws or in a special rule of order, establishing the committee by name.”

**889.** `[C1410 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The article on committees should provide for the _____ of each of the standing committees (50) that it is known will be required.

   *Answer:* establishment — “The article on committees should provide for the establishment of each of the standing committees (50) that it is known will be required.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:44 — “The article on committees should provide for the establishment of each of the standing committees (50) that it is known will be required.”

**890.** `[C1410 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A separate section devoted to each of these committees should give its name, _____, manner of selection, and duties.
   A. composition
   B. Procedures
   C. interrupted
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) composition. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:44 — “A separate section devoted to each of these committees should give its name, composition, manner of selection, and duties.”

**891.** `[C1411 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The number and nature of the standing committees that may be named in individual sections of this article will depend on the size and object of the _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “The number and nature of the standing committees that may be named in individual sections of this article will depend on the size and object of the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:45 — “The number and nature of the standing committees that may be named in individual sections of this article will depend on the size and object of the organization.”

**892.** `[C1411 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The standing committees most frequently _____ by local societies are few in number;
   A. convention
   B. Information
   C. established
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* C) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:45 — “The standing committees most frequently established by local societies are few in number;”

**893.** `[C1411 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: they may include a committee on _____, a program committee, and sometimes a finance committee.

   *Answer:* membership — “they may include a committee on membership, a program committee, and sometimes a finance committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:45 — “they may include a committee on membership, a program committee, and sometimes a finance committee.”

**894.** `[C1411 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (A section relating to the _____ committee, when included, is usually located not in this article but in the article on officers, where nomination and election procedures are usually prescribed.) In national or state bodies more committees may be needed, but local units should not try to establish a committee to correspond to each one in the superior body, and the superior body generally should not require them to do so.
   A. introduced
   B. nominating
   C. authorizes
   D. convention

   *Answer:* B) nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:45 — “(A section relating to the nominating committee, when included, is usually located not in this article but in the article on officers, where nomination and election procedures are usually prescribed.) In national or state bodies more committees may be needed, but local units should not try to establish a committee to correspond to each one in the superior body, and the superior body generally should not require them to do so.”

**895.** `[C1411 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The number and nature of the standing committees that may be named in individual sections of this article will depend on the size and object of the _____.
   A. Preparation
   B. organization
   C. undebatable
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:45 — “The number and nature of the standing committees that may be named in individual sections of this article will depend on the size and object of the organization.”

**896.** `[C1412 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of special committees is usually provided for in a separate section that may also, as indicated above, provide for the appointment of additional standing committees.

   *Answer:* Appointment — “Appointment of special committees is usually provided for in a separate section that may also, as indicated above, provide for the appointment of additional standing committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:46 — “Appointment of special committees is usually provided for in a separate section that may also, as indicated above, provide for the appointment of additional standing committees.”

**897.** `[C1412 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Appointment of special _____ is usually provided for in a separate section that may also, as indicated above, provide for the appointment of additional standing committees.
   A. persuasive
   B. committees
   C. incidental
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* B) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:46 — “Appointment of special committees is usually provided for in a separate section that may also, as indicated above, provide for the appointment of additional standing committees.”

**898.** `[C1413 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Without such a provision, he has no vote within the _____, nor can he attend their meetings except as invited by a particular committee.

   *Answer:* committees — “Without such a provision, he has no vote within the committees, nor can he attend their meetings except as invited by a particular committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:47 — “Without such a provision, he has no vote within the committees, nor can he attend their meetings except as invited by a particular committee.”

**899.** `[C1413 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ committee should always be expressly excluded in a provision making the president an exofficio member of committees.
   A. convention
   B. Permission
   C. nominating
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) nominating. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:47 — “The nominating committee should always be expressly excluded in a provision making the president an exofficio member of committees.”

**900.** `[C1413 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It may also be advisable to exclude all disciplinary committees—such as trial and _____ committees—both from a provision making the president an ex-officio member of committees and from a provision authorizing the president to appoint committees.

   *Answer:* investigating — “It may also be advisable to exclude all disciplinary committees—such as trial and investigating committees—both from a provision making the president an ex-officio member of committees and from a provision authorizing the president to appoint committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:47 — “It may also be advisable to exclude all disciplinary committees—such as trial and investigating committees—both from a provision making the president an ex-officio member of committees and from a provision authorizing the president to appoint committees.”

**901.** `[C1413 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Without such a provision, he has no vote within the _____, nor can he attend their meetings except as invited by a particular committee.
   A. subsidiary
   B. privileged
   C. committees
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:47 — “Without such a provision, he has no vote within the committees, nor can he attend their meetings except as invited by a particular committee.”

**902.** `[C1413 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ committee should always be expressly excluded in a provision making the president an exofficio member of committees.

   *Answer:* nominating — “The nominating committee should always be expressly excluded in a provision making the president an exofficio member of committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:47 — “The nominating committee should always be expressly excluded in a provision making the president an exofficio member of committees.”

**903.** `[C1414 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no article on committees is included in the bylaws, standing and special committees are _____ as directed by the society (see 13, 50).

   *Answer:* established — “If no article on committees is included in the bylaws, standing and special committees are established as directed by the society (see 13, 50).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:48 — “If no article on committees is included in the bylaws, standing and special committees are established as directed by the society (see 13, 50).”

**904.** `[C1414 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If no article on committees is included in the bylaws, standing and special committees are _____ as directed by the society (see 13, 50).
   A. adjournment
   B. Information
   C. established
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:48 — “If no article on committees is included in the bylaws, standing and special committees are established as directed by the society (see 13, 50).”

**905.** `[C1415 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ authority—through the adoption of which a society establishes its rules of order—should be prescribed in a one-sentence article reading: “The rules contained in the current edition of… [specifying a standard manual of parliamentary practice, such as this book] shall govern the Society in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with these bylaws and any special rules of order the Society may adopt.” Societies can adopt special rules of order as they are needed to supplement their parliamentary authority, as explained in 2.
   A. parliamentary
   B. Announcements
   C. deliberative
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:49 — “The parliamentary authority—through the adoption of which a society establishes its rules of order—should be prescribed in a one-sentence article reading: “The rules contained in the current edition of… [specifying a standard manual of parliamentary practice, such as this book] shall govern the Society in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with these bylaws and any special rules of order the Society may adopt.” Societies can adopt special rules of order as they are needed to supplement their parliamentary authority, as explained in 2.”

**906.** `[C1415 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In other cases, it may be _____ but is not binding upon the society.

   *Answer:* persuasive — “In other cases, it may be persuasive but is not binding upon the society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:49 — “In other cases, it may be persuasive but is not binding upon the society.”

**907.** `[C1415 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ authority—through the adoption of which a society establishes its rules of order—should be prescribed in a one-sentence article reading: “The rules contained in the current edition of… [specifying a standard manual of parliamentary practice, such as this book] shall govern the Society in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with these bylaws and any special rules of order the Society may adopt.” Societies can adopt special rules of order as they are needed to supplement their parliamentary authority, as explained in 2.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “The parliamentary authority—through the adoption of which a society establishes its rules of order—should be prescribed in a one-sentence article reading: “The rules contained in the current edition of… [specifying a standard manual of parliamentary practice, such as this book] shall govern the Society in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with these bylaws and any special rules of order the Society may adopt.” Societies can adopt special rules of order as they are needed to supplement their parliamentary authority, as explained in 2.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:49 — “The parliamentary authority—through the adoption of which a society establishes its rules of order—should be prescribed in a one-sentence article reading: “The rules contained in the current edition of… [specifying a standard manual of parliamentary practice, such as this book] shall govern the Society in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with these bylaws and any special rules of order the Society may adopt.” Societies can adopt special rules of order as they are needed to supplement their parliamentary authority, as explained in 2.”

**908.** `[C1415 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In other cases, it may be _____ but is not binding upon the society.
   A. authorizes
   B. Particular
   C. committees
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* D) persuasive. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:49 — “In other cases, it may be persuasive but is not binding upon the society.”

**909.** `[C1416 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ bylaws should always prescribe the procedure for their amendment, and such provision should always require at least that advance notice be given in a specified manner, and that the amendment be approved by a two-thirds vote.

   *Answer:* The — “The bylaws should always prescribe the procedure for their amendment, and such provision should always require at least that advance notice be given in a specified manner, and that the amendment be approved by a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:50 — “The bylaws should always prescribe the procedure for their amendment, and such provision should always require at least that advance notice be given in a specified manner, and that the amendment be approved by a two-thirds vote.”

**910.** `[C1416 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The bylaws should always prescribe the procedure for their amendment, and such provision should always require at least that advance notice be given in a specified manner, and that the amendment be approved by a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “The bylaws should always prescribe the procedure for their amendment, and such provision should always require at least that advance notice be given in a specified manner, and that the amendment be approved by a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:50 — “The bylaws should always prescribe the procedure for their amendment, and such provision should always require at least that advance notice be given in a specified manner, and that the amendment be approved by a two-thirds vote.”

**911.** `[C1416 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In making a requirement that notice be given by submitting the amendment at a meeting in advance of the one at which it is to be considered, the provision should always specify submission at “the previous meeting,” and not “a” previous meeting, since the latter would permit indefinite delay and would defeat the object of giving notice—namely, to alert the members to the proposed amendment so that all those interested can arrange to be present at its _____.
   A. undebatable
   B. Organization
   C. immediately
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:50 — “In making a requirement that notice be given by submitting the amendment at a meeting in advance of the one at which it is to be considered, the provision should always specify submission at “the previous meeting,” and not “a” previous meeting, since the latter would permit indefinite delay and would defeat the object of giving notice—namely, to alert the members to the proposed amendment so that all those interested can arrange to be present at its consideration.”

**912.** `[C1416 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In making a requirement that notice be given by submitting the amendment at a meeting in advance of the one at which it is to be considered, the provision should always specify submission at “the previous meeting,” and not “a” previous meeting, since the latter would permit indefinite delay and would defeat the object of giving notice—namely, to alert the members to the proposed amendment so that all those interested can arrange to be present at its _____.

   *Answer:* consideration — “In making a requirement that notice be given by submitting the amendment at a meeting in advance of the one at which it is to be considered, the provision should always specify submission at “the previous meeting,” and not “a” previous meeting, since the latter would permit indefinite delay and would defeat the object of giving notice—namely, to alert the members to the proposed amendment so that all those interested can arrange to be present at its consideration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:50 — “In making a requirement that notice be given by submitting the amendment at a meeting in advance of the one at which it is to be considered, the provision should always specify submission at “the previous meeting,” and not “a” previous meeting, since the latter would permit indefinite delay and would defeat the object of giving notice—namely, to alert the members to the proposed amendment so that all those interested can arrange to be present at its consideration.”

**913.** `[C1417 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The manner _____ for giving notice should suit the needs of the particular assembly.

   *Answer:* prescribed — “The manner prescribed for giving notice should suit the needs of the particular assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:51 — “The manner prescribed for giving notice should suit the needs of the particular assembly.”

**914.** `[C1417 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some may require only a general _____ of the purport of the amendment;
   A. statement
   B. Crestwood
   C. expressly
   D. extending

   *Answer:* A) statement. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:51 — “Some may require only a general statement of the purport of the amendment;”

**915.** `[C1417 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some may require only a general statement of the purport of the _____;

   *Answer:* amendment — “Some may require only a general statement of the purport of the amendment;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:51 — “Some may require only a general statement of the purport of the amendment;”

**916.** `[C1417 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the bylaws require only previous notice of an amendment without limitation of the period within which it must be acted upon, and a committee is appointed to revise the bylaws and report at a specified meeting, the appointing action is all the notice required, and the amendments can be _____ acted upon at the time the committee reports.
   A. interrupted
   B. immediately
   C. Incidental
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:51 — “If the bylaws require only previous notice of an amendment without limitation of the period within which it must be acted upon, and a committee is appointed to revise the bylaws and report at a specified meeting, the appointing action is all the notice required, and the amendments can be immediately acted upon at the time the committee reports.”

**917.** `[C1417 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The manner _____ for giving notice should suit the needs of the particular assembly.
   A. Incidental
   B. landscaped
   C. prescribed
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* C) prescribed. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:51 — “The manner prescribed for giving notice should suit the needs of the particular assembly.”

**918.** `[C1419 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Where assemblies meet regularly only once a year, instead of requiring amendments to be submitted at the previous annual meeting, the bylaws should provide for both notice and copies of the proposed amendment to be sent to the member delegates or _____ societies a specified minimum number of days in advance.

   *Answer:* constituent — “Where assemblies meet regularly only once a year, instead of requiring amendments to be submitted at the previous annual meeting, the bylaws should provide for both notice and copies of the proposed amendment to be sent to the member delegates or constituent societies a specified minimum number of days in advance.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:53 — “Where assemblies meet regularly only once a year, instead of requiring amendments to be submitted at the previous annual meeting, the bylaws should provide for both notice and copies of the proposed amendment to be sent to the member delegates or constituent societies a specified minimum number of days in advance.”

**919.** `[C1419 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Where assemblies meet regularly only once a year, instead of requiring amendments to be submitted at the previous annual meeting, the bylaws should provide for both notice and copies of the proposed amendment to be sent to the member delegates or _____ societies a specified minimum number of days in advance.
   A. Procedures
   B. constituent
   C. adjournment
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) constituent. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:53 — “Where assemblies meet regularly only once a year, instead of requiring amendments to be submitted at the previous annual meeting, the bylaws should provide for both notice and copies of the proposed amendment to be sent to the member delegates or constituent societies a specified minimum number of days in advance.”

**920.** `[C1420 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is a _____ separate from the bylaws, the requirement for amendment of the constitution should be made more difficult than that for amendment of the bylaws;

   *Answer:* constitution — “If there is a constitution separate from the bylaws, the requirement for amendment of the constitution should be made more difficult than that for amendment of the bylaws;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:54 — “If there is a constitution separate from the bylaws, the requirement for amendment of the constitution should be made more difficult than that for amendment of the bylaws;”

**921.** `[C1420 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ there would be no purpose in having separate documents.
   A. amendment
   B. Secretary
   C. extending
   D. otherwise

   *Answer:* D) otherwise. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:54 — “otherwise there would be no purpose in having separate documents.”

**922.** `[C1420 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In either case, however, the _____ vote should be at least two thirds.

   *Answer:* necessary — “In either case, however, the necessary vote should be at least two thirds.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:54 — “In either case, however, the necessary vote should be at least two thirds.”

**923.** `[C1420 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is a _____ separate from the bylaws, the requirement for amendment of the constitution should be made more difficult than that for amendment of the bylaws;
   A. constitution
   B. subsequently
   C. Particular
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* A) constitution. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:54 — “If there is a constitution separate from the bylaws, the requirement for amendment of the constitution should be made more difficult than that for amendment of the bylaws;”

**924.** `[C1420 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ there would be no purpose in having separate documents.

   *Answer:* otherwise — “otherwise there would be no purpose in having separate documents.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:54 — “otherwise there would be no purpose in having separate documents.”

**925.** `[C1421 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In prescribing the vote necessary for the adoption of an amendment, the expression “a vote of two thirds of the members” should never be used in ordinary societies, especially in large _____.
   A. organizations
   B. interrupted
   C. undebatable
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:55 — “In prescribing the vote necessary for the adoption of an amendment, the expression “a vote of two thirds of the members” should never be used in ordinary societies, especially in large organizations.”

**926.** `[C1421 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such societies two thirds of the entire _____ would rarely, if ever, be present at a meeting.

   *Answer:* membership — “In such societies two thirds of the entire membership would rarely, if ever, be present at a meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:55 — “In such societies two thirds of the entire membership would rarely, if ever, be present at a meeting.”

**927.** `[C1421 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In prescribing the vote necessary for the adoption of an amendment, the expression “a vote of two thirds of the members” should never be used in ordinary societies, especially in large _____.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In prescribing the vote necessary for the adoption of an amendment, the expression “a vote of two thirds of the members” should never be used in ordinary societies, especially in large organizations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:55 — “In prescribing the vote necessary for the adoption of an amendment, the expression “a vote of two thirds of the members” should never be used in ordinary societies, especially in large organizations.”

**928.** `[C1421 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such societies two thirds of the entire _____ would rarely, if ever, be present at a meeting.
   A. landscaped
   B. membership
   C. persuasive
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:55 — “In such societies two thirds of the entire membership would rarely, if ever, be present at a meeting.”

**929.** `[C1422 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The wording of this article should avoid redundant phraseology such as “_____, alter, add to, or repeal,” or “alter or amend,” or “amend or in any way change.” The word amend covers any change, whether a word or a paragraph is to be added, struck out, or replaced, or whether a new set of articles is to be substituted for the old one.

   *Answer:* amend — “The wording of this article should avoid redundant phraseology such as “amend, alter, add to, or repeal,” or “alter or amend,” or “amend or in any way change.” The word amend covers any change, whether a word or a paragraph is to be added, struck out, or replaced, or whether a new set of articles is to be substituted for the old one.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:56 — “The wording of this article should avoid redundant phraseology such as “amend, alter, add to, or repeal,” or “alter or amend,” or “amend or in any way change.” The word amend covers any change, whether a word or a paragraph is to be added, struck out, or replaced, or whether a new set of articles is to be substituted for the old one.”

**930.** `[C1422 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Efforts to define the meaning of such expressions as “_____ vote” should also be avoided in the wording of this article, since these definitions are found in the parliamentary authority.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A two-thirds vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* C) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:56 — “Efforts to define the meaning of such expressions as “two-thirds vote” should also be avoided in the wording of this article, since these definitions are found in the parliamentary authority.”

**931.** `[C1422 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The wording of this article should avoid redundant _____ such as “amend, alter, add to, or repeal,” or “alter or amend,” or “amend or in any way change.” The word amend covers any change, whether a word or a paragraph is to be added, struck out, or replaced, or whether a new set of articles is to be substituted for the old one.

   *Answer:* phraseology — “The wording of this article should avoid redundant phraseology such as “amend, alter, add to, or repeal,” or “alter or amend,” or “amend or in any way change.” The word amend covers any change, whether a word or a paragraph is to be added, struck out, or replaced, or whether a new set of articles is to be substituted for the old one.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:56 — “The wording of this article should avoid redundant phraseology such as “amend, alter, add to, or repeal,” or “alter or amend,” or “amend or in any way change.” The word amend covers any change, whether a word or a paragraph is to be added, struck out, or replaced, or whether a new set of articles is to be substituted for the old one.”

**932.** `[C1422 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Efforts to define the meaning of such expressions as “two-thirds vote” should also be avoided in the wording of this article, since these definitions are found in the _____ authority.
   A. President-Elect
   B. specification
   C. undebatable
   D. parliamentary

   *Answer:* D) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:56 — “Efforts to define the meaning of such expressions as “two-thirds vote” should also be avoided in the wording of this article, since these definitions are found in the parliamentary authority.”

**933.** `[C1423 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some societies may have cause to include _____, such as those mentioned above, bearing on the subjects of finance, duties of officers, and an executive committee of the board of directors.

   *Answer:* additional bylaw articles — “Some societies may have cause to include additional bylaw articles, such as those mentioned above, bearing on the subjects of finance, duties of officers, and an executive committee of the board of directors.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:57 — “Some societies may have cause to include additional bylaw articles, such as those mentioned above, bearing on the subjects of finance, duties of officers, and an executive committee of the board of directors.”

**934.** `[C1423 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some societies may have cause to include _____ bylaw articles, such as those mentioned above, bearing on the subjects of finance, duties of officers, and an executive committee of the board of directors.
   A. Convention
   B. additional
   C. Unfinished
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* B) additional. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:57 — “Some societies may have cause to include additional bylaw articles, such as those mentioned above, bearing on the subjects of finance, duties of officers, and an executive committee of the board of directors.”

**935.** `[C1423 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a national organization, an article providing for constituent societies or units at regional, state, or local levels and establishing their relationships within the _____ structure may be required.

   *Answer:* organizational — “In a national organization, an article providing for constituent societies or units at regional, state, or local levels and establishing their relationships within the organizational structure may be required.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:57 — “In a national organization, an article providing for constituent societies or units at regional, state, or local levels and establishing their relationships within the organizational structure may be required.”

**936.** `[C1423 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In _____ divided into departments, the article establishing them—titled “Departments”—follows the article establishing committees.
   A. associations
   B. Indefinitely
   C. requirement
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) associations. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:57 — “In associations divided into departments, the article establishing them—titled “Departments”—follows the article establishing committees.”

**937.** `[C1423 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In _____ and some other societies there may be an article on disciplinary procedure;

**938.** `[C1424 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Titles of sections are optional, but they may be felt to be desirable, _____ if the bylaws become elaborate, as may be the case, for example, in a complex national organization.

   *Answer:* particularly — “Titles of sections are optional, but they may be felt to be desirable, particularly if the bylaws become elaborate, as may be the case, for example, in a complex national organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:58 — “Titles of sections are optional, but they may be felt to be desirable, particularly if the bylaws become elaborate, as may be the case, for example, in a complex national organization.”

**939.** `[C1424 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Titles of sections are optional, but they may be felt to be desirable, _____ if the bylaws become elaborate, as may be the case, for example, in a complex national organization.
   A. particularly
   B. Announcements
   C. consideration
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* A) particularly. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:58 — “Titles of sections are optional, but they may be felt to be desirable, particularly if the bylaws become elaborate, as may be the case, for example, in a complex national organization.”

**940.** `[C1425 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of this Society shall be limited to two hundred members.

   D. membership

   *Answer:* membership — “The membership of this Society shall be limited to two hundred members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:61 — “The membership of this Society shall be limited to two hundred members.”

**942.** `[C1426 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The officers of the Society shall be a President, a First _____, a Second Vice- President, a Recording Secretary, a Corresponding Secretary, a Treasurer, and four Directors.
   A. Responsibilities
   B. specification
   C. Vice-President
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) Vice-President. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:62 — “The officers of the Society shall be a President, a First Vice-President, a Second Vice- President, a Recording Secretary, a Corresponding Secretary, a Treasurer, and four Directors.”

   A. parliamentary

   D. Information

**946.** `[C1427 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The regular meetings of the Society shall be held on the second Tuesday of each month from _____ to May inclusive unless otherwise ordered by the Society.
   A. Committee
   B. Precedent
   C. expressly
   D. September

   *Answer:* D) September. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:63 — “The regular meetings of the Society shall be held on the second Tuesday of each month from September to May inclusive unless otherwise ordered by the Society.”

**947.** `[C1427 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The regular meetings of the Society shall be held on the second Tuesday of each month from _____ to May inclusive unless otherwise ordered by the Society.

   *Answer:* September — “The regular meetings of the Society shall be held on the second Tuesday of each month from September to May inclusive unless otherwise ordered by the Society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:63 — “The regular meetings of the Society shall be held on the second Tuesday of each month from September to May inclusive unless otherwise ordered by the Society.”

**948.** `[C1428 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The officers of the Society, including the Directors, shall _____ the Executive Board.

   *Answer:* constitute — “The officers of the Society, including the Directors, shall constitute the Executive Board.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:64 — “The officers of the Society, including the Directors, shall constitute the Executive Board.”

**949.** `[C1428 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The officers of the Society, including the Directors, shall _____ the Executive Board.

**950.** `[C1429 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Finance Committee may from time to time submit amendments to the budget for the current fiscal year, which may be adopted by a _____ vote.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A majority vote
   C. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* B) A majority vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:65 — “The Finance Committee may from time to time submit amendments to the budget for the current fiscal year, which may be adopted by a majority vote.”

**951.** `[C1429 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A Finance Committee composed of the _____ and four other members shall be appointed by the President promptly after each annual meeting.

   *Answer:* Treasurer — “A Finance Committee composed of the Treasurer and four other members shall be appointed by the President promptly after each annual meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:65 — “A Finance Committee composed of the Treasurer and four other members shall be appointed by the President promptly after each annual meeting.”

**952.** `[C1429 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It shall be the duty of this committee to prepare a budget for the fiscal year _____ the first day of April, and to submit it to the Society at its regular meeting in March.
   A. beginning
   B. paragraph
   C. extending
   D. secondary

   *Answer:* A) beginning. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:65 — “It shall be the duty of this committee to prepare a budget for the fiscal year beginning the first day of April, and to submit it to the Society at its regular meeting in March.”

**953.** `[C1429 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Finance Committee may from time to time submit _____ to the budget for the current fiscal year, which may be adopted by a majority vote.

See RONR (12th ed.) §56:65 — “The Finance Committee may from time to time submit amendments to the budget for the current fiscal year, which may be adopted by a majority vote.”

**954.** `[C1429 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A Finance Committee composed of the _____ and four other members shall be appointed by the President promptly after each annual meeting.
   A. rejecting
   B. debatable
   C. paragraph
   D. Treasurer

**955.** `[C1430 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ The rules contained in the current edition of Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised shall govern the Society in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with these bylaws and any special rules of order the Society may adopt.

   *Answer:* ARTICLE VIII: PARLIAMENTARY AUTHORITY — “ARTICLE VIII: PARLIAMENTARY AUTHORITY The rules contained in the current edition of Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised shall govern the Society in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with these bylaws and any special rules of order the Society may adopt.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:66 — “ARTICLE VIII: PARLIAMENTARY AUTHORITY The rules contained in the current edition of Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised shall govern the Society in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with these bylaws and any special rules of order the Society may adopt.”

   A. Disposition of
   B. Other Officers
   C. Filling Blanks
   D. Robert’s Rules

   *Answer:* D) Robert’s Rules. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:66 — “ARTICLE VIII: PARLIAMENTARY AUTHORITY The rules contained in the current edition of Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised shall govern the Society in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with these bylaws and any special rules of order the Society may adopt.”

**957.** `[C1431 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: ARTICLE IX: AMENDMENT OF BYLAWS These bylaws may be amended at any regular meeting of the Society by a _____ vote, provided that the amendment has been submitted in writing at the previous regular meeting.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “ARTICLE IX: AMENDMENT OF BYLAWS These bylaws may be amended at any regular meeting of the Society by a two-thirds vote, provided that the amendment has been submitted in writing at the previous regular meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:67 — “ARTICLE IX: AMENDMENT OF BYLAWS These bylaws may be amended at any regular meeting of the Society by a two-thirds vote, provided that the amendment has been submitted in writing at the previous regular meeting.”

**958.** `[C1431 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: ARTICLE IX: AMENDMENT OF BYLAWS These bylaws may be amended at any regular meeting of the Society by a _____ vote, provided that the amendment has been submitted in writing at the previous regular meeting.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:67 — “ARTICLE IX: AMENDMENT OF BYLAWS These bylaws may be amended at any regular meeting of the Society by a two-thirds vote, provided that the amendment has been submitted in writing at the previous regular meeting.”

**959.** `[C1432 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In preparing bylaws and interpreting them, the following principles of _____—which have equal application to other rules and documents adopted by an organization—may be of assistance.

   *Answer:* interpretation — “In preparing bylaws and interpreting them, the following principles of interpretation—which have equal application to other rules and documents adopted by an organization—may be of assistance.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:68 — “In preparing bylaws and interpreting them, the following principles of interpretation—which have equal application to other rules and documents adopted by an organization—may be of assistance.”

**960.** `[C1432 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In preparing bylaws and interpreting them, the following principles of _____—which have equal application to other rules and documents adopted by an organization—may be of assistance.
   A. interpretation
   B. constitution
   C. presentation
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) interpretation. See RONR (12th ed.) §56:68 — “In preparing bylaws and interpreting them, the following principles of interpretation—which have equal application to other rules and documents adopted by an organization—may be of assistance.”

**961.** `[C1433 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to amend the bylaws is a particular case of the motion to _____ (35);

   *Answer:* Amend Something Previously Adopted — “A motion to amend the bylaws is a particular case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:1 — “A motion to amend the bylaws is a particular case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35);”

**962.** `[C1433 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to amend the bylaws is a _____ case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35);
   A. particular
   B. Definitely
   C. substitute
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:1 — “A motion to amend the bylaws is a particular case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35);”
