**963.** `[C1433 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: it is therefore a main motion, and it is subject to the same rules as other main motions with the following _____:

   *Answer:* exceptions — “it is therefore a main motion, and it is subject to the same rules as other main motions with the following exceptions:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:1 — “it is therefore a main motion, and it is subject to the same rules as other main motions with the following exceptions:”

**964.** `[C1433 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: it is therefore a main motion, and it is subject to the same rules as other main motions with the following _____:
   A. introduced
   B. settlement
   C. exceptions
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) exceptions. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:1 — “it is therefore a main motion, and it is subject to the same rules as other main motions with the following exceptions:”

**965.** `[C1434 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of amendments to the bylaws will determine the method of handling them, as follows:

   *Answer:* extensiveness — “The extensiveness of amendments to the bylaws will determine the method of handling them, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:2 — “The extensiveness of amendments to the bylaws will determine the method of handling them, as follows:”

**966.** `[C1434 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of amendments to the bylaws will determine the method of handling them, as follows:
   A. extensiveness
   B. deliberative
   C. presentation
   D. Vice-President

   *Answer:* A) extensiveness. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:2 — “The extensiveness of amendments to the bylaws will determine the method of handling them, as follows:”

**967.** `[C1435 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a series of _____ to the bylaws are needed to achieve one end—such as abolishing the office of “Historian” and eliminating all references to it—the changes should be offered in a single motion.

   *Answer:* isolated changes — “When a series of isolated changes to the bylaws are needed to achieve one end—such as abolishing the office of “Historian” and eliminating all references to it—the changes should be offered in a single motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:3 — “When a series of isolated changes to the bylaws are needed to achieve one end—such as abolishing the office of “Historian” and eliminating all references to it—the changes should be offered in a single motion.”

**968.** `[C1435 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If only an isolated change is to be made in the bylaws, it can be treated as any motion to _____ Something Previously Adopted (35), subject to the particular rules indicated immediately above.
   A. Amend
   B. limit
   C. board
   D. floor

   *Answer:* A) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:3 — “If only an isolated change is to be made in the bylaws, it can be treated as any motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), subject to the particular rules indicated immediately above.”

**969.** `[C1435 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If only an isolated change is to be made in the bylaws, it can be treated as any motion to Amend _____ (35), subject to the particular rules indicated immediately above.

   *Answer:* Something Previously Adopted — “If only an isolated change is to be made in the bylaws, it can be treated as any motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), subject to the particular rules indicated immediately above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:3 — “If only an isolated change is to be made in the bylaws, it can be treated as any motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), subject to the particular rules indicated immediately above.”

**970.** `[C1435 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a series of isolated changes to the bylaws are needed to achieve one end—such as abolishing the office of “Historian” and _____ all references to it—the changes should be offered in a single motion.

   *Answer:* eliminating — “When a series of isolated changes to the bylaws are needed to achieve one end—such as abolishing the office of “Historian” and eliminating all references to it—the changes should be offered in a single motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:3 — “When a series of isolated changes to the bylaws are needed to achieve one end—such as abolishing the office of “Historian” and eliminating all references to it—the changes should be offered in a single motion.”

**971.** `[C1436 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes a more extensive change is proposed involving the _____ of an entire section, group of sections, or article.

   *Answer:* substitution — “Sometimes a more extensive change is proposed involving the substitution of an entire section, group of sections, or article.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:4 — “Sometimes a more extensive change is proposed involving the substitution of an entire section, group of sections, or article.”

**972.** `[C1436 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, often only a few separated passages are actually involved in the changes, and they are offered in the form of a single proposed substitute in order to avoid _____ separate action on each change.
   A. timeconsuming
   B. consideration
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) timeconsuming. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:4 — “In such a case, often only a few separated passages are actually involved in the changes, and they are offered in the form of a single proposed substitute in order to avoid timeconsuming separate action on each change.”

**973.** `[C1436 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, often only a few separated passages are actually involved in the changes, and they are offered in the form of a single proposed _____ in order to avoid timeconsuming separate action on each change.

   *Answer:* substitute — “In such a case, often only a few separated passages are actually involved in the changes, and they are offered in the form of a single proposed substitute in order to avoid timeconsuming separate action on each change.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:4 — “In such a case, often only a few separated passages are actually involved in the changes, and they are offered in the form of a single proposed substitute in order to avoid timeconsuming separate action on each change.”

**974.** `[C1436 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes a more extensive change is proposed involving the _____ of an entire section, group of sections, or article.
   A. subsequently
   B. Definitely
   C. constitution
   D. substitution

   *Answer:* D) substitution. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:4 — “Sometimes a more extensive change is proposed involving the substitution of an entire section, group of sections, or article.”

**975.** `[C1437 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Changes of the bylaws that are so extensive and general that they are scattered throughout the bylaws should be effected through the substitution of an entirely new set of bylaws, called a _____.
   A. decision
   B. Division
   C. revision
   D. Approved

   *Answer:* C) revision. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:5 — “Changes of the bylaws that are so extensive and general that they are scattered throughout the bylaws should be effected through the substitution of an entirely new set of bylaws, called a revision.”

**976.** `[C1437 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Changes of the bylaws that are so extensive and general that they are scattered throughout the bylaws should be effected through the _____ of an entirely new set of bylaws, called a revision.

   *Answer:* substitution — “Changes of the bylaws that are so extensive and general that they are scattered throughout the bylaws should be effected through the substitution of an entirely new set of bylaws, called a revision.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:5 — “Changes of the bylaws that are so extensive and general that they are scattered throughout the bylaws should be effected through the substitution of an entirely new set of bylaws, called a revision.”

**977.** `[C1437 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Notice of such a revision is notice that a new document will be _____ that will be open to amendment as fully as if the society were adopting bylaws for the first time.
   A. Signature
   B. secondary
   C. submitted
   D. Precedent

   *Answer:* C) submitted. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:5 — “Notice of such a revision is notice that a new document will be submitted that will be open to amendment as fully as if the society were adopting bylaws for the first time.”

**978.** `[C1437 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In other words, in the case of a revision, the assembly is not confined to _____ of only the points of change included in the proposed revision as submitted by the committee that has drafted it.

   *Answer:* consideration — “In other words, in the case of a revision, the assembly is not confined to consideration of only the points of change included in the proposed revision as submitted by the committee that has drafted it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:5 — “In other words, in the case of a revision, the assembly is not confined to consideration of only the points of change included in the proposed revision as submitted by the committee that has drafted it.”

**979.** `[C1437 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The revision can be perfected by first-degree and _____ amendments, but as in the case of any other bylaw amendment, the old document is not pending;
   A. requirement
   B. adjournment
   C. Information
   D. second-degree

   *Answer:* D) second-degree. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:5 — “The revision can be perfected by first-degree and second-degree amendments, but as in the case of any other bylaw amendment, the old document is not pending;”

**980.** `[C1438 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An affirmative vote adopting such an arrangement is not subject to a motion to _____, nor may a later, separate amendment be offered as a substitute for a pending one.
   A. Incidental
   B. Reconsider
   C. incidental
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:6 — “An affirmative vote adopting such an arrangement is not subject to a motion to Reconsider, nor may a later, separate amendment be offered as a substitute for a pending one.”

**981.** `[C1438 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: a motion to rearrange the amendments requires a second, is not debatable, is amendable, and requires a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “a motion to rearrange the amendments requires a second, is not debatable, is amendable, and requires a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:6 — “a motion to rearrange the amendments requires a second, is not debatable, is amendable, and requires a majority vote.”

**982.** `[C1438 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A revision of bylaws or a lengthy amendment involving more than one section should be _____ seriatim as described in 28.
   A. considered
   B. recognized
   C. landscaped
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* A) considered. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:6 — “A revision of bylaws or a lengthy amendment involving more than one section should be considered seriatim as described in 28.”

**983.** `[C1438 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If notice is given of several _____ which conflict so that all cannot be given effect, the chair should arrange them in a logical order, much as in the case of filling blanks (12), generally taking the least inclusive amendment first and the most inclusive last so that the last one adopted is given effect.

   *Answer:* amendments — “If notice is given of several amendments which conflict so that all cannot be given effect, the chair should arrange them in a logical order, much as in the case of filling blanks (12), generally taking the least inclusive amendment first and the most inclusive last so that the last one adopted is given effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:6 — “If notice is given of several amendments which conflict so that all cannot be given effect, the chair should arrange them in a logical order, much as in the case of filling blanks (12), generally taking the least inclusive amendment first and the most inclusive last so that the last one adopted is given effect.”

**984.** `[C1438 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: That _____ of the amendments can be altered by the assembly;
   A. arrangement
   B. constitution
   C. requirement
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* A) arrangement. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:6 — “That arrangement of the amendments can be altered by the assembly;”

**985.** `[C1439 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and it is the bylaw language _____, rather than the previous language, which any bylaw amendments subsequently considered would now propose to modify.

   *Answer:* as thus amended — “and it is the bylaw language as thus amended, rather than the previous language, which any bylaw amendments subsequently considered would now propose to modify.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:7 — “and it is the bylaw language as thus amended, rather than the previous language, which any bylaw amendments subsequently considered would now propose to modify.”

**986.** `[C1439 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This procedure does not violate the normal _____ rule as might appear, because when any bylaw amendment is adopted, that amendment becomes a part of the bylaws immediately;
   A. Information
   B. parliamentary
   C. President-Elect
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:7 — “This procedure does not violate the normal parliamentary rule as might appear, because when any bylaw amendment is adopted, that amendment becomes a part of the bylaws immediately;”

**987.** `[C1439 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and it is the bylaw language as thus amended, rather than the previous language, which any bylaw amendments _____ considered would now propose to modify.

   *Answer:* subsequently — “and it is the bylaw language as thus amended, rather than the previous language, which any bylaw amendments subsequently considered would now propose to modify.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:7 — “and it is the bylaw language as thus amended, rather than the previous language, which any bylaw amendments subsequently considered would now propose to modify.”

**988.** `[C1439 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This procedure does not violate the normal _____ rule as might appear, because when any bylaw amendment is adopted, that amendment becomes a part of the bylaws immediately;

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “This procedure does not violate the normal parliamentary rule as might appear, because when any bylaw amendment is adopted, that amendment becomes a part of the bylaws immediately;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:7 — “This procedure does not violate the normal parliamentary rule as might appear, because when any bylaw amendment is adopted, that amendment becomes a part of the bylaws immediately;”

**989.** `[C1440 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an amendment that has not been considered no longer presents a rational _____ because it was applicable only to language which has disappeared from the bylaws in this process, such a bylaw amendment must, of course, be dropped;

   *Answer:* proposition — “If an amendment that has not been considered no longer presents a rational proposition because it was applicable only to language which has disappeared from the bylaws in this process, such a bylaw amendment must, of course, be dropped;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:8 — “If an amendment that has not been considered no longer presents a rational proposition because it was applicable only to language which has disappeared from the bylaws in this process, such a bylaw amendment must, of course, be dropped;”

**990.** `[C1440 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but this situation should generally not arise if the _____ are taken up in proper order as indicated above.
   A. incidental
   B. amendments
   C. convention
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* B) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:8 — “but this situation should generally not arise if the amendments are taken up in proper order as indicated above.”

**991.** `[C1440 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an amendment that has not been considered no longer presents a rational _____ because it was applicable only to language which has disappeared from the bylaws in this process, such a bylaw amendment must, of course, be dropped;
   A. adjournment
   B. Convention
   C. proposition
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) proposition. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:8 — “If an amendment that has not been considered no longer presents a rational proposition because it was applicable only to language which has disappeared from the bylaws in this process, such a bylaw amendment must, of course, be dropped;”

**992.** `[C1440 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but this situation should generally not arise if the _____ are taken up in proper order as indicated above.

   *Answer:* amendments — “but this situation should generally not arise if the amendments are taken up in proper order as indicated above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:8 — “but this situation should generally not arise if the amendments are taken up in proper order as indicated above.”

**993.** `[C1441 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The final vote on a bylaw amendment should be counted and recorded in the minutes unless it is nearly _____.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “The final vote on a bylaw amendment should be counted and recorded in the minutes unless it is nearly unanimous.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:9 — “The final vote on a bylaw amendment should be counted and recorded in the minutes unless it is nearly unanimous.”

**994.** `[C1441 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The final vote on a bylaw _____ should be counted and recorded in the minutes unless it is nearly unanimous.
   A. secretary
   B. rejecting
   C. amendment
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* C) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:9 — “The final vote on a bylaw amendment should be counted and recorded in the minutes unless it is nearly unanimous.”

**995.** `[C1442 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While amendments to a proposed bylaw amendment can be made in both the first and the second degrees (as applicable) and can be adopted by a _____ vote without notice, they are subject to restrictions on the extent of the changes they propose.

   *Answer:* majority — “While amendments to a proposed bylaw amendment can be made in both the first and the second degrees (as applicable) and can be adopted by a majority vote without notice, they are subject to restrictions on the extent of the changes they propose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:10 — “While amendments to a proposed bylaw amendment can be made in both the first and the second degrees (as applicable) and can be adopted by a majority vote without notice, they are subject to restrictions on the extent of the changes they propose.”

**996.** `[C1442 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While amendments to a proposed bylaw amendment can be made in both the first and the second degrees (as applicable) and can be adopted by a majority vote without notice, they are subject to _____ on the extent of the changes they propose.
   A. restrictions
   B. Permission
   C. subsequently
   D. Information

   *Answer:* A) restrictions. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:10 — “While amendments to a proposed bylaw amendment can be made in both the first and the second degrees (as applicable) and can be adopted by a majority vote without notice, they are subject to restrictions on the extent of the changes they propose.”

**997.** `[C1443 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, if the bylaws place the annual dues of members at $10 and an amendment is pending to strike out 10 and insert 25, an amendment to change the 25 to any number between 10 and 25 would be in order, but an amendment to change the number to less than 10 or greater than 25 would not be in order, even with _____ consent.

   *Answer:* unanimous — “Thus, if the bylaws place the annual dues of members at $10 and an amendment is pending to strike out 10 and insert 25, an amendment to change the 25 to any number between 10 and 25 would be in order, but an amendment to change the number to less than 10 or greater than 25 would not be in order, even with unanimous consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:11 — “Thus, if the bylaws place the annual dues of members at $10 and an amendment is pending to strike out 10 and insert 25, an amendment to change the 25 to any number between 10 and 25 would be in order, but an amendment to change the number to less than 10 or greater than 25 would not be in order, even with unanimous consent.”

**998.** `[C1443 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This _____ prevents members from proposing a slight change and then taking advantage of absent members by moving a greater one as an amendment to the amendment.
   A. committees
   B. Unfinished
   C. Information
   D. restriction

   *Answer:* D) restriction. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:11 — “This restriction prevents members from proposing a slight change and then taking advantage of absent members by moving a greater one as an amendment to the amendment.”

**999.** `[C1443 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This restriction prevents members from proposing a slight change and then taking advantage of absent members by moving a greater one as an _____ to the amendment.

   *Answer:* amendment — “This restriction prevents members from proposing a slight change and then taking advantage of absent members by moving a greater one as an amendment to the amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:11 — “This restriction prevents members from proposing a slight change and then taking advantage of absent members by moving a greater one as an amendment to the amendment.”

**1000.** `[C1443 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, if the bylaws place the annual dues of members at $10 and an amendment is pending to strike out 10 and insert 25, an amendment to change the 25 to any number between 10 and 25 would be in order, but an amendment to change the number to less than 10 or greater than 25 would not be in order, even with _____ consent.
   A. A majority vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* B) A unanimous (general consent) vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:11 — “Thus, if the bylaws place the annual dues of members at $10 and an amendment is pending to strike out 10 and insert 25, an amendment to change the 25 to any number between 10 and 25 would be in order, but an amendment to change the number to less than 10 or greater than 25 would not be in order, even with unanimous consent.”

**1001.** `[C1444 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The same principle applies to an amendment in the nature of a _____ for sections or articles (short of a revision), as already indicated above;

   *Answer:* substitute — “The same principle applies to an amendment in the nature of a substitute for sections or articles (short of a revision), as already indicated above;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:12 — “The same principle applies to an amendment in the nature of a substitute for sections or articles (short of a revision), as already indicated above;”

**1002.** `[C1444 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The same principle applies to an amendment in the nature of a _____ for sections or articles (short of a revision), as already indicated above;
   A. persuasive
   B. recognized
   C. substitute
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* C) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:12 — “The same principle applies to an amendment in the nature of a substitute for sections or articles (short of a revision), as already indicated above;”

**1003.** `[C1445 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ to strike out a sentence, paragraph, or section deserve special care.

   *Answer:* Amendments — “Amendments to strike out a sentence, paragraph, or section deserve special care.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:13 — “Amendments to strike out a sentence, paragraph, or section deserve special care.”

**1004.** `[C1445 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such cases, the existing bylaw is not itself open to _____, but only the amendment.
   A. Announcements
   B. requirement
   C. deliberative
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:13 — “In such cases, the existing bylaw is not itself open to consideration, but only the amendment.”

**1005.** `[C1445 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If notice is given to strike out a provision of the bylaws and some members feel it should be retained with certain changes whose substance would be outside the scope of that notice, those members should _____ give notice of the amendments to the existing provisions which they think are advisable.

   *Answer:* immediately — “If notice is given to strike out a provision of the bylaws and some members feel it should be retained with certain changes whose substance would be outside the scope of that notice, those members should immediately give notice of the amendments to the existing provisions which they think are advisable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:13 — “If notice is given to strike out a provision of the bylaws and some members feel it should be retained with certain changes whose substance would be outside the scope of that notice, those members should immediately give notice of the amendments to the existing provisions which they think are advisable.”

**1006.** `[C1445 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Otherwise, friends of the existing provision will be cut off from _____ to work out compromises for its partial retention by perfecting the existing language.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. Substitute
   C. opportunity
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* C) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:13 — “Otherwise, friends of the existing provision will be cut off from opportunity to work out compromises for its partial retention by perfecting the existing language.”

**1007.** `[C1445 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ to strike out a sentence, paragraph, or section deserve special care.
   A. introduced
   B. Unfinished
   C. Amendments
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) Amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:13 — “Amendments to strike out a sentence, paragraph, or section deserve special care.”

**1008.** `[C1446 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If notice is to be given at a meeting, this is usually done under new business, although it can be done at any time, even after it has been voted to adjourn if the chair has not actually declared the meeting _____.

   *Answer:* adjourned — “If notice is to be given at a meeting, this is usually done under new business, although it can be done at any time, even after it has been voted to adjourn if the chair has not actually declared the meeting adjourned.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:14 — “If notice is to be given at a meeting, this is usually done under new business, although it can be done at any time, even after it has been voted to adjourn if the chair has not actually declared the meeting adjourned.”

**1009.** `[C1446 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A bylaws committee can give notice in that part of the order of business set aside for committee _____.
   A. reports
   B. Inquiry
   C. carried
   D. censure

   *Answer:* A) reports. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:14 — “A bylaws committee can give notice in that part of the order of business set aside for committee reports.”

**1010.** `[C1446 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If notice is to be sent with the call of the meeting at which the amendment will be introduced, the society is _____ for paying the cost of sending such notice, not the member proposing the amendment.

   *Answer:* responsible — “If notice is to be sent with the call of the meeting at which the amendment will be introduced, the society is responsible for paying the cost of sending such notice, not the member proposing the amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:14 — “If notice is to be sent with the call of the meeting at which the amendment will be introduced, the society is responsible for paying the cost of sending such notice, not the member proposing the amendment.”

**1011.** `[C1446 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The notice should fairly inform the members of the changes _____.
   A. Particular
   B. contemplated
   C. undebatable
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) contemplated. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:14 — “The notice should fairly inform the members of the changes contemplated.”

**1012.** `[C1446 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If notice is to be sent with the call of the meeting at which the _____ will be introduced, the society is responsible for paying the cost of sending such notice, not the member proposing the amendment.

   *Answer:* amendment — “If notice is to be sent with the call of the meeting at which the amendment will be introduced, the society is responsible for paying the cost of sending such notice, not the member proposing the amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:14 — “If notice is to be sent with the call of the meeting at which the amendment will be introduced, the society is responsible for paying the cost of sending such notice, not the member proposing the amendment.”

**1013.** `[C1447 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Either method requires only a _____ vote.

   *Answer:* majority — “Either method requires only a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:15 — “Either method requires only a majority vote.”

**1014.** `[C1447 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An amendment to the bylaws goes into effect _____ upon its adoption unless the motion to adopt specifies another time for its becoming effective, or the assembly has set such a time by a previously adopted motion.
   A. immediately
   B. assemblies
   C. requirement
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:15 — “An amendment to the bylaws goes into effect immediately upon its adoption unless the motion to adopt specifies another time for its becoming effective, or the assembly has set such a time by a previously adopted motion.”

**1015.** `[C1447 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While the amendment is pending, a motion can be made to amend the enacting words of the motion to amend by adding a clause such as this: “… with the proviso that [or, “… provided, however, that”] this amendment shall not go into effect until after the close of this annual meeting.” Or, while the amendment is pending, an _____ motion can be adopted that, in the event of the amendment’s adoption, it shall not take effect until a specified time.

   *Answer:* incidental — “While the amendment is pending, a motion can be made to amend the enacting words of the motion to amend by adding a clause such as this: “… with the proviso that [or, “… provided, however, that”] this amendment shall not go into effect until after the close of this annual meeting.” Or, while the amendment is pending, an incidental motion can be adopted that, in the event of the amendment’s adoption, it shall not take effect until a specified time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:15 — “While the amendment is pending, a motion can be made to amend the enacting words of the motion to amend by adding a clause such as this: “… with the proviso that [or, “… provided, however, that”] this amendment shall not go into effect until after the close of this annual meeting.” Or, while the amendment is pending, an incidental motion can be adopted that, in the event of the amendment’s adoption, it shall not take effect until a specified time.”

**1016.** `[C1447 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Either method _____ only a majority vote.
   A. Previous
   B. Assembly
   C. requires
   D. Question

   *Answer:* C) requires. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:15 — “Either method requires only a majority vote.”

**1017.** `[C1447 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is a mistake to encumber the bylaws _____ with provisions which have effect for only a limited time.

   *Answer:* themselves — “It is a mistake to encumber the bylaws themselves with provisions which have effect for only a limited time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:15 — “It is a mistake to encumber the bylaws themselves with provisions which have effect for only a limited time.”

**1018.** `[C1448 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Amendments to the article on officers may raise _____ in relation to the time at which adopted changes take effect, unless special care is taken.

   *Answer:* difficulties — “Amendments to the article on officers may raise difficulties in relation to the time at which adopted changes take effect, unless special care is taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:16 — “Amendments to the article on officers may raise difficulties in relation to the time at which adopted changes take effect, unless special care is taken.”

**1019.** `[C1448 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A society can, for example, amend its bylaws so as to affect the _____ and duties of the officers already elected, or even to abolish an office;
   A. authorizes
   B. sustaining
   C. landscaped
   D. emoluments

   *Answer:* D) emoluments. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:16 — “A society can, for example, amend its bylaws so as to affect the emoluments and duties of the officers already elected, or even to abolish an office;”

**1020.** `[C1448 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and if it is desired that the amendment should not affect officers already elected, a motion so _____ should be adopted before voting on the amendment, or the motion to amend can have added to it the proviso that it shall not affect officers already elected.

   *Answer:* specifying — “and if it is desired that the amendment should not affect officers already elected, a motion so specifying should be adopted before voting on the amendment, or the motion to amend can have added to it the proviso that it shall not affect officers already elected.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:16 — “and if it is desired that the amendment should not affect officers already elected, a motion so specifying should be adopted before voting on the amendment, or the motion to amend can have added to it the proviso that it shall not affect officers already elected.”

**1021.** `[C1448 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: There is virtually a contract between a society and its officers, and while to some extent action can be taken by either party to modify or even terminate the contract, such action must be taken with reasonable _____ for the other party.
   A. consideration
   B. deliberative
   C. requirement
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:16 — “There is virtually a contract between a society and its officers, and while to some extent action can be taken by either party to modify or even terminate the contract, such action must be taken with reasonable consideration for the other party.”

**1022.** `[C1448 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Amendments to the article on officers may raise _____ in relation to the time at which adopted changes take effect, unless special care is taken.
   A. undebatable
   B. difficulties
   C. Incidental
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* B) difficulties. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:16 — “Amendments to the article on officers may raise difficulties in relation to the time at which adopted changes take effect, unless special care is taken.”

**1023.** `[C1449 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is important to note that, although the time when a bylaw amendment _____ can be delayed by the assembly, the amendment becomes part of the bylaws immediately upon adoption.

   *Answer:* takes effect — “It is important to note that, although the time when a bylaw amendment takes effect can be delayed by the assembly, the amendment becomes part of the bylaws immediately upon adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:17 — “It is important to note that, although the time when a bylaw amendment takes effect can be delayed by the assembly, the amendment becomes part of the bylaws immediately upon adoption.”

**1024.** `[C1449 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is important to note that, although the time when a bylaw amendment takes effect can be delayed by the assembly, the amendment becomes part of the bylaws _____ upon adoption.
   A. Preparation
   B. assemblies
   C. immediately
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:17 — “It is important to note that, although the time when a bylaw amendment takes effect can be delayed by the assembly, the amendment becomes part of the bylaws immediately upon adoption.”

**1025.** `[C1449 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the amended bylaws are printed, a footnote or similar device should indicate that the amended language is not yet in effect and, if language was removed by the amendment, the text of that provision should be given if it is still applicable in the _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “If the amended bylaws are printed, a footnote or similar device should indicate that the amended language is not yet in effect and, if language was removed by the amendment, the text of that provision should be given if it is still applicable in the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:17 — “If the amended bylaws are printed, a footnote or similar device should indicate that the amended language is not yet in effect and, if language was removed by the amendment, the text of that provision should be given if it is still applicable in the organization.”

**1026.** `[C1449 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the amended bylaws are printed, a footnote or similar device should indicate that the amended language is not yet in effect and, if language was removed by the amendment, the text of that provision should be given if it is still applicable in the _____.
   A. organization
   B. deliberative
   C. consideration
   D. specification

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:17 — “If the amended bylaws are printed, a footnote or similar device should indicate that the amended language is not yet in effect and, if language was removed by the amendment, the text of that provision should be given if it is still applicable in the organization.”

**1027.** `[C1450 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It was formerly _____ to permit the secretary to fill in captions, headings, and article, section, or paragraph numbers or letters, and the like, after the assembly had adopted bylaws or other long documents.

   *Answer:* customary — “It was formerly customary to permit the secretary to fill in captions, headings, and article, section, or paragraph numbers or letters, and the like, after the assembly had adopted bylaws or other long documents.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:18 — “It was formerly customary to permit the secretary to fill in captions, headings, and article, section, or paragraph numbers or letters, and the like, after the assembly had adopted bylaws or other long documents.”

**1028.** `[C1450 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such _____ were treated as mere marginal notations which could be clerically modified.
   A. requirement
   B. Convention
   C. designations
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) designations. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:18 — “Such designations were treated as mere marginal notations which could be clerically modified.”

**1029.** `[C1450 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is now the usual practice to include these subtitles or _____ numbers or letters as an integral part of what is adopted by action of the assembly.

   *Answer:* identifying — “It is now the usual practice to include these subtitles or identifying numbers or letters as an integral part of what is adopted by action of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:18 — “It is now the usual practice to include these subtitles or identifying numbers or letters as an integral part of what is adopted by action of the assembly.”

**1030.** `[C1450 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It was formerly _____ to permit the secretary to fill in captions, headings, and article, section, or paragraph numbers or letters, and the like, after the assembly had adopted bylaws or other long documents.
   A. delegates
   B. paragraph
   C. debatable
   D. customary

   *Answer:* D) customary. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:18 — “It was formerly customary to permit the secretary to fill in captions, headings, and article, section, or paragraph numbers or letters, and the like, after the assembly had adopted bylaws or other long documents.”

**1031.** `[C1450 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such _____ were treated as mere marginal notations which could be clerically modified.

   *Answer:* designations — “Such designations were treated as mere marginal notations which could be clerically modified.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:18 — “Such designations were treated as mere marginal notations which could be clerically modified.”

**1032.** `[C1451 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the process of amending previously adopted documents of this kind, _____ necessary changes in designation by number or letter may be presumed to have been included in the assembly’s action even if they were not mentioned.

   *Answer:* indisputably — “In the process of amending previously adopted documents of this kind, indisputably necessary changes in designation by number or letter may be presumed to have been included in the assembly’s action even if they were not mentioned.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:19 — “In the process of amending previously adopted documents of this kind, indisputably necessary changes in designation by number or letter may be presumed to have been included in the assembly’s action even if they were not mentioned.”

**1033.** `[C1451 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the process of amending previously adopted documents of this kind, indisputably necessary changes in _____ by number or letter may be presumed to have been included in the assembly’s action even if they were not mentioned.
   A. constitution
   B. assemblies
   C. Preparation
   D. designation

   *Answer:* D) designation. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:19 — “In the process of amending previously adopted documents of this kind, indisputably necessary changes in designation by number or letter may be presumed to have been included in the assembly’s action even if they were not mentioned.”

**1034.** `[C1451 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Only the assembly can amend captions or headings under the rules _____ to bylaws or other papers if such change could have any effect on meaning, and this authority may not be delegated.

   *Answer:* applicable — “Only the assembly can amend captions or headings under the rules applicable to bylaws or other papers if such change could have any effect on meaning, and this authority may not be delegated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:19 — “Only the assembly can amend captions or headings under the rules applicable to bylaws or other papers if such change could have any effect on meaning, and this authority may not be delegated.”

**1035.** `[C1451 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Corrections of article or section numbers or _____ that cannot result in a change of meaning can be delegated, however, to the secretary or, in more involved cases, to a committee.
   A. Responsibilities
   B. cross-references
   C. adjournment
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) cross-references. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:19 — “Corrections of article or section numbers or cross-references that cannot result in a change of meaning can be delegated, however, to the secretary or, in more involved cases, to a committee.”

**1036.** `[C1451 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An assembly may delegate its authority in this _____ in a particular case, by adopting, for example, a resolution such as the following:

   *Answer:* connection — “An assembly may delegate its authority in this connection in a particular case, by adopting, for example, a resolution such as the following:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §57:19 — “An assembly may delegate its authority in this connection in a particular case, by adopting, for example, a resolution such as the following:”

**1037.** `[C1452 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As commonly understood in parliamentary law and as used in this book, the word _____ refers to an assembly of delegates (other than a permanently constituted public lawmaking body), who are usually chosen specially for each session as representatives of the constituent units or subdivisions within a larger group of people, to sit as a single deliberative body acting in the name of the entire group.

   *Answer:* convention — “As commonly understood in parliamentary law and as used in this book, the word convention refers to an assembly of delegates (other than a permanently constituted public lawmaking body), who are usually chosen specially for each session as representatives of the constituent units or subdivisions within a larger group of people, to sit as a single deliberative body acting in the name of the entire group.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:1 — “As commonly understood in parliamentary law and as used in this book, the word convention refers to an assembly of delegates (other than a permanently constituted public lawmaking body), who are usually chosen specially for each session as representatives of the constituent units or subdivisions within a larger group of people, to sit as a single deliberative body acting in the name of the entire group.”

**1038.** `[C1452 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As commonly understood in parliamentary law and as used in this book, the word convention refers to an assembly of _____ (other than a permanently constituted public lawmaking body), who are usually chosen specially for each session as representatives of the constituent units or subdivisions within a larger group of people, to sit as a single deliberative body acting in the name of the entire group.
   A. rejecting
   B. delegates
   C. president
   D. Crestwood

   *Answer:* B) delegates. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:1 — “As commonly understood in parliamentary law and as used in this book, the word convention refers to an assembly of delegates (other than a permanently constituted public lawmaking body), who are usually chosen specially for each session as representatives of the constituent units or subdivisions within a larger group of people, to sit as a single deliberative body acting in the name of the entire group.”

**1039.** `[C1452 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Other terms by which such a convention may be described in some organizations include _____, general assembly, house of delegates, and house of representatives.

   *Answer:* congress, conference, convocation — “Other terms by which such a convention may be described in some organizations include congress, conference, convocation, general assembly, house of delegates, and house of representatives.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:1 — “Other terms by which such a convention may be described in some organizations include congress, conference, convocation, general assembly, house of delegates, and house of representatives.”

**1040.** `[C1452 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Other terms by which such a convention may be described in some organizations include congress, conference, convocation, general assembly, _____ delegates, and house of representatives.
   A. house of
   B. do harm
   C. Roll Call
   D. speed up

   *Answer:* A) house of. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:1 — “Other terms by which such a convention may be described in some organizations include congress, conference, convocation, general assembly, house of delegates, and house of representatives.”

**1041.** `[C1452 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The most common type of convention is that of an _____ state or national society—in which the delegates are selected by, and from among, the members of each local unit.
   A. established
   B. Particular
   C. constitution
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:1 — “The most common type of convention is that of an established state or national society—in which the delegates are selected by, and from among, the members of each local unit.”

**1042.** `[C1453 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____, or house of representatives, is often applied particularly in the case of learned or professional associations, to distinguish the voting body of delegates from large numbers of other members of the constituent units, who come to the convention to attend seminars, workshops, educational or social activities, or the like.

   *Answer:* house of delegates — “The term house of delegates, or house of representatives, is often applied particularly in the case of learned or professional associations, to distinguish the voting body of delegates from large numbers of other members of the constituent units, who come to the convention to attend seminars, workshops, educational or social activities, or the like.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:2 — “The term house of delegates, or house of representatives, is often applied particularly in the case of learned or professional associations, to distinguish the voting body of delegates from large numbers of other members of the constituent units, who come to the convention to attend seminars, workshops, educational or social activities, or the like.”

**1043.** `[C1453 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term house of delegates, or _____, is often applied particularly in the case of learned or professional associations, to distinguish the voting body of delegates from large numbers of other members of the constituent units, who come to the convention to attend seminars, workshops, educational or social activities, or the like.
   A. house of representatives
   B. ARTICLE VII: COMMITTEES
   C. Corresponding Secretary
   D. Vacancies in a Committee

   *Answer:* A) house of representatives. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:2 — “The term house of delegates, or house of representatives, is often applied particularly in the case of learned or professional associations, to distinguish the voting body of delegates from large numbers of other members of the constituent units, who come to the convention to attend seminars, workshops, educational or social activities, or the like.”

**1044.** `[C1454 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ vary in size, duration, and complexity of operation.

   *Answer:* Conventions — “Conventions vary in size, duration, and complexity of operation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:3 — “Conventions vary in size, duration, and complexity of operation.”

**1045.** `[C1454 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ small state society may hold a one-day convention consisting of two or three meetings at which all delegates are present.
   A. assemblies
   B. Substitute
   C. substitute
   D. relatively

   *Answer:* D) relatively. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:3 — “A relatively small state society may hold a one-day convention consisting of two or three meetings at which all delegates are present.”

**1046.** `[C1454 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A week’s convention of a national scientific or _____ association, on the other hand, may be divided into a number of specialized sections meeting separately at the same time, with only a few meetings when the entire body of delegates gathers in one hall.

   *Answer:* educational — “A week’s convention of a national scientific or educational association, on the other hand, may be divided into a number of specialized sections meeting separately at the same time, with only a few meetings when the entire body of delegates gathers in one hall.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:3 — “A week’s convention of a national scientific or educational association, on the other hand, may be divided into a number of specialized sections meeting separately at the same time, with only a few meetings when the entire body of delegates gathers in one hall.”

**1047.** `[C1454 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ vary in size, duration, and complexity of operation.
   A. incidental
   B. Conventions
   C. constitution
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) Conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:3 — “Conventions vary in size, duration, and complexity of operation.”

**1048.** `[C1454 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ small state society may hold a one-day convention consisting of two or three meetings at which all delegates are present.

   *Answer:* relatively — “A relatively small state society may hold a one-day convention consisting of two or three meetings at which all delegates are present.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:3 — “A relatively small state society may hold a one-day convention consisting of two or three meetings at which all delegates are present.”

**1049.** `[C1455 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, a convention is sometimes called for the purpose of forming an _____ or federation;

   *Answer:* association — “In addition, a convention is sometimes called for the purpose of forming an association or federation;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:4 — “In addition, a convention is sometimes called for the purpose of forming an association or federation;”

**1050.** `[C1455 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or (like a mass meeting, 53) it may be convened to draw interested parties or _____ of interested organizations together in acting upon a particular problem.
   A. immediately
   B. representatives
   C. Indefinitely
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* B) representatives. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:4 — “or (like a mass meeting, 53) it may be convened to draw interested parties or representatives of interested organizations together in acting upon a particular problem.”

**1051.** `[C1455 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition, a convention is sometimes called for the purpose of forming an _____ or federation;
   A. Substitute
   B. Reconsider
   C. association
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* C) association. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:4 — “In addition, a convention is sometimes called for the purpose of forming an association or federation;”

**1052.** `[C1455 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (like a mass meeting, 53) it may be convened to draw interested parties or _____ of interested organizations together in acting upon a particular problem.

   *Answer:* representatives — “or (like a mass meeting, 53) it may be convened to draw interested parties or representatives of interested organizations together in acting upon a particular problem.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:4 — “or (like a mass meeting, 53) it may be convened to draw interested parties or representatives of interested organizations together in acting upon a particular problem.”

**1053.** `[C1456 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This chapter is limited to the features common to most _____ and relates principally to the convention of an established society.

   *Answer:* conventions — “This chapter is limited to the features common to most conventions and relates principally to the convention of an established society.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:5 — “This chapter is limited to the features common to most conventions and relates principally to the convention of an established society.”

**1054.** `[C1456 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This chapter is limited to the features common to most _____ and relates principally to the convention of an established society.
   A. conventions
   B. Information
   C. Incidental
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:5 — “This chapter is limited to the features common to most conventions and relates principally to the convention of an established society.”

**1055.** `[C1458 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Basic provision for the voting body of members may be worded in the bylaws as appropriate to the particular _____—for example, as follows:

   *Answer:* organization — “Basic provision for the voting body of members may be worded in the bylaws as appropriate to the particular organization—for example, as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:7 — “Basic provision for the voting body of members may be worded in the bylaws as appropriate to the particular organization—for example, as follows:”

**1056.** `[C1458 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Basic provision for the voting body of members may be worded in the bylaws as appropriate to the particular _____—for example, as follows:
   A. immediately
   B. organization
   C. Reconsider
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:7 — “Basic provision for the voting body of members may be worded in the bylaws as appropriate to the particular organization—for example, as follows:”

**1057.** `[C1459 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, the bylaws at the level on which the convention is held should prescribe: (1) the conditions for a constituent unit to be in good standing for purposes of the right to _____—commonly including a minimum membership requirement;

   *Answer:* representation — “In addition, the bylaws at the level on which the convention is held should prescribe: (1) the conditions for a constituent unit to be in good standing for purposes of the right to representation—commonly including a minimum membership requirement;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:8 — “In addition, the bylaws at the level on which the convention is held should prescribe: (1) the conditions for a constituent unit to be in good standing for purposes of the right to representation—commonly including a minimum membership requirement;”

**1058.** `[C1459 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and (2) the number of delegates to which a unit shall be entitled depending on its size—usually by specifying, for example, that each unit shall be _____ by its president, plus one additional delegate if the unit has more than a certain number of members, or two additional delegates if the unit has more than twice that number of members, and so on.
   A. assemblies
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Organization
   D. represented

   *Answer:* D) represented. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:8 — “and (2) the number of delegates to which a unit shall be entitled depending on its size—usually by specifying, for example, that each unit shall be represented by its president, plus one additional delegate if the unit has more than a certain number of members, or two additional delegates if the unit has more than twice that number of members, and so on.”

**1059.** `[C1459 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition, the bylaws at the level on which the convention is held should prescribe: (1) the conditions for a constituent unit to be in good standing for purposes of the right to _____—commonly including a minimum membership requirement;
   A. constitution
   B. Parliamentary
   C. resolutions
   D. representation

   *Answer:* D) representation. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:8 — “In addition, the bylaws at the level on which the convention is held should prescribe: (1) the conditions for a constituent unit to be in good standing for purposes of the right to representation—commonly including a minimum membership requirement;”

**1060.** `[C1459 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and (2) the number of delegates to which a unit shall be entitled depending on its size—usually by specifying, for example, that each unit shall be _____ by its president, plus one additional delegate if the unit has more than a certain number of members, or two additional delegates if the unit has more than twice that number of members, and so on.

   *Answer:* represented — “and (2) the number of delegates to which a unit shall be entitled depending on its size—usually by specifying, for example, that each unit shall be represented by its president, plus one additional delegate if the unit has more than a certain number of members, or two additional delegates if the unit has more than twice that number of members, and so on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:8 — “and (2) the number of delegates to which a unit shall be entitled depending on its size—usually by specifying, for example, that each unit shall be represented by its president, plus one additional delegate if the unit has more than a certain number of members, or two additional delegates if the unit has more than twice that number of members, and so on.”

**1061.** `[C1462 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Ways in Which Voting _____ Comes About.

   *Answer:* Membership — “Ways in Which Voting Membership Comes About.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:11 — “Ways in Which Voting Membership Comes About.”

**1062.** `[C1462 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Voting membership in a convention of an _____ society generally comes about in one of the following ways:
   A. established
   B. committees
   C. Procedures
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* A) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:11 — “Voting membership in a convention of an established society generally comes about in one of the following ways:”

**1063.** `[C1462 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Ways in Which Voting _____ Comes About.
   A. Particular
   B. settlement
   C. convention
   D. Membership

   *Answer:* D) Membership. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:11 — “Ways in Which Voting Membership Comes About.”

**1064.** `[C1462 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Voting membership in a convention of an _____ society generally comes about in one of the following ways:

   *Answer:* established — “Voting membership in a convention of an established society generally comes about in one of the following ways:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:11 — “Voting membership in a convention of an established society generally comes about in one of the following ways:”

**1065.** `[C1463 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Filling of Vacancies Arising in a _____.

   *Answer:* Delegation — “Filling of Vacancies Arising in a Delegation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:12 — “Filling of Vacancies Arising in a Delegation.”

**1066.** `[C1463 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the president of a constituent unit is unable to be present at the convention of an established society, his place there is taken by the vicepresident (or by the second, third, or ranking available vicepresident if necessary and if there are such officers), just as for any other duty in which the _____ acts in the president’s place.
   A. vice-president
   B. adjournment
   C. specification
   D. Responsibilities

   *Answer:* A) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:12 — “If the president of a constituent unit is unable to be present at the convention of an established society, his place there is taken by the vicepresident (or by the second, third, or ranking available vicepresident if necessary and if there are such officers), just as for any other duty in which the vice-president acts in the president’s place.”

**1067.** `[C1463 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Filling of Vacancies Arising in a _____.
   A. Permission
   B. Delegation
   C. persuasive
   D. landscaped

   *Answer:* B) Delegation. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:12 — “Filling of Vacancies Arising in a Delegation.”

**1068.** `[C1463 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the president of a constituent unit is unable to be present at the convention of an established society, his place there is taken by the vicepresident (or by the second, third, or ranking available vicepresident if necessary and if there are such officers), just as for any other duty in which the _____ acts in the president’s place.

   *Answer:* vice-president — “If the president of a constituent unit is unable to be present at the convention of an established society, his place there is taken by the vicepresident (or by the second, third, or ranking available vicepresident if necessary and if there are such officers), just as for any other duty in which the vice-president acts in the president’s place.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:12 — “If the president of a constituent unit is unable to be present at the convention of an established society, his place there is taken by the vicepresident (or by the second, third, or ranking available vicepresident if necessary and if there are such officers), just as for any other duty in which the vice-president acts in the president’s place.”

**1069.** `[C1464 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To ensure as complete _____ at the convention as possible, the bylaws at the convention level should provide that each unit shall elect a certain number of alternates—frequently equal to the number of delegates.
   A. representation
   B. Responsibilities
   C. Information
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) representation. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:13 — “To ensure as complete representation at the convention as possible, the bylaws at the convention level should provide that each unit shall elect a certain number of alternates—frequently equal to the number of delegates.”

**1070.** `[C1464 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To ensure as complete _____ at the convention as possible, the bylaws at the convention level should provide that each unit shall elect a certain number of alternates—frequently equal to the number of delegates.

   *Answer:* representation — “To ensure as complete representation at the convention as possible, the bylaws at the convention level should provide that each unit shall elect a certain number of alternates—frequently equal to the number of delegates.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:13 — “To ensure as complete representation at the convention as possible, the bylaws at the convention level should provide that each unit shall elect a certain number of alternates—frequently equal to the number of delegates.”

**1071.** `[C1465 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Alternates normally are elected with a designated order, in which they will be called to serve, if available, as vacancies arise in the delegation of their _____ unit.

   *Answer:* constituent — “Alternates normally are elected with a designated order, in which they will be called to serve, if available, as vacancies arise in the delegation of their constituent unit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:14 — “Alternates normally are elected with a designated order, in which they will be called to serve, if available, as vacancies arise in the delegation of their constituent unit.”

**1072.** `[C1465 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a unit has more than one delegate, an elected alternate (other than the _____) is not associated with any particular delegate.
   A. vice-president
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* A) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:14 — “When a unit has more than one delegate, an elected alternate (other than the vice-president) is not associated with any particular delegate.”

**1073.** `[C1465 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Alternates normally are elected with a designated order, in which they will be called to serve, if available, as vacancies arise in the delegation of their _____ unit.
   A. constituent
   B. committees
   C. undebatable
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) constituent. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:14 — “Alternates normally are elected with a designated order, in which they will be called to serve, if available, as vacancies arise in the delegation of their constituent unit.”

**1074.** `[C1465 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a unit has more than one delegate, an elected alternate (other than the _____) is not associated with any particular delegate.

   *Answer:* vice-president — “When a unit has more than one delegate, an elected alternate (other than the vice-president) is not associated with any particular delegate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:14 — “When a unit has more than one delegate, an elected alternate (other than the vice-president) is not associated with any particular delegate.”

**1075.** `[C1466 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases where the _____ delegates within a unit’s delegation represent particular areas or groups, it may sometimes be desirable to make exception to the foregoing rule by providing, in the bylaws at the convention level, for the pairing of each alternate with a specific delegate.

   *Answer:* individual — “In cases where the individual delegates within a unit’s delegation represent particular areas or groups, it may sometimes be desirable to make exception to the foregoing rule by providing, in the bylaws at the convention level, for the pairing of each alternate with a specific delegate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:15 — “In cases where the individual delegates within a unit’s delegation represent particular areas or groups, it may sometimes be desirable to make exception to the foregoing rule by providing, in the bylaws at the convention level, for the pairing of each alternate with a specific delegate.”

**1076.** `[C1466 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The disadvantage of such a system arises when both a particular delegate and his only alternate are unable to attend the convention—thus depriving a constituent unit of part of the _____ to which it is entitled.
   A. subsequently
   B. representation
   C. Information
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) representation. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:15 — “The disadvantage of such a system arises when both a particular delegate and his only alternate are unable to attend the convention—thus depriving a constituent unit of part of the representation to which it is entitled.”

**1077.** `[C1466 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where the _____ delegates within a unit’s delegation represent particular areas or groups, it may sometimes be desirable to make exception to the foregoing rule by providing, in the bylaws at the convention level, for the pairing of each alternate with a specific delegate.
   A. Reconsider
   B. settlement
   C. individual
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* C) individual. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:15 — “In cases where the individual delegates within a unit’s delegation represent particular areas or groups, it may sometimes be desirable to make exception to the foregoing rule by providing, in the bylaws at the convention level, for the pairing of each alternate with a specific delegate.”

**1078.** `[C1466 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The disadvantage of such a system arises when both a particular delegate and his only alternate are unable to attend the convention—thus depriving a constituent unit of part of the _____ to which it is entitled.

   *Answer:* representation — “The disadvantage of such a system arises when both a particular delegate and his only alternate are unable to attend the convention—thus depriving a constituent unit of part of the representation to which it is entitled.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:15 — “The disadvantage of such a system arises when both a particular delegate and his only alternate are unable to attend the convention—thus depriving a constituent unit of part of the representation to which it is entitled.”

**1079.** `[C1467 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an alternate is to replace a delegate who has registered, proper evidence of that delegate’s withdrawal from such status must be presented to the Credentials Committee, and the alternate must be _____ as the new delegate before he can sit or vote as a member of the convention.

   *Answer:* reregistered — “If an alternate is to replace a delegate who has registered, proper evidence of that delegate’s withdrawal from such status must be presented to the Credentials Committee, and the alternate must be reregistered as the new delegate before he can sit or vote as a member of the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:17 — “If an alternate is to replace a delegate who has registered, proper evidence of that delegate’s withdrawal from such status must be presented to the Credentials Committee, and the alternate must be reregistered as the new delegate before he can sit or vote as a member of the convention.”

**1080.** `[C1467 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an alternate is to replace a delegate who has registered, proper evidence of that delegate’s withdrawal from such status must be presented to the _____ Committee, and the alternate must be reregistered as the new delegate before he can sit or vote as a member of the convention.
   A. Credentials
   B. Particular
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* A) Credentials. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:17 — “If an alternate is to replace a delegate who has registered, proper evidence of that delegate’s withdrawal from such status must be presented to the Credentials Committee, and the alternate must be reregistered as the new delegate before he can sit or vote as a member of the convention.”

**1081.** `[C1467 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless the rules of the body provide otherwise, no alternate or other person can “_____” for a delegate who remains registered.

   *Answer:* substitute — “Unless the rules of the body provide otherwise, no alternate or other person can “substitute” for a delegate who remains registered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:17 — “Unless the rules of the body provide otherwise, no alternate or other person can “substitute” for a delegate who remains registered.”

**1082.** `[C1467 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In other words, a delegate’s temporary absence from the convention hall does not entitle an alternate to make motions, speak in debate, or cast the delegate’s vote —even with the delegate’s _____—unless a rule of the body permits this procedure.
   A. authorization
   B. presentation
   C. adjournment
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* A) authorization. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:17 — “In other words, a delegate’s temporary absence from the convention hall does not entitle an alternate to make motions, speak in debate, or cast the delegate’s vote —even with the delegate’s authorization—unless a rule of the body permits this procedure.”

**1083.** `[C1467 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the rules of the body provide otherwise, no alternate or other person can “_____” for a delegate who remains registered.
   A. Particular
   B. sustaining
   C. substitute
   D. authorizes

   *Answer:* C) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:17 — “Unless the rules of the body provide otherwise, no alternate or other person can “substitute” for a delegate who remains registered.”

**1084.** `[C1468 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a member of a _____ unit has accepted election as a delegate, he has the obligation to attend the convention, with such expense allowance as the unit may provide;
   A. Procedures
   B. constituent
   C. requirement
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) constituent. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:18 — “When a member of a constituent unit has accepted election as a delegate, he has the obligation to attend the convention, with such expense allowance as the unit may provide;”

**1085.** `[C1468 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: he should not leave it to an _____ to serve in his place except for serious reason.

   *Answer:* alternate — “he should not leave it to an alternate to serve in his place except for serious reason.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:18 — “he should not leave it to an alternate to serve in his place except for serious reason.”

**1086.** `[C1468 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At the convention, the delegate has the duty to be present at the business meetings, and to be prepared on returning from the convention to present to his unit an _____ report of what transpired.
   A. information
   B. committees
   C. constitution
   D. convention

   *Answer:* A) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:18 — “At the convention, the delegate has the duty to be present at the business meetings, and to be prepared on returning from the convention to present to his unit an information report of what transpired.”

**1087.** `[C1468 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A delegate is free to vote as he sees fit on questions at the convention, except as his constituent unit may have instructed him in regard to particular matters scheduled for _____.

   *Answer:* consideration — “A delegate is free to vote as he sees fit on questions at the convention, except as his constituent unit may have instructed him in regard to particular matters scheduled for consideration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:18 — “A delegate is free to vote as he sees fit on questions at the convention, except as his constituent unit may have instructed him in regard to particular matters scheduled for consideration.”

**1088.** `[C1468 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a member of a _____ unit has accepted election as a delegate, he has the obligation to attend the convention, with such expense allowance as the unit may provide;

   *Answer:* constituent — “When a member of a constituent unit has accepted election as a delegate, he has the obligation to attend the convention, with such expense allowance as the unit may provide;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:18 — “When a member of a constituent unit has accepted election as a delegate, he has the obligation to attend the convention, with such expense allowance as the unit may provide;”

**1089.** `[C1469 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: a meeting of this kind is usually called a _____.

   *Answer:* caucus — “a meeting of this kind is usually called a caucus.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:19 — “a meeting of this kind is usually called a caucus.”

**1090.** `[C1469 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Prior to or during a _____, members of a delegation may need or wish to meet as a group to decide how they will act with reference to certain matters to come before the convention;
   A. introduced
   B. convention
   C. recognized
   D. variations

   *Answer:* B) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:19 — “Prior to or during a convention, members of a delegation may need or wish to meet as a group to decide how they will act with reference to certain matters to come before the convention;”

**1091.** `[C1469 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless instructed otherwise by its parent society or unit, such a caucus is governed by the rules of procedure applicable to committees (50), since the delegation is in effect a committee to represent and act at the convention for the _____ society or unit that chose it.

   *Answer:* constituent — “Unless instructed otherwise by its parent society or unit, such a caucus is governed by the rules of procedure applicable to committees (50), since the delegation is in effect a committee to represent and act at the convention for the constituent society or unit that chose it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:19 — “Unless instructed otherwise by its parent society or unit, such a caucus is governed by the rules of procedure applicable to committees (50), since the delegation is in effect a committee to represent and act at the convention for the constituent society or unit that chose it.”

**1092.** `[C1469 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the president of a constituent society (or in his absence the _____) is automatically a delegate to a convention, he usually acts as chairman of his delegation;
   A. Announcements
   B. vice-president
   C. specification
   D. President-Elect

   *Answer:* B) vice-president. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:19 — “If the president of a constituent society (or in his absence the vice-president) is automatically a delegate to a convention, he usually acts as chairman of his delegation;”
