**1093.** `[C1469 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: a meeting of this kind is usually called a _____.
   A. ballot
   B. accept
   C. agenda
   D. caucus

   *Answer:* D) caucus. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:19 — “a meeting of this kind is usually called a caucus.”

**1094.** `[C1470 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes caucuses are held of different groupings of delegates, as, for example, all delegates from a certain district, territory, or other geographic area as defined by the _____;

   *Answer:* organization — “Sometimes caucuses are held of different groupings of delegates, as, for example, all delegates from a certain district, territory, or other geographic area as defined by the organization;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:20 — “Sometimes caucuses are held of different groupings of delegates, as, for example, all delegates from a certain district, territory, or other geographic area as defined by the organization;”

**1095.** `[C1470 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and they are similarly governed by the rules generally _____ to committees.
   A. Convention
   B. subsidiary
   C. convention
   D. applicable

   *Answer:* D) applicable. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:20 — “and they are similarly governed by the rules generally applicable to committees.”

**1096.** `[C1470 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes caucuses are held of different groupings of delegates, as, for example, all delegates from a certain district, territory, or other geographic area as defined by the _____;
   A. Procedures
   B. specification
   C. requirement
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:20 — “Sometimes caucuses are held of different groupings of delegates, as, for example, all delegates from a certain district, territory, or other geographic area as defined by the organization;”

**1097.** `[C1470 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and they are similarly governed by the rules generally _____ to committees.

   *Answer:* applicable — “and they are similarly governed by the rules generally applicable to committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:20 — “and they are similarly governed by the rules generally applicable to committees.”

**1098.** `[C1471 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but a society can, by instructing its delegation, bind it to vote as a unit (that is, to cast all of its votes in accord with the decision of the _____ of the delegation) on all issues, on a particular class of business, or on certain matters to be acted on by the convention.

   *Answer:* majority — “but a society can, by instructing its delegation, bind it to vote as a unit (that is, to cast all of its votes in accord with the decision of the majority of the delegation) on all issues, on a particular class of business, or on certain matters to be acted on by the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:21 — “but a society can, by instructing its delegation, bind it to vote as a unit (that is, to cast all of its votes in accord with the decision of the majority of the delegation) on all issues, on a particular class of business, or on certain matters to be acted on by the convention.”

**1099.** `[C1471 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As in the case of any committee, in the absence of a superior rule to the contrary a _____ society or unit can instruct its delegation, although this is not always a good practice in ordinary societies.
   A. Organization
   B. adjournment
   C. undebatable
   D. constituent

   *Answer:* D) constituent. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:21 — “As in the case of any committee, in the absence of a superior rule to the contrary a constituent society or unit can instruct its delegation, although this is not always a good practice in ordinary societies.”

**1100.** `[C1471 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such _____ are binding upon the delegation to the extent that the convention’s presiding officer and other officials have a duty to enforce instructions of which they have been properly and officially notified.

   *Answer:* instructions — “Such instructions are binding upon the delegation to the extent that the convention’s presiding officer and other officials have a duty to enforce instructions of which they have been properly and officially notified.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:21 — “Such instructions are binding upon the delegation to the extent that the convention’s presiding officer and other officials have a duty to enforce instructions of which they have been properly and officially notified.”

**1101.** `[C1471 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As stated above, the delegates are free to vote as they see fit except where an _____ has been given;
   A. interrupted
   B. Preparation
   C. instruction
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) instruction. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:21 — “As stated above, the delegates are free to vote as they see fit except where an instruction has been given;”

**1102.** `[C1471 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but a society can, by _____ its delegation, bind it to vote as a unit (that is, to cast all of its votes in accord with the decision of the majority of the delegation) on all issues, on a particular class of business, or on certain matters to be acted on by the convention.

   *Answer:* instructing — “but a society can, by instructing its delegation, bind it to vote as a unit (that is, to cast all of its votes in accord with the decision of the majority of the delegation) on all issues, on a particular class of business, or on certain matters to be acted on by the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:21 — “but a society can, by instructing its delegation, bind it to vote as a unit (that is, to cast all of its votes in accord with the decision of the majority of the delegation) on all issues, on a particular class of business, or on certain matters to be acted on by the convention.”

**1103.** `[C1472 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____ is also sometimes applied to a similar meeting of all the known or admitted partisans of a particular position on an important issue—in a convention or any other deliberative assembly—who meet to plan strategy toward a desired result within the assembly.

   *Answer:* caucus — “The term caucus is also sometimes applied to a similar meeting of all the known or admitted partisans of a particular position on an important issue—in a convention or any other deliberative assembly—who meet to plan strategy toward a desired result within the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:22 — “The term caucus is also sometimes applied to a similar meeting of all the known or admitted partisans of a particular position on an important issue—in a convention or any other deliberative assembly—who meet to plan strategy toward a desired result within the assembly.”

**1104.** `[C1472 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term caucus is also sometimes applied to a similar meeting of all the known or admitted partisans of a particular position on an important issue—in a convention or any other _____ assembly—who meet to plan strategy toward a desired result within the assembly.
   A. Preparation
   B. specification
   C. deliberative
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* C) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:22 — “The term caucus is also sometimes applied to a similar meeting of all the known or admitted partisans of a particular position on an important issue—in a convention or any other deliberative assembly—who meet to plan strategy toward a desired result within the assembly.”

**1105.** `[C1472 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a meeting may be held on the presumed informal _____ that those who attend will follow the decisions of the caucus.

   *Answer:* understanding — “Such a meeting may be held on the presumed informal understanding that those who attend will follow the decisions of the caucus.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:22 — “Such a meeting may be held on the presumed informal understanding that those who attend will follow the decisions of the caucus.”

**1106.** `[C1472 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a meeting may be held on the presumed informal _____ that those who attend will follow the decisions of the caucus.
   A. requirement
   B. understanding
   C. interrupted
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* B) understanding. See RONR (12th ed.) §58:22 — “Such a meeting may be held on the presumed informal understanding that those who attend will follow the decisions of the caucus.”

**1107.** `[C1473 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Most conventions must operate on a closely controlled schedule and transact a large amount of business quickly— often with rented facilities available only for a prearranged length of time and with each added day of meetings entailing _____ expense both to the association and to the delegates personally.

   *Answer:* considerable — “Most conventions must operate on a closely controlled schedule and transact a large amount of business quickly— often with rented facilities available only for a prearranged length of time and with each added day of meetings entailing considerable expense both to the association and to the delegates personally.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:1 — “Most conventions must operate on a closely controlled schedule and transact a large amount of business quickly— often with rented facilities available only for a prearranged length of time and with each added day of meetings entailing considerable expense both to the association and to the delegates personally.”

**1108.** `[C1473 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Maximum effort toward a _____ convention is therefore essential.
   A. deliberative
   B. presentation
   C. well-organized
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* C) well-organized. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:1 — “Maximum effort toward a well-organized convention is therefore essential.”

**1109.** `[C1473 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Most conventions must operate on a closely controlled schedule and transact a large amount of business quickly— often with rented facilities available only for a prearranged length of time and with each added day of meetings entailing _____ expense both to the association and to the delegates personally.
   A. considerable
   B. Convention
   C. adjournment
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) considerable. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:1 — “Most conventions must operate on a closely controlled schedule and transact a large amount of business quickly— often with rented facilities available only for a prearranged length of time and with each added day of meetings entailing considerable expense both to the association and to the delegates personally.”

**1110.** `[C1473 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Maximum effort toward a _____ convention is therefore essential.

   *Answer:* well-organized — “Maximum effort toward a well-organized convention is therefore essential.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:1 — “Maximum effort toward a well-organized convention is therefore essential.”

**1111.** `[C1474 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The work of organizing and preparing for a convention normally begins weeks or months in advance and involves many committees, under the general direction of the officers and the board of the _____.

   *Answer:* association — “The work of organizing and preparing for a convention normally begins weeks or months in advance and involves many committees, under the general direction of the officers and the board of the association.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:2 — “The work of organizing and preparing for a convention normally begins weeks or months in advance and involves many committees, under the general direction of the officers and the board of the association.”

**1112.** `[C1474 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The work of organizing and preparing for a convention normally begins weeks or months in advance and involves many committees, under the general direction of the officers and the board of the _____.
   A. Convention
   B. Incidental
   C. association
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* C) association. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:2 — “The work of organizing and preparing for a convention normally begins weeks or months in advance and involves many committees, under the general direction of the officers and the board of the association.”

**1113.** `[C1475 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and (3) the Program Committee, which works out a convention program combining a suitable _____ (41) with special features designed to promote and develop the association or society as a whole.

   *Answer:* order of business — “and (3) the Program Committee, which works out a convention program combining a suitable order of business (41) with special features designed to promote and develop the association or society as a whole.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:3 — “and (3) the Program Committee, which works out a convention program combining a suitable order of business (41) with special features designed to promote and develop the association or society as a whole.”

**1114.** `[C1475 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The principal _____ functions most directly connected with the formal organization of the convention itself are performed by three committees, each of which has been appointed by the president or the board as prescribed in the bylaws.
   A. representatives
   B. parliamentary
   C. undebatable
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:3 — “The principal parliamentary functions most directly connected with the formal organization of the convention itself are performed by three committees, each of which has been appointed by the president or the board as prescribed in the bylaws.”

**1115.** `[C1475 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: These three committees are: (1) the Credentials Committee, which prepares and certifies to the convention the list of officers, delegates, and alternates that it has registered after finding them entitled to _____;

   *Answer:* accreditation — “These three committees are: (1) the Credentials Committee, which prepares and certifies to the convention the list of officers, delegates, and alternates that it has registered after finding them entitled to accreditation;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:3 — “These three committees are: (1) the Credentials Committee, which prepares and certifies to the convention the list of officers, delegates, and alternates that it has registered after finding them entitled to accreditation;”

**1116.** `[C1475 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (2) the Committee on Standing Rules, which drafts rules of operating procedure specially required for the _____ convention;
   A. particular
   B. privileged
   C. recognized
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) particular. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:3 — “(2) the Committee on Standing Rules, which drafts rules of operating procedure specially required for the particular convention;”

**1117.** `[C1475 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and (3) the Program Committee, which works out a convention program combining a suitable order of business (41) with special features designed to promote and develop the _____ or society as a whole.

   *Answer:* association — “and (3) the Program Committee, which works out a convention program combining a suitable order of business (41) with special features designed to promote and develop the association or society as a whole.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:3 — “and (3) the Program Committee, which works out a convention program combining a suitable order of business (41) with special features designed to promote and develop the association or society as a whole.”

**1118.** `[C1476 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, depending on the size of the convention, one or more committees concerned with the necessary physical arrangements, such as securing the hall, hotel _____, and related services, should be appointed.

   *Answer:* accommodations — “In addition, depending on the size of the convention, one or more committees concerned with the necessary physical arrangements, such as securing the hall, hotel accommodations, and related services, should be appointed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:4 — “In addition, depending on the size of the convention, one or more committees concerned with the necessary physical arrangements, such as securing the hall, hotel accommodations, and related services, should be appointed.”

**1119.** `[C1476 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition, depending on the size of the convention, one or more committees concerned with the necessary physical _____, such as securing the hall, hotel accommodations, and related services, should be appointed.
   A. deliberative
   B. consideration
   C. arrangements
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* C) arrangements. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:4 — “In addition, depending on the size of the convention, one or more committees concerned with the necessary physical arrangements, such as securing the hall, hotel accommodations, and related services, should be appointed.”

**1120.** `[C1477 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Another important committee that usually works before as well as during the convention is the _____ Committee.

   *Answer:* Resolutions — “Another important committee that usually works before as well as during the convention is the Resolutions Committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:5 — “Another important committee that usually works before as well as during the convention is the Resolutions Committee.”

**1121.** `[C1477 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This committee screens and recommends _____ action on resolutions and original (as distinct from incidental) main motions to come before the convention.
   A. Incidental
   B. convention
   C. undebatable
   D. appropriate

   *Answer:* D) appropriate. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:5 — “This committee screens and recommends appropriate action on resolutions and original (as distinct from incidental) main motions to come before the convention.”

**1122.** `[C1477 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Another important committee that usually works before as well as during the convention is the _____ Committee.
   A. Organization
   B. Resolutions
   C. Particular
   D. committees

   *Answer:* B) Resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:5 — “Another important committee that usually works before as well as during the convention is the Resolutions Committee.”

**1123.** `[C1477 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This committee screens and recommends _____ action on resolutions and original (as distinct from incidental) main motions to come before the convention.

   *Answer:* appropriate — “This committee screens and recommends appropriate action on resolutions and original (as distinct from incidental) main motions to come before the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:5 — “This committee screens and recommends appropriate action on resolutions and original (as distinct from incidental) main motions to come before the convention.”

**1124.** `[C1478 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The duties of the _____ committees are more fully explained in the succeeding portions of this chapter.

   *Answer:* convention — “The duties of the convention committees are more fully explained in the succeeding portions of this chapter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:6 — “The duties of the convention committees are more fully explained in the succeeding portions of this chapter.”

**1125.** `[C1478 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The duties of the _____ committees are more fully explained in the succeeding portions of this chapter.
   A. variations
   B. settlement
   C. Reconsider
   D. convention

   *Answer:* D) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:6 — “The duties of the convention committees are more fully explained in the succeeding portions of this chapter.”

**1126.** `[C1479 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each of the regular standing committees of the association —in _____ with the presiding officer, the executive body or board, and the Program Committee—should also carefully plan the presentation and management of the convention business that is the particular committee’s concern.

   *Answer:* consultation — “Each of the regular standing committees of the association —in consultation with the presiding officer, the executive body or board, and the Program Committee—should also carefully plan the presentation and management of the convention business that is the particular committee’s concern.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:7 — “Each of the regular standing committees of the association —in consultation with the presiding officer, the executive body or board, and the Program Committee—should also carefully plan the presentation and management of the convention business that is the particular committee’s concern.”

**1127.** `[C1479 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each of the regular standing committees of the association —in _____ with the presiding officer, the executive body or board, and the Program Committee—should also carefully plan the presentation and management of the convention business that is the particular committee’s concern.
   A. Permission
   B. Particular
   C. consultation
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* C) consultation. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:7 — “Each of the regular standing committees of the association —in consultation with the presiding officer, the executive body or board, and the Program Committee—should also carefully plan the presentation and management of the convention business that is the particular committee’s concern.”

**1128.** `[C1480 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ meeting of the board of the association is often held in the convention city a day or more in advance of the convention opening.

   *Answer:* preconvention — “A preconvention meeting of the board of the association is often held in the convention city a day or more in advance of the convention opening.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:8 — “A preconvention meeting of the board of the association is often held in the convention city a day or more in advance of the convention opening.”

**1129.** `[C1480 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A pre_____ meeting of the board of the association is often held in the convention city a day or more in advance of the convention opening.
   A. Substitute
   B. introduced
   C. convention
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:8 — “A preconvention meeting of the board of the association is often held in the convention city a day or more in advance of the convention opening.”

**1130.** `[C1481 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A key consultant in the preparation for a convention should be the parliamentarian, who should be engaged well in _____.

   *Answer:* advance — “A key consultant in the preparation for a convention should be the parliamentarian, who should be engaged well in advance.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:9 — “A key consultant in the preparation for a convention should be the parliamentarian, who should be engaged well in advance.”

**1131.** `[C1481 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A key consultant in the preparation for a convention should be the _____, who should be engaged well in advance.
   A. deliberative
   B. specification
   C. parliamentarian
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* C) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:9 — “A key consultant in the preparation for a convention should be the parliamentarian, who should be engaged well in advance.”

**1132.** `[C1481 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is desirable that this person be a professional—the more seasoned in actual operating experience within _____, the better.

   *Answer:* organizations — “It is desirable that this person be a professional—the more seasoned in actual operating experience within organizations, the better.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:9 — “It is desirable that this person be a professional—the more seasoned in actual operating experience within organizations, the better.”

**1133.** `[C1481 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chairmen of the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, the Program Committee, the Resolutions Committee, the Elections Committee if there is one, and the standing _____ who are to present business to the convention should all consult with the parliamentarian during this time;

   *Answer:* committees — “The chairmen of the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, the Program Committee, the Resolutions Committee, the Elections Committee if there is one, and the standing committees who are to present business to the convention should all consult with the parliamentarian during this time;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:9 — “The chairmen of the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, the Program Committee, the Resolutions Committee, the Elections Committee if there is one, and the standing committees who are to present business to the convention should all consult with the parliamentarian during this time;”

**1134.** `[C1481 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is desirable that this person be a professional—the more seasoned in actual operating experience within _____, the better.
   A. organizations
   B. Preparation
   C. adjournment
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:9 — “It is desirable that this person be a professional—the more seasoned in actual operating experience within organizations, the better.”

**1135.** `[C1482 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before a convention can transact any other _____, it must officially form itself into a single voting body—which is done at the first business meeting.

   *Answer:* business — “Before a convention can transact any other business, it must officially form itself into a single voting body—which is done at the first business meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:10 — “Before a convention can transact any other business, it must officially form itself into a single voting body—which is done at the first business meeting.”

**1136.** `[C1482 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before a _____ can transact any other business, it must officially form itself into a single voting body—which is done at the first business meeting.
   A. convention
   B. committees
   C. Definitely
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* A) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:10 — “Before a convention can transact any other business, it must officially form itself into a single voting body—which is done at the first business meeting.”

**1137.** `[C1482 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Preliminary ceremonies— whether at the _____ of the convention or at the beginning of each day—are not regarded as business.

   *Answer:* commencement — “Preliminary ceremonies— whether at the commencement of the convention or at the beginning of each day—are not regarded as business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:10 — “Preliminary ceremonies— whether at the commencement of the convention or at the beginning of each day—are not regarded as business.”

**1138.** `[C1482 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A separate formal opening of _____ nature can be held, if desired, before the convention is officially organized.
   A. inspirational
   B. consideration
   C. recommendation
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) inspirational. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:10 — “A separate formal opening of inspirational nature can be held, if desired, before the convention is officially organized.”

**1139.** `[C1482 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the assembly of delegates of an _____ society has been so organized in accordance with the bylaws or other governing rules as described below, it then acts as and in the name of the whole society and may be referred to as “The Tenth Annual Convention of the National Society of ” or, as common formerly, “The National Society of in convention assembled.”

   *Answer:* established — “When the assembly of delegates of an established society has been so organized in accordance with the bylaws or other governing rules as described below, it then acts as and in the name of the whole society and may be referred to as “The Tenth Annual Convention of the National Society of ” or, as common formerly, “The National Society of in convention assembled.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:10 — “When the assembly of delegates of an established society has been so organized in accordance with the bylaws or other governing rules as described below, it then acts as and in the name of the whole society and may be referred to as “The Tenth Annual Convention of the National Society of ” or, as common formerly, “The National Society of in convention assembled.””

**1140.** `[C1483 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The official organization of the convention is brought about by the separate _____ and adoption of the reports of three committees mentioned above—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee, in that order.

   *Answer:* consideration — “The official organization of the convention is brought about by the separate consideration and adoption of the reports of three committees mentioned above—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee, in that order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:11 — “The official organization of the convention is brought about by the separate consideration and adoption of the reports of three committees mentioned above—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee, in that order.”

**1141.** `[C1483 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The official organization of the convention is brought about by the separate _____ and adoption of the reports of three committees mentioned above—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee, in that order.
   A. resolutions
   B. immediately
   C. consideration
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:11 — “The official organization of the convention is brought about by the separate consideration and adoption of the reports of three committees mentioned above—the Credentials Committee, the Committee on Standing Rules, and the Program Committee, in that order.”

**1142.** `[C1484 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As each report comes up for _____, it is presented to the convention by a reporting member, normally the respective committee chairman.

   *Answer:* consideration — “As each report comes up for consideration, it is presented to the convention by a reporting member, normally the respective committee chairman.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:12 — “As each report comes up for consideration, it is presented to the convention by a reporting member, normally the respective committee chairman.”

**1143.** `[C1484 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As each report comes up for consideration, it is presented to the _____ by a reporting member, normally the respective committee chairman.
   A. substitute
   B. Unfinished
   C. committees
   D. convention

   *Answer:* D) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:12 — “As each report comes up for consideration, it is presented to the convention by a reporting member, normally the respective committee chairman.”

**1144.** `[C1484 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: a _____ practice is for the recording secretary of the convention or a qualified member of the committee to do so.

   *Answer:* convenient — “a convenient practice is for the recording secretary of the convention or a qualified member of the committee to do so.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:12 — “a convenient practice is for the recording secretary of the convention or a qualified member of the committee to do so.”

**1145.** `[C1484 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No second is _____ if the motion is made by a member of the committee.
   A. required
   B. assuming
   C. decision
   D. Division

   *Answer:* A) required. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:12 — “No second is required if the motion is made by a member of the committee.”

**1146.** `[C1484 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If no one offers the motion _____, the chair can call for it, or can assume it by stating, for example, “The question is on the adoption of the report of the Program Committee.”

   *Answer:* promptly — “If no one offers the motion promptly, the chair can call for it, or can assume it by stating, for example, “The question is on the adoption of the report of the Program Committee.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:12 — “If no one offers the motion promptly, the chair can call for it, or can assume it by stating, for example, “The question is on the adoption of the report of the Program Committee.””

**1147.** `[C1485 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each of these committee reports is _____ and amendable.
   A. debatable
   B. Privilege
   C. paragraph
   D. secretary

   *Answer:* A) debatable. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:13 — “Each of these committee reports is debatable and amendable.”

**1148.** `[C1485 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an ordinary _____ of a society, however, debate or proposals for amendment of any of them seldom occur, and the reports are likely to be adopted without dissenting vote—if the committees have done their work well.

   *Answer:* convention — “In an ordinary convention of a society, however, debate or proposals for amendment of any of them seldom occur, and the reports are likely to be adopted without dissenting vote—if the committees have done their work well.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:13 — “In an ordinary convention of a society, however, debate or proposals for amendment of any of them seldom occur, and the reports are likely to be adopted without dissenting vote—if the committees have done their work well.”

**1149.** `[C1485 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each of these committee reports is _____ and amendable.

   *Answer:* debatable — “Each of these committee reports is debatable and amendable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:13 — “Each of these committee reports is debatable and amendable.”

**1150.** `[C1485 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an ordinary _____ of a society, however, debate or proposals for amendment of any of them seldom occur, and the reports are likely to be adopted without dissenting vote—if the committees have done their work well.
   A. settlement
   B. authorizes
   C. convention
   D. introduced

   *Answer:* C) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:13 — “In an ordinary convention of a society, however, debate or proposals for amendment of any of them seldom occur, and the reports are likely to be adopted without dissenting vote—if the committees have done their work well.”

**1151.** `[C1486 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The specific duties of the _____ Committee are listed below.
   A. Definitely
   B. authorizes
   C. constitution
   D. Credentials

   *Answer:* D) Credentials. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:14 — “The specific duties of the Credentials Committee are listed below.”

**1152.** `[C1486 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Items (1) through (4) must of necessity be spread over a _____ period of time in advance of the convention.

   *Answer:* considerable — “Items (1) through (4) must of necessity be spread over a considerable period of time in advance of the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:14 — “Items (1) through (4) must of necessity be spread over a considerable period of time in advance of the convention.”

**1153.** `[C1486 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Items (1) through (4) must of necessity be spread over a considerable period of time in advance of the _____.
   A. incidental
   B. convention
   C. landscaped
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* B) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:14 — “Items (1) through (4) must of necessity be spread over a considerable period of time in advance of the convention.”

**1154.** `[C1486 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The specific duties of the _____ Committee are listed below.

   *Answer:* Credentials — “The specific duties of the Credentials Committee are listed below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:14 — “The specific duties of the Credentials Committee are listed below.”

**1155.** `[C1487 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In societies that maintain a permanent _____ headquarters, most of the clerical duties required of the Credentials Committee in advance of the convention usually develop into a routine technique preserved from year to year and performed largely by the regular paid staff;

   *Answer:* administrative — “In societies that maintain a permanent administrative headquarters, most of the clerical duties required of the Credentials Committee in advance of the convention usually develop into a routine technique preserved from year to year and performed largely by the regular paid staff;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:15 — “In societies that maintain a permanent administrative headquarters, most of the clerical duties required of the Credentials Committee in advance of the convention usually develop into a routine technique preserved from year to year and performed largely by the regular paid staff;”

**1156.** `[C1487 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but the authority and _____ for general direction of this work remain with the Credentials Committee.
   A. Preparation
   B. responsibility
   C. requirement
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* B) responsibility. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:15 — “but the authority and responsibility for general direction of this work remain with the Credentials Committee.”

**1157.** `[C1487 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In societies that maintain a permanent _____ headquarters, most of the clerical duties required of the Credentials Committee in advance of the convention usually develop into a routine technique preserved from year to year and performed largely by the regular paid staff;
   A. Parliamentary
   B. Information
   C. interrupted
   D. administrative

   *Answer:* D) administrative. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:15 — “In societies that maintain a permanent administrative headquarters, most of the clerical duties required of the Credentials Committee in advance of the convention usually develop into a routine technique preserved from year to year and performed largely by the regular paid staff;”

**1158.** `[C1487 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but the authority and _____ for general direction of this work remain with the Credentials Committee.

   *Answer:* responsibility — “but the authority and responsibility for general direction of this work remain with the Credentials Committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:15 — “but the authority and responsibility for general direction of this work remain with the Credentials Committee.”

**1159.** `[C1488 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a convention of any size lasting for a number of days, registration may begin one or _____ before the convention opens, and provision should be made to handle a heavy volume of registrations during the afternoon and evening before the opening business meeting, as well as on the morning of that meeting.

   *Answer:* two days — “In a convention of any size lasting for a number of days, registration may begin one or two days before the convention opens, and provision should be made to handle a heavy volume of registrations during the afternoon and evening before the opening business meeting, as well as on the morning of that meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:16 — “In a convention of any size lasting for a number of days, registration may begin one or two days before the convention opens, and provision should be made to handle a heavy volume of registrations during the afternoon and evening before the opening business meeting, as well as on the morning of that meeting.”

**1160.** `[C1488 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The times and the place of _____ should be announced in the printed convention program.
   A. registration
   B. Convention
   C. Announcements
   D. deliberative

   *Answer:* A) registration. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:16 — “The times and the place of registration should be announced in the printed convention program.”

**1161.** `[C1488 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a convention of any size lasting for a number of days, registration may begin one or two days before the convention opens, and provision should be made to handle a heavy volume of _____ during the afternoon and evening before the opening business meeting, as well as on the morning of that meeting.

   *Answer:* registrations — “In a convention of any size lasting for a number of days, registration may begin one or two days before the convention opens, and provision should be made to handle a heavy volume of registrations during the afternoon and evening before the opening business meeting, as well as on the morning of that meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:16 — “In a convention of any size lasting for a number of days, registration may begin one or two days before the convention opens, and provision should be made to handle a heavy volume of registrations during the afternoon and evening before the opening business meeting, as well as on the morning of that meeting.”

**1162.** `[C1488 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The times and the place of registration should be announced in the printed _____ program.
   A. convention
   B. subsidiary
   C. recognized
   D. sustaining

   *Answer:* A) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:16 — “The times and the place of registration should be announced in the printed convention program.”

**1163.** `[C1489 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The method used by the _____ Committee to register the delegates and alternates will vary according to the size of the convention.
   A. Substitute

   D. Organization

   *Answer:* C) Credentials. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:17 — “The method used by the Credentials Committee to register the delegates and alternates will vary according to the size of the convention.”

**1166.** `[C1489 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ in common use is outlined as follows:
   A. postponed

**1167.** `[C1490 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The entire _____ holding the convention is divided into parts, such as states, districts, or counties, and a separate section of the register—often prepared in triplicate, as noted below—is set up for each subdivision.

   *Answer:* association — “The entire association holding the convention is divided into parts, such as states, districts, or counties, and a separate section of the register—often prepared in triplicate, as noted below—is set up for each subdivision.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:18 — “The entire association holding the convention is divided into parts, such as states, districts, or counties, and a separate section of the register—often prepared in triplicate, as noted below—is set up for each subdivision.”

**1168.** `[C1490 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Each section of the register contains—arranged in an appropriate logical order— the typed names of the constituent societies or units located within the _____ geographical area;
   A. representatives
   B. Vice-President
   C. recommendation
   D. corresponding

   *Answer:* D) corresponding. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:18 — “Each section of the register contains—arranged in an appropriate logical order— the typed names of the constituent societies or units located within the corresponding geographical area;”

**1169.** `[C1490 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and under each unit’s name are typed _____ the names of the delegates and alternates that have been sent in on the unit’s credentials blanks, provided that these persons have been found eligible by the committee.

   *Answer:* alphabetically — “and under each unit’s name are typed alphabetically the names of the delegates and alternates that have been sent in on the unit’s credentials blanks, provided that these persons have been found eligible by the committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:18 — “and under each unit’s name are typed alphabetically the names of the delegates and alternates that have been sent in on the unit’s credentials blanks, provided that these persons have been found eligible by the committee.”

**1170.** `[C1490 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The entire _____ holding the convention is divided into parts, such as states, districts, or counties, and a separate section of the register—often prepared in triplicate, as noted below—is set up for each subdivision.
   A. Substitute
   B. association
   C. Definitely
   D. Incidental

**1171.** `[C1490 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Each section of the register contains—arranged in an appropriate logical order— the typed names of the constituent societies or units located within the _____ geographical area;

   *Answer:* corresponding — “Each section of the register contains—arranged in an appropriate logical order— the typed names of the constituent societies or units located within the corresponding geographical area;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:18 — “Each section of the register contains—arranged in an appropriate logical order— the typed names of the constituent societies or units located within the corresponding geographical area;”

**1172.** `[C1491 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During the initial period when the bulk of _____ take place, usually two committee members are assigned to a separate and conspicuously marked table or station for each section of the register.

   *Answer:* registrations — “During the initial period when the bulk of registrations take place, usually two committee members are assigned to a separate and conspicuously marked table or station for each section of the register.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:19 — “During the initial period when the bulk of registrations take place, usually two committee members are assigned to a separate and conspicuously marked table or station for each section of the register.”

**1173.** `[C1491 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a large convention, ushers may be helpful in guiding delegates and alternates to the proper section, where they present their _____ and sign the register to the right of their typewritten names.
   A. assemblies
   B. Reconsider
   C. credentials
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) credentials. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:19 — “In a large convention, ushers may be helpful in guiding delegates and alternates to the proper section, where they present their credentials and sign the register to the right of their typewritten names.”

**1174.** `[C1491 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: During the initial period when the bulk of _____ take place, usually two committee members are assigned to a separate and conspicuously marked table or station for each section of the register.
   A. registrations
   B. representatives
   C. immediately
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* A) registrations. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:19 — “During the initial period when the bulk of registrations take place, usually two committee members are assigned to a separate and conspicuously marked table or station for each section of the register.”

**1175.** `[C1491 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a large convention, ushers may be helpful in guiding delegates and alternates to the proper section, where they present their _____ and sign the register to the right of their typewritten names.

   *Answer:* credentials — “In a large convention, ushers may be helpful in guiding delegates and alternates to the proper section, where they present their credentials and sign the register to the right of their typewritten names.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:19 — “In a large convention, ushers may be helpful in guiding delegates and alternates to the proper section, where they present their credentials and sign the register to the right of their typewritten names.”

**1176.** `[C1492 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: At least one, and frequently two, duplicate registers (or _____ sets of the register pages) are desirable in addition to the Credentials Committee’s master copy—one duplicate list to be submitted as an attachment to the committee’s report, the other for later use by election tellers in verifying the eligibility of voters.

   *Answer:* photocopy-reproduced — “At least one, and frequently two, duplicate registers (or photocopy-reproduced sets of the register pages) are desirable in addition to the Credentials Committee’s master copy—one duplicate list to be submitted as an attachment to the committee’s report, the other for later use by election tellers in verifying the eligibility of voters.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:20 — “At least one, and frequently two, duplicate registers (or photocopy-reproduced sets of the register pages) are desirable in addition to the Credentials Committee’s master copy—one duplicate list to be submitted as an attachment to the committee’s report, the other for later use by election tellers in verifying the eligibility of voters.”

**1177.** `[C1492 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By use of the latter copy, counting procedure in an election can be expedited by dividing the tellers into _____ groups for each section of the register, according to the same pattern as in the case of the Credentials Committee members during registration.
   A. Information
   B. subcommittee
   C. Definitely
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* B) subcommittee. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:20 — “By use of the latter copy, counting procedure in an election can be expedited by dividing the tellers into subcommittee groups for each section of the register, according to the same pattern as in the case of the Credentials Committee members during registration.”

**1178.** `[C1492 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The delegates’ badges can also be _____ labeled or numbered to facilitate identification with the correct section.

   *Answer:* correspondingly — “The delegates’ badges can also be correspondingly labeled or numbered to facilitate identification with the correct section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:20 — “The delegates’ badges can also be correspondingly labeled or numbered to facilitate identification with the correct section.”

**1179.** `[C1492 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At least one, and frequently two, duplicate registers (or _____ sets of the register pages) are desirable in addition to the Credentials Committee’s master copy—one duplicate list to be submitted as an attachment to the committee’s report, the other for later use by election tellers in verifying the eligibility of voters.
   A. Announcements
   B. adjournment
   C. consideration
   D. photocopy-reproduced

   *Answer:* D) photocopy-reproduced. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:20 — “At least one, and frequently two, duplicate registers (or photocopy-reproduced sets of the register pages) are desirable in addition to the Credentials Committee’s master copy—one duplicate list to be submitted as an attachment to the committee’s report, the other for later use by election tellers in verifying the eligibility of voters.”

**1180.** `[C1492 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By use of the latter copy, counting procedure in an election can be expedited by dividing the tellers into _____ groups for each section of the register, according to the same pattern as in the case of the Credentials Committee members during registration.

   *Answer:* subcommittee — “By use of the latter copy, counting procedure in an election can be expedited by dividing the tellers into subcommittee groups for each section of the register, according to the same pattern as in the case of the Credentials Committee members during registration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:20 — “By use of the latter copy, counting procedure in an election can be expedited by dividing the tellers into subcommittee groups for each section of the register, according to the same pattern as in the case of the Credentials Committee members during registration.”

**1181.** `[C1493 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Cases of contested seats in a delegation will seldom arise except in political _____.

   *Answer:* conventions — “Cases of contested seats in a delegation will seldom arise except in political conventions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:21 — “Cases of contested seats in a delegation will seldom arise except in political conventions.”

**1182.** `[C1493 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the rare event of a contest between two delegates or groups of delegates and serious doubt as to which is entitled to be seated, the committee should omit both from the list and report the fact of the contest to the _____ as explained below.
   A. Procedures
   B. settlement
   C. convention
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:21 — “In the rare event of a contest between two delegates or groups of delegates and serious doubt as to which is entitled to be seated, the committee should omit both from the list and report the fact of the contest to the convention as explained below.”

**1183.** `[C1493 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, on the other hand, after hearing the facts, the committee thinks the contest is not justified, it should enter on the list only the names of the delegates whose claim it finds to be _____.

   *Answer:* legitimate — “If, on the other hand, after hearing the facts, the committee thinks the contest is not justified, it should enter on the list only the names of the delegates whose claim it finds to be legitimate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:21 — “If, on the other hand, after hearing the facts, the committee thinks the contest is not justified, it should enter on the list only the names of the delegates whose claim it finds to be legitimate.”

**1184.** `[C1493 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The same rules apply to the more common case of delegates chosen by a local unit that is not entitled to _____ or has chosen delegates in excess of its entitlement.
   A. interrupted
   B. deliberative
   C. representation
   D. specification

   *Answer:* C) representation. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:21 — “The same rules apply to the more common case of delegates chosen by a local unit that is not entitled to representation or has chosen delegates in excess of its entitlement.”

**1185.** `[C1493 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Cases of contested seats in a delegation will seldom arise except in political _____.
   A. deliberative
   B. immediately
   C. conventions
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* C) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:21 — “Cases of contested seats in a delegation will seldom arise except in political conventions.”

**1186.** `[C1494 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even, for example, a motion relating to the validity of the holding of the _____ is not in order at such a time.
   A. persuasive
   B. assemblies
   C. Permission
   D. convention

   *Answer:* D) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:22 — “Even, for example, a motion relating to the validity of the holding of the convention is not in order at such a time.”

**1187.** `[C1494 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is, therefore, essential that the committee establish and hold itself to a deadline for _____ to be included in its first report, which will leave it time to prepare that report.

   *Answer:* registrations — “It is, therefore, essential that the committee establish and hold itself to a deadline for registrations to be included in its first report, which will leave it time to prepare that report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:22 — “It is, therefore, essential that the committee establish and hold itself to a deadline for registrations to be included in its first report, which will leave it time to prepare that report.”

**1188.** `[C1494 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The opening ceremonies will afford some _____ for this work, and, while it is in process, delegates can continue to register—but not to be included in the committee’s initial report.
   A. authorizes
   B. adjournment
   C. opportunity
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* C) opportunity. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:22 — “The opening ceremonies will afford some opportunity for this work, and, while it is in process, delegates can continue to register—but not to be included in the committee’s initial report.”

**1189.** `[C1494 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the report is not ready in time, the convention may continue with other _____ matters, such as speakers, or may stand at ease or take a recess.

   *Answer:* nonbusiness — “If the report is not ready in time, the convention may continue with other nonbusiness matters, such as speakers, or may stand at ease or take a recess.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:22 — “If the report is not ready in time, the convention may continue with other nonbusiness matters, such as speakers, or may stand at ease or take a recess.”

**1190.** `[C1494 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even, for example, a motion relating to the validity of the holding of the _____ is not in order at such a time.

   *Answer:* convention — “Even, for example, a motion relating to the validity of the holding of the convention is not in order at such a time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:22 — “Even, for example, a motion relating to the validity of the holding of the convention is not in order at such a time.”

**1191.** `[C1495 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Normally the list of delegates and alternates is not read unless a portion of it is read upon request, for _____.
   A. assemblies
   B. adjournment
   C. Convention
   D. information

   *Answer:* D) information. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:23 — “Normally the list of delegates and alternates is not read unless a portion of it is read upon request, for information.”

**1192.** `[C1495 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is an unresolved contest between delegates, the _____ are stated, as well as the fact that the names of the contesting or contested delegates do not appear on the roll.

   *Answer:* particulars — “If there is an unresolved contest between delegates, the particulars are stated, as well as the fact that the names of the contesting or contested delegates do not appear on the roll.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:23 — “If there is an unresolved contest between delegates, the particulars are stated, as well as the fact that the names of the contesting or contested delegates do not appear on the roll.”

**1193.** `[C1495 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The committee chairman concludes the report by saying, “On behalf of the committee, I move that the roll of delegates hereby submitted be the official roll of the voting members of the _____.” The report with the attached list of names is then handed to the chair or to the secretary.
   A. Procedures
   B. Unfinished
   C. committees
   D. convention

   *Answer:* D) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:23 — “The committee chairman concludes the report by saying, “On behalf of the committee, I move that the roll of delegates hereby submitted be the official roll of the voting members of the convention.” The report with the attached list of names is then handed to the chair or to the secretary.”

**1194.** `[C1495 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Normally the list of delegates and alternates is not read unless a portion of it is read upon request, for _____.

   *Answer:* information — “Normally the list of delegates and alternates is not read unless a portion of it is read upon request, for information.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:23 — “Normally the list of delegates and alternates is not read unless a portion of it is read upon request, for information.”

**1195.** `[C1495 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is an unresolved contest between delegates, the _____ are stated, as well as the fact that the names of the contesting or contested delegates do not appear on the roll.
   A. resolutions
   B. particulars
   C. requirement
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) particulars. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:23 — “If there is an unresolved contest between delegates, the particulars are stated, as well as the fact that the names of the contesting or contested delegates do not appear on the roll.”

**1196.** `[C1496 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless there is debate or proposed amendment, the chair, before taking the vote on the adoption of the report, asks, “Are there any questions on the report?” If seat(s) are contested, an amendment can be offered _____ in this form: “To amend by adding ‘provided that the name of George J.

   *Answer:* substantially — “Unless there is debate or proposed amendment, the chair, before taking the vote on the adoption of the report, asks, “Are there any questions on the report?” If seat(s) are contested, an amendment can be offered substantially in this form: “To amend by adding ‘provided that the name of George J.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:24 — “Unless there is debate or proposed amendment, the chair, before taking the vote on the adoption of the report, asks, “Are there any questions on the report?” If seat(s) are contested, an amendment can be offered substantially in this form: “To amend by adding ‘provided that the name of George J.”

**1197.** `[C1496 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Morse be added to the roll of _____ as submitted, as a delegate from the state of Missouri.’” The name of the rival delegate can then be offered in a secondary amendment, for example, “to strike out ‘George J.
   A. exhausted
   B. secondary
   C. Privilege
   D. delegates

   *Answer:* D) delegates. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:24 — “Morse be added to the roll of delegates as submitted, as a delegate from the state of Missouri.’” The name of the rival delegate can then be offered in a secondary amendment, for example, “to strike out ‘George J.”

**1198.** `[C1496 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Morse’ and insert ‘Frank Norton.’” Whether or not a contest is reported, it is in order to move such _____ or even to move to substitute an entirely different set of delegates for any delegation in the reported list, but no such amendment is permitted to include more names than those of a single challenged delegate or delegation all of whom are challenged on the same grounds, together with any claimants involved.

   *Answer:* amendments — “Morse’ and insert ‘Frank Norton.’” Whether or not a contest is reported, it is in order to move such amendments or even to move to substitute an entirely different set of delegates for any delegation in the reported list, but no such amendment is permitted to include more names than those of a single challenged delegate or delegation all of whom are challenged on the same grounds, together with any claimants involved.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:24 — “Morse’ and insert ‘Frank Norton.’” Whether or not a contest is reported, it is in order to move such amendments or even to move to substitute an entirely different set of delegates for any delegation in the reported list, but no such amendment is permitted to include more names than those of a single challenged delegate or delegation all of whom are challenged on the same grounds, together with any claimants involved.”

**1199.** `[C1496 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless there is debate or proposed _____, the chair, before taking the vote on the adoption of the report, asks, “Are there any questions on the report?” If seat(s) are contested, an amendment can be offered substantially in this form: “To amend by adding ‘provided that the name of George J.
   A. Crestwood
   B. postponed
   C. amendment
   D. president

   *Answer:* C) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:24 — “Unless there is debate or proposed amendment, the chair, before taking the vote on the adoption of the report, asks, “Are there any questions on the report?” If seat(s) are contested, an amendment can be offered substantially in this form: “To amend by adding ‘provided that the name of George J.”

**1200.** `[C1496 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the question of adopting the Credentials Committee’s report or on motions connected with its _____, only those persons whose names are on the list of voting members reported by the committee (as this list stands after any amendment already approved by the convention) are entitled to vote.
   A. undebatable
   B. Organization
   C. Parliamentarian
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:24 — “On the question of adopting the Credentials Committee’s report or on motions connected with its consideration, only those persons whose names are on the list of voting members reported by the committee (as this list stands after any amendment already approved by the convention) are entitled to vote.”

**1201.** `[C1497 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if there are changes, the committee chairman concludes his report by saying, “On behalf of the committee, I move that the revised roll of delegates hereby submitted be the official roll of voting members of the convention.” Although this motion might appear to be one to amend something previously adopted (35), it requires only a _____ vote for its adoption, since it is always understood that the roll will be altered as delegates arrive late or leave early, and alternates may thereby be shifted in status.

   *Answer:* majority — “but if there are changes, the committee chairman concludes his report by saying, “On behalf of the committee, I move that the revised roll of delegates hereby submitted be the official roll of voting members of the convention.” Although this motion might appear to be one to amend something previously adopted (35), it requires only a majority vote for its adoption, since it is always understood that the roll will be altered as delegates arrive late or leave early, and alternates may thereby be shifted in status.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:25 — “but if there are changes, the committee chairman concludes his report by saying, “On behalf of the committee, I move that the revised roll of delegates hereby submitted be the official roll of voting members of the convention.” Although this motion might appear to be one to amend something previously adopted (35), it requires only a majority vote for its adoption, since it is always understood that the roll will be altered as delegates arrive late or leave early, and alternates may thereby be shifted in status.”

**1202.** `[C1497 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the report of the _____ Committee is adopted, it is thereby ratified as the official roll of voting members of the convention—subject to changes through later reports.
   A. Convention
   B. Credentials
   C. Procedures
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* B) Credentials. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:25 — “When the report of the Credentials Committee is adopted, it is thereby ratified as the official roll of voting members of the convention—subject to changes through later reports.”

**1203.** `[C1497 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A voting member who registers after the _____ of the first report assumes his full status as soon as he has done so, if his status is not questioned;

   *Answer:* submission — “A voting member who registers after the submission of the first report assumes his full status as soon as he has done so, if his status is not questioned;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:25 — “A voting member who registers after the submission of the first report assumes his full status as soon as he has done so, if his status is not questioned;”

**1204.** `[C1497 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the report of the Credentials Committee is adopted, it is thereby ratified as the official roll of voting members of the _____—subject to changes through later reports.
   A. persuasive
   B. Particular
   C. Reconsider
   D. convention

   *Answer:* D) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:25 — “When the report of the Credentials Committee is adopted, it is thereby ratified as the official roll of voting members of the convention—subject to changes through later reports.”

**1205.** `[C1497 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the Credentials Committee normally makes a _____ report only at the beginning of each day, it may be called upon to do so at other times, such as immediately before an important vote.

   *Answer:* supplementary — “Although the Credentials Committee normally makes a supplementary report only at the beginning of each day, it may be called upon to do so at other times, such as immediately before an important vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:25 — “Although the Credentials Committee normally makes a supplementary report only at the beginning of each day, it may be called upon to do so at other times, such as immediately before an important vote.”

**1206.** `[C1498 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the bylaws or the convention’s standing rules do not prescribe a quorum (40)—which they should do—the quorum is a _____ of the number of voting members who have actually registered at the convention as in attendance, irrespective of whether some may have departed.

   *Answer:* majority — “If the bylaws or the convention’s standing rules do not prescribe a quorum (40)—which they should do—the quorum is a majority of the number of voting members who have actually registered at the convention as in attendance, irrespective of whether some may have departed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:26 — “If the bylaws or the convention’s standing rules do not prescribe a quorum (40)—which they should do—the quorum is a majority of the number of voting members who have actually registered at the convention as in attendance, irrespective of whether some may have departed.”

**1207.** `[C1498 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ Committee’s master roll of currently registered voting members of the convention must be maintained at all times in such a way that their exact number can be promptly determined.
   A. Credentials
   B. Preparation
   C. Organization
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* A) Credentials. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:26 — “The Credentials Committee’s master roll of currently registered voting members of the convention must be maintained at all times in such a way that their exact number can be promptly determined.”

**1208.** `[C1498 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Accuracy of the list of _____ is essential, since it may affect the outcome of elections or closely contested issues.

   *Answer:* registrants — “Accuracy of the list of registrants is essential, since it may affect the outcome of elections or closely contested issues.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:26 — “Accuracy of the list of registrants is essential, since it may affect the outcome of elections or closely contested issues.”

**1209.** `[C1498 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the bylaws or the convention’s standing rules do not prescribe a quorum (40)—which they should do—the quorum is a majority of the number of voting members who have actually registered at the convention as in attendance, _____ of whether some may have departed.
   A. Announcements
   B. Information
   C. irrespective
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) irrespective. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:26 — “If the bylaws or the convention’s standing rules do not prescribe a quorum (40)—which they should do—the quorum is a majority of the number of voting members who have actually registered at the convention as in attendance, irrespective of whether some may have departed.”

**1210.** `[C1498 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ Committee’s master roll of currently registered voting members of the convention must be maintained at all times in such a way that their exact number can be promptly determined.

   *Answer:* Credentials — “The Credentials Committee’s master roll of currently registered voting members of the convention must be maintained at all times in such a way that their exact number can be promptly determined.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:26 — “The Credentials Committee’s master roll of currently registered voting members of the convention must be maintained at all times in such a way that their exact number can be promptly determined.”

**1211.** `[C1499 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Committee on Standing Rules drafts and submits for _____ a group of rules known as “The Standing Rules of the Convention,” which, as adopted, will apply to that one convention only.
   A. Information
   B. President-Elect
   C. consideration
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:27 — “The Committee on Standing Rules drafts and submits for consideration a group of rules known as “The Standing Rules of the Convention,” which, as adopted, will apply to that one convention only.”

**1212.** `[C1499 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: These rules must in no way conflict with the bylaws of the society, but (in contrast to ordinary standing rules in a local society) they can involve _____ of rules contained in the parliamentary authority prescribed by the bylaws.

   *Answer:* modifications — “These rules must in no way conflict with the bylaws of the society, but (in contrast to ordinary standing rules in a local society) they can involve modifications of rules contained in the parliamentary authority prescribed by the bylaws.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:27 — “These rules must in no way conflict with the bylaws of the society, but (in contrast to ordinary standing rules in a local society) they can involve modifications of rules contained in the parliamentary authority prescribed by the bylaws.”

**1213.** `[C1499 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The standing rules of a convention usually contain both “parliamentary” rules relating to the conduct of business, and _____ rules, so that in some ways they resemble a combination of special rules of order and ordinary standing rules (2).
   A. Information
   B. recommendation
   C. adjournment
   D. nonparliamentary

   *Answer:* D) nonparliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:27 — “The standing rules of a convention usually contain both “parliamentary” rules relating to the conduct of business, and nonparliamentary rules, so that in some ways they resemble a combination of special rules of order and ordinary standing rules (2).”

**1214.** `[C1499 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since their effect expires at the close of the session that adopts them, however, they differ from either of the latter types of rules in certain _____.

   *Answer:* respects — “Since their effect expires at the close of the session that adopts them, however, they differ from either of the latter types of rules in certain respects.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:27 — “Since their effect expires at the close of the session that adopts them, however, they differ from either of the latter types of rules in certain respects.”

**1215.** `[C1499 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Committee on Standing Rules drafts and submits for _____ a group of rules known as “The Standing Rules of the Convention,” which, as adopted, will apply to that one convention only.

   *Answer:* consideration — “The Committee on Standing Rules drafts and submits for consideration a group of rules known as “The Standing Rules of the Convention,” which, as adopted, will apply to that one convention only.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:27 — “The Committee on Standing Rules drafts and submits for consideration a group of rules known as “The Standing Rules of the Convention,” which, as adopted, will apply to that one convention only.”

**1216.** `[C1500 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The standing rules of successive _____ held by a society often become developed to a point where little change in the rules adopted by the preceding convention is necessary.

   *Answer:* conventions — “The standing rules of successive conventions held by a society often become developed to a point where little change in the rules adopted by the preceding convention is necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:28 — “The standing rules of successive conventions held by a society often become developed to a point where little change in the rules adopted by the preceding convention is necessary.”

**1217.** `[C1500 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, the work of this committee may sometimes require extensive research into past proceedings of the _____.
   A. organization
   B. specification
   C. Reconsider
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* A) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:28 — “On the other hand, the work of this committee may sometimes require extensive research into past proceedings of the organization.”

**1218.** `[C1500 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any case, the _____ should always be consulted regarding the convention’s standing rules, and he often prepares a first draft for submission to this committee.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “In any case, the parliamentarian should always be consulted regarding the convention’s standing rules, and he often prepares a first draft for submission to this committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:28 — “In any case, the parliamentarian should always be consulted regarding the convention’s standing rules, and he often prepares a first draft for submission to this committee.”

**1219.** `[C1500 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The standing rules of successive _____ held by a society often become developed to a point where little change in the rules adopted by the preceding convention is necessary.
   A. deliberative
   B. conventions
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:28 — “The standing rules of successive conventions held by a society often become developed to a point where little change in the rules adopted by the preceding convention is necessary.”

**1220.** `[C1500 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, the work of this committee may sometimes require extensive research into past proceedings of the _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “On the other hand, the work of this committee may sometimes require extensive research into past proceedings of the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:28 — “On the other hand, the work of this committee may sometimes require extensive research into past proceedings of the organization.”

**1221.** `[C1501 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A copy of the “Proposed Standing Rules of the _____” that the committee is to recommend—usually printed in the official program—should be handed to each person when he registers.

   *Answer:* Convention — “A copy of the “Proposed Standing Rules of the Convention” that the committee is to recommend—usually printed in the official program—should be handed to each person when he registers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:29 — “A copy of the “Proposed Standing Rules of the Convention” that the committee is to recommend—usually printed in the official program—should be handed to each person when he registers.”

**1222.** `[C1501 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A copy of the “Proposed Standing Rules of the _____” that the committee is to recommend—usually printed in the official program—should be handed to each person when he registers.
   A. Unfinished
   B. variations
   C. substitute
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* D) Convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:29 — “A copy of the “Proposed Standing Rules of the Convention” that the committee is to recommend—usually printed in the official program—should be handed to each person when he registers.”
