**1223.** `[C1502 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ and Adoption of Convention Standing Rules.

   *Answer:* Presentation — “Presentation and Adoption of Convention Standing Rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:30 — “Presentation and Adoption of Convention Standing Rules.”

**1224.** `[C1502 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The report of the Committee on Standing Rules is presented to the convention _____ after the adoption of that of the Credentials Committee.
   A. Preparation
   B. Unfinished
   C. immediately
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:30 — “The report of the Committee on Standing Rules is presented to the convention immediately after the adoption of that of the Credentials Committee.”

**1225.** `[C1502 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As part of this report, the proposed rules are read in their entirety—unless the rules are not _____ changed from those adopted in the last few conventions and every delegate has been provided with a copy when registering at the convention or previously, in which case the committee chairman or other reporting member may omit their reading.

   *Answer:* substantially — “As part of this report, the proposed rules are read in their entirety—unless the rules are not substantially changed from those adopted in the last few conventions and every delegate has been provided with a copy when registering at the convention or previously, in which case the committee chairman or other reporting member may omit their reading.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:30 — “As part of this report, the proposed rules are read in their entirety—unless the rules are not substantially changed from those adopted in the last few conventions and every delegate has been provided with a copy when registering at the convention or previously, in which case the committee chairman or other reporting member may omit their reading.”

**1226.** `[C1502 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ and Adoption of Convention Standing Rules.
   A. consideration
   B. constitution
   C. resolutions
   D. Presentation

   *Answer:* D) Presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:30 — “Presentation and Adoption of Convention Standing Rules.”

**1227.** `[C1502 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The report of the Committee on Standing Rules is presented to the convention _____ after the adoption of that of the Credentials Committee.

   *Answer:* immediately — “The report of the Committee on Standing Rules is presented to the convention immediately after the adoption of that of the Credentials Committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:30 — “The report of the Committee on Standing Rules is presented to the convention immediately after the adoption of that of the Credentials Committee.”

**1228.** `[C1503 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair states the question by saying, “It is moved to adopt the Standing Rules of the _____ as printed [or, “as just read”],” but any voting member of the convention then has the right to have the proposed rules read by the chair or secretary if they were not read by the committee chairman or other reporting member.

   *Answer:* Convention — “The chair states the question by saying, “It is moved to adopt the Standing Rules of the Convention as printed [or, “as just read”],” but any voting member of the convention then has the right to have the proposed rules read by the chair or secretary if they were not read by the committee chairman or other reporting member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:31 — “The chair states the question by saying, “It is moved to adopt the Standing Rules of the Convention as printed [or, “as just read”],” but any voting member of the convention then has the right to have the proposed rules read by the chair or secretary if they were not read by the committee chairman or other reporting member.”

**1229.** `[C1503 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair states the question by saying, “It is moved to adopt the Standing Rules of the _____ as printed [or, “as just read”],” but any voting member of the convention then has the right to have the proposed rules read by the chair or secretary if they were not read by the committee chairman or other reporting member.
   A. Particular
   B. Committee
   C. Incidental
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* D) Convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:31 — “The chair states the question by saying, “It is moved to adopt the Standing Rules of the Convention as printed [or, “as just read”],” but any voting member of the convention then has the right to have the proposed rules read by the chair or secretary if they were not read by the committee chairman or other reporting member.”

**1230.** `[C1504 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After debate or _____ (if any), a single vote normally is taken on the complete body of rules.

   *Answer:* amendment — “After debate or amendment (if any), a single vote normally is taken on the complete body of rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:32 — “After debate or amendment (if any), a single vote normally is taken on the complete body of rules.”

**1231.** `[C1504 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be _____, however, that although the rules may be organized to have the appearance of being a single document, they are in fact a group of separate main motions being offered by the committee under one enacting motion.
   A. incidental
   B. subsidiary
   C. understood
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* C) understood. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:32 — “It should be understood, however, that although the rules may be organized to have the appearance of being a single document, they are in fact a group of separate main motions being offered by the committee under one enacting motion.”

**1232.** `[C1504 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a demand is made for a separate vote—which is advisable only if a serious matter appears to be at stake—the remainder of the rules are acted on first, and then those separated out are acted on _____.

   *Answer:* individually — “If a demand is made for a separate vote—which is advisable only if a serious matter appears to be at stake—the remainder of the rules are acted on first, and then those separated out are acted on individually.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:32 — “If a demand is made for a separate vote—which is advisable only if a serious matter appears to be at stake—the remainder of the rules are acted on first, and then those separated out are acted on individually.”

**1233.** `[C1504 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an additional rule is proposed during the _____ of the committee’s proposed rules (or later during the convention), it is similarly acted on separately after the committee’s proposals have been voted on.
   A. Parliamentarian
   B. immediately
   C. undebatable
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* D) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:32 — “If an additional rule is proposed during the consideration of the committee’s proposed rules (or later during the convention), it is similarly acted on separately after the committee’s proposals have been voted on.”

**1234.** `[C1504 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After debate or _____ (if any), a single vote normally is taken on the complete body of rules.
   A. extending
   B. reception
   C. amendment
   D. president

   *Answer:* C) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:32 — “After debate or amendment (if any), a single vote normally is taken on the complete body of rules.”

**1235.** `[C1505 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In putting the question to a vote, the chair may _____ omit reading the proposed rules.

   *Answer:* initially — “In putting the question to a vote, the chair may initially omit reading the proposed rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:33 — “In putting the question to a vote, the chair may initially omit reading the proposed rules.”

**1236.** `[C1505 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, if they have not been read even once, any _____ then has the right to demand that they be read before the vote.
   A. delegate
   B. chairman
   C. division
   D. Assembly

   *Answer:* A) delegate. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:33 — “However, if they have not been read even once, any delegate then has the right to demand that they be read before the vote.”

**1237.** `[C1505 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even if _____ read, any delegate has the right at that time to demand the reading of any rule that has been amended since being read.

   *Answer:* previously — “Even if previously read, any delegate has the right at that time to demand the reading of any rule that has been amended since being read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:33 — “Even if previously read, any delegate has the right at that time to demand the reading of any rule that has been amended since being read.”

**1238.** `[C1505 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In putting the question to a vote, the chair may _____ omit reading the proposed rules.
   A. Reporting
   B. presiding
   C. initially
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* C) initially. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:33 — “In putting the question to a vote, the chair may initially omit reading the proposed rules.”

**1239.** `[C1505 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, if they have not been read even once, any _____ then has the right to demand that they be read before the vote.

   *Answer:* delegate — “However, if they have not been read even once, any delegate then has the right to demand that they be read before the vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:33 — “However, if they have not been read even once, any delegate then has the right to demand that they be read before the vote.”

**1240.** `[C1506 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A group of proposed standing rules requires a _____ vote for its adoption whenever it includes any rule that would require a two-thirds vote for its adoption if voted on individually (see next paragraph below).

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “A group of proposed standing rules requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption whenever it includes any rule that would require a two-thirds vote for its adoption if voted on individually (see next paragraph below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:34 — “A group of proposed standing rules requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption whenever it includes any rule that would require a two-thirds vote for its adoption if voted on individually (see next paragraph below).”

**1241.** `[C1506 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A group of proposed standing rules requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption whenever it includes any rule that would require a two-thirds vote for its adoption if voted on _____ (see next paragraph below).
   A. adjournment
   B. individually
   C. undebatable
   D. specification

   *Answer:* B) individually. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:34 — “A group of proposed standing rules requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption whenever it includes any rule that would require a two-thirds vote for its adoption if voted on individually (see next paragraph below).”

**1242.** `[C1507 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a standing rule of a convention is voted on individually, the vote necessary for its adoption is in some cases two thirds and in others a _____, depending on the nature of the rule:

   *Answer:* majority — “If a standing rule of a convention is voted on individually, the vote necessary for its adoption is in some cases two thirds and in others a majority, depending on the nature of the rule:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:35 — “If a standing rule of a convention is voted on individually, the vote necessary for its adoption is in some cases two thirds and in others a majority, depending on the nature of the rule:”

**1243.** `[C1507 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Vote Required for Adoption of an _____ Rule.
   A. privileged
   B. resolution
   C. incidental
   D. Individual

   *Answer:* D) Individual. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:35 — “Vote Required for Adoption of an Individual Rule.”

**1244.** `[C1507 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a standing rule of a convention is voted on _____, the vote necessary for its adoption is in some cases two thirds and in others a majority, depending on the nature of the rule:

   *Answer:* individually — “If a standing rule of a convention is voted on individually, the vote necessary for its adoption is in some cases two thirds and in others a majority, depending on the nature of the rule:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:35 — “If a standing rule of a convention is voted on individually, the vote necessary for its adoption is in some cases two thirds and in others a majority, depending on the nature of the rule:”

**1245.** `[C1507 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Vote Required for Adoption of an _____ Rule.

   *Answer:* Individual — “Vote Required for Adoption of an Individual Rule.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:35 — “Vote Required for Adoption of an Individual Rule.”

**1246.** `[C1508 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Vote Required to Amend or Rescind a Convention _____.

   *Answer:* Standing Rule — “Vote Required to Amend or Rescind a Convention Standing Rule.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:36 — “Vote Required to Amend or Rescind a Convention Standing Rule.”

**1247.** `[C1508 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Vote Required to Amend or Rescind a _____ Standing Rule.
   A. Convention
   B. landscaped
   C. recognized
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* A) Convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:36 — “Vote Required to Amend or Rescind a Convention Standing Rule.”

**1248.** `[C1508 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To amend or rescind a standing rule of a convention requires a two-thirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered, except that a rule _____ adoptable by a majority vote can be amended or rescinded by a majority vote after notice on at least the preceding day.

   *Answer:* individually — “To amend or rescind a standing rule of a convention requires a two-thirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered, except that a rule individually adoptable by a majority vote can be amended or rescinded by a majority vote after notice on at least the preceding day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:36 — “To amend or rescind a standing rule of a convention requires a two-thirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered, except that a rule individually adoptable by a majority vote can be amended or rescinded by a majority vote after notice on at least the preceding day.”

**1249.** `[C1508 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To amend or rescind a standing rule of a convention requires a two-thirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered, except that a rule _____ adoptable by a majority vote can be amended or rescinded by a majority vote after notice on at least the preceding day.
   A. interrupted
   B. individually
   C. Announcements
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) individually. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:36 — “To amend or rescind a standing rule of a convention requires a two-thirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered, except that a rule individually adoptable by a majority vote can be amended or rescinded by a majority vote after notice on at least the preceding day.”

**1250.** `[C1509 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ of a Convention Standing Rule.

   *Answer:* Suspension — “Suspension of a Convention Standing Rule.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:37 — “Suspension of a Convention Standing Rule.”

**1251.** `[C1509 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any standing rule of a convention (except one prescribing the _____ authority) can be suspended for a particular specified purpose by a majority vote, even if the rule required a two-thirds vote for its adoption.
   A. consideration
   B. parliamentary
   C. president-elect
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) parliamentary. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:37 — “Any standing rule of a convention (except one prescribing the parliamentary authority) can be suspended for a particular specified purpose by a majority vote, even if the rule required a two-thirds vote for its adoption.”

**1252.** `[C1509 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No standing rule of a convention can be suspended for the remainder of the session, and no standing rule which has only a single _____ can be suspended, since this would be equivalent to rescinding the rule, and the case would have to be treated accordingly.

   *Answer:* application — “No standing rule of a convention can be suspended for the remainder of the session, and no standing rule which has only a single application can be suspended, since this would be equivalent to rescinding the rule, and the case would have to be treated accordingly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:37 — “No standing rule of a convention can be suspended for the remainder of the session, and no standing rule which has only a single application can be suspended, since this would be equivalent to rescinding the rule, and the case would have to be treated accordingly.”

**1253.** `[C1509 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ of a Convention Standing Rule.
   A. Suspension
   B. incidental
   C. resolution
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* A) Suspension. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:37 — “Suspension of a Convention Standing Rule.”

**1254.** `[C1509 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any standing rule of a convention (except one prescribing the _____ authority) can be suspended for a particular specified purpose by a majority vote, even if the rule required a two-thirds vote for its adoption.

   *Answer:* parliamentary — “Any standing rule of a convention (except one prescribing the parliamentary authority) can be suspended for a particular specified purpose by a majority vote, even if the rule required a two-thirds vote for its adoption.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:37 — “Any standing rule of a convention (except one prescribing the parliamentary authority) can be suspended for a particular specified purpose by a majority vote, even if the rule required a two-thirds vote for its adoption.”

**1255.** `[C1510 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sample Set of _____ Standing Rules.

   *Answer:* Convention — “Sample Set of Convention Standing Rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:38 — “Sample Set of Convention Standing Rules.”

**1256.** `[C1510 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The standing rules of a convention must vary with its size, type, and _____.
   A. responsibilities
   B. Indefinitely
   C. President-Elect
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* A) responsibilities. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:38 — “The standing rules of a convention must vary with its size, type, and responsibilities.”

**1257.** `[C1510 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While it is not possible to frame model rules which are _____ applicable, the following sample set illustrates the nature of the standing rules adopted by many conventions.

   *Answer:* universally — “While it is not possible to frame model rules which are universally applicable, the following sample set illustrates the nature of the standing rules adopted by many conventions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:38 — “While it is not possible to frame model rules which are universally applicable, the following sample set illustrates the nature of the standing rules adopted by many conventions.”

**1258.** `[C1510 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sample Set of _____ Standing Rules.
   A. Unfinished
   B. sustaining
   C. Convention
   D. persuasive

   *Answer:* C) Convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:38 — “Sample Set of Convention Standing Rules.”

**1259.** `[C1510 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The standing rules of a convention must vary with its size, type, and _____.

   *Answer:* responsibilities — “The standing rules of a convention must vary with its size, type, and responsibilities.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:38 — “The standing rules of a convention must vary with its size, type, and responsibilities.”

**1260.** `[C1511 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (a) The Credentials Committee, directly after the opening ceremonies of the first business meeting, shall report the number of delegates and alternates registered as present with proper credentials, and shall make a _____ report after the opening exercises at the beginning of each day that business continues.

   *Answer:* supplementary — “(a) The Credentials Committee, directly after the opening ceremonies of the first business meeting, shall report the number of delegates and alternates registered as present with proper credentials, and shall make a supplementary report after the opening exercises at the beginning of each day that business continues.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:39 — “(a) The Credentials Committee, directly after the opening ceremonies of the first business meeting, shall report the number of delegates and alternates registered as present with proper credentials, and shall make a supplementary report after the opening exercises at the beginning of each day that business continues.”

**1261.** `[C1511 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (a) The Credentials Committee, directly after the opening ceremonies of the first business meeting, shall report the number of delegates and alternates registered as present with proper credentials, and shall make a _____ report after the opening exercises at the beginning of each day that business continues.
   A. supplementary
   B. representatives
   C. resolutions
   D. characteristics

   *Answer:* A) supplementary. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:39 — “(a) The Credentials Committee, directly after the opening ceremonies of the first business meeting, shall report the number of delegates and alternates registered as present with proper credentials, and shall make a supplementary report after the opening exercises at the beginning of each day that business continues.”

**1262.** `[C1512 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For admission to the assembly hall, to facilitate _____ and seating, members, alternates, and others shall be required to wear the badge issued by the Credentials Committee upon registration.

   *Answer:* identification — “For admission to the assembly hall, to facilitate identification and seating, members, alternates, and others shall be required to wear the badge issued by the Credentials Committee upon registration.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:40 — “For admission to the assembly hall, to facilitate identification and seating, members, alternates, and others shall be required to wear the badge issued by the Credentials Committee upon registration.”

**1263.** `[C1512 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For admission to the assembly hall, to facilitate _____ and seating, members, alternates, and others shall be required to wear the badge issued by the Credentials Committee upon registration.
   A. identification
   B. subsequently
   C. Announcements
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* A) identification. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:40 — “For admission to the assembly hall, to facilitate identification and seating, members, alternates, and others shall be required to wear the badge issued by the Credentials Committee upon registration.”

**1264.** `[C1513 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ offered by an individual member shall be in writing, signed by the maker and the seconder—each of whom shall be a voting member of the convention—and shall be sent directly to the desk of the Recording Secretary.

   *Answer:* resolution — “A resolution offered by an individual member shall be in writing, signed by the maker and the seconder—each of whom shall be a voting member of the convention—and shall be sent directly to the desk of the Recording Secretary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:41 — “A resolution offered by an individual member shall be in writing, signed by the maker and the seconder—each of whom shall be a voting member of the convention—and shall be sent directly to the desk of the Recording Secretary.”

**1265.** `[C1513 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ offered by an individual member shall be in writing, signed by the maker and the seconder—each of whom shall be a voting member of the convention—and shall be sent directly to the desk of the Recording Secretary.
   A. resolution
   B. Definitely
   C. authorizes
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:41 — “A resolution offered by an individual member shall be in writing, signed by the maker and the seconder—each of whom shall be a voting member of the convention—and shall be sent directly to the desk of the Recording Secretary.”

**1266.** `[C1514 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (a) All resolutions except those proposed by the Executive Board [or “Board of Directors,” “Board of Managers,” etc.] or by committees, and all _____ made in reports of officers or committees of the convention that are not in the form of resolutions, shall be referred without debate to the Resolutions Committee;

   *Answer:* recommendations — “(a) All resolutions except those proposed by the Executive Board [or “Board of Directors,” “Board of Managers,” etc.] or by committees, and all recommendations made in reports of officers or committees of the convention that are not in the form of resolutions, shall be referred without debate to the Resolutions Committee;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:42 — “(a) All resolutions except those proposed by the Executive Board [or “Board of Directors,” “Board of Managers,” etc.] or by committees, and all recommendations made in reports of officers or committees of the convention that are not in the form of resolutions, shall be referred without debate to the Resolutions Committee;”

**1267.** `[C1514 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (a) All _____ except those proposed by the Executive Board [or “Board of Directors,” “Board of Managers,” etc.] or by committees, and all recommendations made in reports of officers or committees of the convention that are not in the form of resolutions, shall be referred without debate to the Resolutions Committee;
   A. resolutions
   B. authorizes
   C. Organization
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:42 — “(a) All resolutions except those proposed by the Executive Board [or “Board of Directors,” “Board of Managers,” etc.] or by committees, and all recommendations made in reports of officers or committees of the convention that are not in the form of resolutions, shall be referred without debate to the Resolutions Committee;”

**1268.** `[C1515 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No member shall speak in debate more than once on the same question on the same day, or longer than two minutes, without permission of the convention granted by a _____ vote without debate.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “No member shall speak in debate more than once on the same question on the same day, or longer than two minutes, without permission of the convention granted by a two-thirds vote without debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:43 — “No member shall speak in debate more than once on the same question on the same day, or longer than two minutes, without permission of the convention granted by a two-thirds vote without debate.”

**1269.** `[C1515 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No member shall speak in debate more than once on the same question on the same day, or longer than _____, without permission of the convention granted by a two-thirds vote without debate.
   A. Strike Out
   B. was yielded
   C. two minutes
   D. Dilatory Use

   *Answer:* C) two minutes. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:43 — “No member shall speak in debate more than once on the same question on the same day, or longer than two minutes, without permission of the convention granted by a two-thirds vote without debate.”

**1270.** `[C1516 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: All reports and other material for the permanent record or printed proceedings shall be in typing and, immediately on _____, shall be sent to the Recording Secretary.

   *Answer:* presentation — “All reports and other material for the permanent record or printed proceedings shall be in typing and, immediately on presentation, shall be sent to the Recording Secretary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:44 — “All reports and other material for the permanent record or printed proceedings shall be in typing and, immediately on presentation, shall be sent to the Recording Secretary.”

**1271.** `[C1516 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: All reports and other material for the permanent record or printed proceedings shall be in typing and, immediately on _____, shall be sent to the Recording Secretary.
   A. presentation
   B. Announcements
   C. constitution
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* A) presentation. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:44 — “All reports and other material for the permanent record or printed proceedings shall be in typing and, immediately on presentation, shall be sent to the Recording Secretary.”

**1272.** `[C1517 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Nominations for each office to be filled by the convention shall be limited to one nominating speech of _____ and one seconding speech of one minute for each nominee.

   *Answer:* three minutes — “Nominations for each office to be filled by the convention shall be limited to one nominating speech of three minutes and one seconding speech of one minute for each nominee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:45 — “Nominations for each office to be filled by the convention shall be limited to one nominating speech of three minutes and one seconding speech of one minute for each nominee.”

**1273.** `[C1517 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Nominations for each office to be filled by the convention shall be limited to one nominating speech of three minutes and one seconding speech of _____ for each nominee.
   A. one minute
   B. Strike Out
   C. Request for
   D. Discharge a

   *Answer:* A) one minute. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:45 — “Nominations for each office to be filled by the convention shall be limited to one nominating speech of three minutes and one seconding speech of one minute for each nominee.”

**1274.** `[C1518 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Notices for announcement to the convention shall be in writing, signed by the person (or a proper _____ of the persons) under whose authority the announcement is issued, and shall be sent to the desk of the Recording Secretary.

   *Answer:* representative — “Notices for announcement to the convention shall be in writing, signed by the person (or a proper representative of the persons) under whose authority the announcement is issued, and shall be sent to the desk of the Recording Secretary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:46 — “Notices for announcement to the convention shall be in writing, signed by the person (or a proper representative of the persons) under whose authority the announcement is issued, and shall be sent to the desk of the Recording Secretary.”

**1275.** `[C1518 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Notices for announcement to the convention shall be in writing, signed by the person (or a proper _____ of the persons) under whose authority the announcement is issued, and shall be sent to the desk of the Recording Secretary.
   A. representative
   B. presentation
   C. Responsibilities
   D. Announcements

   *Answer:* A) representative. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:46 — “Notices for announcement to the convention shall be in writing, signed by the person (or a proper representative of the persons) under whose authority the announcement is issued, and shall be sent to the desk of the Recording Secretary.”

**1276.** `[C1519 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The rules contained in the current edition of _____ Order Newly Revised shall govern the convention in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with the bylaws of the Society [or “Federation,” “Association,” etc.] and these standing rules.

   *Answer:* Robert’s Rules of — “The rules contained in the current edition of Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised shall govern the convention in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with the bylaws of the Society [or “Federation,” “Association,” etc.] and these standing rules.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:47 — “The rules contained in the current edition of Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised shall govern the convention in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with the bylaws of the Society [or “Federation,” “Association,” etc.] and these standing rules.”

**1277.** `[C1519 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The rules contained in the current edition of Robert’s Rules of _____ shall govern the convention in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with the bylaws of the Society [or “Federation,” “Association,” etc.] and these standing rules.
   A. Before Its Adoption
   B. Conduct of Business
   C. Preferential voting
   D. Order Newly Revised

   *Answer:* D) Order Newly Revised. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:47 — “The rules contained in the current edition of Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised shall govern the convention in all cases to which they are applicable and in which they are not inconsistent with the bylaws of the Society [or “Federation,” “Association,” etc.] and these standing rules.”

**1278.** `[C1520 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the program is adopted by the voting body, with or without amendment, it becomes the _____ of the entire convention session (41).

   *Answer:* order of business — “When the program is adopted by the voting body, with or without amendment, it becomes the order of business of the entire convention session (41).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:48 — “When the program is adopted by the voting body, with or without amendment, it becomes the order of business of the entire convention session (41).”

**1279.** `[C1520 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Program Committee plans and submits the proposed schedule of meetings, _____, and special events of the convention.
   A. Unfinished
   B. requirement
   C. proceedings
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* C) proceedings. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:48 — “The Program Committee plans and submits the proposed schedule of meetings, proceedings, and special events of the convention.”

**1280.** `[C1520 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Program Committee plans and submits the proposed schedule of meetings, proceedings, and special events of the _____.

   *Answer:* convention — “The Program Committee plans and submits the proposed schedule of meetings, proceedings, and special events of the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:48 — “The Program Committee plans and submits the proposed schedule of meetings, proceedings, and special events of the convention.”

**1281.** `[C1520 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The program also commonly includes—interwoven throughout the convention timetable—a series of addresses, forums, workshops, exhibits, tours, and other activities designed for _____, motivational, or entertainment value.
   A. president-elect
   B. interrupted
   C. requirement
   D. membership-training

   *Answer:* D) membership-training. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:48 — “The program also commonly includes—interwoven throughout the convention timetable—a series of addresses, forums, workshops, exhibits, tours, and other activities designed for membership-training, motivational, or entertainment value.”

**1282.** `[C1520 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the program is adopted by the voting body, with or without amendment, it becomes the _____ of the entire convention session (41).
   A. Previous Question
   B. Cumulative Voting
   C. General Procedure
   D. order of business

   *Answer:* D) order of business. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:48 — “When the program is adopted by the voting body, with or without amendment, it becomes the order of business of the entire convention session (41).”

**1283.** `[C1521 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The nature of a convention Program Committee’s _____ is considerably more complex than for a committee of the same name in a local society that includes a “program” as a part of each meeting.

   *Answer:* responsibilities — “The nature of a convention Program Committee’s responsibilities is considerably more complex than for a committee of the same name in a local society that includes a “program” as a part of each meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:49 — “The nature of a convention Program Committee’s responsibilities is considerably more complex than for a committee of the same name in a local society that includes a “program” as a part of each meeting.”

**1284.** `[C1521 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The overall program must cover all aspects of the society’s work and commitments on the level at which the convention is held (district, state, national, etc.)—reviewing the period since the preceding convention and _____ the course of the society until the next convention.
   A. resolutions
   B. anticipating
   C. adjournment
   D. Preparation

   *Answer:* B) anticipating. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:49 — “The overall program must cover all aspects of the society’s work and commitments on the level at which the convention is held (district, state, national, etc.)—reviewing the period since the preceding convention and anticipating the course of the society until the next convention.”

**1285.** `[C1521 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition to enabling the convention to handle all business that it should consider within the time available, the program should be of such nature as to stimulate each delegate to an evaluation of the society’s policies, _____, and opportunities, inasmuch as benefit from the convention to the general membership may depend largely on the impression that each local president or delegate transmits to the unit he represents.

   *Answer:* accomplishments — “In addition to enabling the convention to handle all business that it should consider within the time available, the program should be of such nature as to stimulate each delegate to an evaluation of the society’s policies, accomplishments, and opportunities, inasmuch as benefit from the convention to the general membership may depend largely on the impression that each local president or delegate transmits to the unit he represents.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:49 — “In addition to enabling the convention to handle all business that it should consider within the time available, the program should be of such nature as to stimulate each delegate to an evaluation of the society’s policies, accomplishments, and opportunities, inasmuch as benefit from the convention to the general membership may depend largely on the impression that each local president or delegate transmits to the unit he represents.”

**1286.** `[C1521 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The nature of a convention Program Committee’s _____ is considerably more complex than for a committee of the same name in a local society that includes a “program” as a part of each meeting.
   A. presentation
   B. Preparation
   C. responsibilities
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* C) responsibilities. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:49 — “The nature of a convention Program Committee’s responsibilities is considerably more complex than for a committee of the same name in a local society that includes a “program” as a part of each meeting.”

**1287.** `[C1521 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The overall program must cover all aspects of the society’s work and commitments on the level at which the convention is held (district, state, national, etc.)—reviewing the period since the preceding convention and _____ the course of the society until the next convention.

   *Answer:* anticipating — “The overall program must cover all aspects of the society’s work and commitments on the level at which the convention is held (district, state, national, etc.)—reviewing the period since the preceding convention and anticipating the course of the society until the next convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:49 — “The overall program must cover all aspects of the society’s work and commitments on the level at which the convention is held (district, state, national, etc.)—reviewing the period since the preceding convention and anticipating the course of the society until the next convention.”

**1288.** `[C1522 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Program Committee usually begins work soon after the preceding convention closes, and its duties continue throughout the convention that it plans—so that it functions as virtually a standing committee of the _____.

   *Answer:* organization — “The Program Committee usually begins work soon after the preceding convention closes, and its duties continue throughout the convention that it plans—so that it functions as virtually a standing committee of the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:50 — “The Program Committee usually begins work soon after the preceding convention closes, and its duties continue throughout the convention that it plans—so that it functions as virtually a standing committee of the organization.”

**1289.** `[C1522 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Program Committee should work in close contact with the president and the _____.
   A. President-Elect
   B. parliamentarian
   C. subsequently
   D. specification

   *Answer:* B) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:50 — “The Program Committee should work in close contact with the president and the parliamentarian.”

**1290.** `[C1522 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Program Committee usually begins work soon after the preceding convention closes, and its duties continue throughout the convention that it plans—so that it functions as virtually a standing committee of the _____.
   A. Definitely
   B. immediately
   C. organization
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:50 — “The Program Committee usually begins work soon after the preceding convention closes, and its duties continue throughout the convention that it plans—so that it functions as virtually a standing committee of the organization.”

**1291.** `[C1522 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Program Committee should work in close contact with the president and the _____.

   *Answer:* parliamentarian — “The Program Committee should work in close contact with the president and the parliamentarian.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:50 — “The Program Committee should work in close contact with the president and the parliamentarian.”

**1292.** `[C1523 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although the program must come before the _____ for adoption and can be amended by it, many details must be decided far ahead.
   A. convention
   B. subsidiary
   C. sustaining
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:51 — “Although the program must come before the convention for adoption and can be amended by it, many details must be decided far ahead.”

**1293.** `[C1523 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Prior to the convention, the Program Committee must have the authority (sometimes with designated members of the executive committee or board as advisers, and often acting in cooperation with a Convention _____ Committee) to engage outside speakers or entertainers, to work out an order of business allotting appropriate amounts of time to each subject, and to make all necessary advance arrangements.

   *Answer:* Arrangements — “Prior to the convention, the Program Committee must have the authority (sometimes with designated members of the executive committee or board as advisers, and often acting in cooperation with a Convention Arrangements Committee) to engage outside speakers or entertainers, to work out an order of business allotting appropriate amounts of time to each subject, and to make all necessary advance arrangements.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:51 — “Prior to the convention, the Program Committee must have the authority (sometimes with designated members of the executive committee or board as advisers, and often acting in cooperation with a Convention Arrangements Committee) to engage outside speakers or entertainers, to work out an order of business allotting appropriate amounts of time to each subject, and to make all necessary advance arrangements.”

**1294.** `[C1523 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the program must come before the _____ for adoption and can be amended by it, many details must be decided far ahead.

   *Answer:* convention — “Although the program must come before the convention for adoption and can be amended by it, many details must be decided far ahead.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:51 — “Although the program must come before the convention for adoption and can be amended by it, many details must be decided far ahead.”

**1295.** `[C1523 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Prior to the convention, the Program Committee must have the authority (sometimes with designated members of the executive committee or board as advisers, and often acting in cooperation with a Convention _____ Committee) to engage outside speakers or entertainers, to work out an order of business allotting appropriate amounts of time to each subject, and to make all necessary advance arrangements.
   A. Announcements
   B. subsequently
   C. Arrangements
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) Arrangements. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:51 — “Prior to the convention, the Program Committee must have the authority (sometimes with designated members of the executive committee or board as advisers, and often acting in cooperation with a Convention Arrangements Committee) to engage outside speakers or entertainers, to work out an order of business allotting appropriate amounts of time to each subject, and to make all necessary advance arrangements.”

**1296.** `[C1524 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some societies send a tentative skeleton program to the _____ units several weeks beforehand as part of a printed “Call to Convention.” The complete program that the committee expects to recommend should be printed at the latest practical time for handing to each person as he registers at the convention.

   *Answer:* constituent — “Some societies send a tentative skeleton program to the constituent units several weeks beforehand as part of a printed “Call to Convention.” The complete program that the committee expects to recommend should be printed at the latest practical time for handing to each person as he registers at the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:52 — “Some societies send a tentative skeleton program to the constituent units several weeks beforehand as part of a printed “Call to Convention.” The complete program that the committee expects to recommend should be printed at the latest practical time for handing to each person as he registers at the convention.”

**1297.** `[C1524 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some societies send a tentative skeleton program to the _____ units several weeks beforehand as part of a printed “Call to Convention.” The complete program that the committee expects to recommend should be printed at the latest practical time for handing to each person as he registers at the convention.
   A. constituent
   B. Organization
   C. Preparation
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) constituent. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:52 — “Some societies send a tentative skeleton program to the constituent units several weeks beforehand as part of a printed “Call to Convention.” The complete program that the committee expects to recommend should be printed at the latest practical time for handing to each person as he registers at the convention.”

**1298.** `[C1525 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The order of business for the complete series of a convention’s business meetings normally includes, in expanded form, the elements of the one followed in ordinary meetings of the society’s _____ units (41).

   *Answer:* constituent — “The order of business for the complete series of a convention’s business meetings normally includes, in expanded form, the elements of the one followed in ordinary meetings of the society’s constituent units (41).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:53 — “The order of business for the complete series of a convention’s business meetings normally includes, in expanded form, the elements of the one followed in ordinary meetings of the society’s constituent units (41).”

**1299.** `[C1525 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of the convention, however, greater detail and precision are necessary for two principal reasons: (1) Adherence to a prearranged schedule is imperative if the convention is to complete its work—timing being an especially important factor if there are to be features carried by radio and television at particular hours, addresses by government officials, or appearances by _____ artists.
   A. presentation
   B. professional
   C. Announcements
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) professional. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:53 — “In the case of the convention, however, greater detail and precision are necessary for two principal reasons: (1) Adherence to a prearranged schedule is imperative if the convention is to complete its work—timing being an especially important factor if there are to be features carried by radio and television at particular hours, addresses by government officials, or appearances by professional artists.”

**1300.** `[C1525 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (2) Each member has the right to know at which meeting and at what _____ time a particular matter can be expected to come before the convention, so that he may avoid absence from the hall during important debates or votes.

   *Answer:* approximate — “(2) Each member has the right to know at which meeting and at what approximate time a particular matter can be expected to come before the convention, so that he may avoid absence from the hall during important debates or votes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:53 — “(2) Each member has the right to know at which meeting and at what approximate time a particular matter can be expected to come before the convention, so that he may avoid absence from the hall during important debates or votes.”

**1301.** `[C1525 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The order of business for the complete series of a convention’s business meetings normally includes, in expanded form, the elements of the one followed in ordinary meetings of the society’s _____ units (41).
   A. Procedures
   B. committees
   C. authorizes
   D. constituent

   *Answer:* D) constituent. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:53 — “The order of business for the complete series of a convention’s business meetings normally includes, in expanded form, the elements of the one followed in ordinary meetings of the society’s constituent units (41).”

**1302.** `[C1525 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of the convention, however, greater detail and precision are necessary for two principal reasons: (1) Adherence to a prearranged schedule is imperative if the convention is to complete its work—timing being an especially important factor if there are to be features carried by radio and television at particular hours, addresses by government officials, or appearances by _____ artists.

   *Answer:* professional — “In the case of the convention, however, greater detail and precision are necessary for two principal reasons: (1) Adherence to a prearranged schedule is imperative if the convention is to complete its work—timing being an especially important factor if there are to be features carried by radio and television at particular hours, addresses by government officials, or appearances by professional artists.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:53 — “In the case of the convention, however, greater detail and precision are necessary for two principal reasons: (1) Adherence to a prearranged schedule is imperative if the convention is to complete its work—timing being an especially important factor if there are to be features carried by radio and television at particular hours, addresses by government officials, or appearances by professional artists.”

**1303.** `[C1526 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The second part, listing the items or classes of business set for each meeting, is then known as the _____ (41).

   *Answer:* agenda — “The second part, listing the items or classes of business set for each meeting, is then known as the agenda (41).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:54 — “The second part, listing the items or classes of business set for each meeting, is then known as the agenda (41).”

**1304.** `[C1526 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Some _____ divide the printed convention program into two parts, the first of which gives the times and places of special events and—for each business meeting—only the hours of the call to order, adjournment, and any scheduled recesses.
   A. subsequently
   B. Announcements
   C. Indefinitely
   D. organizations

   *Answer:* D) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:54 — “Some organizations divide the printed convention program into two parts, the first of which gives the times and places of special events and—for each business meeting—only the hours of the call to order, adjournment, and any scheduled recesses.”

**1305.** `[C1526 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The second part, _____ the items or classes of business set for each meeting, is then known as the agenda (41).

   *Answer:* listing — “The second part, listing the items or classes of business set for each meeting, is then known as the agenda (41).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:54 — “The second part, listing the items or classes of business set for each meeting, is then known as the agenda (41).”

**1306.** `[C1526 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some _____ divide the printed convention program into two parts, the first of which gives the times and places of special events and—for each business meeting—only the hours of the call to order, adjournment, and any scheduled recesses.

   *Answer:* organizations — “Some organizations divide the printed convention program into two parts, the first of which gives the times and places of special events and—for each business meeting—only the hours of the call to order, adjournment, and any scheduled recesses.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:54 — “Some organizations divide the printed convention program into two parts, the first of which gives the times and places of special events and—for each business meeting—only the hours of the call to order, adjournment, and any scheduled recesses.”

**1307.** `[C1527 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While it is not possible to set out a model program that would be suitable for all _____, the following principles are commonly applicable:

   *Answer:* conventions — “While it is not possible to set out a model program that would be suitable for all conventions, the following principles are commonly applicable:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:55 — “While it is not possible to set out a model program that would be suitable for all conventions, the following principles are commonly applicable:”

**1308.** `[C1527 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While it is not possible to set out a model program that would be suitable for all _____, the following principles are commonly applicable:
   A. Unfinished
   B. resolutions
   C. Definitely
   D. conventions

   *Answer:* D) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:55 — “While it is not possible to set out a model program that would be suitable for all conventions, the following principles are commonly applicable:”

**1309.** `[C1528 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is often advisable to schedule a meeting of the executive body or board of the _____ a day after the close of the convention, asking the board members and other necessary personnel to remain in the convention city for this purpose.

   *Answer:* association — “It is often advisable to schedule a meeting of the executive body or board of the association a day after the close of the convention, asking the board members and other necessary personnel to remain in the convention city for this purpose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:56 — “It is often advisable to schedule a meeting of the executive body or board of the association a day after the close of the convention, asking the board members and other necessary personnel to remain in the convention city for this purpose.”

**1310.** `[C1528 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is often advisable to schedule a meeting of the executive body or board of the association a day after the close of the _____, asking the board members and other necessary personnel to remain in the convention city for this purpose.
   A. Reconsider
   B. convention
   C. landscaped
   D. subsidiary

   *Answer:* B) convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:56 — “It is often advisable to schedule a meeting of the executive body or board of the association a day after the close of the convention, asking the board members and other necessary personnel to remain in the convention city for this purpose.”

**1311.** `[C1529 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The program is the president’s guide as to the order of business during the initial _____, even before it has been formally adopted by the convention.

   *Answer:* proceedings — “The program is the president’s guide as to the order of business during the initial proceedings, even before it has been formally adopted by the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:57 — “The program is the president’s guide as to the order of business during the initial proceedings, even before it has been formally adopted by the convention.”

**1312.** `[C1529 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Directly after the adoption of the standing rules, the report of the Program Committee is _____—normally by the committee chairman—somewhat as follows: “Mr.
   A. Signature
   B. Crestwood
   C. presented
   D. rejecting

   *Answer:* C) presented. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:57 — “Directly after the adoption of the standing rules, the report of the Program Committee is presented—normally by the committee chairman—somewhat as follows: “Mr.”

**1313.** `[C1529 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: President, a printed copy of the program as proposed by the Program Committee is in the hands of each _____ for the convention.

   *Answer:* registrant — “President, a printed copy of the program as proposed by the Program Committee is in the hands of each registrant for the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:57 — “President, a printed copy of the program as proposed by the Program Committee is in the hands of each registrant for the convention.”

**1314.** `[C1529 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By direction of the committee I move the adoption of the program as printed.” (If the chairman or other person _____ the report is not a voting member of the convention, he omits the motion for adoption.
   A. landscaped
   B. Permission
   C. assemblies
   D. presenting

   *Answer:* D) presenting. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:57 — “By direction of the committee I move the adoption of the program as printed.” (If the chairman or other person presenting the report is not a voting member of the convention, he omits the motion for adoption.”

**1315.** `[C1529 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The program is the president’s guide as to the order of business during the initial _____, even before it has been formally adopted by the convention.
   A. proceedings
   B. Organization
   C. Particular
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) proceedings. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:57 — “The program is the president’s guide as to the order of business during the initial proceedings, even before it has been formally adopted by the convention.”

**1316.** `[C1530 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ vote adopts the program—even if it contains special orders.

   *Answer:* majority — “A majority vote adopts the program—even if it contains special orders.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:58 — “A majority vote adopts the program—even if it contains special orders.”

**1317.** `[C1530 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While the making of a special order requires a _____ vote under ordinary circumstances, the situation is different in the case of a convention program, where the special order is part of a complete order of business being adopted for the current session.
   A. A two-thirds vote
   B. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   C. A three-fourths vote
   D. A majority vote

   *Answer:* A) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:58 — “While the making of a special order requires a two-thirds vote under ordinary circumstances, the situation is different in the case of a convention program, where the special order is part of a complete order of business being adopted for the current session.”

**1318.** `[C1530 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If _____ changes in the program have become necessary, the chairman can make his report by saying: “Mr.

   *Answer:* last-minute — “If last-minute changes in the program have become necessary, the chairman can make his report by saying: “Mr.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:58 — “If last-minute changes in the program have become necessary, the chairman can make his report by saying: “Mr.”

**1319.** `[C1530 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: President, because of… [briefly indicating reasons], the Program Committee recommends the following _____ in its proposed program which has been printed and placed in the hands of each registrant for the convention:… [clearly stating each change, with reference to page and line in the printed program].
   A. requirement
   B. Organization
   C. consideration
   D. modifications

   *Answer:* D) modifications. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:58 — “President, because of… [briefly indicating reasons], the Program Committee recommends the following modifications in its proposed program which has been printed and placed in the hands of each registrant for the convention:… [clearly stating each change, with reference to page and line in the printed program].”

**1320.** `[C1530 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By _____ of the Program Committee, I move that, with these changes, the printed program be adopted.” This motion is debatable and amendable.

   *Answer:* direction — “By direction of the Program Committee, I move that, with these changes, the printed program be adopted.” This motion is debatable and amendable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:58 — “By direction of the Program Committee, I move that, with these changes, the printed program be adopted.” This motion is debatable and amendable.”

**1321.** `[C1531 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To change the _____ after its adoption requires a twothirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered —or unanimous consent, which can usually be obtained with no difficulty in cases where a departure from the program is justified.

   *Answer:* program — “To change the program after its adoption requires a twothirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered —or unanimous consent, which can usually be obtained with no difficulty in cases where a departure from the program is justified.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:59 — “To change the program after its adoption requires a twothirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered —or unanimous consent, which can usually be obtained with no difficulty in cases where a departure from the program is justified.”

**1322.** `[C1531 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To change the program after its adoption requires a twothirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the _____ who have been registered —or unanimous consent, which can usually be obtained with no difficulty in cases where a departure from the program is justified.

   *Answer:* convention — “To change the program after its adoption requires a twothirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered —or unanimous consent, which can usually be obtained with no difficulty in cases where a departure from the program is justified.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:59 — “To change the program after its adoption requires a twothirds vote or the vote of a majority of all the delegates or other “voting members” of the convention who have been registered —or unanimous consent, which can usually be obtained with no difficulty in cases where a departure from the program is justified.”

**1323.** `[C1531 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, an affirmative vote to adopt the program cannot be _____.
   A. Procedures
   B. reconsidered
   C. Organization
   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* B) reconsidered. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:59 — “Thus, an affirmative vote to adopt the program cannot be reconsidered.”

**1324.** `[C1531 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, an affirmative vote to adopt the program cannot be _____.

   *Answer:* reconsidered — “Thus, an affirmative vote to adopt the program cannot be reconsidered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:59 — “Thus, an affirmative vote to adopt the program cannot be reconsidered.”

**1325.** `[C1533 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The complex _____ necessary to a convention generally require the coordination of many additional details that are outside the province of any of the other committees mentioned in this chapter.

   *Answer:* arrangements — “The complex arrangements necessary to a convention generally require the coordination of many additional details that are outside the province of any of the other committees mentioned in this chapter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:61 — “The complex arrangements necessary to a convention generally require the coordination of many additional details that are outside the province of any of the other committees mentioned in this chapter.”

**1326.** `[C1533 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Most often the committee’s membership is largely made up of members of the _____ society or societies acting as convention hosts;
   A. Preparation
   B. constituent
   C. Convention
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* B) constituent. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:61 — “Most often the committee’s membership is largely made up of members of the constituent society or societies acting as convention hosts;”

**1327.** `[C1533 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: it should, however, include persons who have had experience in similar work at prior _____.

   *Answer:* conventions — “it should, however, include persons who have had experience in similar work at prior conventions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:61 — “it should, however, include persons who have had experience in similar work at prior conventions.”

**1328.** `[C1533 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where there has been competition between cities for the convention site, it is often well to place on this committee local members who were _____ in obtaining the selection of their city.
   A. requirement
   B. instrumental
   C. Preparation
   D. Parliamentary

   *Answer:* B) instrumental. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:61 — “In cases where there has been competition between cities for the convention site, it is often well to place on this committee local members who were instrumental in obtaining the selection of their city.”

**1329.** `[C1533 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The complex _____ necessary to a convention generally require the coordination of many additional details that are outside the province of any of the other committees mentioned in this chapter.
   A. Information
   B. requirement
   C. undebatable
   D. arrangements

   *Answer:* D) arrangements. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:61 — “The complex arrangements necessary to a convention generally require the coordination of many additional details that are outside the province of any of the other committees mentioned in this chapter.”

**1330.** `[C1534 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Convention _____ Committee may be empowered to consult experts, who may include professional convention managers.

   *Answer:* Arrangements — “The Convention Arrangements Committee may be empowered to consult experts, who may include professional convention managers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:62 — “The Convention Arrangements Committee may be empowered to consult experts, who may include professional convention managers.”

**1331.** `[C1534 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assistance frequently is obtainable also from staff members of hotels where _____ are held, and from convention bureaus in many cities.
   A. Unfinished
   B. conventions
   C. Reconsider
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) conventions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:62 — “Assistance frequently is obtainable also from staff members of hotels where conventions are held, and from convention bureaus in many cities.”

**1332.** `[C1534 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Convention _____ Committee may be empowered to consult experts, who may include professional convention managers.
   A. Arrangements
   B. Information
   C. deliberative
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* A) Arrangements. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:62 — “The Convention Arrangements Committee may be empowered to consult experts, who may include professional convention managers.”

**1333.** `[C1534 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assistance frequently is obtainable also from staff members of hotels where _____ are held, and from convention bureaus in many cities.

   *Answer:* conventions — “Assistance frequently is obtainable also from staff members of hotels where conventions are held, and from convention bureaus in many cities.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:62 — “Assistance frequently is obtainable also from staff members of hotels where conventions are held, and from convention bureaus in many cities.”

**1334.** `[C1535 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Depending on the size and duration of the convention, duties ordinarily assigned to the Convention Arrangements Committee are sometimes delegated to _____ or even distributed among separate committees.

   *Answer:* subcommittees — “Depending on the size and duration of the convention, duties ordinarily assigned to the Convention Arrangements Committee are sometimes delegated to subcommittees or even distributed among separate committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:63 — “Depending on the size and duration of the convention, duties ordinarily assigned to the Convention Arrangements Committee are sometimes delegated to subcommittees or even distributed among separate committees.”

**1335.** `[C1535 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: At the outset, the convention headquarters must be selected and advance arrangements made concerning room _____ in as many hotels or motor inns as may be necessary.
   A. accommodations
   B. deliberative
   C. specification
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* A) accommodations. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:63 — “At the outset, the convention headquarters must be selected and advance arrangements made concerning room accommodations in as many hotels or motor inns as may be necessary.”

**1336.** `[C1535 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The committee may work with the Credentials Committee in _____ room reservations for delegates with their registration for the convention.

   *Answer:* coordinating — “The committee may work with the Credentials Committee in coordinating room reservations for delegates with their registration for the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:63 — “The committee may work with the Credentials Committee in coordinating room reservations for delegates with their registration for the convention.”

**1337.** `[C1535 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Ensuring that _____ and honored guests are met at the airport or other point of arrival may also be one of the latter committee’s functions.
   A. Definitely
   B. dignitaries
   C. immediately
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) dignitaries. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:63 — “Ensuring that dignitaries and honored guests are met at the airport or other point of arrival may also be one of the latter committee’s functions.”

**1338.** `[C1535 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Depending on the size and duration of the convention, duties ordinarily assigned to the Convention Arrangements Committee are sometimes delegated to _____ or even distributed among separate committees.
   A. constitution
   B. subcommittees
   C. Information
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* B) subcommittees. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:63 — “Depending on the size and duration of the convention, duties ordinarily assigned to the Convention Arrangements Committee are sometimes delegated to subcommittees or even distributed among separate committees.”

**1339.** `[C1536 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Printed or reproduced material assembled in cooperation with the Program Committee for distribution to the delegates in advance of the convention should include directions for getting to the convention by the various means of _____ available, and information about the locality, points of interest, restaurants, entertainment, tours arranged by the Program Committee, and parking facilities for those driving to the convention.

   *Answer:* transportation — “Printed or reproduced material assembled in cooperation with the Program Committee for distribution to the delegates in advance of the convention should include directions for getting to the convention by the various means of transportation available, and information about the locality, points of interest, restaurants, entertainment, tours arranged by the Program Committee, and parking facilities for those driving to the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:64 — “Printed or reproduced material assembled in cooperation with the Program Committee for distribution to the delegates in advance of the convention should include directions for getting to the convention by the various means of transportation available, and information about the locality, points of interest, restaurants, entertainment, tours arranged by the Program Committee, and parking facilities for those driving to the convention.”

**1340.** `[C1536 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Printed or reproduced material assembled in cooperation with the Program Committee for distribution to the delegates in advance of the convention should include directions for getting to the convention by the various means of _____ available, and information about the locality, points of interest, restaurants, entertainment, tours arranged by the Program Committee, and parking facilities for those driving to the convention.
   A. Preparation
   B. characteristics
   C. deliberative
   D. transportation

   *Answer:* D) transportation. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:64 — “Printed or reproduced material assembled in cooperation with the Program Committee for distribution to the delegates in advance of the convention should include directions for getting to the convention by the various means of transportation available, and information about the locality, points of interest, restaurants, entertainment, tours arranged by the Program Committee, and parking facilities for those driving to the convention.”

**1341.** `[C1537 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Careful attention should be given to seating _____ within the hall, voting members always being located in a separate section if other persons are assigned seating space on the convention floor.

   *Answer:* arrangements — “Careful attention should be given to seating arrangements within the hall, voting members always being located in a separate section if other persons are assigned seating space on the convention floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:65 — “Careful attention should be given to seating arrangements within the hall, voting members always being located in a separate section if other persons are assigned seating space on the convention floor.”

**1342.** `[C1537 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Pages, messengers, ushers, and _____—who are essential to the good order of all but the smallest conventions—should be trained to perform their duties in a calm and courteous manner.
   A. doorkeepers
   B. Indefinitely
   C. constitution
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) doorkeepers. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:65 — “Pages, messengers, ushers, and doorkeepers—who are essential to the good order of all but the smallest conventions—should be trained to perform their duties in a calm and courteous manner.”

**1343.** `[C1537 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Careful attention should be given to seating arrangements within the hall, voting members always being located in a separate section if other persons are assigned seating space on the _____ floor.

   *Answer:* convention — “Careful attention should be given to seating arrangements within the hall, voting members always being located in a separate section if other persons are assigned seating space on the convention floor.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:65 — “Careful attention should be given to seating arrangements within the hall, voting members always being located in a separate section if other persons are assigned seating space on the convention floor.”

**1344.** `[C1537 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Pages, messengers, ushers, and _____—who are essential to the good order of all but the smallest conventions—should be trained to perform their duties in a calm and courteous manner.

   *Answer:* doorkeepers — “Pages, messengers, ushers, and doorkeepers—who are essential to the good order of all but the smallest conventions—should be trained to perform their duties in a calm and courteous manner.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:65 — “Pages, messengers, ushers, and doorkeepers—who are essential to the good order of all but the smallest conventions—should be trained to perform their duties in a calm and courteous manner.”

**1345.** `[C1538 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The staffing of an information desk throughout the convention may lie within the province of the Convention Arrangements Committee, together with additional functions in the areas of _____ and public relations.

   *Answer:* communications — “The staffing of an information desk throughout the convention may lie within the province of the Convention Arrangements Committee, together with additional functions in the areas of communications and public relations.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:66 — “The staffing of an information desk throughout the convention may lie within the province of the Convention Arrangements Committee, together with additional functions in the areas of communications and public relations.”
