**1346.** `[C1538 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the meetings are to be covered by the press, the _____ of the various media must be kept informed of developments and provided with an area on the floor near the platform, or in some other point of vantage in the hall.
   A. president-elect
   B. Indefinitely
   C. representatives
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* C) representatives. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:66 — “If the meetings are to be covered by the press, the representatives of the various media must be kept informed of developments and provided with an area on the floor near the platform, or in some other point of vantage in the hall.”

**1347.** `[C1538 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is often helpful to have facilities for typing and copying close at hand, as well as for the _____ of literature.

   *Answer:* distribution — “It is often helpful to have facilities for typing and copying close at hand, as well as for the distribution of literature.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:66 — “It is often helpful to have facilities for typing and copying close at hand, as well as for the distribution of literature.”

**1348.** `[C1538 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In very large conventions it is wise to investigate existing telephone and other _____ facilities and to provide for their augmentation if necessary.
   A. constitution
   B. communication
   C. specification
   D. Organization

   *Answer:* B) communication. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:66 — “In very large conventions it is wise to investigate existing telephone and other communication facilities and to provide for their augmentation if necessary.”

**1349.** `[C1538 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The staffing of an information desk throughout the convention may lie within the province of the Convention Arrangements Committee, together with additional functions in the areas of _____ and public relations.
   A. deliberative
   B. President-Elect
   C. specification
   D. communications

   *Answer:* D) communications. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:66 — “The staffing of an information desk throughout the convention may lie within the province of the Convention Arrangements Committee, together with additional functions in the areas of communications and public relations.”

**1350.** `[C1539 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The Resolutions Committee—also sometimes called the _____ or, in certain cases described below, the Platform Committee—has as its basic purpose the screening of all original main motions (10) that have not been screened by another committee and that come—or are to come—before the convention.

   *Answer:* Reference Committee — “The Resolutions Committee—also sometimes called the Reference Committee or, in certain cases described below, the Platform Committee—has as its basic purpose the screening of all original main motions (10) that have not been screened by another committee and that come—or are to come—before the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:67 — “The Resolutions Committee—also sometimes called the Reference Committee or, in certain cases described below, the Platform Committee—has as its basic purpose the screening of all original main motions (10) that have not been screened by another committee and that come—or are to come—before the convention.”

**1351.** `[C1539 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The Resolutions Committee—also sometimes called the Reference Committee or, in certain cases described below, the _____—has as its basic purpose the screening of all original main motions (10) that have not been screened by another committee and that come—or are to come—before the convention.
   A. Platform Committee
   B. in the affirmative
   C. Particular Session
   D. on a different day

   *Answer:* A) Platform Committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:67 — “The Resolutions Committee—also sometimes called the Reference Committee or, in certain cases described below, the Platform Committee—has as its basic purpose the screening of all original main motions (10) that have not been screened by another committee and that come—or are to come—before the convention.”

**1352.** `[C1540 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Variations in Rules Relating to the Resolutions _____.

   *Answer:* Committee — “Variations in Rules Relating to the Resolutions Committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:68 — “Variations in Rules Relating to the Resolutions Committee.”

**1353.** `[C1540 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Variations in Rules Relating to the _____ Committee.
   A. constitution
   B. Convention
   C. Procedures
   D. Resolutions

   *Answer:* D) Resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:68 — “Variations in Rules Relating to the Resolutions Committee.”

**1354.** `[C1540 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of a Resolutions Committee in a convention represents a limitation on the ordinary right of members to propose any number of motions from the floor without notice—such limitation arising from the need for keeping within a schedule and disposing of a large amount of business within a short time.

   *Answer:* establishment — “The establishment of a Resolutions Committee in a convention represents a limitation on the ordinary right of members to propose any number of motions from the floor without notice—such limitation arising from the need for keeping within a schedule and disposing of a large amount of business within a short time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:68 — “The establishment of a Resolutions Committee in a convention represents a limitation on the ordinary right of members to propose any number of motions from the floor without notice—such limitation arising from the need for keeping within a schedule and disposing of a large amount of business within a short time.”

**1355.** `[C1540 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The degree of limitation imposed and the manner in which the committee functions vary _____, depending on the organization, in particulars such as the following:
   A. considerably
   B. Permission
   C. Incidental
   D. Definitely

   *Answer:* A) considerably. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:68 — “The degree of limitation imposed and the manner in which the committee functions vary considerably, depending on the organization, in particulars such as the following:”

**1356.** `[C1540 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of a Resolutions Committee in a convention represents a limitation on the ordinary right of members to propose any number of motions from the floor without notice—such limitation arising from the need for keeping within a schedule and disposing of a large amount of business within a short time.
   A. requirement
   B. resolutions
   C. establishment
   D. representatives

   *Answer:* C) establishment. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:68 — “The establishment of a Resolutions Committee in a convention represents a limitation on the ordinary right of members to propose any number of motions from the floor without notice—such limitation arising from the need for keeping within a schedule and disposing of a large amount of business within a short time.”

**1357.** `[C1541 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Variations in the time when a resolution can be _____.

   *Answer:* introduced — “Variations in the time when a resolution can be introduced.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:69 — “Variations in the time when a resolution can be introduced.”

**1358.** `[C1541 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ in the time when a resolution can be introduced.
   A. Variations
   B. privileged
   C. authorizes
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* A) Variations. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:69 — “Variations in the time when a resolution can be introduced.”

**1359.** `[C1541 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A place in the program or agenda should then be provided at each meeting, under a heading such as “New Business,” for the _____ of resolutions.
   A. deliberative
   B. Organization
   C. constitution
   D. introduction

   *Answer:* D) introduction. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:69 — “A place in the program or agenda should then be provided at each meeting, under a heading such as “New Business,” for the introduction of resolutions.”

**1360.** `[C1541 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A place in the program or agenda should then be provided at each meeting, under a heading such as “New Business,” for the introduction of _____.

   *Answer:* resolutions — “A place in the program or agenda should then be provided at each meeting, under a heading such as “New Business,” for the introduction of resolutions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:69 — “A place in the program or agenda should then be provided at each meeting, under a heading such as “New Business,” for the introduction of resolutions.”

**1361.** `[C1542 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To save even this time in a convention, an _____ can be made whereby resolutions are submitted to the recording secretary without being formally moved and read in open meeting, and the secretary then must promptly deliver them to the chairman of the Resolutions Committee.

   *Answer:* arrangement — “To save even this time in a convention, an arrangement can be made whereby resolutions are submitted to the recording secretary without being formally moved and read in open meeting, and the secretary then must promptly deliver them to the chairman of the Resolutions Committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:70 — “To save even this time in a convention, an arrangement can be made whereby resolutions are submitted to the recording secretary without being formally moved and read in open meeting, and the secretary then must promptly deliver them to the chairman of the Resolutions Committee.”

**1362.** `[C1542 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To save even this time in a convention, an _____ can be made whereby resolutions are submitted to the recording secretary without being formally moved and read in open meeting, and the secretary then must promptly deliver them to the chairman of the Resolutions Committee.
   A. resolutions
   B. arrangement
   C. committees
   D. undebatable

   *Answer:* B) arrangement. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:70 — “To save even this time in a convention, an arrangement can be made whereby resolutions are submitted to the recording secretary without being formally moved and read in open meeting, and the secretary then must promptly deliver them to the chairman of the Resolutions Committee.”

**1363.** `[C1543 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases it is advisable to provide that resolutions can also be introduced at the convention if permitted by a _____ vote in the individual case.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “In such cases it is advisable to provide that resolutions can also be introduced at the convention if permitted by a two-thirds vote in the individual case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:71 — “In such cases it is advisable to provide that resolutions can also be introduced at the convention if permitted by a two-thirds vote in the individual case.”

**1364.** `[C1543 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If many resolutions are customarily proposed by members, a permanent rule or provision in the bylaws can be adopted by the _____ requiring all resolutions to be submitted to the committee, or to the executive secretary for delivery to the committee, a number of days, weeks, or even months in advance of the convention.
   A. requirement
   B. Permission
   C. organization
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* C) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:71 — “If many resolutions are customarily proposed by members, a permanent rule or provision in the bylaws can be adopted by the organization requiring all resolutions to be submitted to the committee, or to the executive secretary for delivery to the committee, a number of days, weeks, or even months in advance of the convention.”

**1365.** `[C1543 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This system can be arranged to allow time for sending copies of all resolutions considered by the Resolutions Committee to the constituent societies and their delegates in advance of the convention, thereby giving time for _____ and, possibly, instruction of delegates.

   *Answer:* consultation — “This system can be arranged to allow time for sending copies of all resolutions considered by the Resolutions Committee to the constituent societies and their delegates in advance of the convention, thereby giving time for consultation and, possibly, instruction of delegates.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:71 — “This system can be arranged to allow time for sending copies of all resolutions considered by the Resolutions Committee to the constituent societies and their delegates in advance of the convention, thereby giving time for consultation and, possibly, instruction of delegates.”

**1366.** `[C1543 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If many _____ are customarily proposed by members, a permanent rule or provision in the bylaws can be adopted by the organization requiring all resolutions to be submitted to the committee, or to the executive secretary for delivery to the committee, a number of days, weeks, or even months in advance of the convention.
   A. resolutions
   B. Convention
   C. convention
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:71 — “If many resolutions are customarily proposed by members, a permanent rule or provision in the bylaws can be adopted by the organization requiring all resolutions to be submitted to the committee, or to the executive secretary for delivery to the committee, a number of days, weeks, or even months in advance of the convention.”

**1367.** `[C1543 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such cases it is advisable to provide that resolutions can also be introduced at the convention if permitted by a _____ vote in the individual case.
   A. A three-fourths vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A unanimous (general consent) vote

   *Answer:* B) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:71 — “In such cases it is advisable to provide that resolutions can also be introduced at the convention if permitted by a two-thirds vote in the individual case.”

**1368.** `[C1544 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Variations in permitted origin of _____.

   *Answer:* resolutions — “Variations in permitted origin of resolutions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:72 — “Variations in permitted origin of resolutions.”

**1369.** `[C1544 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the ordinary case only the members of the convention—that is, the delegates—are allowed to introduce resolutions for _____ by the assembly, and other members of constituent societies (who are not convention members) are allowed to speak for the purpose of suggesting motions only with the consent of the convention.
   A. immediately
   B. resolutions
   C. consideration
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* C) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:72 — “In the ordinary case only the members of the convention—that is, the delegates—are allowed to introduce resolutions for consideration by the assembly, and other members of constituent societies (who are not convention members) are allowed to speak for the purpose of suggesting motions only with the consent of the convention.”

**1370.** `[C1544 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Variations in permitted origin of _____.
   A. Preparation
   B. Incidental
   C. resolutions
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* C) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:72 — “Variations in permitted origin of resolutions.”

**1371.** `[C1544 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the ordinary case only the members of the convention—that is, the delegates—are allowed to introduce resolutions for _____ by the assembly, and other members of constituent societies (who are not convention members) are allowed to speak for the purpose of suggesting motions only with the consent of the convention.

   *Answer:* consideration — “In the ordinary case only the members of the convention—that is, the delegates—are allowed to introduce resolutions for consideration by the assembly, and other members of constituent societies (who are not convention members) are allowed to speak for the purpose of suggesting motions only with the consent of the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:72 — “In the ordinary case only the members of the convention—that is, the delegates—are allowed to introduce resolutions for consideration by the assembly, and other members of constituent societies (who are not convention members) are allowed to speak for the purpose of suggesting motions only with the consent of the convention.”

**1372.** `[C1545 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some cases any member of a _____ society, whether he is a delegate or not, is permitted to offer a resolution.

   *Answer:* constituent — “In some cases any member of a constituent society, whether he is a delegate or not, is permitted to offer a resolution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:73 — “In some cases any member of a constituent society, whether he is a delegate or not, is permitted to offer a resolution.”

**1373.** `[C1545 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In other _____ a resolution is required to have the sponsorship of a constituent society itself.
   A. subsequently
   B. undebatable
   C. president-elect
   D. organizations

   *Answer:* D) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:73 — “In other organizations a resolution is required to have the sponsorship of a constituent society itself.”

**1374.** `[C1545 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In any society, when an officer or committee simply makes one or more _____, the Resolutions Committee is customarily assigned the task of putting the recommendation(s) in the form of resolution(s).

   *Answer:* recommendations — “In any society, when an officer or committee simply makes one or more recommendations, the Resolutions Committee is customarily assigned the task of putting the recommendation(s) in the form of resolution(s).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:73 — “In any society, when an officer or committee simply makes one or more recommendations, the Resolutions Committee is customarily assigned the task of putting the recommendation(s) in the form of resolution(s).”

**1375.** `[C1545 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some cases any member of a _____ society, whether he is a delegate or not, is permitted to offer a resolution.
   A. Procedures
   B. convention
   C. Particular
   D. constituent

   *Answer:* D) constituent. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:73 — “In some cases any member of a constituent society, whether he is a delegate or not, is permitted to offer a resolution.”

**1376.** `[C1545 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In other _____ a resolution is required to have the sponsorship of a constituent society itself.

   *Answer:* organizations — “In other organizations a resolution is required to have the sponsorship of a constituent society itself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:73 — “In other organizations a resolution is required to have the sponsorship of a constituent society itself.”

**1377.** `[C1546 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: while in still others, by vote of the committee—sometimes a _____ vote—the substance of the resolution can be altered and the resolution can be reported to the assembly in the altered form as though the committee had originated it.

   *Answer:* two-thirds — “while in still others, by vote of the committee—sometimes a two-thirds vote—the substance of the resolution can be altered and the resolution can be reported to the assembly in the altered form as though the committee had originated it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:74 — “while in still others, by vote of the committee—sometimes a two-thirds vote—the substance of the resolution can be altered and the resolution can be reported to the assembly in the altered form as though the committee had originated it.”

**1378.** `[C1546 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Variations in the power of the _____ committee.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. interrupted
   C. undebatable
   D. resolutions

   *Answer:* D) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:74 — “Variations in the power of the resolutions committee.”

**1379.** `[C1546 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the simplest _____, the Resolutions Committee has only the power to put resolutions in proper form, eliminate duplication where similar resolutions are offered, and ensure that all resolutions relating to a specific subject will be offered in a logical sequence.

   *Answer:* arrangement — “In the simplest arrangement, the Resolutions Committee has only the power to put resolutions in proper form, eliminate duplication where similar resolutions are offered, and ensure that all resolutions relating to a specific subject will be offered in a logical sequence.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:74 — “In the simplest arrangement, the Resolutions Committee has only the power to put resolutions in proper form, eliminate duplication where similar resolutions are offered, and ensure that all resolutions relating to a specific subject will be offered in a logical sequence.”

**1380.** `[C1546 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In other cases the committee is given the authority to make _____ alterations in a resolution, but only with the sponsor’s consent;
   A. Organization
   B. Permission
   C. substantive
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) substantive. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:74 — “In other cases the committee is given the authority to make substantive alterations in a resolution, but only with the sponsor’s consent;”

**1381.** `[C1546 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: while in still others, by vote of the committee—sometimes a _____ vote—the substance of the resolution can be altered and the resolution can be reported to the assembly in the altered form as though the committee had originated it.
   A. A unanimous (general consent) vote
   B. A two-thirds vote
   C. A majority vote
   D. A three-fourths vote

   *Answer:* B) A two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:74 — “while in still others, by vote of the committee—sometimes a two-thirds vote—the substance of the resolution can be altered and the resolution can be reported to the assembly in the altered form as though the committee had originated it.”

**1382.** `[C1547 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except as the rules may provide otherwise, the _____ Committee is required to report all resolutions referred to it;
   A. Resolutions
   B. assemblies
   C. Organization
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) Resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:75 — “Except as the rules may provide otherwise, the Resolutions Committee is required to report all resolutions referred to it;”

**1383.** `[C1547 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but the committee can, if it wishes, report a resolution with “no _____.” If the committee is given the power “not to report” a resolution—thus withholding it from consideration by the convention—a requirement of an unusually high vote within the committee (such as a three-fourths vote or a vote of two-thirds of the committee’s members) should always be imposed;

   *Answer:* recommendation — “but the committee can, if it wishes, report a resolution with “no recommendation.” If the committee is given the power “not to report” a resolution—thus withholding it from consideration by the convention—a requirement of an unusually high vote within the committee (such as a three-fourths vote or a vote of two-thirds of the committee’s members) should always be imposed;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:75 — “but the committee can, if it wishes, report a resolution with “no recommendation.” If the committee is given the power “not to report” a resolution—thus withholding it from consideration by the convention—a requirement of an unusually high vote within the committee (such as a three-fourths vote or a vote of two-thirds of the committee’s members) should always be imposed;”

**1384.** `[C1547 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except as the rules may provide otherwise, the _____ Committee is required to report all resolutions referred to it;

   *Answer:* Resolutions — “Except as the rules may provide otherwise, the Resolutions Committee is required to report all resolutions referred to it;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:75 — “Except as the rules may provide otherwise, the Resolutions Committee is required to report all resolutions referred to it;”

**1385.** `[C1547 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but the committee can, if it wishes, report a resolution with “no _____.” If the committee is given the power “not to report” a resolution—thus withholding it from consideration by the convention—a requirement of an unusually high vote within the committee (such as a three-fourths vote or a vote of two-thirds of the committee’s members) should always be imposed;
   A. consideration
   B. Preparation
   C. representatives
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* D) recommendation. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:75 — “but the committee can, if it wishes, report a resolution with “no recommendation.” If the committee is given the power “not to report” a resolution—thus withholding it from consideration by the convention—a requirement of an unusually high vote within the committee (such as a three-fourths vote or a vote of two-thirds of the committee’s members) should always be imposed;”

**1386.** `[C1548 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In political and certain other types of _____, the Resolutions Committee is required to prepare and report a platform for adoption by the organization, setting forth its views, aims, and aspirations.
   A. interrupted
   B. organizations
   C. Announcements
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* B) organizations. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:76 — “In political and certain other types of organizations, the Resolutions Committee is required to prepare and report a platform for adoption by the organization, setting forth its views, aims, and aspirations.”

**1387.** `[C1548 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Other _____ occasionally require the committee to draft statements of policy or similar documents that take the form of a platform rather than of a resolution.

   *Answer:* associations — “Other associations occasionally require the committee to draft statements of policy or similar documents that take the form of a platform rather than of a resolution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:76 — “Other associations occasionally require the committee to draft statements of policy or similar documents that take the form of a platform rather than of a resolution.”

**1388.** `[C1548 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In political and certain other types of organizations, the _____ Committee is required to prepare and report a platform for adoption by the organization, setting forth its views, aims, and aspirations.
   A. Information
   B. Resolutions
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Incidental

   *Answer:* B) Resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:76 — “In political and certain other types of organizations, the Resolutions Committee is required to prepare and report a platform for adoption by the organization, setting forth its views, aims, and aspirations.”

**1389.** `[C1548 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Other _____ occasionally require the committee to draft statements of policy or similar documents that take the form of a platform rather than of a resolution.
   A. Procedures
   B. associations
   C. Definitely
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* B) associations. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:76 — “Other associations occasionally require the committee to draft statements of policy or similar documents that take the form of a platform rather than of a resolution.”

**1390.** `[C1549 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the body of the paper, each paragraph, instead of opening with the enacting words “_____, That,” begins with a verb denoting an attitude or position—for example, “Affirms…,” “Assures…,” “Condemns…,” “Calls upon…,” and the like.

   *Answer:* Resolved — “In the body of the paper, each paragraph, instead of opening with the enacting words “Resolved, That,” begins with a verb denoting an attitude or position—for example, “Affirms…,” “Assures…,” “Condemns…,” “Calls upon…,” and the like.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:77 — “In the body of the paper, each paragraph, instead of opening with the enacting words “Resolved, That,” begins with a verb denoting an attitude or position—for example, “Affirms…,” “Assures…,” “Condemns…,” “Calls upon…,” and the like.”

**1391.** `[C1549 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is a preamble, instead of beginning each paragraph with the word “Whereas,” a _____ is used;
   A. sustaining
   B. participle
   C. convention
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* B) participle. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:77 — “If there is a preamble, instead of beginning each paragraph with the word “Whereas,” a participle is used;”

**1392.** `[C1549 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: thus, “Believing in the…, [etc.].” Each paragraph is terminated by a semicolon and, in the case of the _____ paragraph, the word “and.” The last paragraph of the preamble may be followed by the word “therefore.” Each new paragraph of the preamble begins with another participle.

   *Answer:* next-to-the-last — “thus, “Believing in the…, [etc.].” Each paragraph is terminated by a semicolon and, in the case of the next-to-the-last paragraph, the word “and.” The last paragraph of the preamble may be followed by the word “therefore.” Each new paragraph of the preamble begins with another participle.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:77 — “thus, “Believing in the…, [etc.].” Each paragraph is terminated by a semicolon and, in the case of the next-to-the-last paragraph, the word “and.” The last paragraph of the preamble may be followed by the word “therefore.” Each new paragraph of the preamble begins with another participle.”

**1393.** `[C1549 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is a preamble, instead of beginning each _____ with the word “Whereas,” a participle is used;
   A. paragraph
   B. reception
   C. Precedent
   D. extending

   *Answer:* A) paragraph. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:77 — “If there is a preamble, instead of beginning each paragraph with the word “Whereas,” a participle is used;”

**1394.** `[C1549 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As in a _____, no paragraph should contain a period within its structure.

   *Answer:* resolution — “As in a resolution, no paragraph should contain a period within its structure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:77 — “As in a resolution, no paragraph should contain a period within its structure.”

**1395.** `[C1550 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition to its duties in regard to the resolutions which are referred to it and which usually relate to policy matters, the Resolutions Committee is often charged with the duty of drafting and presenting to the assembly any _____ that may seem appropriate.

   *Answer:* courtesy resolutions — “In addition to its duties in regard to the resolutions which are referred to it and which usually relate to policy matters, the Resolutions Committee is often charged with the duty of drafting and presenting to the assembly any courtesy resolutions that may seem appropriate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:78 — “In addition to its duties in regard to the resolutions which are referred to it and which usually relate to policy matters, the Resolutions Committee is often charged with the duty of drafting and presenting to the assembly any courtesy resolutions that may seem appropriate.”

**1396.** `[C1550 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition to its duties in regard to the _____ which are referred to it and which usually relate to policy matters, the Resolutions Committee is often charged with the duty of drafting and presenting to the assembly any courtesy resolutions that may seem appropriate.
   A. resolutions
   B. Organization
   C. Reconsider
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* A) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:78 — “In addition to its duties in regard to the resolutions which are referred to it and which usually relate to policy matters, the Resolutions Committee is often charged with the duty of drafting and presenting to the assembly any courtesy resolutions that may seem appropriate.”

**1397.** `[C1550 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Ordinarily, courtesy resolutions express the appreciation of the convention to those who arranged _____ for its physical needs or rendered it service.

   *Answer:* accommodations — “Ordinarily, courtesy resolutions express the appreciation of the convention to those who arranged accommodations for its physical needs or rendered it service.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:78 — “Ordinarily, courtesy resolutions express the appreciation of the convention to those who arranged accommodations for its physical needs or rendered it service.”

**1398.** `[C1550 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Ordinarily, courtesy resolutions express the appreciation of the convention to those who arranged _____ for its physical needs or rendered it service.
   A. Information
   B. Preparation
   C. constitution
   D. accommodations

   *Answer:* D) accommodations. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:78 — “Ordinarily, courtesy resolutions express the appreciation of the convention to those who arranged accommodations for its physical needs or rendered it service.”

**1399.** `[C1551 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Ordinarily the Resolutions Committee should make known, through the program or _____, the times and places it will meet.
   A. recommendation
   B. president-elect
   C. announcements
   D. consideration

   *Answer:* C) announcements. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:79 — “Ordinarily the Resolutions Committee should make known, through the program or announcements, the times and places it will meet.”

**1400.** `[C1551 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is best to allow any sponsor of a _____ to appear before the committee to explain it and answer any questions about it;

   *Answer:* resolution — “It is best to allow any sponsor of a resolution to appear before the committee to explain it and answer any questions about it;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:79 — “It is best to allow any sponsor of a resolution to appear before the committee to explain it and answer any questions about it;”

**1401.** `[C1551 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and interested delegates also may be allowed to attend and even _____ in discussion.
   A. requirement
   B. participate
   C. interrupted
   D. Procedures

   *Answer:* B) participate. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:79 — “and interested delegates also may be allowed to attend and even participate in discussion.”

**1402.** `[C1551 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and interested delegates also may be allowed to attend and even participate in _____.

   *Answer:* discussion — “and interested delegates also may be allowed to attend and even participate in discussion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:79 — “and interested delegates also may be allowed to attend and even participate in discussion.”

**1403.** `[C1551 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ may be asked to attend the committee’s meetings.
   A. parliamentarian
   B. representatives
   C. Indefinitely
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* A) parliamentarian. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:79 — “The parliamentarian may be asked to attend the committee’s meetings.”

**1404.** `[C1552 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In reporting, the _____ Committee follows the procedure of any committee reporting back a resolution referred to it, as described in 51.

   *Answer:* Resolutions — “In reporting, the Resolutions Committee follows the procedure of any committee reporting back a resolution referred to it, as described in 51.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:80 — “In reporting, the Resolutions Committee follows the procedure of any committee reporting back a resolution referred to it, as described in 51.”

**1405.** `[C1552 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In reporting, the _____ Committee follows the procedure of any committee reporting back a resolution referred to it, as described in 51.
   A. Incidental
   B. constitution
   C. Resolutions
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* C) Resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:80 — “In reporting, the Resolutions Committee follows the procedure of any committee reporting back a resolution referred to it, as described in 51.”

**1406.** `[C1553 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the committee recommends amendments to a resolution, in cases where it is not empowered to _____ them itself, its chairman reports as follows:

   *Answer:* incorporate — “When the committee recommends amendments to a resolution, in cases where it is not empowered to incorporate them itself, its chairman reports as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:81 — “When the committee recommends amendments to a resolution, in cases where it is not empowered to incorporate them itself, its chairman reports as follows:”

**1407.** `[C1553 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the committee recommends amendments to a resolution, in cases where it is not empowered to _____ them itself, its chairman reports as follows:
   A. Procedures
   B. incorporate
   C. Indefinitely
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* B) incorporate. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:81 — “When the committee recommends amendments to a resolution, in cases where it is not empowered to incorporate them itself, its chairman reports as follows:”

**1408.** `[C1554 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In instances where it is advisable for a resolution of overriding importance to be considered as a special order rather than as a part of the main body of _____ reported by the Resolutions Committee, this is arranged through liaison with the Program Committee.

   *Answer:* resolutions — “In instances where it is advisable for a resolution of overriding importance to be considered as a special order rather than as a part of the main body of resolutions reported by the Resolutions Committee, this is arranged through liaison with the Program Committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:82 — “In instances where it is advisable for a resolution of overriding importance to be considered as a special order rather than as a part of the main body of resolutions reported by the Resolutions Committee, this is arranged through liaison with the Program Committee.”

**1409.** `[C1554 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If desired, the committee’s report can include a preliminary motion establishing special rules for the _____ of the resolution, similar to the practice of the U.S.
   A. specification
   B. consideration
   C. resolutions
   D. Parliamentarian

   *Answer:* B) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:82 — “If desired, the committee’s report can include a preliminary motion establishing special rules for the consideration of the resolution, similar to the practice of the U.S.”

**1410.** `[C1554 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ is an example of such a rule:

   *Answer:* following — “The following is an example of such a rule:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:82 — “The following is an example of such a rule:”

**1411.** `[C1554 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In instances where it is advisable for a resolution of overriding importance to be considered as a special order rather than as a part of the main body of _____ reported by the Resolutions Committee, this is arranged through liaison with the Program Committee.
   A. requirement
   B. Preparation
   C. resolutions
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) resolutions. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:82 — “In instances where it is advisable for a resolution of overriding importance to be considered as a special order rather than as a part of the main body of resolutions reported by the Resolutions Committee, this is arranged through liaison with the Program Committee.”

**1412.** `[C1554 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If desired, the committee’s report can include a preliminary motion establishing special rules for the _____ of the resolution, similar to the practice of the U.S.

   *Answer:* consideration — “If desired, the committee’s report can include a preliminary motion establishing special rules for the consideration of the resolution, similar to the practice of the U.S.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:82 — “If desired, the committee’s report can include a preliminary motion establishing special rules for the consideration of the resolution, similar to the practice of the U.S.”

**1413.** `[C1555 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The leaders are usually the more ardent or _____ advocates of the two positions, and frequently they speak first and save themselves enough time so that at the end they can close debate for their side.

   *Answer:* persuasive — “The leaders are usually the more ardent or persuasive advocates of the two positions, and frequently they speak first and save themselves enough time so that at the end they can close debate for their side.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:83 — “The leaders are usually the more ardent or persuasive advocates of the two positions, and frequently they speak first and save themselves enough time so that at the end they can close debate for their side.”

**1414.** `[C1555 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ procedure is to assign a longer period of debate to Mr.
   A. alternative
   B. interrupted
   C. assemblies
   D. Convention

   *Answer:* A) alternative. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:83 — “An alternative procedure is to assign a longer period of debate to Mr.”

**1415.** `[C1555 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: B, and _____ other members to adhere to a shorter limit.

   *Answer:* require — “B, and require other members to adhere to a shorter limit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:83 — “B, and require other members to adhere to a shorter limit.”

**1416.** `[C1555 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Often it is helpful to require general debate to be conducted first before _____ are allowed, but this provision can be dispensed with.
   A. landscaped
   B. Unfinished
   C. amendments
   D. convention

   *Answer:* C) amendments. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:83 — “Often it is helpful to require general debate to be conducted first before amendments are allowed, but this provision can be dispensed with.”

**1417.** `[C1555 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The leaders are usually the more ardent or _____ advocates of the two positions, and frequently they speak first and save themselves enough time so that at the end they can close debate for their side.
   A. persuasive
   B. Reconsider
   C. resolution
   D. Particular

   *Answer:* A) persuasive. See RONR (12th ed.) §59:83 — “The leaders are usually the more ardent or persuasive advocates of the two positions, and frequently they speak first and save themselves enough time so that at the end they can close debate for their side.”

**1418.** `[C1556 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A convention called only for a specific purpose not involving a permanent _____, or one called to form a state or a national society or a federation, is similar to a mass meeting as described in 53 in that when called to order it has no bylaws or officers.

   *Answer:* organization — “A convention called only for a specific purpose not involving a permanent organization, or one called to form a state or a national society or a federation, is similar to a mass meeting as described in 53 in that when called to order it has no bylaws or officers.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:1 — “A convention called only for a specific purpose not involving a permanent organization, or one called to form a state or a national society or a federation, is similar to a mass meeting as described in 53 in that when called to order it has no bylaws or officers.”

**1419.** `[C1556 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Because it has no bylaws, added difficulty may be _____ in determining who are the properly appointed delegates.
   A. encountered
   B. Preparation
   C. committees
   D. Permission

   *Answer:* A) encountered. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:1 — “Because it has no bylaws, added difficulty may be encountered in determining who are the properly appointed delegates.”

**1420.** `[C1556 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A convention called only for a specific purpose not involving a permanent _____, or one called to form a state or a national society or a federation, is similar to a mass meeting as described in 53 in that when called to order it has no bylaws or officers.
   A. Incidental
   B. Procedures
   C. requirement
   D. organization

   *Answer:* D) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:1 — “A convention called only for a specific purpose not involving a permanent organization, or one called to form a state or a national society or a federation, is similar to a mass meeting as described in 53 in that when called to order it has no bylaws or officers.”

**1421.** `[C1556 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Because it has no bylaws, added difficulty may be _____ in determining who are the properly appointed delegates.

   *Answer:* encountered — “Because it has no bylaws, added difficulty may be encountered in determining who are the properly appointed delegates.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:1 — “Because it has no bylaws, added difficulty may be encountered in determining who are the properly appointed delegates.”

**1422.** `[C1557 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The group sponsoring the convention should appoint a Convention Arrangements Committee, as described above, to secure the hall and _____ for the delegates, make the preliminary arrangements for the convention, and perform the other coordinating and arranging duties assigned to it.

   *Answer:* accommodations — “The group sponsoring the convention should appoint a Convention Arrangements Committee, as described above, to secure the hall and accommodations for the delegates, make the preliminary arrangements for the convention, and perform the other coordinating and arranging duties assigned to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:2 — “The group sponsoring the convention should appoint a Convention Arrangements Committee, as described above, to secure the hall and accommodations for the delegates, make the preliminary arrangements for the convention, and perform the other coordinating and arranging duties assigned to it.”

**1423.** `[C1557 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The group sponsoring the convention should appoint a Convention Arrangements Committee, as described above, to secure the hall and _____ for the delegates, make the preliminary arrangements for the convention, and perform the other coordinating and arranging duties assigned to it.
   A. accommodations
   B. specification
   C. characteristics
   D. recommendation

   *Answer:* A) accommodations. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:2 — “The group sponsoring the convention should appoint a Convention Arrangements Committee, as described above, to secure the hall and accommodations for the delegates, make the preliminary arrangements for the convention, and perform the other coordinating and arranging duties assigned to it.”

**1424.** `[C1558 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Someone designated by the sponsoring group—sometimes the chairman of the Convention _____ Committee— calls the meeting to order and presides during any opening exercises and the election of a temporary chairman.

   *Answer:* Arrangements — “Someone designated by the sponsoring group—sometimes the chairman of the Convention Arrangements Committee— calls the meeting to order and presides during any opening exercises and the election of a temporary chairman.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:3 — “Someone designated by the sponsoring group—sometimes the chairman of the Convention Arrangements Committee— calls the meeting to order and presides during any opening exercises and the election of a temporary chairman.”

**1425.** `[C1558 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Someone designated by the _____ group—sometimes the chairman of the Convention Arrangements Committee— calls the meeting to order and presides during any opening exercises and the election of a temporary chairman.
   A. sponsoring
   B. Substitute
   C. settlement
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* A) sponsoring. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:3 — “Someone designated by the sponsoring group—sometimes the chairman of the Convention Arrangements Committee— calls the meeting to order and presides during any opening exercises and the election of a temporary chairman.”

**1426.** `[C1558 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Next comes the appointment of the Credentials, Rules, and Program Committees, or the _____ of the prior selection of these committees.
   A. consideration
   B. immediately
   C. Announcements
   D. ratification

   *Answer:* D) ratification. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:3 — “Next comes the appointment of the Credentials, Rules, and Program Committees, or the ratification of the prior selection of these committees.”

**1427.** `[C1558 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Next comes the appointment of the Credentials, Rules, and Program Committees, or the _____ of the prior selection of these committees.

   *Answer:* ratification — “Next comes the appointment of the Credentials, Rules, and Program Committees, or the ratification of the prior selection of these committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:3 — “Next comes the appointment of the Credentials, Rules, and Program Committees, or the ratification of the prior selection of these committees.”

**1428.** `[C1559 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ Committee and other organizing committees were not appointed in advance and are not, therefore, ready to report, the time they need to prepare their reports is usually spent in listening to talks, perhaps on various phases of the convention’s object.

   *Answer:* Credentials — “If the Credentials Committee and other organizing committees were not appointed in advance and are not, therefore, ready to report, the time they need to prepare their reports is usually spent in listening to talks, perhaps on various phases of the convention’s object.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:4 — “If the Credentials Committee and other organizing committees were not appointed in advance and are not, therefore, ready to report, the time they need to prepare their reports is usually spent in listening to talks, perhaps on various phases of the convention’s object.”

**1429.** `[C1559 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Otherwise the reports of the organizing committees are received in the same manner as that described for organizing a convention of an _____ association.
   A. interrupted
   B. established
   C. Convention
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) established. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:4 — “Otherwise the reports of the organizing committees are received in the same manner as that described for organizing a convention of an established association.”

**1430.** `[C1559 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a permanent _____ is not contemplated, a permanent chairman and secretary can, but need not, be elected at this time, after which the convention proceeds with the business for which it was called together.

   *Answer:* organization — “If a permanent organization is not contemplated, a permanent chairman and secretary can, but need not, be elected at this time, after which the convention proceeds with the business for which it was called together.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:4 — “If a permanent organization is not contemplated, a permanent chairman and secretary can, but need not, be elected at this time, after which the convention proceeds with the business for which it was called together.”

**1431.** `[C1559 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the _____ Committee and other organizing committees were not appointed in advance and are not, therefore, ready to report, the time they need to prepare their reports is usually spent in listening to talks, perhaps on various phases of the convention’s object.
   A. Organization
   B. Indefinitely
   C. Credentials
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* C) Credentials. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:4 — “If the Credentials Committee and other organizing committees were not appointed in advance and are not, therefore, ready to report, the time they need to prepare their reports is usually spent in listening to talks, perhaps on various phases of the convention’s object.”

**1432.** `[C1559 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Otherwise the reports of the organizing committees are received in the same manner as that described for organizing a convention of an _____ association.

   *Answer:* established — “Otherwise the reports of the organizing committees are received in the same manner as that described for organizing a convention of an established association.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:4 — “Otherwise the reports of the organizing committees are received in the same manner as that described for organizing a convention of an established association.”

**1433.** `[C1560 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the convention is called to form a permanent _____, permanent officers are not elected until later (after the adoption of the bylaws), but a resolution should be adopted at this point in the proceedings expressing an intention to form such a permanent association, as in the case of forming a permanent local society (see 54).

   *Answer:* organization — “If the convention is called to form a permanent organization, permanent officers are not elected until later (after the adoption of the bylaws), but a resolution should be adopted at this point in the proceedings expressing an intention to form such a permanent association, as in the case of forming a permanent local society (see 54).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:5 — “If the convention is called to form a permanent organization, permanent officers are not elected until later (after the adoption of the bylaws), but a resolution should be adopted at this point in the proceedings expressing an intention to form such a permanent association, as in the case of forming a permanent local society (see 54).”

**1434.** `[C1560 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A set of bylaws should have been carefully drawn up before the meeting of the convention, either by a Bylaws Committee appointed by the organizing group, with the _____ being ratified by the convention, or by members of the sponsoring group who thereafter hand them to a Bylaws Committee appointed at the convention.
   A. adjournment
   B. appointments
   C. Definitely
   D. Information

   *Answer:* B) appointments. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:5 — “A set of bylaws should have been carefully drawn up before the meeting of the convention, either by a Bylaws Committee appointed by the organizing group, with the appointments being ratified by the convention, or by members of the sponsoring group who thereafter hand them to a Bylaws Committee appointed at the convention.”

**1435.** `[C1560 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A set of bylaws should have been carefully drawn up before the meeting of the convention, either by a Bylaws Committee _____ by the organizing group, with the appointments being ratified by the convention, or by members of the sponsoring group who thereafter hand them to a Bylaws Committee appointed at the convention.

   *Answer:* appointed — “A set of bylaws should have been carefully drawn up before the meeting of the convention, either by a Bylaws Committee appointed by the organizing group, with the appointments being ratified by the convention, or by members of the sponsoring group who thereafter hand them to a Bylaws Committee appointed at the convention.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:5 — “A set of bylaws should have been carefully drawn up before the meeting of the convention, either by a Bylaws Committee appointed by the organizing group, with the appointments being ratified by the convention, or by members of the sponsoring group who thereafter hand them to a Bylaws Committee appointed at the convention.”

**1436.** `[C1560 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the convention is called to form a permanent _____, permanent officers are not elected until later (after the adoption of the bylaws), but a resolution should be adopted at this point in the proceedings expressing an intention to form such a permanent association, as in the case of forming a permanent local society (see 54).
   A. undebatable
   B. organization
   C. presentation
   D. requirement

   *Answer:* B) organization. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:5 — “If the convention is called to form a permanent organization, permanent officers are not elected until later (after the adoption of the bylaws), but a resolution should be adopted at this point in the proceedings expressing an intention to form such a permanent association, as in the case of forming a permanent local society (see 54).”

**1437.** `[C1561 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After adoption of the bylaws, a Nominating Committee, selected in a manner as close as possible to that prescribed in the bylaws, nominates candidates for office, and those elected to these permanent offices take up their duties _____, unless other provision is made.

   *Answer:* immediately — “After adoption of the bylaws, a Nominating Committee, selected in a manner as close as possible to that prescribed in the bylaws, nominates candidates for office, and those elected to these permanent offices take up their duties immediately, unless other provision is made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:6 — “After adoption of the bylaws, a Nominating Committee, selected in a manner as close as possible to that prescribed in the bylaws, nominates candidates for office, and those elected to these permanent offices take up their duties immediately, unless other provision is made.”

**1438.** `[C1561 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After adoption of the bylaws, a Nominating Committee, selected in a manner as close as possible to that prescribed in the bylaws, nominates candidates for office, and those elected to these permanent offices take up their duties _____, unless other provision is made.
   A. Preparation
   B. Definitely
   C. Permission
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* D) immediately. See RONR (12th ed.) §60:6 — “After adoption of the bylaws, a Nominating Committee, selected in a manner as close as possible to that prescribed in the bylaws, nominates candidates for office, and those elected to these permanent offices take up their duties immediately, unless other provision is made.”
